Report_1_Synopsis: A TRACON Controller reported aircraft on approach were initiating go-arounds due to wind shear. A regional jet climbed uncontrollably 3;000 ft. above its assigned altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported the Ramp Agent gave the flight crew an incomplete wheelchair battery form during preflight. The Captain located the passenger and was able to properly configure the wheelchair for flight and completed the form prior to departure.
BE35 pilot reported loss of oil pressure in flight and after landing and parking; oil was observed along the entire fuselage. Inspection revealed an STC-approved quick drain oil plug was missing.
B767 Captain reported a gate return due to excessive force required to actuate the rudder pedals that was the result of a leaking rudder ratio module.

C172 flight instructor reported a complete failure of communication and navigation equipment while on an instrument approach and in reduced visibility. The instructor broke off the approach; remaining in visual conditions; and was eventually able to contact ATC on a cell phone and make a successful approach and landing.
A321 pilot reported the number 4 brake locked up during taxi-out requiring maintenance to meet the aircraft out on the taxiway.
B737 First Officer reported the aircraft encountered moderate wake and/or jet blast turbulence on short final at LAX from a departing B747.
GA pilot reported flying through severe turbulence near RDU airport resulting in difficulty controlling the aircraft and a descent below the MDA.

Air carrier Captain reported an intermittent fume event initially noted during boarding which dissipated after engine start. The strong odor resumed after takeoff which prompted the Captain to execute an immediate and safe air return for maintenance.
C172 student pilot on training flight with instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported taxiway incursion during taxi for takeoff in heavy rain.
First Officer reported ADC (Air Data Computer) #1 failure affected the cabin pressurization system; requiring a descent and a switch to #2 ADC; which restored cabin pressure control.
Air taxi Captain reported during short final approach another aircraft landed on the same runway; opposite direction. Air taxi flight crew performed a go-around and returned to land.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern of non-towered airport DYB. The instructor took control and maneuvered to avoid the other aircraft based on the ADS position on the flight instruments; then re-entered the pattern and landed safely.
Embraer Captain reported rejecting the takeoff due to #2 engine vibration after ingesting a bird on takeoff roll.
B787 First Officer reported during engine start they received a fire warning in one engine and were towed back into the gate.
Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC while doing basic maneuvers requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft during cruise near a non-towered airport. The instructor maneuvered away from the other aircraft to avoid the opposite direction conflict.

TRACON Controller reported not issuing a clearance correctly and then forgetting to advise the relieving controller about the aircraft's release.  Aircraft departed and turned into a higher MVA than was expected.
B737 Flight Crew reported a bird strike right after takeoff; resulting in an angle of attack vane to shear off.
CL350 pilot reported receiving a low altitude warning from ATC while being vectored for approach. Flight crew climbed to a safe altitude and continued approach.
Air Taxi flight crew reported not hearing ATC for a while during cruise.  Reportedly; they had not been properly handed off to the next Center.
Lancair Evolution pilot reported pitot static system icing in climb. The pilot diverted and landed without incident.

Flight Instructor reported that during landing rollout the student lost directional control of the aircraft through misapplication of the rudder pedals and locking up the brakes. The Flight Instructor regained control of the aircraft and safely exited the runway.
GA pilot on short final landing at a non-towered airport reported a conflict with an opposite direction arrival which had been transmitting their runway intentions on the wrong frequency.
EMB-175 flight crew reported they failed to secure the cockpit door for departure; citing multiple distractions as contributing.

B787-9 Technician reported noticing a chronic issue with the main landing gear shock strut door having loose hardware and broken safety wire and suggested there be inspections on the pedestal fittings.

CRJ-700 First Officer reported a low altitude warning from ATC while maneuvering for a visual approach in mountainous terrain during daylight VMC conditions. The crew corrected altitude then landed safely.
Helicopter Pilot reported an NMAC during pre-takeoff hover checks on runway.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported engine failure in flight. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
A320 pilot reported an engine stall warning and other erratic readings so they diverted to a suitable airport.
General aviation pilot reported a vehicle crossed the runway as the pilot was rotating. With inadequate distance to stop; the pilot continued takeoff and flew over the vehicle.

B737-800 flight crew reported encountering multiple bird strikes and upon landing; realized there was no nose wheel steering. The flight crew found out there was a hydraulic leak and the aircraft was towed to the gate.
Captain reported an engine fuel filter bypass light illuminated. Captain elected to request priority handling to land at destination airport.

A321 flight crew reported a fume event during preflight while parked at Gate. After trouble shooting with no change the Captain refused the aircraft; a logbook entry was submitted. A different aircraft was assigned.
Captain reported a NMAC with a glider while on arrival.

Cessna 172 pilot reported loss of directional control during landing roll and initiated a go-around during which pilot heard a bump sound. After return to base pilot learned they had struck a runway light resulting in aircraft damage.
Air carrier First Officer reported continuing the takeoff even after receiving a predictive wind shear warning because the calm ambient conditions seemed to preclude the possibility of wind shear.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a Ramp check upon arrival at destination which recovered a Hazmat documentation error. A discrepancy report was submitted by the Inspection Team.
Air carrier flight crew reported a passenger misconduct incident during flight that initiated due to non-compliance with face mask policy.

Air carrier Flight Crew reported being informed by ATC of previous sightings of a UAS. Flight crew experienced an airborne conflict with the UAS while climbing away from a Class B airport during departure.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence departing ORD in trail of an A320.
A TRACON Controller reported clearing an aircraft for a new approach and issued a crossing restriction below the MVA.
Air carrier pilot reported descending too low on final approach. Flight crew corrected and landed uneventfully.
Air Carrier Captain reported the RNP Runway 5 approach to RDU '...brings you in well left of runway centerline...'. The reporter has experienced this several times in different aircraft.
B-737 air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming during cruise in domestic airspace. The flight continued to the destination and landed.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC cancelled their takeoff clearance during takeoff roll due to an aircraft crossing the runway. Flight crew performed a rejected takeoff.

Pilot reported a critical ground conflict during the takeoff roll when another aircraft entered the runway at the opposite end. Pilot continued the takeoff roll and overflew the opposite end aircraft.

First Officer reported a critical ground conflict occurred when they entered the runway for departure at the same time as another departing aircraft due to clearance confusion. The First Officer stopped the aircraft and was instructed by ATC to exit the runway.
A Center Controller reported an Air Carrier was left on a heading too long and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Pilot reported a loss of GPS signal; possibly attributed to 5G interference.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a terrain alert while conducting a visual approach.
Flight Instructor with student reported alternator failure and a return to departure airport.
B787 Captain reported returning to departure airport after experiencing an engine overheat during initial climb.
A320 First Officer reported during gate pushback; another aircraft taxied by at a high rate of speed requiring the A320 crew to stop push to avoid a collision.
Flight Attendants reported physical distress and signs of hypoxia requiring medical attention after landing. Reportedly; there was a continuous loud noise in the cabin and pressurization issues.
C172 pilot reported a NMAC while on the traffic pattern of 3DW non-towered airport requiring evasive action.

MD-11 pilot reported the failure of [Inertial Reference Unit] 2 during climb led to the failure of the yaw damper and LSAS. The Flight Crew elected to perform an air turn back and make a precautionary landing at departure airport.
Ramp Agent reported the retaining strap between the aircraft and tug broke during towing allowing the aircraft to roll backwards on its own.
Flight instructor on training flight with student in a PA23 aircraft reported engine failure in cruise. Flight instructor diverted and landed without incident.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control during cruise flight in weather.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during approach. Flight crew climbed back to correct altitude and continued.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPS jamming on a flight near Iranian airspace.
A TRACON Supervisor reported an aircraft reported loss of engine power and intention to land in a field but than regained power when they turned on the fuel pump and landed safely at the airport.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion with the LGA airport La Guardia 7 SID engine out procedures.
Brooke Wolf Velocity pilot reported the landing gear horn and warning lights came on after takeoff. Pilot remained in traffic pattern and landed safely.
B737 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing BOS in trail of an Airbus.
Light twin pilot reported an NMAC shortly after takeoff at RUQ airport when another aircraft executing a training maneuver descended into the downwind leg of the pattern.
Q400 Captain reported of loss of fluid in the #2 hydraulic system resulted in an air turn back and precautionary landing.
Pilot reported glideslope problems while inbound to GLS airport.
Corporate pilot reported hearing a significant amount of chatter on guard frequency that was not related to safety of flight.

General aviation cargo pilot reported a near miss with another general aviation aircraft in marginal VFR weather conditions; during daylight; outside of the tower controlled; airport traffic area. Both aircraft maneuvered abruptly to avoid a collision when turning toward the airport.
RV-8 pilot reported they set an incorrect altitude in the autopilot during approach which resulted in a rapid descent and terrain alert. Pilot climbed back to altitude and continue the approach.
Flight crew reported executing a go around due to being below altitude; uncomfortable with the approach; and the airport environment.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAS shortly after takeoff. No evasive action was taken.
Center Controller and the Controller in Charger reported a general aviation aircraft encountered icing and temporarily lost control descending below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
CRJ-200 Captain reported dual MFD failure in cruise.
Air Carrier Captain reported the airport chart for MMQT should be updated.
PA-28 instructor pilot reported the student flying lost directional control after landing in a gusty crosswind and the aircraft departed the runway with no damage.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a fume event shortly after takeoff. After trouble shooting and evaluating the situation flight crew returned to departure airport.
B757 Captain reported a hydraulic system malfunction during cruise. Flight continued to destination and landed.

Ground Service Agent reported the tails of widebody aircraft parked at Gate G6 at SFO protrude over the vehicle service road and personnel doing a walkaround will need to walk onto that road; where there are vehicles driving.

MBB-BK117 pilot reported the rotor wash of the helicopter while performing a hover taxi over the taxiway caused a nearby aircraft that was fueling to move. The other aircraft did not have a parking brake function and the wheels were not chocked.
SR22 pilot reported the aircraft had a complete electrical failure but the pilot was able to perform a successful diversion and landing.
Air carrier EMB-175 flight crew reported loss of FMS sensors in cruise flight.
Ground personnel reported DG was assigned to the aircraft on the load plan but there was no DG to load. Aircraft returned to the gate to ensure no DG was loaded.

C150 instructor and student pilot reported an unknown equipment issue caused climbing performance issues during departure prompting an immediate return to the airport.  Tower had advised them of visible smoke coming from the aircraft.
GA pilot reported conducting a city tour and flying close to multiple towers within close proximity to the Class B airspace.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a flap overspeed during visual approach.  It was the First Officer's first flight after being out due to furloughs.
A TRACON Supervisor working a sector reported they vectored an aircraft away from conflicting traffic which placed the aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Cirrus Vision pilot reported a runway incursion and a ground conflict with another aircraft that was on final approach that had to perform a go-around.
PA-28 pilot reported landing gear retraction malfunction with vibration after departure. Upon landing; the aircraft veered off the runway.

EMB-190 Captain reported an engine failure after touchdown. After turning onto a taxiway; the flight crew contacted ground and explained their situation. ATC dispatched ARFF to observe and escort the aircraft to the gate; where passengers deplaned normally.

Air carrier First Officer reported a long landing possibly outside of the landing zone.  Reporter attributed discrepancy to possible company policy of adding speed to straight in landings and the aircraft being light in weight.
Pilot flying BE35 aircraft reported NMAC in cruise with SJ50 aircraft.
A319 pilot reported multiple ECAM and fault messages during cruise. Flight diverted to nearest suitable airport.
MD-11 pilot reported flight control difficulties in a turn during departure; followed by an aileron disagree message. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
General aviation Pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft while in a congested traffic pattern at a towered airport.
CL30 flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning during approach to an airport at night. The crew leveled off and resumed the approach once the warning ceased.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an RA fault on short final at FLL; citing possible 5G interference as the cause of the event.

B777 Captain reported refusing the aircraft due to extreme weather and low temperatures; lack of effective ground heaters and preconditioned air and a deferred APU made it impossible to warm the aircraft to operating temperatures.
GA student pilot with instructor reported an NMAC while on the downwind leg to PLU non-towered airport.
B737-800 air carrier pilot reported an IRS malfunction on the First Officer flight instruments during the climb. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed overweight.
Flight Attendant reported the gate agent advised them the exterior cabin door handle was in the open position after landing.
PA-28 flight Instructor with student reported the Tower Controller issued them a heading in the traffic pattern that could have put them on a collision course with another aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.
B737 crew reported receiving an 'oil filter bypass' warning just after reaching cruise altitude. The crew shut down the engine per checklist and returned to the departure airport.
BE-33 Flight Instructor reported the trainee turned to a heading that was different from what they had communicated to the Tower Controller and caused an NMAC.
CL600 Captain reported entering an incorrect altimeter setting during initial approach; resulting in aircraft being 1000 feet below their assigned altitude and flight towards terrain.
Air carrier First Officer reported an NMAC with a UAS during descent. No evasive action was taken.

B737 flight crew reported the landing gear system was malfunctioning during approach. After opening and closing the manual gear extension door to confirm it was secured; the flight crew was able to extend landing gear; and the flight continued to safe landing.
Air carrier pilot reported possible 5G interference while landing at LAX Runway 25L.
A TRACON Controller reported an Air Carrier descended to 500 feet above conflicting traffic due to the controller missing a read back or misstating an altitude clearance.
DA40 pilot with instructor reported an NMAC while practicing an ILS approach with an aircraft with no Mode C; nor ADS-B.  Pilot took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
Helicopter maintenance technicians reported that after scheduled maintenance they learned a pilot discovered during preflight inspection the tail rotor gearbox oil cap was not installed.
EMB Legacy pilot reported a CFTT event after receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during a localizer approach. Flight crew continued to landing after receiving a visual approach clearance.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported NMAC with Blackhawk helicopter.
Flight crew reported possible 5G interference at cruise; 20 NM North of CLT.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft after takeoff from a non-towered airport in daylight visual conditions. The pilot reported the aircraft in sight and adjusted the climb to provide separation; then departed the traffic pattern.
RV7 Pilot reported electrical failure inflight. Returned to departure airport.
A Tower Controller reported an aircraft instructed to hold short of the runway taxied on to the runway with another aircraft on short final.
Pilot on a post maintenance test flight flying CE-550 aircraft reported throttle and flap control malfunction inflight.
C172 Flight Instructor reported the aircraft lost power on initial climb necessitating a return to the airport.

A321 Captain reported while stopped during taxi another taxiing aircraft making a turn almost came in contact with the aircraft nose and flight deck windows. Captain stated the other aircraft corrected their path at the last moment and passed 20 feet from reporter's aircraft.

Single Corporate Pilot reported a Critical Ground Conflict while taxiing to the assigned take-off runway. The pilot states; approaching a taxiway intersection a 737 was vacating the runway and did not appear to be stopping. The pilot took evasive action by turning out of the way of the oncoming 737. Inquiring ATC about the incident; the controllers apologized and said they had failed to stop the airliner.
Flight instructor in holding pattern reported NMAC with another aircraft entering the approach.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported NMAC with another aircraft in traffic pattern.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below the MVA as a result of getting bumped around due to turbulence and mountain wave activity.

B737 First Officer reported while they were holding short for takeoff; tower instructed a smaller jet to taxi around the B737 with inadequate room to safely clear it. The Captain advised Tower of the problem and was then cleared for takeoff.
B777 Captain reported an increasing fuel Imbalance in cruise. The flight crew monitored the observed fuel imbalance and elected to continue to the destination airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported responding to a TCAS RA on final approach to PHL that was caused by a helicopter flying beneath them. Flight crew followed RA instructions and resumed approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported Ground Conflict with another aircraft during taxi to gate.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they learned of an FAR violation during a post flight review.
Flight Instructor flying BL8 aircraft reported the engine malfunctioned during descent.

EMB175 technician reported while troubleshooting the L1 arm/disarm mechanism it was found to be restricted. When they opened the door; the slide fell out; was partially blown and not attached properly. The diaphragm to door had been leaking and indicated zero psi.
EMB-145 Captain reported the nose steering locked up during taxi out resulting in having to be towed back to the gate for maintenance action.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert and a low altitude alert from ATC on approach to BOI when they misunderstood their descent clearance.
E145 First Officer reported the nose landing gear door had opened during cruise. The flight crew diverted; lowered the gear and landed safely.

Citation X First Officer reported they received Cabin Door Open and Cabin Vent Open CAS messages followed by a pressurization failure indication during initial climbout. They decided to return to the departure airport.

B737-800 flight crew reported receiving an engine overheat indication during departure climb. Flight crew performed an inflight engine shutdown and returned to departure airport for an uneventful landing.

General aviation jet Captain reported a traffic conflict when ATC sequenced their aircraft between two airliners on final approach with speed constraints to maintain the spacing. The Captain flew the assigned speed; but the spacing for the aircraft in trail was not able to be maintained and the trailing aircraft was directed to go around to maintain separation.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported burning odor 'similar to burning Lithium Ion battery' during climb. FA investigated the area and determined the 'possible' cause was spare Lithium batteries in passenger carry on bag. Flight executed a safe air return.

B767 flight crew reported unreliable air speed indications on the First Officer's side with EEC 1 and EEC 2 EICAS messages after take off. The messages cleared on their own along with an Aileron Lockout EICAS Message. The flight continued to the destination airport.
Pilot of an open cockpit biplane reported a misunderstood communication from the Tower resulted in a runway incursion and a go-around from the aircraft on approach.
B737-700 First Officer reported climbing through an altitude restriction departing DEN. A distracting wake turbulence encounter was cited as contributing.
Pilot reported issues with the condition of DWA airport including faded runway and taxiway markings and a rotating beacon with a non-operational light.

Air carrier crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while descending below the vertical path on the initial approach in visual conditions to GJT. The Captain stopped the descent then continued on a visual approach to a safe landing.
B737-700 flight crew reported they discovered a brake fire while parking and the plane was evacuated.
PA28 student pilot reported conflicting traffic indicated on display resulted in evasive action and a NMAC event.
B737-800 flight crew reported an engine fire was observed by another flight crew during taxi out. Flight crew decided to return to the gate.
Air carrier Captain reported flying into a normal controlled airport; but Tower was closed due to COVID and having numerous issues in the air and on the ground with other aircraft.
Captain reported a GPWS pull up alert during the initial approach phase of a night visual approach. The First Officer immediately pulled up; then continued the approach to a safe landing.
Captain reported a Rejected Take Off after receiving a #2 generator drive fault light which was a repeat write up.
General aviation pilot reported an NMAC at SMQ non-towered airport while entering the pattern with traffic operating from both runways.

JAN Tower Controller reported the fire alarm goes off periodically for no reason.  This is a known reoccurring issue.  Reporter stated not knowing if it is a real fire or a false alarm every time it goes off is an issue which causes distraction.
Pilot reported a distraction while learning the autopilot system led to a course deviation and a NMAC with another aircraft.
B737-800 Captain reported refusing an aircraft due to improper MEL application.
BE36 pilot landing experienced a wind shear on short final and lost control resulting in a damaged nose wheel assembly and a prop strike.

A220 pilot reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing false GPWS terrain warnings in cruise flight. The warnings became continuous and extremely distracting during the diversion; and the aircraft was taken out of service for maintenance.
Air carrier Captain reported an aircraft generated CFTT on approach into UNV airport in VMC. The Captain stated a normal approach was accomplished.

B767 flight crew reported they shut down one engine due to loss of oil pressure; diverted to the nearest airport; and after landing with one engine; the rear brakes caught fire which was extinguished by airport personnel.
Gulfstream 500 pilot reported they rejected the takeoff due to a flight control system no dispatch Crew Alert System message indication.

Air carrier First Officer reported ATC issued a low altitude alert during approach after the Captain established a high rate of descent to regain glide path. The flight crew corrected and returned to a stabilized approach.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during cruise in ZOA Class E airspace.
Air Carrier flight crew reported aircraft started an uncommanded roll on engine start with an expected bias for push back under control of ground crew resulted in a ground loss of control.
B737 MAX 8 First Officer reported finding unusual wear on a main tire which maintenance deferred for a later replacement.  First Officer was informed that it was a known problem.

Champion Citabria 7ECA flight crew reported a loss of directional control during Tail Wheel landing training which resulted in a runway excursion. On post flight inspection it was revealed that the right tail wheel spring had departed the plane; a damaged landing light and no injuries reported.
A Light Sport Aircraft pilot reported on landing roll they observed an Air Carrier departing from an intersecting runway and braked immediately to stop prior to the intersection.
Flight Instructor reported experiencing a NMAC while on a base leg in the traffic pattern that required evasive action to avoid another aircraft.
C172 Student Pilot doing solo touch and goes reported hitting a runway light.
PA-16 Flight Instructor and student pilot runway excursion during landing.
A Mooney pilot reported the engine was sputtering due to loss of fuel due to loss of fuel cap in flight and then quit while taxiing on the ramp.
Air carrier First Officer reported electrical malfunctions and flap disagree indications.
B777 pilot reported during taxi into gate a vehicle drove under their nose.
GA pilot reported they were on a runway holding short of an intersecting runway when they observed an aircraft about to land on their runway at ISP. They taxied off and advised Tower.
First Officer reported experiencing a TCAS Resolution Advisory to remain clear of conflicting traffic after departure from Newark airport.

An Air Carrier pilot reported aircraft taxiing on Taxiway ED in front of the Rwy 17R landing threshold at DEN can be a distraction to aircraft on short final as they appear to be crossing the runway. The reporter recommends notes on charts to warn crews.
Cessna 150 pilot reported an engine malfunction while in cruise flight over the ocean between islands. The pilot diverted to an alternate non-towered airport and landed safely.
Air Carrier pilot reported they contacted TRACON after a go around and were issued a new heading and a Low Altitude Alert.

Air carrier pilot reported a possible runway incursion while taxiing on Runway 6 with instructions to hold short of Runway 12L. Pilot recommends a 'Hot Spot' flag/designation for this location on the airport diagram to warn of potential for incursion.
Military Pilot reported an airborne conflict in the traffic pattern.
Challenger 350 Technician reported that MEL placards were not installed after life rafts were removed for overhaul.
A Corporate Jet Captain reported engine failure during initial climb and diversion to a nearby airport.
Captain reported a lack of taxiway lines and markings led to confusion regarding a taxiway and its proximity to the runway overrun area at the end of runway 18 at LIT airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a speed deviation under Class Bravo and then overshooting assigned altitude.  The Captain cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.

General aviation pilot reported during that their initial climb; they had a near mid air conflict with another aircraft due to their assumption that they were on a runway heading. They took evasive action to prevent a collision.

Air carrier pilot reported a fumes event where the flight attendants and passengers were experiencing symptoms that worsened over time from the slow deplaning process as the flight crew were not given a gate.

Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without LAANC authorization under the assumption that unlocking the manufacturer geofence was sufficient. Law enforcement officers approached the pilot after the flight and questioned them.

EMB-120 Captain reported an engine fire loop began to indicate erratically on climb. Pneumatic pressure began to fluctuate and cabin altitude could not be controlled. Flight crew performed an air turn back to departure airport.
Piper J3 Cub pilot reported the mageneto casing broke and detached from the engine during cruise resulting in loss of power and a successful off airport landing.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a bird strike while flying which resulted in damage to the UAS.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPWS alert on approach in addition to CRM failure.  Flight crew landed uneventfully.

TRACON Controller and pilot reported the pilot descended through the assigned altitude and continued below the minimum vectoring altitude. The controller issued a low altitude alert and instructed a climb back to the original cleared altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft rolled forward after setting parking brake at the gate. Captain applied brake pedals and stopped aircraft roll.
B777-200 First Officer reported diverting and making an overweight landing due to unreliable fuel quantity indications.
Flight Instructor with student reported a near miss during approach to UUU airport. Flight Instructor maneuvered to avoid traffic.
Air Carrier B737NG flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B757 on approach into EWR.
EMB-170 flight crew reported receiving multiple advisory messages on descent; then experienced nose wheel steering failure after landing; necessitating a tow into the gate.
C172 student pilot reported momentary loss of control on landing.

GA flight instructor reported a ground conflict during taxi in as a BE23 pilot lost control of the aircraft during rev up in the hangar area.  The flight instructor took evasive action while the BE23 impacted a hangar.

Air carrier flight crew reported they had to stop taxiing abruptly to avoid a collision with a vehicle that unexpectedly passed in front of the aircraft. The abrupt stop resulted in several flight attendants getting injured when they fell and requiring medical attention.
Supervisor of collegiate flight program reported student on solo cross country experienced a runway excursion. Supervisor dispatched a crew to assess the situation and retrieve the student.

PA-28-180 Flight Instructor reported a near mid air collision with a helicopter on a converging course immediately after becoming airborne from a tower controlled field.  Instructor took controls and made an evasive maneuver to avoid the helicopter.
Air Carrier Captain reported an NMAC with another air carrier crossing underneath them on final approach. The reporter stated they did not receive a TCAS/RA or traffic advisory from ATC.

Flight instructor and student reported that during landing practice in a Citabria tailwheel aircraft; the student applied aggressive braking which caused the tail to rise resulting in a propeller strike.

Cessna 140 pilot reported a runway excursion during landing.  Reportedly; the flying pilot was high and fast and bounced upon touchdown then they were blown off to the left by unexpected right crosswinds.
CE-680 pilot reported a primary and secondary stabilizer trim failure; which resulted in an immediate return to the departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a runway incursion at DFW when they misunderstood a taxi clearance. Aircraft on short final executed a go-around.
Pilot reported a propeller strike on a low wall during taxi after landing.
CVG Controller issued an IFR clearance while below the MVA resulted in a CFTT and an traffic conflict situation.
Air carrier flight crew reported during climbout ingesting a large bird into an engine. Flight returned to departure airport safely.
B737 NG FO reported his aircraft was 'significantly upset' just before touchdown at EWR by jet blast from a taxiing B777.

Technician reported an aircraft had to return to the departure airport after the forward avionics compartment door was left open during a maintenance action; causing the aircraft to be unable to pressurize.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they were flying in an approved location. The UAS had a near miss with a low flying rotor wing aircraft which departed from an off airport location.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence that resulted in an upset on descent into DEN and safely recovered.
MD-11 flight crew reported they found an unknown weight and balance form on the aircraft; but no other paperwork. Upon landing; the flight crew learned there was undocumented HAZMAT on board.
D01 Controller reported aircraft acknowledged traffic in sight then returned on frequency to report a NMAC.
PA-28 student pilot and their instructor; who was observing from the airport; reported the student veered off the runway after landing.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft experiencing icing could not maintain altitude and descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Pilot reported Maintenance Control incorrectly placed an MEL on a discrepancy.
Air Taxi Captain reported a Cabin Door warning light during cruise at 40;000 ft. The Captain performed some of the required checklist items and diverted to an alternate airport.
Captain reported getting an incorrect ACARS fuel weight from Dispatch.
Air carrier Captain reported a burning plastic odor during descent with no aircraft indications. The flight crew diverted where a safe landing was completed.
Captain reported concerns over push back procedures that had his wing tip pass over the wing of a parked aircraft on an adjacent gate.
General aviation pilot reported that their safety pilot saw a UAS pass near by their aircraft at 2;500 ft MSL. The incident was reported to the TRACON Controller.
Air carrier First Officer reported the aircraft experienced transponder and GPS failure due to potential GPS spoofing.

A Center Controller reported during a period of aircraft weather deviations into one sector no action was taken by Management or Traffic Management Unit (TMU) staff to reroute aircraft away from the sector causing it to exceed the designated maximum safe traffic numbers for that sector.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a complete loss of engine throttle control; resulting in an immediate return to the departure airfield.
Tower Controller reported a student pilot landed off runway after an engine failure due to fuel starvation while in the traffic pattern.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a 'Right Eng Oil Press' EICAS message during cruise due to oil pressure fluctuation. The flight crew requested special handling to a diversion airport where a safe landing was completed.
Air carrier flight crew reported while climbing on departure ATC warned them of a UAS reported by a preceding plane. Flight crew experienced a near midair collision with the reported UAS.
A319 technician reported discovering the crew O2 bottle shut off valve was safety wired backwards and would not keep the valve from being rotated to the closed position.
A300 flight crew reported an engine in flight shut down and diversion caused by incorrect fuel quantity indications.
B737-800 Captain reported concerns over maintenance practices at a station that caused unnecessary delays and aircraft refusals.
EMB 190 Captain reported finding damage to the right main landing gear on a post flight walk around inspection.

BE-33 pilot reported electrical smoke in the cockpit during cruise flight. The aircraft had recently been in maintenance servicing the autopilot and turn coordinator. The pilot reported returning to the origin airport.

Air carrier ground personnel reported the final weights manifest indicated Hazmat cargo was loaded onboard when in fact Hazmat cargo was not loaded. The final weight manifest was invalidated immediately and corrected.
Air carrier Captain reported an FMC failure in cruise prior to oceanic entry point resulted in return to departure airport.
DA-20 student pilot reported an NMAC event while conducting a check ride and transmitting on the wrong frequency. Reporter took evasive action to avoid the other aircraft.

Single Engine Pilot reported an engine failure due to fuel starvation. The pilot landed off airport and found the fuel tanks were dry. The pilot later landed at the nearby airport to refuel and continued to the original destination.

A319 flight crew reported a fumes event during pre-flight. Captain requested maintenance and submitted an AML discrepancy which resulting in the aircraft being removed from service and flight cancellation.
LAX Tower Controller reported an aircraft did not follow printed taxi instructions on 'the North route'. Reporter stated this has been an ongoing issue.
TPA TRACON Controller reported repeated frequency outages occur creating unsafe situations.
C172 pilot reported taxiway excursion onto grass area while turning on a wet taxiway. Pilot regained control and returned to paved surface. Post flight inspection revealed damage to propeller.
Citation Captain reported a baggage compartment smoke indication on descent. After landing; an inspection did not find any evidence of smoke.
G-IV flight crew reported a cockpit window shattered inflight. The crew followed the Quick Reference Handbook procedure and continued to destination.
B787 Captain reported encountering possible wake turbulence that resulted in a slight altitude deviation and flap overspeed.

Single pilot reported a NMAC with a flight school aircraft while at cruise altitude. The pilot stated the other aircraft was traveling the opposite direction and not communicating. The reporter took evasive action.
A321 pilot reported SLAT fault ECAM with SLATS failure during approach. The aircraft landed and taxied to the gate.

BE20 pilot reported a compressor stall on the left engine during descent; and elected to perform an inflight shutdown of the affected engine. The pilot then proceeded to make a precautionary landing at the destination airport.
B767 Captain reported receiving an aft cargo overheat message and erratic indications on the attitude director during cruise. Flight diverted and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew flying 777 aircraft reported engine failure during climbout.

Pilot of a Beechcraft Premier jet reported loss of directional control after takeoff. After regaining control and returning to the airport; a fuel imbalance of about 1300 pounds between the main fuel tanks was discovered.
Dispatcher reported a block turn back due to failed nose wheel steering actuator.
Air Carrier Captain reported correction on unstable approach resulted in an ATC altitude alert and a CFTT event.

General aviation pilot reported being instructed to hold their position by ATC after having been cleared for takeoff. The pilot had only rolled a few feet before ATC instructed them to hold their position. The pilot heard ATC instruct an aircraft to go-around and observed an aircraft fly directly overhead after stopping their aircraft on the runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC vectored them to intercept the ILS approach course but the ILS was set up for the opposite runway resulting in a temporary loss of aircraft control.
B757 First Officer reported an engine malfunction during cruise. The flight continued to the destination and landed.

Ramp Agent reported being inadvertently locked in a baggage pit when the cargo door was closed at the gate. Maintenance performed a pressurization check of the aircraft with the Ramp Agent in the baggage compartment. When aircraft was depressurized Ramp Agent exited without injury.
Captain reported diverting after losing cooling to both Inertial Reference Systems; which caused the failure of numerous systems.

Air carrier Captain reported attempting to exit the runway after landing only to discover that the taxiway had been barricaded. After contacting multiple parties; airport personnel finally assisted by temporarily removing the barricades to allow the aircraft to pass through. The reporter contributed the poor lighting of the runway and taxiway environment; the lack of NOTAMs referencing the barricades; and the airport's short-staffing contributed to the event.
Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern. While the instructor was descending over the airport another aircraft not communicating on CTAF flew directly beneath them.

Flight Instructor with a student at the controls of a Skyhawk 172 reported that the engine began to lose power during climb. The instructor then took control of the aircraft and landed.; "C680 First Officer reported EICAS Messages; EMERGENCY PRESSURIZATION and ACM BLEED LEAK were received. The flight crew immediately donned oxygen masks and descended. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing."
Air carrier flight crew reported conflicting ATC instructions during descent to VVNB airport.

Captain reported the airport weather reporting system; AWOS; was unreliable; as well as the PAPI vertical visual approach guidance system; and neither had been reported in the FAA NOTAM system for that airport.

Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported TSA failed to follow protocol regarding the handling of confiscated Hazmat items. Confiscated Hazmat items required personal handling and not allowed to be sent on baggage belt system.
A321 flight crew reported safely returning to departure airport after the #1 engine failed shortly after takeoff.

Challenger 300 Captain reported Right Engine Vibration after takeoff. The flight crew returned to departure airport. The Engine Vibration Indication Light remained on thru landing and after engine shut down.

A319 Captain reported there were multiple continuous ECAMs for an ENG 1 Reverser Fault during climb-out. After being unsuccessful in contacting Dispatch or Maintenance; the flight crew opted to perform an air turnback.
Air carrier First Officer reported a NMAC while on approach. The First Officer followed the procedures for an RA event.
Instructor pilot reported a NMAC on final approach to a non-towered airport with an aircraft not in communication on CTAF.

Air carrier Captain reported departure ramp personnel loaded a battery powered mobility device was loaded in cargo compartment without following required company or FAA Hazmat procedures. This resulted in the flight transporting undeclared Hazmat.
Center Controller reported an aircraft lost radios and transponder became inoperative. The aircraft deviated from its route and into conflict with another aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an RA while descending over VNY airport on approach to BUR. Flight crew complied with the RA and continued the approach.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence passing FL320 on descent into SEA.

Ground push crew member reported halting the tow abruptly as another airline entered the ramp unannounced to this push crew. The ground crew member also stated they waited for three other aircraft to enter the ramp that were being directed to do so from the tower.
PItts S-1C pilot reported a ground loop during landing resulted in a runway excursion and broken tailwheel assembly.

Air carrier First Officer reported miscommunication with SCT TRACON led to descending too early during approach to MRY airport resulting in a CFTT event.  ATC gave the crew a low altitude alert and instructed them to climb.

Flight Instructor had a bounced landing and while attempting to land the aircraft again; the nose impacted the runway. Postflight; the pilot learned the nose gear had separated during landing and airport operations had retrieved it from the runway.
Pilot in the VFR Pattern at a non-towered airport reported an aircraft on downwind behind them turned in front of them resulting in a NMAC.
B737-800 Captain reported experiencing an apparent fuel leak in the left tank during climb. Flight returned safely to departure airport.
AA5A Pilot reported an engine malfunction inflight and landed at a nearby airport.
A Lektro tug driver reported a taxiing aircraft maneuvering around another aircraft on the ramp almost collided with the tug and two ramp agents.
A Tower Controller reported a flight school aircraft on short final reported a NMAC with another flight school aircraft that had deviated from their instruction to land on the parallel runway.
C177 pilot flying in IMC and strong winds reported spatial disorientation and a temporary loss of control while being vectored for an approach.
B737 Captain reported a fumes event shortly after takeoff which dissipated after 3-4 minutes allowing flight to continue to its destination.
AFW Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict and cited airspace design as a contributing factor.
Pilot flying M20C aircraft reported engine power loss due to low fuel situation inflight.
Pilot reported they taxied off the runway at night at FMN airport; striking a taxiway light.

PA-28 instructor pilot reported a loss of control during landing on a training flight resulting in a runway excursion. The instructor was unable to prevent the aircraft exiting the runway into the grass and damaging a taxiway light and a sign.
Air carrier Captain reported an automation error between two similar sounding fixes; WITEN and WINEN; resulted in ATC issued course correction.
Pilot reported having a near mid-air conflict with another aircraft that was cleared on the same approach path at the same time.

A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft turned to the wrong heading and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude. The course deviation placed the aircraft in an area of poor radio coverage where ATC could not communicate with them.
Air carrier Captain reported that SJU TRACON informed the crew that the STAR they requested was NOTAMed out of service; however; other sources did not show any such NOTAMs.
E145 Flight Attendant reported there was smoke and burning in the flight deck's windshield and prepped the cabin for landing.

Commercial Pilot reported a parking brake malfunction while arriving at a ramp position after shutting down engines and attempting to set the parking brake. The aircraft rolled forward then the pilot applied the brakes manually. The aircraft was connected to a tug to reposition and no damage or injury occurred.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported a sudden non-mountainous wave activity in cruise.
CRJ900 pilot crew reported the #1 air data computer malfunctioned causing numerous instrument failures and an altitude deviation.
Pilot reported descending to an altitude; then having ATC call and advise they were below the MSA.
A320 First Officer reported failure of ground crew communication on pushback resulted in uncommanded aircraft movement and personnel to close to engine safety area.
C172 pilot reported engine issues soon after takeoff resulting in an immediate return to landing.  Post flight maintenance inspection revealed a cylinder had cracked.
B787 First Officer reported various malfunctions including loss of the right VHF radio after takeoff. The flight crew coordinated with ATC and returned to the departure airport.

EMB 170 flight crew reported an electrical malfunction during takeoff which resulted in two rejected takeoffs and a return to the gate on a training flight. The flight departed after maintenance deferred the generator drive unit.

General aviation air taxi pilot reported a near miss in MVFR flight conditions with another general aviation air taxi pilot in non-towered controlled airspace. The pilot indicated the other pilot maneuvered immediately to avoid the reporting aircraft.
CRJ-900 reported a rejected takeoff at approximately 50 knots for a 'L ENG DEGRADED' caution. Crew said N1 was at 40% when Captain rejected takeoff.
B737 flight crew reported loss of engine oil during cruise. Flight diverted and landed safely.

CRJ-900 flight crew reported they were not given a frequency change and were unable to reach ATC as they approached the airport. While attempting to establish communications the flight crew inadvertently descended below the minimum altitude for the area. Flight crew corrected altitude and established communication with ATC.
BE-33 pilot reported an alternator bearing failure resulted in an Air Turn Back and precautionary landing.
Pilot reported an NMAC with a UAS during descent. No evasive action was taken.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near midair collision with a UAS while on approach. No evasive action was taken.
Beech Super King Air pilot reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing engine issues related to low fuel quantity.

Small transport pilot reported an altitude deviation occurred while trying to reprogram the aircraft's autopilot that may have been potentially caused by a GPS anomaly. The aircraft's autopilot; which was set up for an RNAV approach; initiated a turn off course. As the pilot disconnected the autopilot and tried to determine the cause of the issue; the aircraft descended below the assigned altitude and the pilot was admonished by ATC. After landing; ATC stated that there had been numerous cases of GPS issues on the approach as of recent and asked if the pilot may have also experienced a GPS anomaly.
Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on approach to CYYJ; citing differences between the airplane's autoflight systems and the simulator used in training as contributing factors.
Air carrier Captain reported a loss of cabin pressure control during cruise. The crew made an immediate descent and landed at a nearby airport.
Cessna 172 pilot reported multiple bounces on landing that resulted in a prop strike and nose gear damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert while on approach.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they flew in controlled airspace without authorization.

A Center Controller working combined sectors reported they became distracted and could not focus on the safe operation of their sector after being notified of the requirement to respond to an email from OPM to describe their work by the end of the day.
An air carrier pilot reported MCO ramp has a pothole that required excessive thrust to taxi out of.
ZSE Center Controller reported an aircraft going below the Minimum IFR Altitude during a Runway 25 approach into DLS. The flight crew reportedly miss-programmed their descent profile.

Air carrier flight crew was given a traffic alert from air traffic control about a UAS near the final approach path. At 3;500 feet and near the final approach fix the flight crew saw a UAS pass 50 feet away. Aircraft landed without incident.
E175 Pilot not Flying reported that distractions from malfunctioning systems in the cockpit led to communications issues; resulting in a landing without clearance.
Dispatcher stated an aircraft reported multiple MFD's had failed and was returning to the airport.
G280 First Officer aircraft reported an altitude deviation in cruise flight.
Air carrier Captain reported a loss of nose wheel steering due to hydraulic leak and the aircraft had to be towed to the gate.
Two flight instructors in different aircraft reported they were involved in an NMAC in the pattern at APA when one of the aircraft cut off the other.

Captain reported a near miss with another aircraft while on an initial approach and communicating with ATC. The PF responded to a TCAS RA and maneuvered to avoid the other aircraft; then landed safely.
ERJ-170 First Officer reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing loss of cabin pressure.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS terrain warning on a visual approach to ROA.

B737-900 Captain reported disagreement and failure to follow procedures during a maintenance delay. The aircraft was subsequently removed from service after the Captain declined to remove a valid entry from the log book. The aircraft was swapped and the flight continued with another aircraft.
TRACON Departure Controller reported an aircraft deviated from their assigned heading and flew below the minimum vectoring altitude.
GIV-SP Captain reported a cracked forward left windshield during cruise in oceanic airspace. The flight crew performed the proper checklist and proceeded to divert to an alternate airport.
ZLA Controller reported an airspace deviation when an air carrier went through two sectors undetected; until the third sector Controller identified the aircraft.
B737-800 Captain reported the electric trim system failed on climb-out and the flight crew performed an air turnback. According to Maintenance Control; this was a repeat issue.

Air Carrier flight crew reported an engine failure during climb out. The pilots notified ATC regarding the failure and the probability of fumes in the cabin that had stricken two flight attendants. With clearance from ATC the aircraft returned to the departure airport and accomplished an overweight landing.
Light aircraft Flight Instructor reported experiencing an NMAC with another light aircraft in the pattern at HRU airport.
Tower Local Controller reported a departing aircraft flew over another aircraft taxiing across the runway.
Tower Controller reported a possible minimum vectoring altitude violation due to a miscommunication in regards to the assigned altitude of a go around instruction.

Cessna 172 Instructor Pilot reported an oil pressure decrease and high oil temperature while in the airport traffic pattern after takeoff; during VMC on an instructional flight. The Instructor turned direct for the runway and landed safely.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported waste tanks were indicating full during cruise at the halfway point on a 15 hour flight. The flight continued to the destination and landed safely.

EMB-145 flight crew reported during climb in icing conditions; they got an engine #2 ice fail message and a bleed 2 over temperature passing 10000 feet MSL. After switching to engine #1 bleed system; they received the same messages as the #2 engine.
A student pilot and their instructor reported the student turned the fuel selector switch off resulting in engine shutdown and a safe landing in a field.

C172 student pilot reported taking off while an aircraft was back taxiing on the same runway at a non-towered airport.  The pilot continued the takeoff and took evasive action by maneuvering to the right of the runway.
B737 Captain reported at push back with engines running; ramp personnel approached; opened cargo door and loaded a box without permission; resulting in immediate shutdown of engines.
Air carrier First Officer reported a 30 degree bank angle while turning final to PHX airport and also received a GPWS alarm.

General Aviation pilot reported an inadvertent entry into IMC and a momentary loss of aircraft control during initial climb. Pilot became reoriented and with ATC assistance was able to safely exit the area and continue to destination.
C172 student pilot and their instructor reported the solo student lost control of the aircraft while landing and ran off the runway.

A320 Captain reported an intermittent fumes event described as a 'dirty socks' odor that dissipated and returned during troubleshooting and while flying in and out of clouds. Fumes remained constant from approach descent to arrival gate.

Ramp tower reported identifying an aircraft moving when the push crew disconnected without notifying the pilots. Another push crew was requested to re-connect the aircraft to continue the push back without injury or damage.
Customer Service Representative reported personal issues with the removal of safety shields relating to health concerns.

Phenom 300 Flight Crew reported a critical ground conflict during landing after receiving landing clearance. Tower subsequently issued a go-around call on short final that was not received by the flight crew because the radio had inadvertently been switched to ground control.

Corporate turbine Captain reported a near miss with a glider while VMC on a IFR flight plan descending into a tower controlled airport on an ATC vector. The glider traffic was not seen until the last minute; and no avoidance maneuver completed. The flight continued to a safe landing.
Single Pilot Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training with another instruction flight on intersecting runway. Instructor Pilot departed the pattern after the event.
Military helicopter instructor pilot reported a light aircraft overflew them by about 300 feet while they were taxiing on the runway at OBI without communicating their intentions.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
An Airbus A320 pilot reported an Engine 1 Compression Vane Fault as take off power was applied; resulting in a rejected takeoff and return to the gate.

MDT TRACON Controller reported numerous conflicts occur between VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC and IFR aircraft on their final approach course. This is reportedly an ongoing issue and the reporter suggested the MDT airspace should be redesigned as a Class C.
Global 5000 Captain reported an over reliance on automation caused them to go below altitude on approach; requiring a climb and subsequently continuing the approach.

HS-125 Captain reported an electric trim failure caused vertical oscillations; which resulted in an autopilot failure. The flight crew elected to descend out of RVSM airspace and continued the flight to destination airport.
C172 pilot reported a Near Mid Air Collision while in the traffic pattern and took evasive action.

Ramp tower agent reported a ramp incursion between a ground vehicle and an aircraft taxiing on the ramp. The ground vehicle did not yield to the aircraft; resulting in the aircraft stopping urgently with no damage or injury.

Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming while flying through Ankara's airspace just south of Ukrainian airspace which caused the aircraft to turn toward Ukrainian airspace. Captain avoided the airspace and notified ATC.
Pilot reported loss of electrical power while on approach to landing. Pilot continued to land while asking for priority handling.
Columbia 400 Single Pilot reported a temporary loss of control resulting in a runway excursion due to nosewheel component failure on landing.
Lead Ramp personnel reported Dangerous Goods parcel flew undeclared without Captain notification.

Captain reported that during a post maintenance engine run up the helicopter experienced excessive vibration and was shut down. It was discovered that the tail rotor blades had been installed backwards.

B757 Captain reported complications and communications problems caused the aircraft to begin rolling without control on push back. The Captain stopped the aircraft with normal brakes and was informed that the aircraft had struck the tow bar; resulting in damage to the tow bar and both Nose Wheel Assemblies. The aircraft was moved back to the gate and had both Damaged Nose Wheel Assemblies removed and replaced.
G550 Captain reported a wake turbulence encounter contributed to an altitude overshoot departing MCO in trail of a B757.
Air carrier Line Check Airman reported loss of pressurization. The crew switched pressurization controllers and returned to the departure airport.
BE55 student and Flight Instructor reported the gear handle was inadvertently moved during a practice touch and go resulting in the gear collapsing and the aircraft sliding off the runway.

Air carrier flight crew reported that the left side PFD and MFD malfunctioned at the same location on Runway 22L at ORD during two takeoff attempts. Another aircraft experienced a similar issue at same location.
Air carrier flight crew reported missing re-setting the auto-brake system before takeoff and cited dealing with a passenger not complying with face mask policy as a contributing factor.
Air carrier flight crew reported while taxing for departure another aircraft crossed in front of their aircraft in an unsafe manner and distance.
C172 Flight Instructor reported an aircraft landed behind them on the same runway before they had exited the runway at a non towered airport.
GA pilot reported while practicing ground base maneuvers observing a UH-60 in close proximity to their aircraft resulting in the pilot taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the UH-60.

B737 NG flight Crew reported a Hydraulic System A EDP pressure low light on during cruise. The flight crew elected to continue the flight. The A Hydraulic System subsequently lost most system quantity. On descent; The System B EDP also failed. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at the destination airport.
Ramp personnel reported a vehicle driving behind an aircraft during push back.
A C172 pilot entering the pattern at a non towered airport reported a NMAC with traffic on downwind.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the loading form was not finalized and the Load Planner was told to finalize the form as it was already checked by Ramp; but could not be verified.

B737 First Officer reported a wrong heading input that was not caught during cross-check led to disorientation and unintended turns during a departure in moderate rain and night conditions. Time constraints and mismanagement of the autopilot during high workload also contributed to the error.
J-3 Cub pilot reported engine failure during takeoff climb resulting in an immediate turn back and landing.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a cell phone slipped into the fuselage cargo area and Maintenance delayed boarding to try to find the cell phone. The issue was deferred and the flight departed without the cell phone being retrieved.
Air carrier A321 flight crew reported a helicopter crossed over the runway while they were on short final.
A VFR pilot in cruise flight at 15;500 feet noticed a small UAS about 1000 feet below them flying in the opposite direction.
Flight crew reported engine 2 oil filter bypass light illuminated on climb. The crew performed an inflight shut down; followed by an air turn back and landing at departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported the Captain had to suddenly brake to avoid another taxiing aircraft which passed in front of them.

Air carrier First Officer reported a GPWS Terrain warning while on the ELP RNAV RNP Z 26L approach. The pilots believe the aircraft was on path and the warning did not coincide with the aircraft's position. During descent the crew stated they had also received a momentary GPS fail indication.
Air carrier pilot reported incorrect charting for EWR taxiways A9 and A10. Pilot stated the markings on the ground conflict with what is charted on the airport diagram.
B777 flight crew reported a mechanical problem with both engine generators during climb. The flight crew returned to the departure airport for an overweight landing.

Helicopter Pilot reported the MGT gauge indicated an overtemp condition. The pilot stated the problem had occurred several times before and was corrected by maintenance or corrected itself and chose to fly the aircraft with the overtemp condition in violation of policy.
Air Carrier pilot reported a loss of nose wheel steering caused the aircraft to be towed to the gate after landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control due to severe turbulence encountered during final approach. The flight crew regained control of the aircraft and continued to landing.
Pilot reported their navigation database does not contain the fix CADRE for the ROCKT1 SID from LUK airport.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of GPS on approach to DEN related to possible 5G interference.
Pilot reported ground handlers informed the flight crew that an orange safety cone had been ingested by the left engine during parking procedures for gate arrival.
Tug driver reported aircraft breakaway at gate resulted from nose gear coming out of cradle. Aircraft was stopped.
GA flight instructor reported pilot flying mistook taxi instructions resulting in a runway incursion.

General Aviation single-engine pilot reported taking off a from mountain airport and had difficulty climbing above the rising terrain on all sides of the aircraft. The pilot eventually gained enough altitude and then safely continued the flight.
Air carrier Captain reported a lack of airport signage and runway identification markings at EIDW/DUB as the taxiway was undergoing re-painting.

Air taxi crew reported a runway excursion when landing on a snow covered runway. The aircraft stopped beyond the last taxiway exit into the overrun; turned around and exited the runway without damage.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.
Air carrier pilot reported loss of aircraft control during taxi for takeoff. Captain regained control and flight departed eventually.
Cessna 172 pilots reported a NMAC; poor communications and taking evasive action at a non-towered airport.

A321 First Officer reported receiving a Low Hydraulic System Warning just after takeoff. The flight crew burned fuel to below maximum landing weight and performed a precautionary landing at the departure airport.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC with another helicopter that was supposed to be maintaining visual separation from the reporter.
TRACON Controller reported they vectored an air carrier to a localizer approach at an altitude below MVA. Controller issues a low altitude alert and climbed the aircraft to the appropriate altitude.
CRJ 550 Captain reported a brake overheat warning during cruise climb. Flight diverted and landed safely.
PA-18 Flight Instructor reported elevator damage found during subsequent student's preflight inspection.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into CLT from a B747 inbound to GSP.

Air Taxi flight crew reported discovering a prohibited electric scooter that was transported as cargo in violation of Hazmat Regulations. The violation was detected during unloading at the destination.

ZMP Center Controller reported many pilots have asked for a fix on approaches to Canadian airports due to the close proximity to ZMP's airspace and boundary but the lack of information on fixes in Canadian airspace made it difficult for ATC to provide assistance.

Private pilot reported a NMAC with vintage military aircraft. The pilot stated that the vintage aircraft pilot was making few if any radio calls regarding intentions and maneuvers at the non towered airport.
Pilot reported their aircraft had a complete electrical failure and returned to their departure airport .
A Center Controller reported a TBM9 departure did not contact them in a timely manner and stopped their climb below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a primary pitch trim failure during climb. The crew regained control through the back up system and returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Cessna 172 pilot and passenger reported an engine failure and off airport landing.

EMB-145 flight crew reported receiving a hydraulic system low quantity warning message after takeoff and eventual system failure. Flight crew returned to departure airport and after landing the aircraft was towed off the runway.
EMB-145 First Officer reported during final approach the GPWS Terrain Warning sounded at the FAF. The pilot reported the aircraft was near the correct altitude. The GPWS immediately corrected.

B737 Captain and First Officer reported the aircraft was creeping forward unnoticed by the flight crew while waiting for pushback. The Marshaller signaled the flight crew and they stopped the aircraft from rolling further.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported a NMAC during takeoff with helicopter that ATC cleared to cross in front of the PA28. Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
CL-60 First Officer reported loss of control including track and altitude deviations during severe turbulence encounter at FL410 associated with thunderstorm activity.
Air carrier flight crew reported the EGPWS alert sounded during takeoff resulting in an additional climb to avoid terrain.
Dispatcher reported aircraft crew stated a fuel leak with engine shut down resulted in a divert.
ERJ 145 First Officer reported they descended below the Final Approach Fix altitude and Tower issued them a low altitude alert. Flight crew initiated a go around and landed safely.
A321 Captain reported having to declare minimum fuel after the flight was delayed due to multiple radar vectors and holding. The flight continued to the original destination and landed.

B737-700 Captain reported a late runway change resulted in a programming confusion that caused a slight track deviation that was complicated by a wake turbulence encounter on descent into ATL. The Captain; who was pilot monitoring; did not catch the changes in the box that came from the runway change.

Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict during gate parking while following the directions of the marshaling crew. No stop signal was provided when a truck cut across the path of the aircraft.
Cessna C25 Captain reported ATC low altitude alert on approach.

Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict at LAS airport with outbound aircraft while taxiing to the gate. Reporter stated that Ramp Control did not hold them short of the alley to wait for traffic to exit the ramp.
C182 pilot reported engine exhaust manifold failure during departure climb.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC on approach to GON airport.
Air carrier Captain reported getting too low while on a visual approach to IND airport.

Corporate jet Captain reported executing a go-around from approach to SMO after experiencing GPS anomalies. Reporter stated he was notified that other aircraft experienced similar anomalies in that area.

First Officer reported that system annunciator lights began to illuminate so Flight Crew requested priority handling and made a landing at the destination airport. Maintenance determined an integrated drive generator had failed and on phase was not working.
Air carrier pilot reported a low altitude alert from ATC while inside the final approach fix.
B737 Captain reported the ground crew did not provide notification of dangerous goods being onboard and had it removed.
UAS Instructor reported they taxied in a movement area without a clearance. ATC then informed them of this incursion.
Air carrier ramp vehicle driver reported a near-collision with an aircraft that was taxiing into the gate area.
NCT Controller reported Aircraft X pilot misunderstood Tower instructions led to early level off below the MVA resulted in a CFTT event.
A flight Instructor conducting training for a single engine landing reported a prop strike and gear up landing resulting in a go around followed by a successful landing.
C152 pilot reported an engine malfunction during takeoff climb resulting in a return to the departure airport.
B777 Captain reported engine failure on auto throttle spool up required a rejected take-off and a return to the gate for maintenance action.
A Boeing 737-800 pilot reported a compressor stall; in the #1 engine; at cruise altitude; resulting in an inflight engine shutdown; and diversion to a suitable airport.

Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing in which a non reporting aircraft neglected to conduct a standard entry into the downwind. The flight Instructor executed an evasive maneuver to avoid a collision.
Pilot reported the low fuel pressure warning light illuminated during takeoff roll. Pilot rejected takeoff and discovered the fuel selector knob was not in the proper position.
A319 Captain reported loss of Green Hydraulic System in flight. The flight crew elected to continue to destination airport and requested a tow off the runway to the gate.

Air carrier Captain reported finding damaged and missing wing seals during preflight. Pilot reported they did not document all the discrepancies when they were advised by management to not document everything in order to avoid putting the aircraft out of service.

CRJ-900 Captain reported a transient left aileron malfunction described as aileron non-responsive to pilot's inputs.  The event lasted 15-20 seconds before returning to normal. Reporter did not provide any logical cause and no additional information provided.
EMB-145 flight crew reported an engine failure on takeoff resulting in an air turn back and a precautionary landing.
A300 First Officer reported a fumes event during takeoff which dissipated by 3;000 ft AFE. The odor was described as dirty gym socks.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving ADS-B FAULT messages and no GPS signal on the aircraft and was later informed by ATC upon inquiry that GPS jamming was occurring in the area. The GPS signal later returned with no navigation degradation throughout the GPS disruption.
C172 flight instructor with student reported a loss of engine power while maneuvering. Flight instructor flew to nearby airport and landed safely.

C-172 CFI reported a student pilot on his third solo landing experienced a loss of control and runway excursion.  Instructor stated the student did not implement appropriate cross wind corrections during landing.
Air carrier Captain reported the Captain's copy of the NOTOC was not in the aircraft's DG envelope was missing at destination arrival.
First Officer reported loss of the right hand generator with associated load shedding on take off. The flight crew performed an air turn back and landing at departure airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported severe turbulence at cruise altitude resulted in an altitude deviation and a Flight Attendant injury. The Captain recovered the aircraft and continued to destination where medical personnel met the flight.
EMB ERJ 170/175 First Officer reported the failure of MAU 2B in cruise. The Flight Crew made a precautionary landing at destination airport and the aircraft was towed to the gate.
Air carrier Captain reported temporary GPS navigation anomalies during descent.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported two incompatible Dangerous Goods shipments were loaded in same container.
Flight Instructor reported solo student pilot drifted off the runway during landing and onto the taxiway.
PA28 Solo Student Pilot and Instructor reported student loss of communication with ATC resulted in proceeding to diversion airport.
B737-800 flight crew reported a loud bang during takeoff roll and a rejected takeoff.
Air carrier pilot reported; while on a STAR into IST; Istanbul; a map shift occurred and the aircraft did not continue to the next waypoint. The pilots received radar vectors from ATC and continued.
Air carrier pilots reported a dual generator failure on their B-777 aircraft. The APU was started for electric power; ATC provided priority handling and a return to the origin airport was completed.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft missed a turn to intercept an airway and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B767 First Officer reported safely returning to departure airport after a cockpit window outer pane cracked during climb.
A319 Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing loss of the Green hydraulic system during cruise.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a clearance to fly direct to BRNDN intersection when ATC's intent was for the flight crew to fly direct to the Brandon VOR; YBR. The two fixes were both pronounced the same but spelled differently.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a fume event during descent which was characterized as a 'sweet magic marker type' odor. While the flight crew was proceeding with the appropriate QRH procedure the odor dissipated only to return on short final.
Government UAS crew reported an engine positioning control issue caused the UAS to enter into an uncontrollable spin. Efforts to control the spin failed and the UAS crashed.
EMB-175 flight crew reported severe turbulence in cruise flight that resulted in injuries to a passenger and a flight attendant.
General aviation pilot reported an NMAC with what they believed to be a kite or UAS. No evasive action was taken.

B737-800 flight crew reported the Final Flight Release was not amended to indicate Hazmat onboard prior to departure. The flight release error was due to a communications breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch.
A320 Captain reported a Flight Attendant reported 'burnt rubber fumes' during approach descent fumes.
An air carrier pilot reported a rejected takeoff due to a thrust reverser malfunction indication.

B737 Captain reported during pushback the aircraft wingtip was very close to an adjacent pushback aircraft. Pilot lost communication with tug driver who stopped abruptly resulting in the tow bar shear pin to break.
Air taxi flight crew reported that during short final approach an aircraft entered the runway for takeoff without making any radio calls requiring the flight crew to go around.
B737NG FO reported a passenger was injured during a wake turbulence encounter with an opposite direction B737 at FL340 in Jacksonville Center airspace.
EMB-145 flight crew reported go-around requested by tower due to an aircraft on the runway.

PA28 pilot reported a spatial disorientation event when his safety pilot elected to climb into IFR conditions despite not being qualified.  Pilot reported recovering from an undesired aircraft state at 800 AGL as they exited the cloud layer.
