Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during takeoff roll with a none reporting Eurocoper which passed within 300 feet above on a cross runway. The Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
EMB-145 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL326 enroute to PHL.
King Air pilot reported high engine oil temperature after engine start.
Student pilot and Instructor flying PA-28 aircraft reported engine power loss due to fuel starvation and successfully landed on the reverse runway.

A321 Captain reported a Flight Attendant notified them of burning fumes in the cabin which dissipated within five minutes. Flight continued to destination for an uneventful landing. Maintenance action was requested.
Flight Instructor with student reported a near miss with another aircraft while maneuvering in an area of extensive training. The instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
AT-400 pilot reported engine failure requiring an off airport landing.
PA-18 Pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing. A taxi light was damaged; the aircraft sustained a dent in the cowl; a prop strike; and no injuries.

Maintenance Technician reported after replacing a defective part the technician felt uncomfortable with management wanting them to sign off job as complete when the technician only updated the log page with their findings.
A single engine light aircraft pilot reported main landing gear collapsed while turning off the runway onto the taxiway.
Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with a VFR aircraft both under ATC control. The Captain responded to the TCAS commands and then continued descent as the VFR aircraft passed.
B737 Captain reported misgivings when the aircraft assigned for the flight had a faulty pressurization system and he would be operating in to and out of a high altitude airport.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering moderate turbulence on descent that resulted in large airspeed and altitude deviations.
ORD ramp worker reported returning a cargo loader to the terminal hardstand resulted in a collision with another loader parked in an inadequately lit area.

Air carrier Captain reported flying an approach that required aircraft to have a 5G filter with an aircraft that did not have a 5G filter installed. Captain stated the flight crew was confused over the 5G NOTAM and which approaches were allowed without a 5G filter.
Pilot reported an FMS error caused them to deviate off course. The pilot reported prior to the deviation they were issued a reroute to avoid military airspace that is uncharted.
MD-11 Captain reported unsafe gear indication after gear retraction. After consultation with Maintenance Control; the Captain elected to continue to the destination airport for landing.
Sailplane pilot reported the canopy departed the aircraft during takeoff. The pilot contacted ATC; dropped the tether and returned to land.
Citation 560XL pilot reported cracked windshield during climbout. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier ramp employee reported another ramp employee had to take evasive action to avoid an aircraft whose Captain had released brakes before being cleared to do so.
Technician reported landing gear pins were not removed after routine maintenance; resulting in an air turnback due to the landing gear not retracting after takeoff.

Airbus 319 Captain reported a malfunction of the parking brake during taxi. The aircraft failed to stop when the parking brake was set. The Captain returned to the gate and the aircraft was taken out of service.
Pilot reported receiving a runway crossing clearance while another aircraft was on take off roll and had yet to pass the crossing intersection.
EMB 175 Flight Crew reported taking evasive action when receiving a TCAS RA.

The pilot of a home-built; kit aircraft; while on an instructional flight; reported an engine failure; and subsequent loss of battery power; and all communications. This resulted in landing without receiving a clearance from the tower.
Air carrier Captain reported navigation displays disappeared while on approach and First Officer hand flew aircraft to a safe landing.

Air carrier Flight Attendant crew reported the simultaneous failure of the cabin electrical power and a fume event during cruise. After notifying the flight deck crew and communicating with Dispatch the flight was directed to continue to destination.
Pushout Driver reported the tow bar pins broke and the tow bar damaged the aircraft's nose gear.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they were concluding an autonomous flight mission when the UAS veered away from the landing spot; resulting in a fly away. The UAS was lost and unrecoverable.
MD-11 First Officer reported after landing Maintenance notified them of damaged fan blades in one of the engines.
Cessna 182 pilot reported an electrical failure approximately 12 miles from the destination. Receiving light gun signals from the tower; the pilot landed safely.
Instructor Pilot reported having to take evasive action to avoid a mid air collision while in a designated practice area.
CRJ flight crew reported multiple caution message after takeoff. The flight crew performed an air turn back and made a precautionary landing at departure airport.

Lear 35 Captain reported erratic ITT indications; a right engine flame out; and an unresponsive throttle at idle on the left engine; led to a dead stick approach and successful landing at a nearby airport.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a NMAC during descent to land.
Air carrier pilot reported glideslope indication deflected full scale on approach. Flight crew disconnected autopilot and continued approach using PAPI guidance.

B737 First Officer reported a loud noise while braking on the landing roll; then observed the cockpit door was stuck by a galley cart and damaged. The pilots taxied to the gate; but had to exit through the emergency cockpit door with no injuries.

Air carrier First Officer reported the flight pushed back from the gate before receiving the Final DG documents and transmitting the DG acknowledgement. After completed the pushback; the crew waited until the Final DG documents and DG acknowledgement was received and completed before initiating taxi.
A Flight Crew reported they initiated a go around on short final due to a VFR aircraft on TCAS which appeared to be on a conflicting course.
Air carrier flight crew reported an encounter with severe turbulence during cruise resulted in temporary loss of aircraft control and a flight attendant injury.

Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training caused by a reckless maneuver of another aircraft in the landing pattern. The other pilot denied the event and refused to accept his reckless maneuver.

General aviation instructor and student reported a wingtip strike with another aircraft after landing and while taxiing on an intersecting taxiway during a training flight at a non-towered airport. The student added power and too much rudder before the instructor could react.
Ground personnel reported a ground cart driving under the tail of an aircraft during push back. Aircraft was brought to a stop.
A321 flight crew reported aircraft loss of control during parking procedure at gate.

B737 First Officer reported a baggage tug and cart cut in front of the aircraft as the Captain was turning into the gate. The First Officer gave an urgent verbal warning to the Captain who quickly locked up the brakes to ensure adequate separation. After the tug passed; the Captain taxied into the gate.

B-737 First Officer reported a malfunction of the Inertial Reference System platform which resulted in loss of the First Officer Primary Flight Display instrument during cruise. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.

Flight crew reported autopilot descending below glideslope with false signals resulted in disconnect to rejoin the ILS. Captain uncertain whether this was ILS critical area incursion; or ground ILS equipment failure.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft became stuck in the grass during taxi; but was not towed off the taxiway until the following day.
Air Carrier flight crew reported being inside a known GPS jamming airspace that led to multiple systems being negatively affected and momentarily also led to a low remaining fuel issue.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported after arrival at the gate; the gate agent informed the FA the main door handle was not properly stowed for flight.
CL-65 Captain reported a engine bleed air message on climb. Crew returned to departure airport and landed without incident.

Pilot reported an Engine fuel leak during flight. A fuel imbalance developed in cruise; resulting in an in flight flame out of the Right Engine. Pilot requested diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier Captain reported during approach to a Class B airport they saw a UAS pass below them and notified ATC.
Flight Crew reported a family of Canadian Geese on the runway during landing rollout.
B787 Captain reported loss of one GPS and clock failure that led to requiring voice reports to report position. The reporter noted checklists and bulletins failed to offer guidance for the situation.
Student pilot reported they lost control of the aircraft landing in a crosswind resulting in a runway excursion.
PA-28 student pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing training which he was unable to control resulting in a runway excursion.
Light aircraft Instructor Pilot reported a near mid-air collision with another light aircraft on arrival into IND.
Light aircraft pilot reported a near-collision with another light aircraft on a taxiway at VNY airport.

A-320 Captain reported an electrical power failure while starting APU and taxiing to the gate at night on one engine. The Captain stopped the aircraft and power was restored after the APU started; then requested a tow to the gate.
Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action to avoid a NMAC with another aircraft in the ATC controlled traffic pattern.
CE-501 First Officer reported a rapid decompression during cruise at FL360. The flight crew diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
GA pilot reported unmarked power lines near 3KY4 airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the LAS NITZ1 RNAV Engine Out for Runway 8L page diagram depiction of FLAAR and the 12.7 ring is confusing.

Instructor pilot reported Engine Failure during stall recovery training. The Instructor Pilot immediately assumed the controls and the flight crew asked ATC for vectors and began to divert. The engine recovered without any input from the flight crew; so the flight crew continued to divert and make a precautionary landing.

Aircraft Owner with a CFI reported the aircraft ran out of fuel and the CFI landed the aircraft in a field with no damage. The owner used the incorrect fuel dip stick to check their fuel prior to departure.
B787 Captain reported moderate mountain wave with subsequent altitude and airspeed gains and losses.
CE-680A crew reported uncommanded movement after releasing the parking brake during shutdown checklist. Crew thought ground personnel signaled that wheel chocks were in when they were not.
Flight Instructor reported the right brake failed while the student was maneuvering for run-up and aircraft departed the taxiway.
Air carrier Captain reported a battery powered wheelchair that was loaded on board was not referenced in the manual. It was later determined the battery met all the requirements.
A TRACON Controller reported they lost communication with an aircraft on a vector due to a frequency outage and the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier flight crew noticed and came within 10 feet of a UAS while at 6;000 feet.
Ultralight aircraft pilot reported engine failure in downwind leg of traffic pattern. Pilot returned and landed immediately.

Air Carrier Pilots reported there were late runway changes in this mountainous area. Then while executing an RNP approach in a Heavy Aircraft; they received two GPWS Terrain Warnings and conducted a missed approach. There was a tailwind on the RF leg and each pilot stated they think the terrain warning was caused by the excessive speed from the tailwind. The airport was turned around after their missed approach.

Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict at GUM airport while holding short of the ramp area as another aircraft approached them at a high rate of speed. The Captain had to shout over the radio to the other crew to stop in order to avoid a collision.
Maintenance Technician reported one of their aircraft was found to have wiring issues causing the ADS-B to malfunction. The aircraft had been in storage for an extended time period.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss while on final approach to land at a non-towered airport in VMC conditions. The pilot did not have the conflicting traffic in sight during the approach and landing; but observed the other pilot perform a go around as the general aviation pilot was on their landing roll.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from Tower but they indicated a normal glide path; the flight crew continued the approach and landed safely at the airport.
C150 Flight Instructor reported landing as a succeeding C172 landed approximately 1;000 feet behind them resulted in taking expedited action to resolve a critical ground conflict.
B737 Flight Attendant reported oxygen masks did not deploy during a decompression event in cruise. Flight Attendants did not have time to complete checklist.
A Corporate jet pilot on an ILS Approach reported they took evasive action to avoid a mid air collision with opposite direction climbing VFR traffic not in communication with the TRACON.

Cessna 525 Captain reported a loss of speed; and unable to maintain altitude; after climbing to 45;000 ft. when temperature was higher than standard. The Captain immediately requested a lower altitude with ATC; descended; then continued the flight.
Skydive aircraft pilot reported after landing a pilot told them there was a NMAC between their two aircraft which the reporter was unaware of.
Air carrier First Officer reported a near mid air collision with a UAS while they were descending on arrival.
Student pilot flying C-150 aircraft on final approach reported NMAC with another aircraft.

EMB-170/175 Captain reported becoming distracted by reported traffic and losing command of the RNAV RNP approach sequence. The conditions were VMC. The crew then asked for and was given a visual approach by ATC. Later the pilots realized they had busted the Minimum Vector Altitude.

CRJ-200 Captain reported loss of the sole operating air conditioning pack resulted in loss of cabin pressure control. The flight crew requested priority handling and performed an immediate descent to 10;000 ft. An air turn back and precautionary landing were performed at departure airport.
GA instructor with a student pilot reported they flew into Class B airspace without a clearance.
Pilot reported loss of aircraft control during taxi following landing rollout that resulted in a taxiway excursion.

A321 Captain reported a 'dirty socks' odor during descent. After arriving at the gate; the Captain performed a walkaround and discovered oil residue on the APU service doors and inlet flap area and notified Maintenance. Maintenance found no issues.

B717 flight crew reported hearing a popping sound and a Compressor Stall EICAS message on rotate. The flight crew requested priority handling and elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at destination airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported the Engine Fail Procedure for Runway 31 out of COS may be incomplete.
B767 Captain reported experiencing a #1 engine compressor stall at cruise altitude; resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Air Taxi flight crew reported during landing the aircraft landed with the pilot flying applying the brakes resulting in the nose wheel coming down hard on the runway.
TPA TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict between an aircraft climbing out of SPG and an aircraft on approach to Runway 36 at PIE airport.
Air carrier Captain reported wheelchair batteries should not be carried in cargo.
Pilot and a Tower Controller reported the pilot flew to the wrong airport. The pilot reported the error was likely due to the pilot's GPS giving false indications or being jammed.
Corporate rotor wing pilot reported taking evasive action while on approach at a heliport to avoid hitting a UAS.

Air carrier crew reported receiving an ATC alert of a ground obstacle from ATC while descending below the FAF altitude on a visual approach at night prior to a published segment of the approach. The First Officer climbed to the FAF altitude then continued the approach to a landing.
Cessna 560XL pilot reported returning to departure airport after experiencing loss of cabin pressure.
Air carrier pilot reported confusing and contradictory information on MHR taxiway weight limits.
Pilot reported an experimental aircraft; not on CTAF; that taxied onto the runway resulting in a ground conflict.

C150 pilot flying in the middle of the night had an Electrical Power failure and could not activate airport lighting. When the pilot flew to the nearest commercial airport which was open with lighting and approached to land they had to sidestep to a parallel runway due to an opposite direction departure.
Air carrier First Officer reported that a high rate of descent during arrival triggered an obstacle caution message nearby an antenna.
Air carrier Line Captain and TRACON Controller reported an NMAC between two air carriers on final approach due to the other aircraft flying through the final approach course.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported a mispark of aircraft at gate resulting in a reposition to prevent slide deployment after passengers were deplaned.
Air carrier First Officer reported ignoring a false ATC instruction heard; which would have resulted in a collision threat.
Air carrier pilot reported a vehicle drove in front of the aircraft during taxi and did not stop.

Air carrier flight crew reported observing an object pass by the aircraft; followed by a metallic impact noise during arrival descent. After landing; the flight crew found a mark on the aircraft which they suspected could have been caused by collision with a UAS.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported a rejected takeoff due to multiple systems failures.

Air carrier First Officer reported a ground conflict with another aircraft during taxi into gate. There was inadequate wingtip clearance on the taxiway for two aircraft; and the flight crew stopped to allow the other aircraft to pass. Ramp Control seemed to be situationally unaware of the other traffic.

Air carrier flight crew reported an unstable approach; culminating with a bounced landing and incorrect go-around sequence. Both pilots cited the First Officer's lack of experience and recency as contributing.
B767 First Officer reported a high left engine vibration was felt after takeoff resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action when another aircraft entered the traffic pattern incorrectly at a non-towered airport; resulting in a NMAC.
Air Carrier Captain reported the LAS Choww3 STAR Runway 01 transition charts the fix CHOWW at or below 16;000 feet and the subsequent fix PACKK at or above 16;000 feet.

A319 flight crew reported receiving anti ice caution messages on initial climb; followed by increasing divergence of Captain's and First Officer's altimeter indications. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
Air carrier pilot crew reported a EGWPS terrain warning created by ATC. The pilots took evasive action and returned for a normal landing.
Technician reported during troubleshooting of HPSOV EICAS messages they found shorting plug was not removed and canon plug not installed. Maintenance removed shorting plug and reconnected canon plug.
EMB-170 flight crew reported an electrical system failure in cruise affecting multiple aircraft systems. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
B737 flight crew reported an engine failure at 60-65 knots during takeoff. Crew rejected takeoff and returned to gate.
B767-300 flight crew reported an in flight shut down and air turn back were performed after an engine fuel leak was discovered.

Dispatcher reported an MD11 flight crew contacted them with a right main landing gear indication issue during climbout.  They were not able to discuss the issue with Maintenance due to a radio communication problem; so the flight crew decided to return to the departure airport.

Business jet Captain reported a GPWS terrain alert while on the initial approach to a mountainous airport in visual conditions. The Captain did not make a correction to the descent and continued the approach to a safe landing.
B757 First Officer reported on departure experiencing a strong gust of wind resulting in a momentary stick shaker.
B777 flight crew reported engine failure inflight. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
C750 Captain reported a conflict with an airport operations truck while taxiing to their hangar.  The truck was reportedly traveling at about 40 MPH before it swerved to avoid a collision.
Air Carrier Captain reported ramp personnel loaded package of dry ice in excess of max weight allowed. Captain refused hazmat cargo.
PA-28 pilot performing an engine break in flight reported the aircraft burned more fuel than planned and they ran out of fuel.
B737 crew reported left nose wheel and axle were found to be missing after parking at gate. Crew noticed a bump during taxi in but attributed it to taxiway surface defects.
Beech 1900 Captain reported the smell of electrical smoke during the flight. After landing the smell returned and the Captain turned the aircraft over to maintenance.

EMB-135 First Officer reported the loss of the #2 air data computer in cruise. The flight crew observed erratic air speed and altitude indications and disconnected the autopilot to continue the flight.
A Cessna 172 pilot reported an NMAC while approaching a non-towered airport to land.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a helicopter at night; near a non-towered airport. The general aviation pilot maneuvered away from the helicopter and both coordinated altitude changes to provide separation on the radio.
Flight crew reported a balloon on the glideslope on final and was late to configure for landing due to being distracted by the balloon.
Corporate Captain reported a low altitude alert; issued by ATC; while on an instrument approach. Flight crew executed a missed approach and landed.

B737-800 air carrier crew reported high engine EGT temperatures during climb out during high temperature conditions. The engine temperatures returned to normal after climb thrust was selected and the flight continued to the destination.
A TRACON Controller reported they turned an Air Carrier at 4;000 feet away from an unidentified VFR aircraft also at 4;000 feet averting a possible mid air collision.
A flight school instructor reported right engine power loss and return to land at departure airport.

B737 First Officer reported a #2 Engine Filter Bypass Illuminated on descent into destination airport. The flight crew requested holding to run the QRH and checklists. The flight crew retarded the #2 Engine Throttle to idle to keep the Bypass Light out and continued to land at destination airport.
A Boeing 717 pilot reported experiencing an engine compressor stall while on final approach; resulting in an engine shutdown; and single engine landing.

Instructor pilot reported a gear collapse on a familiarization flight where the flying pilot incorrectly raised the gear on the landing rollout. The pilots safely evacuated the aircraft after the aircraft came to a stop on the Runway.
A Saab 340 pilot reported an engine failure on initial climb out; resulting in return to departure airport and a single engine approach and landing.
Captain reported baggage compartment door was not secured correctly and departed the aircraft in flight. The flight crew continued to destination airport where the missing door was confirmed.
Air Traffic Controller reported a vehicle inspecting the runway did not yield to an aircraft requiring it to take evasive action to avoid a collision with the vehicle.

A VFR pilot in a holding stack awaiting their turn to conduct a practice approach to a non-towered airport reported an NMAC when another aircraft flew through the holding pattern not communicating with other aircraft.
GEG TRACON controllers reported frequency problems with both 128.75 and 133.15 have been occurring for years and need to be fixed.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a false localizer signal on approach to JAC that resulted in an early turn to final approach course; potentially putting them in conflict with terrain. Reporter disconnected the autopilot and corrected the course.
Flight instructor reported a near mid air conflict with another aircraft while descending to enter the traffic pattern at FVX. They took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Pilots of two aircraft reported they each had an NMAC with a Bonanza that cut them off in the traffic pattern.
A319 Captain reported a fume event in the cabin during initial climb. After running the appropriate checklists; the flight crew elected to perform an air turnback.
A320 Captain reported experiencing a 'brief' fumes event at the top of descent. Simultaneously; the flight Attendants reported a temporary 'high pitch squeal' accompanied by a floor vibration.

Air carrier pilot reported a loud noise while at the gate and was believed to be ground equipment. During taxi; the noise continued along with an odor throughout the cabin. An EICAS message appeared on the MFD; Vent Blower Fault" and the crew returned to the gate for maintenance."

Air Traffic Controller reported aircraft turning and descending off the instrument procedure resulted in flight towards terrain. Aircraft rejoined the approach procedure and landed safely at the airport.
CRJ-550 flight crew reported a taxi collision risk with another aircraft during taxi out for takeoff.
B737-800 pilot reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing a loss of power and engine vibrations in cruise flight.

B767 flight crew reported landing gear and gear door anomaly upon gear retraction during climbout. Flight crew was able to extend gear normally and returned to departure airport where after landing damage to landing gear and brakes was found.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS terrain warning on descent into BUF in night IFR conditions.

Technician reported inconsistencies and lack of safety warning with job cards led to an inadvertent discharge of an engine fire bottle while accomplishing a routine Engine Driven Pump Shut Off Valve check.
Flight Instructor reported an engine fire on the ground during engine start. A hand held fire extinguisher was deployed to extinguish the fire.
Air carrier Captain reported an unreliable glideslope signal at SJU Airport.
Air Carrier pilot reported they believe the SFO METAR reports consistently issue incorrect wind directions.
C680 FO reported the aircraft brakes were mistakenly released due to a communication problem with the Captain and the jet began to roll when the chokes were removed.
B737 NG Captain reported loss of navigation systems and instruments on lift off. Captain elected to return to the departure airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without LAANC authorization.
EMB-175 Captain reported the aircraft began to roll back after parking at the gate. Aircraft was brought to a stop and the parking brake engaged.
Flight Instructor reported engine roughness and power loss while in the traffic pattern and returned to the departure airport for a precautionary landing.
Corporate pilot reported severe turbulence caused a temporary loss of control resulting in fluctuating airspeed and a loss of altitude.

General aviation pilot reported a hard landing during gusty wind conditions. The pilot determined after landing the propellers' tips were damaged from striking the ground and decided to fly the aircraft back to the home airport for maintenance and landed safely.
B737 Captain reported avoiding an inadvertent escape slide deployment when gate agent tried to open an armed aircraft door.

Captain reported trailing edge flap failure EICAS message on approach. The flight crew discontinued the approach; ran the check list and QRH procedures; and flew a no flap landing at destination airport.
G100 pilot reported receiving a cabin pressurization alert during cruise. Flight crew descended and continued to destination at lower altitude.
Light aircraft pilot reported descending too low on a visual approach to 2R2 airport.
B757 Captain reported having to abruptly stop the aircraft due to observing a baggage tug with carts cross in front of the aircraft during initial taxi start.
B737-700 flight crew reported communication equipment problems following pilot illness incident.
B737 Captain reported the leading edge flaps remained extended after flap retraction. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.

C182 instructor pilot providing familiarization to a licensed pilot reported the pilot performed an unstable approach in a crosswind and touched down too far down the runway. The pilot's actions to stop the aircraft at the end of the runway caused two blown tires.
A Corporate pilot reported GPS interference.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during initial takeoff. After troubleshooting and conferring with the Captain the flight returned to the departure airport.
Single pilot reported the complete loss of electrical failure during initial climb. The Pilot contacted the departure airport by mobile phone which facilitated a safe air return.
Small aircraft Flight Instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another small aircraft at OEB airport.

A320 First Officer reported an EICAS message warned of a malfunction with the #1 pressurization system during climb out. Later another warning regarding the pressurization system appeared. The pilots descended to a lower altitude and returned to the departure airport.
PA-44-180 instructor reported a mechanical problem with the nose wheel steering.
Single engine pilot reported a NMAC while departing a non towered airport. The pilot stated while making the departure an unannounced helicopter was downwind and too close to the departure path.
RV-12 Flight Instructor conducting a first flight with a student reported due to a loss of engine power they diverted to the nearest suitable airfield and landed.
EMB-135 Captain reported a loss of electrical power caused multiple systems failures on descent. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
Flight Instructor on a training flight with student were cleared for takeoff by the tower when another aircraft was on short final. This resulted in a near miss between these two aircraft.
Air Carrier Pilot reported on approach the VNAV indicated the aircraft was below the flight path while the FMA indicated on path. Pilot stated this could have been caused by possible 5G interference.

B737 First Officer reported trailing edge flaps failed to extend on approach. The flight crew performed a go around to trouble shoot; then use the alternate flap extension procedures. This resulted in a flap asymmetry condition. The flight crew received vector back to make a precautionary landing at destination airport.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported; while conducting a pre-flight walk around; the left wing leading edge was coated in ice. Maintenance technicians arrived at the aircraft and informed the Captain they were performing a procedure to release a frozen left wing anti-ice valve.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC during visual approach to BHM requiring the crew to adjust the descent rate to avoid a possible collision.

C680 flight crew reported landing gear failed to extend after the gear handle was moved to the down position. The flight crew asked for vectors and ran the checklists and QRH; succeeding to manually blow down and extend the landing gear. Priority handling was requested and a precautionary landing was made at destination airport.

Technician reported the clamp on the pressure seal at the aft keel beam in the main wheel well was missing and the insulation for the APU pneumatic duct was torn indicating pressurization leaks. The aircraft was removed from service for repair.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming in the vicinity of Gallup; NM.
Air taxi pilot reported receiving a GPWS terrain warning departing PAJN airport while flying the wrong departure procedure.
A C172  pilot reported they misidentified traffic they were to follow and turned base leg early resulting in a NMAC with traffic on final approach.
C182 pilot reported a force landing due to a fuel issue during landing approach.
Air carrier pilot flying reported GPS jamming occurring from cruise all the way to initial approach; and subsequent GPS system degradation. ZAB Center stated that the jamming was a normal occurrence.

Flight instructor reported a NMAC event during traffic pattern training when the conflict aircraft overtook the flight instructor's aircraft in close proximity. There was no evasive maneuver since the event happened very quickly.
A321 Captain reported a pneumatic bleed leak malfunction during the climb to cruise altitude. The flight returned to the departure airport.
Pilot flying Merlin IIIB aircraft reported loss of Nose Wheel Steering control during landing rollout. Post flight inspection revealed shuttle valve in Nose Wheel Steering was inoperable.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported ordering taxing aircraft to return to gate to correct Hazmat configuration error.

GA pilot reported a fueling truck at LRU airport parks too close to buildings and parked aircraft; is not properly marked as flammable; and fire extinguishers are not conspicuously apparent on the truck as required.

Relief Pilot reported approximately 3.5 hours into flight six (6) status messages presented; ENG REVERSER L; HYD VLV SUPPLY PRI PUMP L; HYD VLV AUTO BLEED RSVR L; DET FIRE 2A ENG L; DET FIRE 4A ENG L & ENG 3A ENG L. The flight crew returned to departure airport.
SR20 pilot reported demonstrating a maneuver which resulted in temporary loss of aircraft control.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS interference on departure from RKSI.

B757 Captain and a Dispatcher reported a loss of left hydraulic system quantity on departure event. On approach to destination airport; the indications returned; causing a go-around. The Captain requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing; requiring a tow off the runway.
An air carrier CRJ-700 Pilot reported a loss of GPS on departure.
A TRACON Controller reported a military aircraft conducting practice approaches did not comply with the Missed Approach assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

C525 pilot reported failure to set parking brake correctly led to the aircraft rolling forward when the pilot left his set to check the main cabin door. The pilot returned to his seat and stopped the aircraft safely.

Technician reported an aircraft arriving at station with an APU duct pressure seal clamp not installed in the aft bulkhead in the wheel well keel beam. Due to the extensive time required for repair;  the aircraft was removed from service.
PA-28 instructor pilot reported engine power loss on climbout. The instructor pilot requested priority handling and performed an air turn back to make a precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported elevator trim system failed during cruise. Flight crew diverted to a suitable airport.
Center Controller reported poor coordination leading to an aircraft flying towards a higher MIA; Minimum IFR Altitude.

C182 Pilot reported receiving no airspeed indications as well as the side cockpit window opening during climb out. Due to the lack of airspeed indications a runaway trim situation developed and the pilot immediately returned to land where it was discovered there was an insect in the pitot tube.
Pilot on final approach to a non towered airport reported an experimental aircraft had departed without communicating via CTAF resulting in a NMAC.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving a Dangerous Goods Form with incomplete Hazmat data. After an extensive delay with no responses from company ground personnel the Captain refused all Hazmat cargo and departed safely.

Air Traffic Controller reported that a Local Controller having a departure off a parallel runway whose flight path would cross the parallel runway resulted in a departure being assigned an altitude below the MVA to separate the aircraft.
CRJ-200 Captain reported after takeoff; the remaining air conditioning pack malfunctioned. The flight crew elected to make an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
A flight crew reported they attempted to taxi off the runway on a closed taxiway. They stopped when they saw cones but were still partially on the runway causing a subsequent arrival to go around.
B737 Captain reported a possible fuel leak during cruise climb resulted in return to departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with a UAS during initial approach into LAX airport.
Flight Crew reported trailing edge flaps stuck at 37 degrees when flaps 45 degrees were selected. The flight crew continued the approach to successful landing.
A ramp wing walker reported they used their wands to stop a truck on a temporary roadway abeam the ramp from coming into contact with an aircraft being pushed back.

B777 flight crew reported a track deviation occurred after being distracted by wake turbulence from a preceding heavy Airbus while departing EWR. The flight crew also failed to notice the FMC was improperly programmed and did not have the proper departure procedure loaded.

Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with a ground vehicle while taxiing into the gate; while under parking guidance from a ramp marshaller. The Captain stopped the aircraft; then continued to the gate; when the vehicle turned around and the safety zone was cleared.
Controller reported an aircraft descended without permission or communication with ATC twice; due to a pressurization problem resulting in a loss of separation.
Pilot landing a tailwheel aircraft reported the main gear malfunctioned upon initial touchdown resulting in a runway excursion due to loss of control.
Air carrier flight crew reported an NMAC with a UAS during departure. No evasive action was taken.
Pilot reported an engine failure on climb out. The pilot returned back to the airport and landed safely.

G280 First Officer reported multiple rejected takeoffs while attempting a maintenance check flight. The Flight Crew taxied to an FBO for parking and discovered a large bumble bee obstructing the pitot tube.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume/smoke event in cockpit during departure taxi when windshield heat was activated.
Maintenance Technician reported an inadvertent escape slide deployment during troubleshooting of a slide indication problem.
General aviation pilot reported a wheel brake failure while taxiing which resulted in a runway incursion.
Air carrier Captain reported a breakaway during push back from the gate. The tow bar was not correctly attached to the aircraft.

B757 first officer reported multiple data link updates with incorrect weights resulted in manual input for corrected final weights.  First Officer reported that load planning failed to correct entry error.
A320 pilot reported aircraft loss of control during gate push-back procedure.
A321 Flight Attendant reported a 'burning rubber odor' in the cabin to the Captain during descent. The odor dissipated when the Captain turned off an ' Air Conditioning Pack.'
Banner Tow aircraft pilot reported while on location observed another aircraft just feet from their aircraft; resulting in the other aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier flight crew reported temporary loss of control and injuries to flight attendants due to severe turbulence.

Flight Instructor and Student reported during descent the engine began to fail due to fuel starvation. After switching fuel tanks power was restored and it was discovered post flight that a fuel gauge was indicating incorrectly.
Air carrier First Officer reported while flying through an area of poor GPS and radio signals they missed an ATC frequency change.
C172 pilot reported; to stay in VFR conditions; the pilot split the difference between the base of the cloud layer and a ridge line. Pilot states next time to stay safe; file IFR.

Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported undeclared Hazmat shipment violations of Lithium Ion batteries. Shipment was transported without required Hazmat Declaration; exterior label; and packing list. Reporter notified shipping vendor of the errors.
C172 pilot reported losing the transponder and radio while enroute and flew back under the Bravo shelves.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a strong dirty socks odor upon arrival at the aircraft. Flight Attendant called in sick due to multiple physiological symptoms.

Captain reported observers informed the flight crew of a significant amount of fuel was seen venting from the aircraft on take off. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport after it was determined that all of the fuel in the center tank had vented overboard.
C182 pilot reported a near midair collision as another aircraft was landing on the opposite runway and took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Cessna 172 pilot and instructor reported a single brake malfunction resulting in a taxiway excursion. The aircraft was pushed back onto the taxiway and taxied to the parking spot.
A320 Captain reported a fume event during descent which was isolated to the flight deck of undetermined source.

Tail Wheel Biplane pilot reported loss of control and exiting the runway into the grass during landing. Pilot stated a crosswind was a factor and low pilot time in the aircraft contributed to the excursion.

C172 pilot reported an alternator failure during a night time personal flight which resulted in a total electrical failure. The pilot was able to relay a special handling request via cell phone and complete a safe landing.
Flight Instructor reported two separate NMACs while in the practice area around LMO airport.
B737 Captain reported autoflight system malfunction. The Captain disengaged the autoflight system and returned to departure airport.
Falcon 2000 flight crew reported an NMAC event during departure climb. Pilot flying followed the TCAS RA and reported the incident to ATC.

B737 flight crew reported an unstable approach at night with no vertical or ground guidance resulting in an unusual attitude situation. Flight crew regained aircraft control with upset recovery procedures.

Baron 55 pilot reported the autopilot disengaged on final approach resulting in a descent below IFR altitude and a low altitude warning issued by ATC. Pilot climbed back to correct altitude and continued the approach.
Flight Instructor reported a near midair collision with two UAS while on approach. No evasive action was taken.

Pilot reports the condition of runways and airport facilities are falling into disrepair. Pilot states grass runway conditions are becoming unsafe for landing aircraft because the grass has grown too high.
Air carrier Captain reported not being notified prior to departure that dry ice was onboard.
CRJ200 First Officer reported triggering a GPWS caution alarm while on approach due to rising terrain.
A Flight Instructor conducting training at a non towered airport reported a NMAC with a succeeding departure turning crosswind at the same time as they were.

DA20 Flight Instructor with student reported the aircraft avionics and cooling fan malfunctioned in flight resulting in partial loss of communications. Instructor returned to departure airport with ATC assistance and landed safely.
B737 flight crew reported observing a B777 under tow crossing the runway departure end on landing rollout; which resulted in a critical ground conflict.
EMB-175 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into IAH in trail of a B787.
B747 Captain reported taxiing into the gate a helicopter crossed over the wing resulting in a critical ground conflict event.
A321 Captain reported that push back operator advised that a fuel leak was present on aircraft belly.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC during approach when another inbound aircraft did not acknowledge ATC instructions and the Tower Controller turned the first aircraft head on to the other.

Air Carrier Dispatcher reported Load Control personnel erroneously loaded Hazmat cargo in a regular container instead of a Hazmat pallet where it was accessible. Dispatcher notified all appropriate personnel.
C172 instructor pilot with student reported the left tank began leaking fuel after takeoff prompting the pilots to immediately return to the departure airport with ATC assistance.
Gulfstream V Captain reported activation of the cabin smoke evacuation valve triggered the pressurization emergency descent mode.
C172 Instructor Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft while doing unusual attitude maneuvers.

Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway excursion from Taxiway B at LAX at night in the rain. Reportedly; a lack of green taxi centerline lights and poor centerline reflectivity contributed to the event.
C182 pilot reported they were temporarily NORDO when they selected the wrong radio on their audio panel and flew below the ATC Minimum vectoring Altitude.
Air Carrier Captain reported a near miss with a UAS while on approach.
Flight Crew reported a flap failure on departure and a return to the airport.
Air carrier pilot reported ramp exit spots on movement and non movement areas at DFW have been painted the same yellow color resulting in a taxiway incursion from Spot 32.
Captain reported taking evasive action to avoid a NMAC while on a SID resulting in a missed crossing restriction.
Government UAS pilot reported striking power lines during takeoff. The UAS sustained major damage.
C-402 Captain reported the crew emergency escape hatch opened in flight resulting in an Air Turn Back..
Air carrier First Officer reported a near miss with another aircraft while descending with an ATC clearance. The crew followed the TCAS RA guidance to avoid the conflict.
Light aircraft non-flying pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert on approach to TPA.

CRJ-900 flight crew and Maintenance reported the nose wheel steering was turning the aircraft in the direction opposite of rudder pedal input. The flight crew aborted the takeoff and returned to the gate. The Maintenance technician who had serviced the aircraft reported the rudder pedal assembly had not been checked for movement in the correct direction after performing a maintenance procedure.
PA-23 pilot reported the nose gear collapsed during landing rollout. Once the aircraft was stopped; the left and right main gear also collapsed.
Challenger-500 flight crew reported the stick shaker activated during departure. They performed stall recovery and returned to the assigned course with minimum loss of altitude.
Pilot reported the engine began running rough; resulting in an off airport landing. Post flight maintenance inspection determined the engine had failed.
HS25B Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after inflight loss of right engine. Reporter stated they experienced a track deviation in the holding pattern.
Flight Instructor reported an area of the taxiway was fenced off and taxi lines repainted at DXE airport. Pilot states there was no NOTAM warning pilots of the change and obstruction.
Air carrier ground personnel reported three of six parcels were not marked as hazmat but were loaded with hazmat cargo.
Air Carrier First Officer reported an encounter with severe turbulence. The pilots regained control of the aircraft and the FO stated there were no injuries.
A Flight Attendant reported the flight returned to departure airport due to an engine malfunction.

B787 Captain expressed concerns regarding application of MEL for a Cabin Air Compressor (CAC) problem that had existed for a number of days for what he learned was a history of burnt contractor pins.  The Captain spent hours working with Maintenance and Dispatch with crew duty time limits in the background to depart with contingency plans for flight only to have flight be unexpectedly cancelled during taxi out.
B737-700 flight crew reported a ground conflict with a tug during taxi.
PUB Tower Local Controller reported the other Local Controller did not notice an aircraft turned to follow the wrong aircraft resulting in a NMAC.
EMB-145 Flight Attendant reported engine loss.
B737-800 Ground Service Technician reported that while towing the aircraft; due to the lack of proper safety envelope markings and poor lighting; the aircraft collided with the service stairs.
C182 pilot reported the loss of aircraft control during engine start when the aircraft moved forward and entered a taxiway.
DA50 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL in trail of an Airbus.
Flight Crew reported the total loss of left hand hydraulic system fluid quantity; resulting in an air turn back and a precautionary emergency landing.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the pattern at ZZZ airport.
Air carrier Captain refused an aircraft due to a deferred Slat Flap Controller Computer; citing concerns that flight control protections would be reduced if other computers were to fail in flight.
Flight Instructor reported aircraft main landing gear collapsed during taxi for takeoff.
HCF Approach Controller reported an aircraft deviated from the published approach procedure and flew below their Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a passenger boarded with a motorized suitcase. The hazmat safety Supervisor approved the transport of the case after it was properly configured.
B737 Captain reported refusing the aircraft after the reason for a hydraulic leak was not addressed correctly.

EMB-190 pilot reported refusing an aircraft that required excessive trim to fly stable. Maintenance was unable to address the trim issue to the satisfaction of the flight crew and the aircraft was removed from service.

Air Carrier Pilots reported Ground Control was acting as both Ground and Clearance Delivery on the Ground Control frequency. The pilots were cleared to cross the runway and then told to immediately stop as a high wing single engine aircraft executed a missed approach and flew over them.

A90 TRACON controllers stated the Wind Shear Alert only alerted at the Supervisor desk and the Controller working the final approach could not hear the warning. Reporter stated at times it is necessary for the Watch Supervisor to not be at the desk where the alert sounds.
C750 pilot reported they could not control the engine with the thrust lever during initial approach so they shut the engine down.
Pilot reported a NMAC with a military aircraft while on an IFR flight plan descending for approach.
B737 Captain reported a critical ground conflict while exiting the runway after landing when a taxiing aircraft failed to give way requiring the Captain to stop on the taxiway to avoid a collision.
Experimental aircraft pilot reported engine problems developed during cruise; resulting in an off-airport landing. Pilot suspected that the fuel supply system was not working properly.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving an incomplete Final Dangerous Goods document which was resolved after conferring with Dispatch. Subsequently the Flight was cleared to pushback from gate which resulted in a less severe Ground Conflict; which was resolved with the assistance of another tug crew.
C172N Flight Instructor reported an engine failure possibly due to fuel exhaustion resulted in a successful restart with expeditious handling to a successful landing.

B737 Captain reported a Lithium Ion battery powered wheelchair was incorrectly configured for transport. After conferring with ground personnel the Lithium Ion battery was securely attached to the wheelchair and required documents provided to the Captain; the aircraft departed safely.
Challenger 300 flight crew on a short ferry flight reported engine vibration on departure and continued to their destination.
Air carrier flight crew reported flying to the wrong fix after ATC cleared and acknowledged TACTT. A short while later and after querying the flight crew; ATC informed that the cleared fix was VEECK.
Air Carrier Captain reported a CFIT event during final approach caused by a deviation below glideslope. The Captain disconnected the automation and recapture the glideslope and landed safely.
PA28 pilot entering the pattern reported they lost use of their ailerons. Pilot was able to land safely.
EMB-145 Captain reported diverting to a nearby airport due to smoke in the cockpit. The reporter also reported difficulty in communicating with the oxygen mask on.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported a passenger notified the airline in flight they had a lithium battery in a checked bag. Dispatcher stated they were not updated about the situation.

EMB-505 First Officer reported Received a Duct 1 Over Temp CAS Message at cruise. The flight crew ran the check lists and QRH. The flight crew requested priority handling and elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier Captain reported a lack of ground markings or signs while taxiing on specific taxiways at MCI makes identifying taxiways at night difficult.

I90 TRACON Controller reported that another controller had to issue a traffic alert to an airliner on final approach due to a VFR aircraft that was operating directly below the Bravo; which happens all the time.

General aviation instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft on a training flight while in a practice area; in day; VMC conditions; while trying to communicate unsuccessfully with the other aircraft on a designated practice area CTAF frequency. The instructor maneuvered away from the other aircraft and landed safely.
C208 pilot reported runway excursion during back taxi maneuvering.
Learjet 45 flight crew reported a MAIN HYD QTY LO Message; followed by a AUX HYD QTY LO Message in cruise. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
EC-130 Maintenance Inspector reported finding an NR sensor discrepancy due to the shims not installed properly.
B737 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing #1 engine anomalies that reduced thrust to idle.

Air carrier Captain reported setting the parking brakes without verifying the brakes had engaged which resulted in the aircraft moving backward. The Captain immediately engaged the parking and coordinated with ground personnel to tow aircraft back into correct position with no damages or injuries.
ZSE ARTCC Controller reported that complacency led to missing an incorrect altitude readback from an aircraft and resulted in said aircraft descending below the MVA.
Captain reported loss of GPS signal and VNAV on GPS approach resulted in change to a visual approach.
Air Carrier Captain reported FO; who had not flown in over a month; experienced tailwind that resulted in an unstable approach with two go-arounds and failed CRM procedures.

Air carrier crew reported a traffic conflict with a delayed departing aircraft while on final approach resulting in an ATC-directed go around to provide separation. The crew performed the go around then returned for a landing.
PA18 Supercub pilot reported runway excursion on landing resulted from loss of control on landing.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported an autopilot anomaly in cruise climb.
B767-300ER Captain the inbound aircraft had indications of improperly configured Dangerous Goods along with documents suggesting undocumented transport of Hazmat cargo.
Additional Crew Member reported assisting the Flight Crew when the #1 engine became unresponsive to thrust lever movement. The engine was shut down and flight continued.
Corporate Captain reports low altitude warning from ATC during approach to SNA airport.
Beech F90 pilot reported an NMAC during final approach.
Non-flying pilot reported an aborted takeoff after encountering birds.
B737 Flight Attendants reported a Ramp Agent discovered a lithium-ion battery in a bag in the cargo compartment that was then handed over to a Flight Attendant.
A-321 Captain reported a successful resolution of a Hazmat cargo loading violation resulting in completing a safe transport of Hazmat cargo.
A300 pilot reported receiving an ECAM message GREEN RSVR LO LEVEL in cruise. After troubleshooting and discussing options the crew continued to destination airport and made a safe landing.
Flight Attendant reported a gate return due to APU failure which resulted in deplaning the passengers due to extreme temperatures; with the cabin temp going as high as 96 degrees F.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during passenger boarding. After troubleshooting and conferring with flight attendants a logbook entry was submitted which ultimately resulted in an aircraft swap.
ZAB Center Controller reported planned GPS jamming in their sectors near the TXO VOR caused an air carrier and other aircraft to experience navigation difficulties.
B737 flight crew reported an engine failure and cockpit smoke after a bird strike after takeoff. The crew returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Air Carrier Pilot reported there was no Taxiway signage for Kilo while taxing on Bravo. The pilot stated after being given a clearance 'taxi B to K'; Kilo was difficult to identify.
C172 pilot reported a ground strike and momentary loss of aircraft control during landing.  Reportedly; the pilot may have over-flared which caused the tail to strike the runway surface.
Air carrier flight crew reported engine failure on initial climb and return to departure airport.
CL300 flight crew reported striking the rudder of a Pilatus while taxiing for parking under a marshallers guidance.
Two flight instructors operating at a non-towered airport reported a NMAC between them in the pattern. Also; they reported one of the aircraft landed while the first aircraft was still on the runway.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported unmanifested dangerous goods were mistakenly transported. The cargo had been removed off the documentation however the cargo remained on the aircraft.
Captain reported the left engine would not develop full takeoff power during takeoff roll and rejected the takeoff.
Pilot reported a red CAS message indicating high ITT on takeoff roll and rejected the takeoff.
DC-3 pilot reported having an inflight hydraulic system failure in the landing gear up line which allowed the gear to deploy into the down and locked position. Pilot diverted and landed successfully.
PA-32 pilot reported as they entered the pattern according to Tower's instructions a banner tow aircraft they were unaware of reported a NMAC with them.

Government UAS crew reported attempting to have UAS return for an auto landing. Due to high winds; an auto landing wasn't possible and they conducted a successful manual emergency landing without engine power.
Flight crew reported horizontal trim became inoperative with binding when attempting to use manual trim wheels. Flight crew elected to perform an air turn back.
Technician reported the P3 inlet sensor on an A320 was installed incorrectly and the damage caused required an engine change.
A320 flight crew reported receiving a HYD Y RSVR LO LVL message during cruise. Flight crew continued to destination airport and landed uneventfully.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft flew through final approach course and the Monitor position did not issue the Low Altitude Alert to the aircraft.
B787 flight crew reported going below glide slope during approach and receiving a GPWS alert. Flight crew corrected and continued approach to landing.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a prohibited Lithium cooler was air transported.
Air carrier pilot reported they were on downwind vectors for EUG Runway 16R at their assigned altitude when ATC issued at a low altitude alert.
A SR20 pilot on short final to a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with an aircraft departing opposite direction.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation while on the VKTRY 2 arrival to DAL airport.  Similar sounding fix names FAWNT and PHAUP were cited as contributing to the event.

Air carrier Captain reported being notified the next day that undocumented Hazmat was transported transported on the flight. The Captain did not receive the NOTOC and no loading documents indicated Hazmat on onboard.

L30 TRACON Controller reported the adjacent sector had to vector a departing aircraft to avoid a conflict with two arriving aircraft. The reporter states the published arrival and departure procedures are unsafe and do not ensure separation between aircraft.

Instructor reported during the takeoff roll a door became open and a rejected takeoff was initiated. The instructor states there was a runway overrun; damaging a runway threshold light and no resulting damage to occupants or aircraft.

Ramp personnel reported while attempting to connect a towbarless tractor to the aircraft they asked for brake release that resulted with the aircraft rolling forward and the Captain slamming on the brakes to avoid aircraft damage or injury.
Pilot reported receiving a terrain warning and then executing a missed approach.
Ramp Controller reported observing a truck drive in front of a taxiing aircraft; cutting them off; then proceeding to stop behind a parked aircraft.
Pilot inbound to airport reported a NMAC with an aircraft passing above them and then the pilot reported doing a 360-degree turn because of concerns of another aircraft overtaking them from behind.
B737-800 Captain reported engine fire during taxi was extinguished and they returned to the gate.
Air Carrier First Officer reported failure to notice glideslope out of service resulted in ATC low altitude alert; low PAPI indications.
Flight Attendant reported temperatures rose to high levels during cruise and endangered passengers because of an air conditioning pack failure.
Air Carrier Captain reported the tug operator disconnected without telling flight crew to set brakes resulting in the aircraft rolling on the ramp.
Pilot flying TBM 940 aircraft reported cabin pressurization problem in cruise. Descent was initiated to lower altitude and cabin pressure stabilized.
The pilot of a small aircraft reported a near miss with a large UAS while flying on an IFR flight plan. The pilot was able to see and avoid it in time.
C172 pilot reported violating Class Bravo airspace due to possible erroneous altimeter from static system water contamination.
B757 pilot reported a compressor stall during cruise climb. The engine stabilized and flight continued to destination.

Air Carrier flight crew reported an intermittent fumes event while at gate preflight and during departure taxi. After completion of the appropriate QRH procedure and conferring with MX the flight returned to departure gate.
C182 pilot reported a brake malfunction resulted in loss of control and taxiway/runway excursion and contact with taxiway signage.
Pilot reported large dog on runway during takeoff. Pilot took evasive action to avoid hitting dog.
B777 flight crew reported an NMAC during the departure climb
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with two ultralights on climbout from LMO airport.
Citation I flight crew reported an inverter failure caused the autopilot to fail resulting in a temporary loss of control.
A TRACON Controller reported a corporate jet on departure did not fly the SID as published and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

B777-200 flight crew reported a glideslope failure during final approach resulted in two go-arounds.  The crew was able to land by executing an RNAV approach.  The flight crew also reported an altitude deviation during the first go-around.

An air carrier flight crew conducting a visual approach received a Ground Proximity Warning even though they were slightly above glideslope of the RNAV approach. Crew complied with warning and landed uneventfully.
Pilot reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while they were on approach.
A Center Controller reported after they took over a busy combined sector they failed to notice an air carrier conflicting with an unidentified VFR target.
B737 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of another air carrier on approach to BOS.
Air carrier flight crew reported flying in RVSM airspace with the autopilot off due to stabilizer trim issue.
CR900 pilot reported receiving a Resolution Advisory for traffic flying 400 feet below them during approach to land.
Pilot flying a visual approach reported a near CFIT incident in day VFR conditions.
Airliner 99 Captain reported the right engine became unresponsive to throttle movement. The flight crew elected to perform an in-flight shut down and continued the approach to landing.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving an ACARS message during cruise stating undeclared DG onboard. After conferring with Dispatch the flight continued to a landing at destination.
Pilot flying AEST aircraft reported nose gear collapse during landing rollout.

B737 flight crew reported a passenger's vape pen experienced a spontaneous combustion event that resulted in sparks and smoke coming from the passenger's backpack that contained the item. The FAs immediately initiated a jetway evacuation while the flight deck notified airport personnel and secured the aircraft before also evacuating.

Air Carrier Captain reported ramp agent came into view from under nose area of aircraft after plane had taxied to a ramp departure location.  Captain was unaware agent had approached aircraft to secure an access door that was left open.

Medium transport Captain reported that Runway 31R/13L is closed but still has the runway identifiers on the ends; despite the runway now operating as Taxiway H. The Garmin database also depicts it as both a runway and taxiway; leading to potential confusion.
Pilot reported receiving several MFD error messages during departure from GSO and cited possible 5G interference as contributing.
Lancair IVP pilot reported landing with the left main gear up.  Reportedly; the pilot repeatedly tried to manually put the gear down without success before deciding to land the aircraft.
Beech Bonanza pilot reported observing an oil leak on top of the engine cowling during climb. The pilot returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Flight crew reported receiving a Ground Proximity Warning while on final approach due to conducting the approach below the authorized minimum air temperature.
Air carrier Captain reported a safety concern regarding a lot of loose asphalt and FOD being on the taxi route.

Air Carrier flight crew reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of an unruly passenger spraying an unknown aerosol spray on a row of seats during preflight. Customer Service Supervisor was summoned which resolved the spray issue resulting in a safe flight departure with no further issues.
B737 First Officer reported distraction prevented noticing Pilot Flying descended below path; resulted in ATC low altitude alert and GPWS terrain alert.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS interference in Myanmar/Yangon and China/Kunming airspace.
BE35 pilot reported wind shear encounter resulted in momentary loss of aircraft control.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on a visual approach. Flight crew climbed and continued approach.
BE-55 pilot reported landing gear collapsed after electric failure.
Air carrier Captain reported being notified by a flight attendant of odors in the cabin during final approach. Flight crew requested and received special handling and continued for a safe landing.

B737-800 flight crew reported a passenger lost a Lithium Ion battery powered electronic device during pre-flight. Passenger deplaned while maintenance conducted an unsuccessful search and provided an AML entry before departure.

E190 Captain reported rejecting a takeoff due to vibration in the #1 Engine. Significant ice build-up was found on the nose cone and vanes of the affected engine after inspecting the aircraft after returning to the gate; even though deicing was performed.
A320 fight crew reported a strong odor in the aircraft during approach and landing; requiring maintenance action.
C172 pilot reported while on a practice approach another aircraft reported that they flew right over them and that they took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Cessna 152 pilots reported engine RPM fluctuating during a training flight in VFR conditions and diverted to an airstrip. The pilots then took off and experienced more engine vibration and diverted and landed at another tower controlled airport safely.
EMB-145 Captain reported radio altimeter failed resulted in autopilot disconnect; a GPWS alert; and a CFTT event.
Large transport flight crew on final approach to PIE reported that they received a TCAS/RA Alert for a helicopter maneuvering in their flight path.
Citation jet flight crew reported a wheel fire during taxi. Flight crew requested airport fire response crews and passengers were evacuated.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported unsafe handling of heavy Hazmat pallet during unloading due to excessive size and inadequate equipment.
A300 Captain reported rejecting the takeoff at 80 kts when a split between Captain and FO airspeed indicators was noted.
Air Carrier Captain reported a near collision with two separate ground vehicles while taxiing at night on the ramp.  The Captain abruptly stopped both times to avoid a collision.
G-V flight crew reported an electric pitch trim anomaly; resulting in a return to the departure airport; utilizing manual pitch trim.
B767 flight crew on departure reported two loud bangs; then returned to departure airport.
Ramp Controller reported some confusion and miscommunication occurred when three aircraft in adjacent gates attempted to push back simultaneously.

A321 crew reported an electrical malfunction during the climb resulting in an electrical odor and various components including the cabin lavatories unpowered. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed.

Fractional jet First Officer reported a disagreement between the identified landing runway on the ATIS and that given by ATC upon check-in; which caused confusion for the correct routing and crossing altitudes on the STAR. This resulted in an altitude alert from ATC with the crew climbing back to the assigned altitude and a vector to continue the approach to a landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported not following the departure procedure resulting in the flight climbing towards terrain below the MEA in VMC conditions.
Air Carrier Captain reported 'GNSS Not Available EICAS' message received during taxi. Local maintenance stated it was a known issue and cleared to depart.

EMB-145 Flight Attendant reported a cabin pressurization issue first noticed on the ground and then during climbout.  The flight returned to the departure airport and also experienced Radio Altimeter issues during final approach.

B787 crew pilot crew reported a cabin door malfunction described as intermittent humming noise during climb; later three flight attendants reported being ill. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
A General Aviation pilot reported entering Class D airspace after assuming it had a ceiling similar to where this pilot usually flies.
C182 skydive pilot reported a NMAC during initial climb with a non-reporting opposite direction landing aircraft. Pilot executed an evasive maneuver to avoid a collision.
CRJ-200 Captain reported landing safely after experiencing a flap failure on final approach.
B737 Captain reported a fuel filter bypass caution light during climb. The Captain coordinated a diversion to an alternate airport and landed safely overweight.
Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict with two departing military fighter aircraft while landing.
B787 pilot reported the engine N1 value was less than expected after engine start. Maintenance was not able to fix the problem and the aircraft was taken out of service.
RV-6A pilot reported an aircraft electrical failure while performing a final approach in VFR. The pilot landed without communication and taxied into a non-movement area.
Air Carrier Captain reported First Officer windshield and heating element failed leading to multiple EICAS messages; resulting in transfer of control and divert.
General aviation pilot flying reported confusing a runway for a taxiway at TRK airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported hydraulic system malfunction in cruise flight.

B737 Captain reported receiving a traffic advisory with traffic that was behind the aircraft and landing on the parallel runway. The flight crew performed a go-around after complying with the resolution advisory.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into CLT in trail of an A350.

A320 First Officer reported a cabin smoke event from a passenger cell phone charger prior to landing. The cabin crew contained the malfunctioning device in water; the plane landed safely with no injuries; some charring to a passenger seat frame; and cabin furnishings.
EMB-145 Captain reported the anti-ice protect system failed during climb out in icing conditions at 17;000 feet. The crew executed a return to the departure airport.
A Medevac pilot reported they set too low an altitude for the approach procedure and received a terrain warning alert.
Air Traffic Controller reported trees obstruct the BGM Tower's view of the ramp area creating a blind spot that requires extra communication with aircraft.
Pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft shortly after being advised of an aircraft overtaking on the left by ATC.
Tower Controller reported a departing helicopter air taxied onto the runway for departure with an aircraft on short final for landing. Tower issued go around instructions to the arriving aircraft.
captain reported refusing the aircraft because it was not equipped to fly to the destination airport.
GA pilot reported an altitude deviation during departure and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.

Corporate jet Captain reported a near miss on final approach; then another near miss from another aircraft entering the non-towered AUO airport traffic pattern. The Captain maneuvered from each near miss to avoid a collision.
C650 Captain reported the left thrust reverser deployed after takeoff. The flight crew requested priority handling and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
General aviation pilot reported they did not follow the assigned obstacle departure procedure which resulted in ATC providing vectors to provide obstacle clearance.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a dual AHRS failure resulted in a request for priority handling and an air turn back to make a precautionary landing at departure airport.
B737 Captain reported performing an air turnback due to an engine overheating indication. After discussion; the flight crew made the decision to make an overweight landing.
B737 Captain reported Tower issued a Low Altitude Alert and a go-around.
Dispatcher reported they did not issue an alternate as required by a 5G NOTAM.

B767 flight crew reported the left engine experienced multiple compressor stalls during cruise. The flight crew completed the QRH procedure and continued to destination where a safe landing was executed.
B737-800 flight crew reported push crews lack of attention resulted in winglet damage by contact with truck ladder.
Technician reported finding missing bushings and barrel nuts on a thrust reverser sleeve that was removed for repair.

A321 Captain reported a firm touchdown during landing by the flying pilot with no exceedances or aircraft damage. Pilot added the loss of elevator authority which could possibly be caused by CG being too far forward.

Air carrier flight crew reported an unusual aircraft vibration during arrival. A flight attendant informed the crew that a portion of the flap was missing. The flight crew continued to destination and completed a safe landing.
Cessna 172 pilot reported the alternator failed in flight. Reporter contacted ATC with a hand held radio and landed without incident.
Gulfstream GIV pilot reported hydraulic system failure resulted in flight due to incorrect hydraulic hose installation.

Enroute Air Traffic Controller reported having an aircraft begin a rapid descent towards another aircraft and both aircraft were not responding on frequency.  Both aircraft responded to TCAS alerts to avoid a collision.
Ground employee reported observing a fuel truck drive through the Operational Safety Zone while an aircraft was taxiing into the gate.
Pilot reported the airport beacon at LKU is very weak and difficult to see.
GA pilot reported an NMAC while in cruise flight requiring evasive action. The other aircraft did not have any lights on.
G650 pilot reported an aileron control failure during cruise flight and had to immediately descend to avoid a stall. The pilot diverted to an alternate airport and safely landed.
Flight Instructor and corporate jet pilot reported a near miss at a non-towered airport resulted when the jet took off while the Flight Instructor was on short final approach to the same runway.
A319 Technicians reported confusion over required inspection procedures after a man lift impacted the fan cowl of an engine; causing damage.
Air carrier Flight Attendants reported smoke filled the passenger cabin along with a fire smell while on initial approach. The flight continued to a safe landing.

Flight instructor reported while in the pattern they observed an aircraft on the Runway waiting to depart at an intersection down field and another aircraft on the Runway waiting to depart full length who was not aware of the other aircraft.  Flight instructor advised the aircraft waiting full length of the other aircraft and they departed after the other aircraft departed.
A J3 Cub pilot in the pattern at a private airstrip reported an NMAC when a helicopter passed close by.
Ground Personnel reported that a passenger bag with lithium batteries was transported in cargo.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a NMAC with another aircraft while in the I69 non-towered airport traffic pattern.
Air carrier Captain reported the loss of the air condition and pressurization packs during cruise. The flight returned without incident.
An instructor pilot departing a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with opposite direction landing traffic which had not been communicating on the CTAF.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported hearing a clunking sound during gear extension. After landing; aircraft was towed to gate due to gear door restricting nosewheel movement.
Pilot reported loss of engine power on lift off and elected to land on the remaining runway safely.
