B747-400 flight crew reported an EICAS message FLAPS DRIVE at cruise and elected to dump fuel and return to the departure airport.

Air carrier First Officer reported while taxiing onto the runway; the aircraft slid on the icy surface. The Captain used reverse thrust and braking to stop the aircraft and it did not depart the runway. Reporter stated the RCAM values were incorrect and runway surface was a sheet of ice.
B737-800 flight crew observed nonstandard procedures by ground crew during pushback.
C172 flight instructor reported an NMAC when they took off from a non-towered airport in front of an aircraft on short final.
Air Carrier Captain reported being notified at destination that an incorrect Dangerous Goods Form was delivered at departure and accepted by Captain.
A Fractional Jet First Officer reported a Wing Overheat warning on initial approach to their destination.
Pilot instructor reported after takeoff at 650 MSL the engine shutdown due to inadequate fuel. Pilot requested priority handling and landed safely.
B767 First Officer reported the aircraft encountered mountain wave turbulence causing a speed increase above the maximum operating speed.
Air carrier First Officer reported loss of GPS signal on arrival into DEN; possibly related to 5G interference.
Mooney 20C pilot reported operating an aircraft with an open discrepancy in the log book for an inoperative oil temperature gauge.
A319 crew reported left wing leak ECAM during cruise portion of a ferry flight. Flight crew elected to divert and landed safely.

Air Carrier pilot reported an air carrier taxiing onto the runway had to take evasive action to avoid the reporter's aircraft which was at the hold short line. B737 and A320 aircraft taxiing on the new Taxiway J centerline markings at DCA do not remain clear of the north-south hold short line.
B787 Captain reported starting the pushback without the final Dangerous Goods document.
SCT Front Line Manager reported observing an aircraft below the MVA; advised Controller working to issue a low altitude alert. Controller did not act as instructed by Front Line Manager.
Solo pilot reported encountering windshear during landing flare which caused the aircraft to departed the runway surface.
DCA Tower Controller reported the suspension of visual separation and the use of minimum radar separation at DCA Airport has led to increased complexity; risk; and go-arounds for arrivals.
A320 Captain reported possible control law malfunction during approach.
Helicopter flight instructor reported another helicopter on a converging resulted in taking immediate evasive action and a NMAC event.
Air carrier Captain reported a conflict between published crossing restrictions and the FMS database of both the KEDMA1B SID and the XUDED1B STAR at MMPR airport.

Air Carrier Captain reported encountering severe turbulence during climb while on a SID. The weather caused a speed and altitude deviation. The Captain stated; at one point; the pilots were along for the ride. Reports to ATC; Dispatch and Maintenance were made. A Logbook entry was completed.

Air carrier First Officer reported a tug driver pushed them back to the wrong spot then disconnected without communicating; resulting in the aircraft rolling uncontrollably until pilots applied the brakes.

A321 Captain reported receiving an ECAM message: Fuel Left Wing Tank Overflow in cruise. The flight crew ran the ECAM procedure and reviewed the ECAM Supplemental Manual. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to land.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a Lithium Ion battery powered skateboard checked in cargo. The passenger and skateboard were removed from the flight.

ZMA Controller reported aircraft responded a RA from a non-tracked target that just appeared within altitude filter limits; resulted in a NMAC situation.  Controller also reported operations manager stated this as a non-event.
Aeronca Champion pilot reported the pilot flying encountered a wind gust during landing resulting in a runway excursion; ground loop; and the aircraft hitting an runway sign.
First Officer reported a breakdown in Crew Resource Management led to a cabin altitude exceedance and an immediate descent to minimize the possible affects of hypoxia on the crew.
C172 pilot reported an NMAC event with another aircraft while trying to land at the airport and bouncing on the landing; and proceeded to perform a go-around procedure.
EMB-145 flight crew reported an engine failure in cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported low winged converging traffic on downwind entry resulted in evasive action and an NMAC.
A319 Captain reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of a 'faint musty dirty socks odor' during initial approach.
Flight Instructor reported their student lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a bent propeller and nose gear damage.
A321 flight attendants reported a fumes event during cruise. Flight attendants were met by paramedics upon gate arrival to evaluate physiological symptoms.
B737-700 flight crew reported an APU failure during taxi and returned to the gate to deplane.
Flight crew reported a high speed Rejected Take Off after extreme precipitation and wind shear caused visibility to go to zero.
B737 flight crew reported the fire detection system malfunctioned after takeoff. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Pilot reported efforts to avoid traffic resulted in taking evasive action and a NMAC.

Decathlon 8KCAB pilot reported an NMAC event with an aircraft on the crosswind leg while entering the left downwind in the pattern. After the close encounter the pilot realized the radio was on the wrong frequency; communicated to the other pilot after correcting the frequency; and returned to the pattern.
FAA Designated Pilot Examiner reported the right main tire came off the wheel after touchdown. The aircraft was tugged off the runway with no other damage.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported that during a training flight the engine fuel pump failed. Flight returned to the departure airport.
A General Aviation pilot reported turning onto crossing Runway 11 instead of Taxiway H after landing on Runway 34 at HPN.
Reporter stated they saw an aircraft fly close by their aircraft in opposite direction resulting in what appeared to be a NMAC.
Flight Instructor reported a dual ECU failure and a resulting diversion to a nearby airport.
A300 flight crew reported they were informed by the ramp agent that the cargo container in the forward compartment was not properly secured. No damage was found on the aircraft or the container.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
EMB-145 Flight Attendant reported a cockpit window failure after parking at the gate.
B737 MAX First Officer reported the aircraft alerting system volume is extremely loud. Alerts interrupt all other communications.

GA pilot reported receiving the COVID-19 vaccine and flying before the 48 hour wait period requirement.  Pilot did not read the FAA circular regarding the vaccine and developed a headache during the flight.
An Air Carrier pilot reported an 'ENG L BRG OVHT' message and shut down the engine. The pilot reported difficulty communicating with ATC due to radio interference.
TRACON Controller reported reporting to work and learning of new procedures that were not briefed resulting in confusion in the TRACON and Tower.
C172 single pilot reported a runway excursion during landing due to an unstable approach. The aircraft reported no damage and flew to home airport safely.
B757 Captain reported on climbout they received a Leading Edge Flap Asymmetry EICAS message and returned to their departure airport and landed.
Air Carrier Captain reported they had to slow down significantly while taxiing to avoid collision with a tug pulling an aircraft across their path.
PA-28 Flight Instructor with student reported NMAC with another aircraft.
A319 Captain reported failure of a generator; autopilot; autothrust; and flight and navigation displays during cruise. Flight continued to destination and landed.
A321 Flight Attendant reported a fumes event upon arrival at aircraft for pre-flight. Captain was notified and directed the cabin crew to deplane to jetbridge and wait for maintenance to arrive.
MD11 flight crew reported unsafe landing gear indications after takeoff and returned to land at departure airport.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a fumes event during taxi-out resulting in a return to the gate.

Captain reported a hydraulic system quantity EICAS message after aircraft was fully configured for landing. After noticing degraded steering on the ground; the flight crew stopped the aircraft on the taxiway and proceeded to shut the aircraft engines down and called for a tow to the gate.
A319 Captain reported they had to suddenly stop taxiing to avoid another taxiing aircraft.
Pilot reported engine failure inflight during descent. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Captain reported rejecting the takeoff due to loss of aircraft control. The reporter states that it may have been caused by a heavy aircraft that was departing on an intersecting runway.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was seated in the company social distancing blocked seat.
SR-20 pilot reported a ground conflict while on approach due to another aircraft on the runway. Pilot executed an ATC-directed go around and returned to land.
SR-22 pilot reported a runway excursion during takeoff roll that resulted in a collision with a runway light. Pilot continued takeoff and returned for a safe landing.
A80 TRACON Controller reported AUO Tower's shout line has been out of service for an extended period and has made timely communication and coordination with the Tower difficult.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a CFIT situation due in part by an ATC assigned extended downwind vector in mountainous terrain area. As the flight crew were discussing altitude concerns ATC issued a low altitude alert and assigned a climb and heading correction. Flight crew complied expeditiously and continued to a safe landing.
B737-800 crew reported a blown tire during takeoff. Crew continued on to destination and landed uneventfully.
CE750 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence departing FLL in trail of an A320 that resulted in a 45 degree roll.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported a loose piece of concrete on the ramp at JVL struck and caused damage to the propeller during taxi.
CRJ-200 Captain reported a hydraulic malfunction during cruise flight. The Captain diverted to an alternate airport; landed safely; then needed to be towed to the ramp.

ATC Ground Controller reported giving incorrect taxi instructions to a crew resulting in a ground conflict.  Controller reported the crews had not flown into their airport in a while due the COVID-19 pandemic; which may have contributed to the event.
Air carrier flight crew reported while in cruise flight noticing a strong acrid smell and smoke in the flight deck resulting in the flight crew returning to the departure airport.
Air Carrier Pilot Crew encountered a laser while on final approach; reported that the Captain became ill after the event. First Officer completed a normal landing.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving ACARS DG message for two planned DG items during pushback but did not received the final DG summary indicating the DG cargo was not loaded. The flight crew did not send the acknowledgement code and departed safely.

EMB-505 First Officer reported a loss of cabin pressure at cruise altitude and as the flight crew attempted to perform an emergency descent under ATC control; the aircraft completed the descent automatically. The flight crew was under high workload and descended below the level-off but got back to the correct altitude soon after.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Maintenance stores personnel reported an inflated tire was found in the unserviceable rack of the Maintenance department.

B737-800 Flight Attendant reported waste water and sewage were leaking from a lavatory during a flight sequence. After reporting it to the Captain; the discrepancy went unaddressed by the flight crew and maintenance.

Flight crew reported that another aircraft saw fire coming out of their left engine during take off roll. They rejected the takeoff and with the absence of fire warning or detection indications taxied back to the gate.
Air carrier pilot reported observing another aircraft perform a missed approach due to runway incursions at MHR airport.
DA42 Flight Instructor reported on departure opposite direction traffic was closer than expected resulted in evasive action and a NMAC.

A Tower Ground Controller reported tower positions were combined due to lack of staffing and no one in the tower noticed the Local Controller taxied an aircraft across a runway at the same time another aircraft was departing.
DA40 pilot reported an NMAC during final approach to a non-towered airport with a Helicopter overflying the airfield.

Air carrier First Officer reported disagreement between the Captain and First Officer flight displays during descent in icing conditions resulted in altitude and airspeed deviations. Post flight inspection revealed layers of ice on probes.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion while taxiing at night at RJTT and cited the close proximity of the taxiways; poor lighting; and confusing signage as contributing to the event.

Air carrier pilot reported Runway 17R at DFW is unsafe for departures due to its shortened length and does not have enough length for potential rejected takeoffs. There was an active construction zone at the end of the runway; which added to the safety hazard for takeoffs utilizing that runway. The airport pages were also still showing the runway with its original; longer length.
Bonanza 35 Pilot reported being distracted during taxi and inadvertently making contact with a vehicle parked next to the taxiway.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on a visual approach to HTS airport.
Flight Attendants reported the aircraft diverted to due to mechanical issues and upon landing experienced a small fire in the landing gear due to the brakes overheating.

Air carrier crew reported an APU fire while loading at the parked position on the ramp. The crew observed smoke at the tail of the aircraft without cockpit warning; activated fire bottles; then evacuated safely by airstairs.
C182 pilot reported receiving terrain alerts when his aircraft was unable to maintain safe altitude because of ice accumulation.
ABQ Tower Local Controller reported they issued a turn and altitude restriction to a departure to avoid VFR aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air traffic Controller reported being distracted with briefings and sector updating while an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude.
A321 Pilot reported a tug driver pushed them back into the wrong direction to the wrong spot twice.
PA-18 pilot reported a prop strike occurred when the brakes locked during landing roll.
Pilot reported safety concerns related to inadequate staffing that could result if employees take leave in order to not comply with the company's vaccine mandate.
A319 First Officer reported descending to a lower altitude after experiencing #1 engine compressor stall at FL350.

Flight instructor reported that during landing rollout another aircraft began takeoff roll from the opposite end of the runway without making any radio calls. Reporter exited the runway and stated that the other aircraft stopped on the runway.
EMB-145 Captain reported the crew oversped the flaps during climbout when they were distracted by ATC traffic calls.
Gulfstream GV Captain reported landing safely after the left engine rolled back with an apparent compressor stall possibly related to a bird strike.
Flight Crew reported excessive engine vibration and elected to continue the approach with the #2 engine thrust lever at idle.
Air carrier pilot reported uncommanded autopilot engagement during departure climb.
Trainee Pilot reported alternator failure resulted in a diversion and a precautionary landing.
MD-11 First Officer reported loss of the #3 hydraulic system in flight which resulted in a precautionary landing.
A Tecnam P2006 Flight Instructor reported a tail strike on landing; due to an airspeed anomaly; caused by a malfunctioning Pitot-Static system.

Air carrier flight crew reported an Air Data Computer issue during approach resulted in multiple flight instrument malfunctions. Flight crew requested priority handling and continued to a safe landing.

NCT TRACON Controller reported they vectored and turned an aircraft they temporarily lost radar contact with. The aircraft may have been below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude when flying the assigned heading.
Diamond Star pilot reported their navigation displays failed at night resulting in an unstable approach and a prop strike.
Air carrier Captain reported the transport of prohibited Hazmat cargo due to improper labeling of Hazmat cargo.
Center Controller reported reneging on a handoff for a helicopter that was off the filed airway; below the MEA; and the minimum IFR altitude.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported a company aircraft committed a taxiway incursion by turning onto the wrong taxiway; coming nose to nose with another aircraft. ATC then resolved the situation.
Pilot reported ADC (Air Data Computer) 1 failing inflight; causing numerous false warnings and requiring Flight Crew to rely on ADC2 for the remainder of the successful flight to destination airport.

A Tower Controller reported an aircraft turn final in front of the aircraft it was supposed to follow resulting in the trailing aircraft reporting a NMAC. The reporter stated they warned the Local Controller and their Trainee of the impending confliction but neither one of them took timely action to resolve the situation.

Air carrier Captain reported there were no guide lines for parking or taxiing on the East Pad at SAN and it was difficult to determine if the aircraft was clear from other objects. Another air carrier approached the area and also could not determine if there was proper wing tip clearance. Airport Operations had to be called to clear the conflict.
Airbus flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to PHX in trail of a Boeing commercial jet that contributed to flight stability issues and a momentary flap overspeed.
Air taxi Captain reported they crossed runway hold short line without clearance which resulted in ATC issuing a go-around to a landing aircraft.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported briefly exceeding their authorized altitude. Pilot descended and completed mission as planned.
B777-200 flight crew reported failure to notice that the Dangerous Goods manifest indicated the wrong destination when loaded on the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported GPWS Terrain warning while following ATC clearance at Minimum Vectoring Altitude during approach to ELP airport.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported autopilot malfunction affecting pitch trim. Instructor dis-engaged autopilot and regained aircraft control.
C172 pilot reported landing safely after experiencing engine failure on short final.
General aviation pilot reported they flew low over terrain to avoid entering overlying Class C airspace.

Captain reported that the Captain's windshield made a loud pop and the outer pane shattered. The flight crew requested immediate descent to reduce stress on the windshield and continued to make a landing at an airport where maintenance was available.
Captain reported trailing edge flaps failed to extend past 25 degrees during final approach. The flight crew performed a go around and elected to divert due to weather.
CL-350 flight crew reported descending below their assigned altitude resulting in flight towards terrain.  ATC issued an alert and climb to safe altitude.
A near collision during taxi at DFW was reported by two flight crews.
Air taxi flight crew reported a NMAC while descending for a visual approach under ATC control. The flight crew took evasive action and then continued on course to landing.
Captain reported autopilot failed and disconnected on approach with false EGPWS warning. After landing; Captain reported multiple EICAS messages indicating a total trim failure.
PC-12 pilot reported cabin depressurization. Pilot descended and diverted to land.
Part 107 UAS crew reported a battery which began to deplete rapidly and the UAS initiated a return to home (RTH). During the RTH the UAS may have flown over a populated area.
A Bonanza pilot reported a bounced landing caused the front landing gear to collapse.
Air carrier Captain reported a severe turbulence encounter during cruise. Altitude was stabilized and the flight continued.
B777-200 flight crew reported overweight configuration inflight due to overfueling.
C-172 pilot reported runway incursion with another aircraft preparing to land and forced to do a go-around at a non-towered airport.

Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they were using manual flight mode during cruise flight when UAS made a noncommanded right hand turn into a nearby tree. The UAS suffered minor damage and was recovered.

Experimental aircraft pilot reported while maneuvering in the practice area another aircraft was transitioning the practice area not transmitting on the UNICOM resulting in both aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier Captain reported ATC warned them of a UAS in their area. The First Officer saw the UAS and no evasive action was taken.
Air carrier First Officer reported GPS and Transponder failure along with course misalignment indications on localizer.
CE-700 First Officer reported on climb out they were unable to trim the aircraft resulting in returning to the departure airport where they landed safely.

Air carrier Captain reported being notified by Dispatch while enroute that undocumented HAZMAT was improperly processed and loaded in cargo. Ramp personnel failed to issue a Dangerous Good form; which would have notified Dispatch and flight crew.
A321 Captain reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of strong cabin fumes during taxi. Flight crew returned to gate for maintenance and medical evaluations.

B737-800 inbound and outbound flight crews reported extensive water damage to the cockpit and related systems after a cockpit sliding window was left open after an aircraft reposition. The aircraft was shut down and logbook entries made; with the aircraft being turned over to maintenance.
Air carrier Captain reported a taxi deviation at CVG and indicated that a lack of signage contributed to the event.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving aircraft terrain warning during approach. Flight crew corrected and continued.
A TRACON Controller reported an air carrier pilot reported an NMAC with an unidentified VFR aircraft that was not displaying a transponder or in communication with ATC.
Pilot reported a NMAC on base leg resulted in taking evasive action.

B737 pilot reported rejecting a takeoff in response to a spoiler configuration warning horn. Pilot stated there is an increased frequency of these warnings and recommended maintenance action to determine the cause.
Student Pilot on a solo flight and the student's Flight Instructor reported a runway excursion occurred during crosswind landing.

Air Carrier pilot reported excessive frequency congestion at EWR airport due to VFR transitioning aircraft using the same Tower frequency as arrival and departure traffic. The reporter stated these operations used to have separate frequencies to alleviate the congestion.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving a terrain alert while on base leg of a visual approach.
B767 flight crew reported fluid leaking over wing; resulted in divert.
UAS pilot reported losing ability to control new drone on three test flights.

C-150 flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training with another training aircraft. Both aircraft lost separation during the crosswind turn resulting in the C-150 Instructor electing to depart the pattern.
An observer reported seeing footage of a UAS possibly flying above 400 feet AGL and flying near two aircraft.
Aircraft maintenance technician reported aircraft overwing slide inadvertently deployed due to unclear work instructions on job card.
Air carrier Captain reported the Captain's signed NOTOC copy did not remain on board prior to departure.
Air carrier technician reported while opening the overwing exit hatch on the B767-300 to expose the electrical connector for the inspector; the exterior step disengaged.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving indications of a cargo fire just prior to reaching their cruise altitude. The crew initiated a diversion to an eventual safe landing.
ARTCC Controller reported receiving a low altitude alert on an aircraft. Controller issued a low altitude alert to the pilot who updated the altimeter setting and climbed to safe altitude.

A321 flight crew reported a rejected takeoff after noticing a discrepancy in the First Officer's airspeed indicator.  Post flight maintenance inspection revealed debris in the pitot static system was the cause of the malfunction.
EMB-170 flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control during taxi out due to snow and weak braking action.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a lost link after liftoff causing the UAS to crash into a tree.
Part 107 pilot reported they flew into controlled airspace without LAANC approval. The pilot exited the airspace and landed the UAS.

Embraer 145 air carrier crew reported numerous equipment malfunction alerts including landing gear; autopilot; and terrain warnings during descent. The crew performed a procedure outside of Flight Manual guidance to silence the alarms and landed safely.
An Instructor Pilot reported a NMAC approaching the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport.
Air Traffic Controller reported aircraft failed to hold assigned altitude descending below MVA resulted in a low altitude alert and a CFTT event.
A320 flight crew reported the aircraft veered right after applying takeoff power resulting in an aborted take off and a return to the gate.
Pilot departing VNC; a non-towered airport; reported experiencing an NMAC on departure; and expressed concern that VNC needs a Tower to enhance safe operations.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving an EGPWS alert during visual approach.
Air carrier Ground personnel reported a Hazmat placement and tie down violation when toilets are shipped as checked baggage.
A319 flight crew reported receiving ECAM messages for multiple aircraft system malfunctions after takeoff. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed without incident.
GA flight instructor with student reported a NMAC in the vicinity of X65 non-towered airport requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
B777 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on initial climb out of HNL from another B777 landing on an intersecting runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of several systems in cruise that required a diversion due to the inability to continue the flight as planned in forecast icing conditions.

Beechjet 400 Captain reported a loss of flight control while on initial approach. The Captain stated the aircraft began to pitch down then up before it was recovered for a missed approach. Flight icing was present.
Air carrier First Officer reported a terrain caution while in a descent on a visual approach in an area of mountainous terrain. The pilot leveled the aircraft then safely continued the approach.

B747-400 flight crew reported a main cargo deck fire indication during taxi out and requested fire trucks. There were no fire indications found so the flight crew returned to the gate for maintenance action.
Air carrier Captain reported a Terrain Warning during approach to BGR airport; and the First Officer disconnected the autopilot and added power to climb; after which the Terrain Warning silenced.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they inadvertently flew into controlled airspace without authorization.

BOI Tower Controller reported an aircraft executing the published ILS missed approach in foggy conditions passed too close to the Control Tower. Reporter stated aircraft passing close to Tower on this procedure has occurred on several occasions; and recommended a change to the procedure.
Air carrier First Officer reported a taxi error at RJTT due to taxiway sign being obscured by tall grass.
B737 flight crew reported receiving airspeed disagree flag during cruise. Flight crew continued to receive unreliable airspeed indications as they continued to destination and landed.

C172 Flight Instructor reported the LRY and LWC airports are referred to using the same name and share the same CTAF; with similar airfield layouts that resulted in confusion with pilots and ATC. Flight Instructor also reported these two airports are 47nm apart creating radio overlap.
Air carrier 737-300 Captain reported a loss of pressurization at cruise altitude and returned to the departure airport.
B737 Captain reported a failure of the #2 Generator Drive; followed by an APU auto-shutdown due to a fault. Flight continued to destination.
First Officer reported exceeding flap limit speed on descent into CLT; citing wind gusts and a possible wake turbulence encounter contributing to the cause of the event.
EMB-505 flight crew reported brake failure while holding short on taxiway. Flight crew maneuvered away from the area and set the parking brake.

Air carrier technician reported while troubleshooting an A319 door escape slide arm-disarm lever resistance; the technicians opened the door with the escape slide lever in the disarm position.  When the door was operated the last time; the girt bar was engaged in the floor fittings and the escape slide deployed but did not inflate.

C172 Flight Instructor with student reported losing engine power during a cross country training flight resulting in an off-airport landing. Upon inspection; maintenance discovered water in the fuel strainer suggesting fuel contamination.

EMB-170 flight crew reported the aircraft rolled backwards after releasing the brakes; though the Tug Driver had given the flight crew approval for brake release. The Captain then applied the brakes to stop the aircraft and reconfirmed with the Tug Driver. The pushback was completed uneventfully.
M20E pilot reported engine running rough and subsequently losing power during cruise flight.  Pilot was able to divert with assistance of ATC for an uneventful landing.

Corporate jet pilot reported a near miss with an aircraft while descending on final approach which resulted in a TCAS RA. The pilot leveled off following the TCAS RA guidance; then continued to landing.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft filled with smoke while parked at the gate. The Captain directed the crew to evacuate and requested emergency equipment.
C208 Captain reported on departure flying the wrong SID resulting in flight towards terrain event.  ATC vectored the aircraft back on course and climb above the terrain.

A321 flight crew reported #2 engine bleed air failure at top of descent. This was followed by a cabin pressure failure to maintain cabin altitude. The flight crew descended to a safe altitude and continued to landing at destination airport.
Helicopter pilot reported that while they were hover taxiing a tug pulling a cargo container passed directly in front of the helicopter under the rotor disk.
CRJ-900 Captain reported sliding past the hold short line for the active runway and informing the Tower Controller who instructed a landing aircraft to go around.
Air carrier Captain reported a system malfunction during pre-flight that may have been caused by GPS interference testing.

Air carrier flight crew reported momentary loss of aircraft control during landing caused by wind gusts. The aircraft moved to the left with the left main gear touching the grass off the runway before the Captain was able to correct and bring the aircraft back to centerline.

EMB-145 Captain reported that flight departed with one pack on MEL and during cruise climb the second pack failed. The flight crew reset the failed pack and continued to destination at a lower altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported erroneously entering SKNNR intersection prior to departure that sounded and was spelled similarly to their cleared SKNRR intersection. The intersections are 209 miles apart. Captain caught the error but expressed concerns for a potential navigation deviation when entering the fix manually.
A corporate jet flight crew reported ATC turned them on to final approach above glide path and too close in to the airport resulting in an unstable approach to the wrong runway.
MYF Tower Controller reported low wing single engine aircraft departure deviated into parallel Runway traffic resulted in a NMAC.
A321 flight crew reported a ground conflict with a baggage vehicle during taxi to the gate.

Flight Instructor and pilot trainee reported causing a NMAC during initial climb when their aircraft drifted toward an aircraft departing from the parallel runway. Tower Controller separated both aircraft.
A Tower Controller reported a NMAC between two aircraft at the same point on final approach 200 feet apart with the higher aircraft descending.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported there was a mobility scooter and accompanying battery that did not have the proper paperwork that was to be stowed in the cabin. With the Captain's approval and required paperwork; the item was stowed in the cabin closet and the flight was slightly delayed due to this event.

Air carrier flight crew reported tower cancelled takeoff clearance while flight was on takeoff roll due to conflict with landing traffic on another runway. Flight crew rejected takeoff and returned to the gate.
A320 Captain reported a pervasive dirty socks odor throughout the aircraft prior to passenger boarding. Captain submitted a logbook entry and notified Maintenance and Dispatch.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on approach into CHO airport.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported another instructor informed them they caused an NMAC while performing holds resulting in the other instructor taking evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported runway excursion during landing/go-around event.

Gulfstream V Captain reported climbing thru 41000 ft; receiving two Amber CAS messages. First was L Hydraulic System Fail and L Hydraulic Quantity Low. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
B757 flight crew reported tail strike on landing. Flight crew performed a go-around and landed uneventfully.
General aviation pilot reported a fixed wing Large UAS flying near them during cruise flight.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL300 that resulted in an upset. Control was reestablished and the flight landed safely.

CRJ-900 FO reported a critical ground conflict. Making an approach into a non-towered airport and entering VMC; the FO stated there were snow plows on the runway. After executing a missed approach it was determined the FO had been transmitting on the wrong CTAF frequency.
C172 pilot reported while landing in windy conditions using improper braking resulting in a runway excursion. The pilot was able to taxi the aircraft to parking.
Air carrier Captain reported entertainment and power systems failed during flight and had to use fight attendants to record data so the affected passengers would receive some type of reimbursement.
Air Carrier Captain reported during push back operations at BOS airport; a lack of communication and visual with tug resulted in aircraft rolling into tug.
Flight crew reported multiple displays; the integrated standby instrument system; and the autopilot failed during initial climb in IMC. Flight crew returned to landing at departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported the aircraft rolled forward at the gate resulting in the aircraft striking the jet bridge.
SK64 pilot reported a lack of flight time limitations of U.S. registered aircraft operations in foreign countries resulted in severe fatigue situations.
Airliner 99 pilot reported an air turn back and precautionary landing after the landing gear would not retract during takeoff.

Flight Instructor reported loss of power when attempting to make a simulated engine out landing during a touch and go. The Instructor lowered the landing gear and the aircraft touched back down in the grass past the runway.

Air carrier First Officer reported the flight crew loaded the wrong fix and flew approximately 8 miles off course before being corrected by ATC. The reporter stated that the fixes KP12E and KP21E are quite similar and also close in proximity; which led to the mistake going unnoticed during route verification.
Repair Station Manager reported receiving and installing an incorrect turbine engine due to paperwork inconsistencies for both the aircraft and engine manufacturers.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported a lost link with a UAS that another crew member was flying. The pilot was unable to regain control of the UAS and it flew away.

Air carrier Captain reported intermittent GPS loss during cruise prior to descent and informed ATC; who also stated that other aircraft were experiencing GPS loss. There were no NOTAMs indicating GPS jamming or spoofing in the area.

TRACON Controller reported an aircraft did not follow the departure procedure and entered two areas below minimum altitude. Controller issued a low altitude alert and pilot continued with their flight plan.
King Air F90 Captain reported a window failure during cruise flight; resulting in a rapid depressurization. The aircraft performed a rapid descent and returned to the airport.

EMB-175 First Officer reported a low airspeed condition which resulted in a stick shaker and an altitude deviation when being vectored off the final approach course. The Captain took the controls; recovered the airspeed and altitude; then returned the controls to the First Officer for landing.
Instructor pilot reported loss of engine power on descent and continued to landing at the destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict while holding short of the runway due to a UAS flying around the runway. The pilot stayed in position until the UAS was out of the takeoff path.
Air carrier First Officer reported MYR airport diagram indicates taxiway A4 as Z.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying their UAS in an area where airspace apps showed conflicting airspace information.
Air carrier first officer reported a hard landing resulting in underbelly panels opening.
First Officer reported excessive aileron trim was required to maintain controllability. The flight crew diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported an intermittent burning odor during initial approach. The flight crew continued to a safe landing and evacuation.
Light aircraft Flight Instructor reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern at GSO during initial climb.

Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on final approach when the aircraft descended below the assigned altitude on a visual approach. The First Officer recovered the altitude loss and re-captured the glide slope.

Air carrier flight crew reported a bird strike on final approach resulted in hydraulic system damage and loss of steering control. The crew called attention to the bird hazards at MUHA/HAV airport; and suggested trash accumulating along the perimeter fences might be attracting birds to the area.
F11 Controller reported an unsafe scenario with two aircraft that were in a conflict due to a STAR and a VFR practice area being in close proximity.

B737-800 air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while on approach after an Autoflight malfunction. The Captain performed a missed approach and safely landed on the second approach.
Air carrier Captain reported a door warning light illuminated during takeoff and the initial climb. The warning light then went out.
Air Carrier Pilot reported the taxiway signage is misleading at DEN airport.
G650 flight crew reported erroneous airspeed and altitude indications after takeoff.
Pilot reported loss of hydraulic fluid resulted in a precautionary emergency landing.

Air carrier Flight Attendants reported a fume event after arrival at the destination. All passengers and flight crew deplaned safely at the gate where Captain and the Flight Attendant submitted required reports.
PA-28 instructor pilot reported a partial electrical power loss in cruise. The flight crew elected to divert.
First Officer reported encountering wind shear on final approach; receiving a EGPWS alert; and continuing to land even though a missed approach was appropriate.
GA pilot reported losing control and stalling aircraft during landing.
A321 flight crew reported a reported rejected takeoff due to the aircraft nose yawing to the left.  The crew stated Maintenance found faults with the FMC 1 system which could have caused the issue.
Private pilot reported a loss of control when the aircraft's exterior door opened in-flight. The pilot was able to regain control of the aircraft; close the door and return to the origin airport.
ERJ 170 flight crew reported loss of aircraft control due to microburst and windshear on final approach. Flight crew regained aircraft control and conducted a go-around.

Light transport flight crew and Air Traffic Ground Controller reported the light transport crossed the runway hold short line as instructed by ATC with another aircraft on takeoff roll resulting in a critical ground conflict. Departing aircraft rejected takeoff.
B737 Captain reported a main cabin door light during the low speed regime of the takeoff roll. The Captain rejected the takeoff and returned to the gate for maintenance.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying their UAS in controlled airspace. Pilot reported they subsequently obtained the TRUST certificate and now knows where to fly.
Air carrier Captain reported a new procedure regarding flight crew acknowledgement of the receipt of the final dangerous goods summary is unnecessary.
ARTCC Controller reported C172 vectored for ILS descended below assigned altitude resulted in low altitude alert.

Air carrier crew reported a loss of control during a higher altitude cruise climb in an area of thunderstorms and severe turbulence. The crew regained aircraft control of speed and altitude then continued to destination.
C182 pilot reported while on a visual approach; ATC issued a possible erroneous low altitude alert.
Air carrier flight crew on approach reported a NMAC with a GA aircraft doing pattern work on a different runway. Pilot flying took evasive action to avoid a collision.

PA-31 Pilot reported Left and Right Fuel Boost Pump failures in climb. Both engines began to run rough and lose power. The Pilot was unable to make it to any airfield and was forced to land in a field 1.1 miles short of the airport.
TRACON Controller reported vectoring an aircraft below the MVA towards a lower MVA and away from conflicting traffic. When clear of traffic ATC climbed the aircraft without further incident.
Traffic Management Developmental reported working in the TMU without being completely certified due to a sick leave call in.

BE33 general aviation pilot reported the floorboard becoming hot during climb from a missed approach on a training flight. It was determined that a piece of the exhaust manifold detached from the aircraft.
A320 Captain reported a fume event of 'dirty socks' during descent that lasted until gate arrival.
GA pilot reported landing on a closed runway at PAE airport.
A pilot reported a NMAC while practicing ground reference maneuvers in the traffic pattern of a non-towered private airport.
Air carrier Captain reported after encountering severe turbulence on approach they diverted.
Air carrier flight crew reported not checking a cargo door that had a MEL on it due to a distraction before departing resulting in a divert.
Flight Instructor flying C-172 aircraft reported NMAC with another aircraft during go-around.
C182 Flight Instructor reported on take off observing unannounced traffic climbing from crossing runway; resulted in taking evasive action to avoid NMAC.
TRACON Controller conducting training reported the trainee descended an aircraft to an altitude 100 feet below the minimum vectoring altitude.
C525 Captain reported engine oil pressure warning resulted in engine shutdown.
Air carrier Captain reported pushing back from the gate prior to submitting the DG acknowledgement code due to an intermittent ACARS at the gate; but did complete that task before taxiing.

Flight Instructor and Student reported that while they were departing the traffic pattern; the engine malfunctioned followed by excessive vibration and partial loss of power.  They returned to the airport and experienced complete engine failure upon landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported while on the ILS PRM 10C approach at ORD the monitor frequency was being used by ATC to issue taxi instructions. Flight crew stated concern that this created a safety of flight issue should a PRM breakout take place.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the taxi line at MSLP airport is too near the grass area for a large aircraft; creating a safety concern.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing GPS jamming in the vicinity of SJN VORTAC.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fumes event during approach. The odor was described as a 'burning oil and acrid'. Flight crew donned oxygen masks and executed a safe landing. Upon gate arrival an aircraft logbook entry was submitted to maintenance.
Pilot reported alternator failure during IFR training flight over terrain. The flight crew diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Flight Instructor on training flight reported electrical malfunction inflight. Returned to departure airport.
C172 pilot reported a CFIT event during a night approach in which the aircraft had difficulty in maintaining a climb gradient to clear terrain due to the weather.
Technicians reported a fuel spill was caused by lack of proper coordination and communications.

Air carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation during departure from FLL on the VACAY 1.  The Captain stated they missed going to FEMTO during the departure as expected by ATC. Reporter also stated the Controller mentioned there is confusion with the procedure and that this was not the first time it happened.

Student pilot reported a traffic conflict with another aircraft on final approach during takeoff roll at a non-tower airport; and also during initial climb when the trailing aircraft performed a go-around. The student pilot increased speed once airborne and offset course to provide separation.

Air carrier pilots reported receiving an RA while descending. The pilot flying started a climb until receiving ' clear of conflict.' The other aircraft was also an air carrier tracking towards the reporting pilots. There was no alert from ATC according to the reporter.
ERJ-170 Captain reported experiencing a violent wake turbulence upset departing MIA in trail of an A320.

A320 First Officer reported hearing two loud bangs during their pre-flight process and boarding.  The First Officer stated that during the fueling and boarding process the jet bridge and aircraft door hinges made contact causing enough damage for Maintenance to take the aircraft out of service.
A B757-200 flight crew reported speed brakes did not deploy on short final; resulting in a temporary loss of control and a possible hard landing.
A321 reported the left main gear brakes froze while taxiing in and caught fire so the aircraft was evacuated at the gate. The fire was extinguished and both left tires were flat.
Captain reported on climb out the Stab Out of Trim annunciator light illuminated. The flight crew continued to destination airport and landed.
A Flight Instructor departing from a non-towered airport reported that they taxied on to the runway with another aircraft on short final.
Pilot reported a runway excursion on landing rollout that resulted in a collapsed nose wheel.
Dispatcher reported Flight Crew conducted an air turn back after landing gear would not retract.
Cessna 550 Captain reported the GMU PAPI appeared to be mis-aligned in the vertical path; compared to the aircraft derived vertical path while on final approach.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported Hazmat overflight notification missed on NAT route flight release. The flight arrived at destination without notification.
Cessna 172 flight instructor and student reported an engine malfunction on a training flight at night while practicing landings. The instructor coordinated with ATC and landed safely.
ERJ-175 First Officer reported an altitude excursion after encountering wake turbulence while in cruise flight at FL330.
A319 pilot reported a large swerve during takeoff roll resulted in rejected takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported an airframe vibration during cruise flight and during troubleshooting; an engine oil filter warning illuminated. The aircraft diverted to an alternate airport and landed.
C172 Flight Instructor and student reported loss of directional control during landing resulted in runway excursion.
Float plane pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft at A11 that was on its takeoff run.
Pilot reported the VFR sectional chart does not adequately identify AUO airport as a towered airport with class D airspace.

Dispatcher reported that information on crane status; needed for the proper application of several temporary approach restrictions at AFW; was unavailable from the agency described in the text of the NOTAMs as having that knowledge regarding the impact of the construction cranes on AFW operations. When the crane is up; CAT 1 minimums are affected and planes are restricted from CAT 2 and 3 approaches. Calls to Airport Operations and ATC reportedly yielded no information.
Air carrier pilot reported GPS interference 100 miles southeast of the DLN VOR during cruise.

A320 pilot reported multiple fumes events on assigned aircraft. Reporter suggested there needs to be collaboration between the FAA; aircraft manufacturer; and air carrier to remedy frequent fume events.
A320 Captain reported concerns with being dispatched with an inoperative Slat Flap Control Computer.

Malibu pilot reported while taxing proceeding slight beyond a hold point resulting in crossing traffic aircraft to take evasive action to avoid a collision with the aircraft.  ATC had the Malibu continue their taxi to the FBO.

C172 Flight Instructor reported an NMAC while in the traffic pattern on a training flight at a non-towered airport in visual conditions. The instructor visually acquired the other aircraft and took evasive action to avoid a collision. After passing the aircraft; the flight instructor returned to the traffic pattern and landed.
Flight Instructor reported the engine lost partial power during initial climbout. As the instructor returned for immediate landing they had a NMAC with another aircraft in the traffic pattern.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported unable to communicate with a flight for over 4 hours during cruise in oceanic airspace. The flight regained communications with the Dispatcher and ATC when entering VFR radio coverage.
Flight Attendant reported being distracted by a disruptive passenger and forgetting to advise the flight crew the cabin was secure and they were ready to depart.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a fume event during flight. After conferring with flight attendants the Captain elected to return to departure airport where a safe landing was completed and maintenance support was provided.
Private pilot reported while on short final another aircraft entered the runway and began to takeoff.
An aerobatic pilot who has a FAA waiver for a designated Aerobatic Practice Area reported traffic routinely flies through the airspace when it is advertised in use.
Single Pilot reported flying into icing conditions and the control surfaces freezing.

During door closure for departure; Flight Attendant reported a door did not close properly and was advised by maintenance there was no problem. In flight; the door began leaking and the Flight Attendant became incapacitated; resulting in an air turn back.
B737 flight crew reported AFT CARGO FIRE WARNING and Fire Bell during cruise.  Crew returned to departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported a near miss during taxi with an aircraft that turned in front of them.
GA flight instructor with student reported an NMAC in OKC Class E airspace requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
Light Twin Engine Pilot reported loss of GPS signal which resulted in loss of altitude reporting.

B787 Captain reported the DG documentation erroneously listed a 'wet cell battery' was onboard; with no way of correcting the error. After communicating with multiple parties; the Captain received an ACARS message that stated that no such battery was loaded onboard; and the flight was able to depart.
Pilot reported a NMAC while entering the traffic pattern.

A TRACON Controller reported a Cessna departing in marginal weather reported their Attitude Indicator failed and they needed to return to their departure airport. The Controller vectored the aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude to assist its return for an approach.

Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving a windshear alert during landing approach. Flight crew followed the FM guidance and performed a normal go-around which was followed by ATC provided directions for a normal approach and a safe landing.
Tug driver reported they had to stop pushback suddenly when an aircraft at a nearby gate began pushback and caused a critical ground conflict.

Tecnam P-Mentor pilot reported during landing roll the aircraft pulled to the left uncontrollably. Pilot stopped aircraft on runway where maintenance personnel hit the rudder with a mallet to unlock the brakes.

An air carrier pilot conducting an approach at night to SKBO reported they were cleared for a 'RNP Visual Flight Proc.' approach but charts and the Flight Manual were not clear if they were authorized to fly this procedure.
Grumman AA5 pilot reported unstable approach and landing resulted in momentary loss of control and a prop strike.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a malfunction with the UAS that caused a rapid climb above 400 feet AGL. The pilot was able to regain control of the UAS and land safely.

B737 flight crew reported a tail pipe fire on #2 engine after arrival to the gate and coordinated with ground personnel to motor the engine to extinguish the fire. The aircraft was removed from service for inspection.

Cessna 177 pilot reported a NMAC while descending to avoid clouds and to maintain VFR. The pilot indicated the traffic was previously obscured until the descent; the pilot maneuvered and increased the descent rate to avoid the aircraft.

A small aircraft Student Pilot reported they were on short final when an aircraft on approach to an intersecting runway did a touch and go instead of a full stop and flew directly into their path causing a NMAC.
Air carrier Captain reported the cabin crew informed of a burning plastic odor in the aft galley of unknown source. Flight crew completed the odor removal procedures and continued to destination.
Air Carrier Captain reported passing over a significant bump on the Runway 23L surface at MMMX that caused the premature and uncommanded rotation of the aircraft as the nose gear passed over it.
C172 Pilot reported a partial loss of engine power on initial climb. Pilot requested and was provided priority handling to departure airport where a safe landing was executed.
GA pilot reported the SNA airport Tower was closed possibly due to COVID-19 resulting in difficulty closing the IFR flight plan.
Center Controller reported multiple read back errors while wearing a mandated face mask.
Flight Crew flying 737-400 aircraft were notified by ATC of crossing restriction not complied with.
Air carrier flight attendants reported a fumes event during descent which resulted in cabin crew experiencing health issues.

Air carrier Technician reported the lighting on the ramp in Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport; PHX is insufficient for the pushback crews and ramp workers in dark lighting conditions. The existing lights need to be adjusted to better illuminate the alleyway during pushback and disconnect operations.
A320 Captain reported compressor stalls required the in flight shut down of the #1 engine. Priority handling was requested and the aircraft diverted to make a precautionary landing.
B767-300 Captain reported refusing an aircraft after loss of an electrical bus and repair could not be made prior to scheduled departure time.
Flight crew reported getting an altitude alert from ATC after descending below the minimum safe altitude during arrival to DEN airport.

TRACON Controller reported an aircraft experienced a generator problem resulting in lost communications and no transponder. The aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude while diverting to a nearby airport.
Government UAS pilot reported the UAS struck a parked vehicle during ascent.

A319 Captain reported on pushback; aircraft wing encroached upon taxiway resulted in a evasive action and a critical ground conflict. Captain also reported that ATC stated that a collision is not possible from that gate; yet ground crews state that ATC is not allowed to clear traffic on that taxiway when pushing from this gate.
Ground personnel reported during pushback the tow bar disconnected from aircraft unexpectedly and the aircraft rolled toward them. Captain set brakes to stop roll.
Cessna 150 student pilot flying a solo training flight experienced a complete electrical failure due to alternator and battery issues.
Fractional Captain reported experiencing GPS interference while flying over northeastern Egypt. The flight crew preemptively prepared to have backup navigation sources and stayed on course.
CE700 First Officer reported failure to properly program the FMS along with CRM communication confusion resulted in an ATC low altitude alert and a CFTT event.
Light jet Captain reported receiving a TCAS RA Climb command departing MVY airport.

Air carrier technician reported that while reviewing the aircraft logbook of a B787 they found a carry forward item with incorrect limits applied to a high-pressure stage one turbine blade that had damage which caused an unscheduled engine change.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC with an air carrier while receiving flight following from ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported a taxi error at EWR due to new taxiways and ramp markings not being depicted on charts.
An instructor pilot conducting training reported Tower issued last second go-around instructions which placed them head on to air carrier traffic resulting in an NMAC.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a wind-shear encounter that resulted in a momentary stick shaker activation.  Crew reported not having enough speed buffer to offset the wind gusts they encountered.

Air Carrier Captain reported a temporary loss of aircraft control on final approach. Flight crew completed the company upset recovery procedures and continued to a safe landing where maintenance was notified.
B767 flight crew reported operating the aircraft in revenue service with an incorrect fuel management panel installed. The aircraft was later removed from service and the correct panel was installed.
Pilot reported not having flown in a while; having difficulty finding an airport; and having issues with ATC.
Flight Attendant reported injuring a finger while operating the jump seat; causing a diversion and a potential minimum crew violation.
B737 flight crew reported the wingtip made contact with other aircraft's horizontal stabilizer during taxi to the gate.

Air carrier First Officer reported an NMAC as alerted by a TCAS RA with a helicopter on approach to PHL. The Captain; who was the pilot flying; decided to continue the approach because of possible conflicting traffic if they executed a go-around.
A321 Captain reported a dirty socks odor during gate pre-flight. After completing Maintenance's instructions; the odor decreased significantly.
Air Carrier flight crew flying a B737-700 aircraft reported the aircraft contacted a building structure during pushback.
Tower Local Controller reported a twin aircraft cleared for takeoff held their position partially on the runway due to a single engine on short final.

General aviation pilot reported a NMAC during initial climb from a non-towered airportwith an aircraft that took off right after them with inadequate spacing. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Air carrier First Officer reported ATC issued a low altitude alert while maneuvering for a visual approach. ATC issued the alert as the aircraft descended below the altitude restriction; but the First Officer still had the airport in sight and continued the approach to a landing.
EA50 Pilot reported severe turbulence and heavy rain encounter resulted in autopilot disconnect and inability to hold altitude.
General aviation pilot reported confusing taxiway markings at APA airport.
Instructor Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid an aircraft that was obscured by weather.

B767 technician reported during troubleshooting of crew oxygen system; the technician performing the work failed to open oxygen valve to the full open position causing the flight crew to refuse aircraft.
B737-700 Captain reported overshooting a climb restriction departing DEN; citing a wake turbulence encounter and improper autoflight programming as contributing.
Ground personnel reported push crew failed to follow instructions resulted in a critical ground conflict where both aircraft immediately stopped.

B737 Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported not performing a required bird strike inspection as the reporter did not see evidence of the bird strike on the engine intake during a walkaround inspection of the fuselage.
Stearman pilot reported cosmetic damage to a wingtip occurred when directional control was lost on landing. Reporter cited landing gear strut extension issues as contributing.

A321 pilot reported to commend ground controller for professionalism; handling multiple aircraft due to staffing shortages. Pilot suggested that companies provide guidance to crews to be attentive and professional in situations such as this.
A320 flight crew experienced three unrelated systems failures after takeoff and elected to return to the departure airport.
Gulfstream IV flight crew reported the loss of some Navigation Dynamics components during a maintenance test flight and returned to the departure airport.
Flight Attendant reported pressurization issues during flight and the aircraft remained pressurized after landing.
B737 flight crew reported an aircraft loss of control due to turbulence while descending on approach. The Captain recovered the aircraft and landed safely with no injuries or damage to the aircraft.
EMB-145 First Officer reported executing a missed approach after receiving a GPWS Sink Rate during a non-precision continuous descent final approach.

Air Carrier Flight Attendants reported fumes similar to musty laundry. The fumes were mostly in the aft cabin area where flight attendants alternated time in order to spend time in the Forward Galley where the odor was less intense.
GA pilot reported a NMAC during approach to OAK airport while on the SHARR ONE ARRIVAL.  Reporter stated an evasive maneuver was executed after receiving a TCAS RA.
B737 flight crew reported a diversion after engine power loss on takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported an autopilot malfunction which caused a loss of speed and altitude. The autopilot was disengaged a third time before the system operated normally.
Air Carrier Captain reported a malfunctioning radio altimeter while parked at the TPA terminal. The Captain suspects this malfunction is related to 5G interference.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
PA28A pilot reported an uneventful diversion due to critical engine malfunction.
GA pilot reported taking evasive action after ATC-assigned heading resulted in close conflict with another aircraft.

Flight Crew reported the landing gear would not retract after take off. The crew ran the non-normal checklist which instructed to open and close the manual gear retraction door and verify it is fully closed; followed by cycling the landing gear handle to the down position followed by the up position. Upon cycling the gear lever; the gear retracted normally.

Citabria pilot reported a runway excursion during a training flight after a balked landing with an instructor when confusion and communication issues resulted in opposite control inputs by the student and instructor; resulting in descending back to the runway surface and the aircraft stopping in the grass.

Air Carrier Dispatcher reported a communication breakdown between Dispatch Operations and flight crew regarding a Hazmat issue during pre-flight. Hazmat issue was resolved but highlighted discrepancies with Dispatch communications procedures.
Air carrier Captain reported that due to the FMS and other equipment on MEL; navigation was problematic throughout the flight.

MD-11 Captain reported shortly after Takeoff Power set we received Master Caution Lights Flashing for a Level 1 Unable RNP alert. Per the briefing the takeoff was rejected. The flight crew returned to the gate for maintenance and the flight was cancelled.

An air carrier Ramp Personnel reported the undocumented air transport of aircraft batteries. Reporter stated the shipment could not be scanned on the Ramp. He loaded the shipment and did not notify Load Planning.

Learjet 60 flight crew reported loss of hydraulic systems during precision approach evaluations and elected to request priority handling. Flight crew elected to divert and made a safe precautionary landing.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported rejected takeoff due to rudder control malfunction.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a fume event during departure taxi. The Captain was notified who elected to return to gate for maintenance.
Light aircraft flight instructor reported an airspace violation occurred when they departed from their cruise altitude after experiencing GPS jamming in the HSV area.
B777-200 Captain reported Hazmat documents contained Hazmat cargo weight errors caused by Weight & Balance software/automation  issues.
A321 flight crew reported similar sounding call signs led to the confusion of being cleared for takeoff while another aircraft was lining up for departure on the same runway.
A320 flight crew reported a Hydraulic Reservoir Overheat on climbout.

A319 flight crew reported loss of green hydraulic system while in cruise. After communication with dispatch and maintenance; they elected to request priority handling and continue to destination airport.
Pilot reported traffic in close proximity was turned by Tower resulting in an NMAC and requiring evasive action.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported a Captain was reporting the failure of a GPS unit prior to oceanic airspace. The Dispatcher had difficulty convincing the Captain that the aircraft was in a known area of GPS jamming.
Air carrier flight crew reported a rejected takeoff when ATC cancelled their takeoff clearance due to traffic taxiing across the runway.
VL3 Light Sport pilot reported a tail strike during the landing rollout resulted after increasing pitch to increase aerodynamic braking. After inspection; damage to the rudder was discovered.

Instructor pilot reported violent flight control fluttering after takeoff and elected to land on the remaining runway rather than chance a flight around the pattern. The aircraft had been deiced prior to takeoff.
B747-400 flight crew reported a bleed valve malfunction inflight resulting in a return to the departure airport.
EMB-145 air carrier flight crew reported windshear and malfunction with LOC signal on approach. Flight crew performed a go around and divert.
B767-300 flight crew reported loss of hydraulic fluid shortly after takeoff.
Flight Instructor on training flight reported NMAC with another aircraft in airport traffic pattern.
B737 Captain reported a cockpit window clicking sound during cruise flight. After landing maintenance determined that the window bolts were loose and not properly secured.

Falcon 50 Captain reported loss of cabin pressure control resulted in a descent while the crew completed checklists. The problem was resolved and the flight crew continued the flight to the destination airport.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported the aircraft had a second pressurization malfunction during the flight. Flight Attendant experienced physiological symptoms that rendered them unable to perform all duties after landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported they failed to properly secure the ADG door prior to gate departure. The discrepancy was discovered by ground personnel at destination.
EMB-175 flight crew reported possible damage to aircraft flight controls after an encounter with extreme turbulence in cruise flight at 35;000 feet.
Air carrier Ground personnel reported a TSA agent discovered a Hazmat item in a passenger bag which was removed prior to departure.

Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC during approach to GDK non-towered airport with an aircraft that flew toward them at a high rate of speed. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming in ZAB airspace.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported a gate agent opened an armed door resulting in a partial escape slide deployment.
Air carrier Captain reported executing an ATC directed rejected takeoff. The event was due to an aircraft encroaching the runway hold short line.
A320 First Officer reported a hydraulic system failure and a successful diversion.
Corporate pilot reported a wing fuel leak during cruise. The flight crew diverted and after landing Maintenance found two missing wing rivets.
B737 pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter after takeoff following a vector from ATC.  Helicopter pilot descended to avoid colliding with the B737.
Sail plane pilot reported partial power loss resulting in an immediate return to departure airport for a safe landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported on short final a large flock of birds climbed into the aircraft's flight path; resulting in several bird strikes and aircraft damage. The air crew continued the approach and landed without further event.
CRJ-900 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering possible wind shear and wake turbulence on approach to JFK from a preceding heavy jet.
ZBW Controller reported limited radio capability to issue a climb clearance resulted in an aircraft entering a higher MVA and a CFTT event.
C172 Flight Instructor and student reported a blown tire during the landing roll after a simulated engine failure. Aircraft was towed off the runway.
Technician reported the B737 MAX main wheel brake assembly had failed and was seized into the main wheel assembly; and parts were found on the ramp.
Law enforcement personnel reported a UAS flying in a No Drone Zone crashed into a government building.
CL604 First Officer reported numerous issues with a new avionics system which caused airborne conflict.
Pilot conducting a VOR 18 approach at night to TUP reported bright white lights to the left of the runway could be confused with PAPI lights and give a pilot a false glideslope indication.
B737-800 flight crew reported multiple bird strikes during takeoff.
A321 flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control due to encountering turbulence while in a holding pattern.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they flew a drone in a national park without obtaining permission.
B737 Captain reported a cockpit window failure while in climb to cruise altitude. The Captain diverted to a nearby airport and landed safely.
A319 Captain reported a rejected takeoff due to Integrated standby airspeed anomaly.
PA28 flight instructor on training flight with student reported rudder pedal anomaly after takeoff. Returned to airport and landed uneventfully.
C172 instructor pilot reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to GPT airport in trail of a B737.

Air carrier flight crew reported a momentary loss of speed resulting in a stick shaker event. The pilots stated there was a heavy workload at the time and an unfamiliar FMC required programming change for the First Officer.
Air carrier pilots reported the loss of oil quantity and pressure during cruise flight. The pilots stated they shut the engine down and diverted.
B757 Captain reported Tower reported smoke coming from the engine during landing followed by hydraulic system failure and thrust reverser malfunction.
Flight Attendant reported the gate agent opened the 1L door before the door unarmed signal was given. The door automatically disarmed during opening; preventing an escape slide deployment.
Pilot reported suspect alterations to the aircraft; engine and propeller logbooks after an alternator bearing failure in flight.

C172 Flight Instructor reported executing a go around to avoid a potential collision with a King Air landing on an intersecting runway.; "M-20 Pilot reported experiencing and recognizing a spacial disorientation event that resulted in control loss and recovery by trusting instruments."
Air carrier first officer reported GPS interference while holding for takeoff at OJAQ; King Hussein International Airport.
Learjet 40 flight crew reported while in cruise at FL400; the right engine rolled back. Flight crew shut down and restarted the engine successfully and returned to the airport.
DA62 pilot reported landing safely after a low altitude upset occurred on short final to TPA when they encountered wake turbulence from the preceding B737.
C150 pilot reported a rough running engine and RPM loss during cruise required a diversion and landing at a nearby airport.

A319 pilot reported an uncommanded aircraft movement during push back when the tow bar was disconnected without the parking brake being set. The pilot applied the brakes and stopped aircraft movement.

Turbo Commander 840 pilot reported aircraft did not accelerate normally during takeoff roll. Pilot retracted landing gear during rotation resulting in aircraft belly contacting runway before aircraft began climbing.
An air ambulance helicopter pilot approaching to land at an automobile accident site reported a NMAC with a fixed wing aircraft.

Ramp personnel reported the aircraft rolled after disconnecting the towbar. The pilots did not respond to ramp personnel attempts to signal the pilots to apply brakes until the reporter tapped on the aircraft fuselage.
Flight crew reported high terrain near airport resulting in climb to safe altitude.
SR20 Instructor Pilot reported an engine malfunction during initial takeoff power application resulted in rejected takeoff.
PA-34 pilot reported executing an off-airport landing after both engines failed in flight due to fuel exhaustion.
Air carrier Captain reported a CFIT event during final approach that was caused by the First Officer deviating below glide path. The First Officer took correct action and landed safely.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a Rejected Takeoff.
ERJ-170 Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on an ILS approach in visual conditions when he deviated from the charted procedure.
B747-400 flight crew reported transporting special cargo without a Special Cargo Designee's approval.
A319 Pilot reported on take off they received a master caution F/CTRL FLAPS LOCKED WING TIP BRAKE ON. The flight crew returned to departure airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot was conducting a demo flight when a lost link occured. After the lost link the UAS flew into a building.
Center Controller reported when they issued a clearance to CRL VOR; the pilot responded that the fix CRL was in Mexico according to their database.

Air carrier flight crew reported the undocumented transport of a Lithium Ion powered mobility device during preflight at an intermediate stopover airport. Flight crew was not informed or provided required Hazmat documents at the originating departure airport.
Cirrus Vision SJ50 pilot reported on visual approach; observed multiple aircraft in conflict resulting in taking evasive action and an NMAC.
C180 pilot reported an NMAC event during final approach landing when a Beechcraft Baron flew 100 feet above the Cessna. Cessna pilot immediately banked right and landed with no further issues.
BE35 pilot reported a loss of generator while in night IMC.

EMB-145 Captain reported an engine #2 bleed air leak on climb out. The flight was operating in conditions of known icing with the #1 pack deferred when cabin pressure became uncontrollable. An air turn back and precautionary landing were made at departure airport.

Captain reported diverting after Flight Attendant reported smoke and fumes in the cabin. The smoke and fumes were apparently associated with the Cabin Commercial Push Button on the CIDS (Cabin Intercommunication Display System) Panel.

B757 Captain noted a loss of center system hydraulic quantity after accomplishing flight control checks.  After returning to gate crew was told by maintenance there was a leak in the tail section of aircraft.
GA pilot reported an NMAC with a helicopter during initial climb from AUN airport. Pilot stated they checked for traffic before departing but did not see the helicopter.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported losing visual line of sight of their UAS and flying within an active TFR without authorization.
A Flight Instructor reported a NMAC entering the traffic pattern at a non towered airport.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported traffic taxied onto the landing runway without radio communications resulted in a critical ground conflict and a go-around.

Air carrier Captain reported confusion surrounding an ATC assigned taxi clearance resulted in the need to brake abruptly to avoid a conflict with another taxiing aircraft. The Captain had the First Officer clarify the correct aircraft to follow; then continued the taxi for takeoff.
Air carrier Pilot reported a ground conflict while taxing to the gate area. Another aircraft communicated over ground controls frequency the distance between the two aircraft was too close.
Corporate First Officer reported a near midair collision with a UAS while they were on approach.
Pilots reported the First Officer lost brake control while attempting to hold short of runway.
Fractional aircraft Captain reported mismanagement of Autopilot and error set into navigation resulted in an altitude overshoot and a CFTT event.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft cleared for a Visual Approach descended towards the wrong airport and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence and momentary loss of control. Passengers were injured so the flight diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
