Center Controller reported vectoring a departing aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitude to avoid a VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC.
Air carrier First Officer reported SKED ATC issued a clearance to an unsafe altitude which puts responsibility for terrain clearance on flight crew.
B737 ground personnel reported unintentionally leaving cargo off a flight and had finalized the flight with another person.
Air Carrier First Officer reported they observed an aircraft pass 500 feet overhead and received a TCAS/RA.

Air carrier crew executed a go-around after observing two aircraft that appeared to be taxiing across their landing runway at the far end. Neither pilot realized there was a taxiway that ATC utilized for access to the parallel runway and recommended a reference to this being communicated to pilots.

Air carrier crew reported while taxing into the gate the ground crew did not chalk the aircraft resulting in the aircraft making an uncommanded movement. The Air crew was able to apply the brakes and have the aircraft re positioned for the arrival to the gate.
B737-700 Captain reported severe turbulence resulting in altitude and airspeed exceedances. The Captain took control and stabilized the aircraft after descending out of the turbulence.
Dispatcher reported a flight crew reported they were diverting because the aircraft had a suspected fuel leak.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing a momentary stick shaker departing ORD; citing atmospheric and wake turbulence as additional contributing factors.
Pilot reported a dual FMS failure during flight. The fight crew performed an air turn back to departure airport.
B757 First Officer reported returning to departure airport after experiencing loss of oil in the left engine.
Corporate F/O reported getting low on a visual approach. The Captain and Tower also gave a too low alert. The F/O suggests more training is needed in this LR-45 on visual approaches.
Pilot flying reported runway excursion during landing rollout.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported a strong electrical insulation odor during initial climb. The Flight Crew returned to the departure airport and landed.
Air Carrier Captain reported the Jeppesen FD Pro App has charting errors depicting the Anchorage Arctic FIR boundary notes and the Gander Oceanic Transition Area.

Air Carrier Captain reported multiple EICAS fault messages regarding the aircraft's braking system while descending on the STAR. After landing the aircraft was difficult to steer and an abnormal procedure was accomplished. The pilots were able to taxi to the assigned gate where maintenance was available. The Captain; after more maintenance delays; called crew scheduling stating pilot fatigue.
Air carrier First Officer reported spots Q and R are not depicted on the AMM at IAH Airport.

Air carrier ground employee reported an aircraft that was parked at the gate with nose wheel chocks installed rolled back about 1.5 feet because deice fluid on the ramp allowed the chock to skid. Chock was reinserted and no damage reported.

A Tower Local Control trainee reported they cleared a turbojet for takeoff behind a slower moving aircraft which had began its turn away from the runway. The first aircraft unexpectedly turned back into the path of the jet departure and stopped climbing resulting in a Low Altitude Alert.
Flight Instructor reported loss of engine power on short final. Instructor assumed control and was able to glide to landing with no damage or injuries reported.
Air carrier Captain reported that glide slope anomalies on approach to SFO ILS 28R led to an unstabilized approach and a low altitude alert from ATC.

Air carrier flight crew reported improper troubleshooting to silence aural autopilot warning resulted in autopilot engagement below 50 ft. and with the yoke locking up and arresting the rate of descent during landing.
Air carrier Captain reported descent below glide path on final approach resulting in a Glide Slope Alert.
C510 Pilot reported an NMAC event during approach by descending from assigned altitude before confirming they were cleared to descend.
EMB-175 First Officer reported that the MEL where the First Officer's seat adjustment mechanism was not functional was not safe for aircraft release.
A319 First Officer reported returning to departure airport after experiencing flap failure during retraction after takeoff.
B767 Captain reported a track deviation occurred departing IAD when a wake turbulence encounter interrupted the flight crew's workflow.

Air carrier flight crew reported discovering that a portable oxygen concentrator was checked without the flight crew's knowledge. The concentrator also had batteries and one of the batteries was found missing. The missing battery was not found and with the potential chance that it may have been transferred to another aircraft's cargo bay; the Captain notified Dispatch and the Chief Pilot.
Air Carrier ground employee reported a jet blast encounter during pushback due to crossbleed start procedure.
Air carrier pilot reported during push back ramp tower advised them to stop push due to their proximity to an aircraft parked near them.

A TRACON Controller; their trainee; and the Controller in Charge working Flight Data reported Flight Data released an aircraft from a satellite airport at too low an altitude and an inappropriate direction of flight which resulted in the aircraft flying below the minimum vectoring altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported a delayed DG acknowledgement form sent during pushback. A wheel chair tag was also provided to the flight crew during pushback.

Captain reported refusing the aircraft after numerous write ups for engine performance were not addressed. The aircraft had sat on the ground all day with no maintenance action and had been previously refused by another Captain.
Airline Captain reported momentary loss of aircraft directional control after encountering non-visible taxiway ice during night taxi following a landing in freezing drizzle conditions at ORD.
Captain reported a tree strike during landing on a narrow airfield 150 feet wide resulting in damage to the left wing.

C172 pilot reported a sudden partial power loss in cruise during a training flight.  Instructor and pilot were able to reach an airport with reduced power rather than landing off airport as initially planned and landed safely.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence descending into ORD five miles in trail of a B747.
Flight crew reported they navigated direct to the wrong fix during initial climb and received a terrain warning from ATC.
Air Carrier flight crew reported there is no signage for the hold short line for Runway 31 on Taxiway H at BNA airport.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during takeoff from TIW airport requiring evasive action. The pilot stated misinterpretation of ATC's clearance contributed to the event.
EMB-145 flight crew reported Air Conditioning Pack and Bleed Air Failure in climb.
ZDV Center Controller reported an aircraft experienced a CFTT event while diverting due to weather.

After ingesting a bird during landing roll out; Captain reported undue pressure from Maintenance Control to defer a required engine inspection and fly the aircraft but contract maintenance refused to sign the deferral.
Air carrier Captain reported a near mid air collision with a possible UAS while they were descending on arrival.
A320 flight crew reported a diversion caused by a rapidly changing fuel imbalance.
Pilot on short final to a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with an aircraft on a parallel course to them.
B757-200 pilot reported loss of left hydraulic system and nosewheel steering resulted in a go around to a safe landing.

Air carrier Captain reported an EGT fail on the #1 engine and other erratic engine indications during approach. The flight continued to a single-engine approach and landing; with normal passenger deplanement.
General aviation First Officer reported a near miss with a departing aircraft on a visual approach.
CL-300 flight crew reported a runway excursion during take-off roll due to a severe wind gust. Flight crew rejected the take-off and taxied back to the FBO.
GA pilot reported observing a UAS pass by their aircraft.  After this distraction they forgot to descend as planned and entered controlled airspace.
PA28 Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a near miss with another aircraft in the airport traffic pattern.
A TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft turned to the wrong heading and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported a Hazmat cargo load configuration error which resulted in the collapse of two adjacent Lithium Ion battery pallets while enroute.
Air carrier Captain reported similar sounding fixes on the AMA RNP Z 22 approach; PULBE and TELVE; which caused a clearance and heading deviation.
Pilot flying a tail wheel aircraft reported a ground loop during taxi.
PA-44 First Officer reported an engine failure during cruise flight. The pilots secured the engine and continued the flight to an airport where maintenance was available.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a heading and clearance deviation when they understood CADAB but were actually cleared to CABAB.

Flight Instructor and student pilot reported noticing another aircraft in the area and flying opposite direction to them during cruise; resulting in the Flight Instructor taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.
C172 instructor pilot with student reported an NMAC with a helicopter while on final approach.  The reporter took evasive action and was eventually instructed to go around by Tower.

ZDC ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and separation was lost with another aircraft. The ZDC Controller reported their Standard Operating Procedures cause TCAS/RA events to occur.
A320 Captain reported a weak 'de-ice odor' on departure which dissipated shortly into the climb with no further issues noticed.
Pilot reported while conducting jump operations the trailing aircraft flew over the top of their aircraft resulting in both aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
Dispatcher reported not following procedures due to time constraints and staffing issues.
While on a road trip to an out station; Technician reported being ordered to return to home station because they were writing too many things up.
ZLC Center Controller reported an aircraft on the ILS approach to IDA airport entered holding with left turns instead of right turns and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

EMB-145 First Officer reported smoke and odor operating at FL250 in both the flight deck and passenger cabin with one Air Conditioning Pack already inoperative per MEL. Crew diverted to a nearby airport where maintenance reported the remaining Air Conditioning Pack had also failed.
Pilot reports a NMAC during climb out after takeoff from LOM airport.
Piper PA-18 pilot reported an engine failure due to fuel starvation was the result of suspected malfunctioning fuel gauges that had indicated more fuel than actual was available.

Air carrier flight crew reported that during an unannounced FAA ramp check the flight crew was informed of the illegal transport of Hazmat cargo due to incorrect documentation. The Hazmat cargo was removed from the flight.
Technicians report incomplete documentation during creation of a Deferred Maintenance Entry in the log book. This was discovered and corrected by other Technicians.
Flight Instructor was advised by ATC of traffic closing from behind and took evasive action to avoid a near mid air collision.
B737-800 Check Airman reported a possible fuel leak. The engine was shutdown; ATC assistance requested and a divert commenced.
M20E pilot reported failure to identify landing gear light status resulted in a prop strike on go around and return to a safe landing.
B737-800 technicians reported that as the tail jack failed in the middle of jacking the aircraft; the aircraft tail dropped hard on the tail jack; causing significant damage to the tail section.
B767-300 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to MEM that resulted in a momentary stick shaker event.

Air carrier First Officer reported hazmat documentation errors and missing required documents during preflight. The document errors were corrected and the hazmat associated with the missing documents was unloaded prior to departure.
C152 flight instructor with student reported the right rudder cable failed during takeoff.  The crew advised Tower and immediately returned to the departure airport.
A320 Captain reported a 'musty/oily' odor during preflight. The odor became stronger which prompted the Captain; in agreement with Maintenance; to secure the APU and deplane passengers.

Technician reported ground damage occurred when attempting to balance hydraulic system fluid levels. The aircraft move crew removed the wheel chocks without informing the technicians; who released the parking brake; allowing the aircraft to roll and strike L2 door on the jet way frame.
B737-800 pilot reported inflight shutdown of #1 engine due to low oil pressure. Flight diverted and landed safely.
Flight instructor reported the aircraft's wheel got stuck in a taxiway crack that had just been tarred over.
C172 Instructor Pilot reported wake turbulence from a military tanker overflying their aircraft resulted in an upset.
Cessna T210L pilot reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing power loss and low oil pressure at FL250. A safe landing ensued; and the flight continued after adding oil.
Corporate pilot reported a taxiway incursion; indicating non-standard faded taxiway markings as a contributing factor.
Air carrier pilot reported conflicting information concerning the JFK WA taxiway operational status as identified in a NOTAM; and the Jepp Pro AMM taxiway chart.
PA-46 pilot reported a rapid decompression during cruise which was followed by the flight crew executing a rapid descent and diverting to a precautionary landing.

Air carrier Captain reported severe turbulence and a wind shear encounter while on initial approach to a non-towered airport with thunderstorms in the vicinity. The pilot performed a go-around and returned to the departure airport.
Reporter stated Border Agents inspecting the E&E compartment of the aircraft had loose items hanging from their belts that could cause damage in the compartment.
Flight crew flying BE-400 aircraft reported hydraulic malfunction on descent. Landed at destination without incident.
Air carrier pilot reported entering cumulus build up with subsequent loss of altitude. Crew continued to destination.
A319 flight rew reported a temporary loss of control while taxiing off the runway due to a nose wheel steering failure.
Air Carrier flight crew reported the aircraft left main landing gear left the taxiway while the aircraft was on the taxiway center line at BTR airport.

B737 flight crew reported a SPD LIM flap on The Captain's PFD in flight. The stick shaker on the Captain's control yoke began to activate repeatedly for 1 to 2 seconds at a time. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.

A320 Captain reported 2; N1 fan blades bent and not noted in logbook. Maintenance showed as reported and deferred several times. Captain stated they have no access to history of previous reports or deferments; to determine airworthiness.

B737-700 flight crew reported being notified by the flight attendants of an electronic device igniting in the cabin during initial climb. Flight crew returned to the departure airport where it was determined no injuries were found on the crew or passengers.

A319 Captain reported the Tug driver interrupted the pushback to secure the DG cargo. The Captain further stated that Dispatch took 30 minutes to inform the flight crew that the DG cargo was exempted from providing a NOTOC during pre-flight.
A flight instructor taxiing in a Sling reported the student lost control of the aircraft and the instructor had to take evasive action to avoid another aircraft resulting in a taxiway excursion.
C340 pilot reported noticing oil leaking from the left engine and performed an in-flight shutdown. The pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
B737 pilot reported non-standard push-back communications release procedure by ground crew.
A321 Pilot reported loss of airspeed indications on both the Captain's and first Officer's PFDs. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Piper Arrow pilot reported their left main landing gear collapsed during landing resulting in a runway excursion and damaged runway light.
Instructor Pilot reported that during night VFR approach; the runway was being swept by a truck while the tower closed and there was no indication that the runway was closed.
B737 Captain reported approaching ramp at night; a passenger bus failed to yield to aircraft resulted in abrupt braking to avoid a collision.
C172 Flight Instructor reported student improperly attempted landing; lost control; which resulted in runway excursion and aircraft damage.

Air carrier Captain reported GPWS terrain warning while descending at a high rate; on a visual approach at night; in mountainous terrain. The Captain started a climb until the warning stopped then continued the approach to landing.
Examiner on flight with student reported NMAC with another aircraft while in airport traffic pattern.

Air carrier Captain reported that the Park Visual Runway 31 and RNAV (GPS) X Runway 31 approaches at LGA are both selected using RNVX 31." However; using that designation only loads the RNAV (GPS) X approach; making it impossible to load the Park Visual approach. The reporter states that the two approaches use different fixes and missed approach procedures; which can lead to confusion for pilots."
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a RA for an aircraft 300 feet below them while on final approach.
Pilot reported an expedited descent after a bleed air supply duct failed and cabin pressure was lost. Pilot reported making a normal landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported a hydraulic leak in the wheel well. Aircraft was taken out of service to address hydraulic leak and pooled fluid.
Pilot reported a runaway autopilot caused the ailerons to bind ; making the aircraft difficult to control for landing.

ZJX Center Controller reported that they issued a 360 degree turn to a departure which placed it into confliction with another aircraft; and to resolve the confliction; the Controller descend one of the aircraft into an adjacent facility's airspace without coordination.
Pilot reported a near-collision on the ramp at AGC when two vehicles drove within a few feet of his spinning propeller.
Pilots reported while taxiing and attempting to navigate their aircraft around a parked aircraft; the wings clipped one another.

EMB-175 Captain reported landing gear pins were found to be still installed; with a corresponding log book entry stating that they had been removed and stowed in the cockpit. The Captain called for maintenance to remove and stow the landing gear pins as required and made an additional log book entry to account for the additional maintenance actions.
Helicopter pilot reported encountering hail while leaving an area of convective activity. The windscreen shattered and the pilot elected to divert for an immediate landing.
Captain reported a loss of separation was caused by distractions during in flight systems malfunctions.
A321 flight crew reported communications issues led to a go around and descent below minimum vectoring altitude during approach phase of flight.
A320 Captain reported a persistent 'strong cheese odor' during approach. Upon arrival a Maintenance logbook discrepancy was submitted.
Cessna pilot reports evading a near miss with a taxiing aircraft during go around to prevent a runway excursion after landing.

EMB-145 flight crew reported pressurization issues during cruise resulting in an immediate descent and ran appropriate QRC/QRH items. Pressurization returned to normal at 12;000 feet and the crew decided to continue to destination.
ERJ-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence while climbing out of PHL that caused a speed deviation.
An Air Carrier Captain reported a NMAC with a VFR aircraft while on final approach.
B737 flight crew reported a rapid loss of fuel after takeoff requiring an immediate return to the departure airport.

Cessna 182 pilot passenger reported the flying pilot mis-interpreting the runway and a near miss with electrical towers at dusk on a visual approach. The pilot maneuvered away from the towers when highlighted by the passenger; then re-aligned with the runway and landed safely after a hard landing.
General aviation flight instructor on a training flight reported a near miss with a possible UAS during cruise and in controlled airspace.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported checked bags with batteries not removed for cargo compartment.
Air carrier First Officer reported an engine failure in oceanic airspace. The crew and company dispatcher decided on an alternate airport and proceeded to a safe landing.
Embraer 145 flight crew reported a low hydraulic quantity EICAS message during flight. The flight landed safely and taxied to the gate.
Pilot of a turboprop single engine aircraft reported encountering severe turbulence over the MYS VOR at 17000 feet.

Student Pilot conducting a practice approach reported the TRACON issued a late frequency change to Tower resulting in a Class D airspace incursion. Tower instructed the student to extend their circling maneuver which placed them in unsafe proximity to terrain requiring a climb to avoid.
Pilot reported a NMAC while being distracted by a headset malfunction which resulted in both aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
TRACON and Tower Controller reported they were made aware by a pilot that an aircraft had flown above them while on descent causing an NMAC.
Air carrier pilot reported an EGPWS terrain warning during an unstable approach.
GA pilot reported the pilot flying flew into a box canyon resulting in a controlled flight toward terrain event. Flight cleared terrain and continued.
Air carrier Captain reported the FliteDeck Pro application's airfield diagrams for MMGL are outdated and do not match with the taxi information that ATC provides.

A Tower Controller reported they turned an aircraft off of the final approach course due to being overtaken by traffic on TRACON frequency. TRACON turned their faster aircraft too resulting in an NMAC.
Ground personnel reported two hoverboards were removed from a passenger's luggage prior to departure.

Helicopter pilot reported during final approach decided to go around and break off their approach. As they made their turn; another aircraft that was behind them on approach broke off their approach and ended up turning towards them nearly causing a near midair collision.
Fractional FO reported an NMAC while on approach to an airport.

PA-28 pilot reported loss of Engine RPM after takeoff. The pilot executed an air turn back and landing at departure airport. It was deduced that water had contaminated the fuel and had not been properly sumped out during pre-flight.
A320 Captain reported course deviations due to FMGC failure resulted in critical equipment failure requiring ATC guidance.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a UAS while on final approach. No evasive action was taken and the flight continued without further incident.
Air Carrier ground personnel reported upon aircraft arrival fresh seafood container damaged in transit resulting in spillage of 'blood/fluids' leakage.

C172 Flight Instructor reported expediting a previous landing and braking hard to make a taxiway contributed to a later occurrence of a nose wheel failure and a temporary runway closure at the airport.
There was an eyewitness of a UAS operating at a University. The eyewitness believed the UAS was flying at an unsafe altitude due to the proximity of a nearby airport.
Flight Crew reported engine flameout on short final and continued on to land successfully.
A flight instructor reported their student solo pilot lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
Air Carrier Captain reported failure to utilize vertical speed mode on a steep visual approach to ANC airport resulted in a GPWS alert.
Bell BH47 Pilot examiner reported a runway excursion of a single pilot C172 that lost control from a hard landing; resulted in the BH47 taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
Captain reported loss of control while exiting runway at the high speed turnoff.

B737 flight crew reported the First Officer's pitch trim control moved the pitch trim in the opposite direction as intended after being replaced by Maintenance. The Captain assumed flying duties as the Captain's pitch trim control was working properly and the flight crew continued to destination airport.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a violent vibration and ENG 2 FAIL message during takeoff roll. The crew rejected the takeoff and returned to the gate where the aircraft was turned over to maintenance.
Air carrier flight crew reported low altitude alert while on approach in mountainous terrain.
Pilot flying PA28 aircraft reports NMAC in airport traffic pattern.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported wind shear encounter during landing caused a temporary loss of control resulting in a damaged tie down point.
A321 Captain reported an engine compressor stall event in cruise. After completing the QRH procedure; the crew continued to the destination airport with the affected engine at idle power.
PA23 pilots reported both of the engines failed. They were able to restart the engines and continued to destination.
A TRACON Controller working busy combined sectors reported a departing jet pilot reported a NMAC when they overtook a slower moving aircraft.

C172R pilot reported a near mid-air collision with a Cessna that departed in front of them while on short final approach.  Pilot went around and established lateral offset from the other aircraft; re-entered the pattern and landed normally.

Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict as they taxied onto the runway for takeoff and another aircraft was final approach to the same runway. The flight crew stopped on the runway past the hold short line and the controller directed a go-around for the landing aircraft.
A Tower Supervisor reported Tower received a Low Altitude Alert for an aircraft on approach which had been issued the wrong altimeter setting.
PA-32 Pilot reported an NMAC on short final with a crop duster departing opposite direction.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the vicinity of 7S3 airport.
Captain reported a hydraulic system failure after takeoff during gear retraction. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at the departure airport.

Bell 407 technicians reported that pilots experienced uncontrolled yaw motion in the helicopter during lift off. The technicians discovered that the control tube for the tail rotor pitch change was not connected to the bell crank under the tail rotor gearbox.
Flight Attendant reported a fume event that started one hour prior to landing resulting in physiological symptoms.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a near miss with a helicopter during cruise flight.
Flight Instructor reported the student lost control of the aircraft landing in a crosswind and the aircraft's wing clipped a taxiway sign.

Flight Instructor with student reported nose gear failure during landing. The flight crew secured the aircraft and noticed the nose gear had broken at a weld point and was completely severed from the airframe.
BE55 pilot reported a fuel system malfunction inflight.

Air carrier Captain reported unable to exit parallel runway after landing due to another aircraft blocking a taxiway resulting in a runway incursion and go-around. Captain indicated airborne traffic on final while their aircraft was blocked from exiting the runway onto the taxiway by a conflicting aircraft taxiing very slowly and not expediting. ATC instructed the aircraft on final to go-around due to the aircraft remaining on the landing runway and was blocked from entering the taxiway.
A fractional jet pilot reported they stopped their climb due to opposite direction traffic which passed 400 feet overhead.
Air Carrier Safety Analyst reported a Hazmat was transported without providing a NOTOC to the flight crew prior to departure.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported the UAS crew was approached by airport staff during a flight near an airport. The UAS crew chose to end the flight.
EMB-145 Captain reported loss of aircraft control for a short period of time when they entered an area of severe turbulence.

Learjet 45 Captain reported a left engine warning light followed by loud boom; aircraft yaw and vibration while in cruise. The flight crew requested priority handling and continued to destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported they received a ground proximity warning to pull up while descending on a visual approach.
Mooney M20J pilot reported returning to departure airport and landing safely after experiencing a significant loss of power shortly after takeoff.

Pilot reported a GPWS terrain warnings on final approach twice; with no published approaches but recent runway construction. After conducting terrain escape maneuvers twice; pilot landed after third approach.
A B777 Flight Crew reported refusing the aircraft due to non compliance with a dual CDL procedure that would have two wing access panels removed for flight.
Mooney pilot reported a pitot tube blockage resulted in unreliable airspeed indications. Pilot landed safely and maintenance technician cleared blockage.
Technician reported during disassembly of air cycle machine found parts missing in assembly.
Falcon 2000 Captain reported momentary loss of control after encountering wake turbulence on approach to BFI airport.

B737 flight crew reported receiving a bank angle alert after takeoff that was quickly corrected. The flight crew believes this may have been caused by either wake turbulence from a preceding aircraft or a gust at altitude.
Pilot reported a digital electronic engine control warning; and lack of training for aborted takeoff; resulted in lack of braking and runway overrun.
CRJ200 flight crew reported during initial climb the left pack failed and cabin pressurization caution message was received. Flight crew returned to departure airport.
Aircraft maintenance technicians reported performing maintenance procedure improperly resulted in damaged engine cowling.
An air taxi pilot reported a rough running engine and diversion to a nearby airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a fuel leak enroute at cruise altitude and continued to their destination due to lack of suitable alternates.
B737 pilot reported conducting a go-around on short final approach due to an aircraft on runway.
MD11 Captain reported APU fire alert warning resulted in a divert.

Captain reported a malfunctioning Remote Cabin Sensor already on MEL failed and caused erroneous cabin altitude readings. This caused the passenger O2 masks to deploy and required an immediate descent. The Crew continued to a destination airport.

C172 Flight Instructor reported the student overshot clearance altitude which resulted in a low altitude alert. The flight immediately initiated a climb to clearance altitude and resumed the approach correctly.
ERJ-145 flight crew reported a BAGG SMOKE EICAS on landing and requested ARFF to check the aircraft.
HS 125 Captain reported the co-pilot had applied brakes to lock the wheels before touchdown resulting in all main landing gear tires blown during landing.
Cessna 150 pilot reported the loss of electrical power and returned to the departure airport.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they had control issues with certain flight controller modes during takeoff. Pilot overcame the issue and was able to safely continue the flight.
C172 Flight Instructor reported losing engine power on takeoff and needing to land as soon as possible; informed ATC that the aircraft will overshoot the runway and roll into the grass.
C172 pilot reported cross winds on landing and failed to take proper action; resulting in a runway excursion.

Technician reported that when using the maintenance application for electronic log book sign offs; it allows the use of 'Not Applicable' for entries; which is not allowed by the Engineering Authorization.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a UAS and reported the incident to ATC.
Air Carrier First Officer reported confusion regarding the taxi instructions from MMGL ground control and the taxi chart.
Air carrier flight crew flying CRJ-700 aircraft reported depressurization due to bleed air leak resulting in an air turn back.

A319 Captain reported they received a Yellow System 'Low Level' warning during cruise at 36;000 feet. The crew ran the appropriate ECAM and QRH procedures and contacted Dispatch and Maintenance Control.  The crew decided to continue to their destination airport with ATC assistance.
Air carrier flight crew reported flying to the wrong fix on the approach after mistakenly hearing FITGU instead of FAHEY.
Cessna 421 pilot reported an engine malfunction during climb. The pilot reduced power on the malfunctioning engine then diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.

Air carrier flight crew reported a traffic conflict with ground vehicles after push back and before taxi from the gate. The vehicles suddenly moved in front of the aircraft as the parking brake was released to taxi and the aircraft remained stationary to avoid the conflict.
A320 pilot reported airframe vibration resulted in a divert with expeditious handling to a safe landing.

C172 pilot reported the main tires blew out on landing due to having to use maximum braking to avoid a head-on collision with a helicopter. The helicopter pilot had thought the reporter's aircraft was still on base according to what was seen on the ADS-B.
Air carrier flight crew inadvertently released brakes at the gate before aircraft was chocked.
RV-8 pilot reported the landing gear struck an approach landing light during landing resulting in aircraft damage.
Pilot reported having to stop for a vehicle in the aircraft's path during pushback.
Center trainee reported they observed a MSAW Alert for an aircraft too low for radio coverage that was above their Minimum IFR Altitude but on the boundary of another facility.

Air Carrier Captain reported he was notified the day following the flight that he had signed the Dangerous Goods Form in the wrong location rather than in the Captain's signature block. The Captain stated he was trying to expedite because was the flight operating was late.
737-800 Flight crew reported moderate to severe turbulence during descent.
Air Carrier Captain reported IAH is using an unpublished Tower frequency.

DG-1000 sail plane pilot reported losing control of the aircraft upon landing after the instructor who was sitting in the rear seat; unaware of the open spoiler position; pulled the spoiler. The reporter suggested that the aircraft should have markings or placards to indicate the position of the spoiler control lever.; "C172R Pilot reported engine knocking after takeoff resulted in expeditious handling return to departure airport with a flat tire on landing."

Deadheading Captain reported difficulties in opening the main cabin door. Deadheading Captain also reported contacting CFR when it was apparent that there was a need for them to be at the aircraft in case a fire broke out.
Air carrier First Officer reported glide slope and takeoff configuration warning anomalies on arrival into MCO; citing possible 5G interference as the cause of the issues.

A TRACON Departure Controller reported a departing aircraft entered the subsequent sector without a handoff and into possible confliction. The reporter was unfamiliar with the newly designed RDVS and could not accomplish the required coordination in a timely manner.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported a fumes event due to an uncontrollable cabin temperature system which resulted in cabin smoke. The flight crew was provided priority handling where a safe landing was accomplished.

B757 Captain reported Oxidize 5.1 ADG (Accessible Dangerous Goods) ULD was incorrectly loaded onto an IDG (Inaccessible Dangerous Goods) ULD Unit Load Device during preflight. Ultimately the Oxidizer was removed to be correctly loaded onto an ADG ULD on a subsequent flight.
GA7 pilot reported rejecting the takeoff after experiencing violent nose wheel vibrations due to shimmy damper failure.
EMB-145 flight crew reported another aircraft crossing runway while they were on short final.
Air taxi flight crew reported ATC assigned an altitude below the Minimum Enroute Altitude on the STAR and they received a Ground Proximity warning.
Flight Instructor with student reported executing a low altitude go-around at TTN airport when another aircraft taxied onto the runway.
Embraer 175/170 First Officer reported multiple system equipment failures at cruise that affected landing distance. The crew diverted and landed safely at the alternate airport.

Aircrew reported while transitioning Class D airspace to their destination ATC advised them of traffic and to expedite their descent.  Aircrew reported traffic insight and was told frequency change approved.  Aircrew reports while entering downwind they took evasive action to avoid the same traffic.  The aircrew then continued to land without further incident.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion at MCI. First Officer stated an outdated taxi diagram and snow obscuring taxi lanes contributed to the event.
B737-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into IAH 5 miles in trail of a B737 MAX 9.
Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action to avoid an aircraft with loss of power returning to field.
A Decathlon pilot reported loss of oil pressure than engine failure due to oil cap not being secured.

Air carrier flight crew reported GPS jamming and spoofing occurred and the aircraft lost RNP navigation. Due to the aircraft not being where the flight crew thought it was; the flight crew did not call ATC ahead in time.
B757 Captain reported releasing the parking break and initiating gate pushback prior to sending Dispatch the DG Acknowledgement message.
A321 flight crew reported fueling door damaged during flight retraction at gate.
Air carrier pilot reported ramp personnel failure to follow procedures resulted in being inches from chocked nose wheel as aircraft was still parking.
Tower Controllers reported ground vehicles failed to communicate and follow hold short instructions resulted in taking evasive action to avoid landing traffic.

A321 First Officer reported an ECAM Caution Message: WHEEL N/W STRG FAULT in  cruise. The flight crew ran the QRH and check lists. The flight crew requested priority handling and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
PC-12NG pilot reported a pressurization failure resulted in an expeditious descent and diversion to a safe landing.

PA-28 General Aviation Pilot reported while flying VFR; with flight following from ATC; receiving traffic advisories they observed another aircraft crossing their flight path. ATC did not provide any traffic conflict advisories as the conflicting aircraft crossed the same flight path and 300 feet below; resulting in an NMAC. The pilot continued to destination after the airborne conflict with no comment from ATC.

Air carrier flight crew reported a runway incursion while taxiing in low visibility weather in BOS and taxied past the ATC assigned runway hold short point. The Captain contacted ATC and received further taxi instructions.
C172 pilot reported engine roughness and loss of power in cruise. The pilot diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported executing a go-around at SAN when it became clear another air carrier aircraft was in position for takeoff on their landing runway.

B737 MAX Captain reported airframe vibration in flight which increased during climb and acceleration. The flight crew was unable to determine the cause of the vibration and landed the aircraft safely.
EMB145 First Officer reported an autopilot disconnect and stick shaker activation after a wind-shear encounter during approach.  Crew executed a go around and landed normally.
CE-525 Pilot reported taxiway excursion due to icing.
Pilot reported an unstable approach caused them to land long and go off the end of the runway onto the overrun. While attempting to taxi back onto the runway the nose gear collapsed.
Cessna 172 pilot reported a rejected takeoff due to loss of power.
Pilot reported aborting takeoff at 75 feet AGL after engine power loss and landing on remaining runway.
An Air Carrier Captain reported they attempted to turn out of their gate after push back and the aircraft slid on ice toward an aircraft parked at an adjacent gate.
Flight Instructor reported debris from Canadian fires has been found in pitot tubes on multiple aircraft causing pitot static related instrument failures/malfunctions.

Ramp Agent reported an aircraft near miss with a ground object while flight crew utilizing an automated parking device. The Agent observed the aircraft misalignment; signaled the Captain to stop the aircraft; preventing aircraft damage.
B737 First Officer reported during pre-flight walk around finding a hydraulic line disconnected in the wheel well. No documented maintenance of disconnected line in maintenance history.
Challenger 300 pilot reported an auto pilot malfunction resulted in a course deviation and low altitude alert from ATC.
A319 Captain reported an APU failure in flight with the #2 IDG on MEL. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted.

A321 Captain reported confusion during a Bump Thrust takeoff which resulted in the aircraft entering Alpha Floor; requiring the Captain to assume control and descend 300 additional feet to recover airspeed.

Flight Instructor reported a near miss during takeoff from a non-towered airport; with a helicopter flying directly over the takeoff runway. The instructor rejected the takeoff when another aircraft identified the possible near miss and called a reject on the radio.
C172 flight instructor reported ATC failure to separate traffic in pattern resulted in an NMAC on downwind entry to CMA.
Ramp Agent reported observing a parked aircraft had struck a power tow unit which was inside the safety zone.
Air carrier flight crew on approach reported receiving a low altitude alert by ATC.
Pilot reported that charts for PGSN airport advise there are VASI lights; but there aren't any and there is no plan to install them.
PC-12 pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC when they descended below glide slope significantly after entering visual conditions on approach.
PA28 Instructor and student reported turning after landing with excessive speed resulting in a loss of control and taxiway excursion.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported cabin altitude discrepancies during the climb. The flight crew then donned oxygen masks and safely diverted to an alternate airport.
MU2 pilot reported main gear collapse on taxi out. Pilot and passengers evacuated aircraft safely.
Piper Cherokee Pilot reported being advised by ATC that another aircraft had reported a NMAC with them. They had been issued the traffic but never got it in sight.
PA28 flight instructor on training flight with student reported engine malfunction inflight.

Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported the Captain failed to set the parking brake and the ground crew failed to insert chocks at gate arrival. The aircraft began to roll back before brakes were applied. No injuries or damage occurred.

B737 flight crew reported after changing to the next Controller they were ask which fix they were flying too. The Controller changed the fix on the STAR and said you are getting too close to high terrain.
Cessna 172 student pilot reported loss of directional control on landing roll; resulting in a runway excursion.  No damage or injuries reported.
Flight crew and Dispatcher reported catastrophic failure of the #2 engine in climb and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Flight Instructor and student reported their single engine aircraft lost power on short final and they landed safely in a field short of the runway.

Air taxi turbine pilot reported a duct and bleed fail light during high altitude climb to cruise altitude prior to oceanic entry point. The pilot requested a return to the departure airport and landed overweight safely.

Air carrier flight crew reported cargo that should have been unloaded from a previous flight was found during a postflight walkaround. This resulted in an incorrect weight and balance as the hazmat was undocumented.
Air carrier ground shipping personnel reported the erroneous shipment of a dangerous goods parcel as regular cargo instead of following hazmat shipment procedure.
DA40 flight school instructor and student reported a dual ECU failure and diversion to nearby airport.
B767 air carrier flight crew reported an engine malfunction during climb to cruise altitude. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
C172 pilot reported a divert due to high oil pressure and experienced a down draft during landing.
A321 Flight Attendant and Captain reported a fumes event during initial approach for landing.
Hawker 4000 flight crew reported complying with a TCAS RA during departure climb out from a non-towered airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported being notified that the flight departed without the NOTOC on a previous flight. The flight crew turned in the flight documents at destination and could not verify whether the document was submitted or not.
B737 pilot reported they inadvertently descended below the glideslope.

B737 Captain reported during preflight checks; the crew O2 pressure dropped to below minimums. The Captain called maintenance to check the Crew O2 Bottle Valve and found it to be opened a quarter turn and the Technician made repairs as required.
B757 flight crew reported loss of B hydraulic system caused a go around and a precautionary landing.
Tower Instructor reported a conflict when an arrival aircraft lined up for the incorrect runway.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training. The Instructor took evasive action to avoid a none reporting aircraft.
G200 pilot reported the slat and flaps did not extend during approach. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
A Tower Controller reported they had to vector an IFR helicopter that departed from a nearby hospital below the minimum vectoring altitude to avoid conflicting traffic.

B737 Captain reported a loss of control on ramp when plane rolled back into a ramp depression at the gate.  While attempting to pull further forward Captain noticed a ramp person with chocks under his window prior to it being safe to approach the aircraft.
C172 pilot reported loss of directional control during landing rollout resulted in runway excursion and contact with runway light.
C182 pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid aircraft crossing through traffic pattern; resulted in a NMAC situation.
C177 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control due to a windshear event during takeoff. The event resulted in a runway excursion with no injuries or aircraft damage.
SR20 Flight Instructor and student reported receiving a high cylinder head temperature warning message in flight. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Air carrier pilot reported the useable portion of Taxiway Y at ORD airport was difficult to distinguish from the unusable portion; which was blocked by flashing barricades.
PA-34 Flight Instructor reported they had to lower the gear manually due to a landing gear malfunction.

Air carrier pilot reported ground crew pushed the aircraft back toward another aircraft; resulting in a near collision. After that conflict was resolved; the tug driver then almost pushed the aircraft into a catering truck.
DA40 Flight Instructor reported aircraft had a hard landing and or return to airport loss of brake control resulted in runway excursion.
Captain reported a near upset shortly after takeoff from DFW when his aircraft was affected by jet blast from a taxiing wide body aircraft.
B777 flight crew reported a 'Gear Door Position Disagree' EICAS message when selecting gear up after takeoff. Crew diverted and made a normal landing.
Air carrier Captain reported takeoff delay due to minimal spacing with a landing aircraft. The flight was re-cleared once arriving aircraft had passed.
EMB-145 Flight Attendant reported receiving a call from the pilots that the flight was returning to the departure airport due to an engine malfunction. The flight landed and taxied to the gate.

PC-12 air ambulance pilot reported a ground collision with a golf cart on the ramp due to aircraft movement after the pilot set the parking brake and while shutting down the engine. The ground crew attempted to alert the pilot of the aircraft movement but the pilot was unaware of the movement or ground crew warnings.

Air Carrier Captain reported a 'dirty socks' odor of increasing intensity during departure taxi. After receiving a flight attendant call regarding the fumes the Captain requested a Ground return where Maintenance assigned an aircraft swap.
ZDC Controller reported issues relating to changes in the Florida Metroplex routing.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported the EFIS Control panel malfunctioned preventing them from setting the correct Touchdown Zone Elevation altitude. The reporter stated this is a recurring issue on the MAX 8 aircraft and could pose a safety of flight issue.
Pilot reported flying low over mountains to avoid poor weather conditions while continuing to destination with the ammeter discharging.
Dispatcher reported a B757-200 flight had a slat disagree light resulted in an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
B737 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL325 after passing underneath a B747 that was approximately 12 - 13 miles away.

General aviation pilot reported being notified that his aircraft was involved in a near miss during daylight; VMC conditions at cruise. The pilot indicated they were not communicating with ATC; in uncontrolled airspace; was not alerted by ADS-B in the aircraft; and not aware of the near miss until being contacted after the event.
Air taxi First Officer reported having communication problems with ANC Ground Control that may be due to interference from construction causing a radio dead zone on the ramp area.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern while following an incomplete ATC clearance. The pilot then maneuvered away from traffic on the parallel runway; to avoid a collision
Inspector reported non compliance with required procedures for the inspection of the super crown area of a B737-700 in for overnight service.
SR20 flight crew reported the loss of aircraft control during a soft field takeoff in which the aircraft sustained a wingtip strike.
C172 instructor pilot reported the student's final approach was too slow and a gust of wind lifted the wing resulting in a runway excursion and contact with a runway light.
GA pilot reported a NMAC while in the traffic pattern of a non-towered airport requiring evasive action.

Flight instructor reported an NMAC event during departure for a training flight with an intruder aircraft entering the traffic pattern incorrectly. Flight instructor altered the landing pattern to avoid a conflict.

Embraer 145 Flight Attendant reported a hot cabin with passengers suffering after a maintenance delay and passenger loading discrepancy. The discrepancy was resolved and the flight departed for the destination.
Recreational/Hobby UAS pilot reported flying a UAS with expired registration.

B747 flight crew reported receiving a fire alert in the aft cargo compartment while on final approach. Flight crew completed appropriate checklist and continued to landing where they were met by airport response crews who determined indications of fire no longer existed.
Technician reported striking the fuselage of the aircraft with an oil cart during routine engine service. The aircraft was removed from service for repairs.
TRACON Controller reported H25B failed to respond to radio communication while descending and flying erratic course; resulted in entering a higher MVA.
B737 NG Captain reported ground personnel attempted to open the main cabin door while it was armed.

GA pilot reported an NMAC while turning to enter the traffic pattern when an aircraft flew directly below them without announcing their position. The pilot made an immediate climb to avoid the aircraft then returned for landing.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC and had observed their solo student on short final with another aircraft also on short final abeam them.
A pilot reported they took evasive action while departing AUO non-towered airport to avoid an aircraft overflying mid field 500 feet below Traffic Pattern Altitude.
A320 flight crew reported a 'sweaty socks' odor which lasted for 5 minutes during descent and also at gate.
Cessna Twin Piston aircraft pilot reported the windshield anti-ice system was inoperative in icing conditions.
Pilot reported an NMAC while entering the traffic pattern at an airport with an operating control tower. Tower controller vectored the reporter's aircraft away from traffic.
A small aircraft Flight Instructor reported a NMAC at a non-towered airport.
First Officer reported severe turbulence encounter resulted in a temporary lose of control.
Air carrier flight crew reported a Wing Body Overheat warning and returned to departure airport.
PA-28 pilot reported a decrease in engine RPMs during climbout resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.

C680 First Officer reported EICAS Messages; EMERGENCY PRESSURIZATION and ACM BLEED LEAK were received. The flight crew immediately donned oxygen masks and descended. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.

Air Carrier Captain reported receiving information the push-out tug driver stated the aircraft's wing passed over the driver's tug after the salute. The Captain states being unaware of this occurrence and was never contacted until the safety committee ask for a report.
Grob Glider pilot reported during training maneuver; entanglement of tow line resulted in temporary loss of control with aircraft damage.

Experimental aircraft pilot reported the aircraft had intense engine vibrations that led to vital components coming off the aircraft that then resulted in an off-airport landing. The aircraft was having problems days before the flight that seemed to be resolved; but the pilot stated the crankshaft failure may be unrelated.
Pilot reported encountering another aircraft during taxi and was forced to deviate around with his wing passing under the other aircraft's wing.
Do 328 flight crew reported a runway excursion during landing rollout and cited a strong crosswind and patchy ice on the runway as contributing factors.
EMB-145 flight crew reported severe weather in cruise.
PA-28 pilot reported descending too low toward terrain; then climbing back up.
A330 flight crew reported inflight engine fire. Flight crew returned to departure airport and performed an overweight landing.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a malfunctioning toilet resulted in harsh fumes released into the cabin that caused physiological reactions among crew and passengers.
A319 Captain reported a Flight Attendant notified the flight crew on three occasions of fumes in the passenger cabin. Upon arrival Captain entered the discrepancy in the aircraft logbook.

Air carrier Captain reported there is reduced taxi clearance at BNA during the construction. The Captain states there are only inches of space between aircraft winglets and believes one taxi line; instead of two; would improve the situation.
B757 flight crew reported a fumes event during descent and continued to a safe landing at destination.

Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with an aircraft under tow while taxiing to the gate. The tug driver did not follow ramp instructions and the Captain had to stop the aircraft short of the gate to avoid a conflict.

B737 Flight Attendant reported hearing a loud noise from cabin boarding door due to air leak during climb. The flight returned to the airport and maintenance found a clogged lower spring seal not closing properly.
Reporter stated a student had a ramp excursion during taxi which resulted in the aircraft going into a ditch and incurring damage.

PA-28 student pilot reported during landing they lost control of aircraft on touchdown due to wind gust. The student pilot over corrected with right rudder and applied incorrect brake pressure which caused them to veer left off the runway.
Air Carrier First Officer (PNF) reported engine bangs and the right engine ITT momentarily going to a high reading. All occurred shortly after takeoff. The aircraft returned to the departure airport.
A319 air carrier flight crew reported an engine malfunction during cruise. The crew continued to destination and landed without incident.
Air carrier flight crew reported they were advised of possible wingtip contact with a pole during taxi at BNA in their efforts to avoid another air carrier aircraft.
B767 Captain reported releasing the brakes without acknowledging receipt of the dangerous goods due to not receiving the form and summary.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing SEA in trail of an A321; commenting that spacing appeared insufficient.
Pilot reported being cut off by a vehicle during taxi at FAI airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control during a severe turbulence encounter. Reportedly; the aircraft sustained minor damage in the cabin area and some passengers were thrown from their seats.

First Officer reported #1 Engine failed to start using the right hand ignition system. The aircraft was operating with MEL XX-X-##; so Both was selected and the engine started normally. After arrival at destination airport; the flight Crew made a log book entry to ensure the aircraft and MEL were correct configuration.
Pilot reported witnessing an aircraft departing from an intersecting runway while on final approach over the runway threshold. The pilot flew over the other aircraft and completed their landing.
Fractional jet Captain reported a GPS jamming event while on a RNAV arrival in domestic airspace. The Captain changed to heading mode and notified ATC; then the GPS jamming cleared before landing.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported Hazmat shipment refused due to missing required Hazmat documents as well as being improperly configured for transport.
Maintenance Technicians reported missing a procedural step when deferring a thrust reverser while the aircraft was at the gate prior to departure.
ZAB Enroute Controller reported a flight crews navigational capability was affected by White Sands Missile Range GPS jamming.

Ramp agent reported a taxiway incursion while an observer in a tug was towing an aircraft on the airport surface to the hangar. When the driver received a clearance to hold short of a taxiway he then crept past the hold short line. The crossing aircraft had to slow down as they became unsure if the tug was going to stop.
A light sport aircraft pilot reported a rough running engine on initial climb and a return to the airport. While landing they had a brief runway excursion.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a light aircraft on descent into PVU.

ARR Tower controllers and C90 TRACON Controller trainee reported the TRACON Controller turned a departing aircraft into a confliction with an aircraft in the Tower pattern. The TRACON Controller did not coordinate their action with Tower and turned the aircraft below the minimum vectoring altitude.

Flight Instructor with student reported a gust of wind combine with the student's control inputs caused the aircraft to depart the taxiway surface. After inspecting the aircraft and pushing it back on the taxiway; the flight crew continued with their planned flight.
Air carrier Captain reported starting an engine at the gate per Maintenance instructions and not being aware that ramp personnel were near the engine.
Air carrier First Officer on approach into SFO reported ATC issued Low Altitude Alert.
B737 Captain reported inadvertent escape slide deployment during boarding at the gate.

MD-11 flight crew reported after takeoff upon raising gear we encountered L MAIN gear UNSAFE(RED) and secondary UNSAFE (Red) indication on configuration page. The flight crew ran the check lists and QRH and restored system functions. The flight crew continued to destination airport and made a precautionary landing.
Light Transport aircraft First Officer reported a CFTT event during approach to HWD airport.  The crew immediately corrected after ATC made them aware of the issue.

B737-800 flight crew reported a fuel imbalance after departure. The crew ran the QRH checklist and determined there was a possible fuel leak. The crew made the decision to shutdown the suspect engine and return to their departure airport for maintenance.
B737 flight crew reported engine malfunction in cruise flight. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.

Air carrier pilot reported they were assigned a gate at MBPV/PLS airport that was not listed on Jeppesen charts. Pilot also reported confusing taxi instructions along with a lack of ground support created multiple hazardous situations.
PA-34 Flight Instructor reported during landing the aircraft started leaning to the right and the right main landing gear was retracting resulting in the propeller making contact with the ground.
Air taxi pilot reported a ground conflict with a vehicle on the runway on approach for landing. They decided to land due to icing conditions.

Captain reported the PFD malfunctioned during an ILS approach to AVP airport. While attempting to resolve the issue; the flight crew received and responded to a terrain warning. Captain cited an incorrect altitude selection and possible 5G interference as contributing factors.

General aviation Flight Instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern during a training flight. The tower instructed the other aircraft to perform a maneuver to separate the traffic and changed the Instructor pilots landing sequence to provide more separation in the traffic pattern.
An air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming.
DA-42 Twin Star multi-engine aircraft pilot reported loss of directional control due to gusty wind conditions during take-off. Pilot regained control and rejected take-off.
Pilot reported experiencing radio and GPS problems when trying to activate the runway lights.
Flight crew reported the First Officer hand flying a steeper than usual Visual Approach with a terrain warning in the flare.
A TRACON Controller reported new RNAV SID and STAR procedures at their facility do not provide procedural separation between arrivals and departures.
C320 pilot reported landing without clearance due to low fuel caused by a go-around and conflict with military jet aircraft and a commercial passenger jet that didn't clear the runway in time.
B767 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL345 from a preceding aircraft on their trans-Atlantic route.
A pilot reported they descended below their assigned altitude and received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
A320 Captain reported a 'dirty socks' odor while parked at gate. The flight crew completed appropriate procedures and requested maintenance who had everyone deplane for troubleshooting.

Air carrier Captain reported uncommanded aircraft movement on the ramp when the aircraft parking brake was released without a tug attached and then the engines started resulting in the aircraft movement. The ground personnel told the Captain to stop the aircraft; the Captain set the brakes; and no damage or injury occurred.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported a near-collision occurred on the ramp in EWR with two aircraft pushing back from adjacent gates.
B757 flight crew reported the left engine flamed out during cruise and the crew attempted restart. Restart was unsuccessful; so a diversion was made for a precautionary landing.
B737 pilot reported loss of pressurization at 37;000 feet required an immediate descent to 10;000 feet.
Captain reported an LSAS system failure caused control issues on descent and approach to landing resulting in a precautionary landing.

Helicopter pilot reported an NMAC while turning onto final approach; which led the pilot to take evasive action and land on a parallel taxiway. Pilot stated that the Tower Controller had not advised of the conflicting aircraft.
Flight instructor reported experiencing a loss of aircraft control caused by a wind gust; resulting in departure from the runway surface and aircraft damage.

PA-34 Flight Instructor reported that the student pilot retracted the landing gear instead of resetting the wing flaps; resulting in a gear collapse on the runway. Pilots secured the aircraft and evacuated without injury.
MD-11 flight crew received a level 2 HYD 1 OFF alert and elected to monitor systems and continue to destination airport.
Air carrier pilot reported they initiated a go around when they observed a vehicle on the runway.
GA pilot reported a loss of communication with the ATC Center resulting in an ADS-B traffic alert.  Reporter cited possible COVID-19 related ATC staffing issues may have contributed to the event.

Captain reported a course deviation when cleared by ATC to BEETS during climb out. When notified by ATC of the deviation; ATC further clarified the name of the fix was BEADS; not the similar sounding BEETS.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a fume event during preflight at gate. Passengers and flight crew deplaned during maintenance action.

A320 flight crew reported a Green Hydraulic System Reservoir Overheat light illuminated in flight. The flight crew performed the QRH procedures and secured the Green system Pump. The flight crew requested priority handling and reactivated the Green System Pump closer to the destination airport to conduct a precautionary landing.
CRJ-700 Lead Technician reported that the incorrect length fasteners were used during the replacement of a malfunctioning AOA sensor; resulting in an air leak in an RVSM critical area.
A Customer Service/Gate Agent reported yellow stow circles and other paint lines on the ramp have faded away which could result in an improper jet bridge positioning.
C182 pilot reported entering a runway without an ATC clearance. The pilot quickly exited the runway; but the aircraft on final approach had to perform a go-around.
Air carrier Captain reported abruptly stopping the aircraft due to ground vehicle on service road failing to yield to the airplane.
Air carrier flight crew reported a low altitude alert from ATC during in the climb. The flight received ATC vectors on course and continued the climb.
B767 First Officer reported a high speed rejected takeoff due to an airspeed discrepancy.
C172 Flight Instructor who reported loss of engine power on initial climb was able to descend back down and land.
B737 flight crew reported windshield de-ice system failure resulted in a divert to avoid weather.

GEG TRACON Controller reported they issued a clearance that placed an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude. The Controller's radar map was inaccurate and the Low Altitude Alert did not function.

Air carrier pilot reported there are insufficient FAA regulations and references in flight operations manuals for ATC equipment failures that could impact multiple aircraft; especially during holding procedures for ILS approaches at EWR.
Captain reported refusing the aircraft over confusion and communications issues regarding the MEL procedures for a Engine Oil filter Bypass Light Illuminated.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC during departure climb from DPA when an aircraft departing the parallel runway flew directly overhead them. Reportedly; ATC was not aware of the conflict.
C560XL Captain reported finding a discrepancy between the AFM and the performance data from Dispatch and noted that the performance data provided on the release did not follow the FAR.
Air carrier Captain reported they deviated slightly from the SID due to a discrepancy between the Captain's and First Officer's Flight Director and Navigation Display.
B737 Captain reported a track heading deviation due the aircraft not being able to intercept the LOC during final approach resulting in a go-around.
ERJ-175 Captain reported being unfamiliar with the departure procedures and did not follow the obstacle procedures upon departure; which resulted in crossing the VOR lower than the required altitude.

N90 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft failed to follow the Kennedy 5 Departure; resulting in a course deviation that put the aircraft into potential conflict with LGA arrivals. Controller vectored the aircraft back to JFK airspace.

Flight crew reported loss of fluid from the left hydraulic system in flight. After completing the required checklists the flight crew elected to continue to destination airport and made a safe landing.
M20F pilot reported another aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision with them; they landed safely at the airport.

PA-44-180 instructor and student pilot reported a conflict with agricultural aircraft taking off in the opposite direction on a training flight at an uncontrolled airport who was not communicating on the frequency. The instructor rejected the takeoff and maneuvered to the side of the runway; as the agriculture aircraft passed overhead; then taxied off the runway with a blown tire.
Flight Instructor in the pattern at a non-towered airport reported taking evasive action to avoid another aircraft on downwind which turned directly toward their flight path.

Air carrier ground personnel reported two airways bills were improperly manifested at departure airport. The shipments contained Pharmaceutical items ultimately arrived at the wrong airport without required DG documents and missing required DG labels.

B787 First Officer reported a CFTT event and an unstabilized approach while Captain was hand flying a visual approach.  Pilot reported full scale 'low' deviation on glide slope; an improperly set altitude alerter during event and the need to climb to return to glide slope.

Air carrier Captain reported descending too low on the approach and triggering a ground proximity warning. The glideslope at the airport was out of service and the flight crew missed that information in the NOTAMs.
C172 Flight Instructor on a training flight with student reported a near miss with another aircraft.
Pilot reported a taxiway incursion at CRQ resulted from confusion of a blue box" with a 'blue line' to taxi to the customs ramp."
Air Carrier First Officer reported a baggage cart drove in front of the aircraft as it was taxiing to parking in the ramp and they had to brake to avoid.

B777 Captain reported a battery-powered wheelchair was incorrectly filled in on a tag form; leading to the flight crew requesting assistance from Operations. A Baggage Handler then came on board to verify and correct the tag information.
A300 Captain reported a Yellow Hydraulic system malfunction; rejected the takeoff; and returned to the gate for maintenance.
B737-700 pilot crew reported an in flight engine shutdown due to a loss of oil quantity.

Air taxi Captain reported during departure taxi another aircraft taxied toward them at a high rate of speed and did not give way as instructed by ATC. The Captain applied brakes firmly to avoid a collision.
C680A aircraft Captain reported a CFTT incident during approach.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss when another aircraft entered the runway for takeoff while the pilot was on short final at a non-towered airport. The pilot performed a go around and landed safely.

B757 First Officer reported a pack temperature control problem expanded to the loss of both packs and cabin altitude control. The flight crew descended to 10;000 ft. and diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
Flight Instructor reported when lined up on runway for takeoff; a NORDO aircraft overflew them and landed. Pattern traffic warned instructor to not takeoff.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported having a near miss with a flock of birds and flew above their authorized LAANC approval altitude to avoid a collision.
A321 pilot reported dual flight management computer failure during cruise. Flight diverted and experienced two flat tires during landing.
Mooney pilot reported diverting to the nearest suitable airport after experiencing a rough running engine.
C150 Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a near miss with another aircraft in the airport traffic pattern.
Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict while following Tower's clearance to cross a closed runway. A truck drove in front of the aircraft causing the flight to stop abruptly.
Air carrier crew reported an NMAC at BOS during initial climb when another aircraft executed a go-around on an intersecting runway.
PA28 student pilot reported loss of directional control on takeoff that resulted in a runway excursion and minor damage to that aircraft.
A C172 pilot reported while landing at a non towered airport they lost control of the aircraft resulting in a runway excursion.
Air Carrier pilot taxiing to the ramp at SLC reported Tower taxi instructions did not correlate with the 10-9 chart depiction.

Acrobatic single pilot reported a CFIT event during ATC assigned vectors. The pilot executed a 360 left turn due to ATC's lack of communication responsiveness to pilot's repeated calls for vector update.
Ground Agent reported paint and paint thinner spilled out of a passenger's bag during baggage unloading. TSA confiscated spilled items and also similar items from the passenger's second bag.
CL65 Captain reported they a return to the departure airport due to pressurization issues and an inoperative air conditioning pack.
A Cirrus SR-20 pilot reported brake failure during landing resulted in a loss of control and runway excursion.

Student Pilot on a solo cross-country flight reported an NMAC with another aircraft seen on ADS-B. The reporter was receiving flight following from ATC but had inadvertently turned down the volume of the radio and missed the traffic advisory for the aircraft.
C172 pilot reported that the left tank fuel pressure became unported during a forward slip; causing fuel starvation and leading to a loss of engine power.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported while in the pattern encountering another aircraft resulting in the Flight Instructor taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.

Flight crew reported autopilot issues caused uncommanded aircraft movements resulting in deviations of course and altitude. The flight crew continued to the destination airport and turned the aircraft over to maintenance.
B737 flight crew reported three occurrences of uncommanded right rolls during cruise climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported hydraulic system malfunction in cruise flight. Flight crew diverted and returned to departure airport.
A Center Controller reported a military UAS flew an unexpected route outside of their designated airspace. The reporter had to vector another aircraft to avoid a confliction with the UAS.

B777 flight crew reported a passenger had dropped a phone under a seat; it got stuck; and began to spark and smoke. The flight attendants used fire extinguishers; retrieved the phone; and secured it inside a thermal containment bag.
B737 Captain reported a fume event during taxi resulting in ground gate return.

B737-800 pilot reported unreliable airspeed and altitude reporting during a ferry flight from long term storage. The flight crew requested priority handling and made an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Captain reported undocumented transport of Hazmat cargo.
Air carrier flight crew reported an NMAC with another aircraft during departure climb.

Inspector reported non-compliance with procedures concerning quarantine of an ELAC following vendor repair. The unit was released from quarantine; subsequently installed and then failed; resulting in returning it to the vendor.
Air Carrier Captain reported temporary ADS-B EICAS advisory and GPS failure from suspected electronic jamming event.
Air Carrier Captain reported the runway hold short markings for BUF Runway 32 are faded on Taxiway A.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the aircraft had to return to the gate to move unsecured cargo.
Flight Instructor reported during taxi the student added excess power and insufficient braking resulting in loss of control and impact of a wing with poles.
Small transport Captain reported the right engine failed to produce sufficient power during the takeoff roll and rejected the takeoff.

Air carrier B737 crew reported a low airspeed condition when the Captain was not aware the autopilot disconnected during a go around procedure. First Officer identified the slow energy state; called it out; and the Captain recovered the aircraft and safely completed the go around.
Air taxi Captain reported faded hold short markings and lack of warning lights resulted in a runway incursion.

Air carrier ground personnel reported a Ramp Agent attempted to take a lithium ion battery from a passenger's bag to the cabin for transport. Ramp Agent was redirected to Ramp Supervisor for proper procedure.
Pilot reported a heading deviation after takeoff that was caused by confusing pre-takeoff clearance instructions.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a UAS while on approach. The pilot advised ATC about the UAS and landed safely.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported a flap malfunction during final approach resulting in a go-around.  The crew completed the pertinent QRH procedures and landed.
B767-300ER Captain reported a pitch limit indication and stick shaker while in turbulence resulted in temporary loss of aircraft control.
EMB-170 First Officer reported a flap overspeed occurred departing EWR when they encountered wake and atmospheric turbulence shortly after takeoff.
Cessna 172 pilot reported loss of oil pressure caused engine failure during final approach. Pilot was unable to reach the runway and landed in a field near the airport.
C182 pilot reported nose gear collapsed during landing.
Fractional jet Captain reported they experienced GPS jamming over Myanmar. All systems recovered to normal operations after leaving the jamming area.
Air carrier First Officer reported they were cleared to cross 8L at HNL even though another air carrier aircraft was on short final for landing.
First Officer reported loss of power on the #1 engine during approach but landed safely.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported Terrain Warning during departure climb.
CE-680 pilot reported temporary loss of aircraft control in severe turbulence during descent.
