A320 Captain reported the aircraft was about to descend below the JABBR crossing restriction on the ANTHM4 arrival to BWI until they intervened and stopped the descent. Captain indicated they had encountered this issue three separate times in different A320 aircraft.

PA-28 fight Instructor reported a NMAC event during takeoff with a landing aircraft. The landing aircraft executed a go around with 'zero offset' which promoted the flight Instructor to call for an early crosswind to provide separation.
A321 flight crew reported a possible compressor stall on the number 1 engine shortly after takeoff which resulted in a return to the departure airport.
CRJ-200 Captain reported shortly after takeoff L 14th Duct warning message appeared; extinguished; then appeared again.  Crew returned to departure airport.
Flight Instructor reported a confusing taxiway configuration near the approach end of Runway 31 at CVH airport needs better signage and as well as clarification on an airport diagram.
Air carrier Technician reported that while they were troubleshooting post flight reports; they were distracted by management due to time pressure.
B737-800 Captain reported being notified at destination arrival that the signed copy of the Notification to Captain form was not provided to the Operations Agent prior to departure.
Air carrier Maintenance Technician reported observing an escape slide deploying on a parked aircraft when a caterer opened the armed R1 door from inside the plane.
Air carrier Captain reported passengers were unruly and not complying with face mask policy despite multiple warnings by the crew resulting in a diversion; and removal of the passengers.

Technician reported communications problems and confusion caused the incorrect Ignition system to be deferred and components replaced without fixing the problem. The aircraft returned to the gate for additional maintenance action.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported TCAS Alert on descent.
Pilot reported landing on an occupied runway by a disabled aircraft due to a low fuel condition.

Air Traffic Controller reported aircraft descended out of their assigned altitude resulting in the aircraft flying below the MVA and towards terrain. The aircraft did not respond to ATC instructions until communications was reestablished and aircraft was issued a climb that they acknowledged.

CE-550 air taxi crew reported descending below the glide slope on an ILS approach in instrument weather and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC. The First Officer executed a missed approach; then landed safely on the next approach.
A319 flight crew reported loss of auto flight systems in cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.

CR-J200 flight crew reported prior to takeoff the Right Pack and APU were on MEL's. During climbout the Left Pack overheated and failed. The pilots asked ATC for assistance; requested a lower altitude and returned to the field.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a strong horrible odor of unknown source during boarding. Flight Attendant notified the Captain who ordered passengers and flight crew to deplane.
Cessna 172 student pilot reported a runway excursion during landing on a training flight. No injury or airport damage occurred; and the pilot taxied to the ramp.
CRJ-700 Captain reported a malfunction with nose wheel steering during takeoff roll.
A light single engine aircraft pilot reported the engine quit when they pulled the mixture control knob instead of carburetor heat.
Air carrier Technician reported during a rigging procedure on the B-787 spoiler power control unit; the leading-edge slats extended and impacted both engine inboard thrust reverser doors.
GA pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern with an aircraft that turned in front of them. Pilot took evasive action to prevent a collision.
Flight Instructor reported observing a NMAC and being involved in a NMAC with the same aircraft while in the pattern; resulting in them taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
DA40 pilot reported an alternator failure that failed their Garmin 1000 resulted in the pilot's loss of radio and transponder ability to communicate.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below their assigned altitude and flew below the MVA.
Pilot reported possible 5G interference on short final landing in IND.
B737-800 pilot reported flap malfunction during cruise flight. Flight diverted and landed safely.

EDC Tower Controller reported an arriving aircraft made an incorrect position report and flew across the departure end of the active runway without notifying ATC. The reporter stated there is no radar display at the facility and Controllers cannot see most of the aircraft until they are within close proximity.
B787 flight crew reported leading edge flap and slat fail EICAS messages in flight and running the associated checklists with a landing at the destination airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert during approach to land.

Air carrier First Officer reported having to call in sick due to fatigue resulting from long work hours. First Officer stated many consecutive work days with minimum days off and lack of scheduling flexibility are common issues at the company.
EMB-145 First Officer reported receiving a GPWS during approach descent.
ORD Controller reported departure aircraft failure to follow departure clearance resulted in a wrong turn. Controller stated that this is an unsafe ongoing issue that fails to be addressed.

Air Carrier Captain reported a strong 'dirty socks' odor upon arriving at the aircraft. The Captain submitted an AML discrepancy; a company fumes report and assisted with an engine run before deplaning due to physiological symptoms.
EMB-170 Captain reported while in cruise flight observing an air bubble and cracks in the Captain's windshield resulting in the crew descending to a safe altitude and continued to their destination.

Bonanza 35 pilot reported while executing a touch and go landing the pilot inadvertently raised the landing gear resulting in the aircraft striking the runway and becoming disabled on the runway.  A FBO was able to tow the aircraft off the runway.
ERJ 135 First Officer reported a rejected take off and return to the gate after a SPS2 Fail red EICAS message were displayed.

Caravan 208B Captain reported a loss of bank control while flying an approach in instrument conditions; resulting in an unstablized approach. The Captain regained control of the aircraft and performed a go around then returned for another approach.
Flight Crew reported a loss of engine oil quantity and a loss of oil pressure caused an inflight shut down of the engine and an ATC guided landing.

General aviation instructor pilot reported descending low over a densely populated area to remain below Class B airspace at night on a visual training flight. The flight descended below FAA minimum altitude for a congested area while lined up with the runway but far from the airport.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing severe airframe vibration while on departure; resulting in the flight crew returning to the departure airport where they landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with traffic from the opposite direction during cruise flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a momentary GPWS terrain alert on initial approach.
Air carrier Captain reported returning to the departure airport after an MEL'd right high pressure shutoff valve caused several other pressurization failures.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with a UAS during final approach. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
C172 Flight Instructor reported an ATC failure to separate traffic on a missed approach resulted in a NMAC.

Flight Instructor with student reported observing an NMAC at a non-towered airport when a military jet performed a touch-and-go on a runway that was not being used by the other traffic and overflew an aircraft in the pattern.
Air carrier Pilot reported a fumes event during descent described as a dirty socks odor. The fumes dissipated after troubleshooting.
An air carrier flight crew on visual approach to a Class B airport observed a drone pass along the aircraft at 5;000 feet. Crew notified ATC.

Air carrier flight crew reported they were unable to comply with a crossing restriction on the RKSTR 3 arrival into LAS airport. Flight crew cited a late runway change from ATC and aircraft performance limitations as contributing.
Air Carrier Captain reported difficulty understanding Center controllers and believes the problem maybe the controllers are wearing face masks.
Light Transport aircraft Captain reported a CFIT event during visual approach to ASE requiring evasive action.
C510 pilot reported the pilot side window shattered during climb. Flight diverted to nearest suitable airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an ATC low altitude alert during final approach. Reporter stated that they were further out on approach than expected.
Flight Attendant reported a cabin pressurization problem and having to take their seats earlier than expected.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported pressurization system failure resulted in return to departure airport.
Captain reported on visual approach receiving a confusing ATC low altitude alert.
C172 Flight Instructor reported landing safely after experiencing a substantial power loss shortly after takeoff.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported landing safely when the engine lost power shortly after takeoff. Reporter discovered on the ground that the selected fuel tank was dry.
Air carrier FO reported difficulty contacting a busy Approach Controller. This resulted in a missed approach to avoid an NMAC with another air carrier.
A320 flight crew reported having a stuck mic which prevented them from receiving a takeoff clearance.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported a smart bag arrived improperly configured for air transport.

Corporate pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during departure when the autopilot turned the aircraft to a fix that was incorrectly programmed. ATC vectored the aircraft back on course.

ERJ-170 Flight Attendant reported a passenger had a laptop in a bag that was gate checked. The flight crew disarmed the main cabin door to retrieve the bag to remove the laptop before stowing the bag away and going through the door closing procedures again.
Maintenance Controller reported confusion and communications issues resulted in an MEL being applied to the wrong Ignition System. The aircraft returned to the gate for maintenance action.
Air carrier pilot reported an engine failure due to contaminated oil. The aircraft was dispatched with an MEL regarding the 'Oil debris above limit'. ATC cleared the flight to divert.
Air carrier Captain reported refusing a lithium metal battery due to a potential aircraft fire suppression system that may not extinguish a cargo fire.
Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict with a service vehicle while exiting the ramp.
Pilot reported overshooting an altitude on departure and attributed the event to lack of recent flying and poor use of HUD.

B737 flight crew reported receiving a ground proximity terrain warning during approach while descending below the minimum altitude. After hearing the ground proximity aural warning; the flight crew conducted a go-around to a safe landing.
Air carrier Captain reported a severe turbulence encounter which resulted in loss of control of airspeed and altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPS interference over Europe. GPS did not regain function after exiting area of interference.
CRJ-700 Captain reported a rejected takeoff due to a stall fail caution message and popped APU CB resulting in a return to gate.
Aircraft instructor and student reported taking off at JFX before the preceding landing traffic had cleared the runway.

GA pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern while preparing to land. The pilot reported the other aircraft did not follow instructions from the Tower. The intruding aircraft caused the reporter to take evasive action.

Air carrier Ramp Lead reported a communication misunderstanding between the Ramp personnel and the gate CSA regarding the late arriving Dry Ice parcel and the missing NOTOC. After resolving the NOTOC issue the Ramp Person recommended refresher DG course.
B737-800 Mechanic reported software and communications problems caused an overflight event when an actuator was not replaced on time.
Air carrier First Officer reported the aircraft rolled forward uncommanded while parking at the gate; requiring the Captain to apply prompt braking.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported getting close to an IFR aircraft while conducting maneuvers for an engine break-in training flight at night.
MD-11 Captain reported a fuel quantity used alert that showed excessive fuel used. The flight crew elected to dump fuel and divert to make a precautionary landing.
A319 First Officer reported gusty winds caused an unstable approach at 50 feet resulting in a bounced landing and flap overspeed during the go around.
B737-800 pilot reported vibrations during climb that affected the responsiveness of the flight controls. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
C172 Flight Instructor reported engine loss of power during takeoff resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Cessna 172 Instructor Pilot reported difficulty controlling the aircraft on short final when a regional aircraft's jet blast upset the aircraft.

Light Transport aircraft First Officer reported receiving a TCAS RA during visual approach to Runway 36 to PIE airport.   The TCAS RA was followed and was reportedly triggered by a VFR aircraft transiting below the TPA Class B airspace.
A Flight Instructor in a C172 reported an NMAC in the pattern of a tower controlled airport.
Small Transport aircraft Captain reported performing an air turnback due to unreliable airspeed and altimeter functions caused by the open static system drain.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a passenger wheelchair equipped with a non-removable battery was transported although transport of these is prohibited.
B737 Captain reported trailing edge flaps failed to extend beyond 25. Crew executed a missed approach and returned to land.
Air carrier Relief First Officer reported landing safely after the flight descended well below the glideslope on the approach to WSSS/SIN airport.
Instructor Pilot reported a prop ground strike while instructing a Student Pilot. The Instructor was late to take control of the aircraft and a prop strike occurred.
PA-28 pilot reported being issued a go around by ATC due to lining up on the wrong runway.

Mooney M20 pilot reported a loss of oil pressure during cruise and the need to divert to a nearby airport for landing. Upon inspection; it was discovered a missing rocker cover caused an oil leak and only a small amount of oil remained in the engine.
TRACON Controller reported turning an aircraft below the MVA.
Cessna 340 Pilot reported a rejected take off after a probable tail strike caused damage to the fuselage tail cone and jammed the elevators.
B737 Captain descending on a STAR reported sighting an unknown object near their flight path.
A321-NEO Flight Crew reported encountering a flock of birds at rotation resulting in multiple bird strikes and a return to the departure airport.
Pilot flying BE20 aircraft reported electrical smoke and arcing at circuit breaker panel inflight. Diverted without incident.

UAS safety manager reported they believe that aerial agriculture operations pose a danger to UAS operations in the form of near misses due to no requirement for agricultural operators to have ADS-B out.
C172 Student Pilot reported selecting the improper flap and carburetor settings resulted in a runway excursion.
Two Jacksonville Center Controllers reported FAA Command Center would not comply with their request for traffic flow restrictions for known weather; causing operational challenges.
Ground ramp employee reported a truck almost hit an aircraft during pushback.  Reportedly; the truck driver was looking down and not paying attention.
Flight crew flying CE680 aircraft reported hydraulic failure during departure climb. Flight diverted and landed uneventfully.
A320 Captain reported a hydraulic malfunction which resulted in a low speed rejected takeoff due to one hydraulic system indicating low. The aircraft returned to the gate.
A corporate pilot reported they descended below a crossing restriction on an approach and received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported DG notification was received after pushback. Flight crew subsequently received DG summary; completed acknowledgement and proceeded to a safe departure.

PA-32 Flight Instructor reported losing engine power while returning to home base. After performing engine restart procedures to no avail; the pilot made an off-field landing on a highway with no damage to the aircraft or injuries.
Pilots reported the aircraft was sliding on the taxiway after landing due to possible surface contamination with ice.

C172 pilot reported performing evasive action after encountering a NMAC at a non-towered airport. The pilot was focused on maintaining separation and making radio calls with one aircraft and lost situational awareness of another aircraft that was turning to join downwind.
B757 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing LAX in trail of an A320; and stated the spacing seemed abnormally tight.
Air carrier First Officer reported exceeding 250 knots below 10;000 feet due to a reduced passenger load on climbout.
B737 Captain reported aircraft controllability issues after takeoff. The flight crew elected to request priority handling and divert to make a precautionary landing.
Fractional Captain reported they removed the aircraft chocks before the parking brake was set resulting in the aircraft rolling into the taxiway before it was brought to a stop.
First Officer reported an air turn back after cabin would not pressurize.

ORD Tower Controller and a flight crew reported the crew departed from a recently constructed runway; turned the wrong direction and contacted the wrong Departure Controller. The reporter states this is a recurring issue.

A320 Captain reported receiving an 'EGT OVERLIMIT ECAM' alert for the number 2 engine during initial climb. After contacting Maintenance; the flight crew performed an air turnback and submitted logbook entries for event.
Air carrier Relief Pilot reported an altitude deviation on initial approach which resulted in a terrain warning from ATC. The crew corrected the descent profile and landed safely.
MD-11 Dispatcher reported a flight a high speed rejected takeoff due to a tire fail annunciator.
Biplane pilot landing at a non towered airport reported they lost control of the aircraft resulting in a ground loop and damage to the aircraft.

Air Carrier Captain reported during taxi that the flight received a notification to acknowledge that Dangerous Goods was loaded. The acknowledgement form with correct data was submitted shortly thereafter.
EMB 145 Captain reported the autopilot malfunctioned and would not track the localizer.
Light aircraft pilot reported confusing signage at DVT airport led to a taxi deviation down a vehicle roadway.

SR20 Student Pilot reported during engine malfunction at night; CFI froze leaving student to fly and handle situation to a landing. Student reported being in an unsafe situation by CFII who failed to assist and handle situation.

A321 Captain reported a #1 engine low oil pressure indication on the upper ECAM display and elected to continue the approach to landing; subsequently shutting the engine down after exiting the runway.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported Hydraulic failure after takeoff and performed an air turn back to landing.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported being contacted by law enforcement during a flight and instructed to land due to several alleged infractions.

General aviation Flight Instructor with student reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The instructor took control and maneuvered to avoid a collision; then completed a traffic pattern to a safe landing.

B777 Captain reported a gate return for load issues. Maintenance met the aircraft and informed the Captain that a tail pipe fire was reported. After engine runs; a faulty fuel valve was diagnosed and the aircraft was removed from service for repair.

B737 air carrier crew reported a TCAS RA command to descend on final approach due to aircraft established on the parallel runway. The Captain followed the TCAS descend command until a minimum altitude then selected TCAS TA only and continued to a safe landing.
B737 flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during approach. Flight crew corrected and continued approach.
Flight Instructor with student reported an aircraft rolled onto the runway while they were on short final. Student performed a go around.

Recreational/Hobby UAS pilot reported flying at an uncontrolled airport; conducting a photo shoot of a manned aircraft. Prior to the flight; the Airport Manager told the pilot that UAS were not allowed at the airport but the reporter cited regulatory guidance to the contrary and conducted the operation as planned.
PA34 pilot reported engine oil leak during during cruise. Pilot shut down engine and diverted.

Air carrier pilot departing DFW reported being issued a revised departure procedure while taxiing at DFW that could not be located in any published material. Company dispatch was also unaware of a procedure with this name.
Pilot flying reported an engine failure and while holding for a safe approach; an adjacent airport allowed an aircraft to depart into the flight path of holding pattern.

Cessna 172 Instructor pilot reported a near miss on final approach during a training flight at a tower controlled airport. The pilot indicated a helicopter departed from a taxiway; the Instructor performed an immediate maneuver to avoid contact; then performed a go around and landed.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss on final approach at a non-tower airport while proficiency training. The pilot performed an evasive maneuver at low altitude to avoid the other aircraft; re-entered the pattern and landed safely.
Air Carrier Captain reported they missed sending a DG company code prior to brake release. Upon realizing the issue; the missed code was sent.
General aviation pilot reported causing an NMAC when they turned to avoid other aircraft.
A321 Captain reported a parking brake failure at the gate resulted in temporary uncommanded movement of the aircraft.
EMB-145 flight crew reported nosewheel steering failure after landing required a tow to the gate.
B737-700 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport after observing indications of a fuel leak in the #1 tank.
First Officer reported receiving a Vent Blower Fault ECAM on initial climb out. An air turn back and precautionary landing was made at the departure airport.

C340 pilot reported a failure of the electronic flight display system during cruise flight in IFR conditions that resulted in an in-flight upset. After correcting the upset by referencing backup instruments; the pilot was able to regain control and proceed to VFR weather where a safe landing was accomplished.
Aircraft maintenance Inspector reported inadvertently missing a step during a windshield replacement that required the re-connection of wiring of the PL40 panel to the crash switch.
Bonanza V-35 pilot reported altitude deviation in severe turbulence while in IMC.

Air carrier flight crew reported during a visual approach and on downwind for Runway 21 at GEG; seeing towers and stopping the flight's descent. The pilot suggested a notation is needed on the charts for all pilots describing the tower's location and height.
A320 Captain reported the Lead Flight Attendant reported a 'dirty socks' odor during descent. Flight accomplished a safe landing and submitted a logbook discrepancy upon gate arrival.
MD-11 Captain reported the First Officer's PFD failed just after rotation. Flight crew performed troubleshooting and after resetting computers for all engines continued to destination.

Cessna 182 pilot reported becoming distracted during single pilot operation in IMC on approach and entering an unusual attitude. The pilot took immediate actions to recover the aircraft and elected to do an approach into a nearby airport.

PA-28 Student Pilot reported losing directional control of the aircraft on takeoff roll; rejected the takeoff; and proceeded to go off the runway and into the grass. The underside of the left wing; where the pitot mast is located; had struck an airport sign.
Hobby UAS pilot reported their UAS had a fly away for unknown reasons and was unrecoverable.
B767-300 Captain reported they found out post flight the Captain's NOTOC copy was mistakenly removed prior to departure.
A319 Pilot reported engine oil quantity rapidly decreased from 18 quarts to 2 quarts. A diversion and an in flight shut down were performed and a landing was made at a diversion airport.
Air carrier Captain reported that during pushback the aircraft became disconnected from the tow bar; and was brought to a safe stop using the aircraft brakes.
SLC Tower Controller reported they issued instructions to an aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Safe Altitude.
Captain reported a left engine flame out during flight and elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier pilot reported during pre-flight several ECAM alerts was displayed. Maintenance found pitot line disconnected and reconnected line.
Air Carrier pilots reported two similar sounding waypoints on this SID which caused a heading deviation and TCAS traffic warning.

Flight Crew reported a go around was caused by a Leading Edge Flaps Transit Light remained illuminated after flaps were fully configured on approach. The Flight Crew ran the Quick Reference Handbook procedures; then requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
Helicopter pilot reported a near miss during lift off with another helicopter on approach on the same helipad. Both pilots took evasive action to avoid a collision.
GA Pilot reported an inoperative VHF radio and while attempting to land at an uncontrolled airport; encountering another landing aircraft from the opposite direction.
Student Pilot reported a rough running engine after takeoff and returning in the pattern for an immediate landing.
Captain reported receiving engine overheat and fire warning system indications which resulted in return to departure airport.
C-210 pilot reported a ground strike due to the nose gear collapse during landing. Pilot stopped the aircraft on the runway.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a fume event during boarding. The rest of the flight crew also noticed the odor and fumes. After maintenance arrived; the passengers and flight crew deplaned and the aircraft was taken out of service.
GA flight instructor reported taking evasive action from traffic ATC failed to report which resulted in NMAC at DWH airport.
B737-800 Captain reported they received a hydraulic low pressure light during cruise in System A prompting the crew to divert.
Challenger 300 flight crew reported they rejected takeoff due to erratic airspeed indications. They had missed the Captain's side pitot tube cover during preflight.
GA pilot reported an NMAC at S72 non-towered airport.

Flight Crew reported being informed that maintenance had discovered a wheel bearing on the left hand nose wheel assembly had failed and damaged the axle. Flight crew reported no difficulties or unusual sounds during normal operation.

Part 107 UAS pilot reported they conducted a photo mission near a building and were approached by a public official. The pilot learned of nearby non-charted UAS prohibited areas they may possibly have entered.
PA-32 pilot reported loss of a fuel cap after takeoff.
B737-800 flight crew reported an electric stabilizer trim failure during climb. The flight crew diverted to an alternate airport with longer runways and landed overweight.
Air carrier flight crew flying B777 aircraft reported a malfunction with the pressurization system. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Cessna 152 pilot reported loss of directional control on landing roll that resulted in a runway excursion and damage to a runway light and the aircraft propeller.
Helicopter pilot reported experiencing uncommanded rising RPM after landing resulting in the pilot cutting the mixture and the engine shutting down.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a fumes event during taxi-out.

A300 First Officer reported AC BUS 1 failed during descent resulting in multiple electrical system issues including the cockpit going dark. The crew complied with applicable QRH procedures and requested ATC assistance for an expedited descent and landing.
Reporter stated a UAS had the battery changed and software rebooted after flight issues. Upon launching again the Crew was unable to maintain control of the UAS and it crashed.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a pressurization system malfunction during climb.
ERJ-175 flight crew reported executing a go-around from approach to Runway 28L at SFO after experiencing an upset from a severe wake turbulence encounter with a B747 on the parallel runway.
Light sport aircraft pilot reported the engine lost power on departure so they immediately landed. Upon landing the pilot lost control of the aircraft resulting in a ground loop and runway excursion.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. Pilots of both aircraft maneuvered to provide separation; the general aviation pilot departed the traffic pattern for a different airport.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft instructed to intercept the localizer flew through the localizer twice; once resulting in flight below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported it is difficult to receive the DFW ATIS due to ORK airport ATIS being on the same frequency.
A Center Trainee Controller reported they lost communications with an aircraft resulting in the aircraft flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737-800 Captain reported not completing the Hazmat procedure requirement to verify NOTOC prior to departure.
Air Carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation during approach due to low altimeter pressure setting.
Air Carrier Captain reported an NMAC event on extended final approach with an unknown VFR traffic. Flight received a TCAS RA; no adjustments required.
A TRACON Controller reported they descended an aircraft to an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737-800 First Officer reported a flight deck fumes event during cruise.  The crew ran the appropriate QRH checklist and continued to destination.
C402C Captain reported a loss of hydraulic fluid caused the landing gear to not extend and autopilot to not disengage; causing an air turn back.

Air carrier First officer reported confusion regarding Hazmat documents provided to the flight crew. After further collaboration with Operations personnel it was determined that it was safe to continue to destination.
Dispatcher reported releasing an aircraft to an airport that did not have a 5G AMOC for the aircraft type and did not meet weather requirements.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported 'a possible DSTG' revision during preflight load planning. Aircraft departed 'with the assumption' of no change to cargo.

A321 Captain reported being notified by the Flight Attendants of a 'dirty sock' odor during final descent. The Flight crew completed the odor isolation procedures and upon landing; submitted a logbook entry.
Air carrier Captain reported after push back from gate and prior to taxiing clear; another air carrier taxied in a dangerous and unsafe proximity to aircraft; into the same gate.

Air carrier Captain reported the EMZOH3 and OAKES2 arrivals into OAK airport result in aircraft going below a Class B shelf and at risk of violating the 200 knot airspeed restriction in that airspace.
L-16A pilot reported engine power loss after takeoff resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
Falcon 900B Captain reported one engine developed a thrust control problem; thrust control was stuck at full power. The engine was shut down at the top of descent.
Experimental Aircraft Pilot reported loss of control while taxiing with winds and gusts resulted in runway incursion.
CRJ-900 air carrier flight crew reported air conditioning unit malfunction in flight.

DA20 Flight Instructor with student reported the nose wheel separated from the aircraft during takeoff roll. Flight crew continued climbout and returned to their home airport where upon landing the nose gear collapsed.
B757-200 flight crew reported sparks emitted from the circuit breaker panel during climbout; resulting in loss of multiple systems resulting return to the departure airport.
Air Carrier flight crew reported there is no maximum tail height charted for Concourse A bridge at LGA.
Corporate jet pilot reported receiving a low-altitude alert from ATC after takeoff from VNY airport.
First Officer reported on departure fault of NAV and ADS-B possibly due to interference or jamming.

C172 pilot reported while on final approach an aircraft was taking off from the same runway. Reporter took evasive action and was informed by Tower Controller they had entered controlled airspace without a clearance.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control during pushback from gate.
Flight Crew reported Customer Service Agent opened the cabin door without a thumbs up approval from Flight Attendant.

Helicopter Instructor reported hitting a kite while climbing out from an airport. Instructor landed and found kite string wrapped around tail section and the helicopter was grounded until maintenance could inspect aircraft.
Air taxi pilot reported a UAS passed by their aircraft when approaching to land. The pilot continued the approach and landed normally.

Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS jamming that was first noticed at the departure gate in LTFM; and they later regained functionality with one GPS but not the other. GPS abnormality continued throughout flight.
Air carrier Captain reported sending the DG Acknowledgement 1 minute after brake release. Company procedure requires the Acknowledgement prior to brake release.
C172 pilot reported taxiing onto the runway at the same time another aircraft was on final approach. The landing aircraft conducted a go around to avoid a collision.
A-321 Captain reported a loss of one of the hydraulic systems during climb. The flight returned to the departure airport and performed a safe overweight landing.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a switch in the incorrect position caused a flight malfunction.
Air carrier flight crew reported an ATC loss of separation while following an aircraft on a visual approach. ATC directed a go around and the flight returned to land.

Ramp employee reported observing multiple procedural and safety issues of other ramp employees operating ground equipment. Reporter expressed concern that a new hire ramp employee was allegedly training new hires and may not have the operational experience to do so.
B787 Flight Attendant reported a DOT violation due to no operative lavatories with passengers on board.

Air carrier Captain reported during initial climb the Captain's side radio altimeter displayed a negative value and the flight director did not indicate correct bank angle or pitch attitude. Captain cited 5G interference as possibly contributing to the event.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger forgot to remove Lithium Ion batteries from a gate checked bag prior to gate push back. Ground Operations removed the bag from the cargo bin.
A Flight Instructor reported a small aircraft taxiing at a high rate of speed shouted a warning to them as it passed by them without warning from ATC.
A321 flight crew reported multiple failures of redundant systems which caused the aircraft to revert to alternate law. The flight crew diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
Technicians reported a scissor lift contacted the forward cargo door during lock out tag out procedures; resulting in damage to the aircraft.
A321 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing SLC in trail of a B757. Captain suggested that increased separation should be used behind the B757 type aircraft.
Flight Instructor reported a loss of engine rpm on takeoff. Instructor immediately returned to runway and made a safe landing.
Corporate jet pilot reported a near midair collision with a UAS while on final approach. The pilot landed safely and reported the UAS to ATC.
UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace with an incorrect ATC squawk code.
Air Taxi First Officer reported not recognizing they had descended below final approach altitude. The flight crew returned to the correct altitude and continued to landing.

B757 Captain reported during cruise flight the ECIAS SPOILER message displayed. There was a violent vibration confirming at least one spoiler was extended. The pilots were given priority handling from ATC; ran the QRH; and made an uneventful landing.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC when the aircraft descended before being established on the final approach course. Captain conducted a go-around and completed approach.
Flight Attendant in Charge; along with two other flight attendants; reported a fume event resulting in physiological symptoms. Three flight attendants were evaluated as unfit to fly.

CRJ-200 Flight Attendant reported receiving a command from the Captain to prepare the cabin for a return to the origin airport. The Captain explained there was a landing gear mechanical issue that required a return to the airport.
Air carrier Captain reported departing the gate before receiving the required DG documents. The documentation was eventually received and confirmation was sent to dispatch prior to departure.
A TRACON Controller reported two aircraft inbound to an airport switched to Tower frequency on their own so the controller could not issue appropriate arrival instructions resulting in a NMAC.

Ground personnel reported that the aircraft flight crew failed to stop taxiing to the gate after being signaled multiple times by marshallers to stop. Flight crew eventually stopped at appropriate position.
C680A pilot reported a malfunction in the landing gear indications so they flew by the Tower for gear down confirmation and landed.

B737 Captain reported during taxi an aircraft at a gate was pushed back toward them requiring abrupt braking to avoid a collision. As a result; a flight attendant fell and hit their head and the flight returned to the gate.
A320 Captain reported pressurization pack failed which resulted in a decision to return to the departure airport.
Ramp Agent reported a belt loader was left inside the safety area when an arriving aircraft began to taxi into the gate.

B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported on arrival at aircraft; a signed off discrepancy stating that the #1 engine auto shut down on start three times. The flight crew questioned the sign off and inspected the engine finding excessive coking present to the engine exhaust vent tubes. Maintenance acquired an engineering waiver to operate the aircraft and the flight crew departed. On a later flight; the Captain found excessive coking on another aircraft and that aircraft was removed from service. This led the Captain to question the validity of the previous aircraft's engineering waiver.

Student pilot with instructor reported a near miss in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport with an aircraft that entered the pattern on top of them. Student and instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier flight crew reported a conflict with a vehicle during taxi-in at ATL airport. The vehicle nearly hit the aircraft's Number 2 engine before the Captain stopped the aircraft.
B737 pilot reported the aircraft rolled violently left 20 degrees during cruise; followed by a slight Dutch roll.

Air carrier Captain reported while on short final observing a vehicle cross the runway under them resulting in a runway incursion. The Captain completed the landing and reported the incident to the Tower.
Air carrier flight crew reported during descent encountering moderate turbulence resulting in an un-commanded right roll; the flight crew regained control of the aircraft and landed safely.
B737-800 Captain reported an engine malfunction during climb. The crew reduced power on the engine and returned to the departure airport; and landed.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported rejecting the takeoff after experiencing directional control issues on initial power application for unknown reasons.
B737 Captain reported a rejected takeoff after receiving a Master Caution for Cowl Anti-Ice at approximately 90 knots. Crew returned to the gate after consulting with maintenance.
C208 Captain reported encountering mountain wave action which resulted in momentary loss of control and altitude excursion.
Ramp agent reported a near collision between the jet bridge preconditioned air unit and aircraft during pushback.
Mooney M20 pilot reported loss of electrical power due to a generator failure during cruise. Pilot was able to continue to destination airport and land safely.
BE300 Pilot reported failure to report airport in sight in descent; resulted in ATC issuing a low altitude alert and a CFTT event.
PA-28 Archer instructor reported while lining up for takeoff; sighting another aircraft landing on the opposite runway without communications.
Center Trainee Controllers reported an aircraft had an engine malfunction resulting in a loss of separation with an aircraft flying below.
A319 flight crew reported nose wheel steering problem during landing roll.

Beechcraft pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing resulting in a runway excursion.  Aircraft veered off the runway due to windy gusting conditions with no damage of any kind or injuries.
Flight Instructor reported NMAC with aircraft in the traffic pattern of JZP airport.

Air carrier pilot reported during initial climb an object passed across the windscreen and collided with the aircraft. The flight crew returned to land at departure airport and believe they struck a UAS while in flight.
A Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during initial climb with opposite direction landing aircraft. Flight Instructor stated the landing aircraft provided inaccurate position radio calls.
A319 Flight Crew reported soon after takeoff the aircraft alerted a VENT SKIN VALVE FAULT. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.

EMB-170 Captain reported a critical ground conflict while initiating runway crossing clearance at the same time Tower cleared a 'position and hold' aircraft take-off clearance. Captain stopped immediately avoiding a collision.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a taxi deviation due to poor marking and signage on this taxiway in LAS airport.
A TRACON Controller reported an NMAC event during a heavy traffic period.  Controller stated being short staffed during these heavy traffic periods contributed to the event.

Cessna 172 student pilot reported a loss of aircraft control on a taxiway when a thunderstorm moved over the airport. The extreme winds caused the airplane to tip forward onto the propeller and wingtip. The pilot contacted ATC and was safely evacuated.
A321 Captain reported an autoflight system malfunction resulted in the aircraft entering a steep climb while on final approach. Flight crew performed a go-around.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported a Nose Wheel Steering INOP caution message after the landing gear were selected down on final. The flight crew performed a go-around and ran the QRH and checklists. Priority handling was requested and the flight crew made a precautionary landing at destination airport.

BE-300 Captain reported oil uncontrollable temperature on the left engine during cruise. The flight crew descended and slowed the aircraft; allowing time for oil temperature to decrease and continue to destination airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a conflict after landing at SFO airport requiring the Captain to immediately stop the aircraft.  The conflicting aircraft was sent on a go-around.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during initial climb. Flight crew dawned oxygen mask and executed a safe diversion.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported an anomaly with the braking system during the landing roll; resulting in a momentary loss of directional control.

Flight crew reported #1 Engine failure on take off. The flight crew performed an in flight shut down and feathered the #1 propeller. The flight crew made an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a pressurization problem inflight. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.

B777 flight crew reported a fuel quantity indication malfunction which was treated as a fuel leak during cruise flight in oceanic airspace. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed; maintenance engine run ups did not finding indications of a fuel leak.

BNA Tower Controller reported an aircraft stopped on the runway after confusing two unmarked access roads for possible taxiways causing another aircraft on final approach to go around. The Controller stated this is a common occurrence with these unmarked access roads.
C680A First Officer reported uncommanded aircraft movement pre-flight during passenger briefing requiring the reporter to rush to the flight deck and apply brakes.

A military Flight Instructor reported during initial climb TRACON assigned them a heading which turned them into a commercial aircraft departing the parallel runway. The reporter took evasive action and reported a NMAC.
B757 flight crew reported pressurization system malfunction during climb. Flight returned to departure airport and landed safely.

PA-32 pilot reported a pitot/static malfunction on the takeoff roll; resulting in no airspeed during the climb. The aircraft returned to the departure airport and landed hard which caused a tire failure requiring a tow to the parking spot.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event upon arrival at the aircraft. Aircraft logbook had an open discrepancy by previous flight crew who had rejected the aircraft due to a fume event.
Pilot reported taxiing on to the runway and taking off in front of an aircraft on final approach that was forced to execute a go-around to avoid a collision.
EMB-175 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence shortly after takeoff from CLT in trail of an A321.
B737-800 First Officer reported an unstable approach and go-around.
BE C90 crew reported a taxiway excursion during transfer of controls to Co-pilot. Captain was distracted and Co-pilot's seat was not properly positioned to allow for full access to rudder pedals.
A Flight Instructor completing the procedure turn on an RNAV approach reported they took evasive action to avoid another aircraft a mile outside of Class D airspace.

Dash 8 flight crew reported a flap malfunction on short final. The crew reported the fuel amount company dispatched them with did not give them time to sufficiently troubleshoot the issue or conduct a missed approach.
Flight Instructor reported the engine shut down by itself while they were holding short for takeoff.
A TRACON Controller reported they misread the flight strip of a departing aircraft resulting in the aircraft flying below the minimum vectoring altitude.

Air Carrier Captain reported a miscommunication with ground crew during push back led to the ground crew disconnecting the tug prior to the pilot setting the brakes causing the plane to roll about two feet.

Pilot flying C-402 aircraft after recent maintenance work on the aileron control cable reported flight control anomaly during climb out. Pilot returned to departure airport and returned plane for maintenance.
Pilots reported losing control of the aircraft after landing and sliding off the runway.

Air carrier flight crew reported that while in cruise; the flight experienced severe turbulence resulting in a gain and loss of altitude and airspeed.  The flight crew was able to regain control of the aircraft and landed safely at their destination.
A Powered Paraglider pilot reported they did not take sufficient actions to prevent an airborne conflict with a known skydiving aircraft operating in the same airspace.
Pilot reported a near miss with an aircraft while transitioning tower controlled airspace in visual conditions. The pilot descended and passed the aircraft and continued to destination.
GA pilot reported a near miss during cruise with an opposite direction aircraft. Pilot took evasive action.
Embraer Legacy Captain reported windshear encounter resulted an overbanked situation with altitude loss.
EMB-505 First Officer reported an Auto Pressurization Fail annunciation just after takeoff.  Crew elected to return to departure airport for a normal landing.
Pilot reported mistakenly lining up for the wrong runway on final approach causing a NMAC with an aircraft already in the pattern.

Flight Instructor and student reported the student deviated from assigned altitude resulting in a low altitude alert from ATC; failed to perform the procedure turn on approaches; did not fly the published missed approach procedure and the student would not relinquish aircraft controls to the Instructor.

Cirrus SF50 pilot reported both fuel flow valves failed during departure. Due to concern with possible fuel imbalance; the pilot elected to return to the departure airport where a safe landing was made.
CR70 flight crew reported an unstabilized approach; terrain alert; and go-around.
Fractional First Officer reported an unstable approach leading to a sink rate call by the aircraft.

B757 flight crew reported after takeoff; when the flap handle was moved from 20 to 5; received an EICAS TE FLAP ASYMMETRY Message. The flight crew ran the check lists and QRH. After consulting with Dispatch and Maintenance; priority handling was requested. The flight crew returned to the departure airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported FLL taxiway signage is confusing and could be improved.
Student pilot with instructor reported an NMAC with an agricultural aircraft that gave an inaccurate position in the landing pattern at PVB; a non-towered airport.
EMB-145 First Officer reported receiving a terrain warning while crossing a ridge during final approach. Flight crew corrected altitude and continued to landing.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss on final approach at a non-towered airport; when another aircraft departed opposite direction during a training flight. The instructor took control and maneuvered away from the other aircraft.
C172 Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft flying overhead the field while on the missed approach procedure to an airport.
A319 Flight Attendant reported a fume event during climb reoccurred during descent resulting in health issues.
HS-125 First Officer reported they had communication issues with ATC while in cruise flight that resulted in an altitude excursion.
B737 Captain reported experiencing disagreement and resistance from other departments after refusing an aircraft for an uncorrected hydraulic system leak.

Tower Front Line Manager and Tower Controller reported a conflict between a landing aircraft and an aircraft getting ready for takeoff.  The Controller stated the landing aircraft was not responsive to communications which contributed to the event. The Manager and Controller recommended they re-institute pilot safety briefings to minimize similar events from occurring.
An air carrier flight crew reported a NMAC with a VFR aircraft on short final which resulted in a go-around.
A320 Captain reported making an aggressive stop to avoid a collision with a truck approaching at high speed on a surface road.

B767 flight crew reported a temporary loss of control and a momentary 'stick shaker' activation due to encountering moderate turbulence. Passengers reported injuries some of whom were not wearing seat belts.

Agriculture pilot reported they were informed by a company pilot of an airborne conflict with a UAS while both were performing aerial applications. Both aircraft were operating in and around the same fields.
General aviation pilot reported receiving an audible terrain warning during approach; resulting in a missed approach.
C182 pilot reported electrical failure resulting in no flaps and having no radio communications.  The pilot was able to safely land and saw light signals after landing.
Ground personnel reported a critical ground conflict occurred during push back when an aircraft inbound to a nearby gate taxied into the alley requiring the push crew to stop to avoid a collision.
BE90 Captain reported excessive exhaust gas temperatures indicated during takeoff and aborting the takeoff.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a bird strike during landing. Flight crew executed a missed approach and returned for a safe landing.
Air carrier Captain reported there are two L1 taxiway markings to the gate area in EWR.

B-747 air carrier flight crew reported an electrical malfunction which affected other systems during climb including a stick shaker. The crew recovered from the aircraft malfunctions and continued to destination.

CRJ-900 flight crew reported an EICAS caution for flaps half speed MEL appeared around 10000 ft. on climb out. While complying with the QRH; the autopilot was disconnected; which resulted in an abrupt nose up pitch of the aircraft. The flight crew regained control of the aircraft elected to divert to make a precautionary landing.

Technician reported ambiguity in MEL process during trouble shooting of a thrust reverser unlocked indication. The MEL did not specifically prohibit dispatch with an unlocked indication present with the thrust reverser locked out.

A TRACON Controller reported they did not realize an aircraft read back their descent clearance incorrectly and the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude. The Controller reported their Low Altitude Alert never activated.
Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict when a baggage tug passed in front of the taxing jet and taking quick action to maintain separation.
Air carrier technician reported finding a B767-300 tire with a flat spot with a history of several tire changes. During troubleshooting and further inspection; they found the hydraulic lines crossed.

Air carrier flight crew reported a smoke event with associated air conditioning pack EICAS message while parked at the gate and preparing for push back. The Captain ordered an evacuation through the main cabin door onto the ramp; with no injuries reported.

DG-1000 sail plane pilot reported losing control of the aircraft upon landing after the instructor who was sitting in the rear seat; unaware of the open spoiler position; pulled the spoiler. The reporter suggested that the aircraft should have markings or placards to indicate the position of the spoiler control lever.

Flight crew reported they failed to meet a crossing restriction while descending on a new arrival into TPA after misinterpreting the crossing restrictions and ATC clearance. Issue was further complicated by the use of the same fixes across multiple arrivals.
C172 student pilot reported a runway excursion during landing.

Air carrier First Officer reported GPS jamming during cruise in an area known for jamming; but the GPS did not recover when exiting the area of GPS jamming. The flight coordinated with ATC for a clearance with the reduced navigational capability in oceanic airspace.
An experimental aircraft pilot reported a hard landing caused the landing gear to collapse resulting in a prop strike and runway excursion.
B737 Pilot reported an abnormal left engine EGT indication led to the decision to return to the departure airport where an overweight landing was made.

Air carrier flight crew reported the First Officer was hit with a laser strike during approach to EWR airport. The First Officer experienced strained vision; but was able to continue without further issue.
Airbus 321 pilot reported an engine malfunction at cruise under ATC control. The crew returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported upon destination arrival that the Captain's NOTOC copy was discovered missing. After extensive investigation the missing document was not located.
Pilot reported a diversion caused by additional NAV systems inoperative and not addressed in an MEL applied to a GPS system.
EMB145 Captain reported loss of aircraft control from a turbulence encounter during cruise at FL330. Crew recovered aircraft control and continued to destination with no injuries or damage reported.
PA28 pilot reported high oil temperature and diverted to a nearby airport.
A319 flight crew reported hydraulic system malfunction after takeoff.
Part 107 pilot reported using meters instead of feet to determine altitude leading to a clearance violation.  The pilot recognized the error and descended to appropriate altitude.
B737-800 flight crew reported loss of aircraft control due to severe turbulence. After the Captain regained aircraft control the flight continued to destination with no injuries reported.
Pilot reported inadvertently taxiing into a restricted ramp area at STJ and suggested that clear surface markings and lights should be put in place to prevent other similar incursions.
C90 TRACON Controller reported there is a recurring equipment issue with the frequency 125.0 that is repeatedly reported about but nothing is done to fix the issue.
Air Carrier First Officer reported poor lighting for taxiway signage resulted in a taxiway incursion at IND airport.
Air carrier Captain reported they had to aggressively brake to avoid a collision with a truck on the ramp at CVG.
DA40 pilot reported a bounced landing resulting in a prop strike.
B757 pilot reported engine malfunction in cruise flight. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Student pilot reported a near miss with an aircraft at a non-towered airport. Student pilot exited the pattern to avoid the conflict; then returned for a landing.
Air carrier pilot crew reported the taxi instructions given by the CLT ramp controller do not coincide with the instructions printed on the EFB chart.

Air carrier flight crew reported the loss of aircraft pressurization during climb. The QRH procedures were completed and received clearance to departure airport where a safe overweight landing was completed.
A320 pilot reported a hydraulic system ECAM during cruise. Flight diverted and landed.
Flight Crew reported icy conditions; blowing snow; and gusty wind resulted in a taxiway excursion.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported freight was inadequately packaged and incorrectly configured for transport. One of the three drums which contained unknown liquid leaked during transit.
Super King Air 200 pilot reported the main entry door opened about one quarter of an inch at its top during climb. The Captain returned to the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported during gate arrival several service vehicles drove quickly in front of the aircraft; requiring flight crew to brake to avoid collisions.
Pilot reported that his number two aircraft broke off the wrong way and was directed by ATC to correct his heading to avoid a collision with another aircraft.

Light Transport aircraft Captain reported ATC issued a low altitude warning during visual approach to EKO airport. Captain replied that airport and terrain in sight. Flight executed an uneventful landing.
Instructor Pilot reported loss of situational awareness led to a near mid air collision when another aircraft that had announced their intentions and was encountered.
A319 flight crew reported a fumes event during cruise resulting in a diversion.
C182 pilot reported they descended due to a NMAC encounter and then received terrain and obstruction alerts before climbing back to an appropriate altitude.
Air Carrier First Officer reported fatigue related concerns with the scheduling of particular international flights since the COVID-19 pandemic began.
C210 pilot reported engine power loss on climb-out and elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing.

Metro aircraft pilot reported a landing gear unsafe indication on approach. The pilot ran the QRH procedures; but was unable to get a safe landing gear indication. The pilot requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
A TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft on vectors was issued a climb clearance but descended and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Captain reported failure of the #1 Rudder Travel Throw Limiter System in flight with the #2 system already deferred causing the Captain to question the airworthiness of the aircraft.
B737 Captain reported while taxiing out from ramp a baggage cart failed to stop resulting in a prompt stop to avoid the ground conflict.
TRACON Controller reported that Center handed an aircraft off to them that was below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
GA Flight Instructor reported an NMAC during final approach to DED non-towered airport after an aircraft crossed the runway hold short line before stopping.
Air carrier Captain reported procedure disagreements between the FMC and the chart exist on the PVR OBRUG 1B Arrival.
Air Carrier First Officer (PM) reported a CFIT event during night visual approach. The flight completed an uneventful landing after re-establishing stable approach criteria at 1;000 ft.
Flight Attendant reported several passengers would not comply with the face mask policy during boarding.
Flight Instructor reported student flying SR20 aircraft encountered possible fuel leak inflight.
E-190 pilot reported electrical system failures resulting in numerous system malfunctions while on approach to land. Crew landed after performing checklist procedures.

Air carrier flight crew reported that during takeoff roll the aircraft veered to the left. When the pilot flying applied right rudder to correct; the aircraft continued turning left. Post-flight it was determined that a nose wheel steering component had been incorrectly installed causing inverse steering commands.
Single pilot reported a NMAC with a fire fighting aircraft in the vicinity of a fire.
G280 flight crew reported a stabilizer trim malfunction in cruise. The crew diverted and landed without incident.
M20M pilot reported experiencing a rapid accumulation of unexpected icing despite icing equipment and reported other equipment failures; which resulted in deviation.
A321 Technician pushback crew reported they heard a loud noise; stopped the tug; and determined the tug cradle either malfunctioned or was improperly operated.

Flight crew reported a loss of situational awareness led to an attempt to taxi without disconnecting from the tug and tow bar. The Captain applied brakes; then reset the parking brake; and directed the push crew to disconnect and clear for taxi.
Gulfstream G-V flight crew reported an engine failure in cruise flight. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict with a service truck during taxi out from gate.
Flight Crew reported #1 engine became unresponsive to thrust lever movement in flight. The flight crew continued to landing with the engine at idle.
Flight Instructor reported their student pilot taking evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft that was not on the practice area frequency.
Air carrier flight crew reported a low altitude alert from ATC and CFTT event; and cited distraction and incorrect mode and altitude entry in FMC as contributing.

BE20 flight crew reported the left engine's performance was deteriorating inflight and the Captain; believing there to be possible fuel contamination; performed an inflight engine shutdown and restart. The right engine began to have abnormal operations after the left engine began to operate normally; and the flight crew opted to divert. After landing; Maintenance found a contamination in the fuel and filters that was determined to be the cause.

Air Traffic Controller reported aircraft unable to climb due to experiencing a TCAS RA resulting in the aircraft flying below the MVA. ATC issued vectors to the aircraft to be sequenced to the airport.
B737 Captain reported deviating and exceeding 250 kt. below 10;000 ft. after departing LAX following a wake turbulence encounter.

C402 Pilot reported the left main landing gear failed during taxi for take off. The aircraft was removed from the runway with the help of airport operations and the aircraft taken back to the ramp for maintenance.
Cessna 152 pilot reported a conflict with a King Air aircraft under tow while taxiing into the ramp area requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
Air carrier Captain reported realizing during pushback the flight crew forgot to send out of the DG acknowledgement code and sent it out immediately.
CRJ-700 pilot reported the tug driver had to suddenly slam on the brakes to avoid a collision with a vehicle moving behind the aircraft.
Air carrier pilot reported an NMAC with a UAS during descent. No evasive action was taken.
General aviation pilot reported scraping a wingtip during landing due to inadequate crosswind correction.
Learjet 45 pilot reported cockpit windshield shattered after level off. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Pilot reported a loss of power in flight requiring a forced off field landing in which the nose landing gear failed. Minimal aircraft damage and no injuries were reported.
Air carrier personnel reported that an engine oil filter bypass light illuminated on taxi and took off with an open log item.
EMB-505 Captain reported being unable to execute the required climb rate while trying to accomplish an RA maneuver; as directed by the TCAS system.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a ground proximity warning while conducting a visual approach.
TRACON and Tower Controllers reported confusion of aircraft assigned altitude on a through IFR clearance and automation status resulted in altitude below the MVA and a CFTT event.

Air carrier flight crew and Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during cruise on the flight deck. The flight crew completed the appropriate QRH procedures and requested priority handling to a suitable diversion airport where the aircraft was removed from service.
B777 flight crew reported low engine oil quantity in right engine and a generator fault during cruise. Flight crew diverted to suitable airport and landed safely.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the route received through CPDLC did not mirror the ATC route.
Air carrier First Officer reported Hydraulic System failure and diversion to nearest suitable airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event characterized as a 'sweet; musky odor' during descent. After gate arrival the cabin crew reported the odor was emanating from the cockpit.
C150 Pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft departing an intersecting runway while on short final landing at a non-towered airport.
B737-800 pilot flying reported encountering light wake turbulence while on initial approach and coupled with the timing of the flap selection during the glideslope intercept; led to an overspeed.
Flight Crew reported that the Flight Attendants reported an aft galley oven fire and were able to successfully extinguish the fire; allowing the flight to land safely at destination airport.
C210 pilot reported weak left brake resulted in taxiway excursion and loss of aircraft control on ground.
C182 pilot reported that a strong gust of wind during touchdown nearly flipped the airplane. The pilot regained aircraft control and the aircraft sustained minor damage. No injuries occurred.
B737-800 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing leading edge flap issues.
Citation Captain reported the FMS did not fly the departure procedure even though the crew verified all the data was correct prior to departure.
MD-11 air carrier flight crew reported a fuel quantity EICAS message during cruise flight. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed with no fuel leak observed.

Air carrier Captain reported excessive rubber build-up in the SAN Runway 27 touchdown zone created a reflective surface that was very disorienting and made it difficult in determining how high the aircraft was off the runway. The Captain recommended the airport remove the excess rubber build-up on a more regular basis to mitigate this hazard.
Air carrier flight crew reported unstable approach and bounced firm landing.
C172 Student Pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft while in the traffic pattern.
Air carrier Captain reported; while on a 5 mile final with the autopilot on; the glide slope became erratic; the aircraft pitched down and the EGPWS issued a terrain warning.
Pilot reported a lack of taxiway markings as well as difficult to see runway markings at the LHW Airport.

A319 flight crew reported while flying a visual approach descending early resulting in flight towards terrain. ATC issued a low altitude alert and the flight crew climbed to a safe altitude and continued the approach to the airport.
A321 First Officer reported a loud bang occurred during the climb-out and the number one engine began to have abnormal indications. The flight crew then performed an air turnback.
Fractional company pilot reported they descended below their assigned altitude and received a low altitude alert from ATC.

Air carrier Cargo Load Crew Chief reported undeclared Hazmat cargo transported. The cause was a communications breakdown between Load Crew Chief and loading crew which was discovered after aircraft departure.
C172 pilot reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on final approach from a nearby Chinook helicopter.
Citation pilot reported a vibration on the side of the aircraft during descent and postflight found a portion of an engine cowling had partially separated.
Helicopter pilot reports Air Traffic Control did not have proper procedure.
Air carrier Ramp Agent reported being contaminated by a parcel containing HAZMAT that resulted in physiological symptoms that then led to hospital evaluation.

Cessna 172 Instructor with a trainee reported the loss of power and engine roughness while practicing flight maneuvers. The instructor contacted ATC through a nearby ATC Tower; squawked 7700 and landed with help from Approach Control.
Navion pilot reported an engine malfunction during climb. The pilot landed the aircraft without injuries.
ZID Controller reported a fixed RADAR target generated shadowed code and altitude information to nearby aircraft resulted in false conflict alerts with unreliable RADAR information.
EA50 pilot reported engine cowling delamination.
Two Tower Controllers reported the Local Controller allowed an aircraft to land with only 400 feet of spacing behind a departure.

Two Center Controllers and an Operations Manager reported an aircraft on approach was not in communication due to being issued a frequency which was out of service. The aircraft descended below the Minimum IFR Altitude and was in conflict with an adjacent airport departure and other VFR aircraft in its path. The Center assumed airspace from the TRACON due to TRACON radio site outage even though the Center Controllers had no training on the procedures for the airspace.
C172 Pilot reported another aircraft cut them and another aircraft off while in the final approach phase.
Flight Crew reported both engines began to surge on take off and elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Utility Pilot reported oil pressure drop resulted in ATC expeditious handling to safe landing to a nearby airport.
B737 flight crew reported flight attendants informed them of an electrical burning odor in the cabin during cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
A CMH TRACON Controller reported a frequency published for AOH airport (121.82) is not available for TRACON Controllers to use; nor have they been briefed about it.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported receiving a failure message which required an immediate landing of the UAS.
ZKC Center Controller reported a MIA/MVA violation with an aircraft.
A321 flight crew reported they were unable to continue the taxi out when their brakes locked for unknown reasons. Passengers were deplaned and bussed to the terminal.

A pilot reported they did not receive the obstacle clearance departure procedure or a SID from ATC or the Foreflight App. The reporter suggested obstacle departure procedures [ODP] automatically populate on the Foreflight software.
B757 Captain reported not receiving the final dangerous goods report and after some research; was verbally notified that there were no dangerous goods on board.
General aviation flight instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft while on final approach. The instructor took control from the student and maneuvered to avoid a collision.
General aviation pilot reported that security needs to be improved at PABV/BCV Airport; with multiple open gates providing unrestricted access to the field.
GA pilot reported a loss of aircraft control during landing; resulting in a runway excursion and aircraft damage.
Air carrier Captain reported they were notified in flight the DG summary was not sent or acknowledged before departure.

B737-700 flight crew reported being informed post flight that they had struck a temporary taxi light during taxi out from their departure airport.  The crew stated they did not feel or sense anything abnormal during the taxi out.

Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion and ground conflict resulted from receiving ATC taxi instructions during a high workload landing roll and not being able to clarify with Ground Control.
Dispatcher reported the Number 2 engine flamed out due fuel starvation caused by failure to complete the fuel manual checklist.
Air carrier Captain reported an aircraft issue during taxi out that resulted in the aircraft not being useable for the flight.
Air carrier pilot reported unable to maintain altitude for several minutes due to encountering downdrafts and turbulence.

Air carrier flight crew reported a possible runway incursion at ORD airport. While taxiing on a snow covered taxiway and no lights indicating a hold short position; the incursion may have taken place. The pilots reported the Jeppesen airport chart does not show an accurate location for hold short lines.
C180 pilot reported observing an NMAC between two other aircraft in the pattern resulting in one aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
B777 pilot reported smoke in the cockpit requiring oxygen masks to be donned. Flight crew performed inflight engine shutdown and diverted to suitable airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported refusing the assigned aircraft due to three labor intensive MEL's on aircraft. The Captain 'felt the workload and distractions were too great'.
C172 student pilot reported losing control of the aircraft due to not using enough right rudder while trying to depart from a touch and go.
Aircraft First Officer reported an ATC failure to provide traffic separation that resulted in a NMAC.
EMB-145 flight crew reported they went below glideslope during approach; resulting in a 'Don't Sink' aural warning and subsequent go-around to landing.
EMB-145 F/irst Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to CLT that caused a 'hard roll' and disconnected the autopilot.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported undeclared Hazmat transported.

Air carrier flight crew reported a NMAC while taking off. The crew was cleared for takeoff while another aircraft was cleared to land on an intersecting runway. The tower controller; realizing the mistake; instructed the landing aircraft to go-around. As the landing aircraft commenced go-around the air carrier crew reported they flew 300 feet under the go-around aircraft.
B777 Captain reported ARFF told the flight crew after gate arrival there was a wheel fire. An ARFF truck sprayed water on the wheel and extinguished the fire.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported briefly flying above 400 feet AGL while the UAS was in an automatic RTH. Upon realizing the altitude the pilot took manual control and descended the UAS.
Air carrier Captain reported a flap disagree during climb. Flight continued to destination airport and made a zero flap landing.

Air Carrier First Officer reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of a 'burning odor' in the cabin during initial climb. Flight crew requested special handling to the departure airport. A safe landing was executed followed by the aircraft taxi to gate for an aircraft swap.
A320 Captain reported a new hire pilot failed to flare soon enough resulting in a hard landing.
Student Pilot on training flight reported NMAC with helicopter during takeoff roll.
Student pilot reported they lost control of the aircraft while landing in gusty winds and veered off the runway into the grass.

ORL Local Tower Controller reported two instances of traffic conflictions due to confusing chart depictions and procedures for aircraft arriving and departing via the south downwind of Runway 07/25. The reporter stated this is a recurring 'unsafe' occurrence at ORL.
Air carrier pilots reported during climb the EICAS showed a #2 engine vane fault. The engine began to produce engine stalls with each throttle movement. The crew returned to the departure airport.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported a passenger brought a motorized electric tricycle on board.
Mooney instructor pilot reported the gear not extended for landing.
Small aircraft pilot reported feeling too low on approach despite altimeter readings; and realized after safely landing that the altimeter was improperly set during the initial run-up.

A Flight School Instructor reported a fuel servo failure resulting in engine failure and return to departure airport. Reporter stated this flight school's aircraft have repeatedly experienced this problem.
A 737-800 pilot reported a failure of the electric pitch trim system during initial climb; resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
Air carrier Dispatch reported confusion with the MDE airport NOTAMs that were issued for a pothole located near the threshold of Runway 1.
Helicopter Pilot reported a NMAC with another helicopter on approach while practicing auto-rotations.

Technician reported omitting the re-installation of trailing edge flap access panels during routine maintenance. The aircraft suffered control ability issues on a subsequent flight and the missing panels were reinstalled.
Air carrier Captain reported that during boarding a family was not compliant with face mask policy. After other passengers expressed concern; the Captain had the family removed from the flight.
C172 student pilot reported a NMAC on final approach and intermittent communications with ATC tower controller.

Air carrier Captain reported being notified by Flight Attendant of a fume event during descent. After gate arrival Captain entered a fume event in the AML; submitted a fume report; and notified appropriate personnel.
