Pilot reported a portion of Runway 07-25 at PKMJ/MAJ airport was changed to an ungrooved designation affecting braking; and not identified as ungrooved in the NOTAM system.
Flight Attendant reported possible 5G interference with the aircraft from passengers not setting phones to aircraft mode as instructed.
Center Controller reported a VFR aircraft unexpectedly turned into the path of a climbing air carrier resulting in a NMAC.
Pilot reported an airborne conflict with a banner tow aircraft during takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported the step-down altitudes between the FMS and iPad (FliteDeck Pro) for the IAH LINKK1 RNAV arrival.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported Lightning strike during climb.
Pilot reported engine failure during cruise descent and executed a safe landing in a field.

C210 pilot reported an alternator failure occurred during a test flight that led to lost communications. Some of the communications returned but out of caution the landing gear was manually retracted for landing.
B747 flight crew reported a flap drive EICAS alert during approach. The flight crew completed the QRH procedures and landed safely.
B737 First Officer reported speed and altitude deviations on arrival into DEN; citing distraction from a wake turbulence encounter as contributing.
An Air Carrier flight crew reported they received a Ground Proximity warning after initiating a go around.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving an aircraft generated altitude and terrain alert.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude alert from ATC while on approach to AMA airport.
A Flight Instructor observing their solo student land reported the student lost control of the aircraft while landing resulting in a prop strike and wingtip damage.
Air carrier pilot reported during initial approach they had a loss of air speed with a stick shaker. They were unstable on their approach due to encountering wind-shear.
B737 First Officer reported engine failure on takeoff. The flight crew rejected the take-off and returned to the gate for maintenance action.
Air carrier Captain reported it is unclear if the pavement west of Runway 8R at ELP is designated as a displaced threshold. There are no markings on the pavement or airport chart.

Tower Local Controller reported a pre-coordinated UAS flight deviated from its published procedure into the path of air carrier traffic on final approach; requiring the Controller to direct a go-around to avoid the UAS.
B737-800 flight crew reported a ground conflict during taxi-out. The crew took evasive while the reckless driver sped away with no information of the vehicle.
Government UAS pilot reported they lost control of the UAS after launch. The reporter believes a GPS error caused the loss of control.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a UAS while in cruise flight. The reporter stated they did not take evasive action and contacted the TRACON upon arrival.
B737 MAX Captain reported a loss of the B hydraulic system during cruise. The Captain diverted to an alternate airport and landed the aircraft safely.
C172 Flight Instructor with student reported low voltage indications during cruise. The flight crew diverted and made a precautionary landing.

Fractional jet Captain reported a conflict with another aircraft while on final approach at a non-towered airport; when the other aircraft started a takeoff roll on the opposite direction runway; without communication. The Captain performed a go-around; then returned for a visual approach.
Pilot reported encountering engine issues during initial climb and immediately returned to the airport where it was discovered the aircraft had been mistakenly fueled with Jet-A.
Pilot reported understaffed and overworked controller may have caused a traffic alert; rejected takeoff; and long delay for the next takeoff at FXE.
UAS flight crew member reported GPS interference errors with several UAS during the flight and chose to land all of the UAS.

Pilot reported having multiple TCAS RA's while attempting to land at a non towered airport resulting in the crew taking evasive action to avoid a collision with other aircraft.  The aircrew was able to land safely after multiple approaches to the airport.
TRACON Controller reported an IFR pop up aircraft was given an IFR altitude resulting in an airborne conflict with an IFR departure.
Flight crew reported the need for an FOM revision to clarify the transport of smart bags with a non-removable battery in passenger cabin.
B737 Captain reported a temporary loss of directional control while taxiing on a wet surface. The Captain was able to keep the aircraft on the runway in spite of the  locked brakes.

B737 Captain reported an engine surge during climb from an engine that had been written up on the previous flight.  Crew reduced thrust until surge/vibrations stopped and returned to the departure airport.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported NOTAMS were not updating through the company's system.
Air carrier First Officer reported a CFIT event during approach under Approach Control which was followed by an uneventful visual landing.
General aviation student pilot reported a runway excursion due to improper rudder inputs during landing. The aircraft was not damaged and no injuries were reported.
C-172 Student pilot reported runway excursion on landing rollout.
B-737 air carrier crew reported a pressurization malfunction at cruise before entering oceanic airspace. The crew returned to an alternate airport and landed safely.

PA-28 Instructor Pilot reported during landing losing the right brake resulting in a runway excursion. The Flight Instructor was able to taxi back onto the runway and exit the runway. The Flight Instructor departed the airport to return to their home airport without incident.
A321 Captain reported an engine bleed malfunction during the climb on an ETOPS flight. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed overweight.
First Officer reported #1 Engine loss of oil quantity followed by loss of oil pressure. The flight crew performed an in flight shut down of the engine and continued to the destination airport.
Champion pilot reported a significant engine performance loss during initial departure climb. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
Technician reported that they inadvertently deployed a door slide while repairing a cabin conditioned air gasper that had fallen out.

Captain reported receiving a Yellow Master Caution and a R-BLD AIR OVERHEAT Message on  take off. The Flight Crew reduced power on the #1 Engine and diverted to make a precautionary landing. The engine was shut down on approach and ARFF equipment was requested.

Air carrier flight attendants reported a fume event during initial descent in which the flight crew and passengers experienced physiological symptoms. Upon arrival the aircraft was removed from service and after an extensive delay the flight attendants were attended by a medical doctor.
Technicians reported while starting engines aircraft rolled approximately ten feet. They applied brakes and stopped the aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported loss of communication with ramp personnel during push back using wireless headsets. The Captain was unable to hear brakes set command; and ramp personnel left the push back without communication with the Captain. The Captain contacted the station to send a ramp crew out to the aircraft with an operable headset to confirm that; push back was complete; and the aircraft was safe for taxi.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported an 'Air quality incident' with no additional information provided.
Twin engine pilot reported loss of aircraft control while in weather the pilot described as either a micro-burst or tornado.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported while flying the UAS over a body of water the onboard sensors did not recognize the water; resulting in the UAS crashing.
Captain reported loss of GPS signal and ADS-B fault.
Private Pilot reported runway incursion occurred during taxi and cited being new to the airport as a contributing factor.
B747-400 pilot reported an inflight shutdown of #4 engine due to oil filter caution message.
Flight Instructor conducting instrument training in IMC conditions reported taking evasive action after a NMAC with an aircraft climbing into them.
Two general aviation flight instructors reported a near miss during a training flight at a non-towered airport when one took off opposite direction.
Air carrier technician reported they were dispatched to a B737-800 at the gate to evaluate possible damage to door 1 left due to the jet bridge contacting the door and lifting it.
An air carrier flight crew reported the autopilot descended the aircraft below glide slope causing a Ground Proximity Alert and Low Altitude Warning from the Tower.
B737 Flight Attendant reported a fume event which resulted in a diversion and cabin crew experiencing physiological symptoms.
Aeronca Champion pilot reported an engine malfunction during climb resulting in an off airport landing while attempting to return to the departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported their weather radar failed. Due to known weather along their route they returned to their departure airport and made an overweight landing.
A320 Captain reported a fumes event during pre-flight after APU bleeds were activated.
B737 Captain reported being notified by ramp personnel of an engine tailpipe fire after engine shutdown at the gate. The fire extinguished on its own.
A light aircraft pilot entering the pattern at L70; a non-towered airport; reported an NMAC with another light aircraft on downwind.
Tug operator reported that during push back another driver went behind the aircraft causing the tug operator to slam on the brakes.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported the Dispatcher's Hazmat computer records were not retrievable until after flight departure.
EMB-145 pilot reported smoke entering the cockpit from Captain side vents. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.

GA IFR pilot reported a flight in heavy IMC conditions. The 328 hour pilot with little IFR experience reported heading deviations; altitude deviations and slow reaction time to changing weather conditions.
RSW Tower Controller reported radio issues at the facility contributed to a conflict that resulted in one of the aircraft's executing a go-around.
C172 Flight Instructor and student reported attempted rejected takeoff resulted in runway excursion and damage to aircraft and a runway light.
EMB-145 crew reported the takeoff roll was rejected when the Number 2 engine failed soon after power was set.
Air carrier flight crew reported entering the wrong altitude in the MCP and descending below FAF altitude which resulted in Air Traffic Control issuing a low altitude alert.

GA pilot reported while in cruise flight ATC advised them of traffic and this aircraft flies abeam them and banked towards them resulting in the pilot taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.
B737-700 Captain reported they failed to notify Dispatch to add late arriving aircraft batteries to Final Dispatch Release. The Flight Release was corrected for the next flight segment.
Air carrier Captain reported a nose wheel and brakes malfunction during descent. The Captain diverted to a suitable airport.
Corporate Captain reported a near midair collision with a balloon while in cruise flight.
A Flight Instructor reported their student lost control of the aircraft during landing roll resulting in a runway excursion.
Bonanza 35 pilot reported an engine failure. They were preparing to land on a highway when the engine regained performance so they diverted and the engine failed again on short final.

A320 pilot reported a flight control unit failure during cruise which resulted limited ability to program the modes on the FCU (Flight Control Unit). The pilot diverted the aircraft and landed safely.

Air carrier Captain reported a safety concern highlighting uneven ramp asphalt near the gate stopping position; resulting in high thrust and jet blast in the ramp area to maneuver the aircraft to the stop point.
EMB505 pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with a flight of four aircraft descending through their altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving an ACARS message indicating the DG manifest not properly annotated. After conferring with the appropriate ground personnel it was determined it was safe to continue to destination.

A321 Captain reported a fumes event described as a strong dirty socks odor resulting in health issues.  The odor was first noticed during boarding; but it dissipated after the engines were started.  The odor returned during climbout at which point the crew decided to divert.
A Cessna 172 pilot reported a complete electrical system failure; which necessitated a diversion to a suitable airport using only basic instrumentation.

Air Carrier Captain and First Officer reported that 3 spoilers on the left outer wing deployed during rotation and climb. The pilot crew had difficulty in maintaining aircraft control and returned to the departure airport. The pilots reported a similar event had been recently reported and MEL'ed on this aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew flying A320 aircraft reported runway incursion and loss of aircraft control during taxi off the runway.
C172 student pilot and his instructor reported the student had directional control difficulties on a solo flight and briefly departed the runway into the grass.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control during pushback procedure due to parking brake not set.
Air carrier pilot flying reported experiencing potential GPS jamming at cruise altitude over Kentucky and momentarily lost GPS signal.
C172 pilot reported an NMAC on downwind to a non-towered airport.
Technician discovered a missing turbine blade when borescoping an engine for slow to spool up on start.
B767 Captain reported a Rudder Ratio EICAS message and loss of cabin pressure control resulted in a diversion.

TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict and possible descent below minimum altitude occurred when the controller became distracted with the data position; social distancing requirements and supervising a trainee.
Air carrier Captain reported a strong odor upon boarding the aircraft. After further research and conferencing with the appropriate departments it was determined to refuse the aircraft.
General aviation pilot reported flying below the minimum altitude for that sector during approach. Pilot climbed to safe altitude and to fly clear of nearby cranes.
Air carrier flight crew reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while they were descending. The crew stated they suspected the drone had collided with the aircraft.

B787 Captain reported loss of Center Hydraulic System fluid in flight. The flight crew continued to landing at destination airport. After shutting down engines on the runway; the aircraft was towed to the gate.

Phenom 300 flight crew reported the cabin pressurization system malfunctioned while in cruise and descended to stabilize the cabin altitude. Deciding against diverting to an unknown airport in a foreign country; the flight crew performed an air turnback.
Air Carrier pilots reported they could not program the SID correctly and flew off course. The reporters referenced a fix with the same name but starts with a different letter exists in the NAS.
Flight instructor reported observing his student during first solo flight lose control of the aircraft during landing resulting a landing gear collapse.
Air carrier Captain reported a Maintenance Hazmat violation regarding the transport of incorrectly configured Class 9 Hazmat.

B787 flight crew reported a GPS jamming and/or spoofing event that resulted in the loss of some communications; GPS malfunction and failure; and inaccurate clock times. Additionally; there were false EPGWS warnings that had to be disabled during final approach.
GA jet instructor reported GPS jamming while on initial approach in CONUS airspace. The instructor notified ATC and returned to the departure airport.
A Flight Instructor reported the student lost control of the aircraft while landing resulting in a runway excursion; horizontal stabilizer damage and a flat tire.
PA-28 pilot reported engine misfiring at 14;000 feet then losing the engine completely for several minutes before eventually starting back up at 5;200 feet.
Pilot flying C421 aircraft reported a NMAC with two other aircraft during departure climb.
Air carrier Captain reported a CFTT event during visual approach to BHM airport.
Air carrier flight attendant reported during landing felt the aircraft suddenly pull up due to the pilot's go around maneuver to avoid an aircraft on the runway.
Light transport jet flight crew reported runway excursion during landing due to gusty winds. Flight crew shut down aircraft and exited.
Air carrier Captain reported track; airspeed; and altitude control difficulties when they flew into a thunderstorm departing MIA.
Air carrier flight crew came within 100 feet of a drone while on final approach at LAS airport.
C172 pilot reported several systems failures and smoke in the cockpit during cruise and diverted to a nearby airport.
Air Carrier Pilot reported seeing a UAS pass by their aircraft at 7;000 feet while descending to land.
A Hot Air Balloon pilot reported unexpected wind currents forced the balloon to descend into a waterfall and river but they were able to regain control and climb away to a safe landing area.
Dispatcher reported 13 attempts were required before flight crew could receive or transmit on ACARS.
General aviation pilot reported a NMAC on final approach caused by an aircraft that turned in front of them. Pilot performed a go-around and the same aircraft then caused another NMAC.
First Officer reported multiple electrical failures after the #2 IDG failed in flight.

Air carrier Captain reported there was only one electrical outlet working on the flight deck and was concerned if charging for the EFB's became necessary this could present a problem during the flight.
G280 crew reported a loss of control during landing rollout after deploying thrust reversers.  Steering tiller inputs had no effect and aircraft departed the runway surface.
A SAT TRACON Controller reported they had to turn and cancel descent for an aircraft cleared for an ILS approach to avoid an unidentified VFR aircraft crossing the approach course.
Maintenance Lead Technician reported a Maintenance repair error by clearing an MEL and returning aircraft without accomplishing all required procedures.

Pilot reported a near mid air collision when another aircraft over flew the aircraft while on approach to landing. ATC advised the Pilot to break off the approach and provided vectors to reenter the approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported terrain warning alert on approach. Flight crew pulled up and continued approach.

First Officer reported setting the aircraft parking brake but not charging the brake accumulator with battery power prior to removing the wheel chocks. First officer reported that the aircraft began to roll; moving 20 feet before hitting a parked vehicle.

Captain reported refusing an aircraft due to an MEL for the R/H Wing/Body Overheat System that also deferred the APU and R/H Pack. The aircraft was scheduled for a destination with ground temperatures over 95 degrees.
A320 flight crew reported loss of Green Hydraulic System fluid in flight. The flight crew requested priority handling and performed an alternate landing gear extension; followed by a safe landing.
N90 Controller reported an aircraft on the JFK5 SID turned the wrong way on initial climb. Reporter stated this is a recurring issue for departures from Runway 31L.
CRJ-900 Captain reported the landing gear failed during approach requiring emergency extension for a safe landing.
Light aircraft pilot reported descending below 1500 feet to stay below the clouds while enroute to destination.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing loss of hydraulic A system.
Pilot reported concerns with the lack of NOTAM publications at N40 airport for known issues. Pilot stated the AWOS-3 has been inoperable for at least a year and has never been NOTAMed.
Pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft departing 7F3 airport and took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
Flight Attendant reported the left wing came into contact with a building during push back from the gate.
Air Carrier Captain reported a track deviation occurred in the vicinity of the Black Sea as a result of GPS jamming and an FMC malfunction.
Air carrier First Officer reported a quick stop due to a conflict with a catering truck while taxiing to gate. No injuries resulted and aircraft continued uneventfully to gate.
Air carrier Pilots reported thinking they were cleared for the approach and descended early.
Corporate jet pilot reported they rejected takeoff due to the engine surging on takeoff roll.

SBN Local Controller reported an aircraft exited on wrong taxiway; resulting in the Controller sending traffic around.  Reporter was notified that there is a lack of Taxiway A3 signage due to construction.

GA flight instructor with a student reported an NMAC during climbout from Runway 22L at JRF airport requiring evasive action.  Reportedly; the conflicting aircraft was NORDO and on approach to Runway 4R.
Cessna 177B Pilot reported loss of electrical power in flight resulting in a radio failure and a diversion to a nearby airport.
Air carrier pilot reported loud bang after take-off. Flight returned to departure airport for an uneventful landing.
A320 First Officer reported landing safely after the in flight shutdown of the number 2 engine.
Beechjet 400 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport for a safe landing after experiencing problems with their pressurization system.
A321 flight crew reported a fume event during initial climb that necessitated an expedited diversion. The flight crew coordinated with ATC and the flight executed a safe overweight landing.
ZAU Center Controller and their trainee reported they missed an erroneous altitude read back and the aircraft descended below the Minimum Enroute Altitude.
Air carrier pilots reported while proceeding into their gate; a pilot taxing behind them reported the aircraft had a tailpipe fire.
Air carrier ground safety analyst reported that prohibited aerosol container of firearm lubricant was confiscated during security screening. PAX was notified and aerosol container was removed.
C172 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control on takeoff resulted in near contact with airport signage and a difficult climb out of the airport environment.
Two pilots of a Piper PA32 reported landing safely after the engine lost power. Post flight inspection revealed fuel starvation due to improper tank selection.

Cessna 210 pilot reported they were already deviating for obstructions when ATC relayed instructions through another aircraft to avoid the obstructions. The reporter stated this area has poor radio reception which 'could have led to a disaster.'
Flight Instructor reported the engine failed shortly after takeoff. Pilot returned the aircraft to the airport and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew reported radio altimeter malfunction on approach to MEM airport and speculated possible 5G interference as a contributing factor.

LAX Tower Controller reported clearing an aircraft for takeoff when the ASDE alarm went off to signal there was another aircraft on the runway; which turned out to be due to the other aircraft missing the hold line. The first aircraft took off without problem; but the reporter noted the design of the new taxiways has been causing incursions to be a recurring issue.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a momentary GPWS terrain warning on descent into HTS airport.

B767 Captain reported a thrust loss at cruise when attempting to balance fuel with an improper configuration. Captain reconfigured fuel pumps; had First Officer balance the fuel and continued on to destination.
Air carrier pilot reported real time radar weather data via WSI is no longer available when overflying the Taiwan Strait near China.
An Instructor pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft which took off behind them and turned crosswind too early cutting them off.
B737-800 flight crew reported fuel quantity discrepancy during departure climb. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming and spoofing in flight.

Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC at AUO non-towered airport when another aircraft entered the pattern and flew directly toward them. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Cirrus Pilot reported on landing the left brake did not work resulting in the aircraft leaving the taxiway and coming to a rest in the grass.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS jamming over the Black Sea.
PA-34-200 pilot reported a gear up landing after landing gear failed to extend during the approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported evasive maneuvers to avoid a NMAC resulted in an unstable approach and a go around.
Gate agent reported a near miss of an aircraft engine with a jet bridge while parking at the gate. The gate agent raised the jet bridge to avoid a collision.
Pilot and passenger reported upon touchdown the aircraft yawed to the left resulting in a runway excursion and aircraft damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported ADC failure inflight causing temporary aircraft loss of control.
B737-800 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing flight control issues related to Mach Trim anomalies.

EMB 135 First Officer reported Cabin Pressure problems during cruise. With 20 minutes of flight left; hypoxia symptoms were noted by both Pilots. The Flight Crew donned oxygen masks immediately and monitored Cabin Pressure through to landing at destination airport.
A Flight Instructor reported engine failure and landing aircraft in a field.
Flight Attendant reported concern regarding the face mask policy as it covers a passenger's identity and creates a possible way to hide unwanted items under the mask.

Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict. As the crew was taxiing; another air carrier taxiing on a parallel taxiway cut in front at an intersection requiring an abrupt stop. No warning or notice was given by ATC.
Pilot flying approach reported low altitude alert from ATC during approach in IMC.
A319 Captain reported an NMAC during approach to a class D airport under a Bravo shelf requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
C172 departing a non-towered airport reported the pilot taxied on to the runway and departed with another aircraft on short final which performed a go-around.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving a terrain alert during approach. Flight crew corrected course and continued to landing.

B737 First Officer reported the need to reject a takeoff after receiving an master caution warning during the takeoff roll. After a maintenance inspection; a reverser fault was discovered and corrected and the aircraft was released for service.

Air carrier flight crew reported a bird strike during approach which resulted in a strong odor throughout the aircraft. The flight crew completed a safe landing with the ground personnel providing appropriate support.

Airline Captain reported observing a UAS pass by their aircraft while climbing out on a departure procedure.  The incident was reported to the TRACON controller; then the flight continued to its destination as planned.

C172 Flight Instructor reported losing radios and communication systems while on while on final approach at a towered airport. After failing to establish communication with Tower; Flight Instructor proceeded to go around and departed the airspace to land at departure airport.
Pilot reported engine failure during cruise and diverted to a safe landing. Post flight it was found that a bolt connecting the mixture cable to the carburetor had failed.

ERJ-170 Captain reported procedures were not followed by Contract Maintenance during a ground pressurization check to ensure proper operation of the cabin pressure control system. The pressurization check was done with the flight attendants on board and did not follow the AMM procedures.

Air carrier Captain reported that on approach; the First Officer's AP (Autopilot) button became stuck when attempting to disconnect the AP. The button could not be unstuck; which resulted in inoperative pitch trim and continuous AP aural alert during the approach. The First Officer was able to overcome control forces to land the aircraft. Post-flight it was learned that maintenance had applied a placard with adhesive which had caused the AP button to stick.
Pilot on training flight in SR-20 aircraft reported NMAC with another aircraft in cruise.
C172 pilot reported a runway excursion and equipment issues.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while in the traffic pattern at a congested airport.

Captain reported inadvertently leaving the nose landing gear steering pin uninstalled prior to takeoff. Upon gear retraction; the crew received a gear unsafe indication and decided the best course of action would be to continue to the destination airport where better services were available.
B737-800 pilot reported a failure of the Pressurization System at cruise altitude; necessitating a descent; and diversion to an alternate airport.
B737-800 First Officer reported a fumes event in the flight deck during cruise resulting in a diversion.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying for recreation in controlled airspace without LAANC authorization.
A Tower Controller reported they received a Minimum Safe Altitude Warning for an aircraft on short final and advised the aircraft.
Ground Personnel reported observing aircraft using excessive thrust during taxi near aircraft gates causing damage to other aircraft.
Small aircraft pilot reported an NMAC and also experienced wake turbulence from a twin-engine turboprop that had dove in front of the reporter's aircraft while on final approach to F70.
Pilot of small aircraft reported a NMAC while departing the traffic pattern. After the event the other aircraft radioed they did not see the other aircraft.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported NMAC with another aircraft in airport traffic pattern.

Air carrier flight crew reported a passenger's laptop fell through a gap between the seat and the sidewall into an inaccessible cargo area during cruise. Flight crew diverted and performed an overweight landing.

B737-700 Captain reported a Cabin Altitude Warning Horn and a Cabin Altitude of 9;500 feet in cruise. The Captain requested priority handling and directed the First Officer to descend to 10;000 feet. The flight crew elected to continue to destination airport at 10;000 feet and make a precautionary landing.
B737 pilot reported they rejected takeoff after Tower cancelled their takeoff clearance due to conflicting traffic landing on the intersecting runway.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL in trail of an A321.
A320 pilot reported landing gear failed to extend during approach. Flight crew performed manual gear extension and landed safely.
Mooney M20 pilot reported a gear up landing.
Flight Instructor reported a runway excursion when student attempted exiting runway at an excessive speed after landing. Aircraft drifted into grass with no damage to aircraft or injury to occupants.
B737 MAX 9 flight crew reported multiple autoflight anomalies that ultimately resulted in their refusal to accept the aircraft.
BE30 pilot reported a NMAC on final approach with a helicopter that reportedly was not communicating on UNICOM frequency.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a late instruction from ramp control; a miscommunication with the push back crew and a momentary loss of control of the aircraft during push back from the gate.
A General aviation pilot reported loss of GPS signal. The reporter had five GPS receivers in the aircraft.
B737-800 flight crew reported engine vibrations along with smoke and odor in the cabin. They shut down the number two engine; diverted; and landed safely.
First Officer reported another aircraft conducted a go-around after his aircraft took the runway for takeoff to beat a clearance void time.
BE35 pilot reported the propeller began malfunctioning in cruise requiring a diversion and landed safely.

MD-11 Captain and Dispatcher reported a hydraulic system malfunction during climb; resulting in a landing gear indication malfunction. The flight returned to the departure airport; landed safely; and were towed to the gate.
Pilot reported ground conflict during approach at a non-towered airport with another aircraft taking off on same runway in the opposite direction.
C172 Flight Instructor reported engine roughness during cruise with a student.  Instructor was unable to stop the roughness and diverted for a normal landing.
A Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while their student was performing ground reference maneuvers.

Technicians reported that during post maintenance engines runs; procedures were not followed which resulted in a hand held radio being left in the engine inlet. The radio was ingested and damaged several fan blades that required replacement before the aircraft was released.

Air Carrier First Officer reported on final approach to Runway 35L in DEN the Master Caution warning sounded for the loss of GPS and ADS-B. Informing DEN ATC; the pilots were told it had been occurring all day.
PA-28 Instructor Pilot reported landing on a highway after experiencing engine failure shortly after takeoff.

EMB-505 Captain reported their inexperienced First Officer was conducting a visual approach and received an EGPWS terrain warning; and an ATC altitude alert inside the final approach fix. Flight crew climbed and reacquired a normal descent profile for landing.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during departure taxi which dissipated. The dirty sock odor resumed more noticeably during climb which prompted in the flight crew to request priority handling for an air return. A safe landing followed.

A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft being vectored for a Visual Approach deviated from its assigned course and altitude resulting in them flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude and into conflict with VFR traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported a runway incursion resulted from incorrect NOTAM; incorrect runway markings and lighting.
M20 pilot reported the landing gear collapse upon touchdown.
Citation C-501 pilot reported loud noise coming from the main cabin door area and a rise in cabin pressure altitude in cruise. Pilot descended; continued to destination airport and landed.
B737 flight crew reported having a firm landing that resulted in a bounced landing. Flight crew recovered and continued landing roll.
An air carrier First Officer reported release had SID fixes listed rather than the transition resulted in missed fix and a CFTT event.  First Officer also stated that fatigue factor affected mistake.
ZDC Center Controller reported an aircraft resumed clearance to PAAZZ after deviating for weather but ATC had cleared them to PAACK. The reporter stated the similar named fixes caused the confusion.
Mooney M20E Pilot reported gear collapse on landing rollout.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported ground conflict on post landing taxi with aircraft being towed.
C-172 Flight Instructor with student reported brake malfunction during landing rollout.
B737 Captain reported that the final DG was not provided to the flight crew prior to departure and requested it from Dispatch after takeoff.

Pilot reported a loss of elevator control after take off during a post maintenance check flight. The pilot made an immediate air turn back and precautionary landing. A post flight inspection found the elevator control cables were not reconnected during maintenance.
Cessna 182 Flight Instructor reported stopping the aircraft on the runway after a loss of brake pressure.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a balloon at FL410 in ZHU airspace.
B757 First Officer reported an inflight upset while climbing through FL356 after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B767-300.
Air carrier flight crew reported braking action nil on their landing at JAC; and were unable to stop on the runway; overshooting a few feet into the overrun area.
Hobby UAS pilot had a controller malfunction while flying. Pilot landed UAS safely.
Air Carrier Captain reported nearly being hit by a speeding vehicle while walking under the aircraft's wing during the pre-flight walk around.

A319 Captain reported that the ECAM HYD Y RSVR LO LVL appeared while in cruise. As the flight crew ran and followed the QRH and checklists; there was confusion regarding a procedure with a hydraulic pump. After consulting with Dispatch and Maintenance Control; the flight crew performed an air turnback.

Beechcraft 50 pilot reported sensing a strong smell of fuel during flight. Upon inspection; it appeared that SCAT tubing was pressing against a fuel crossfeed line located in the landing gear bay creating a fuel leak.
Air Carrier Ramp Personnel reported the removal of Hazmat parcel from cargo due to Hazmat label 'Cargo aircraft only'.
Ground employee reported a ground conflict between aircraft and ramp vehicles. The reporter stated this is a common occurrence at this location in the airport ramp.
Air Carrier Ramp Agent reported concerns with having to wear a face mask outside when the weather is getting hot making it difficult to breathe.

CRJ-900 Captain reported while being vectored for an RNAV approach ATC changed the approach to a visual approach and the flight crew needed to adjust their flight path resulting in flight towards terrain. The flight crew executed a go-around and landed safely at the airport.
Captain reported a failure to follow SOPs and communications procedures caused the aircraft to roll forward under engine power when the tug and tow bar were disconnected.
A Light Sport Aircraft pilot reported a gear up landing due to a faulty squat switch.
PA-34 Flight Instructor reported the nose gear collapsed during a hard landing.
B757 flight crew reported they rejected takeoff twice due to the loss of control of the aircraft veering to the right of the runway center line.

B737 flight crew reported an unstabilized approach resulted in a go-around as the aircraft touched down on the runway. As the aircraft performed the go around; the First Officer felt a bump indicative of a tail strike. The aircraft landed and upon post flight inspection damage was discovered to the tail stinger.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Bonanza pilot reported running off the end of the runway while rejecting takeoff due to an open cabin door.

A321 First Officer reported loss of fluid from Green Hydraulic System during cruise. At top of descent; the fluid level went into the low range. The flight crew continued to monitor and made the decision to divert and make a precautionary landing.
B737 pilot reported a cockpit window malfunction at cruise. The crew diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an alert from ATC that they were descending below MSA on approach to SLC.
Cirrus Vision SJ-50 Pilot reported smoke in cabin during climbout. The pilot diverted and landed safely.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported flying through the approach course and temporary loss of aircraft control.

Air Carrier Dispatcher reported receiving a call from an MD-11 flight crew regarding an uncorrectable fuel quantity issue. After conferring with Maintenance; the flight was diverted to a suitable airport for a safe landing.
Captain reported a crossing restriction was not met due to an occasional software glitch which reportedly causes the descent to shallow out temporarily.

PA-28 student pilot reported loss of control during a practice solo landing event. The aircraft left the pavement; proceeded through a grass strip and stopped on a taxiway. No injuries; damage to the aircraft or airport property were reported.
Aeronca Champ Instructor Pilot reported the student pilot experienced a hard landing and prop strike while landing in gusty wind conditions.
Recreational / Hobbyist FPV UAS pilot reported their visual observer lost Visual Line of Sight (VLOS). VLOS was re-established and the flight continued without issue.
General aviation pilot reported their passenger saw a UAS off their wingtip in cruise flight. The encounter was reported to ATC.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning to pull up due to too early of a descent during initial approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict during landing with a departing aircraft that was still on the runway. A missed approach was executed and returned for a safe landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported failure of the autoflight system during departure climb resulted in a diversion. Maintenance inspection revealed a failed generator control unit.
A Tower Controller reported a blind spot from the Tower due to airport buildings prevented the Controller from seeing a vehicle enter the runway at the same time an aircraft was landing.
B737 Captain reported a fumes event after engine start and during gate departure. Flight returned to the gate for maintenance and the same problem occurred again during engine start.
Ground employee reported that the procedure manual does not call out for the use of a tail stand when loading and unloading passengers and cargo from a 737 MAX aircraft.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC during landing pattern training when another aircraft flew an improvised pattern entry which caused the Instructor to take evasive action to prevent a collision.
Dash 8 flight crew reported the First Officer's windshield shattered during cruise.After completing the required checklists the flight continued on to the destination.
C-172 pilot reported the carburetor heat failed and the engine lost power. The pilot was able to land safely.

Fractional Jet Pilot reported a NMAC while turning base leg for the final approach when a light twin aircraft cut under their aircraft and causing an RA event. The pilot took evasive action by climbing away from the twin and ATC sequenced them again for landing.

UAS Flight crew was operating a large fixed wing UAS in low visibility conditions and returned for landing after being advised by ATC the flight crew did not have the proper authorization for conducting operations in those conditions.
B737 Captain reported during taxi they almost collided with a regional jet that failed to give way. Captain stopped aircraft abruptly to let the jet pass.
Certified Flight Instructor reported noticing some engine roughness while on final approach for landing. They decided to turn 180 degrees and land in a field.

Instructor pilot flying right seat in a B-55 Baron reported the pilot flying mistakenly retracted the landing gear instead of the flaps on the takeoff roll; resulting in damage to the propellers and landing gear doors.
B737-800 F/O reported returning to the departure airport after experiencing strong fumes in the cabin.
Air carrier Captain reported the Jeppesen FD-PRO is missing the engine out procedure due to terrain for ITO airport.
ERJ-175 pilot reported an uncommanded disconnect from the tug during the push back maneuver resulting in the aircraft rolling back around 20 feet; before brakes were applied.
B737 flight crew reported during pushback the aircraft rolled uncommanded after the tug disconnected. Flight crew applied brakes to stop
Air carrier Captain reported a position error during takeoff on the runway at Hanoi airport and rejected the takeoff. Reporter stated that GPS jamming may have contributed to the error.
A321 pilot reported during cruise had an APU auto shutdown with #2 generator MEL'd; per the QRH the flight crew landed immediately.
A student pilot reported they lost control of the aircraft while landing and veered off the runway suffering nose gear damage.
Air Traffic Controller reported aircraft not following taxi instructions resulting in multiple aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the aircraft.

Air carrier flight crew reported that ATC cleared them to cross a runway that had another aircraft on takeoff roll. Upon seeing the other aircraft; the Captain stopped their aircraft past the hold short bars while the other aircraft continued their takeoff.
B737-700 Captain reported Dispatch notification during cruise stating Hazmat cargo weight not included in the aircraft Weight & Balance data. Weight & Balance data corrected and flight continued.
ZBW Center Controller reported handoff related issues with ZNY Center.
Center Controller reported an aircraft lost its autopilot and instrumentation requiring a diversion to a nearby airport.
EUG TRACON Controller reported vectoring an aircraft below an unlabeled MVA. Controller stated that display MVA labeling should be improved.
Ground employee reported a tractor hit a parked aircraft during baggage loading.
C208 Pilot reported when ready to depart and entering the runway they observed another aircraft opposite direction approximately 25 feet above them.
Turboprop pilot reported during departure climb to have penetrated bravo airspace without a clearance.
Cessna 150 pilot reported entering the pattern below the published altitude at a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with traffic on downwind.
Pilot reported being told by ATC that due to the cross wind the pilot had drifted off course during takeoff; resulting in an NMAC.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a fume event during descent characterized as strong musty odor in the forward cabin area. First Officer and flight attendants reported experiencing physiological symptoms once parked at the arrival gate.
Private Pilot reported the cause of the deviation was not flying for 2 months during the pandemic and was rusty and needed practice.
Controller reported military aircraft flew below the Minimum IFR altitude after permission for special activity was denied.
B777 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing EDDF (Frankfurt; Germany) in trail of an A340.

Air carrier Captain reported ATC advised them of a nearby primary radar target during departure climb. The flight crew reported observing Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UAP) that appeared to maintain the same track as the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported an inability to verify a taxi clearance due to ATC's use of a taxiway designation not charted on the airport diagram.
Air Carrier Captain reported an electrical burning smell from the forward equipment exhaust fan that was resolved by turning the switch off; enabling them to continue to their destination.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported two ELT batteries which are prohibited to be air transported were discovered at destination arrival.
Technician reported ramp agents noted damage to a cargo door; requiring maintenance to inspect and defer the cargo door for operations.
CL-350 fractional jet Captain reported an engine malfunction on the final descent and following checklists shut down the engine. The flight landed at the destination without incident.
Helicopter pilot reported All Engines Operating parameters were exceeded on approach; resulting in a go around to a landing.
General aviation pilot reported a NMAC during departure with an aircraft that descended on top of them entering the traffic pattern. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Air carrier flight crew reported a high workload environment on approach into DEN airport; which included several runway changes; confusion over arrival procedures; and an unstable approach resulted in a go-around.
PC12 pilot reported multiple instances of heading and altitude deviations possibly due to avionics anomalies.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported a suspected design issue regarding the lack of safety wire on the main landing gear valve stem assembly.
B737-700 Captain reported high EGT on right engine just after take-off. Captain reduced thrust to stay within limits for the duration of flight to original destination.
B737 Captain reported communication difficulties exacerbated conditions during bad weather causing a diversion to refuel and later arrive safely at original destination airport.
GA Flight Instructor reported being distracted while trying to troubleshoot a GPS information error resulting in an altitude deviation and CFTT during final approach to UES airport.
Lead Technician reported non compliance with MEL procedures on a transit aircraft was noticed after records review and were corrected.
Airframe & Powerplant technician with Inspection authority reported they released an aircraft for service without visually verifying that the maintenance task has been completed.
PA34-200 pilot reported an unresponsive throttle which resulted in a runway excursion; landing gear collapse and propeller ground strike.

B767-300 air carrier crew reported upon parking the nose tire was damaged at an international airport. The crew highlighted that there were potholes and cracks on the taxiway they crossed at a slower taxi speed.
B737 flight crew reported the aircraft rolled forward uncommanded during push-back. Captain applied brakes to stop aircraft.
A321 Flight Attendant reported a galley oven began producing heavy smoke during use; triggering a fire alarm in a nearby lavatory. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
Tug driver reported being hit by a catering van. The driver of the van was reportedly heads down not paying attention.
Citation X  Captain reported a NO TAKE OFF EICAS Message and rejected the takeoff. The Captain reported that the message was caused by a rudder trim not aligned condition.

B737-700 Flight Crew reported autopilot problems and an uncommanded in flight shut down of the right engine during a ferry flight of an aircraft scheduled for dis-assembly. The engine restarted on it's own; so the flight crew continued to destination airport and landed.
B787 Reporter states that notification was made of an air turn back caused by excessive vibration on an aircraft with a recent engine change.
Center Controller reported an airspace violation associated with presumed equipment issues.

C152 pilot reported noticing a burning smell; proceeded by lights flashing; circuit breakers popping; and flames appearing behind the electrical panel in the cockpit. The pilot managed to turn off the master switch and diverted to the nearest airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported unforecast weather appeared during a flight. The UAS began to pick up ice and was landed.
B737-900 Flight Crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into SEA.
Technician reported failure to follow procedures during a multiple shift A Check resulted in not reactivating the Leading Edge Slats.

Corporate pilot reported ZJX Center rerouted them such that flying time was increased considerably and caused a low fuel situation. Reporter stated concerns that reroutes using the new RNAV procedures in the area may be inefficient and creating unsafe situations.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC directed a low-altitude go around due to departing aircraft past hold-short line; but not airborne.
A319 flight crew reported aircraft pressurization failure during climb out. Flight crew descended and returned to departure airport.

GA pilot reported becoming disoriented while on a visual approach that then entered IMC resulting in an altitude deviation. ATC issued an immediate climb to maintain terrain separation; and issued vectors for an ILS approach; and landed safely.
ZSU Controller reported chronic transceiver malfunctions resulted in serious unsafe air traffic situations.

First Officer reported a loss of GPS signal to both GPS receivers at cruise in a known area of GPS jamming. The crew was unable to recover the GPS signal for the remainder of the flight and continued to destination.
ERJ-145 flight crew reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence climbing through 800 ft departing CLT in trail of an A321.
Technician reported resisting pressure from management to install unserviceable side stays during a MLG strut change and refused the work.
Check Pilot reported after landing on Runway 26R in Atlanta the crew had difficulty finding Taxiway E37 because of faded paint.
B757-200 First Officer reported girt bar failed to retract when ground crew opened door. Escape slide pulled out of disarmed door but did not deploy.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of pressurization during descent to their destination.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS alert while flying a visual approach into the RNO airport.
Pilot flying M20-E aircraft reported gear up landing.
Flight crew reported Radio Altimeter malfunction during climb out from LAX.
CRJ-900 Captain reported that Maintenance deferred a forward cargo squib 2 caution indication. During cruise; they got a master caution for the forward cargo squib 2.

Air Carrier Maintenance Inspector reported while performing a borescope inspection the number two engine; found a one-inch crack on the convex side of the stage two high pressure turbine nozzle guide vane. The engineer recommended grounding the aircraft.
CL-30 flight crew reported pressurization malfunction inflight.
PA-28 pilots reported while climbing to avoid turbulence the aircraft experienced an engine malfunction which resulted with a landing at the nearest airfield.
DA-40 flight instructor reported landing safely after experiencing engine failure shortly after takeoff.
Air carrier First Officer reported calling in fatigued due to several maintenance related delays and company scheduling techniques that push the limits of legal rest periods.
Flight Crew reported auto throttle anomalies on take off and again on landing required a go around.

Pilot reported electrical system failure in flight. As the pilot diagnosed the failures; the pilot used a cell phone to contact ATC and request priority handling. The pilot received vectors to destination airport and extended the landing gear using alternate extension procedures. During landing the main landing gear unlocked and the aircraft settled onto the partially extended main gear. The pilot secured the aircraft and waited for assistance in moving the aircraft.
Center Controller reported an air carrier experiencing lost communications violated FAR's when they continued to their destination instead of landing at the nearest suitable airport.

Air carrier First Officer reported that during initial descent the radio altimeter displayed erroneous indications. Reporter stated they did not complete a maintenance write up because they believed the event was due to 5G interference.
Ramp person reported a cargo loader hit the aircraft causing damage.
PA-28 pilot reported a runway excursion on landing rollout.

C172 instructor pilot reported loss of engine performance in cruise and diverted. After landing safely; pilot determined the engine degradation was due to environmental factors; departed; then safely completed flight to destination.

Air carrier First Officer reported a Dispatch notification during taxi which stated that no flight crew acknowledgment message was received prior to gate departure. The First Officer re-transmitted the message successfully and departed safely.
Flight crew inbound to LAS reported they noticed their assigned COKTL2 arrival routes aircraft through restricted airspace.

Air carrier flight crew reported 'Loss of reliability with navigation nav 1 and nav 2' and 'Intermittent Loss of navigational signal experienced intermittently by both pilots' during two back to back approaches to CHA airport.  The crew elected to divert instead of trying a third time.

Flight Attendants reported an escape slide deployment near miss when a main entry door was opened prior to doors being disarmed. Flight attendants were waiting for the seat belt sign to be extinguished prior to disarming doors.
Air Carrier Captain reported temporary loss of control due to severe turbulence.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported a communication breakdown with Maintenance regarding Thermal Containment Bag replacement.
B777 First Officer reported battery powered wheelchair documents contained errors and also missing required Hazmat labels.
Flight Attendant reported fatigue and being unable to perform duties due to multiple return to gate incidents and plane swaps.
A321 First Officer reported electrical systems loss lead to cockpit instruments and lighting to fail. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Cessna 172 pilot reported a violent wake turbulence encounter on short final at SNA that resulted in an inflight upset with recovery accomplished within 50 feet of the ground.
Ramp agent reported an aircraft taxiing into the gate at a high rate of speed nearly collided with passenger stairs.

Air carrier flight crew reported the transport of undocumented Hazmat cargo. At destination unloading; it was discovered that in addition to the unloaded Dry ice; a parcel of lithium batteries was also discovered.
B737 flight crew reported the loss of both FMCs during arrival. Due to poor weather conditions at destination; flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
A320 First Officer reported an ENG 2 BLD ABNORM PR ECAM at cruise. The flight crew elected to divert due to weather at destination airport.

PA-44-180 instructor reported a runway excursion while instructing the student on simulated aborted single engine takeoff procedures in the aircraft. The instructor simulated the aborted takeoff condition by applying rudder displacement and the student responded by locking the brake resulting in a loss of control on the runway. The aircraft looped 135 degrees from the takeoff position.

Air carrier First Officer reported missing a frequency change due to misreading the frequency numbers. Reporter stated that although they were monitoring guard frequency so ATC could contact them; they had the volume turned down due to the excessive amount of chatter.
Technicians reported non compliance was caused by communications issues; resulted in a CRJ-200 air turn back days later.
B737 flight crew reported by following several TCAS RA on charted visual side-by to SFO RWY28R resulted in a low altitude alert from tower with a CFTT event.

General aviation pilot reported inadvertently entering a thunderstorm while in contact with ATC and experienced severe turbulence and precipitation. The pilot turned the aircraft around and exited the adverse weather. There were no injuries or aircraft damage.
General aviation Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC with another aircraft at HMP non-towered airport in the traffic pattern. The instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
SR20 student pilot reported losing control of aircraft on landing due to over correcting for wind-shear.
ZAB Controller reported receiving a handoff from adjacent facility at a non compliant altitude with departure procedure resulted in the aircraft being below minimum altitude.
Captain reported a diversion to a precautionary landing following the loss of both Flight Management Computers which caused the loss of Cockpit Display Units and most navigation systems.
EMB-505 captain reported inoperative anti-ice systems with bleeds 1 and 2 off CAS messages during taxi out. During climb; the cabin did not pressurize and the flight diverted to a safe landing.

C402 Captain reported an alternator malfunction during initial climb resulting in a dual alternator failure. The flight crew was able to restore one alternator and returned to departure airport for precautionary landing.

Air carrier Captain reported a discrepancy between Flight Release (stating no Hazmat cargo onboard) and the NOTOC (listing dry ice on board). After extensive consultation with ground personnel it was determined that dry ice was in fact onboard and was loaded in agreement with the NOTOC prior to departure.
Captain reported receiving an altitude alert from ATC while on visual approach after descending below profile.  Flight crew returned to glide path and continued to landing.
TRACON Controller reported they left an aircraft on a vector longer than intended and it flew below the minimum vectoring altitude.
C210 pilot reported NMAC with a flight of four F-18 aircraft requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
B737-800 flight crew reported incorrect airspeed indications on HUD display during takeoff roll and a subsequent rejected take off.
A321 Flight Attendant stated the aircraft has been reconfigured; but the required reference documentation had not been updated.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during initial climb which dissipated and resumed during descent. The flight continued to safe landing at destination where a logbook entry and an aircraft odor form were submitted.
Fractional First Officer reported the Captain conducted a visual approach to the wrong runway resulting in terrain warnings.
A319 flight crew reported a fume event occurred during cruise; resulting in a diversion.
B737-700 flight crew reported retracting gear before flaps during a go-around procedure.

Cirrus SF50 jet pilot reported multiple warning messages after takeoff during the initial climb which resulted in the pilot returning to the departure airport and landed. The pilot discovered the pitot tube cover was not properly removed before flight.
A321 Captain reported a fuel imbalance during flight. Captain reported an incorrect MEL was applied to the aircraft when it made the return flight to ZZZ with another crew.

A Light Sport aircraft pilot reported losing control of the aircraft while landing resulting in a tail and rudder ground strike while skidding off the runway. The pilot was able to taxi the aircraft back onto the runway and park.
C172 pilot in cruise deviating for weather in the vicinity of a MOA reported a NMAC with a B52.
Government UAS pilot reported an airborne conflict with another aircraft during a training flight.
A321 Captain reported an NMAC during takeoff requiring evasive action.
Student pilot reported being high and slow on approach which resulted in a high flare and a large bounce.
Air carrier ground personnel reported receiving improperly configured ground shipments containing DG that also lacked appropriate documentation.

B777 flight crew and maintenance personnel reported the aircraft rolled back while maintenance was troubleshooting a brake system issue. The 2L door contacted the gate causing it to shear off and the aircraft then stopped.

A Center Controller and their trainee reported an aircraft they took a hand off on deviated from course and descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude while still in the previous sector airspace while returning to the departure airport due to an aircraft mechanical problem.
Air carrier Captain reported executing a go-around from low altitude on final approach to CLT when the aircraft taking off on that runway seemed to take longer than normal.
Gulfstream G650 Captain reported a trailing edge flap failure and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing.
P28A Flight Instructor reported returning to departure airport after experiencing electrical system issues. Aircraft landed safely; and all electrical power was lost after landing.

PA-28 student pilot and Instructor reported while changing fuel tanks during flight the engine began to sputter. After switching cross feed back to the original tank the engine ran normally and the flight continued to destination.
CRJ200 flight crew reported they forgot to review MEL actions for a deferred pack prior to departure resulting in a pressurization failure during climb and a return to departure airport.

CE-182 pilot reported they inadvertently took off with the tow bar still attached to the aircraft which caused the nose wheel to be severed from the airplane. The pilot continued to destination airport and landed resulting in further damage to the aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported observing potentially unsafe ramp procedures after pushback at DFW.

GA flight instructor with student reported a track heading deviation during departure from SQL airport resulting in a CFTT (Controlled Flight Toward Terrain). ATC questioned their heading and gave the pilots a new clearance.
EMB-505 reported while taxing to the ramp a Piper aircraft departed and flew right over their aircraft at about 80 feet. The EMB-505 pilot stopped their aircraft to avoid a collision.
DA-42 Flight Instructor reported failure of prop governor after takeoff. Returned to departure airport.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported a Hazmat load configuration error after gate departure. The aircraft was summoned to departure gate to correct load configuration error.
Air carrier First Officer reported encountering mountain wave at 35000 feet resulted in a temporary loss of control.
Flight Instructor and solo student on a C172 reported a runway excursion during landing
B737-800 Captain reported a flap disagree during approach. The flight crew executed a missed approach; were able to set the flaps using an alternate method; and continued to a normal landing.

B747-400 Captain reported oil quantity was low and decreasing on #2 engine. The Flight Crew was advised to return to departure airport for additional maintenance. The Flight Crew conducted an air turn back and dumped fuel prior to landing.

Air carrier Captain reported a CFIT event during approach due to a miscommunication with ATC on approach which resulted in a 'low altitude' call. Flight crew climbed to the correct altitude and was re-cleared for the approach.

Challenger Captain reported while on final to an uncontrolled airport having an aircraft enter the runway opposite direction to their aircraft resulting in the flight crew executing a go around and returning to the airport.
Dispatcher reported a new Flight Crew reported smoke in the cockpit on initial climb. Flight crew performed an air turn back to make a precautionary landing. This was a repeat discrepancy.

Air Carrier Dispatcher reported a flight crew received conflicting Hazmat documents during preflight regarding whether Hazmat cargo had been loaded onboard. After conferencing between Captain and ground personnel; it was determined that Hazmat cargo was loaded onboard allowing flight to depart with correct documents.

ERJ 170 Captain reported a wind-shear encounter which led to an altitude deviation on the missed approach.  Captain reported that the First Officer was flustered and unable to select appropriate modes of automation; flap configurations or respond to radio calls for the remainder of the flight.
Air Carrier Captain reported encountering a large balloon with equipment attached several thousand feet directly above their flight path while crossing the Pacific Ocean enroute to Hawaii.
Air carrier Dispatcher planned flight unaware of aircraft speed limitation noncompliance with STARs; resulted in need for an FAA exemption.

Flight crew reported their aircraft turned the wrong direction when intercepting final. ATC advised the reporters that three other aircraft did the same thing leading the pilots to think it might be 5G interference.

A321 First Officer reported receiving an ECAM on flap system 1 after flap retraction. After conferring with Dispatch and Maintenance; the flight crew elected to continue to destination airport and requested priority handling on arrival.
Air carrier Maintenance Supervisor reported a HAZMAT package was rejected multiple times due to improper processing and incomplete documentation.
PA28-140 student pilot reported brake malfunction resulted in successful landing and purposeful exit to the dirt between taxiways.

EMB/ERJ-145 flight crew reported continuing to an uneventful landing in spite of CFIT indications of multiple GPWS alerts during a visual final approach. Flight crew maintained visual clearance of the rising terrain.
B787-800 Captain reported a loud constant sound during climb and cruise. Flight crew requested priority handling to return to departure airport where a safe landing was executed.
PA-44 instructor pilot with trainee reported a loss of power from the right engine during the takeoff roll. The takeoff was rejected and returned to maintenance.
Waco YKS-6 pilot reported not shutting off the static collection can valve during pre-flight resulting in oil pressure loss during cruise flight. The pilot decided to divert to a nearby airport.
A320 Captain reported receiving an ECAM for Engine 1 Compressor Vane during takeoff roll. Flight crew rejected takeoff and returned to the gate.
MD-11 Captain and Dispatcher reported a loss of fuel quantity during cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a prohibited Hover Board was mistakenly allowed to be transported on flight. The error was discovered after the flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported obstacle alert while on approach to DCA.
Dispatcher reported loss of center hydraulic system resulted in the flight crew performing an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
DA40 pilot reported the engine sputtered after takeoff. Pilot circled back to land.
Flight Instructor providing instruction at a non towered airport reported a NMAC with a cropduster aircraft that was not communicating on CTAF.
Air carrier First Officer reported a hydraulic system failure on final approach to a non-towered airport. The flight landed and stopped on the runway and was towed to the jet bridge.
Air carrier Captain reported a near-collision during taxi at DCA airport.
Single Pilot reported a forced landing due to a malfunctioning engine. No injuries; no damage to aircraft or property.
Pilot flying Beech v35 aircraft reported exiting onto a runway instead of taxiway per clearance resulting in a go-around by another aircraft on approach.
B777 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL320 in trail of a B747.

A single engine pilot reported a loss of control while attempting to land. The aircraft drifted left of center and while airborne made contact with some runway lights. With help from the accompanying CFI; a go-around was accomplished and then a normal landing.
B767-300 flight crew reported a LE Slat Disagree light on approach. The flight crew performed a missed approach and ran the QRH and check lists and landed at the destination airport.
A321 pilot reported landing gear failed to extend on approach. The flight crew performed a go-around; extended the landing gear via alternate means; and landed.
A320 First Officer reported windshear during take off climb resulting in execution of windshear escape maneuver.
General aviation pilot reported runway excursion during landing. Pilot taxiied back onto runway and then to parking.
B737-700 Captain reported a near miss with a parked aircraft during push back. The Captain stopped the push back crew; disconnected the aircraft; and safely taxied from the ramp.
Captain reported loss of the Left Air Data Computer in cruise. This caused unreliable airspeed indications. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
B737 flight crew reported the aircraft veered uncontrollably to the right during takeoff roll. Captain rejected takeoff and returned to the gate.
CRJ-900 Captain reported on departure experiencing fuel smell in the flight deck and flight attendant reporting smoke in the cabin resulting in returning to the departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported seeing a UAS pass 500 ft. below the aircraft while approaching to land.
B737 flight crew reported a yaw damper fail message and flap malfunction while on approach. The crew broke off the approach; ran checklists; and configured the aircraft for a successful landing.
Pilot reported loss of situational awareness and entering an unusual attitude after inadvertently entering unforecast IMC conditions and icing.
B757 First Officer reported multiple system anomalies resulted from the inflight structural failure of the radome.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported losing telemetry data while in cruise and an automated return to home was imitated.
B737 Captain reported engine cowl anti-ice caution light during cruise. The crew continued to the destination and landed safely.
Pilot reported engine vibration while in cruise. The flight crew requested completed a diversion and precautionary landing.
C206 pilot reported a tire failure on landing rollout.
CE-680A flight crew reported a Low Altitude Alert and Glide Slope Warning while on approach.

C-152 pilot reported a critical ground conflict by crossing the runway hold short line during departure taxi. A landing aircraft executed a safe go around while the departing aircraft taxied behind hold short line.

Air carrier flight crew reported during push back from gate the ground crew prevented a ground conflict with another aircraft that was pushing back behind them by proactively pulling them back into the gate.

Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain was having difficulty establishing communication with the push back crew. With the Captain distracted the aircraft began to roll backward. The Captain reset the brakes.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported the door handle lifted about 8 inches into the opening position while the aircraft was still in flight; during the landing.
Air carrier Captain reported executing a sudden stop while taxiing at ORD to avoid a collision with a fuel truck.
Flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning on the RDG RNAV (GPS) Runway 31 approach for nearby towers even though they flew the approach accurately.
B767 flight crew reported performing a rejected takeoff due to loss of EGT indication on the left engine. Postflight; Maintenance found an EGT sensor had been improperly secured and vibrated loose.
HLN Tower Controller reported a NMAC between two opposite direction aircraft.
Duchess 76 Flight Instructor reported a nose gear collapse during landing. The Instructor secured the aircraft and waited for airport operations to assist.
Student Pilot flying C-172 aircraft reported runway excursion on takeoff roll due to ice on runway.
Air carrier Captain reported vibrations in the rudder pedals and flight attendant reported vibrations in the cabin. Flight diverted to nearest airport as a result.
CRJ-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL in trail of a B767-400.
Flight Attendant reported the plane landed so hard they injured their back and passengers were screaming.
C680 First Officer reported while conducting a Visual Approach they turned to avoid a tower and received a low altitude warning from ATC and a TCAS/RA for traffic climbing off an adjacent airport.
Pilot reported encountering a loss of control due to severe turbulence and wind shear on descent and approach into SEA.

Flight Crew reported the Flight Director commanded them to level off while conducting an RNAV approach due to failure to set the missed approach altitude. The crew continued the approach visually and became unstable resulting in a low altitude alert from the tower.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the pattern at EDU airport.
PC-12 Captain reported a cockpit window malfunction during cruise. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
Cessna 206 pilots reported they flew a non-airworthy aircraft after they had been informed by maintenance and management the aircraft was airworthy.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while under flight following with ATC.
B737 MAX 8 Flight Crew reported after a flight the wheel fuse plugs melted while using normal braking.
A tug driver and wing walker reported while pushing back an aircraft the aircraft disconnected from the towbar and rolled over the towbar.
Air carrier flight crew reported poor radar equipment failed to indicate the severity of weather resulted in wind shear encounter and temporary loss of vertical control.

Air Carrier First Officer reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of smoke in the aft cabin during initial climb. Flight crew immediately requested priority handling for an air return where a safe landing was executed. Airport support personnel met and escorted flight to parking gate.
Air carrier Captain reported a possible airborne conflict with a UAS or a laser event during arrival. No evasive action was taken.

Air Carrier First Officer reported the pilot crew realized; in cruise; the weight and balance data were incorrect. The pilots did not question the small passenger load; which is common during the COVID-19 pandemic; before takeoff.
Military flight crew flying a T-45 aircraft reported an NMAC during approach to landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported a GPS interruption while on approach to Runway 16L at DEN.

Air carrier First Officer reported while in cruise experiencing turbulence resulting in an aircraft upset; the flight crew was able to regain control of the aircraft and landed safely at their destination.

Air carrier flight crew reported during initial approach they were instructed to go around due to reported FOD on the runway. As they were initiating the go around; they did not raise the flaps and they got a landing gear aural warning.

A319 flight crew reported a Flight Control/Slat anomaly when Flaps were selected to position 3. This resulted in several associated flight control issues; and the aircraft reverted to Direct Law; then Alternate Law; before a landing at the destination airport was accomplished.

Tug Driver reported another tug collided into the reporter's vehicle and continued driving away; despite the reporter stopping to allow the other vehicle to pass. The reporter stated the path is too narrow for two tugs to pass and has insufficient lighting.

A Flight Instructor reported an aircraft conducted unsafe aerobatic maneuvers over the airfield after the Control Tower closed. The reporter stated many pilots fly 'recklessly' over the airspace since the hours of the Tower were reduced due to lack of staff.
Air carrier Captain reported difficulty seeing the spot locations at CLT due to a lack of lighting at night and expressed concern about minimal clearance between aircraft on designated taxi lines.
Flight Instructor with student reported a corporate jet cut them off during taxi requiring the Flight Instructor to brake to avoid a collision.
Air carrier ground personnel reported during unloading there were DG parcels that had a sticker labeled 'do not fly; with the sticker also on the DG placard."
Captain reported ATC issued a go around instruction at approximately 50 feet due to an aircraft crossing the active runway and complied with instructions.
Pilot reported engine roughness and unable to control engine RPM after take off. The Pilot requested priority handing and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
EMB-145 First Officer reported an unexpected wind gust resulted in a temporary loss of control and rejected takeoff.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event of 'dirty socks odor' during descent to destination.
Pilot reported on climb out; taking evasive action to avoid traffic on unannounced nonstandard pattern entry; resulted in a NMAC and airspace violation.
C172 Flight Instructor reported the engine cowl flap detached during climbout resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
