Flight crew flying CL350 aircraft reported uncommanded rudder trim during climb out. Flight crew diverted and returned to departure airport.
First Officer reported excessively high engine vibration in flight; followed by #1 engine failure. The flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
A320 Captain reported a fumes event of varying intensity from preflight to arrival at destination where maintenance removed the aircraft from service.
Flight Instructor reported an a momentary loss of situational awareness caused the aircraft to descend to about 500 feet AGL in rising terrain.
Pilot reported waypoints dropped out of the aircraft's Honeywell FMS. The reporter suggested upgrade from current software due to 'known Issues' with the current software.
Aircraft technician reported using incorrect lubricating oil when servicing IDG's.
A pilot reported the fuel filler cap came off in flight. The reporter landed and refueled but failed to have a mechanic inspect the cap.
B777-300 ground personnel reported flight crew did not set brakes prior to tow bar being disconnected.; "PA28 flight instructor reported solo student loss of control on landing."
Air carrier Captain reported a de-ice truck struck the right winglet while the aircraft was parked at the gate.
EMB-175 flight crew reported deviating from cleared airspeed due to a misunderstanding.

General aviation instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft while operating IFR on a training flight during cruise in instrument conditions. The instructor descended from the assigned altitude to avoid the other aircraft.
B767 Captain reported numerous delays were caused by mechanical; communications and ground weather. The flight eventually cancelled and the Captain called out fatigued due to the situation.
C172 pilot reported low oil pressure on approach. Pilot landed uneventfully.
A TRACON Controller reported they allowed Tower to issue an aircraft a heading off the SID which resulted in the aircraft flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Air carrier First Officer reported a fume/smoke event during cruise. After troubleshooting the flight crew returned to the departure airport where Maintenance met the flight and assigned an aircraft swap.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving an ACARS message during cruise regarding radioactive cargo that was loaded incorrectly. The flight continued safely to destination.
Air carrier pilot reported while on short final a twin piston aircraft taxied onto the runway; resulting in executing a missed approach to avoid collision.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a fume event during climb described as musty 'locker room' odor. The Captain elected to request priority handling to execute an air return to departure airport. A safe overweight landing was accomplished and maintenance was requested.
C172 pilot departing from a non towered airport reported a NMAC during initial climb.
EMB-145 flight crew reported responded to EGPWS on a night RNAV backed visual approach. Crew reported ambiguity with company page and procedure.
B777 flight crew reported a loud bang on climbout resulting in a return to the departure airport.
CJ3 Citation First Officer reported on takeoff roll; main tire failure and subsequent brake failure resulted in a critical equipment issue; ground loss of control and runway excursion.
B737 pilot reported during taxi they lost AC power after shutting down the APU. They lost both #1 and #2 engine driven generators; restarted the APU and restored AC power and returned to the gate.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported disarming the door at the gate before the seatbelt sign was turned off.
First Officer reported Captain accidentally shut off both generators while performing the after takeoff flow.
PA28 pilot reported engine failure on short final and continued to a successful landing.
B767 First Officer reported operating an aircraft in revenue service with the incorrect fuel management panel installed. The aircraft was later removed from service and the correct panel installed.
Tow driver with aircraft reported a ground conflict after making a quick stop to avoid an aircraft taking off.
EMB flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence in descent; unknown whether turbulence was related to wake of preceding aircraft or clear air turbulence.
A320 flight crew reported that a gate agent opened the armed aircraft door and partially deployed the escape slide. Maintenance was called and aircraft had to be swapped.
Air carrier Captain reported the Final Dangerous Goods report contained multiple errors. With no simple solution available; the Captain had the Dangerous Goods shipment unloaded prior to departure.
B737 air carrier pilot reported during pushback the Captain requested to be pushed back further. Ground crew told them 'to wait' after which the aircraft began to roll forward on top of the towbar.
Center Controller reported that the ZZZ Center Contingency Plan was not followed for COVID -19 ATC cleaning of an underlying Approach facility.
Small aircraft pilot reported performing an off airport landing due to fuel exhaustion after experiencing greater than anticipated fuel burn in flight.
Vans RV-14 pilot reported a NMAC with an IFR air carrier flight that was being vectored for an ILS approach to a nearby airport.
Air carrier Captain reported failure of GPS and ADSB inbound to DEN.

Air carrier maintenance personnel reported overwing slide bottle received in maintenance stores without required Hazmat label. Reporter stated that without proper labeling of Hazmat aircraft components result may in improper handling and storage of Hazmat item.

Air carrier flight crew reported they lost their GPS signal in Korean airspace due to GPS jamming; and the GPS system never returned to normal; even after leaving the area where jamming was occurring. Loss of GPS reportedly led to a minor course deviation at destination.
PA-28 Cherokee pilot reported an engine fire occurred as the pilot was staring the engine on the ramp. The pilot used a fire extinguisher and called for help.

EMB-190 pilot reported a Landing Gear Weight On Wheels System Fail EICAS message after gear retraction. The flight crew elected to make an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
C172 Flight Instructor reported cockpit smoke and fumes during climb. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier Captain reported performing a high speed rejected takeoff when ATC cancelled the takeoff clearance due to a conflict with landing traffic on another runway.
A320 flight crew reported experiencing 'strong' airframe vibrations during climb which resulted in a return to the departure airport.

Captain reported being informed of a tail pipe fire and originally thought it was the #1 Engine; instead of the #2 Engine. Airport CFR responded and the Captain dry motored the engine to extinguish the fire on the #2 engine.

A320 First Officer reported receiving an ECAM for Engine 2 EGT over limit. A go-around was performed and the flight crew ran the QRH and check lists. Priority handling was requested; the affected engine was shut down and a precautionary landing was performed at destination airport.
Air Carrier Maintenance Clerk reported mistakenly shipping a Defibrillator by air shipment instead of the required ground shipment.

CRJ-900 air carrier crew reported an altitude deviation and GPWS alert for a low altitude while descending on downwind leg for the approach. The Captain reported incorrectly hearing and mis-setting an altitude of 2000 ft. in the altitude selector of the mode control panel; instead of the assigned altitude of 3000 ft. When the GPWS alert sounded the Captain disconnected the autopilot; and started a climb to 3000 ft.; and resumed the approach.
A300 pilot reported engine oil quantity in Engine 1 decreased then later indicated zero during cruise. The flight crew diverted and after landing; shut down the affected engine.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they made a flight with their UAS unaware of the FAA regulations.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Pilot reported inadequate radio reception below 8000 ft. while flying through ZBW airspace on airway T216.
A319 Captain reported First Officer descended below glidepath resulting in a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
BE-35 pilot reported fuel problems; being VFR in IMC and ultimately diverting to the nearest airport.

First Officer reported a trailing edge flap asymmetry indication and performed a go around. After running the QRH procedures; it was determined to be only an indication problem and the flight continued to landing at the destination airport.

EMB-145 crew reported their aircraft continued rolling after normal and parking brake application failed to hold position at end of push-back. Crew reported aircraft stopped after electric hydraulic pumps were selected on.
Air carrier Check Captain reported a 'burning oil' odor during boarding. All passengers and crew members were deplaned.
B737-700 flight crew reported an upset occurred when they encountered wake turbulence climbing through FL350 in the vicinity of AUS.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that after the aircraft encountered turbulence an electrical fire odor was detected in the cabin. The flight continued to landing.
Helicopter pilot reported a bird strike that resulted in a forced off field landing.
A300 flight crew reported low altitude warning from ATC during descent.
CRJ550 flight crew reported Landing Gear failed to retract during climbout. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
MD-11 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B777 on descent into MEM that resulted in an inflight upset. Control was regained and the flight landed safely.
Air carrier First Officer reported they descended below terrain minimums on a visual approach and received a low altitude alert from ATC.

Part 107 UAS instructor reported they were given LAANC approval for the flight. There was no overlay of the approved airspace and the UAS crew was unsure if they remained in approved airspace for the entire flight.
An A11 TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft not communicating with ATC flew into conflict with two separate aircraft conducting instrument approaches. One of the conflicts was a NMAC.

A Center Controller reported a skydiving operation aircraft was operating along the departure path of two air carrier departures. The first air carrier was issued a traffic alert and the second departure turned off course below the Minimum IFR Altitude to avoid jumpers.
A student pilot reported loss of control during landing resulted in a runway excursion before joining a taxiway to parking.
B737 NG flight crew reported the First Officer continuing the takeoff after the Captain's seat rolled back during the takeoff roll at approximately 120 kts.

PA-44 Flight Instructor reported student entered an unusual attitude and lost aircraft control after entering IMC conditions during a night VFR cross country flight. Instructor regained control after descending 1500 feet and continued on to destination.
Ramp agent reported a ground tug tractor rolled back toward the aircraft when the tractor was left in neutral and the brake was not set. The tug tractor jack knifed and did not damage the aircraft.

ZMA Oceanic Controller reported radar and communication equipment failures led to a loss of non-radar separation between multiple aircraft. The reporter noted this is a recurring issue and needs to be repaired without delay.

ZAB Center Controller reported transmitter sites routinely fail and the equipment issues are not being addressed appropriately creating unsafe situations. An additional site is needed to ensure ATC communications.
B737 Captain reported a leading edge slat malfunction on final approach. The flight crew continued to an uneventful landing.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported the company manuals do not state the specific dry ice limitations for the B787 model aircraft.
General aviation flight crew reported a near miss with an unidentified object. The flight crew avoided the object and continued their flight.

Cessna 210 pilot reported the electric trim moved full down and could not be disconnected by using the yoke switch. Reporter stated they reset the circuit breaker and the trim then functioned normally.
C-310 pilot reported loss of aircraft control due to cabin door opened inflight. Pilot was able to partially close door and regain control.
A Flight Instructor on downwind leg to a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with another aircraft on downwind which took evasive action.
Air carrier flight crew reported a blown main gear tire during takeoff roll.
Fight Instructor reported a UAS in the traffic pattern caused near midair collisions with two training aircraft.

Pilot reported a Ground Critical Conflict with an airport truck. The pilot stated the truck crossed the runway hold short line as the pilot was taking off with no communication from the airport vehicle.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to SEA from the preceding aircraft.
Air carrier pilot reported a track heading deviation due to miscommunication with NCT departure about a vector to the next fix.

Pilot reported turning off the #2 Fuel Boost Pump switch when attempting to balance fuel for an MEL procedure; resulting in an engine flame-out. The engine was successfully restarted and the flight continued.
Pilot reported landing without use of PAPI lighting while landing at PTKK airport.  PAPI's were out of service but a NOTAM had been not issued.
Air carrier Load Lead ground personnel reported Hazmat cargo was not correctly packaged and not properly sealed for air transport. The Load Lead person had the Hazmat package held off.
M20P pilot reported runway excursion at non-towered airport due to windshift during landing rollout.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a conflict between an IFR arrival and a high speed highly maneuverable aircraft just clear of the PSP Class Delta/TRSA.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported an electric wheelchair improperly configured for transport in bulk cargo. The Ground Personnel also stated the staging guide was missing the cargo weight.

A319 Captain reported the failure of ELAC2 in flight and discovered that it was only re-settable on the ground. The Captain elected to continue to destination airport and entered the discrepancy in the aircraft log book.
Air carrier flight crew reported shutting down one engine due to loss of oil pressure. Flight crew diverted to nearby airport.
Light transport jet Captain reported a NMAC while on final approach with 2 military aircraft that had passed just off the right wing and approximately 200 feet overhead.
GA pilot reported an NMAC with a skydiver and jump plane during takeoff and climb from MWO non-towered airport.
B737 flight crew reported a ground proximity warning during final approach. Flight crew initiated a climb and then continued the approach.
Air carrier pilot reported a near midair collision with a UAS while they were on approach. The flight continued without incident.
Pilot reported a runway excursion due to an inability to effectively manipulate the rudder pedals while wearing sub optimal footwear.
C172 pilot reported a jet blast event while taxiing behind a corporate jet aircraft.
EMB-145 flight crew reported low engine oil pressure on final approach and continued the approach to a precautionary landing.
Flight Instructor and trainee reported that during a training flight the left main gear collapsed on landing; causing the aircraft to veer off the runway. Both pilots exited the aircraft safely.
B737 flight crew reported compressor stalls during climb and elected to return to the departure airport.

EUG TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict between an air carrier aircraft and a VFR helicopter. Controller suggested EUG should be a Class C airport requiring VFR aircraft to be in contact with ATC while crossing air carrier approach/departure paths.
Air carrier First Officer reported that during pushback from the gate another air carrier aircraft passed dangerously close to their aircraft and the pushback crew.

EMB-170 air carrier pilots reported a APPR 2 NOT AVAIL EICAS message in the climb; resulting in a GPWS terrain warning. The First Officer continued to fly the departure procedure and the warning stopped after a few seconds.

CRJ-900 air carrier crew reported an unstabilized approach which resulted in a low altitude alert from ATC. The crew corrected the low altitude condition and then stabilized the aircraft below stabilization minimums.
ERJ-175 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B777 on descent into DEN.
C172 Check Pilot reported conducting a private pilot checkride and getting in close proximity to another aircraft that was on an IFR flight plan.
Air carrier First Officer reported a near midair collision with a silver object; possibly a weather balloon; while they were on descent.
Air carrier Captain reported while attempting to start the aircraft engines receiving several ECAM message for both engines resulting in securing the engines and refusing to accept the aircraft.
Medevac pilot reported they flew off course on their departure and received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC as they were below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a UAS while on approach to landing.

A321 flight crew reported inexperienced ground personnel could not disconnect the towbar from the nose gear. During the final attempt the aircraft disconnected from the towbar and rolled onto the towbar.
EMB-145 Captain reported Flight Attendant's company provided battery equipped bag was refused in aircraft cargo. Captain elected to carry the bag in the passenger cabin.
Air taxi flight crew reported flying too low and close to terrain during departure.
Pilot flying LA-4 aircraft reported Landing Gear malfunction on landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during a visual approach. Flight crew continued approach and landed.
Student pilot on downwind leg of the traffic pattern reported NMAC with another aircraft on departure climb.
Flight Instructor reported while departing having to take evasive action to avoid a collision with an aircraft that departed the parallel runway;
PA28 pilot reported runway incursion during taxi.
Flight Instructor with student pilot reported loss of control during final approach and landing resulted in runway excursion and damage to the aircraft and an airport sign.
A321 pilot reported during gate arrival they were abruptly stopped by ground personnel to avoid hitting a tug parked nearby. Pilot cited poor ramp lighting as contributing to the event.

GA flight Instructor reported a student on a solo flight experienced a hard landing and bounced the aircraft twice before regaining control and taxiing off. The aircraft was flown by other pilots a few days after the incident before it was found to have fire wall damage during a scheduled maintenance inspection.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a fumes event prior to pushback. Maintenance was requested and passengers were deplaned.

Air taxi Captain reported going onto the wrong taxiway due to confusion with the taxiway signage at HND Airport as the non-movement taxiway was not depicted with a name on the chart nor marked at the airport itself.
B737 flight crew flying reported erroneous flight guidance information on approach.
A LR40 pilot reported that the cockpit windshield cracked during climb and a return to departure airport.
SR22 pilot reported smoke in the cabin due to a failed PFD panel.

B777 crew reported high engine vibrations during cruise on engine that had just been changed but not 'balanced' which would have been customary with an engine change.  Crew was advised by Maintenance that the vibrations may be higher but were uncomfortable flying plane over water for extended period with limited guidance in their manuals as to flight limits vs. maintenance limits.
Pilot reported sun in eyes interfered with seeing the altimeter; resulting in a descent towards the ground at low altitude.
Liberty XL-2 instructor pilot reported aircraft engine problems after takeoff resulting in a return to departure airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a Ground Proximity Warning during initial approach into HLN airport.
Air Carrier flight crew reported the Captain unknowingly allowed a prohibited type of suitcase on the aircraft and did not realize until after deplaning.
TRACON Controller reported their trainee issued a descent clearance to an aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported an airborne conflict resulting in a TCAS RA.
B777 Maintenance Technicians reported failure of the middle rudder power control unit during job; resulted in aircraft damage and a hydraulic leak.
Air carrier Captain reported seeing an airport vehicle on the runway during takeoff roll. Flight crew continued takeoff and overflew vehicle.
Air carrier Captain reported conducting a go-around following an unstable approach. During the second approach attempt the flight crew received a caution obstacle" alert."
Maintenance technician reported failing to detect a cracked piston during a 50 hour engine borescope inspection. The crack that had gone unnoticed was the cause of the engine failure.
Flight Attendant reported concern after a passenger stated he and his wife had recently recovered from COVID-19.

A319 flight crew reported in the descent receiving a HYD G RSVR LO LVL ECAM resulting in the flight crew diverting to an airport with a longer runway; the flight crew landed safely at the divert airport.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAS during climb. No evasive action was taken.

General aviation pilot reported the DYO NDB on the RUT ODP is incorrectly listed as a fly-by waypoint instead of a flyover waypoint; and could cause the GPS to direct the aircraft into the path of mountainous terrain.

Dispatcher reported receiving an ACARS message from an MD-11 flight crew stating they had a HYD 3 Fail warning.  The Dispatcher coordinated with Maintenance Control and ATC as the flight returned to the departure airport for a safe landing.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they learned of a possible airspace violation after landing from their flight.
B767 flight crew reported an unstable approach coupled with an EGWPS terrain warning and non-adherence to SOP's contributed to the crew descending below minimums on an RNAV approach in poor weather.
Pilot reported entering TFR without a clearance.
B737 flight crew reported momentary loss of aircraft control during taxi out for takeoff.

A319 pilot reported multiple bangs and significant yawing during climb with the #2 engine showing 1 quart of oil. Pilot reduced thrust to idle; returned to airport; and shut engine down after a normal landing.
General aviation pilot reported a runway incursion when the pilot taxied past the assigned taxiway hold point and a landing aircraft was instructed to go around by ATC.

EMB-145 First Officer reported a diversion caused by a malfunctioning hydraulic pump that led to a loss of system fluid. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at the diversion airport.
Air carrier flight crew flying 737 aircraft reported electrical system malfunction inflight. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
Piper PA-28 Pilot reported setting his altimeter incorrectly resulting in a Class D airspace penetration; followed by a terrain warning while attempting a practice instrument approach.

Air carrier First Officer reported while on visual approach; pilot flying decided to descend to FAF altitude early; resulted in ATC issuing a low altitude alert.  First Officer stated more training was needed for visual approach techniques.

Air carrier technician reported that they were informed by the pilot that the aircraft had been struck by a ground service vehicle; and the supervisor allegedly deviated from proper procedure in resolving the damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported firm braking was required to avoid a collision with another air carrier aircraft during taxi.
PA-23 pilot reported an unsafe gear warning. After a tower flyby the gear appeared down and locked. During the landing the nose wheel gear collapsed. The pilot reported minor damage.
B767 Captain reported on preflight the APU failed twice resulted in aircraft refusal due to ETOP requirement.
Air carrier Ramp personnel reported a smart bag was transported with battery not removed prior to loading in cargo compartment.
A Flight Attendant reported a catering truck came into contact with an open door which could have resulted in an escape slide deployment.
Flight Instructor reported left engine failure during final approach. The flight crew also reported erratic fuel gauge indications yet the flight landed without incident.

EMB-170 flight crew reported landing gear would not retract after takeoff. The flight crew went into holding until fuel was burned to below maximum landing weight; then made a precautionary landing at departure airport.

B757 flight crew reported on departure experiencing wind shear conditions resulting in abnormal pitch attitude and climb rate; over speeding the flaps. The flight crew regained control of the aircraft and continued to their destination.

Pilot reported Auto Throttle problems on climb out. Excessive force was required to manually move the Left Throttle and Auto Throttle was unresponsive. The Flight Crew determined the best course of action was continue to destination airport; operating the Throttles manually.
767 flight crew reported engine fuel bypass EICAS light came shortly after departure. Flight diverted and landed uneventfully.
C-162 pilot reported a cabin entry door opened in flight. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
A319 flight crew reported receiving a GPWS alert while on approach.

B737-800 flight crew reported they failed to shut down both engines during their shutdown flow. The crew stated distraction caused by a conflict with a tug contributed to the event. Fatigue was also cited as a factor.
B737 first officer reported during cruise descent they got a low oil pressure indication on the number 1 engine. They ran the QRH and performed the engine shutdown procedures.
C152 reported an NMAC event during landing training.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported the aircraft was tugged to within inches of jet bridge and was stopped by maintenance personnel from hitting it.

SR22 Flight Instructor reported engine roughness and a knocking sound coming from the engine while in cruise flight. As the problem got worse; the flight crew opted to safely divert at a nearby airport.
B757 flight crew reported they lost the RT Hydraulic System on approach; complied with QRH; requested priority handling and landed at destination airport.
Flight Instructor on training reported RTO due to engine malfunction.
Cessna 172 Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid an aircraft that disregarded the Tower's instructions.

Air carrier crew reported on vectors to a visual approach on the base leg they received an EGPWS alert while above the MIA.  Air crew reported another EGPWS alert on final and above the published altitude. Air crew were in VMC conditions and above the published altitudes during the entire approach and landed safely at the airport.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a fume event during boarding described as dirty socks odor. Flight Attendant crew experienced multiple physiological symptoms.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported selecting the wrong checklist when they experienced a stabilizer trim runaway on climb out. The problem was corrected and the flight continued to destination.
A TRACON Controller conducting OJT reported an air carrier reported a NMAC with a VFR aircraft.
Maintenance Technician incorrectly closed an MEL on a B767 item due to not reading all of the logbook updates.
GA Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC during takeoff requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

A TRACON Controller reported a GA pilot became disoriented in IMC and deviated off course when their attitude indicator failed. The GA Aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude and into a confliction with an aircraft on approach.
Air carrier pilot reported the NOTOC was signed and DG had been loaded. The load summary stated that 'No Restricted Articles' were loaded.

Champion Citabria instructor pilot reported loss of directional control after landing resulted in a runway excursion. It was later determined that the heel brake pedals had been installed incorrectly and may have contributed to the event.

A319 Captain reported during the descent receiving a GPWS alert with the aircraft flying towards terrain resulting in the flight crew climbing to a safe altitude and ATC cleared them for the approach where they landed safely.
A320 Captain reported being dispatched with an MELed IDG and experienced the loss of the APU inflight; causing a diversion due to single generator operations.

PA-28 Instructor Pilot reported a blockage of the pitot mast resulted in a loss of IAS; and a diversion to the nearest airport. Upon landing; the aircraft departed the runway surface and struck a snow bank.
Remote PIC reported their commercial UAS crew was contracted to assist a public safety agency. The crew learned after the operation was complete they had operated without a required waiver.

Air carrier cargo pilot reported an autopilot flight control malfunction message and degraded flight controllability issues at cruise and in the descent. The crew had difficulty maneuvering to intercept the localizer and executed a missed approach then returned for a safe landing.
C680 pilot reported the failure of one air data computer at cruise altitude. Pilot used the other air data computer to continue to destination.

Air taxi First Officer reported a waypoint's position in the company database and Jeppesen was different from what ATC provided. ATC had issued an update to the ELVAE fix location but the aircraft's FMS did not contain the updated position nor was the flight crew aware of the update; resulting in a course deviation.
Air carrier Safety Analyst reported undeclared dangerous goods cargo arrived at destination airport. The event was caused by a communication breakdown during TSA departure screening.
Small transport pilot reported a near mid-air collision occurred while maneuvering in the traffic pattern of PVG airport.
Air carrier Captain reported excessively bright lighting in the MEM gate area floods the flight deck with light; making it difficult to see the aircraft marshaller while taxiing into the gate.
EMB-145 pilot reported engine failure during departure climb. Flight returned to departure airport and landed.
A319 ramp crew reported damage to the engine cowling due to contact with jetbridge during gate parking procedure.
Technician reported concerns over trouble shooting and the correct application of an MEL while working in a B777-200.
Air carrier flight crew reported Ground Conflict with deice vehicle while exiting deice pad.
ATC TRACON Controller reported descending an aircraft to an altitude below the MVA resulting in the aircraft flying towards the terrain.

Beech BE36 pilot reporting diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing issues with the alternate air door; and then engine problems that led to an inflight shutdown after landing was assured.
Flight Instructor on short final reported adequate obstacle clearance was not maintained from crane situated on approach path to runway.
Tug driver in the process of pushing back an air carrier aircraft was forced to slam on his brakes to avoid a collision with a ground service truck transiting the area.
Flight Instructor reported a gear-up landing while conducting a flight review in an unfamiliar aircraft at an unfamiliar location.
Air carrier flight crew reported the tow bar broke off during push back and came in to contact with the aircraft.
A tower Local Controller reported a taxiing air carrier had an engine fire. There was confusion between the Controller and Supervisor as to the proper procedure for handling the situation.
Pilot taxiing at night at 0J4; Florala Municipal Airport; reported a runway excursion and contact with a runway light which was not operating or visible at night.

General aviation Flight Instructor reported an engine failure during the takeoff roll on a training flight and rejected the takeoff on the runway. The instructor was unable to start the engine and was towed back to the hangar.

Air carrier Load Planner reported being notified of a NOTOC documentation error where an article was showing in the wrong location. The flight was delayed to correct the discrepancy prior to departure.
BE55 pilot reported a runway excursion resulted from a blown left main tire. The pilot requested assistance airport operations.

Light aircraft pilot reported he was about to take the runway at CEW; a non-towered airport; when a ground observer alerted him to traffic landing opposite direction who was not communicating on CTAF frequency.
Captain reported an ECAM for HYD Y RSVR LO LVL at cruise. The flight crew continued to destination airport and landed.
GA pilot in the traffic pattern at nighttime reported Tower extended their downwind toward an unlit mountain peak which they were aware of but could not see.
B737 Captain reported an NMAC during approach resulting in evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

TLH TRACON Controller reported a trainee controller descended aircraft below the MVA resulting in the aircraft flying towards an obstruction. ATC climbed and turned the aircraft away from the obstruction.
B737 flight crew reported safely recovering from an upset on initial approach to ORD related to wake turbulence from a preceding aircraft.
A320 Captain reported a cracked windshield during cruise resulted in a diversion.
Air carrier flight crew reported executing a missed approach after a EGPWS terrain warning on a night visual approach to CHO.
Pilot reported reported a NMAC while practicing maneuvers near a non-towered airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported an airspeed exceedance beyond Vmo and altitude deviation caused by severe turbulence.
Air carrier Safety Agent reported a wheelchair with a 35 kg battery in cargo was found unsecured at destination arrival.
BOI TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below a crossing restriction due to confusion on the pilot's part.
Aircraft owner reported the agricultural pilot who was flying the aircraft had an NMAC with a UAS during flight. No evasive action was taken.

A Single Light Aircraft Pilot; with low flight time because of the pandemic; reported a NMAC turning final after getting confused because of similar call signs. The Tower controller separated the aircraft and a normal landing was accomplished.
B737 Captain reported pushing back from the gate without receiving the DG summary.

Vans RV-6 single pilot reported an NMAC event with an aircraft located behind and 500 feet below which was not in contact with ATC. The intruder aircraft continued on a diverging course with no additional issues.
Pilot reported they became disoriented after takeoff in marginal VFR conditions. The aircraft entered a stalled condition and the pilot landed off airport.
EMB170 First Officer reported a loss of airspeed resulting in an unstable approach in gusty wind conditions. The crew performed a go around and landed safely on a different runway.
Air carrier Captain reported flying to a waypoint in the flight plan that was not in the FMC; Jeppesen database; nor was it known by ATC.
Air Carrier flight crew flying a B737 aircraft reported a GPWS alert on approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported delaying their rotation and initial climb to avoid a helicopter crossing their course.
Technician reported not reinstalling the Captain's Air Data Module after troubleshooting and installing the radar transceiver; causing an air turn back and precautionary landing.
B737-800 flight crew reported an uneventful Hazmat diversion which happened to coincide with a passenger medical event.
A321 Captain reported a Flaps/Slats Fault ECAM during approach which was corrected by completing the QRH procedure. The Captain elected to return to the departure airport with no further incidents.
C152 pilot reported an NMAC and a wake turbulence encounter departing VNC.
Pilot reported a rejected take off after communication problems with ATC resulted in Ground Conflict.
A319 flight crew reported a nose gear malfunction after takeoff resulting in a return to departure airport.

Regional Jet Technician reported the wrong component was removed for the aircraft and not documented correctly. The aircraft was removed from service and components were correctly identified and replaced as required. Aircraft records were annotated to correctly report the aircraft configuration.
B737 flight crew reported that a Flight Attendant sustained a minor injury due a braking event on the runway to avoid a truck not giving way to the taxiing aircraft.
General Aviation pilot reported taking evasive action during cruise in response to a near mid-air collision with another aircraft that had departed from a nearby airport on an IFR clearance.
Air Carrier flight crew reported while on final experiencing a TCAS RA from traffic overshooting their final and ATC directing a go around due to traffic disabled on the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported the elevator felt abnormally heavy on rotation. Flight crew returned to departure airport and experienced the same issue during landing.

CL-350 flight crew reported receiving a TAWS alert during the approach. The airport was busy with other flight school traffic. Due to the rising terrain; ATC set up the approach to be higher than normal.
Gulfstream G650 pilot reported a false localizer capture resulted in a course deviation and a terrain warning from ATC.
B757 Captain reported an inflight upset and a minor injury to a Flight Attendant after encountering wake turbulence on descent into IAD in trail of a B767.
C152 instructor pilot reported their feet became entangled in their headset cord while taxiing and they could not stop the aircraft at the hold short line they were supposed to hold short of.
Flight Attendant reported a nose landing gear issue resulted in an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Pilot reported engine failure during approach to landing caused an off airport landing; resulting in aircraft damage.
PA28 pilot reported on initial climb after Tower cleared them for takeoff a landing aircraft on short final  initiated a go around resulting in a NMAC.

B737 flight crew reported a ground conflict with an airport vehicle during landing roll at an airport that was non-towered at the time of the event. The crew did not make the proper CTAF radio announcements prior to landing.

PA28 pilot on a night training flight reported the engine stopped working and they made a successful engine-out landing at an airport. Post flight inspection revealed the engine failure resulted from fuel exhaustion.

Captain reported loss off thrust with the #2 Engine resulted in an in flight shut down of the affected engine. The flight crew diverted and began fuel jettison procedures and a subsequent overweight landing was made at the diversion airport.

ZJX Center Controller reported insufficient ATC staffing and lack of sector flow management initiatives despite requests from Controllers resulting in sectors overloaded with traffic; frequency congestion; and Controllers refusing to take handoffs. The reporter was unable to concentrate and lost ability to perform required tasks. Other Controllers could not return to the work area after breaks due to undue stress.

A Flight Instructor reported the student lost control of the aircraft during the landing flare and would not relinquish controls in a timely manner resulting in landing on the grass next to the runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an amended clearance due to a ATC Center going ATC Zero and questioned if they had violated an FAR by flying in uncontrolled airspace.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending below glide path on approach to CHS after the altimeter was mis-set by 0.1 inches.

ERJ 170/175 Flight Crew reported Engine #2 vibrations in flight. The Flight Crew ran the checklist and QRH; solving the vibration problem. Engine #2 then failed. The Flight Crew performed an in flight shut down; and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
A320 Captain reported receiving a hydraulic fluid low level message during climb. Flight returned to departure airport and aircraft was towed to the gate.

Flight Instructor reported observing a UAS pass 100 feet above their aircraft as they departed a Class D airport.  The pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision and then reported the incident to ATC.

B737 Captain reported ILS signal interference causing autopilot to pitch up and down excessively during an ILS PRM approach. Captain reported descending below crossing restriction on the approach when taking over aircraft control after the autopilot disconnected.
Flight Attendant reported the aircraft cleaners are not adhering to the social distancing policy.
Pilot reported a NMAC while departing IFR as a result of ATC not separating traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported he was distracted by the late retraction of the wing flaps as they parked at the gate; and the aircraft rolled backwards about 10 feet.
B737 flight crew reported a bird strike on takeoff had sheared off the Angle of Attack vane resulting in a loss of airspeed and altitude information and a return to the departure airport.
C150 instructor pilot and student reported an engine failure resulting in an off airport landing.
RV-12 Flight Instructor reported on departure experiencing a rough running engine with power drop resulting in the return to the departure airport where they landed safely.
Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with an aircraft operating without communications within the Class E Airspace.
BE-200 pilot reported a loss of engine oil pressure inflight.
Fractional jet First Officer reported putting in the wrong fix; GOLLF instead of the correct fix GOLFF; and was heading towards the wrong direction until ATC queried the flight crew.
Ground employee reported a vehicle stopped behind an aircraft being pushed back for departure; requiring the push back crew to stop operations.
B787 Captain reported a passenger dropped a personal iPad under the seat and damaged it during cruise. The damaged electronic device was placed into a thermal containment bag for safety.
C182 pilot departing an airport before the Tower opened reported a conflict with a hot air balloon which was on the side of the runway fully expanded.
An Air Taxi pilot practicing no flap landings at a non towered airport reported they temporarily lost control during landing due to an icy runway and ran over a runway edge light.

Part 107 UAS pilot was flying in an area of a natural disaster. Prior to the flight the UAS pilot checked for NOTAMs/TFRs and saw none. Reporter learned during post flight that they flew in a TFR that had been created after takeoff.

Pilot instructor reported a near miss while entering the traffic pattern at X04 non-towered airport during a training flight. The instructor identified the other aircraft by ADS-B; and performed an immediate climb to avoid the aircraft conflict.
Flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert on arrival into DEN after having to override automation in an attempt to comply with ATC instructions that kept them high on approach.

E175 Captain reported a momentary stick shaker while responding to an RA event during initial climb. The Captain recovered from the stick shaker and after confirming the flight was clear of conflict; rejoined the departure procedure.
Small Jet Captain reported after takeoff the nose gear would not retract. After troubleshooting and aggressive maneuvering; the crew was able to extend the gear and land safely.
B737 flight crew reported the master caution and number one engine overheat lights illuminated during departure climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
C172 student pilot and Instructor reported the engine lost power on initial climb from a go around and return to the airport.
Glider pilot reported that while maneuvering to land; they struck a taxiway sign; causing damage to a wing.
A Tower Controller reported they issued an aircraft on ten mile final a left 360 delay vector which place the aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Pilot of Light Transport aircraft reported a temporary loss of aircraft control when encountering a strong updraft; resulting in significant altitude gain.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during gate preflight. After assisting maintenance with the engine run ups and maintenance checks which resulted in no improvements the flight was cancelled.

Air carrier flight crew reported a DG acknowledgement document error during taxi when a second DG summary was received which indicated the DG cargo was reduced from two items to one item. After contacting Dispatch; another revised DG summary was issued which corrected the cargo back to two items.
Inspector reported no personnel at station were qualified to perform CFM56-7B fan blade replacement. Inspector reported that The Company elected to send a qualified individual from another station.
B737-800 Captain reported stabilizer trim became inoperative and elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Mitsubishi 2B-60 pilot reported during cruise the left engine failed. They requested assistance and landed.
MDT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on approach went below the assigned altitude into a lower MVA; after being in an airborne conflict with another aircraft on approach.
Air carrier Captain reported ummanifested Hazmat was loaded.
A320 Captain reported receiving an ECAM HYD B RSVR LO LVL Message on final approach. ECAM procedures were performed and the flight crew continued the approach and landing to destination.

G550 First Officer reported climbing past 41;000 feet up to 45;000 feet; hearing a distinct pop. A few seconds later; two Amber CAS Messages - L Hydraulic System Fail and L Hydraulic Quantity Low were received. The flight crew diverted to make a precautionary landing.
CRJ 200 pilot reported hydraulic system low quantity indication in cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed safely. Hydraulic fluid leak was found post-flight.
Air carrier First Officer reported the flight returned to the departure airport due to an unexplained banging noise from underneath the cabin floor.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC instructed them to go around at 400 feet AGL due to a conflict with landing traffic.
PA28R pilot reported nose gear lights failed to indicate gear down and locked resulted in precautionary safe landing and tow.
B767 flight crew reported the outer pane of the First Officer's windscreen shattered in cruise. Flight returned to departure airport and landed safely.
MIA TRACON Controller reported confusion that pilots are having with climb instructions on the FOLLZ2 SID.
C172 pilot reported loss of engine RPM that progressively got worse through the flight and diverted.
B737 NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on short final approach to DEN in trail of a B757.
B737-700 Captain reported Dispatch release missing required Hazmat information.
TRACON Controller reported missing a readback which resulted in the aircraft being below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPWS Alert on approach to TUS airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported they departed an airport after the Tower closed without obtaining approval from Center to depart.

Air carrier Captain reported a passenger handed a noticeable bulged cell phone to the Flight Attendant during cruise. As a precaution the phone was placed in a thermal containment bag until destination arrival with no further issues.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a fly away occurred for several minutes while attempting to land the UAS. Pilot regained control and landed the UAS.
Air carrier flight crew reported passing an unknown balloon at FL350.

Air carrier flight crew reported a clearance deviation due to the misunderstanding of similar sounding fixes; DYLIN and DOOIN; while descending on an arrival into EWR. ATC re-cleared the aircraft to another fix to intercept the localizer and continue the approach.
A PA28 student pilot reported an NMAC while on final approach to a non-towered airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the DG acknowledgement message was not sent prior to gate pushback as required by company policy. The DG acknowledgement message was sent prior to taxi.

B737 Captain reported almost contacting another aircraft on the ramp during push-back after another aircraft crew shouted over the radio for them to stop their push-back. Captain was able to have his crew stop and later learned that one of the wing-walkers was a 'fill-in' who may not have been trained. Communications with ground personnel were complicated by wireless headsets that didn't work necessitating using a wired headset by the push-back crew.
Air carrier Captain reported responding to TCAS RA resulted in a NMAC event while at cruise altitude in Cairo (HECC) airspace.
B737 flight crew reported they descended below the glidepath on their approach resulting in an unstable approach and a  terrain warning but the flying pilot continued the approach and landed.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to IAD airport that resulted in an inflight upset. Control was regained and a safe landing followed.
Flight Instructor with student reported aborting the takeoff when they received a call from another aircraft warning of an aircraft on the runway.
A private pilot practicing holding for an approach to a non-towered airport reported a NMAC when another aircraft passed overhead.

B767-300 Captain reported a 'terrible odor' was present upon approaching the aircraft. After the source of the smell was removed from the aircraft and the smell dissipated; the flight continued uneventfully.
Tecnam P2012 pilot reported the engines began surging after takeoff. Pilot returned to departure airport.
Pilot reported striking tree top during approach to land at a non-towered airport.

A Center Controller working TRACON Airspace which was ATC Zero due to staffing reported a departing aircraft climbed to an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude due to misunderstanding their clearance.
B757 Captain reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing pressurization issues; and low oil pressure in the left engine.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern at a non towered airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC as well as a 'too low flaps' aural warning on a visual approach to EWR.
Technician reported rudder locking tool damaged the rudder Power Control Unit during installation as another technician applied hydraulic power and centered the rudder.
B767 Captain reported the Hazmat documents provided to the flight crew contained weight errors and description errors of Lithium Ion batteries cargo.
Air taxi flight crew reported a CFTT event during approach to a non-towered airport. The crew disconnected the autopilot and added thrust to clear the terrain warning.

A Center Controller reported an Air Carrier which they were not communicating with descended from its assigned altitude. The aircraft which had shut down one engine had acknowledged a frequency change via CPDLC but remained on the previous sector's frequency.
Honda Jet flight crew aircraft reported Hydraulic Pump failure inflight.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported one of the Flight Attendant crew armed the cabin door prior to the Lead Flight Attendant door arming announcement; which could have led to an escape slide deployment.

B737-800 flight crew reported receiving a report from a passenger that was relayed by the Flight Attendant of a wing access panel not flush. Maintenance was called to inspect and found the panel attaching hardware loose and worn; with a wing support piece missing. The aircraft was then refused.
Part 107 UAS pilot learned after a flight they had flown inside controlled airspace without a clearance. Flight was completed safely without incident.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported autopilot malfunction in cruise flight resulted in the airplane pitching up followed by a TCAS RA.
PA28 Flight Instructor and student pilot reported a hard landing during training resulted in loss of aircraft control and landing gear damage.
A-75 Stearman Pilot reported loss of aircraft control on landing resulted in a wing tip scrape.

A321 flight crew reported multiple bird strikes on take-off and subsequent ECAM messages indicating nacelle and cowl damage to the #1 engine. The flight crew coordinated a fly by of the Tower to check the landing gear condition and then returned for a safe landing.
A321 Captain reported inadvertently deploying an escape slide at the gate shortly after arrival from a repositioning flight.
BE350 pilot reported landing safely after the left engine was shut down due to fuel starvation related to fuel pump failure.
Air carrier flight crew reported possibly flying through a thin layer of volcanic ash during cruise. The crew maintained the flight plan buffer distances and submitted a PIREP.
GA pilot reported during takeoff roll experiencing excessive shimmy on the nose wheel resulting in aborting the takeoff and return to the ramp.
Air Carrier Captain reported on departure the gear handle would not move to the up and retracted position resulting in the flight returning to the departure airport.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft while doing a practice approach at a non-towered airport. The pilot conducted a missed approach and departed the area.

Air Carrier First Officer reported a thick mist accompanied by a 'burning plastic' odor during Gate preflight. Captain turned off the APU bleeds and ordered all personnel off the the plane. Necessary maintenance checks were completed which eliminated the mist and odor.

Small transport pilot reported an engine failure during the climb to cruise and performed an air turnback. After landing and receiving a taxi clearance; the pilot stated to have been affected by the engine failure and may have not followed the taxi clearance.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of pressurization and diverting to a nearby airport.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a NMAC with another aircraft in traffic pattern at a non-towered airport.
B737NG Captain reported they failed to complete the landing checklist because of distractions and loss of situational awareness.
B737 MAX 9 First Officer reported a possible wake turbulence encounter on descent into DEN in trail of another B737.

A Center Controller reported two VFR military aircraft from Eglin AFB entered a holding pattern at non VFR altitudes (14;000 and 15;000 feet) adjacent to R2915 and in conflict with the major traffic flows through the sectors. The previous day the military's request for these aircraft to perform this operation on IFR flight plans had been denied.

Corporate pilot reported a traffic conflict with another aircraft when departing a non-towered airport at night with another aircraft on final approach. The aircraft on final side-stepped once they identified the aircraft departing on the runway.

Air carrier First Officer reported the aircraft began to indicate navigational jamming per an ECIAS indication. The GPS jamming was in the region transiting route that borders Egypt; Israel; Jordan and Saudi Arabia.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near midair collision with a UAS while they were on initial approach. The UAS was reported to ATC and the flight landed without further incident.

Citabria Instructor Pilot training new aircraft owner reported while attempting a crosswind landing; bounced; tried to recover landing but then applied brakes and lost directional control and veered off the runway into a fence damaging wing.

Air carrier pilot reported a possible touch down in the displaced threshold area on landing at SBP.; "Amphibious aircraft pilot reported a drop in engine oil pressure in cruise. The pilot continued to destination airport and landed."
Lancair pilot reported experiencing an inflight upset related to loss of autopilot and primary flight instruments.
Corporate Captain reported they inadvertently crossed a runway threshold without clearance resulting in ATC directing a go-around for landing traffic.
A321 First Officer reported uncommanded aircraft movement due to towbar breakaway during pushback resulted in loss of aircraft control and damage to nose gear and towbar.
CL-350 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach and opted to fly above the glide slope to avoid the effects of the wake.

B777-200 Flight Attendant reported a 'fish-like odor' fumes event during taxi which continued into the climbout. After advising the Captain and discussing the situation with a deadheading pilot; it was determined safe to continue to their destination.
C-172 Flight Instructor with student on training flight reported NMAC with another aircraft on final approach.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming occurred while on an RNAV arrival into LTFM; Istanbul; Turkey. ATC gave the carrier vectors to a successful ILS landing.
Helicopter Instructor pilot with student reported a NMAC with an agricultural aircraft while maneuvering requiring the Instructor to take evasive action to avoid a collision.

E175 flight crew reported ground personnel indicated aircraft movement when the chocks were pulled after previous maintenance work on the hydraulic accumulator. The Captain turned on hydraulic pumps and cycled parking brake; then was notified of the uncommanded aircraft movement by the ramp personnel.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported during gate marshaling the aircraft overshot the gate. After no additional ground assistance the marshaller proceeded with deplaning the passengers.

Student Pilot with an Instructor reported colliding with a Mylar party balloon on final approach. Minor damage resulted. A few scratches and a small dent near the tail section of the aircraft. There was no damage done to the control functions.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving traffic alerts from his TCAS even though ATC reported no aircraft in his vicinity.

B737 Captain reported an incomplete lithium ion battery document was provided to the Captain during pre-flight. The Captain; with the help of the First Officer was able to obtain the missing information prior to departure.
Air carrier Captain reported finding a Hazmat cargo shipment damaged and unsuitable for transport. The Hazmat cargo was unloaded prior to departure.
B737 Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing the apparent failure of an air data computer that resulted in multiple system anomalies.
Pilot reported confusing airport taxiway markings and taxi instructions at ACY airport.
E145 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on short final.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a strong burning odor accompanied by rapid increase in temperature throughout the aircraft. Flight crew requested priority handling to a suitable airport without any further incident.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a military helicopter while entering the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. Reporter was able to safely maneuver away and noted the military aircraft using civilian fields for training overwhelms and creates constant conflict with civilian traffic.
EMB 145 First Officer reported hydraulic system malfunction during climb out resulted in loss of autopilot. Flight continued to destination and landed.
DA40 student pilot reported an engine malfunction during climbout. The aircraft returned to the airport for landing.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during descent described as 'sour milk/gym socks'. The Captain was notified and the flight continued to a safe landing at destination airport.

Cessna 172 instructor reported a loss of control and runway excursion while on a training flight with a student during landing practice. There was no substantial damage to the aircraft and no injuries.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving NOTOC messages during cruise.
Air carrier Captain reported heavy braking was required when the Tower issued a late clearance to hold short of an intersecting runway that was not in accordance with normal procedures.
C172 Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC when another C172 departing a parallel runway turned into their flight path. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
A319 First Officer reported miscommunication with Captain about the parking brake resulted in uncommanded aircraft movement and a ground loss of control.

Beechcraft pilot reported a NMAC while on a GPS approach. After ATC had given a traffic alert; the pilot's traffic system became active and a light twin engine jet passed 50 feet below. The pilot continued the approach while the Controller apologized for the mishap.
B737-800 Captain reported a 'dirty socks odor' during engine. Captain returned to gate and refused aircraft due to previous fume issues.
Air Carrier Flight Attendants reported an inflight fume event consisting of musty/dirty socks odor.

Pilot and safety pilot of a light twin aircraft reported returning to departure airport for a safe landing after striking a deer on takeoff. Post flight inspection showed damage to the left propeller.
CHA TRACON Controller Instructor reported an MVA violation during a training session.
C172 Student Pilot reported a runway excursion; then gaining control of the aircraft and taxiing to the ramp.
TRACON Controller reported that an aircraft was at an altitude below the minimum vectoring altitude for the area and directed an immediate climb.
Technician reported during IDG servicing of the #2 engine IDG it was noticed that the engine driven pump depressurization valve cannon plug was disconnected.
Flight Attendant reported aircraft made a hard landing resulting in injury and maintenance inspection.
Air carrier Captain reported self-initiated go-around due to aircraft on the runway.  ATC vectored the aircraft to another approach resulting with the aircraft landing safely at the airport.
CE-560 pilot reported cabin pressurization system malfunction during climb. Flight crew descended and returned to departure airport.
Flight Attendant notified by flight crew to prepare cabin for possible gear collapse on landing. Landed without incident.
PA-28 instructor pilot with trainee reported descending below the initial approach altitude after misreading the approach chart.
Cessna 182 pilot reported a momentary loss of control during gusting cross wind landing resulted in a propstrike on landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported receiving a thrust reverser master caution in flight followed by engine failure while on landing roll.

Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern at a towered airport when another aircraft turned in front of them on final approach. The instructor took evasive action to avoid the other aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a UAS during climb.
Air carrier flight crew reported a passenger informed them they saw a UAS fly under the aircraft on final approach. Flight crew stated they had not seen the UAS.
Center Controller reported an aircraft on an instrument approach took evasive action in response to a TCAS/RA for a VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC.
Pilot reported inadvertent airspace violation due to poor chart verbiage.
C-182 pilot reported a runway excursion during the landing rollout resulted in a prop strike after coming to rest past the end of the runway.

Ramp worker reported a safety concern when another ramp worker drove up to an aircraft under push back in front of a running engine to load a bag. The other ramp worker stopped the aircraft; sent the ramp worker away and reported the safety incident.
Pilot reported observing traffic on climb out opposite direction not on CTAF; resulted in pilot delaying departure.
Tailwheel pilot reported that during landing rollout their shoe became stuck on the rudder pedal and eventually slipped off resulting in a partial group loop.
Air carrier Captain reported ATC frequency does not match published frequencies on Jeppesen approach charts for DEN airport.
Captain reported the aircraft had a leak in one of the hydraulic systems and returned to the departure airport.
PA28 pilot reported an NMAC with a military trainer type aircraft in the pattern traffic of a non-towered airport requiring evasive action to avoid a collision.
Pilot reported a ground conflict occurred during landing rollout when another aircraft took off from the opposite end of the runway.
B737 First Officer reported loss of aircraft control during turbulence encounter in cruise resulting in bank angle exceedance. Flight crew regained control and continued safely.
B757 Captain reported that a low oil level in the APU resulted in a divert. Maintenance found the APU was several quarts low and that the indicator may have erroneously been showing it was full.

Air carrier flight crew reported autoflight anomalies in cruise flight in the vicinity of MEI VORTAC; citing 5G interference as possibly contributing.  Reporter noted that two other air carrier flights in the same area were experiencing similar issues.
Corporate Captain reported a hazardous arrival procedure and accompanying charts that require modification to conform more closely with safe air traffic control for arrival to MQY airport.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported after the aircraft was shut down at the gate; the left engine started on its own possibly due to electrical power interruption related to the power cart.
A320 Flight Attendant crew reported a fume event during boarding resulting in flight delay/cancellation.

ATC Tower Controller reported a NMAC between aircraft on final and another aircraft in the base turn that was not on the Tower's frequency and talking to the radar facility. Aircraft in the base turn was sequenced back to the airport.
Pilot reported operating an aircraft with a malfunctioning number 1 Alternator which was required by the POH for flight.
Ramp personnel reported during push back the aircraft was parked crooked. When they proceeded to straighten out the aircraft the tow bar disconnected and the aircraft rolled onto the tow bar.
Pilot reported engine vibration in cruise flight and elected to divert for a precautionary landing. Upon further inspection; a broken rocker arm was found to be the cause.
Student pilot reported runway excursion during landing which resulted in the aircraft striking a runway light.
Air carrier Captain reported the flight crew failed to acknowledge the final Dangerous Goods summary due to multiple distractions and issues occurring while prepping for departure.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported a track deviation occurred departing ORD when they encountered wake turbulence from the preceding B757.
C182 Flight Instructor and Student reported that a tire failed during taxi resulting in the aircraft being disabled on a taxiway. The aircraft was towed off the taxiway.

Air carrier Captain reported the autopilot descended the aircraft prematurely before the final approach fix resulting in an ATC altitude alert. Captain disconnected autopilot; climbed to safe altitude and continued approach.
Pilot flying CTSW ultralight aircraft reported engine malfunction after takeoff and made an immediate turnback to landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported they turned on to the wrong taxiway at EWR Airport and cited confusing taxiway signage and fatigue as contributing factors.
MD11 flight crew and a dispatcher reported the aircraft's number 3 engine had a compressor stall on departure.
B757 flight crew reported a loss of hydraulic pressure indications and returned to departure airport.
A320 Pilot reported a fume event during descent characterized as a 'very strong chemical odor'. Reporter experienced multiple physiological symptoms.
A Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern which deviated from Tower instructions.
B737 flight crew reported possible malfunction with aircraft brakes during taxi to runway. Requested tow back to gate.
Pilot reported poor to nil braking action after landing at an airport in mountainous terrain.
E175 First Officer reported overshooting the final approach course after encountering wake turbulence during descent into BOS.
Flight Instructor reported on traffic pattern entry; departing traffic failed to follow standard procedure and turned into pattern traffic resulting in a NMAC.
Air carrier flight crew reported deviating from SOP in pushback procedure.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an incomplete company mobility device tag during preflight. The Captain had the gate agent complete the missing information.
Pilot reported being advised after landing that the aircraft had damage to the exterior from an undetermined cause.
B767-300 flight crew reported failure of fuel pump and hydraulic pump inflight. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Dassault Falcon 20 pilots reported engine failure during takeoff.
Flight Instructor in the pattern at a non-towered airport reported they observing a NMAC between two other aircraft turning onto the final approach course at the same point.
Flight Instructor on a training flight with a student reported a runway excursion during takeoff roll.
Air carrier First Officer reported a NMAC with a UAS during climb out. No evasive action was taken.
Maintenance technician reported a dual ECU failure on a DA-40 occurred right after rotation resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain deadheading on a company flight reported a passenger would not conform to the face mask policy and was hostile; aggressive; belligerent towards the flight attendants.
Center Controller reported a departing aircraft did not comply with the departure procedure and was issued a vector below the Minimum IFR Altitude to avoid Restricted Special Use Airspace.

PA-30 pilot reported losing control of the aircraft during the landing roll-out while the student pilot attempted to free the shoe that got stuck in the rudder/brake pedal. A runway excursion occurred and the aircraft's propeller struck a light.
ZDC Controller reported concerns about aircraft not having correct routing due to Metroplex changes.
DA40 Flight Instructor and student reported a dual ECU failure during a go-around resulting in a continual decrease in load. Flight crew returned to land and engine failed on ramp before shutdown.
Pilot reported the PAPI's at HPN Runway 16 being reversed.

An C172 Instructor pilot and their student reported as they were taxiing the instructor took the controls and turned off the taxiway to avoid another aircraft which had lost control and was head on to them.

Air Carrier flight crew reported the aircraft was dispatched with ferry fuel. Upon arrival at BUR they were unable to land on Runway 8; the planned runway; due to a tailwind and a reported wet runway. During the missed approach and maneuvering for Runway 33 the crew received a EGPWS Terrain Warning. The crew's clearance was then changed to the RNAV Visual Runway 33.
A321 Captain reported abnormal bleed air pressure on left engine during climb. Captain returned to departure airport due to icing restrictions and made an overweight landing.
C172 flight instructor with student reported an NMAC during traffic pattern entry at non-towered airport with a departing aircraft. Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Flight Instructor reported a student lost control during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
A300 flight crew reported failure of both pitch trim and autopilot systems during climbout. Flight crew performed a diversion using manual aircraft control and landed.
Cessna T206H pilot reported suspected fuel contamination and the need for theirs and another pilot's aircraft fuel system to be rebuilt after a joint trip with multiple stops.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported on a crosswind landing; student misapplied corrective control input that resulted in loss of aircraft control on touchdown.
C140 pilot reported loss of power on initial climb from a touch and go and a safe landing in a field adjacent to the airport.

Reporter identified a safety concern regarding uncharted landmarks on BFI VFR transition routes and difficulty in locating the BFI VFR transition route procedures through normal flight planning methods.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a pressurization failure.
Air Carrier flight crew flying into Tel Aviv airport reported descending below a crossing restriction on the AMOS1E STAR due to distractions from previous GPS outages during their flight and fatigue.

B787 crew reported a taxiway excursion after landing. The Captain reported the aircraft did not respond to steering and brake inputs after clearing the runway due to the FO inadvertently turning off the hydraulic switches. The Captain directed the hydraulic switches turned on; restoring the brakes and steering; however; the nose wheel crossed beyond the edge of the taxiway and towing was required to position the aircraft onto the taxiway.

C172S pilot reported a fuel sump drain could not be reseated while performing the preflight check. After completing the flight; the pilot found out that the aircraft had a history of leaking fuel sump drains.
Flight attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.

Local Controller reported they cleared an aircraft to descend; but they went below the minimum altitude for the area and out of radio contact. Controller reestablished contact with the pilot and climbed them back to a safe altitude.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported engine running rough during takeoff roll and performed an aborted takeoff.

Air Carrier flight crew reported while holding short of a taxiway an aircraft exiting runway near them stopped due to insufficient wingtip clearance. This aircraft had not yet cleared the runway; resulting in a go around of another aircraft.

Police helicopter Captain reported a NMAC event when a medical helicopter on a collision course did not respond to multiple calls to deviate heading. The Police helicopter took evasive action to avoid a collision.
CRJ-700 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL230.
A TRACON Controller reported a corporate jet did not comply with their heading assignment due to an aircraft malfunction and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight Instructor on training flight reported a NMAC with another aircraft while on approach.
A321 pilot flying reported Engine 2 began to vibrate and continue to progressively worsen until it failed while in cruise. Flight crew returned to departure airport; landed; and taxied to gate.
CRJ900 First Officer reported finding numerous instances of missing components in main gear wheel wells during preflight.

Queen Air 65/70 pilot reported the airspeed indicator reading zero on takeoff roll. The pilot rejected the takeoff and applied pitot heat and tested on high speed taxi. After departure; airspeed indicator failed again; resulting in pilot landing without airspeed indication.
Air carrier crew reported while climbing they experienced multiple EICAS messages on multiple systems. The air crew was able to overcome the equipment problems and land safely.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a warning message during departure that a door was open; although it was reportedly locked and closed. The flight crew returned to departure airport safely. Maintenance cleared the flight to depart and the same message appeared during descent to next airport.

Air carrier ground personnel reported being pressured and directed by the Supervisor to ship oxygen generators despite notifying the Supervisor of being unfamiliar with the process of shipping this type of HAZMAT.

Pilot reported they diverted to the nearest airport after encountering severe turbulence and heavy rain flying through weather resulting in '...severe hail damage on leading edges on wings.' The nose cone and vertical stabilizer also had hail damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported the Captain released the parking brakes at the departure gate prior to receiving the final dangerous goods summary.
PA-28 pilot reported an electrical failure resulted in loss of all communications and collapse of the landing gear when landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported mechanics finding impact damage on engine cowling during post-flight inspection. Reporter suggested damage could have resulted from hitting a drone on takeoff.
Student pilot reported landing short of the displaced runway threshold line.
Air carrier Captain reported while stopped during taxi the aircraft appeared to be rolling. Captain applied brakes firmly resulting in a possible flight attendant injury.
Air Carrier Captain reported a distracting light at the opposite end of the runway during the takeoff roll in COS.
Air carrier flight crew reported being advised the aircraft had a reported tail strike that they had not noticed; and distractions from wearing masks to do required checks.
Flight Instructor with student reported having a near miss with another aircraft in cruise flight. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming northwest of Solon intersection. The crew deviated from the assigned altitude and then returned after resetting the GPS system.
Air carrier pilot reported a laser event on short final Runway 36R at MEM originating from a loading dock.

B777-200 flight crew reported a Flap Drive failure occurred after retracting the flaps during the initial climb. The flight crew then elected to perform an air turnback. This was the second air turnback for this aircraft after a previous departure also prompted the same system failure.
Corporate pilot reported descending below a crossing altitude on approach prior to the fix resulted in an ATC low altitude alert. Pilot performed a missed approach.
A321 Captain reported an NMAC during visual approach with a Citation that was on approach to a different runway. The Citation flight crew took evasive action.
Ramp tug driver reported evasive action was needed when an aircraft entered a gate without marshals present.

Air carrier Captain reported selecting an improper altimeter setting as received on the ATIS from ATC; resulting in a low altitude alert while on an instrument approach in mountainous terrain. The crew leveled the aircraft then continued the approach; and after landing; ATC updated the ATIS to the correct altimeter setting.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported that the IDG drain plug on B737 aircraft has been found to be cracked due to possible over-torquing.
Flight crew reported a NMAC with a glider while they were on a STAR inbound to SNA.
