Flight Attendant reported they were too fatigued to work after getting 6 hours of sleep over a 50 hour period due to company scheduling policies and flight delays.

Helicopter Instructor reported a runway incursion occurred when the student air taxiied across the runway while traffic was waiting to take off. Instructor stated they did not realize the student's requests to ATC for crossing were blocked both times.
Pilot of a twin-engine turboprop aircraft reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on descent into HDN airport.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported avoiding an inadvertent escape slide deployment when catering tried to open an armed aircraft door.

Air carrier Captain reported the IAD Ramp area; multiple taxiway lines; and the lead-in line and gate centerline for multiple gates are obscured and difficult to see when it is wet and/or during nighttime conditions.
ERJ 170/175 Captain reported they thought they had set the brakes after parking but had not and the aircraft rolled back about two to three feet before the Captain set the brakes.
Air Carrier First Officer reported wind shear encounter on approach resulted in a ground proximity warning.

Center Controller reported an aircraft conducting an RNAV approach flew off course and began to descend to an altitude while not on a published segment of the approach. The controller issued a low altitude alert and then climbed the aircraft to be above the minimum IFR altitude.
B767-300 technician reported not downgrading the aircraft after replacing the Thrust Management Computer.
Flight Attendant reported a tow truck collided with the aircraft during pre-flight while parked at the gate. Aircraft was removed from service.
B737-800 flight crew and flight attendants reported a fumes event during climb. The flight crew diverted and landed safely.
C172 pilot reported reported taking evasive action to avoid an aircraft reported by ADB-B TCAS as 100 feet below altitude and very close.
PA-11 pilot reported hitting a runway edge light after losing directional control on landing roll when the pilot's foot slipped off the heel brakes.

Balloon pilot reported while landing a wind velocity higher than expected forced a divert to a different landing site. After landing the basket tipped over and the passenger fractured an ankle. The pilot was not injured and balloon was not damaged.
Air carrier Captain reported losing ADS-B and GPS signals inbound to DEN. ATC advised the crew several other aircraft were experiencing ADS-B and GPS system issues possibly due to 5G interference.
EMB-175 Flight Attendant reported they had a cracked cockpit windshield and return to departure airport.
Pilot trainee with Flight Instructor reported loss of aircraft control on landing resulted in shutting down aircraft on the runway.
GA pilot reported during landing lost control of aircraft while taxiing slowly; they exited the runway into the grass.
Flight Crew reported discovering that the wrong MCDU was placed on MEL and the aircraft was possibly not airworthy for the flight.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported STALL FAIL message during climb.
A320 air carrier pilot reported a cargo door ECAM malfunction after takeoff. The flight returned to the departure field where the cargo door was put on MEL by maintenance.
C172 pilot reported a NMAC; witnessing a Cessna landing on runway in use; while a Stearman took off opposite direction.
Single Private Pilot reported a NMAC at this location during final approach.
A homebuilt kit aircraft pilot reported engine loss while approaching their destination and made a successful landing.
Flight Attendant reported concerns over inoperative seat belt signs; crew rest area air supply; and inoperative flight attendant's control panel.
Cessna 525 Captain reported rejecting the takeoff after experiencing directional control difficulties on the takeoff roll. Crew evacuated the aircraft after the Tower advised aircraft was on fire.
Pilot reported an NMAC while taking off from ONZ airport with a non-communicating helicopter that was proceeding directly to the airport to land.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported Brazil uses a type of NOTAM system that could result in flight planning systems missing applicable NOTAMs.
A319 flight crew reported a pressurization system malfunction in climb. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.

Air carrier Captain reported a hard stop by tug driver while pushing back from the gate during daylight VMC at DEN airport. A catering vehicle maneuvered close to the aircraft and the tug driver aggressively stopped the push back. After a maintenance inspection and a new tow bar; the flight operated normally with no injuries reported.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with a helicopter in the vicinity of PLU airport.
A319 pilot reported cabin altitude climbing during descent. Crew expedited descent after donning oxygen masks and landed normally.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported a left gear disagree annunciation during initial climb. The flight crew returned to the departure airport and landed.

An Air Carrier pilot reported GPS jamming caused an unstable approach at night. The reporter stated the jamming was a known issue but they were never advised and ATC vectored them to an RNAV Approach.
EMB-170 Captain reported that while they were stopped in the alley waiting to be marshaled into the gate; a ramp worker walked in front of the aircraft.

Small transport Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from the preceding aircraft upon takeoff. The passenger in the copilot seat then grabbed the yoke and attempted to perform dangerous maneuvers that would have worsened the condition. The Captain pushed the passenger's hands off the yoke and maintained positive control of the aircraft; and calmed the passenger.

B757-300 Captain reported excessive rudder trim required during normal flight. The aircraft was taken out of service at destination airport. The aircraft had a long history of excessive rudder trim required in flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while they were on approach.
B747-400 First Officer reported severe turbulence in cruise flight resulting in temporary loss of aircraft control.

Air carrier flight crew reported an aircraft was performing a high power run-up near the takeoff runway at SFO. The flight crew was concerned that if control of the aircraft was lost; it could collide with departing traffic.

A300 Captain reported receiving a Dispatch ACARS message stating onboard Dry Ice data was incorrect. Dispatch further stated the erroneous data did not affect the flight safety which enabled the flight to continue to destination safely.
Tower Controller reported issuing an instruction to fly runway heading prior to instructing the aircraft to execute the published missed approach; which was a violation of the MVA.
An air carrier flight crew was on final approach descending past 3;000 feet when they noticed a UAS above them and notified ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported a rejected takeoff due to another aircraft past a hold-short line on the runway.

Air carrier Captain reported a course deviation occurred during departure from JFK as a result of the climb fixes not being properly loaded in the FMS. ATC provided vectors to a fix and handoff to another controller.
Air taxi First Officer reported during taxi a truck crossed in front of the aircraft within 5-10 feet. Captain applied brakes to avoid a collision.
C-172 pilot reported a maintenance diversion due to temporary engine power loss. Normal engine power was restored and a safe landing was accomplished at a nearby airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported the pilot flying drifted below the glideslope on visual approach resulting in a GPWS alert and evasive action.

Air carrier First Officer reported glide slope anomalies and autopilot failure to capture the localizer during approach resulted in steep descent and an aircraft terrain warning. The flight crew disengaged the autopilot; conducted a go around and completed the approach.
C-172 Flight Instructor on short final reported a runway incursion.
A320 Captain reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of a fumes event in the cabin after gate arrival.
Ground vehicle driver reported brake failure of power stow resulted in loss of control and contact with aircraft.
CRJ700 pilot reported cabin door latching mechanism was not properly positioned prior to flight.

Flight Crew reported at top of climb an increase in the Right Engine Vibration. As The Flight Crew reduced power; an increase in EGT was observed. The Flight Crew performed an in flight shut down and requested priority handling. An air turn back and precautionary landing were made at departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported the ramp crews are not compliant with new push back procedures at many different airports.
A320 flight crew received a Cargo Smoke Warning ECAM and elected to perform an air turn back and overweight landing at departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported not making a crossing restriction during arrival.
Air carrier First Officer reported a loss of GPS signal on A201 in the Kunming airspace.
Air carrier Captain reported there were multiple taxiways that were closed at IAH but not stated on the ATIS and the traffic cones identifying the closures were unlit.
EMB-145 First Officer reported executing a go-around after experiencing wake turbulence on short approach.

Captain reported they stopped taxiing due to vehicles crossing the taxiway from a service road. After this incursion they turned into their gate and had to abruptly stop due to another vehicle crossing on the ramp.
Technician reported the aircraft's engine was disassembled after magneto failure and it was found that the required magneto bearings had not been reinstalled after overhaul.
TRACON Controller reported during that OJT an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier First Officer reported they were informed a passenger's crushed electronic device was placed in a thermal containment bag during cruise as a precautionary measure.
A321 flight crew reported an MEL deferral on a flight control computer was incorrectly performed by maintenance.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne ACARS notification that an aircraft battery was loaded onboard without providing the required NOTOC prior to departure.

Technician reported safety pins were never removed from the escape slides during and after maintenance action. The aircraft operated in revenue service and the pins were removed after a paper work audit discovered the discrepancy.
B737 Captain reported having to brake hard during taxi to avoid a vehicle crossing their path.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence departing CLT in trail of an Airbus. Reporter suggested increased spacing on departures.
B737-800 flight crew reported exceeding flap extend speed when ATC changed their runway assignment late in the approach. Wake turbulence was also cited as contributing.
A pilot departing SZT non-towered airport reported they taxied on to the runway to depart when they noticed another aircraft on short final. The pilot taxied off the runway onto the grass.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a battery discharging caution in flight. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
B757 flight crew reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence on approach to MEM in trail of a B767.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported DG cargo was removed from cargo compartment during preflight without notifying ramp personnel.
A Widebody First Officer reported GPS interference departing ICN.
EMB-145 Captain reported going to work while extremely fatigued and attributed the challenging schedules to staffing issues at their company.
B737-800 Captain reported potentially encountering jet blast from aircraft around the runway during takeoff.
B737-800 Captain reported a fuel imbalance indication during climb out and the need to return to the departure airport.

Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported a lost link during a flight resulting in the UAS briefly climbing above 400 feet AGL. The pilot reported also flying in controlled airspace without LAANC authorization.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain descended below charted altitude on a visual approach to STL.

Air Traffic Controller reported instructing an aircraft to line up and wait but two aircraft took the control instruction resulting in sending an aircraft on final around and exiting one of the aircraft off the runway.
Air Carrier Captain reported DG pallet was inadequate for proper DG cargo transport. Captain refused DG cargo.
Eurocopter AS 350 pilot reported low battery voltage caused an engine over temperature on start. The engine start was discontinued and maintenance was notified.
B737 Captain reported distractions from a wake turbulence encounter on arrival into DEN contributed to altitude and speed deviations.
General aviation pilot flying reported the runway 36L hold markings at VQQ were faded and covered by tall grass.
Flight Instructor reported smoke in cockpit during cruise. The Instructor found source of smoke; rectified it; and continued to destination.
MD-11 First Officer reported indications of a fuel leak and the flight crew elected to divert to the nearest suitable airport to make a precautionary landing.
B777 flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control during departure in an area of convective weather.
PA46-T Captain reported an AHRS failure in cruise resulted in a diversion to a nearby airport.
Air carrier Captain reported they descended below the published altitude of an RNP Approach and ATC issued a low altitude alert.

B757-200 flight crew reported a 'burning rubber odor' during initial climb. After troubleshooting the flight crew requested priority handling to a diversion airport where a safe landing was executed. Maintenance met the aircraft at company ramp.
EMB-145 flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning during approach in IMC. Flight crew climbed to safe altitude and were vectored for the approach.
GA pilot reported descending below published minimums during a practice VOR approach.
BBD-700 flight crew reported the fuel tanks were not being routinely sumped by the maintenance crew.
B737-800 flight crew reported failure of multiple navigation and display systems with smoke in the cockpit resulted in requesting priority handling and a precautionary landing at destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported two VHF radios had heavy static for a short period during cruise disrupting communications.
DA40 Instructor Pilot reported while in traffic pattern; took evasive action to avoid traffic resulted in a NMAC.
Captain reported a track deviation after takeoff from CVG. The crew navigated to KENNL but they were cleared to the similar sounding KENLN
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported unsecured DG freight on taxing aircraft. Aircraft was summons to return to gate where the unsecured DG issue was resolved.
Pilot Examiner reported a NMAC with another aircraft on takeoff climb during a check ride with a student pilot.
Flight Crew reported a high speed rejected take off and returned to the gate for maintenance.
Air carrier Captain reported the inadvertent brake released prior to verifying the DG Final Summary and submitting the flight crew acknowledgement message.
Captain reported binding ailerons after takeoff and a decision to immediately return to the departure airport.
A General Aviation pilot reported a gear up landing.

Air carrier First Officer reported a terrain obstacle warning while descending on a charted visual approach in daylight VMC conditions. The crew identified the obstacle; corrected the flight path; and landed safely.
BE-99 pilot reported landing safely after inflight shutdown of #2 engine because of low oil pressure. Post flight inspection indicated possible failure to fully seat oil dipstick.
Light Jet Captain reported a NMAC with multiple aircraft during departure that required evasive action.
A321 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on short final at CLT.
B737-800 Captain reported an ELEC light illuminated in flight and elected to make an air turn back to departure airport.
Technicians reported refusing to clean portable O2 masks after use and for reuse due to a lack of proper materials and references.

PA-28 instructor pilot reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training at a non-towered airport. After two incidents with same aircraft which required evasive maneuvers flight Instructor elected to depart the landing pattern.
Student pilot reported a runway excursion caused by improper procedures that resulted in aircraft damage.
Technician reported they replaced the torque motor filter on an Embraer 170 and were notified later that the B-nuts were not torqued.
Pilot flying BE-35 aircraft reported emergency exit window departing aircraft inflight.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving an ACARS message which stated that the ground crew failed to provide the flight crew the NOTOC during preflight. The flight crew acknowledged the message and continued to destination normally.
A320 flight crew reported oil loss on number one engine requiring diversion.
B737 MAX pilot reported Runway Awareness and Advisory System (RAAS) and Altitude Alerts were too loud; blocking out ATC transmissions.
J3 Cub pilot reported as they turned onto their ATC assigned taxiway they saw a C172  entering the same taxiway and the aircraft wingtips came into contact.

A319 Captain reported Load Planning requested a gate return to correct a Hazmat loading error. Flight crew complied and informed Dispatch and Load Planning that flight crew was not provided with the required NOTOC prior to gate departure. Both loading error and missing NOTOC were rectified.
First Officer reported engine failure on take off roll; resulting in engine damage and a return to the gate.
Air carrier pilot flying reported failing to make a logbook write-up after experiencing GPS spoofing where the GPS became inoperative for the rest of the flight.
Air carrier Captain reported the flying FO accidentally engaged the go-around switch during initial approach and an unstable approach ensued.

ZMA Center controller reported an unsafe situation due to lack of coordination from the Front Line Manager and the Traffic Management Unit. Controller also made reference to the added sector complexity due the metroplex project changes.

Caravan 208B pilot reported a fuel exhaustion event shortly after instructing the skydivers to exit the plane. The pilot descended with the prop feathered and engine off safely to the ground and was able to exit on to a taxiway with the plane's own momentum.
Flight Instructor reported a loss of aircraft control during landing which resulted in a runway excursion.

Air carrier Captain reported a conflict with a GA aircraft after landing at ECP airport requiring immediate brake action by the Captain to avoid a collision.  The Tower was closed at the time of the event.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported an uneventful mechanical air return during departure climb.
Center Controller reported they took over a sector with an aircraft entering an area below the minimum IFR altitude.

TRACON Controller reported erroneously giving an aircraft an incorrect altitude; below the sector's MVA. After the Controller was alerted by automation the altitude was corrected. The Controller reviewed the recordings afterwards.
Pilot reported differences in MEL procedures listed for a TKS deicing system that was not installed on the aircraft.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they inadvertently flew above 400 AGL for a few moments. Upon realizing the error; they descended.
B737 Captain reported a Flight Attendant incurred a minor injury during a wake turbulence encounter climbing through FL240.
C-182 pilot reported flight controls were difficult to move in flight. After landing; ground inspection revealed autopilot servo malfunction.
Ramp Supervisor reported a ramp worker had hit the aircraft engine with a baggage cart.
Air Carrier flight crew reported altitude deviation and temporary loss of control during initial approach due to severe turbulence resulting in a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
Government pilot reported a near miss with a UAS while orbiting in Class B airspace.
Technician reported during a pre-tow inspection found the right nose wheel tire deflated. Upon further investigation; 10 of the 12 tie bolts holding the rim halves together were loose.

B767 Captain reported receiving a new final DG notification that stated no Dangerous Goods were planned while departing from a diversionary stopover. The flight then departed with an erroneous final DG document as no cargo changes took place during the stopover.
CL30 flight crew reported an NMAC from a light aircraft on approach to DFW.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft when in descent under VFR conditions on a training flight. The instructor executed an emergency descent when visually acquiring the other aircraft to avoid a collision.

B757 Captain reported reviewing flight plan for the flight and noticed a chip inspection performed by Maintenance the day before. The flight crew received a RT OIL PRESS LOW EICAS message followed by a RED boxed 0 oil pressure indication in cruise. The engine was observed to roll back and failed. The flight crew decided to divert to the nearest suitable airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported transporting Hazmat cargo with incomplete required documents.
ZLC Controller reported aircraft descended below the MVA resulted in a low altitude alert and a CFTT event.
Air carrier Captain reported the PF overshot the assigned altitude during arrival and attributed it to fatigue; workload and the PF had not flown in a year.
EMB-145 pilot reported during descent they got an amber CAS message for pressurization auto fail. They ran the QRH and manually operated the pressure controller and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from Air Traffic Control due to descending below a cross restriction during approach.

GA pilot conducting a practice RNAV 25 Approach to I19 airport reported CMH TRACON advised them they were off course even though multiple aircraft navigation sources and visual cues indicated the aircraft was on course. The reporter believes TRACON's depiction of fixes on their radar display may be erroneous.
B737 Captain reported rejected takeoff due to illumination of electrical system and master caution light on takeoff roll.
DA42 Flight Instructor reported main landing gear tire failure after touchdown resulted in loss of aircraft control.
First Officer reported executing a go-around from 50 feet AGL after encountering wake turbulence from preceding aircraft.

Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported late arriving cargo contained Dangerous Goods cargo which was not processed in accordance with Hazmat procedures. The aircraft returned to gate to provide the flight crew the NOTOC and correct Final Flight Release.
SR-22 pilot reported the loss of directional control resulting in a taxiway excursion due to the brake system failure. Aircraft was towed to hanger for maintenance.
A319 Captain reported refusing the aircraft due to multiple occurrences of ELAC failures.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer became confused on a visual approach to MCO and got a low altitude alert from ATC.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during approach. Reporter cited limited autopilot capability as contributing.
Captain reported confusion over a new SID procedure out of MIA; which resulted in missing a crossing restriction.
B737-800 flight crew reported a hard landing at night in the rain.
Air carrier First Officer reported hearing the gear warning horn and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during visual approach. Flight crew climbed to safe altitude and continued to landing.
Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC while maneuvering in a practice area. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.

CRJ700 flight crew reported a loss of braking and directional control while rolling out on landing. The crew managed to stop the aircraft but was unable to be removed from the runway without the assistance of a tug.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS alert and a low altitude alert from ATC on a nighttime River Visual approach to DCA.
B737 MAX flight crew reported that the Runway Approach Alert System volume was so loud that it drowned out the ATC taxi instructions during taxi to the gate.
Single engine private pilot reported an airspace violation and an aircraft produced obstacle warning while attempting to fly under class B airspace.
Corporate pilot reported an engine failure in a PA31T Piper Cheyenne.
CL-350 pilot crew reported several abnormal electrical CAS messages after takeoff. The pilots diverted and landed overweight.

Ground ramp agent reported observing a fueler walking near an operating engine that was started with a ground air cart. The ramp agent signaled the fueler away from the operating engine to prevent possible injury; and the fueler indicated they were not aware of the operating engine hazard.
Center Controllers reported operational difficulties; holding and congestion in various sectors resulting from changing weather.
A320 Flight Attendant reported being notified after arriving at destination that a passenger packed a prohibited personal oxygen machine in checked baggage.
A vehicle driver reports driving with caution behind an aircraft; but was later advised the push-back driver made a panic stop resulting in aircraft damage.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported an aircraft loss of control while landing in gusty wind conditions. The aircraft scraped the wing tip during touchdown; which was not discovered until the post-flight walk around.
B737 Captain reported pushing back but was then notified to return to the gate to remove DG despite receiving a message during preflight that there was no DG on board.

CRJ-700 flight crew reported Gear Disagree Waning Message after takeoff. Flight crew returned to departure airport.; "Student Pilot reported a loss of engine power and a diversion to another airport."
Single Engine Pilot reported the engine cowling was torn off the aircraft while in descent.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a fumes event during cruise. The QRH Fumes checklist was completed which caused the fumes to dissipate. After conferring with Dispatch the flight continued to a safe landing at destination.
Pilot reported airspeed and altitude errors on approach to landing. After landing and taxi in; the pilot suspected a frozen Pitot Static System was the cause of the instrument errors.
A person reported observing a UAS flying outside their office window. The UAS landed and was retrieved by a person in a car.

A corporate pilot reported on initial climb they encountered a downdraft associated with wind shear at the departure end of the runway. They were unable to comply with the SID and Tower issued them a Low Altitude Alert.
CL-350 flight crew reported a near miss with another aircraft while on approach in airport traffic pattern.
Corporate pilot reported a loss of control of the aircraft causing a taxiway excursion.
Pilot reported loss of propeller control and engine oil pressure resulted in an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Tower Controller reported clearing a helicopter to enter the pattern for the runway while there was a vehicle operating on the runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported the Captain experienced loss of flight director and ILS on approach to EWR; resulting in a low altitude alert from ATC.
Corporate flight crew was in cruise flight at 16;000 feet when they noticed a UAS about 500 feet below them and notified ATC.
MD-11 flight crew reported unreliable airspeed and pitch indications on climb out. The flight crew elected to monitor the airspeed and pitch carefully and continued the flight to destination airport.
Pilot of an amateur-built aircraft reported a windshear-caused ground loop on landing rollout.

PA-42 pilot reported performing a return to service test flight. On initial climb; the pilot observed smoke coming from Right Engine. The pilot performed an in flight shut down of the Right Engine and returned to the departure airport.
Maintenance Technician reported poor WiFi at LAX.

Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer noticed the HAZMAT was placed in the wrong orientation and notified an agent. The flight crew then conferred with Dispatch and the Duty Officer and placed the box in the right direction.
GA Flight Instructor reported a near miss with another landing aircraft while landing on an intersecting runway.
A Tracon Controller reported a military aircraft deviated from their assigned route and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B787 flight crew reported a buffet and a flight control spoiler assembly alert message; resulting in a return to the departure airport and an overweight landing.
Pilots reported they were cleared for and flew the RNAV 28 Approach into C65 airport at night which is not authorized due to terrain issues.
Pilot reported an NMAC while departing the traffic pattern of non-towered OBE airport when another aircraft entered the pattern using non-standard procedures and not communicating their intentions.

A Center Controller reported a C208 rapidly descended below its assigned altitude; was not responding to ATC calls; and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude; later the pilot established communications; climbed to a safe altitude and returned to its point of departure.
PA-28 pilot reported not knowing aircraft was not properly repaired per Piper specifications when flown.
Learjet First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC after descending early on a cleared instrument approach.
C172 pilot reported erroneous airspeed indications after take off resulted in return to departure airport
EMB-175 flight crew reported mistakenly aligned with a wrong airport during approach. Flight crew was correcting low altitude and course error when ATC notified flight crew of the error.
Flight Instructor reported an aircraft descended in the traffic pattern; unaware of the aircraft below which resulted in a NMAC.
B737 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into IAH in trail of an A320.
Air Carrier Captain and Flight Attendant reported a fume event during passenger boarding. Passengers were deplaned and Maintenance was requested.

B787-900 flight crew reported that while entering the runway for takeoff; the Tower Controller directed them to stop immediately for landing traffic which was instructed to go around. Controller stated the takeoff clearance was an ATC error.
B737 flight crew reported engine failure during taxi out. Flight returned to gate.
Global Express flight crew reported windshear and loss of aircraft control when entering a thunder cloud in cruise flight.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported an input error when sending runway landing data to a flight crew request. The flight landed safely with the incorrect data.
A321 Flight Crew reported a fumes event described as a 'dirty socks' odor during gate pre-flight. The Flight crew refused the aircraft which was taken out of service.
Flight Crew reported a diversion and precautionary landing after the trailing edge flaps failed and would not reposition.
Pilot reported a prop strike occurred shortly after touchdown when encountering a bump in the runway.

Part 107 pilot was flying in an area they believed to be available for UAS operations. After further review the pilot learned they were in fact in airspace not available to UAS operations which was not noted by any of the UAS apps.
An air carrier Captain reported they received a TCAS/RA alert to maintain altitude for a VFR aircraft.
C172 pilot reported they aborted takeoff due to the engine not developing full power.
B737 NG flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on final approach to ORD in trail of a B777.

PC-12 Captain reported the Bleed Air switch was positioned incorrectly and led to a failure to pressurize on climb. The Captain reviewed the procedures; repositioned the Bleed Air switch correctly; and fully restored the systems.
MIA TRACON Controller reported a potential loss of separation between two aircraft at a hotspot for VFR traffic near the OPF airport.
Air carrier Captain reported finding ice accumulations on the aircraft after a gate return due to deteriorating weather conditions and after deicing was complete.
B767 flight crew conducting a preflight walk around reported pin flags used to deactivate wing slides were left hanging from the aircraft during the previous flight.

Ground Agent reported moving a cart to an area that appeared safe for equipment storage then assisted in towing an aircraft to the same location. The towed aircraft collided with the cart; resulting in damage to the aircraft.

CRJ-900 flight crew reported an NMAC event with a balloon while in cruise. The First Officer disconnected the autopilot and promptly executed an evasive maneuver to avoid a collision and afterwards the Captain reported the balloon to ATC.
CRJ-200 pilot reported during climb the #2 engine started to vibrate and the flight crew heard two loud bangs. The flight diverted to the nearest airport.
SR20 pilot reported an engine malfunction during cruise flight on a IFR clearance. The pilot diverted to the departure airport and landed safely.
Commercial Cessna 404 pilot reported an alternator failure and received priority handling from ATC.
A Ramp worker reported an aircraft unexpectedly rolled toward them prior to disconnect coordination while their headset was still connected to the aircraft.
The non flying pilot reported the flying pilot made an unstable approach resulting in a hard landing damaging the wheel pant of the C206.
A Corporate pilot reported they received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC while climbing on the SID.
Captain reported similar sounding fixes; HYVAL and HIVAL; resulted in a clearance deviation.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the vicinity of BJC airport.

DA40 Flight Instructor reported a fuel pump transfer problem during takeoff resulting in a diversion. Reporter stated fuel transfer pump issues are 'becoming more frequent' with aircraft left outside on the ramp making them susceptible to water contamination in the fuel tanks.
A319 First Officer reported a momentary loss of aircraft directional control during aircraft line up after completing back taxi for takeoff.
B737 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to LAX in trail of an A320 that resulted in a hard bank and loss of 100 feet of altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported new MetroPlex arrival charts in need of clarification of 'Descend via' clearance and early runway assignment. Current procedures results in high FMS workload in terminal area.

Flight crews; Dispatcher; and Ground Personnel reported that during pushback the aircraft wing hit an aircraft at the adjacent gate. Prior to impact; a wingwalker gave multiple verbal instructions and hand signals to the pushback crew to stop the pushback; but the aircraft was not stopped in time.

B767 crew reported a gradual loss of center system hydraulic quantity during last half of cruise flight. Crew made a reduced flap setting landing at destination after executing checklist procedures and consultations with maintenance.
Technicians reported FOD damage to fan blades during a post Engine Change Engine Static run.
Single pilot reported the aircraft began to sink on short final causing it to hit the runway approach lights. The only damage reported was to the flaps.
B737 First Officer reported loss of engine oil pressure requiring inflight shutdown and diversion.
EMB-145 flight crew reported an engine failure during cruise flight. Flight crew diverted to nearest suitable airport.
A320 flight crew reported an engine fire and engine cowling burn-through while troubleshooting an engine reverser fault on the ground with Maintenance Control.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing LAX in trail of an A320.
Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC while practicing maneuvers. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
GA pilot reported failure to announce a time pressured takeoff in close proximity to previous departure; resulted in a NMAC conflict.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during initial approach to JYO airport.
EMB-170 Captain reported possible erroneous aural bank angle and terrain too low warning on takeoff resulted in continued climb without escape maneuver.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion by crossing into the ILS Critical area without clearance at LGB airport. The pilots reported there are no markings on the taxiway; no reference on ATIS and no reference to hold short lines on the airport diagram. In addition; the pilots reported LGB Ground did not inform them the critical area was active.
Air carrier pilot flying reported ramp location spots depicted on the Jeppesen airport diagram are different from the actual spot numbers at MMPR Airport.
C-172 Pilot reported an asymmetrical flap failure during approach to landing. After assessing the remaining controllability of the aircraft the Pilot performed a successful approach and landing.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a passenger's laptop began smoking during cruise. The Flight Attendant and Purser secured the laptop in a Thermal Containment Bag safely for the remainder of the flight.

An ARTCC Controller reported confusion between video map display overlays while controlling an aircraft with malfunctioning navigation and deicing equipment resulted in descent below MVA and a CFIT event.
HA-20 pilot reported the loss of brake pressure while entering the ramp resulted in a collision with a trailer.
Flight Instructor and student reported brake malfunction during taxi resulted in taxiway excursion.
C402 pilot reported a taxiway excursion due to brake failure.
EMB-170 First Officer reported a 'Steer Fail' message was annunciated during landing. Aircraft cleared runway and was towed to gate.
SFO Tower Controller reported a departing aircraft flew off course and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

B737 flight crew reported an engine tailpipe fire while taxiing onto the runway. Flight crew shut down the affected engine; fire was extinguished; and aircraft taxied to gate where it was taken out of service.

DTW Tower Controller reported the ASDE-X gave a false alarm when a construction vehicle passed by a taxiway. Controller stated the alarms are increasingly being triggered by vehicles in the taxiway construction area and suggested driving restrictions be put in place or the ADSE-X alert areas be adjusted.

CRJ700 Captain reported there was a strange odor that appeared and dissipated shortly after takeoff and during the descent to the destination airport; the flight crew began to experience physiological symptoms. The flight attendants and passengers were unaffected by the fume event.

A319 flight crew reported a trailing edge flaps fail ECAM on approach and elected to make a go around to run the QRH procedures. The flight crew successfully cleared the fault and returned to the approach and made a landing at destination airport.

GA pilot reported a ground conflict resulting in an aborted takeoff as another aircraft was taking off at the opposite end of the same Runway. The reporter was not on the correct CTAF frequency which was the cause of the conflict.
PA34 pilot reported they were unable to stop on the runway remaining during a high speed taxi; which resulted in a runway excursion and a propeller hitting airport lights.
Flight Instructor reported total electrical system and engine malfunction during taxi to the runway for departure. The flight was cancelled and the plane was towed back to the FBO.
Pilot reported pitot system malfunctions caused the loss of the AHRS and airspeed after take off. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
SFO Local Controller reported an E175 pilot executed a go-around from short final after encountering wake turbulence from another E175 on final approach to the parallel runway.
Pilot reported an off airport landing after fuel feed issues caused an engine failure.
ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft flying in icing and snow conditions was unable to climb above MVA resulting in a CFTT event.
Air carrier flight crew reported they descended below the glide slope on short final.
Air taxi flight crew reported that the aircraft's brakes locked up during landing causing the tires to burst.
Corporate pilot reported there are no NOTAMs or information regarding a TFR around NPA for overflying aircraft.
General aviation flight instructor reported they turned in front of the other aircraft causing a near miss. In response; the other aircraft performed a go around.
Air carrier First Officer reported aircraft rolled forward at the gate after parking due to a failed parking brake.

Pilot reported landing gear failed to indicate extended and during alternate extension procedures; communications problems and failure to follow check lists resulted in the landing gear collapsing on landing roll out.
B737 First Officer reported during climb aircraft began a subtle shake followed by a right then left wing drop resulted in pilot descent which stabilized aircraft.
Fractional Captain reported a possible GPS jamming event that resulted in the aircraft slowly descending and veering off course and the autopilot disconnecting without any audible alert.
Flight Crew reported an unstable approach with a high descent rated resulted in a GPWS alert and a CFTT event during approach to ROA.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern at TTD.
PA-42 pilot reported a propeller failed to disengage from the pitch locks on takeoff; resulting in a rejected takeoff and a runway excursion.
Small aircraft pilot reported while taking off; an airport mower entered the runway resulting with the aircraft overflying the mower.
C560 Captain reported a hydraulic system failure followed by a divert to a suitable airport.
Cheetah pilot reported a prop strike upon landing.
A300 flight crew reported loss of Green Hydraulic System on climb out and returned to departure airport.
Pilot reported a rejected takeoff due to an aircraft configuration error message.
An air carrier pilot on a night time visual approach reported they received a low altitude alert from ATC.
Air taxi pilot reported while on downwind at a non-towered airport; a non-communicating business jet took off opposite direction creating a NMAC.
Technician reported confusion over steps contained on a work card while installing a galley.
SR20 student pilot reported engine RPM remained stuck while leveling off for cruise. The flight returned to the departure airport with no issues.
GA pilot reported an NMAC with an aircraft entering the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. Reporter descended to avoid traffic and eventually departed the pattern.
C501 pilot reported that while handflying an approach ATC advised them that they had descended below the MVA for the segment before the FAF.
Air carrier flight crew reported clearance confusion by ATC resulted in a low altitude alert and a CFIT event.
Air carrier First Officer reported a critical ground conflict while on final approach resulting in a missed approach and declaration of minimum fuel.

C525 flight crew reported loss of Cabin Pressure Control during climb. The flight crew requested priority handling and immediately descended. Oxygen masks were deployed and donned. The flight crew was unable to control the cabin pressure and elected to continue at a lower altitude with a depressurized cabin to destination airport.
Gate Agent reported B787 door window remained dark; requiring the Flight Attendant to use a flashlight to improve visibility and allow the passenger door to be opened.
CE525 pilot reported an inflight uncommanded engine shut down and subsequent engine relight; and continued to destination airport.
A320 Captain reported lights from a baggage facility and airport construction obstruct the flight crew's vision at night.
Pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter that was overflying the traffic pattern at a non towered airport.

A320 Captain reported a fire warning/ECAM/triple chime/Master Caution/Warning and fire handle illuminated on climbout. The flight crew conducted an air turn back and performed a precautionary landing at departure airport.
ZMA Center Controller reported a spillout resulting in a NMAC with an air carrier.
Air Carrier Captain reported a discontinuity on the FMC legs page; on the same route; in the same place; two consecutive times.
Pilot Examiner reported the leading edge of the right flap had 'buckled'; causing a return to departure airport during an applicants checkride.

A321 Pilot reported Flight Attendant informed flight crew of 'manure-like' odor in passenger cabin during climb. Flight crew secured Pack 2 which caused the odor to dissipate. After conferring with Dispatch and Maintenance the flight continued to destination.
Air carrier flight crew reported landing in turbulent conditions resulting in a wing tip strike.
Gulfstream GIV pilot reported failure of the Flight Guidance Computer during departure in IMC resulted in return to departure airport.

A Tower Controller reported they issued a landing clearance to a departing aircraft which encountered engine failure and requested special handling even though there was another departing aircraft on the runway.
C172 Flight Instructor landing for a touch and go at a non-towered airport reported an aircraft entered the runway from the opposite direction just as they were touching down.

Air carrier Captain reported moderate turbulence which resulted in a roll authority warning and the autopilot disconnecting. The pilot regained control of the aircraft and returned to the assigned altitude and continued the approach.
First Officer reported at assigned altitude above MVA; on vectors in VFR conditions with terrain displayed; an aircraft GPWS was alerted resulted in a CFTT event.
AutoGyro Cavalon pilot reported the nose gear broke off during a smooth landing roll.

Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict while taxiing to the runway with another aircraft taxiing on a crossing taxiway. The First Officer abruptly applied brakes avoiding a collision and ATC later informed the pilots they were in the middle of shift change during the incident.
Air carrier flight crew reported a bird strike during rotation. Maintenance at the destination found damage to fan blades in the right engine.
Air carrier Captain reported lack of lighting at JAN airport ramp area.

Agriculture pilot reported a near miss with power lines that were obscured by smoke and haze while performing aerial application of fertilizer. The pilot mis-judged the distance to the power lines; decided to fly under the power lines and was able to avoid hitting the power lines during the maneuver.

Air carrier Customer Service Agent reported there was a passenger with a pressurized oxygen tank carry-on that had to be taken to DG storage. The passenger had stated that the oxygen tank had been allowed to pass through the previous airport's security and in general has been transported many times.
PC-24 Captain reported multiple wake turbulence encounters on descent into SNA in trail of a B737.

ERJ Captain reported a critical ground conflict with the deice truck after initiating taxi following deicing.  Captain stated he slammed on the brakes and that the vehicle did not respond to Captains radio transmissions.
Cessna 170 Flight Instructor reported passenger side window departed aircraft. Post flight it was discovered that the hinges had broken.
Air carrier First Officer reported ORD ATC does not give flight crews an opportunity to provide a readback after a clearance and miscommunication tends to occur.

B747-400 flight crew reported a rejected takeoff due to a vibration and tire failure while accelerating during the takeoff roll. The Captain stopped the aircraft and cleared the runway where airport personnel identified two tires disintegrated; then rising brake temperatures deflated 3 other tires.
Ground personnel reported that a tug came close to an approaching aircraft.

Pilot of a tailwheel aircraft reported that as they reduced power during a high speed taxi the aircraft began weaving across the runway and eventually departed the runway; resulting in aircraft damage.
MSY TRACON Controller reported MVA obstruction no longer exists and facility failure to update display resulted in an inaccurate low altitude alert.

A321 flight attendants reported a fumes event described as a 'chemical odor' during approach. Flight crew was immediately notified and the flight was met by Maintenance at gate arrival after a safe landing was accomplished.
C206 Pilot reported a safe return for a dead stick landing at departure airport after the engine seized shortly after departure.

PA-11 pilot reported an NMAC with an aircraft during cruise when the conflict aircraft descended and reportedly neither pilot could see the other aircraft due to both being in each other's blind spots.
B737-800 flight crew reported of being informed several days later of failure to report aircraft damage that was obscured on preflight.
Air carrier flight crew reported Low Altitude Alert from ATC on approach.
A319 flight crew reported missing paperwork for DG that had been loaded on the aircraft. The flight departed without the DG summary onboard.
Air carrier Captain reported two DG pallets were removed during preflight after discovering the pallets were improperly built; with concern for one of the pallets having a leak.
C172 Flight instructor on training flight with student reported runway excursion on landing rollout.
First Officer reported a rejected take off when spoiler caution messages illuminated. The flight crew returned to the Gate for maintenance action.
Air carrier Captain reported a stabilizer trim failure during climb and a diversion to a nearby suitable airport.

GA pilot reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport during departure with an aircraft on approach for the opposite direction runway. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
B737 Flight Attendant reported a fumes event after takeoff described as a 'dirty socks' smell.

Air carrier pilot reported a unstabilized approach and long landing due to an unpublished displaced threshold and difficulty seeing the lights marking the threshold. The pilot remained high on the approach to overfly vehicles on the runway and landed.
A C152 pilot in the Tower downwind pattern reported a NMAC when a departing aircraft turned early into their course.
Air carrier flight crew reported seeing a UAS near the airport while they were taxiing. The crew waited until the UAS was gone and then they departed.
B737-800 Flight Attendants reported a 'burning electrical' odor during pre-flight. Maintenance was requested. Flight crew deplaned and waited for a replacement aircraft.
Pilot reported engine roughness in flight and made a precautionary landing at a nearby airport.
C172 pilot reported the engine overheated requiring a return to the departure airport.
Air carrier Check Pilot reported failure to follow SOP resulted in crossing the final approach fix at MGGT one dot low.  No terrain alert was received; and the aircraft landed safely.

A TRACON Controller reported they attempted to climb an aircraft but lost communications and the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude. The sector frequencies were taken out of service and the controller was not notified.
B777 Captain reported during pushback; the cockpit screens went blank then returned with numerous EICAS massages and an automatic engine shutdown. This resulted in a return to gate.
EA300 Pilot reported having an electrical failure after takeoff and returned to departure airport.
DA20 pilot reported they lost control of the aircraft while landing in a strong crosswind resulting in a runway excursion causing the nose wheel to break off and a prop strike.
Air carrier First Officer reported a near midair collision with a UAS while they were on climb out. Reporter stated they did not have time to take evasive action and reported the event to ATC.
C172 instructor pilot reported an engine malfunction during initial climb on a training flight. The instructor returned to the departure airport and landed.
B737 Flight Crew reported after being cleared for a Visual Approach the Pilot Flying turned toward the airport inside the Final Approach Fix and received a low altitude alert from ATC.
B-737 First Officer reported a cockpit window malfunction that resulted in a return to the departure airport. The flight performed a safe overweight landing.
Flight instructor reported that on-board 'Homesafe' system automatically activated the emergency radio and transponder broadcasts.
TRACON Controller reported an airspace violation and cited not being sure if another sector was open or not open as a contributing factor.

Air carrier ground personnel reported a smart bag with a non-removable battery had been transported on several aircraft against regulation and the bag had to be returned to the passenger via ground freight.
G650 flight crew reported loss of data after takeoff. Diverted and landed uneventfully.

CRJ-900 Captain reported a critical ground conflict when another airline; taxiing at a high speed; came within 50 feet. After inquiring with ATC; the Captain was told by ground control they did not realize the other aircraft was taxiing so fast. Later; during the same taxi; the Captain stated evasive action was needed to avoid a tug that cut in front of the tug.

Student Pilot reported a NMAC event during landing pattern training in which an non-reporting aircraft entered the airspace without making any required calls. The student pilot had to make an evasive turn crosswind and arrest the climb to avoid a collision.
Air Carrier A320 pilot reported an engine failed in flight and was preceded by the flight crew receiving high engine vibration and oil chip detector messages on prior legs.
Air Carrier flight crew reported they began to taxi with ground personnel still under the aircraft before receiving the salute.
Air Carrier Captain reported airport taxi chart does not agree with the current airport layout.
Air Carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict with another air carrier that cut in front of this Captain. Evasive braking was required. Similar call signs may have contributed.
Air carrier maintenance personnel reported bringing HAZMAT onboard a reposition flight and was told afterwards that it actually needed to be shipped out separately.

Helicopter pilot reported the inability to use the TXK airport lighting as a visual aid to navigate due to the airport lighting being turned off while the Tower is open at night. Upon questioning; ATC stated the lights are turned off when there are no inbound aircraft as a cost cutting measure.
General aviation pilot reported a maintenance truck drove onto the runway to change lights while aircraft were taking off and landing. Truck driver was eventually stopped.

Air carrier flight crew reported an unstabilzed approach which resulted in an ATC low altitude alert and GPWS warning in the cockpit during daylight VMC weather conditions. The crew stopped the descent; then continued the approach to landing.
ERJ-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on a visual approach to SEA in trail of a B737. ATC insistence on speed adherence was cited as contributing.
B737 flight crew reported an engine inflight shutdown in cruise due to an oil filter bypass issue.
B767 flight crew reported the flaps would not properly retract after takeoff. After unsuccessful troubleshooting; the flight crew opted to perform an air turnback.
Challenger 350 flight crew reported a dual generator failure at FL380 cruise altitude resulting in a diversion to nearest suitable airport.
Flight Instructor reported they had to take evasive action while taxiing to parking to avoid a vehicle which Tower cleared on to the same taxiway.
A Tower Local Controller and Ground Controller reported an aircraft taxied across a runway without a clearance the same time as another aircraft was departing.

BE-30 pilot reported the oil pressure indications of one engine fluctuated between normal and abnormal. The pilot shut down the engine; diverted and made a precautionary landing. After adding more oil to the engine; the pilot departed and completed the flight safely.
Learjet 60 flight crew reported numerous warnings and messages during approach. The Captain hand flew the approach and continued to land at destination airport.
ZOB ARTCC Controller reported during flight breakup exiting refuel track one of the aircraft took evasive action to miss a low wing aircraft not in contact with ATC.
An ATC Supervisor reported instructing a controller to issue an IFR clearance through a Restricted Area due to priority situation.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC caused them to take the controls from a student and perform an evasive maneuver.
EMB-190 pilot reported a Slat Failure during approach. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation during approach due to communication issues with ATC.  The First Officer cited the Captain wearing a face mask on the flight deck may have contributed to the communication problems with ATC and between them.
A Tower Local Controller reported a departing aircraft reported an NMAC with an aircraft on downwind for an intersecting runway.
Air Carrier Pilot reported 3 false terrain warnings on approach in a non-RNP aircraft.
Part 107 UAS pilot report flying a photo mission when they were distracted by tourists. This caused the UAS pilot to fly higher than authorized and past their LAANC authorized time.

CRJ-700 Captain reported slamming on the brake to avoid a collision with a fuel truck that ignored aircraft movement and marshalers and continued at high speed in front of the aircraft. After the truck cleared the gate area; the aircraft continued to taxi into the gate.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported a communication breakdown between maintenance stores personnel and supervisory personnel regarding shipping a Lithium Ion battery powered tool.
J3 Cub flight Instructor reported ground loop during tail-wheel instruction.
A B757 pilot reported airspeed restrictions resulted in a request for priority handling and an air turn back to make a precautionary landing at departure airport.
B747 flight crew reported a cabin pressure problem while climbing through 31;000 feet.  Crew descended to keep cabin altitude in normal range and continued to destination at the lower altitude.
CRJ-550 flight crew reported a Brake Overheat Master Warning during the initial climb that was unresolved after completing troubleshooting procedures; resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at LYH when there was confusion in the cockpit regarding sequencing.

B747-400 Captain reported taking off with flaps operating in primary mode only.  Reportedly; this condition makes the flaps sluggish when retracting.  The condition was made worse when the crew encountered windshear during climbout resulting in a momentary stick shaker.
C172 Flight Instructor and student reported the throttle control became stuck at 2000 RPM while conducting training maneuvers. The flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
B737-800 flight crew reported icing conditions resulted in unreliable airspeed indications; followed by a descent and diversion to a precautionary landing.
C172 flight instructor reported partial engine failure in cruise. Instructor returned to departure airport and landed safely.
A TRACON Controller reported they thought they assigned an aircraft 6000 ft but stated 4000 ft. and the aircraft descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

MD-11 flight crew reported a #2 engine flame out and observed a roll back on the engine instruments. The crew ran checklists and QRH; and attempted an in flight restart. The restart was unsuccessful and the crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a likely erroneous Ground Proximity Warning while on base leg of a visual approach.

Air carrier First Officer reported an engine failure on a B-747 during cruise in oceanic airspace. The crew elected to continue in oceanic airspace and planned a diversion to an alternate airport on the mainland.

Cargo flight crew and Dispatcher reported being notified after landing that they had conducted the flight with an out of CG load.  Loading agents at origination airport 'voided' the automated calculation to show a valid load for departure and failed to coordinate and/or remove the weight per understanding with the Captain.
General aviation pilot reported FYJ's AWOS is inaccurate and is concerned that this can lead to an unknowing pilot being misled by what is reported versus actual winds.
Technicians reported a damaged Bulk Cargo Door Seal was replaced incorrectly resulting in the aircraft unable to pressurize and returning to departure airport.
PA-28 pilot reported that while on final approach reported near miss with another aircraft taking the runway for takeoff.
B737 pilot reported during gate pushback they almost collided with an inbound aircraft approaching a gate. Pilot applied brakes abruptly to avoid collision.
Seaplane pilot reported a loss of separation from a watercraft while circling to land at a lake.
A B747 flight crew reported a NMAC with a Cessna at 4500 feet after they were issued a descent clearance from ATC for 6000 feet to 4000 feet.
Air Carrier Ground Service Lead Technician reported Contractor company attempted to load Hazmat cargo clearly labeled as Hazmat 'Surface Transport only' on aircraft.
SoCal TRACON Controllers reported an NMAC between two SNA arrivals.
B757 Captain reported the flight crew misidentified the planned Dangerous Goods (DG) message as the final DG message and had mistakenly sent the DG acknowledgement code.
Ground personnel reported that the aircraft brakes were released before the push back tug was connected resulting in the aircraft rolling back.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft could not maintain assigned altitude due to weather conditions and descended below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Flight crew reported an uncommanded abrupt pitch up during descent. The flight crew disconnected the auto pilot and continued to land at destination airport.
DV20 flight instructor and student pilot reported fuel starvation after they cleared the runway. Fuel quantity prior to departure indicated they should have had another 0.3 hours of fuel remaining.
B737-800 pilot reported a flap indication issue during final approach; resulting in a terrain warning. The aircraft was reportedly on profile; so the crew elected to continue the landing.

Flight crew reported seat failure of a Kitfox V aircraft that resulted in a runway excursion on takeoff. The flight controls were binding and required extra effort to move the ailerons. The flight crew was able to land immediately after takeoff. Inspection revealed broken seat pan had collapsed onto the control cables and restricted their movement.

Flight Attendants reported a main cabin door malfunction during taxi out when the Captain called indicating a door was unlocked. A Flight Attendant reset the door handle and the light extinguished; and then aircraft returned to the gate for a maintenance check of the door.

Hot Air Balloon pilot reported the balloon was blown into a tree during landing which resulted in the fabric becoming entangled in the branches. The balloon envelope was damaged during deflation and removal from the tree. There were no injuries to pilot or passengers.
GA pilot reported on landing; observed an opposite direction go-around flew over; resulting in an NMAC event.
GA pilot reported an NMAC at non-towered airport.
Recreational/Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they learned during post-flight they flew in controlled airspace without authorization.
ZLC Controller reported failure; due to fatigue; to verify clearance met MIA limit; resulted in CFTT low altitude alert event.

SA 227 flight crew reported confusion and communications issues resulted in an attempted takeoff with the landing gear control handle in the up position. The nose landing gear retracted; resulting in a rejected takeoff.
First Officer reported an In Flight Shut Down and Air Turn Back; caused by a number two engine low oil pressure warning.
Instructor pilot reported the need to take evasive action after encountering a NMAC.

Air carrier flight crew reported Tower issued go around instructions on final approach due to an aircraft that had not cleared the landing runway. During the go around the aircraft experienced a flap over speed then returned for a landing.
Pilot reported descending below Minimum Vectoring Altitude and climbed above minimum altitude when advised by ATC to do so.

Air Carrier A321 flight crew reported the aircraft had a lightning strike during arrival descent which disabled the weather radar system. Flight crew received vectors away from the area of weather and completed a diversion with safe landing.

Air carrier First Officer reported a bleed valve malfunction and wing anti ice failure while climbing in icing conditions. The pneumatic malfunction affected aircraft pressurization and the flight returned to the departure airport and safely landed.
Flight Instructor reported; while instructing a student pilot on landing; the student had a loss of control and landed hard causing damage to helicopter.
CRJ 900 pilots reported loss of control while landing in a crosswind and a wing tip striking the pavement.

A320 flight crew reported a loss of nosewheel steering while parking into the gate for parking; and two ECAMS appearing. Engine #2 had already been shut down earlier. The flight crew shut down the remaining operating engine to get towed into the gate by a tug driver. The First Officer surmised that the problem may have potentially been caused from forgetting to turn on the Electric Hydraulic Pump after shutting down Engine #2 during taxi in.
Student solo pilot entering the downwind at a non-towered airport reported a NMAC when another aircraft entered downwind at the same position.
RV-6 pilot reported ground loop during landing resulted in runway excursion and damage to runway light.
ZBW Controller reported frequency charting error on a third party chart; and also confusion with the Jeppesen charts for BTV airport.
Learjet 35 flight crew reported engine bleed air malfunction. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Corporate jet Captain reported executing a go-around after climbing in response to a TCAS RA on approach to VNY airport.
A C172 pilot reported they rejected takeoff due to high oil pressure indication.
Air carrier First Officer reported passing SIYOB on arrival into IAD without turning toward MIKEJ and based on ATC's comments the reporter noted that this deviation may be a common occurrence.

Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming during cruise in a known area of GPS interference. One of the two GPS receivers returned to normal operation later in cruise and the flight continued to destination.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming during taxi in TVL and again during flight in Israeli; Cypriot and Turkish airspace.
Balloon pilot reported the wind shifted during landing resulting in the balloon envelope making contact with power lines. The envelope was removed from the lines and sustained minor damage.

DA-40 pilot reported an electrical failure at cruise while receiving flight following from ATC in VFR conditions. The electrical problem resulted in communication issues with ATC; but the pilot navigated to the airport and landed safely.
Cessna 172 pilot reported experiencing smoke coming from the vicinity of the Electric Trim Switch on the yolk; resulting in a diversion to the nearest airport.
Pilot reported a NMAC during cruise below Class B airspace.
Pilot reported evasive action to avoid another aircraft identified by the aircraft TCAS system.
Flight Instructor on training flight reported engine failure during takeoff climb.
Air Carrier Captain reported spotting a UAS while on approach.
Pilot reported a Right Engine Bleed problem resulted in a return to the departure airport and an aircraft change.
B737-800 pilot reported the need to brake aggressively to avoid a collision with another aircraft during taxi to the gate.
Air carrier Captain reported having difficulty in ensuring wingtip clearance and performing a safety scan due to the IAH RON ramp's poor lighting conditions; especially at night.
EMB-145 Captain reported receiving two terrain warnings during approach descent which were corrected both times by evasive action.
A departing air carrier flight crew and an arriving air carrier Captain reported the arrival initiated a go around which caused a confliction with the other reporters departing aircraft.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported there was conflicting chart information and traffic pattern indicators as to the correct traffic pattern at PCZ Airport.
B767 Captain reported refusing to operate the aircraft due to an applied MEL that compromised safety during ETOPS operations.
Instructor Pilot reported an air turn back after engine roughness was experienced on take off. A precautionary landing was made successfully at the departure airport.
EMB-145 flight crew reported low fuel indication during climb. Diverted and returned to departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported GPS interference and then recovery between IMB and KU75M.

Air carrier Captain reported having an inoperative APU and requested that the destination ramp crew be advised of their situation; this information was not received by the ramp personnel resulting in a unsafe ramp situation.
Ground Personnel stated they parked the tug too close to equipment and when they backed up they hit a vehicle.
EMB-505 pilot reported hearing a loud pop and experienced air frame vibration. Pilot returned to the departure airport.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in a restricted area without authorization.
M20 pilot reported runaway trim during go around procedure. Pilot regained control and landed uneventfully.
GA Flight Instructor reported an NMAC during approach to KLS non-towered airport after failing to switch over to local traffic frequency. Both pilots reportedly took evasive action.
Air carrier Captain reported a Flight Attendant was slightly injured when they made an abrupt stop on the taxiway at LFPG/CDG airport to avoid a collision with a van.
Aeronca Champion pilot reported not seeing traffic on final while taxiing onto runway; resulting in a runway incursion and a critical ground conflict. Landing aircraft safely executed a go-around.
EMB-145 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to CLT in trail of an A321 that resulted in an aggressive roll exceeding 45 degrees of bank angle.
Air carrier Load Supervisor reported the container with a box of dry ice was not properly secured. Since the box showed no damage the box and container were subsequently properly secured.

Air Carrier Captain reported being notified of a 'dirty socks' odor at the arrival gate . The Flight Attendant noticed the odor during approach until gate arrival. After conferring with Maintenance the number two Pack was MEL'ed for the return trip to the crew base where the aircraft was removed from service.

Pilot reported an altitude deviation while descending on the initial approach in hazy flight conditions which resulted in a low altitude alert issued by ATC in an area of ground obstacles. The pilot initiated a climb to the assigned altitude.
G150 Captain reported returning to departure airport after the flaps failed to retract after takeoff.

Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain took the wrong turn after landing at SBP and proceeded onto Taxiway M in apparent violation of procedure. The First Officer stated that there were no NOTAMs or any indication on charts of that taxiway not being allowed for use at the airport.
CRJ-700 Captain reported a Flight Attendant notified of smoke and odor in the cabin. The Flight Crew returned to the gate for maintenance.
Pilot reported misinterpreting the airport charts and as a result; the airport's traffic pattern; resulting in a near midair collision.
B747 Captain reported an APU FIRE WARNING displayed on EICAS. The flight crew immediately ran the QRH and evacuated the aircraft.
ZAU Controller reported IND Tower and SBN TRACON use the same UHF Frequency of 257.8 which caused confusion when the controller attempted to issue frequency change.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a bird strike during cruise caused the UAS to crash.
Captain reported landing without clearance after being distracted by a wake turbulence encounter on the approach.
Student pilot reported loss of control during takeoff roll resulting runway excursion.

Air carrier pilot flying reported known GPS jamming/spoofing led to slight degradation in position accuracy due to unreliable GPS readings and other failures. All systems but one GPS recovered until the aircraft was back on the ground at the destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a failure to sign the NOTOC.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft turned the wrong direction after takeoff and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Captain reported frustrating delays and communications issues caused by the electronic maintenance program currently used.
Flight crew reported Captain's windshield shattered in cruise due to a malfunctioning windshield heat system. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.

ATC TRACON and Tower Controllers reported a departing aircraft turned the wrong way resulting in flight towards terrain. The TRACON Controller issued a low altitude alert and heading to get the aircraft safely back on their route.

A320 Captain reported a 'moderate chemical odor' during descent. Flight crew completed the appropriated Flight Crew Operations procedures which cause the odor to dissipate. Logbook entry and company odor form were submitted at destination.
Air Carrier Pilot reported a 'dirty socks' odor during preflight. The odor intensified resulting in securing the aircraft and the removal of flight crew.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a UAS during cruise flight.
Pilot reported confused wording and ATC intent of the SFF airport Felts Four Departure resulting in a pilot deviation to avoid traffic.
First Officer reported undocumented transport of Hazmat cargo due to improper DG tracking.
Cessna 150 pilot reported a loss of control during a crosswind landing at a non-towered airport. The pilot was blown to the left; attempted a go around; but landed safely on a taxiway.
A321 pilot reported returning to departure airport after experiencing an APU auto shutdown; which resulted in single power source operation since they were dispatched with a generator inoperative.
Air carrier pilot reported a ground conflict during takeoff roll resulting in a reject takeoff.

Air carrier ground personnel reported being called into the Supervisor's office due to the closeout stating the reporter had held off DG; but the reporter stated that was impossible as it was outside the reporter's authority to be able to do so.

Air carrier Captain reported a GPWS terrain warning due to a late turn to the final approach course and an overshoot of the final approach. The pilot climbed and re-intercepted the final approach and landed safely.
Instructor reported taking evasive action when two ultralights maneuvered too close in ZJX Class E airspace.
Pilot reported a Left Engine Failure after take off. The affected engine began to operate normally and the flight crew returned to landing at departure airport.
Captain reported confusion over MEL procedures and aircraft condition during preflight; but elected to take the aircraft.
TRACON Controller reported communication problems with an aircraft while the facility was being cleaned/disinfected.

B767 flight crew reported receiving a radio call from the tug push back operator that the tow bar broke during push back. The Captain set the parking brake; had the nose gear inspected; then ramp crew installed a new tow bar and the push back was resumed.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC in the vicinity of DLH airport.
Pilot reported a NMAC occurred on downwind when another aircraft crossed in front while entering the traffic pattern. Pilot stated this a common occurrence at the airport.
PA-28 pilot reported losing engine power after carburetor heat was applied during initial climb. Flight safely returned to departure airport.
B737 First Officer reported a fuel imbalance developed in cruise. Flight crew shut down an engine; rebalanced fuel and diverted to a landing.
A319 test flight crew reported returning to departure airport after inflight shutdown of #1 engine because of low oil pressure.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported troubleshooting a malfunctioning autopilot while on an RNAV approach resulted in low altitude alert from ATC and a CFTT event.
Controller reported similar calls signs may have contributed to an aircraft taking another aircraft's assigned altitude. Controller also cited staffing issues as a contributing factor.

A320 First Officer reported after a normal departure and gear retraction; noticing significant Noise/Banging Sound coming from the Nose Gear Area. Eventually; the flight crew received a Landing Gear Control Interface Unit 2 Master Caution and decided to return to the departure airport.

Air Carrier Captain reported undeclared Hazmat transported due to a communications breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch. The required NOTAC was not provided to the flight crew prior gate departure.
Flight Instructor reported there is a sunken spot on the taxiway at FPR Airport; where the aircraft's propeller nearly made contact with the pavement.
SLC Tower Controller reported an air carrier which had been instructed to taxi into position and hold began their takeoff roll with another air carrier taxiing across the runway.

Air Carrier Dispatch reported a communication breakdown between two different airport ramp personnel which resulted in undeclared Hazmat cargo being loaded on aircraft. Dispatcher notified flight crew.

Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict during taxi when ATC cleared them to cross a runway that had an aircraft on takeoff roll. Flight crew stopped past runway hold-short lines and departing aircraft rejected takeoff.
C172 flight instructor with student reported the aircraft began to shake violently during takeoff roll. Instructor rejected takeoff and returned to ramp.
PA 28-181 Flight Instructor reported an alternator failure during cruise; while on a training flight. The Instructor took control of the aircraft; diverted and landed safely.
TRACON Controller reported Tower departed two C172 aircraft and they had to turn one of the aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude to ensure required minimum separation.
