Air Carrier Captain reported an VHF radio dead zone.
Air carrier flight crew reported following a false glide path on the ILS 19 approach at ABL; resulting in a CFTT incident.

A320 Maintenance Technician reported that the incorrect part was listed in the Illustrated Parts Catalog (IPC) and needs to be corrected and updated. The installation of the incorrect part as instructed in the current IPC could lead to possible malfunction of the aircraft's thrust reverser system.
UAS pilot reported they unknowingly flew their UAS into a TFR. Reporter stated there were no TFRs prior to launch and the TFR became active during the flight.
Air carrier Flight Attendants reported being notified after a flight of a partial escape slide deployment. It was later determined it was a mechanical issue.

Single pilot aircraft reported a NMAC during climb just after takeoff. The pilot had been given a traffic warning by ATC; but the pilot states the wrong aircraft was identified. The passenger sighted the conflicting aircraft from a different direction.

Citation V Captain reported excessive vibration on the #2 Engine during cruise. The flight crew performed an in flight shut down of the affected engine after it was determined that the vibration remained uncontrollable and then diverted to a precautionary landing.
Albatross pilot reported that the aircraft's communication system malfunctioned in flight causing weak transmissions and eventually a loss of communications.
A320 Captain reported loss of both ELACs after take off. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Instructor reported the aircraft became hard to control; causing a return to home base and a precautionary emergency landing.
Air Carrier pilot reported non-emergency flight related transmissions on Guard frequency. Reporter stated this resulted in interrupted communications; creating a safety of flight issue.
Technicians reported a damaged winglet during towing operations.
C550 pilot reported aircraft oil temperature issues and returned to departure airport.

TRACON Controller reported a corporate jet on approach crossed through the final approach course and descended below the published crossing restriction resulting in flight below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A320 Flight Attendant reported a fume event in passenger cabin during landing and gate arrival.
Commercial jet flight crew reported confusing runway over-run markings at Cheddi Jagan International Airport in Guyana.
GA pilot reported a NMAC and Class B violation while executing evasive maneuvers.
Captain reported lack of clear communications by Aircraft X pilot resulted in taking evasive action on climb out; and a NMAC event.
A321 flight crew reported ground conflict with another aircraft during taxi into gate.
A Center Controller reported a cargo carrier had to descend unexpectedly due to pressurization failure.
Chancellor 414A Pilot reported Brake failure on landing rollout ending in a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft.
B737 MAX First Officer reported numerous performance and weight issues caused a delay and required off-loading passengers and baggage multiple times and multiple clearance requests before departing.

LR-60 Captain reported a Controlled Flight Toward Terrain (CFTT) event while executing a RNAV approach that had been assigned late after they had briefed another approach. Their FMS did not reflect an intermediate fix or corresponding altitude which led to an ATC Low Altitude alert and Go around instruction.
Air carrier Captain reported seeing a UAS while they were on final approach. Flight continued to landing.
Cessna Flight Instructor reported parked aircraft and vehicles blocking a taxi lane causing other aircraft to take evasive taxi routes to avoid.

Light aircraft pilot reported landing and while slowing down for a turnoff at Bravo 1 taxiway at SMO; both main tires ended up going flat. The pilot requested assistance from Maintenance and the aircraft was towed off the runway; but only after a long delay related to pavement condition.
Air carrier pilot not flying reported a course deviation due a navigation error to the wrong waypoint; as the waypoint KOMAR had showed up in two different locations that were not far apart.
CE680 Captain reported receiving a TAWS warning while on a circling approach resulted in a CFTT event. Flight crew corrected and continued approach.
CE-680 Captain reported the left windshield cracked during climb necessitating a diversion to a nearby airport.

ZBW Controller reported receiving a a loud squeal heterodyne tone in their ear which caused ear pain while working the Cambridge Sector. The reporter stated they have experienced dozens of these occurrences.
Air carrier First Officer reported a pressurization malfunction during climb. After completing the QRH the pilots returned to the departure airport.

Air Carrier safety analyst reported during preflight that a DG cargo Unit Load Device was incorrectly labeled. The cargo department was notified and a correct label was placed on the ULD prior to departure.
C560XL pilot reported autopilot captured a false glideslope resulting in descent below glidepath and Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
PA28-180 pilot reported an oil line break during a maintenance flight check. Pilot executed a return to the airport and made a normal landing.
BE36 pilot reported an engine cylinder head temperature exceedance; which resulted in an immediate diversion to a suitable airport.

Instructor Pilot reported the Trainee over controlled a maneuver and put the aircraft in stall. The Instructor was able to regain control of the aircraft. Later; damage to the aircraft was found and the aircraft grounded per Maintenance. The Instructor states the Trainee has not returned to the flight school.

Air carrier flight crew reported the standard taxi route chart does not match the depiction on the IAH airport information chart; and that a turn was difficult to identify; resulting in taxiway incursion.

Air carrier flight attendants reported that the service door girt bar became caught on the door frame; the catering personnel held the bar in place; and the slide did not deploy. Maintenance reinstalled the girt bar.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC during approach training with VFR opposite direction departure traffic. By taking an evasive action an airborne collision was avoided.
Technician reported aircraft arrived at station APU duct pressure seal clamp not installed at aft bulkhead in wheel well keel beam area. The aircraft was removed from service for repair.
DA40 student pilot reported that the rear door separated from the aircraft during climb. Flight landed safely.
Flight Instructor reported loss of engine thrust; causing a return to the departure airport for a precautionary landing.

CRJ-900 Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while conducting a visual approach. The Captain indicated the aircraft was vectored closer to the airport and upon receipt of the ATC low altitude alert; leveled the aircraft; stabilized; and landed safely.
Flight instructor flying C-172 aircraft reported high Cylinder Head Temperature in flight.
C182 pilot reported a generator failure resulting in landing at the destination airport with limited communications with ATC.
A TBM700 pilot reported they descended below the crossing restriction on the approach and received a Low Altitude Alert from Tower.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the lack of adequate staffing and lack of clear communication from ramp leadership resulted in last minute cargo reconfiguration of DG cargo.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported a lost link during flight. Connection was re-established leading to a safe landing.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported pressure from Maintenance Control department to install incorrect part onto airplane.

ARTCC controller reported having control for an aircraft from another facility but the frequency used didn't provide coverage at the aircraft's position and altitude while the aircraft was on a heading and altitude that would be below the MIA. Communications was established and the aircrew reported VMC and was issued a climb to the MIA.

EMB-135 First Officer reported they had a LG AIR/GND FAIL EICAS message during approach resulting in a go-around.  ATC eventually confirmed the gear was down allowing the pilots to safely land the aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported a hard landing due to a sudden wind speed decrease during the last 100 feet before landing.
Flight Attendant reported that life vest installed on an in-service aircraft were from a retired aircraft type and not approved for installation on this aircraft.
B737 First Officer reported a fuel imbalance during climbout.  Crew ran fuel leak checklist and returned to the departure airport after shutting done an engine.

B737-800 flight crew reported that another airline crew and ATC communicated that their aircraft had a fuel leak from the top of the wing. The flight crew elected to make an air turn back and a precautionary landing.
An Air Carrier pilot reported they were assigned an approach which was not available in their FMC Database.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL360 from a B747 that had climbed through his altitude earlier.
Air carrier Captain reported ATC failed to separate landing traffic from traffic on the runway resulted in a go-around and critical ground conflict.
First Officer reported low right engine oil pressure and quantity in cruise. The flight crew performed an inflight shut down and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
A321 flight crew reported after parking at the gate and setting the parking brake the aircraft rolled back; and the Captain quickly applied the brakes.
B737 flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control during approach due to severe turbulence.
Corporate flight crew reported a TA and RA during departure climb from CCR airport requiring evasive action.
A pilot departing AWG non-towered airport reported during takeoff roll they passed 200 feet in front of an aircraft taking off from an intersecting runway.
Corporate aircraft captain reported a low altitude alert from tower during an unstable approach. Pilot flying corrected and flight continued to landing.
First Officer reported a loss of thrust on the right engine. The Flight Crew elected to perform an in flight shut down of the affected engine and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
B737 flight crew reported a #2 Engine spool down and subsequent failure at cruise. A fuel imbalance followed and the flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft that required evasive action to avoid a collision.
Ground personnel reported a critical ground conflict with a tractor during pushback; requiring a sudden stop to avoid collision.
Air carrier flight crew reported descent below glidepath on initial approach due to incorrect altimeter setting resulted in a Ground Proximity Warning.
Tower Local Controller reported a C172 lined up on the runway without a clearance with a C750 on short final. The C750 was issued go around instructions.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported loss of directional control for unknown reasons after landing that resulted in a runway excursion.
Jump pilot reported having a near miss with another aircraft that had been vectored by ATC into the designated skydive area directly underneath them.
Flight Instructor reported their student lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a prop strike and left wingtip striking the surface.
An Instructor pilot in the pattern at a non-towered airport reported a NMAC.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a message from Dispatch during climbout that destination TRACON was closing before arrival.  Dispatch told crew to return to departure airport.

PUB Tower controllers reported while changing an aircraft's landing runway due to wind the instructions resulted in the aircraft flying towards an obstruction. ATC issued further instructions to keep the aircraft away from the obstruction and the aircraft landed safely at the airport.
B737 MAX flight crew reported an issue with the landing gear failing to indicate a proper retraction; which resulted in a return to the departure airport after a successful gear extension.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Runway 34R SEATTLE 7 SID initial departure heading out of SEA diverges from intended outbound course.

Air carrier flight crew reported SPS Tower issued an intermediate level off at the departure end of the runway; when issuing the takeoff clearance. The unpublished low altitude level off assigned by ATC resulted in a nonstandard departure profile and altitude exceedance required overriding aircraft automation in order to comply.
C150 instructor pilot reported fuel starvation resulted in engine shut down and an off airport landing.
Airbus A320 air carrier pilot reported electrical problems while climbing to cruise altitude. The crew coordinated with the cabin crew to troubleshoot the problem; diverted; and landed safely.
B777 Technician reported a partial inadvertent escape slide deployment while checking the door. More training was suggested.
Air taxi pilot reported the left brake pedal went to the floor during parking brake release. Pilot returned to parking.
Corporate aircraft Captain reported overshooting assigned altitude during departure due to a late ATC Clearance. Captain also cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
A321 Captain reported an uncommanded roll occurred departing PHX that may have been related to a wake turbulence encounter or an engine stall event.

Air carrier Captain reported a loss of nosewheel control when entering the ramp due to icy surface conditions while taxiing to park at night at a non-towered airport. The Captain could not maintain directional control and received a tow safely to the gate with a tug.
Air carrier flight crew flying A319 aircraft reported aircraft loss of control due to severe turbulence at cruise altitude.
Aircraft tug driver reported tug steering system failure while towing aircraft.
Embraer first officer reported altitude and heading deviations caused by unanticipated turbulence.
C525 pilot reported a partial loss of engine power in cruise. Pilot diverted and landed.

Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC at non-towered airport in the traffic pattern. The other pilot's transmissions were difficult to hear and when the student observed the aircraft it was already taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
Student pilot with instructor reported a NMAC during cruise requiring evasive action to avoid a collision.

Technicians reported they were called out to the aircraft for both Overwing exit door slides not showing armed. Maintenance declined to apply MELs. Technicians went out and found on both doors the slide arming levers were not slid / engaged into the door frames. The aircraft was repaired as required.
Air carrier First Officer reported the cockpit fire extinguisher was leaking and as a result; they diverted to a nearby airport.
B747-400 flight crew reported during approach they had a #1 engine failure which caused the aircraft to turn left. They corrected and were stable on the localizer and landed without incident.
Flight Attendant reported disarming the door slide improperly causing the slide pack to partially fall out.
Helicopter pilot on final approach into Class D airport took evasive action to avoid a collision with a UAS. The pilot notified ATC.
Captain reported push back from gate without required Hazmat documents.
PA-28 instructor pilot with trainee reported an engine fire during engine run-up prior to takeoff.

Pilot reported that powered paragliders crossed the runways causing an airplane to abort takeoff roll. The paragliders also entered an area with airborne skydivers. Reporter stated close calls with paragliders are an ongoing issue.
Cessna 150 student pilot reported an engine malfunction while on a solo training flight practicing maneuvers. The student turned to a nearby non-towered airport and landed safely.

Technicians reported misindexing a fan blade set during a routine fan blade lube procedure. The aircraft was returned to the hangar and the fan blades were removed and reinstalled in their correct positions.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below their assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Aircraft began taxi while ground signal to hold was still being presented by ramp guide person. Other ground personnel quickly completed tasks and moved out of hazard area to safety.
Pilots reported a ground conflict with an unknown aircraft while looking for ATC issued traffic.
Pilot reported confusing ground markings leading to a spot out of the terminal area in MIA.

Captain reported Auto Throttle; Auto Pilot and erratic AOA indications during climb. During troubleshooting; it was noticed that the Trailing Edge Flaps had failed to retract fully. The flight crew diverted to make a precautionary landing.
B787 Captain and First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence 25 miles in trail of an A330 in cruise flight at FL360 that resulted in a minor Flight Attendant injury.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported receiving an RA on approach requiring an evasive maneuver.

Cessna 172 pilot and ground personnel reported a critical ground conflict with a Citation jet at a non-towered airport when the pilot's headset malfunctioned and they landed opposite direction on the same runway as the jet.
Air carrier pilots reported an airspeed indicator; that was previously reported as malfunctioning; began to display error flags and caused a rejected takeoff.
Captain reported cabin did not pressurize on climb and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing.
B767 Captain reported cabin pressurization malfunction during cruise flight. Flight crew donned oxygen masks; descended to safe altitude; and continued to destination.
A320 Flight Attendant reported a fume event during initial departure. 'Dirty socks' odor dissipated shortly thereafter.
B787 First Officer reported the nosewheel steering system malfunctioned during taxi-out to the runway requiring the aircraft to be towed back to the gate.
GA flight instructor reported making a go-around to avoid departing traffic from intersecting runway resulted in an NMAC.

B767 Captain and Dispatcher reported misunderstanding of the Dry Ice Supplemental Procedures in the QRH; but a Supervisor found a supporting document that provided further information regarding the MEL's restrictions on dry ice for the flight to continue safely.
B737 First Officer reported a Flight Attendant had been injured after the flight crew encountered wake turbulence on descent into ORD in trail of a B777.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported a need for delay vectors to burn fuel and when cleared back to a fix for the approach they were below the MIA for the area resulting in ATC issuing a low altitude alert; climb; and vector back on course.
Helicopter Flight Instructor reported while practicing a power off approach to land on a taxiway they were unable to comply with Tower landing instructions and flew 30 feet over a taxiing Cessna.
CL-350 pilot reported during cruise climb hearing a loud noise and crackling sound coming from the cabin sidewall. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
Air carrier captain reported GPS jamming and spoofing over Myanmar airspace.

B737 flight crew reported exceeding 250 knots below 10;000 feet after planning to slow from an assigned 280 knots several thousand feet above 10;000 feet to accommodate the Captain's relative inexperience.

B737 NG Flight Crew reported receiving a terrain warning on short final approach to DEN airport due to flap misconfiguration. Flight crew stated they received a late runway change which resulted in a higher-than-normal rate of descent and not completing a checklist.
Technician reported signing for identical work on both engines of an ETOPS aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported the dimly painted taxiway markings at SEA airport combined with dark and rainy conditions make the taxi lines difficult to see.

A Tower Local Controller and the Controller in Charge reported the Local Controller anticipated the landing aircraft would exit the runway but it stopped to ask for taxi instructions as a departing aircraft was taking off behind it.
A319 Captain reported aircraft limitation due to APU and fuel transfer pump failure resulted in aircraft refusal.

Flight Instructor and Student reported while taxiing onto the runway for departure experiencing a brake failure resulting in an uncommanded 180 degree turn.  The pilots were able to regain control of the aircraft and shut down the aircraft.
Corporate jet flight crew expressed concerns about the new Runway 18 VFR Departure procedure out of SPS.

TBM pilot reported the aircraft started to vibrate during climb in icing conditions. Pilot descended to warmer conditions which caused the propeller to sling off accumulated ice and the vibrations to stop.
Student pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport.

Maintenance technician reported a ramp employee noticed smoke coming from the left main landing gear on push back from the gate. Maintenance crew investigated and found the #4 main landing gear bearing had failed.
Pilot flying DHC-6 aircraft reported cabin door departed inflight.
A321 flight crew reported loss of generator drive and APU inflight. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
C172 pilot reported a NMAC with another high wing aircraft at a non-towered airport. C172 pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving an airborne notification that the Captain's signed NOTOC copy was mistakenly kept by the ground personnel instead of placing the Captain's NOTOC in the aircraft's DG pouch.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during approach to X04 non-towered airport requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

C182 Flight Instructor with student reported the student misapplied inputs during an attempted go-around prompting the instructor to help control the aircraft. They overflew a hangar during the go-around.

M20K pilot elected to divert to an intermediate airport after fuel indications did not reflect fuel calculations.  Pilot reported some coordination issues with ATC regarding destination and alternate selections.
Aircraft Technician reported missing step in maintenance procedure to restore circuit breakers for VOR antenna.

Air carrier Captain reported rejecting the takeoff after receiving a GLD UNSAFE caution message and observing inaccurate radar altimeter indications. Maintenance replaced the radar altimeter sensor and the flight was re-dispatched.
Avionics Lead Technician reported ground damage from an improperly positioned scissors lift contacting the TE right OB flap.
Air carrier First Officer reported collision hazards on visual approach from STAR and indicated need to highlight them on Jeppesen approach chart to OMA airport.
B787 First Officer reported smoke and cargo heat indications resulted in a diversion. First Officer noted the lack of a checklist for cargo heat bulk warning.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a ground proximity warning after feeling rushed to execute a short approach assigned by ATC.

CRJ-700 flight crew reported a gear disagree warning after takeoff due to landing gear not fully retracting. The flight crew extended the landing gear and returned to departure airport where they landed safely.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a ground conflict while lined up and waiting for takeoff clearance.
Cherokee 180 pilot reported loss of control just after touchdown; resulting in a runway excursion. No damage was reported.

A B737 Captain reported the B737 FMS routinely descends below the PERKN crossing restriction on the CAVLR6 STAR to IAD; and expressed concern that the profile on this STAR is too steep for B737-800 and -900 aircraft.
B737 Captain reported receiving multiple copies of disorganized preliminary and final DG documents during taxi and climb-out; which the reporter found unusual to occur.

An Air Carrier flight crew departing HNL reported they followed an incorrect taxiway resulting in a potential conflict with an arriving aircraft turning off the runway. The crew reported poor taxiway signage at HNL is confusing.
Pilot reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern with an overtaking aircraft.
TRACON Controller reported they allowed an IFR aircraft to descend below MVA when the controller mistakenly thought they were VFR. Pilot reported they had terrain in sight and cancelled IFR.
Controller reported aircraft descended below assigned altitude resulted in a CFIT event.
B747-400 flight crew reported diverting after A/C Packs would not maintain cabin altitude with Number 1 pack already deferred.

Captain reported a near miss with a general aviation aircraft while under air traffic control in IMC conditions; descending on a published arrival procedure. The Captain responded to the TCAS RA; reported the TCAS event to ATC and continued the descent to a landing.

B767-300 First Officer reported a loss of thrust on the right engine after reaching cruise altitude. Flight crew was unable to restart the engine which had an MEL for the right thrust reverser being inoperative and landed uneventfully at departure airport.
Air Carrier Maintenance Technician reports creating an RII in error for 777 IDG feeder cable installation.

ERJ 145 Captain reported that the Captain of the inbound flight reported the aircraft had a bird strike during approach. A maintenance inspection was accomplished on the ground and damage to the number 1 engine was found.
Air carrier pilot reported abruptly braking to avoid a collision with an airport vehicle while taxiing that was escorting two other vehicles.
A SR-22 Pilot reported overflying another aircraft while on approach to land.
Pilot reported losing the alternator on a ferry flight and landing NORDO.
Pilots reported being too fast on final; resulting in an unstable approach and a low altitude alert.
B767-300 Flight Attendant reported the transport of Li Ion battery cargo with insufficient number of containment bags.
GA instructor reported smoke in the cockpit after takeoff and elected to do an Air Turn Back and a precautionary landing.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving a false GPWS 'too low terrain' warning on short final at DFW.
ATC Controllers reported an airport vehicle did not hold short of the runway for landing traffic.

B737 Captain reported aircraft rapidly pitched up during a power-off test flight that required full forward yoke to restore hydraulic power. A postflight debrief later revealed the elevator tabs were inappropriately adjusted due to a misinterpretation of the chart in the task card.
Air Carrier flight crew reported incorrect taxi clearance was issued by ATC and cited possible staffing issues at the controlling facility as a contributing factor.
Pilot reported communications and procedural errors caused two near midair collisions while conducting training at LMO uncontrolled airport.

B-737 Captain reported a ground conflict with the pushback ramp agent; when the Captain did not release the ramp agent from pushback duties and began to taxi the aircraft while the ramp agent remained near the aircraft. The ramp agent ran away from the aircraft and no injuries were reported.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a loss of UAV control during flight and a subsequent flyaway. During post flight review the UAS crew learned of a GPS failure within the UAS.
T-6 Instructor pilot reported G-Limit exceedance during windshear recovery and NMAC with lead aircraft.
E175 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering severe wake turbulence in trail of a B747 on approach to SFO.

A Center Controller reported an aircraft descended from its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude. The Controller reported the Minimum Safe Altitude Warning did not activate.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during preflight at departure gate. Maintenance was requested resulting in an aircraft swap.
CRJ900 Captain reported flight attendant notification of smoke in cabin during initial climb and elected to return to the departure airport.
Government UAS pilot reported the UAS struck a wire during cruise flight and crashed.

A320 First Officer reported the IDG oil temperature was spiking during cruise and received an ECAM memo for an overheat indication. The ECAM; along with the poor weather at destination; led the flight crew to divert to a nearby airport.
PA-18 pilot reported losing directional control on landing roll in a crosswind situation.

Air carrier Captain reported a terrain alert while descending on a visual approach to reach the published altitude at the final approach fix. The crew performed the terrain alert SOP; then continued the approach and landed.

BE35 pilot reported engine failure after selecting the right fuel tank with the cross-feed valve. The pilot immediately selected the left tank; restarted the engine; and returned to departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported an inflight upset resulted from an encounter with severe turbulence in the vicinity of LWL VOR.
Air Carrier flight crew reported observing a UAS pass their aircraft while descending through 5;000 ft. No evasive action was taken and the aircraft landed normally.
C560 Pilot reported being vectored by ATC directly into a cloud cell and lost several hundred feet of altitude and 50 knots of airspeed resulting in a subsequent slow climb back to assigned altitude.
SR20 flight instructor on training flight with student reported an airframe overspeed incident while practicing unusual attitudes.
Air carrier Captain reported recovering from an upset related to a wake turbulence encounter on approach to CLT in trail of a heavy jet.
Helicopter instructor pilot reported an NMAC with a light plane in the pattern at 1B9 non-towered airport.
B737-800 flight crew during preflight oxygen test reported the O2 valve was not fully open.

Air carrier pilot reported that while enroute from SJU to MEM the flight crew was navigating to fix VANZE but their clearance was direct VENDS. The confusion was due to the similar sounding fix names.

UAS pilot reported that while the UAS was in cruise flight being flown by the autopilot the flight crew made changes to the flight profile; resulting in an undesirable aircraft state. The pilot saw the error and made a manual correction before there was a loss of aircraft control.
Air carrier flight crew had a near miss with a UAS while descending on a STAR.
ZLA Controller reported SCT failed to follow letter of agreement resulted in aircraft being in a higher MVA and a CFTT event.
C172 pilot reported a jammed stabilizer/elevator while attempting to land the aircraft; resulting in a balked landing; a blown tire; and a runway excursion.

Technicians reported that during troubleshooting an aircraft for erroneous fire warning indications; the supervisor on duty directed them to not document the swapping of components used so as to not have to comply with a landing gear operational check that was required and would require an RII action.
B737-800 flight crew reported the #1 Engine failed during the climb and proceeded to shut down the affected engine. The flight crew then performed an air turnback.
Air Carrier Captain reported there is an unlighted island that is very difficult to see at night or during inclement weather during taxi; Lima between spot 20 and 23; at LGA.

B737 NG First Officer reported a go-around after the trialing edge flaps did not move to the commanded position. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
Air carrier Dispatch reported the aircraft had to return to the gate for a hazmat issue and upon blocking out again; the aircraft received an erroneous aircraft or call sign mismatch message.
Technician reported that an aircraft arriving at his station was discovered to have the right pack exhaust diffuser installed incorrectly. The aircraft was removed from service for repair.

Cessna 441 Pilot reported a left main landing gear failure on landing. The aircraft became uncontrollable and departed the runway resulting in damage from colliding with runway lights. The aircraft was removed from the runway and taken to a hangar for maintenance action.
B737NG flight crew reported a distraction from wake turbulence contributed to climbing through an altitude restriction departing DEN in trail of a B777.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying inside a building which was located in controlled airspace.
M-20 E pilot reported the landing gear collapsed during normal landing.
Pilot on VFR cross country flight inadvertantly entered IMC and exited back to VFR conditions with ATC assistance.
EMB145 Flight Attendant reported cabin door handle snapped off when attempting to open door at gate.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported NMAC with another aircraft in climb.

Tower Local Controller reported a departing Air Carrier encountered a NMAC with a VFR survey mission aircraft. The Controller and their trainee failed to issue traffic information to either aircraft and handed them off to adjacent airspace frequencies.

A321 First Officer reported a Low Brake Pressure EICAS message when setting the parking brake after a pushback; resulting in the aircraft moving on its own when the parking brake malfunctioned. The Captain activated setting the parking brake and no injury or damage occurred.
Corporate jet Captain reported GPS jamming in ZBW airspace. The jamming began as another aircraft passed overhead and lasted approximately five minutes.

A319 flight crew reported a TCAS RA event and a difficult time seeing the visible TCAS solution guidance on the PFD display due to sunlight and dark CRT screens. The crew received a clear of conflict message and continued the approach and landing.
B737-800 pilot monitoring reported encountering wake turbulence while descending on the arrival behind an A220.
A321 flight crew reported malfunction with FADEC. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Air taxi First Officer reported an altitude deviation during arrival and cited short staffing; fatigue and stress due to the COVID-19 pandemic as contributing factors.
B787 flight crew reported a loss of nose wheel steering when lining up on the runway for takeoff. The Captain stopped the aircraft on the runway and a tug was sent to tow the aircraft to the gate.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming as probable cause of GPS navigation and transponder failure; instead of temporary interference.
A Center Controller reported a departing air ambulance flight on initial climb turned back to the departure airport flying below the minimum IFR altitude and in conflict with an arriving air carrier.
CL-350 flight crew reported descent toward terrain during approach. Flight crew climbed back to safe altitude and continued approach.

GA pilot reported an NMAC while on final approach. As the pilot neared the runway at 300 feet and approximately 1/4 mile; a faster low wing aircraft passed by 100 feet higher and slightly left; for the same runway. The pilot stated there was no traffic advisory or warning from the Tower Controller prior to or during the event.
Air carrier pilot reported the need to take evasive action during taxi when a tug drove in front of the aircraft.
Flight crew reported a cargo fire during cruise and a subsequent diversion to a nearby airport.
A320 Captain reported the landing gear failed to extend during final approach. The Captain executed a go around and completed a safe landing.
A fractional flight crew reported the aircraft deviated off course on initial climb and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier First Officer reported a taxiway and runway incursion at ORD and the need for a hot spot designation at the intersection of taxiways A2 and G.
Corporate jet Captain reported receiving a terrain alert on initial approach.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported the windshield cracked during climb and there was a burning smell in the cabin. Flight diverted and landed safely.
Air carrier Check Airman reported receiving a low altitude alert from tower during a night visual approach.
Flight crew reported pressurization failure in cruise and returned to the departure airport.
DCA Tower Controller reported a runway incursion between two air carriers.

GA pilot reported Alternator Failure in cruise. Smoke was coming from the panel; so the flight crew turned the Alternator switch to off and turned back to the departure airport while requesting priority handling. The flight crew made a precautionary landing and shut down to evacuate on the runway.

F900 Captain reported receiving a ground proximity warning while descending as there was an obstacle on a ridge on approach. The flight crew made a minor altitude correction; continued the approach; and landed.

A321 Captain reported a fume event during preflight which necessitated a request for maintenance. Captain deplaned the entire flight crew and suggested a call to medical services if anyone experienced physiological symptoms.
Captain reported the First Officer read an old page from their QRH which resulted in incorrect landing data for the aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unusual persistent acrid smell upon boarding aircraft. Since Maintenance was unable to correct the odor the flight continued with a different aircraft.

Air carrier flight crew reported they descended to an ATC assigned altitude and heading during arrival which turned them toward terrain. Flight crew stopped descent and ATC advised a climb; stating a change in runways contributed to the event.
Pilot reported a return to departure airport was due to Single Power Source; due to loss of Engine Generator and the APU being on MEL Placard.
S46 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below their assigned altitude and flew below the minimum vectoring altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a vector to final for a visual approach to BHM from ATC that created a conflict with a published ground obstacle.
C172 Flight Instructor and student reported large fluctuations in engine RPM during cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
GA pilot reported running low on fuel with the runway closed and without sufficient fuel to divert; elected to land on a taxiway.
Air carrier flight crew reported deviations from standard practices due to distractions in the cabin; causing check list omissions.
Air carrier pilot reported a missed approach due to glideslope intercept on ILS 13L at JFK being located at a low altitude.  Reporter suggested a notation about this aspect of the approach.
Ground Personnel reported baggage loader contact with aircraft.
PA28 pilot reported loss of engine power and elected to execute an Air Turn Back to a precautionary landing.
B737-800 Technician reported aircraft arriving at station was found to have the APU Pneumatic Duct Pressure Seal Clamp not installed. The aircraft was removed from service for Maintenance action.

B767-300 flight crew reported the engine oil temperature exceeded limits and elected to perform an in flight shut down of the affected engine. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.

Technician reported a Ramp/Cargo Manager cleared an aircraft for operation following a reported engine leak. The Technician reported this to Maintenance and the aircraft was directed to return to the gate for Maintenance action.
Captain reported a Rejected Take Off caused by a left engine compressor stall followed by over temperature and uncommanded shut down.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC due to descending below a crossing restriction on the approach.
Beech Baron 55 instructor and trainee reported they experienced a tire failure on landing roll.
UAS pilot did not check location during flight planning and conducted a flight within controlled airspace.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft departed a non towered airport located adjacent to the final approach course at SAT and climbed on a conflicting course with an IFR arrival on final approach.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported learning the Remote ID Broadcast Module stopped operating for an unknown amount of time during the flight.
Ground employee reported plastic shields should be added to the tables in a break room.
Air carrier pilot reported difficulty viewing instructions from the aircraft marshaller during gate arrival due to bright lights from the jetway that obscure pilots' vision.

A319 flight crew reported confusion and communications problems with MEL recording and release information. An additional MEL was overlooked that was missing. The discrepancies were not discovered until after departure.
Flight Instructor reported a near miss with a UAS while in the traffic pattern and took evasive action.
C172 pilot reported partial power loss shortly after takeoff and returned to departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC event during approach with a military aircraft attempting to land on same runway. The air carrier took evasive action to avoid a collision.
King Air C90 E90 pilot reported during approach the autopilot was inadvertently disconnected; resulting in an excursion from assigned altitude and a low altitude warning from ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming near the fix EGSIS.

Air carrier Captain reported an unannounced tow bar disconnect during pushback resulting in an aircraft breakaway. Captain realized the situation and stopped the aircraft movement enabling the tow crew to reconnect headset complete a safe pushback.
B757-200 flight crew reported the progressive loss of the Right Hydraulic System Quantity in cruise. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Pilot reported drifting left of course on approach and elected to fly a missed approach. Pilot further reported spotting a cell tower protruding thru the clouds and maneuvered to avoid it.
B737-800 First Officer reported an electric trim failure during climbout resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport
Tug driver reported while pulling aircraft to gate the tow bar disconnected from the aircraft resulting in the loss of control of aircraft and damage.
Citation Captain reported during the climb experiencing left engine low oil pressure warnings resulting in the flight crew returning to the departure airport where they landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported undocumented transport of Lithium Ion battery powered wheelchair.
MD11 flight crew reported multiple systems failures that required attention and distracted them; resulting in exceeding an airspeed limitation.
T-6 Texan pilot reported evading a descending aircraft during climb-out.
Air Carrier flight crew reported the gear failed to retract after takeoff. After completion of the QRH the crew returned to the airport and made an overweight landing.
TRACON Controller reported the Tower Local Controller issued go around instructions to an aircraft which placed the aircraft below the MVA.
Air carrier pilot reported ramp agent approached the aircraft and opened the cargo door while the engines were running.
Air carrier pilot reported observing a UAS pass by their aircraft while descending to 10;000 ft. The incident was reported to ATC.
EMB-505 Captain reported electrical and cabin pressurization malfunction during cruise. Flight diverted and landed safely.
Pilot departing a non-towered airport reported an NMAC with a glider landing opposite direction in the grass abeam the runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC due to descending below crossing fix altitude on approach.
B777 Captain reported a crew rest compartment smoke message illuminated during cruise. The flight crew diverted and landed safely.

RV-9 pilot reported a fuel system malfunction in flight required a diversion to a nearby airport. Inspection revealed an unsecured fitting on the gascolator had allowed air enter the fuel pump; leading to fuel starvation.

A321 Captain reported an undocumented transport of Dangerous Goods. Dangerous Goods were loaded onboard without the flight crew receiving and completing the required Dangerous Goods Form prior to gate departure.
PA-46 pilot aircraft reported engine failure during taxi after landing.
PA-28 Flight Instructor with student reported engine roughness and power loss while executing a missed approach. Flight crew returned to land at departure airport.
Fractional captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during approach descent.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they had a near miss with a military helicopter during a training flight and took evasive action.
DA40 Flight Instructor and trainee reported a rejected takeoff due to an RPM over speed.

Air carrier Captain reported becoming unstabilized due to turning onto a short final during a clearance for a visual approach. The Captain directed a go around but the First Officer maneuvered to correct the approach path; received a sink warning; then stabilized the approach.
B737-800 flight crew performed a rejected takeoff due to Aft Entry warning light.
Part 107 UAS Remote Pilot in Command reported the UAS experienced a lost link during the flight and crashed moments later.
A Fractional Jet pilot reported Cabin Pressurization malfunction; loss of oil pressure and several other systems malfunctions causing them to divert to a nearby airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported unstable approach and CFIT alert. They performed a go around and landed uneventfully.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport in daylight visual conditions during a training flight. The instructor identified the other aircraft performing a non-standard entry into the traffic pattern and maneuvered abruptly to avoid a possible collision; then landed safely.

Air carrier crew reported an unstabilized approach where the flight was low on the final approach profile due to an incorrect altimeter setting. The Captain identified the low visual profile and regained the glide slope and landed normally.
A321 Captain reported a fume event during arrival taxi. At arrival gate maintenance accurately diagnosed the issue.
A321 Captain reported the landing gear required multiple attempts for extension to occur.
Technician reported aircraft arrived with multiple issues attached as log page items and some not captured as log page items.
An air carrier pilot reported on initial climb they received a TCAS warning for an aircraft which departed the parallel runway.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported two pallets of life vests (Hazmat) received for shipping. The shipment exceeded the weight limitation and was refused.

Air carrier Captain reported the flight crew received two TCAS Resolution Advisories while on approach to SFO Runway 28L due to traffic on approach to the parallel runway. The flight crew completed a go-around; a second approach attempt; and landing. The Captain made several recommendations regarding parallel approach operations at SFO.

Air carrier flight crew reported a cancelled takeoff clearance and low speed rejected takeoff after landing aircraft failed to timely clear the runway. ATC told a third aircraft that was landing to go around.
EMB-135LR First Officer reported the autopilot failed on descent; possibly related to a pitch trim issue.
M20 pilot reported an encounter with heavy rain and severe downdrafts resulted in a temporary loss of control.
PC-12 pilot reported a passenger reported seeing smoke under a seat shortly before landing. Pilot continued to safe landing.

Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported during the landing roll-out ATC changed the taxiway they were to use to exit the runway. The problem; as reported by the pilots; the runway was much slicker than advertised and the jet began to slide out of control. The taxiway given by ATC does not have a connection to the runway and once stopped the jet needed a ground tug and snow removal equipment to proceed. The pilots stated; lack of information regarding runway condition and the late taxiway change complicated the situation.
Tow tug driver reported near collision with a 737 taxiing while towing another 737 aircraft.

MD-11 Captain reported Engine 3 Lubrication System malfunction during cruise resulting in airframe vibration. After troubleshooting the issue; the flight crew shutdown the engine and requested priority handling to a diversion airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a flight attendant was slightly injured after falling when the Captain had to abruptly stop to avoid a collision with a ground vehicle during taxi at SEA.
Flight Instructor and student reported an NMAC at BMC non-towered airport.  The pilot of the other aircraft admitted being on the wrong frequency which caused the conflict.
Instructor Pilot reported a loss of power in a Cessna 172 during cruise flight and performed an off airport landing.
B737 First Officer reported during a single engine taxi into gate aircraft nosewheel traction was compromised due to deicing fluid on the ramp.

Air carrier Captain reported the proximity or distance sensor of the AccuPark system guidance at LPPT/LIS remote park stand 412 was malfunctioning and preemptively stopped to get assistance from ramp personnel.

CE-550 flight crew reported a loss of directional control while initiating the take-off roll. The aircraft came to a stop as the nose wheel exited the right edge of the runway with the nose wheel on the grass area with no damage.
Flight Instructor reported the student lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event described as a strong dirty socks odor during preflight when starting the APU and opening APU bleed valve. Bleed was secured and maintenance was summoned.
B737 NG Captain reported transiting through a forcasted area of Ozone after missing an ACARS message.
B767 First Officer reported experiencing wake turbulence from a preceding aircraft while using SLOPs.

Flight Crew reported when selecting Flaps Up I heard a loud bang (pop); a TE FLAPS DISAGREE EICAS (no TE Light Illuminated) and Flap Indicator was between Up and 1 after takeoff. The Flight Crew requested vectors and ran the check lists and QRH. After talking with Operations and a SME; the Flight Crew requested priority handling and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Baron 58 Pilot reported a fuel bladder rupture caused a substantial fuel leak while in flight.

Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an ATC clearance to a waypoint that was not in the aircraft navigation database or Jeppesen publication; but was identified in the weather app on the EFB. ATC provided a heading to the waypoint.
Beechcraft A36 pilot reported track and altitude excursions occurred in heavy turbulence during cruise flight at 11;000 feet.
MYF Tower Controllers reported a Cessna Citation failed to follow initial departure instructions resulted in a NMAC.
A small single engine GA pilot reported GPS outage caused their auto pilot to fail and they descended below their assigned altitude.
Deadheading pilot reported feeling as if one of the wheels hit an object during takeoff roll and advised the crew.  Post flight inspection revealed there was wheel damage and also a fuel leak.
C172 instructor pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft climbing underneath them.

EMB-145 flight crew reported receiving a HYD1 LO QTY indication during cruise followed by a HYD SYS 1 FAIL message.  The crew ran all pertinent checklists and decided to continue to their destination.

CRJ700 Captain reported a malfunction that precludes flight into icing and resulted in failure of ATC and Company Dispatch to understand crew requests. ATC's failure to expedite escalated flight to a fuel critical situation.
E145 flight crew reported an engine failure in flight caused an air turn back and precautionary landing.
B737 flight crew reported they braked suddenly while taxiing to avoid a conflict with another taxiing aircraft crossing their path.
Air carrier Captain reported a near mid-air collision with a drone while on approach to MIA.

B737 flight crew reported an unstabilized approach in windshear alert conditions reported by ATC. The pilot flying continued the approach after speed increased by 10 knots at low altitude which caused the flaps to retract causing a EGPWS terrain warning for flaps in a non-landing configuration. The crew elected not to go around due to moderate rain at the far end of the runway; and landed safely.

BE95 Flight Instructor reported shutting down the #1 engine at 13000 feet msl and attempted to restart it several times. At 10000 feet msl; they realized the right engine alternator was not working; advised ATC; and landed.
Airbus Captain reported discrepancies between the charted procedure and the FD PRO App on the PHIBO3 arrival to EWR.

Air carrier First Officer reported returning to departure airport after experiencing inflight failure of #1 engine. Separately; the reporter described being pressured to start an engine at the gate that had a significant fuel leak.
B767 Captain reported refusing an aircraft that had an inoperative back up generator.
Air carrier First Officer reported deviating from ATC clearance due to the confusing message format of the CPDLC clearance.
Small Transport aircraft pilot reported a ground conflict with a vehicle during takeoff from LXT airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a NMAC with another aircraft on approach.
C501 First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence that resulted in the inability to maintain altitude.

CRJ-900 crew reported a L Bleed Warning and subsequent R PACK going offline during climb after bleed warning had been worked on for over 7 hours prior to departure.  Crew returned to departure airport and landed normally.
General aviation pilot flying reported the pilot-controlled lighting system at PVB Airport was inoperative and there were no NOTAMs for the issue.
Bristell pilot reported loss of directional control on landing resulted in a runway excursion.

Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict with a vehicle which crossed on the service road section in front of the gate at a high rate of speed. The Captain executed a sudden stop to avoid a collision.

B757 Captain reported receiving a leading edge slat asymmetry message during climb out. The flight crew discussed options with company and continued to destination where an uneventful landing was made.
Air carrier Flight Attendants reported a passenger had notified the cabin crew that there were lithium batteries in the checked bag; and the batteries were then retrieved from the bag.
TBM 900 pilot reported during approach in gusty conditions; the ESP System activated at 5 feet above ground; pushing the nose over as in a stall condition; resulting in a propeller strike.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a NMAC with another aircraft in the traffic pattern. The pilot of the other aircraft also reported the event.
Light aircraft pilot reported experiencing smoke in the cockpit when the battery was dislodged by a wake turbulence encounter.
B737 flight crew reported a large difference in bleed duct pressures during climb. Flight returned to departure airport for maintenance.
TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft on a SID flew the wrong transition and was below the minimum vectoring altitude.

Air Carrier Captain reported the First Officer did not comply with ATC speed assignments and an altitude assignment. The Captain reported he did not monitor the flying pilot close enough and became distracted with side conversations and set the flaps improperly.
A319 pilot reported open indication on cargo door inflight. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near-collision with another air carrier on the ramp in ORF.
Flight Attendant reported a loss of aircraft pressurization during cruise resulted in sinus; head; and ear pain.
Light aircraft student pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the pattern at SYR due to misidentifying the aircraft cleared to follow.
Flight Instructor reported the need for evasive action during landing to avoid a maintenance worker on the runway.
C172 flight instructor with student reported a taxiway excursion due to brake failure.
Embraer 500 Captain reported speed and altitude excursions after encountering either wake turbulence or wind gust in cruise flight at FL400.
B737-900 First Officer reported a configuration warning Illuminated at 100 kts during take off. The Captain performed a rejected take off and the flight returned to the gate for an aircraft swap.
Air carrier pilot reported multiple flight deck oxygen masks were missing required components.

B737 MAX Captain reported systems failures and communications problems led to a landing on the wrong runway instead of performing a Go-Around. A Tower Controller also reported on the wrong runway landing.
Air carrier Captain reported FO steep approach angle initiated a terrain alert due to older technology limitations.
Air Carrier Captain reported he mistakenly handled the Dangerous Goods Form by giving Captain's copy to ground personnel rather than keeping the Captain's copy on aircraft to destination.
SR22 pilot entering downwind to a non-towered airport reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern.

B787 flight crew encountered an unreported and sudden severe mountain wave during cruise flight; resulting in an over speed condition and uncontrolled loss of altitude. The crew recovered from the speed exceedance and loss of altitude; reported the incident to ATC and maintenance; and continued the flight to destination.
A320 pilot reported a 'dirty socks' fumes event during passenger deplaning. Maintenance determined the fumes were caused by an APU oil leak.
A pilot reported observing a UAS pass by their aircraft at 7000 feet while flying in controlled airspace. The incident was reported to the Approach Controller; then the flight continued normally.
A321 flight crew reported while parked awaiting the jetbridge ramp personnel approached the aircraft and opened the cargo door while the engines were running.
PA-24 pilot reported failing to notify TRACON of safe landing as requested following precautionary landing after experiencing a runaway propeller.
General aviation pilot reported newly installed power lines with wire markers are lower than the existing power lines and the higher power lines do not have any warning indicators.

Flight Instructor reported while conducting training on a simulated engine failure on takeoff; the student retracted the flaps completely; resulting in the aircraft sinking back down to the runway and hitting the landing gear hard.
B757 Flight crew reported a Right Engine Tail Pipe fire after push back and requested airport CFR and shut the engine down. The aircraft was taxied back into the gate with the left engine.

S-58T pilot reported a diversion after decreasing engine torque on #2 engine.  After a manual restart and ferry flight to a maintenance base; borescope of the affected engine found major damage to the internal components of the affected engine.
SR22 pilot reported a runway excursion after hydroplaning during landing.
Center Controllers reported the Radar Controller issued a descent clearance to an altitude below the Minimum IFR Altitude and did not notice it until the MSAW activated.
Pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. Both pilots took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Air carrier Captain reported severe turbulence during landing which resulted in a missed approach. During the initial climb the flight experienced a windshear event; the flight crew executed the escape maneuver and continued to the alternate airport.
C152 flight Instructor reported a ground conflict with the preceding aircraft as the aircraft turned on to the departure runway. The departing aircraft's right wing came within 5 feet of Cessna 152.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC with another helicopter departing from a rooftop.
BE400A Captain reported an uncommanded nose gear extension in flight resulted in diversion to a successful landing.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during final approach.
Flight crew reported a parking brake accumulator failed to hold pressure; resulting in the aircraft rolling during engine start and striking a parked truck.
Air taxi Captain reported there are nonstandard or mislabeled taxiway identifiers at ANE airport that result in confusion in determining taxiways.
B767 flight crew reported hydraulic system failure in cruise. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
A319 Captain reported cargo door amber light resulted in return to departure airport.
Gulfstream 200 flight crew reported a hydraulic failure inflight at FL380.
Air carrier Captain reported ANP values were not in line with needed values for departure; so crew disabled the system per company guidance in flight.
CL601 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to SJC in trail of a B767.

CE-560 Captain reported a runway excursion onto the adjacent grass after landing at night at a non-towered airport. The Captain was able to maneuver back onto the runway with rudder control and taxied off the runway without damage.

B747 flight crew reported receiving a Fire Warning Bell with no associated EICAS. The overhead panel Main Deck Fire Light was illuminated. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at the departure airport.
Cessna 206 pilot reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing loss of manifold pressure.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern resulting from an aircraft being vectored toward them. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

Air Carrier Captain reported during final approach the Tower Controller cleared a jet to take off and an aircraft to cross the runway prior to the Captain landing; creating extremely close spacing. On short final approach Tower directed a go-around.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported aircraft cargo equipment malfunctioned during preflight loading.
Air Carrier flight crew reported while on approach receiving an EGPWS alert resulting in the crew executing a climb and go around; the flight crew landed safely on the second approach.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during final approach to CVH non-towered airport.  Reporter turned left to avoid a possible collision and allow the other aircraft to continue to land.
B737 Captain reported they had a hard landing that was later brought to their attention as requiring a write-up.
Air carrier Captain turned toward mountainous terrain after takeoff and the flight crew received a terrain warning. The Captain continued the turn and departure with no correction made.
Pilot reported an NMAC event during landing with a non-reporting opposite direction aircraft. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.

GA aircraft flight instructor holding short for takeoff at JNX reported observing an aircraft take off in the opposite direction of the runway being used for landings; causing a landing aircraft to take evasive action.

Flight crew reported spotting another aircraft at the opposite end of the runway lining up to depart at a non-towered airport. The flight crew taxied off the next exit to avoid a conflict; waited for the other aircraft to take off; and then retook the runway for departure.

C99 Captain reported landing gear retraction problems after takeoff. The flight crew cycled the landing gear relay circuit breaker; which allowed the gear to extend and returned to the departure airport for landing.

GA student reported that during an instrument training flight as they approached the AND airport Decision Height; the safety pilot saw another aircraft flying directly at them at approximately the same altitude. Both aircraft maneuvered away from each other.

A Pilot who routinely flies into Cleveland; Texas airport (6R3) reported the PAPI and runway lighting have been out of service for three years and the outages are not published in a NOTAM. The reporter stated the airport manager parks a pickup truck with the headlights on to facilitate nighttime operations.
Flight crew reported a near collision with a baggage tug during taxi for take off. The Captain aggressively braked to avoid the collision and the tug altered course and drove off.
CRJ700 Captain reported the aileron trim and the rudder trim were not operating correctly and returned to departure airport.
Corporate Captain reported during approach seeing targets on their PFD they suspected were UAS.  Flight crew took evasive action to avoid a collision.
PA-25 pilot reported momentary loss of aircraft control during taxi resulting in a runway excursion.
LR-60 Captain reported a pressurization malfunction along with a navigation compass system malfunction during climb. The crew diverted to an alternate airport and landed.
Corporate jet First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC departing VNY to the west. The First Officer noted that the rate of climb was too low.
EMB-505 flight crew reported the incorrect altimeter setting was entered and the flight crew received an obstacle alert during final approach.
Air carrier pilot reported an NMAC shortly after takeoff from JFK when a helicopter passed 200 feet below them.
B757-200 Captain reported loss of Left Hydraulic System quantity and pressure resulted in a diversion and hold to burn off fuel; followed by a precautionary landing.
A Pilot descending into MDPC reported temporary GPS loss.
Glasair pilot reported landing safely after noticing fuel was leaking into the cockpit shortly after takeoff.

Airport operations observer reported a near miss between one aircraft on final approach; and the other aircraft entered the runway without communication. The landing aircraft performed a go-around and maneuvered away from the runway to avoid the departing aircraft.
SR-22 Lead Technician reported a failure of starter engagement. After further inspection they found the accessory drive gear attach bolts sheared.
Recreational/hobby UAS pilot reported flying during an active Temporary Flight Restriction (TFR). Once they learned of the TFR the pilot exited the airspace by landing.
A319 Flight Attendant reported a fume event during deplaning. Maintenance was requested resulting in extensive delay and flight cancellation.
A319 Flight Attendant reported a fume event during boarding resulting in deplaning passengers.

A321 Captain reported refusing an aircraft due to extensive maintenance performed and damage to the main landing gear doors. The flight crew eventually ferried the aircraft to a company maintenance base for service.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported descent below glidepath and flight toward terrain in IMC. Flight crew corrected and continued to landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported a firm landing that resulted in a bounce. Flight crew performed a go-around and returned to land.
Air carrier Captain reported that a foreign airport under COVID-19 closure scheduling does not allow for proper pre-flight procedures.

Center Oceanic Controller reported an aircraft deviated for weather without ATC clearance due to ATC not having time to respond due to their workload. The reporter stated numerous aircraft flight plans were filed through known weather areas which contributed to sector workload.
CRJ900 Captain reported a fume event during cruise. Flight crew diverted to a suitable airport.
Flight instructor reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while in the traffic pattern during a training flight. The student performed a go around to avoid the UAS.
Air carrier Captain reported being notified by the flight attendants of a fume event during descent. All flight attendants experienced physiological symptoms but no medical assistance was requested.
A320 flight crew reported receiving multiple hydraulic system fault messages during departure. Flight crew returned to the airport and stopped on runway due to loss of nosewheel steering.

Air carrier flight crews reported that during taxi from the gate the aircraft's right winglet struck the horizontal stabilizer of another aircraft at an adjacent gate. The flight crew of the other aircraft reported they were waiting for the marshallers to get into position at the gate and felt the aircraft move when it was struck.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported a Horizontal Stabilizer Trim Run Away condition during approach. The flight crew ran the QRH and the Run Away conditioned ceased. The flight crew continued the approach to landing at destination airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering a strong mountain wave resulting in an exceedance of Vmo/Mmo and an altitude deviation.
BE60 pilot reported shutting down the right engine because of roughness and loss of power. Flight landed safely at destination; which was the nearest suitable airport.
B737NG First Officer reported encountering jet blast on takeoff from a taxiing Widebody aircraft.
POU Tower Ground Controller reported a hanger blocks the view of the runway and issues related to the blockage.
A light plane pilot reported a NMAC with another light aircraft during climb. Pilot reported event to tower and flight continued.

PA-31 Flight Instructor reported the right engine pressure began to drop during training. The instructor decided to secure the engine; but erroneously shut down the left engine. Reporter requested assistance from ATC and was eventually able to re-start the left engine and executed a single engine landing.
B757-200 pilot reported an engine malfunction during climb resulting in a degraded engine. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed.

B767 Captain reported receiving 'Gear Doors' and 'Gear Disagree' master cautions upon raising the gear during takeoff.  After troubleshooting; the messages would not dissipate; so the crew elected to return to the departure airport.
UAS crew reported during a test flight the UAS departed its preprogrammed path and ascended above 400 ft. The UAS crew took manual control of the UAS and initiated a return to home.

Air carrier flight crew reported a loss of control and runway excursion during landing. There was a strong crosswind reported according to the pilots. The Captain made a go-around from the safe area off the runway. The second landing on a different runway was successful.
Air carrier Captain reported a near midair collision with a UAS during departure climb. Aircraft was inspected after landing for any damage due to possible collision.
PA 28-180 reported a loss of electrical power and comm radios at cruise altitude. Pilot diverted and landed safely.
Captain reported animals and an airport vehicle on the intended landing runway. ATC directed a missed approach to a different runway.
Pilot instructor reported an NMAC at 1RB non-towered airport.
Ground employee reported the flight crew released the brakes causing the aircraft to roll back before they were ready for it.
First Officer reported uncontrollable cabin altitude after take off. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and a precautionary landing at departure airport.

Air carrier Captain reported at destination deplaning the flight crew had to assist a stranded wheelchair passenger to enter terminal area. Once inside the terminal the Captain realize they had transported an undocumented battery powered mobility device.

Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach while maneuvering at night in visual conditions. During the maneuver the Captain determined they could not maintain terrain clearance; initiated a climb; and was provided vectors by ATC and landed on the opposite runway.
General aviation pilot was on climb out when a UAV came within 100 feet of their aircraft.
B737 flight crew reported receiving a bank angle warning when the aircraft exceeded 35 degrees of bank during climb. Flight crew performed recovery procedures and continued flight uneventfully.
An air carrier flight crew reported the First Officers window opened during takeoff roll.
Ground personnel report stated a loud boom heard as jetbridge made contact with aircraft.
Air carrier pilot reported while they were taxiing out of the ramp another carrier entered opposite direction on the same route. Reporter stopped until the other aircraft passed by.

EMB-145 Captain reported a reroute and increased headwinds caused fuel reserves to drop to minimum. The reporter stated fuel gauge indications fluctuated indicating below minimums at times and FMS was not working properly. Flight crew received priority handling and landed at destination airport.
C172 pilot reported an off airport landing was caused by engine malfunctions after maintenance.
B737 Flight Crew reported a UAS passed near the aircraft while on final approach into SFO.
Pilot Examiner reported a gear up landing occurred during a flight examination with an applicant; resulting in minor aircraft damage.
Maintenance Technician reported the B767 aircraft returned to departure airport following Main Landing Gear Disagree and Door Disagree amber lights illuminated after takeoff.

A Tower Ground Controller reported they observed an Air Carrier landing at the same time a flight of two military aircraft were taking off from an intersecting runway. The second aircraft in the flight flew less than 100 feet over the air carrier.
Pilot reported a miscommunication resulted in the operation of an aircraft with logbook errors that made the aircraft unairworthy.

EMB-145 Captain reported a Rudder Overboost EICAS message on initial climb. The flight crew performed trouble shooting and QRH procedures and determined the #2 Rudder Hydraulic system was inoperative. The flight crew continued to destination airport and swapped aircraft.

A300 flight crew reported a controlled flight toward terrain event while being vectored for a visual approach in smoky/hazy conditions.  ATC issued a low altitude alert to crew who climbed back to the cleared altitude and subsequently made an uneventful landing.
BE55 Pilot reported a rough running number one engine; shut it down; and diverted.
Student pilot reported a runway excursion into the grass after landing on a solo flight at a tower controlled airport.
B777-200 flight crew reported ATC failure to provide separation resulted in a NMAC.
Air carrier Captain reported the inability to routinely acquire the SFB ATIS information in a timely manner due to interference from the shared frequency of SAV Tower.
Flight Instructor with a student reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern during take-off with an aircraft landing opposition direction runway.

Air Carrier flight crew reported not following SOPs during the takeoff and departure phase and forgetting to select VNAV mode resulting in a delayed flap retraction. Captain stated lack of flying contributed to the event.
Flight Instructor reported the nose gear collapsed completely with the propeller striking the runway on landing. The Instructor secured the engine and aircraft and waited for assistance.
GA pilot reported entering Class C airspace without clearance.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported on pre-flight that both O2 walk around bottles in the aft section had their masks attached to the low flow port instead of the Hi flow port as required.
A Flight Instructor reported loss of engine power while descending to their destination.
Flight Instructor reported the failure and separation of an engine cowl while conducting training; resulting in a diversion to the nearest suitable airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a near midair collision with a UAS during arrival descent. No evasive action was taken.
Air Carrier Flight Crew flying EMB-120 aircraft reported engine failure inflight.

Flight Instructor reported an ATC assigned vector contributed to a NMAC and the need to conduct an evasive maneuver to avoid a collision while in the pattern at an airport with an overworked contract tower.

Center Controller reported that a pilot asked to divert due to an engine problem then said the engine had failed. Due to unclear radio transmissions; Controller was not sure what pilot intentions were for landing location.
B757 flight crew reported they unknowingly pushed back from the gate with the ramp agent not clear of the aircraft and still connected via headset.

B737 flight crew reported rejected takeoff when one engine failed to spool up resulting in differential thrust causing the aircraft to veer off centerline.  Flight crew regained aircraft control and returned to gate.
TRACON Controller trainee and air carrier pilot reported due to a miscommunication the air carrier descended below their assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Beechjet 400 Captain reported trim control malfunction on departure resulted in temporary loss of aircraft control. Flight crew regained aircraft control and continued to destination.
Air carrier Captain reported a Hydraulic System malfunction during cruise. Flight crew continued to the destination airport and landed safely.
CL-350 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into BWI in trail of a heavy jet.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported a recurring practice of incorrect Hazmat cargo coding.

B737-800 flight crew reported Landing Gear would not retract after takeoff when the Gear Handle was move to the up position. The flight crew made an air turn back and landing at departure airport. Post flight inspection revealed the Landing Gear Pins were never removed prior to flight.

Tower Controllers reported a UAS demonstration with several hundred UAS operating near a Class B airport without the control tower's knowledge. Controllers reported an airborne conflict between a UAS and helicopter resulted in temporarily suspending local helicopter operations.
GA pilot reported a lack of published and verbal clarification of taxiway status that resulted in an incursion of a closed taxiway.

STT Tower Controller reported a tall-masted sailboat was in unsafe proximity to approach end of landing runway resulting in a go-around and a critical ground conflict. Controller also reported failed enforcement of vessels to remain clear of runway.
B737 flight crew reported the aircraft rolled forward after chock removal and contacted an air stair truck.
B737 Captain reported receiving a Planned DG report with 'unclear' DG details of the contents. After extensive delay with Dispatch; the exact data was provided to the flight crew.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss at a non-towered airport when on the landing roll was overtaken by the other aircraft on a go-around passing just above the instructors aircraft. The instructor performed a full stop and then watch the same aircraft land behind another aircraft who was still on the runway.
B737 flight crew reported encountering possible 5G interference while inbound to IAH.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported an unstabilized approach; requiring maneuvering; resulted in a GPWS alert and CFIT.

B747-400 pilot reported an unsuccessful auto land in VMC conditions at a Tower controlled airport. The air carrier crew missed briefing a note on the approach plate prohibiting auto coupled approaches to the intended landing runway.
