The PF (pilot flying) disconnected the Autopilot in the flare due to erratic inputs and landed manually.
Air carrier Captain reported gate return to removed improperly secured DG before departure.

J3 Cub pilot reported a near miss with two other aircraft while on runway after landing at a non-towered airport. The pilot had safety concerns regarding the other aircraft formation flying and performing a low pass during an airport event.

Flight Instructor reported the CTAF/UNICOM frequency at the non-towered U76 Airport provides the weather advisory broadcast as well as control over the airfield lighting at the airport; and also shares that same frequency with several other surrounding airports. This results in frequency congestion and along with the growing traffic at U76; creates the potential for a conflict in the traffic pattern.

SR20 flight Instructor reported a ground conflict when the student landed on a runway with opposite direction departing traffic. Reporter realized afterward they had been broadcasting landing intentions on the AWOS weather frequency instead of CTAF; which resulted in other aircraft not hearing them.
Air carrier Captain reported ground crew failure on push back resulted in tow bar disconnect and uncommanded aircraft movement.
Air carrier pilot reported an airborne conflict with a UAS during initial climb. No evasive action was taken.
B777 flight crew reported they descended below the glide slope and received a low altitude alert from ATC. Flight crew became re-oriented and landed safely.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a safety concern regarding the company's handling of gate checked bags that contain spare lithium-ion batteries and electronics with lithium-ion batteries.

CRJ-700 Captain reported an autopilot malfunction resulting in rapidly increasing pitch oscillations. The Captain immediately disconnected the autopilot and hand flew the aircraft the rest of the flight.  The Captain stated this is a recurring malfunction and the cause is not yet determined.

Flight Instructor reported while in the pattern experiencing another aircraft turning into them on the downwind and another aircraft overflying the airfield 300 feet above their aircraft resulting in the Instructor Pilot taking evasive action to avoid a collision with both aircraft.
Pilot flying C-152 aircraft reported entering Class B airspace without a clearance.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported a NMAC with another aircraft that required evasive action while descending for landing.

Flight Instructor reported during landing the student applied brakes resulting in locking the brakes and blown tires; closing the runway. Private pilot reported while arriving noticing a disabled aircraft on the side of the runway and landed their aircraft beyond the disabled aircraft resulting in landing on a closed runway.
Flight Instructor flying PA-28 aircraft reported NMAC during departure climb.

Pilot reported a near miss while on takeoff and climb from a non-towered airport in visual conditions; with an aircraft on final approach in the opposite direction. The pilot immediately maneuvered to avoid a collision and climbed out of the airport traffic area.
Air Carrier Captain reported the aircraft database was a few revisions 'out of date' and suspects the aircraft was 'parked' during the pandemic for an extended period of time.
A321 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival and landing at PHL in trail of a B787. Reporter recommended increased separation from B787 type aircraft.

An air carrier Captain reported the Gate Agent delivered an incomplete final document release to the flight crew at pushback. The Captain directed the agent to locate the missing document and deliver it to the flight prior to pushback.
Captain reported refusing the aircraft after ELAC2 was placed on MEL.
Challenger 650 pilot reported a runway excursion due to crosswind created by a passing train.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a bounce on landing that required a go-around. On the second approach both engine Bleed Fail lights illuminated. After landing; the Captain wrote them up for maintenance.

Maintenance technician reported that while performing a maintenance check list in a Cessna C680; they found both right hand main landing gear brake lines pinched/smashed between the fixed main landing gear leg and the trailing link leg.
Technician reported conflicting Revised Engineering Orders caused confusion between Technicians and Engineering. The Aircraft was removed from service for additional maintenance.

EMB-145 First Officer reported receiving a GPWS 'Terrain Pull Up' warning during descent to intercept localizer while wind shear advisories were in effect.  Crew disconnected autopilot and continued with their visual approach as previously cleared and landed normally.
A Flight Instructor in the traffic pattern and the Tower CIC reported a NMAC when an aircraft executed a missed approach and turned toward the Flight Instructor's aircraft.

B737-700 flight crew reported receiving a cabin altitude warning horn and light during climb out at 12;000 feet. The flight crew ran the appropriate QRH procedures and decided to return to the departure airport.
B757 flight crew reported having a high workload and being tight for time; and had forgotten to send the Dangerous Goods (DG) acknowledgement code prior to aircraft movement.
EMB-145 Captain reported a pressurization malfunction during initial descent. The crew followed QRH procedures and landed at the destination.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported a bird strike on departure which resulted in engine damage and a return to departure airport.
B737 flight crew reported an airborne conflict with a small aircraft while on initial approach in IMC. Captain took evasive action to avoid a collision and conducted a go around to a landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported a cabin door handle would not stay in the closed position. Flight returned to the departure airport.

Technician reported an aircraft was released for service after engine checks were performed.  Upon aircraft landing; the #1 Thrust Reverser failed to deploy. It was determined that the Reverser was still locked out from maintenance.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported they were notified that HAZMAT they scanned into the computer system did not get properly processed in the system.
Captain reported GPS signal loss in ORBB foreign airspace caused distractions which resulted in them failing to send a mandatory position report.

Small aircraft flight crew reported receiving a PFD fan fail message during cruise followed by a burning smell and smoke in the cockpit. Flight crew diverted; performed an off-airport landing; and evacuated the aircraft.
B737 flight crew reported hydraulic failure in cruise resulting in a return to departure airport.
A321 ground personnel reported not noticing the second of two DG parcels in the cargo compartments. The two parcels were loaded in separate compartments; which contributed to the error.
GA pilot on short final to MTJ; a non towered airport; reported an NMAC with an air carrier on short final for an intersecting runway not communicating on CTAF.
C550 Captain reported they detected hissing sound and attempted to identify source. The flight crew immediately donned oxygen masks and diverted to a precautionary landing.

A320 Captain reported the Autopilot 1 disengaged during cruise and multiple other aircraft systems malfunctioned; including hydraulic system; during remainder of flight. Flight crew continued to destination and landed.
Air carrier Captain reported an abrupt stop was necessary to avoid a collision while taxiing from the gate at DEN airport.
Air carrier Captain reported they temporarily lost control of the aircraft while initiating an unexpected go around.
Air carrier pilot reported during push back the tug slid temporarily out of control on an icy ramp. Later on at the deicer pad a deicing truck collided with the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported seeing a drone during arrival descent to LGA.
A BE58 pilot reported an engine failure and divert to a nearby airport.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a Hazmat procedural violation regarding missing required information on MAWB.
Light aircraft student pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft shortly after takeoff at IZA airport; citing noise abatement procedures as possibly contributing.
CE550 Captain reported a loss of aircraft control and taxiway excursion while performing thrust reverser test during taxi out. Crew extracted plane from grass/mud and continued on to destination.
Small jet flight crew reported loss of directional control during taxi in snowy and icy conditions at BED that resulted in an excursion off the taxiway.
Air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming in Jeddah FIR airspace. The location was in the vicinity of Red Sea/Sinai Peninsula.
A321 flight crew reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of a 'burning oil odor' by R2 and R3 doors during approach. Flight continued for a normal landing and taxied to the gate.

C550 single pilot Captain reported a runway excursion into the over run area when attempting to land in moderate rain. The Captain selected the anti-skid on; in accordance with SOP; but the brakes were not responding during the landing roll; resulting in a slow speed excursion at the runway end into the over run area. There was no injuries or damage to the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported Hazmat items were not accounted for on the DG Paperwork during preflight. A corrected DG document was issued to the flight crew.
B737 Captain reported being held at the gate due to not having the required Final DG Summary prior to pushback. The pushback crew were unaware of the company policy and requirement.
A319 flight crew reported both windshields shattered when the aircraft encountered hail during arrival. Flight continued to destination and landed safely.
B737 flight crew reported a fuel imbalance developed while at cruise. After the imbalance worsened; the flight crew performed an in flight engine shut down and continued to the destination airport.

B767 Captain reported Relief Officer noticed the left fuel quantity indicator was blank and later on; a fuel configuration EICAS message appeared. After consulting with Maintenance; the flight crew performed an air turnback.
Air carrier flight crew reported that an engine compressor stall occurred during takeoff roll; causing the aircraft to swerve and the crew to reject the takeoff.
C172 student pilot on a solo flight reported a loss of aircraft control event during an attempted touch and go resulting in a runway excursion.
Dispatcher reported a ramp crew pushed the aircraft improperly such that it came very close in proximity to an aircraft at adjacent gate. Ramp controller directed a stop push.
General aviation pilot reported a wind shear event on landing that resulted in a loss of aircraft control and a landing gear collapse.

PC12 Captain reported a critical ground conflict during landing rollout with a departing helicopter on intersecting runway. Both aircraft had received appropriate ATC clearances. Captain suggested a LAHSO clearance would have prevented loss of aircraft separation.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported the stop marks and VSR are faded and difficult to see in rainy and night time conditions.
Air Carrier First Officer reported while landing at IAD; ATC allowed a helicopter to cross the runway resulting in an unsafe condition if a go-around was required.
B737-700 flight crew reported lavatory leaking into cabin and cockpit area.
Air carrier First Officer reported they observed jet blast blowing dust and debris onto construction workers and their equipment across from Concourse A on Taxiway AS at DEN.

B757 Captain reported a go-around was caused by the trailing edge flaps showing an asymmetry condition and stopped at 20 degrees. The Captain accepted vectors to rejoin the approach and made a successful landing.
B737-800 flight crew reported differential power when advancing power for takeoff resulted in aircraft swerving and rejected takeoff.

Helicopter pilot reported flying in a non-airworthy aircraft when maintenance logbooks indicated work compliance and aircraft was airworthy. Maintenance signed off on repairs and inspections that were observed to have not been completed.
Student pilot reported an engine failure in flight flying a DA40 aircraft. The student diverted and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier flight crew reported duct leak in the bleed air system was detected before pushback. Flight crew refused the aircraft.

Cessna 172 pilot reported an NMAC event with a flight of three non-communicating aircraft. Upon establishing visual contact with the formation; the Cessna pilot executed a successful evasive maneuver.
A321 pilot reported an upset occurred at FL290 after encountering a strong updraft in moderate turbulence; resulting in speed and altitude excursions.
ORD Tower Controller reported aircraft data tags frequently drop off their display while aircraft taxi across an area of the airport.
Ground Agent reported the jet bridge did not stop when commanded. The bridge may have made contact with the aircraft.
A pilot conducting an RNAV Approach to a non-towered airport misread their chart and descended below a minimum altitude for a segment of the approach. ATC advised them to check their altitude.

TRACON Controller reported misidentifying an aircraft's location resulting in issuing a descent below the MVA. When the issue was identified the aircraft was already entering an area that was safe for their altitude.
CE-680 Captain reported experiencing GPS anomalies apparently related to solar activity.
Part 107 pilot reported flying over persons with a UAS that was not in the correct size category for the type of operation. UAS pilot learned during the post flight of their error.
Pilot reported the solid rubber tail wheel developed a flat spot and tore away from the rim. The aircraft was taxied back to its hangar and repairs were made.
Air carrier First Officer reported the flight crew inadvertently initiated gate pushback prior to receiving the final DG summary.
First Officer reported AIR/GND FAIL and REV ENG 1-2 DISAGREE EICAS message on take off. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.

A300 flight crew reported a Bleed Valve 1 Light illuminated during climb. Bleed Valve 2 was already on MEL. The flight crew requested priority handling and descended immediately. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
B787 flight crew reports engine vibration shortly after takeoff. Returned to departure airport.
Captain reported that during a maintenance action to MEL an Anti Ice problem; and emergency exit light battery pack exploded; causing the aircraft to be evacuated and CFR to be called.
Flight Crew flying Phenom 100 aircraft reported a fuel Imbalance after takeoff.

Sabreliner 60 Check Airman reported a track deviation occurred departing TPA that was possibly related to an FMS malfunction. Reporter also stated they experienced wake turbulence from the preceding B737.
King Air Pilot reported while descending to their airport taking evasive action to avoid a collision with an ERJ; Pilot landed safely at the airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft rolled forward uncommanded when the ground crew removed the chocks without telling the flight crew to set brakes. Captain applied brakes and stopped aircraft.

EMB-175 flight crew reported a failure of the air data system affecting multiple aircraft systems during the climb in communication with ATC. The crew continued toward the destination but diverted to an alternate due to icing conditions at the destination; and landed safely.
Pilot reported engine failure on landing and was forced to make an off field landing due to fuel starvation.
Pilot reported an NMAC during approach to BLM airport.
EMB-175 First Officer reported they were about to enter a taxiway they had not received clearance to enter when ATC instructed them to stop to avoid another aircraft on the taxiway.
A TRACON Controller reported they released an IFR Departure at a non towered airport from a runway the departure procedure was not authorized for due to obstructions.
Air carrier First Officer reported this crew rotation/pairing is not safe due to a fatigue factor.
Flight Instructor in a C172 reported the flaps would not retract on initial climb and return to departure airport.
A320 flight crew reported compressor stall on takeoff with the right engine. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.

Air carrier Captain reported the need to perform an abrupt stop while taxiing. The Captain states that another air carrier was rounding the corner in front of their aircraft at a high rate of speed and did not yield.
Pilot reported rudder remained at full deflection right and elected to return to the departure airport. It was discovered by maintenance that the rudder spring required replacement.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming before descent into ABQ and regained GPS coverage on downwind.
B737 Captain reported an Oil Filter Bypass Light caution message appeared while climbing that required an engine shutdown as part of the checklists. The flight crew then performed an air turnback.
Air carrier Captain reported no NOTOC final message with DG code was received and no DG acknowledge message submitted prior to gate pushback.
Flight Attendant reported caterers opened the door while it was armed. They were able to stop them before the escape slide deployed.
A80 Controller reported failure to climb aircraft before entering a higher MVA resulted in a CFTT event.
Glider Flight Instructor reported a NMAC between their tow plane and converging traffic prior to their release from the tow plane.
A320 First Officer reporter the inner pane of the Captain's side window cracked during approach. Flight crew continued approach to a safe landing.
BE35 pilot reported a critical ground conflict involving an opposite direction aircraft during takeoff.
B737 flight attendants reported being alerted to an engine shutdown which resulted in priority diversion to a safe landing.
B737 MAX 8 air carrier flight crew reported an audible bang during taxi after landing. The Captain stopped the aircraft and called maintenance where the two main gear tires were deflated.
B737-800 flight crew reported a fuel imbalance in flight. The flight crew performed an air turn back; an in flight shut down and precautionary landing at departure airport.

General aviation student pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft when taking off from a non-towered airport. The student pilot saw the other aircraft on short final; landing opposite direction on the same runway; and rejected the takeoff and cleared the runway.
Captain reported cabin pressure controllability issues caused an air turn back and precautionary landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while on initial approach.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a terrain alert during missed approach.
Air carrier First Officer reported an altitude deviation that may have put them below the Class B airspace during a visual approach to DEN airport.
EMB-175 flight crew reported pressurization failure in cruise. Diverted and landed uneventfully.

A321 Captain reported an engine starter warning during push back and engine start. The Captain improperly responded to the ECAM and immediately applied braking and shut down the engine to regain control of the aircraft.

B737 First Officer reported a lack of consensus when an engine was shut down for a start valve open light illuminating as to request priority handling and divert or to continue to destination airport.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving a terrain proximity warning while on ATC vectors to final approach course at MMGL. Pilot maneuvered around terrain and joined approach course.
A330 ground personnel reported two CO2 cartridges were removed during checked bag screening.
B737 flight crew reported the aircraft wingtip came into contact with a blast fence during pushback at LGA even though the pushback was accomplished correctly to the right spot.

Air carrier flight crew reported missing a crossing altitude; an airspeed deviation; and receiving a terrain warning during approach. Flight crew stated the short flight and high cruising altitude resulted in a short timeline to complete procedures and contributed to events.

Air carrier pilot reported while flying an RNAV approach to SLC they descended below charted altitude and ATC issued a low altitude alert. The flight crew climbed; rejoined the glide path; and landed safely.
BE-200 pilot reported an aircraft reverser problem resulting in a runway excursion.

B-787 crew reported uncommanded aircraft movement after parking at a gate and setting the parking brake. The aircraft rolled forward after parking brake released; the crew reset the parking brake; and the aircraft did not damage equipment or injure personnel.
IAD local controller failed to provide separation to a go around and departure due to communications error and workload; resulted in a critical ground conflict.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of GPS signal while in descent to Denver airport.
SA-227 AC Metro First Officer reported deviations from SOP during a Cabin Pressurization Event.
A320 Captain reported a stuck Press To Talk button hampered communications; requiring a divert and precautionary landing.
Gulfstream V Captain reported a loss of oil pressure in one engine resulting in an air return for a single engine landing.
A321 flight crew reported ECAM messages appearing and experiencing flight degradation due to Maintenance performing improper procedures around an MEL and inactivating the wrong spoiler pair.
Diamond DA-40 flight instructor reported during climb they got a ECU A and B annunciation.
BE20 pilot reported electrical system malfunction after takeoff resulting in a return to the departure airport.
A light sport pilot reported an NMAC with a helicopter on short final to a non-towered airport.

C182 pilot reported a temporary loss of aircraft control and flight toward terrain during initial climb due to a loosened throttle control friction lock which resulted in the throttle moving to idle position.
B767-200 Captain reported an uncontrollable EPR indication on the right engine during climb. The flight crew communicated with dispatch and maintenance and continued to the destination airport.
B757 Captain reported a loss of hydraulic fluid in the center hydraulic system that continued to decrease during cruise.
Corporate pilot reported a NMAC during arrival descent and responded to a TCAS Resolution Advisory to avoid the conflict.
Technician reported tailpipe fires on two consecutive days and expressed misgivings over maintenance actions on aircraft to correct the issues.

Balloon pilot reported a loss of vertical control while attempting to land which resulted in colliding with a light pole. The pilot took evasive action and landed safely with minor damage and no injuries.
General aviation pilot reported an NMAC event while landing at a non-towered airport. Flight crew took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the aircraft rolled uncommanded as the tug was disconnecting at the gate. Wing walkers placed a stop sign.
AT6 pilot conducting aerial application reported a NMAC with another aerial spraying aircraft requiring them to take evasive action which resulted in a possible encroachment into Class C airspace.
A321 pilot reported they came to an immediate stop while taxiing to their assigned spot on the ramp to avoid another air carrier taxiing to the same spot.
Corporate Jet Captain reported exceeding the 250 knot speed restriction below 10;000 feet and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.

Light transport Captain reported a near miss with another aircraft while setting up to land at a non-towered airport. Reportedly; the other aircraft had done a touch-and-go and was not paying attention to the frequency.
Pilot on a practice instrument approach reported a NMAC while descending into VNC airport.
PA-28 flight Instructor reported an aircraft flew up close behind them in the traffic pattern requiring evasive maneuvering to avoid a collision.
Flight Instructor reported they followed another aircraft for landing; lost sight of the aircraft; and proceeded to land while the other aircraft was still on the runway.
PA 28R instructor pilot and student reported a landing gear malfunction which resulted in an airspace incursion. The pilot exited the airspace; then landed safely.
Pilot reported a navigational problem with the Honeywell Epic system. The pilot states ATC and Honeywell are aware of the navigational issue.
Student pilot reported a NMAC with another training aircraft at non-towered airport on final approach when another aircraft made a non-standard turn without a radio call.
Air Carrier Captain reported a change in airport procedures; due to COVID-19; caused the flight crew to miss the required signing of the Flight Release.
Flight Instructor with student reported a partial loss of engine power during cruise. Flight returned to departure airport.

G200 flight crew reported experiencing wind shear conditions on departure resulting in excursions from assigned altitude and deviation from the departure procedure.  The flight crew was able to regain control of the aircraft and proceeded to their destination.
C172 pilot reported engine malfunction during takeoff climb and returned to airport.

UAS Visual Observer reported that after liftoff the UAS began climbing rapidly and was at risk of exceeding 400 ft. AGL. The UAS was landed and it was learned the autonomous flight plan was created using meters instead of feet.
Air carrier pilot reported the taxi diagram at MSLP is not correct.
Flight Crew flying SF50 aircraft reported Autoflight malfunction after takeoff.
Pilot reported an observed discrepancy between the depiction of Taxiways A1 and T from Runway 3L at PRC and the actual taxiway location.

Flight Instructor reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern when a crop duster flew directly below them without announcing their position on frequency. Flight Instructor took evasive action and then warned others on frequency of the hazard posed by the other aircraft.

Air carrier pilot reported a ground conflict after being cleared to taxi to departure runway. Pilot stopped taxi after realizing that landing traffic was about to land on a runway the reporter had been cleared to cross.
Corporate jet Captain reported encountering wind shear departing ASE that resulted in airspeed loss and a left bank. Control was regained and flight continued without incident.
Air carrier flight crew reported an airborne conflict with a UAS during final approach. No evasive action was taken.
GA pilot reported a heading deviation while on the CATHEDRAL ONE Departure from PSP airport resulting in a CFTT event.
Captain reported severe nose wheel vibration on take off. The flight crew elected to divert for a precautionary landing.
Dispatcher reported aircraft taxing to the gate had to take evasive action to avoid a collision with tractor and containers.
PA-24 Pilot reported landing gear extension problems on approach. A go around was executed and several low passes performed while manually extending the landing gear.
Air Carrier Captain reported observing an object; possibly a UAS; pass by their aircraft while descending through 15;500 ft.  The incident was reported to ATC.
Center Controller reported an airspace deviation associated with a procedure that the receiving Controller may not have been understood.
Air carrier flight crew reported a hard landing during a training flight resulted when the trainee released back pressure during the round out. A passenger reported a sore back due to the impact.
B737 Captain reported receiving a low altitude from ATC during visual approach when the flight descended too low. Captain corrected and continued approach with visual reference to PAPIs.
B737 Captain reported engine overheat during takeoff climb. Flight returned to departure airport.
BE36 Pilot reported confusion referencing traffic to follow in traffic pattern resulted in a NMAC with aircraft taking evasive action.

Air carrier Captain reported a traffic conflict on a taxiway when starting taxi with an ATC clearance; when another aircraft was taxiing at a high rate of speed on the same taxiway; requiring the Captain to stop quickly. The Captain confirmed the taxi clearance and proceeded to taxi.
A M20 pilot reported on initial climb executing a go around from an unstable approach their propeller struck a runway light.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported the lack of backlighting to the stab trim cutout switches makes seeing them at night difficult; and is unsafe.
B737-800 Captain reported an upset at FL340 descending into LAX in trail of a B787.

A320 First Officer reported there was a malfunction with the aircraft's pressurization control system and the flight crew safely performed an air turnback. The reporter also noted that this had been a reoccurring issue with this aircraft; and was also present in other Airbus aircraft.
B737-800 First Officer reported an engine failure during cruise. The crew requested priority handing with ATC and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew reported non-compliance with the obstacle clearance charted procedure while climbing from a non-towered airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported two cargo items with incomplete documents were loaded on to the aircraft prior to departure. The next day the flight crew was notified that the added items were hazmat cargo and the flight was not approved to transport it.
Corporate flight crew reported they were vectored toward another aircraft which resulted in an NMAC requiring the crew to take evasive action to avoid a collision.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported a stabilizer trim issue that resulted in the autopilot disengaging during climbout. The flight crew elected to return to the departure airport.

B767 flight crew reported upon departure as the aircraft was climbing through 3;500 feet; the Master Caution Illuminated. It was determined that the Master Caution was associated with the Main Cargo Door Unlocked Light; which was illuminated. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported incorrect FMS altitude entry; tuning out radar altimeter aural warning and failure to noticed low altitude; resulted in evasive action.

A BNA TRACON Controller reported they had to vector an arriving aircraft away from a conflicting VFR aircraft not in contact with ATC. The reporter states this is an ongoing problem and the '...VFR traffic around BNA has become unsafe and too much for safe passenger travel.'
A Tower Controller in Charge and the Local Controller reported the Airport Surface Detection alerted when an aircraft taxied onto the same runway another aircraft was taking off from.
Global 6000 Pilot reported on departure not having the automation configured correctly resulting in temporary loss of aircraft control.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of ADS-B 1 and 2 on departure out of DEN. Reporter cited 5G as possibly contributing; and the systems returned to normal a few minutes later.
A319 Captain reported during takeoff a shuddering of the entire aircraft resulted in a rejected takeoff.
A319 flight crew refused aircraft due to a malfunctioning cabin cooling system. Excessive heat caused passenger injury.
AutoGyro Cavalon pilot reported a composite propeller blade failed and separated in flight and subsequently the engine died. The reporter landed safely at the departure airport.
Flight crew reported receiving a 'caution terrain' aural warning message due to an antenna near the FAF to ORF airport.
Corporate jet pilot reported an NMAC during approach to FXE when an aircraft flew under them; causing an RA. Flight crew took evasive action then continued the approach.
Air carrier Captain reported missing the mobility device form for a wheelchair listed in the Final DG Summary. After communicating with Dispatch it was determined that no wheelchair was loaded.
B737 MAX 9 Captain reported unusual stiff flight control movement when performing the flight control check on the ground after engine start. The Captain refused the aircraft for flight.
DA40 Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another aircraft.
An Air Taxi flight crew reported smoke in the cockpit due to an electrical malfunction and diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
B747-400 First Officer reported during climb they got a pack 1 EICAS message and the flight crew decided to return to the airport.
A Flight Instructor in a C172 reported as they were executing a touch and go Tower instructed them to go around due to faster Air Carrier traffic landing behind them.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC during initial approach to IAH airport when an aircraft not in communication with ATC flew below them.
Air carrier flight crew reported hydraulic system failure during go-around.
C172 Pilot reported a runway excursion possibly due to a gear problem or winds at the airport.
Pilot reported a NMAC at a non-towered airport.
BE58 pilot reported engine and fuel system anomalies after takeoff and returned to departure airport.  Plane had just come out of maintenance for fuel cell replacement.
Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with a UAS while on a visual approach to a Class B airport. Flight crew notified ATC.
B737-800 flight crew reported they mistakenly stopped their climb and received a low altitude alert from ATC.
C172 pilot reported they had to go-around at a non-towered airport due to an aircraft taxiing on to the runway when they were on short final.

Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC event during approach with a non-communicating Helicopter. After completing the RA maneuver and with stable approach criteria; the Captain continued the approach and a safe landing.
Argon 916iS experimental light sport pilot reported a loss of engine power during cruise with a forced off field landing.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing GPS jamming while flying across the Middle East and losing the Right GPS for the rest of the flight.
EMB-170 flight crew reported Terrain Warning while on approach.

DA20 Flight Instructor reported experiencing a burning smell coming from the electrical system upon reaching upwind; with the engine oil temperature going into the red at 300 degrees; and performed an air turnback.
GA pilot reported during takeoff roll; an aircraft on short final initiated a go around resulting in a NMAC.
DA40 Flight Instructor reported ATC gave a vector heading into conflicting traffic resulted in taking evasive action and a NMAC event.
Air carrier captain reported having inadequate data regarding the shortened takeoff distance for runway 23 at ORF; Norfolk International Airport; Norfolk; Virginia.
Light aircraft pilot reported a runway incursion during taxi and cited lack of current flying experience due to COVID restrictions as a contributing factor.
Air carrier captain reported the ground equipment safety lines at a gate are missing or need repainting.
GA Pilot reported a NMAC. While approaching the airport and talking to Tower they took evasive action to avoid a banner tow aircraft.
Flight Crew reported engine vibration and RPM decay resulted in a diversion and precautionary landing.
A319 First Officer reported receiving a Yellow Hydraulic Reservoir low ECAM message during cruise requiring a diversion.
Twin Engine Pilot reported the BKL taxi chart does not show taxiway G as part of the ramp area. This caused confusion and a possible runway incursion.
Pilot reported a terrain warning during visual approach into ROA Airport.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported being notified by Ramp personnel that Hazmat cargo missing from DG manifest.
EMB-175 Captain reported failing to get a new release or request priority handling while diverting for a hydraulic system problem.
C172 pilot reported a loss of engine power in flight required a diversion to a nearby airport with the assistance of ATC.
First Officer reported a Master Warning light with CAB ALT light and elected to make a rapid descent and air turn back to a precautionary landing.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported loss of aircraft control while taking the runway for takeoff. Flight crew regained control and departed uneventfully.
Sail Plane pilot reported turbulence which required an unplanned landing at airstrip.
Air carrier First Officer reported the flight crew FMS's intermittently syncing during flight. The issue was resolved by verifying the assigned LOC ID and Frequency to stay on correct course.
Pilot reported the airport diagrams for ANI airport is missing Taxiway G and Taxiway TA.
Houston Center Controller reported unsafe practices regarding Management and weather.
B777 Captain reported there was an ADG that was emanating an acrid odor during preflight. Ramp personnel removed the container that was the source of the odor.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported installing incorrect Hydraulic Line on aircraft.

PA-32 pilot reported a thump sound after rotation; resulting in a slight decrease in engine RPM. The instructor returned to the departure airport and after landing; observed damage to the nose gear pant and a propeller blade.

Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with a helicopter departing a hospital under the approach course. The Captain leveled off to avoid the traffic; performed a go around; then returned to land safely.

A TRACON Controller in Charge and Controller working a sector reported the sector controller did not respond in a timely manner to the Controller in Charge's instruction to climb and vector two aircraft head on at 8;000 feet resulting in a NMAC.
Pilot reported landing and a tire blowing resulting in a short runway excursion.
Air carrier Captain on approach reported low altitude alert from ATC due to glideslope anomaly.  Flight crew corrected and landed normally.
PC-12 pilot reported an NMAC event while on an IFR flight plan with an unknown aircraft on a collision course. The pilot executed an evasive maneuver which prevented a collision.
Local Controller reported clearing aircraft for takeoff on runway that had a vehicle operating on the runway.
B737-700 Captain reported encountering strong wake turbulence departing LAX in trail of a B757; and recommended increased separation behind B757 and similar aircraft types.
A319 First Officer reported an aircraft steering failure while exiting the runway. The Captain steered clear of the runway; remained on the taxiway and notified ATC to coordinate a tug to the gate.
MD11 air carrier pilot reported a tire failure warning upon landing. The flight vacated the runway and was inspected by the airport authority.

GA pilot reported an engine failure in flight when one fuel tank ran out of fuel and they were unable to select the other tank. Pilot eventually was able to move fuel selector and restart engine during descent.
B737 MAX 9 Captain reported the shear pin broke during pushback and they failed to have maintenance inspect the aircraft as required.
B737 Captain reported while holding short of the gate they didn't have sufficient foot pressure on the brakes resulting in the aircraft rolling backwards until the Crew applied brake pressure.
Single pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern occurred during departure. The pilot of the other aircraft reportedly did not announce their position and stated the reporter had almost hit him.
An Air Carrier pilot reported a foreign air carrier does not include crew rest time during flights as flight time and is not complying with FAR's regarding crew rest after long overseas flights.
Eyewitness Pilot reported observing a critical ground conflict during taxi when an aircraft taxiied onto a runway while another aircraft was on takeoff roll at ORD airport.

General aviation pilot instructor reported a near collision during takeoff at a non-towered airport on a training flight with another aircraft taking off on an intersecting runway. The instructor rejected the takeoff and stopped prior to the crossing intersection to avoid the conflict.
Recreational/Hobbyist pilot of a RC fixed wing aircraft reported a control issue causing the RC aircraft to crash.
CL-350 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while being 3 miles in trail of an E175 on arrival into BOS.

Captain reported a near miss with another aircraft while getting a taxi clearance to cross a runway which was later rescinded by ATC. The Captain stopped the aircraft past the hold short line and ATC issued a go around clearance to the aircraft on final approach.
Air carrier First Officer reported their radio altimeter malfunctioned momentarily while on the EAGUL6 STAR into PHX. The pilot suspects 5G interference.
Super King Air Pilot reported the need to return to the departure airport after the wing locker opened on takeoff releasing baggage onto the runway.

B757 Captain reported the First Officer had sent the wrong DG acknowledgment code that resulted in the correct code being sent after blocking out; which was not in accordance with SOP. The Captain was making several requests to obtain the final DG report and had only received it just before departure.
TRACON Controller reported numerous issues while working traffic and problems associated with the workload and staffing.
Viking Pilot reported a broken alternator belt caused electrical system failure. Pilot diverted and made a precautionary landing.
Small aircraft pilot reported a low altitude alert on final; a wind shear; and then a second low altitude alert and safely landed the aircraft.
B777 flight crew reported during cruise got a C HYD QTY EICAS message. They requested ATC assistance and landed safely.
CE-680A Captain reported a near miss with another aircraft departing on a crossing runway at a non-towered airport.
ATC Ground Controller reported aircraft not following taxi instructions resulted in a taxiway incursion and two aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision with each other.
B737 flight crew reported they experienced moderate/severe turbulence on descent resulting in the aircraft being uncontrollable at times until descending below the turbulence.
B737-700 First Officer reported '...2 events of aileron roll resistance left/right followed by an excessive bank...'.

A TRACON Controller reported a Corporate departing an airport where the Tower closed early due to staffing turned the wrong direction and flew below the minimum vectoring altitude and into confliction with VFR overflight traffic.
Flight crew reported Low Altitude Alert from ATC following go-around due to high winds on approach to SUN airport.

Air carrier Captain reported after conclusion of spoofing airspace procedures; the flight crew was unable to recover both GPS units; had ADS-B In problems; no SATCOM voice; and also eventually lost CPDLC capability.
A321 First Officer reported landing safely after experiencing loss of green hydraulic system fluid.
A light aircraft flight crew reported the pilot flying temporarily lost control of the aircraft during landing.
A student pilot reported a NMAC in the VFR pattern at a non-towered airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported PGD Tower's failure to provide separation on departure resulted in the flight crew needing to avoid another aircraft; and expressed concern about Tower procedures.

GA flight instructor with student reported an NMAC while on takeoff roll resulting in evasive action to avoid a collision.  Reportedly; an aircraft was landing on the opposite end of their runway without having made any radio calls.
Air carrier flight crew reported they lost control of the aircraft after releasing the parking brake as requested by the ground crew. The Captain applied the brakes to stop the aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPWS terrain warning on approach believed to be a system malfunction.
Air carrier Captain reported accepting an RNAV approach into an airport that required a 5G filter. The aircraft did not have the filter and the pilots did not receive a visual approach clearance.
A321 flight crew reported heavy vibration during flight that was also noticed by the Flight Attendants. The crew diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Solo student pilot reported departing the runway surface on landing resulting in damage to the aircraft.
Flight Crew reported an APU exceedance indication in the cockpit was actually an APU fire that was extinguished with portable fire extinguishers by maintenance.
Pilot reported accidentally taxiing onto an unmarked service road on a large paved area at SYR after mistaking it for a taxiway.
A319 Mechanic reported heavy corrosion was found on components of the fuel tank.
Student pilot reported a wind gust pushed their aircraft to the side of the runway where they contacted a runway light during landing.
B757-200 flight crew reported engine stall and temperature exceedence during initial climb. Returned to airport and landed uneventfully.
P180 pilot reported a runway excursion while landing in gusty crosswinds. No injuries or significant damage reported.
Learjet 31 First Officer reported fire warning indications on the right engine in climb necessitated a diversion to a nearby airport.
B757-200 Dispatcher reported a flight that had to shut down an engine due to low a low oil pressure and quantity indication. The flight crew diverted for a landing at a nearby airport.
An Embraer ERJ-145 First Officer reported an engine failure while on final approach; resulting in an expedited landing at the destination airport.
Flight Instructor and student reported a near midair collision with a UAS while they were on approach. No evasive action was taken and ATC was notified.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported exceeding the permitted altitude during flight. The UAS pilot immediately descended to below the permitted altitude.
B737 flight crew reported encountering significant auto flight system malfunctions during climb out and returned to the departure airport for an overweight landing.
Air carrier Captain reported a vehicle ran a stop sign into the path of their aircraft during taxi which required hard braking to avoid a collision.
Air carrier First Officer reported temporary loss of control due to unexpected severe turbulence.

B767-300 Captain reported being dispatched with dry ice supplement procedures on an aircraft with a history of left pack issues. During cruise the left pack failed and the flight crew followed the QRH procedure which enabled the flight to continue to destination safely. However; the reporter was concerned with staying in safe CO2 levels with a failed left pack over oceanic routes.
Air carrier Captain reported a fuel leak and subsequent engine shut down during cruise. Flight diverted and landed safely.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving a Terrain Proximity Alert on final approach.
C525 Flight Crew reported thrust attenuator deployed on takeoff roll resulted in rejected takeoff.
B737 Flight crew reported on unstabilized approach receiving a terrain alert resulted in failure to execute a go-around procedure.
A B747 First Officer reported EICAS displayed ENG 3 OIL FILT so they shut down an engine and returned to departure airport.
Captain reported failure of the Anti-skid system during a high speed taxi functional check which resulted in brake overheating and tire deflation.
Aero Commander 500 pilot reported left engine failed during initial climb. Pilot landed safely and discovered post-flight left engine fuel shutoff switch was in the off position.
Pilot reported loss of control while conducting an ILS approach to a non towered airport.  The reporter believes the event was caused by a vehicle driving by the ILS critical area.

Pilot reported that after writing a discrepancy for an engine oil leak; the technician signed the log book entry as acceptable for service without inspecting; cleaning or operating the engine to determine the airworthiness of the aircraft.

TRACON and Tower Controllers reported traffic landed on closed parallel runway at night; without a clearance. Controllers reported that the lit X closure indicator was not in place and it was difficult for tower to determine aircraft runway alignment at night.

Air carrier pilot reported other aircraft and Flight Attendants told them they observed smoke coming from one of the engines while taxiing for departure. Passengers opened the overwing exits but did not exit the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported a wind shear and micro-burst encounter while tracking to intercept the localizer; resulting in various deviations.
Air carrier Captain reported that while taxiing to the gate an aircraft began its pushback requiring the reporter to stop the aircraft to avoid a collision.

Air Traffic Controller reported an aircraft had been instructed to hold short of a taxiway but the aircraft turned onto the taxiway resulting in a taxiway incursion.  ATC reports that this is a continuing issue at this taxiway.

Air Carrier Captain reported being notified by the Lead Flight Attendant during descent of 'burning wood odor' in the cabin. The fumes dissipated but returned stronger shorty thereafter. The flight crew requested priority handling and were met by airport support personnel. At the arrival gate a logbook entry was submitted.

Flight Instructor reported a student's runway excursion due to excessive landing speed which resulted in a loss of positive aircraft control on runway. Flight Instructor returned onto the runway and reported no aircraft or airport damage.
A Part 107 pilot reported accidentally flying their UAS into small tree branches causing it to fall into a lake.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training flight. After establishing communication with the intruder aircraft; that aircraft took evasive action to prevent a collision.

Air carrier First Officer reported they inadvertently descended more than intended during short final approach which activated a glideslope warning. Pilot immediately corrected to the proper glideslope and landed safely.
Government UAS pilot reported a possible battery disconnection which caused the UAS to fall to the ground during flight resulting in it being destroyed.

Light sport pilot reported a runway incursion occurred when he failed to turn onto the taxiway he was cleared to.  Distractions related to anticipation of a wake turbulence encounter were cited as contributing.

Air carrier Captain reported being notified during pushback of Hazmat that was loaded with documents that required the Captain's signature. The flight was tugged back to the gate where the Captain signed the document and departed safely.
An EMB 145 pilot reported loss of pressurization at 36;000 ft and diverting to a nearby suitable airport.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming in the area between SARPI and about 150NM prior to KARIL.
Air carrier First Officer reported a terrain warning while descending during the approach at night. The Captain took control; climbed; then continued the approach to a safe landing.

Captain reported the autopilot commanded an abrupt pitch up during departure. Captain regained manual control of the aircraft; re-engaged the autopilot and continued the flight; citing software issues as contributing to the event.
Air carrier Captain reported on departure roll hearing a loud bang from the right side of the aircraft.  The flight crew rejected the takeoff; shut down engine 2; and returned to the gate.
A Corporate Captain reported during landing roll they observed a departing aircraft from an intersecting runway fly overhead.

Air carrier Captain reported not performing the Before Taxi Checklist and subsequently had not set the flaps to the takeoff setting prior to moving the aircraft.  Reporter cited distraction from dealing with face mask issues in the cabin area may have contributed to the event.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of nose wheel steering during post landing taxi.
B737 Captain reported the yoke nose trim switch on both sides would not command nose trim down during climb out. Flight crew continued to destination airport and landed safely.
B737 Captain reported after opening the First Officer's side window it became jammed in the full open position. Maintenance determined the window was assembled incorrectly.
CRJ-200 flight crew and Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during initial climb resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Center Controller reported descending an IFR aircraft 1;000 feet below the MIA to get the aircraft to VFR conditions and below icing.
Air Carrier Captain reported responding to an RA during approach and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC.
Air carrier First Officer reported radio altimeter anomalies on final approach to SNA.
ERJ 135 flight crew reported GPWS alert along with erroneous indications from Radio Altimeter during descent to land.
PA28 pilot reported an off-airport landing resulted when he experienced fuel starvation in flight related to multiple vectors around TFR areas.
Air carrier Captain reported stopping abruptly to avoid a collision on the ramp at DEN when a vehicle failed to give right of way to the aircraft.
EMB-145 flight crew reported Stick Shaker activation during departure climb.
C177RG flight instructor reported a malfunction in the brake system during taxi for takeoff.
General aviation pilot reported a rough running engine and loss of power in cruise. Pilot executed an off-airport landing.
An air carrier flight crew reported they taxied past the ILS critical area hold line due to snow obscuring their view.
EMB-145 Captain reported receiving a 'terrain alert'  turning final while on a visual approach. Captain climbed back up and continued the approach to landing.
Multiengine aircraft pilot reported a runway excursion after touchdown on an ice-covered runway in gusty wind conditions. The pilot reported no damage and no injuries.

B737 First Officer reported after pulling to the gate a Ramper alerted the flight crew that a ground power receptacle was on fire; which made part of the aircraft catch on fire as well. The flight crew requested assistance from Ramp Control and told the flight attendants to get the passengers off through the jetway.

Air carrier Pilot reported a communications breakdown between flight crew and ground personnel regarding the scheduled Dangerous Goods cargo loaded on aircraft. After a gate return it was verified that the DG cargo was in fact loaded.
Air carrier flight crew reported descent below their planned minimum altitude during visual approach. Flight crew climbed back to correct altitude and continued approach.
TRACON Controller reported having to repeat transmissions to pilots because they could not understand the instructions; due to reporter wearing a face mask.
Flight Instructor reported that during a student taxi lesson in a tail wheel aircraft; directional control was lost and the aircraft made contact with a taxiway sign.
During taxi into the gate; airline pilot reported receiving conflicting directions from the marshaller and the automated docking system.
After several unsuccessful start attempts in a CRJ-700; fuel fumes were reported in the cabin. After gate return fuel leak reported on ramp.
Air carrier flight crew reported a ramp and taxiway incursion during arrival due to airport construction at LGA airport and subsequent temporary routing.
Captain reported loss of hydraulic fluid during climb out. The crew continued to the destination airport and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew reported distraction caused failure to monitor pilot flying starting early descent which resulted in ATC issuing low altitude alert.
Center Controller reported aircraft descended below MVA twice without clearance which resulted in a CFIT event.
General aviation pilot reported the aircraft ran off the ramp at VAY; citing uncharted construction changes as contributing.

B757 flight crew reported being directed to return to the gate due to unsecured HAZMAT cargo; of which the flight crew was unaware of having due to receiving a no Dangerous Goods (DG) message earlier.

B737 Captain reported a landing gear disagree message on all three gear on gear extend. The flight crew elected to continue the approach and make a normal landing after determining that the message was a false indication problem.

B737-800 Captain reported refusing an aircraft after the previous Captain had also refused the aircraft. The aircraft had a deferred air conditioning pack and required repair. Ground temperatures were very high.

Center Controller reported having difficulty communicating with pilots while wearing a face mask and stated that the requirement to wear face masks causes distractions and limits the ability to safely conduct air traffic control procedures.
Flight crew reported both VHF communication systems malfunctioned; and they elected to continue to destination airport.

Glider pilot reported a canopy malfunction resulting in a partial separation from the aircraft during a training maneuver. The pilot along with the check pilot passenger stabilized the aircraft and returned to the airport safely.
B767 flight crew reported while on the ILS approach the aircraft descended below the glide slope resulting in flight towards terrain. Flight crew recovered and continued.
A321 Maintenance Inspector reported that a repetitive write-up that should have required additional maintenance actions went unnoticed and there was a non-compliance with procedures.

Piper Aztec pilot reported a landing gear malfunction after gear retraction during climb. The pilot pumped the landing gear down by the manual procedure and the landing gear indicated locked in the proper position.
A319 Captain reported a lack of support in his decision to reject an aircraft due to numerous write-ups.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain chose not to wear a mask for safety of flight concerns while wearing glasses.

Air Carrier flight crew reported being notified by a flight attendant of a fume event in the passenger cabin during climb. Flight crew executed an air return to departure airport where maintenance identified odor as a fuel leak.

B737 flight crew reported the failure of the Mode Control Panel resulted in the autopilot; autothrottle; and VNAV malfunctioning in cruise. The flight crew then decided to descend below RVSM airspace; and proceeded to continue the flight and safely landed at the airport.
Airport Ramp Controller cleared an aircraft to taxi into gate while another aircraft was obstructing the path causing the wing tips to collide.
Aerobatic pilot reported a blown tire on landing after brake application. Pilot reported that high local temperatures and pedal forces for type of plane may have been contributory.

Military Helicopter pilot reported an NMAC during approach to a non-towered airport.  A higher performance aircraft on the same approach caused the conflict requiring the reporter to take evasive action.
Pilot reported an airborne conflict with a possible UAS during final approach. Pilot did not take evasive action and continued the approach.
B747-400 Captain reported an engine malfunction during the takeoff roll. The Captain rejected the takeoff and taxied to parking.
Pilot reported there were no NOTAMs issued after a storm and subsequent flooding led to large debris being scattered throughout 56S airport.
An observer reported seeing two UAS in the area of commercial and or military aircraft.
A320 pilot reported missing engine pylon panel following post flight inspection.
PA-28 instructor reported taking evasive action after airborne conflict during approach to landing.
Air Carrier flight crew reported Landing Gear malfunction and return to departure airport.
L5 Stinson pilot reported in flight power loss resulted in off airport landing on road. Pilot reported later to be debris in the carburetor.
Air carrier ground personnel reported being notified of an update which inaccurately permits securing DG with a wheelchair. The ground reporter alerted the appropriate personnel.
C172 pilot reported an NMAC while in cruise flight and took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Air Carrier Captain reported a 'cargo door open' EICAS illuminated during initial climb. After troubleshooting and conferring with Dispatch the flight executed a safe air return where Maintenance put the cargo door indicator on MEL and released the aircraft.
Light aircraft instructor reported loss of directional control on a snowy taxiway while exiting the runway.
C172 Pilot reported having to take evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft while entering the pattern.
EMB-145 Captain reported the flight was dispatched with one pack inop. During climbout the other pack failed. The crew received priority handling and return to the origin airport.

A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on base leg reported malfunctioning flight controls and vibrations. The aircraft turned on its own towards the airport on a conflicting heading with another aircraft on final approach.
DA-40 Flight Instructor and student reported a possible fuel leak during a training flight. The flight diverted and found fuel leaking from under the engine.
A flight school instructor reported an aircraft landed behind them before they had fully exited the runway.
GA pilot reported that while on short final to JZP; a non-towered airport; another aircraft taxied onto the runway and was not communicating on CTAF.
Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict with another aircraft during pushback from gate.

B757 Captain reported encountering turbulence from departing Super A380 just before landing at SFO. Reporter expressed concern that SFO ATC is consistently operating with insufficient spacing in these type of events.

Air carrier Ramp Lead personnel reported a computer malfunction which was not producing a dangerous good form for the flight crew. After extensive trouble shooting the Hazmat cargo was unloaded which eliminated the dangerous good form requirement.
Experimental aircraft pilot reported an NMAC occurred during cruise. A helicopter flying in the opposite direction was at the wrong altitude while traveling eastbound.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported unmanifested Dry Ice had been transported to destination.

Cessna 310 pilot reported that both engines failed sequentially after the pilot left the fuel selector on AUX tanks during an approach. Pilot stated that after switching back to MAIN fuel tanks; power was restored in one engine and a single-engine approach was continued to landing.
Maintenance Technician reported that a training class on the B737 MAX was not long enough and did not adequately cover the Maneuvering Characteristics Augmentation System (MCAS).
Air carrier Captain reported not making a crossing restriction after realizing they had overflown the top of descent.
B737 Captain reported a horizontal stabilizer trim malfunction during initial climb. The crew hand flew the aircraft with manual trim inputs to the destination and landed.
BE-36 pilot reported landing gear malfunction inflight. Pilot lost control of aircraft on landing.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported a battery discharge annunciation in flight and elected to make an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.

EMB-170 First Officer reported an electrical malfunction when starting the descent which affected multiple display screens in the cockpit with associated EICAS messages. The flight landed and was towed to the gate by maintenance.
Embraer Captain reported deviating from their assigned heading on departure; resulting in flight towards terrain. ATC issued a low altitude alert and vectors away from terrain.
Pilot reported a sudden drop in engine RPM; popping noises; and significant vibration while in cruise flight. The pilot diverted to the nearest airport for a safe landing.
A Private Pilot reported a conflict with another aircraft on an opposing base leg and took evasive action.
B737 Captain reported taxiing to within 50 feet of a small aircraft while approaching a runway hold line during taxi to parking.
B737-800 flight crew reported an Engine Number 2 malfunction before top of descent point and shut down the engine. The Captain continued to their destination.
B767 flight crew reported windshear on landing resulting in tailstrike.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported landing safely at an alternate airport after experiencing loss of hydraulic system 3 shortly after takeoff.

B787 Captain reported receiving a call from a flight attendant who smelled smoke during the initial climb from the galley area. The flight returned to the departure airport; jettisoned fuel; and landed.
B737 First Officer reported during final approach they were instructed by ATC to go-around due to traffic not clearing the runway quickly enough.
Student Pilot reported a runway excursion at LNK airport upon landing due to winds.
B737-800 Captain reported engine failure along with FADEC and reverser lockout malfunctions during climb that resulted in a return to the departure airport.
A Center Controller in training reported they issued a clearance off of a non towered airport to 5;000 ft. which is below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

A Center Controller reported a conflict between an air carrier departure and a satellite airport skydiving operator who was conducting jumps in the vicinity of charted departure and arrival procedures. The reporter stated they anticipate the same situation will become a recurring problem.
ERJ 145 crew reported a NMAC after receiving an RA while in congested airspace.
Air carrier flight crew reported an inflight upset resulted when they encountered severe turbulence at FL340.
Captain reported an EICAS message; BLEED ENGINE LEAK 2 after takeoff. The flight crew elected to return to the departure airport for a precautionary landing.
Flight Instructor reported while practicing emergency landings severe turbulence caused a temporary loss of control resulting in terrain alert and descent below 500 ft over a populated area.
Flight instructor with student reported an NMAC in the downwind leg of the ISM airport traffic pattern when another aircraft turned crosswind in front of them.
A321 Ramp Crew reported being notified of a Dangerous Goods parcel that was inadvertently missed during unloading by his ground crew. DG parcel remained on next flight.

Air carrier ground personnel reported being summoned to the Supervisor's office after an improper HAZMAT shipment. Supervisor stated there was no investigation being held and dismissed the ground personnel.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from tower during visual approach while in the process of correcting to glidepath.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported failed steering inoperative message resulted in expeditious handling to a safe landing.

Air Carrier B737 Captain reported stopping aircraft on incline because ground marshaller used non standard and vague signals; resulted in using unsafe amount of power to stop aircraft rolling backwards. Pilot reported this endangered ground personnel.
Air carrier Captain reported undeclared dry ice was transported. No NOTOC or other documents were provided during preflight.

Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported a critical ground conflict while holding on the taxiway; per ATC clearance; adjacent to their gate. A Cessna; taking off from a parallel runway; made an early turn and flew over the  airliner. The air carrier crew stated they were contacting the tower referencing the incident.

Tower Controller and pilot reported a helicopter cleared for a touch and go hovered over the approach end of the runway causing the pilot to take evasive action which resulted in them leaving the runway surface into the grass.
Pilot reported flying below the minimum safe altitude and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training which resulted in the student executing a go-around to avoid a collision. The conflict Pilot stated no radio calls were heard.
Air carrier pilot reported receiving EGPWS warning on approach. Flight crew stopped descent and intercepted glideslope.
During Maintenance Inspection on an A320 aircraft; it was reported that the Crew O2 Bottle Service Port Cap was loose and not sealing.

B737 flight crew reported an inflight upset occurred at FL330 when they encountered wake turbulence ten miles in trail of a B777. Control was regained and after changing cruise altitude the flight continued to destination.

Air carrier ground personnel reported discovering the aircraft took off with incorrect weights when undocumented dangerous goods were found in a container whereas the Departure Staging Guide (DSTG) showed it as empty.
Tecnam P92 Instructor reported engine roughness resulted in a safe off airport landing. Instructor suspected fuel contamination.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming in international airspace at cruise altitude. The Captain indicated the GPS jamming lasted for approximately 20 minutes.
Flight Instructor with a student flying a Cessna 172 reported a NMAC on final approach.
Air carrier First Officer reported an NMAC during arrival with what was possibly a UAS. No evasive action was taken.

B737 Flight Crew reported they inadvertently descended below minimum vectoring altitude during arrival due to not recognizing that the MCP had been incorrectly set. The Flight Crew subsequently climbed back to the correct altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unstabilized approach due to lack of recent flying experience; weather; and ATC instructions.
EWR Tower Controller reported solar glare approximately 1.4 miles S/SE of EWR Tower.
B787 Captain reported they stopped taxiing to avoid colliding with another taxiing aircraft which deviated off center line to go around them.
Falcon 20 flight crew reported pressurization control system malfunction inflight.
Pilot reported experiencing debilitating symptoms due to carbon monoxide poisoning in flight. Pilot temporarily lost then regained consciousness and landed the aircraft.
Pilot reported inadvertently using a paved area for taxi rather than the correct taxiway due to a lack of clear markings at a non-towered airport.
C402 Captain reported during climb; ice began to accumulate on the wings and the boots did not deploy. The Captain diverted and landed.
Pilot reported reoccurring difficulties in communicating with ZMP ARTCC via multiple frequencies in the vicinity of MBL airport.
B737-800 First Officer reported the trailing edge flap indicator had a split between left and right flap.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event with during landing training followed by a ground conflict with same aircraft operating in a nonstandard manner.

CE-680A First Officer reported uncommanded aircraft movement on a ramp area when parked on an an area of ice at night; after releasing the parking brake. The crew set the brakes to stop the movement; no damage occurred; then repositioned the aircraft to another area on the ramp.
DA40 Flight Instructor with student reported a tire popped after touchdown and aircraft stopped on runway; requiring a tug to parking.
B737-800 Captain reported an unknown noise and lack of power while starting the number two engine.
B737 MAX 8 pilot reported extending speed brakes past a limitation. Flight crew retracted speed brakes and continued approach.

Air Carrier Captain reported two windshear alerts in close proximity in which the escape maneuvers were properly executed. After safely completing the recovery; the flight was vectored for another approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported smoke in cockpit when APU started during pre-light. Flight crew shutdown APU and evacuated aircraft.
C172 pilot reported flying too low over rising terrain to avoid clouds.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC with another helicopter while during approach
Flight Instructor reported their student was the cause of a NMAC while in the traffic pattern during the student's solo flight.

Flight crew reported a taxiway incursion at EGLL airport when First Officer became distracted while requesting updated takeoff performance data.  Flight crew suggested that poor taxiway signage contributed to the incursion.
Air carrier pilot reported experiencing expected GPS jamming in Georgia and Turkish airspace. Their GPS did not return as expected after reaching Budapest FIR airspace.

Pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern during visual conditions. The pilot indicated they maneuvered to avoid the aircraft and the tower told the other aircraft to break out of the pattern; then the pilot completed the pattern and landed.
EMB-170 flight crew reported loss of aircraft control during gate arrival.
Flight instructor with student on training flight reported a NMAC with a helicopter while in the traffic pattern of a non-towered airport.
C172 pilots reported excessive cylinder head temperature resulted in an air turn back.
Flight crew reported having a stick shaker activation during a go around; which was attributed to a hydraulic system failure.
Skydiving operations pilot reported going through assigned altitude and not wanting to change the center of gravity for the jumpers; started a slow descent to assigned altitude.

Air carrier crew reported while taxing to the gate they had to take evasive action to avoid a bus not yielding to them on the taxiway.  After the bus drove through the taxiway the air crew taxied to the gate without further incident.
PA46 Flight Instructor with student reported a momentary loss of aircraft control during landing resulting in a 'bend' in the right main landing gear.
ERJ-170 flight crew reported executing a go-around after a low altitude wake turbulence encounter led to a bounced landing at JFK.
A Tower Local Controller and a Ground Controller reported the Local Controller cleared an aircraft for takeoff before a previous arrival had taxied off the runway.
HA420 flight crew reported performing a go-around due to traffic conflict and upon being in the traffic pattern for another approach; may have violated Class C airspace.
CRJ700 First Officer reported the aircraft EICAS system failed in flight but was able to continue to the destination.
BE35 pilot reported the landing gear collapsed during taxi after landing.
Pilot on training flight with flight instructor reported engine malfunction when attempting a go-around which led to landing on the taxiway instead.
Air carrier First Officer reported there are two possible boundary lines for the pad area at ORD airport and the chart does not specify which one to use.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported a Generator Control Unit caught fire during their initial climb so they returned to their departure airport.
PA-28 engine pilot reported an NMAC while entering the pattern at a non towered airport.
EMB-175 Captain reported confusion during taxi into the gate because of multiple lead in lines and marshaller standing by while the crew used Safedock.

Balloon pilot reported was ascending near a cliff in preparation for landing when the balloon encountered a rotor/unexpected wind. The pilot lost control and the basket struck the ground into an immediate landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported they were assigned an airspeed above the FAR maximum airspeed below 10000 ft by ATC due to a traffic conflict. The flight crew flew the assigned speed and later determine the FAR speed was not waiveable by ATC.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing high engine vibrations that led to inflight shutdown of the left engine.

B767-300 Captain reported a strong burning odor and proceeded to investigate the source with a ramp agent. While the cause was undetermined; the aircraft was carrying items that were dangerous when wet and the departure conditions were rainy. However; inspection prior to departure was normal.

Air carrier Captain reported during arrival on PHX BRUSR 1 STAR they were given a confusing clearance for a visual approach for Runway 8. Flight crew was using RNAV Runway 8 approach which reportedly does not share a common fix with BRUSR 1 STAR to provide continuity; but the ILS Runway approach 8 does. The Captain stated the approaches are so similar; the inbound fixes should be the same.

EMB-170 First Officer reported during a short approach to landing that ATC issued a low altitude alert; resulting in a momentary level off and being slightly high on approach. The pilot flying corrected and continued to a landing.
Technicians reported attaching bolt was left uninstalled during a Fuel Nozzle installation. The aircraft suffered damage to a Thrust Reverser Translating Sleeve due to burn through.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying several missions in excess of approved LAANC altitudes. Reporter stated stress and fatigue contributed to the incidents.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported learning another UAS pilot had a fly away due to possible GPS issue. The reporter also expressed concerns about the company's safety culture.
B737 Captain reported a bank angle warning while diverting to an alternate airport due to thunderstorms and turbulence. The flight regained control and landed at the diversion alternate.
ERJ190 pilot reported the Captain's windshield shattered during descent. The flight continued to landing without further incident.
Pilot taxiing from FBO at LUK mistakenly entered Runway 21. The reporter cited confusing signage as one of the factors.
General aviation pilot preparing for departure at CLS reported communication with Seattle Approach was extremely poor on the ground and remained poor even after departure.
Flight Instructors reported after informing ATC they would fly the published missed approach at a non-towered airport; the instructors performed other maneuvers without informing ATC.

B737 Captain reported the ramp crew wanted to remove DG due to concerns that it was not properly sealed; but the flight crew was unsure of the procedures for a load change. Ultimately; the flight crew had the dangerous goods removed and ensured there was no spillage in the cargo department before departing.
EMB-190 pilot reported the main landing gear tire failure during landing resulted in loss of control and tug to the gate.
B757-200 First Officer reported IAS errors resulted in return to airport to find pitot covers still installed.
EMB-145 Captain reported an engine failure after leveling off at FL280 resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC during initial climb.; "Air Carrier Captain reported spotting a UAS while on approach."
Flight Instructor on training flight reported NMAC with another aircraft.
B737 tow crew reported towing at dark with poor lighting resulted in wing contacting a service stair stand.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported activating a GPWS Obstacle alert during a visual approach. After maneuvering to avoid obstacles flight executed a safe landing.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a NMAC during cruise with an aircraft overtaking them head-on. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Air carrier Ground Personnel reported a passenger carrying a case of Lithium Polymer batteries was deplaned to determine if the quantities were within the company limits. After further review; it was determined that the quantities were within the company's limits and passenger was allowed to fly on later flight.
