CRJ-200 Captain reported low oil pressure indications on the left engine during cruise. The flight crew elected to perform an inflight shut down of the affected engine and continue the descent and approach to original destination airport.

Vintage aircraft Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training with Aircraft Z erroneously landing opposite runway in use. Aircraft Z also cause a critical ground conflict with Aircraft Y.
Lead Technician reported a spar bumper fell out of the main wheel well while the aircraft was in the hangar.
B737-800 flight crew reported receiving an engine overheat caution light during climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and after thermal inspection taxied to the gate.
Pilot reported engine failure in cruise; resulting in an in flight shut down and diversion to make a precautionary landing.
GA pilots reported an NMAC occurred when they departed the traffic pattern using standard left traffic procedures; although the Tower Controller expected them to use right traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering severe turbulence in cruise flight in oceanic airspace that resulted in an upset; including excessive bank angle.
Air carrier Captain reported a new flight crew procedure of confirming that DG has been received could be improved to prevent noncompliance.

General Aviation pilot reported they performed a go-around using the standard; expected procedure at the airport and were told by ATC that they made the initial turn too early. Pilot also reported witnessing a near mid-air collision at this airport between an aircraft on short final and an aircraft conducting a go-around on an intersecting runway.
B777 air carrier First Officer reported pressurization system failure in cruise flight and returned to departure airport for uneventful landing.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported anomaly with FMS equipment possibly due to 5G interference during climbout.

B737-700 flight crew reported experiencing wake turbulence while climbing to assigned altitude and proceeded to overshoot the altitude. ATC was informed and the Captain corrected the aircraft back to the assigned altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported final DG documents contained a loading placement error. The DG issue was corrected prior to gate departure.

GA pilot reported an NMAC while on the downwind at non-towered EDU requiring evasive action.  The pilot of the other aircraft had reportedly not been communicating properly; which contributed to the event.
Reporter states that due to congestion on the hangar floor; a larger man lift was used to conduct maintenance and resulted in damage to the aircraft.

B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported a Fuel Imbalance condition in cruise. The flight crew ran the QRH and Check lists and performed an in flight shut down of the #1 Engine for a Fuel Leak. The flight crew diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
TBM 960 pilot reported malfunction with autopilot system leading to GPWS warning resulting in disconnecting the autopilot and returning to the correct heading.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported experiencing loss of engine power during takeoff resulting in rejected takeoff
EMB-175 Captain reported numerous equipment failures while enroute and resulting in a diversion.
A high wing single engine aircraft pilot reported during initial climb they observed a low wing single engine aircraft taxi across the runway in front of them.

Fractional flight crew reported while holding short of a runway; a general aviation aircraft lined up as if to land on the taxiway where the fractional aircraft was. On very short final approach; the GA aircraft maneuvered to land on the runway.

Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft overshot final as it tracked the inbound course lined up almost halfway in between SFO runways 28L and 28R during the RNAV approach. This led to a potential NMAC with the aircraft ahead.
Air carrier maintenance personnel reported HAZMAT were mistakenly shipped out via air transport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported an RA event and blocked ATC communications due to possible COVID-19 ATC staffing issues.
Citation pilot on approach reported a NMAC with two helicopters crossing approach path to runway. Flight crew accomplished a go-around.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC event after ATC assigned a level off during initial climb after takeoff from MSP.  The flight crew complied with the TCAS RA and notified ATC.
A Corporate pilot reported descending below their assigned altitude and received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
B737 MAX First Officer reported the maintenance procedure to manage electric hydraulic pumps is not included in pilot manuals; resulting in non standard brake accumulator pressure variance.
Air carrier Captain reported departure gate pushback without receiving the DG summary. Summary was received after pushback.
A319 Flight Attendant crew reported a fumes event during descent resulting in crew experiencing physiological symptoms. Maintenance met the aircraft on arrival.
Pilot reported engine power loss and surging at cruise and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
ERJ-170 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into ORD that resulted in a minor injury to a Flight Attendant.
RV12 pilot reported engine loss of power during takeoff climb. Pilot returned to land immediately at departure airport.

EMB-145 Captain reported an air turn back after anti-ice systems failed after takeoff. After trouble shooting restored system operation; Captain reported Dispatch pressured him into continuing to destination airport with additional maintenance work.
Piper PA-28 pilot reported a complete loss of electrical power; in night VFR conditions; resulting in a diversion to the nearest suitable airfield.
Air carrier flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control after encountering turbulence near CAMJO Intersection in ZJX airspace.

B757 Captain reported on the decent into destination airport; at  approximately 13;000 feet; the right engine failed. The flight crew immediately ran the QRH procedures and successfully restarted the engine for a normal landing.
Corporate jet First Officer reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on approach to LTFE; citing ATC clearance confusion as a contributing factor.
EMB-170 Captain reported multiple in flight malfunctions on an aircraft that was released from previous maintenance action. The flight safely returned to the departure airport.

PA-44 Flight Instructor reported a runway excursion during landing after the aircraft veered to the left regardless of pilot input until the aircraft stopped in the grass.  The Instructor stated the cause may have been a rudder connection issue.
D01 TRACON Controller reported a busy session with another sector controller that led to communication issues and a TCAS RA/NMAC.

Air carrier Captain reported a communication breakdown between the Captain and two FAA Inspectors during a 'Ramp Check' in which Inspectors were tracking two Hazmat items while the Captain stated two items were packaged in one box. Ultimately the Hazmat was unloaded and the flight departed safely.

Technician reported while troubleshooting an over wing emergency exit door indication issue it found that the RH AFT over wing exit door could not be opened. The top of the exit door would contact the bottom of the overhead bin preventing the door from opening more than one or two inches. The aircraft was removed from service for maintenance action.
C560 pilot reported that while climbing they experienced a number 1 engine thrust loss resulting in landing at the first available airport.

PA-44 flight instructor with student and observers on board reported during engine start that a fire developed on the cowling. Instructor then shut down the engine; stopping the fire; and everyone evacuated the aircraft safely.

B757 crew reported an altitude excursion and loss of control after penetrating a building cumulonimbus cloud during climb in an area of convective activity.  Crew had requested multiple deviations previously which were denied prior to this encounter.

Two CFI's reported having a near mid-air collision on initial climb. The other aircraft failed to respond to the tower instructions to follow preceding aircraft after takeoff and turned crosswind towards the other aircraft. Both pilots took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier Captain reported a convoy of semi-trucks crossing a taxiway in front of the aircraft; requiring a stop.

Ramp marshaller reported a ground conflict while parking an air carrier aircraft at the gate. The Marshaller was signaling an 'X' as a stop signal to the Captain; while another ramp worker was positioning chocks to the nose gear; when the pilot increased thrust and the ramp worker had to quickly jump out of the way of the aircraft under a ramp stop signal.

Air traffic approach controller reported an aircraft transitioning the airspace descended toward another aircraft on final approach causing the aircraft to descend below the MVA to avoid the descending aircraft.
B777 Captain reported rejecting the takeoff and returning to the gate when the aircraft initiated a hard left turn early in the takeoff roll.
Air Taxi flight crew reported during takeoff the nose wheel was vibrating due to the nose gear pin not secured so they rejected the take off and taxied back to the FBO.
PC-12 reported diverting and making a precautionary landing after engine oil system indications showed low oil pressure and high oil temperature.
Aircraft technician reported signing off a service bulletin for a rudder position operational check that they did not perform.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending to an unsafe altitude during visual approach and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC. Flight crew leveled off and continued approach.
Pilot reported performing a go-around due to an aircraft cleared to take off but not in communication with FSD ATC.
Pilot reported a runway excursion during landing at BOI due to ambiguous lead-off runway and taxiway edge lighting configuration.
A321 pilot reported during push-back from gate they had a conflict with another aircraft pushing back from another gate without clearance.
GA pilot on a Visual Approach to IWS non-towered airport reported an NMAC with an unidentified VFR target. The pilot was still on Approach frequency and had not switched to CTAF.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying before sunrise and in controlled airspace without authorization.
Tower Controller reported low staffing levels during periods of complex traffic combined with undue outside pressures have created an unsafe situation.
Captain reported an airborne conflict with a general aviation aircraft that departed the airport ahead of the reporters aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering jumpers while in level cruise on V186 airway near TANNR Intersection.
Air carrier Captain reported noting radar altimeter readings as low as 900 feet AGL on a night visual approach to SAT.
Air carrier flight crew reported near miss on runway due to incorrect ATC clearance at SNA airport.
Corporate jet First Officer reported an NMAC with another aircraft on descent that resulted in a TCAS RA.

Pilot reported a propeller strike in his tail-wheel aircraft after a sudden gust of wind lifted the tail off the ground causing contact with surface. No injuries or damage to plane except for the propeller.
C172 flight instructor reported a student pilot turned in conflict resulted in taking evasive action and a NMAC.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported the transport of an aircraft O2 bottle that wasn't added to the manifest. The flight departed with
Recreational/Hobbyist UAS pilot reported a lost link occurred while flying a UAS in uncontrolled airspace. During return to home; the UAS had a near miss with a helicopter.
Flight Instructor reported a rough running engine on initial climb and a return to the departure airport.
Flight Instructor on training flight reported smoke in the cabin inflight and an immediate landing.

Embraer 190 pilot reported an ATC directed go around due to a loss of separation with another aircraft not clearing the runway while on final approach for landing. The aircraft landed safely on the second approach and the number one engine failed as the aircraft rolled out on the runway and then safely taxied off the runway.
Pilot flying PA-28 aircraft reported engine malfunction and fuel exhaustion during cross country flight.
Air carrier captain reported GPS jamming over the Sinai peninsula at the southern tip of Israel and Jordan to just inside of Saudi Arabia.
An air carrier pilot descending into MCO reported they deviated from their assigned clearance due to navigating to NICKI intersection instead of MICKI Intersection.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported an airborne conflict with a helicopter. The UAS avoided the helicopter.

Pilot reported a loss of aircraft control in IMC on approach after they mistakenly thought they had disconnected the autopilot. Pilot eventually disconnected the AP and completed the approach. Pilot also stated they overleaned the engine after landing; causing it to die while the airplane was not completely clear of the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAS during initial approach. No evasive action was taken.

Air carrier First Officer reported a GPWS terrain warning while on a night visual approach inside the FAF. Flight crew continued the approach without following the terrain avoidance procedure and landed.
Approach Controller reported issuing a clearance for the ILS approach; only to discover later that ILS approaches were not authorized per the NOTAMs.
Air taxi First Officer reported an aircraft attempted to taxi and park between them and another parked aircraft with inadequate clearance.

Pilot reported a rejected take off at 80 knots due to the #2 engine not producing required thrust. This was accompanied by aircraft yaw and N1 fluctuations. The flight crew returned to the gate for maintenance.

Tailwheel pilot reported loss of aircraft control occurred during landing rollout with a tailwind when a gust of wind pushed the aircraft. The pilot applied rudder inputs and braking; causing the tailwheel to malfunction; resulting in a runway excursion.

Cessna 182 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from several B737 aircraft overflying his track. ATC reportedly refused his request for a lower altitude into ORD Class B airspace to avoid the wake encounters.
Air carrier Captain reported a near collision with a company aircraft during pushback at STL.
Air carrier dispatcher reported receiving communication from a flight crew of a engine tailpipe fire on engine start. The crew returned to the gate safely and contacted maintenance.

Technician reported an aircraft returned to the gate after flight control checks revealed both Out Board Ailerons were not functional. Maintenance reset circuit breakers and the plane blocked back out.
A321-NEO Flight Attendant reported failure to follow disarming procedure resulted in another FA's door opening and escape slide deployed.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported their student on a solo flight experienced a runway excursion after overcorrecting from being off center line during landing.
EMB-505 flight crew reported a fume event during initial climb resulted in a diversion.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported the Captain initiated a call to discuss a passenger's portable refrigeration device in use on the aircraft and plugged into the AC power outlet. After discussion and research it was determined the unit should be unplugged and the passenger provided with ice to keep the medical contents sufficiently cool.
A320 pilot reported temporary loss of control during landing due to number three brake malfunction.
PA28R Pilot reported engine compartment fire and gear up landing.
GA pilot reported a runway excursion during landing resulted when the aircraft encountered a gust of wind.
Kodiak 100 pilot reported they misprogrammed their FMS during a missed approach and flew off course resulting in a low altitude alert from ATC.
Pilot flying PA28 aircraft reported nose wheel steering failure during landing rollout; resulting in a runway excursion.
Regional jet captain reported declaring minimum fuel with ATC after Tower initiated missed approach at PHL.  Flight landed without issue.
B757-200 First Officer reported the escape slide pack fell out while trying to close the doors; citing pads on aircraft floor as a contributing factor. Maintenance was called.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a flock of birds circled the UAS during its descent. One of the birds struck and cracked a single prop. The UAS landed without further damage.
C172 flight instructors reported during descent the flight attitude instruments gave conflicting information. The flight crew exited IMC conditions and performed a diversion in VMC conditions.
A320 Captain reported the aircraft rolled after pushback and before the ground crew had cleared the area; even though the brakes were set.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported a close encounter with a tower when he was heads-down working a checklist.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported a malfunctioning Lithium battery powered charger was refused by flight crew  The flight crew submitted report but failed to follow proper procedures.
Flight Instructor reported they failed to take control of the aircraft when the student did not make a radio call and climbed into traffic which resulted in a NMAC.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while inbound to DSM airport with an aircraft flying opposite direction at the same altitude; requiring evasive action to avoid a collision.
MSN Controller reported issued relating to the Oshkosh fly in and departure.
A ramp worker marshaling an aircraft into parking reported the pilot continued taxiing after being signaled to stop and almost running over the reporter.
Captain reported cockpit window 3L shattered during climb and elected to perform an air turn back to a safe precautionary landing.
Tower Controller reported a pilot took the wrong runway for departure; resulting in takeoff clearance cancellation and traffic for another aircraft.
Pilot reported cracks were discovered in the SR22 nose landing gear strut assembly during annual inspection. Pilot suggested the same problem may exist in other SR22 aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported GPS interference and the GPS began to malfunction in cruise; and ATC advised the flight crew of GPS interference. The GPS returned later in the flight and RNP was back to normal.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported glideslope failure on approach.

Captain reported loss of power brakes during approach to landing. The flight crew went missed approach to troubleshoot before returning to land. The aircraft stopped on the runway and was chocked in place until a tug could be dispatched to tow the aircraft to the FBO for maintenance.

B767 flight crew reported the ground controller created a taxiway conflict along with clearance onto an active runway with traffic on takeoff roll resulted in critical ground conflicts requiring evasive action. Flight crew also stated that ATC ICAO procedures were not followed with use of multiple languages leading to confusion.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported unintentionally flying in controlled airspace without authorization during a photo shoot. Pilot stated they misread the UAS app and believed they had LAANC authorization.
Citation 680 Captain reported diverting after the rudder system jammed. A precautionary landing was made and the rudder worked normally during landing.

SR20 instructor reported a Crew Alert System message with a high temperature indication during take-off roll. The Instructor rejected the takeoff and requested assistance to be towed back to the maintenance hangar.
Lancair pilot reported the nose gear collapsed during landing resulting in a propeller strike. Reportedly; a bolt on the drag link had come loose and caused the nose gear to fail.
EMB-170 flight crew reported during pushback they started one engine which caused the aircraft to roll forward due to the ground crew having disconnected the tug without notifying the flight crew.
Pilot flying C-182 aircraft reported an electrical burning odor during descent to landing. After landing inspection revealed electrical burn marks around hot terminal of alternator.

Tug operator reported ready to pushback when the Crew Chief removed the headset and ended their shift and left the aircraft without wheel chocks. The tug driver identified the aircraft was not chocked; exited the tug; then aircraft moved and the tug driver quickly put chocks under the wheels without aircraft damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported while on a visual approach they made an unstable approach resulting in ATC issuing a low altitude alert. The flight crew landed safely at the airport.
Pilot reported an NMAC event with 5-6 hang gliders while descending toward destination with VFR Flight Following.
Air carrier First Officer reported on climb out receiving multiple ECAM messages resulting in the flight crew returning to the departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported on initial climb out they had a medical emergency and requested a return to the airport. As they expedited their return; they were not stabilized on the approach.
B737 Captain reported a cracked L1 cockpit window during descent and a taxi clearance deviation once on the ground.
Flight Instructor with student reported a UAS hovering over the runway while they were on final approach. The UAS moved as they got closer but stayed within the vicinity of the airport.
C-99 Captain reported incomplete submission of required Hazmat documents.
Air carrier First Officer reported a CFIT event during a visual approach. Pilot took evasive action to silence the terrain warning and stated the terrain warning system was 'overcompensating.'
An Observer at the airport witnessed a NMAC between two similar aircraft; one circling and the other taking off.
Air taxi Captain reported fixes on the approach were not loaded and they descended below published altitudes resulting in a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
Pilot reported nose landing gear unsafe light on gear retraction after takeoff; causing an air turn back and precautionary landing.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a fume event of unknown source during preflight prior to boarding. Maintenance removed aircraft from service and Flight Attendant was removed from trip.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported engine RPM fluctations during takeoff roll resulted in rejected takeoff.
A319 Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported incorrectly issuing an MEL item.
Air carrier ground personnel reported ammunition was scattered in passenger bag and did not meet airline guidelines.
B737 Captain reported a rejected takeoff due to a compressor stall on the #2 engine.

C172 single engine pilot reported on final approach; with a high airspeed; unable to flare the aircraft. The pilot landed on the nose wheel and the nose wheel tire deflated. The aircraft was unable to taxi and was towed to the ramp.
Air Carrier flight crew reported in flight experiencing a fuel leak resulting in landing at their destination with less fuel than planned for the arrival.
BE400 Captain reported HSI; compass; transponder; and communications equipment failure resulted in a return to departure airport.

Pilot reported routinely losing communications with the NCT sector south of the KLIDE intersection in flight. After regaining contact; a conversation with the Controller revealed this is a known problem in the area.

Flight Attendant reported the aircraft cleaners were slipping onboard to clean the aircraft before the passengers were completely off. One last passenger; resembling a cleaner; remained intermixed with crew and cleaners during the crew's exit.
Flight Crew reported RA during climbout and missed ATC heading.

Bellanca pilot reported an engine failure during post maintenance flight. After troubleshooting the engine multiple times the engine failed completely. Requested ATC assistance and executed a safe off airport landing with no aircraft damage or injuries.

General aviation pilot reported a critical ground conflict resulted when they attempted to land on the wrong runway which also had a departing aircraft. Pilot performed a go-around and landed safely on runway as cleared by ATC.
A Tower Controller reported an aircraft that was instructed to hold short of a taxiway taxied across the runway at the same time as an air carrier aircraft was taking off.

Beech 1900 Captain reported after turning on the engine bleeds during climb; a foul smell and smoke began to fill the flight deck. The Captain asked for assistance from ATC and returned to the departure airport. Engine cleaning the night before was a suspected cause.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a ground proximity warning while performing a visual approach and performed corrective actions.

B737 Captain reported that after pushback; there was a message about dangerous goods (DG) not being accepted. The reporter never received notification that dangerous goods had been loaded onto the aircraft.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported a ground conflict with inbound aircraft during gate pushback. Captain added that tug driver communication was nonstandard and that wing walkers were not wearing headsets.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported overshooting their cleared altitude when trying to control their speed in the descent.

Air carrier Captain reported the DG cargo documentation erroneously stated that DG cargo had been off loaded prior to departure. During a Sat-Com call with Dispatch it was determined that the DG cargo was not off loaded; so documentation was correct.
Air carrier flight crew reported EGPWS due to terrain on approach.
Flight Attendant reported a loud bang as they were about to take off.  Takeoff was rejected and aircraft was towed back to gate after both engines were shut down.
C550 First Officer reported a dynamic fuel quantity indication resulting in Maximum Take Off Fuel quantity exceedance.
Technician reported circuit breakers were not reset after thrust reverser maintenance. The aircraft returned to the gate for a no ignition during engine start.
Light transport aircraft flight crew reported putting in the incorrect altitude to descend to causing a low altitude alert during approach to MKE airport.

Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict during taxi with an aircraft under tow. The Tower Controller cleared the Captain to cross a taxiway and confirmed the towed aircraft would give way; which it failed to do.
B757 pilot reported left engine failed to produce takeoff power resulting in rejected takeoff.
PA28R pilot reported increased pressure required on yoke when adjusting pitch.
A319 flight crew reported during initial climb a reverse unlocked warning indication appeared for engine #1. The engine was secured and a diversion was initiated.
C152 Instructor and student pilot reported a forced landing resulted when the engine failed to start following a simulated engine failure.
Fractional pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC when they descended on an approach that they had not been cleared for. Pilot received clearance and continued to landing.

CRJ-900 Captain reported a hard landing when the rate of descent was not reduced during the landing flare. There were no injuries; the aircraft taxied to the gate; and a write up was sent to maintenance.
Pilot reported safety concerns landing at night on Runway 30 at CVN due to a powerline and tall fence near the approach end which does not have VASI equpment.
Technician reported missing a step to unplug the pressure switch while working on the engine anti-ice valve to be locked open.
Corporate flight crew reported NMAC on approach to land.
EMB-505 Captain reported receiving a gear disagree CAS message during departure resulting in a diversion.  The crew made a low pass and had Tower check the gear before they safely landed.

C525 Captain reported after engaging the autopilot; the aircraft initiated a right-hand turn and deviated from the departure route. ATC was notified of a terrain alert and informed the flight crew of the deviation.
C172 pilot reported an NMAC after an aircraft turned the wrong way during a go-around and verbalized a lengthy radio call; preventing ATC from warning of traffic conflict.
Air carrier Captain reported while in cruise noticing a burning electrical odor resulting in the flight diverting as a precaution.
B505X Instructor reported observed single engine aircraft landing loss of control with runway excursion.

A helicopter pilot reported a UAS approached their aircraft requiring the pilot to maneuver to avoid a collision. The UAS approached the helicopter again requiring further evasive action.  After the second event the UAS landed.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while on departure climb out.
Air carrier flight crew reported landing aircraft had not cleared runway when they had started takeoff roll. Flight crew rejected takeoff and cleared runway.
A Piper Arrow pilot reported NMAC with opposite direction traffic climbing towards them.
GA Flight Instructor reported a NMAC occurred while climbing out after takeoff.
EMB-505 flight crew reported while in cruise flight experiencing an AHRS failure resulting in the flight crew hand flying the aircraft to their destination airport.
Cessna 170 pilot reported loss of directional control after landing that led to a runway excursion into the grass. No damage to aircraft or airport equipment resulted.

B777-200 flight crew reported loud noise from Nose Gear on Landing Gear Retraction. The flight crew elected to continue the flight to destination airport and requested priority handling to make a precautionary landing.
A319 check pilot reported there may have been a short aircraft break away during push-back. The ground crew reported no damage.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming on the descent into ELP. Captain stated there was a late approach change; by ATC; contributing to an unstable approach.
Pilot reported discrepancies between a NOTAM and the 10-7 page showing the opening time of the airport.
An air carrier flight crew reported a rejected takeoff when they observed an aircraft on short final in the opposite direction.
Air carrier First Officer reported losing both GPS signals shortly before the top of descent into ABQ and did not regain GPS signal until final approach.
Air carrier Captain reported a rejected takeoff due to the loss of nose-wheel steering.
A B737 Captain reported during taxiing they had to stop abruptly to avoid another taxiing aircraft resulting in a Flight Attendant injury.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Maintenance Instructor and Trainee reported a training mistake during a training session and both cited being off for an extended time contributed to the event.
PA28 flight instructor reported solo student loss of control on landing.
Flight crew reported being confused by the poor taxiway signage at DAL Airport and joined the wrong taxiway while taxiing to the runway.
DA40 flight instructors reported alternator malfunction during initial climb followed immediately by engine failure. Pilot flying landed aircraft next to runway in grass.
A Center Controller reported Iowa City VOR and Ottawa Canada VOR identifier is IOW which resulted in a navigation error for an aircraft.

PA-18 general aviation pilot reported an engine failure while entering the traffic pattern for landing due to fuel exhaustion in one of the two tanks. The pilot glided the aircraft to a safe landing; then the engine restarted and the pilot taxied to parking.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC during cruise with a descending aircraft. The flight crew took immediate evasive action that resulted in mach buffet.
ATC Center Controller reported an aircraft was experiencing intermittent loss of engine power; but was able to land safely at their destination airport.

Flight Instructor and student reported that the engine lost power after takeoff and the landing resulted in a runway excursion. Postflight; it was discovered the student had moved the fuel selector near the off position and the guard preventing this was broken.
Medium Transport aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with an aircraft on final that overshot its final to BJC airport.
Aeronca Champ pilot reported engine failure inflight. Pilot made an off airport landing.

Part 107 UAS pilot reported conducting a UAS flight near a law enforcement helipad in a nearby airport's Class D airspace with a LAANC authorization. During the flight; law enforcement officers approached and informed the UAS pilot stating the proper procedure for authorizing the UAS flight was not followed.
PA-24 pilot reported engine failure inflight. Pilot diverted and landed uneventfully.
A320 flight crew reported nose gear did not retract on take off due to the nose gear pin being left in.
Dispatcher reported being notified by flight crew of a loss of pressurization. The flight crew diverted to the alternate airport and landed.
C172 Flight Instructor reported announcement of entering the downwind traffic pattern with conflicting traffic resulted in taking evasive action to avoid an NMAC.

EMB-145 flight crew reported an engine failure during climbout at 22;000 ft. The Captain stated they received no warnings prior to the engine failure and attempts to re-start it were unsuccessful. The crew decided to return to the departure airport.
Dispatcher reported an air turn back after the APU failed after take off with an Integrated Drive Generator already on MEL.
B737 Captain reported an upset climbing through FL364 after encountering wake turbulence from a B777 at FL370.
Air Carrier Captain reported the AUS ramp area is subject to frequent ground conflicts; in large part because the ramp is not controlled by ATC.
GA pilot reported a taxiway incursion at TYR airport attributed to no hold lines or taxiway signs as depicted in the AIM or airport diagrams. Pilot also had communication problems with ATC.
B737-800 flight crew reported forward entry door warning light resulted in a rejected takeoff.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a missing engine out procedure for RWY 27 BOS. Pilot reports the procedure was in charts previously and matched the APS chart for BOS.

Air carrier flight crew reported on departure roll the aircraft deviated to the right and they were unable to arrest the deviation resulting in a rejected takeoff.  The flight crew taxied back to the gate.
An Air Carrier pilot reported a compressor stall in the right engine while taking off.
A TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft turned the wrong direction and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier First Officer reported the loss of the #2 Hydraulic System while at cruise altitude.
A new Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a miss connect between Dispatch and maintenance regarding matching the aircraft logbook and electronic logbook differences.
Air carrier Captain reported momentary loss of aircraft control while flying through turbulence over FILPZ intersection at 12;000 feet.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a UAS while they were on final approach.
B737 Captain reported engine failure on takeoff. The flight crew rejected the takeoff and returned to the gate for maintenance action.
A flight school pilot reported after restarting their engine in the run up area smoke billowed from the engine cowling and they shut down the engine.

CL-350 First Officer reported Cabin Pressurization System failure during climb. Flight crew executed immediate action items followed by requesting special handling to a diversion airport where a safe landing was accomplished.
B737 Flight crew reported trailing edge flap failure during approach. The flight crew performed a go around and landed at destination airport.
B737-800 Captain reported a flap malfunction and divert to land at the nearest suitable airport. The reporter also stated the software app to access the QRH is burdensome.
Pilots of two aircraft reported a near miss with each other while approaching a non-towered airport.
Captain of a training flight reported lights near HEF Airport caused confusion during an IFR approach.
Technicians reported an engine borescope plug was left uninstalled and was discovered post maintenance.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict during taxi with a truck that failed to give way to taxiing aircraft. Captain abruptly stopped the aircraft to avoid a collision.
Maintenance technicians reported that while they were working on an aircraft with a flap issue the company management was not supportive of AMT's working to find root cause of the problem.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying the UAS before registering it with the FAA.
Cessna 172 pilot reported having an intermittent alternator inoperative light during cruise at 6000 feet in IMC. The pilot was able to communicate with ATC via phone and hand held radio and land.
A330 Captain reported concerns over loss of crew coordination and situational awareness during a rejected take off event.
Widebody Captain reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on approach to ZZZZ.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss after landing with another aircraft attempting to land opposite direction on the same runway; during a training flight at a non-towered airport. The landing aircraft performed a go-around and flew over the instructor's aircraft at low altitude; then returned for landing.
B737-800 flight crew reported on final approach receiving master caution warning for low hydraulic pressure resulting with the flight crew landing safely and being towed to the gate.
A220-300 Captain reported rejecting takeoff due to an engine fault and while troubleshooting the engine fault; the engines runs exceeded the recommended limitations with customers onboard.
PC-12 pilot reported a landing gear disagree indication while extending landing gear. Pilot used hand pump to extend gear and continued to a safe landing.
C172 pilot reported a rough running engine during initial climb and returned to land.
Flight Crew reported engine problems on descent and after performing an inflight shut down of the #3 engine; continued to landing at destination airport.
CRJ900 flight crew reported rejecting the takeoff roll due to momentary loss of control on acceleration related to failure to center nosewheel steering.
B757 Captain reported during cruise flight they experienced a right engine overheat EICAS resulting in aircraft operating with reduced performance.  The crew continued to their destination airport.
CE-172 pilot reported loss of engine power during cruise. Pilot diverted and was unable to reach an airport so landed off-field.
Flight Instructor reported the student landed instead of doing a touch-and-go as cleared resulting ATC directing an aircraft on approach to going around.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unreliable airspeed event during departure climb. The crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
Air Carrier flight crewmember reported a strong sweat sock odor during preflight. Passengers were deplaned and Maintenance was requested.
B737-700 Captain reported abnormal airspeed indications on the MFD speed tape display. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
A Tower Local Controller reported they cleared an aircraft onto the runway with another aircraft on short final.
A319 First Officer reported diverting to an alternate airport after shutting down #2 engine following loss of oil quantity.
UAS encountered wind shear and was unable to maintain altitude. Flight crew was able to regain control of the UAS and return to previous altitude.
C99 Captain reported confusion over the system operations for the Master Caution Annunciator. The Captain elected to make a log book entry after reaching destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported taxiing on ramp without clearance and stated they had lost situational awareness as a result of a long and fatiguing day.
Air carrier ground personnel reported discovering at destination arrival that the cargo was loaded was in direct opposite of what was planned.
B757 Captain reported a critical ground conflict during taxi to arrival gate. ATC later apologized for failure to notify the flight crew of conflicting traffic.
Pilot reported a ruptured hydraulic line the cockpit resulted in a gear up landing. The POH was inaccessible and contributed to the emergency extension procedures not be complied with.

G650 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing LAX and also received an RA after flying towards the wrong waypoint after climb-out; which the flight crew later realized was due to an error being made while setting up the FMS for the departure.
Flight Attendant reported a total loss of electrical power to the galleys and lavatories to the Captain; who elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing.
EMB170 flight crew reported amber EICAS message appeared and silenced the alert. The Message did not reappear until the next crew operated the aircraft on the following outbound flight.
Bonanza pilot reported a loss of steering control during taxi. Post flight inspection revealed hardware missing from the strut steering mount.
Cessna Citation Latitude pilot reported both HF radios were inoperative during flight and indicated a suspected grounding issue was the cause.
Captain reported a near miss with a ground vehicle on the runway; while on landing rollout in reduced visibility conditions at a non-towered airport.
Pilot reported a NMAC with two unidentified aircraft in IMC.

Air carrier Captain reported flying into a thunderstorm and turbulence; resulting in altitude deviations from the cleared altitude. After exiting the thunderstorm the flight climbed back to the cleared altitude with no injuries reported.
Air Carrier flight crew flying a CRJ-200 aircraft reported a GPWS warning during initial approach.
A300 pilot reported the aircraft had unreliable airspeed indications during cruise. Flight crew returned to departure airport and during taxi the tiller wheel jammed requiring a tow to parking.
Air carrier flight crew reported being notified by ground personnel during destination arrival that a passenger's Lithium Ion battery powered bag was transported in the baggage compartment.

Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while flying in a known practice area with an aircraft that was not communicating on the practice area frequency. The instructor turned the aircraft left to avoid a collision and the other aircraft flew 300 feet above them.

Citation Captain reported while in cruise the ACM overheat and EMER PRESS annunciators activated followed by the cabin altitude starting to increase. The Captain began an immediate descent to 9;000 feet to stabilize the cabin pressure and continued to their destination airport.
A321 flight crew reported a dirty sock" odor was detected in the cabin during cruise resulting in a diversion."
Part 107 UAS pilot reported while flying their UAS for a photo shoot; persons not related to the UAS mission walked under the path of the UAS.

P2006T instructor reported a landing gear malfunction during the initial climb on a training flight at a non-towered airport. The instructor took the controls and after manual gear extension; returned for a safe landing.
B737 MAX 8 Maintenance Technician reported the design of the forward lavatory handle and flight deck door handle results in the handles overlapping and locking together.
EMB-175 flight crew reported temporary loss of control and excursion from assigned altitude due to severe turbulence.
Air taxi Captain reported the marshaler guided them too close to a parked aircraft; resulting in a near miss.
Air carrier first officer reported during landing they received GPWS obstacle warning due to encountering wind shear on short final. Wind shear caused a loss of altitude resulting in the warning.
Bonanza pilot reported miscommunication with ATC due to the noise from the cabin door opening inflight.
B767 Captain the ground crew at departure airport possibly calculated GTOW in kilograms and reported that number as pounds; leading to a 20;000 LB mistake in actual Take Off Weight.
Cessna 182 pilot reported a NMAC event while taxing into position and hold. The conflict was caused by ATC utilizing misleading phraseology in their instructions.

Air carrier Captain reported a recent change in altitude crossing restriction on the LAX 11-7 page; ILS 25L requires a steep descent between waypoints that is impossible to accomplish. Additionally; this causes multiple steep descent" messages to generate after loading the approach into the FMC."

Tower Controllers and a flight school instructor reported the instructor initiated a go around from short final due to another flight school aircraft being on the runway. The Controllers reported a mix up in communications; their inability to see the runway involved; and no ground radar contributed to their lack of awareness of an aircraft on the runway.

Light Transport aircraft Captain reported another pilot on frequency was experiencing engine issues and was asked by ATC to write down a phone number while trying to immediately land.  The Captain reported concerns with asking a pilot in distress to get 'superfluous information' while trying to focus during an emergency type situation.
Ramp Service Agent reported ground damage to the L4 door. Communication issues complicated determining when the damage actually occurred.
C172 flight instructor with student reported the engine began to malfunction right after takeoff prompting the instructor to take over the controls and land in a grass area near a taxiway.
Business jet Captain reported GPS jamming and spoofing during cruise. The Captain identified the GPS interference threat in the region and the GPS was restored later in the flight.
EMB-175 Captain reported a loss of aircraft control while new First Officer was flying a manual approach. Captain regained control and landed after executing a go around.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft rolled back after a ground crew trainee called for brake release before the tug and tow bar were in place. Captain applied brakes and stopped the roll.

Air Traffic Controller reported aircraft descended below assigned altitude resulting in aircraft flying towards terrain. ATC canceled the approach clearance; climbed the aircraft and the aircraft diverted to another airport due to the weather.
Air Carrier Captain reported he failed to sign and send Dispatch the Company material paperwork prior to gate departure.

Air Carrier First Officer reported a fume event during cruise. After completing the appropriate procedures the Captain requested priority handling to a suitable airport where a safe landing was completed.
Pilot reported loss of aircraft brakes on landing roll out caused the aircraft to over run the runway.
GA single pilot reported the aircraft's landing gear would not extend; requiring a gear up landing on a nearby grass strip.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported NMAC on final approach.

Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict after landing and during their runway exit on a high speed taxiway. The pilots stated an abrupt stop was required to avoid a collision. The crew reported there was no instruction or comment from the ATC ground controller.
Air taxi flight crew reported NMAC with another aircraft not on ADS-B while in climb.
Bonanza K35 pilot reported landing safely on the remaining runway when the engine lost power shortly after takeoff due to selection of a dry fuel tank that was reading 1/4 full.
Part 107 UAS crew reported completing a mission with the UAS.
C172 Pilot reported complete electrical failure and landed at the nearest towered airport instead of returning to their departure airport.
Ramp personal reported a service truck cut through the aircraft parking envelope while an active taxi phase was in progress.
E175 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into SEA in trail of a B737.
C162 pilot reported a loss of control due to the aircraft's entry door opening in flight after not being properly secured. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
Air carrier flight crew reported flight attendant notification of 'faint smoke smell' in cabin. Flight crew completed a safe air return.
BNA Trainee CPC-IT reported having to re-clear an aircraft to land after getting vehicles off the runway; and recommended that ground vehicles be equipped with transponders and ADSB technology.
PA-28R pilot reported fuel control problems resulted in an air turn back a precautionary landing at departure airport.
A321 flight crew reported loss of right engine oil quantity and pressure in climb. The flight crew performed an in flight shut down of the right engine and elected to divert for landing.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict with a drone during final approach to SDF airport.
A Tower Controller in Charge reported a NMAC between two Cessna 172's on short final.
Boeing 737 First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence resulting in a temporary loss of aircraft control; and an altitude deviation of several hundred feet.

Air Carrier Captain reported telling the Lead Flight Attendant that he would not be wearing a face mask in the flight deck due to safety of flight reason as his glasses fog up when wearing a face mask.

BE-35 pilot reported the engine quit after having selected the wrong fuel tank during scheduled fuel management procedure.  The pilot decided to successfully divert and land at the nearest airport rather than troubleshoot in the air.
B737 MAX 9 flight crew reported the Master Caution light illuminated and an electrical system annunciation displayed during departure climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport.
PC-24 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while in descent from a suspected heavy aircraft that resulted in two rapid 45-degree rolls.
Aircraft technician reported helicopter engine cowling came loose inflight and damaged rotor blades.
B767-300 Captain reported an engine malfunction/surge during climbout.  The flight crew decided to return to the departure airport.

CE 560 Single Pilot reported multiple avionics and communication failures after the PFD; radios and other avionics failed during climb. Eventually communication was re-established with ATC to obtain help and special handling for landing.

BE-50 pilot reported the right engine lost power in flight. The reporter landed safely and discovered the right fuel tank was empty even though they had requested the fueler to fill it prior to departure.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported the TRACON Controller vectored them on a conflicting course with a VFR aircraft 400 feet above them resulting in a TCAS/RA.
PA-32 pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC after encountering icing during cruise. Pilot entered VMC conditions and continued flight.

MD-11F flight crew reported a loss of fuel pressure during cruise flight which resulted in an engine malfunction. The crew followed QRH procedures; restarted the engine; and continued the flight to destination.
Cessna 172 Flight Instructor reported a brake failure while taxiing; resulting in a loss of aircraft control and excursion from the taxiway surface.
C441 pilot reported erratic engine indications during descent resulted in precautionary shutdown to a safe landing.

A320 Captain reported a fume event that was identified by the flight attendants as a 'dirty socks' odor during descent. After arriving at the gate; the flight crew contacted Maintenance to inspect the aircraft.
PA-28 student pilot reported landing in a field after having directional control difficulties while attempting a practice short field takeoff.
Air carrier Pilots reported they flew through a crossing altitude and received an altitude alert from ATC.
Air carrier flight crew reported Hydraulic System failure and diversion to a nearby suitable airport.
Flight Instructor reported landing in a field after engine trouble precluded returning to departure airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict during taxi in at ORD airport with a Heavy aircraft. Captain stated ATC was extremely busy and that gate related communications issues contributed to the event.

A pilot departing O61 non-towered airport reported they observed an arriving aircraft head on to them as they were taking off and had to turn to avoid them. The reporter thought they heard the aircraft report inbound to the opposite direction runway they departed from.
CRJ-900 air carrier crew reported an engine oil malfunction during cruise flight. The crew diverted to an alternate airport and landed and a hydraulic leak was discovered as well.
Air carrier pilot reported a CFTT event during arrival to MROC airport resulted when ATC vectored the flight toward terrain.
B737 MAX Flight Attendant reported confusion over seat pocket safety cards being the correct type or not for the aircraft.
Pilot reported an NMAC on short final with an aircraft overtaking them at a towered airport.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported encountering strong wake turbulence descending into MDW in trail of another air carrier aircraft.
B737 Flight Crew reported flaps failed to retract after takeoff which resulted in return to departure airport.
A321 Captain reported a pressurization malfunction during a cruise climb. The Captain requested a lower altitude and continued to destination.
Bonanza 35 pilot reported performing a manual landing gear extension. The pilot communicated with ATC to verify landing gear extension and landed safely.

Air carrier flight crew reported confusion at a taxiway intersection based upon a mismatch between what was visually seen and the chart depiction; exacerbated by a lack of signage. Ground Control helped provide assistance and the flight continued taxiing to the runway.
A321 Captain reported a Green Hydraulic System HYD G RSVR LO LVL ECAM message on departure. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at departure airport.
B767 FO reported a track deviation occurred in their attempts to exit wake turbulence from the preceding A380 on their oceanic route.
B757 flight crew reported during initial climb they got an EICAS message for a leading-edge slat asymmetry during flap retraction. They decided to return to the airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a large bump on the runway during the takeoff roll causing a jolt and startle effect. The airport NOTAMs did not identify this specific runway surface issue.
B737 Captain reported a bounced landing that resulted in a tail strike. The aircraft tail skid was damaged and a maintenance inspection was requested.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the Hazmat documents for the arriving COMAT shipment incorrectly coded the contents of the shipment.
Flight Instructors flying PA-28 aircraft on training flight reported engine malfunction in flight.
Air carrier Captain reported executing a low altitude go-around at LGA when another aircraft taxied onto their landing runway.

Flight crew reported a right engine driven hydraulic pump EICAS message on take off roll and performed a rejected take off. The flight crew returned to the gate and swapped aircraft to complete the flight.
ZLC Center Controller reported that an aircraft changed radio frequency as well as deviated from the approach course and approach altitude before being given ATC approval.

B-737-800 Captain reported a hydraulic malfunction during cruise flight. The flight continued to the destination and landed but the main gear tires failed during the landing and the aircraft required a tire change on the runway.
Ramp worker reported during pushback a vehicle crossed through the path of the push and the tug operator immediately applied brakes to avoid a collision.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a terrain alert during descent on visual approach. Captain climbed to safe altitude and continued approach.
A Flight Instructor maneuvering in a VFR practice area reported a NMAC with another aircraft that turned into their path.
Pilot reported a near mid air collision while in the traffic pattern with other aircraft which passed underneath by 75 feet.

CRJ-200 Captain reported right thrust reverser deployed on takeoff with simultaneous right side navigation system failure. The flight crew performed an air turnback and precautionary landing at departure airport.
C172 flight crew reported NMAC on approach.

B757 Captain reported receiving a flap asymmetry caution when selecting flaps 1 while configuring for an approach. The flight crew received vectors to reenter the approach and performed a safe landing at destination airport.

Pilot departing SNA where the Tower was closed reported they began to enter the runway with another aircraft on short final which had not reported its positions properly. The aircraft on short final advised the reporter to stop taxiing.
Learjet 36 First Officer reported a malfunction of the nose wheel steering during the landing rollout resulted in wing contact with a taxi light.

GA pilot reported they lost communications with ATC while on approach to VPZ in SBN TRACON airspace. The reporter was issued a new frequency to re-establish communications and was advised by ATC that communications loss in this area is a known problem.
Air carrier First Officer reported that while on approach; an aircraft taxing on the runway missed their assigned exit taxiway resulting in an ATC directed go around.

PA28R pilot reported trim malfunctioned during climbout resulting in strong nose-down pitch. Pilot returned to departure airport where maintenance inspection found trim control incorrectly replaced during aircraft annual inspection.
C560XL pilot reported the aircraft incurred damage to the rudder damage from jet blast from BD-700 aircraft during taxi out.
A TRACON Controller reported they forgot the route they had issued to an aircraft would cause it to descend below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Captain reported receiving a terrain alert during climb out. Flight crew continued climb via the departure.
EMB-505 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach into SLC.
BE76 Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a taxiway excursion due to brake failure.
A 757-200 pilot reported a cabin pressure warning light; while leveling at cruise altitude; resulting in an immediate descent and return to the departure airport.
Cessna 172 student pilot reported loss of directional control possibly related to brake problems on landing roll that resulted in a runway excursion.
Air carrier Captain reported a CFTT event while on the GPS Y Runway 24 approach to ROA.
Air carrier First Officer reported engine control malfunction inflight.
Citation X Captain reported generator and bus failures on approach. The Flight Crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
B737 NG flight crew reported they failed to get the Final DG Summary prior to brake release. The flight crew requested and received the Final DG Summary and notified Dispatch prior to pushback.
PA-31 pilot reported they taxied off the taxiway and into a snow berm due to flat light conditions at the airport.

Two Cessna aircraft pilots reported a NMAC while in the traffic pattern at non-towered airport. The instructor; with trainee; and other pilot were practicing approaches to the same runway and did not hear any communication from each other. Both pilots reported taking evasive action.
C172 pilot reported during landing roll the aircraft veered to the left resulting in runway excursion. Pilot taxied to ramp and then flew back to departure airport.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported a Critical Ground conflict during takeoff roll and executing an uneventful rejected takeoff due to a taxiing aircraft crossing the runway.
DA40 instructor pilot reported directing the student to climb to avoid a helicopter that was cleared by Tower into the traffic pattern at the same altitude.
Maintenance technicians reported incomplete information in aircraft engine repair job cards as well as a breakdown in mechanic communication between shifts.

SR22 pilot reported losing most avionics during descent to destination airport.  Due to the failure of radio; transponder and electric power; the pilot decided to divert and in the process of trying to remain below Class B airspace; may have entered a restricted area airspace.
Low wing aircraft Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action to avoid traffic resulted in a NMAC event.
Air Carrier Captain reported an engine fire indication after takeoff and returned to the departure airport. After an AFR inspection the aircraft returned to the gate.
CE-680 pilot reported safely returning to departure airport after experiencing smoke in the cockpit and a baggage fire warning; possibly related to a failed seal on an air conditioning pack.
Learjet 75 Captain reported during the initial climb suddenly heard a loud clunking noise and observed the left side windshield cracked. They notified ATC and returned to the airport.
A student pilot in a C172 reported a complete electrical failure and divert to a nearby airport.

TPA TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft was crossing the localizer while an IFR aircraft was on the localizer and had the IFR aircraft maintain altitude to avoid an NMAC. The reporter noted that VFR aircraft routinely traverse the localizer at the glide path altitude for the Runway 18 localizer at PIE Airport.
B737-800 Captain reported an air turn back after receiving an IAS DISAGREE on both PFD's.
B737 First Officer reported the landing gear failed to retract after departure but was able to successfully raise the landing gear after leveling off and following the QRH.
A-300 Captain reported Hazmat shipment incorrectly loaded. Flight crew had Hazmat cargo reconfigured in accordance to Hazmat regulations prior to departure.

HPN Local Controller reported RA response with non communicating traffic resulted in an low altitude alert and CFTT event. Controller also reported the traffic volume exceeds requirements for Class C which will prevent these incidents.
B777-300 First Officer reported the Captain opened the aircraft door with the girt bar attached; escape slide did not deploy. Maintenance was called.
Air Carrier Captain taxiing for departure from PHL reported confusing taxiway signage.

Air carrier pilot reported ATL ATC informed them they failed to hold short at a taxiway intersection; but there were no chart notes indicating the hold short and ATC did not issue hold short instructions.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during climb which dissipated in a matter of minutes. The fumes returned during descent and the flight diverted to a suitable airport for maintenance support.
B777 flight crew reported both right engine fire detection loops failed so they diverted to the nearest airport.

C172 flight instructor reported the student only pressed the left rudder during rudder use training resulting in a momentary loss of aircraft control.  The aircraft struck a taxi light before the instructor took over and corrected the inputs.
Pilot reported observing an aircraft breaking off a practice approach turn into the path of an aircraft on downwind for an intersecting runway at a non-towered airport.
After parking the Embraer 170 aircraft in hangar; air carrier technicians reported opening the door to exit the aircraft and the escape slide deployed.
B-737 First Officer reported a loss of aircraft control after encountering severe turbulence while at cruise.  Crew regained control and landed normally.
A319 flight crew member reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing the failure of #2 engine on takeoff.
BOI TRACON Controller reported an arrival cleared for an ILS approach to a satellite airport turned and descended prior to the IAF and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Pilot reported issues related to flying into PTKK Chuuk International Airport; Weno Island; Micronesia; at night.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving a 'Return to Gate' message during departure taxi to correct a loading configuration error. After conferring with Dispatch and clarifying that no dangerous goods notification was required; the dry ice cargo was configured correctly and the flight departed safely.

Air carrier Captain reported having a near miss with hot air balloons that were along the instrument approach corridor and suggested prohibiting the operation of hot air balloons around established and active arrival paths at SBGR Airport.
Air carrier ground personnel reported TSA agent discovered an unauthorized hoverboard in passenger bag during security screening. Gate agent notified that the item was removed from their baggage.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft deviating for weather flew at an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway excursion while taxiing for departure and returned to gate for aircraft check. Flight crew stated the taxiway markings do not allow for adequate room to maneuver and recommended a taxi line be moved to provide more clearance.
C182 pilot reported they received a terrain warning while entering the pattern at a non towered airport and after landing discovered their altimeter was off by 450 ft.
Air carrier flight crew reported a loss of directional control during landing rollout which required maximum brake effort by both pilots to avoid a runway excursion.
Air Carrier flight crew flying A319 aircraft reported Flap Control system malfunction on final approach.
B737 Captain reported pressurization pack failure resulted in critical equipment issue and expeditious handling to a safe altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported two separate flammable liquid containers were loaded in different cargo bins in an aircraft that can only fight one fire at a time. Shortly thereafter the Captain received an ACARS message that the entire Hazmat cargo was removed prior to departure.
PA28 pilot reported departing on the wrong frequency resulting in a NMAC with another aircraft that was landing in the opposite direction.
Air carrier Captain reported of being fatigued by company schedule.
Air carrier flight crew reported an aircraft loss of control during push back procedure.
Air carrier First Officer reported the chocks were removed with no communication and as a result the aircraft; began to roll and was damaged.

Pilot reported getting a terrain alert after not noticing the aircraft was not on glideslope. Reporter stated they were focused on the poor weather conditions and did not monitor the approach progress.

Air carrier First Officer reported while turning onto a taxiway the aircraft wheels departed the paved surface and became stuck on the grass. ATC was notified and the aircraft was towed out of the grass.

Flight Instructor with student reported observing a NMAC between two other aircraft in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The Instructor stated the aircraft that caused the NMAC later overflew the instructor's aircraft on final approach and then subsequently cut off the aircraft that was involved in the first NMAC.

Air Carrier Pilot reported a critical ground conflict with a service van at a service road crossing. The van stopped but blocked the taxiway. The pilot stated the van needed to move first before taxi could continue.
GA pilot reported a momentary loss of aircraft control while on the RNAV 28 approach to C29 airport in IMC conditions.
General aviation instructor reported a propeller governor failure resulting in shutdown of one of the engines during a training flight. The instructor landed safely at the airport.
TRACON Controller reported turning a departing aircraft below the minimum vectoring altitude to prevent a conflict with an unidentified SFRA aircraft.
B737-800 Captain reported executing a rejected takeoff due to airspeed disagree message on primary flight display. Captain returned to gate for maintenance and brake cooling.
Air carrier pilot reported an unstabilized approach.
King Air 90 pilot reported encountering inflight icing at FL250 that resulted in temporary loss of aircraft control.
Air Traffic Controller reported the interior working area in the BHM Control Tower has a configuration that blocks the Ground Controller's view of the airport movement areas.

Air carrier Captain reported a taxi chart discrepancy at LAX identifying a specific intersection on the taxi chart in the EFB. The performance data identified the intersection but the Captain indicated the taxiway intersection was not identified on the charts.
Ramp personnel reported during pushback the aircraft rolled back and then forward over the towbar. Aircraft was towed back to gate for Maintenance inspection for possible damage.
PA--28 Flight Instructor reported an emergency air return immediately after takeoff due to Carbon Monoxide poisoning resulting in a taxiway landing.
A321 flight crew reported a high speed rejected takeoff due to an engine failure.
Technician reported engines stored in hangar are not secured or protected from damage or FOD.
A flight Instructor reported a NMAC while turning base leg at a non towered airport.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported flight control anomalies were noted during climb that were related to a malfunctioning rudder trim switch. The flight continued to destination where a safe landing was performed.
C206 pilot reported they descended below the ATC assigned altitude while on a vector to intercept the final approach course.

C550 flight crew reported excessive control wheel input was required to stop the aircraft from rolling off course. Aileron trim was ineffective. The flight crew elected to return to the departure airport.
TRACON Controller reported keeping an aircraft below the MVA due to icing conditions.
A320 Captain reported thrust reverse unlock in flight resulted in a return to the departure airport.

Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming and spoofing during cruise in a known area or reported GPS interference. The GPS signal was restored after transitioning the area and the flight continued to destination.
B737 First Officer reported a late flare on landing leading to a hard landing.
PA28 student pilot reported misjudged distance of conflict traffic resulted in evasive action and an NMAC.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a fume event during cruise and executed a diversion; receiving a TCAS RA during diversion which was handled with no issues.

B737-800 flight crew reported the nose of the aircraft began to lift off the ground while parked at the gate when the aircraft was unloaded from the front with a heavy load of aft cargo and a strong tail wind.
B757 pilot reported left engine EPR issue in flight. Flight crew returned to departure airport.
Flight instructor reported an NMAC during visual approach to SMD non-towered airport.
EMB-145 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence in trail of a heavy jet on approach to PHL.
Air Carrier pilot reported unclear ATIS information and a lack of markings resulted in a taxiway incursion at SJU.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a taxiway deviation. The FO stated the chart listed a routing different than as assigned by ATC and suggested a notation for clarity.
ABQ TRACON Controller reported they did not detect an aircraft read back the wrong altitude assignment resulting in the aircraft descending below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A flight instructor conducting an instrument approach to MQY airport reported flight school aircraft from an adjacent airport flew into potential conflict with their approach course. The reporter had an NMAC with one of the aircraft requiring them to take evasive action.

B787 Captain reported an Auto Throttle failure and the associated Indication and Warning Systems allowed the aircraft's airspeed to become dangerously low. The flight crew took immediate action upon recognizing the threat and was able to continue the approach to landing at the destination airport.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss after takeoff with an aircraft landing opposite direction on the same runway; during a training flight at a non-towered airport. The instructor performed a side step maneuver to avoid the aircraft approaching head-on.
SR22 pilot reported engine roughness during cruise. Pilot attempted troubleshooting; returned to departure airport and landed safely.
CL-30 flight crew reported fumes from galley area during cruise. Flight crew returned to the departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier pilot reported a near midair collision during descent with an UAS.

Air Carrier Captain reported a flight control malfunction shorty after takeoff which cause the flight controls to be extremely stiff. Captain was able to overcome the stiff controls to complete a safe landing.
PA-44 instructor pilot reported loss of right engine shortly after takeoff on a training flight. Aircraft landed safely after inflight shutdown.
Air carrier Captain reported ATC issued a low altitude alert warning while the Captain was executing an ILS circle-to-land approach.
Pilot reported off airport landing due to engine failure.

A319 First Officer reported a pneumatic hot air valve malfunction during cruise; causing excessive temperatures in the cockpit and cabin. The crew continued to destination and landed; where maintenance determined the valve was deferred in the incorrect position.
EC145 pilot reported encountering issues with the onboard weather radar system and suggests these issues are common within the entire fleet.

Air carrier flight crew reported a momentary loss of aircraft control and stick shaker alert while maneuvering on a visual approach. After the stick shaker activated; the Captain took evasive action retracting the speed brake which stopped the stick shaker and a normal stabilized approach was completed.

EMB-170 flight crew reported wind shear conditions on final approach and on the go-around resulting in a momentary loss of aircraft control; and altitude deviation.  The flight crew landed safely on the second approach.
B737-800 flight crew reported recurring fuel imbalance during cruise. After landing flight crew observed puddles of fuel on ground and flight attendants reported strong fuel fumes in cabin.
C172 pilot reported engine power loss during takeoff resulted in a rejected takeoff. The pilot taxied back to the ramp and turned the aircraft over to Maintenance.

B777 First Officer reported a problem with the APU at top of climb which was running to comply with an inoperative engine generator per MEL necessitating it's shutdown. Crew shut down APU and returned to departure airport.
Pilot reported loss of all Pitot/Static air systems in IMC; requested immediate assistance and diverted to a precautionary landing.
GA pilot reported filing an IFR flight plan for a flight in IMC. The flight was completed as planned. The pilot realized later; the Obstacle Departure Procedure should have been flown as published.
Technicians reported that an aircraft anti skid module was improperly placed on MEL and dispatched for service.
General aviation pilot reported they did not follow ATC departure instructions resulting in failure to meet obstacle clearance requirements. Pilot corrected and continued on course.

Student pilot reported while making a visual approach the tower controller gave the instruction to go-around; then changed to side step left then go-around; in an urgent tone. Later the student learned a pilot; who had lost communication; had been cleared to land via mobile phone.

B767-300 flight crew reported aircraft was dispatched with both engine EECs placed on MEL. This caused controllability issues due to the auto throttles not being available during flight in heavy weather.

A Ramp Wing Walker reported one of the wing walkers noticed two aircraft taxiing toward the same spot they were pushing back an aircraft to and instructed the tug driver to pull the aircraft back into the gate area.

First Officer reported a taxi deviation at BFI which resulted in taxiing on a taxiway that was not suitable for the aircraft's wingspan. The First Officer indicated there was no advisory from ATC regarding wingspan restrictions.

Air carrier flight crew reported an inadvertent runway incursion was the result of confusion regarding the hold short lines on Taxiway A for Runway 7 at Montego Bay/Sangster International Airport. The lines had been painted over with black paint and are more prominent than the current hold lines.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a motor failure that caused their UAS to crash. Reporter stated no property damage or injury occurred.
B737-800 flight crew reported receiving low altitude and glide slope warnings during approach. Flight crew climbed back to correct altitude and continued approach to landing.
PA-28 pilot reported a high amperage indication. During the descent and approach; in VFR conditions; the pilot reported a terrain warning.
B737 Captain reported the Slat/Flap Amber Light Illuminated on approach. The flight crew performed a go-around and landed at destination airport.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported the ASOS at TIST is malfunctioning and the airport does not have a legal weather reporting system.
General aviation pilot reported an NMAC while landing at a non-towered airport when another aircraft not communicating on CTAF took off opposite direction from the same runway.
Air carrier pilot reported ORD ILS 09C glideslope oscillated when a taxiing aircraft crossed the runway at Taxiway Z resulting in them flying below the glidepath.
B777-300 pilot reported an aft cargo door light illuminated during climbout. Flight returned safely to departure airport.

A319 Captain reported while deviating around thunderstorms at cruise altitude; the aircraft was stuck by lightening resulting in the failure of the onboard weather radar. The Captain proceeded away from the area of thunderstorms; then diverted to an alternate airport.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft went NORDO and flew into a higher MVA.
C206 pilot reported the engine quit during final approach.  The pilot was able to restart the engine following POH procedures and successfully landed the aircraft.
Medium Transport Crew reported conflicting departure instructions issued by ATC verses the MDW3 SID narrative; resulted in a deviation.
ATR-72 First Officer and Dispatcher reported maintenance failed to follow proper fueling procedure resulting in engine flame out on landing.
Aircraft maintenance technician reported maintenance on engine prior to inflight engine malfunction.

Flight crew reported during flight they experienced several equipment issues. While on descent deploying spoilers; aircraft made a rapid inadvertent roll. The flight crew regained control of the aircraft and landed safely.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during approach and landing roll at LVJ airport.  The pilot stated the aircraft's radio was disabled which contributed to the event.
Captain reported noticing the Captain's side Attitude Indicator failed after take off. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Reporter stated aircraft brakes were not set causing it to roll back 3-4 feet.
