A-320 air carrier crew reported an engine compressor stall during cruise flight prior to descent on a training flight. The crew shutdown the engine and landed at the destination.

A300 Captain reported communications issues during take off with FADEC MEL that required the Engines to be in N1 Mode. The #1 Engine lagged significantly behind and a rejected take off was made. The flight crew returned to the Gate for Maintenance.
Bellanca Cruisemaster pilot reported engine power loss during cruise. Pilot performed a power off landing at an airport.

B787 Captain reported First Officer display screens displayed AHRU R status message followed by EFIS/DISP Panel R; and WEATHER RADAR SYS L fault messages during cruise. Flight returned to departure airport.

General aviation student pilot reported a traffic conflict with a military aircraft when turning final on a visual approach at a non-towered airport during a training flight. The military aircraft position and performance did not allow time for an evasive maneuver to avoid the conflict.
Air carrier pilot reported encountering severe turbulence resulting in an injury to a Flight Attendant by a dislodged fire extinguisher.
A TRACON Controller reported a NMAC between an aircraft on an approach and an unidentified VFR aircraft flying through the final approach course.
B767 flight crew reported experiencing partial loss of airspeed indication and flight director along with other EICAS messages during departure. Flight crew returned to departure airport.
EMB-145 air carrier flight crew reported unsafe gear indication. Landing gear held for a successful landing.
Air Carrier Safety personnel reported undeclared Dry Ice shipment at destination where it was discovered. Hazmat cargo of Dry Ice was not listed on Departure Staging Guide at departure airport.

Both a Tower and a TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft lost all its navigation capabilities resulting in course and altitude deviations. One reporter stated this has been a recurring issue with this aircraft.
B737 NG flight crew reported an inflight upset after encountering wake turbulence on descent into MIA from a preceding B777. Aircraft control was regained and the flight landed safely.
Air Traffic Control Instructor reported developmental incorrectly issued control instructions which resulted in MVA violation and a CFTT event.
Flight Crew reported an air turn back after birds were ingested by the right engine on takeoff.
TRACON Controllers reported an aircraft dropped 3;500 ft. in a matter of seconds due to wind and went below the MVA.
C402 flight crew was notified of white smoke coming from the engine by ATC during their landing. The Captain exited the runway onto a taxiway; shut down the aircraft; requested fire trucks.
King Air C90 pilot reported they were fast on their approach on short final and porpoised the landing resulting in a prop strike.

Cessna 182 pilot reported an engine malfunction during cruise; the pilot returned to the departure airport and landed. The pilot discovered the propeller control cable had separated from the propeller governor.
Air carrier Captain reported an apparent undocumented transport of Hazmat cargo. A NOTOC was provided to the Captain during pre-flight but at push-back the push crew indicated not loading anything.

A320 First Officer reported encountering navigation system failures after departure that would preclude RVSM operations and there was insufficient fuel to complete the flight. The flight crew diverted to another airport and made a normal landing.

B737 Captain reported stopping the aircraft during a taxi due to uncertainty about wing clearance from a helicopter's moving rotor blades. Clarification was requested with Ground Control; but ATC was also unsure. The helicopter pilot then used the rotor brake to stop the rotors and it was then that the reporter determined clearance to be good enough to continue the taxi.

Air carrier flight crew reported a number 2 engine fire warning message during arrival taxi. Flight crew completed the QRH procedures and requested Airport Rescue and Fire Fighting assistance who followed aircraft to parking since fire message had extinguished.

B757 flight crew reported smoke was detected in the cabin and a lavatory smoke caution illuminated shortly after takeoff. One of the packs were then turned off; which dissipated the smoke. The flight crew proceeded to perform an overweight landing and air turnback.

Air carrier First Officer reported a violation notification of two scooter transport well after the flight completion. The First Officer responded that scooter 1 was transported in compliance with the FOM procedures while the second scooter was loaded without flight crew notification.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported briefly flying above 400 feet AGL due to high winds.
B787 Technician reported seeing deterioration of the panel seals on numerous aircraft and suggested there be an investigation into the cause of the problem.
B737 flight crew reported hydraulic system pressure and quantity fluctuations at cruise and elected to continue to destination airport to make a precautionary landing.
B737 NG Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after Number 1 engine was shut down due to a suspected fuel leak.
Flight Instructor on training flight reported NMAC with a helicopter during takeoff roll.

B737-700 Captain reported an accumulation of ice on the fan blades of both engines required the use of unfamiliar de-icing procedures. Shortly after takeoff the crew noticed engine vibrations that stopped after leaving icing conditions.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported a hydraulic system malfunction at cruise. The flight continued to the destination; landed safely; and was towed off the runway to the gate.
Cessna 421 pilot reported a visual engine oil leak during climb on one of the engines. The pilot returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Corporate jet Captain reported there is an altitude discrepancy on the STAR FROGZ3 arrival into MIA. The Captain stated the chart is also confusing with too many overlapping courses.

Air carrier pilot reported during cruise flight and while on break the aircraft experienced an Integrated Drive Generator message resulting in the flight crew returning to the departure airport without coordination with the Relief Pilots on break.
B737-800 pilot reported an aircraft refusal during flight planning when assigned to fly a ferry flight to reposition the aircraft with a known engine defect.
First Officer reported receiving a Master Caution with a Y ENG 2 PUMP LO PR ECAM Message in cruise. The flight crew elected to divert.
PA-28 student pilot reported engine roughness and partial power loss during cruise. Pilot diverted and landed safely.
A320 First Officer reported Hydraulic Failure and return to departure airport.
Technician reported confusion in reference data during return to service maintenance on B737 MAX stored aircraft.
Helicopter Single Pilot reported a near miss with a drone at 1;200 ft.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the Dangerous Goods shipment that was supposed to held off was not.
Ground personnel reported that while hooking up carts that one cart broke away and was stopped from hitting the plane by a wing walker.
A single pilot reported a Near Mid-Air Collision (NMAC) with another aircraft entering the landing pattern. Sun position reportedly inhibited early visual contact.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a wheelchair lithium battery was incorrectly transported in cargo compartment instead of aircraft cabin.

B737 Captain reported that the First Officer became distracted while hand flying the aircraft and exceeded the allowable bank angle resulting in the bank angle alert to sound. Flight crew corrected back to normal bank.
C172 pilot reported loss of engine power and requested priority handling to expedite arrival at destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the taxiway lines are faded and confusing from taxiway N up to and including taxiway LL at ORD Airport.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft during climb from a non-towered airport in visual conditions on a training flight. The pilot and the instructor were distracted with the avionics; then the pilot maneuvered to avoid the traffic conflict with two aircraft.

First Officer reported cabin pressure was uncontrollable in flight. The flight crew reset the Cabin Pressure Controllers to manual and regained control; then continued to destination at a lower altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported the need to abruptly stop taxiing to avoid a collision with another aircraft entering their taxiway.
Air carrier First Officer reported that during single engine taxi on overpass taxiway-bridge; ice contamination resulted in aircraft sliding.
General aviation pilot reported flight toward terrain while maneuvering during cruise.
Air carrier flight crew reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while on arrival. Flight crew reported they also had an airborne conflict with another aircraft and responded to a RA commanded climb.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported one of the assigned aircraft reported a NMAC and the pilot crew would submit the necessary reports.
Air Carrier flight crew reported inadvertently failed to comply with the Dry Ice handling requirements while transporting Dry Ice. Flight continued to destination safely.
A Bonanza 35 Pilot reported engine power loss on initial departure climb and later identified an issue with the fuel pump.
Cessna Citation V pilot reported two aircraft stalls due to icing conditions while descending for an approach. The pilot recovered the aircraft and landed.

BE-40 Captain reported a Critical ground conflict after an inadvertent runway incursion resulting in a opposite direction landing aircraft to executed a go-around. Airport Tower had recently closed and flight crew had only monitored CTAF for a short time and had not heard any position radio reports or made any position reports.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain had to abruptly apply the brakes to avoid a collision with a truck on Taxiway Charlie at LAX airport.
B737 Captain reported a HAZMAT documentation error consisting of a confusing entry. The error was corrected prior to gate departure.
Air carrier pilot reported the loss of both GPS signals over northern New Mexico that according to ATC; was due to GPS jamming from military activity south of ABQ.

PIAT (Light Sport Aircraft) pilot reported the aircraft became airborne for 1/2 a second after touchdown due to possible gusts resulting in a blown front tire; loss of aircraft control; and a near runway excursion.
Pilot reported experiencing momentary engine roughness and propeller overspeed after takeoff. The Flight Instructor immediately assumed the controls and returned to the departure airport.
Pilot reported during takeoff they received an 'inadvertent' stall warning and stick shaker that persisted throughout the climb and returned to the airport.

Air taxi pilot reported a near miss after takeoff in a climbing turn from a tower controlled airport in VMC conditions. The pilot followed the TCAS guidance; then once clear of the traffic; continued the flight.
Center Controller reported descending an aircraft into another Controller's airspace; thinking the D-Side had pointed the aircraft out.
Pilot reported taking evasive action entering the traffic pattern to avoid a departing aircraft resulted in an NMAC event.

Pilot reported a CFTT event during a visual approach in marginal weather conditions when the co-pilot turned the wrong way on a published missed approach procedure; lost 1;000 feet of altitude; and joined the traffic pattern while flying in and out of clouds.
Captain reported long delay due to fuel imbalance and thrust reversers inhibited caused by human factors during return to service from long term storage.

Tower and Ground Controllers stated that coordination confusion during training resulted in evasive action and a critical ground conflict event. Controllers also state that management failed to create a mandatory occurrence report.
B737 Captain reported avoiding an inadvertent escape slide deployment when gate agent did not wait for an all clear signal and attempted to open the main cabin door upon arrival.

Air carrier Captain reported an RA on final approach to SFO Runway 28L due to an encroaching aircraft on approach to the parallel runway. The flight crew made a missed approach and was vectored for another approach to landing.

C150 Flight Instructor reported loss of the propeller spinner after rotation which caused banging; vibration and an apparent partial power loss. Instructor returned to departure airport and found minor damage to the propeller and cowling.

GA Flight Instructor reported a conflict during landing at non-towered JWG airport. Reporter stated an aircraft had taxied on to their same landing runway requiring a go-around right after touching down.

B787 First Officer reported a temporary loss of aircraft control during departure climb in a light aircraft in which CRM miscues resulted in a high workload situation. The crew worked together to regain control safely.
B767 Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after receiving a R ENG FUEL FILTER EICAS message.
CRJ900 pilot reported wing tip made contact with a fuel truck during taxi into the gate.
Recreational/hobbyist UAS pilot reported controllability issues flying their UAS and believes the GPS module or remote controller are possible causes.
Air carrier flight crew reported a water leak in the lavatory in cruise flight.
Gulfstream IV Captain reported the loss of control of the cabin pressurization. The pilots operated the system in manual and returned to the origin airport.
J3 Instructor reported that contaminated fuel resulted in a loss of engine power followed by a forced landing.
Air carrier pilot reported a pressurization problem in cruise flight. The flight crew returned to departure airport.
Amphibious Pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing resulting in striking runway lights. Aircraft taxied to parking with differential braking.
A Tower Controller reported an aircraft overshot the final approach courses on their base turn resulting in an NMAC with a helicopter in the pattern.
A330 Loadmaster reported a PMC container was not marked with the chemical it was carrying and also placed next to another PMC container that contained chemicals it was not compatible with.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft while in cruise flight and took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Citation X (C750) Flight crew reported no takeoff EICAS warning during takeoff roll. Aborted takeoff and discovered 'Engine Sync' on from previous approach.
ZLC Controller reported aircraft descended below assigned altitude and MVA; flying an approach not cleared for; resulted in a CFTT event.
B737 Captain reported a rejected takeoff at 140 knots after receiving a Takeoff Configuration warning.

Air Carrier flight crew reported being notified that the NOTOC was not signed by not signed by the Captain on a previous flight. The flight crew was not informed of Hazmat on board and not provided with a NOTOC.

CRJ-200 Captain reported a Trailing Edge Flap failure on approach. The flight crew executed a go-around and ran the check lists and QRH procedures. The flight crew reentered the approach and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.

B777 Captain reported a center fuel boost pump failure at the gate. After reviewing the MEL and other documents; it was discovered that other procedures and checks were required to safely dispatch the aircraft.
Air Carrier B737-800 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence 10 miles in trail of another B737 at FL340 resulting in an inflight upset.

Air carrier Captain reported a traffic conflict with a general aviation aircraft both under ATC control; while in a descent on a published arrival to PHL. The Captain responded to a TCAS RA and maneuvered to avoid the traffic conflict.; "Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a balloon at FL410 in ZHU airspace."
DA40 flight instructor reported a runway excursion during training with student pilot. Reportedly; the student applied the brakes to aggressively during a simulated avionics failure on takeoff roll.

EMB-175 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence; resulting in a momentary loss of aircraft control and an altitude deviation of 600 ft. After exiting the turbulent conditions; the flight continued to the destination airport; with only a minor injury sustained by a Flight Attendant.
Captain reported a high workload led to the flight crew missing the restoration of engine bleeds; leading to a pressurization issue until it was recognized.
Air carrier flight crew reported critical ground conflict during taxi out for departure; requiring braking to avoid a collision.

Air carrier First Officer reported on a night visual approach being high and fast resulting in overshooting the final approach course and ATC issuing a low altitude alert. The flight landed safely at the airport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the radio altimeters began to malfunction while on initial approach to PHX. The pilots began to receive RA and EFIS COMP MON caution messages.
Pilot reported the stabilizer trim malfunctioned after takeoff; requiring great physical strength to control aircraft pitch. Pilot performed a successful diversion to a landing.
Technician reported replacing fan blades on the incorrect engine of a B777.
C172 instructor reported electing to take control of aircraft and return to the airport after they had a trim malfunction.
Small aircraft Flight Instructor reported a rough running engine during initial climb and returned to safe landing at departure airport.

Air Traffic Controller reported receiving a request from an airliner to side step an area of wake vortex. The controller granted the request and realized they failed to communicate the type of aircraft the airliner was following.

Air carrier First Officer reported a Flight Attendant notified the flight crew that a passenger's electronic charging device had become enlarged during cruise. The device was placed in a thermal containment bag until destination arrival with no further issues.

Flight Attendant crew reported a Self Balancing (Hover Board) device in the overhead during boarding. The device is not permitted per company policy and the passenger chose to deplane with the device.
Cessna 421 pilot reported an engine malfunction during climb on a IFR flight. The pilot shut down the malfunctioning engine and returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Aircraft technician reported replacing an igniter on a CRJ-200 aircraft. The technician later found out that the engine caught fire and the igniter was not installed on the engine.
A light sport experimental pilot reported an unstable approach and long landing resulted in a runway excursion and tail strike.
Embraer Phenom Captain reported encountering severe wake turbulence on descent into SLC in trail of an air carrier aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPS malfunction at 34000 ft. in the vicinity of IAD. Dispatch informed the flight crew there were system-wide outages for both Boeing and Airbus aircraft.

Fractional flight crew reported visually misidentifying the destination airport and receiving a caution obstacle warning due to flying by a tower while on visual approach. Flight crew later received a warning they were below glide slope but continued the approach to landing.
An Air Carrier pilot reported descending below the glideslope on short final and received a Low Altitude Alert from the Tower.

Flight Crew reported stabilizer trim failure at takeoff resulted in fuel jettison with failure of the jettison system that led to holding for fuel burn to below max landing weight and a return to the departure airport.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported a near miss with a UAS during cruise. Evasive maneuvers were taken and ATC was informed.

CL600 First Officer reported loss of oil pressure on #2 engine at cruise. The flight crew elected to perform an inflight shut down of the affected engine. Priority handling was requested and the flight crew performed a precautionary landing at destination airport.
ERJ-170 Maintenance Technician reported flap failure resulting from non-compliance with maintenance procedure.
Pilot reported NMAC during sightseeing flight.
An Instructor Pilot and student reported a prop strike resulting from a hard landing.
C172 pilot reported they suffered power loss immediately after takeoff. Pilot immediately landed and rolled off the end of the runway.
B737 pilot reported while in cruise flight experiencing engine oil quantity decreasing resulting in the flight diverting to an alternate airport.

Air carrier Captain reported a violent temporary shimmy during landing deceleration with no apparent aircraft damage. During post flight inspection it was discovered that two damaged flap linkages caused the issue resulting in the aircraft being removed from service.
EMB 505 Flight Crew reported a GPWS Warning on descent for landing.
B767 flight crew reported receiving a gear disagree message and the landing gear would not retract after takeoff. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer advanced thrust levers to TOGA without releasing the parking brake.
Pilot not flying reported the pilot flying an PA-28 aircraft landed long and could not apply adequate braking to stop resulting in a runway excursion and hitting a light.

Air carrier First Officer reported having to go-around/missed approach several times due to weather at MDW airport.  When they finally landed the crew experienced some sliding and skidding due to braking action being 'poor' contrary to the reported 'good braking action.'

Falcon 900 pilot reported that a fuel computer failed followed by an out of trim advisory message. They disengaged the autopilot to respond to the problems and encountered turbulence that caused an altitude excursion.

Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control in cruise during severe turbulence encounter resulting in significant altitude and airspeed deviations. Flight crew regained control and continued flight.
A320 Captain reported that during departure; the primary display and navigation display lost all data and an ADR fault ECAM was displayed. The flight crew made a return to the departure airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported they taxied past Taxiway B14 at night in the rain which is the last taxiway to access PHX Runway 26 and entered a maintenance ramp. The flight crew reported inadequate lighting; ambiguous taxi instructions and no advisory on the chart contributed to the event.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway excursion and loss of control while taxiing on a slippery taxiway surface as directed by ATC. The Captain shut down an engine and requested tow to the gate.
CL-605 flight crew reported the Cabin Light Controller failed and began to emit sparks and smoke. The flight crew descended and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported a NMAC at a non-towered airport. No evasive action was taken.

Air Carrier Captain reported a strong toxic odor emanating from a container prior to gate departure. Since no Hazmat was listed on the Load Manifest the Captain requested the container be removed. After discussing with the Load Supervisor the container was unloaded and aircraft departed safely.
SR20 student pilot reported engine power fluctuations on takeoff roll resulted in rejected takeoff.
PA-23 pilot reported an engine malfunction on one engine after takeoff from a non-towered airport. The pilot returned to the departure airport and landed.
BE36 pilot reported the cockpit door opened inflight resulting in a return to the departure airport.
A TRACON Controller reported a small aircraft on approach in IMC repeatedly deviated from their course and descended below assigned and published altitudes flying below Minimum Vectoring Altitudes.
EMB-175 Captain reported the aircraft had a abnormal start; and rolled uncontrolled while parking at the gate due to their failure to set the parking brake.
Air carrier Captain reported while taxing to the gate encountering two vehicles resulting in the air crew taking evasive action twice to avoid a collision with the vehicles.

Air carrier Ramp Agent reported being unable to close out and contacted the Load Planner for assistance; who was able to close the flight out. The flight then returned to the gate and the DG had to be moved to a different location.

B737-800 Flight Attendant reported initiating the door disarming procedure steps before the jetbridge was at the aircraft then repeated steps at appropriate time. FA gave the thumbs up in error to gate agent and escape slide deployed.

Cessna 172 instructor reported an engine throttle cable malfunction during descent which resulted in a power loss and an off airport landing during a training flight. The instructor landed safely in a field with no damage to the aircraft and the crew evacuated safely.
C172 Pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter passing 300 ft. horizontally while on approach to a non towered airport.
Flight Instructor reported while on upwind there was excessive engine vibration and a 'loud popping' noise so they returned to the airport.
Eugene TRACON Controller reported an unsafe operation with numerous VFR aircraft at the CVO uncontrolled airport.
Bonanza 33 Pilot reported that engine roughness resulted in a return to the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with another aircraft while intercepting the final approach course. Captain stated no evasive action was needed.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the Jeppesen approach chart for DEN RNP Runway 26 has an unusual hard speed requirement at the Initial Approach Fix which causes operational issues.

Air Carrier flight crew reported ATC issuing a descent below minimum vectoring altitude while on a clearance to an approach fix in the area of terrain during IMC weather avoidance. The crew accepted the ATC assigned headings and altitudes with no cockpit warning and intercepted the ILS for a safe landing.
B737 flight crew reported the Generator One Drive light illuminated during cruise. Flight crew was unable to start the APU per procedure and diverted to a safe landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during destination arrival. Flight crew completed the appropriate QRH procedure and reported the odor dissipated upon landing.
An air carrier pilot reported GPS jamming.
Bonanza 33 pilot reported engine power loss after take off and returned to land at the departure airport.
CRJ-200 Captain reported a go-around after the trailing edge flaps did not extend. The flight crew requested priority handling and returned to make the approach and precautionary landing.
A TRACON Controller reported they vectored an aircraft off the final approach course to avoid unidentified VFR traffic but the aircraft turned the wrong direction resulting in a NMAC.
Air carrier flight crew reported the fuselage surface around an aux static port had peeling paint and the attempted repair made the situation worse. The flight crew refused the aircraft.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they learned after flying they had inadvertently flown without proper LAANC authorization.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while on a visual approach; and the aircraft annunciated a flap warning indicating flaps were not at the proper position for the final approach and landing. The Captain recovered from the unstabilzed approach and landed.
Center Controller reported a small aircraft experienced an electrical failure and lost communications. The aircraft safely diverted to a nearby airport.
Air carrier Safety Analyst reported cargo that was offloaded due to being at max capacity was loaded back into the cargo compartment by the Loading Supervisor.
SR20 pilot on short final reported a critical ground conflict with another aircraft taking off.
AFW Tower Controller reported an unsafe event where a VFR aircraft is transitioning an arrival path for air carriers outside of Class Delta causing an airborne conflict.
EMB-145 flight crew reported unstabilized approach.
B757 pilot reported the aircraft rolled uncontrolled while parking at the gate due to a misunderstanding with the ground crew.
A321 Flight Attendants reported an inadvertent escape slide deployment after aircraft returned to gate due to a door issue and maintenance was conducting a door inspection.

B737 Captain reported was informed by mechanic that three MLG tires had low press and had to be replaced along with a cracked brake. Pilot noted the on the same flight number a day prior and a day after with low tire pressures.
Ramp workers reported they used the wrong tow bar for a B737 MAX 9 aircraft and when they removed it the aircraft rolled because the brakes were not set.

B737-800 flight crew reported an airspeed unreliable condition during the takeoff roll. The crew stated the condition was near or through Vr (rotation) and they continued. Once airborne the crew completed the QRH and returned to the origin airport.

B737 Captain reported 3 of the ramp personnel were displaying hand signals for brake fire on the left side of the aircraft during arrival to the gate. The passengers and crew deplaned normally through the L1 door and jet way. The fire had extinguished with minimal damage to the aircraft.

A321 flight crew reported a fumes event during engine start-up and initial taxi-out.  The flight returned to the gate for inspection after a Flight Attendant also reported a burning smell in the cabin.
SR20 instructor and student pilot reported they diverted to the nearest suitable airport when the engine began to lose oil pressure.

GA pilot reported a near miss during final approach with an agriculture aircraft performing an aerobatic maneuver at the departure end of the runway and then landing on the runway before the GA pilot had exited.

Air carrier flight crew reported engine failure on climb out. During return to departure airport the flight crew inadvertently used landing data from a different airport that resulted in incorrect FMC display.  Flight crew landed safely.
First Officer reported a taxiway excursion resulted after following the appropriate yellow taxi line while making a turn from B to A taxiways at GKY airport.
B777 flight crew reported uncommanded asymmetric spoiler deployment in flight.
B737 NG Captain reported EQUIP SMOKE light illuminated inflight.
C-172 pilot reported departing the runway surface after landing due to suspected brake malfunction.
Embraer ERJ 190 First Officer reported cracking of the Captain's windshield resulted in a request for priority handling and an immediate descent to make a precautionary landing at diversion airport.
Flight instructor with student reported during takeoff roll the student applied hard left rudder resulting in loss of aircraft control and runway excursion.
Flight crew reported during taxi they observed a service vehicle driving fast and coming towards them. They had to apply brakes to avoid a collision.
Air carrier ground personnel reported that the HAZMAT cargo was incorrectly labeled.
HS 125 Captain reported diverting after a hydraulic system overheat annunciator illuminated during cruise.
Flight Crew reported returning to departure airport following a malfunction of the engine bleed air valve.
Flight Crew reported main gear tire deflation on landing rollout.

Corporate Captain reported the poor; faded runway markings at PDK Airport make landing at Runway 21L difficult. Wet and low light conditions at the airport also add to the difficulty during takeoffs and landings.
C185 pilot reported the left landing gear leg separated from the aircraft during landing resulting in the aircraft coming to a rest on the left wing. Wind gusts reportedly contributed to the event.
Controller reported an aircraft was on incorrect heading and not climbing to required altitude; resulting in a MIA deviation.

PA-28 flight instructor with student reported being instructed to execute a go-around while on final approach due to ATC clearing an aircraft to takeoff without proper spacing. An NMAC between the two aircraft ensued during the go-around requiring further evasive action.
Air Carrier Captain reported the temporary lighting setup at JAN airport in the gate area is extremely bright and a safety hazard.

Air carrier Captain reported severe weather conditions; problems with airspeed / altitude control and mechanical problems. These issues led to a loss of control event; FAR speed deviation; and FAR altitude deviation.
Center Controller reported a departure which was supposed to be assigned 14;000 feet checked on their frequency level at 4;000 feet so the aircraft flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

Technician reported concerns after discovering a failed Trim Motor when diagnosing a Horizontal Stabilizer Trim Failure during a gate return. The motor was replaced and the aircraft was returned to service.
A Flight Instructor reported a taxiing aircraft collided with them while they were stopped on the ramp.

CRJ-200 Captain reported a cabin pressure control failure after departure. With one pack on MEL and the failure of the second pack; the flight crew elected to perform a return to the departure airport.
PA-28 pilot reported low oil pressure during cruise resulted in diversion to landing.
B737-800 flight crew reported thrust reversers did not deploy on landing rollout.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that after the last flight; a door was left armed and resulted in the escape slide pack falling out of the housing when catering opened the door.
C172 flight instructors reported ATC Tower failure to separate aircraft resulted in both taking evasive action and a NMAC event.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC with a UAS during cruise flight and took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported being informed a flight was experiencing GPS jamming south of Israel. The flight was rerouted to stay in VHF communication.
B737 flight crew reported a Horizontal Stabilizer Trim motor failed during taxi out and returned to the gate for maintenance.
EMB-145 Captain reported executing a go-around at 200 ft AGL on approach after encountering wake turbulence.
GA Flight Instructor reported a near miss while operating in a designated practice area; during a training flight. The instructor maneuvered away from the other aircraft to avoid a collision.
Flight Instructor with Student reported a loss of power after take off. The instructor took control; aborted the takeoff; and landed safely.
Air Carrier Ramp Lead reported a communication breakdown between company ramp personnel and other ground personnel [consulting group] regarding Hazmat cargo load.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported being notified during taxi that a passenger had a lithium battery in the checked luggage. The flight crew returned to the gate and the passenger was removed from the flight along with the baggage.

B767 flight crew reported low hydraulic fluid level in center system and suspected leak during cruise. Flight crew returned to departure airport as fluid level continued to decrease and landed safely.

Air carrier crew reported severe turbulence and an unstabilized approach when on the initial approach which resulted in altitude and speed exceedances. The crew performed a missed approach and diverted to an alternate airport.
Air Traffic Controller reported aircraft not following taxi instructions resulting in another aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
Bonanza 33 pilot reported autopilot did not intercept the localizer and the glide slope displayed erratic indications. Pilot disconnected autopilot and conducted a missed approach.
Flight instructor reported near miss with another aircraft and a helicopter while on approach.

MRY Tower Local Controller reported TRACON handed off two aircraft with a large overtake that required immediate action to resolve. The reporter stated TRACON routinely hands off aircraft to them that are in confliction.

Fractional pilot reported brake malfunction prior to the aircraft being towed. Pilot contacted maintenance and made a logbook entry. Upon reviewing the logbook later; pilot found the entry was no longer there and the brake malfunction was still present.
C680 pilot reported right thrust reverser did not deploy upon landing.
GA pilot reported the oil filter was installed improperly and caused smoke in the aircraft.

A320 Captain reported a Skin Valve Fault on ECAM after departure. The flight crew ran the QRH and checklists. The aircraft would not pressurize correctly so The flight crew requested vectors and performed an air turn back to the departure airport.
Air carrier pilots reported suspected GPS jamming in Chinese airspace near location ALARA on Airway A326.

Epic E1000 pilot reported ATC did not approve their weather deviation request which resulted in them encountering Wind Shear and an uncontrolled 3000 ft. climb. Pilot regained control of aircraft and returned to assigned altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported engine overheat indication on initial climb and return to the airport for an overweight landing.

B737 flight crew reported while flying the RNP approach they descended below the FAF crossing altitude resulting in a CFTT situation.  The flight crew disconnected the autopilot and landed safely at the arrival airport.
Air Carrier pilot reported the aircraft sounded Enhanced Ground Proximity Warning System 'terrain' while executing a night visual approach with an early descent. A missed approach was conducted.
Bonanza pilot reported after receiving an IFR climb clearance while airborne they descended to avoid weather and received a low altitude warning from ATC.
B737-800 pilot reported encountering severe clear air turbulence in cruise flight at FL340 that resulted in roll to the right and loss of 200 feet of altitude.

EMB-175 Captain reported a flight control anomaly where the control column moved momentarily during cruise flight without an EICAS warning message in flight. The crew diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely overweight.

A RAL Tower Controller reported Runway 16/34 south end is not visible from the Tower so the FAA prohibits the use of this runway resulting in aircraft having to land in very strong crosswinds when the wind is from the east. Previously a camera was installed to view this area but is out of service and FAA has not repaired the camera.
Maintenance Stores Personnel reported he mistakenly advanced a hazmat parcel as a 'No hazmat on board NOTOC'. This mistake resulted in the undocumented air transport of hazmat.
Instructor Pilot reported descending too low on approach for landing; requiring a climb back above minimum altitude to safely continue the approach.
Corporate Captain reported hitting a bird and then having to deviate.
TRACON Controller reported they were not aware an aircraft did not read back their clearance correctly and the aircraft descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier inspector reported they were notified that safety hardware was found missing on the front engine mount after they signed off the final inspection of the complete engine installation.

G7500 Captain reported receiving an alert message during departure that a service door was open. Flight crew returned to departure airport and found the lavatory service door had departed the aircraft and damaged multiple components.
Light aircraft pilot reported returning to departure airport after experiencing loss of engine power.  Post flight inspection revealed insect debris in the fuel vent.
A student pilot reported they turned crosswind to follow traffic as instructed by Tower but they turned behind the wrong aircraft resulting in a NMAC with other traffic in the pattern.

Air taxi flight crew reported a bounced landing. After the bounce; the aircraft contacted the runway off centerline; bounced again and departed the runway surface. The flight crew maneuvered the aircraft back onto the runway with some damage to aircraft and runway lights.
Falcon 7X flight crew reported observing B737 over runway hold line resulted in rejected takeoff.
Flight Instructor on IFR training flight with student reported NMAC with another aircraft in cruise.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying a UAS without obtaining LAANC authorization. The reporter also reported other UAS were flying in the same airspace.
ZTL Controller reported lack of proper interphone coordination released an uncoordinated departure; resulted a MVA CFTT event.
Air carrier flight crew reported during takeoff an aircraft taxied across the runway resulting in rejected takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported autoflight anomalies on initial approach into CLT; citing 5G interference as possibly contributing.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a 'slam dunk' approach clearance that resulted in a go around. Tower provided vectors for a second approach which landed safely.
B737 flight crew reported slowing below assigned airspeed to avoid wake turbulence from aircraft ahead on approach to ORD; forcing traffic in trail of them to go around.
Pilot reported a hydraulic line failed on takeoff; causing the gear to remain up and locked. Pilot elected to make an air turn back and landed with the nose landing gear still up.

Air Carrier First Officer reported Aft Flight Attendant notified flight deck of 'dirty socks' odor during approach. Two aft cabin FA's who experienced physiological symptoms received medical assistance upon gate arrival.

Pilot reported pulling the prop through to lubricate cylinders. Engine started because magnetos were left on. Aircraft ran into the neighbors hanger damaging aircraft. There were no reported injuries.
CRJ-200 Captain reported during taxi out they received hydraulic system and steering malfunction messages. Flight crew contacted maintenance and were towed back to the gate.
A TRACON Controller reported a VFR helicopter below the Minimum IFR Altitude contacted them requesting an IFR clearance after they entered IMC weather.

C172 student pilot reported landing hard on the right wheel and did not properly correct the plane as it was leaning to the right. The plane leaned enough to the point that the prop hit the ground and the plane proceeded to veer off the runway afterwards.
BD4 pilot reported on test flight aircraft engine failed to produce full power resulted in fly below published pattern altitude.
A321 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into PHX in trail of an MD-11 that resulted in a 45-degree bank.
Captain reported missing signage for Taxiway P at AUS airport contributed to a taxiway deviation.
CRJ900 Captain reported they had to apply hard braking while taxiing into the ramp area to avoid a collision with an aircraft that passed in front of them.
A320 Captain reported detecting smoke in the aircraft after a maintenance procedure at the gate and deplaned the passengers immediately via the jet bridge.
Air carrier flight crew flying 737 aircraft reported severe turbulence in cruise flight.
B737 Captain reported wake turbulence incidents have become more prevalent and suggested there to be more consideration with wake turbulence recategorization.
BE36 pilot reported electrical failure in flight. Pilot diverted and landed safely

B777 Captain reported a passenger attempted to board wheelchair labeled as 'Airline Approved' by vendor. After further research; it was determined the airline's additional restrictions required the battery to be removed and transported in the cabin prior to departure.

B757-200 flight crew reported two main tire assemblies deflated on landing roll out due to excessive manual braking applied due to three different MELs on the aircraft. The aircraft was towed to the gate to complete the flight.
Flight Crew flying G-IV aircraft reported altitude deviation on approach.
B787 Captain reported an intermittent fumes event during pushback which was getting stronger. Shortly thereafter the Left Pack tripped off which prompted a return to gate for maintenance.

Pilot reported that a passenger reported smoke in the cabin during cruise. The pilot elected to secure the cabin pack and the smoke dissipated. Pilot elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Pilot reported an MEL for a below flush brake wear indicator pin was not in compliance with the General Procedures Manual. Also reported were instances of changed or missing log book items.

B737 First Officer reported operating an aircraft with a long history of leading edge flap asymmetry discrepancies. This aircraft had a repeat discrepancy occur during approach to landing. The flight crew performed a go around and complied with the QRH procedures and rejoined the approach and landing at destination airport.
B737 pilot reported a NMAC during approach resulted in a TCAS RA event.

Two C172 flight instructors reported a conflict between their respective aircraft; one taking off and one landing.  Pilots disagreed on the details of the event including height (AGL); communications; and degree of risk.
Air carrier pilot reported that during taxi; they had to quickly stop aircraft due to vehicles not yielding to the aircraft.

Citation Longitude (C700) flight crew reported the brakes were not set before the chocks were removed resulting in aircraft movement. The Captain immediately set the brakes after being advised of the movement by ground personnel.
Flight Instructor with trainee reported a loss of oil pressure and engine failure resulting in an off-airport landing.

B737-800 pilot reported a near miss with a ground vehicle while taxiing into the gate under ramp agent guidance. The Captain applied the brakes to prevent a collision as the van crossed the path of the aircraft; no injuries were reported.
Pilot flying PA32 aircraft reported NMAC with another aircraft on final approach.
Air carrier Captain reported during taxi observing another aircraft taxing towards them resulting in the Captain taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.

YNG Tower Controller reported an aircraft entered Class D airspace without contacting ATC and landed at Griffin/Sloas (800H) airport which is uncharted. Controller recommended the private airport be added to VFR Sectional Chart to raise awareness of required communication with ATC.
Mechanic reported a truck almost hitting an aircraft that was taxiing out of the ramp.
B767 First Officer reported a hydraulic malfunction during cruise flight. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed safely.

PC-12 Captain reported loss of pitot static anti ice systems during cruise and unreliable air data information on both the Captain's and First Officer's air data systems. The flight crew diverted to a nearby airport.
Center Controller reported confusing coordination with the TRACON for a military UAS approved to fly through a restricted airspace.
Instructor pilot; with trainee; reported the cabin carbon monoxide detector activated during the pre-takeoff check due to a cracked exhaust system.

C172 instructor pilot reported an NMAC while on a IFR flight plan and approaching the airport using the localizer approach. The instructor stated that the Tower Controller had a Citation jet make a 360 degree turn; 400 feet above their aircraft; to rejoin final. The instructor was ask to call the tower on the ground.
A First Officer reported a UAS passing within 10 ft of their aircraft while leveling off for cruise and reported the incident to ATC. No evasive action was taken and the aircraft continued normally.
B747-400 flight crew reported a flaps drive anomaly in cruise resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Student Pilot reported engine failure during a check flight. The flight returned to the departure airport where a safe single engine landing was executed.
EMB-505 flight crew reported receiving an aural stall warning and stick pusher activation at rotation during takeoff. Flight crew performed a high speed rejected takeoff.

Air carrier flight crew reported a shear pin on the tow bar failed after the push back crew abruptly stopped the aircraft pushback to avoid a conflict with a fuel truck driving in the gate area. After maintenance confirmed there was no damage to the aircraft the flight departed.
M20 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control and deviation from assigned altitude due to severe turbulence.
BD700 pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC due to descent below assigned altitude. Flight crew climbed back to assigned altitude.

Air carrier Captain received a TCAS RA on a visual approach from another air carrier maneuvering on a RNAV approach to the parallel runway. The Captain determined that he had the conflicting traffic in sight and did not follow the TCAS RA solution and continued the approach to landing.
A ZID Center Controller reported a loss of separation between two aircraft when one of the aircraft cleared direct KENLL mistakenly programmed the fix KENLN into their navigation.
Dispatcher reported planning a flight through HF required airspace on a non-HF equipped aircraft.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing LAS in trail of a B757.
C172 flight instructor reported overlooking the position of the carburetor heat control; allowing the engine to fail on approach to landing due to fuel starvation.
ZAB Controller reported frequent radio equipment failures with limited coverage and failure of MSAW feature resulted in an aircraft entering a higher MVA at too low of an altitude.

Captain reported refusing the aircraft after discovering an empty crew oxygen bottle and after reviewing the AML (Aircraft Maintenance Logbook) that the crew oxygen had been replaced 5 times in the last 30 days.
B737 Captain reported Engine #1 began to overheat during cruise and reduced the engine power to idle. The flight crew then diverted to an alternate airport.
Air carrier crew received a GPWS terrain alert while on a visual approach. Crew took evasive action and landed without further incident.
Technician reported failing to remove Leading Edge Flaps Lock Out Pins from the Standby Drive Shut Off Valve during the reactivation process.

Air carrier pilot reported that although ATC issued alerts for weather balloons in the area at cruise altitudes; the presence of the balloons in vicinity of fast-moving aircraft is hazardous and should be addressed.

Pilot reported the ATC Tower at POU airport closed mid-afternoon during the COVID-19 pandemic; but unbeknownst to the reporter; the Tower had returned to normal hours of operation during this flight.  Reportedly; there was no notice or NOTAM advising of the change.
Ground personnel reported the taxi lanes at LGA are difficult to see in inclement weather.
Air ambulance helicopter pilot reported a NMAC during departure with an aircraft that ATC had cleared to land in front of the helicopter.
Fractional jet flight crew reported receiving a TAWS obstacle warning on a circling approach to TEB Runway 01.
B737 Captain reported during approach and selecting flaps to one position; the LE Flap Transit Light remained illuminated. The flight crew continued the approach and landed.
A321 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing loss of cabin pressure. They had been dispatched with a pack inoperative.
An Air Carrier pilot reported the crew would not have heard a taxi instruction to give way to another aircraft if they had not been monitoring a different  frequency than the one they were assigned.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control following pushback and tug disconnect.
Pilot reported autopilot system failure caused difficulties on approach and landing.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a UAS during arrival descent. No evasive action was taken.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the requirement to use 15 bags to secure DG loaded in the cargo compartment was not being followed.
Air carrier Captain reported having to abruptly brake to avoid collision with a vehicle who failed to yield to the aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported hitting jet bridge during taxi into gate.

B767 First Officer reported a strong electrical burning smell during climb in the cabin. After troubleshooting and conferring with Maintenance and Dispatch the flight crew requested priority handling to a suitable airport where a safe landing was executed.
B737-700 Captain and First Officer reported a rejected takeoff due to the inability to keep the aircraft on the center line. The crew was unclear if this was a mechanical failure or pilot error.
Flight crew departing IAD experienced a simultaneous false Terrain Alert and false TCAS/RA advisory.

Air carrier Captain reported an un-commanded aircraft rollback during gate pushback due to the tug brake not properly set. The Tug Driver set the brake immediately which prevented any injury or damage.
C340 Captain diverting to a suitable airport due to a rough running engine.
PA-28 student pilot made a wrong turn on a taxiway after landing. ATC issued a new clearance and the pilot continued their taxi to parking.
Air carrier Captain reported almost hitting a ground employee while taxiing to their parking spot at MZBZ airport.  Captain cited communication issues with ramp personnel as a contributing factor.
Air carrier Captain reported ATC re-sequenced them because they slowed to final approach speed at around a 6-mile final inbound to ATL.
C172 flight crew reported during crosswind takeoff instruction student over controlled resulted in momentary loss of control until instructor safely took control.

ORD Tower Controllers reported aircraft departed the south complex turning northeast bound when an aircraft landing the north complex goes around resulting in an airborne conflict and the go-around aircraft altitude being restricted and the aircraft flying towards terrain.

Reporter stated there is a growing risk with the new portable lithium battery powered coolers that are frequently being brought on board aircraft for medical organ transport even though the user manual states 'Not permitted on aircraft'.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the assigned taxiway Alpha was not on the EFB Jeppesen 10-9 airport chart.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported not notifying Ramp personnel of aircraft MEL limitation preventing Hazmat cargo. Dispatcher suggests clarifying FOM and MEL verbiage.
Air Carrier Captain reported the undocumented transport of dangerous goods due to the flight crew not issued the required departure HazMat documents.
C-206 First Officer reported rejecting the takeoff when the aft cargo door opened on the takeoff roll.
PA-28 instructor pilot reported returning to departure airport after experiencing a partial power loss in the initial climb.

B737 MAX Captain reported RAAS (Runway Awareness & Advisory System) alerts and callouts interfered with ATC communication during the flight. Reporter stated the volume of the alerts is 'entirely too high.'
Flight crew reported taking evasive action after receiving a TCAS RA while on departure.

B737 flight crew reported flaps could not be moved from their setting during final approach and the gauge was 'frozen'. Flight crew conducted a go-around and diversion and landed using alternate flap extension procedure.

Air carrier Captain reported ATC did not provide guidance on how close aircraft can be and slightly prolonged the approach and landing to avoid multiple aircraft in the area. The reporter also noted that the RWSL appears to have been disabled for Runway 13 at LGA.
A321 First Officer reported compressor stall on #2 engine. Flight Crew elected to make an in flight shut down of the affected engine and continue the approach to landing.

A321-231 pilot reported on departure got a Landing Gear Control Interface Unit fault and could not determine status of gear because all 6 lights remained on. They lowered the gear; diverted; and landed.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC in cruise requiring evasive action to avoid a collision.
A Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern which deviated from ATC instructions.

Air carrier First Officer reported a descending TCAS RA on final approach which resulted in ATC issuing a low altitude alert. The aircraft performed a missed approach; then returned for a safe landing.
PA28 Flight Instructor with student reported the engine shut down as they prepared for takeoff. After restarting the engine the flight was continued with no further problems.
Cessna 172 pilot and instructor reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing severe engine roughness.
Air carrier Captain reported that a passenger had brought a prohibited engine component onboard which was emitting fuel odor during cruise.

Air Carrier flight attendant crew reported a fumes event during cruise which was reported to the Captain. The odor was described as a burning smell which was more noticeable in the aft galley area and became less intense as the flight progressed.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming.
C172 pilot reported a rough running engine and loss of power on initial climb required a return to the departure airport.
Light Transport Corporate Pilot reported encountering wake turbulence departing OAK in trail of an A321.

Flight Instructor with Student reported the DA-20 engine RPM's were too high with the throttle at idle. The instructor landed; applied brakes; flattened a tire; left the pavement and hit a taxiway sign. No injuries and no major damage.

EMB-170 First Officer reported receiving multiple caution and warning messages; including landing gear malfunction; during cruise. Flight crew diverted and after landing; the aircraft was towed to the gate.
Pilot reported a runway excursion caused by gusty winds.
B-787 flight crew reported refusing Hazmat cargo due to communications breakdown between flight crew and ground personnel regarding accuracy of DG data.
Air Carrier Captain reported they were on the runway in position and holding when an arriving aircraft on short final executed a go around and overflew them.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported receiving a call from Ramp agent stating Un-Manifested Dry Ice onboard. Dispatcher instructed the Ramp agent that the Dry Ice must be manifested. Dispatcher sent flight crew an ACARS message with the corrected manifest.
General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss during the initial approach on a training flight at a non-towered airport.

TRACON Controller had given a skills check to an ATC developmental who had issued an incorrect clearance that allowed an IFR aircraft to enter a higher MVA and a CFTT event. Reporter stated that the FAA National Training Order fails to allow suspending training of developmental non performance.
B777 flight crew reported exceeding 250 kts departing LFPG after incorrectly programming the FMC. Wake turbulence encounter was also cited as a contributing distractor.
Student pilot reported they had a low engine oil pressure caution warning during descent. CFI took control of aircraft and landed.
A pilot reported a temporary loss of control while landing resulting in a runway excursion and contact with taxiway sign and runway light.
Ground crewmember reported the nose wheel steering bypass pin was dropped on the ground and ingested by the #1 engine as the aircraft taxied. The aircraft returned to the gate for maintenance action.
General aviation aircraft pilot reported failure to maintain altitude due to turbulence while in cruise resulted in an airspace violation.
A319 Captain reported landing safely at an alternate airport after inflight shutdown of the right engine.
A TRACON Controller reported an air carrier Captain advised them they encountered GPS interference while flying over Korea.

A TRACON Controller reported soon after assuming control of the sector they observed an aircraft which had already been cleared for an approach descend below the Minimum IFR Altitude due to the previous controller not issuing the appropriate crossing restriction.
Student Pilot reported following the wrong aircraft on pattern entry resulted in taking evasive action to avoid a conflict.
Diamond DA-40 pilot reported engine failure and a controlled landing in a field.
B737 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence while climbing on departure that caused a temporary loss of control resulting in a climb above the SID crossing restriction.

TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on approach descended below the glideslope and the minimum vectoring altitude. As the pilot seemed to be behind the aircraft; the controller provided delay vectors for the aircraft to ensure an easier and longer approach.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported a near miss while taxiing into the gate on the ramp at night. The First Officer reported the Captain made a hard stop to avoid the other aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported the newly opened taxiway at MDST Airport is not charted on the Jeppesen charts or noted on the 10-7 pages.
EMB-145 First Officer reported the aircraft banked from a wind gust or wake turbulence from a previous aircraft while on short final at MMSL.

Air carrier flight attendants reported a cabin door would not arm and the lever was difficult to move during gate departure. Maintenance removed plastic ware found stuck in the door frame and door functioned normally.
C152 Flight Instructor reported left brake failure while taxiing resulted in a taxiway excursion.
Air carrier Captain reported undeclared Hazmat transported. The event was caused by incorrect dry ice limitations provided to flight crew during gate departure.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a near miss with another aircraft while on approach.

Small transport aircraft pilot with a trainee reported disorientation and confusion during taxi at SMO. The reporter stated the lighting and taxiway markings are poor in this area of the airport and they taxied onto an unmarked ramp at night.
EMB-505 flight crew reported they transported prohibited Hazmat cargo. Reportedly; misinterpretation of their manuals contributed to the incident.

B-737-900 Flight Attendant crew reported a communications breakdown between Flight Attendant crew and Customer Service Agent; Captain; Company SOP's/ Manuals regarding the air carrier's policy of transporting Lithium-Ion powered Hoverboards in passenger cabin.
Tampa TRACON Controller reported an NMAC between two aircraft as one was landing PIE and the other was departing SPG airport.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering turbulence; possibly related to wake; departing IAH.
Citation 650 flight crew reported runway excursion during landing rollout resulting in shattered main gear light.
B737 First Officer reported a late landing flare input resulting in a hard landing. The crew landed and wrote up a hard landing in the logbook for inspection.
B767 Flight Crew reported a bird strike during takeoff roll resulted in inability to raise the landing gear so they returned to the airport.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL in trail of an A321 that resulted in a momentary stick shaker and stick pusher event.
Air carrier Captain reported glide slope anomaly on approach to MCO; and then takeoff warning horn issues during taxi in.
Air carrier Captain reported while entering gate area to park; a catering truck was parked near the gate. The FO shouted 'stop' to avoid a near collision.
B737-800 Captain reported discovering multiple aircraft had worn nose wheel snubber pads. A flight was completed on an affected aircraft; where the wrong MEL was applied.
C-414 pilot reported the left main gear failed on landing. Aircraft departed the runway damaging the propeller and flaps.

Air carrier flight crew reported the failure of the aircraft's electrical power system shortly after takeoff. The flight crew completed the appropriate QRH procedures under extremely high workload conditions.
ZJX Instructor reported that rude and belittling behavior towards a flustered developmental resulted in failure to separate traffic.

Two different Air Carrier Safety Analysts reported DG violations on same flight. Departure Safety Analyst reported a USG-13 exceedance resulting in off loading partial DG shipment to comply with USG-13 limitations. Arrival Safety Analyst at stopover point reported DG shipment arrived without required acceptance checklist. Remaining DG shipment was offloaded until checklist is received.
Instructor pilot reported a NMAC at a non-towered airport with a non communicating aircraft.
Flight Instructor and student reported a ground conflict and NMAC with another aircraft in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport.

Air carrier Captain reported a passenger's Lithium Ion battery powered thermos experienced a thermal runaway during boarding. The Captain removed the thermos to the ramp where the fire department took command of the situation.
Pilot flying SR20 aircraft reported engine failure in cruise. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
ERJ-170 Captain reported they descended below a crossing restriction on approach and received a low altitude alert from ATC.
B737 pilot reported a terrain alert on final approach due to flap blow back. Flight crew decided to continue the approach.
A320 Captain reported a very strong; foul odor filled the entire aircraft shortly after APU startup during preflight. Maintenance removed the aircraft from service.
Corporate Captain reported receiving and responding to a TA and an RA due to GA aircraft maneuvering below a Class B shelf and not in communication with ATC.
CRJ-900 air carrier flight crew reported a cockpit window failure during descent. The Captain requested priority handling with ATC and landed safely.
Small aircraft flight crew reported an NMAC with another aircraft while on final approach and performed a go-around.

Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft was flaring for the landing at SFO Runway 28R and noticed what looked to be an aircraft crossing the runway. The apparent conflict aircraft was actually on Taxiway Z; and the Tower stated there was no requirement to advise landing aircraft.
A320 pilot reported a bang and faint smell during descent followed by an engine stall ECAM. Crew followed ECAM procedures; shut down the engine and landed single engine.
Front Line ATC Manager reported an airborne conflict and cited staffing levels as a contributing factor.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending too low while conducting an approach at night.
TRACON Controller reported failure to hear readback altitude error; resulted in descent below MVA and a CFTT event.
Tug driver reported a ground conflict with another similar type aircraft that was not on frequency.
Air carrier pilot monitoring reported the MRLB/LIR PAPIs were not illuminated and was informed by ATC that they have not worked for a decade.
Air carrier pilot reported an NMAC during descent with a possible UAS. No evasive action was taken.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a drone during final approach to LAX over FUELR intersection.

Air carrier Captain reported nearly colliding with a tug that had stalled after they had cleared the crew for pushback at a foreign airport. The Captain immediately 'jumped' on the brakes after seeing the tug was still there.

Pilot reported incorrectly maintained aircraft logbooks were identified and immediately removed the aircraft from service until the exact discrepancies could be identified and corrected or repairs were made as required.
Flight crew reported a runway incursion caused by lack of communication between crew members and not confirming the taxi route.
Air carrier Captain reported transporting HAZMAT that were not allowed in the reporter's operations.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from TEB Tower on a visual approach.  Lack of awareness of FMS mode was cited as contributing.
C172 pilot with instructor reported engine roughness and returned to the departure airport. Maintenance personnel suggested the roughness was due to carburetor ice.

Air carrier flight crew reported during initial approach receiving a low altitude alert from ATC. Flight crew immediately leveled off and climbed back to the correct altitude and continued the approach.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on an approach and an unidentified VFR aircraft had a NMAC when the VFR aircraft climbed towards the aircraft on approach outside of the Class B airspace.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported an electrical burning odor in the cockpit and forward cabin that was corroborated by the Captain. The Captain completed a diversion and landing.
B737 flight crew reported landing gear indication malfunction during cruise. Flight crew landed uneventfully.
B737 First Officer reported unreliable airspeed indications during cruise climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.

Air carrier Captain reported a loss of aircraft control when approaching parking at the gate with one engine shutdown and contamination on the ramp surface. The Captain regained aircraft control and ended up beyond the stop mark and needed to be moved back to the proper position by ramp personnel.
A321 pilot reported the ACARS generated Eng Out Acceleration Altitude (EOAA) was not the same as the pilot company data recommended EOAA for this aircraft configuration and runway in use.

A220 crew reported a DMC 2 failure followed thereafter by other system failures and communication problem during cruise.  Crew returned to departure airport after executing appropriate checklists and landed safely.
B767 flight crew reported a Main Gear Door did not close so they returned to departure airport.
Pilot reported receiving a terrain warning while inbound to ROA.
Controller reported failure to hear altitude readback of pilot resulted in aircraft descending below minimum vectoring altitude.
Technician reported confusion in signing off items when returning an aircraft to service after storage.
Air carrier flight crew reported bird ingestion in the engine on takeoff climb. Flight returned to airport.
Air carrier pilot reported problems with the B737-800 weather radar not working correctly on numerous aircraft.
Pilot reported landing in the water and as expected the aircraft flipped over and pilot escaped unharmed.

Air carrier Captain reported a near collision with a van on the ramp at CLT airport while turning into the parking gate. The Captain indicated the van was speeding and he had to brake hard to avoid a collision; then continued to the gate.
RV8 Pilot reported on short final an aircraft taxi onto the runway resulted in taking evasive action and a NMAC.  Pilot also reported taxing aircraft had radio problems.
Cessna 182T instructor pilot reported experiencing a loss of engine power in cruise. Pilot diverted and landed safely.
GA Flight Instructor reported an NMAC when another aircraft in the pattern unexpectedly turned towards them contrary to ATC instructions.

B777-300 Captain reported a long delay due to (MFD) Multi-function Flight Display faults; caused by the headset adapter block being placed in close proximity to the nose landing gear air/ground sensor. This led to the aircraft cycling rapidly in and out of Air Mode; which triggered the MFDs to malfunction.

CE-750 pilot reported receiving a Mach Trim Fail warning and numerous additional aircraft system alerts during cruise. Flight crew hand flew the aircraft to a nearby airport and after landing were towed off the runway.
Captain reported the pilot controlled lighting system malfunctioned at a FOK. Reporter stated they advised ATC but no NOTAM has been issued.
GA pilot reported a critical ground conflict at non-towered airport during landing rollout with a jet on approach to same runway. Jet performed a go-around to avoid a collision.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a UAS during initial approach after ATC asked if they had seen a UAS.
Firefighting pilot reported engine failure resulted in a return to a safe landing.
A Home Bult aircraft pilot reported a possible blown tire resulted in loss of control while landing causing a ground loop and runway excursion.
Air carrier First Officer reported a possible conflict and loss of GPS on approach to DEN.
A Flight Instructor reported they descended to 150 ft over a populated area.

A320 pilot reported the loss of the hydraulic system after departure and return to departure airport. The flight crew was assigned a different aircraft and departed; but had to divert when destination airport weather deteriorated below minimums.
B757-200 Captain reported having to use significant force to move the control wheel left or right during the final flare at around 50 ft. AGL.

A321 flight crew reported that the Captain forgot to set the parking brake after disconnecting from the tug during push back and the aircraft rolled into the towbar. Aircraft was returned to the gate for maintenance action.
B737 Captain reported receiving a Master Caution light; a right generator drive light; and a right engine failure; and proceeded to divert to another airport.
TEB Controllers reported poor runway markings almost led to a collision on the ground with a taxiing aircraft and a departing aircraft.
Pilot reported a runway excursion after the left hand main tire and wheel assembly failed.

A321 Captain reported an APU auto shut down after departure. With one Engine IDG already on MEL; the flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and overweight landing rather than attempt ETOPS to destination airport.
Falcon 900B pilot reported a failure of the Air Data Computer resulting in an autopilot disconnect and temporary loss of aircraft control.

C680A Captain reported receiving an unsafe gear indication and the subsequent audible warning system that kept blaring during approach was distracting and made communication with ATC difficult. The flight crew proceeded to divert and safely landed.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported certain 737 MAX aircraft may have programming errors that cause the aircraft to change from the programmed descent speed to the 240 knots / 10;000 feet restriction too early.
Air carrier Captain reported a main tire failure during takeoff.
Aircraft reported that another aircraft entered the traffic pattern incorrectly; creating a conflict.
Flight crew reported GPWS annunciations when beginning the approach. The flight crew had terrain in sight and elected to continue the approach and landed safely.
Pilot reported OBE; X21 and X06 airports are assigned the same CTAF frequency which results in frequency saturation; interference; and general communication problems.
B737-800 flight crew reported that they experienced an uncommanded Number 2 engine shut down while taxiing to the gate after landing. The aircraft continued safely to the gate.

GA pilot reported an NMAC while on climbout from GYI airport requiring evasive action to avoid a collision.  Reporter stated the conflicting aircraft was not communicating with Tower and probably never saw their aircraft.
B737-700 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into PHX in trail of an A320 that resulted in the temporary loss of LNAV.
Corporate jet Captain reported VNY Clearance Delivery issued a departure clearance that was confusing to follow with the verbiage of the SID due to a crossing restriction.
BE55 pilot reported both alternators failed and returned to the departure airport.
A pilot reported loss of aircraft control during landing resulted in a runway excursion.
ERJ145 flight crew reported engine failure resulted in divert to a safe landing.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a lost link during flight. During the flyaway the UAS struck a tree and crashed.

Instructor Pilot reported the aircraft had nose wheel steering issues although it had been returned to service after maintenance action for the same issues. Pilot later learned the issues resulted from damage caused by a hard landing.
GA flight instructor reported an aircraft not making any radio transmissions crossed their runway as they landed at a non-towered airport.

PA-28 Flight Instructor and student reported the ailerons became stuck during departure climb. During return to departure airport; flight instructor turned off Autopilot master restoring aileron control and flight landed safely.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a communication breakdown between flight crew and ground personnel regarding the uncertainty of whether or not DG cargo was loaded on the aircraft.
Pilot reported that during taxi-out; the morning sun interfered with being able to see and they inadvertently taxied onto a runway at MYF airport.
Air taxi pilot reported the aircraft nose wheel became stuck in the grass during back taxi on the runway.

Air carrier Captain reported a lack of flying proficiency of First Officers in the one to three years in the 737 fleet. Some issues manifested themself during the final approach stage which added an additional workload on the Captains.
GA pilot flying to non-towered TRM airport reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern.
ATC reported observing an aircraft below their assigned altitude resulting with the aircraft being below the MIA.

Air Carrier Captain reported confusion while flying the VICKI9 STAR into KOA. The Captain asked ATC for their interpretation of the arrival but they were unable to supply an answer. The Captain maintains the transition is unclear regarding which routing to fly.
B737 MAX Flight Attendant reported a safety concern with the drop-down cabin monitors not following the policy and procedures requirement of automatically retracting for taxi; takeoff; and landing.
Flight Instructor reported that the loss of all electrical power resulted in a diversion; and a precautionary emergency landing.
A319 Captain reported the need to apply heavy braking to avoid a collision with another aircraft that had been taxiing too quickly and without regard to other traffic.
B767-300 Captain reported having a GPS issue resulting in a track heading deviation.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below their assigned altitude at the Initial Approach Fix and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Maintenance Technician reported misapplication of an MEL was made for an ELEC light that remained on during revenue service. It was discovered that the standby Inverter had failed; causing the ELEC light to remain on instead of going out.
A321 pilot reported after documenting discrepancies into the aircraft logbook; the aircraft was taken out service due to corrosion and cracks on the rudder.
A320 First Officer reported numerous delays during the day was caused by MEL non compliance and confusion over procedures. The aircraft was turned over to Maintenance for action.
C172 pilot reported rejecting the takeoff when the left main gear tire failed; causing slow acceleration.
B737 Captain reported the flight crew released the parking brake before final Dangerous Goods (DG) acknowledgement.

Air carrier pilot reported receiving reports of pilots crossing the illuminated red stop bar lights for Runway 03 in GEG airport; after receiving a takeoff clearance. The red lights are located with the ILS hold short line and not the Runway hold short line.
