Air carrier Load Lead personnel reported a late arrival package prevented the Final DG Summary from screening prior to gate departure. After verifying the item was pitted and secured correctly and the flight left without incident.
CRJ900 pilot crew reported receiving a lower altitude from ATC while on the approach and then an aircraft generated terrain warning.
SA-227 Captain reported a taxiway excursion during taxi out for takeoff.
Controller reported an aircraft experienced a pressurization problem requiring expeditious descent resulted in an airborne conflict.
C172 pilot and a Tower Controller reported the C172 pilot turned the wrong way during an evasive action for traffic.
Flight Instructor with student reported a low altitude event while maneuvering into a non-towered airport traffic pattern during a night training flight.
Single pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft at SPK non-towered airport.
Air carrier Captain reported there were no NOTAMs on GPS jamming but ATC was aware.
Flight Instructor with student reported the engine failed during low altitude departure turn. Instructor turned back and landed safely on runway.
CL350 Captain reported receiving a GPWS warning while on a circling approach with surrounding terrain in sight and continued to land resulted in a CFTT event.
C172 instructor pilot with student reported fuel indication issues during cruise flight resulting in a diversion.  Reportedly; the cause of the issue was a faulty wire for the analog gauges.
C172 student pilot reported they lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
Dispatcher reported communications issues resulted in maintenance beginning work on the aircraft for a scheduled check with pilots on board and ready to depart.
ZJX FLM reported traffic for a sector being over the Monitor Alert Parameter for the sector.
General Aviation pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from a military transport in the vicinity of SUU airport.

SFO Controllers reported the building's windows fogged up so the outside became obscured and the ground radar also malfunctioned; and with no traffic management program; the reporters felt it was unsafe to be working traffic at normal speeds.
Captain reported assuming control after First Officer's seat slid back and prevented him from reaching the flight controls.
Flight crew reported ATC failed to issue the proper approach resulting in receiving erroneous glide slope references; low altitude alert and incorrect crossing altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming while close to Israeli border. The Captain suggests that this not be a maintenance write-up because it is such a common occurrence on this route.
BE99 Pilot reported they inadvertently turned the starters on in flight which resulted in an electrical failure.
Air carrier flight crew reported a lightning strike damaging co-pilots windshield. The flight crew diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
Balloon pilot reported during final descent the basket contacted a power line causing a spark and breaking a suspension line. Pilot continued to landing with no damage or injuries reported.

Air carrier Captain reported a track deviation during arrival to ORD after turning left at CLUSO Intersection rather than KAYTO Intersection as cleared. Captain stated CLUSO and KAYTO can be similarly pronounced and therefore confused by crews.
CRJ-900 pilot reported a Low Altitude Alert from ATC on approach.

B737 flight crew reported a Cockpit Fire Extinguisher fell from its mounts and struck the Emergency Gear Extend Door. On take off; all gear unsafe lights illuminated and the landing gear did not retract. The flight crew contacted maintenance and was advised to re-close the door and the landing gear were successfully retracted.
Air carrier pilot reported during taxi to the gate a ramp worker walked in front of the aircraft to short cut to the jet bridge. Pilot slowed taxi and waited until worker exited safety zone.
B737 Captain reported forgetting to acknowledge the Final DG Summary due to distractions at the gate. The reporter remembered while airborne and contacted Operations for assistance.
Hobbyist UAS pilot was distracted by an animal while flying. The UAS flew into an area with high winds and the UAS pilot was unable to maintain control of the UAS which crashed a short time later.
Corporate jet Captain reported braking abruptly short of the runway they were cleared to cross because they observed an aircraft on the takeoff roll on that runway.
An Air Carrier Captain reported loss of oil pressure in one engine caused them to shut down the engine and divert to a nearby airport.
Technician reported a B737 arriving at station had an APU shroud drain mast completely abraded thru. The aircraft was removed from service for repairs.

A small jet pilot reported ramp personnel did not remove the towing adapter when preparing for departure which resulted in the nose gear stuck in a partially retracted position during gear retraction. The reporter was able to land safely with almost no damage.

A319 Captain reported gate agent closed and reopened the main aircraft door. Escape slide did not deploy.; "GA pilot reported an NMAC with a skydiver in the vicinity of Pleasants Peak; California while on approach to SNA airport."
Technician reported ground damage after a lower winglet strake impacted a set of air stairs during a push back and tow procedure.
C172 Flight Instructor reported engine roughness while IFR with malfunctioning transponder; resulted in a divert to a VFR airport.
General aviation pilot lost control of the aircraft during landing. The aircraft veered off the runway for a short period a time before the pilot regained control.

C172 instructor reported a tire failure after landing and exiting the runway when the student applied excessive pressure to the rudder pedals and locked up the brakes. The instructor took control and the aircraft stopped on the taxiway and was towed to the hangar.
C172 flight instructor and student reported flying towards terrain while entering the traffic pattern during night VMC conditions.
A320 Pilot reported autopilot failed to capture glideslope and aggressively pitched down resulted in ATC issuing low altitude alert.
737-80 flight crew reported loss of engine oil inflight.
Air carrier flight crew reported a loss of aircraft control due to the failure of the Inertial Reference System during cruise. The flight crew regained control and resumed the flight.

EMB-145 flight crew reported a burning odor in the cockpit and cabin smoke during cruise. The flight crew completed the memory action items; donned oxygen masks; and diverted for an immediate landing.
Light aircraft reported that after takeoff from TCY he became aware another aircraft broke off their landing attempt because of the conflict with his departure.
Air carrier flight crew reported exceeding bank angle during climbout in IMC. Flight crew corrected bank angle and continued flight uneventfully.
Air carrier First Officer reported service truck crossed in front of aircraft at a high speed; resulted in Captain forced to brake hard to avoid a collision.
B737-800 flight crew reported loss of hydraulic pressure and malfunctioning yaw damper. After an oil bypass valve failure indication the flight diverted to a nearby airport.

Cessna 172 student pilot reported a loss of aircraft control during landing due to gusty winds; resulting in a runway excursion onto the grass; then entered a taxiway. The student did not report any injuries or damage to the aircraft.
Air carrier pilot monitoring reported GPS jamming in an area west of ELP but all systems returned to normal after flying out of that area.
C402 pilot reported the left engine surging during climbout. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
B737 flight crew reported receiving a roll report of a 45 degree bank after parking at the gate that did not match their actual flight profile.
Bonanza 33 pilot reported experiencing a vibration and different engine sound while in cruise flight resulting in the pilot diverting to the nearest airport where they landed safely.
Tower Controller reported a busy session when a pilot thought they were told to line up and wait causing a go around and runway incursion.

Air taxi First Officer reported the aircraft missed a turn at a waypoint while on autopilot. It went unnoticed until ATC asked the flight crew about being on the assigned route. The screen had froze for a moment before the displays synced back up.
A321 Captain reported a fuel transfer problem caused a diversion and precautionary landing.
A Tower Local Controller and their trainee reported TRACON shipped an aircraft to their frequency without issuing conflicting orbiting traffic in TRACON airspace which resulted in a NMAC.
GA pilot reported several non-normal pilot actions lead to an airspace violation.
B767 flight crew reported an airspeed indicator malfunction during takeoff resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Citation Excel pilot reported cabin pressurization and cabin door malfunctions during cruise flight. The flight crew diverted to an appropriate airport.
B737 First Officer reported a hydraulic system malfunction during the climb to cruise altitude. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed.
B737-900 Captain reported a ground conflict during taxiout and stated low visibility winter conditions contributed to the event.
EMB-145LR flight crew reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing cabin pressurization anomalies.
GA pilot reported they misidentified the traffic in the RNT airport pattern after being instructed to follow and turned onto final too soon resulting in a NMAC with preceding traffic.
Air carrier flight crew reported a CFIT event during approach after descending from the assigned altitude. The flight crew climbed to the assigned altitude and the flight continued without issues.
C404 pilot reported partial power loss on takeoff resulted in expeditious return to departure airport.
A Student Pilot reported an Engine Control Unit failure and returned to the departure airport to land.
TRACON Controller reported a NMAC with an IFR aircraft and VFR aircraft that had departed from an underlying airport.

BE-200 flight crew reported losing communications and flight instruments after takeoff. The flight crew performed an air turnback. The engine starter system was found to be on the incorrect setting; which overrode the generators.
Air carrier pilots and flight attendants reported that while the aircraft was being de-iced they felt it rock back and forth as a result of being struck by the de-ice truck.

Light Sport Aircraft flight instructor reported experiencing a fuel pressure issue resulting in difficulty maintaining altitude and power/speed.  Pilot decided to land the aircraft with partial power.

Flight instructor reported multiple instances of near mid-air collisions while operating in the traffic pattern at LVM non-towered airport. Reporter recommended the FAA emphasize adherence to proper traffic pattern procedure especially at non-towered airports.
Air Carrier Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence from previous arrival on an intersecting runway.

EMB-145 Captain reported the aircraft began to roll after the parking brake was set and a momentary Parking Brake Low Press EICAS message was displayed during push back from the gate. The crew was able to re-engage the parking brake and ground crew was able to disconnect the tow bar.
Air carrier Captain reported the dark lighting conditions; wet surface; and dull paint markings at ORD made following the taxiway centerline difficult.
Air carrier pilot reported a new engine out procedure from SBGR is not charted in Jeppessen PRO software.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported receiving a Dispatch message to load a mishandled DG cargo. A Dispatch follow up message stated that cargo could not be added after the fact.

DA40 Instructor pilot reported an ECU B Failure in climb. The Instructor pilot assumed the controls while the student pilot ran the checklists. Additional cautions displayed; switching between Dual ECU Failure and ECU B Fail. They requested vectors to return to the departure airport.
Flight Instructor reported a pilot knowingly departed from a non-towered airport opposite direction to the reporter who was on final approach resulting in a NMAC.

A320 First Officer reported a bird strike shortly after takeoff. Geese were observed striking the aircraft and the number one engine resulting in heavy vibrations and severe damage to the engine. The crew returned to the departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control during pushback from gate due to towbar not attached prior to brake release.

B737-800 flight crew reported braking issues during landing rollout in wet conditions resulting in a loss of aircraft control and runway excursion.  The crew suggested ATC should provide braking action reports; especially when reversing runway landing direction.
C172 flight instructor reported pattern traffic failed to follow clearance which resulted in an NMAC.
Technician reported ground damage during maintenance checks. The Leading Edge Devices moved and contacted the open Thrust Reversers when the aircraft was places in Air Mode to accomplish the checks.
Flight Instructor reported a wing tip scrape during training flight while attempting to land in windy conditions. Flight Instructor executed a go-around and return to complete a safe landing.

B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported while preparing for approach; the aircraft alerting system displayed a reliability issue with the Captain's PFD and a Stab Out of Trim warning. The flight crew continued to landing at destination airport.
Fractional company personnel reported the flight crew had an NMAC with a UAS during final approach. No evasive action was taken.
First Officer and Technician reported an air turn back for electrical fumes after take off. A circuit breaker had been reset and failed to protect the potable water supply heaters from overheating.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an obstacle alert on approach to LGA when they descended below charted altitude because they had a wrong altimeter setting selected.
Air carrier First Officer reported proceeding to the wrong fix; caused by a miscommunication that occurred due to the fix names sounding similar to one another (RADDS/RAPZZ).
C-172 pilot reported another aircraft overflew him and landed while cleared he was beginning his takeoff roll on the same runway.
Air taxi Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while they were climbing.

A TRACON Controller; trainee and the Controller in Charge reported an IFR departure was assigned a VFR departure procedure and assigned a VFR data tag identifier which resulted in the aircraft flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier pilot reported that while taxiing entering the ramp; a baggage cart drove in front of the aircraft. The pilot had to aggressively apply the brakes to come to an emergency stop.
CRJ-700 First Officer reported a fume event during descent in the passenger cabin. The flight was close to their destination airport; so the crew elected to continue for a successful landing.
A321 pilot reported aircraft loss of control during push back procedure.
Air carrier flight crew reported pitot covers were left installed after push back.
An air carrier flight crew conducting a Visual Approach reported they received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
B767 Captain reported delaying pushback due to the ground crew having difficulty communicating in English and not following push procedures.

B767-300 First Officer reported the complete loss of standby power while on approach. The QRH tasks were completed and a landing with no autobrakes and anti-skid system was completed. The outboard tires overheated and the deflated.
B787 flight crew reported roll and yaw controlability issues and elected to jettison fuel and perform an air turn back.
C172 pilots reported the engine quit in cruise when the Pilot Flying turned the fuel selector to off. Flight crew diverted to the closest airport.
TRACON controller in charge reported sector training was being conducted by instructor who consistently fails to follow procedures resulted in a CFTT event.

B737 First Officer reported a flap asymmetry problem on approach. The Flight Crew executed a go-around and complied with the Checklist/QRH procedures. The Flight Crew then flew the approach to landing at destination airport.

A ZJX Controller reported they vectored and descended an aircraft in an attempt to avoid a conflict but the heading and descent placed the aircraft into conflict with a third aircraft. The reporter was working a sector containing more aircraft than guidelines deem operationally safe.
Mechanic marshaling an aircraft being towed into a hardstand for maintenance work reported they had to run up to the tug driver to stop them from contacting an air stair ruck.

General aviation pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The other aircraft descended on top of the C172 requiring evasive action. The aircraft also caused an NMAC with another aircraft.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying within a TFR without authorization.

Air Carrier First Officer reported a fume event during climb. After being notified by flight attendants that the odor was also detected in the cabin the flight crew requested priority handling to departure airport where support personnel met the flight.

Cessna 182 pilot reported a failure of the nose gear to properly lock in the down position; the aircraft landed safely on the main gear but collapsed on the nose and the pilot and passenger evacuated safely.
A B737 Captain reporting while taxiing they had to stop suddenly to avoid another B737 taxiing across their route.
B737-900 Captain reported a stuck control yoke during landing; but was able to complete the landing with no other issues.

Air carrier First Officer reported inadequate lighting in the vicinity of taxiway A and T2 at FSD airport; resulted in difficulty seeing taxiway markings creating unsafe taxi conditions in the ramp area.

B-777 Captain reported a burning odor while taxiing into the gate after starting the APU. Upon parking and shutdown maintenance discovered burned electrical components in the lower electrical compartment.
Single engine pilot reported while on final approach another aircraft proceeded to cross the landing runway. The reporter executed a go-around.

Balloon pilot reported a loss of control during landing when the balloon encountered a gust of wind. The pilot deflated the balloon to prevent any passenger injuries; or structural damage to the balloon.
B777 captain reported a failure of the center hydraulic system after a loss of all hydraulic fluid.

Center Controllers dealing with unusual flow requirements; weather deviations and extra workload caused by activation of special use airspace in their sector reported a military aircraft awaiting clearance into special use airspace lost an engine and could not comply with ATC instructions which resulted in an airspace violation.
DA40 Flight Instructor reported a door open light and an open rear door resulted in a return to the departure airport.
A300 First Officer reported safely returning to departure airport after experiencing low pressure in the Green hydraulic system.

E190 Captain reported the ground personnel were unaware of the company requirement to provide the pilot in command the notification requirement of an electric wheelchair being loaded onto the aircraft. The Captain elected to depart without the document but found the requirement in the flight operations manual while inflight.
Flight Instructor reported the aircraft got a flat tire at VNC after landing due to running over a screw. The reporter discovered FOD damage is a recurring issue at this airport.

General aviation pilot reported they were given an incorrect departure climb altitude restriction by ATC which resulted in a terrain alert from the aircraft and ATC. Pilot climbed to clear the alert and received further climb clearance from ATC.
EMB-135LR Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing PHX in trail of a B737 NG that resulted in a slight track deviation.

EMB-190 Captain reported a BLEED 2 FAIL EICAS Message during cruise. The flight crew ran the QRH and check list and began to descend due to the Cabin Altitude beginning to climb. Oxygen masks were donned and the flight continued to destination airport at 10;000 feet.

Air carrier Captain reported a sharp acrid odor during cruise. After conferring with Dispatch the odor was determined to be caused by a volcanic ash event which was remedied by climbing to a higher altitude.
Flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning while above a waypoint on the approach due to the close proximity of towers.
A cargo carrier pilot reported hearing thuds coming from the lower cargo compartment and after landing discovered the forward cargo container locks had not been engaged.
Air carrier Flight Crew reported difficulty understanding an arrival clearance and non-standard phraseology used by ZSHA ATC during arrival at ZSPD foreign airport.
Captain reported being advised that a passenger told the Flight Attendant they may have been exposed to COVID-19.
Pilot flying C-206 aircraft reported loss of Prop Control inflight.
Air carrier flight crew reported activation of stick shaker on approach to DCA airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported lack of flight mode awareness resulted in an ATC low altitude warning on descent into SFO. Crew climbed back to correct path and landed without further issue.

Air carrier pilot reported during landing at BJX airport they had to avoid a makeshift barrier on the runway. Reportedly; there was no information on the ATIS nor was there a NOTAM advising of the barrier.
A300 Captain reported a loss of the green hydraulic system inflight resulting in an expedited descent and landing.
Air carrier flight attendant reported the flight attendant crew was informed by the pilots of a possible cargo fire. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.

Flight Crew reported a Stall Fail Caution message after take off and continued to destination airport. A piece of speed tape covering a static port was not noticed during the preflight walk around and was later discovered as having caused the malfunction.
Trainee pilot with instructor reported taking evasive action to avoid a NMAC at a non-towered airport.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a passenger deactivated the forward lav smoke detector while she was apparently smoking or vaping. Airport authorities escorted passenger off the aircraft at the arrival gate.

B767 pilot reported receiving several indications of right engine malfunction during after landing taxi. After engine shut down; maintenance advised that a piece of right inboard engine cowling was missing.
GA Pilot reported flying too low along a river then climbing and landing at the destination.
General aviation pilot reported a traffic conflict during taxi with another aircraft and highlighted the lack of clearly marked taxiway yellow lines as a contributing factor in the taxi incursion.
CRJ-200 Captain reported an NMAC with a helicopter during approach. The flight crew complied with TCAS instructions until clear of traffic and resumed the approach.
Center Controller reported a helicopter; departing an airport; leveled off at an altitude below their assigned Minimum Enroute Altitude.
B747-400 First Officer reported severe turbulence resulting in a loss of control and an altitude deviation during cruise. The Captain took over the controls and returned to safe flight.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported main gear tire failure during landing rollout.
CRJ-200 Captain reported shuddering during flight that was more pronounced at higher speeds. The flight crew elected to request priority handling and divert to make a precautionary landing.
B200 pilot reported the wing contacted a hangar door while they were taxiing the aircraft.
Ramp agents reported during pushback another aircraft's jet blast pushed one of the ramp agents towards the pushback aircraft's running engine.

B737 MAX 8 pilot reported failure of both FMC displays and Captain's side navigation display and PFD during climb. While the flight crew was working through the QRH both FMCs returned to normal; and the crew continued to destination.

B737-800 pilot reported loss of the Captain's PFD in cruise. After discussion with Dispatch and Maintenance; it was decided to perform and air turn back to prevent the aircraft from being grounded at an out station.

Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft's nose may have potentially crossed over the hold-short line after taking the high speed Taxiway V at LAX Airport. The reporter noted only Taxiway Y has a warning on the chart; and suggested there should be a warning applied to all taxiways exiting Runway 24R.

Small aircraft pilot reported cruising at the wrong altitude for direction of flight and while getting distracted by an overheating electronic; noticed another aircraft that was quite close in vertical distance.

Air carrier Captain reported the removal of a Hazmat can that was emitting strong chemical odor during preflight. After the flight completion the Captain stated he failed to send the company required safety form.
Pilot Examiner reported NMAC with another aircraft in airport traffic pattern due to airport over congestion.
Air carrier Ramp Lead reported mechanics started an engine with ramp personnel and equipment in close proximity.
Air carrier Captain reported possible jamming or interference resulted in GPS failures and reliance on INS; inertial navigation.

EMB-170 flight crew reported while parking at the gate they released the brakes without coordinating with the ground crew resulting in the aircraft moving forward until the Captain stopped the aircraft.
DA42 instructor pilot reported returning to departure airport for a safe landing after experiencing an RPM overspeed shortly after takeoff.
Light aircraft pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC after descending below cleared altitude on approach to UGN airport.
Corporate Captain reported a near miss with a UAS while they were climbing.

B777 Captain reported a fuel odor and EICAS fuel valve message after engine shutdown at the gate; then was notified to motor the engine by ground maintenance. After dry motoring the engine; the odor dissipated and the Fire Department personnel reported no fire or fuel spill to Captain.

EMB-190 pilot reported after takeoff and upon entering IMC; they received ice condition advisory message on EICAS. Within less than a minute we received A-I wing fail. The flight crew performed an air turn back and made a precautionary landing at departure airport.
EMB-145 Captain reported a flap fail warning appeared on the EICAS after landing. After the aircraft was secure at the gate; Maintenance inspected the aircraft and found two broken components.

PA-44 commercial pilot applicant and check pilot reported an aircraft loss of control while performing a single engine approach circle to land in visual conditions. The check pilot took control from the applicant; maneuvered to another runway; and landed without a landing clearance from the tower.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported potentially flying above 400 feet AGL due to descending terrain underneath the UAS while in cruise flight.

A Center Controller reported an aircraft reported temporary Radio Altimeter and Ground Proximity Warning System failures.  The pilot of the aircraft told the Controller it was possibly due to 5G interference.
Pilot reported a near mid air collision and maintained separation to avoid additional conflict.
General aviation pilot reported an NMAC with an aircraft while maneuvering to land. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Student Pilot reported while landing in a crosswind they lost control of the aircraft and exited the runway into the grass.
DA-40 general aviation pilots reported an engine malfunction during initial climb on a training flight. The instructor took the controls and returned to the departure airport for a normal landing.
Captain reported severe turbulence encounter with loss of control; resulted in altitude deviation and ATC failure to understand crew recovery request.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported engine failure during climb. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier Captain reported a cabin depressurization due to a faulty main door seal. The pilots returned to the departure airport where an overweight landing was accomplished.
EMB 170 flight crew reported they were informed that an escape slide was inadvertently deployed after the ferry flight due to the door not being disarmed.
GIV-SP Captain reported a loss of cabin pressurization during cruise and diverted uneventfully.
G200 Captain reported limited aileron controllability during climbout. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
A320 flight crew reported unreliable airspeed indicator during takeoff roll. Flight crew rejected takeoff.
Captain reported the failure of two elevator power control units and expressed concern over the quality of the components being acquired for installation on company aircraft.
Air carrier pilot reported a fuel leak indication and diversion to land at the nearest suitable airport.
Air carrier Pilot reported a fumes event during descent described as a dirty socks odor on the flight deck. The fumes dissipated after completing the appropriate QRH  procedure.
Dispatcher reported a B767-300 aircraft had electrical issues during departure climb and diverted.

EMB Legacy 500 pilot reported on arrival having a miscommunication with ATC resulting with the pilot descending below their assigned altitude and flying towards terrain. ATC issued a low altitude alert and new clearance.
Air carrier Captain reported making an abrupt stop during taxi into the gate at LGA due to a conflict with a vehicle.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported not being able to maintain company communications with a flight in oceanic airspace; as well as the flight not maintaining communications with ATC. The flight was able to communicate later in the flight.
Ground personnel reported while towing an aircraft for repositioning a critical ground conflict occurred when they were cut off by another companies service truck.
Student Pilot reported a loss of engine power and a diversion to another airport.
Air carrier ground personnel reported Dangerous Goods documents incorrectly listed dangerous goods on board. No DG cargo was loaded onboard.

Air Carrier Captain reported during takeoff roll left engine torque did not rise resulting in the aircraft making a sharp left turn; the captain was able to regain control of the aircraft and departed safely.

PA-28 Flight Instructor reported a runway incursion occurred as an aircraft that was landing did not comply with ATC's instruction to exit the runway at a specific taxiway. To ensure there was proper clearance between aircraft; the Flight Instructor requested a different taxiway to safely exit off the runway.

B737 Captain reported veering from the runway centerline during a crosswind landing when a newer First Officer did not apply the correct flight controls during a crosswind landing. The Captain took control and returned the aircraft to the centerline with no damage.

Air carrier flight crew reported an APU failure after push back with high outside temperatures. The cabin temperature became excessive and passengers became ill. The flight requested an immediate return to any gate; which was eventually granted.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a GPWS ' Terrain Warning'. The Captain was aware of over correcting for a cross wind on approach.
Air carrier First Officer reported the failure of a main hydraulic system during descent.
Air carrier flight crew reported a wheelchair which exceeded the Lithium Ion battery limitation was loaded on the aircraft. Wheelchair was unloaded before gate departure.
Ground employee reported an agent started to deplane a B737 aircraft before a tail stand was used causing the aircraft to bounce up and down.
General aviation pilot reported a malfunctioning engine while starting a descent. The pilot diverted and landed safely without injury or damage.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization. Pilot reported being unable to update the mapping software prior to the flight due to internet connectivity issues.

B777 First Officers reported anomalies with Air Data Computers; Electronic Engine Controls; and auto-flight functions prior to level off at cruise altitude. Aircraft had a history of these problems and since crew was unable to verify correct altitudes with ATC in RVSM airspace they elected to return to departure airport.

Air Carrier Captain reported a burning oil odor during takeoff and initial climb which prompted the flight crew to request an immediate return to departure airport. After a safe landing the flight continued to the gate where maintenance met the aircraft.

SR20 instructor pilot reported an engine control malfunction which resulted in engine power being unresponsive and aircraft controllability issues during a training flight. The flight landed safely as a precaution and taxied to parking.
First Officer reported the loss of the Right Hydraulic system in flight. The flight crew ran the checklists and QRH and continued to the destination airport.
Pilot reported distractions lead to a collision on a taxiway with another aircraft during preparations for formation operations.
Part 107 pilot reported flying a UAS in an active TFR. It was during the post-flight the UAS pilot learned of the TFR.
Flight Instructor with student reported that after landing and stopping on the runway another aircraft executed a missed approach overhead and close to the Instructor's aircraft.
A flight instructor reported Alternator failure and diversion to nearby airport.
Flight Crew reported the forward avionics compartment access door seal failed on take off; resulting in an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported inadvertently flying their UAS nearby a sports event after they did not find any airspace restrictions. Pilot landed UAS immediately.
C172 pilot reported being unable to develop full power on climb out. Pilot returned to departure airport and discovered the carburetor heat cable had become disconnected from the actuator.
GA pilot reported crossing the runway extended centerlines of a Class C airport while flying under the airspace; causing an air carrier RA.

Air Carrier Captain reported a GPS interruption while on departure climb from DEN. The Captain reported the interference to DEN ATC and was told several other aircraft were reporting GPS interference. A good GPS signal was acquired and the flight continued.
A319 flight crew reported the center fuel tank pump failed and they returned to their departure airport.
Air Carrier pilot reported the LAS FRNCK1 SID does not have an Engine Out Procedure.
Captain reported hydraulic pressure malfunction and returned to departure airport.
B737 First Officer reported uncommanded leading edge flap deployment in cruise flight. Flight crew made an uneventful landing at destination.

B777 Captain reported being notified by the Purser of an e-cigarette charger experiencing a thermal runaway during cruise. The flight attendants placed the smoking charger in the Thermal Containment Bag which extinguished the smoking device and enabled the flight to continue with no further issues.
TBM 940 pilot reported two separate incidents in which the G3000 autopilot initiated an uncontrolled pitch up attitude resulting in an unsafe speed reduction during the initial climb phase.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence from preceding B737-800 on arrival into JFK.
Air Carrier flight crew reported an 'oily smell' odor during descent until gate arrival. An AML discrepancy was submitted resulting in aircraft being removed from service.
First Officer reported electrical failure on take off. The Flight Crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at the departure airport.
Captain reported a fumes event during cruise resulting in a diversion.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while in the pattern at a non towered airport. The instructor; with trainee; took evasive action to avoid the small business jet.
Air carrier pilot reported ground conflict with another aircraft during taxi out that required quick braking to avoid a collision.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming in the HECC FIR through OJAC FIR that continued even after landing at OJAI Airport.
PA34 Flight Instructor with student reported one engine failed to produce full power in cruise. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
ZDC Controllers reported an aircraft made a missed approach due to encountering severe turbulence; which resulted in momentary loss of aircraft control.

SCT TRACON Controller reported re-occurring airborne conflicts between VFR aircraft executing aerobatic maneuvers and IFR arrivals into PSP.  Reporter advised they deal with these types of situations every day; which could be easily avoided by having non-voluntary protected airspace.
787-9 Maintenance technician reported damage to APU oil cap 'O' ring.
B767 flight crew reported altitude deviations on approach related to wake and atmospheric turbulence.
Captain reported receiving a erroneous wind shear warning with no other indications during approach to DCA airport.
GA pilot reported taking evasive action while on visual approach in order to avoid another aircraft during final approach to TME airport.
Part 107 pilot reported they were informed that they flew into controlled airspace and had not checked airspace before the flight.

Single engine instructor pilot reported an NMAC while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The instructor and trainee were on left downwind when an approaching helicopter from the right flew through the final approach course and evasive action was taken. The instructor was unable to contact the helicopter pilot.
Air Carrier Captain reported temporary loss of aircraft control in turbulence during cruise flight.
Pilot reported an NMAC event while entering traffic pattern.
Flight Instructor on training flight in a BE-95 aircraft reported the Nose Landing Gear collapsed on landing.

CRJ-200 Captain reported they entered the wrong ILS frequency in the pilot flying's autopilot causing it to disconnect resulting in an altitude loss and a Low Altitude Alert from ATC. The crew corrected the altitude and had the airport in sight and continued to a normal landing.

Pilot reported a runway excursion during gusty wind conditions while receiving training in a tail-wheel aircraft. The instructor took over the controls as the aircraft rolled into a grass area undamaged; then taxied to the hangar.

B737-800 First Officer reported never receiving the Dangerous Goods Form during preflight. Flight crew amended the flight release after Dispatch confirmed that the form was on the aircraft; but the form was never brought to the attention of the flight crew.
Cessna 172 student pilot reported running off the end of the runway after a high speed bounced landing.
Flight instructor with student reported an NMAC while performing flight maneuvers in a popular practice area. Flight instructor performed evasive maneuver and checked for traffic.

LAS Tower Local Controller reported an NMAC when a Runway 19L arrival unexpectedly initiated a go-around and flew over the top of an aircraft on short final to Runway 8R. The reporter states published procedures in place for this arrival configuration do not adequately protect for this occurrence.

B777 flight crew reported diverting after engine vibrations and erratic engine oil pressure and quantity indications were received after takeoff. Weather and temperature were extreme and likely caused the problems with ice on the engine fan blades and frozen oil coolers and oil filters.

Air carrier Captain reported confusion with the setup of the special engine-out approach at SKRG; as there is some ambiguity that may lead to the use of the wrong VOR frequency and misidentification of the critical DME point.
Air carrier Captain reported no NOTAM was issued for runway painting and closing at MLI airport. ATC stated to the pilot that MLI does not issue NOTAMs for runway painting and closing.
Air carrier pilot reported the tug came in contact with the aircraft during push back and the aircraft was taken out of service.
EMB-170 Captain reported landing gear indication and retraction problems on takeoff. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
C172 student pilot reported a loss of aircraft control on landing resulted in the failure of the nose wheel assembly and a blown nose tire. The pilot contacted Ground Control for assistance.
A319 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B787 on approach to ORD.
Fractional jet pilot reported they programmed the FMS incorrectly and flew 1;000 feet below the prescribed profile.
C680 Captain reported receiving a 'BATT OTEMP L' warning message during takeoff resulting in a return to the departure airport for an overweight landing.
Light aircraft flight instructor reported an NMAC with another light aircraft on final approach at LAL airport.
Vans RV-12iS pilot reported landing safely after experiencing low fuel pressure at cruise altitude.

B757 Captain reported the left thrust lever did not advance normally during autothrust takeoff roll and felt blocked from forward movement when the Captain attempted to advance it. Flight crew returned to departure airport and performed a normal landing.
B787 Technician reported being marshaled into the gate when a service vehicle entered the safety area passing close by the Technician and Marshaller.
A TRACON Controller reported they issued a vector to an aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
PC-12 pilot reported confusion with ATC regarding the direction of flight. After receiving a new instruction the pilot reported over banking the aircraft and losing altitude.
Air carrier captain reported a new taxiway; B; is not depicted on the airport chart for MMGL; Guadalajara; Mexico.
PA28 pilot reported loss of the alternator during a night VFR flight. The pilot established communications using a cell phone; continued to destination airport and landed.
Captain of medium transport aircraft reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into CLT in trail of an A321.
BHM Tower Local Controller turned an IFR departure towards higher MVA; while training; which resulted in a CFTT event.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying after their Certificate of Authorization expired. Pilot landed the UAS upon realizing the error.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC after the Tower instructed them to turn crosswind into conflict with an aircraft departing the parallel runway.

Mooney instructor pilot reported a gear collapse after touchdown at a non-towered airport during a training qualification flight in VMC weather. The aircraft skidded to a stop on the runway and the instructor and student evacuated safely.

Bell UH1H helicopter pilot reported an engine failure and auto-rotation landing due to a fuel switch being turned off on a training flight. The aircraft landed safely adjacent to an airport; then moved to the airport ramp.
Captain reported loss of System B engine driven hydraulic pump and the number 2 engine filter bypass light illuminating resulted in an inflight shutdown of the number two engine and a diversion.
Cessna 421 pilot reported a failure of aircraft communication systems after takeoff resulted in entering class D airspace without clearance.
Air carrier flight crew reported Low Hydraulic Quantity in flight.

A320 Captain reported that on climb-out; there was a temperature exceedance in the engine #1 EGT with a fuel flow of 800 lb. over what engine #2 was showing. Upon landing; a message showed a possible FADEC error.

B787 Captain reported an uncommanded roll of the aircraft while performing an autoland in visual conditions while on final approach. The Captain disconnected the autopilot; landed; then wrote up the unsuccessful autoland for maintenance action.
B777 Captain reported nose gear failed to retract after takeoff. Flight crew returned to departure airport and made a normal landing.
Flight Attendant crew reported a fume event which resulted in physiological symptoms and medical treatment.
B757 flight crew reported that they set the wrong final approach target speed on approach to IAH; citing fatigue and workload as contributing to the error.

B767 Captain reported Hazmat documents provided to the flight crew contained numerous weight and description errors. After making multiple attempts to correct the issue without resolution; the flight crew refused the Hazmat shipment and the flight departed safely.
Pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft conducting a non-standard pattern entry and cited an overly congested CTAF shared by nearby airports as a contributing factor.
C-172 Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a runway incursion and a near miss with another aircraft at a non-towered airport.
A Flight Crew reported they were unable to communicate via HF Radio and utilized CPDLC.
Pilot reported striking two different parked aircraft while attempting to taxi out of parking.
RV10 pilot reported failure to disconnect tow bar resulted in loss of aircraft control on landing; prop strike; and runway excursion.
Air carrier Pilot reported seeing a UAS while descending in Class B airspace. The flight continued normally after the encounter.
C-172 pilot reported malfunction/failure of flaps in flight. Pilot returned to departure airport and completed a no flap landing.
Air Carrier flight crew reported severe turbulence in cruise flight.
TRACON Controller reported aircraft descended below assigned altitude and MVA; resulting in a CFTT event.
B737-700 Captain reported a ramp worker providing false assessment of wing icing.
Air Carrier Captain reported a Flight Attendant and passengers reported a burning smell in the cabin during taxi. Aircraft returned to the gate.

Air carrier First Officer reported a critical ground conflict during final approach with a departing aircraft on the runway. ATC instructed the flight to go around and they returned for another approach and landed safely.
B757 Captain reported the loss of the left generator in flight. The flight crew determined the left AC Bus was now also unpowered. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported an EFIS COMP MON annunciated during takeoff. The flight crew continued the takeoff and elected to request priority handling and perform an air turn back. A precautionary landing was conducted at the destination airport.
Fractional pilot crew reported an engine surge and burning smell while in cruise flight. The pilots reported receiving priority handling and diverted to a suitable airport.
Captain reported undeclared Hazmat cargo discovered during unloading at destination.
Maintenance Technician reported misapplying a MEL on an aircraft that was a No Dispatch MEL.
Instrument Rated Single Engine Pilot reported an unstable approach in IMC; resulting in a missed approach during which the pilot overflew two airliners on the adjoining taxiway.
A Flight Instructor and student reported an engine failure causing the cowling to detach and damage the windshield.
Pilot reported loss of engine thrust on take off and complied with QRH procedures for a suspected engine stall. The flight crew returned to the departure airport for a precautionary landing.

SAT TRACON Controller reported an unidentified aircraft departed 5C1 airport underneath the SAT ILS final approach course and climbed into confliction with an Air Carrier. The Controller climbed and vectored the Air Carrier to avoid the traffic but the Air Carrier responded to a TCAS/RA and descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Instructor reported winds pushed aircraft off of center line during landing and caused aircraft to run off the runway and strike a runway light.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an EGPWS warning during descent over mountains. Flight crew responded and warning dissipated.

Air carrier aircraft Captain noticed a light aircraft about 600 feet from them on approach to a different runway. Reporter stated they did not hear any radio transmissions from the light aircraft on the common frequency.
Experimental aircraft pilot reported the check ride examiner opted to not do the check ride because the condition of the aircraft's wooden propeller was not acceptable.
Helicopter pilot reported a near miss with another helicopter when making a communication call to ATC prior to the airport traffic area. The pilot contacted the Tower and continued to the airport.
A CRJ700 First Officer reported that the Captain flying temporarily lost control during landing in a strong crosswind resulting in a wingtip strike.

GA pilot reported an altitude deviation and CFTT while on the RNAV 16 Approach to PWK airport. The Tower Controller advised the pilot of a Low Altitude warning prompting the pilot to climb back to assigned altitude.
ZAU Center Controller reported an aircraft vectored for a Visual Approach descended below their assigned altitude and below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Ground personnel reported having to stop an operator repositioning an aircraft to avoid a collision with a jetway that was undergoing maintenance.
Flight crew reported an exhaust gas temperature overtemp indication resulted in a rejected take off.
Two air carrier pilots on break reported noticing the effects of possible wake turbulence on their trans-Pacific flight.

EC135 helicopter Captain reported an engine malfunction requiring a landing precaution on a taxiway on an ambulance flight prior to Tower operating hours. The pilot remained with the aircraft until company maintenance arrived then walked on an active taxiway to the FBO; resulting in a pedestrian violation from ATC once the Tower opened.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the vicinity of SGU airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported the flight crew missed the turn onto Taxiway B at ZGGG due to unclear or inadequate signage.

Pilot reported an unexpected track heading deviation during departure from APC airport while on the NAAPA4 SID.  Reporter stated the SID was properly loaded in the FMS; yet the aircraft turned the wrong heading.
Air Carrier First Officer reported an 'Elevator Split' caution message during initial climb. The flight crew returned to the departure airport and executed a safe landing.
B747-400 flight crew reported during a CAT1 autoland approach; the aircraft pitched up 10 degrees at 20 feet AGL; resulting in a go-around.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending below charted altitude on a visual approach to COS; citing time pressure as contributing to the incident.
Pilot reported a near miss with a TV tower during visual approach to MKC airport.

Helicopter Pilot reported a NMAC with a general aviation aircraft in Class B airspace. The helicopter pilot was issued a traffic alert by ATC; and that the general aviation aircraft entered Class B airspace without radio contact. The Helicopter Pilot performed an evasive maneuver to avoid the general aviation aircraft.
C172 pilot reported a complete electrical loss due to a failed battery. Pilot returned to departure airport.
B757-200 Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after the right cockpit window shattered.

GA instructor pilot reported that as they started the takeoff roll a truck began driving toward them from the opposite end of the runway. Pilot elected to continue takeoff and truck drove off the runway; allowing plane to continue.

B737-800 Captain reported a flight control system malfunction and hydraulics system quantity decreasing made flight control sluggish during cruise. The crew decided to return to the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the constant construction and poor signage at WSSS/SIN Airport led to the flight crew passing the point where ATC wanted the aircraft to stop.

E145 Captain reported there was a bleed leak during the climb with moderate icing conditions and the aircraft began to lose cabin altitude. The flight crew descended and was able to stabilize the cabin altitude by turning the bleed back on and the anti-ice system to work long enough to exit out of icing conditions.
B787 flight crew reported ground crew initiated pushback cutting off another air carrier that was supposed to taxi behind the pushback aircraft before movement started.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of ADS-B and GPS departing DEN.
Robinson R44 helicopter pilot reported during liftoff at 50 feet AGL had to initiate an autorotation and land due to getting a low rotor horn and engine power dropping.
B737-800 pilot reported stab out of trim light on multiple times; erratic pitch control with autopilot on; manual trim worked okay.
Small Aircraft Pilot reported they failed to hold short of an active runway.
Flight Crew reported loss of oil pressure during cruise with the #2 Engine. Flight crew performed an in flight shut down of the affected engine and completed a diversion.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a Stabilizer Out of Trim problem during climb out. They completed the appropriate QRH checklist and performed an air turn back.
Air carrier flight crew reported Terrain Alert on approach to BOI airport.

Air taxi Captain reported the flight crew unknowingly entered a closed taxiway at LAX while trying to expeditiously exit the runway; and noted the closed taxiway was not outlined as closed on the airport charts; still had its signage up; and lacked any closure markings.
UAS pilots had a near miss with a helicopter on climb out and took evasive action.
Air Carrier Captain reported a near midair collision during descent to DEN airport.
C172S Flight Instructor reported conflicting traffic failed to go around safely as announced resulting in evasive action a conflict.
Flight Instructor reported that while practicing maneuvers at low altitudes; they flew lower than planned over an area with housing.
A319 Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during boarding. The aircraft was removed from service after Maintenance was called.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground gate delay to retrieve a Lithium Ion battery powered laptop from checked baggage. Flight departed after a long delay.

A Tower Controller reported a departing Cessna reported a rough running engine and returned to land. The Controller allowed another aircraft to make an approach before they had permitted a runway inspection from Ground Personnel.
C441 pilot reported potentially violating FAR 91.405 for flying a gear inoperable flight without a required Ferry Permit.
C-172 pilot reported a runway excursion during landing rollout thought to be the result of a patch of ice with poor directional control.

Flight Instructor with student pilot reported an NMAC while maneuvering to enter the traffic pattern at a non towered airport.  The instructor took control of the aircraft and executed an evasive maneuver to avoid a collision.
Air carrier Captain reported the runway braking action was not as reported and the aircraft required the use of reverse thrust to stop from sliding off the runway.
C182 pilot reported taxi and lateral control difficulties on the first flight after an annual inspection due to a suspected failed or mis-calibrated steering bungee.
B787 flight crew reported a Flight Attendant suffered a minor injury during a wake turbulence encounter at FL380 in cruise flight over the Atlantic.
Citation pilot reported a NMAC on initial climb with an air carrier departing an adjacent airport. Citation flight crew took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

Air Carrier Captain reported altitude deviations due to the loss of VNAV Path and a malfunction with the Autopilot with regard to directional control. After resetting the Autopilot the control problem was resolved and the approach completed.
Pilot reported a Critical Ground conflict during landing rollout with opposite direction non-reporting traffic.
A319 flight crew reported a fume event during destination descent. Flight crew requested priority handling and executed a safe landing.
Air Carrier pilot reported they responded to a TCAS/RA for traffic passing 300 ft. below them.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without LAANC authorization.

Cessna 310 pilot reported a faint electrical odor while flying at cruise altitude; resulting in a return to the departure airport for an uneventful landing. The electrical anomaly was later attributed to a faulty fuel gauge.
B737 flight crew reported engine failure during cruise descent. Flight crew shut down engine and performed a diversion and safe landing.
Technicians reported an air turn back for a fuel leak after Maintenance replaced an engine fuel pump assembly.
C172 pilot reported that while practicing maneuvers near the airport; they sank too far below regular FAR's over a congested city. They took evasive action and exited the area.
Ground Observer reported a possible Near Mid Air Collision the FAA and reporting Pilot provided details of incidents.
EMB-145 First Officer reported receiving an EICAS pressurization amber warning during takeoff resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
Technician reported discovering during a scheduled A check; that the crew oxygen bottle had the valve safety wired backwards and made repairs.

PA-28 pilot reported the RPMs and speed were not rising as expected during takeoff roll resulting in a rejected takeoff and a runway excursion.  ATC was advised and assisted the pilot in returning the aircraft back to the ramp.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported a stabilizer trim system malfunction during descent in daylight visual conditions while communicating with air traffic control. The Captain landed safely using the manual trim system.
C525 flight crew reported engine failure and diversion to a nearby airport.
Tecnam P2012 pilot reported a fuel indicator showed a rapid drop. Pilot diverted to a nearby airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported the UAS crew conducted a flight with FAA authorization and failed to obtain additional LAANC approval to fly within controlled airspace.
Flight Instructor reported a loss of control by a student during a presolo lesson in which the aircraft veered left off the runway. No injuries or damage reported.
Air carrier flight crew reported another carrier flight crew commented about flames exiting engine tailpipe during takeoff roll. Flight crew returned to departure airport.

Technicians reported Aircraft X arrived in ZZZ with 'B' system hydraulics failure; found #2 EDP leaking and damaged. Performed removal and replacement of #2 hydraulic pump per AMM and accomplished hydraulic system flush of 'B' system. Serviced B system hydraulics; performed multiple leak checks; no defects or leaks found. Aircraft X departed and crew reported #2 Engine hydraulic low pressure light illuminated.

Bell 206L pilot flying reported limited cyclic control movement while maneuvering and diverted to a nearby airport. Upon inspection; Maintenance found a manifest container wedged between the cyclic knuckle and foot floor plate.
Pilot reported burning plastic odor and visible fumes in the cockpit during approach. Pilot requested special handling and was given landing clearance where a safe landing was executed.

PA-28 pilot reported an NMAC while departing a non-towered airport when they took off in front of an aircraft which had reported turning from base leg final initiated a go-around as the reporter took off.
BE99 Captain reported a trim runaway problem on takeoff and performed an air turnback and precautionary landing.
A TRACON Controller reported that an aircraft deviated from the SID and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC during descent requiring the copilot's abrupt action to avoid a non transponder GA aircraft.
Cessna 150 pilot reported loss of all AC power after take-off. The pilot performed an immediate air turn back and made a precautionary landing at departure airport.
B737 MAX Captain reported noise on headset when using air/ground communications systems.
PA-44 flight instructor reported they could not restart the engine after shutting it down for training and diverted to the nearest airport.
Helicopter pilot reported a near miss with a military helicopter during final approach. Reporter took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
A light sport aircraft pilot reported they received a low altitude warning for obstacle and climbed into Class B airspace without a clearance.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence descending through FL350 on arrival into DFW.
General aviation pilot reported they taxied past hold runway short lines with an aircraft on short final approach; resulting in a critical ground conflict event.
Pilot reported a runway excursion during landing rollout.

Maintenance Controller reported a rejected take off was caused by a #1 GEN/IDG Light illuminated. The Flight Crew performed a part power run to confirm that there was no fault indicated and departed the airport. The aircraft was removed from service at destination for #1 IDG low oil quantity  after the light illuminated again in flight.
A300 Captain reported diverting due to unreliable airspeed and altitude indications.
SCT Controller reported issuing a descent that led to an aircraft flying below the next point of the STAR; and below the MVA earlier than normal.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict on final approach with a UAS. No evasive action was taken.
Premier 1 Captain reported the engine ITT rose out of limits and elected to return to departure airport. It was determined that a clamp on the engine bleed air ducting failed and the ducting broke.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported a high speed rejected takeoff due to an APU Caution Light.

Air carrier flight crew reported a passenger's electronic device experienced a thermal runaway during cruise. The flight crew requested and was provided priority handling to a diversion airport where the flight landed safely.

Flight Instructor with student reported that during initial climb at a non-towered airport; another aircraft appeared behind them initiating a turn in the same direction requiring the Flight Instructor to stop their turn to avoid a collision.
EMB-170 flight crew reported severe wind gust resulted in loss of directional control; rejected takeoff; and a gate return.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with a helicopter on approach to LZU airport.
Flight Attendants reported the escape slide partially deployed when the agent opened the door at the gate. Maintenance removed the slide to allow deplaning.

Air carrier flight crew reported being issued a low altitude alert while on a visual approach. The crew immediately climbed; recaptured the glide slope; and executed a stable approach and safe landing.

B737 First Officer reported a sudden loss in altitude of 500 feet during cruise while the aircraft was passing through turbulence. Captain recovered aircraft and flight continued with no injuries or damage reported.
BE-76 pilot reported left landing gear collapsed after landing.
Corporate pilot reported after being cleared for takeoff they began to enter the runway then stopped when they observed a Cessna on short final.

Air carrier Captain reported encountering surface conditions that led to NIL braking action resulting in loss of control of the aircraft during taxi.  The aircraft came to a stop on the taxiway and they coordinated with airport operations for recovery.
A320 Captain reported a Flight Attendant falling and getting injured during an abrupt braking to avoid a wingtip collision with another aircraft during taxi-out.
Tug driver reported they had to stop pushback to avoid a collision with a jet that taxied behind the pushback.
Fractional jet First Officer reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on a visual approach to TUPJ/EIS airport.

B737-700 flight crew reported hearing a loud bang from the nose gear area during the takeoff roll. In flight; the hydraulic system malfunctioned and the crew performed a manual gear extension prior to landing.
ZMA Center Controller reported repeated radar and communication site outages in the Oceanic Area create unsafe situations. The reporter states there are no backup systems to the main sites.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they flew in controlled airspace without authorization.

Air carrier Captain reported a frequent runway icing hazard on LGA's runway 04-22 that is not depicted in charts or NOTAMs. The Captain states that due to the unique construction of the elevated runway over water; a section of the runway freezes earlier than the rest creating a reduced braking action hazard.
B757 flight crew reported fault messages for multiple systems appeared during take off and continued during climb out. The flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
Air carrier Captain notified Dispatcher during cruise of Dry Ice in lower forward compartment with no overburn applied to flight plan. The Dispatcher sent an amended data to flight crew via ACARS.
A320 Captain reported a wheelchair was loaded in the cargo compartment; but failed to verify that the wheelchair was included in the aircraft loading document prior to gate departure.

Air carrier technician reported during scheduled maintenance they discovered an oil leak on #1 engine in the turbine rear frame area. The oil leak was caused by an oil inlet cover bolts that backed out due to not being safety wired per AMM.
B737 flight crew reported forgetting to reengage the autopilot and overshot the cleared altitude after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B737 on descent into ORD.

Air carrier Captain reported an overweight landing at destination airport caused by failure to monitor fuel burn during flight.  Captain cited rusty pilot skills caused by COVID inactivity as a contributing factor.
Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC during departure with an aircraft that was entering the traffic pattern at a nearby airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict with a ramp vehicle during taxi into gate.

A Boeing 737 flight crew reported the #1 engine surged several times during the approach phase of flight; which necessitated reducing power on the engine for the remainder of the approach and landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported a near mid-air collision with a UAS while they were on final approach.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion over NOTAM clarity and an ATC clearance resulted in an incursion to a closed taxiway at HNL.
GA flight instructor reported pattern entry as another aircraft did also resulted in evasive action and an NMAC.
C560XL flight crew reported a STAB MIS COMP Light Illuminated during cruise. The crew elected to divert to a nearby airport for a precautionary landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPWS alert during approach. Pilot readjusted visual aim point and landed uneventfully.

Military instructor helicopter pilot reported a near miss while conducting training operations at a remote site for student training; when a helicopter performed a low altitude go-around and conflicted with another helicopter in a formation; then had a near miss with the lead helicopter in the formation.

General aviation pilot reported an engine failure due to lack of fuel resulting in an off airport landing; near the non-towered airstrip. No injuries or damage occurred and once fuel was added; the aircraft was flown to the nearby airstrip.
B767-300 First Officer reported a Hazmat loading configuration error during preflight. The Hazmat box was crushed on the bottom of the stack even though the box was labeled 'Do Not Stack'.
Corporate flight crew flight reported a fume event shortly after take off resulted in return to departure airport.
B777-200 Captain reported a main wheel fire was reported by ground personnel. ARFF extinguished the fire and maintenance was notified.
C208 Captain reported effort to extinguish smoke and flame in engine compartment per checklist; resulted in engine over-temp and airport rescue and firefighting action.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported a Hazmat parcel missing the shipper's required information attached to the box.
Maintenance Technician reported missing insulation in the cargo pit causing high temperatures due to the pneumatic duct.
A321 Captain reported receiving a Caution / ECAM displayed for HYD Green Reservoir Low Level Message on descent. The flight crew continued to landing at destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported using Taxiway RB instead of the cleared Taxiway RA due to the concern that the wingtip clearance via Taxiway RA for widebody aircraft may be insufficient.
Air Carrier Captain reported an oil odor during boarding with 'APU Bleeds On'. Maintenance removed the aircraft from service due to repeat APU malfunctions.
Student pilot reported losing control of the C-172 during landing. The aircraft left the pavement; hit a taxiway light and was then brought to a complete stop in the adjacent grass area.
Captain reported an airborne fume event in which he experienced persistent physiological symptoms.

First Officer reported suffering gastrointestinal distress after landing which precluded an immediate post arrival walk around. While the First Officer was in the lavatory;  a brake fire was noticed by ground personnel and that information was relayed to the crew.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while entering the downwind for landing and the need for evasive action to avoid a collision.
A320 Captain reported the aircraft rolled forward after brake release when push crew failed to communicate and follow procedures for disconnect. Captain applied brakes to avoid hitting the tug.
GA flight instructor; a student on a solo flight; and the instructor of the solo student reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern during approach.
A319 flight crew reported loss of both ELAC's (Elevator and Aileron Computer) at cruise. Flight Crew requested priority handling and continued to destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported having to abruptly stop the aircraft while turning onto the gate lead at ORD due to a potential collision with baggage tug.
B737 flight crew reported multiple avionics instrument failures in cruise flight. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
ERJ-145 First Officer reported on a maintenance test flight receiving a failed thrust reverser disagree alert resulted in a rejected takeoff and return to maintenance.
An Air Carrier pilot reported the auto park system failed while parking. When they initiated parking again with ramp agents the aircraft rolled backwards one to two feet.
B747 Captain reported a main landing gear failure on the right hand body gear on taxi after landing. The aircraft stopped on the taxiway and was raised up to free the strut and was towed to the gate.
SA-227 Captain reported the inflight shutdown of the right engine because of high oil pressure. Reporter stated that he was advised by maintenance personnel that the oil may have been over serviced.

Ramp agent reported the pilots released the brakes while the aircraft was being loaded; resulting in aircraft movement. Reporter cited new language for pushback communications as a contributing factor.

CRJ700 Captain reported gate return for anti ice warning messages resulted in maintenance failure to correct issue.  Captain also reported when aircraft was re-positioned the company intended to put aircraft back in service.
Instructor pilot reported an aircraft landed behind them on the runway before they had exited the runway.
DA40 Instructor pilot reported a fuel transfer problem resulting in diverting to another airport and landing safely at that airport.
C152 pilot reported engine failure and a precautionary landing after an engine overhauled by himself failed in flight.
Student pilot reported landing with a slight crosswind and the right wheel 'locked up' resulting in a runway excursion.
Air carrier Captain reported a near miss with a UAS during final approach.
Cessna 172 pilot reported experiencing an alternator failure during a reposition flight; resulting in the return to the departure airport where the pilot landed safely.
Ramp Agent reported a malfunction of the push back tug capture system resulted in damage to the aircraft.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported someone shooting a firearm at their UAS during cruise flight. The reporter had to briefly fly past visual line of sight to land from another direction.
Cessna 177RG pilot reported during roll out from landing the nose gear collapsed.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a Stall Protection System abnormal in flight and diverted to a suitable airport.
Instructor and student reported descending below Minimum Descent Altitude; which they attributed to an incorrect altimeter setting; and turbulence.
General aviation pilot reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern at GPH non-towered airport resulted in taking evasive action.
BE35 pilot reported a safe landing following a partial power loss shortly after takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported during taxi to the runway; an aircraft taxied in front of them requiring the Captain to apply brakes abruptly to avoid a collision.
Navion pilot reported loss of throttle control on takeoff. Pilot returned to departure airport and after landing found the throttle cable linkage was disconnected.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported discovering a container with CAO [Cargo Aircraft Only] label during aircraft loading process.
GA pilot reported an aircraft overtook and flew overhead them resulting in a NMAC during departure.
Flight Instructor reported their PA28 experienced a loss of power and diverted to a nearby airport.
A320 pilot reported loss of hydraulic pressure and diversion to nearby suitable airport.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported their UAS altitude data displayed measured altitude in meters instead of feet; resulting in flying above their LAANC authorized altitude.
PA28 Pilot reported NMAC during departure climb from airport.
Air ambulance helicopter pilot reported an airborne conflict with a UAS while on approach to the helicopter pad.

A319 First Officer reported receiving an ECAM for ENG 1 Thrust Reverser Fault shortly after takeoff around 1;000 ft. The Captain set the affected engine to idle and the crew requested vectors for an immediate return to the departure airport.
E145 Captain reported a number 2 engine failure at cruise followed by a diversion with a single engine landing.
CNO Tower Controller reported a light sport aircraft engine failure during takeoff roll.
Air taxi flight crew reported the hold-short and runway clearance markings are close to each other on Taxiway Echo at HNL and they had crossed the hold-short lines without realizing it.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a fume event during boarding. The Captain elected to execute a gate evacuation into the passenger terminal and requested ARFF equipment.
Flight Instructor reported striking a light post during parking was due to distraction while talking with a student.
Cessna 208 pilot reported engine cowling began to rise after takeoff. Pilot returned to departure airport and found the cowling had not been properly latched.
Maule M5 pilot reported engine malfunction in cruise flight. Post-flight maintenance inspection revealed exhaust leaks and cylinder damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported a TCAS RA with another aircraft on a parallel approach to ONT airport.
An air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming affect of aircraft systems resulted in multiple navigation system malfunctions.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near midair collision with a UAS while on arrival. They reported the incident to ATC and landed with no other problems.

GA flight instructor with student reported an NMAC while practicing maneuvers in ZME Class E airspace near area A-291C.  The instructor took over the controls and executed a shallow dive to avoid a possible collision with the other aircraft.
BE90 pilot conducting an RNAV approach descended below the FAF altitude and received a terrain warning.

Instructor and trainee reported a loss of aircraft control during the takeoff phase of a touch and go landing. Instructor applied excessive braking during rejected takeoff after hearing ATC directions meant for another aircraft.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported their UAS flew into tree branches during a preprogrammed flight. The UAS was unrecoverable.
Flight Instructor flying a Piper Archer aircraft reported engine malfunction in traffic pattern.
Air carrier flight crew reported an engine failure on short final. The crew initiated a go-around procedure and diverted to an alternate airport for a safe landing.

First Officer reported after the gear handle was selected to the down position; both the main landing gear indicated a triple chime and a 'GEAR DISAGREE' aural a few seconds after selecting gear down. This was followed by an amber HYD 3 LO PRESS message and the hydraulic synoptic page displayed 0% in the HYD 3 reservoir. The flight crew performed a go around to a landing.

Air Carrier Safety Analyst reported undeclared Dangerous Goods had been shipped without required Hazmat documentation. Packaged was missing Hazmat label and improperly packaged for air shipment. Package smelled of petrol and package was damp due to spillage.
PA-30 pilot reported landing gear collapse due to torque tube failure. Pilot also reported non-compliance with an AD requiring landing gear bungee cord replacement.

A320 pilot reported a hydraulic system overheat malfunction after climbing to cruise altitude. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and the hydraulic system returned to normal temperature and the aircraft landed safely.
Flight Instructor reported being unaware of the 48 hour no fly rule and instructing the day after being vaccinated for COVID-19.

Honda Jet PIC reported a loss of directional control during landing rollout when attempting to correct a center line drift after taking controls from the SIC. Ultimately the aircraft stopped on runway with only a left blown tire.
Air carrier maintenance store personnel reported an ongoing Hazmat violation due to the lack of proper storage cabinet during Hazmat handling.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported a loss of numerous systems after takeoff. The flight crew requested priority handling and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air carrier maintenance personnel reported HAZMAT had been shipped undeclared and without the proper documentation.

B737 MAX 8 First Officer reported noticing the nose strut was fully extended during pre-flight of the aircraft and then compressing back down traveling 'approximately' two feet.  The First Officer expressed concerns with possibly being struck while being under the aircraft as the nose compresses. Reporter suggested the use of tail stands should be implemented on all MAX 8 aircraft to prevent this from occurring.
