AMD Alarus pilot reported they could not control the engine RPMs due to a loose throttle cable. Pilot landed safely after shutting down the engine.
B737 flight crew reported releasing the parking brake without acknowledgement and confirmation of the Final Dangerous Goods (DG) Summary.
Air carrier First Officer reported an EGPWS sink rate warning; an unstable approach and firm landing.
B757 flight crew reported that a hydraulic system failure resulted in a precautionary landing.
Air carrier Captain turning into their gate reported they had to stop unexpectedly to avoid a collision with vehicles crossing their path.
Air carrier Captain reported uncommanded movement by the push crew while at the gate. Flight continued the push back.
Mooney pilot reported alternator failure at cruise. The pilot lowered the landing gear early and landed safely.
B737 Captain reported forgetting to send the final DG summary message prior to brake release but got it in immediately after brake release.
B737 pilot conducting pre-flight reported crew oxygen system not working. Maintenance discovered oxygen bottle valve closed.
A Flight Attendant reported an inadvertent slide deployment.
B777 Captain reported experiencing wake turbulence and deviating from assigned airspeed to mitigate the effects and maintain a stable approach without informing ATC of intentions.
An observer reported they saw an airborne conflict between a UAS and a fixed wing aircraft near an airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an ACARS notification of unmanifested Hazmat on board. Flight crew continued to destination without incident.
C150 pilot flying reported a loss of oil pressure and erratic engine RPMs after leveling off in cruise. The reporter safely diverted to a nearby airport.
Flight crew reported partial engine power loss during cruise. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier Captain reported during departure from IAH the mode control panel altitude window indicated incorrectly and the altitude alerter sounded continuously.
Flight Attendant reported that while preparing the doors for arrival; the gate agent opened the door before it was disarmed causing the slide to deploy.
A319 First Officer reported being alerted by TCAS and noticed VFR traffic in the approach corridor.

B737 Flight Crew reported the Number 1 Engine Oil Bypass Light illuminated during the climb out. After conferring with Dispatch and Maintenance; the decision was first made that the engine did not need to be shut down. However; as the flight continued to the destination and the Oil Bypass Light would not extinguish; the engine was then shut down.
B767 Captain reported they turned the incorrect direction on initial climb and when correcting exceeded 30 degrees of bank; resulted in two bank angle warnings.
Air carrier pilot reported they had to go-around due to several erroneous radio altimeter indications while above the runway. On the second approach the radio altimeter operated normally.
Flight Crew reported engine loss of power during takeoff roll.
Ground employee reported difficulty moving aircraft off a specific gate due to reduced clearance.
Air taxi First Officer reported temporary loss of aircraft control on base leg of a visual approach resulting in a stall warning.

Air Carrier Captain is questioning the logic behind the decision to descend to FL290 during the emergency decompression or emergency drift-down maneuver. The Captain believes the correct altitude should be FL250.
A320 First Officer reported CFIT event during approach that was due to maintenance before performed on the glide slope equipment but not being announced on ATIS.
PA-24 pilot reported an in-flight loss of oil during cruise in daylight VMC. The pilot diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.

Air carrier pilots reported a critical ground conflict at a non-towered airport. After receiving takeoff clearance from ATC and crossing the hold line; an unseen and unheard aircraft announced a go around.
BE36 pilot reported the engine failed on short final approach and during landing a flap struck a runway light.
An Air Carrier pilot reported the Tower hours of operation for STT are not accurate in the Jeppesen Chart and company pages.
Flight Attendant reported a hard bounced landing which caused the Flight Attendant back and hip pain.

Technicians reported a rig pin attached to a red streamer was left installed in the #2 engine when the engine cowls and thrust reversers were closed and a post maintenance engine run was accomplished.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS jamming during cruise which resulted in the failure of RNP requirement. ATC was advised and flight continued to the destination.

Pilot reported being instructed to communicate with DAB tower through the frequency 118.1 instead of the published frequency 120.7 and that it is common local knowledge to routinely use the unpublished 118.1 frequency.
CRJ-200 Captain reported hazardous tug operations resulted in taking evasive action

B737 First Officer reported experiencing severe turbulence during cruise that resulted in the autopilot disconnecting with slight airspeed and altitude deviations. There was no damage to the aircraft and no injuries to any persons. The flight continued uneventfully.

General aviation pilot reported not being able to see the runway numbers at PMP while on final approach and inadvertently overflew the runway and became disoriented. After safely landing; the reporter confirmed with a line person that there have been issues with the runway numbers which would be repainted soon.
B737 Flight Crew reported two large birds hit the nose cone during departure; resulting in an airspeed disagree message. The Flight Crew completed an air turn back to landing.
First Officer reported compressor stalls forced an air turn back and precautionary landing.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during cruise in MCO Class E airspace requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
PA-28 Flight Instructor with student reported an off-airport landing resulted following engine failure related to fuel starvation.

B737 Captain reported ground personnel advised his left main landing gear was on fire after setting brakes at gate.  Captain reported that maintenance observed a fluid leak from brake line and no apparent burn damage after CFR personnel sprayed the gear area.
Flight instructor reported on takeoff observing a crop duster fly opposite direction not in communication.
Flight Instructor reported on initial climb out experiencing a large loss of engine power resulting in return to the airport where they landed safely.
Single Engine Pilot reported a possible airspace violation. The pilot suspects a GPS error contributed to the event and questions whether it could be 5G related.
Helicopter Mechanic reported incorrect installation of the main rotor mast was discovered by another mechanic and subsequently corrected.
Small aircraft pilot reported turning too early on an IFR departure with a high rate of descent and unknowingly cleared the airport hangars by 150 ft.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an altitude alert from ATC during approach. Flight crew returned to safe altitude and continued to landing.
B777 flight crew reported the cockpit filled with heavy smoke during takeoff roll. Flight crew rejected takeoff; cleared the runway; and was towed to ramp.
C172 instructor pilot with trainee reported a lose of engine power in their single engine aircraft. The instructor; with assistance from ATC; divert to a closer airport and landed safely.
B737-700 Captain reported not checking in with Dispatch and ensuring that HAZMAT was correctly noted on the flight release form.

Flight Instructor reported making evasive maneuvers 'several' times to avoid another aircraft in the pattern. The Instructor reported the Tower Controller's instructions caused one of the NMAC's. The reporter stated Flight Schools' use of similar call signs contributed to the confusion.
B737 Captain reported Final Dangerous Goods document not onboard. After pushback; flight crew requested and received Final Dangerous Goods summary and departed without further issues.
Air carrier Captain reported reacting to an RA triggered by a light aircraft during their arrival into PVD; and then executing a go-around after noticing FOD on the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported a potential altitude deviation during descent on the FROGZ4 arrival; and recommended the procedure be modified for clarity.

Air carrier First Officer reported a banging sound was heard during initial descent and during the subsequent aircraft pitch changes that followed. Upon arrival; Maintenance found a cargo pallet had shifted from one zone to another.
B737 flight crew reported shutting down the left engine on a taxiway after being alerted by another aircraft that there were flames coming out of the left engine.
An air carrier flight crew reported they braked suddenly while taxiing into the hold bay to avoid colliding with a commercial fixed wing taxiing to the same spot.
A320 Captain reported a landing gear malfunction.

Air carrier Captain and another air carrier First Officer reported a critical ground conflict between the two aircraft during a taxi in night conditions. The former aircraft's Captain was able to abruptly stop the aircraft after noticing the other aircraft.

Air carrier Ground Personnel reported a communication breakdown between Gate Agent and a none English speaking passenger regarding Li-Ion battery in checked bag. Flight Attendant assisted with the communication issue.
GIV flight crew reported smoke in the passenger cabin during cruise. Flight crew then safely diverted to a nearby suitable airport.

A321 Captain reported a ground conflict with a truck traveling at a high rate of speed requiring evasive action. Captain also reported overall concerns with the lack of SOP procedures utilized by ground personnel.
Flight Attendant reported calling in fatigued due to scheduling and physiological issues. Reporter stated that reserve flight attendants were being overworked due to short staffing issues.

General aviation air taxi pilot reported a traffic conflict while assigned a STAR under IFR and ATC control. ATC descended the aircraft on the arrival; and the pilot maneuvered the aircraft away from the traffic in response to a TCAS traffic alert.

A320 pilot reported a hydraulic system failure during cruise. After consulting with maintenance and dispatch the flight continued to the destination where the post flight inspection confirmed a hydraulic fluid leak.

Inspector reported that a Technician did not ensure an Inspector was present during maintenance action on a Horizontal Jack Screw AD. The Manager and Inspection Supervisor signed the Inspection blocks instead of ensuring the step were performed correctly.
A320 Flight Crew reported a Rejected Take Off when the Yellow Hydraulic system showed low quantity and had to be towed back to the gate.
C172 pilot reported left brake malfunctioned on landing roll out causing a runway excursion.
Air Carrier First Officer reported issues at MYNN with taxiway signage and faded hold short lines; resulting in confusion about the aircraft's position at a runway.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a gate agent opened a disarmed door before Flight Attendant had told them the door could be opened. Escape slide did not deploy.
Pilot reported Rotor RPM decay and made an off airport landing. After maintenance inspection; pilot flew back to base airport.
Chicago Center Controller reported an aircraft deviated from direct MOBIL clearance because they entered the fix MOBLE in their FMS which is also a fix in the NAS.
LR45 flight crew reported diverting and landing safely after experiencing multiple system anomalies that led to a precautionary shutdown of the left engine.
B737-700 flight crew reported loss of electrical power in cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
B777 captain reported during pre-flight planning they refused the aircraft due to a carry forward item for the number 2 engine that requires removal in 5 cycles or less.
Instructor pilot reported a conflict prior to landing. A non-communicating aircraft entered the runway forcing the instructor to take evasive action.
B737 pilot crew reported the aircraft began to roll aft unexpectedly when the brakes were released for push back requiring a quick reapplication of the brakes resulting in an abrupt stop.

Air carrier flight crew reported they received a TCAS RA caused by an aircraft landing on the parallel runway during final approach to Runway 16R at DEN airport.  The crew responded to the RA; but descended too low and received a glideslope warning. Once the RA was resolved; the crew were able to stabilize the aircraft and continued their approach and landing.

Air carrier Captain reported refusing an aircraft that had an expired Residual Disinsection Certificate. If operated; the aircraft would require fumigation procedures using permethrin (PER) at an international destination with crew and passengers aboard. Captain also reported the use of PER is not allowed in the United States as evidence suggests use may have a variety of toxic effects.
MD11 Captain reported failure to properly program FMS prior to takeoff.

Flight Instructor with student reported experiencing a near miss during taxi with a back-taxiing aircraft. Flight Instructor also observed a near miss between the same aircraft and a departing aircraft.
Adjacent TRACON Controllers reported a traffic conflict between their sectors when one of the aircraft did not climb as anticipated by one of the controllers.
B737 Captain reported a panel behind the right main landing gear separated from the aircraft while on approach.  Flight crew landed safely and observed flap track fairing damage during post flight.
MD-11 Captain reported loss of #1 and #2 air systems in cruise and elected to perform an air turnback and precautionary landing at departure airport.

Air carrier wing walker reported a Ground Conflict; Critical during gate pushback when a company vehicle drove behind the pushback aircraft and ignoring verbal warnings. The event was a violation of driving on the ramp.
Cessna pilot reported the right main gear strut collapsed on taxi out damaging the propeller. The aircraft was jacked up and the strut extended; and was towed to the ramp area for maintenance.
Instructor Pilot with Trainee reported a NMAC while on final approach and a subsequent go around in a busy traffic pattern.
Air Carrier B737 pilot reported uncontrollable cabin pressure during climb. Flight crew performed an air turn back and made a precautionary landing at departure airport.
B777 flight crew reported a rejected take-off due to inoperative airspeed indications. Post flight inspection found blockage of pitot tubes.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft on final approach in IMC deviated off course and descended below terrain minimums. Radio transmissions from the aircraft were intermittent and broken.
Air carrier Captain reported having to divert due to fuel issues related to ATC Zero Center closure and re-routes.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flight into controlled airspace without authorization.
PA-30 Flight instructor on training flight with student reported gear up landing.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported installation of a used engine cylinder without proper paperwork.
C172 student pilot and instructor reported a taxiway excursion into the grass due to the brakes malfunctioning during taxi.
Captain reported receiving a LAHSO clearance while on high speed landing roll. The Captain highlighted this as an unsafe practice and questioned the safety of the procedure.
A VFR pilot reported a NMAC with a small aircraft which was not communicating on the published CTAF frequency.
A321 flight crew reported a loss of aircraft control during climb near an area of convective weather. Crew reported auto-thrust seemed sluggish for selected mode and took over manual control.
Air Carrier Captain reported confusion regarding information on the Jeppesen 10-9 vs. instructions given by ATIS and Ground Control.
C172 pilot reported the aircraft turned to the right and stopped during landing rollout.
B777 flight crew reported encountering severe wake turbulence 20 miles in trail of another B777 on their trans-Pacific flight.

Flight crew reported striking a baggage cat that was staged inside the gate area when taxiing under mashaller's control for parking. The baggage cart caused damage to the wing; requiring maintenance action.
Agricultural pilot reported an airborne conflict with a UAS that was crop-spraying in a nearby field. The pilot took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

Learjet 70 flight crew reported receiving a triple chime and a Red R Pylon Overheat CAS Message while climbing through FL340. The flight crew ran checklists and shut down the affected engine for a diversion and immediate landing at a nearby airport.
A321 pilot reported multiple engine stalls then engine failure.  Flight crew diverted to nearest airport and landed safely.
Air carrier Ground Load personnel reported an arrival container was incorrectly labeled with Dangerous Goods contents. The container did not contain any Dangerous Goods.
General aviation pilot reported being told they had landed on a runway already occupied by an aircraft.

CRJ Captain reported a CFIT event during an unstable approach which was followed by a safe approach and landing. The Captain added that the approach for aircraft type should be modified for the specific model.
ERJ 170 pilot reported bird strike during takeoff climb. Returned to departure airport.
Captain reported a powered wheelchair notification form was not completed by ramp agent before departure. Form was completed during flight.
CE-680 corporate crew reported discovering main tire damage during post flight. The crew was informed they had struck runway edge lights during takeoff roll.
Flight instructor reported experiencing a NMAC while conducting instrument approach instruction with a student using a vision-limiting device.
SR22 pilot reported loss of engine power during initial climb. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
B737 Captain reported #2 air conditioning pack control problems in cruise. The flight crew elected to divert and make an uneventful landing.
Single pilot reported a propeller strike while exiting the runway.

Ramp personnel reported uncommanded aircraft movement without coordination with flight crew when entering the pushback vehicle. The aircraft movement was stopped by the chocks which were still in place.

Technicians reported communication and coordination problems led to not installing a brake axle spacer when replacing a main wheel tire assembly. The tire and wheel assembly failed catastrophically in service.
B737-800 Captain reported a Flight Attendant sustained a minor injury during a wake turbulence encounter with a preceding MD11 at FL380.
Air carrier flight crew reported aircraft movement while parked at the gate due to parking brake not set.

JMB VL3 pilot performing first solo; upon landing drifted left of center line; and applied brakes which locked up and resulted in a loss of control and a runway excursion. The aircraft struck an airport sign; and stopped in a grass area of the runway; where a partial landing gear failure occurred. The pilot evacuated and was not injured.

B767-300 Relief Pilot reported the crew experienced a Forward Equipment Cooling issue followed by a Cabin Altitude Master Warning during arrival. The crew ran the appropriate checklist and as the aircraft was descending the cabin altitude was holding at 10;000 feet; which allowed the crew to continue to their destination.
Pilot reported a NMAC during base turn for landing with a helicopter also on base turn. The pilot completed a safe landing although with less spacing than intended.
PA-44 pilot reported an engine failure in cruise resulting from loss of fuel due to fuel line leak. Pilot secured engine and landed safely.
CRJ-700 Captain reported a rejected takeoff after a LH Engine Bleed Amber Caution Message was displayed. The flight crew contacted Maintenance Control and returned to the gate for Maintenance.
C680A Captain reported a hydraulic failure and a decision to divert.
First Officer reported charting error on page 10-9 on the Airport Diagram.

B737 MAX Air carrier Captain reported erroneous altimeter setting error warnings while on final approach. After verifying with ATC the correct altimeter setting and aircraft altitude; the flight crew continued the approach and the warning subsequently cleared.
Air carrier flight crew reported anomaly with approach altitude allowing descent below MSA.
C172 Flight Instructor reported engine power loss and returned to the departure airport and made a successful landing.
C172 instructor pilot reported a power loss resulting in a landing off airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing unexpected turbulence at FL400 during initial descent to SKBO airport.
CRJ-900 Captain reported ATC failure to separate taxiway traffic resulted in evasive action taken and a critical ground conflict event.

PA-46 pilot reported a runway excursion upon landing occurred when the rudder pedals did not respond to input. Pilot stated that during landings prior to the event the aircraft's brake and rudder system appeared to not be functioning normally and questioned if maintenance had been properly done.

Air Taxi Captain reported while preparing to take off at a non-towered airport; another aircraft took off from the same runway opposite end without making any radio calls. Captain held position on runway until other aircraft had passed.
Learjet 45 Captain reported a R ENG CMPTR FAULT amber CAS message illuminated in cruise. The flight crew performed an in flight shut down and returned to departure airport.

A320 Flight Crew reported a bird strike during takeoff resulting in a crack on the First Officer's lower front edge window.  The flight crew informed ATC and Dispatch of the situation and decided to return to the departure airport.

Flight Instructor reported that during the landing rollout another aircraft took off from the same runway and flew directly over them. While the flight instructor was discussing the event over the radio with other aircraft in the traffic pattern; the departing aircraft asked 'what the problem was' with his departure.
C210 pilot reported losing consciousness at FL250 after his oxygen hose became kinked.
BE9L pilot reported during taxi after landing; a propeller struck a taxiway light.
GA pilot reported an NMAC while in cruise flight with another small aircraft that was executing practice maneuvers.
Small aircraft Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with an aircraft enroute in cruise flight in the vicinity of SAV VOR.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported; during preflight checks of the flight attendant jump seat; it was discovered the harness and seat belt are not long enough to safely strap in.
B767 Flight Crew reported pressurization problem inflight.

Air taxi Captain reported crossing a LAHSO line at HPN after following the yellow taxi lines and having to perform a tight turn. The reporter's larger aircraft did not have the turn radius to properly complete this turn.
Air taxi Captain reported lack of clarity and EFB display of airport taxiway restrictions resulted in a taxiway incursion.  Captain also reported the need for clarification of taxiway restrictions.
B767-300 crew reported a bird strike prior to V1; causing high vibration readings on engine. Crew returned to departure airport.
Falcon 2000 Captain reported safely returning to departure airport after experiencing multiple electrical system anomalies.
Gulfstream G-IV flight crew reported loss of both flight guidance computers on final approach. Reporter stated this is a recurring issue.
B767-300 Captain reported the Master Caution Light and multiple gear warnings annunciated after takeoff. The flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
Air carrier flight crew reported engine malfunction during departure climb. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Pilot on approach to land reported a NMAC caused by non-adherence by other aircraft to special NOTAM for arrivals.
An air carrier flight crew reported while taxiing for departure they observed another aircraft on a conflicting route and braked suddenly to avoid a collision.
B737 Captain reported a cracked flight deck window and diverted to an alternate airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the tug operator disconnected from the aircraft before pilot set brakes and the aircraft rolled over the towbar.
Pilot of a light twin aircraft departing T67 airport reported passing within 50 feet of a car shortly after takeoff.
Flight Crew reported a pack valve failure caused the cabin to not pressurize and resulted in descent and precautionary landing.
B777 Captain reported releasing the parking brakes and after learning that there was dry ice on board; contacted Dispatch to receive and acknowledge the missing Final DG Summary.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during approach. Flight crew corrected and continued to landing.

ZNY ARTCC Controllers reported the wrong aircraft was given a climb instruction due to being on the incorrect frequency and indicated similar frequencies at Boston ARTCC and New York ARTCC causing communication errors is a frequent occurrence.
B737-800 Captain reported a ground conflict during taxi-in to Gate with a truck requiring aggressive braking action to avoid a possible collision.
An Air Carrier Captain on short final to Runway 1 at Las Vegas reported strobe lights from a street event were a distraction.
C172 pilot reported being notified of cutting off an aircraft on final after taking off from a non-towered airport.
Flight Attendant reported unlocking the door before it was disarmed. Flight Attendant held door open a few inches so escape slide did not deploy.
GA Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC while on the approach pattern of LAF airport requiring evasive action to avoid a collision.
Flight Instructor and Student reported they rejected takeoff due to a CAS message and the aircraft did not stop until past the runway end in the grass.
Flight Instructor on initial climb from a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with an aircraft crossing their upwind leg.
Aeronca Champ pilot reported an NMAC and a wake vortex encounter with a light twin aircraft on arrival into OGD.

Air carrier captain reported during the initial approach a late change to the STAR arrival; runway change; a problem loading the FMS with the new STAR and continual GPS jamming in LTBB airspace. The captain states these events caused a track deviation.
B737-800 Captain reported the thrust levers auto advanced re-accelerating the aircraft after touchdown requiring immediate action to move the levers to idle and slow the aircraft down.
Pilot flying H269C helicopter reported drive shaft bearing blew in cruise flight. Pilot landed off airport uneventfully.
GA pilot reported while in cruise flight encountering mountain wave conditions resulting in loss of engine performance and loss of altitude. The GA pilot diverted to the closest airport.
Baron 58 pilot reported during taxi they stopped to clean the windshield due to poor visibility. The aircraft continued to roll off the side of the runway into the mud.
Flight Instructor in a C172 reported a NMAC with a faster aircraft which overtook them during their initial climbs from a non-towered airport.
CE-700 Captain reported several crew alerting system (CAS) messages during cruise flight and again during landing.
Flight Instructor and solo student pilot reported the student lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
A319 First Officer reporter a loss of aircraft control after executing a rejected takeoff.  Reporter stated surface contamination contributed to the event.

Air carrier First Officer reported seeing a lighted UAS off the right side of the aircraft during final approach into a Class B airport during night VMC conditions. The flight reported the UAS to the control tower and landed safely.

A320 Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during descent which was localized to the cockpit. Flight crew completed the appropriate fumes procedures and submitted company fumes forms as well as a logbook discrepancy write up upon gate arrival.
Air carrier Captain reported identifying two separate NOTAMs amending the minimums for the same approach resulting in confusion.
Reports of PAPI lights to PTKK inoperative but not NOTAM'd out of service. Reporter states PAPI and VASI do not operate at PGSN but Jeppesen charts state VASI is available.
Flight Instructor flying C340 aircraft reported engine failure inflight.
Pilot reported the left main brake seized during landing. The aircraft departed the runway and the aircraft was damaged with no injuries.
Cirrus SF50 pilot reported encountering severe turbulence in cruise flight that resulted in loss of 800 feet of altitude.
Citation II Captain reported a safe landing following an incident involving autopilot anomalies that made the aircraft difficult to control.
GA pilot reported RIC airport taxiway signage confusing around HS1 resulted in a taxiway incursion.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC in SEA Approach airspace and during evasive maneuvering they climbed into Bravo airspace.
Flight Crew in cruise reported on another aircraft requesting clearance to descend due to pressurization issue.
Captain reported receiving a RED master warning/gear disagree with the selection of gear handle down and a HYD 3 LO PRESS Caution message. The flight crew returned to landing at destination airport.

Air carrier Captain reported loss of control during taxi when the nose wheel began to skid during a tight turn. The Captain states it is possible the nose began to skid on the taxiway paint. Maintenance changed the tires.
A Flight Instructor reported a NMAC on downwind after departing when a subsequent departing aircraft deviated from their clearance.
Air Carrier flight crew reported Dispatch notification of undeclared Hazmat shipment onboard. Missing Inaccessible Dangerous Goods information was sent to flight enroute.
B737 flight crew reported an excursion from assigned altitude occurred during the approach because they set the wrong altitude in the window.
Flight Attendant reported misgivings over Crew Coordination and Communication over compressor stalls during this and other flights; including ETOPS.
Air carrier Captain reported flying VOR Runway 25 approach to MRLB airport and being 5 degrees or more off centerline and offset to the runway.
DA20 student and Flight Instructor reported a gear collapse and prop strike event after a series of bounced landings.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported malfunction in flight deck instrumentation during descent. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
B737-800 Captain reported dispatch and maintenance mishandling of the flight Operations including the aircraft; destination issues and a viable backup plan.

Experimental aircraft pilot reported the engine quit during approach due to it sucking air from the right fuel tank. Pilot was high enough on the approach to perform a normal glide landing.  Reportedly; the right tank transfers fuel to the left-hand tank when the fuel selector is on the 'BOTH' position causing the right tank to go low on fuel.
Air carrier Captain reported spurious aircraft ADSB and GPS faults occurred during descent into DEN.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing GPS jamming and/or spoofing while in cruise. The GPS was rendered inoperable for the rest of the flight and the flight crew had to use iPads for navigation.
ZLC Center Controller reported issues with A320 aircraft that are receiving erroneous TCAS RA's when there is no other traffic around the aircraft.
Part 107 UAS Pilot reported learning of an airspace violation during a post flight meeting with their company's operations management.
CRJ-900 Captain reported after setting the heading cursor on the Primary Flight Display with the autopilot engaged; the aircraft began to wander off course.
B737 MAX 8 Flight Attendant reported excessively warm cabin temperatures during several flights resulted in passenger and crew illness.
Pilot reported another aircraft taxied over their aircraft wing; and reporter attempted to contact other pilot.  Other pilot did not appear to care about what had just happened.
Flight Instructor reported they observed their solo student take off from the non-towered airport; enter downwind and have a NMAC with an aircraft entering the pattern.
Air carrier flight crew reported responding to a TCAS RA to avoid traffic resulting in an NMAC.
B737 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing Number 1 engine failure in flight.
Air carrier flight crew noticed a UAS while on climb out and advised ATC.

Piper Cub pilot reported violent shaking of the engine just after takeoff. After turning back to land and completing a safe landing it was discovered that a portion of the aluminum propeller blade tip had gone missing.

B767 flight crew reported feeling a pressure bump on takeoff and continued. The flight crew determined that the left air conditioning pack had failed and elected to dump fuel and make an air turn back. The flight Crew made a precautionary landing at departure airport.
B777 flight crew reported an engine malfunction on departure resulting in an inflight shutdown and return to departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported the flight crew entered a closed taxiway at ORD and stated there were no NOTAMs regarding the closed taxiway.
A TRACON Controller reported they accepted a handoff from the adjacent facility of an aircraft that was below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight Instructor landing at a non-towered airport reported they observed an aircraft on short final from the opposite direction and expedited their taxi off the runway.
B767 Captain reported pressurization failure and a return to the departure airport.
Air Carrier flight crew reported TCAS RA on initial climbout from SAV airport.

General aviation rotor craft Instructor pilot reported a near miss while in the airport traffic pattern. The Instructor performed a low pass; re-entered the pattern; then lost communications with the tower and conflicted with another aircraft; then turned away from the conflict and returned to the home airport.
A Center Controller reported a UAS operated at the ceiling of the Special Use Airspace requiring the Controller to issue traffic calls to enroute traffic overflying airspace.
Air carrier Captain reported being informed by First Officer and Relief Pilot of a sudden 25 kt increase which resulted in an airspeed exceedance and altitude excursion.
B747-8F Flight crew reported loss of pressurization in cruise flight. Flight crew returned to the departure airport and landed uneventfully.

General aviation pilot reported receiving a low altitude warning from ATC while intercepting the initial approach in IMC weather; resulting in an unusual attitude on the missed approach. The pilot recovered and returned to land.

A319 Captain reported engine vibration indication lead to rejected takeoff and subsequent brake malfunction.  Captain also reported the possible cause was freezing temperatures; icing; and maintenance procedure failure.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in LAX Class B airspace.

Air carrier First Officer reported they intercepted the initial charted visual approach from a high altitude; resulting in over correction to establish on the glide slope and a low altitude alert from ATC. The Captain arrested the descent and landed.
Dispatcher reported an aircraft was pushed back past its assigned drop location too close to an aircraft pushing back from another gate.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported numerous diversions and deviations while navigating through severe weather; exacerbated by an MELed speed bug resulting in hail damage when finally reaching destination airport.
Student pilot reported they lost control of the aircraft landing in a gust of wind resulting in a runway excursion and prop strike on their first solo.

PA-60 Aerostar Captain reported being notified by a passenger of the aircraft cabin door not latched during the climb; then the door unlatched and separated from the aircraft. The Captain returned to the departure airport and landed safely; the passengers were not injured but the Captain suffered an arm injury due to the force of the relative wind.

Pilot Instructor reported a NMAC while on final approach. Another aircraft converged and turned in front of the Instructor's aircraft. The Instructor preformed a 360 degree turn before returning to land.
Ramp Agent reported a tow tractor malfunction allowed the aircraft to break free from the tug; spilling oil and hydraulic fluid onto the taxiway. The aircraft was not damaged.
MD-11 Captain reported #3 engine failure after takeoff and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing.
Technician reported a B737-700 arrived at the station without Krueger Flap Deflectors installed on #2 engine thrust reverser sleeve.
PA-28 Instructor Pilot and Student reported evacuating the aircraft on the ramp when they experienced and engine fire following multiple start attempts.
B737 pilot reported overbanking during approach resulted in a bank angle warning.
Government UAS pilot reported they conducted a UAS flight while out of currency for Part 107 requirements.

Learjet 35 flight crew reported hot air and smoke in the cockpit on takeoff. The flight crew requested priority handling made an immediate air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Instructor pilot reported a bounced landing resulted in a runway excursion on a training flight. The Instructor and Student evacuated the aircraft without injury and noticed the propeller damage.
Lear 45 First Officer reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after they experienced loss of cabin pressure at FL320.
TRACON Controller reported an LOA violation which caused confusion and a MVA violation.
Mooney M20 instructor pilot reported landing safely in a field after the engine quit in flight.
SNA Tower Instructor reported that an aircraft did not offset 15 degrees on takeoff as required per the AFD and came close to a commercial airliner also departing; on a parallel runway.

CRJ 900 First Officer reported an engine temperature exceedance at cruise and then during descent a bleed warning malfunction resulting in a cabin depressurization. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
EMB XRJ-145 Captain reported auto flight system malfunctioned during departure and arrival phase by uncommanded reversion to Roll-Go Around mode.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during approach to land when an aircraft on the runway delayed their takeoff. The aircraft on approach conducted a go-around and returned to land.
Air carrier pilot reported during approach that erroneous altitude and RA warnings continued until touchdown at MEM; citing possible 5G interference.
Ground employee reported a van failed to give way to an aircraft under tow resulting in the conflict.
B737-800 flight crew reported a fumes event during preflight while executing a special procedure for operating ground air and starting engines. After a delay the flight ultimately departed.
Technician reported a lockable latch that was installed on a B737 engine cowling was removed and replaced by a non lockable cowling latch when they were out on leave and was not documented correctly.
DA40 Flight Instructor and student reported alternator failure and diversion to nearby airport.
EMB-145LR Captain reported an inflight upset occurred when they encountered wake turbulence from a B737 at cruise altitude in ZJX ARTCC airspace.

Helicopter pilot reported an NMAC during departure with a military fighter doing a high speed pass over the runway. Tower controller issued helicopter a takeoff clearance which put them into the flight path of the fighter.
A B777-200 pilot reported weather radar failure during climbout towards forecast thunderstorms; resulting in an air turn back to departure airport.
ZMA Center Controller reported extended radar outages is causing ATC to lose radar contact with aircraft transitioning through the airspace.
Captain reported that dispatch was unable to supply the correct weight and balance information for a scheduled maintenance reposition flight.
Great Lakes TA2 pilot reported a loss of engine power during takeoff at low altitude and landed the aircraft off airport.
B737NG Captain reported loss of directional control while attempting to taxi in the gate. Gate area reportedly had de-icing fluid contamination that affected the available traction.

General aviation instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport on a training flight. The other aircraft was on the opposite direction approach then turned in close proximity to the instructors aircraft and followed the instructor in traffic pattern without prior communication.
B737-700 First Officer reported a communication breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch regarding missing signed NOTOC document.
PA-28 pilot reported engine failure on takeoff resulted in a return to the airport and a runway excursion during landing.

Helicopter pilot reported an NMAC with another Helicopter while transiting through Class B HNL airspace.  Reportedly; ATC never pointed out the traffic and an evasive maneuver was required to avoid a possible collision.
General aviation instructor reported a near miss during cruise flight to a practice area. The instructor performed an evasive maneuver to avoid a conflict.
SAT TRACON Controller reported vectoring two airliners off approach into SAT due to VFR traffic from 5C1 that was crossing the SAT final approach path.
Air carrier passenger reported noticing a wake turbulence encounter departing DCA airport.

Instructor with trainee reported a critical ground conflict. The instructor stated after clearing the water landing area to practice water landings; a small craft launched unknown to the pilots. The instructor reported the trainee sidestepped to the other side of the landing site.
B737-800 flight crew reported a near miss with another aircraft on approach.

Air Carrier Captain reported a communication breakdown between the Captain and Dispatch/Ramp regarding the missing DG and the new Hazmat verification SOP. Ultimately the DG arrived; loaded; complied with DG verification procedure and departed safely.
Corporate pilot reported SoCal Approach issued holding instructions to a fix; SKATE; while arriving into SAN. The fix was not available in the aircraft's database.
Pilot reported they almost taxied into a grass area at BLM airport due to lack of taxiway lighting; poor aircraft taxi lights; and insufficient warning of the hazard on the Jeppesen chart.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert while descending on the BFL FASTO2 arrival.  The crew expressed confusion over ATC's clearance verbiage; the lack of crossing restriction altitudes depicted other than MEA; and the operator's SOP for the conduct of the RNAV arrivals of this sort.
C525 Pilot reported a runway excursion on wet runway during the landing rollout.
C182 flight instructor on a training flight with student reported stuck throttle inflight resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC during approach and leveled off to avoid the other aircraft. Reportedly; the pilot of other aircraft was on the same approach but not in communication with ATC.
A flight crew reported observing a UAS pass by their aircraft at 5;500 ft while descending.  The incident was reported to the TRACON controller; then the flight continued and landed normally.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reports a NMAC and other questionable actions by another aircraft also on a training flight.
Pilot flying C182 aircraft reported NMAC with a King Air on climb out at a non-towered airport.
C172 pilot landing at a non-towered airport reported upon touchdown they saw a vehicle on the runway and conducted a touch and go to avoid it.
Air carrier First Officer reported response to RA resulted in an aircraft terrain warning. The flight executed a go around and returned to land.

Air carrier Captain reported that the monitor frequency when conducting PRM approaches at ORD is compromised by other ATC communications. Communications on the monitor frequency overlap primary radio frequency communications during this procedure.
Air carrier ground personnel reported Hazmat cargo shipped in wrong container.
A300 flight crew reported severe turbulence and loss of aircraft control during climb.

Air carrier flight crew reported a Dispatch request to return to the gate during taxi to correct the DG weights and documentation. After DG weights and documentation were corrected the flight departed safely.

Flight Service Specialist stated several UAS operators received authorization by an Enroute Facility to fly within airspace that requires communication with the FSS.  The reporter stated these operations had been not been coordinated with the FSS and they were unable to advise traffic in the area of specific UAS operations.

Flight Instructor reported a critical ground conflict during take-off roll when a helicopter entered the same runway unannounced from a taxiway further down the runway. The Flight Instructor continued takeoff and flew over the helicopter; narrowly clearing it.

Flight crew reported a dual ADC loss in cruise. The flight crew ran the QRH and checklists and began to descend. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.

Captain reported a stabilizer main trim runaway on take off and were able to disconnect and use alternate trim to control the aircraft. Captain elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing.
Survey pilot reported a near miss during cruise that required evasive action to avoid a collision.

Dispatcher reported confusion about whether 5G company policy allowed a CAT II approach at an airport. A previous ACARS message to flight crew indicated 5G NOTAM was in effect; but was unclear regarding CAT II approaches with AMOC approval.
B737-700 flight crew reported an asymmetrical trailing edge indication leading to a go around; then a normal landing.

Air carrier crew reported a loss of separation while on final approach when the preceding aircraft on the runway failed to turn off the runway in a timely manner. ATC directed the landing aircraft to go around; the aircraft returned and landed safely.
B757 Captain reported they descended below the glidepath on final approach and received a low altitude alert from ATC.
Pilot crossing a runway at a non-towered airport reported a near collision with another aircraft taking off on the same runway.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported encountering moderate turbulence and wind shear at FL330 that resulted in large airspeed variations; including a stall warning.
Center Controller reported assigning an altitude that was below the minimum IFR altitude to an aircraft approaching an airport. The minimum IFR altitudes in the local area had recently changed.
Ramp employee reported that while towing an aircraft to the gate; a nearby aircraft began to push back; almost clipping the wings of the aircraft being towed.

LAS Tower Controller reported Taxiway B5 causes confusion as it is wide enough for two aircraft in opposite directions to pass and is painted with passing lines as though it is two separate taxiways; but is labeled as a single taxiway.
A321 crew reported a possible bird strike during climb; which resulted in a cockpit window crack. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported the UAS app did not update its date and time; but did not interfere with the flight.
Flight Instructor reported a near midair collision with a UAS while they were on final approach. The reporter took evasive action and reported the encounter to ATC.
A319 First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence at FL380 that resulted in altitude and airspeed excursions.
Pilot flying C-172 aircraft reported electrical failure inflight and landed at departure airport.

CRJ-900 Captain reported being notified by company that during a previous flight they had flown off course while conducting an approach and below the minimum terrain altitude for the procedure. The reporter stated they never received a terrain alert in the aircraft.
PA-44 Flight Instructor reported the right main landing gear collapsed on takeoff roll.
Air carrier flight crew reported they did not receive Final Dangerous Goods (DG) summary prior to pushback. Shortly thereafter the flight crew received the Final DG Summary and resumed pushback.

A320 First Officer reported they experienced cabin pressurization fluctuations and noticed both engines surging simultaneously during cruise. Flight crew elected to return to the departure airport and landed safely.

B737-700 flight crew reported a rejected takeoff when observers informed them of fire coming from the #1 Engine. The flight crew exited the active runway and called for CFR. After inspecting the aircraft; CFR escorted the aircraft back to the gate.
CE-560 pilot reported a controlled flight toward terrain event during a night visual approach. Flight crew performed a go-around and landed.
Flight Crew flying CE-525 aircraft report severe turbulence after takeoff.
Air Carrier Captain taxiing in IAH reported they turned on to the wrong taxiway due to a lack of taxiway signage.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported being notified of an incomplete Hazmat cargo loading error. The missed cargo was confused as part of the pallet/skid.
Air carrier Captain reported a Lithium battery was retrieved from cargo during preflight in order to avoid a Hazmat violation. A security inspection was completed and the flight departed late.
Captain reported unusual airframe vibrations at cruise speeds. The flight crew made aircraft maintenance log entries but neglected to fill out the vibration report forms.
Corporate jet Captain reported descending below the minimum vectoring altitude. Captain states it was caused by similar call signs.
Ramp service worker reporter the tug driver lost control of the tug while pushing back an aircraft.

HLN TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew 2;000 feet below the Minimum IFR Altitude. Reporter stated they did not know the aircraft descended because their facility does not have radar.

Air carrier pilot reported being given a last minute change to runway assignment during final approach due to a UAS flying at the approach end of the runway. The approach to the new runway became unstabilized; but the pilot continued to landing.
Ramp Agent reported a tow bar malfunctioned during push back of an A321.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported they shut an engine down during cruise climb due to excessive vibrations and loss of power and returned to departure airport.
A320 Air carrier First Officer aircraft reported Hydraulic System Malfunction on descent.

General aviation instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft while in the traffic patter at a non-towered airport during a training flight. The instructor maneuvered away from the other aircraft on the downwind leg to provide separation.
An air carrier First Officer reported unsafe gear indication followed by a go around and return to an uneventful landing.
Air carrier Captain reported a safe rejected takeoff executed due to an ECAM ENG 1 Compressor Vane light illuminated during takeoff roll. The Captain stated this anomaly has been a recurring issue.
Zenith 701 pilot reported wind gust during landing pushed the aircraft off the runway into the grass.

EMB-145 flight crew reported while climbing through 7;000 feet; they encountered icing conditions. Moments later they got a stab anti-ice fail message followed by ice condition anti-ice inop. They contacted ATC; descended to 5;000 feet and returned to the airport.
Tower Ground Controller reported they cleared an aircraft to cross a runway at the same time that another aircraft was departing from the same runway.
Air Carrier Captain reported incorrect taxiway signage at from Taxiway J onto SS at ORD depicts a 135 degree turn when it is a 45 degree turn.
Corporate Captain reported third party vendor TOLD software is missing runway slope option which may compute inaccurate TOLD data.
B737-800 flight crew reported pressurization malfunction during descent.
Flight instructor reported a near miss with an aircraft while departing TOI airport and took evasive action to avoid a collision. Reportedly; the other aircraft was not communicating their position.
Loss of Engine Bleed Air resulted in an emergency descent caused by loss of cabin pressure and anti ice protection; requiring a diversion.

Small aircraft pilot reported inadvertently entering Class C airspace just after takeoff while maneuvering to avoid collision with another aircraft that was entering the VFR traffic pattern at a nearby airport.
Air taxi First Officer reported after engine start on the ramp the aircraft rolled uncontrolled due to the parking brake not being set.
B747-8F flight crew reported on takeoff roll; the aircraft pulled to the right and the FO stated they had full left rudder applied. They rejected the takeoff below 30 knots.
Pilot flying PA28 aircraft reported Electrical System failure inflight.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported an inadvertent discharge of the cargo pit fire extinguisher bottle during a maintenance check.
C152 Flight Instructor with student reported multiple and frequent drops in engine RPM during a training flight. Flight Instructor diverted and landed safely.

EMB-145 First Officer reported they received a WG A/ICE FAIL master caution warning during descent at FL260.  The crew ran the appropriate QRH items and notified ATC they needed to descend out of the icing conditions.
Air carrier Captain reported a NMAC when a small aircraft flew over the carrier on final approach. Captain continued approach to landing.
B737 Captain reported a higher than normal descent on final approach resulting in an ATC low altitude warning. The flight crew corrected the descent then continued to a normal landing.
Pilot reported a runway incursion at HPN airport and cited taxiway signage as a contributing factor.

Air carrier Captain reported that while the First officer was hand-flying a visual approach; they descended below glide slope and observed four red lights on the PAPI. The crew leveled off and continued to landing.

Air carrier First Officer reported encountering a vehicle crossing the runway as their aircraft was accelerating in the takeoff roll. The crew continued the takeoff; and ATC instructed subsequent aircraft to go around.
Student pilot reported a near miss while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The Instructor took control and maneuvered to avoid a collision.
Air carrier flight crew reported two military jets were cleared by ATC for a low pass over the air carrier after it had landed and was still on the runway.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported discovering upon review of the flight log that they flew in controlled airspace without authorization.
Air carrier EMB-145 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence departing PHX from an A321 that had departed from a parallel runway.

Air carrier First Officer reported a possible jet blast encounter during landing flare at IAH airport.  First officer stated they were landing on Runway 8R and the jet blast was from an aircraft taking off on Runway 15L.
A321 Captain reported that after releasing the brakes; ground crew did not respond to the Captain's attempts to reach them and there was a period of time when no ground crew was at the aircraft.
A320 Captain reported a rejected takeoff and return to gate caused by the First Officer's rudder pedal adjustment failing.
Air Carrier Check Captain reported new hire First Officer experienced a hard landing during an OE training flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during descent below glide path. Flight crew made corrected flight path and continued approach.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported briefly exceeding 400 feet AGL during an automated RTH due to an incorrect setting with the UAS system.
CRJ-900 Flight Attendant reported an odor onboard the aircraft.

Multi person UAS flight crew was conducting a crew swap while UAS was flying near weather and building clouds. After crew swap the UAS was flown inadvertently into icing conditions and was able to exit.
PA-28 Pilot reported alternator issues led to low voltage inflight with radio failure and possible ADS-B failure.
EMB-145 First Officer reported descent below published safe altitudes; resulting in terrain conflict alert.

Air carrier Pilot not Flying First Officer (FO) reported after reaching cruise altitude the left generator failed. The FO stated the aircraft was already MEL'd with an inoperative APU. The pilots safely diverted to a suitable airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported flight release missing required HazMat amendment during cruise.
A TRACON Controller reported they assumed a descending aircraft was descending via a published procedure but they were not and descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Diamond DA 20 student pilot and flight instructor reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern when the student accepted a clearance turn for another aircraft with a similar call sign. ATC issued a traffic alert and the DA20 pilot took evasive action to avoid the collision.
A321 Captain reported a communication breakdown with Ground Control while figuring out an issue with the aft cargo compartment smoke warning light and ECAM message.
A320 Captain reported a loss of green hydraulic system prior to top of descent. Crew performed checklists and landed normally.
Flight crew reported observing multiple flight control status and caution messages during climb out. Flight crew diverted and made a precautionary landing.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC while conducting training maneuvers.

EMB-175 flight attendant reported being notified of a nose gear malfunction by the Captain while on approach and asked the flight attendants to emergency prepare the cabin. The aircraft diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
B787 flight crew reported they descended two dots below the glide slope on a visual approach; citing automation mode awareness; fatigue; and workload as contributing.
B777 Captain reported two flight spoilers deployed in the unlocked position during cruise. Captain requested priority handling and diverted after accomplishing appropriate checklists.
Pilatus P3 Pilot reported unsafe landing gear indications resulted in gear collapse on landing.

First Officer reported receiving a Stall Fail caution message on takeoff roll. Flight crew rejected takeoff and returned to gate where maintenance drained water from the standby pitot system and returned the plane to service.
A320 First Officer reported that during approach the aircraft descended below glide path and a speed warning annunciated. Flight crew conducted a go-around and returned to landing.
EMB-145LR Captain reported an inflight upset at FL370 after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B737.
Embraer Captain reported the stall protection system reacted in an unusual manner after a minor wake turbulence encounter.
A321-NEO flight crew reported during push back from gate; the tow bar shear pin broke and the crew had to apply brakes to stop the aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported the cockpit inflight temperature gradually became colder to the point where only cold air was available. The flight crew diverted and was unable to reproduce the anomaly on the ground.
ERJ-145 First Officer reported they received a terrain alert while descending on a visual approach. Flight crew climbed and continued to a landing.
B737 pilot reported a one-inch crack on the captains L1 window inner pane during cruise. Flight diverted.

CRJ 900 Captain reported a ground collision; while parking; with another aircraft at an adjacent gate. The Captain states the ground crew was not paying attention while they were signaling the aircraft into the gate.
Captain reported unreliable airspeed and stall protection system indications and elected to request expedited handling and an air turn back to a successful precautionary landing.
A319 First Officer reported a diversion after the operating pack failed with the other pack already deferred. The flight crew determined that there was insufficient fuel to continue to destination.
A32 flight crew reported they rejected takeoff due to the side stick priority light flickered and the dual input aural warning sounded.
Air carrier pilot reported during departure from PHX airport the Captain's radio altimeter and various displays malfunctioned. Pilot cited possible 5G interference as contributing.

Flight Attendant reported a passenger seat was overheating to the point that it became unbearable for the passenger.  The flight returned to the gate and aircraft was taken out of service for maintenance.
Flight crew reported stiffness in the Aileron Controls after take off. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
A319 Captain reported executing a missed approach at EWR after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B787.

B737 Captain reported uncommanded aircraft movement when the tow bar separated from the aircraft during pushback from the gate. The Captain abruptly applied the brakes and two flight attendants were injured.

Air Carrier Captain reported after parking at the gate and upon exiting the aircraft; outgoing flight crew reported engine fire on ramp to pilots. The Captain returned to the flight deck; pulled engine fire handles; obtained a fire extinguisher; and went to ramp. The engine tailpipe fire had subsided; and remaining fire was extinguished by the Captain.
C680 Captain reported the undocumented transported of a 500W portable lithium battery which exceeded the company Hazmat maximum of 160W. The flight crew was not notified of the 500W battery.
A319 flight crew reported a Cargo Smoke ECAM message and began checklist operations. Ground personnel signaled to shut down APU and APU auto shutdown. Passengers were removed from aircraft.
EMB175 Flight Crew reported autoflight improperly set resulted in higher rate of descent and a low altitude alert on approach.

Glider pilot reported a complex firmware update is required for the FLARM TCAS unit to provide traffic avoidance and advisories. The reporter stated no advance notice of the update is provided by the company.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported a NMAC with another aircraft in traffic pattern.
A320 flight crew reported the aircraft rolled forwards uncontrolled after the flight crew failed to set the brakes after push back.

Air Carrier Captain reported after pushback being notified of a passenger bag with Lithium Ion batteries was erroneously gate checked to cargo compartment. Aircraft returned to gate; moved bag to cabin; and subsequently continued without further issues.
PA-28-140 pilot reported during takeoff the engine had some roughness and got worse. Pilot returned to airport and landed.
Air carrier Captain reported the nose of the aircraft slid due to jet blast from a nearby aircraft during taxi to gate. The flight crew applied brakes to stop sliding then continued taxi to the gate.
AG-CAT pilot reported a vehicle was stuck on the runway while on final approach. Due to fuel concerns; the pilot decided to land overflying the truck by 20 feet.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported brake failure on landing.

FXE Air Traffic Controller reported coordinating a runway crossing with an aircraft holding in position; then cleared the aircraft for takeoff before the crossing traffic crossed the runway.  This resulted in canceling the takeoff clearance and once the crossing aircraft exited the runway they cleared the aircraft for takeoff.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a CPDLC clearance with an incorrect altimeter value for the arrival airport creating concern regarding conducting the approach within mountainous terrain.

B737-800 Captain reported the cockpit fire extinguisher failed at cruise causing a loud bang in the cockpit and releasing CO2. The flight crew went on oxygen and passed the fire extinguisher back to the flight attendants for storage. The flight continued to destination airport.
G-IV pilot reported a fuel leak during departure climb. Flight crew returned and landed uneventfully and the aircraft was removed from service.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported loss in cabin pressurization during descent. Flight continued to destination and landed overweight.

Air carrier Captain reported a ground traffic conflict with an aircraft blocking a taxiway preventing exiting a Runway while another aircraft was on final approach. The flight was finally able to contact ATC and received clearance to taxi and exited the Runway safely.
Air carrier Captain reported issues at KOA gate where parking is unsafe due to poorly lit taxiways and ramp area.
B737 First Officer reported the centerline markings on several taxiways at ORD Airport are worn and difficult to see at night.
GA pilot reported a conflict during final approach to BQ1 non-towered airport resulting in a go-around.
GA pilot reported while taking off at a non-towered airport; an NMAC occurred involving an opposite direction landing aircraft.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on approach descended below the minimum vectoring altitude. The reporter stated their minimum safe altitude warning did not alert activate.
Air Traffic Controller reported a continued frequency issue resulting in a significant disruption of HCF ATC operations and creating a potential safety hazard.
Air carrier First Officer reported an NMAC on approach to MCI following an unstabilized approach.

Fractional Captain reported ATC cleared them for takeoff while an aircraft was less than 100 feet from landing on the same runway. Fractional crew stopped taxi then departed after the landing aircraft cleared runway.
DA-42 instructor pilot reported landing safely with the landing gear up when the left main gear failed to extend.
PA-28 flight instructor reported an aircraft in the pattern was not using correct procedures or communicating with other aircraft and caused near midair collisions with multiple aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a UAS during descent on initial approach. No evasive action was taken.
Private Pilot reported observing a UAS pass near their aircraft while flying at 5;500 ft.  The pilot stated evasive action was not needed but UAS came within 500 ft. of aircraft.
Ramp worker reported a near collision of a catering truck and ground service equipment due to lack of guide person directing the catering truck.
Air carrier flight crew were informed of an engine tailpipe fire during pushback. Tailpipe fire was extinguished and aircraft returned to gate.
B737 First Officer reported that people are using 121.5 for other than necessary functions.
Flight Instructor with student reported a near miss with an airliner while crossing an arrival corridor. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Air carrier crew reported an altitude deviation and ATC low altitude alert while on approach to a foreign airport over terrain in moderate rain conditions. The flight crew quickly corrected the altitude deviation and completed the approach and landing safely.
King Air C90 flight crew reported elevator trim wheel stuck in the neutral position. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
PA28 pilot reported an open fuel sump leaked fuel during flight causing a power loss and return to the departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported mismanagement of the FMS VNAV resulted in overshooting STAR crossing restriction.

B737-400 First Officer reported loss of cabin pressure control after pack failure. The flight crew requested priority handling and made an immediate descent. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a near midair collision with a UAS during arrival descent. No evasive action was taken.

B737-700 Flight Attendant reported passenger mobility device required the battery to be 'deactivated' in order to transport in passenger cabin. Reporter further requested the DG section of the FA's SOP be updated.
B757 flight crew reported an engine oil pressure issue; resulting in an inflight engine shutdown. A successful heavy weight landing was accomplished; after returning to the departure airport.
Pilots reported a NMAC during departure and cited ATC instructions as contributing to the event.
B737 Captain reported a temporary loss of control while taxiing due to nose wheel steering failure. The aircraft was stopped on the ramp and towed to the gate.
A319 First Officer reported a fume event during preflight at the gate due to an APU oil leak.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while in the downwind of the traffic pattern at a towered airport.

Air carrier First Officer reported the aircraft became disconnected from the tow bar during push back; and was rolling free for a few moments before the Captain brought the aircraft to a safe stop. There was reportedly no communication from the ground crew during the incident.
A321 Captain reported a hydraulic system malfunction resulting in a diversion to the nearest suitable airport.
BE35 pilot reported engine failure in cruise flight. Pilot landed off-airport.
ZMA Center Controllers reported a loss of separation attributed to weather and new Metroplex procedures.
A pilot conducting a touch and go reported a loss of control during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
A Light Sport aircraft pilot reported engine failure during a maintenance test flight and safe landing in a field.

Pilot reported during arrival to MSY the gear position disagreement lights illuminated and aural warning occurred and continued until landing gear was lowered. The First Officer's Radio Altimeter had erratic indications.
B767 flight crew reported momentary airspeed loss on approach due to windshear conditions.
EMB-145 flight crew reported fuel pumps cycling on and off caused a fuel imbalance. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
CRJ-900 First officer reported wind gusts and LLWS encounter resulted in a wing scrape on landing.
DA40 Fight Instructor with student reported the left main wheel locked up during landing causing the tire to rupture.
Pilot reported cabin pressure and door warning lights illuminated during departure climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and maintenance found a door was not properly closed.
SR20 Flight Instructor with student reported they rejected the takeoff due to the engine RPMs dropping to 2500 before rotation.
B767 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B777 aircraft at FL357 in Gander airspace that resulted in a minor injury to a flight attendant.
B737-800 First Officer reported after takeoff and during descent both PFD's went blank and came back on when VNAV was engaged. During descent there was also a burning rubber smell in the flight deck.
Air carrier Captain reported taxiway lighting issues at OAK airport in the vicinity of taxiways W5 and  W.
Tower Local Controller reported a departing small general aviation aircraft reported smoke in the cockpit returned and landed on a runway before an air carrier could exit the runway.
B787 flight crew reported an engine fire and inflight shutdown. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.

Air carrier Captain reported tug operator failed to use proper communications; comply with push instructions; nor operate in a safe manner; resulted on close contact with another aircraft. Event was observed by crew; ground personnel and passengers as reckless and dangerous.
GA pilots reported a NMAC while in the airport traffic pattern and cited communication issues with ATC contributed to the event.
C172 pilot reported airspeed indicator malfunction during the takeoff roll and rejected the takeoff.

Instructor Pilot reported engine RPM loss after take off. The flight crew immediately turned back to the airport and requested priority handling. The Instructor Pilot landed and taxied back to the ramp; followed by Airport Ops and ARFF vehicles.
Pilot reported an NMAC in cruise with an aircraft that was making 360 degree turns in their vicinity. Pilot took evasive action to avoid the other aircraft.
Tailwheel Pilot reported a loss of directional control on landing resulting in a runway excursion and a ground strike. Reporter stated brake system was squealing on touchdown.
A TRACON instructor and their Trainee reported the Trainee issued a descent clearance to an aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict while taxiing clear of the runway when an aircraft taxied in front of them at a high rate of speed. Flight Instructor reported an altitude overshoot due to unfamiliarity with aircraft equipment.

EMB-145 flight crew reported being notified at destination arrival of the undocumented transport of a battery powered mobility device in the cargo compartment. The battery powered device was not processed in compliance with company Hazmat procedures.

Air Carrier Check pilot reported the APU on their 737 MAX continued to shut down while in service at the gate while in a heavy wet snow storm. The pilot also stated that another MAX aircraft at an adjacent gate was experiencing the same scenario.
Air carrier flight crew reported they executed a go-around due to an aircraft crossing their runway while they were on final approach.
Air carrier First Officer reported an obstacle related CFTT event requiring a pull up maneuver during approach to ALB airport.
A TRACON Controller reported a Corporate jet deviated off course from the approach and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
EMB 175 First Officer reported receiving five EICAS caution messages for multiple system failures during departure. Captain decided to return to departure airport.
Center Controller reported an understaffed shift and multiple sessions on position resulted in mental fatigue leading to an airborne conflict.
Pilot reported ATC low altitude alert on approach to JZI airport.

A321 Captain reported a fume event during initial climb; that was also reported by the First Officer and the Flight Attendants in the cabin. The Flight Crew completed the QRH procedures and performed an air turn back.
Air carrier flight crew reported after they were issued line up and wait clearance they stopped past the hold short line when they observed a landing aircraft on short final.
During a ferry flight; the Captain elected to divert due to unreliable fuel quantity indications.
GA pilot reported a near miss with an airliner while on an ATC assigned heading and altitude and took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
EMB-145 First Officer reported that confusion over MEL procedures resulted in the aircraft operating with both thrust reversers being locked out.
An ultralight experimental aircraft pilot reported they lost control of the aircraft while landing due to wind gusts resulting in a bounced landing and prop strike.

Flight crew reported after arrival at gate; jet blast knocked a ground handler down and rolled him over twice. The aircraft was in an engine cool down period. No injuries were sustained by the ground handler.
C-150 Student pilot reported electrical failure during taxi for takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported that during push back from the gate; the push crew exceeded the turn limits of the nose gear.
First Officer reported a loss of pitch trim control caused an altitude deviation. No other issues were reported.
B737 flight crew reported engine overheat indications during departure climb. The crew returned to the departure airport and landed without incident.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in A90 airspace.
A Flight Instructor reported their student flying solo temporarily lost control during landing roll due to a flat tire.
B737-900 Captain reported they failed to make a crossing restriction departing SEA because a wake turbulence encounter prompted them to reduce their climb rate to minimize their exposure to the wake.

Air carrier Captain reported a blown pneumatic duct and bleed leak during takeoff which filled the passenger cabin with excessive noise and airborne particulates. Multiple flight attendants calling cockpit during event was distracting and created difficulty communicating with cabin crew on inter-phone to assess situation in cabin and to advise of planned return to field for an overweight landing aircraft.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a large transport aircraft while approaching an airport. Pilot took evasive action and continued to airport.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a hydraulic system failure during climbout.
Air carrier First Officer reported a near miss with a UAS while on arrival.
A Center Controller reported a non RVSM military aircraft flight was approved to climb to 30;000 feet underneath an Air Carrier at 31;000 feet.
B767 flight crew reported uncommanded aircraft movement after chocks were released. Brake application did not stop the aircraft so chocks were placed.
Cessna 206 pilot reported engine roughness during initial climb. Pilot returned to the departure airport and landed safely.

A320 Captain reported receiving a call from the Flight Attendants who reported a 'dirty socks' odor in the passenger cabin during the top of the descent. After arriving at the gate; Maintenance tested the APU and the odor was no longer there.
Air carrier Ground personnel reported that an unserviceable battery was loaded onto the aircraft without following the appropriate HAZMAT shipping protocol.
Captain reported an immediate descent and air turn back after loss of both packs during climb.
TRACON Controller and a pilot reported a NMAC due to the Controller not being aware the Controller they had just relieved had descended another aircraft.
CRJ-550 pilot reported a ground conflict with another aircraft landing in the opposite direction at a non-towered airport.

Part 107 UAS crew was operating with a Certificate of Authorization (COA). There was a short moment during the cruise portion of the flight when the UAS was operating outside the parameters of the COA.
Denver Center Controllers reported problems when trying to get priority handling with Denver TRACON on two MEDEVAC aircraft.

Air carrier flight crew reported encountering a GPS jamming incident where one of the GPS malfunctioned and proceeded to work intermittently; which led to the flight crew having to hand-fly the approach as the final approach course never captured. The flight crew also noted that other aircraft were experiencing the same issue.
