G280 Captain reported safely returning to departure airport after experiencing multiple systems failures related to a malfunctioning air data computer.
EMB-145 Flight crew reported failure of landing gear to retract after takeoff. Returned to departure airport and landing uneventfully.
Honda jet pilots reported they received a low altitude alert during arrival descent. Flight crew crew climbed and corrected altimeter setting.
B747 First Officer reported low engine oil quantity resulted in a divert.

A CRJ700 Flight Attendant reported being informed inflight that the aircraft's landing gear was not operating correctly. The pilots ask that the FA crew prepare for a non standard landing in the event there was an incident. The aircraft made a normal landing.

King Air C90 pilot reported right engine loss of power resulting in controllability; performance and maneuverability issues in the landing pattern. The pilot stabilized the aircraft; secured the engine and landed safely.

Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an aircraft terrain caution during descent into RNO. The Captain immediately stopped the descent; the caution disappeared; and the flight crew continued the landing.
G500 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing MDW in trail of a B737 that resulted in an uncommanded 45-50 degree bank.
Boeing 777-200 flight crew reported receiving a left engine low oil pressure EICAS message during initial climbout. The crew shut down the engine and returned to the departure airport.
ERJ-190 pilot reported the push tug made contact with the aircraft during push-back from the gate.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with a helicopter in the pattern at HSD.
GA Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC during departure climb from JCA non-towered airport requiring evasive action.  Reporter stated language barrier was a factor in the incident.
PA-24 pilot reported the engine stopped during approach due to carburetor icing as a result of the pilot not applying carburetor heat. Pilot landed safely.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft did not turn to assigned heading on departure.
C172S Flight Instructor reported conflict aircraft made a non standard traffic pattern entry that resulted in a NMAC.
B737-800 Captain reported aircraft departure with flight release missing required 'Hazmat cargo' amendment note.
C182 pilot reported an alternator failure and landed the aircraft in IMC weather.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying above 400 feet AGL. The UAS was set to meters and the UAS pilot was unaware until after landing.
ZAN Controller reported developmental issued IFR clearance below the MVA.
A Flight Instructor reported that the left main gear collapsed upon landing.

Single-engine aircraft pilot reported while departing a helicopter departed a parallel taxiway resulting in the single-engine aircraft pilot rejecting their takeoff to avoid a collision with the helicopter.
B737 MAX pilot reported speed deviations during autopilot-controlled descent.
Air carrier pilot reported the runway 07/25 at MHRO; Honduras; is uneven and roughly patched which could cause damage to transport category aircraft.
Pilot reported that the seat lap belt attaching hardware came off during flight.
Pilot reported that after landing; while taxiing on the runway for a 180; the aircraft developed an engine problem causing evacuation of the aircraft on the runway.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a rough running engine and loss of power while returning to the departure airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported they were not informed of Weight & Balance issue.
Air carrier pilot reported the radio altimeter failed and a terrain warning was activated during initial climb. The pilot reported all terrain was in sight and there was no conflict.
Air carrier Captain reported Taxi Spot 1A at PHX is not depicted on any publication. The Captain stated ground control issued taxi instructions containing reference to Spot 1A.
Air carrier Captain reported a fuel leak while in cruise flight and a diversion was accomplished.
Pilot reported airport was being used for car racing without NOTAMs being posted.
ZLC Center Controller reported they were unable to vector a departing air carrier away from a conflicting unidentified VFR aircraft because the air carrier was below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
ZBW Center Controller reported they transposed digits in an approach clearance and descended an aircraft to an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

B757 First Officer reported they received a low oil pressure EICAS message during cruise at FL330 followed by a zero oil quantity indication.  While troubleshooting; the engine rolled back.  The crew continued to destination for a single engine landing.
Pilot reported intentionally flying closer to a vehicle than briefed during a planned photo shoot.
PA-23 pilot with instructor reported unable to restart engine that was shut down for training. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
MU2B-40 pilot reported a brake malfunction on taxi.
PA46 Meridian pilot reported nose gear collapse on landing; resulting in a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft.
B737 flight crew reported an engine failure after take off; resulting in an air turnback and precautionary landing at the departure airport.
Flight Instructor and student reported; while taxiing for departure; a fuel leak which resulted in return to FBO.
B737 flight crew reported hydraulic system malfunction after takeoff. Flight crew returned to departure airport and performed an overweight landing.

MBK117 air ambulance pilot reported maintenance failure to install tail rotor gearbox bolts after maintenance action. Pilot reported management pressure to get helicopters back online lead to maintenance errors.
Learjet 35 pilot reported an engine throttle lever became sluggish during descent resulting in a large split between engine indications. Flight crew shut down engine and continued to safe landing.
Air carrier Hazmat ground inspector reported incorrect DG data found during per-flight loading.The pallet weight errors were corrected prior to departure.
Flight crew reported a lower/aft cargo fire indication in flight and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Flight Crew reported a Parking Brake Not Released EICAS message after gear retraction. The Flight Crew returned to landing at departure airport.
Flight crew reported battery fuel spar valve cannon plug was not properly re-installed after maintenance check. Found during preflight functional check.
Air carrier F/O reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on approach to BUF.

MD-11 Captain reported a diversion after the Cabin Outflow Valve failed to close after cycling due to live stock on board. Insulation was found to be jammed in the Cabin Outflow Valve; preventing it from closing.
A320 flight crew reported receiving the final dangerous goods report during pushback. The flight crew final dangerous goods acknowledgement response was submitted prior to taxi.

GA instructor pilot reported while training in the VFR pattern they noticed an aircraft on the EFB in the pattern not transmitting on the CTAF.  The other aircraft remained in the pattern and made an opposite direction approach that resulted with the crew taking evasive action to avoid a NMAC between the aircraft.  Both aircraft landed safely at the airport.
Air carrier pilot reported descending to the Final Approach Fix altitude instead of the ATC assigned altitude received with the approach clearance due to automation mismanagement.

P180 Avanti pilot reported observing Decreasing Left Engine Oil Pressure and Stable Oil Temperature on a post maintenance check flight. The pilot requested vectors for a return to the field. Engine Oil Pressure continued to Decrease. The pilot performed an in flight shut down of the Left Engine and requested priority handling. A precautionary landing was made; with directional control not maintained; resulting in a runway excursion. The pilot secured the aircraft and waited for assistance.
A320 Captain reported a 'dirty socks' odor during descent. Flight crew completed appropriate procedures and landed without any further issues.

A320 Captain reported a fumes event during deplaning at destination. Flight attendants refused to fly with the persistent odor after aircraft had been cleaned. Captain and First officer agreed to ferry the aircraft to maintenance base for repairs.

B737 First Officer reported ATC issued a descent clearance by CPDLC to the aircraft in cruise flight which was accepted in the ATC system; but the flight never received the clearance to allow acceptance.
Flight crew flying a CE-680 aircraft reported an EGPWS alert on approach.
Air Carrier Captain reported while departing the ramp in DEN another aircraft taxied in front of them; forcing the Captain to come to an abrupt stop to avoid a collision.
Air carrier flight crew reported their windshield wipers failed during an approach to an airport in the rain so they diverted to a nearby airport.

Beechcraft Bonanza pilot reported descending toward terrain while attempting to remain VFR below lowering cloud deck. Pilot climbed into clouds and after re-entering VFR conditions diverted to a suitable airport.

Pilot reported several lights associated with the approach system and airport lighting are inoperative at PTKK airport. These include PAPI; runway edge; taxiway; runway end lights and several in areas of the ramp. The pilot states there are no NOTAMs.
A319 First Officer reported aircraft generated CFIT Warning while on approach.
BNA Tower Controller reported a helicopter passed directly underneath an air carrier that was on final approach.
Air Carrier ramp personnel reported he failed to give the NOTOC to the Captain prior to pushback. Flight executed a ground gate return to correct Hazmat issue.

Inspector reported an engine hub was overhauled instead of being scrapped as required. This hub returned to the Company from the manufacturer as serviceable and was made available in the supply system before being re-identified.
A Tower Local Controller reported a departing aircraft turned too early and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported notification from a flight crew of an engine malfunction. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed overweight.
Technician reported after signing off the task card the assisting technician left the flight control switch in the off position.
B737 flight crew reported a master caution light annunciation and noticed the hydraulic engine pump light flickering and the hydraulic quantity was low. They diverted to the most convenient airport.
A320 Captain reported encountering moderate turbulence resulted in loss of airspeed and altitude with momentary loss of aircraft control.
C172 Flight instructor on training flight with student reported a near midair collision with another aircraft in the airport traffic pattern.
A company owner reported one of their helicopters had a near miss with a UAS. The owner stated this happens often and recommended that it should be addressed for safety.
Cruisemaster 14 pilot reported the main gear collapsed during landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported a Hazmat shipping violation during cargo offloading. Cargo consisting of 48 Lithium battery powered laptops were improperly configured for air transport.
B737 MAX Captain reported a flight control malfunction.
Cessna Longitude First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC as the Captain was making a visual approach. The Captain corrected the altitude and continued the landing.

Flight Attendant reported the lavatory trash receptacles in two lavs were missing the self closing flapper doors and had not been repaired when previously written up. There is no MEL relief for these missing doors; except locking out the lavatory.
A321 pilot reported surging EPR indication and aircraft yawing resulted in return to departure airport.  Pilot also stated the company fleet is aging with increasing maintenance issues.
C182 pilot reported a complete engine failure during initial climb out; resulting in an off-field landing on a nearby highway.
Air Traffic Controller reported an aircraft had flown through the final approach course which resulted in a NMAC with another aircraft on approach to the parallel runway.
B737-800 Flight Attendants reported avoiding an inadvertent escape slide deployment when catering tried to open an armed aft aircraft door.
Pilot reported an unsafe gear indication was received upon gear extension. Flight crew diverted and after landing aircraft was towed to parking.
Air carrier First Officer reported an engine stall during cruise and requested priority handling with ATC.
B777 Captain reported uncommanded autothrottle actuation during taxi. The flight crew returned to the gate for an aircraft swap.

PA-32 pilot reported Nose Landing Gear failed to extend on final approach when the landing gear selector was moved to the down position. The pilot performed a go-around and ran the checklists and QRH. The pilot was unable to get a down and locked indication for the nose gear. The pilot made a final attempt to land; during which the nose gear collapsed.
GA pilot reported that just before rotation a bird impacted the left wing and the pilot then rejected the takeoff.
BE35 pilot reported fuel starvation in the traffic pattern and a successful engine out landing.

General aviation pilot reported multiple traffic conflicts in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport on a training flight with an instructor. The pilot maneuvered away and slowed; to provide separation from another aircraft not following non-towered procedures.

Embraer 145 Captain reported upon landing at a Tower controlled airport; utilizing maximum reverse thrust; a jolt and yaw was felt and a Hydraulic Low Quantity warning annunciated. Upon parking it was discovered the reverser section of the engine separated from the aircraft with fluid loss.
Captain reported a nose tire and wheel assembly departed the aircraft nose gear axle during taxi. The flight crew was still in the ramp area and parked for maintenance action.
B757 Captain reported receiving a TE FLAPS DISAGREE message on short final. The flight crew performed a go-around and returned to land.
A TRACON Controller reported a corporate jet descended below their assigned altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude and through a satellite airport Class D airspace.

Cessna Stationair pilot reported an alternator failure and subsequent loss of all electrical power and battery during cruise. Pilot continued to destination airport using portable backup communication and navigation devices.
Flight Instructor reported while on short final observing a vehicle approaching the runway resulting in them executing a go around to avoid the vehicle.
Pilot reported a heading deviation after proceeding to an incorrect navigational fix due to its similar sounding name.

King Air pilot reported the First Officer over torqued the left engine while advancing power on takeoff roll. The pilot reduced power and the First Officer failed to reduce rudder input which resulted in a sharp aircraft yaw. Flight crew rejected the takeoff and exited the runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported a possible near miss while preparing to taxi across runway with another aircraft on takeoff roll.

Fractional Captain reported after departure the flight was cleared to a fix that was verbally difficult to understand from the Controller who reprimanded the pilots when they asked for clarification. Reporter stated they previously have had similar communication difficulties with this facility.
Air carrier Captain reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of burning odor in the cabin. The Captain conducted a diversion and completed a safe landing.
Tug driver reported a loss of control of aircraft after aircraft rolled into towbar during disconnect process due to a communication breakdown between pilots and tug driver.
Air carrier Captain reported; at a foreign station; due to non standard push back hand signals the aircraft had been released and begun to roll back.

ZMA Controller reported the GDT site stopped working and with it; the radar and frequency also went out of service before returning intermittently a good amount of time later. The reporter stated there are no back-ups in place and the equipment is unreliable.
PA34-200T pilot reported being unable to restart an engine during single engine phase of Multi-engine check-ride.    Pilot's returned to departure airport and landed single engine.
B737 flight crew and a Technician reported that the aircraft tipped back onto its tail after cargo was loaded.
C172 Flight Instructor with student reported a runway excursion after landing in crosswind conditions. Reportedly; the student did not make the appropriate control inputs.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving an ATC fault and loss of GPS signals shortly after being informed by ATC that several aircraft were experiencing similar problems in the area. After moving further away from the area; the flight crew began to receive normal GPS signals again and there were no issues for the rest of the flight.
General aviation pilot reported loss of aircraft control when they became distracted with a door unlocked indication. Pilot recovered aircraft and continued flight.
A320 flight crew on final approach reported main gear not down and locked.
MD-11 First Officer reported unreliable airspeed indications and a return to the departure airport.
GA flight instructor reported a NMAC after turning right rather than left as cleared during departure. The reporter became aware of the issue and corrected the heading.
DA40 Pilot reported while performing landings and takeoffs during a check ride an engine failure occurred on climb out. The examiner took the controls and landed the aircraft.

Student pilot reported while holding short of runway; ATC instructed them to hold short of runway but mistakenly interpreted the instruction to hold short on the runway. The pilot taxied onto the runway and caused another aircraft to go around.
A319 pilot reported a rejected takeoff due to receiving an ECAM message for the #2 engine compressor vane.
Air carrier Captain reported temporary loss of control due to wind shear during two landing attempts resulted in a diversion to a nearby airport.
Helicopter Pilot reported that while inbound for landing they did not stop the aircraft as directed by ATC; resulting in an airborne conflict with a jet landing on another runway.

Learjet 75 flight crew reported taxing at night with rain and taxing beyond the approach end of the runway and ATC cleared them for takeoff resulting in the aircraft entering the parallel runway and aircraft on final taking evasive action to avoid a collision with them.
Cessna 172 pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the pattern at 7S5 airport.
A ZAB Center Controller reported they had to request the Operations Manager to coordinate ceasing intentional GPS jamming so a medical flight could conduct a RNAV approach to LRU.
Single Private Pilot reported a conflict in the traffic pattern.

Air carrier flight crew reported the missed approach procedure for LAX ILS 25L has too many steps to complete before capturing a low missed approach altitude. In addition; the Captain reported the Controllers were issuing instructions so quickly; it created confusion on the flight deck among the pilots.

Air carrier pilot reported while on final approach ATC advised them of a helicopter that was maintaining visual separation from them; but the pilot had to reduce the descent rate to avoid a collision with the helicopter.
Pilot reported a near miss during takeoff roll with another aircraft that was possibly departing from the opposite end of the runway at MYEM.
B767 Captain reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing a loss of both FMC's during climb.
PA28 pilots reported the aircraft engine lost power completely during cruise climb. Pilots restarted engine and diverted to a safe landing.
Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC during departure and stated tower did not advise them of the traffic.
ATR-42 Captain reported maintenance instructed crew to do unsafe procedures and failed to help disconnect GPU.
B767 flight crew reported receiving an EICAS message for cargo door issue and rising cabin altitude during cruise. The crew descended and landed safely at an alternate airport.
SA-227 AC Metro III Captain reported a R/H WING OVHT after take off. Captain performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer descended below glidepath on a visual approach and they received a low altitude alert from ATC.
B767-200 flight crew reported the aircraft failed to pressurize during cruise flight and decided to divert to a suitable airport.
TOL Local Controller reported they vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a premature cabin door opening after another FA gave the gate agent a thumbs up prior to verifying the doors were disarmed. Escape slide did not deploy.
Air carrier pilot conducting a visual approach to SBY reported a glideslope deviation message even though they were properly aligned with the PAPI lights.
A320 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing a landing gear door malfunction.
Tug driver reported during aircraft push back from the gate; another aircraft approached from behind and tug driver stopped.
A Flight Instructor reported engine roughness with RPM loss and returned to the departure airport.

Captain reported cabin failed to pressurize during climb. During troubleshooting it was noticed that the cabin pressure source knob was in the off position. Flight crew moved the switch to both position; restored cabin pressure and continued to destination airport.

A320 Captain reported rejecting the takeoff after receiving a Master Caution for ENG 1 Low Oil Pressure; noting that the aircraft had been written up for this anomaly several times in the last few days.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported a broken tow bar caused damage to a nose wheel and required replacement. The aircraft returned to the gate for maintenance action.

First Officer reported as the Captain called V1 rotate; I rotated the aircraft and the flight controls felt very light; almost unresponsive and the nose started rotating abruptly. The  aircraft controls started feeling normal around 500 ft. After post inspection we noticed scrape at the tail skid section of the aircraft.B737 flight crew reported flight control issues while rotating for takeoff. Upon arrival at the destination airport it was discovered a tail strike had occurred and a previous write up included an inoperative elevator feel and centering unit.

Air Carrier Captain reported while being deiced at the gate being advised that there was a significant amount of contamination on the wing resulting in the Captain requesting the aircraft be deiced again.

EMB-175 flight crew reported the autopilot did not capture the altitude selected of 2;000 feet during approach.  The crew did not notice the issue until they were at 1;500 feet and immediately corrected back to 2;000 feet.

Pilot reported taxiing onto the active runway without announcing their intentions on CTAF and observed a business jet on takeoff roll. The Pilot immediately exited the runway; with the jet passing overhead; well above.

A320 flight crew reported landing gear failed to report extended on gear selected down. The flight crew requested priority handling and performed a go-around. After running the check list and QRH procedures; they performed a fly by to ascertain the landing gear condition. The flight crew made a precautionary landing and were towed to the gate with landing gear pins installed.
Dispatcher reported  an unmanageable desk and asked for help.
B737 Flight Crew reported a fume event of unknown source during initial climb. The Flight crew completed appropriate QRH procedures and performed an air turn back.
A321 Mechanic reported overlooking requirements for a MEL and allowing the aircraft to fly in an unairworthy condition.
Air carrier Check Airman reported after applying intermediate power prior to takeoff; one engine continued to advance; leading to a rejected takeoff.

B737 flight crew reported multiple MELs on the aircraft required a complicated and unusual engine start sequence involving maintenance assistance. Flight crew recommended to oncoming crew that they refuse the aircraft.
Small aircraft pilots reported receiving TCAS alerts while flying the same traffic pattern at PAO airport resulting in an NMAC.
C172 student pilot reported misjudging turn onto taxiway resulted in loss of aircraft control and a taxiway excursion.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.
Air carrier flight crew reported the failure of a main system hydraulic line during initial climb.
Designated Pilot Examiner reported the cargo door opening during takeoff rotation while conducting a checkride.
PA-24-400 pilot reported the main landing gear failed to extend normally. Pilot was able to manually extend gear and land uneventfully.
Piper PA-28 pilot reported a landing gear extension problem; subsequently resolved; which resulted in course and altitude deviations while troubleshooting the landing gear issue.
B767 flight crew reported vibration in the flight control system. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
A321 pilot reported the aircraft began to roll during pushback when the tug driver disconnected without confirming with flight crew that brakes were set. Captain applied brakes to stop roll.
B737 Flight Crew reported a CFIT event during final approach and an ATC issued low altitude warning. The Flight Crew climbed to the correct altitude and continued the approach.
Air carrier ground personnel reported there was miscommunication regarding aft pit loading restrictions and the decision was made to hold off hazmat cargo.
A320 Captain reported safely returning to departure airport after experiencing a problem with a pack that led to a loss of cabin pressure.

Paraglider pilot reported a NMAC while approaching the airport when a PA-25 aircraft overflew the paraglider's centerline flight path. The reporter stated they made a diving turn to avoid the aircraft and continued to landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported a traffic conflict with another aircraft while in IMC weather on a ILS approach. The flight received a TCAS RA and followed the guidance then landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew reported arcing and cracking in the cockpit windshield and elected to divert to the nearest airport.
EMB-145 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing LAX in trail of an Airbus.
B737 Captain reported the flight attendants reported a smell of smoke and electrical burning during engine start. Flight returned to gate for maintenance action.
A light sport aircraft pilot reported gusty winds during landing caused a temporary loss of control and runway excursion.
Air carrier mechanic reported during MEL compliance procedure they moved the door arming lever causing the escape slide to deploy. Passengers were then deplaned.
Pilot reported; while on short final; an aircraft had taxied onto the runway requiring a go around. Pilot suggested that a renaming of the airport contributed to confusing CTAF calls.

B737-900 Flight Attendant reported notifying Captain during taxi of passenger prohibited Lithium battery items in checked bag. Flight returned to gate where passenger retrieved items; returned to passenger cabin and flight departed safely.

GA pilot reported an electrical failure resulting in the destination tower using light signals for clearance to land. After one missed approach due to no light signals; the next approach was successful.

A330 pilot reported receiving an aircraft terrain alert during approach and executing a missed approach. Reporter stated the approach PAPI indicated well below approach path while the aircraft altimeter indicated on path.
Inspector reported non compliance with AD procedures and deviations from repair instructions were discovered during maintenance of a C421 aircraft.

Part 107 UAS pilot reported they inadvertently flew in controlled airspace without authorization due to an error made when using a LAANC app.; "Flight instructor on training flight with student reported NMAC while in REI airport traffic pattern."
Air carrier Captain reported failure of autothrottles on approach into IND; citing possible 5G interference as contributing.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported a box containing DG was sent to the aircraft without complying with required tie down restrictions for air transport.

Pilot reported ground conflict with a hovering helicopter on an FBO ramp as both aircraft responded to the same marshaling signals. The pilot stopped and turned away from the helicopter to avoid further conflict.
A Flight Instructor reported frequent GPS signal failures at MVY are suspect of GPS jamming event.
A320 First Officer reported descending below an altitude restriction on descent into PHX; citing distractions from a wake turbulence encounter and multiple ATC clearances as contributing.
Air carrier Captain reported documentation and forms required for the flight operation were not stocked at the departure station and was forced to depart without them.
A321 First Officer reported decreasing engine oil quantity on #2 Engine in cruise. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.

TRACON Controller reported aircraft on departure turned towards area of higher terrain when assigned runway heading.  The Student Air Traffic Controller vectored the aircraft away from the higher terrain resulting with the aircraft returning to their flight plan.
Pilot reported many runway and taxiway lights at PTYA (YAP) airport do not work.
Piper Saratoga Pilot reported an off-airport landing after an engine failure while on approach to landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported taking evasive action in response to a TCAS RA on arrival into IAD.

B737-800 First Officer reported fuel imbalance developed during departure climb. First Officer stated the Captain did not perform the Fuel Leak QRH procedure to avoid inflight engine shutdown and the flight returned to the departure airport.

General aviation pilot reported while holding short of runway a landing aircraft was directed by ATC to exit at the same taxiway which resulted in a near collision. Controller stated this procedure is done all the time.
Cessna 560XL pilot reported experiencing visible smoke and fumes in the cockpit and cabin; resulting in an immediate diversion; followed by a successful landing and evacuation.
PA-44 flight instructor on training flight with student reported a hydraulic leak inflight.
C560 Captain reported misrigged engine throttle cables caused an dual inadvertent inflight shut down of the engines. Successful restarts were made and the flight crew returned to departure airport.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported being notified by another crew member after the flight that a service entry door had not been disarmed. No escape slide was reported as deployed on a future flight.
Air Carrier Captain reports the JEPP IAP identifications labeling of X; Y; Z RNAV IAPs should be standardized based on equipment required to conduct the approach; not as required by the airport.
While on a reinstatement check ride; the pilot stated; the Tower informed the flight they were below the MDA on approach.
Corporate Turboprop Pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from LAN Tower on approach to 10R.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a flight attendant bumped her head during a wake turbulence encounter from the preceding A319.

C-208 flight crew reported a runway excursion while landing in shifting and gusty winds at a non-towered airport. The aircraft stopped in the grass just beyond the end of the runway with no injuries or damage to the aircraft or airport property.
Pilot on a training flight landed hard damaging the nose wheel and engine.
B737-800 Captain reported the landing gear failed to retract after takeoff and the problem was apparently related to the manual gear extension door integrity.
Albatross L-39 pilot reported the canopy separated from aircraft during takeoff rotation. The pilot remained in the traffic pattern and landed safely.
B737 NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on final approach to EWR in trail of a B777.

Pilot reported a HYD B LO AIR PRESS ECAM displayed during boarding. Maintenance drained hydraulic fluid and cleared the fault. During takeoff; just prior to 80 knots; ECAM displayed HYD B RSVR LO LVL. The Captain rejected the takeoff around 120 knots; cleared the runway and taxied to gate without further incident.
GA pilot reported noticing the fuel reserve tank approaching zero while in cruise resulting in a divert to the nearest airport.
B767-300 Captain reported possible tail strike on landing.
Reporter advised of a vehicle almost hitting an aircraft that was pushing back.
PA-28 pilot reported experiencing communications failure during cruise.  Reporter continued to destination while trying to avoid Class C airspace.
Air carrier flight crew reported a GPWS Alert on initial approach to CHO airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the DAL Runway 31R glide slope has had interference from following aircraft and aircraft crossing on the ground; and the signal constantly bounces around on approach.

Air carrier Captain reported the undocumented transport of dry Ice due to missing NOTAC at departure gate. The Captain completed an exterior post flight inspection and was able to confirm that dry ice was in fact loaded without flight crew notification.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning during visual approach to CHO airport requiring the crew to disconnect the autopilot and climb before landing without further incident.
B73-800 Captain reported an uneventful air return due to an airborne fuel system malfunction during initial climb.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert warning from ATC during approach to COS airport.

Student pilot reported a NMAC while receiving flight following from CLE TRACON. The student pilot was in cruise when the aircraft ADS-B and the student pilots' Foreflight alerted the pilot to a potential collision with a single engine jet. No evasive action occurred and ATC never issued a traffic alert.
A Cirrus pilot departing a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with a helicopter.
Air carrier flight crew reported ORD Tower assigned them a 180 heading after departure; but were later advised they had been assigned a right turn heading 040.
Air carrier flight crew reported a pressurization malfunction during the initial descent. The crew started an immediate descent to a safer altitude; then continued to a landing at the destination.
Cessna 206 pilot reported a non-normal pitch up during climbout in VFR conditions after maintenance was performed prior to flight. The pilot was able to return to the departure airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported the NOTOC provided to flight crew was disorganized and confusing resulting in a hazardous situation.
EM ERJ 175 Captain reported a fumes event during approach descent. The Captain requested priority handling to a diversion airport where a safe landing was accomplished.

A Center Trainee Controller reported a military aircraft encountered an electrical malfunction and descended out of their assigned airspace in the vicinity of a military UAS flying near the airspace. The Controller attempted to vector the UAS to ensure separation but the UAS did not respond.
B737 Flight Crew reported a GPWS Warning during approach.
Pipistrel Virus pilot reported an engine failure at cruise altitude and glided to a landing on a displaced threshold of a nearby airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the windshield was struck by an object that was blown free due to the proceeding B787s jet blast during taxi. The crew continued to the gate and was met by maintenance.
Government UAS pilot reported flight above 400 AGL without authorization. Reporter stated they were training with new software and did not know the altitude was set using AGL; rather than MSL.
BE-200 First Officer reported an engine failure during climb. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Technician reported finding all of the cargo fire suppression bottles 3 years past their hydrostatic test due dates during a routine maintenance inspection to check the squibs.
General aviation pilot reported on engine test flight being distracted by engine readings resulted in an airspace violation and return to departure airport.
Corporate jet Flight Crew reported they deviated from an ATC clearance. Flight crew was issued a new clearance and landed safely.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another aircraft at a non-towered airport while in the traffic pattern; resulting in the flight instructor taking evasive action.
A319 Flight Attendant reported an air turn back and precautionary landing after a reported engine fire and in flight shut down of the affected engine.
B737 flight crew reported the landing gear failed to fully retract during departure. The crew returned to the departure airport and landed without incident.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC during pattern entry with a departing aircraft. Helicopter pilot hovered until clear of traffic and then landed.
PA-28 student pilot reported loss of directional control resulted in a runway excursion. Flight instructor reported accompanying the student on the return flight to home airport.
A TRACON Controller reported a departing air ambulance failed to turn as depicted on the SID and flew below the minimum vectoring altitude.

Air Traffic Controller reported a CFIT situation in a high traffic area with poor weather. The aircraft was climbing slowly; was below the MVA; and had deviated from the published SID. The Controller gave an altitude alert twice and later suggested emergency vectors would have been a better solution.
Air Carrier ground personnel reported Hazmat cargo labeled ship on 'Cargo aircraft only' was intercepted prior to being loaded.
C-402 Captain reported a taxiway excursion due to left brake failure due to freezing temperatures.
Air carrier Captain reported being notified of an unusual odor that was similar to electrical fumes to the aft of the aircraft during cruise and safely diverted.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy while boarding.
A Lancair Columbia pilot reported a failure of one of the main landing gear brakes during landing roll; resulting in a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft.
Air Carrier pilot reported seeing a drone at 3;000 ft. while on approach. ATC was informed and the flight landed uneventfully.
Air carrier Line Check Airman reported a loss of GPS signal for approximately 300 NM.
A319 flight crew reported an inflight shutdown of engine #1 due to the indication of fire in the engine caused by a bird strike. The flight crew then performed an air turnback.

Flight Attendant reported potable water system contamination with cloudy and foul smelling water. Maintenance later reported missing parts and a main system filter froze in place and irremovable from its housing. It could not be determined when it had been previously replaced.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported being unaware of UAS regulations for flight altitude restrictions.

Captain reported the loss of Hydraulic System #2 on approach to landing. After a normal landing the nose wheel steering was inoperative; but the crew taxied the aircraft to the gate using differential braking and thrust and passengers deplaned normally.
Air carrier flight crew flying an A319 aircraft reported loss of aircraft control during pushback.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing GPS spoofing that resulted in single-GPS operation and an apparently erroneous terrain alert on approach into EWR.
C172 flight instructor reported the aircraft's right wingtip struck a hangar while taxiing to the FBO's maintenance hangars to fix the transponder. The nav and strobe light was damaged by the impact.
B757 air carrier crew reported an Engine Overheat EICAS caution during cruise. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed.

Embraer 550 Legacy flight crew reported a Bleed 1 and subsequent Bleed 2 leak and failure during climbout at 38;000 feet.  The crew ran the appropriate checklists and elected to return to the departure airport.
Corporate Captain reported discovering a discrepancy between what was in the FMS and what was on the charts for the SNFLD Arrival into MCO.

Challenger 300 Captain reported the aircraft became unstabilized during final approach. The Captain directed the First Officer to execute a go-around who was slow in responding resulting in a bounced landing.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a UAS during approach. No evasive action was taken and the flight continued without further incident.
Flight Instructor reported returning to home airport with NAV lights and ADS-B off after an alternator failure.
Captain reported concerns over operation of an aircraft with an out of date NAV database that should have been deferred. The aircraft was later given the correct MEL as required.
A320 First Officer reported a high speed rejected takeoff due to an engine failure. The engine was shut down and the aircraft returned to the gate for maintenance.

Air carrier captain reported stopping short of the gate because the aircraft was not taxiing and braking normally. Upon stopping; two ramp tow in personal began to verbally assault the captain; yelling move the aircraft. The agents were removed from the area by management personal.

Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing a miscommunication with ground personnel during push back resulting in the flight crew starting an engine when the ground personnel were not in position.

EMB-145 First Officer reported a terrain alert occurred while the aircraft was crossing a mountain range as vectored by ATC. The crew notified ATC and received vectors to the approach which was completed normally.
Glider pilot reported during landing a vehicle crossed the runway requiring evasive action by pilot to avoid a collision.
B737-700 Captain reported communications issues and MEL compliance issues complicated a maintenance delay and contributed to down line complications with the MEL procedures.
Air carrier flight crew reported making a wrong turn onto an incorrect taxiway at DCA and suggested signage could be improved to prevent confusion.
Air ambulance Captain reported being notified that they had flown the helicopter in an unairworthy condition.
C182 pilot reported electrical systems overload in cruise. The pilot requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
PA-28 pilot reported electrical system malfunction while in cruise flight resulting in airspace violation with no functional transponder.  PA-28 pilot continued flight to home airport.
Air carrier Pilot reported a musty stinky sock odor during descent. The flight crew trouble shot the odor while continuing to destination for a safe landing.

Air carrier Captain reported that guard frequency is frequently being used to transmit non-aviation related chatter and sounds; resulting in the frequency becoming unusable as a tool in the event of an emergency.

EMB-170 Captain reported a pressurization malfunction during descent including erratic cabin rate of descent; which later corrected. The flight landed at the destination and the Captain insisted on maintenance troubleshooting of the system; which deferred the cabin auto pressurization system.

Corporate Jet First Officer reported during cruise flight the Emergency Electrical Bus failed. The pilot crew reconfigured the electrical system following the QRH procedures and diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
Air carrier pilot reported that if the Runway 02L PAPI guidance at SNA is followed it results in the aircraft crossing the threshold too high for the runway.
BE23 pilot reported a NMAC with an RV12 on base leg to a non towered airport.
King Air C90 Captain reported increasing vibration in the rudder pedals after takeoff. The Captain elected to conduct an air turn back and make a precautionary landing at departure airport.

GA pilot reported they had a near mid air collision with another aircraft while making a left bank turn after climbing heading to traffic pattern altitude. ATC notified them they had cut in front of traffic that was also on downwind.
Pilot reported a NMAC during cruise with an aircraft traveling opposite direction and same altitude.
A321 First Officer reported ECAM alert resulted in a rejected takeoff.
Air Carrier Flight Crew flying CRJ-200 aircraft reported engine throttles were unresponsive during descent.  Flight crew performed inflight shutdown.
Technicians reported that rig pins were left installed after a flight control cable tension check. This was discovered during taxi for takeoff by the flight crew.
Howard DGA-15 pilot reported a loss of control resulting in a runway excursion during landing rollout in strong crosswinds. No aircraft damage was reported.
A321 pilot reported ground crew began unloading bags from the cargo door while engines were still running and with rotating beacon on.

B737-800 pilot reported refusing an aircraft after it was found to be leaking fuel from the refuel manifold.  Maintenance discovered rag cloth fragments protruding from the poppet valve which reportedly prevented proper closure of the valve.
Corporate Captain reported that the flaps failed when they were retracted after takeoff so the crew did an air return and zero flap landing.
Flight crew reported left hand front windshield de-laminated and cracked at FL330. The flight crew diverted to make a precautionary landing.

EMB-120 Captain reported the loss of a hydraulic system while transitioning from climb to cruise flight. The crew manually extended the gear; conducted a flyby of the tower to verify the gear was down; and made a no flap landing at the destination airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported he failed to send Dispatch the confirmation of receipt of Hazmat to Dispatch prior to gate departure.

Flight Instructor and student reported a low altitude terrain alert while practicing instrument procedures in mountainous terrain. The instructor turned away from the terrain; then safely completed the flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of communication with ground personnel due to equipment failure.

Captain reported EICAS messages did not match the diagnosed Flap/Slat systems failure and elected to perform a go around. After retracting the flaps/slats; the EICAS messages disappeared and a normal approach and landing were made.
GA pilot reported not realizing the field was Tower controlled and almost entered a taxiway without clearance.
A320 flight crew reported a drop in the right engine oil quantity shortly after takeoff; resulting in a return to the departure airport.
C172 flight instructor reported an engine failure on a touch and go maneuver resulted in land to a rolling stop.

C-172 Flight Instructor reported an engine failure during training in the traffic pattern. Once safely at the airport the Instructor determined the student had improperly operated the carburetor mixture which simulated an engine failure.
B737 flight crew reported cabin pressurization issue during arrival descent. Crew donned oxygen masks and continue to landing.

DA-40 Flight Instructor with student reported the loss of a cabin door in flight when the Instructor attempted to close the door after it had become unlatched. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed safely.

Beechjet 400 flight crew reported Tail Ice Protection failure during IMC conditions. Flight crew was unable to restore Ice Protection for the Horizontal Stabilizer and continued the approach to landing at destination airport.
B-767 flight crew reported an oil filter EICAS message while on descent from cruise. The crew shut down the engine and continued to destination airport where a safe landing was completed.
Pilot reported losing both MFD and PFD; resulting in a probable airspace violation.

C-152 private pilot and accompanying safety pilot reported fluctuating RPM and a rough running engine during cruise. RPM reductions correlated to a reduction in aircraft speed so pilots elected to divert and land as a precaution.

Gulfstream pilot reported missing a crossing altitude restriction during descent after a loss of aircraft control in severe turbulence and convective weather. Crew regained control and landed normally with no damage noted.

SR20 student pilot reported engine RPM malfunction during takeoff; resulting in rejected takeoff.; "PA-28 Flight Instructor reported getting close to an IFR aircraft while conducting maneuvers for an engine break-in training flight at night."
Flight Instructor reported low rpm and weak climb performance during departure and returned to the airport.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing DTW in trail of an A320.
First Officer reported local ATC amended the departure altitude which resulted in a low altitude alert from Center.

B737-800 flight crew reported a 'dirty socks' odor during takeoff roll. The flight crew rejected the takeoff and requested special handling to return to the departure gate where the passengers deplaned and maintenance was provided.
TRACON Controller and air carrier pilot reported the pilot descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude due to a misunderstanding of the assigned altitude between the two parties.
Corporate flight crew reported the autopilot failed to capture their assigned altitude and they received a low altitude alert from ATC.
EUG TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on vectors failed to intercept the the approach course and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Pilot reported that ATC issued a traffic alert while on departure resulting in a NMAC.
Air carrier Captain reported a rejected takeoff due to a compressor stall on takeoff roll.
Flight Instructor with student reported the engine lost power during climb and they performed an off-airport landing.

EMB-145 First Officer reported an uncommanded 50 degree roll on approach. The flight crew immediately corrected the roll and noticed the autopilot had disconnected. The flight crew continued the approach to landing.
A TRACON Controller conducting OJT reported their trainee issued a vector to an aircraft which placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A300 flight crew reported on climb out experiencing number 2 engine failure resulting in a return to the departure airport.
A319 Captain reported a fumes event at departure gate during pre-flight. Flight crew deplaned; Maintenance was requested and a fumes report submitted.
Air carrier pilot crew reported at cruise altitude the number 2 engine failed. The crew was able to restart the engine and then returned to the departure airport.
Air Carrier ramp personnel reported being notified of Dangerous Goods package mistakenly left in aft cargo compartment.
C550 single pilot reported smoke during cruise. Special handling was requested and ATC provided special handling to a diversion airport where a safe visual landing was executed.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during the arrival descent. No procedures were completed because the odor began to dissipate. Captain met with Maintenance Control after gate arrival.
Check pilot and pilot reported two blown tires on the single engine aircraft after the pilot applied heavy braking.
B737 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence and recovering from an inflight upset on final approach to EWR in trail of a B767.

Grumman AA5B instructor pilot reported the student lost directional control on takeoff and after the reporter took the controls the aircraft sustained minor damage from contact with berms adjacent to the runway.
Flight Instructor reported NMAC on climbout; with traffic turning crosswind early; resulted in the need for evasive action.
Pilot reported that after a prop start the aircraft jumped the chocks and separated from the single tiedown.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving an altitude alert from ATC while on approach. The crew maintained visual separation from obstacles and continued the approach.

B737-800 Flight Crew reported that a gate agent and assistant attempted to open an armed aircraft door from the outside after noticing a jetbridge temperature probe cord protruding from the door. Flight Attendants prevented an inadvertent escape slide deployment.
C172 Flight Instructor with student reported on climbout; partial loss of engine power resulted in an expeditious return to departure airport to a safe landing.
Flight crew reported failure to follow procedures during cross feed operations to correct a fuel imbalance resulted in an inadvertent in flight shut down of the Right Engine.
Reporter overlooked the hazardous material part of the process while shipping parts; resulting in hazardous materials being shipped undocumented.
Captain reported distractions and crew communications resulted in a descent below minimum altitude during approach; causing the crew to climb back to the correct altitude.

EMB-170 flight crew reported unreliable airspeed and pitch attitude indications on the Captain's and First Officer's instruments during initial climb. Flight crew completed an air turn back and landing at departure airport.
Flight Crew reported a safety cone was ingested by a running engine after a ground crewman approached too close to the engine.

Tower Controller working all positions combined alone in the Tower reported a departing aircraft had an engine failure and returned to land safely. The Controller reported they were unable to provide all required services and the situation was unsafe due to them having to work alone in the tower due to insufficient Controller staffing at the Tower.
Cessna 150 single pilot reported an alternator failure during cruise causing multiple electrical issues. Pilot requested priority handling and executed a mechanical diversion to a safe landing.
Air carrier Maintenance Supervisor described maintenance requirements without a published procedure.
Air Carrier Captain reported ATC warned them of a 'Low Altitude Alert' while on the RNAV X Runway 31 approach to LGA airport.
PA28 pilot reported partial power loss followed by a total engine failure during cruise resulting in an off airport landing.
A319 Captain reported exceeding nose wheel steering trim limits to taxi the aircraft straight; resulting in a gate return and aircraft refusal.
PA-32 reported a nose gear collapse on the first takeoff attempt after annual inspection. The aircraft was removed from the runway and moved to a hangar for inspection and repair.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an RA from helicopter traffic below them during a go-around at MIA.
Pilot reported a runaway stabilizer trim system and elected to air turn back and make a precautionary emergency landing.
Pilot of a home built aircraft reported a loss of control during landing that resulted in a runway excursion with no reported injuries or aircraft damage.

ZJX Controller reported working numerous aircraft deviating for weather into military airspace when the controlling facility unexpectedly would not approve anymore deviations. Due to the workload the reporter was not able to issue weather advisories and an aircraft encountered severe turbulence injuring passengers while another Air Carrier advised and deviated into the closed military airspace.
First Officer reported conflicting procedures between B737 NG and B737 MAX flight operating manuals.
C172 pilot reported failing to activate runway lights with multiple approach efforts resulted in violation of safe altitude over populated areas.

Air carrier flight crew reported a course deviation while being vectored for an approach to DAL airport. The flight crew indicated similar sounding fixes SUMLN and JALIM contributed to the event. The course was corrected and the flight continued to a safe approach.

Flight instructor and student pilot reported the student was given a hold short instruction and accidentally entered the runway. Another aircraft on final approach had to perform a go-around and ATC directed the student to turn back to taxiway.
B777-200 pilot reported the door actuator for the escape slide was activated when ramp crew opened the door after a ferry flight. The Captain prevented slide deployment and maintenance was called.

A319 flight crew reported while holding for weather got an FCU2 fault. They performed a reset; but the fault came back on and it did not clear. The pilot hand flew until FD; Autothrust set up and reconnected the autopilot. The weather cleared and they landed visually.
Pilot reported a taxiway incursion onto a closed taxiway at LNC airport; due to the taxiway not identified on the airport diagram or taxi chart.
Commercial rated pilot reported flying over a congested city area; too low; in violation of 91.119.
Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC event during ATC assigned altitude with a large transport aircraft. The flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
B757 air carrier crew reported a flap malfunction on final approach. The crew performed a go-around; then returned for a landing.
Instructor pilot reported his student was cleared to cross a runway that had another aircraft on its takeoff roll; forcing the student to stop abruptly to avoid a collision.
Flight crew reported conducting a missed approach due to severe turbulence; heavy rain and zero visibility along with a diversion to a nearby airport where conditions were better.

Instructor pilot reported Alternator Failure in cruise. The aircraft went NORDO before priority handling could be requested. The flight crew continued to destination airport; received green light gun signals and made a precautionary landing.
Air carrier aircraft Inspector reported that a rudder PCU they had worked on previously was found installed with a missing bushing.
EMB-145LR First Officer reported smoke from vents and cabin area due to equipment failure resulted in expeditious handling and divert to nearest airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported during an automated return to home the UAS was struck by a vehicle due to landing on a roadway.
Air carrier flight crew reported parking brake failure at gate.
Air Carrier ramp personnel reported passenger checked bag contained Lithium powered pack. Ramp personnel successfully removed the bag before flight departure.
CE-680A Captain reported during taxi had no nose wheel steering. They stopped the aircraft; set the brakes and exited the aircraft to connect the nose wheel steering linkage.
Flight Crew reported engine roughness on approach to landing. They were able to continue to a precautionary landing.
M5 pilot reported a CFTT event during final approach that resulted from focusing on an electronic tablet software program instead of their approach.
A320 flight crew reported a parking brake pressure failure during pushback resulted in an uncommanded aircraft movement and a loss of control.

PA-28 Flight Instructor reported engine surging after takeoff at low altitude on a training flight with a student and immediately returned to the departure airport. The aircraft landed safely and maintenance later discovered a leak in a fuel line.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a light aircraft on approach to LAX.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during boarding. Maintenance removed the aircraft from service and another was assigned.

B767 Captain reported temporary loss of hydraulic pressure and subsequent uncontrolled movement of the aircraft on the ramp.  The First Officer mistakenly had shut down the hydraulic pumps; causing loss of hydraulic pressure.

Flight instructor in a Piper Arrow reported encountering wake turbulence that resulted in 60 degree left and right banks on approach to CHS when a B737 overflew and descended ahead of the Piper.; "A321 Maintenance Inspector reported that a repetitive write-up that should have required additional maintenance actions went unnoticed and there was a non-compliance with procedures."

B737 Captain reported stopping the aircraft in the middle of turning to park at the gate to avoid a collision with a vehicle that failed to yield. The vehicle only stopped when the Captain turned on the aircraft's lights.
A300 flight crew reported flap malfunction during takeoff climb. Returned and landed uneventfully.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another VFR aircraft.
Heavy Transport aircraft Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL320 from a preceding Heavy aircraft in RJTG airspace.
Air Carrier pilot reported a fume event upon applying APU bleeds at gate. APU bleeds were turned off and the crew deplaned.
Air carrier Captain reported while waiting for takeoff another aircraft proceeded across a non movement area and cut in front of the Captain's aircraft.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a recurring fume event during pre-flight. As Maintenance was formulating a plan to defer the APU and/or Pack 1; the flight crew timed out resulting in the flight being rescheduled.
Small Aircraft Instructor Pilot reported while holding short of the runway; another aircraft was lined up incorrectly to land and overflew their aircraft.
PA-32 single pilot reported an air return due to a fumes event during initial climb.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a helicopter after landing at RAP airport during taxi in. The helicopter was cleared to depart from a taxiway with limited visibility from the Tower and the helicopter passed just in front of the pilot on the taxiway as he stopped immediately.
C310 pilot reported the right engine began surging after takeoff making aircraft control difficult. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from SFO Tower on a visual approach to Runway 28R when the crew failed to notice a change in autopilot mode.
A320 Captain reported a loss of #1 Engine oil quantity and pressure. The flight crew performed and in flight shut down of the #1 Engine and continued to the destination airport.
Flight Instructor reported while entering the VFR traffic pattern encountering a departing aircraft turning into them resulting in both aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.

B767-300 flight crew reported in cruise flight at FL400; receiving an EICAS Message L OIL FILTER. The flight crew ran the QRH and checklists;then complied with procedures. The Engine Thrust Lever was retarded to idle; with the Message staying Illuminated. The flight crew performed an in-flight engine shutdown and continued to the destination airport.

MD-11 flight crew reported ground personnel reported flames emanating from their number two engine during taxi out. After maintenance inspected the engine and fire personnel reported no fire the flight crew continued their taxi and flight.

Large Transport Captain reported parking at a gate with large aircraft is dangerous due to their wings extending over the vehicle access roads and vehicles driving too close to aircraft while parking.
EMB-170 Captain reported a Hazmat configuration violation during preflight of a ferry flight. Completed an uneventful flight after Hazmat cargo was removed.
B737 Captain reported ground personnel requested APU on overnight; but failed to monitor low fuel pressure.
Flight crew reported distractions from a near mid air collision resulted in the landing check list not being performed and a gear up landing.
Air carrier ground personnel reported an aircraft had to return to the gate to correct a Hazmat loading configuration issue.

A single engine Pilot reported they entered the wrong destination into their avionics resulting in turning the wrong direction and receiving a Low Altitude Alert from ATC. The reporter was diverting from their original clearance destination to refuel the aircraft.
B757-200 flight crew reported gear extension and indication problems on final. A go-around was performed and the flight crew landed at destination airport.

A Center Controller; and a PA32 pilot flying through rain and experiencing turbulence reported the aircraft had a vacuum pump failure causing a temporary loss of control of the aircraft. The aircraft descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude but recovered and landed safely.
C182 Pilot reported an NMAC with a helicopter and unstable approach while attempting a touch and go at a non-towered airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported turning in the wrong direction after landing at CYVR; resulting in an ATC-directed go-around for an aircraft on short final.

Flight crew reported conflicting glidepath information while on a RNAV GPS approach to Runway 20 at CMD in visual conditions. The flight crew received a RAAS 'too high' callout; while the aircraft was on the RNAV GPS glidepath; and the ground PAPI guidance was showing the aircraft high. The aircraft landed in the touchdown zone.
A Tower Controller reported GPS outage.
EMB-145 First Officer reported executing a go-around from low altitude at CLT after encountering wake from an A321 departing on the same runway.
Flight Instructor on training flight reported engine running rough in cruise and returned to the departure airport.

Pilot reported ILS hold short lines on Taxiway V approaching Runway 28R at ORD are depicted as runway hold lines on their multi function display. Reportedly; the lines on the taxiway are ILS hold lines.
BE20 crew reported a CFTT event after descending below the glide path on an RNAV approach. Pilot Monitoring reported being distracted after breaking out and missed the deviation below glide path.

Extra 300 Pilot reported the canopy shattered and unlatched on takeoff requiring the pilot to grab the canopy. Pilot reported that taking hand off the control stick could have resulted in loss of control.
Helicopter pilot reported electrical smell and smoke in the cockpit coming from the floor below the panel. The pilot shut off all electrical power and landed at destination airport.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported a reduction in RPM's while on downwind and was unable to advise ATC due to frequency congestion.
Cessna 172 pilot reported an engine sputter malfunction when switching fuel tanks during cruise. The pilot diverted to an off-line airport and landed safely.
Cessna 172 instructor with student reported the engine begun to run rough after doing a simulated engine failure.  They made an immediate landing at the nearest airport.
C172 Flight Instructor with student reported engine failure during short final approach. Flight crew landed safely.
A TRACON Controller reported an air carrier pilot descending on a STAR reported a NMAC with a motorized glider .
Two air carrier captains reported delays due to incorrect MEL procedures performed by maintenance and a passenger not complying with face mask policy.

A Tower Controller reported an aircraft on a Visual Approach was not coordinated with them as required in their Letter of Agreement with TRACON. The aircraft was too high to land and when maneuvering for their descent they came into conflict with another aircraft in the TRACON airspace.

Center Controller reported they assigned an aircraft an airway whose altitude was below the Minimum Enroute Altitude for that airway. The aircraft was also below the Minimum IFR Altitude for the airspace.
General aviation pilot reported a near midair collision with a UAS while in the traffic pattern. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
G450 First Officer reported a cracked forward windscreen at cruise altitude.  Flight crew diverted to safe landing.
Pilot reported the inadvertent crossing of a hold short line due to unfamiliarity with taxiways after ATC had instructed a last minute runway change while on final approach.
Recreational / Hobbyist  UAS pilot reported they completed the TRUST Certification. They conducted a flight over people and then realized they were not allowed to do that.

PA-28 general aviation pilot reported a runway excursion while landing with a tailwind at a non-towered airport in visual conditions. The pilot regained control and stopped the aircraft after the overrun and evacuated safely.
Air Carrier Ramp Personnel reported removing one of three Hazmat items during preflight due to Hazmat a loading violation.
C172 student pilot on a solo flight reported loss of control during landing resulted in runway excursion.

A Y90 (Yankee TRACON) Controller reported ALB TRACON does not have communication capability in a portion of ALB airspace. Y90 TRACON does have communication coverage in this airspace. Reporter stated coordination difficulties when aircraft transition this airspace.
Air Carrier pilot reported two instances of the B737 MAX Auto Pilot malfunctioning that the reporter did not enter into the maintenance logbook.
C320 pilot reported there was a vibration that was alleviated when power was reduced on the right engine. The pilot then made a precautionary landing at an airport.
Pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft on downwind at a non-towered airport.
C172 flight instructor reported his student experienced a runway excursion during a solo flight.

Air carrier Ramp Personnel reported the transport of a battery powered mobility device that was improperly configured for cargo transport. The mobility device batteries fell off the device during off loading at destination.
Part 107 UAS crew reported during a training flight an incorrect switch was manipulated causing the UAV to make uncommanded movements. The UAS then had a lost link and impacted a field.
An instructor providing instruction for touch and goes on a grass strip reported they temporarily lost control during landing and the airplane nosed over resulting in a prop strike.
A TRACON Controller in Charge reported there was a NMAC during a period of busy traffic during deteriorating weather with sectors combined and training occuring.
Pilot reported Z09/PFKA airport runway lights were out of service but there was no NOTAM advising pilots of this condition.
During preflight; The First Officer reported an APU fire. The aircraft was evacuated and the fire extinguished.

CL-650 Captain reported flying below published minimum safe altitude while on a visual approach and the Controller highlighting an area of tower antennas in the flight path. The Captain climbed back to the last altitude then completed the flight safely.
Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a Near Mid Air Collision.
B737NG First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach in trail of a B747.
Pilot reported taking evasive action during an NMAC with another aircraft.

Air carrier maintenance Inspectors reported they monitored the installation of the ground plate and the right auxiliary pitot probe. The aircraft had a F.O. airspeed data inop failure message in flight and returned to the airport.

Air carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation during cruise after instituting a 2 mile right offset as a SLOP while still under radar contact. Captain cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
EMB-175 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence at FL340 from a B737 that crossed their path 1000 ft. above them.
A General Aviation pilot reported entering CHD Class D airspace at 2;900 feet on a training flight with an instructor; citing the smell of a gas leak as a contributing factor.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported the undocumented air transport of Hazmat cargo.
First Officer reported heavier than normal elevator force was required to control the aircraft after maintenance. The aircraft was returned to maintenance post flight for repair.
Air carrier Captain reported NMAC during initial climb from MSN airport.
Maintenance Technician reported safety concerns related to the having to wear a face mask while performing job duties on airport property and suggested the requirement guidelines should be revisited.

A319 flight crew reported receiving a F/CTL FLAPS SYS 1 FAULT ECAM message appeared during configuring for landing. After aborting the approach to run checklists; a subsequent approach was made to a successful landing.
CRJ-900 Captain reported multiple systems failures on climb. The flight crew performed an air turnback and precautionary landing at the departure airport.
ZDC Controller reported developmental issued incorrect missed approach instructions resulted in an aircraft entering a higher MVA and a CFIT event.
EMB-175 Check Airman reported a critical ground conflict during takeoff initial climb with a taxing aircraft crossing departure runway.

Air carrier flight crew reported the undocumented transport of a gas generator which contained gas. The gas generator was not processed in accordance with company Hazmat procedures and not discovered until destination arrival.

Air carrier First Officer reported landing safely after receiving an EGPWS alert related to the descent commanded by TCAS in response to an RA from military aircraft apparently performing an intercept maneuver on approach to CRP.
EMB-175 Captain reported they landed safely with a brake malfunction but neglected to notify Dispatch of the malfunction.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a bleed leak warning and multiple anti-icing failures while in icing conditions near the FAF. The crew continued the approach to exit the icing conditions and landed.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported the phone number for automated weather information at MFR did not work. The Tower was closed and the phone number was vital to get current weather information in preparation for landing.
Flight instructor with student reported while practicing maneuvers; they had an NMAC with a helicopter that was not making position reports.
Denver Center Controller reported an ongoing problem with Denver TRACON not approving direct routing for MEDEVAC aircraft; to help expedite the aircraft.

E145 First Officer reported the #2 Bleed temperature was low and the Anti-Ice System failed during cruise flight. The flight crew descended to a lower altitude to exit out of icing conditions and continued to the destination airport.
EMB-145 First Officer reported they were given taxi clearance but told the captain to stop when they saw a helicopter pulling up over them to avoid a collision.
A320 Captain reported a strong odor during a ground stop due to lighting. After conferring with maintenance they decided to deplane and submit a logbook entry.
Aircraft technician reported hydraulic leak due to improper fitting installed.

A TRACON Controller reported their radar unexpectedly failed resulting in a loss of separation between two aircraft they were vectoring for approach. One of the aircraft on vectors flew below the MVA.
B737 flight crew reported experiencing an abrupt pitch down event when engaging the autopilot system; resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
A C172 pilot reported a NMAC with a skydiving aircraft while operating near a charted skydiving area.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported heading deviation during approach.
Air carrier ramp loading lead reported being notified that the aircraft cargo was improperly loaded and two cargo carts had not been loaded onto the aircraft.
Cessna 172 pilot reported that the engine quit because of fuel starvation even though the fuel gauges showed fuel was still available. Pilot landed safely.
B737 First Officer reported a forward flight deck inner window pane failed during cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported a light aircraft on approach to landing flew very close to his taxiing aircraft at SNA airport.
Flight Attendant reported there was no social distancing by the aircraft cleaners during deplaning and also stated the Gate Agent was not wearing a face mask.

CRJ-900 Captain reported an apparent failure of the Type 1 and Type 4 deice and anti-icing fluids that had been applied to the aircraft during a heavy snowstorm pre-departure. After noting significant snow accumulation on the wing the crew returned to the gate and noted numerous other aircraft had done the same.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported making several attempts at obtaining LAANC authorization. After failed attempts to obtain authorization the UAS pilot conducted the flight without it.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another light aircraft shortly after takeoff at SFB.
A319 Captain reported they had to stop the climb due to an ECAM for a yellow hydraulic reservoir overheat during climb and resulted in a return to the departure airport.

Air carrier technician reported hearing a pop sound and noticed another technician in the process of closing the thrust reverser cowl. The core cowl support was still attached to the thrust reverser and the support got damaged during closing.

CRJ-700 First Officer reported becoming task saturated during the descent and approach in an unfamiliar foreign country. The pilot states it was very difficult to understand the controller's instructions; the aircraft's path was directly into the sun; the haze and smog made visibility much worse; the pilot's aircraft configuration was initially incorrect for the position on the approach and the GPWS sounded because the aircraft was too low. The pilot reported the aircraft was stable by the FAF.
A109E Captain reported ATC failure to provide separation resulted in evasive action taken to avoid an NMAC.
Bonanza Pilot reported losing control of aircraft due to possibly ineffective braking; resulting in a runway excursion and making ground contact with the aircraft nose.
Flight Crew reported miscommunication lead to a departure with a fuel leak that required a diversion and a precautionary emergency landing.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported experiencing a stick shaker on go-around following an unstabilized approach.

Mooney M-20 pilot reported the aircraft suddenly veered to the right during landing and exited the runway surface; striking a light with the propeller. Pilot observed the wind had changed direction and they had landed with a tailwind.

General aviation pilot reported a tail wheel failure during landing roll out at a tower controlled airport. The aircraft was disabled on the runway and the pilot coordinated with ATC and a mechanic to tug the aircraft to the ramp for repairs.
C172 pilot reported losing control of the aircraft during landing roll resulting in a runway excursion.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a L ENG OIL PRESS master warning on climb; resulting in an In Flight Shut Down and an Air Turn Back to precautionary landing.

Air carrier pilot reported a temporary loss of aircraft control caused by an incorrect aircraft handling while correcting a heading deviation. The airspeed and heading deviations were corrected which enabled the flight to continue uneventfully.
Falcon 7X pilot reported 'number 2 engine began surging in a manner consistent with compressor stalling' shortly after liftoff. Tower provided priority handling for a return to departure airport.
B737 Captain reported releasing the brakes for pushback only to realize that the flight crew did not have the final dangerous goods paperwork.
Part 107 pilot reported observing another UAS operator flying a nearby UAS have a near miss with a general aviation aircraft on climb out.
