ERJ-145 First Officer reported an E2 Anti Ice Fail Caution Light followed by a Bleed 1 Over Temp Warning Light and a Wing Anti Ice Fail Caution Light. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and ATC declared minimum fuel to expedite the approach and landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported a TCAS RA during visual approach to SFO Runway 28L when an aircraft joined the approach to the parallel runway. The flight crew followed RA instructions; performed a go-around; and advised ATC they were not able to accept another simultaneous approach.
CL-350 flight crew reported engine failure inflight. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a formation flight maneuvering below Class B airspace. The pilot maneuvered away from the reported position of the formation flight.
Flight crew reported a flap asymmetry condition and elected to perform a go around. QRH procedures were complied with and the crew reattempted the approach and  landed at the destination airport.
Air carrier pilot reported a terrain warning while descending for a visual approach. The pilot continued the approach and landed.

Two Flight Instructors reported a near miss with each other at a non-towered airport. The aircraft on final approach performed a go-around when the other aircraft landed on the same runway; opposite direction.

Piper pilot reported a critical ground conflict by violating the clearance to hold short of the runway with an aircraft on short final. The Tower issued the landing aircraft a go around clearance to avoid a collision.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a rejected take off for severe nose wheel shimmy.
GA7-Cougar flight instructor reported intermittent vibration in the rudder pedals during cruise flight. A post flight inspection revealed a screw was missing on the rudder.
Flight Crew reported severe controllability issues during initial take off. The flight Crew rejected the take off and returned to the Gate for an aircraft swap to continue the flight.

A321 Captain reported being notified by a Flight Attendant of a passenger's smoldering Lithium Ion battery pack during descent. The battery pack was placed in a Thermal Containment Bag while the flight crew was provided priority handling to the destination where a safe landing was executed.

EMB-170 Captain reported the pushback crew failed to follow the standard communications and pushback procedures resulting in loss of control. The Captain regained control and requested an external inspection before continuing.
Small aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with a UAS during cruise flight. No evasive action was taken.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to ORD that resulted in an upset. Flight crew executed a go-around and returned for a safe landing.
EMB-190 Captain reported the loss of #2 Hydraulic System after landing required a tow to the gate due to no nose wheel steering.
PA-28 pilot reported a runway incursion due to a series of distractions and a brake malfunction.
BE-76 Flight Instructor reported incorrect procedures were used during flight training and resulted in an engine-out landing at departure airport.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft experiencing radio difficulties did not respond to ATC instructions and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a lost link during flight and was unable to recover the UAS.
UAS pilot reported transmitter issues during a cell tower inspection due to possible radio antenna interference.
Air carrier Captain reported a lack of recency flying and a reliance on the muscle memory/normal after start flows contributed to an error in turning the APU off prematurely.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported vibrations in the flight controls that worsened with increased airspeed. Flight crew diverted to a nearby airport.
Student pilot reported they did not exit the runway during landing roll as instructed by ATC; resulting in ATC-directed go-around of the aircraft landing after the student.
A fractional jet pilot reported they deviated for conflicting traffic pointed out to them by ATC when a second unknown aircraft flew towards them resulting in a NMAC.
Small aircraft pilot reported experiencing a NMAC when another small aircraft attempted to make a short approach in front of the reporters aircraft.

CRJ-900 First Officer reported loss of all electrical power during approach to landing. The flight crew elected to continue to landing at destination airport. Priority handling was requested on the ground for assistance in removing the passengers and towing the aircraft off the runway to the gate.
B757 First Officer reported during cruise the aircraft experienced hydraulic system malfunction resulting in landing at an alternate airport.

PC-12 Inspector reported the engine was improperly rigged after an engine change. During a post-maintenance test flight; the torque and propeller RPM was improperly high and the pilot notified Maintenance upon arrival.
C150 pilot reported they ran out of fuel during flight and landed safely in a field.

First Officer reported concerns over maintenance procedures that did not appear to follow established practices during preflight and post flight walk arounds and items entered into the aircraft log book.
GA pilot reported a track heading deviation after entering incorrect information in the GPS and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
PA28 pilot reported rudder trim malfunction caused controllability issues and a diversion to a nearby airport with a suitable runway.
Air carrier dispatcher reported aircraft on go around due to turbulence experienced a wingtip strike.

Ramp Agent reported tug malfunction during an aircraft repositioning event. Ramp Agent reported as the aircraft neared the line at the gate; the tug began to buck and was uncontrollable. After stopping and moving slowly to the correct line; it was noticed that the tow bar shear pin was broken. The Crew Chief was notified.

PA23 Flight Instructor reported rejecting a takeoff after a loud bang and buffet were encountered during the takeoff roll. After returning to parking it was discovered that an inspection panel had not been completely secured in anticipation of further maintenance action and the aircraft had been out of service.
Company safety employee reported an NMAC. The company's aircraft was on approach.
Pilot reported having a near miss during landing with other aircraft landing on opposite end of runway due to miscommunication with ATC.

A321 Captain reported a priority right side stick anomaly during approach and elected to perform a go-around. The Captain disconnected autothrust and autopilot and re-tried the approach and landing after assigning side stick priority to the Captain's side.
Air carrier First Officer reported the yellow hydraulic system failed during climb on the A321.
B737 flight crew reported the Captain's forward windscreen delaminated and shattered. The flight crew performed a diversion to uneventful landing.
EMB-170 pilot crew reported a RA and NMAC while approaching Class B airspace. The PF followed the TCAS commands to avoid the intruder. ATC stated they were aware of the other aircraft.

B737-700 First Officer reported loss of cabin pressure control. The flight crew requested priority handling and descended. The flight crew elected to continue to destination airport and make a precautionary landing.
ATR 72 Flight crew reported an in flight shut down of the #1 engine because of high torque indications. The flight crew made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported obstacle alert on approach.
An Air Taxi pilot reported descending below obstacle clearance minimums while conducting a Visual Approach.

Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying within a TFR without authorization. Reporter stated they checked for TFRs using only the drone manufacturer's flight planning tools and did not see any TFRs listed.

Experimental Pilot reported hitting a power line while landing on an unfamiliar runway. The pilot stated the aircraft's prop was slightly damaged and the power company inspected and repaired the power line.
B777-300 Captain reported the First Officer noticed the NOTOC was missing and the flight crew coordinated to get the required documentation generated.
Air carrier pilot reported the left engine lost oil during cruise and stopped operating. The flight crew shut down the engine; returned to the departure airport; and landed safely.

B767 flight crew reported a GPWS 'Too Low; Terrain'  alert during IFR approach in a stabilized condition. Flight crew executed a 'Go Around'. Flight crew then requested another approach which was completed safely with no further issues.
Student pilot reported a NMAC with a crop dusting aircraft while practicing maneuvers.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a BLEED 1 LEAK Master Warning on descent into IMC icing conditions and after descending out of icing; the warning discontinued.

A321 Captain reported while climbing out the Weather Radar failed during a thunderstorm forecast route. The flight received special handling to the departure airport where a safe landing was executed after also sustaining a bird strike on short final.
C310 Pilot reported throttle control failed in flight. Pilot secured the affected engine and continued to a safe landing.

Two Flight Instructors from the same flight school reported a NMAC while climbing out from the airport. The pilots reported they were given instructions from ATC which created the conflict. Evasive action was taken by both instructor pilots.
Maintenance reported wrong lead in lines resulted in confusion and an aircraft was towed into contact with a hangar door resulting in winglet damage.
Embraer Jet Flight Attendant reported an air turn back due to a pressurization problem due to a cockpit window air leak. A precautionary landing was made at departure airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during initial descent. Flight landed safely and crew provided a logbook entry resulting in the aircraft being taken out of service.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on a visual approach to CPR
Flight Attendant reported forgetting to brief the exit row and cited not flying for five months as a contributing factor.
Pilot reported issues relating to the shortening of the runway at PKWA airport.

C182 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control and a propeller strike while landing in gusty crosswinds with LLWS.  Aircraft pitched over from a gust after landing but returned to it's normal tricycle gear stance allowing for normal taxi.
OMA Controller reported developmental issued radar controller's instruction to avoid a confliction which resulted in entering a higher MVA and a Controlled Flight Toward Terrain event.
Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC during cruise with an aircraft that was not displayed on ADS-B nor communicating on frequency.
Air carrier flight crew reported a main gear tire failed during takeoff roll; resulting in a rejected takeoff. Passengers were deplaned and the aircraft towed to the hangar.
C421 Pilot reported engine failed to produce full power resulted in divert and eventual engine shut down.
Technician reported spinning nut plates were discovered during a SATCOM radome removal. The spinning nut plates were not properly documented and the aircraft continued to operate in service.
AT6A pilot reported loss of control on landing due to wind resulted in wing impacting the runway.  Pilot reported no damage and was able to taxi to tie down.
C172 Instructor Pilot and Trainee reported a loss of control and a runway excursion during student solo training. No injuries or aircraft damage reported.
UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
B737 flight crew reported flap control failure during approach and go around. Crew ran QRH and returned for uneventful landing.
Ramp worker reported a near collision between two aircraft being pushed to their assigned spots.
B737-800 flight crew reported severe weather on approach leading to a flap overspeed and unstabilized approach.
Government pilot reported an NMAC with a UAS during initial approach. No evasive action was taken.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of GPS signal on arrival to Denver.

Air carrier Flight Attendant notified the Captain of a 'burning; smoke-like odor' shortly after takeoff. After troubleshooting with negative results the Captain executed a precautionary return to departure airport where a safe landing was made.

B737 flight crew reported an engine failure during the takeoff roll at approximately 90 knots. The crew performed a rejected takeoff and was notified by ATC of visible smoke from the failed engine. After an inspection by fire crews the flight taxied back to the gate.
GA pilot reported an NMAC near LMO airport.
BE58 pilot reported they experienced a CFTT event while troubleshooting avionics in turbulent flight conditions. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Agriculture pilot reported two near midair collisions with a UAS while approaching their air strip.
A320 Pilot reported loss of cabin pressure and a return to their departure airport where they made an overweight landing.

General aviation student pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft while on a solo training flight at a non-towered airport. The other aircraft entered the downwind at the same point where the student turned from crosswind to downwind.
B777-200 Captain reported that while airborne; Dispatch notified them of an incorrect number of Lithium-Ion batteries listed on flight crew hazmat documents.

ORD Controllers reported an air carrier aircraft initiated a wrong turn resulting in controllers taking expedited measures to maintain separation with two other departing aircraft. Controller stated that this is an extremely unsafe situation that has become a chronic problem.
Cessna 400 pilot reported diverting to a nearby airport after experiencing engine power loss.
B777 Captain reported having problems with the aircraft data link resulting in their flight number not matching with Foreign ATC displays.
B737 flight crew reported the landing gear did not retract after takeoff. Flight returned to the departure airport.
A J3 Cub pilot reported after initiating a go-around at a private grass airstrip they flew close to terrain and structures during initial climb.
Pilot reported a conflict between ramp signage and the airport diagram concerning the correct frequency for ramp movement at BNA.
Light aircraft flight instructor and student reported encroaching Class B airspace after reacting to a terrain alert.
Air carrier flight crew reported a bus hit the left wing while waiting for a gate. Dispatcher is investigating if the aircraft was positioned correctly.
Gyro-copter pilot reported the nose of the aircraft began to move side to side becoming unsafe to fly resulting in an off-airport landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported Stabilizer Trim failed on initial climb and a return to the departure airport.

C172 Pilot reported a NMAC event during pattern work. While on final approach; a skydive aircraft joined the pattern from the opposite direction which required evasive action to be taken to avoid a collision.
Tower controller reported misreading the writing on the flight progress strip which resulted in a critical ground conflict.
B737 Captain reported that a slat failure required a diversion to a nearby airport after a consultation with dispatch and maintenance did not remedy the situation.
A regional carrier Captain reported a pressurization failure on initial climbout. Flight continued to destination airport at a low cruise altitude.
B767 flight crew reported receiving a LE SLAT ASYM message after takeoff. Flight returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Flight Attendant reported possible exposure to COVID-19 and cited no action on the part of the company.  Flight Attendant also stated a reported slide bustle maintenance issue was also ignored.
B747-400 Captain reported landing safely after number 3 engine failed following a problem with the fuel valve.
Reporter stated the arrival shipment that contained Dry Ice was not correctly secured for air transport.
Air carrier flight crew reported the new RUDHH3 STAR to MCI has a crossing restriction requiring a 800 foot descent in 1.8 miles with which the A319 cannot comply.
SNJ-6 Pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing due to a gust of wind which resulted in a runway excursion. The aircraft joined the adjacent taxiway with no damage and no injuries.
Air carrier Captain reported an Air Pack Fault message during cruise and diverted to nearby airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the poor visibility and lack of proper lighting; signage; and ground markings made taxiing to the gate at EWR confusing.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported a ground handling documentation error resulted in the undeclared transport of Hazmat cargo.

Captain reported receiving possible false GPWS alerts while on final approach and during initial climb at F82 resulting in two go around procedures. The Captain landed safely after receiving the alerts and determined them to be a possible GPWS database issue.
PA-32 pilot reported a defective alternator resulted in them flying with ADS-B not transmitting.

Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern when a military helicopter turned directly underneath the aircraft to fly to a nearby heliport. The Flight Instructor made an evasive maneuver to avoid the helicopter.

Tower Local Controller reported a departing aircraft was in conflict with arriving traffic under TRACON control. The reporter advised the departing aircraft who then took evasive action while below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier First Officer reported severe turbulence and temporary loss of aircraft control after takeoff.

Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training at a non-towered airport with an opposite direction departure aircraft that was not communicating. Flight instructor executed an evasive maneuver to avoid a collision and continued to a safe landing.
A300 pilot reported landing gear failed to retract after takeoff. The flight crew performed an air turn back and made a precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the unmanifested transport of Hazmat that was delivered plane side; loaded; and bypassed the required hazmat handling procedures.

C400 single pilot reported receiving a door warning light after takeoff. The pilot performed an air turnback to troubleshoot on the ground and on the subsequent takeoff; the door warning light occurred again. While performing the rejected takeoff; heavy braking was applied and resulted in tire damage.
Instructor Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid another aircraft while conducting a training flight.
Houston I90 TRACON Controller reported an NMAC and stated handoff communication issues with ZHU Center contributed to the event.
A TRACON Controller conducting OJT reported they did not notice an aircraft climbing slower than anticipated and the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air Carrier First Officer reported being notified of a fume event on a completed flight by the Duty Pilot. The following crew rejected aircraft due to odor.
B-737 air carrier pilot reported a generator drive malfunction during cruise on a training flight. The crew diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
Flight Instructor reported airport ground markings for taxi are either missing or faded at the OMN airport.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern at GZH airport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a ground conflict during taxi to gate at TPA airport.

Flight Instructor reported an engine power loss during initial climb while conducting landing pattern training. The Instructor turned to land on the opposite direction parallel runway where a safe landing was completed.

Air taxi pilot reported receiving a lengthy taxi clearance and due to hearback/readback error a taxiway incursion resulted. Pilot stated there was inadequate time to write down and read back clearance which contributed to the event.

A300 First Officer reported an unstablized approach when the aircraft slowed below reference speed resulting in a bounced landing. The crew performed a go around and the aircraft tail struck the runway as reported by maintenance during an inspection after landing.

Cessna- 680A flight crew reported severe turbulence; loss of airspeed; and wind shear on a training flight while executing a go-around. The Captain took over the aircraft from the student after the go-around and then landed safely.
Military Flight Instructor reported a conflict with an airport service vehicle while approaching the runway.
Regional Jet Pilot reported having a MEL for the Right Pack; then when airborne; they received a message that the Left Pack was operating poorly.
B737 Captain reported that during arrival the Speed Trim Configuration Warning light illuminated and the QRH non-normal checklist was completed prior to landing.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with a helicopter in the vicinity of the Statue of Liberty.
Pilot reported slamming on the brakes to avoid a collision with an aircraft taxiing out.
C172 flight Instructor reported traffic conflict on final between blind spot of high and low winged resulted in a NMAC.
C-152 Flight Instructor with student on training flight reported engine malfunction inflight.
M20K Student pilot reported his CFI lost control of the aircraft during an attempted go-around following the reporter's bounced landing.
B737 flight crew reported a leading edge flap asymmetry indication on approach. The flight crew performed a go around and precautionary landing at destination airport.
A fractional flight crew reported the Captain flying released the control column before the autopilot was engaged resulting in a temporary loss of control.
Pilot reported GPS failure in flight as did other flights in the vicinity.

Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict where a vehicle on the taxiway did not yield and cut across the taxiway while the aircraft was taxiing for takeoff. The Captain was forced to stop the aircraft to prevent a collision.
Center Controller reported an unsafe shift due to weather and lack of staffing resulting in handoff and separations issues.

SR-20 Flight Instructor reported a near ground collision with a truck while on takeoff roll at a non-towered airport. Reportedly; the truck crossed the runway without communications resulting in an early rotation to avoid a collision.

A319 flight crew reported a CFIT advisory from ATC. The pilots reported they were given a late runway change; in a mountainous area; turbulent conditions; encountering a tailwind and given a ninety degree turn to final. The approach became unstable and the crew stated they executed a missed approach.

B767-300 Check Airman reported on final approach the pilot flying and on an OE initiated a go-around and reporter took control of aircraft. On the second approach the aircraft was again unstable and the pilot flying did another go-around. The third approach and landing were successful.
B737 Captain reported engine low oil pressure indication during departure climb. Flight crew performed an in flight engine shutdown and return to departure airport.
EMB-145 flight crew reported an EGPWS Alert on approach.

MD-11 flight crew reported receiving an ATC clearance to the waypoint RUMSY; however; the FMS database indicated the nearest RUMSY waypoint was located beyond the aircraft's limits and in the wrong direction. ATC provided a vector and clearance to another waypoint.
Air carrier Captain reported being warned of a UAS in the area while holding short prior to departure. The UAS was not in the departure path and the crew proceeded with takeoff.

Large Transport Captain and Relief First Officer reported; that while holding between the two parallel runways at UAAA airport; their large transport was obstructing both runways. The pilots say the taxi lines are non-standard.
C150 flight instructor reported during touch and go training with student; there was brake failure after landing.
EMB-XRJ Captain reported running QRH procedure of number 2 ACM failure.
Cessna 150 Student Pilot reported loss of engine power in climb led to an off airport landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported after turning on engine anti-ice the aircraft lost several functions on the Captain's Primary Flight Display (PFD) and diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
Air carrier pilot reported a loss of both GPS during cruise flight. One GPS remained unusable for the remainder of the flight.

B757 Flight Crew reported receiving a Master Warning and CABIN ALTITUDE EICAS message at cruise. The Crew immediately descended and ran the QRH procedures and were able to continue to the destination airport.
Maintenance Lead Technician reported finding opened aircraft engine oil cans on the oxygen service cart near the oxygen bottle service valves during a routine inspection.

CRJ700 flight crew reported the autopilot disconnected and the navigation systems on the captain's and first officer's Primary Flight Displays became unreliable after takeoff in night IMC. After entering VMC; the flight crew diverted using their magnetic compass and ATC vectors; and landed safely.

Air carrier crew reported receiving a GPWS warning while maneuvering for a visual approach in a valley when the crew determined the aircraft was in an incorrect position for the visual approach. The crew performed a go around; then returned from another direction; became unstable on that approach; then continued to a landing.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant crew reported a fume event which caused the cabin crew to experience physiological symptoms. At destination; arrival Maintenance removed aircraft from service.

B767 Captain reported TE EDGE FLAP ASYM master caution message on approach to their destination resulting in the flight crew executing a go-around and setting up for an approach at the airport.  The aircraft landed safely at their destination airport.
Captain reported confusion about actual landing weight during an event with an in flight shut down and an air turn back event.
B737 Captain reported the ramp crew pushed their aircraft into another aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported a safety concern regarding minimum taxiway width at PWM for the aircraft while following an ATC clearance to the gate; when there was no source guidance in company materials. The Captain was able to complete the turn and taxi to the gate.
A320 flight crew reported during gate arrival the aircraft began to slide due to contact with ice on the ramp. Crew started the number 2 engine; regained aircraft control and continued to parking.
A319 Captain reported a fume event that initially was signed off by Maintenance reoccurred on subsequent flight. The subsequent fume event was correctly diagnosed and repaired at next destination.
B737 First Officer reported an inflight engine shut down and loss of hydraulic fluid resulted in a diversion.
Pilot reports EGPWS [Enhanced Ground Proximity Warning Systems] alert on approach.
B757-200 First Officer reported that someone outside the armed aircraft door pulled the door open; resulting in the escape slide falling on to the boarding stairs.
B737-900 Captain reported a flight delay due to fuel management panel configured incorrectly and missing log book entries. The Captain requested maintenance action and resolved the issues.
Baron 55 pilot reported loss of power on the Left Engine during approach. The pilot feathered the Left Engine and continued landing at destination airport.
B737 flight crew reported severe turbulence and loss of control in cruise flight.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported while initiating a go-around from an unstable approach in gusty winds the aircraft touched down and hit a runway light.
Air carrier flight crew reported they descended in compliance with a TCAS RA for parallel runway traffic and received a terrain alert from the aircraft and ATC.
A321 flight crew reported EGPWS was triggered during departure climb.
B737 Captain reported the right main landing gear failed to retract after takeoff. Crew elected to return to departure station and landed normally.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with many passengers not wearing face masks in the gate area at airport.
Dispatcher reported landing gear door actuator failed causing doors to remain open; resulting in an air turn back and precautionary landing.
B737NG Captain reported a track deviation occurred departing CVG when he turned off course to avoid wake turbulence.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported steering; rudder and 'Prox System Chan' caution messages on descent. Crew diverted and performed a normal landing.
Airline ground employee driving a vehicle reported taking evasive action to avoid another vehicle that failed to stop at a stop sign.

B767 First Officer reported a Cabin Pressure Warning on climb out as well as difficulties with using the Crew O2 Mask Microphones. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing.
General aviation pilot reported a CFTT event during ILS approach when autopilot failed to capture glide slope. Pilot corrected and continued approach.
Light aircraft Flight Instructor pilot an NMAC with a commercial jet departing OAK.
Pilot reported a safety concern regarding power transmission lines in the vicinity of PUC airport that are not appropriately marked on the local VFR sectional chart.
A319 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into SDF in trail of a B767.
Air Carrier flight crew reported taxi instruction miscommunication led to a ground conflict with another aircraft.
Single Engine Pilot reported entering a conflicting downwind due to confusion and complacency resulted in a taking evasive action avoiding a NMAC.
Air carrier flight crew reported TCAS Alert on approach to MSP airport.

General aviation pilot reported two near misses at a non-towered airport. The pilot performed a go around to avoid the other aircraft taking off; then another evasive maneuver in the go around climb to avoid the aircraft again once it became airborne.
B737-800 flight crew reported a CFIT event during approach resulting in a go-around. A communications breakdown between flight crew and ATC was cited as a contributing factor.

Air carrier Captain reported the taxiway lines at BNA were repainted too close; causing an unsafe situation for taxiing aircraft. With the inbound lane repainted closer to the outbound lane; aircraft taxiing inbound may not have any issues but aircraft taxiing outbound may not have the proper wingtip clearance.
Flight crew flying Falcon 50 aircraft reported IRS failure during initial climb out. Returned to departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported gate departure without required Hazmat documents.
B737-800 flight crew reported Ground Conflict with vehicle parked on taxiway.
B737-800 Captain diverted after receiving HYD annunciation during gear retraction and consultation with Maintenance.
Pilot reported NMAC during takeoff from a nontowered airport.
Air carrier Captain reported there was GPS interference in Sofia; Ankara; and Baku FIRs.
B737-800 First Officer reported engine failure with FADEC and reverser lockout malfunction resulted in an expeditious return to departure airport to an overweight landing.
B737 First Officer reported hydraulic system failure and diversion to a nearby airport.
EMB-145 Captain reported encountering severe wake turbulence on arrival into ORD 10 miles in trail of a B777.
Vehicle driver reported a ground conflict with an aircraft pushing back from gate. The aircraft stopped moving when the guide noticed the vehicle behind the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported the TOREO 1A STAR to MMSM is not visible on the electronic chart selection of the STARS initial page on the Jeppesen EFB.
Air carrier Captain reported the flight crew's failure to verify the Final DG summary and to submit the flight crew acknowledgement message prior to brake release.

General aviation pilot reported a discrepancy at GOV airport between the ATIS and published frequencies and stated the NOTAM has expired and is unclear as to what the correct Tower/CTAF frequency is. The reporter noted this could lead to pilots communicating on different frequencies; potentially causing traffic conflicts.

B-737 air carrier crew reported an unlocked galley cart became loose during landing rollout on a ferry flight. The galley cart damaged the flight deck door and allowed broken dishes from the cart to enter the cockpit and the First Officer suffered a cut on their arm.
Flight instructor at a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern not communicating on CTAF.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an UNABLE RNP EICAS message; which they attributed it to be due to 5G.
A321 Captain reported a fumes event during taxi-in. Aircraft was turned over to Maintenance for inspection.
Air carrier flight crew reported RA frequency interference while on visual approach.

Captain reported diverting due to fuel indication issues and realized that he had been misinformed about a CAS miscomparison message being related to the fuel system MEL. On final approach a Gear Disagree occurred which was resolved through the alternate gear extension procedure.
EMB-170 flight crew reported the autopilot randomly disconnected twice during the approach phase.
General Aviation pilot reported a NMAC while entering the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport.
B737 flight crew reported encountering a wind shear on final approach and executed a go-around; subsequently safely landing on a different runway.

EMB-190 pilot reported an aircraft dispatched with one inoperative bleed system experienced failure of the other bleed system during departure climb that resulted in depressurization. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.

B737-800 flight crew reported communications issues resulted in a descent below assigned altitude during the approach. Flight crew climbed up to correct altitude and continued the approach to landing.
B737 flight crew conducting a Visual Approach reported the flaps were set incorrectly resulting in a Ground Proximity Alert on short final.
A flight instructor providing instruction on base leg at a LMO non-towered airport reported a near miss with another aircraft in the pattern.
Air carrier personnel reported the agent checking luggage failed to ask the required Dangerous Goods questions.
Lear 60 flight crew reported a momentary loss of aircraft control while flying through cloud tops and encountering up- and downdrafts.
Flight instructor on training flight reported a NMAC with another aircraft while on approach to land.
DA40 Pilot reported a dual ECU failure on climb and elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
GA flight instructor reported an NMAC at EMV non-towered airport during takeoff requiring evasive action.
B737 Captain reported the loss of both electrical systems in cruise. Flight crew landed at the nearest suitable airport and passengers deplaned.
C172 reported a runway excursion during landing; citing inadequate crosswind correction control inputs as contributing.

Light Transport flight crew reported upon landing at AHN there were ground personnel and equipment in the grass safety area adjacent to the runway; operating in close proximity to landing aircraft; near the touchdown zone. No NOTAM was issued; the equipment was unmarked; and ATC did not alert the landing aircraft of the hazard.
Single pilot reported a NMAC with a glider during climb out. Reporter and glider pilot took evasive action and a collision was avoided.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fumes event prior to takeoff. After the flight crew conferred with Dispatch and Maintenance the Captain elected to return to the gate.
B737-800 flight crew reported during takeoff; the number two engine exhaust gas temperature indicated higher than normal. Flight crew diverted.
A320 pilot reported a hydraulic main system failure during climb resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant crew reported a fume event during flight which resulted in physiological symptoms.
Flight Attendant reported discovering during preflight activities that the 1R door had already been armed. The purser disarmed the door before any attempts to open it were made.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending to about 500 ft. AGL; significantly below the glide slope; on approach to MCI after mis-setting the altimeter by 1' of mercury.
Air carrier flight crew reported being questioned on arrival regarding Hazmat procedures.
First Officer reported receiving a CFIT Warning from ATC.

A319 pilots reported multiple instrument malfunctions during descent. EICAS indicated airspeed; vertical speed; altimeter; angle of attack and pitot-static system malfunctions. After one missed approach a landing was accomplished.
Pilot reported engine power loss and elected to return to the departure airport.
Air carrier pilot reported experiencing right engine compressor stall on departure roll; resulting in a rejected takeoff and return to the ramp.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain overshot the parking stop point by about 2-3 feet. The First Officer said to stop twice before the Captain applied brakes.
DA42 Pilot reported dual ECU failure resulting in power loss shortly after takeoff resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
Cessna 172 pilot reported the aircraft started rolling after engine start even though the parking brake was set and struck a fence.

CL-30 Captain reported a loss of hydraulic fluid in the right-hand hydraulic system during cruise. While on final approach; the system lost pressure and the flight crew was unable to set the flaps in the full flap position but landed safely.
B737-700 flight crew reported missing an ATC speed assignment on arrival into LAX when they were distracted by a wake turbulence encounter in trail of a heavy B777.

Light Transport Single Pilot reported failing to cross arrival fix at assigned altitude resulting in a loss of ATC separation. Reporter also reported late descent clearance and inflight icing as contributing causes.
Small aircraft pilot reported a propeller strike occurred during taxi due to the uneven asphalt of the surface.
An Air Carrier pilot reported a close encounter with an unidentified object which the reporter researched and believed it may have been an untethered balloon.
Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict while crossing runway and receiving ground taxi clearance. Both aircraft applied heavy braking to avoid a collision.
Tower Controller and flight crews reported a rejected takeoff was necessary due to an aircraft taxiing across the runway in use while another aircraft had been given a takeoff clearance.

Maintenance Manager reported Captain reactivated a deferred APU for ground cooling and did not request maintenance action to reinstate the MEL afterwards. The aircraft departed that station with no action.

Pilot reported smoke in the cockpit during a training flight. Flight Crew turned off all electrical power and the smoke dissipated. The Flight Crew elected to return to the departure airport and make a precautionary landing.

Air carrier First Officer reported receiving erroneous radio altimeter readouts during final approach. Flight crew performed a go-around and completed the approach; citing possible 5G interference as contributing to the event.
Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft that failed to follow established departure procedures.
CRJ-900 First officer reported they landed with the spoilers deployed.

Air carrier Ground Personnel reported two Hazmat shipments were transported without being listed on arrival aircraft staging guide. The originating departure ground personnel incorrectly handled the shipment which resulted in the error.

C150 Flight Instructor reported on departure experiencing a low engine RPM resulting in flight towards terrain; the Flight Instructor was able to return to the departure airport where they landed safely.

MSP Tower Controller reported TRACON instructions to an arrival caused an unstable approach and a go-around. The Tower Controller stated that TRACON issued the instructions to comply with the new Converging Runway Operations (CRO) procedures at MSP; but the Tower Controller sees the procedures as unsafe as many factors are not included in CRO. The Controller indicated the procedures do not provide controllers the capability to ensure positive separation of aircraft.
M20 instructor pilot reported the student flew an unstabilized approach resulting in a hard landing and tail tie down striking the runway.
First Officer reported that the Captain's side window experienced a short in the window heat system; requiring an emergency descent and a diversion to a precautionary landing.
Air carrier B777 flight crew reported lack of response from flying pilot on unstable approach resulted in Pilot Monitoring taking control of the aircraft for a go around and subsequent landing.

ERJ-170 flight crew reported the engine fan blades were covered in ice with one engine frozen in place. A de-ice crew sprayed Type I deicing fluid directly into the fan inlet and on the fan blades without authorization. After consulting with maintenance and disembarking an ill passenger; the aircraft departed four hours late.
ERJ-170 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence during takeoff that resulted in a momentary overspeed.

SCT TRACON Controller reported they did not notice a VFR aircraft climb through the altitude of an aircraft in a holding pattern resulting in an NMAC. The reporter was working combined sectors due to staffing shortages and stated they were too busy to notice the confliction.
PA-28 pilot reported safely diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing a power loss.
Corporate jet Captain reported numerous CAS Fault messages including loss of hydraulic pressure. Flight crew continued to destination airport and after landing was towed off runway.
Air Carrier Captain flying CRJ-701 aircraft reported inflight de-pressurization.
PC-12 pilot reported the left main tire blew when they applied brakes too soon on landing. Aircraft was towed off runway.

Air carrier pilot crew reported GPS spoofing potentially occurring at ZGSZ Airport led to navigational systems malfunctioning while on initial approach. After a go-around and turning off the GPS; the navigation display showed more accurate data and the flight crew landed uneventfully.

Air carrier flight crew reported during climb a low hydraulic pressure annunciation illuminated. The B hydraulic system quantity was zero. The pilots returned to the origin airport and accomplished an overweight landing.
Recreational/hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying their UAS in an active TFR without authorization. The UAS was also not registered.
Light aircraft flight instructor reported an NMAC with a helicopter on approach to APA.
A320 Captain reported a transient fume event during takeoff. Odor described as moderate to strong dirty socks which dissipated within 10 minutes.
A319 First Officer reported an engine stall ECAM during cruise flight. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed overweight.
Pilot reported a TCAS/RA with another commercial jet landing on the parallel Runway which was off course and too close to them.
ATC TRACON Controller reported situation where the adjacent facility was not taking handoffs in a timely manner resulting in a complex and dangerous ATC environment.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported failure of stabilizer electric trim and autopilot. The crew returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.

Pilot reported the alternator switch was inadvertently moved to the off position; resulting in a depleted battery and ATC not able to receive the 7600 and 7700 transponder signals. The flight crew landed without communications.
Flight Instructor reported their student bounced a landing during solo flight resulting in runway excursion. Student regained aircraft control and taxied to parking.

A pilot reported while conducting a high speed taxi for a maintenance check they accidentally became airborne and experienced a rough running engine. The pilot landed off the runway in the grass and but able to taxi back onto the runway.

PC12 Pilot reported shortly after take-off; receiving an Amber 'Flaps' Message followed by an Amber 'Pusher' Message; a Blue 'Pusher Safe Mode' Message; and an Amber Flap Position Indicator that was not moving and was stuck at roughly the 14-degree position. The pilot performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported failing to include the jumpseater in the weight and balance calculations.

A319 flight crew reported a CFIT event during visual approach shortly after being assigned a late runway change. Flight crew utilized an INOP ILS as a visual backup guidance. Flight crew corrected low glidepath by following PAPI glidepath to a safe landing.
Air carrier Captain reported a momentary loss of aircraft control after a gust of wind forced the plane into a bank angle of 35 degrees during approach to DEN airport.

A320 Captain reported a NMAC during initial climb while attempting radio contact after a hand off to center controllers.  Pilots dialed in the wrong frequency and were unable to establish contact with ATC prior to the RA they received and responded to.
B737-700 Captain reported a Master Caution light illuminated during the climb-out and after some reassessment; realized there was an entire system failure. The flight crew performed an air turnback.
A push back driver reported the pilot released brakes while the tow crew was still disconnecting the tow bar. The aircraft began to move.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during traffic pattern training due to inaccurate communication and pattern entry by the conflict aircraft.

Flight crew reported damage to a wing tip while taxiing to the CBP ramp in Brownsville/South Padre Island International Airport; BRO; for customs inspection. The Captain stated an unlit fence; the lack of a marshaller; and no taxiway lead in lines contributed to the cause of the event.
B737-800 flight crew reported a loss of cabin pressure during descent when thrust was reduced to idle. Flight crew continued descent and landed at destination airport.
A helicopter pilot landing at a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with an aircraft which flew over another aircraft on the runway than made another approach into the helicopter's path.
B777 Captain reported the left engine Integrated Drive Generator failed in cruise due to an oil leak. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
C182 Pilot reported a sputtering engine during cruise led to an off field landing and a suspicion that fuel starvation may have been the cause.

An air carrier Captain reported they were holding short of a taxiway waiting for an aircraft to pass when they observed a different aircraft taxiing across their path that would not have room to clear their aircraft. The reporter instructed the other aircraft to stop on Ground Control frequency.

Air carrier flight crew reported that a passenger count and weight and balance error occurred at the diversion airport because the count could not be uploaded through ACARS. The crew stated the flight had to divert due to an ATC Zero Center closure.
C25A Captain reported thumping noise with erratic hydraulic pressure and landing gear unlock indications resulted in divert due to incorrectly installed part.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in special use airspace without authorization. Upon learning of the error; they exited the airspace and landed.
Air carrier flight crew taxiing reported they had to suddenly brake to avoid another taxiing air carrier.

Air carrier Captain reported stopping pushback when they were unsure of the location of another aircraft taxiing to a nearby gate. Reporter stated use of the ramp frequency to communicate intentions was not successful and recommended hiring a Ramp Controller to coordinate aircraft movement.
A320 flight crew reported a Green Hydraulic System Overheat message at cruise. The flight crew performed the QRH procedures and continued to the destination airport.
C172 Instructor Pilot reported an airborne conflict occurred in the traffic pattern when a 'follow traffic' clearance resulted in mis-identifying the aircraft to follow.
GA instructor pilot reported taking evasive action after landing to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway at GXY airport.
Air carrier Captain reported observing a cleaning crew onboard an aircraft not wearing face masks correctly.  Pilot also reported people in terminal not wearing face masks correctly.
Air carrier flight crew reported an NMAC during departure climb when another aircraft apparently departed their cleared altitude prematurely.
Air Carrier Pilot reported the PAPI and runway lights at PTKK airport do not work and there is no NOTAM providing that information.
B737 Captain reported evacuating the aircraft via the jet bridge when a ground employee noted fuel leaking from the right wing.
SR22 pilot reported an NMAC during takeoff requiring evasive action.
B737 pilot reported sudden pitch up when hydraulic power was removed.
B737 pilot reported a rejected takeoff due to a temporary loss of control during takeoff roll due to wind gust.

A321 Captain reported a fume event during departure climb. After trouble shooting and conferring with Dispatch; Maintenance and also flight attendants crew executed an air return due to the persistent fumes.
PA-28 Flight Instructor practicing crosswind takeoffs with a student reported the student lost control of the aircraft resulting in a runway excursion prior to lifting off.
PIT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A320 First Officer reported a hydraulic system overheat during cruise. The flight crew diverted to a nearby airport and made a precautionary landing.
A321 Captain reported pulling the handle to open the aircraft door instead of disarming the door resulting in an inadvertent escape slide partial deployment on ramp after arrival.
Dispatcher reported a B787 was under tow and an A320 taxied around the towing aircraft.

Air carrier First Officer reported a temporary mountain wave encounter which resulted in a high speed exceedance and altitude excursion. The First Officer corrected the aircraft back to assigned altitude and speed.
C172 Flight Instructor reported having to execute an evasive maneuver during an NMAC event while on visual approach as the other aircraft misidentified traffic; resulting in a loss of separation.
Air carrier pilot reported Ground Control issued taxi instructions to cross a runway. Approaching the hold line the reporter noticed an aircraft on short final and stopped taxiing.

B747-400 crew reported unreliable airspeed during climb in rain and thunderstorms resulting in a stick shaker after previous maintenance on the air data system. The crew recovered the aircraft then diverted to an alternate airport; dumped fuel; and landed.
A Maintenance Technician reported when they entered an aircraft to perform work; the escape slide deployed due to a door that was armed.
A TR182 pilot reported a Turbocharger malfunction and continued to land at their destination.
B737 Captain reported acknowledging notification of Dangerous Goods (DG) being removed while taxiing out; only to be notified inflight that it was actually reloaded onto the aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an apparently erroneous momentary GPWS terrain warning on approach to ROA.

B-737 Captain reported a safety concern where ramp personnel disconnected ground conditioned air from the aircraft with an inoperative APU resulting in excessive cabin temperatures. The Captain deplaned passengers and crew for passenger safety and refused to operate the aircraft.

EMB-145 flight crew reported a loss of all hydraulic fluid from the #1 Hydraulic System; which led to losing all nose landing gear control. This resulted in an uncommanded swerve to the right and nose gear damage.
Pilot reported losing engine power and landing in a grass field when the gear collapsed and right wing struck the ground.

CRJ flight crew reported left Brake Temperature thermal runaway during arrival taxi. After completing appropriate QRH procedure and a ARFF wheel inspection the high temperature was due to an indicator failure and aircraft taxied to gate safely.
Flight Instructor reported a runway incursion due to brake failure and loss of control of the aircraft.
Widebody Captain reported suspected GPS jamming or interference in the vicinity of the Black Sea.
Flight instructor with student reported a NMAC while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport when a departing aircraft took off from the opposite end of the runway in use.

Air taxi pilot reported discovering during taxi out that they had left the rudder gust lock installed resulting in difficulty turning the aircraft. Pilot shut down airplane; had ground crew remove the lock; and completed the flight.
B777 flight crew reported an accidental altitude deviation while on approach after the Captain instructed the First Officer to descend below the ATC assigned altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported during taxi to the gate another air carrier taxied in front of them at a high rate of speed and nearly missed impacting the reporter's aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew was notified by ATC of a UAS near the final approach course. Flight crew saw and avoided the UAS.

Air Carrier flight crew reported a fume event during cruise which was caused by Right Recirc Fan failure. After conferring with Dispatch and Maintenance the flight crew executed a diversion followed by a safe landing.
LR-55 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport after precautionary shutdown of #1 engine due to a thrust reverser unlock light illuminating in flight.
CE560 flight crew reported during cruise noticed the number one engine Inter-stage turbine temperature fluctuating. They shut down the engine and diverted.
Air carrier FO reported a critical ground conflict while on final approach when another aircraft failed to exit the runway; resulting in a go around.

A321 FA reported there was a fumes event during preflight that resulted in an aircraft swap. During the descent in the replacement aircraft; one of the flight attendants began to feel unwell and required medical assessment.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC during approach with an aircraft on approach on the parallel runway.
B737 Captain reported a GPWS; GPWS Sink Rate; Wind Shear; Unstable Approach; Speed Deviation; and Exceeded Flap Speed; caused by a strong updraft on approach.
GA Flight Instructor reported a NMAC occurred when another aircraft turned behind them on the flight approach. Both aircraft took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Pilot reported the crew door was not fully closed on take off and a loud whooshing noise was heard. The pilot performed an air turn back and landing at the departure airport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported inaccurate FICON resulted in poor to nil braking action during landing roll.

EMB-145 Captain reported a go-around due to a 'Ground Conflict; Critical' warning; which was caused by improper automation sequencing. The flight remained clear of terrain and complied with ATC instructions; and circled back around to land safely.
Air Carrier First Officer reported DG documents contained inaccurate data. Errors were corrected and flight departed safely.

B737-800 pilot reported the aircraft abruptly pitched up while doing a flight control test and the aircraft was almost uncontrollable. According to the reporter Maintenance had performed incorrect work on flight controls; possibly due to inexperience with the aircraft.

Air carrier Inspector reported; during a fan blade retaining pin torque check on an ERJ 170 aircraft; blade #16 missing its bumper. The bumper was found jammed between the blade and hub. A new bumper was installed and all fan blades; bumpers and pins reinstalled.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization. During a battery change they realized they never submitted their LAANC request.
Citation flight crew reported flying a visual approach aligning to the wrong runway resulting in a go around and landing safely on the second approach.
Global 5500 First Officer reported the 'CG' identifier for the Dutch waypoint/NDB is not available in the aircraft's database.
Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft slid on black ice on the contaminated taxiway while taxiing after landing. The Captain regained control and taxied to the gate.
An Air Carrier flight crew reported a runway incursion when they were instructed to hold short of a runway on a taxiway in between parallel runways that was not long enough to accommodate their B777.

Part 107 pilot was conducting a recreational proficiency flight. The flight occurred during the hours of dusk and winds were starting to increase. The pilot noticed difficulty controlling the UAS near 400 feet therefore descended to a lower altitude to regain control of the UAS.
A tug driver reported the flight crew failed to set brakes after the push resulting in the aircraft rolling uncommanded after the towbar was disconnected until the Captain set brakes.
Tower Controller reported an NMAC on short final was not detected by the Local Control trainee; the Instructor; or the Controller in Charge until the Ground Controller brought it to their attention.
A321 Captain reported a fumes event during approach descent that was described as a 'musty dirty socks'. Flight crew donned O2 mask and completed a safe landing.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended from their assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

MD-11 Captain reported flight control computer issues during approach resulted in loss of all autoflight capability. Pilot also reported the aircraft had been previously refused due to flight control computer malfunctions.

Captain reported multiple FCU failures on descent; but were able to recover one FCU. Captain stated ATC did not respond to multiple requests for help before giving them a different frequency to continue approach and landing.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving GPWS warnings during approach to RNO airport at 5;500 feet MSL prior to OKITE.  The Captain stated they had anticipated the warning due to terrain at 5;101 north east of OKITE.
GA pilot reported a loss of situational awareness resulting in landing on adjacent grass runway at 7B3 airport.
Air carrier pilot reported a course deviation occurred during departure from JFK after they failed to load a transition fix into the FMC. ATC issued a heading correction.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on approach to EINN.

Dispatcher reported miscalculations in passenger court resulted in the aircraft operating in revenue service over it's maximum structural gross weight. The flight crew reviewed their fuel on board and determined that continued flight to destination airport was still acceptable.
Air carrier flight crew reported EGT exceedance on engine during takeoff climb. Returned and landed uneventfully.

Air carrier Captain reported a communication breakdown with Ground Agent which resulted in being unaware of DG on board until departure taxi. The final DG information was received while on the active runway for departure.
General aviation pilots reported a near miss with another aircraft while on an IFR flight plan on an arrival. ATC directed an immediate descent; then pilots resumed the flight.

C172 Flight Instructor with student reported while climbing to exit icing conditions near the aircraft service ceiling the aircraft engine failed. The instructor descended and the engine eventually developed power so they returned to departure airport.

B737-700 First Officer reported an engine failure during climb. The flight crew completed the QRH procedure and was provided priority handling to the departure airport where a safe landing was completed.
Flight Instructor and solo student reported the alternator failed during cruise. The student returned to departure airport and landed.
Flight Crew reported the failure of the operational Cabin Pressure Controller during climb. With a failed CPC and the other CPC on MEL; the flight crew returned to departure airport.
A321 pilot reported during preflight the engine started when the APU bleed valve was selected on. Maintenance found the engine start valve was stuck in the open position.
SA-227 Captain reported a failed elevator trim system resulted in a return to the departure airport.
SR20 Flight Instructor reported aircraft failed to reach necessary power resulted in a rejected takeoff.
A B767 First Officer reported while taxiing on the ramp a parked aircraft's jet blast blew a container off a tug directly into their path causing them to brake suddenly to avoid collision.

General aviation pilot reported performing a maintenance performance test flight; in a multi-engine aircraft and became distracted; and lost situational awareness to rising terrain. The pilot flew below FAR minimum altitude and corrected the altitude deviation.

Air carrier flight crew reported turning toward the wrong runway while on approach into DCA. The flight crew was on the LDA 19 Y approach and misidentified the runway environment; resulting in a turn toward Runway 15 and an early descent below minimum altitude. Flight crew became reoriented and corrected course.
GA pilot reported a gust of wind caused the aircraft to ground loop on the runway during landing.
A319 Captain reported the aircraft started rolling after parking at the gate. The parking brake had been set; but apparently failed; allowing the aircraft to roll back.
Air Carrier Flight crew reported a fume event upon reaching the aircraft for pre-flight. Flight crew vacated the aircraft and submitted a logbook entry.
Part 91 Pilot reported while entering the DMW traffic pattern had a NMAC with another aircraft 300 feet below and passing behind them.
Air carrier Captain reported taxiway signage in San Jose; Costa Rica (MROC) is confusing and some markings are faded.
Piper Twin private pilot flying a charity mission reported unable to retract the gear. The pilot received priority handling from ATC and landed safely at the planned destination.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported the right main gear separated from the aircraft on initial climb and they diverted to a nearby airport to land.
Air carrier pilot reported the Captains side window shattered during flight and decided to continue to destination.
B737-700 flight crew reported a flight control malfunction shortly after takeoff.
B737 First Officer reported temporarily losing altitude control due to continuous moderate turbulence.
Air carrier captain reported the oil quantity was reducing in flight and after gate arrival the oil fill cap was found to be loose.
Tower Controller reported the TRACON Controller turned an aircraft below the minimum vectoring altitude to clear a conflict.

Air taxi Captain reported a safety concern when ATC cleared the flight to an intersection that was similar sounding but slightly different spelling; in the same airspace; which resulted in the flight turning off course. The crew corrected the spelling of the intersection and proceeded to the correct navigation fix.

B747 flight crew reported receiving a Gear Tilt EICAS Message on take off. After confirming the fuel load was sufficient to continue to destination airport; the flight crew encountered fuel management issues on approach resulting in an engine flame out and roll back. The crew continued the approach and landed safely.
B737-700 air carrier flight crew reported autopilot malfunction inflight.
A320 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL320 when a B747 overflew them at FL330.
B737 Flight Crew reported loss of both flight directors on approach resulted in visual landing. Flight crew suspected interruption of ground based facility.

B737 Captain reported ramp personnel opened forward cargo door while the right engine was running. Door was opened prior to ground personnel advising need to load last minute bag causing Captain to shut down the engine.
B737 Pilots reported receiving an airspeed unreliable notification on takeoff and returned to the departure airport.

M-20 pilot reported an NMAC while on descent. Approach Control gave the pilot an alert regarding the traffic as did the pilot's EFB. The pilot became uncomfortable with the spacing of the traffic and took evasive action. ATC ask the pilot to call when able for a debriefing and their need for information.
B777 Captain reported loss of center hydraulic system after departure.  Flight returned to departure airport for landing.
SA-227 Captain reported a nose wheel was lost during takeoff and confirmed post flight.
SR20 pilot reported unstabilized approach resulted in aircraft ground strike with bent propeller blades.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported left engine cowling came off and shattered the right windshield resulted in return to departure airport.
B737 Captain reported momentary loss of GPS signal on climb out.
Single Pilot reported an NMAC event during takeoff with landing aircraft. Pilot stated he forgot to make a 'Takeoff' announcement and was not aware of the landing aircraft's location.
B767-300 Flight Attendant reported a cabin fumes event described as dirty socks odor during initial approach; which lasted until landing.
B737-800 First Officer reported the Autopilot malfunctioned on an approach resulting in a temporary loss of control. Flight crew disengaged the Autopilot and continued the approach.
General aviation flight crew saw a UAS while in the traffic pattern and reported to ATC.
CRJ700 Captain reported a Steering INOP Caution Message on climb out. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
ERJ 145 First Officer reported smoke in the flight deck. After landing; the aircraft produced a pack overheat warning. Passengers exited the aircraft normally.

A321 Captain reported receiving a Vent Skin Valve fault after departing and as the aircraft continued to climb; a few other vent faults occurred. After unsuccessful troubleshooting; the flight crew opted to perform an air turn back.
An Air Carrier flight crew reported Captain flew through the final approach course and received a Ground Proximity Warning.
Center Controller reported communication issues with pilots while wearing a face mask.
Student pilot flying experimental aircraft reported engine power loss on final approach. Conducted off airport landing without incident.

EMB-170 Captain reported cabin pressure control switches were not placed in proper positions and resulted in a CABIN ALT HI annunciation in flight. The flight continued after the flight crew positioned the cabin pressure controls to the correct positions.
A320 pilot reported the parking brake failed during pushback but was recovered after consultation with maintenance.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported they learned post flight they flew in excess of 400 feet AGL without authorization.
Cessna 172 student pilot reported an unstable approach while landing in strong gusty winds on a solo training flight which resulted in a runway excursion.
Air carrier pilot reported they did not comply with an ATC instruction to turn off course for traffic due to their concern for terrain avoidance.
Air carrier flight crew reported multiple EGPWS alerts on approach due to being low on profile and elected to continue the approach using VASI for visual reference.
B767-400 Captain reported a Left Engine Overheat message at FL310 and returned to departure airport.
Dispatcher reported one of their flights encountered severe turbulence on an approach and a temporary loss of control so they diverted to a nearby airport.
GA pilot reported during an instrument approach they incorrectly setup their avionics 200 feet below minimums.
Air carrier flight crew reported while following ZDV center clearance; TRACON reported the descent was through holding pattern traffic.
CRJ-200 Captain reported receiving a 'Steering Inoperative' caution message on gear up selection after takeoff.
Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC with a helicopter during departure while following ATC assigned heading.
A Diamond DA40 Instructor Pilot reported experiencing a rough running engine; accompanied by vibrations; resulting in an immediate return to the departure airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported encountering severe turbulence; loss of aircraft control and a lightning strike.
Pilot reported severe weather and hail during cruise flight leading to temporary loss of control.
SR22 pilot reported loss of effective braking related to a wet runway and tailwind conditions led to a runway overrun with no damage to aircraft or airport equipment.
Pilot reported a loss of engine power in flight and an immediate landing after an insect had clogged a vent line.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported hydraulic failure and continued to their destination for a safe landing.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported during climb the aircraft had made a noise that sounded like an engine out. They made an announcement to the passengers of the situation and they returned to the airport.
C210 Pilot reported a nose gear collapse and subsequent prop strike during the take off roll resulted in the aircraft being disabled on the runway.
SR22 Flight Instructor reported the aircraft was experiencing reduced engine performance during initial climb. The flight Instructor returned to departure airport and landed.

B-737 First Officer reported severe turbulence during cruise resulting in an altitude excursion and near over speed. A Flight Attendant in the cabin was injured during the turbulence and the flight continued to destination.
Flight crew flying CL-30 aircraft reported pylon bleed leak inflight. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
GA pilot reported that while taxiing across the runway at a non-towered airport; an aircraft that did not communicate their intentions it was taking off using a different runway at the same time.
A319 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing multiple system fault messages during cruise.

A pilot reported a concerned citizen called them after landing to request that they do not fly over the citizens' 'drone operation'. The reporter stated other pilots who fly into this airport have also received phone calls from the same person.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence shortly before touchdown on Runway 29 at EWR from an A321 on short final to Runway 22R.
Pilot flying SR22 aircraft reported engine failure inflight and was able to land at a nearby airport.
B757 Captain reported landing safely at destination after experiencing loss of the Right Hydraulic System.
Air Carrier flight crew reported Final Flight Release was missing required Hazmat notification. Flight Release was amended prior to departure.
Air carrier Captain reported a tug entered the safety zone while the aircraft was entering the gate area. The tug driver did not yield to the Captain's aircraft nor the guide in marshal.
Flight Instructor taking off from a non-towered airport reported a rejected takeoff due to a vehicle approaching the runway.
Commercial Pushback Operator reported that they received non standard push instructions from Captain that lead to a ground conflict during pushback.

Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict with a small aircraft that overflew them as they were cleared to line up and wait on a runway. Tower controller stated the small aircraft was performing a missed approach.

POU Tower Controllers reported not being able to see the aircraft of a student pilot after a rejected takeoff due to construction of a new hangar.  The student pilot also reported describing the rejected takeoff.
CRJ-900 Captain reported that while hand-flying during the climb the Mach trim and stabilizer trim were inoperative; leading the flight crew to make a precautionary landing.
Cessna 404 Titan pilot reported that during cruise; they experienced a dual alternator failure. They requested ATC assistance and landed.
B737 Ramp Agent reported a HAZMAT package was onboard and transported while the aircraft loading document indicated the package had been offloaded.
Pilot reported the left gear collapse during landing.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
A319 flight crew reported engine oil pressure fluctuations that resulted in an inflight shutdown. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
A319 flight crew reported an engine compressor stalls in cruise resulting in a diversion.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft had to return to the gate due to unsecured Dangerous Goods.
CRJ flight crew reported uncommanded rudder and aileron trim movement after take-off. The flight crew performed an air turn back and made a precautionary landing at departure airport.

PA-28-181 instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft during taxi while stopped in a non-movement area prior to a taxiway; on a training flight. The other aircraft maneuvered past with little to no clearance or coordination between aircraft; there was no damage and the instructor reported the hazard.
B737-800 Pilot reported recurring caution alerts; missing parts; and false documentation of aircraft maintenance resulted in unsafe aircraft operation.

Pilot reported while landing at non-towered airport XNX an ultralight landed on the opposite end of the runway. After the pilot cleared the runway; the ultralight lifted off passing 50 feet by the aircraft.
ERJ-145 Captain reported a BAGG SMOKE EICAS message during preflight check.
A319 Captain reported a strong odor throughout the aircraft after starting the #1 engine. Aircraft returned to departure gate; where all passengers deplaned.
TRACON Controller reported confusion between similar call signs as aircraft entered a higher MVA which resulted in a CFTT encounter and failure to issue a low altitude alert.

M20G Pilot reported landing gear extension issues during final approach. After using the alternate extension procedure a landing was made during which the aircraft became uncontrollable and exited the runway.
B747 flight crew reported problems with the securing of an oversized load and refused to depart until corrected by the load master.
Ramp Agent reported a baggage cart struck an aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported radio altimeter anomalies climbing out of ILM; citing 5G interference as the possible cause of the event.
Hawker 800XP pilot reported during the approach they extended the landing gear and observed a red indication on the left main landing gear.
Air carrier flight crew reported being notified to hold for a priority package with dangerous goods after brake release. The dangerous goods summary was sent after pushback.

DA40 Instructor Pilot with a Student reported the engine malfunctioned during approach at 550 AGL and began to lose airspeed. The Instructor took over the controls and lined up for a taxiway landing; but was ultimately able to land on a runway.
Air carrier Captain reported problems with the localizer inbound to LCH.
First Officer reported a trailing edge flap failure on approach. During delay vectors; the flight crew configured flaps and then continued to landing at destination airport.
Air carrier Captain commuting in jump seat reported an NMAC during cruise flight with a UAS. No evasive action was taken.
Captain reported diverting due to HSDI FAIL message caused by inflight icing.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a GPWS terrain warning on approach to BGR.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with a UAS in IMC conditions on an IFR flight plan and advised ATC.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into DEN in trail of an A320.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported a 'dirty socks' odor during final descent. Flight crew continued to a safe landing and submitted an electronic logbook entry.
C172 pilot reported a wind gust caught the aircraft on rollout of aircraft on landing and aircraft slid into grass; no damage to aircraft; no damage to property and no Injuries to the pilot.
Air carrier First Officer reported during walk around they discovered speed tape covering the AOA Vanes.
C152 pilot reported after takeoff that the engine lost power and elected to return to the departure airport and make a precautionary landing.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported that the Captain's windshield cracked just before initial descent.
UAS pilot reported flying their UAS in the vicinity of another UAS when a mid air collision occurred. The UAS was lost and unrecoverable.

Tower Controller and Developmental reported an aircraft in the traffic pattern turned in front of another aircraft; causing an unsafe situation. Controller issued a traffic alert and the second aircraft performed a go-around.
Flight Instructor reported they and their student neglected to check NOTAMs about UAS operations at destination airport and failed to fly the correct traffic pattern as advised in the NOTAMs.
Engine shop lead technician reported noticing several discrepancies with part numbers for engine fan blade annulus fillers while generating shop orders.
EMB-170 flight crew reported MCDU displayed incorrect SID and STAR altitude restrictions that misled the flight director and confused the flight crew.
Air Carrier Captain reported concerns with airlines possibly making it mandatory for employees to get the COVID-19 vaccine.

Flight Instructor with student reported a critical ground conflict upon landing with an aircraft that touched down behind the instructor then did a touch-and-go while the instructor was still on the runway.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving master caution and ECAM alerts for engine wing leak on both engines resulting in a cabin pressure loss while in cruise. Flight crew diverted to an alternate airport.
Pilot reported low oil pressure in the right engine during descent. The oil pressure was indicating in the red upon landing and the engine was still running.
Glasair III pilot reported misjudging the height from flair to touchdown and the aircraft touched down firmly; the aircraft nosed over; nose gear collapsed and the aircraft skidded to a stop.

A Dispatcher reported NOTAMs were not available due to equipment outage so they could not release flights. The reporter stated the dispatchers were told by Supervisors to contact airports operations to get NOTAMs but this was not sufficient and in violation of company procedures.
Air carrier Captain reported possible 5G interference on short final at CVG airport.
Center Controller reported a loss of separation and attributed it to being overworked; and short staffed at the Center.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they flew in controlled airspaced without LAANC authorization. Reporter stated they requested authorization well in advance of the flight but did not obtain it.
