PA28 Flight Instructor reported fuel exhaustion resulted in a forced landing in a field without damage or injuries.
A90 TRACON Controller reported possible automation issues and training deficiencies affecting communication with Providence Approach.

SF50 pilot reported failing to remove the AOA cover prior to flight. After takeoff; the pilot heard a noise that did not dissipate upon raising the landing gear and decided to perform an air turnback. Due to having no airspeed indication the pilot had difficulty in controlling the aircraft; which led to a runway excursion.
General aviation flight crew reported engine problems and power loss immediately after takeoff. They were able to return to their departure airport.
CRJ-700 First Officer reported encountering severe wake turbulence on arrival into CLT in trail of an A320.
B737 Captain reported as they pulled into the gate; ground personnel stated the right main landing gear fire resulted in an evacuation and a critical equipment problem.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a conflict with another aircraft while on final approach.

CRJ-900 flight crew reported a loud squealing from the Main Cabin Door shortly after takeoff. Communications was difficult between the flight crew and the Forward Flight Attendant. The flight crew requested priority handling and  burned fuel; then performed and air turn back to land at departure airport.

Small transport Captain reported intersecting taxiways at MOB were confusing and requested that there be two hotspots created; one at the intersection of taxiways C; A4; A and one at the intersections of taxiways B; L; R.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported the FA harness on this particular A319 jump-seat is very difficult to fasten/release and is different than other aircraft.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported GPS jamming affected efforts to utilize SATCOM.
Air carrier First Officer reported a fume event during cruise was identified by the CO2 alarm activating. The flight crew diverted to an alternate airport where a safe landing was completed.
Single pilot reported a hard landing which resulted in damage to the landing gear.

B-737 air carrier crew reported a flight control malfunction during the initial takeoff roll. The crew rejected the takeoff and returned to the gate where a hydraulic leak was identified by maintenance.

B737 pilot reported a loud hissing sound in the cockpit; attributed to a broken and leaking oxygen mask at the observer's seat position. The oxygen system was depleted; and an immediate diversion was accomplished.
Flight Attendant reported a Customer Service Agent opened aircraft doors at gate without looking for the proper signage. One door did not fully open and the escape slide was not deployed.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a lost link during a flight. The reporter stated they believe the lost link may have been caused by possible jamming or counter UAS technology.
B737-800 pilot flying reported losing one GPS for thirty minutes during cruise due to testing that was occurring in the area.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported being confused by the departure procedures provided on the CANOGA 3 SID at VNY.
Pilot reported loss of engine control; resulting in an In Flight Shut Down and a precautionary emergency landing.
Air taxi pilot reported engine malfunction during climb resulted in return to departure airport.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft descended below their assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
CRJ-900 Flight Attendant reported a non-rev passenger was charging an e-cigarette during the flight. The Flight Attendant directed the passenger to discontinue and the passenger complied.
PA28 flight Instructor reported loss of throttle control on takeoff. Flight Instructor was able to return to a safe landing.
Fractional jet flight crew reported that another light jet started their takeoff roll on an intersecting runway at TEB while on final approach. Flight crew executed a go-around and landed safely.

General aviation pilot reported excessive lateral drift from the runway when in the landing flare on a visual approach at a non-towered airport in gusty wind conditions. The pilot performed a rejected landing and go around then diverted to a more suitable landing field.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that a gate agent opened service door without an ok to open hand signal. Escape slide did not deploy.
A TBM pilot reported during initial climb they temporarily lost trim control. They reset the auto pilot and regained full control.

Air Carrier Captain reported a major failure by all ground staff to recognize the dangerous conditions posed by the transport of Dry Ice without adequate ventilation due to inaccurate Final Release documents. Documents were updated and flight continued to destination safely.
A319 Captain reported strong possibility of signal interference which resulted in erratic maneuvering of aircraft to autopilot.
Pilot flying BE60 aircraft reported engine RPM surge inflight.

JAX TRACON Controller reported airspace issues related to GA traffic crossing air carrier final approach paths without communication. Controller suggested the JAX Class C needs to be extended to avoid these issues.
Center Controller reported an aircraft that was climbing below the MIA and wanted permission to do so.
Citation Jet Captain reported hydroplaning and loss of directional control followed by an excursion into the grass as he attempted to turn off the wet runway.
B737 flight crew reported hearing loud bangs during takeoff roll. Flight crew rejected takeoff and the number 2 engine shut down on its own.
PA24 pilot reported fuel mismanagement resulted in an off airport landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported they were cleared by ATC to cross a runway that had an aircraft on take-off roll. As the flight crew taxied toward the runway hold short line; ATC directed them to stop when they realized another aircraft was rolling. Flight crew stopped aircraft and crossed after departure was airborne.
GA pilot reported while on approach a bird strike penetrated the windshield and resulted in considerable noise and wind but pilot made a safe landing.
A Tower Local Controller reported they cleared an aircraft to land on a runway occupied by a vehicle.
Air carrier flight crew reported while troubleshooting an EICAS message during engine start; the aircraft started to roll and the flight crew applied the brakes to regain control of the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported there is a taxi line in use by ATC at IAH that is not shown on the chart.

C172 non flying pilot reported a runway excursion during landing. Flying pilot elected to use reduced flap setting to compensate for bumps and drifted off downwind side of runway after ballooning during landing.
C-208 pilot reported low fuel after takeoff. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed safely.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence climbing out of LAS in trail of an A320 that resulted in stick shaker activation.

Cessna Citation CJ4 flight crew reported that; on departure; the left main landing gear failed to retract. After several unsuccessful attempts to fully retract the landing gear; a return to the departure airport was accomplished.

Pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft that cut off reporter's aircraft while on the downwind.  This happened LMO airport which is reportedly known for parachuting and had jumpers in the air at the time.
Air carrier Captain reported altitude excursion during initial approach due to his failure to properly monitor flying pilot. Altitude excursion was corrected and flight completed a safe landing.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported Lithium Ion powered mobility device battery was not configured for air transport.

Fractional jet crew reported a conflict with another aircraft during climb from a non-towered airport in marginal VMC weather conditions. The crew received a terrain warning while maneuvering away from the conflict then continued the flight to destination.
PA-32 Flight Instructor and student reported engine failure during cruise which resulted in a diversion and a landing on a taxiway.

Flight Instructor reported while in the practice area they noticed traffic on their TIS-B and this traffic began a climb resulting in the student pilot taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.
Flight crew reported bird strike resulted in critical equipment problem and return to departure airport.  Crew also reported the runway is next to the river; wildlife mitigation needs to improve.

Instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft; during daylight VMC on a training flight at a non-towered airport. While climbing on a missed approach; the instructor observed an aircraft landing in the opposite direction; took control and banked sharply to avoid a collision; then resumed the flight.
Air carrier crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while descending on the initial approach to MDW airport. The crew climbed back to assigned altitude and completed the approach.
CRJ900 flight crew reported receiving a GPWS alert during approach.
Airport Management reported an aircraft landed with snow removal equipment on the runway. Reportedly; the aircraft was not on the proper frequency.
B767 Captain reported they received 'ENG BLEED OFF L and PACK OFF L EICAS' messages during climbout. Flight crew returned to departure airport for an overweight landing.
Gulfstream G280 flight crew reported a cracked window resulted in expeditious handling for descent and diversion.
Air carrier personnel reported a passenger attempted to check a bag with spray paint. TSA personnel removed the spray paint.
Pilot reported a NMAC that required compliance with an RA while conducting a go around at LAS.
BE20 pilot reported loss of control resulting in uncommmanded 500' descent on arrival into SDF due to either severe turbulence or a wake encounter.

B777 First Officer reported the flight crew did not send an acknowledgment for the dangerous goods prior to gate pushback as the dangerous goods summary was sent after pushing from the gate. The acknowledgment was sent prior to takeoff.
Pilot reported loss of both alternators in flight during turbulence and icing conditions. The Pilot requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.

A320 pilot reported being assigned to an aircraft that had been previously returned to service after a lightning strike. During initial climb; engine instrument indications became erratic and the flight return to the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict at ORD airport requiring the Captain to immediately stop the aircraft short of the runway for a fast approaching aircraft on Runway 28C.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the vicinity of SABLO intersection.
Flight Crew reported Gear Indication malfunction on approach to land.
PA28 pilot reported alternator loss on descent. The pilot returned to the departure airport and landed.
B-757 crew reported a cabin door malfunction during cruise while experiencing turbulence on an ETOPS flight. The crew returned to the departure airport and landed overweight.
C150 Pilot reported fuel exhaustion caused engine failure and a forced; off airport landing in a field.
Light aircraft pilot reported loss of GPS signal in the vicinity of CKB.

B737 NG flight crew reported non standard communication procedures between the flight deck and the push back ground crew. Lost comm and non standard hand signal use created an unsafe situation during push back and aircraft release. In the end the task was completed without incident.
Recreational Drone Pilot reported inadvertently flying within Class B airspace from the parking lot of a stadium complex. The Pilot was unaware of the incursion until advised by security personnel.
Air Carrier First Officer reported erroneously transported Human Organ (Kidney) on the flight deck in violation of FOM Hazmat policy.

Air carrier Captain reported the ATC clearance and Jeppesen moving map does not provide information on the requirement of contacting Tower for entry into Runway 22L to get into the international ramp at ORD.

Air carrier flight crew reported an issue on the RNDRZ1 approach and possible confusion with how the chart is written. Crew also noted that ATC advised that over 300 aircraft have had the same situation occur.
Small jet Captain reported encountering severe turbulence at FL400 that resulted in an inflight upset. Control was regained and smoother air was found at a lower altitude.
Air carrier First Officer reported that after responding to a TCAS/RA for a helicopter; they initiated a go around.

A300 flight crew reported an AFT/BULK CARGO COMPARTMENT SMOKE indication in cruise. The flight crew requested priority handing and immediately descended. The decision was made to divert and make a precautionary landing.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft while maneuvering into the traffic pattern during visual conditions. The Controller could not maintain the distance between the aircraft and issued a go around to provide separation.

B777 flight crew parked at a hardstand reported they were cleared by ground personnel to release brakes; but when they started the engines the aircraft began rolling because the tug was not yet connected.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of ADS-B systems during approach to DEN airport.  Reportedly; ATC advised them 5G interference had been reported in the area.
A TRACON Controller reported they descended an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a Low Altitude Alert from Tower when they descended below a waypoint on an RNAV Approach
A319 F/O reported failure of ELAC 1 and ELAC 2 during cruise so the flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.

C172 pilot reported an engine malfunction during initial climb from a non-towered airport in night visual conditions. The engine malfunction limited the ability to climb and the pilot was able to maintain an altitude to continue 20 miles to the destination airport and landed safely.
Air carrier operations supervisor reported being notified to inform ramp personnel to ensure enough bag/mail/ballast securement was applied for safe transport.
An air carrier pilot reported loss of GPS signal and a Terrain Alert Warning on departure.

C172 Instructor and student pilot reported poor aircraft performance shortly after takeoff led to the decision to land on the remaining runway. The aircraft ran off the runway after landing; resulting in minor injuries to the student pilot.
Technician reported communications problems during engine start on a departure aircraft. A fuel leak was noticed and no maintenance action was taken prior to aircraft departure.
GA pilot reported a NMAC while in the traffic pattern and preparing to land.

A319 flight crew reported while parked at the gate awaiting pushback they were unaware they had a fluid leak from the landing gear and that a maintenance technician was underneath the aircraft working on the leak.
CRJ200 Captain reported a repeat incident of cabin pressure failure caused an immediate descent and diversion to make a precautionary landing.
Pilot reported inadvertently crossing the hold short lines onto a taxiway after being unable to recognize the faded and unmaintained taxiway markings on the pavement at CXY airport.

B767 First Officer reported the Flight Attendant notified the flight deck of a possible tail strike on rotation. Before takeoff; there were multiple attempts to request data; with some correct and some incorrect. As the runway to be used had to be manually loaded; incorrect weight was mistakenly put in during the process.
Pilot reported issues with scheduling relating to fatigue and overwork by the company.
Pilot thought they were landing on a dry sand bar but the aircraft sunk into the mud and flipped over.
A319 pilot reported engine malfunction after takeoff. Returned and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier flight crew reported confusion over their takeoff clearance when the aircraft's RAAS issued an aural warning at the same time the Tower Controller issued the clearance.

Captain reported a departure delay due to a Dry Cell motorized wheelchair loaded in cargo compartment with incorrect information on the air carrier's Hazmat documents. An uneventful flight completed after flight crew received correct documents.
Air Carrier Captain reported a Master Caution 'LANDING GEAR INOP' message at cruise. The Flight Crew continued the flight to destination airport and landed safely.
PA-28 instructor reported a tire failure upon landing which resulted in a runway excursion with one main tire in the grass.

Air carrier flight crew reported a conflict with a truck while taxiing to their gate. The truck drove in front of them requiring the Captain to apply the brakes abruptly to avoid a possible collision.
An Air Carrier Captain reported stop bars placed in close proximity to each other at EWR causes confusion and stopping at the wrong one could block another aircraft's taxi route.

B767 First Officers reported an engine failure when throttles were advanced to takeoff thrust. The Captain rejected the takeoff and called for Crash Fire Rescue to address the ATC reported fireball Tower observed before being towed back to the gate.
ATC Controller reported a flight of two military trainers exited their training route prior to the coordinated fix and were flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737-900 flight crew reported taking evasive action after receiving a TA and RA.
Deadheading Pilot reported being asked to come to the Flight Deck and act as an ACM (Additional Crew Member) during an inflight event.
B737 First Officer reported a generator drive malfunction during cruise. The crew continued to the destination; which was the nearest suitable airport; and landed.
CE-650 flight crew reported an uneventful air return due to landing gear failed to retract on departure.
Cessna 208B pilot reported an inflight upset occurred; during which the airframe airspeed limitation was exceeded.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a GA aircraft that was crossing the final approach course and not in communication with ATC. Captain took evasive action to avoid a collision.

RV-3 Pilot reported an engine failure while descending into the destination airport. After attempting to restart the engine without success; the pilot was able to glide the aircraft into the airport for a safe landing. Upon inspection it was discovered a grounding wire had come loose and shorted against the ignition switch.
Student pilot on downwind at a non towered airport reported they performed a 360 degree turn and caused a NMAC with a departing helicopter.
Flight Instructor reported the brakes failed during taxi resulting in a taxiway excursion and the propeller hitting a taxiway light.
A C172 pilot reported the yoke stuck during takeoff resulting in a rejected takeoff and runway excursion.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during preflight at departure gate. Reporter requested maintenance resulting in the aircraft being removed from service.
Pilot reported an NMAC event during landing pattern practice with another aircraft entering the pattern for different runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft rolled into a chock during gate arrival when the brakes were not set.

General aviation flight instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport during a training flight. The instructor identified the other aircraft turning final in front of their aircraft and took the controls and performed a go-around.

P2012 First Officer reported during climbout they experienced a fuel pressure low indication resulting in inflight engine shut down and fuel leak and return to the departure airport where they landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported a NMAC during departure climb with a general aviation aircraft that flew under the nose of the reporter's aircraft requiring evasive action to avoid a collision.
B777 Dispatcher reported another aircraft pushed off the gate incorrectly onto the taxiway; causing the taxiing aircraft to abruptly stop.
Air carrier Captain reported having to stop abruptly on the taxiway at ORD to avoid a collision with a wide body aircraft.
C172 Flight Instructor reported shoe caught behind rudder pedal on landing resulted in loss of control and a runway excursion.
Pilot reported that while maneuvering in the traffic pattern another aircraft flew in front of them three times; causing several NMACs.
Air Carrier Tow personnel reported control was lost of an aircraft under tow which slid into a tug denting the aircraft.
B747-400 Captain reported multiple EICAS messages during climb. Flight returned and landed uneventfully at the departure airport.
A321 pilot reported refusing an aircraft that had multiple maintenance issues yet was assigned for international flight.
Corporate jet Captain reported a lack of taxiway signage and unfamiliarity with the HND airport resulted in a possible pilot deviation.
B767 flight crew reported the cabin pressure controller failed during cruise climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Flight crew reported a rejected take off after an ECAM message ENG 2 EGT OVER LIMIT was received. The crew returned to the gate and the aircraft was removed from service for maintenance.
SAT TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict; a MVA violation; and airspace related problems.
Pilot reported a ground conflict with another aircraft during taxi while complying with a taxi clearance at night.

EMB-145 First Officer reported Cabin Altitude climbed above 10;000 feet and was uncontrollable. The flight crew attempted to use manual mode without success and the O2 mask deployed. The flight crew descended to 10;000 feet and continued to land at destination airport.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport below Class C airspace with an air carrier operating within the Class C. The general aviation pilot took evasive action.

Air carrier First Officer reported GPS spoofing during cruise in international airspace as identified by flashing aircraft clocks. The crew verified normal aircraft tracking with ATC; proceeded on course and later; the clocks returned to normal.
Flight Instructor reported the student lost control of the aircraft on landing and the instructor was unable to prevent the runway excursion and propeller damage.
Flight Crew reported a Go Around after the Trailing Edge Flaps did not extend due to flap assymetry.
B777 flight crew reported a fumes event on final approach during landing. After executing a safe landing flight crew took measures to alleviate the fumes followed by requesting maintenance.
B757 First Officer reported an Engine Bleed EICAS message during takeoff roll as PF. Captain assumed control and rejected the takeoff.
B737-800 Captain reported the First Officer flying the departure turned the wrong direction after takeoff from ORD airport.
B767 Captain reported uncommanded aircraft movement during push back not noticed by flight crew until advised by ground crew.  Captain set the parking brake to stop aircraft movement.
Gulfstream G100 pilot reported elevator malfunction on departure roll resulting in rejected takeoff and runway excursion.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they had a brief lost link with the UAS while it was in a hover. Pilot initiated a Return To Home and the UAS landed.
TRACON Controller reported being busy and not catching an aircraft go into a low MVA.

Air carrier crew reported a near miss with a ground vehicle when starting a turn from a gate; at night in rain conditions. The Captain braked hard to avoid hitting the vehicle; and the vehicle continued without yielding to the aircraft movement.
PA-28 pilot reported an engine failure during cruise flight in VMC resulting in an off-field landing on a service road. The pilot landed the aircraft safely and reported no injuries or damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported the APU on their 737 MAX continued to shut down while in service at the gate while in a heavy wet snow storm.
Aerostar 600 pilot reported experiencing spatial disorientation and loss of aircraft control during final approach. Pilot regained aircraft control and continued to landing.
Pilot reported descending early resulting in MVA deviation.
B737-800 ground personnel reported an important package was delivered to aircraft without completing the Hazmat protocol. The Manager reviewed the Hazmat procedures with both ground personnel.
787 Captain reported right hydraulic system quantity began dropping and elected to secure the right engine driven pump and the right electric motor driven pump.
Flight instructor with student reported an NMAC while doing pattern work at RMY non-towered airport. Reportedly; proper radio calls were not being made which contributed to the event.

General aviation Instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The Instructor performed an evasive descent maneuver to avoid a collision.

Pilot reported landing at a non-towered airport and having a ground conflict with an aircraft landing on an intersecting runway. Reporter indicated this event contributed to his inattention to airspace boundaries and an airspace violation later in the day.
Air carrier ground personnel reported rejecting a Hazmat parcel which was incorrectly labeled and improperly processed.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported hearing a rumbling noise from the aft end of the aircraft during climbout. Maintenance determined that the APU door had remained in the full open position on the ground and in flight without a door light caution occurring.
B737 Captain reported cracking of the FO's cockpit window at cruise altitude. Flight crew landed uneventfully.
Air Carrier Captain reported improvements in MIA ramp markings would reduce taxi confusion.

Small transport First Officer reported that the aircraft entered Class D airspace prior to being handed off and ZME Center may not have been in contact with CGI Tower during the handoff. The reporter also noted that CGI Airport's operation hours may be inaccurate on the Chart Supplement.
ERJ175 flight crew reported a clearance deviation due to flight crew communication breakdown during approach.
Pilot reported they took evasive action to avoid a light twin using the opposite direction runway at a non-towered airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported pushing back without the required Final Dangerous Goods form.

Air carrier flight crew reported frequency 127.325 for GGG departure control is not listed as a frequency on their Jepp 10-9 chart and Tower/TRACON operating hours may also be in error. The crew reported they taxied and departed without contacting Tower or Departure Control.
Technician reported several instances of magneto failure causing engine shutdowns.

Air carrier flight crew reported two late arriving boxes were loaded with only the additional weight provided and no additional documents which contained Hazmat cargo that is not allowed on company aircraft.
C172 flight instructor reported noticing exhaust pipe was missing after landing.
Air carrier tractor driver reported during pushback from gate; the tow bar shear pin had sheared. They failed to follow procedures and the aircraft departed without being inspected for damage.

PA28 Pilot reported encountering a rough running engine during simulated engine out training with a Flight Instructor. The Instructor assumed the controls and made a landing at a nearby abandoned airfield.
Sundowner Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing training.
Flight Instructor with student reported they possibly caused a NMAC during final approach.

Flight Instructor with student reported maintenance crews were performing work on the active runway at a non-towered airport and refused to leave the runway; despite multiple aircraft having to perform go-arounds.
PA-34 Flight Instructor reported while taxiing the left brake failed resulting in loss of aircraft control and a taxiway excursion.

Cessna 172 student pilot reported a loss of aircraft control during landing due to gusty winds at a non-towered airport; resulting in a runway excursion. The student pilot did not report any injuries or damage to the aircraft or airport.

PA-60 Pilot reported an engine failure during climb due to turbocharger failure. After feathering and securing the engine; the Pilot attempted an unsuccessful restart and diverted to a precautionary landing.
Cessna 500 pilot reported a cracked cockpit window during climb to high altitude resulting in aircraft depressurization. The flight crew diverted and landed safely.
Air Carrier Captain reported receiving a GPWS Bank Angle warning when the aircraft encountered turbulence at the same time the Captain turned on the autopilot.
C172 pilot reported complete loss of avionics power. The pilot elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Premier 1 Captain reported a cabin altitude warning after leveling off during climb due to an excessive cabin altitude rate of climb.  Aircraft returned to departure airport for maintenance.
Government UAS pilot reported a vehicle drove over a UAS after it landed; destroying it beyond repair.
UAS pilot reported the UAS flew outside of the authorized operations area. Pilot continued flight after receiving ATC clearance into additional airspace.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to LGA in trail of a B737.
A321 pilot reported a bleed air malfunction followed by an IDG temperature increase on the same engine during descent. The flight crew continued to destination and landed.
CRJ-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence shortly after takeoff from PHX in trail of an A321.

C208 Captain reported the undocumented transport of Hazmat material (fire extinguisher). The Captain stated no Hazmat documents were provided and that he failed to notice the fire extinguisher on board.
B757 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on final approach to EWR in trail of a B777.
GA Flight Instructor reported a ground conflict with a vehicle just prior to crossing a taxiway. The aircraft came to an abrupt stop to avoid a ground collision.
Air carrier Maintenance Technician reported cargo spill cleanup procedures were not correctly followed.

B737-700 flight crew reported an abrupt shudder with associated high EGT on the #2 engine. The flight crew elected to make an in flight shut down of the affected engine; made an air turn back; and landed at the departure airport.

Pilot and ground employee reported that an aircraft did not communicate their intentions on CTAF; taxied onto the runway and departed in front of an inbound aircraft causing that aircraft to go-around.

Corporate Captain reported a NMAC while taking off from a non towered airport. The Captain stated becoming aware to late; of the twin engine aircraft on short final; and started the takeoff roll. Then during the initial climb the other aircraft was directly above the Captain's jet; as they were executing a missed approach straight ahead.
Flight crew reported a failure and overspeed of the trailing edge flaps during climb. Crew continued with priority handling to their destination where an inspection by maintenance was made.
Air carrier FA reported a mechanical failure of the nose wheel steering and the need to execute a priority landing. The FA explains there was a feeling of lack of support from the respective company.

An ERJ 170 Captain reported hearing annunciations sounding while in the forward lavatory during a break. The Captain returned to the flight deck and was told that 8 or 10 EICAS Messages had populated including a MAU1 FAIL. The flight crew were able to engage the auto pilot and stabilize the aircraft 1;000 feet below assigned altitude. The flight returned to assigned altitude and continued the flight to destination airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing the auto throttles retard while on departure; resulting in the flight crew disengaging the auto throttles to regain control of the aircraft's speed. The flight crew returned to the departure airport where they landed safely.
General aviation pilot reported a NMAC at a non-towered airport in the traffic pattern. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air taxi Captain reported a NMAC with a UAS while turning onto final approach. Tower Controller closed the airport for a short period of time due to the UAS presence.

CE-560 Captain reported pressurization control issues with an excessive cabin altitude rate of climb with a differential pressure of 8.9 PSI. After descending to a lower altitude; the flight crew returned to the departure airport.
Instructor Pilot reported braking hard to avoid a collision with another aircraft during taxi.
Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action due to a Near Mid Air Collision.

PA-23 Pilot reported an engine lost a significant amount of oil which resulted in a diversion to landing. Maintenance determined a loose screw on a newly installed part caused the leak. After repairs were made the pilot departed without refueling; which resulted in fuel starvation and a diversion to landing.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an alert for the air/ground switch on final approach to OAK.

C172 pilot reported engine power fluctuations during cruise required a diversion to a nearby airport. Pilot stated the checklist does not provide a procedure for this problem and the placement of the placard for switching fuel tanks is in a location that is difficult to view.

EMB-175 Flight Crew reported a controlled flight into terrain event during a visual approach in which a terrain caution followed by a terrain warning were enunciated. Flying pilot took evasive action to the correct flight path.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft rolled toward the tug after brake release during pushback. Captain applied brakes to stop and then tug captured the nose wheel.
Air carrier First Officer reported company charting error for ORD runway 28C LAHSO reference.
A319 Flight Crew reported a Ground Proximity Warning even though they were established at a safe altitude for terrain avoidance.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude warning from ATC during an unstable approach in visual conditions. The pilot performed a go-around and returned for a landing.

ZMA ARTCC Controllers reported aircraft filed and operated in airspace without the proper navigation equipment. Controller reported equipment requirement should be in a NOTAM to prevent route processing errors.
Air carrier Captain reported an Acetone odor upon gate arrival. The flight crew collaborated with the DG Specialists who removed the compromised container which enabled the flight to continue safely.
B737-800 Captain reported a near miss with a baggage cart while maneuvering to enter the gate area and park. The Captain applied the brakes and stopped the aircraft to prevent a collision.

A small aircraft pilot reported complete electrical failure and diverted to the nearest tower controlled airport to land. The FBO staff told the pilot they did not follow the correct procedures for electrical failure.

PA-24 pilot reported having trouble landing and multiple go-around attempts due to shuddering. On the third attempt and upon finally landing; the pilot shut down the aircraft and saw that the wheel hub of the nose gear had broke and a prop strike had occurred.
B737 Captain reported an obstacle pull up" warning when the First Officer turned final approach too early near a high rise building."
GA instructor pilot reported an NMAC during departure from SWO airport requiring an evasive maneuver.

Air carrier crew reported a TCAS RA on a ILS approach at Tower controlled airport with an aircraft in traffic pattern at a general aviation airport below the ILS approach course. The crew complied with the TCAS guidance and safely landed.
A319 Captain reported the aircraft weather radar system failed during cruise as the flight started to encounter poor weather conditions. Flight crew diverted and made an overweight landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported during descent they lost control of aircraft due to having an encounter with a microburst. They took evasive action and regained control of aircraft.
EMB-505 First Officer reported a loss of hydraulic pressure just prior to leveling off at top of climb. Flight crew diverted and main tires on both main gears failed during braking on landing.
Pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft that was exiting the LAX SFRA.
Air Carrier flight crew reported TCAS RA in cruise flight and flight crew took evasive action.

B737 Check Airman reported a ground conflict with unlighted objects in the middle of the ramp area; on a taxi line; while taxiing to the gate at night. The Check Airman stopped the aircraft and shutdown an engine to prevent aircraft damage; then proceeded to the gate.
B787 Flight Attendant reported a fume event during preflight; cabin crew advised the Captain; and everyone deplaned.
MD-11 Dispatcher reported an engine failure and a subsequent diversion were the result of improper fueling at the departure airport.
Captain reported a successful recovery of a CIFT event during approach as a result of temporary loss of Situational Awareness.

PA-28 pilot reported a critical ground conflict at a non-towered airport. The pilot states; with full power applied on the pilot's Cherokee; a Citation entered the opposite end of the runway and began their roll. The pilot moved near the grass on the pavement edge as the jet passed overhead. The pilot states there was no comm from the jet at anytime.
GA pilot and TRACON Controller reported the pilot flying a LDA Approach to SLC airport deviated from their course and altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

B757 Captain reported after leveling off in cruise; they got a master warning and cabin altitude indication. While descending to destination airport flight crew determined cabin altitude control knob was not in correct position.

BE58 pilot reported observing a large streak of oil on left wing and fluctuating left engine oil pressure. The pilot performed a precautionary shutdown and diverted to an alternate for a safe landing.

A TRACON Controller reported when they relieved a controller they did not realize the previous controller had not climbed an aircraft to the altitude in the new arrival and departure procedures resulting in a conflict with another aircraft at the same altitude.

Ground employee reported inaccurate Hazmat weight data provided to the flight during pre-flight. Cargo department discovered the weight and balance error and sent a revised Hazmat weights to airborne aircraft.
Small aircraft instructor reported experiencing an airborne conflict with a commercial jet on an approach to Denver airport.

PA-32 Pilot reported the forward cargo compartment door opened after rotation. The pilot attempted to land on the remaining runway; but the runway was wet and the attempt failed with the aircraft leaving the pavement and striking a berm. The aircraft was damaged but there were no injuries to the pilot.
Captain reported BLEED 1 FAIL EICAS on take off followed by a Fuel PRESS Status light illuminating. The flight crew returned to departure airport.
Flight crew reported an aircraft taxied at high speed in front of them requiring braking to avoid a collision.
CE680A pilot reported aileron control anomaly during approach and stated they had experienced a similar anomaly on another of the company's aircraft.
B767 pilot reported burnt smell in cabin. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.

B757-200 flight crew reported Engine Overheat on takeoff. The flight crew ran the check lists and QRH; and requested priority handling. An air turn back and precautionary landing was made at departure airport with the Left Engine throttle at idle.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported preparing cabin for a diversion after Captain advised of a gas line problem and subsequent engine shutdown.
Navion pilot reported an ADS-B transponder issue and subsequent electrical failure in class B airspace.

PA-23 pilot reported while demonstrating an engine shutdown and restart on a practical flight test; the engine would not restart. The pilot diverted and found after landing that the propeller had not come out of feather during the attempted restart.
DA40 instructor pilot reported landing safely at departure airport after experiencing a power loss in initial climb.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending below charted altitude on approach to ORD in instrument conditions when the First Officer improperly set the MCP altitude.
B737-900 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B737 departing LAS. This distraction contributed to missing a crossing restriction.
A300 flight crew reported the undocumented transport of Dangerous Goods due to a documentation error that stated there were no Dangerous Goods on board.
Air carrier First Officer reported above normal brake temperatures after landing then was advised the tires were deflated post flight.

EMB-145 Captain reported stiffness in the Rudder Pedals during preflight. After maintenance and another check after push back; the flight took off. After takeoff; Cabin Pressure was uncontrollable with loud noise from the forward doors. The flight crew requested vectors and performed an air turn back to land at departure airport.

A320 First Officer reported ECAM message ENG 1 Compressor Vane followed by the aircraft shaking hard four times. Engine EGT went into the red range and ECAM message ENG 1 Fail appeared. Flight crew elected to divert to ZZZ and make a precautionary landing.
General aviation Instructor reported a near miss on a training flight during cruise. The instructor maneuvered to avoid a collision.
CE-551 pilot reported cabin decompression in cruise flight.
GA Instructor Pilot reported an aborted takeoff when they noticed another aircraft was taking off opposite direction on the same runway.
CE-560 Captain reported executing a go-around at touchdown on Runway 29 at EWR when jet blast from  air carrier aircraft on Runway 22 had a destabilizing effect on their aircraft.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported discovering the undocumented transport of an electric scooter at destination arrival gate.
C177 pilot reported engine abnormalities during cruise resulting in a diversion. The engine completely failed right before touchdown.
EMB-145 Captain reported maintenance refusing to accept a write up on the autopilot and departed to destination airport where the autopilot failed; causing an air turn back.
EMB-145 flight crew reported multiple anti-ice system failures resulting in an immediate descent to exit icing conditions.
BE-400 pilot reported horizontal stabilizer de-ice system failure in flight. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Gulfstream pilot reported on final approach the ailerons were malfunctioning in a wind shear condition resulting in an unstabilized approach.

A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft with one engine shut down descended into confliction with parallel runway traffic. The Controller did not realize their trainee had issued the wrong altitude to the aircraft.
A300 flight crew reported receiving an AVIONICS SMOKE RED ECAM warning accompanied with an aural alert. The flight crew donned oxygen masks and continued to land.

Air carrier Captain reported continuous turbulence during cruise that caused altitude deviations. The flight crew notified the company of the weather conditions and submitted a Maintenance logbook write up.

TRACON Controller reported having an aircraft that wanted one approach; but the tower was closed and the approaches were set up for the opposite direction.  Controller reported possibly confusing pilot with clearances.
ZLC Controller reported issuance of IFR clearance to enroute aircraft while below MVA resulted in a CFTT event.
A TRACON Controller giving instruction reported an aircraft descended through its assigned altitude and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Pilot reported inadequate radio coverage in the vicinity of RCK airport when attempting to contact Austin Approach while on flight following.

ORL Local Tower Controller reported two aircraft refused a clearance to fly a south downwind at 900 feet. The reporter stated since the AIM recommends 1;000 feet for downwind leg this procedure should be changed.

Gulfstream V flight crew reported as Flap/Slat Miscompare Light during descent and diagnosed this as a flap asymmetry problem. Flight crew elected to request priority handling and diverted for a precautionary landing.
Air carrier Captain reported the J1 pad was incorrectly marked on the EFB. The pavement is marked correctly but cannot be seen until the aircraft is on the line.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in marginal VFR conditions and did not follow the cloud clearance requirements.

Air carrier crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while on the initial approach when above the glide path on a training flight at night. The Captain made the vertical correction and stabilized the aircraft and landed.
A TRACON Controller and pilot reported the aircraft's new auto pilot malfunctioned while being vectored for an approach causing the aircraft to fly below the minimum vectoring altitude.
GA pilot examiner reported that FSDO reported a near miss to the them three months after the incident and could not recall any details of it.
Learjet 35 First Officer reported loss of hydraulic pressure and return to departure airport.

Air Carrier Dispatcher; Ground Personnel; and Gate Agent reported how passenger and checked bag containing 9 Lithium power banks were rebooked on different flights. The two flights landed at same stopover where passenger and checked bag were reunited and safely completed flight to destination.
A320 flight crew reported low oil quantity indications on initial climb and return to departure airport.
PA24 Flight Instructor and Student reported landing gear failure as the aircraft touched down on landing.
A320 First Officer reported loss of communication with ground crew during pushback.

CRJ-700 flight crew reported two instances of loss of aircraft control due to severe turbulence during climb. A Flight Attendant was injured and a portion of the landing gear seal was damaged as a result.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that a gate agent opened entry door without an ok to open" hand signal to deliver cargo hold paperwork to the pilot; after the door was armed. The gate agent was told the door was armed; the escape slide did not deploy."
Corporate pilot reported TCAS target which followed them on their approach. The crew believed it may have been a UAS.
B767 flight crew reported F/O cockpit window shattered inflight. Flight returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.

Pilot reported a NMAC on final approach.The pilot has talked with local FSDO and has attempted to talk with the tower manager. The pilot is not satisfied with the responses received as of this report date.
First Officer reported problems communicating with ground crew during a pushback.

EMB 190 crew member reported being notified that they had missed a pin/flag had not been removed; rendering an escape slide inoperable. They suggested the placement of the flag inside of the door behind fairings may have also contributed to it not being noticed.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer flying a nighttime Visual Approach was unstable and received a low altitude warning from ATC.
Air carrier pilot reported false terrain alerts and numerous system errors due to GPS interference on a flight while in the vicinity of the Middle East region.
Small aircraft Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while on approach to 79C while the other aircraft was on final approach into ATW. The two airports are in close proximity.
Gulfstream IV flight crew reported the outer panel of a passenger window blew out during cruise and was ingested by the engine. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
A Flight Instructor in a DA40 reported the door came open during takeoff and disconnected from the aircraft during climb. They returned to the departure airport.

EMB-170 Flight Crew reported a wind shear event during final approach in turbulent conditions. The Flight Crew executed a successful wind shear recovery procedure which caused momentary airspeed and altitude deviations.
B737 Captain reported the number 2 engine oil pressure and quantity decreased during cruise. Crew shut down engine and performed a diversion.
C172 Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a near miss with a helicopter.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict occurred during taxi out and stated they were not advised by Ground Control of the approaching aircraft.
C172 flight instructor on training flight with student reported engine malfunction in cruise flight.

Kitfox 2 pilot reported an engine failure while in cruise at 5;000 ft. The pilot was able to restart the engine and continued to land where; upon inspection a clogged fuel filter was discovered. After clearing the clog; the pilot inadvertently crossed the runway hold short line when readying for takeoff.

B737 NG flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence that disconnected the autopilot at FL310 from an aircraft 5 - 7 NM ahead that was on the same route. While descending through FL290; the flight crew saw the other aircraft and climbed back to FL290 and reengaged the autopilot.
The reporter stated a company bulletin regarding operations in the 5G environment is incomplete and hard to comprehend creating a safety concern.
C680 Captain reported getting two RAs during climbout; however; they were unable to visually locate the traffic and ATC advised there was no other traffic in the vicinity.

Flight Attendant reported after almost an entire year of being on extended leave of absence during COVID-19 schedule reductions; the disruption of routine led the FA to forget the safety briefing for the exit row passengers.
Beech 55 pilot reported descending below the glideslope on an ILS approach when the autopilot apparently malfunctioned.
Flight Instructor reported a temporary loss of directional control during landing due to a flat tire. Maintenance was notified who replaced the tire and taxied the aircraft to ramp with no issues.

B737-700 First Officer reported Flight Attendants were experiencing physiological symptoms from 'very strong ' cabin fumes. After consultation with Dispatch it was determined to divert for maintenance action.
B777 Pilot reported failure to remember that no tug was attached on engine start resulted in an uncommanded roll forward and a loss of control event.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a lavatory was emitting a electrical burning odor in flight.
Air carrier Captain reported activating a GPWS Terrain alert while on ATC vectors to intercept final approach course.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a passenger's checked case spilled Lithium batteries on the ramp during loading.

Air carrier Captain reported multiple incorrect final DG weight revisions provided to the flight crew during preflight. After coordinating with Dispatch and after receiving accurate DG weight the flight departed safely.

B737 flight crew reported after brake release the flight received a warning to acknowledge the Final DG Summary. After discovering that there was Dangerous Goods on board; the reporter contacted multiple parties until the Final DG Summary was received.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported starting takeoff roll from LUAW clearance with crossing traffic on the runway resulting in a rejected takeoff.
Embraer 170 Captain reported a flight control malfunction when the autopilot was disconnected during the approach. The aircraft landed safely and the defect written up in the logbook.
Technician reported that as part of a crew; he was instructed to install a jacking pad adapter on the aircraft.
An air carrier flight crew reported their parking brake failed during push back and the aircraft rolled into the towbar.
PUB Tower Controller reported issuing a low altitude alert to a night VFR arrival that entered the pattern below minimum safe altitude; resulted in a CFTT event.

Captain reported numerous EICAS messages and controllability issues from rotation through to landing roll with a thrust reverser malfunctioning on deployment. The aircraft was turned over to maintenance for action.
CE-560XLS Captain reported a pitch runaway during descent from cruise altitude.  Flight crew diverted and landed normally.
Flight Instructor on training flight during landing rollout reported PC-12 aircraft attempting to land in the opposite direction.
Air carrier Captain reported being pressured to depart with incomplete DG documents and an incomplete General Declaration. After extensive delays the Captain elected to call in fatigue.
Air carrier Captain reported not being notified by the flight attendants about an event where the passenger dropped a lithium battery device into the belly of the aircraft.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported multiple landing gear disagree messages at top of climb. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
C172 Flight Instructor reported the throttle became unresponsive during cruise. Flight Instructor diverted and moved mixture control to cutoff position to reduce airspeed for safe landing.
GA student pilot reported observing a twin piston aircraft converging resulted in taking evasive action.
Corporate Captain reported #1 engine propeller blade was damaged during taxi.
Air carrier Captain reported a DCA ILS Runway 01 charting discrepancy that there are different final approach courses for the same ILS localizer on two different published procedures.
Super King Air 200 pilot reported complete electrical failure after take-off.
MD-11 technicians reported failure to reinstall the engine spinner assembly after maintenance. During an engine leak check; the spinner barrel nuts dislodged; causing damage to the fan blades.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving incomplete and erroneous DG documents and refused to push. After talking to the DG load planners; it was determined that Airport Operations did not properly complete the load planning paperwork as it did not include a battery-powered wheelchair that had been loaded onto the aircraft.
Air carrier Pilot reported the push back ground crew disconnected the tow bar without advising the flight crew; resulting in the uncontrolled aircraft rolling on the ramp.
B737-700 flight crew reported a bird strike event during takeoff resulting in left wing leading edge and fan blade damage. The flight crew returned to the departure airport and landed.
Air Carrier First Officer reported GPS jamming.
B757 flight crew reported loss of #1 engine oil pressure after takeoff. Flight returned to departure airport and landed safely.
B777 First Officer reported a fuel leak resulted in an engine shutdown and diversion.
Air carrier Pilot reported a lack of accuracy on the 10-9 chart coupled with unlit signage at night led to an inadvertent taxiway incursion at MMQT.
B737 flight crew reported hearing a loud banging noise and the First Officer saw an object hit the aircraft in cruise flight. Flight crew continued to destination and landed.
A321 flight crew reported a fume event during preflight resulting in aircraft swap and flight attendants experiencing physiological symptoms.
Helicopter pilot and single engine aircraft pilot reported a NMAC between the two aircraft when the single engine aircraft overtook the helicopter on short final during a visual approach.
Flight Attendants reported the jumpseat locations on the aircraft are not safe because they are difficult to reach when passengers are standing outside the nearby lavatories.
Learjet 45 Captain reported a bird strike and engine roll back after takeoff. The flight crew requested priority handling and made an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
EMB-170 flight crew reported a EGPWS warning on final landing approach for no apparent reason. The flight landed safely.

Flight instructor reported while on short final to SAC airport had a near mid air collision with another aircraft 500 feet above them that was also on final approach on the same runway. The other aircraft ended up going around.
Center Controller reported descending an aircraft 1;000 ft. below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

Air Taxi First Officer reported a GPWS sink rate alert resulted in a CFTT event due to terrain factor landing runway 26 to RNO.  First officer stated when wind dictates; not enough vertical guidance is available to this runway; especially at night.
Air Carrier Captain reported receiving an audible landing gear warning horn for one second in the pattern at SAV.  Reporter cited possible 5G interference.
Maintenance crew reported aircraft escape slide detached from entry door.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from Tower during visual approach. Flight crew climbed to correct altitude and continued approach.
A Center Controller reported they accepted a handoff from an adjacent Center of an aircraft descending below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported it was difficult to place the girt bar into the door bracket and suggested the plastic collars be adjusted fix the issue.
Flight instructor reported NMAC with another aircraft while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport.

Air carrier Captain reported Approach Controller notified them during initial approach of a UAS flying near the approach course. The flight had a near midair collision with the UAS during final approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported a jetway walkaway floor surface safety concern at GUM airport.
Air carrier Captain reported explosives and a power source were packaged together as Dangerous Goods. The Captain had the DG cargo removed prior to departure.
Flight crew reported after receiving clearance to line up and wait; they observed another aircraft taxi onto the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming near Ankara airspace. The Captain states further west; when it was expected the instruments would operate; the GPS and ADS-C remained inoperative.

Captain reported the aircraft fuel quantity indicators were on MEL and during descent the right fuel tank indicator showed a rapid loss of fuel. After landing; the fuel tank was found to be empty with no evidence of a leak.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing loss of GPS signal due to signal jamming in the vicinity of White Sands.
Air Carrier flight crew reported pushback from the gate without the required Final DG Summary. Final DG Summary was requested and received shortly thereafter.

B747-400 First Officer reported having concerns over the Captain's unsafe and non-standard procedures to start an inoperative engine; using ground personnel instead of contacting Dispatch or Maintenance Control for assistance.
Aircraft technician reported insufficient repair information in the 767-400 Boeing Structural Repair Manual.
Air carrier pilot reported seeing a UAS pass near their aircraft while climbing through 600 feet. The reporter continued to fly a normal departure and notified ATC about their observation.
Air carrier Captain reported on departure roll experiencing aircraft veering off the center-line with no rudder control resulting in rejecting the takeoff and taxing to the ramp.
Air carrier pilot reported the MIA Class B airspace shelf altitudes contribute to conflicts between VFR aircraft with IFR aircraft in the Class B airspace being vectored for initial approach.
C-182 pilot reported a near miss while in the airport traffic pattern on approach.

C172 flight instructor and student reported while conducting a night training flight in the traffic pattern; the flight instruments began indicating erroneously; giving unreliable airspeed and altitude information. While troubleshooting the situation; the flight crew continued safely to a landing.
A321 Captain reported control difficulties just before landing at PHOG/OGG when rotor wash from a departing helicopter was encountered.
Captain reported lining up incorrectly for gate arrival due to inadequate lighting and markings of taxi and lead in lines.
Pilot reported ATC issued complex and confusing taxi instructions during the landing rollout which led to confusion about being cleared to cross a runway.
A321 Captain reported returning to departure airport after experiencing a #1 engine compressor stall.
ARTCC Controller reported having an aircraft assigned an altitude that was below the MIA resulting in the aircraft flying towards terrain.  The aircraft was climbed to a safe altitude.
Pilot reported eye injury after a laser was directed into the cockpit from a ground source.
Flight Attendant reported; after gate arrival; the oncoming cleaning crew was interfering with passenger deplaning and social distance policy.
Pilot reported the autopilot disconnected due to severe turbulence encounter resulting in altitude and heading deviations. Pilot stabilized aircraft and continued.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near midair collision with a UAS while they were on approach.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a liquid spill from a bottle of glass cleaner labeled as Hazmat which was inadvertently left in cockpit.

Air carrier pilot reported while taxing to the gate at ORD; they lost tiller authority resulting in the aircraft stopping short of the stop point and off centerline. Gate agent was able to pull up the jetway.
Air carrier Captain reported possible GPS jamming near SETSI waypoint in Oman airspace.
B55 pilot reported a dual alternator failure during cruise flight. Pilot accomplished alternate gear extension procedure yet the landing gear collapsed during landing.

Maule M7-260C pilot experienced directional control loss upon landing resulting in a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft. The pilot was uninjured and exited the aircraft; coordinated with authorities; then was able to taxi the damaged aircraft to the hangar.
C172 Instructor and student reported white smoke from instrument panel resulted in an off field safe landing.
A Pilot reported GPS interference.

Small aircraft pilot reported while holding short at a taxiway; another aircraft came extremely close in proximity as it was taxiing. The other aircraft was taxiing at a fast speed and the reporter had to quickly stop while over the runway hold-short markings to avoid a collision.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a heading deviation caused by a new and unfamiliar approach procedure for LAS airport.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported high winds and turbulence during the flight; resulting in the UAS to exceed 400 feet AGL and a momentary lost link. The UAS landed without incident.
A320 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing loss of Y hydraulic system.

Flight Instructor reported while landing the aircraft made an unstable landing resulting in the aircraft departing the runway surface and damaging a runway edge light.  Aircraft was inspected and damage was negligible.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported two separate Hazmat shipments were packaged together which resulted in both being shipped together.
B-757 Captain reported aircraft being returned to service from long term storage suffered an auto throttle malfunction on climb out and subsequently learned that later flights had the same problem.
B737 MAX flight crew reported that while attempting to turn during taxiing the aircraft almost slid off the ORD Z taxiway due to the wet surface and a downhill slope.
Recreational/Hobbyist pilot was flying UAS in an area restricted by state law.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during approach in vicinity of tower obstacles.
Air Carrier First Officer reported inadequate DEN hold spot communications procedure charting.
Air carrier flight crew reported they received a low altitude alert from Tower while descending for a TCAS/RA for parallel runway traffic.
B737-800 Captain reported rotation on takeoff required approximately twice the normal elevator force; and opted to perform an air turnback.
Global 6000 Captain reported track and altitude deviations departing DCA; citing improper autoflight programming and a possible wake turbulence encounter as contributing.
Air carrier flight crew reported a collision with an object that shattered the captain's windshield. The flight diverted and landed as a precaution.
Center Controller reported an aircraft flew below the MVA by 1;300 feet near mountain side terrain.
EMB 145 flight crew reported an engine bleed leak and pressurization failure during departure resulted in return to departure airport.

Citation 680 flight crew reported while breaking out of IMC they were cleared visual approach and were above the visual profile and increased their rate of descent resulting in a Terrain GPWS caution.  The flight crew returned to the glide path and landed safely at the airport.
ERJ-145 Captain reported Baggage Smoke EICAS warning during cruise. Flight crew executed a diversion to nearest suitable airport and completed a safe overweight landing.
Flight Instructor reported during taxi another aircraft taxied directly toward them and did not stop; requiring the Flight Instructor to move off centerline in order to avoid a collision.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported setting incorrect altitude for approach. Flight crew realized mistake; corrected glidepath and landed uneventfully.
C172 pilot reported an engine power loss on takeoff during a check ride and the Designated Pilot Examiner performed an air turnback.
Flight Attendants reported engine malfunction during cruise flight. Flight diverted and aircraft underwent maintenance action after landing.
M-20 pilot reported runway excursion during landing rollout.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence on approach and a wind shear warning went off. This Captain performed a go-around and the flight landed at an alternate airport.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC requiring evasive action.
Air carrier Captain reported descending below glide path on a visual approach to SFO when situational awareness was lost.
Student Pilot reported a NMAC with traffic crossing their path on downwind leg at a non towered airport.
RV-14 pilot reported loss of all electrical power in flight resulting in landing at home airport with no ability to communicate with ATC.
Air carrier flight crew reported a landing gear air/ground fail message on short final approach and after completing a go around; returned to land at departure airport.

Instructor Pilot reported the aircraft experienced a complete electrical failure on a night time IFR training flight resulting in a temporary loss of control. They regained control and diverted to a safe landing.
PC-12 Captain reported a temporary propeller vibration during cruise while in icing conditions. The flight crew decided to divert due to degraded aircraft performance..

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported expediting preparation for landing due to a flap malfunction relayed by the captain. The Flight Attendant ran through the safety procedures with the passengers. A non-eventful landing followed.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported encountering severe wake turbulence shortly after takeoff departing ATL in trail of an A321.
Air carrier pilot reported during cruise climb they received a master warning for cabin altitude. Flight crew descended and diverted to another airport.
A Center Controller reported the Operations Manager would not comply with the Area's Supervisor request to terminate planned GPS jamming contrary to the facility SOP regarding GPS jamming.

Air carrier Captain reported a ground employee walked into the red safety zone as the aircraft was still moving. Reporter stopped the aircraft and signaled the ground employee to leave the danger area.

Air Carrier Captain reported First Officer felt ill due to odor in the cockpit. Captain determined an air return was necessary. The crew requested priority handling and executed a safe overweight landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported cargo compartment fire warning system malfunction.
Air carrier First Officer reported multiple system failures in flight. Flight crew landed uneventfully.
BE36 Pilot reported an engine failure in cruise due to fuel starvation from a poorly sealing fuel filler cap. The pilot was able to switch tanks and landed at a nearby airport.

Air carrier Captain reported a Critical Ground Conflict with an opposite direction landing aircraft at this non-towered airport. The Captain took evasive action to avoid a collision by turning onto a nearby closed runway.
Pilot reported electrical systems failure with smoke and fumes present in cruise and diverted to make a precautionary landing.

Technician reported inadvertently not reinstalling a hydraulic line after performing a maintenance action. A subsequent flight with this aircraft had an issue and a return to the airport was required.

Corporate jet flight crew reported a NMAC during approach to BUR airport with an aircraft that passed below them by approximately 300 feet. They received a TCAS RA to descend but decided not to follow that instruction and rather disengaged the autopilot and began a climb.

B767-300 flight crew reported exceeding 250 kts below 10;000 ft when their unloaded aircraft accelerated more quickly than anticipated. A wake turbulence encounter was cited as contributing to the incident.
TRACON Controller reported having an aircraft below the MVA while on vectors to landing airport.
Instructor pilot reported student having control difficulties during landing resulted in a runway excursion with no damage or injuries.
A320 Captain reported a recurring 'dirty socks' odor during descent. In addition to submitting a logbook discrepancy; the Captain enumerated the recent aircraft fume events to Maintenance.

A321 Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during pre-flight which resulting in an extensive delay and ultimately cancelled flight. Flight attendants and the Captain experienced physiological symptoms from the fumes.

A-300 First Officer reported troubleshooting a Hazmat load violation during climb as a result of a Dispatch ACARS message of a Hazmat cargo load violation. After consulting with Operations experts completed an uneventful flight.

Air carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation during approach to LAS resulting in a CFTT.  The crew mistakenly believed they had been cleared for a visual approach which contributed to the event.
Flight Instructor with student reported a near miss with a UAS while on climb out from a Class D airport.
Flight Instructor reported a left yaw during landing and stopped on the runway. Upon further inspection found the left tire deflated with no damage and was towed off runway.

A student pilot training at an uncontrolled airport reported a NMAC with another aircraft that turned left after announcing it would turn right. The student reported the same aircraft had repeatedly maneuvered near them in different directions then announced including taking the runway to takeoff after stating they would hold short.

A military instructor pilot reported a near miss while maneuvering with a wingman; within a MOA in VFR conditions with another non-authorized VFR aircraft that entered the MOA without communication with ATC. The instructor and the wingman did not receive a call from ATC and continued the sortie.

Flight Instructor with student reported a runway excursion during takeoff while performing a simulated engine failure during multi-engine training. The instructor took control as the aircraft veered off the side of the runway with no reported damage or injuries.
Tower Controller reported ARFF failure to use proper frequency complicated communications with aircraft in distress.
ERJ 145 Captain reported duel pack failure resulting in a diversion where they landed safely.
B757-200 First Officer reported an engine fire and overheat warning at cruise. The crew returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
Ground employee reported aircraft brakes not set causing aircraft to roll into tug.
Controllers reported while accepting airspace of a closing facility; an aircraft entered a higher MVA.
Gulfstream G200 captain reported engine oil pressure failure.
First Officer reported some confusion regarding their taxi clearance after landing at Mexico City because of poor signage and lighting.

A320 Captain reported the aft flight attendants and First Officer detected a fumes event that started during approach descent. The Flight Attendants described the odor as 'dirty socks' which dissipated at gate during engine shutdown.
Cessna 172 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control during landing and let the aircraft roll past the runway edge into the grass.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a lightning strike during approach vectors in IMC conditions. The flight crew requested priority handling and decided to continue to their destination airport.
C210 Pilot with Flight Instructor reported NMAC with a C172.
PC -12 pilot reported executing a missed approach after receiving an altitude alert from ATC.

Flight Instructor reported while instructing student practicing landings; ATC instructed another aircraft to make a left turn into the student and Instructor causing the Instructor to take evasive action to avoid a midair collision.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported Dangerous Goods cargo not listed on aircraft cargo Departure Staging Guide (DSTG). Error corrected prior to departure.
Pilot reported a cylinder failure on take off; resulting in a slow climb rate and sluggish acceleration. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.

General aviation Flight Instructor pilot with student reported a near miss with another aircraft entering the traffic pattern. The instructor took control of the aircraft; turned to avoid the conflict; then returned for a landing.

Air Carrier First Officer reported confusion with ATC during the approach clearance phase and a high and fast unstable approach. This resulted in the crew descending below the GS altitude and a 'Too Low' warning from ATC.
Light twin jet aircraft flight crew reported they received a low altitude alert from SCT TRACON on descent into VNY when the autoflight system apparently overshot their cleared altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer saw a UAS near the aircraft while on final approach to a Class B airport.
C172 pilot reported inadvertently tuning the CTAF frequency of another airport and during final approach had a near mid-air collision as a result of not communicating on the correct frequency.
Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC during approach with an aerobatic aircraft that was maneuvering over the airport.

B767 pilot reported extreme storm conditions during arrival and final approach caused loss of control and significant airspeed deviations. The flight crew performed three missed approaches and ultimately diverted due to fuel limitations.

Instructor pilot reported an NMAC with a fast-approaching aircraft in the vicinity of MTV airport that was disregarding aviation right of way rules. The instructor used an evasive maneuver to escape a collision.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported avoiding an inadvertent escape slide deployment when gate agent opened an armed aircraft door.
Technician reported installing a nose tire and wheel assembly with the chine inboard instead of outboard.
Bonanza pilot reported an NMAC while entering the traffic pattern after another aircraft climbed from directly under the reporter's aircraft requiring an extended downwind to avoid a collision.
TRACON Controller reported they may have vectored a flight of F16 aircraft for an ILS below the minimum altitude for the area.
A Boeing 737 pilot reported a failure of the nose-down pitch trim; resulting in a return to the departure airport.

Air carrier B777 First Officer reported a Flight Attendant notified the Captain of a passenger's battery that was smoking prior to pushback. The Captain had the smoking battery along with other spare batteries removed prior to cabin door closure.
PA-24 pilot reported the landing gear collapsed upon touchdown resulting in a gear up landing.

Student pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft; while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The student pilot indicated the other aircraft did not use proper traffic pattern entry procedures causing the student pilot to maneuver away to evade the other aircraft.

ZKC Air Traffic Controllers reported working an aircraft that was attempting to descend below icing conditions; the controllers were unable to communicate with the aircraft resulting in the aircraft flying towards terrain.  ATC established communication with the aircraft and assigned them a safe altitude to climb to and the aircraft proceeded to there destination.
Pilot departing PABV airport reported an NMAC with a helicopter who was apparently not communicating on CTAF.

Inspector reported two fan blade bumpers were left out after reinstalling the left hand engine fan blades after routine maintenance. This caused excessive vibration on the left hand engine on climb out.

Flight Instructor reported an airborne conflict occurred during cruise flight with another aircraft. Reportedly; the other aircraft was not in communication with ATC or flying a standard altitude for their direction of flight.
A321 pilot reported an APU auto shut down at cruise. The #1 IDG was on MEL so the flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
C172 Flight Instructor reported while flying to a practice area; ATC advised them of traffic resulting in both aircraft taking evasive action to avoid a collision.
C680 pilot reported the right windshield heat failed followed by the right engine shutting down during cruise. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.

Flight Crew reported a dual engine over temperature condition on take off. While reducing power to eliminate the over temperature condition; a lack of climb performance was noticed. The landing gear was still extended so the Captain selected gear up at 285 knots; over speed for gear retraction.
Pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC while on a visual approach.
Tower CIC reported a runway incursion about to happen to the Local Controller who froze. The landing aircraft went around when pilot observed the possible aircraft conflict.

Air carrier flight crew reported the axle on the right main landing gear failed as the aircraft was taxiing onto the runway for takeoff. One nose wheel tire separated from the landing gear and the aircraft was towed back to the gate.
A Tower Local Controller reported non standard phraseology used by the Ground Controller led an aircraft to taxi onto a runway at the same time another aircraft was departing opposite direction.

A319 Captain reported Maintenance failed to follow procedures with applying an MEL that involved a potentially tripped circuit breaker. The Captain requested a review of the MEL procedures with Maintenance prior to accepting the aircraft for service.
