An air carrier Captain reported receiving Terrain Alerts on downwind on two approaches even though they were at altitudes above terrain and as assigned from ATC.  The alerts caused an unstable approach requiring a go around.

Air Carrier Captain reported an intermittent fume event throughout all the phases of the flight. Initially noticed the 'musty' odor during APU start which dissipated and randomly returned for the remainder of the flight. Flight crew reported that the odor was worse with Pack One on and elected to land with Pack One off.
Pilots reported the Runway Holding Position Markings at Runway 26R and Taxiway D at ATL airport are dirty and faded; making them difficult to identify.
C172 pilot reported a loud banging noise and loss of RPM while departing and returned to the airport.
B737 crew reported Hydraulic 'A' system Quantity Low Pressure Lights and partial quantity loss after takeoff.  Crew returned to field for normal landing.
Air taxi Captain reported a brake malfunction while maneuvering to park at the gate; resulting in the aircraft wingtip striking a ground vehicle near the gate.

Single pilot reported clearing R6001-A and entering restricted area R6001-B without clearance and immediately exited upon realization. The reporter noted that the avionics display and unfamiliarity with the airspace contributed to the situation.
Air carrier Captain reported a safety concern regarding the condition of Runway 7 at MKJS airport; which resulted in hard jolts for the aircraft during takeoff.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported an engine malfunction during cruise and returned to departure airport for a safe landing.

Fractional Captain reported instrument and visual guidance was not available to Runway 16R at night due to frequent airport construction; which led to the flight crew to use the guided visual to Runway 34L. The reporter also stated that vectors from ATC led to an unstable; excessively steep approach path and combined with the fuel minimums; there were many safety concerns.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported the kit missing a component while doing the preflight check and informed the other Flight Attendant and the Captain.
DH8 pilot reported an engine fire warning in flight after the aircraft had been in storage. The crew shut down the affected engine and returned to the departure airport.
Pilot reported gear-up landing. The gear switch was down; but gear actuator circuit breaker was found to be open.
Air carrier Captain reported a taxiway incursion at DCA after being unable to see the taxiway centerline at night due to poor markings and a buildup of rubber on the runway.
B787 First Officer reported experiencing mountain wave turbulence during cruise; resulting airspeed and altitude deviations.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported the undocumented transport of DG.  Unmanifested shipment discovered during destination unloading.

Cessna 152 instructor pilot reported a runway excursion during a training flight. Upon touchdown the student veered off the runway; the instructor took control of the aircraft; returned onto the runway; then taxied to parking.
ORD Tower Controller reported the aircraft ADSE data tags frequently drop off their display while aircraft taxi across an area of the airport.
CRJ900 Captain reported a NMAC during climb. Crew followed RA alert and descended. Flight then continued to the destination.
Helicopter pilot reported loss of power and engine roughness during final approach. Pilot landed safely and reported it was determined later the engine was no longer serviceable.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on an instrument approach to a non towered airport initiated a go around due to another IFR aircraft holding for release on the approach end of the runway.
Air Carrier First Officer reported there was a slight perceived bleed through using the PAPI of white on the red light and red on the white lights; meaning the colors washed together.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPS interference on approach to PEK airport.
G-IV flight crew reported receiving a right engine hot EICAS message on takeoff roll. Aborted takeoff and returned to ramp.
Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported a NMAC and RA after takeoff.

ERJ-170 flight crew reported the inability to maintain runway center line during the takeoff roll. The crew rejected the takeoff and returned to the gate where maintenance discovered a faulty sensor on the steering tiller.
Air carrier flight crew reported a cabin pressurization system malfunction during initial climb. The flight returned to the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported performing a go around on short final approach as another aircraft on same runway began their takeoff roll.
Experimental aircraft pilot reported an engine coolant leak and fearing an immediate engine failure; elected to land on a taxiway.

B777 Captain reported uncommanded aircraft movement after push back when the ground crew requested the Captain to release the parking brake to assist in removing the tow bar. The aircraft rolled forward and a loud noise was heard from the nose gear; the aircraft returned to the gate for a maintenance inspection.

A Center Area Supervisor and Operations Manager reported the adjacent Center refused to take hand offs and advised their sectors to hold aircraft without any advance notice. The reporters stated this caused their airspace to become unsafe due to losses of separation and airspace violations.

C525 pilot reported a ground conflict during takeoff roll with a helicopter departing on a parallel taxiway resulting in a rejected takeoff.  The helicopter pilot was reportedly not making any radio calls.

Air carrier Captain reported that after arrival at the gate in a foreign country; Ground personnel in full hazmat suits boarded and sprayed unknown chemicals onto the cargo and galley areas. Flight crew experienced physiological reactions and were concerned over exposure to the chemicals.
Air carrier flight crew reported as they were exiting the runway; another aircraft under tow turned onto the same taxiway. The flight crew immediately stopped to avoid a collision.
Cirrus Vision SJ50 Captain reported multiple systems failures during descent to landing. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported gusts on landing resulted in a runway excursion.
Light Transport aircraft First Officer reported they entered a runway while another aircraft was on final to the same runway.  The other aircraft executed a go-around.
SR22 Flight Instructor reported electrical system malfunction inflight.
Dispatcher reported a flight crew did not respond to multiple messages providing the crew with alternate and divert airports due to deteriorating weather conditions in the arrival area.

RJ-50 Captain reported Electronic Flight Instrument System cooling warnings and subsequent loss of both Primary Flight Displays in climb and later in cruise.  Crew continued to destination in accordance with checklist guidance and landed normally.
M20T Pilot flying reported landing gear collapse on landing rollout.
Student pilot reported during cruise they noticed the fuel flow indication was fluctuating and decided to return to the airport.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft being vectored for an approach descended below their assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

B737 Captain reported while enroute to their destination coordinating the need for electrical power before shutting down at the gate; this information was not received by the ramp personnel resulting in an unsafe ramp operation.

A300 Captain reported Hydraulic Quantity Low Message in cruise. The Flight Crew requested priority handling and manually extended the landing gear on final approach. A precautionary landing was made at destination airport.
SR20 flight instructor reported on upwind the engine began to run erratic resulted in an expeditious return to landing.

Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer overshot final approach after the aircraft had gotten into an undesired energy state; resulting in a go around. Captain stated they should have intervened sooner to prevent the situation from happening.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a TCAS RA that was determined to have been a phantom RA. While responding to the phantom RA; the flight crew descended toward an aircraft resulting in an airborne conflict requiring ATC to deconflict the aircraft.
Air carrier pilot reported poor runway hold line markings and lighting resulted in confusion over runway hold short instructions at MDSD/SDQ Airport.
B737 flight crew reported intermittent problems with the APU Overheat/Detection and Fire Warning systems. An air turn back and precautionary landing were made at the departure airport.

Air Carrier Captain reported strong odors; a PACK 1 FAIL issue and vibrations during initial descent. The flight crew decided to divert to nearest suitable airport for an expedited descent and successful landing.

ATC reported aircraft descended below assigned altitude prior to being established on the approach procedure resulting in flying towards terrain.  The aircraft rejoined the approach procedure and landed safely at the airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported a burning odor and smoke throughout the aircraft during arrival taxi. Flight crew communicated with Ground control and executed an orderly evacuation. Airport Rescue and Firefighting arrived and provided required support and all passengers and crew were safe.
A320 Captain reported engine fire detected after parking at the gate. Fire was quickly extinguished with no injuries.
Light aircraft pilot reported flying too close to tower obstacles in an effort to stay clear of restricted airspace.
Phenom 300 pilot reported a severe turbulence encounter resulted in a 2;000 ft loss of altitude.
Aircraft maintenance technician reported uncommanded movement of aircraft while parked at the gate.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unstabilized approach at CHA and noted a discrepancy between the Final Approach Fix altitude as shown on the Lido chart and in the FMS.
Controller reported an airspace violation due to an aircraft that had a pressurization problem.

A TRACON Controller and Tower Controller reported an aircraft cleared for a Visual Approach turned toward the wrong airport. Prior to the deviation the TRACON Controller had restricted this aircraft's altitude and vectored it through the Final Approach course to avoid unidentified VFR traffic highlighting the need for restrictive airspace around busy commercial airports.
Pilot reported misconfiguring his radio which resulted in unheard traffic calls and a ground conflict with a departing aircraft.
C172 Pilot reported NMAC with another aircraft in the traffic pattern. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Piper 24 pilot reported complete electrical failure after takeoff resulting in an immediate return to the airport.
A Cessna Stationair Pilot reported excessive vibration on initial climb and return to departure airport.
PA28 Flight Instructor and Student reported a loss of engine power and the inability to maintain altitude required a return to the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the Dangerous Goods (DG) documents were not all delivered and the documents that were given were incorrect.
Student pilot flying PA 28 aircraft reported pilot induced oscillations during landing procedure.

King Air Captain reported a yaw on takeoff along with a loss of power on the associated engine. The flight crew continued the climb and performed the Company SOP procedures. The flight crew discovered the Left Engine Friction Lock had loosened due to vibration and would not hold the throttle in place. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
A320 flight crew reported loss of the green hydraulic system resulted in a diversion and precautionary landing.
Air charter First Officer reported receiving a terrain warning while being vectored for approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near midair collision with a drone while they were on final approach. No evasive action was taken and flight proceeded without issue.

PA-28 Pilot reported they flew through a Class D airspace enroute to their destination after assuming the Tower was closed when they received no response to their radio calls. After landing they learned the Tower was not closed and had been responding to their calls.
A319 Captain reported a fume event during preflight that resulted in physiological symptoms in flight and cabin crew members.
Maintenance Technician reported an aircraft with extensive damage to nose was approved for flight before repair.

C-182 Single Pilot reported an NMAC event during landing with another single engine aircraft in which both pilots took evasive action. C-182 Pilot was later informed they were on wrong radio frequency.
First Officer reported an air turn back after smoke was detected during climb out.
B737 First Officer reported receiving a terrain and sink rate GPWS warnings which led to an unstable approach followed by a long landing.
B777 flight crew reported an N2 over-speed on the left engine while on initial approach.

CRJ-900 air carrier crew reported receiving a stick shaker when retracting the flaps and initiating a turn during the climb in turbulence. The pilot flying reduced pitch and the flight continued the flight.

First Officer reported observing the engine number two torque rolling back from MTOP (Maximum Take Off Power) setting just after V1. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at the departure airport.

Air carrier ground personnel reported receiving a notice that a tire shipment was improperly shipped by not following the Hazmat shipping protocol. The reporter submitted a report acknowledging the oversight.
Safety pilot reported while in traffic pattern; additional traffic on entry reported climb to avoid collision.

Air Carrier Captain reported during final descent being notified by F/A 4 that F/A 3 had lost consciousness temporarily due to fumes in the aft cabin. Upon gate arrival Captain submitted a fume report to Maintenance and Flight Attendant crew received Medical evaluation.
Air carrier pilot reported GPWS alert during final approach.
Pilot reported an opposite direction arrival and the reporters aircraft where in a conflict on departure.
Air carrier flight crew reported they were told that DG cargo was removed from the aircraft prior to gate departure. At destination arrival the flight crew discovered the DG was still in cargo.

A119 pilot reported a NMAC at a non-towered airport. The reporter stated that a non-communicating aircraft took off directly in the pilot's path; and the pilot executed evasive action to avoid collision.
PA-28 Pilot reported being distracted and taxiing the aircraft over the runway hold short lines.

SCT TRACON Controller reported they did not detect the wrong aircraft read a descent clearance back to them due to similar sounding call signs. The aircraft descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Air carrier Ramp Lead personnel reported the lack of adequate ramp load staffing and poor ramp communication contributed to an inaccurate departure Weight & Balance data. Additionally; a missing DG parcel location in the cargo compartment was not resolved.
Bonanza 35 pilot reported the nose gear collapsed during landing resulting in a propeller strike.
A319 First Officer reported engine throttle control malfunction.

Single pilot Captain reported the designated left hand traffic pattern direction for Runway 19 and 23 at the non-towered airport E38 results in aircraft flying too low over structures and populated areas.

B737-800 Captain reported a 30 knot jump and subsequent decrease on his airspeed indicator at rotation speed during takeoff.  Indications returned to normal after take-off and remained normal for duration of flight.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported that on descent the aircraft; apparently on its own; selected a slow autoflight speed.

PC-12 pilot conducting a checkride reported a NMAC while executing a published missed approach. They received  a TCAS/TA for an undentified VFR aircraft; leveled off; then had to respond to a TCAS/RA for another unidentified VFR aircraft.
A Tower Local Controller reported a VFR aircraft overshot the final approach course into confliction with VFR traffic on the parallel runway resulting in a NMAC.

Air carrier flight crew reported while conducting an Autoland the aircraft did not begin its descent. While correcting they became unstable and received a Glideslope Alert and Altitude Alert from the Tower due to descending below the Glideslope.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending below a required altitude on approach and not recognizing it until they were advised by Air Traffic Control.

Captain reported full Left Pedal was required to maintain runway center line during take off. After take off; when the gear handle was raised to up; the Landing Gear would not retract. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Captain reported a runway incursion during taxi out at PWM; citing airport signage as contributing.
Air carrier pilot reported ADS-B and GPS jamming in the area led to faults that remained to destination and required Maintenance to reset GPS receivers.
CRJ-900 Captain reported an unstabilized approach and an obstacle alarm on the go-around.
Captain reported Flight Attendant notification after gate arrival of 'dirty socks' odor throughout the cabin.
Air carrier flight crew reported they observed excessive engine temps during takeoff and aborted and taxied back to the gate.

Air carrier Captain reported GPS interference during initial descent and a loss of GPS position in international airspace. The Captain advised ATC they were unable to accept the published arrival and requested vectors to a ground based ILS approach and completed the flight.
Air Carrier pilot reported the push back crew did not follow procedures and pushed them into another parked aircraft.
A319 pilot flying reported receiving an Engine #1 Fire Loop fault followed by a decreasing Engine #1 Oil Quantity. The affected engine was shut down and the flight crew performed an air turnback.

PC12 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control during climb after entering cumulonimbus clouds and IMC conditions prior to receiving IFR clearance. Pilot encountered extreme turbulence and moderate icing conditions after obtaining IFR clearance and continued to destination after exiting the weather.

Dassault Falcon air taxi crew reported an airspeed indicator failure and multiple Crew Alerting System messages during the takeoff roll resulting in the Captain rejecting the takeoff. The First Officer discovered one pitot cover was not removed during the preflight; the pitot cover was removed and the aircraft departed.

A321 Captain reported being notified of a tailpipe fire on one engine at the gate by the First Officer and a maintenance technician. Airport crash fire rescue extinguished the fire; contained to the engine nacelle.
Air carrier flight crew reported an electrical system malfunction accompanied by cabin fumes. Air crew completed the QRH procedure and executed a safe return to departure airport.
Pilot reported there needs to be a NOTAM stating there is a new CTAF frequency at DKX; Knoxville Downtown Island Airport; Knoxville; Tennessee. CTAF 122.95 is temporarily in use; in lieu of 126.6.
CE-560 Captain reported cabin pressurization issues in cruise. Flight crew continued to destination and landed.
B767 First Officer reported fuel imbalance and possible fuel leak after departure. Flight returned to departure airport.
Technician reported during routine service he discovered an emergency exit marker was not installed and the area covered by panlam. The aircraft was removed from service for repairs.
A Flight Attendant reported one engine was shut down due to a fuel leak and diversion to nearby airport.
C172 pilot reported terrain alert while in cruise.
A321 flight crew reported performing an inflight shutdown of #2 engine due to low oil quantity. Flight crew completed a diversion and landing.

TPA TRACON Controller reported a conflict between an aircraft on final approach Runway 36 at PIE airport and a VFR aircraft departing from SPG airport.  The Controller stated VFR aircraft need to be restricted to an altitude that keeps them away from aircraft arriving Runway 36 at PIE.

Student pilot reported that while attempting to take off in between departing and landing aircraft; the landing aircraft had to perform a go-around to avoid a possible collision with the student pilot.
B787 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate airport after experiencing multiple EICAS error messages.

Flight Instructor reported the published airport operating hours are incorrect for AVX; Catalina Airport. After landing; another pilot informed the instructor the airport had closed at 1700 local time. The published time; as open 1600-0100Z; converts to 1800 local time in the month of June.
EMB ERJ 190/195 Captain reported multiple systems failures during cruise. The flight crew elected to make a precautionary landing at a diversion airport.
Flight Attendants reported the A Flight Attendant deployed the escape slide at the gate while attempting to close the main cabin door.
Aircraft technician reported incorrect serial number part was found installed during PW4000 engine disassembly.
B737-900 flight crew reported a stabilizer out of trim annunciation during cruise required a consult with company maintenance and continued onto the destination.
Pilot reported communication issues caused a Near Mid Air Collision at a non-towered airport.
B777 flight crew reported a cargo door EICAS warning during the takeoff roll. Flight continued takeoff and returned to departure airport.
Flight Attendant reported an odor that smelled like dirty socks mid cabin.  Plane returned to gate and passengers were deplaned.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the RDG airport area.

Fractional First Officer reported a conflict with another aircraft landing while back taxiing on the same runway at a non-towered airport. The flight crew immediately stopped the aircraft; then cleared the runway after the conflict.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with a helicopter which flew through the airport traffic pattern. Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.

Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization. Pilot stated they were not aware that the airspace had changed from uncontrolled to controlled while the Tower was operating.
Air carrier pilots reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during approach.

Corporate pilot reported they were listening to; but not transmitting on; the correct MTJ CTAF frequency and flew too close to another arriving aircraft. The reporter stated a nearby airport using the same frequency causing radio congestion and interference also contributed to their confusion.
B767-400 flight crew reported hydraulic system malfunction/failure inflight. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
ERJ 175 Captain reported incorrect MEL maintenance write up for vibration resulted in return to gate and refused aircraft.
Maintenance Controller reported misapplying an MEL to the Cargo Fire Detection/Suppression system and the aircraft flew in a non airworthy status.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported engine power loss due to carburetor icing.
Air carrier Captain reported while lined up on taxiway; they had a ground conflict with another aircraft on the same taxiway.
Air carrier Captain reported failing to properly configure for the approach due to distraction and fatigue.

Airbus Flight Attendant crew reported a fume event during descent which lasted until gate arrival. Captain was notified and medical assistance requested. After much delay Flight Attendants were transported to local hospital for evaluation.
Technician reported B737 MAX nose tire and wheel assemblies were installed on a B737-900 aircraft arriving at their station.

Tower Controller reported an aircraft on departure had an engine failure and returned for a landing. The situation was further complicated by the need to coordinate with numerous people and the pilots repeated replies to stand by.
B737 First Officer reported an airspeed disagree during two takeoff attempts. Flight crew rejected during both takeoff rolls and returned to gate for maintenance.
Flight Crew reported a return to departure airport due to the loss of engine temperature indication on engine number 1.
MD-11 flight crew reported an Autopilot Disconnect Warning in cruise. This was followed by numerous Airspeed Warnings. The flight crew ran the QRH and elected to continue to destination airport.
J3 Cub flight instructor with student reported a runway excursion on landing.

Captain reported an unsafe landing gear indication after gear retraction on takeoff. The flight crew was unable to extinguish the unsafe indication after troubleshooting and running the check lists and QRH. The crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at the departure airport.
Private Pilot made off airport landing due to engine fuel starvation.
G200 Captain reported left windshield shattered in cruise flight. Flight crew descended and landed safely.
A319 Captain reported a rapid descent after cabin pressure became uncontrollable. The flight crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
Technician reported IDG oil level was low and after servicing and a 5 minute idle run; no leak was noted. Technician also reported other instances of this issue.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the vicinity of 2R2 airport.
UAS had a lost link occur during flight. Pilot regained link with UAS and used manual control to recover the UAS.
B-737 First Officer reported a partial loss of one hydraulic system during cruise flight. The flight landed and received a tow to the gate.
A319 First Officer reported failure of the rudder pedal adjustment locking pin during landing. The Captain assumed the controls and a normal landing was performed.
Air carrier flight crew reported the auto pilot was not programmed correctly for the approach and the aircraft descended below the Minimum Safe Altitude.
NCT TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict which turned into an aircraft being below the MVA and having an aircraft equipment problem.
Fractional Company pilot reported they followed the marshaller's parking instructions and upon exiting the aircraft discovered their wingtip had come in contact with an awning support.
Air carrier flight crew reported confusion and disagreement with Maintenance personnel over procedures required to perform a landing gear down maintenance ferry flight.
Air carrier Captain reported during arrival landing gear warnings and intermittent radar altimeter false warnings were received. Post flight maintenance checks did not find any system malfunctions.
Premier 1 First Officer reported loss on multiple systems due to an electrical failure. The flight crew requested priority handling and descended to make a precautionary landing.
Maintenance Inspector reported a connector was mistakenly removed during the replacement of an engine harness. Reporter stated time pressure was a contributing factor.
Air carrier flight crew reported taxiing past the MYNN runway hold-short line due to fatigue; task saturation; and airport signage and marking issues.

Air Carrier First Officer reported the taxi instructions are confusing in HNL when proceeding to Runway 8L. The FO states there is more than one way to get to 8L; during this construction period; using the current taxiway markings. The FO recommends some taxiways should be renamed to avoid confusion.
Air carrier Captain reported ADS-B and GPS issues during arrival to DEN airport.  Reportedly; ATC advised the crew of 5G events going on in the area.
B757 flight crew reported trailing edge flap indication of asymmetrical condition resulted priority handling to a safe landing.
Controller reported a Developmental issued a vector into a higher MVA area for traffic avoidance resulting in a CFTT event.

Small aircraft pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft when entering the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport in VMC conditions. The pilot immediately turned left to avoid the other aircraft and then returned to enter the traffic pattern; only to encounter the other aircraft again. The reporter stated they did not hear any position calls from the other aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported ground gate return policy violates FARs in regards to Captain's Pilot in Command authority.

Air carrier Captain and First Officer reported a fuel valve EICAS message appeared after shutdown at the gate and smoke was visible coming from the engine tail cone. The crew pulled the fire handle and the smoke stopped.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported dual engine failure inflight. Flight crew glided to a grass strip and landed.

R44 pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid another helicopter and after landing; the other pilot refused to communicate to the reporting pilot and was inaccurate with their statements to officials.
B737-700 Captain reported the brakes were not set following push-back and tug disconnect resulting in momentary loss of aircraft control.
B767-300 flight crew reported an engine fire in cruise. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.

Air carrier Captain reported Hazmat cargo was incorrectly loaded due to an aircraft MEL restriction. The Captain had the Hazmat cargo reloaded in compliance with the MEL restrictions prior to gate departure.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported they received an engine overheat and engine fire warning on initial climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport.
C172 Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with a PA-44 in the landing pattern.
Air Carrier flight crew flying B777 aircraft reported jammed Nose Gear Tiller on taxi out.
First Officer reported descending below approach altitude and was advised by ATC to climb and maintain minimum altitude for terrain clearance; which they did and continued the approach to landing.
Pilot taking off from a non-towered airport reported they observed another aircraft taxiing across the runway just as they were rotating. Pilot reported they flew over the aircraft.
C525 flight crew reported loss of oil pressure on #1 engine and elected to perform an inflight shut down and diverted to the nearest suitable airport.
GA instructor with student pilot reported an NMAC during takeoff from a XNX non-towered airport requiring evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

PA-28 flight crew reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with an aircraft that turned base in front of them and then reported that while still on the runway the same aircraft landed behind them.
Pilot reported ELAC failure at cruise. The Flight Crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
An air carrier First Officer reported the ILS was not loaded into the FMS properly resulting in an unstable approach below the glidepath and a low altitude alert from ATC.
TBM 940 pilot reported hearing a loud whine noise during cruise climb. During return to departure airport the noise dissipated.
B737 First Officer reported momentary loss of aircraft control while taxiing in light rain conditions.  First Officer stated taxi speed could have been too high for the conditions.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC with a small GA aircraft which flew over the top of them just as they landed at a non towered airport.

Air taxi First Officer reported being vectored for an approach by TRACON descended below their assigned altitude to avoid VFR traffic which ATC had advised them of but did not issue appropriate instructions to avoid the traffic. ATC advised the pilot they descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported TCAS RA while on descent for landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported another air carrier aircraft taxied very close to their wing at a high rate of speed on the ramp at CMH airport.
Tow team lead reported after parking aircraft at the gate; the aircraft had moved and contacted a pickup truck causing minor damage to the #1 engine cowling.
C-150 pilot reported engine failure during initial climb. Pilot made an immediate return to departure airport and landed.
EMB-175 Captain reported an unstable approach and bounced landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported a runway incursion after failing to see the location of a recently moved hold short line.

Cargo tractor driver reported while approaching the rear of an aircraft at the gate there were no ground crews blocking the road behind the aircraft during an engine run and no communication from crews at the nose of the aircraft.
Bonanza 35 pilot reported a gear up landing occurred as a result of the circuit breaker having tripped and the pilot not checking that gear was down before landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported during initial approach with autopilot engaged they got a terrain warning prior to the final approach fix. They climbed to 2;300 feet and no further warnings occurred.
C182 pilot reported a NMAC during left downwind on final approach.
Air carrier ground personnel reported an issue with the final loading form.
Part 91 pilot reported that there was a ground conflict with another aircraft that crossed the runway while climbing after takeoff.
B757 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence while climbing to cruise altitude; and later learned that two flight attendants had sustained minor injuries during the event.
Champion Citabria flight Instructor reported inadvertent takeoff while practicing high speed taxi runs with student.
A320 Captain reported refusing the aircraft due to a potable water leak in the forward galley that was spraying into the aircraft on take off roll.
Ground reporter reported ground conflict when towing aircraft.
First Officer reported a generator drive failed after take off. The flight crew returned to departure airport after conducting a fuel dump.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a fumes event during cruise which was described as a chemical odor and localized to the forward galley area. Symptoms experienced were dizziness; disorientation and faintness and progressed to include tremors. Aircraft was met by medics and was transported to local hospital for further treatment.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert by ATC while on a visual approach clearance resulted in a CFTT event.

Air carrier pilot not flying reported confusion after being cleared direct DRAKE" as the controller did not mention whether it was the DRK VOR or DRAKE intersection; both of which were located in the same general area."
Tower Local Controller reported a student pilot reported a NMAC with a banner tow aircraft.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported an inaccurate Dangerous Goods cargo load due to late connecting bags and aircraft mechanical issue. Dangerous Goods cargo issues were corrected prior to departure.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low-altitude warning during base leg of a visual approach to AFW airport.

Captain reported communications problems caused the aircraft to begin rolling after the tow bar and tug were disconnected prior to parking brake being set. The Captain used normal brakes to safely stop the aircraft and used visual signals to reestablish communications.
AC50 pilot reported a runway excursion resulted during landing roll out due to runway icing.
B747 Captain reported a malfunction with the main cabin door during climb. The aircraft diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported using new UAS software and failing to obtain LAANC authorization prior to flying.
Commercial Part 107 operator inadvertently flew UAS in controlled airspace.
Cessna 140 Flight Instructor and student reported a loss of aircraft control during landing resulting in a nose over and propeller strike.
Flight crew reported an air start cart supply hose was ingested by the starting engine while at the gate.
C172 pilot reported a complete loss of aileron control that resulted in a forced landing.
Air carrier Captain reported a fume event during preflight described as 'wet socks' prompting the Captain to turn off APU Bleeds and request maintenance. Flight crew exited the aircraft.

A320 Captain reported an intermittent fumes event initially during boarding which dissipated and returned during arrival taxi. Captain requested Maintenance and submitted a logbook entry as well as a completed company fume report.

Pilot reported two separate conflicts with aircraft while conducting pattern work in a crowded traffic pattern at the CDC airport. Busy general aviation traffic; flight training; helicopter operations; and regional airline traffic contributed to frequency congestion and created a difficult environment for safe operations.

P2006T Flight Instructor reported the engine would not start back up after shutting down the engine for flight training. After running through the checklist multiple times and consulting the Assistant Chief and Maintenance; the pilot performed an air turnback.

Caravan 208B pilot on a straight in approach reported a NMAC with an aircraft turning final from a base leg. The reporter stated ATC was not providing sufficient traffic information to aircraft in the pattern.
A321 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into ORD; leading to a speed reduction that prompted comments from ATC.
Air carrier flight crew reported a fume event described as 'Dirty socks' during preflight. After Maintenance performed checks; the aircraft was removed from service.
Pilot reported flying towards an obstacle while in IMC for training and was vectored by ATC back to their home airport.
Boeing 747-800 flight crew reported a gradual loss of cabin pressurization; resulting in decision to descend and return to the departure airport.

B737 MAX flight crew reported the aircraft abruptly pitched down during flap extension while on a visual approach with automation engaged. Pilot flying disconnected automation and executed an uneventful landing.
Pilot flying reported making a wrong turn during taxi due to fatigue and confusion.

A319 pilot reported being distracted by a brake fault ECAM during parking that interrupted his normal shutdown flow. Pilot reported no injuries or damage to the aircraft although the plane rolled forward approximately 10 ft.
Cessna 172 instructor pilot reported taking evasive action after a near mid air collision with another aircraft.
Hawker 800 pilot reported receiving an altitude alert from ATC during the approach although the flight crew reportedly was crossing the fix at the correct altitude.
Pilot reported a runway excursion caused by gusting winds.

TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation occurred and one of the aircraft descended below the minimum altitude for the sector. Controller stated they had two pilot deviations shortly before this and the event caused added stress.
B737 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence while on short final to Runway 28L from a preceding B777 that was landing on the parallel Runway 28R at SFO.
Commercial Captain reported conducting a go around on short final due to departing traffic still on the runway which resulted from ATC's inadequate traffic separation.
A321 pilot reported landing safely after noting a steady drop in oil quantity in #1 engine.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported after push back the brakes were not set; the tow bar was disconnected and the jet began to roll forward. Captain applied brakes to stop aircraft.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported inadvertently flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Air Carrier Dispatcher and a Captain reported the flight departed with hazmat documentation errors and inaccurate data.
Cessna 206 pilot reported an engine malfunction during descent. The pilot maneuvered towards an alternate airport. The engine failed on landing rollout and aircraft towed to the ramp.

Air Carrier Ramp Crew Lead reported being notified notified of a cargo priority package overfly. Priority package was inadvertently left onboard primarily because package was improperly packaged and missing Hazmat labeled.
Flight Instructor reported a runway excursion occurred during a training flight when the student attempted to take off again after landing.

Technician/Inspector reported that a required document package was completed prior to the aircraft's entry into service as required by FARs; making the aircraft non airworthy and not ready for service.
Corporate Captain reported an altitude deviation during approach and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Air carrier flight crew reported a 115 VAC BUS OFF Message displayed during takeoff roll at near 80 knots resulting in a rejected takeoff.

Cessna 180 pilot reported a violent yaw during takeoff; due to rapidly changing wind velocity and direction; which resulted in a ground loop of the tailwheel aircraft. The pilot rejected the takeoff and the aircraft had minor wingtip damage with no injuries.
A UAS pilot reported an airspace violation.

Air Carrier Captain reported a rejected takeoff was performed when the runway status lights for Runway 15R at IAH Airport illuminated red. Tower Controller informed the flight crew the lights were not turned on by ATC and they frequently malfunction to the on position.

DCA Tower Controller reported a previously charted hot spot was omitted from the latest airport diagram and may have been a factor in an aircraft on an instrument approach approaching the wrong runway in marginal weather.
Embraer flight crew reported engine oil temperature in the red zone. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
ERJ-170 crew reported a loss of hydraulic pressure when extending landing gear during approach.  Crew diverted to an airport with a longer runway and experienced a TCAS TA during the diversion.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported an engine malfunction during climb on a training flight. The instructor maneuvered to land on the opposite direction runway.
F900 Captain reported critical hydraulic failure inflight resulted in a divert and landing completed after manual gear extension.

Air carrier ground personnel reported two aircraft made contact in the parking area. Aircraft Y had begun to move despite prior agreement to wait for Aircraft X to move first due to the positioning of the aircraft.
Pilot reported taking evasive action during landing when another aircraft attempted to land on the opposing runway.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing GPS spoofing upon entering the Cairo FIR.

E145 flight crew reported receiving a terrain alert while descending on a visual approach as the descent rate was high in an area with elevated terrain. After leveling off the aircraft with no further alerts; the flight continued with the visual approach.

Ramp agent reported a safety concern where another ramp agent operated ground equipment in the gate area; under an operating engine. The ramp agent highlighted inconsistent procedures among ramp personnel.
CRJ 200 Captain reported fluctuating oil pressure on the left engine caused an in flight shut down. The flight crew ran the appropriate checklists and continued to the destination airport.
Student pilot flying a C-150 aircraft reported alternator malfunction in flight.

B767 flight crew reported a LE flap asymmetry EICAS message on approach. The flight crew executed a go-around; then received vectors to reenter the approach and make a precautionary landing at destination airport.
Mooney pilot reported on departure experiencing a strange shimmy from the aircraft and decided to return to the airport.  A gear-up landing occurred although gear lights indicated gear was extended.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a NMAC occurred when their student turned in front of another aircraft in the traffic pattern. Flight instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.

AA-5 pilot reported experiencing engine issues shortly after takeoff; including failure and re-start; which resulted in the pilot returning to the departure airport where they landed safely. Post-flight inspection revealed water in the fuel system.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying a UAS within a TFR without authorization.

Ramp Crew Chief reported communications issues and failure of ATC to coordinate simultaneous push backs on adjacent gates led to a sudden stop to prevent a collision between aircraft. The Ramp Crew Chiefs pulled aircraft back and re started the push backs with closer coordination and the aircraft departed.
Small transport pilot reported the electronic airport chart showed a high speed taxiway exit that did not exist.
A tail wheel pilot reported loss of control during takeoff due to wind gusts resulted in a runway excursion and minor damage.
B777 Captain reported they had to stop suddenly while taxiing to avoid a vehicle on the taxiway which they eventually followed to parking.
Gate agent operating jetway reported it contacted the airplane engine while being moved into position.
Air carrier flight crew saw two UAS while on final approach to landing and reported the sighting to ATC.
A flight instructor reported an NMAC on final approach with an aircraft that had reported them in sight to the Tower.
Flight Crew reported confusion over MEL procedures led to a Cabin Altitude Master Warning. The Flight Crew performed an expedited descent and air turn back to make a precautionary landing.
Flight Attendant reported confusion relating to LEO's and FAM's onboard an aircraft and if they should know about each other.
8GCBC Pilot reported failure to read NOTAM and flew through an area of skydiving activity resulted in NMAC with a skydiver.
SR22 pilot reported failure to remove the pitot tube covers caused a rejected takeoff and a runway excursion due to loss of airspeed indication.

PA-32 pilot reported they realized the main cabin door latch was not secured during departure and when returning to the airport they switched fuel tanks to an empty tank causing the engine to lose power so they landed on the nearest suitable runway.

Small aircraft pilot reported after landing the aircraft began to veer towards the right side of the runway. After applying full rudder and brakes the pilot reported being forced to turn off the magnetos and nose the aircraft over to prevent a runway departure into a nearby ditch. A prop strike resulted.
General Aviation pilot reported an NMAC with an unannounced aircraft resulting in evasive action to avoid a collision.
B737 First Officer reported during cruise the autopilot disconnected; and they lost all navigational information on the First Officer's side primary flight display.
A Center Controller reported they lost communications with a military UAS and it descended below its assigned altitude.

ZOB Center Controller reported extreme weather and ZMP Center not having the frequency to open another sector led to an oversaturation of aircraft flying through the corridor and multiple airspace violations.
Air carrier flight crew reported they were on a visual approach and descended below terrain minimums. They received a low altitude alert from the Tower.

EMB-145 Flight Crew reported the failure of the HS 1 bleed valve. The pilots stated they ran the QRH; descended to a lower altitude; realized there was not enough fuel to complete the mission at the lower altitude and diverted. The pilots were in communication with their Dispatcher.
Experimental aircraft pilot reported all propeller blades departed the aircraft and the gearbox broke during takeoff climb. Pilot landed off-airport with no injuries reported.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a rancid/locker room odor initially during boarding that was also reported by another Flight Attendant during descent when the odor intensified.
B737 MAX 8 First Officer reported finding a drill tap on the cockpit floor.
A320 flight crew reported an aircraft swap after maintenance could not accurately determine the accuracy of the fuel quantity gauges or the actual fuel load on board.
Air carrier maintenance technician reported inadvertently shipping a set of aircraft parts without a required hazmat label.
A320 flight crew reported gate pushback was initiated prior to sending the company Dangerous Goods acknowledgment form to Dispatch. The acknowledgement form was sent once pushback was completed.
Air carrier Captain reported they flew an unairworthy aircraft after it had been struck by a catering vehicle on the horizontal stabilizer pre-flight.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported the undocumented transport of Hazmat cargo. Hazmat cargo was loaded in contrary to the Hazmat documents that indicated Hazmat cargo was not loaded.
A321 pilot reported a fumes event with smoke occurred during the climb. Flight returned to departure airport and landed safely.
A320 reported receiving engine oil LO warning on climbout; after coordination with company and oil quantity decreasing.  Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a configuration loading error during departure taxi. The aircraft returned to the gate to correct the loading issue and subsequently departed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported a false EGWPS (Enhanced Ground Proximity Warining System) terrain warning during approach.

Air carrier pilot reported that the flight director commanded a turn in the wrong direction during approach to CLT airport.  Reporter advised; 'this has now happened to me 3 times at the same airport on different approaches.'
Pilot reported a 300 foot tower; 0.7nm off the approach end of Runway 7 at MQJ is not depicted on the VFR Chart and is not reported on a NOTAM.
B-767 air carrier crew reported a near miss during taxi with a ground vehicle resulting in an abrupt stop and a Flight Attendant injury.
First Officer reported loss of Hydraulic System 1 on approach. The flight crew requested priority handling and continued to the destination airport.
CRJ550 flight crew reported windshear encounter caused an unstable approach and momentary loss of aircraft control.
Controller reported heading problems associated with a SID.
L30 TRACON Controller providing training reported a departing aircraft turned the wrong direction a flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737 Captain reported they had rudder issues during takeoff roll resulting in a rejected takeoff.
Captain reported communication system failure and having to use a satellite phone to continue with the flight.

Cessna 182 pilot reported executing a go-around after experiencing loss of directional control during landing in gusting winds. During the go-around a runway light was contacted with a wheel pant; damaging both.

C90TRACON Controller reported they did not notice an unidentified VFR aircraft climb to 100 feet below the Class Bravo airspace on a conflicting route with air carrier traffic conducting a Visual Approach resulting in a NMAC.

B737 Captain reported a SOURCE OFF light illuminated during cruise. When running the checklists; the flight crew attempted a start of the APU; subsequently lost many systems and indications; and elected to make a precautionary landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported during push-back having a miscommunication with the ground crew resulting in taking evasive action to stop the aircraft; the aircraft struck the tug damaging the aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported descent below assigned altitude due to broken transmissions resulted in a low altitude alert and a CFTT event.

Captain reported on final approach at 500 feet AGL; encountered wind shear and an EGPWS alert. They performed an escape/go around maneuver and climbed to 2100 feet MSL however; the published missed approach requires an initial 1500 MSL.
A person reported an aircraft conducted low level aerobatics over a populated area and buildings in the vicinity of the runway.
A320 Captain reported taking evasive action while taxiing to avoid a commercial airliner that ATC failed to provide separation from; resulted in a critical ground conflict event.

First Officer reported communications problems led to lack of compliance with established procedures during engine starts on battery power and with a ground power unit that was not approved for use with the aircraft.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a communication breakdown between Flight Attendant and Maintenance personnel regarding proper disposal of Hazmat waste.
PA-44 flight instructors reported engine failure during cruise. Flight crew secured engine; diverted; and landed safely.

B-777 crew reported smoke from the cockpit air vents during the takeoff roll. The Captain rejected the takeoff; the smoke dissipated after stopping the aircraft; and taxied back to the gate after brake cooling.

A220 air carrier pilot reported uncommanded movement of the aircraft when releasing the parking brake for push back; when the tow bar disconnected from the aircraft. The Captain applied the brakes and stopped the aircraft.
BE-400 flight crew reported a Flap Control malfunction on approach.
TRACON Controller reported an Air Carrier descending on an RNAV arrival reported a NMAC with an unidentified VFR aircraft.
Air Carrier pilot reported the need to brake abruptly to avoid another taxiing aircraft which was supposed to give way.

Air carrier Captain reported that pilots and flight attendants are experiencing stress and reduced quality of life due to the upcoming deadline to either get the COVID-19 vaccination or be terminated from employment. Reportedly; this contributes to reduced margins of safety during flight operations.
Cessna 310 Pilot reported being notified of the illegal carriage of Hazmat cargo labeled as Dry Ice. The reporter believed he operated under an exemption to carry Dry Ice.

B737 MAX 9 Technician reported finding the manual stabilizer trim chain loose on the aircraft despite the low number of hours; and the chain was tightened per the Aircraft Maintenance Manual procedures. A couple of days later the reporter found another aircraft with the same problem.
B737 First Officer reported failure to request expeditious handling with abnormal speed indications resulted in a deviation.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a ground proximity warning during approach that may have been caused by a nearby tower. The flight crew climbed to a safe altitude and ATC issued a new altimeter setting that was significantly different from what they were previously using.
BE65 pilot reported both alternators failed during initial climb. Pilot returned to departure airport.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported during destination unloading that a mobility device was improperly loaded and the SOP procedures were not completed.
Pilot reported landing safely after descending below 500 ft AGL on approach to CMA in an attempt to stay below a forming cloud layer.

Air Carrier Tug Driver reported a miss communication with the flight deck. After hearing 'brakes set; clear to disconnect'; the aircraft began to roll forward. The driver took evasive action to avoid contact with the aircraft; however the shear pin and tow bar were damaged.
B737-800 flight crew reported loss of pressurization during descent.

General aviation pilot reported while preparing to take off they had to taxi off the runway and into gravel to avoid a collision with a training aircraft that was landing on the opposite end of the runway without making any communication on CTAF.
Air carrier Captain reported he failed to notify Dispatch to amend the final flight release of the hazmat onboard prior to gate departure.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft that attempted to land on a taxiway during strong crosswind conditions. The reporting pilot communicated to the landing aircraft of the conflict and the landing aircraft performed a go around.
Air carrier Captain reported an uneventful ground gate return due to a fume event caused by air cart radiator hose blowing into the packs.
B767-300 Pilot reported a Cargo Fire B 6 and 8 amber light illuminated and the flight crew completed a diversion to an alternate airport.

CRJ-900First Officer reported the crew oxygen mask failed a First Flight of the Day preflight check. The expanding net that goes over your head was broken. The rubber piping that expands had come apart inside of the nylon casing that protects the rubber. The aircraft was removed from service for repair.

Pilot reported that during pattern entry; they could not see two other aircraft in the pattern and elected to continue downwind instead of turning base; inadvertently entering a nearby Class D airspace.
Captain reported aircraft log book errors were discovered during an interview with the FAA for an occurrence report review.
CRJ-550 flight crew reported failure of anti-ice system while in icing conditions.
C172 flight instructor reported the Tower Controllers instructions for sequencing in the VFR pattern caused them to have an NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern.
Captain reported a conflict between the published altitude crossing restrictions and the aircraft's FMC database on the DARAN2A STAR into MMMX airport.
BUR Tower CIC reported an unsafe situation when the Local Controller mistakenly cleared an aircraft for takeoff with an aircraft on final to a crossing runway.
BE9L flight crew reported a loss of engine oil in-flight. Flight crew diverted and returned to departure airport.

A flight Instructor in a C172 reported the Tower Controller issued them a turn towards mountainous terrain and a Helicopter on a conflicting course to avoid additional traffic which they reported they had in sight.
Air carrier flight crew reported communication issues with ATC regarding their taxi to runway clearance.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported being notified of a box containing two Dangerous Goods (DG) components combined in one box. Reporter stated he was not aware of any DG in the box.
MD-11 First Officer reported hydraulic system 1 failure due to a faulty auto brake controller.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported the student lost directional control on takeoff; departing the runway. Instructor took the controls and safely stopped; with no damage to the aircraft or taxi lights.
A319 flight crew reported low engine oil quantity on descent. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.

Ground personnel reported the tow bar became disconnected from the aircraft while it was being towed resulting in uncommanded aircraft movement. The brake rider applied brakes and aircraft movement was stopped.
Light aircraft Instructor pilot reported a NMAC with another light aircraft in the pattern at VPC airport.

Cessna 172 instructor pilot reported a near miss during a training flight at an uncontrolled airport; when entering the traffic pattern. The pilot maneuvered away from the aircraft then landed safely.

Dispatcher reported the flight crew had landing gear unsafe indications on final approach. The flight crew performed a go around and a low pass to allow ground observers to ascertain the landing gear position and then landed safely at destination airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported unreliable airspeed indicator during descent in severe weather. Diverted to alternate airport in VMC and landed uneventfully.
Ground observer reported a critical ground conflict. The reporter states a taxing corporate jet nearly collided with a parked helicopter.
Air Carrier Captain reported bright lights beyond the airport at a golf range; are too bright and distracting while landing to the North at RNO airport.
ERJ175 Captain reported on visual approach relying on RNAV guidance resulted in low PAPI indications along with ATC low altitude alert.
PC-12 pilot reported the autopilot did not engage during departure and went unnoticed by the pilot; resulting in a flight toward terrain event and low altitude alert from ATC.
PC-12 pilot reported a MAU-A (Master Avionics Unit- A) caused cascading failures which led to altitude and heading deviations. Pilot regained control of aircraft and landed at destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported they were stopped at their assigned spot on the ramp when they observed another aircraft taxiing towards them that did not stop '...until the last minute.'
Technician reported errors in measurements after a firewall was fabricated and replaced on a C172 aircraft. A Design Engineering Representative was engaged to approve the fabrication and replacement.
Air carrier gate agent reported the aircraft entering the gate was marshalled past the stop line and nearly collided with the jet bridge.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported a Ground Conflict with another aircraft during taxi out for take-off.
Flight instructor with student reported an NMAC at a non-towered airport with an aircraft that flew close behind them then flew below them while on final approach.

Air carrier flight crew reported landing gear failed to retract shortly after departure. The flight crew followed the QRH procedures which resulted in the correct landing gear indications and were able to land safely at departure airport.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported during takeoff both pilots heard a loud squeal in their headsets that continued throughout the flight. Post flight; Maintenance found part of a jack pin of a pushback crew headset had broken off in the aircraft panel causing a short in the intercom.
B747-400 flight crew reported severe turbulence in cruise flight.
Cessna 182 pilot reported loss of engine power resulted in off-airport landing with damage to the aircraft.
Air Carrier Load Planner reported a communications breakdown between Load Planner and Ramp personnel resulting in aircraft departure with incorrect Hazmat documentation.
Ground crew reported witnessing the aircraft roll forward after the tow bar was disconnected.
BE-65 pilot reported that the aircraft would not maintain altitude and suspected they were in clear icing conditions.  Pilot exited icing conditions and continued flight with no further issues.
Air carrier pilot reported they were cleared direct BESTT but fix BESST was entered into the FMS resulting in a course deviation and conflict with another aircraft.
Air carrier ground personnel reported Hazmat documents contained quantity errors. The documents were corrected and reshipped.

BE99 trainee reported losing directional control during takeoff roll resulting in a rejected takeoff.  Reporter stated they missed removing the rudder pedal control lock which had been installed incorrectly the previous pilot.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a smoke event during cruise caused by improperly configured spare Lithium Ion batteries in a passenger's carry-on backpack. Flight Attendants extinguished the fire while the Captain executed a safe diversion.

Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported while on final approach the autothrottle failed to keep the aircraft on speed. As the aircraft slowed the pilots disconnected the autothrottle and continued in manual mode to landing. The pilots reported they were aware of 5G interference around LGA.
Air carrier employee reported TSA removed a DG metallic cylinder from a passenger's bag during screening. The DG item was turned over to airline personnel.
Flight crew reported difficulty in raising the trailing edge flaps and elected to divert instead of risking a grounded aircraft at an outstation.
A C150 pilot reported engine failure and diversion to nearby airport.
Air carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation during departure. Captain cited distraction dealing with an ECAM alert and lack of flying contributed to the event.
Citation Captain reported while being vectored to an ILS approach accepting a visual approach and misidentified the airport resulting in turning base for the wrong airport and flying towards terrain.

EMB-175 flight crew reported the ground personnel informed them of a fuel leak on the #1 engine. Maintenance came out to the aircraft; pulled the fire extinguisher handle and as the Captain entered the flight deck they hit their head on the handle and discharged the fire extinguisher.
EMB-145 First Officer reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on short final at PHL.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL in trail of an EMB-170.
C172 Flight Instructor reported ATC issuing incorrect pattern instructions resulted in confusion and a NMAC situation.
Flight Instructor with student reported a NMAC during cruise flight. ATC directed the Instructor to take evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier First Officer reported GPS jamming during cruise. The jamming issue was reported to ATC and the flight continued to a safe landing.
Helicopter crew reported a mid air collision with a UAS while flying at 150 feet in controlled airspace. The crew landed safely after the collision.
Flight Instructor with student reported a near miss with an aircraft that was taking off on the opposite direction runway at a non-towered airport and was not making any radio transmissions.

Bell 407 Pilot reported concerns over the routing of the throttle cable after scheduled maintenance. After consulting a separate mechanic; it was discovered the cable had been misrouted and had to be reinstalled correctly.
GA pilot reported staffing issues at SQL Federal Contract Tower that seem to be getting worse and believes it to be a 'dangerous' situation.  Reporter stated the Tower is closing on some afternoons.
Flight crew reported incorrect FMS data and a GPWS alert due to late runway change and high workload task saturation.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding A350 and jet blast from a departing heavy aircraft just before landing at SFO.
Air carrier flight crew reported an NMAC with a UAS during initial approach. No evasive action was taken.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a discrepancy with final cargo close out which indicated hazmat was held off however the gate indicated the hazmat cargo was not held off prior to departure.
A PA-34-200T pilot reported an Ipad mount became lodged under the pedals preventing braking and directional control resulting in a taxiway excursion.
Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported a critical taxi conflict leaving the gate area.
EMB-145 flight crew reported an initial air leak from main cabin door during climb and subsequent reports of fumes in the cabin resulting in physiological effects on the crew.

CRJ-900 air carrier crew reported uncommanded aircraft movement during engine start and pushback when the Captain became distracted and failed to set the parking brake. The aircraft rolled forward onto the tow bar; but no damage occurred
Air carrier Captain reported that due to construction at ORD; the faded taxi lines at N/EE/LL are unclear and inaccurate.

Flight Instructor with student reported they turned final at a non-towered airport and experienced an NMAC with another aircraft on a straight-in approach which took evasive action to avoid the instructor's aircraft.

C550B Captain reported dual HSI failure at night resulted in heading deviation and an airspace violation.  Captain was able to manually set the HSI to match the magnetic compass and continued on course.

Light Transport aircraft flight crew reported a CFTT event during approach to CHA airport.  The crew selected 3;100 rather than the cleared altitude of 3;600 feet.  ATC announced the terrain alert and the crew took evasive action by climbing.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the pattern at 7S3.
Air carrier First Officer reported they were below glideslope on short final and received a Low Altitude Alert from Tower.
P-28 Pilot reported an NMAC event during takeoff departure. Additionally; pilot reported concerns regarding ATC's handling of airport departure and transiting traffic.
Air carrier flight crew reported performing a low-altitude go-around to due landing aircraft on the runway.
C172 Flight Instructor reported losing electrical power in flight; and all electrical systems were turned off except for the transponder. The flight then diverted to a suitable airport.
General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft when entering the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The pilot maneuvered to avoid the aircraft.
Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert on final and surmised it was due to rain in the vicinity of the runway and continued the approach to landing.
Air Carrier Captain reported an unsafe ground event resulting from unclear procedures at OGG airport.
Air carrier Captain reported communication problems with push crew after wireless headset failed and a traditional cord and head set were not available.
Air taxi helicopter pilot reported a near miss with an aircraft in the traffic pattern. The helicopter pilot maneuvered to avoid the aircraft; then continued to destination.
Air carrier flight crew reported on departure worsening weather and an airborne conflict at MYAM resulted in a GPWS terrain caution.
DJI Mavic 2 Zoom drone pilot reported flying a mission without having the proper LAANC authorization.
Technicians reported disagreement with a Supervisor over damage limits on an aircraft drain mast and felt pressure to sign it off as airworthy instead of replacing it.

Air carrier Captain reported experiencing GPS spoofing while in Egypt airspace. The left GPS began to work properly again after leaving the area; but the right GPS; autoland; and the Enhanced Ground Proximity Warning System (EGPWS) never recovered.
Husky A1-C pilot reported experiencing engine roughness; resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Captain reported refusing the aircraft because a High Pressure Bleed Valve would not open after start and there was a forecast for known icing conditions present.
Flight Crew reported an air turn back after the TAT probe failed; causing the auto throttle to malfunction.
ERJ 145 First Officer reported auto trim fail and autopilot failure on climb out.
Air Carrier First Officer reported flight crew members often discard Hazmat documents that are required to be kept until flight completion per CFR.175.33 and ICAO regulations.

C-172 pilot reported an engine failure due to fuel starvation which resulted from a higher than normal fuel consumption. Pilot landed safely in a field where more fuel was added and departed to destination safely.
Flight Attendant crew reported a fume event during preflight at the gate resulting in physiological symptoms.

B737-700 Flight Crew reported the aircraft began an uncommanded climb at cruise. The Flight Crew determined that the L IRS system had failed and switched over to the R IRS system. The Flight Crew continued to destination airport with no further issues.

Flight crew reported receiving a terrain alert from ATC and a GPWS warning while on approach inside the final approach fix for the RNAV 31 approach to RDG airport. The crew noted they were in compliance with the approach minimums; disconnected the autopilot and continued the approach for a safe landing.
Air carrier pilot reported static on ZME frequency 127.37 makes it difficult to understand ATC instructions and is a recurring issue in this sector.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported the loss of the main hydraulic system during final approach.
B737 MAX Captain reported an FMC database error which causes incorrect indication of the landing altitude indication and landing altitude reference bar.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported learning post-flight they flew in controlled airspace without authorization.
Air carrier flight crew received a caution obstacle aural warning on approach. Flight crew corrected flight path and landed uneventfully.
Regional Jet Captain reported similar sounding fixes EMMLY instead of EMILY; resulted in a turn off course.
ERJ 170 flight crew reported an aircraft generated CAUTION OBSTACLE message; due to too low on a visual approach. Flight crew leveled off; then continued approach.
A321 pilot reported engine component malfunction inflight. Flight continued to destination airport.

Flight Crew reported a loud bang on take off and noticed #3 engine had high EGT. The flight crew then performed an in flight shut down of the #3 Engine; and made an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Government UAS pilot reported operating a medium UAS without the proper waivers.
Air carrier ground crew reported continuous movement of the aircraft after completion of push back.
First Officer reported observing a UAS passing within 500 feet of their aircraft while approaching to land. No evasive action was taken and the aircraft landed normally.
Air carrier First Officer reported executing a go-around on approach to SEA after experiencing atmospheric turbulence or possibly a wake encounter.
GA flight instructor reported while in pattern; traffic made nonstandard pattern entry; disregarded position calls; which resulted in an NMAC.
B737 Captain reported a spacing issue with several aircraft and had jet wash from two departing aircraft while on short final at SFO.
C182 Pilot reported a loss of GPS signal while on vectors for approach.

Cessna 421 pilot reported a loss of control to steer or stop the aircraft on the ground; due to a malfunction of the brakes and rudder steering; resulting in a taxiway excursion. The aircraft collided with a runway sign off the taxiway; collapsing the nose gear; and the pilot evacuated without injury.
Air carrier Captain reported the compass malfunctioned during the takeoff roll on LGA Runway 4. After the First Officer ran the QRH; the compass worked properly for the remainder of the flight.

A319 Captain reported being notified by the number 1 Flight Attendant of 'a lot of fumes' in the cabin during gate arrival. After APU bleed shutdown the fumes dissipated. Captain notified Maintenance and submitted a logbook entry.
Air carrier First Officer reported safety and health concerns with personnel having to wear face masks.
Light aircraft Instructor Pilot reported an NMAC with a corporate jet in the pattern at AUO airport.
C152 pilot reported a near miss in a traffic pattern formation flight when one of the formation aircraft turned in front of them. Pilot took evasive action.
Wing Walker and Tug Driver reported during push back the wing walker was distracted moving a bag left behind the aircraft when the tug driver pushed the aircraft into a catering vehicle.
Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft moving forward uncommanded during pushback. Captain set parking brake.

Air carrier Captain reported during takeoff roll a general aviation aircraft taxied across the MYNN/NAS Runway 28. Captain stated the Airport Moving Map and Jeppesen airport diagrams were different; with the former showing hold-short lines.
Air carrier flight crew reported; after an unstable approach at night to an uncontrolled airport; not executing the published missed approach.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student reported a gear up landing.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported multiple brake rotor drive clips cracked on 737 MAX aircraft.

Technician reported turning the aircraft battery switch to off with the APU running; then turning the battery switch back to on a B757. Later it was reported that there was a fire in the APU compartment.
C-172 flight instructor on training flight with a student reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern at a non towered airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported unusual noises and vibrations but had no malfunction indications so they returned to their departure airport.
DA42 Flight Instructor reported a jammed rudder trim mechanism while conducting a training flight. An immediate return to the departure airport was accomplished.
A Part 107 pilot reported seeing a photograph online taken from what they believe to be a no-fly zone.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near miss with an unidentified object during descent in ZHU airspace.
Light aircraft pilot reported an airborne conflict while at an ATC assigned altitude and at the same time encountering rising terrain.
Air carrier First Officer reported at the gate before push back and during taxi; GPS jamming at LTFM; Istanbul Airport; Turkey.
UAS Visual Observer reported a fly away after a lost link with the controller. The UAS crashed in an area of trees and was recovered by the UAS crew.
PA-28 pilot reported the right brakes malfunctioned during landing leading to a runway excursion.

A Center Controller reported a lack of guidance from management on how to respond to confusing emails from OPM created a stressful work environment and distracted the workforce from conducting a safe operation at their sectors.
Corporate jet Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from SDL Tower on their visual approach to Runway 21.
A TRACON Controller reported they issued a Low Altitude Alert to an aircraft descending below the published altitude. The pilot acknowledged the alert but did not correct their altitude.

A320 Captain reported momentarily losing GPS signal and after regaining it; the flight crew discovered the aircraft was slightly left of course. ATC advised that there had been GPS jamming occurring in the area.
Single engine pilot reported a runway excursion due to the loss of brakes and aircraft control while landing.

Cessna 182 pilot reported a total electrical failure occurred; resulting in loss of communication and navigation equipment. The pilot then navigated using their tablet and entered Class B airspace without operating ADS-B equipment.
B737-800 pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from Tower after descending too low on approach. Flight crew made necessary corrections and landed safely.

C182 pilot reported the engine failed soon after switching the fuel tank selector from 'both' to 'left' position due to uneven fuel burn.  The pilot immediately pitched the aircraft for best glide speed; followed procedures; and was able to regain engine power when the fuel tank selector was placed back to 'both' position.

Air carrier flight crew reported during push-back the tow bar shear pin broke and the aircraft rolled out of control and the flight crew took evasive action setting the brakes as directed by the ground personnel.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with an ultralight aircraft while on final approach.
Air Carrier First Officer reported they descended below their cleared altitude during approach to SDF resulting in a CFTT event before climbing back to the appropriate altitude.
DA40 pilot reported they turned the wrong direction on a taxiway and entered a runway without clearance resulting in an aircraft on short final executing a go around.
Corporate jet pilot reported crossing KICER intersection below the charted altitude on approach to ASE.
General Aviation pilot reported diverting due to an electrical problem. After troubleshooting the alternator failed again on a subsequent flight.

B737 flight crew reported the landing gear would not retract after departure. After discovering the manual gear retraction door was not fully closed the crew was able to retract the gear and continue to the destination airport.
