Air Carrier Administrative personnel reported fumes 'coming through vent system' in the Administrative work area.

Air carrier flight crew reported the ACARS messaging was intermittently inoperative during pre-flight. After extensive consultation the flight crew printed all the necessary DG documents which made the crew legal to depart.

Air carrier Captain reported during preflight a gate agent asked the Captain for handling guidance of a Lithium battery powered scooter. After resolving the handling issue the Captain suggested better training of ground personnel and also improvement of the flight crew FOM.
EMB-145LR flight crew reported a fume event occurred during initial approach.
Ramp agent reported a traffic conflict with an airport vehicle when pushing back an aircraft. The airport vehicle stopped abruptly and had to back up to avoid the collision.
A220 flight crew reported Bleed system failure inflight.
PA-18 Pilot reported while landing in a crosswind losing control of the aircraft resulting in a runway excursion.  The Pilot was able to taxi the aircraft to parking with no damage to the aircraft.

Air carrier Ramp personnel reported he loaded a priority package containing Dry Ice without revising the Dangerous Goods Form to include Dry Ice. Reporter was notified of the error after flight completion.
B737 flight crew reported a ground conflict during taxi that required the crew stop their aircraft to avoid a possible collision with an EMB-175.

Air carrier Captain reported severe turbulence during cruise flight when deviating around thunderstorms; resulting in an altitude and heading deviation. The flight returned to the clearance and no injuries were reported.
Air carrier flight crew reported a pneumatic duct bleed leak during climb. The crew dumped fuel down to maximum landing weight and returned to the departure airport.
EMB 170/175 flight crew reported the aircraft became disconnected from tow bar during pushback resulting in the aircraft rolling uncommanded. Captain set parking brake to stop aircraft.
MRY Tower Controller reported an aircraft on a SID turned the wrong direction on initial climb and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
CNO Tower Controller reported issuing inbound IFR a descent to avoid NORDO traffic resulted in a NMAC.
Small aircraft Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the vicinity of FFZ airport. Flight Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.

C172 Instructor and two Ag aircraft pilots reported an NMAC between each other at a non-towered airport. C712 Instructor also reported a runway incursion between conflicting traffic landing behind their company traffic on the same runway.
B787 First Officer reported a flight deck window shattered in cruise. Flight crew diverted to nearest suitable airport and landed.
EMB-545 flight crew reported receiving a GPWS alert during approach.
CE-680 flight crew reported engine failure during departure climb. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Student pilot on solo flight reported runway excursion due to gusty crosswind on landing.
A300 Captain reported DG cargo was loaded on wrong aircraft during preflight. DG cargo that was meant for a different destination was removed prior to departure.
C421 Pilot reported an NMAC during climb with the previous departure that had crossed the C421's departure path. An evasive maneuver was required to avoid a collision.

EMB-145 crew reported an EGPWS event during flap retraction on an autopilot missed approach after conducting an ILS approach to minimums. Crew took manual control of aircraft and diverted due to fuel considerations and weather at destination.

An Air Carrier pilot reported they could not comply with the climb gradient on the SID and ATC had to vector them for terrain clearance. The pilot reported dispatch advised them they did not need to perform a climb analysis prior to departure.  The reporter had  difficulty contacting company prior to departure to receive guidance related to their concern.
ERJ-190 Pilot reported the engines would not shut down after gate arrival. Both FADECs had failed and allowed the engine to go into reverse during check list procedures for abnormal shut down.
B737 First Officer reported an oil filter bypass light malfunction during cruise flight. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported temporarily losing control of the UAS and it climbed above the authorized altitude. The pilot was able to regain control and bring the UAS back to clearance
A tug driver reported the towbar was not connected properly before pushback causing the aircraft to move on its own before the flight crew was signaled to hit the brakes.
First Officer reported flying with two different Captains who exhibited symptoms from COVID-19.
Air Carrier Captain reported an uneventful flight after a ground gate return at departure to remove spare lithium battery from passenger bag in cargo compartment.
Pilot reported that while beginning the takeoff roll; another aircraft on final performed a go-around to avoid a near midair collision.
Captain reported a rejected take off and return to gate because of aircraft controllability issues.
A Tower Ground Controller reported the Local Controller cleared an aircraft for takeoff while another aircraft was taxiing across the runway.
Pilot reported runway excursion during landing rollout at night.
An Air Carrier pilot reported both GPS units failed.
Air Carrier Pilots reported severe turbulence encountered and a Flight Attendant injured.
BE36 pilot reported a temporary loss of control during approach. The pilot corrected altitude and heading deviations and completed a safe landing.
Cessna Longitude crew reported a loss of cabin pressure during climb and diverted to a nearby airport.

Air Carrier Captain reported being notified by the aft flight attendant during deplaning that the lavatory smoke detector activated briefly and ceased. No smoke or fumes detected. Captain submitted an aircraft logbook entry to maintenance.
Champion 402 Lancer pilot reported a thrust loss on one engine after takeoff.  Pilot performed off airport landing with no damage to aircraft or injuries to occupant.
Fractional flight crew reported during descent they had a terrain warning and initiated a go around maneuver. Flight crew received vectors and completed the approach.
Air Carrier pilot reported a fume event during descent. After collaborating with maintenance and further troubleshooting it was decided to ferry the aircraft to a company maintenance base.
A Tower Ground Controller reported they observed a near collision between an aircraft executing a go-around and an aircraft on short final to an intersecting runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control after the push back due to miscommunications with the ground crew.
General Aviation Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC at 7A3 non-towered airport. The other aircraft executed a go-around.
Cessna 210 pilot reported the hose blew off the turbo and they lost manifold pressure.
Air carrier flight crew reported low altitude alert on approach as well as flap overspeed on go-around.
Air carrier First Officer reported confusion and miscommunication with the ramp crew about which engine they should start first.
Air carrier pilot reported sighting a UAP that appeared stationary at 13;000 feet and advised ATC of the encounter.
Pilot reported engine failure during cruise flight due to fuel starvation resulting in an off-airport landing with no injuries or damage.
Aircraft technician reported a cargo door lift arm shim disbonding during a preflight inspection on a 787-9 aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported difficulty in communicating with Cleveland Center on a specific frequency; and was then switched over to another frequency. The ZOB Controller told the reporter that this was a recurring issue.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported the windshield cracked while in level cruise.
G650 ER flight crew reported a complete steering failure after landing and a runway excursion.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a dispatch notification that no flight crew dangerous goods acknowledgement message had been submitted after gate pushback. Flight crew complied immediately.
ERJ First Officer reported  the DEN Jeppesen 10-9 airfield diagrams do not correlate with the magnetic variation change.
Pilot reported a decision to continue the approach after becoming unstabilized; resulting in a hard landing which collapsed the nose landing gear.

Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft veered left during takeoff resulting in a rejected takeoff. Confusing taxi instructions during the reject caused them to taxi onto a parallel runway which was too small to accommodate them.

Air carrier flight crew reported poorly placed taxiway signage resulted in the crew taxiing past the point were a safe turn onto a crossing taxiway could be made. ATC redirected the crew on an alternate taxi route to the runway.
C172 pilot reported a Low Altitude Alert on approach.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported while hand flying; EFIS failure message resulted in task saturation and an overbank situation.
CRJ200 flight crew reported multiple issues resulted in an unstable approach that would have required a go around. Instead; the crew continued the landing and were stable by 500 ft AGL.
EMB-145 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into BOS in trail of a B737-900.
Air carrier ground personnel reported the aircraft returned to the gate to resolve an issue with unsecured dangerous goods.
EMB-145 First Officer reported an engine roll back during cruise flight.
Air carrier pilot reported an unstable approach resulted in a Ground Proximity Warning.

A320 First Officer reported a near collision with another aircraft during pushback while connected to the tug. The pilot crew alerted the tug operator; along with the ramp agents to stop the aircraft and avoid the collision.

Cessna 172 student pilot reported a loss of aircraft control and runway excursion on the takeoff roll during a solo training flight at a Tower controlled airport. The student pilot took immediate action to stop the aircraft as it exited the runway onto an adjacent taxiway. The student pilot was not injured and coordinated with ATC; taxied to the ramp; and contacted his instructor.
GA pilot reported descending below minimum altitude during approach and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC. Pilot continued to landing.
B757 pilot reported multiple system failures during cruise. Flight crew diverted and during landing experienced a gear door issue. All issues were addressed during subsequent maintenance work.
First Officer reported missing a crossing restriction departing LAS; citing wake turbulence as a possible distraction.
Instructor pilot reported loss of braking action during landing roll out. The aircraft ran off the runway and into a ditch; resulting in damage to the propeller.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into ATL in trail of a B757; which was  following of a Heavy B787.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation due to failed crew communication and the Captain's recent extended time off.
A320 flight crew reported a rejected takeoff after engine fluctuations reoccurred during takeoff roll and returned to the gate.

Air carrier Captain reported during cruise the flight attendants reported physiological symptoms; including a 'bubbling sensation' on skin. Reportedly; passengers also experienced symptoms. After the flight descended; the symptoms dissipated slightly.

Air carrier Ground Agent reported having to abruptly brake while pushing the aircraft that resulted in the shear pins on the tow bar breaking. This was to avoid collision with a vehicle that had not stopped for the wing walkers.
Flight crew reported loss of oil pressure on the #1 Engine after take off. The flight crew performed an inflight shut down of the affected engine and returned to departure airport.

Cessna Citation pilot reported that during the takeoff role on a snow-covered runway; the aircraft would not rotate although more than normal yoke control force was used. The pilot rejected the takeoff and returned to parking.
Flight Instructor and student reported they feathered one engine due to loss of oil pressure and diverted to land at a nearby airport.
Flight Attendant reported after flying an all-nighter; they had to wait 3 hours in the crew lounge to make an additional two hour flight.
Air carrier pilot reported LAS ATC does not allow aircraft to depart from the runway required to comply with climb gradients to avoid terrain due to high outside air temperatures.
A TRACON Controller reported they received a Low Altitude Alert when an aircraft descended below a crossing restriction on an approach.
B737 First Officer reported a left engine oil filter bypass indication on final approach. Flight crew continued and shut down affected engine after landing.

B767-400 flight crew reported receiving an ECIAS message warning of a possible fuel filter bypass. The event; as stated by the crew; occurred just after takeoff. The flight return to the departure airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported a NMAC with a UAS that flew over them while they were on initial approach. The pilots stated that due to the close proximity of the UAS; they did not have time to take evasive action.
Small aircraft pilot reported deviating from ATC heading clearance to avoid terrain since the aircraft was not climbing well in turbulent conditions.
Air carrier Captain reported they had to unexpectedly yield  while taxiing on the ramp to avoid a collision with a vehicle towing a luggage cart.
Air carrier First Officer reported taking evasive action to miss NORDO traffic on climb out; resulting in a NMAC.
ZLC Center Controller reported an aircraft descended below a crossing altitude at WARPU intersection.
Flight Crew reported the loss of Yellow Hydraulic System on climb out. The Flight Crew ran the QRH procedures and was directed to continue to destination airport by Operations Control.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported another UAS pilot was flying a UAS in an unsafe manner and outside of regulations.
Air carrier flight crew reported an NMAC with another aircraft during departure climb.

Musketeer 23 Pilot reported a NMAC event during landing pattern entry when a regional jet flew 300 feet above and separated away. After landing; the pilot discovered the audio panel volume had been turned down.
Flight Crew reported landing gear would not retract after take off. The flight crew returned to landing at departure airport.
Helicopter pilot reported an engine failure caused a precautionary landing on a closed taxiway.
B757 flight crew reported diverting to a nearby alternate airport after experiencing engine surging.
Pilot reported a near mid air collision with an aircraft while in the traffic pattern and landing. The Pilot took evasive action and rejoined the pattern and landed.
GA pilot reported CTAF communication challenges at an airport that normally would have a working Tower facility; but was closed due to COVID-19.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported runway excursion during landing sequence.
A320 Captain reported failure of multiple navigation systems during climb resulted in the flight crew using raw data. Flight crew returned to the departure airport.
General aviation pilot reported landing at an airport reportedly closed for UAS operations. After landing the pilot checked for NOTAMS and saw the airport was closed.
Air carrier Captain reported the lack of taxiway lighting at the end of Taxiway A at MUHA Airport may lead to taxiway excursion.
PA-28 pilot reported engine problems during approach to destination airport. The pilot requested priority handling and continued to destination airport.

Pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft on final approach which was practicing a simulated instrument missed approach procedure during a training flight at a non-towered airport. The pilot maneuvered away from the other aircraft then proceeded to land.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL260 that resulted in minor injuries to a Flight Attendant.
TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation between an air carrier and a small aircraft and cited staffing issues as a contributing factor.
Ground Personal reported the aircraft parked in this ramp location are parked too close together. The agent states there is no stop mark on the ground for the larger sized aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported they descended below glidepath while on approach and received a terrain warning.

Dispatcher reported air carrier requested push-back clearance and the Controller held their request but didn't hear any read back from flight crew due to communication issues. The flight crew continued to push back and blocked a gate.
PRC Tower Controller reported failed to provide separation based on incorrect RADAR and transponder data resulted in evasive action and a NMAC event.
Small aircraft pilot reported due to renamed runways; at their home airport; lead to confusion and alignment of wrong runway resulted in a critical ground conflict and a go-around.

General aviation Flight Instructor reported a near miss with a helicopter while in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The instructor took the controls and maneuvered away from the helicopter then landed safely.

Air carrier flight crew reported Las Vegas TRACON (L30) gave a pilot deviation despite the flight crew adhering to the previous assigned speed of the Los Angeles Center (ZLA). Upon landing and later speaking to a Las Vegas TRACON representative; the flight crew learned that the TRACON facility was using pilot deviations to get the Center's attention to fix an issue where the latter was handing off aircraft at incorrect speeds.
A Flight Instructor reported two NMAC's while on downwind in the Tower VFR pattern.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported EGPWS warning and unstabilized approach.

Air carrier flight crew reported #1 engine compressor stalled during initial departure climb. Air crew requested and was provided priority handling to the departure airport where a safe overweight landing completed.
B737-900 flight crew reported a fumes event during descent to destination.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported a hard landing on an IOE training flight.

B777-200 flight crew reported a ground vehicle was moving fast towards the aircraft on the taxiway as the flight crew was taxiing for takeoff. ATC provided no notification but the flight crew was aware of the vehicle. The vehicle braked before crossing the aircraft's path.
PA-28 Flight Instructor with student reported electrical failure in cruise in IMC. Flight exited IMC; diverted and landed.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on a visual approach to COD airport.
C-172 Flight Instructor reported fuel leak inflight. Post flight it was discovered a fuel cap was missing.

CRJ700 flight crew reported fuel quantity issues during preflight fueling. After takeoff; the total fuel showed to be rapidly decreasing and a fuel imbalance error message appeared. The flight crew then performed an air turnback.
Air carrier flight attendants reported high cabin air temperatures at gate due to inoperative APU.
A319 flight crew reported a hydraulic system temperature malfunction during climb. As the crew returned to the departure airport the malfunction cleared; and the flight landed and taxied to the gate.

Technician reported an ECAM warning for a emergency exit door warning that repeated on a subsequent flight; resulting in a precautionary landing and additional repairs to the door seal to correct the malfunction.
Air carrier Captain reported disagreement between FDPro and the Jeppesen charts indicating whether a fix was a compulsory reporting point; resulting in flight crew not reporting as required.
EMB-145 Captain reported while taxiing; a city vehicle moving very fast swerved near them; resulting in Captain aggressively stopping to avoid collision.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported exhaust fumes throughout the aircraft during cruise. Flight crew diverted to another airport for landing.
Private Pilot flying PA-28 aircraft reported engine malfunction in cruise flight.
C172 Pilot reported abnormal oil pressure and temperature and eventual engine failure resulting in an off field landing.
General aviation pilot reported receiving an altitude alert from ZSE ARTCC due to flying a holding pattern below the published altitude.

Vans aircraft pilot reported a loss of control in flight after takeoff due to a wind gust. After the aircraft bounced on the runway the pilot continued flying while attempting to fix a full nose down trim condition and eventually landed the aircraft with minor damage.
C402 Captain reported high left engine oil temperatures in climb and cruise; possibly due to oil leak. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.

GA Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport with an aircraft that entered the pattern incorrectly and opposite direction of traffic. Both pilots took evasive action to avoid a collision.
EMB-145 flight crew reported receiving an engine oil pressure caution during takeoff roll.  The oil pressure rose to 120 before the crew decided to reject the takeoff traveling at 90 knots.
Air Carrier Captain reported receiving a GPWS 'Terrain' alert on downwind for Runway 24 at ROA.
A TRACON Controller reported they vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier pilot reported the auto pilot malfunctioned on final approach possibly due to GPS interference.
B737 flight crew reported multiple equipment failures during descent to land.

F11 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft arriving into ORL executed a go-around and climbed into conflict with an aircraft that was on final approach to MCO. The reporter stated that the missed approach procedures for ORL need to be changed to not conflict with the approach for MCO.
Air Carrier Captain reported receiving TCAS RA's while on parallel approaches to runways 28 L/R at SFO. The Captain stated it is an unsafe procedure and causes unnecessary missed approaches.
Air carrier First Officer reporter that during taxi to the gate a baggage cart drove directly in front of them requiring the crew to slam on the brakes to avoid a collision.
B737-800 flight crew reported left engine over-temp resulted in priority handling return to departure airport.
An air carrier pilot reported insufficient runway lighting at BOS Runway 33R/4R intersection makes it difficult to see runway markings and could cause taxi errors.
Pilot reported confusion regarding VFR reporting points while arriving into BFI airport.
PA28 pilot reported electrical system malfunction inflight resulting in diversion.
Instructor with trainee reported a NMAC while practicing landings and pattern work at a non-towered airport. The instructor took control and executed a missed approach.
Air Carrier Captain reported gate pushback without Final DG Summary. Flight parked temporally to receive Final DG Summary and continued taxi for departure.
A Center Controller reported they issued a heading to an aircraft that placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
General aviation pilot departed airport VFR when when weather near the airport was marginal with low clouds in the area.
Citation 550 flight crew reported left brake locked up on landing resulted in left main runway excursion; wing struck sign and punctured fuel tank.
Pilot reported high trees around the SEP Airport visually obstruct the base and final segments of the traffic pattern increasing the risk of a NMAC at this non-towered airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation during approach to AVL airport resulting in a CFTT event.  The crew climbed to appropriate altitude and continued the approach.
Air carrier flight crew noticed drone while on final approach to landing.

Air carrier Captain reported a loud explosion in the cabin during engine start. Flight Attendant informed the flight crew that a passenger's thermos filled with dry ice without proper ventilation exploded and blew out a window.
C560 pilot reported their brakes malfunctioned and they had to taxi off the taxiway into the grass to avoid another taxiing aircraft.
Air carrier pilot reported a new temporary taxiway at SAN airport is not charted.
Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported Ground Control instructions and the charted version of this Hotspot at DEN airport are confusing.
B757 flight crew reported a fume event in the flight deck during cruise and diverted safely to a nearby airport.
Air Carrier Captain reported Hazmat cargo refused due to being incorrectly configured for transport.
PA-18 pilot reported oil mist on the windshield during approach. Post-flight it was found a crank seal was the possible cause of the oil leak.
Air taxi Captain reported confusing ATC instructions; faded taxiway marking; and confusing intersections resulted in taxiway and runway incursions at MYNN.
Captain reported experiencing radio altimeter anomalies on approach to PHX.
General aviation pilot reported a NMAC on final approach with an aircraft on final approach to the opposite end of the runway. Reporter took evasive action to avoid a collision.

B737 MAX 9 flight crew reported that during descent the autoflight system malfunctioned and did not respond to inputs. The crew disconnected the autopilot and continued the flight by hand flying the aircraft.

BOI TRACON Controller reported lack of obstruction displayed on RADAR display and DVA procedure change resulted in an MVA violation and a CFTT event. Controller also stated to select correct map would take too much time and attention from monitoring that radar display.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported noise from degraded cabin door seal required diversion to a safe landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported smoke detector activation during initial climb. The cause was attributed to melting tape placed over crew rest smoke detector light.
GA instructor pilot with student reported an NMAC during final approach to CVB airport requiring a 180 degree turn to avoid a collision.
B737-800 Captain reported a large bird on the runway prior to takeoff. The aircraft hit the bird; rejected takeoff and returned to the gate. The bird strike caused damage to the left engine.
Air Carrier pilot reported the aircraft was not 5G NOTAM certified for a Category 2 or 3 landing at JFK airport. The weather had deteriorated and a divert was necessary.
A320 Captain reported unstabilized approach and go around.
CE-560 First Officer reported aircraft movement after parking brakes were set.
DA-42 instructor pilot reported safely returning to departure airport after the left engine would not restart following shutdown for a training scenario.
C560 Captain reported an unstable approach condition due to a late ATC approach clearance. ATC directed a go-around and provided vectors for a subsequent approach which resulted in a safe landing.
Pilot reported confusion over airport layout and entered an active runway with landing traffic; causing critical ground conflict.
DA40 Student Pilot reported latched ECU B Fail Indication in cruise. The flight crew returned to landing at departure airport.

TRACON Controller reported clearance communication issues with an aircraft resulted in a conflict which was saved by another attentive Controller who offered a different heading to avoid further separation issues.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported that the flight crew was immediately told to hold when ATC observed the tug was still under aircraft; despite receiving an all clear.
A319 flight crew reported an inflight upset resulted from a wake turbulence encounter 4.5 miles in trail of a B737-800 on descent into ORD.
Flight Instructor on a training flight with student reported a NMAC with another aircraft while climbing out of a non towered airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Flight Attendant reported that the catering crew returned to complete a service they did not accomplish and opened an armed cabin door after only knocking.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft while in the turn to the downwind leg of the traffic pattern at a towered airport.
Charter flight crew reported malfunction of aircraft flight guidance system causing terrain warning on approach.
Pilot flying in cruise flight received low altitude warning from ATC. Pilot corrected altimeter setting.

Air carrier FO reported a track deviation and restricted area incursion occurred while flying the RIVER visual approach to Runway 19 at DCA. The reporter cited confusion identifying the proper Runway as contributing; since the procedure lines up better with Runway 15 than with Runway 19.
B737 Technician reported communications errors and automation difficulties resulted in an aircraft departing in revenue service with an open discrepancy that could not be properly placed on deferral.
ZAN Controller reported an aircraft encountered icing conditions and descended below ATC published routing which resulted in the aircraft flying below the MIA and a CFIT event.

B737 flight crew reported a main cabin door warning light approaching the high speed regime during takeoff; and rejected the takeoff. The flight attendants indicated the door was correctly closed and armed. The Captain returned the aircraft to the gate for where maintenance removed it from service for the door latch being out of limits.
General aviation Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while in the pattern. Instructor maneuvered his aircraft to avoid a collision.

MD-11 Captain reported engine failure indication accompanied by aircraft yaw during takeoff roll. Captain executed a safe rejected takeoff and returned to maintenance pad where aircraft was removed from service.
DA40 Flight Instructor with student reported smelling a strong odor of fuel and noticed fuel flowing over the right wing during landings practice. Flight Instructor landed and taxied to FBO.
Air Carrier First Officer reported they descended below the minimums on a GPS Approach into deteriorating ceilings and wind shear and were issued a Low Altitude Alert by the Tower.
B747-400 flight crew reported executing a low altitude go-around at ORD after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B787.
PC-24 Captain reported multiple system failures required discontinuing the flight and maintenance action.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported the cabin pressure control panel went blank on climb out. The flight crew elected to divert to an airport where the part was in stock and make an overweight landing at the diversion airport.
ERJ-170 flight crew reported a hard landing that may possibly have been caused by jet blast or wake turbulence.

A319 First Officer reported receiving an ENG 1 STALL ECAM message that was then accompanied by a surging engine noise. The flight crew performed an inflight shutdown of Engine #1 and proceeded to divert.
Air carrier flight crew reported seeing a UAS pass within 100 ft. of the aircraft while climbing at 15;000 ft. and reported it to ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC on final approach with a UAS. No evasive action was taken and the flight continued without further incident.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported airborne flight; which was transporting Class 1 Hazmat cargo; experienced a hydraulic malfunction during cruise. After much troubleshooting and collaborating with multiple departments the flight continued to destination.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with two aircraft flying in formation opposite the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport in visual conditions. The pilot maneuvered their aircraft to avoid the other aircraft; then continued their flight.
B-777 First Officer reported a pneumatic system warning during cruise on an ETOPS flight; entering oceanic airspace. The flight diverted to an alternate airport and landed.
Air carrier flight crew had miscommunication regarding aircraft control during taxi for takeoff.
Air Carrier flight crew reported during engine start experiencing an engine tailpipe fire resulting in ARF response to extinguish the fire and aircraft being towed back to the gate.

B737 flight crew reported releasing the parking brakes and pushing back from the gate before receiving and acknowledging the final dangerous goods report. The flight crew continued the pushback and received and acknowledged the report afterwards.

Medium Transport flight crew reported unstable approach at MFR resulted in a go-around. Flight crew stated that published approaches do not allow approaches to be flown within stabilized approach criteria.
B757 First Officer reported receiving numerous EICAS messages during take off and rejected the take off roll to return to the gate for maintenance.
Houston Center Controller reported problems with unsafe practices involving a weather-related situation.
Air carrier flight crew reported bird strike on takeoff climb. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier First Officer of medium transport reported encountering wake turbulence shortly before touchdown at DFW airport.
CL-350 pilot reported an NMAC during departure climbout.  The conflicting aircraft was reported to be 400 feet below.
GA pilot reported engine failure while climbing out from a go-around.  Reporter successfully landed in a field near the airport.
PA-22 pilot reported flying a simulated engine failure approach resulting in flying towards terrain. The pilot continued the approach to landing.

Air carrier Lead Technician reported undeclared HAZMAT went unnoticed and was transported on two separate flights as the reporter was not informed that the box contained anything hazardous and there were no markings on the box.
Air carrier Captain reported possible GPS jamming in this area per aircraft instrument errors; ATIS information and ATC advisory.
EMB-145 crew reported a cabin pressurization malfunction resulting in an emergency descent. The crew diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
B777 flight crew reported releasing the parking brakes before receiving and sending the final dangerous goods report.
First Officer reported descending below altitude on approach and climbed back to correct altitude to continue the approach to landing.
A320 Captain reported an armed aircraft door was opened after arrival at gate. Maintenance was called and determined the nitrogen tank was punctured but escape slide was not deployed.
A319 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL260 from a preceding B777 that was 10-12 nm ahead of them.
A319 flight crew reported a near miss with a 787 aircraft being pushed back without clearance.
Two different crews involved reported ramp crew communication failure resulted in another company aircraft being pushed back towards them; stopping extremely close.

G200 flight crew reported a loss of control at FL390  after penetrating a rapidly building thunderstorm cell while deviating around weather. Crew regained control after a loss of 800-1000 feet and returned to clearance.
Pilot reported windshear and severe turbulence on approach to TVL. Diverted to alternate airport.

A319 Dispatcher reported the Captain was notified by ramp personnel unloading at destination; of a dangerous good onboard that was not stated on the closeout. The item was found to be addressed for a different airport.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event localized to the flight deck during initial climb which dissipated  and resumed during descent. After troubleshooting and conferring with Maintenance the flight continued to destination.

ZLA Controller reported clearing a pilot for an approach who then went below minimum altitude for the area. Reporter stated the quality of the radio frequency may have caused them to miss an incorrect clearance readback and added that the radio sites in the sector have had problems for many years; making it difficult to understand communications.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight crew reported an air turn back and precautionary landing aft the LG WOW SYS FAIL message was received.
PA-30 pilot reported the left engine failed on final approach. The pilot continued to make the landing with the propeller windmilling.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during departure climb. Flight Attendant notified flight deck of a 'strong musty odor' in the aft cabin. Flight crew executed the appropriate QRH procedures and requested priority handling for a diversion.

General aviation pilot reported flying formation as a flight of two aircraft and the lead had a near miss with another aircraft when turning on final approach in visual conditions. The non formation pilot was cut off by the lead formation pilot aircraft and performed a go around to provide separation; the formation flight landed safely.

SR20 Instructor pilot reported an engine malfunction during initial climb during a training flight. The Instructor took control and returned to the departure airport; landing safely on an opposite direction runway.
Light aircraft instructor pilot departing FCM airport reported an NMAC with another light aircraft.
Captain reported a rejected take off after a right fuel pressure low annunciator was received. The Flight Crew returned to the ramp.

Air carrier Captain reported a return to the gate due to missing required Dangerous Goods documents during departure taxi. After further review it was determined that DG documents were not required due to the type of DG.
Air carrier First Officer reported his Captain had to brake aggressively to avoid a collision with another air carrier during taxi at CLT.
Cessna 182 pilot reported engine failure during climb on a skydiving flight. After skydivers jumped from aircraft; the pilot performed a dead-stick approach and landing at nearby airport.
EMB-145 Flight Attendant reported an engine failure resulted in a safe return to departure airport.
Hobby/Recreational UAS pilot reported they did not notice until airborne they were operating in controlled airspace without authorization.
C172 Instructor Pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from a military transport aircraft on approach to MYR airport.

ATC Tower controller reported receiving a call from a pilot stating the aircraft's engine had failed. The controller stated that Search and Rescue were notified of the location. About the same time the pilot reported the engine was running and requested to land.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on approach descended below the minimum vectoring altitude.
Flight Instructor reported a ground conflict with an aircraft that over flew them while in position on the runway.
Technician reported a misapplied NEF item for a damaged cockpit door peephole was discovered to be not authorized and was changed to a repair order with the aircraft taken out of service.

Air carrier Captain reported releasing the brakes and received a warning to acknowledge DG form. Upon further inquiry; the flight crew discovered that the aircraft had dry ice onboard and requested the final DG form from Dispatch.

ZNY Center Controller reported failure to notice CPDLC message timeout resulted in an aircraft flying too high on a waypoint; leading to the aircraft's encroachment into another airspace. The reporter noted that CPDLC has been extremely unreliable; and there have been several timeout and failure messages with the system.

ERJ-175 First Officer reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC when the aircraft descended below the Final Approach Fix (FAF) as a result of the First Officer having set an incorrect FAF altitude.
B747 flight crew reported receiving an alert message of smoke in crew rest area and a burning odor during initial climb. Flight returned to departure airport and landed.
Commander 112A pilot reported airspeed decayed below stall speed after encountering inflight icing during cruise. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed safely.

Gulfstream V First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into PBI from a preceding A321 that resulted in a roll and taking the flight off course to the right by about half a mile before the flight crew could get back on course.
Pilot reported a runway excursion due to over correction of steering on landing rollout.
GA flight crews reported same aircraft types and similar call signs led to ATC confusion resulted in NMAC event in traffic pattern.
Flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B737 on approach to BOS.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on short final at DEN.
A320 Captain reported multiple malfunctions after takeoff and returned to departure airport where the aircraft was towed to the gate due to loss of nosewheel steering.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported the UAS had a loss of power causing the UAS to crash. Post-flight review indicated a possibly battery issue.
Air Carrier Captain reported the nose wheel was skidding during taxi in to the gate because of the slick ramp conditions at ORD.

TRACON Controller reported turning a aircraft off the ILS approach course to avoid a collision with an opposite direction aircraft not on their frequency resulting with the aircraft flying towards terrain in VMC conditions. The aircraft landed safely at the airport.

Air carrier Marshaller reported giving the aircraft the sign to stop and the aircraft stopping on the line; but the aircraft continued to roll forwards and past the line. The Marshaller threw a chock to the front of the tire of the nose landing gear to stop the aircraft.
Bonanza 33 pilot reported the landing gear collapsed on landing.
Captain reported Cabin Pressure became uncontrollable at 20;000 feet. The flight crew descended to 10;000 feet and continued the flight in Cabin Pressure Controller manual mode.
Maintenance Technician reported chronic low tire pressures as well as cracked brake brackets on multiple B737 MAX 9 aircraft.
Air Carrier flight crew reported departure without required Hazmat documents onboard. Flight crew 'hypothesized' closeout agent accidentally removed the documents.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during departure climb and discovered they had incorrect altitude selected resulting in failure to climb.
Air carrier pilot reported a discrepancy between their charts and their navigation database regarding the distance between WURSOL and JINKAL on the RNAV Runway 14 approach to BUF.
PA-28 pilot reported an NMAC with a Vans RV-6 aircraft during departure climbout.
B737 flight crew reported being unable to retract the landing gear after takeoff. Flight crew ran checklist and resolved problem.

Pilot of a small aircraft flying VFR not in communication with ATC reported taking evasive action to avoid a passenger jet descending towards them. They reported the passenger jet appeared to take evasive action to avoid them too.
Air carrier pilot reported taxiway and ramp clearance concerns for the 777 aircraft at CLT.

Air carrier pilot reported a CFTT event while on a visual approach to DEN airport. The flight crew received a terrain warning and ATC also asked if they had the terrain in sight prompting them to manually fly the aircraft to a normal landing.

B737-800 Captain reported aircraft rolled backwards after believing they had set the parking brake prior to engine shutdown. Captain stopped the aircraft and had ground personnel pull the aircraft back to proper stop point.

CRJ-700 Captain reported on takeoff roll receiving a steering inop caution message resulting in the flight crew rejecting the takeoff and returning to the gate.; "Caravan 208B pilot reported a runway excursion during landing. After safely stopping the aircraft on the grass next to the runway; the aircraft was moved to the ramp for post flight inspection. It was discovered that the left main tire was covered in brake fluid and there was a leaking O ring in the Left Brake System Line."
A321 First Officer reported that right engine reverser failed to deploy. Post-flight inspection revealed that the reverser was locked out in a manner that was not visible during preflight.
A319 Captain reported an engine malfunction during takeoff roll resulted in a rejected takeoff and return to gate.
Air carrier flight crew reported hydraulic system failure during departure climb. Flight crew diverted and returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier Captain reported he failed to contact Dispatch to add Hazmat cargo to the flight release prior to gate departure.
First Officer reported uncontrollable vibration on the #2 Engine during cruise flight. The Flight Crew performed an in flight shut down and diverted to a precautionary landing.
Air carrier Captain reported taxiing into a gate with the use of a Marshall; while the self-parking guidance system was indicating STOP.
A flight instructor reported a NMAC while on final approach to a non-towered airport with an aircraft landing opposite direction not communicating on CTAF.
Light transport Captain reported the autopilot disengaged during arrival descent resulting in pitch down and overbanking. Captain recovered and continued the flight.
Pilot reported airline policy about Flight Attendants that were asked to work while sick.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a passenger deplaned with a rideable; battery-powered suitcase; which is against company policy to use as checked or carry-on baggage.
Air carrier flight attendant reported smoke from galley oven during push back. Oven emptied and Maintenance notified.
Pilot reported engine malfunction occurred during cruise flight that resulted in an air turn back.
Aircraft maintenance technician reported a loss of aircraft control during a tow procedure and the inability to stop the aircraft from impacting another parked aircraft before it came to a stop.
Challenger 604 First Officer reported a NMAC with a small aircraft requiring evasive action that resulted in deviation from assigned altitude.
Beechjet 400 pilot reported a near miss during descent and took evasive action to avoid a collision.
EMB-145 flight crew reported experiencing severe wake turbulence that resulted in the autopilot disconnecting.
ZDV ARTCC Controller reported ASE Approach repeatedly failed to comply with ATC procedures resulted in multiple airspace violations.

General aviation pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft during initial approach to PIE airport. The pilot identified the traffic on ADS-B; alerted ATC to the traffic; then initiated an immediate descent to avoid the conflict and told ATC the traffic had passed.
Air carrier First Officer reported descending below the glide slope on approach to MMMX; in part due to selecting Level Change mode of the FMC.
B737 Captain reported not receiving a final DG summary as there was no planned DG but upon further inquiry; finally received a summary showing that no DG was loaded.
C152 Single Pilot reported losing pedal control causing a runway excursion.

B737 flight crew reported a fumes event during cruise in the cabin that nothing was abnormal. A second flight attendant reported fumes which prompted the flight crew to divert as a precautionary maneuver due to a dry cell wheel chair loaded in fwd cargo.
L-39 Albatros pilot reported ineffective braking and steering after touchdown; resulting in a runway excursion.

C152 flight Instructor reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern occurred when another aircraft flew directly over them in the downwind leg. The flight Instructor took evasive action and then exited the traffic pattern.
PC-12 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on final approach to BOS that resulted in an upset. Control was regained and the flight landed safely.
A90 TRACON Controller reported receiving no information or resources such as maps on a new FAA-approved IFR operation in the airspace of MVY; and stated the situation is unsafe.
Light aircraft pilot reported a runway incursion at HNL that resulted in a conflict with a departing aircraft that was able to climb safely above them.
DA-40 flight instructor reported an engine malfunction while climbing and returned to the airport for a landing.
B767-300 flight crew reported hydraulic system failure during climb. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Check Airman reported receiving a EGPWS Alert on approach.
Float plane instructor reported after landing on a lake the aircraft began to take on water.
Air Carrier fight crew reported a fume event during descent. The odor described as 'dirty socks' lasted for approximately 5 minutes.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported listing tires on DSTG (Departure Staging Guide) which did not arrive prior to aircraft departure.
GA pilot reported a CFTT event during visual approach to SQL airport.
A320 pilot reported a 'dirty socks' odor during destination descent. Flight continued to a safe landing where Maintenance was requested and a fumes report was submitted.

Deadheading air carrier Captain reported noticing from his seat in the cabin that the aircraft had not been properly de-iced; and contacted the flight crew who made the decision to return to the gate at TYS for a more effective de-ice procedure.

General aviation pilot reported a NMAC nearly occurred due to the pilots of the aircraft involved making position report mistakes. The reporter stated that in their opinion the VUO airport's poorly designed airspace structure was the main cause of the NMAC event.
Air carrier Captain reported crossing restrictions and speed issues while flying into RIC airport on the RNAV (RNP) Y Runway 16 Approach.
Air Carrier Captain reported a discrepancy in the preflight Dangerous Goods documents. After conferring with Dispatch the DG cargo was offloaded and accurate documents were issued to the flight crew.
LAX Tower Controller reported a rogue bus breaking regulations and causing problems on an airport alleyway.
Flight Crew reported loss of CPC2 [Cabin Pressure Controller] caused a descent and subsequent maintenance action; resulting in a plane swap at destination airport.
GA pilot reported during cruise they smelled burning oil and noticed the oil pressure was reading zero. Five minutes later the engine failed and they had to complete an engine out landing.
GA pilot reported an NMAC with a skydiver in the vicinity of Pleasants Peak; California while on approach to SNA airport.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported an undeclared Dangerous Goods parcel tendered by the USPS for a connecting flight.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they inadvertently flew about 400 feet AGL as a result of failing to set the remote control to the correct altitude.
Captain reported an engine oil contamination warning light at cruise. The Captain secured the engine and landed safely at the nearest airport which was the destination.
Air carrier Captain reported an engine caught fire after parking at the gate. There was no fire indication message but aircraft status page displayed a Eng Fuel Value R.
Air carrier flight crew reported misunderstanding GSO ATC taxi instructions resulting in a runway incursion.
Air carrier Captain reported Hazmat documents missing required data. After conferring with ground personnel with no corrected documents located; the Hazmat cargo was unloaded.
General aviation pilot reported they failed to extend the landing gear resulting in a gear up landing.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported the stick shaker activated on approach. The crew reconfigured the aircraft and continued on a stabilized approach.
B737-800 flight crew reported a fume event during boarding due to a Lithium Ion battery thermal fire from a passengers Iphone.
Flight Attendant reported being notified during taxi by a passenger that his gate checked bag contained his Laptop. Captain was notified and flight returned to gate to retrieve the Laptop.
Student pilot on initial climb from a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with a helicopter flying through the pattern not communicating on CTAF.

Air carrier flight crew reported both engine thrust reversers failed to operate during the landing roll out. The pilots reported using full braking to stop the aircraft and brake temperatures rose into the caution range.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict with a ground cargo vehicle during taxi.
C-337 student pilot reported a dual Alternator failure inflight. Aircraft landed without incident.

SR-22 pilot reported a tire failure upon hard braking after landing on a night currency flight at a non-towered airport. The pilot cleared the runway; then needed assistance to move the aircraft to the ramp to repair the tire.

Q400 flight crew reported diverting after a flap malfunction while on approach and while handling the situation; may have also potentially went over the airspeed restrictions for the stuck flap configuration.
C150 Pilot reported that during initial climb out a jet doing a low approach from the opposite end of the runway passed directly over the Cessna; requiring evasive action.

Air Carrier flight crew reported being notified by FA1 of a fume event during pushback. Flight crew executed a gate return for maintenance action. No definitive cause was determined but the forward galley oven was the likely cause.

Single Pilot reported after takeoff the mounting bracket for the GPS device broke. The GPS device fell onto the control stick and was dangling by its cord. During this time the pilot reported deviating off course and creating a NMAC with an arriving aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported lavatory hot water tank overheating.

Embraer 550 Captain reported a faulty ground cooling fan caused multiple systems failures and complicated several flight legs. Maintenance continued to suggest to just power down the aircraft and power back up again to rest the fault; which the Captain was dissatisfied with.
TRACON Controller reported having to repeat multiple transmissions due to clarity issues while having to wear a face mask.

Helicopter pilot reported VHF Radio interference at night while landing at the Shady Grove Hospital in Rockville; Maryland creates communications difficulties during a critical phase of flight. This is a recurring issue; reportedly caused by faulty ground lighting at the Landing Zone.
Pilot performing a pipeline survey in the vicinity of Class D airspace reported an NMAC with an aircraft executing a touch and go.  Reporter took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
B787 flight crew reported misdiagnosing a hydraulic system failure as flap and slat systems failure. The flight crew continued to destination airport and made a precautionary landing.
EMB-170 Captain reported a Slat Fail EICAS message on approach. The fault was cleared during trouble shooting; but the Captain neglected to enter a discrepancy in the log book for maintenance.
A TRACON Controller reported they accepted a handoff on an aircraft then noticed the previous sector left the aircraft at an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Citation V pilot reported temporary loss of aircraft control while inadvertently flying into convective weather.
GA pilot reported not contacting ATC at the required DME due to lack a of flying time during the pandemic.
A Boeing 767 First Officer reported a generator failure while at cruise altitude; resulting in an inflight diversion.
Mooney M20J pilot reported left main tire failure likely due to FOD on landing resulting in runway excursion. Aircraft came to stop and was towed to hangar.
Pilot reported a NMAC while on final approach at F70 non towered airport during a training flight. Pilot performed a go-around and continued to landing.
B787 air carrier flight crew reported improper FMS autoflight mode during approach went unnoticed by crew until below specified allowed altitude. Correction was made and flight continued.

A Center Controller reported an aircraft approaching their destination became disoriented when Tower turned the runway lights off. The aircraft descended from their assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Global 5500 pilot reported low altitude alert from the tower on approach.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event during departure taxi. Captain checked with the Flight Attendants who confirmed fumes present throughout the cabin. A safe ground gate return was executed.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a ground conflict with another aircraft who did not properly space their approach at the non-towered airport and landed while the instructor was still on the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported a NMAC during departure from SFB airport requiring a descent as advised by the RA to avoid a possible collision.

B-737-800 First Officer reported uncommanded aircraft movement and confusion during push back when the ground crew disconnected the tow bar and communication from the flight deck before the parking brake was set; due to a language barrier. The Captain set the parking brake and no injury or damage occurred.
A320 First Officer reported an air turn back was caused by a LGICU failure. The flight crew switched aircraft and continued the flight.
A319 pilot reported receiving an ECAM message during cruise for Cond FWD Cargo Duct Overheat and a loss of B system hydraulic fluid. The crew advised ATC and returned to the departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported the PAPI for the DCA RWY15 does not coincide with the RNAV 15 nor a stable visual approach path.
Air carrier ground personnel reported returning to the gate to secure HAZMAT.
Challenger 650 Captain reported a stabilizer trim malfunction during cruise flight. The Captain diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.

Challenger 605 flight crew reported an upset event during climb. The pilot flying immediately disconnected the autopilot and autothrottles and worked with the pilot monitoring to regain aircraft control. The flight crew elected to perform an air turn back to departure airport.

Air carrier Captain reported during push back from the gate; the tug operator stopped the push and requested to return to the gate to avoid a wingtip collision with another aircraft inbound on the same taxiway.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported experiencing failure of the outer pane of the First Officer's cockpit window. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
A Corporate jet pilot reported they taxied onto a closed taxiway at MEI due to insufficient closed taxiway markings and lack of signage.
Air Carrier Flight Attendants reported a fume event during descent. The odor was described as dirty socks and was confirm by the Captain.
A319 flight crew reported landing safely after shutting down #1 engine on descent in response to an ECAM direction.
Air Carrier flight crew on approach to BUF airport reported Ground Spoiler malfunction possibly due to 5G interference.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in a firefighting TFR area without authorization.
A320 flight crew reported receiving a 'pull up' terrain warning while descending on arrival at night in a mountainous area. The flight crew executed an immediate climb and continued the arrival.

Air carrier flight crew reported conflicting company guidance regarding battery configuration of battery powered wheelchair transport. Ultimately the flight crew elected to transport the batteries in the passenger cabin as stated in the FOM.
Air carrier Captain reported extreme fatigue due to the companies scheduling and personal activities. The Captain called in fatigued to the company and was removed from flying duties.
Air carrier Captain reported Ground Personnel approached the aircraft and opened the Forward Cargo Door prior to engine shut down. There were no injuries.
Flight Instructor  reported descending too low in the pattern at COS before becoming aware of the situation and then regained altitude.

EMB-145 flight crew reported smoke in the cockpit shortly after takeoff. Flight crew requested ATC priority handling for an immediate air return to departure airport where a safe landing was executed.
B737 First Officer reported loss of engine oil quantity during cruise. Flight crew diverted and after landing found evidence of oil leak.

Air Carrier A320 Captain reported a momentary loss of aircraft control during taxi with the taxiway appearing to be compacted with ice or snow.  The event required the Captain to use the thrust reversers in order to avoid a taxiway excursion.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPS was jammed departing DXB and never returned. ATC advised the crew they were in airspace that was not authorized due to their limited navigational capabilities.
ATC reported a UAS crew flying a UAS near a controlled airport where they were not authorized to operate.
A flight instructor reported their aircraft had a rough running engine on initial climb out so they returned to the airport.
CRJ700 First Officer reported that a loss of directional control during the takeoff roll led to a rejected takeoff.
GA flight instructor on training flight reported NMAC with another aircraft landing on nearby runway at GFK airport.

B757-200 flight crew reported failure on both left fuel boost pumps in flight. The flight crew requested priority handling and descended to run the check lists and QRH procedures. The flight crew then proceeded direct to destination airport and made a precautionary landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported loss of GPS and transponder on arrival into DEN. Systems returned to normal after landing.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel at destination reported missing required Hazmat documents for Hazmat Dangerous Goods shipment.

A321 flight crew reported after taking the runway for takeoff and setting power; they received an ECAM message ENG 2 FAIL and rejected the takeoff; securing the engine and discharging the engine fire bottle. They required a tow back to the gate due to loss of nose wheel steering.
Air Carrier Captain reported the in-flight loss of one hydraulic system. The pilot crew followed the QRH procedures and landed at their destination.

Small aircraft single pilot reported an NMAC while flying from a non-towered airport to a nearby towered airport. There had been no communication on UNICOM prior; other than the position report. The other aircraft had executed a go-around due to the close proximity.
Air Carrier Pilots reported an un-stabilized approach that set off the EICAS warning 'Don't Sink' near the runway threshold. An uneventful landing was accomplished.
PA-28 flight instructor and student reported engine failure on departure; made an immediate return to the departure airport; and landed.
B767 First Officer reported electronic engine control and throttle malfunction resulted in a return to departure airport.
Cessna 208 pilot experienced a flap system asymmetry; resulting in a significant roll. Flaps were retracted and a no-flap landing was accomplished.
A Tower Local Controller reported they forgot to issue appropriate control instructions to an arriving aircraft and it flew below the minimum safe altitude and initiated a turn away from terrain.
Pilot departing a non towered airport reported they entered the runway with an arriving aircraft on short final which executed a go around to avoid a collision.
C172 pilot reported flying the aircraft back to the runway after noticing poor climb performance shortly after takeoff.

Pilatus PC-12 Captain reported a left brake anomaly while taxiing; which appeared to be resolved after troubleshooting. On a subsequent flight; the left rudder pedal felt stiff; resulting in an asymmetric braking tendency after landing.

TRACON Controller reported that a departure from TEB appeared to be turning and climbing toward an arrival into EWR. Controller attempted to contact the departure aircraft; but they were not on frequency. Tower Controller from departure airport resolved the issue.
Pilot reported a NMAC due to incomplete ATC instructions leading them to turn base leg when ATC wanted them to remain on downwind.
Captain reported refusing the aircraft due to disagreement on maintenance actions.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they learned during post-flight they inadvertently flew in a TFR without authorization.
B737-800 Captain reported a NMAC with another aircraft during departure climbout. Flight crew took evasive action to avoid a collision.
C172 pilot conducting solo training maneuvers reported the engine quit during a maneuver due to the reporter using the wrong air/fuel mixture for their altitude and type aircraft.

A319 Captain reported the aircraft began to slide towards another aircraft during pushback from the gate. As the brakes were ineffective; reverse thrust was needed to stop the uncontrolled slide. Reverse thrust was maintained until the tug was reconnected and the aircraft was towed back to the gate until conditions improved.
Cessna 170 general aviation pilot reported a bounced landing resulting in a runway excursion. The aircraft was damaged with no injuries reported.
Tecnam PC2012 pilot reported while flying into icing conditions; the TKS system failed in high mode and the backup pump wasn't adequate so they decided to return to the departure airport.
B757 flight crew conducting a test flight reported a tail strike during takeoff rotation.
ATR-72 Captain reported receiving a master caution alert for right engine low oil pressure on departure. The flight was able to land safely at the departure airport.
B737 flight crew reported engine failure and airframe vibrations during climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and performed a single-engine landing.

General aviation instructor pilot reported an early turn during a night departure with a student during a training flight; resulting in a departure heading procedure deviation and overflight of the ramp at about 75 feet.. The instructor returned to the departure procedure and continued the flight.
Air carrier Captain reported filing reroute to avoid weather; but TMU issued a reroute resulting in an unsafe flight into weather.
Pilot reported aborting the takeoff roll after he observed an aircraft entering the runway from a taxiway.
C208 pilot reported loss of engine power and a return to the airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported being cleared for CABAB but the flight crew mistakenly proceeded direct to another fix that was similar; CEDAB.
EMB-175 flight crew reported multiple avionics system failures in cruise flight. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
A tower local Controller reported they allowed a military aircraft to land with another aircraft stopped at an intersection on the runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported control difficulties while executing a go-around at DCA after encountering wind shear.

Air Carrier pilot reported a critical ground conflict during taxi with an aircraft that failed to yield as instructed by ATC. Pilot applied heavy braking to avoid a collision resulting in possible Flight Attendant injury.
B787 Maintenance Technician reported the flap track fairing drain tubes have continuously been found broken.
Tug driver reported almost hitting a parked aircraft during pus back.
B737 NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while in the trail of a B747 during a descent.
Air carrier First Officer reported during cruise flight experiencing a cockpit window shattering resulting in the flight crew diverting to another airport where they landed safely.
Cessna 310 pilot reported while performing normal checks in the run-up area; the right engine caught fire. The pilot cut mixtures to both engines; and evacuated the aircraft.
C402 pilot reported a loss of engine power and engine roughness resulted in a return to the departure airport.
Fractional jet flight crew reported they failed to notice the aircraft descended below a published crossing restriction on an approach.
A C172 instructor reported the student descended to 300 feet above the ground while practicing an engine out approach.
A320 Captain reported Brake Sys 1 and 2; plus N/W steering fault ECAM messages in flight and elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
B747-400 Captain reported ground deice crew noticed damage to the wing leading edge from engine cowl ice ejection that grounded the aircraft.
Flight instructor reported an NMAC with a UAS during cruise flight. No evasive action was taken.
ARTCC Controller reported working an aircraft that requested an immediate descent and had traffic below them. The Controller issued a vector and descent resulting in a conflict alert.
Air carrier Captain and Flight Attendant reported the Captain rejected the takeoff when the forward entry door light was illuminated on takeoff.

B777-200 flight crew reported the Accessible Dangerous Goods (ADG) were incorrectly configured for inspection before air transport. After communicating with ground personnel; the cargo was reconfigured to be in compliance with company procedures and the flight crew were able to properly perform the inspection.

Air carrier Captain reported the undocumented transport of a battery powered wheelchair that had been loaded at departure airport.  The Captain stated the flight crew was not notified on any wheelchair being loaded on the aircraft.
B737 NG First Officer reported landing safely following an unstabilized approach to SFO during which they encountered wind shear.

Air carrier flight crew reported a terrain obstacle alert while descending on the initial approach at night on a visual approach. The crew leveled out; then stabilized late on the approach; and continued the visual approach to landing.
A321 flight crew reported receiving an ECAM F/CTL SLATS FAULT on approach. The flight crew requested a go-around and ran the QRH and checklists and landed safely.
A seated passenger reported observing a UAS while on an air carrier flight on final approach to a Class B airport.
CE-680A flight crew reported an encounter with either wake or atmospheric turbulence in cruise flight at FL230 that resulted in minor injuries to the Captain.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving ECAM message regarding IDG overheat during taxi for takeoff. Flight crew returned to the gate.

Mooney 20C instructor pilot reported a landing gear operational issue during landing on a training flight. The gear collapsed upon touchdown and stopped on the runway with aircraft damage and no injuries.
B737 Captain reported receiving a new final dangerous goods summary during taxi; despite having already acknowledged a final summary at the gate.

Center Controller working Flight Data position issued a clearance to an aircraft departing a non towered airport and assigned the aircraft an altitude below the Minimum Enroute Altitude. The reporter misidentified the departure airport due to improper use of automation software.
C310 Pilot reported Landing Gear Failed to Extend during approach. After several low approaches and trouble shooting; the pilot elected to do a gear up landing.
PA-24 pilot reported an electrical failure followed by gear up landing.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft below the MVA; then cleared the aircraft for a visual approach after confirming the aircraft had the airport in sight.

First Officer reported Taxiway A at MMGL airport was undergoing heavy construction and that the 'new' Taxiway A was not listed in the NOTAMs or displayed properly on their taxi diagram charts/display.
EMB Phenom 300 pilot reported a ground conflict with another aircraft during taxi resulting in a taxi way excursion.
Air carrier Captain reported landing at FLL and exiting the runway at an earlier taxiway than normal that then led to an active runway. Reporter suggested a hotspot designation.
A320 Captain reported rejected takeoff due to compressor vane fault ECAM message. Flight crew shut down affected engine and aircraft was taken out of service.
B737-700 flight crew reported encountering moderate to severe mountain wave for which led to a rapid decay of their airspeed.
B737 First Officer reported an air conditioning pack failure caused extreme heat in the cockpit so they diverted to a nearby airport.
Helicopter pilot near a Class D airport witnessed a UAS flying in controlled airspace without authorization. The pilot found the UAS pilot and made them aware of the location and the UAS landed.
A TRACON Controller reported Tower allowed aircraft to depart opposite direction into arrival traffic due to confusion during a runway configuration change.
Corporate pilot reported having to take evasive maneuvers to avoid a UAS during climb out.
DA40 pilot reported departing while there was traffic still on the runway.  The pilot made a steep climb to maximize the altitude over the traffic.

A320 Captain reported the First Officer's rudder pedals became unlocked during landing flare.  The controls were immediately transferred to the Captain; yet the incident still resulted in a firm landing.

EMB-175 flight crew reported the Integrated Drive Generator (IDG) failed while on downwind for the approach and led to the aircraft losing electrical power. The flight crew was unable to restore power through the APU and more EICAS messages began to appear; including a failure with the Ram Air Turbine (RAT). After landing; the flight crew lost nose wheel steering and required a tow back to the gate.

L39 Check Airman/Instructor reported the student mismanaged the aircraft's energy state during a simulated engine flameout pattern approach and landing resulting in a runway excursion.  No damage was incurred.
CRJ700 Pilots reported an EGPWS Terrain Warning while on final approach. The pilots stated their actions interrupted the autopilot's sequence and caused the aircraft to descend below the glide slope.
CE-525 First Officer reported receiving a double caution annunciation; L and R ENG CTRL SYS FAULT; in cruise. Flight crew proceeded to perform an air turnback.
EMB-145 Captain reported getting a terrain alert and stick shaker while intercepting the approach. The flight crew responded to both; which eventually dissipated.
Air carrier flight crew reported a fume event during preflight while parked at gate. After maintenance trouble shooting an aircraft swap was ordered.

General aviation pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert despite being centered on the glide path. According to the reporter; this problem has reoccurred multiple times at GTU with different runways.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on approach to CHO.
Air carrier Captain reported a fumes event during approach which was described as an acrid electrical odor. The flight crew continued to a safe landing.

Air Carrier Captain reported while taxing for departure another aircraft taxied across the nose of their aircraft resulting in the Captain taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.

A321 Captain reported being notified by the flight attendants of a fumes event during initial climb which dissipated shortly thereafter. After conferring with flight attendants; the flight continued to destination where a logbook discrepancy and fumes report was submitted.
Flight Instructor reported the student lost directional control during landing roll resulting in a runway excursion and aircraft damage.
An Air Carrier Captain reported abnormal engine vibrations in flight and while taxiing were due to a previously reported damaged fan blade that maintenance had not documented.
An A320 First Officer reported low speed rejected T/O at 70 knots due to F/CTL ELAC 2 PITCH  FAULT. The flight crew returned to the gate for aircraft repairs.

B737 Captain reported First Officer's Airspeed failed with IAS Disagree was displayed on both PFDs in cruise. The flight crew ran the QRH and check lists; then both EECs went into Soft Alternate Mode and Speed Trim Failed. The flight crew requested priority handling and landed at destination airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported aircraft problems possibly associated with 5G interference inbound to PBI.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported temporary loss of aircraft control due to severe turbulence on approach.
Pilot in an experimental tailwheel aircraft reported a runway excursion after landing in a quartering tailwind.  Right main gear and propeller were damaged with no injuries to pilot.

Air Carrier maintenance technician reported a fume event in an improperly ventilated maintenance hanger during aircraft spray painting. Hanger doors were opened and painting ceased after notifying supervisor.

Helicopter pilot reported that during landing at night they flew over an aircraft that was holding on the runway numbers and did not realize it until the aircraft pilot advised them. Reporter stated the aircraft had been not visible and there were no TCAS warnings observed during approach.
B737-700 flight crew reported improper clean up sequence during departure climb resulting in momentary aircraft performance issues.
Pilot reported an off airport landing after engine power loss precluded an airport landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of some auto flight equipment; including Heads-up Display; Flight Director; and Autothrottle on short final at EIDW; citing possible 5G interference.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from preceding A320 on short final to DTW. Reporter suggested more spacing behind Airbus aircraft.
DA42 Flight Instructor reported a diversion to a nearby airport due to a fuel leak.
A320 Captain reported a fumes event during descent described as a 'musty dirty socks' odor. Flight crew completed the cabin odor isolation procedure which eliminated the odor.
A320 First Officer reported a HYD G RSVR LO LVL ECAM at cruise. The flight crew returned to departure airport and landed.
C170 pilot reported a NMAC while turning crosswind in the traffic pattern when another departing aircraft made an early; unannounced crosswind turn; passing below the reporter's aircraft.
EMB-145 flight crew reported an EGPWS (Enhanced Ground Proximity Warning System) activation during approach.
Pilot reported during final approach to ORD they received several abnormal terrain warnings and elected to continue to landing. Reporter stated 5G NOTAMs were in effect.
General aviation pilot reported an NMAC during descent to a non-towered airport. Pilot took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.

Cessna 680A flight crew reported while being vectored for approach in IMC they got several CAS messages. Engine control faults for both engines; both attitude and heading and air data computer faults.
Pilot of a corporate turboprop aircraft reported deploying maximum braking and reverse thrust to avoid a collision with a truck that entered the runway.
Air Carrier Captain reported Final DG summary provided to the flight crew prior to pushback indicated no DG loaded. Dispatch messaged a revised DG summary during climb stating DG cargo was loaded.
B747-400 flight crew reported cargo improperly handled causing false cargo fire warning on ground.
Air carrier First Officer reported there is a group of lights within the runway center lights at BWI airport that appear to be brighter than the others creating an optical illusion.
Center Controller reported having a loss of separation after assuming a busy sector from a training team.
Hobbyist drone pilot was unaware they were flying in controlled airspace until approached by a public official.
