B737 First Officer reported receiving a Flight Control Master Caution for Feel Differential Pressure and low hydraulic quantity indication during climb out. Flight crew diverted and landed safely.
A TRACON Controller reported they descended an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude due to misreading the Minimum Vectoring Map.

EMB-145 Check Airman and trainee First Officer reported an airspeed deterioration and a stall warning from the aircraft during approach. The new First Officer was pilot flying and thought the configuration of the aircraft was ready for landing. The aircraft experienced the warning and the Captain completed the landing.
General aviation pilot struck a UAS while on approach. After landing and during inspection of the aircraft the pilot found damage to the wing of the aircraft.
Captain reported landing gear did not extend during approach to landing. The Flight Crew used alternate procedures to extend the gear and continued the flight to landing.
B737 flight crew reported receiving an overheat annunication for #2 engine during departure climb. Flight crew reduced thrust on affected engine and returned to departure airport.

Air carrier Captain reported the Flight Directors were not turned on which resulted in no airspeed bugs displaying during the takeoff roll. After the takeoff roll started the takeoff was rejected by the Captain and the flight returned to the gate.

Student pilot reported they had received clearance to taxi and to hold short of the runway for landing traffic but did not stop and crossed the hold short line. The flight instructor took evasive action and applied the brakes.
Flight crew reported ILS Glideslope interference on final inbound to MIA along with a missed frequency change.
A321 flight crew reported uncommanded movement during pushback procedure.
Pilot reported during ramp check and log book review; they discovered a propeller AD was overflown.
Government UAS crew reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
A321 Captain reported encountering significant wake turbulence from an A380 in cruise flight on a trans-Atlantic route.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended much quicker than anticipated and flew below their Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Recreational UAS pilot reported while flying their UAS they heard a fast approaching helicopter. The UAS pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision and landed the UAS.

B747-800 Captain reported a Dual FMS Failure during descent. The flight crew reloaded the route information and continued to land at destination airport. The Captain entered the failures into the aircraft log book for Maintenance.
C152 flight instructor reported an electrical malfunction and eventually lost all electrical power before safely landing.
Corporate Captain reported they deviated off course while climbing out from TEB when they proceeded direct VILLS; but had been cleared direct ZILLS. Both fixes were on the aircraft's route of flight.
A TRACON Controller reported a departing Cessna deviated from the departure procedure and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Beech 99 pilot reported returning to departure airport after receiving a trim out of range warning shortly after takeoff.

PSP Tower Controller reported received IFR handoff from TRACON on a visual approach clearance while heading towards terrain at an unsafe altitude. Controller issued a climb clearance and returned handoff to TRACON.
Pilot on a training flight reported an NMAC in the vicinity of SAC VOR.
Government UAS pilot reported the UAS struck a tree and crashed while they were diagnosing an automation issue. The UAS was damaged as a result.
Air taxi flight crew reported the alternator failed on departure and they returned to the airport.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a malfunction with passenger door in flight. The crew elected to divert back to the departure airport.
PA28 Flight Instructor reported engine did not produce full takeoff power; resulting in rejected takeoff.
First Officer reported that Maintenance Control requested an air turn back due to pitot/static ice protection logic failing. The flight returned to departure airport and landed.

SA-227 Flight Crew reported landing gear failed to retract when selected up after take off. The Flight Crew ran the QRH procedures and elected to perform an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air Taxi Captain on approach to SAT reported a Traffic Alert from ATC followed by a TCAS/RA. Captain took evasive action to avoid an unidentified VFR aircraft outside of the Class C airspace.
Flight crew reported concerns over maintenance practices during an engine parameter exceedance event.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft made a wrong turn resulted in entering a higher MVA. The controller issued a climb to the aircraft.
Air carrier Ramp Agent reported PTKK Airport has a safety problem with jet blast from departing aircraft.

DC-3 flight crew reported engine vibration; smoke; and the illumination of the chip detector light; led to the in flight shutdown of an engine and a diversion to a suitable airport where a safe landing was accomplished.

B737-800 First Officer reported the Captain overbanked the aircraft during arrival while maneuvering away from an area of severe weather. Captain corrected overbank and flight continued on approach to landing.
Air carrier ground personnel reported not being notified that hazmat was added to the flight.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming that was then confirmed by Salt Lake Center.
C182 Pilot reported landing on a runway that was already occupied by a helicopter and the need to employ hard braking to avoid a collision.
ERJ170 First Officer reported the flying pilot's failed planning on a visual arrival routing resulted in an unstable approach and terrain alert.
Public safety UAS pilot reported an NMAC with an uninvolved helicopter during a public safety flight. No evasive action was taken.
B757 reported encountering either severe turbulence or wake from the preceding A319 departing EWR.

Experimental PA-18 pilot reported during cruise noticed the engine oil pressure dropping and immediately turned back to the airport. Engine finally shutdown and the pilot had to land on an unoccupied road.
Air carrier flight crew reported they had to brake abruptly to avoid a collision with a van while taxiing at IAH airport.
C172 pilot reported an oily rag on cabin heat intake caught fire. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.

BN-2 check airman and trainee reported limited control of aircraft after takeoff. Flight crew returned to departure airport where post flight they found a gust lock had not been removed from the tail.
Captain reported two rejected take offs for a Anti-Ice Master Caution. The flight crew returned to the gate where maintenance determined a Cowl Anti-Ice Valve to be intermittently faulting.

B737-800 crew reported a ground hazard event when performing an air cart engine start at the gate when the First Officer started the right engine after the Captain commanded the start of the left engine. The Captain identified the incorrect engine starter engaged and terminated the start.
Ramp Agent reported ground damage to an aircraft due to a malfunctioning belt loader. Maintenance was notified.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on the River Visual approach to DCA Runway 19.
A banner tow pilot on approach to a non towered airport reported they descended below low ceilings to remain VFR and flew too low over terrain and obstacles.
Ground Personnel reported aircraft Dangerous Guide indicated pit 5 planned empty by CLP at departure. Upon arrival unload; two bags of international trash were discovered in pit 5.
Ground personnel reported they were towing an aircraft on a runway when they observed an aircraft on short final initiate a go-around.

GA pilot reported an NMAC event in the traffic pattern after an ATC trainee mistakenly cleared two aircraft to land on same runway simultaneously. ATC issued an immediate 'left 360 for traffic separation'.
B737 NG flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence at FL360 in trail of a narrow body Airbus aircraft.
Gulfstream V flight crew reported impacting another aircraft's wingtip at the start of taxi.

Air carrier Captain reported confusion in maneuvering on a new taxiway at DEN. The First Officer's AMM (Airport Moving Map) looked normal but the Captain's Jeppesen charts; despite being current; did not have the new taxiway charted.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of pressurization due to door not being sealed which resulted in a return to the departure airport.
B737 Captain reported hard braking to avoid a collision with a taxiing aircraft.

Air Carrier Captain reported being unable to comply with ATC speed assignments due to fuel concerns and was unable to comply with a crossing restriction due to ATC stopping their descent for VFR traffic. The Captain reported Center and Approach Controllers criticized and questioned them on the frequency over both issues.
A321 First Officer reported a fume event during descent; which was also experienced by the flight attendants. Flight landed safely and a logbook entry was submitted.

Air carrier Captain reported a severe turbulence encounter during cruise which resulted in large airspeed and altitude deviations. The flight climbed to minimize the turbulence; regained aircraft control; and no injuries occurred.
CRJ550 flight crew reported high engine vibration during takeoff climb. Flight crew diverted and landed uneventfully.
J3 Cub pilot reported during hand-prop engine start the aircraft jumped over the chock and hit a tree.
A321 pilot reported loss of green hydraulic system on climb out. The flight crew diverted to nearest suitable airport.

Center Controller and pilot reported that while attempting to land at a non towered airport at night the runway lights would not activate so the pilot diverted to a nearby airport. The pilot executed a missed approach to divert but did not comply with ATC instructions and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitudes.

Air carrier Captain reported during a CAT III approach in VMC the aircraft began tracking to the left of the course; requiring the Pilot Flying to disconnect the auto pilot and continue the approach to landing.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft was not under positive control for a short time during pushback at MDPC/PUJ airport after experiencing communication difficulties with ground personnel.
A320 Captain reported a fumes event during descent starting at FL180. Fumes dissipated gradually and completely dissipated during approach and landing.

Non-identified report observer identified a safety concern and a near collision with another aircraft while observing a tug driver operating recklessly with an aircraft under tow. The tug driver operated at a high taxi speed and aggressive maneuvering on the taxiway; then parked at a gate.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in a TFR without authorization.
Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft's E/E compartment was flooded; from a severe storm; after the forward cargo compartment was left open.
EMB-505 pilot reported an electrical system malfunction resulted in a diversion.

A321 crew reported an 'ENG 2 EGT OVERLIMIT' ECAM during takeoff.  Crew returned to the field after shutting down the engine but were unstabilized and had to perform a go-around followed by a safe landing.
Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft. It was later determined that the Tower radar was intermittent.
A321 Flight Attendant reported a 'strange musty odor' during taxi. Flight returned to gate and did not take aircraft to destination.
PA-28 student pilot and his instructor reported the aircraft was damaged after the nose landing gear failed on landing resulting in a runway excursion.
A321 Captain reported receiving an alert message of #2 engine EGT limit exceedance during initial climb. Flight crew performed inflight shutdown and returned to departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported pressurization problem inflight. Returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
CE680 Captain reported unusual engine vibration resulted in diversion.
B737 Captain reported a lack of coordination and communications problems overly complicated a maintenance situation and resulted in a late departure after an aircraft swap.
A320 flight crew reported on takeoff roll receiving a ENG 1 COMPRESSOR VANE ECAM message resulting in a rejected takeoff and return to the gate.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a fume event that resulted in a diversion. After landing when the APU bleed and packs were on; the odor returned.
A300 Captain reported hydraulic system malfunction during departure climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely after manually extending landing gear.
Dispatcher reported frustration at the workload on various Desks during normal shift.
A Center Controller reported they did not correct a pilot readback of the wrong frequency resulting in loss of contact with the aircraft as it flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Flight Instructor with student reported a temporary loss of control due to wind gusts on short final resulted in them being aligned with the wrong runway.
MU-2 Pilot reported improper autopilot operation produced a severe nose over pitch resulting in an altitude diversion.
Cessna 172 pilot reported loss of directional control on landing roll that resulted in a runway excursion and a broken runway light.
Air Carrier Captain reported a fumes event during descent characterized as 'acetone/fume' odor. Number 1 Pack selected off which seemed to dissipate fumes.
Air carrier Captain reported the A Flight Attendant notified the flight deck of a fume event during gate pushback. The flight returned to gate.

Air carrier pilot reported GPS spoofing at cruise over the Mediterranean Sea while under ATC control during daylight conditions. The pilot observed the aircraft at an incorrect position; which alerted the pilot to the GPS spoofing event; which lasted for a short duration then resolved.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported possibly flying in a TFR without authorization.
Student pilot reported during landing they lost directional control and initiated a go around. During the go around the aircraft came into contact with a runway edge light.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a strong electrical odor during initial climb. After completing memory items priority handling to departure airport was provided where a safe landing was executed.
Air carrier Captain reported a tug driver drove at high speed directly in front of the marshaller during parking requiring the Captain to brake firmly in order to avoid a collision.
B737 flight crew reported a below glide slope warning while on final resulting in an unstable approach. The crew corrected the low profile glide path and landed.

Tower Controllers reported ground control developmental issued an unsafe altitude on departure which resulted in an MVA violation and a CFTT event. Controllers also reported distracted by a Tower tour group.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a dangerous situation during gate pushback. Tug crew did not follow SOP nor did they use standard phraseology.
Air carrier B737NG flight crew reported a Flight Attendant was slightly injured when they encountered wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL310.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft that ATC did not see or call as traffic.

Air Carrier flight crew reported being notified of a passenger bag containing a Lithium Battery incorrectly checked in cargo. Flight crew had the bag removed and placed in the overhead bin in passenger cabin prior to departure.
C182 pilot reported they had misidentified traffic instructed to follow resulted a NMAC event.

BD700 flight crew reported a loss of nose wheel steering after landing during the rollout. The aircraft departed the runway and stopped on the adjacent hard pavement. After cycling the steering switch; steering control was regained and the jet taxied under its own power to the ramp.
Light aircraft pilot reported descending to approximately 250 feet above the ground on approach to VCB after losing situational awareness.

GA flight instructor reported an NMAC during final approach to CVO non-towered airport.  Reporter stated the other aircraft never made any radio calls during the approach which contributed to the event.
Dash 8-400 Captain reported jammed pitch trim system severe enough to cause a diversion.

EMB-145 Captain reported an NMAC during cruise requiring the crew to disengage the autopilot and execute a climb maneuver to clear the conflict.  The conflicting VFR aircraft was not in communication with ATC.
PA-28 instructor reported a left main gear collapse on landing during a training flight. The aircraft came to a stop on the runway.
A319 Captain reported landing safely after receiving an ECAM message for a nose wheel steering fault.
A320 flight crew reported failure of hydraulic pump in cruise.

Cessna 172 pilot reported an engine malfunction during cruise on a day flight in VMC conditions. The pilot performed checklist procedures; troubleshooting; contacted ATC; and diverted to a non-towered airport and landed safety.
CE550 pilot reported during takeoff roll an aircraft that had just landed was still on the runway. Flight crew climbed over the aircraft.
Pilot reported overlooking a log book entry after annual and flew the aircraft home with a discrepancy recorded against the propeller assembly for a red die leak.
N90 TRACON Controller reported an NMAC with an IFR and a VFR aircraft.
A small aircraft pilot on initial climb from a touch and go at a non-towered airport reported a NMAC with a helicopter departing a parallel taxiway.
Tecnam P2006 pilot reported engine oil leak in flight. Pilot landed as a precaution and oil filter was found to be source of leak.
Employee towing a bag cart collided with a van in the ramp area.
BE-F90 pilot reported a terrain warning during final approach in IMC conditions. Pilot performed a missed approach and diverted to the alternate airport and landed safely.
Air carrier pilot reported incorrect taxiway markings were painted at taxiway intersections at ORD airport.
Flight Crew reported diverting after encountering engine vibrations severe enough to cause multiple systems failures. Flight Crew reported ice build up was most likely the cause of the vibrations.
Air carrier First Officer reported an NMAC event during final approach with a helicopter flying below them.
PA28 Instructor reported transponder failure resulted in a NMAC by traffic overflying them.
BE55 pilot reported a dual prop strike while their Flight Instructor in right seat was doing touch and goes for currency.

Ramp Agent reported procedural deviations and communications problems resulted in maintenance starting an aircraft engine at the gate without anti collision lights illuminated and without properly equipped ground safety observers in place; allowing ramp personnel to approach the area of the running engine.

Air carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation while on the DINGO 5 approach to TUS.  The reporter cited 'Descend Via' vs. 'Descend and maintain' phraseology and confusing chart verbiage contributed to event.

Flight Instruction with student reported during arrival they descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude by more than 500 feet and believes they should have cancelled their IFR clearance sooner and continued with a visual approach to avoid the altitude deviation.
An Air Carrier pilot flew off course when they programmed the fix DENTT in the FMS instead of the correct fix DENNT.
Air Carrier flight crew reported Obstacle Alert warning on approach to LEX airport.
Military helicopter pilot reported an onboard flight instructor reported an NMAC with a UAS during final approach. No evasive action was taken.

Air Carrier Dispatcher reported incorrect cargo weight & balance data and also incorrect Hazmat cargo details had been issued to the flight crew at departure. The correct data was transmitted to the flight crew via ACARS.
A321 Captain reported a fumes event during pre-flight at gate prior to passenger boarding. The Captain submitted an AML discrepancy after flight crew deplaned.
Cessna 421 flight crew reported a nose gear collapse during a maintenance preflight check. Reportedly; the collapse resulted from maintenance test protocols not being followed resulted.
Air carrier First Officer reported being notified by Dispatch that unmanifested DG had been loaded onboard. The flight landed normally at destination.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of aircraft control during taxi into gate due to ramp surface conditions.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported possibly flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
EMB 145 flight crew reported loss of cabin pressure control on climb. The flight crew immediately requested a descent and decided to perform an air turnback.

Cessna 310 pilot reported they were unable to obtain local airport wind information and landed with a possible gusting tailwind; resulting in a runway excursion at a non-towered airport in visual conditions. The aircraft overran the runway; stopping beyond the departure end; there were no injuries to the pilot and the passenger.
C172 pilot reported traffic conflict required taking evasive action to avoid traffic climbing below resulted in a NMAC event.
An air carrier First Officer reported the Jeppesen FD PRO X app did not have the required RNAV approach procedures at COS.
Cirrus Vision SJ50 pilot reported a FADEC Control Degraded Red CAS message and performed a diversion and for a precautionary landing.
B737 Captain reported the DG Summary did not arrive until after pushback start.
GA pilot reported an NMAC near 5I4 non-towered airport and questioned the airmanship of the other aircraft's pilot.

Gulfstream IV flight crew reported while on autopilot the aircraft turned the wrong direction during an approach. While attempting to recover; the aircraft descended below the assigned altitude and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
PA-24 Pilot reported being out of radio contact with ATC while on an IFR flight plan due to distractions.
Air taxi flight crew reported cabin depressurization in cruise flight. Diverted and landed uneventfully.
Learjet Captain reported an engine overheat after takeoff during climb. The crew stabilized the overheat; received priority handling and returned to the departure airport.
C182T pilot reported possible low personal oxygen level led to misalignment with taxiway resulted in ATC initiated go-around and a critical ground conflict.

Center controllers reported an Air Taxi reported a lightning strike caused an electrical failure and a temporary loss of control. The controllers temporarily lost communications with the aircraft and reported it was due to their faulty radio transmitter sites.
M30C pilot reported while on takeoff roll; traffic was observed landing opposite direction not on CTAF. Resulted in taking evasive action.
Air carrier flight crew reported a fume event during cruise that was detected by flight attendants and passengers.
PA-28 instructor pilot reported a Near Mid air Collision and took evasive action.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported receiving a L 14th DUCT warning message during initial approach. After checklist procedures didn't remedy the issue; the flight crew shut down the affected engine and continued the approach to the destination airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported they had to stop taxiing suddenly to avoid another air carrier approaching the same intersection.
A Center Controller reported they issued a Low Altitude Alert to an aircraft which descended below its assigned altitude.
Flight crew of corporate jet reported another business jet cutting in front of them while on approach to 07FA non-towered airport. Flight crew was forced to go-around.
Three DEN Tower personnel reported short staffing and asking for the Airport Acceptance Rate to be lowered.
Air carrier Captain reported and airborne conflict requiring evasive action followed by a loss of GPS signal while inbound to DEN.
Extra E300 pilot reported while attempting to land they took evasive action to avoid a collision with a King Air that entered the runway while the Extra E300 was on short final.
C172 pilot flying reported a ground conflict with another aircraft on the taxiway while taxiing to parking. The pilot immediately stopped proceeding after noticing the traffic.

Air Carrier Ramp Personnel reported noticing a USPS mesh mail sack that contained a box with a battery UN3481 number which are not allowed to be transported on passenger aircraft. Parcel was returned to USPS for correct sorting.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported ramp push crew towed aircraft to the wrong gate resulting in a critical ground conflict with multiple aircraft.

TRACON Controller reported that the military trainer informed ATC an NMAC occurred with a VFR aircraft in the pattern. Upon further inspection; sufficient VFR to IFR separation was found and the VFR aircraft was instructed to maintain visual separation from the military trainer.
Air Carrier Flight Crew taxiing 747-8 aircraft reported taxiway incursion on closed taxiway.
Helicopter owner/pilot reported performing own maintenance due to experimental exhibition category. The helicopter experienced a loss of power during a flight and was damaged upon landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported the Load Supervisor obtained the signed NOTOC but failed to place it in the Dangerous Goods envelope on board the airplane.
Air carrier Captain reported confusing taxiway signage depicting a high speed taxiway off of runway 25L at PHX airport led to the crew inadvertently taxiing onto a NOTAM closed surface area.
C-150 pilot reported engine malfunction during cruise. Pilot diverted and landed safely.
B737 Captain reported they shut down one engine due to an Oil Filter Bypass indication and returned to departure airport.
BD100 flight crew reported landing gear malfunction during approach. The flight crew extended the landing gear manually after multiple attempts and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew reported the magnetic compass was not indicating correctly during climb. ATC issued a direct heading to a fix and the crew continued the flight.

Embraer 505 air taxi crew reported a loss of aircraft control during the climb in instrument conditions; where the autopilot failed to remain engaged and was not noticed by the crew; resulting in a descent and a terrain warning. The Captain took the flight controls and recovered the aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported a near-collision with another air carrier during taxi to MIA Runway 8R.
A320 Pilot reported the refusal of an aircraft due to the APU not working correctly resulting in increased cabin temperatures over multiple legs.

Captain reported failing to ensure a new pilot had completed a pre-departure walk around correctly. The primary and standby pitot probe covers had not been removed; resulting in a rejected takeoff for an airspeed split.
Instructor pilot in a light sport aircraft reported they taxied on to the runway at a non-towered airport when an arriving aircraft flew over them and landed.
Flight attendants reported a passenger's phone went under a seat and began to smoke. Flight attendants retrieved phone and secured it inside a thermal containment bag.
Air Carrier First Officer reported discovering that the Final Flight Release was missing required Hazmat documentation.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending in response to an RA from an opposite direction Airbus on approach to PHX.
Captain reported loss of the left main brake while positioning for takeoff and after being notified of smoke coming from a wheel; cancelled takeoff.
Flight Crew reported airspeed indications became unreliable at cruise so they descended and airspeed returned to normal.
A government helicopter pilot reported a near miss with a UAS in controlled airspace. The helicopter pilot took evasive action to avoid a possible collision.
Air Carrier pilot reported receiving a terrain advisory during approach. Flight crew confirmed no terrain conflict and continued approach to landing.

Widebody Transport Captain reported a concern with adequacy of taxiway lighting at EWR.  Captain also expressed concern over visibility of the center line lighting and markings when raining which could lead to an excursion.

Air carrier First Officer reported there were 3 waypoints in Canada that were not in the navigation database. The First Officer reported the crew was slow to enter the Lat/Long in the FMS; after a request of direct-to from ATC; because the format on the flight release is different than what is accepted by the FMS. First Officer reports more training is needed.
Air carrier Captain reported an incomplete Hazmat document required a return to gate to correct the issue. The document was corrected by Ramp personnel and flight departed normally.

GA pilot reported while transiting MSP airspace they had a near miss with an air carrier on departure climb. Reportedly; the Air Traffic Controller was handling traffic from several airports and may have vectored the pilot incorrectly.

Corporate pilot reported holding short of the runway even though ATC gave takeoff clearance to avoid conflict with another aircraft that was landing. The controller had cleared the reporter's aircraft for takeoff in front of an arriving aircraft.
PA-28 Instructor with trainee reported a NMAC at a non-towered airport when the other aircraft made confusing position reports and then appeared in the traffic pattern 300 feet above his aircraft.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a flyaway that was due to an unknown aircraft issue or possible signal loss. The UAS was recovered with minor damage.
Flight crew reported runway incursion and ground conflict with landing aircraft at TVL non-towered airport.
B737 flight crew reported they refused an aircraft after finding what appeared to be a bird strike in one of the engines.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported losing control of the UAS during flight. The reporter regained control of the UAS by switching flight modes on the controller.
Maintenance Technician reversed hydraulic flex lines when reassembling brake valve causing brakes to lock up on landing.
Flight Instructor with student reported an aircraft followed them closely in the traffic pattern and then landed behind them on the same runway before the Flight Instructor had exited.
SF34 Captain reported engine failure on initial climb out and a return to the airport.

ERJ-170 Captain reported the transport of a powered chair that had its battery removed and carried by the passenger without the proper packaging as required. The Gate Agent also did not notify the flight crew.
B737-800 flight crew reported an engine failure in flight. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.

Air carrier Captain reported a passenger deplaned at destination with two large Lithium Ion batteries which were not configured for cabin transport. The Captain was not notified about batteries in cabin prior to departure.
Air carrier Captain reported while taxiing on the ramp they had to come to an abrupt stop to avoid a service vehicle crossing their path.
Air carrier First Officer reported a ground conflict during taxi and reported no comment was heard from ATC.
Air carrier flight crew reported uncommanded go-around and loss of aircraft control while on approach into IND.

Air carrier flight crew reported the undocumented transport of Hazmat class1 explosives. The issue was discovered at destination arrival where it was determined the departure ramp crew failed to unload seven cargo pallets.

Instructor Pilot reported a NMAC while in the local practice area. The instructor had completed clearing turns and was beginning maneuvers when a twin engine aircraft passed through the area without warning. The instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision. There was no communication from the twin engine aircraft.
PA-28 pilot reported experiencing a total electrical failure during cruise; resulting in a divert to precautionary landing.
First Officer reported the Captain temporarily lost control of the aircraft while turning off the runway after landing.
Ultralight pilot reported an engine failure during cruise; diverted; and landed safely.
BNA TRACON Controller reported a conflict between an air carrier on downwind and a VFR aircraft not in communication with TRACON.
Challenger CL600 First Officer reported unable to pressurize cabin and diverted to a precautionary landing.
C172 Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the traffic pattern. The other aircraft refused to communicate and did not maintain safe spacing.
Beechcraft Premier pilot reported flap failure after takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported inadequate taxi markings at BUR airport.
PA-28 pilot reported bouncing twice during landing resulting in a momentary loss of aircraft control and runway excursion before the pilot was able get back on the runway.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below the MVA.

A General Aviation pilot reported confusion regarding an IFR cancellation that was not acknowledged by the Controller prior to switching to CTAF. This miscommunication resulted in the Center not receiving the pilot's IFR cancellation.
Citation I Captain reported a pressurization system failure and a priority descent from ATC.

Air carrier flight crew reported a communication issue with ATC resulting from a frequency change mishap. Aircrew questioned an ATC issued direction of turn that would have resulted in flying towards terrain. Aircrew received new instructions and landed safely at the airport.
Air Carrier Stores Clerk reported being notified that a Hazmat shipment was shipped with a non-hazardous label.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported being notified of a Hazmat violation regarding a cargo pallet shipment that was incorrectly labeled.

Technician reported installing a High Pressure Shut-Off Valve and insulting blankets incorrectly; with the Bleed Duct Overheat sense elements under the insulation blanket. The aircraft made an air turn back due to a Bleed Overheat problem.
A320 Captain reported a ground conflict with a vehicle during taxi out for takeoff.

Eurocopter Captain reported discovering tail rotor retaining bolts installed incorrectly on a routine inspection. Inspection of other Company aircraft revealed one other aircraft with the same incorrect bolt installation.
Pilot reported they missed their landing spot and contacted the threshold with one tire before executing a go around.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported briefly exceeding their LAANC approved altitude.
C152 pilot reported engine issues during takeoff that eventually resulted in a diversion to the nearest airport.

B767 First Officer reported being notified by the flight attendants of a fire in the aft cabin during short final that was safely resolved by the time the aircraft had landed and exited the runway to taxi to the gate.
B737 First Officer reported Ground Proximity Warning System (GPWS) alert on approach. Crew performed a go-around and landed uneventfully.
Cessna 402 flight crew reported they rejected takeoff due to the left engine not producing maximum power.
GA flight instructor reported a student pilot flew without the required certificates being issued.

EMB-170 Captain reported an engine fire after shutdown that was observed during the First Officer's post flight walk around. Captain reported being unaware of need to followup with company personnel since ground maintenance had extinguished the fire and had debriefed the crew.
Flight Design CTSW pilot reported landing left of centerline and veered left resulting in runway excursion with no damage.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL340 from a preceding B747.
Airline Captain reported exceeding clearance altitude.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported while at the gate maintenance personnel advised them of a glow from the engine resulting in the flight crew inspecting and extinguishing a tailpipe fire.
GA Maintenance Technician reported learning that a C140 aileron was installed incorrectly during restoration of aircraft leading to an off airport landing.
A320 Pilot reported Compressor Vane ECAM message on climb out. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
CE-525 pilot reported EFIS failure inflight. Pilot reset EFIS and continued flight.
Air carrier pilot reported a flap malfunction on approach; initiated a missed approach; ran the QRH procedures; and landed with flaps 25 instead of 30.

R-44 Flight Instructor reported while attempting an auto-rotation landing another aircraft entered the runway resulting in the Flight Instructor taking evasive action to avoid a collision with the other aircraft.
B737-800 flight crew reported during descent; the right windshield started arcing and the outer pane cracked. They advised ATC and ran the QRH.
Pilot with instructor reported descending over 1;000 feet below the designated altitude for a maneuver. Pilot ended maneuver and climbed to higher altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported numerous overlapping problems caused them to depart for destination airport with reduced IMC capability and minimum fuel; resulting in minimum fuel before landing.
Aerostar pilot reported engine failure during departure climb. Pilot performed inflight engine shut down and returned to departure airport.

Air Carrier First Officer reported being notified by flight attendants during departure climb of a persistent odor. Flight crew determined odor was not dissipating and elected to execute a safe air return.

Instructor pilot reported a near miss during a training flight in day VMC conditions at a non-tower controlled airport; while in the traffic pattern. The instructor took the flight controls and maneuvered away from an aircraft entering the pattern; and then performed another evasive turn and descent to avoid a collision with another aircraft; then landed safely after the maneuver.
Air carrier Captain reported being informed of an aircraft position discrepancy and noted that only one GPS recovered after leaving an area that had GPS interference and/or spoofing.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion between similar sounding fixes; ESPNN and ESTYN; on the IAD ILS RWY 1R approach.
C172 Flight Instructor reported on downwind to base turn observing an aircraft cross below; which resulted in a decision to go around.

B787 flight crew reported a malfunction of the master brightness control during cruise resulted in loss of flight deck instrument displays. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed normally.

A319 flight crew reported communication and coordination problems with ground personnel during push-back resulting in damage to the aircraft. Reporters stated procedures and language barrier contributed to the event.
Air carrier pilot reported during the climb they experienced severe turbulence resulting in significant excursions of speed; altitude and pitch.
GA pilot reported losing power and landing in a field with no injuries or damage.

Air carrier pilot reported since temporary construction has begun; spot 42 is not identified on the AMM or Jeppesen charts and the ramp hold line is not clearly marked which could result in a potential ground conflict.
Air carrier Captain reported a Dangerous Goods manifest error during preflight. The Captain produced a new manifest according to procedure.
Pilot reported a critical ground conflict at a non-towered airport. During takeoff roll another aircraft departed the opposite direction runway and overflew the reporter's aircraft.
PA28 pilot reported a NMAC with crossing traffic.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at CKZ non-towered airport.
Light aircraft instructor pilot reported an NMAC with opposite direction traffic at LKR non-towered airport.
General aviation pilot reported experiencing two critical ground conflicts at a non-towered airport; and observing a landing aircraft overflying an aircraft still on the runway.
Cirrus Vision SJ50 instructor pilot and student reported an uncontrolled turn to the right on initial climb from a missed approach.
Ramp agent reported a tow bar breakaway from the tug during pushback resulting in uncommanded aircraft movement The aircraft was stopped and there was no injury or damage to the aircraft.
Falcon DA-50 pilot reported an engine failed during cruise climb. As the pilot was diverting a second engine failed; then restarted briefly and flight crew continued to a safe landing.
Engineer reported safety concerns with the Gulfstream GVII-G500 and Gulfstream GVII-G600 Airplane Flight Manual related to the Accelerate-Go and Accelerate Stop distances.
B767 Captain reported multiple EICAS messages after take off and elected to make an air turn back and precautionary landing.
C172 Pilot reported an NMAC event shorty after takeoff caused by deviating from assigned heading. Pilot took evasive action and apologized for bad communication performance.

PA-23 instructor pilot and trainee reported an engine failure in the traffic pattern. They were able to re-start the engine after switching fuel tanks; and received a low altitude alert from ATC during the trouble-shooting.
Air carrier First Officer reported failing to get temperature taken at screening point.
Sailplane pilot reported a NMAC event during takeoff with a non-communicating plane departing on an intersecting runway. The sailplane pilot executed an evasive maneuver to avoid a collision.

B757 First Officer reported while parking the aircraft and shutting down the engines the aircraft continued to move forward resulting in the air crew losing control of the aircraft and hitting ground support equipment.

Pilot Instructor reported a NMAC while performing ground based maneuvers. Instructor states while executing the said maneuvers another single engine high wing aircraft appeared with no communication. The Instructor had been broadcasting on UNICOM and until the near miss had not heard any communication.
B757-200 First Officer reported multiple alerts and a total loss of the Captain's instrument suite on initial climb; resulting in a return to departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported an unstable approach and cited it was difficult to understand the controllers at this foreign location.
B737 flight crew reported during receiving an engine annunciation light during takeoff. Flight crew returned to departure airport.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a new Company procedure that pushes untrained personnel into the Lead position resulted in HAZMAT being improperly processed during a diversion.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft while in the climb in VFR conditions on a training flight. The instructor did not see the other aircraft approach and pass from behind; then stopped their climb to avoid the conflict.
A319 flight attendant crew reported a fumes event during passenger boarding. Shortly thereafter; the Captain informed the crew to stop boarding and that maintenance would assign an aircraft swap.
A321 Captain reported a rejected takeoff after a significant yaw was experienced; caused by excessive winds.

C172 Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action to prevent a collision with a landing aircraft after receiving ATC clearance to cross runway. Instructor stated that ATC had breakdown in coordination between Tower and Ground Controllers.

CRJ-200 First Officer reported loss of Cabin Pressure Control during compliance with an MEL procedure for bleed swap during climb. The flight crew requested priority handling and descended to a lower altitude. The flight crew continued to destination airport with the packs in off position.
Cessna 206 pilot reported rejecting the takeoff when the aft cargo door opened on the takeoff roll.

C550 Captain reported a Master Caution with a L FUEL FILTER BYPASS Annunciator lights illuminated after takeoff. The lights extinguished and reappeared 45 minutes later. A decision was made to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Pilot reported a malfunctioning fuel gauge and a diversion to a nearby airport after they were unable to complete an approach to their destination airport.
Air traffic local controller reported aircraft stopped on the runway resulting in sending around air carrier on short final to the same runway.

Flight crew reported receiving a level 3 AIR MANF 3 FAIL alert on climb out. The crew ran the QRH and after consultation with Dispatch and Operations; conducted an air turn back and precautionary landing at the departure airport.

Air Carrier flight crew reported multiple left engine malfunctions during takeoff. Flight crew performed an air turn back to departure airport where a safe landing was completed and maintenance was provided.

B737 Flight Attendant reported the 2R door handle moved out of position slightly several times after takeoff so they continuously pushed handle back into position. Flight returned to departure airport where it was found that the outside door handle was not latched.
B767 flight crew reported landing gear malfunction; a popped circuit breaker; and possible hydraulic system issues during climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew reported recovering safely from an inflight loss of control event related to strong turbulence at FL340.
Air carrier Captain reported they performed the takeoff with the GPS off because the ATIS stated the GPS signals were unreliable.
PA-32 pilot reported a landing gear indication problem during approach resulted in manual extension of the landing gear and a safe landing.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a light aircraft after departing SWF.

Air carrier First Officer reported safety related concerns with flying into and out of a challenging foreign airport.  Reporter cited lack of flying; training and fatigue inducing scheduling all increase the risk of flying into this location.
EMB-145 flight crew reported smoke in cockpit during cruise resulting in a diversion.
B737-700 flight crew reported a fuel imbalance and suspected fuel leak during climb.  Crew returned to departure airport after shutting down left engine per checklist.
MD-11 pilot reported the #2 engine rolled back during cruise. The flight crew noticed a fuel imbalance and performed an in flight shut down of the #2 engine and continued to destination airport.
B737 MAX 8 pilot reported a rejected takeoff due to an electronic engine control malfunction.
A-320 crew reported a near miss while taxiing to the gate. Captain stopped to allow other aircraft to pass and the flight continued to the ramp after traffic passed.
Beechcraft Barron pilot reported aircraft vibrations during initial climb in VMC conditions. The pilot requested priority handling with ATC; returned to the departure airport and landed safely.
PA28 Maintenance Technician reported errors in documentation of an aircraft's annual inspection resulted from the process being poorly managed by company.
Air carrier First Officer reported similar sounding fix names resulted in a course deviation. ATC alerted the crew and the flight navigated to the correct fix.

B737-800 flight crew reported on departure the pilot flying turned early resulting in receiving a low altitude alert and aircraft flying towards terrain.  ATC issued an expedited climb and the flight crew resumed the departure.

General aviation pilot reported ATC instructed them to remain out of the airspace due to congestion when arriving VFR to BFI. The reporter stated their options were limited due to adjacent airports Class B and D airspace and potential traffic.
Flight crew reported trailing edge flap problem resulted in fuel dumping and a precautionary landing at departure airport.
B757 First Officer reported the flight deviated left of course on an oceanic route to avoid wake turbulence; but later realized deviations were only authorized to the right.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft that was transiting the non-towered airport traffic pattern without communicating during a training flight. The instructor took control and maneuvered to avoid the other aircraft before re-entering the traffic pattern.
A319 Captain Check Airman re-qualifying another Captain reported the Captain failed to comply with a crossing restriction issued by ATC during approach.

A319 Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC after becoming low on an approach while being confused with the approach automation. The Captain performed a go around then landed safely on the next approach.

RV-12 pilot reported a being overflown by another aircraft after touchdown at a non-towered airport on a training flight. The other aircraft was not communicating in the traffic pattern during the event; performed a go around; then returned for a landing.
B777 flight crew reported improper alternate engine start procedures caused an engine fire; resulting in the aircraft being towed back into the gate.

A320 Captain reported the #1 engine stalled during cruise climb. The engine recovered but stalled again in cruise and a Compressor Vane ECAM occurred. Flight crew continue to destination using manual thrust.
Air carrier Captain reported a vehicle drove across their path while taxiing resulting in the need to take evasive action to avoid a collision with the vehicle.
Air carrier Storekeeper reported the improper packaging and shipment of Hazmat tools by a fellow Storekeeper.
Air Carrier Captain reported taxi lines need repainting on this ramp for safe operation during all lighting conditions.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing an R 14 DUCT warning message.
Trainee pilot on a solo flight reported a NMAC with a helicopter while in the pattern at a non towered airport.
G-IV First Officer reported when takeoff thrust was applied the right engine never spooled up resulting in the flight crew rejecting the takeoff and returning to the ramp.

Flight Instructor reported a near miss while on the landing rollout with another aircraft that attempted to land in the opposite direction without communication. The instructor maneuvered off the runway to avoid a collision; resulting in a flat main tire.
Student pilot reported that a gust of wind during landing caused the aircraft to depart the runway.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported inadvertently rolling backwards at the gate after mistakenly not setting the parking brake.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a bank angle alert during climbout from IAH airport requiring immediate correction. The flight continued to their destination.

Beechcraft Sundowner pilot owner reported an unstabilized approach and a bounced touchdown short of the landing runway while allowing another more experienced pilot to fly and familiarize with the aircraft. The aircraft bounced short of the runway and the pilot owner called for a go around; then landed safely with no aircraft damage.
A321 flight crew reported the tow bar became disconnected during push back resulting in a temporary loss of control and the aircraft rolling over the disconnected tow bar.
Lancair ES pilot reported the main cabin door departed the aircraft in flight; likely because it was improperly latched.
DA-20 pilot reported runway excursion during landing.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported excessive pitch up and right rudder input by the student resulted in a runway excursion during takeoff.
Instructor Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a mid air collision while operating in a practice area.
PA22 pilot and passenger reported losing control of the aircraft and striking a ground service installation after refueling when there was an apparent brake failure.

Air carrier flight crew reported the First Officer inadvertently turned off the number one engine rather than engine number 2 during taxi-in resulting in a momentary EGT spike. Both crew members cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
C25C First Officer reported multiple system failures during departure climb. Flight crew returned to departure airport uneventfully.
A Center Controller reported a Learjet 55 experienced an electrical failure resulting in loss of communications and radar contact.

GA pilot reported letting passenger/pilot land the aircraft which resulted in a bounced landing and a runway excursion.  The aircraft had damage to the wing and the tail; the pilot and pilot/passenger evacuated with no injuries.
Air Carrier flight crew reported confusing ATC clearance instructions on approach to DEN airport.
Air carrier Captain reported taking evasive action to miss a medium sized helium balloon while on visual approach.
Light aircraft pilot reported running off the end of the runway after rejecting the takeoff because of slower than normal acceleration; probably due to high density altitude.
DA 42 Pilots reported the LH prop being stuck in an overspeed condition and being unable to find solutions in the Aircraft Flight manual.
Captain reported an APU Fire Indication on push back. The APU auto shut down. The Captain had the aircraft towed back to the gate and deplaned the passengers.
B757-200 air carrier flight crew reported a flight deck window failure at cruise. The crew descended then diverted to an alternate airport and landed safely.
Controller reported vectoring an aircraft during approach such that the aircraft's rate of turn resulted in them entering an area with an MVA higher than their assigned altitude.

Glider pilot landing on a grass strip; at a non-towered airport; reported a critical ground conflict with a vehicle that had crossed the grass runway and parked parallel facing in the departure direction.
A-321 air carrier pilot reported a loss of hydraulic quantity during cruise. The flight diverted safely and was towed off the taxiway to the gate.

An Air Carrier pilot reported the automated parking procedure at one of the gates does not give a countdown to stopping like at other gates and requires the crew to use pavement markings. The reporter stated poor lighting and obscured pavement markings make this gate's procedure unsafe and should staff a marshaller..
Captain reported extending the landing gear to silence a continuous Landing Gear aural warning.
Flight Attendant reported passengers were boarded before the pilots arrived at the aircraft. No air conditioning was available and passengers began removing their face masks.
B200 pilot reported engine failure inflight. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier Captain reported a CFTT event during final approach to EWR airport.

Air carrier First Officer reported a loss of control during cruise flight in oceanic airspace due to significant moderate turbulence resulting in an overspeed condition and loss of altitude. The crew regained control of the aircraft; returned to altitude and no injuries were reported.
Air carrier flight crew reported the tow bar broke during pushback. Captain applied brakes and aircraft was towed back to gate for maintenance inspection.

An Air Carrier Captain reported ORD ATIS advertised ORD Runway 28C Land and Hold Short taxiway Z procedure. The Jepp 20-7 page only indicated a LAHSO for Runway 28C Taxiway BB. There is no taxiway BB at ORD.

Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot checked and used an app created by the manufacturer of the UAS being flown. The app has several no-fly areas listed but did not list the Class D airspace of a nearby airport. The UAS flew within the Class D airspace without proper authorization.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering moderate wake turbulence on descent into CLT. The aircraft was inspected afterwards by maintenance with no problems found.
Air carrier Captain reported that while descending on approach they sighted UAS. The UAS posed no conflict and the crew continued the approach.
DA40 Flight School Instructor reported a NMAC when a departing aircraft flew near them at a non-towered airport.
Wing Derringer Flight Instructor with pilot trainee reported the landing gear collapsed on landing even though the gear down light was on.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported inadvertently descending below final approach fix altitude before the fix and not recognizing the low altitude until the 1;000" call by the radar altimeter."
B737 flight crew reported loss of Hydraulic system fluid and hydraulic pumps that resulted in a diversion.
C680 First Officer reported a loss of aircraft control and momentary stick shaker during arrival in weather conditions.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fumes event during cruise described as a mild odor. Flight crew completed the QRH procedure which eliminated the odor and enabled flight to continued to destination safely where a logbook entry was submitted.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying without proper LAANC authorization.
Flight crew reported the First Officer's control column stuck after auto pilot disconnect during landing. It became free after 1 to 2 seconds of force applied and the landing was continued.
Air carrier First Officer reported an NMAC with a UAS during final approach. Reporter was distracted due to high workload and the Captain witnessed the event.

GA pilot reported overtaking a slower aircraft during departure from PAO airport resulting in an airborne conflict.  Reporter also reported an SFO Class B airspace violation after taking evasive action and losing situational awareness due to a problem with the iPad's moving map display.

EMB-170 flight crew reported that while being vectored for a night visual approach ATC had the aircraft high and fast resulting in the flight crew trying to configure the aircraft that had them overshoot the final approach course and flight towards terrain.  The flight crew was able to make a stable approach to the airport.

Fractional jet Captain reported ATC issuing a low altitude alert when the flight descended below a published altitude during a night IMC approach in an area of terrain. ATC issued a clearance to climb and the flight was vectored for another approach and landed safely.

Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft parking brake disengaged for unknown reasons after parking at the gate. The aircraft rolled back about 8 to 10 feet before the Captain stopped the aircraft and reset the brakes.
A Flight Instructor reported they encountered a rough running engine during initial climbout and returned to the airport.
Air Carrier Check Pilot reported an erroneous RA from the First Officer's radio altimeter while on approach to IAH. The Captain stated this was the second such event and in a different aircraft.
R-22 Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing pattern training. R-22 executed an evasive maneuver to prevent  a collision with a solo student on parallel runway.
C177 pilot reported alternator failure inflight. Pilot landed uneventfully at destination airport.
Pilot reported loss of normal brakes on landing. The flight Crew ran QRH procedures and regained braking control to stop the aircraft and taxi off the active runway using differential braking.
Air carrier pilot reported severe turbulence encounter during cruise and ATC communication problem that resulted in altitude excursion; track deviation and conflict with other aircraft.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern at CBF airport.

Single Engine Student Pilot reported a NMAC while traveling through a  Bravo TCA. The  Student first became aware of the other aircraft from warnings on the onboard EFB device. The Student Pilot then detected the intruder via a visual sighting. The Student began a climb to avoid the intruder. After the climb was started; ATC advised of traffic.
Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict during taxi out from ramp. The First Officer transmitted 'STOP' command to the other aircraft and avoided a collision.

General aviation pilot flying reported the aircraft had momentarily lost GPS signal and began to climb while established on the approach; possibly due to GPS jamming occurring near the final approach path.
A320 First Officer reported experiencing Number 1 engine compressor stalls during climbout.  The crew decided to reduce the power to the Number 1 engine and return to the departure airport.
Air taxi flight crew reported climbing through their assigned altitude. On read-back to ATC the pilots were not corrected that their stated altitude was incorrect. There was no RA.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
CRJ-200 Captain reported failure of stall protection and windshear guidance system inflight with windshear reported at destination airport.

A320 pilot reported receiving fault messages for both thrust reversers after deployment on landing. During taxi in; steering and all electrical power was lost except the battery which also failed as the aircraft was being towed to the gate.
An air carrier pilot reported after pushback they observed another aircraft being pushed back towards their aircraft. The pilot yelled 'stop' on ramp frequency to avoid the collision.
Single-piloted Chancellor pilot reported impacting a parked aircraft while taxiing.
MD-11 flight crew reported a First Officer airspeed indicator failure during the takeoff roll. The Captain performed a low speed rejected takeoff and returned to the gate.
Air carrier flight crew reported their aircraft; picked up from recent maintenance; failed to pressurize during the repositioning ferry flight.
Pilot reported the eastern 353 ft. of the runway at TTD was permanently closed and the chart supplement and airport diagram have not been updated to show the runway length difference.

Corporate flight crew reported ATC stated a pilot deviation occurred as the aircraft descended the charted altitude on the localizer approach before being cleared for the visual approach. The flight crew denied a pilot deviation occurred; as the aircraft was established on the localizer course outside the waypoint with the altitude selector above the floor.
Air carrier First Officer reported; during their landing roll; another aircraft landing on an intersecting runway flew less than 100 ft. above them.
EMB-175 Captain reported a slight flap overspeed occurred following a turbulence encounter that may have been wake from the preceding heavy aircraft on approach to ZZZ.
C206 pilot reported crosswind landings resulting in two unstabilized approaches and go-arounds resulting in momentary loss of control before successfully landing.
Air carrier Maintenance personnel reported undeclared dry ice being loaded. Dry ice was removed prior to departure.

Air Carrier Captain reported a fume event upon arrival at the aircraft. The APU was operating in spite of two active APU MELs. After troubleshooting and conferring with Maintenance; Dispatch and Chief Pilot an aircraft swap was requested.
Air carrier First Officer reported radio altimeter anomalies in cruise flight at FL350; citing possible 5G interference.
Air Carrier Captain reported a discrepancy between approach charts referencing the final approach course for this runway.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to PHX in trail of a B737-900 that resulted in a sudden roll to the left.
BE-200 pilot reported loss of the Number 1 inverter resulted in a return to the departure airport.
EMB 145 flight crew reported a near miss with another aircraft while on approach.
GA pilot reported a ground conflict during taxi to parking.
Air carrier First Officer reported confusion with the '250 Knot' wording on the SID.
Medium Transport Captain reported receiving an RA on approach to BFI requiring evasive action.
CRJ 900 Captain reported a rejected take off caused by the #1 Engine IDG caution message. The flight crew returned to the gate for maintenance.
G450 flight crew reported cockpit window shattered in cruise flight. Flight crew diverted.
Air carrier flight crew and DFW Ground Controller reported weather ground stop; airport and frequency congestion with miscommunication resulted in a critical ground conflict.
C172 pilot reported they had a runway excursion after touchdown due to applying braking on one side instead of using rudder pedals. The aircraft came to a stop on a taxiway.
Challenger 350 flight crew reported an air conditioning and pressurization pack failure and a return to the departure airport.
A319 pilot reported receiving a terrain alert when they accepted an ATC vector that took them in the direction of terrain. Flight crew corrected heading to avoid terrain.
Pilot Monitoring reported Pilot in Command lost control while landing in a crosswind.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft in the pattern at VPZ airport.
B737-800 Captain reported an air turn back and over weight landing was caused by an engine anti ice valve failing to operate.
Private Pilot reported loss of control during a taxi test due to a wind gust.
A321 Captain reported and engine fire warning during taxi out for takeoff. Captain shut down engine and returned to gate.
LGB Tower Controller reported they cleared a helicopter for takeoff from a helipad at the same time as they cleared an aircraft for takeoff from a converging runway resulting in an NMAC.
Ramp Agent reported there are multiple large bumps on an ORD taxiway that pose a hazard to ramp vehicles and aircraft.

Air carrier pilot climbing on departure reported a NMAC with an unidentified VFR aircraft just above the Class B Airspace ceiling. ATC stated they 'did not see' the aircraft so did not issue traffic to the air carrier.
Air carrier pilots reported they were advised by ATC that their aircraft was below the minimum vectoring altitude.
Air Carrier Pilot Crew received an ECIAS Baggage Fire Warning. The pilots were unable to confirm a fire; diverted; and landed in an overweight condition.
B737-800 flight crew reported the tow bar was disconnected without flight crew approval and the aircraft began to roll.
Air carrier Captain reported a GPS jamming event occurred in RJJJ airspace during climb.

B737 First Officer reported a left engine roll back at cruise. The flight crew attempted to adjust the engine thrust; but the engine continued to roll back and failed. The Flight Crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier Captain reported a preflight DG documentation error which resulted in the aircraft departure with undocumented DG cargo on board.
A Pilot departing from a non towered airport reported they rejected takeoff to avoid a near collision with a corporate jet landing on an intersecting runway.

C340A Maintenance Technician reported a repair to the vacuum pump and the following engine test run was incorrectly completed. The pilot flew the aircraft after the first repair and oil began to stream from the cowling and the pressure fluctuated; which led to the discovery of the problem.
Air Carrier Fist Officer reported a fume event during takeoff roll. Captain executed a safe rejected takeoff and returned to departure gate for maintenance action.
B737-700 Captain reported hydraulic pump problems in cruise and elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported they experienced GPS jamming flying through the Cairo FIR and missed a frequency change to the adjacent control facility.
Air carrier Captain reported releasing the parking brake before realizing that the Final Dangerous Goods (DG) Summary had not been received yet.
Pilot reported confusion during taxi for takeoff and lined up on the wrong runway for departure from STS airport. ATC notified pilot and the pilot taxied to the correct runway.
C172 instructor and trainee reported a NMAC while under ATC flight following. The intruding aircraft was not observed by ATC.
CRJ-900 Captain reported an NMAC event during descent for landing.TCAS RA commands were followed and the crew resumed the descent when clear of conflict.
A330 flight crew reported elevated Brake Temps and subsequent Brake fire following arrival and taxi into gate.
B737NG Captain reported two flight attendants were slightly injured in a probable wake turbulence incident while descending into EWR.
Lead Technician reported communication issues resulted in logbook errors and a delay to an outbound flight.
PA-34 pilot with instructor reported while in cruise the left engine seized and could not be feathered. The flight crew made a forced landing on a dirt road and called for assistance.

B777-200 flight crew reported the aircraft started to roll back after parking at the gate and being told by the ground handler that chocks were in. The pilots were told to reset brakes. Later the pilots were informed the aircraft had struck a fuel truck.
Air taxi Captain reported receiving an altitude alert from ATC when descending below final approach fix altitude. The Captain leveled the aircraft to correct the glidepath and landed.
Captain reported hard braking to avoid collision with a fuel truck being operated outside of the vehicle access roads in the gate area.
Air carrier flight crew reported GPS jamming in Egyptian airspace.
GA pilot reported a critical ground conflict during landing with military aircraft on the runway. GA pilot performed a go-around.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a passenger's Lithium Ion battery powered laptop had been gate checked and loaded in cargo. The Captain was notified and decided to leave the laptop in cargo.
B767 flight crew and Dispatcher reported the right forward window shattered during cruise. Flight diverted and landed normally.
An Air Carrier flight crew reported they descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

First Officer reported a bird strike during approach. The flight crew continued to landing. After landing; major damage was discovered to the First Officer's pitot probe and surrounding fuselage skin and structure.
Air carrier flight crew reported a miscommunication with ATC. A change of STAR and instrument approach resulted in a crew communication error and response to GPWS alert.
A320 Captain reported a dirty socks odor during arrival taxi. After conferring with Dispatch and Maintenance; two engine engine runs were completed and APU was placed on MEL.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer observed a UAS pass nearby their aircraft while on final approach.  No evasive action was taken and the aircraft landed normally.
Pilot departing a non-towered airport reported they were holding short of the runway when a landing aircraft passed a few feet directly overhead.
Captain reported that the Flight Attendant could not open the main entry door and had written the door up four times with no corrective action.
ERJ-175 flight crew reported loss of performance shortly after takeoff that may have been due to wake turbulence and/or wind shear.

B737-800 Technician reported failing to fully open the valve on the crew oxygen bottle and also improperly read the pressure gauge during a system check per the Aircraft Maintenance Manual. Another Technician later discovered the error during an operations check. The discrepancy was addressed and fixed.
Flight Attendants reported a fuel smell on the aircraft prior to passenger boarding which resulted in the Captain refusing the aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported descending to an uncomfortably low altitude on approach to MSO airport.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a speed deviation occurred during the SID procedure and stated that a prolonged period of time off for both pilots was the main cause.
BDL Air Traffic Controller reported confusion of runway status due to poor communications with airport operations resulted in a go-around.
Air carrier flight crew reported a loss of aircraft control during pushback.  Crew reported applying brakes when tow bar itself broke into two pieces.
A Flight Crew reported they descended 800 ft. below the Final Approach Fix altitude and received a Low Altitude Alert from ATC.
Flight Crew reported that while on final approach another aircraft turned in front of them causing a NMAC and requiring the flight crew to take evasive action.
First Officer and Dispatcher reported communications issues with a foreign country ATC resulted in a EGPWS terrain alert on arrival.
C152 Flight Instructor reported they flew over another aircraft which had taxied on to the runway.

EMB-145 flight crew reported battery over-temp indications and inadequate QRH guidance; resulted in a complete aircraft shutdown on hardstand with prolonged delay getting passengers deplaned. Flight crew stated failure of operations to provided assistance resulted in unsafe passenger situation.

C172 instructor pilot reported that while the student was taxiing a strong gust of wind rotated the aircraft and it went off the taxiway. Flight instructor regained control and taxied back onto the taxiway.
B737-800 Captain reported receiving the hazmat form to carry medical radioactive materials just prior to gate departure. Captain signed the hazmat form but failed to amend flight release.
PA-28 pilot reported a loss of pitot static system and airspeed indicator after climbing into icing conditions. ATC cleared pilot to descend into warmer air and systems returned to normal.

Flight Attendant reported an ELT was missing from the aircraft during preflight. The flight crew reported this to Maintenance and were told to conduct the flight because the aircraft was not an extended over water aircraft.
B787 crew reported aircraft damage (hail and or lightning strike) that was detected post-flight after penetrating a thunderstorm while on descent for landing.
A pilot departing a non towered airport reported engine failure and landing in a field.

Air carrier pilot reported a near miss on final approach with a general aviation aircraft that ATC cleared through the airspace requiring them only to maintain visual separation. Flight crew responded to the TCAS RA which resulted in crossing the Final Approach Fix below charted altitude.
ATR 42 Captain reported a conflict during takeoff with an aircraft that taxied onto the runway without clearance.

General aviation Check Airman reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern at a non-towered airport. The Check Airman maneuvered aggressively to avoid a collision then re-entered the pattern for a landing.

A321 First Officer reported an unstable approach during a maintenance test flight resulted from improper use of automation. The flight became low and slow on the visual approach; and the pilot monitoring called for a go around; then flew another pattern and landed.
CE-680A flight crew reported low altitude alert from ATC during initial approach.
Flight Instructor with student reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern that resulted in evasive action to avoid a collision.
Cabin crew flying on a B777 aircraft reported that the aircraft had a cargo fire and diverted.

ATR-72 First Officer reported a warning chime during takeoff roll that was evaluated after takeoff indicating fluctuating oil pressure. Later in flight oil pressure fluctuations went into yellow range so crew performed precautionary shutdown and diverted in for a single engine landing. Crew observed flat tires on left side after entering ramp area causing airplane to list to that side during parking.
B737-800 flight crew reported an inflight upset occurred after encountering wake turbulence departing MIA in trail of a B767.

Air carrier ground personnel reported receiving a pallet of aircraft parts for air transport with incomplete hazmat documentation. Multiple deficiencies identified and not properly prepared for air transport.

Small transport pilot reported ATC notified the pilot of converging traffic and the pilot saw an airliner. The pilot took evasive action to avoid the conflict; notified ATC; and proceeded on with the flight.
SR22T Pilot reported divert due to low oil pressure indication.
Flight Attendant reported that latches and retaining brackets for the portable oxygen bottles failed on landing; allowing the bottles to come loose and catapult through the cabin.
Air carrier flight crew reported new taxiways A and A2 at SAN airport are not depicted in any charting publications while construction is ongoing at the airport.
An Air Carrier Captain reported GPS jamming.
A pilot reported red safety lights were not operating on construction cranes near the Venetian Resort in Las Vegas; NV.
B737-800 air carrier crew reported an engine failure after landing and while clearing the runway. The flight was able to taxi to the gate on the operable engine.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported the engine cowling opened after rotation during the takeoff. The Flight Instructor took controls from the student and landed on the remaining runway.

Gulfstream IV Captain reported both Flight Guidance Computers failed during departure which prompted the flight crew to execute an air return. Post-flight investigation revealed the temporary registration had expired.

A321 pilot reported a decreasing oil quantity during cruise from one of two previously reported high oil consumption engines. The crew diverted and landed safely at an alternate airport before an engine shutdown was required.
Cessna 150 Flight Instructor reported a minor tail strike on landing on a training flight with a student after student failed to apply proper rudder inputs.
Air Taxi Captain reported landing safely after encountering wake turbulence on approach to BOS in trail of an A320 that resulted in a 70-80 degree bank.

Air carrier flight crew reported an advisory from ATC to stop taxi and then observed a tractor pulling three cargo dollies cross the taxi way between aircraft on the vehicle service road; causing them to stop abruptly.
Lear LR-60 Captain reported returning safely to departure airport after experiencing multiple failures; including autopilot; flight director; and the First Officer's air data computer.
GA instructor pilot reported an NMAC during initial climb on a VFR flight.
C172 flight Instructor with student reported during landing rollout an aircraft landed behind them before the Instructor had exited the runway causing a critical ground conflict.
Lead Technician reported conflicting instructions from supervisors led to a fuel leak requiring additional time to resolve; causing a delay in returning the aircraft to service.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a main landing gear tire lost its tread during takeoff.
Zenith CH 750 pilot was performing a touch and go when a loud bang and associated airframe shudder caused an immediate return to the airport for a precautionary landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported being cleared for the BUR Runway 8 approach but having to go around after receiving TCAS RA alerts for VFR traffic at underlying airport VNY.

Air Carrier Captain on a post maintenance test flight reported smoke and fumes filling the flight deck from the sidewall. The pilots established priority handling with ATC and returned to the origin airport. The Captain indicated the events were challenging while wearing the oxygen mask.

B737-700 flight crew reported being issued a line up and wait clearance from the Tower Controller while another air carrier was on short final for the same runway. The flight crew continued across the runway per ATC instructions and the other air carrier executed a go-around.
A320 Captain reported a fumes event during cruise requiring one Flight Attendant to be put on oxygen.
