C172 Flight Instructor reported pattern traffic failed to communicate which resulted in a NMAC
Air Carrier First Officer reported a possible fuel leak during climb; resulting in fuel imbalance. Flight crew returned to the departure airport and landed.
Air carrier Captain reported seeing a UAS while descending on arrival to BOS.
Air Carrier flight crew reported ramp crew did not follow standard procedures to handle aircraft departure from gate.
SCT TRACON Controller reported they did not detect an aircraft read back the wrong altitude assignment and it descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported there is a lack of lateral separation with SFO's procedure of pairing aircraft for landing on the parallel runways; leading aircraft to fly wingtip-to-wingtip from the FAF to landing.
B737-700 flight crew reported abnormal flight control response in pitch during departure phase. They elected to divert to a nearby suitable airport.
B737 NG Captain reported rejecting the takeoff at 100 kts when #1 engine EGT exceeded the red line
SMS Analyst reported two cockpit windows shattering on two separate aircraft. Both windows were sent back to the manufacturer for analysis.

Air carrier First Officer reported an unstabilized approach while circling for a visual approach at night; which resulted in a GPWS warning. The Captain performed a go around and returned for a landing.

Air carrier flight crew reported conflicting messages regarding whether DG cargo was loaded during preflight. After further review it was determined that no regular DG cargo was loaded and that only non-regular DG was loaded.
ZAB Controller reported the complexity and workload levels at sectors were unsafe due to scheduled GPS jamming causing navigational errors and frequency congestion.
B737 MAX Captain reported turning back to departure airport after experiencing failure of the Captain's window outer pane.
Air carrier relief pilot reported GPS jamming which caused an excursion from the assigned altitude.

Flight crew reported that ground personnel observed a main wheel brake fire after taxi in and engine shut down. CFR was notified and the aircraft was evacuated using the main cabin door. Flight crew reported that the fire extinguished itself before the ground crew could extinguish the fire with an extinguisher.
Hawker 4000 flight crew reported the cockpit window breaking away inflight resulted in an explosive decompression. The flight crew performed a successful diversion.
Cessna 172 student pilot reported a runway excursion during the landing. The aircraft stopped in the grass and the pilot evacuated safely.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a delay deplaning passengers after flight attendant did not disarm door correctly and the escape slide partially deployed. Flight Attendant cited fatigue and illness as contributing factors.

ERJ 145 flight crew reported an advisory HYD1 Low Quantity Message; followed by a ENG1 HYD Pump Fail message. After communicating with Maintenance; the flight crew elected to return to the departure airport where; during the approach; additional hydraulic system messages appeared. The crew completed a safe landing and noted leaking hydraulic fluid on the post flight walk around.

Air Carrier pilot landing DEN reported Denver Center advised them they had to wait until communicating with Denver TRACON to receive an arrival runway which caused confusion and uncertainty as to which transition to fly. The reporter heard other arriving aircraft experience the same confusion with Denver TRACON over the correct route to fly.
Cessna U206G pilot reported an electrical malfunction while in cruise flight. The flight continued to destination and landed safely; where maintenance found a broken regulator wire.
A Tower Local Controller reported they cleared an aircraft for takeoff in front of another aircraft on short final. The landing aircraft initiated a go-around.

A Cessna 310 pilot reported a tower-directed missed approach due to landing gear appearing not to be fully extended. After a subsequent approach and landing; an inspection revealed damage to both engine propellers.
Pilot reported a conflict with another aircraft while taxiing to parking. Informal airport procedures and a lack of radio equipped aircraft were cited as contributing factors to the incident.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach into SEA in trail of an A321.
Air carrier Captain reported finding out a FAA facility was going to close via personal sources rather than through the flight release information.
Pilot reported the chart for the complex special procedure for an engine failure departing Runway 8L on the RATPK Departure from LAS airport to be confusing and possibly incorrect.

SR22 pilot reported engine roughness during cruise for a post-maintenance ferry flight. The pilot diverted to another airport where the engine shut down on its own after landing and exiting the runway.
A student pilot reported that they lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a runway excursion and prop strike.

Air carrier Captain reported encountering raised asphalt during the takeoff roll on Runway 23 at PVD. Captain expressed concern over the unknown condition of the nose gear; tear; or steering element upon landing.
Pilot flying C310 aircraft reported a runway excursion on landing rollout.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving a TCAS RA departing PHX when they passed 400 feet below a light aircraft.
C680 Captain reported excessive terrain closure on approach due to improper FMS programming of company required glide path. Two missed approaches were performed due to being unstabilized.
Air carrier Captain reported climbing through an altitude restriction departing DEN after encountering turbulence associated with weather and wake from another aircraft.
RV6 pilot reported a near miss with a C172 aircraft while in descent.
Dispatcher reported the information regarding an ATC Zero event was not received in time to change the aircraft's fuel load and prevent a diversion.
Center Controller reported the need to intervene after witnessing a Radar Controller have difficulty sequencing an approach resulting in an aircraft descending below the minimum IFR altitude.
A B767 flight crew reported pressurization malfunction on initial climb and return to departure airport.
B737 MAX flight crew reported an unstabilized approach and associated GPWS warning due to flap misconfiguration. The flight crew set flaps correctly and continued to landing.
Air carrier pilot reported the aircraft rolled when they released the brakes before the aircraft was connected to the tug during pushback.
A319 crew reported a hydraulic malfunction during climb. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed.
King Air pilot reported loss of directional control after landing resulted in a runway excursion.
B737 First Officer reported the flight crew forgot to send the DG acknowledgement code prior to brake release. The DG acknowledgement code was sent immediately once the DG Summary was received.
B737 Captain reported the lithium-ion battery on board was incorrectly processed on the forms given by Ramp.
C172 Pilot reported a rough running engine resulted in diverting to nearest airport to safe landing.
Hot Air Balloon operator on approach had a mid air collision with a drone. The drone caused damage to the balloon and struck the pilot in the head. Balloon was landed shortly after.
Air Carrier Captain reported on approach experiencing severe turbulence with momentary loss of control and over speed resulted in diversion.
PA-32 pilot reported an engine malfunction during cruise. The pilot requested and was provided priority handling to a suitable airport where a safe landing was completed.
Pilot flying PA-28 aircraft reported the engine RPM dropped significantly immediately after takeoff. Pilot reduced power to idle and landed straight ahead on the same runway.
UAS flight crew operating at an uncontrolled airport had a near miss with a helicopter.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization. Pilot exited airspace after realizing an incursion had taken place.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported an uneventful rejected takeoff due to an uncommanded auto-throttle disengagement.

Small aircraft pilot reported that during landing; a gust of wind caused the aircraft to yaw and they lost control of the aircraft as it went off the edge of the runway. They applied full power and performed a go around.

B757 Captain reported both left fuel boost pumps failed during cruise. The flight crew requested priority handling and descended to follow the QRH procedures. The Flight crew requested direct to destination airport and made a precautionary landing.

Air carrier First Officer reported a return to gate after Dispatch told them they had an improperly signed maintenance item.  The aircraft had several maintenance items that needed to be addressed before the flight due to it just coming out of long term storage.

Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft was taken out of service after returning to the gate to address the failures of the radio altimeter and the TCAS system. Reporter cited 5G interference as a probable cause.
Tug driver reported that while they were driving on a taxiway; an aircraft taxied onto the same taxiway caused the tug driver to take evasive action by driving onto the grass area.
Air carrier crew reported a EGPWS warning during a go around in VMC conditions at a tower controlled airport. The crew flew the assigned ATC traffic pattern altitude and landed safely.
Pilot flying Piper Aerostar 602P aircraft reported a malfunction with turbocharger system in climb; resulting in a return to departure airport.

GA pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft that was entering the traffic pattern passing below them. They climbed higher to increase separation and Tower instructed a 360 to correct the spacing.

ATC Controller reported during a position change with another controller; two aircraft entered into an airborne conflict situation. The conflict was not immediately noticed because the ATC personnel were social distancing.
Air carrier Inspector reported inspections on holes on the B787 cargo door hinge brackets are not being accomplished and being signed off as complete by an outside vendor.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported briefly flying above their LAANC authorized altitude.
Student Pilot reported a NMAC while on initial climb out.

Air carrier Captain reported the AMM and airport charts for ORD are confusing and do not depict a division of taxiways; but only one single piece of concrete. There are also no NOTAMs or construction taxi diagrams to clarify the taxiways.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported a brief entry into controlled airspace without authorization. The pilot immediately exited the controlled airspace.
ARTCC Controller reported C172 was not able to climb to altitude which resulted in controller issuing routing with pilot concurrence to maintain terrain clearance until MVA met.

CE-680A First Officer reported sliding off the taxiway during taxi from the ramp area on an ice covered taxiway. The aircraft was removed after the taxiway was treated by a deicer. There was no observed damage to aircraft or airport equipment.
First Officer reported a SATCOM antenna became dislodged on take off; repeatedly striking against the fuselage. Flight crew elected to divert for a precautionary landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported confusion with ATC's clearance resulted in a missed approach.

A220 pilot reported during the descent they dialed in the new altitude from ATC; then all of a sudden the aircraft started a steep angle of descent. They regained control of the aircraft back to normal descent and speed.
B767 Technician reported that engine oil cap was unsecured after servicing the engine causing a low oil pressure indication in flight.

B737 Flight Attendant reported being notified by pilots of a 'slight odor' due to an aircraft issue. Reporter stated the odor was very noticeable when flight deck door was opened during deplaning at destination.

Cessna pilot reported that while departing an uncontrolled airport; encountering an opposite direction aircraft that flew underneath and to the right of their aircraft resulting in the opposite direction aircraft. The pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Pilot reported diverting after the pitch trim indication system failed.

TRACON Controller reported an aircraft deviated from departure procedure and entered an MVA. The reporter issued a low altitude alert and questioned the pilot's ability to maintain own terrain and obstruction clearance.
Flight Instructor on training flight with student was notified of a NMAC after the flight was completed.

CRJ-700 First Officer reported receiving a Steering INOP message during final approach. Flight crew conducted a go-around and after troubleshooting returned for landing; stopping on the Runway to await towing.

Learjet 55 flight crew reported additional aileron input was required on takeoff roll. After landing and taxing to parking at destination airport; significant damage was discovered to the left wing with fuel leaking from the integral tank.
B787-9 Captain reported landing safely after diverting to an alternate airport following the failure of the right engine at FL270.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported undocumented 'Hazmat labeled cargo' during unloading.
B757 Captain reported flap control handle malfunction during taxi out. Flight returned to gate for maintenance action during which control of flap position failed completely.
TRACON Controller reported a MVA violation.

B777 Captain reported that upon arrival; the girt bar on 2L door was still attached to the floor; handle disarmed. Pilot manually unlocked girt bar from floor in order to egress aircraft. Escape slide did not deploy.
Air carrier Captain reported taxiing into the gate when a truck failed to yield resulted in the Captain braking hard to avoid a collision. The Captain reported the driver was heads down texting.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAS during initial approach. No evasive action was taken and the flight continued without further incident.
MD-11 flight crew reported fatigue and confusion resulted in a Rejected Take Off due to misdiagnosing a Bleed Air/ Pack/ Anti Ice Issue.
A319 flight crew reported a bird strike occurred during rotation followed by significant airframe vibration. Flight crew returned to airport and landed safely.
B737 flight crew reported that shortly after takeoff; the First Officer's flight director began malfunctioning; leading to the MCP and heading bug also being inoperative.

Air carrier flight crew reported while on the SFO BDEGA4 star and given a runway change; the FMS drops the active leg and leaves the aircraft in heading and altitude hold. Reporters cited a possible FMS coding error as contributing.

Air carrier First Officer reported an FD Pro chart discrepancy at DEN discovered during taxi for the ATC Tower frequency listed on various airport charts. The reporter identified different published Tower frequencies for the assigned takeoff runway.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported an issue with the approved time of a LAANC authorization. The pilot flew the mission during a time when he did not have LAANC authorization.
ILG Tower Controller reported losing radios due to maintenance actions.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying on the incorrect day of their LAANC authorization.

Single Pilot in a single engine aircraft reported a complete electrical failure. The pilot used a handheld radio and contacted the ATC tower and had them visually check the landing gear was down. A successful landing was accomplished.
Flight instructor reported student pilot lost directional control after landing and experienced a runway excursion.

A321 First Officer reported a problem with an ELAC fault that reappeared during cruise and contributed to elevator servo jack failure. The flight crew continued to destination airport and landed safely.
Part 107 pilot was approached by a disruptive person while flying. The pilot landed and requested assistance from the local police department.
Air carrier flight crew flying EP45 aircraft reported aborted takeoff due to EICAS low fuel pressure message.

ERJ-175 FO reported an upset event after encountering wake turbulence on final approach to LAX in trail of a B787. The flight descended below the glide slope on the approach; likely contributing to the encounter.

Captain reported he and technicians refused to sign an aircraft as airworthy until MEL procedures were accomplished. The MEL required cycling of the landing gear control handle; which they refused to do until the passengers were deplaned.

Shorts SD3-60 Captain reported loss of the normal braking system led to a taxiway excursion during taxi to take off. The flight crew secured the aircraft and awaited a tow crew to retrieve the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer was descending on a visual approach and they received a terrain caution followed by a terrain pull up warning.
CRJ550 flight crew reported copilot window failure resulted in descent and divert to a safe landing.
General Aviation pilot reported inadvertent BNA airspace incursion while on approach to JWN airport. The pilot exited the airspace once they became aware of their incursion.
Flight Instructor flying a C-172 reported a NMAC in the airport traffic pattern.

Flight crew reported a mountain wave encounter that disrupted the aircraft's altitude and speed. ATC was notified and the pilots were given a block altitude in order to return to their assigned altitude.
Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a mid-air air collision with an aircraft.
C172 pilot reported engine roughness during cruise. Pilot reported receiving vectors from ATC and landed safely.
B787-800 Flight Attendant reported avoiding an inadvertent escape slide deployment when gate agent re-opened armed aircraft door.
C337 pilot reported a NMAC with opposite direction traffic that required evasive action.

B767 flight crew reported during flap retraction after takeoff; the speed indicator jumped above the aircraft speed and the stick shaker activated. Flight crew performed stall recovery and continued flight.

A TRACON Controller reported an Air Carrier conducting an RNAV Approach advised them they were not authorized to conduct the approach or the published missed approach so the Controller had to issue vectors below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B777 First Officer reported loss of oil quantity on the number 1 engine. The flight crew diverted and landed safely.
A pilot reported they flew off course while departing on a SID and received a Low Altitude warning and expedited climb instruction from ATC.

A Helicopter pilot reported they received clearance into Class C airspace from the Tower Controller and after handoff to TRACON the TRACON Controller accused them of entering the Class C airspace without authorization.
B757-300 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing LAX in trail of an Airbus.
An Air Carrier pilot reported encountering severe turbulence causing a temporary loss of control when the aircraft entered a thunderstorm not depicted on their weather radar.
Light aircraft pilot inbound to DAL reported an NMAC with a glider that was apparently not equipped with ADSB Out.

An Air Carrier pilot reported the PHL Runway 09R Localizer is unreliable and caused a course deviation. The reporter stated maintenance personnel told him it was a known issue and a NOTAM was published but their are no NOTAM's addressing this issue.

CE-500 pilot reported departing the taxiway after losing directional control while taxiing. Aircraft was towed to the hangar where inspection revealed the left rudder pedal had caught on the carpet that was installed adjacent to the rudder pedal.
Pilot reported inability to control engine RPM in flight and experienced engine roughness. Pilot returned to departure airport and landed.
B777-200 flight crew reported a rejected takeoff due to receiving a main gear configuration warning on takeoff roll.
DA42 Flight instructor on training flight made an off field landing due to engine malfunction.
EMB-175 flight crew reported an aircraft loss of control after gate arrival.
A-321 Flight Attendant reported an improperly configured large wheelchair Lithium Ion battery was transported in passenger cabin.
A319 flight crew reported a loss of aircraft control during taxi in to the gate due to the parking brake getting stuck and not releasing.
Solo student and Flight Instructor reported the student lost aircraft control while landing resulting in a runway excursion with damage to the airport and no damage to the aircraft.
C185 pilot reported being notified by ATC of radio malfunctioning.
B737 Captain reported potentially encountering wake turbulence on approach to ATL in trail of another air carrier.
Passenger on an air carrier flight reported a safety concern due to a return to the departure airport after takeoff for mechanical problems and insufficient fuel. The flight landed safely.
Air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAS during arrival to BWI airport. No evasive action was taken.
A320 Captain reported experiencing loss of G hydraulic system in cruise flight. Flight continued to destination airport and landed safely.
Small aircraft pilot reported a loss of proper terrain clearance while being vectored for landing at JYO due to ATC airspace constraints.
Pilot flying military trainer reported NMAC with 737 aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported the CYYZ Runway 23 PAPI and ILS are not in agreement and provide different results from one another on the glidepath.

Student pilot with Flight Instructor reported two critical ground conflicts with an aerial applicator aircraft at a non-towered airport. The aircraft attempted to land on the same runway the student's aircraft was on; then after going around overflew them and landed on runway while student aircraft was still on the runway.
B737 air carrier flight crew reported loss of hydraulics on descent. Flight crew landed uneventfully at destination.
GA Flight Instructor reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the vicinity of SAX VOR.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported forgetting to inform ATC of the conclusion of the mission as required by their COA.

C172 pilot reported power loss during cruise. The pilot requested priority handling and began to divert. ATC suggested using carburetor heat; which quickly solved the problem. The pilot cancelled the request for priority handling and continued flight to destination airport.
A321 First Officer reported a fume event during approach which he characterized as 'dirty socks'. Fumes persisted until gate arrival.

ZOB Center Controller reported deviating aircraft from a line of thunderstorms and precipitation that was on the border of several Center facilities and the busy nature of the complex and tense situation led to potential airspace violations.
CL-350 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach into SFO in trail of a B777 that resulted in 70-degree banks in both directions.
Air carrier Captain reported ramp markings at CLT airport were not updated in charts to reflect recent construction related changes; nor were they indicated via NOTAMs.
Ramp crew reported uncommanded aircraft movement after flight crew confirmed brakes set during pushback.
Air carrier Captain reported that the MSLP ATUMA3B SID depiction differs from the narrative.
Air Carrier Ramp crew chief reported a weight discrepancy on the planned cargo load configuration. A corrected load plan was developed and cargo reconfigured prior to departure.
B737 MAX 8 flight crew reported an air turn back caused by a malfunctioning central warning computer.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into PHX in trail of a B737-800.
Air carrier pilot reported observing broken runway and taxiway lights that have not been repaired for an extended period of time.
B737NG flight crew reported safely returning to departure airport after receiving a Master Caution warning for #1 engine Cowl Anti-Ice.
Air Carrier Captain reported 'blood platelets' parcel was incorrectly transported in the rear cargo instead of the forward compartment.
Pilot reported engine failure after completing a low approach and landed on a nearby road with no injury or damage and was towed to the airport.
A Flight Instructor reported a NMAC after turning to a heading assigned by the Tower.
Light aircraft Student Pilot reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on final approach at AFW airport.
C-172 pilot reported experiencing aircraft control difficulty caused by the yoke-mounted iPad.
Ultralight pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid an air carrier aircraft resulted in a NMAC situation.

DA-40 Flight Instructor reported a braking malfunction during landing on a training flight. The instructor aborted the landing and diverted to an alternate airport with a longer runway and landed with partial braking.
Air carrier First Officer reported the loss of aircraft pressurization during descent.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a drone during departure from SEA airport.
Small aircraft pilot reported an NMAC occurred while entering the OBE airport traffic pattern when another aircraft flew by them at high speed.
SR20 pilot reported an electrical failure in flight. Pilot landed safely.
Wide body air carrier F/O reported receiving a low altitude alert from ANC Tower on a visual approach to Runway 33.
C172 Flight Instructor reported as power was increased aircraft shook and failed to get full power resulted in a priority return to departure airport.
A B737-800 pilot reported unreliable airspeed indications due to icing during initial approach. Flight crew went around and landed safely.

C-172 Flight Instructor with student reported aircraft throttle was stuck at full power while entering a VFR traffic pattern. Instructor received vectors to another airport and placed mixture to cutoff when a safe landing was assured.
B777 Captain reported multiple EICAS messages resulted in expeditious handling and divert to a safe landing.
A Ground Controller reported an aircraft turned the wrong direction on a taxiway placing it in conflict with another aircraft.
Citation V First Officer reported that during a Fire Warning event in flight; the Captain declined to follow checklists and established procedures and elected to continue the flight to destination.

C172 pilot reported while on final approach they inadvertently turned on the autopilot resulting in yoke binding and aircraft wanting to roll left. Pilot turned off autopilot and flight controls functioned normally.
C441 pilot reported auto pilot malfunction during descent caused a temporary loss of control.

Air Carrier Captain reported being notified by the flight attendants of a 'chemical/exhaust' odor after departure. The fumes dissipated after a brief period only to resume during descent. Flight Attendants received medical evaluation after the flight.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS jamming starting 10 minutes before descent and continuing for 45 minutes into RNO.
GA pilot reported after landing at 07FA non-towered airport they held in position at the end of the runway prior to back taxiing due to another aircraft landing behind them.
EMB-120 flight crew reported a main and auxiliary generators failed in cruise and the APU could not be started resulting in a diversion and landing using battery power.
A320 Captain reported during landing at night on a snow covered runway the aircraft did not stop until entering the overrun.
Captain reported a high altitude cabin depressurization event causing an immediate descent and subsequent request for expedited handling and diversion.
E145 Flight Crew reported the pitch trim controls on the yoke failed on both sides during initial climb. Flight crew performed an air turn back to landing.
Light aircraft pilot reported clearing a golf cart by about 40 feet after it drove onto the runway just before he took off at 1B2 airport.
C-172 pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing rollout due to a flat tire on landing. The aircraft stopped safely on the runway and was provided a tow to the FBO.

Pilot of a single engine aircraft reported losing engine power on departure; returned to the airport; and landed safely. The reporter stated after landing they discovered the fuel gauge was inoperative.
LR-55 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to LAX in trail of an A320.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict during push-back with another air carrier aircraft pushing back at the same time.
Air Carrier Captain reported being notified by a Ramp Agent that a passenger bag containing a Li Ion battery was removed from aircraft after landing.

EMB-170 flight crew reported the Parking Brake Not Released EICAS message displayed in cruise. After running the QRH; the message disappeared; but flashed several times. During descent; the message reappeared and would not clear. The Flight Crew requested priority handling and made a precautionary landing at destination airport.
A320 First Officer reported a dual system FADEC loss in cruise. The flight crew requested priority handling and continued to destination airport at a lower altitude.
A Cubcrafter pilot reported an NMAC with a Citation when both aircraft departed intersecting runways from a non-towered airport at the same time.
Citation pilot report that the unsafe landing gear light illuminated on initial descent. Landing gear extended normally on approach to the divert airport.

CRJ-700 flight crew reported during a Category II approach at approximately 150 feet AGL; the aircraft began to pitch down at an increasing rate with the autopilot engaged. The flight crew conducted a go around and while entering a hold without autopilot; the aircraft overbanked to 45 degrees. Flight crew recovered and returned to departure airport.
B777 Flight Crew reported push crew alerted of tow bar failure resulted in loss of control event due to uncommanded aircraft movement.
Mooney M20 pilot reported a hard landing; resulting in a failed nose wheel structure; and prop strike; necessitating a tow to the FBO ramp.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during a training flight with an oncoming aircraft. The Instructor took evasive action to avoid a collision.
An Instructor pilot conducting a practice instrument approach reported a NMAC with opposite direction traffic.
Air carrier flight crew reported a rejected takeoff due to a Runway Awareness and Advisory System alert.

Air Carrier First Officer reported a fumes event during preflight when a pneumatic ground cart was connected. Upon activating the packs; a foul odor of musty socks permeated the entire aircraft which necessitated the packs and cart to be deselected.  Shortly thereafter Maintenance assigned an aircraft swap.

Corporate jet Captain reported another aircraft pilot performed a go-around due to the close proximity of Captain's aircraft during climb-out. Captain suggested the airport needs a temporary Tower or VFR traffic restriction due to the increase of flight traffic during the winter.

Learjet 75 Captain; conducting a medical airlift flight; experienced several auto shutdown/restarts with the left engine. The engine stabilized at reduced power and successful diversion was accomplished.
B737 pilot reported finding during walk-around that critical fasteners were missing on modification to flap track canoe fairing.
Air carrier ramp employee reported the DFW Terminal E hardstand has poor lighting and presents a safety hazard for aircraft movement during non-daylight hours.
Air carrier Captain reported discovering after pushback that dry ice was on board without proper notification. The flight crew returned to the gate and the dry ice was removed from the aircraft.
A TRACON Controller reported an unidentified VFR aircraft flew into Class B airspace into confliction with an air carrier being vectored for a visual approach resulting in a NMAC.
EMB-505 Captain reported on climbout a fuel pump and cross feed failure resulted in a fuel imbalance; an expeditious divert and uneventful landing.
Student pilot observed a NMAC between two other aircraft in the pattern of a non towered airport.

C182 pilot reported the failure to open the crossfeed valve for the fuel tanks potentially led to the engine to begin sputtering and returned to normal after switching fuel tanks. ATC was advised of the power loss and gave the pilot priority handling.
Flight instructor on training flight with student reported NMAC with another aircraft on approach.
An Air Carrier pilot reported a change in flight deck configuration procedures could result in aircraft departing with undetected maintenance issues.

Air carrier technician reported the Right Hand Main Landing Gear Downlock Pin was not removed prior to flight; causing the aircraft to return to the airport due to the Main Landing Gear not retracting on takeoff.

PA-46 pilot reported loosing autopilot; autothrottle; yaw damper and trim during climbout resulting in inability to hold altitude.  Pilot returned to the departure airport with ATC assistance.  Allegedly; the issues were caused by an update to the G3000 system.

Air taxi pilot reported entering the wrong taxiway. Pilot stated signage was a factor and that the published FAA taxi diagram available in the flight deck does not show DEN's Taxiway CC. Pilot further stated the Jeppesen diagram may also have inaccurate taxiway depictions.

Air Carrier First Officer reported; for the MCO ILS 18R; from the IAF altitude to the GS intercept altitude there is not enough distance to make a smooth transition. Pilot suggests the IAF crossing altitude should be re-evaluated and be 1;000 feet lower.
B737 Captain reported an inflight upset at FL360 while being 7 miles in trail of another B737.
CL-30 Captain reported on departure with First Officer as pilot flying; they were not climbing at a safe rate resulting in flight towards terrain and ATC issuing a low altitude alert.
Tug driver reported while pushing an aircraft from the gate another aircraft taxiing in was supposed to give way but did not and almost hit the wing of their aircraft.
Cirrus pilot reported a prop strike occurred during a landing attempt after encountering jet blast from a preceding heavy aircraft.
Part 91 pilot reported during taxi they had to suddenly stop due to another aircraft that crossed in front of them at a high rate of speed.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported a courier submitted a Liquid Nitrogen container with no required Hazmat labels and no shipping documents.
E545 Captain reported conducting a test flight and receiving a traffic alert and advisory.

CRJ-700 Captain reported sealant began to smear across the windscreen after takeoff. The Captain's windshield had been replaced and speed tape applied over the uncured sealant to expedite departure. The flight crew requested priority handling and made an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Air carrier crew reported multiple bird strikes during climb; then observed engine damage and cockpit odor. The flight returned to the departure airport and landed safely.

Air carrier Dispatcher reported that Load Planning was notified of an MEL weight restriction during preflight. Load Planning responded to the Dispatcher that the MEL had been removed; but learned from Maintenance Control that it was never closed in the system.

General aviation Flight Instructor reported a near miss while in takeoff position on a runway with an aircraft turning final approach to land on the opposite runway at a non-towered airport. The pilot waited for the traffic to go around then safely performed their takeoff.
A flight crew conducting a Visual Approach reported parallel runway traffic deviated off course causing them to receive a descending TCAS/RA which resulted in a Low Altitude Alert from the Tower.
C172 student pilot reported misapplication of brake rather than rudder on landing resulted in runway excursion.

GA pilot reported during takeoff another aircraft was in the traffic pattern for the opposite direction runway. The departing pilot made a turn just after liftoff to avoid the opposite direction inbound aircraft.

B777-200 Captain reported Hazmat final documents missing details of a Hazmat apparatus that was loaded on broad. After an intensive attempt to obtain details of the item were located the Captain had the apparatus removed.
ZSE Controller reported aircraft in holding issued a descent clearance made an incorrect turn resulted in entering a higher MVA and a CFTT event.

Air carrier pilot reported while taxing to the gate they had to take evasive action to avoid a vehicle driving across the protected gate area.  After yielding to the vehicle the flight crew taxied into the gate.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported low altitude alert on approach. Performed go-around and landed uneventfully.
A319 Captain reported flight attendant notification of intense odor in cabin prior to boarding.

An Air Carrier Captain reported they spilled a beverage into the trim control panel prior to departure which caused the trim indicators to malfunction while on final approach to their destination. Maintenance signed the aircraft off as airworthy prior to the departure.
Technician reported not installing a cotter pin on throttle control cable; causing a loss of power and an emergency landing on a roadway.
PA-46 pilot reported pressurization malfunction and the auto pilot would not trim correctly during climb. The pilot manually trimmed the aircraft; reset pressurization; and resumed climb.
A320 First Officer reported an ECAM warning for the #1 engine's thrust reverser being unlocked appeared just after takeoff. The flight crew ran through checklists and then performed an air turnback.
A319 pilot reported a dual engine stall at cruise altitude in smooth air. Crew followed ECAM and checklist and continued on to destination.
Air carrier FO reported a dual magnetometer and dual AHRS failures during taxi.
Flight Instructor reported the student lost directional control during takeoff.

Eurocopter Technician reported installing tail rotor retaining bolts incorrectly during bearing replacement. The bolts were reinstalled correctly after a routine inspection discovered the discrepancy.

Technician reported non compliance with MEL (M) procedures were discovered during trouble shooting and deferring of a right ram air full open light during flight. The correct procedures were complied with and the aircraft was returned to service.
Flight Instructor with student practicing maneuvers in a practice area reported a NMAC with another aircraft flying through the area.
B777 First Officer reported a rejected take off as the result of a rudder control issue.
HCF TRACON Air Traffic Controller reported an aircraft failed to track the STAR resulted in entering a higher MVA.
Pilot reported taking evasive action in response to an NMAC with a solo student pilot in the traffic pattern who did not follow ATC instructions to remain on the west side of the freeway.
Air taxi Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into PHX in trail of a regional jet that resulted in a steep bank and an altitude overshoot.
J3 Cub pilot reported loosing tailwheel control and ground looping the aircraft during landing.
A321 pilot reported experiencing an upset event in cruise due to turbulence; resulting in altitude loss.

Extra 200/300 Series pilot reported an NMAC event during initial climb. After the flight; the pilot reported the Tower Controller's sequence instructions were given when both aircraft were already airborne and in close proximity.
Flight Instructor in a light sport aircraft reported while in the VFR traffic pattern the engine quit resulting in the flight instructor taking the controls and landing safely at the airport.
GA flight instructor reported unusual attitude event during instrument training with student.
Aerostar pilot reported controlled flight toward terrain event while sightseeing during descent. Pilot responded to onboard warning to avoid terrain and landed uneventfully.
EMB-145 Captain reported a possible lightning strike during severe turbulence. The flight crew reported a burning smell in the aircraft which dissipated gradually.
C150 Student reported a rough running engine due to a cracked cylinder on climbout resulted in a return to a safe landing.
GA Pilot reported SQL ATIS as consistently unreadable and unreliable.
An Air Carrier pilot reported an incorrect crossing restriction for a STAR in the FMC Database.
C182 Pilot reported while landing the winds were shifting; the aircraft bounced causing a propeller ground strike.

B757-200 flight crew reported losing cabin altitude and receiving a cabin altitude master warning and EICAS warning in cruise. As the aircraft began to descend; the cabin altitude warning went away and the flight crew continued to perform an air turnback.
An Air Carrier flight crew reported the Captain braked suddenly while taxiing on the ramp to avoid another aircraft on the same taxi route.

While taxiing for takeoff; Captain reported that another aircraft crew observed an engine fire on their aircraft. The crew of the taxiing aircraft shut down the engine; and after inspection; taxied back to the gate.

Small aircraft Flight Instructor reported not seeing the other aircraft and ended up taking evasive action due to being in close proximity with said aircraft. ATC had not provided assistance in helping maintain aircraft separation.
B757 pilot reported left hydraulic system failure during descent. Flight crew landed and after clearing runway was towed to ramp.
B737 Captain reported while on short final; a helicopter flew within 400 feet; resulting in a NMAC.
PA-46 corporate pilot reported an engine failure with smoke under the cowling during cruise flight. The pilot drifted down and diverted to a close airport and landed safely without damage or injury.
Air carrier Captain reported the taxiway diagram at MYR airport does not match the ground signage.

Air carrier Captain reported a chart safety concern highlighting the lack of parking gates/spots on the STT airport diagram; contributing to ground conflicts due to the lack of position awareness of other aircraft in the terminal area and minimal area to maneuver.
Air Carrier Captain Line Check Airman reported the aircraft database for the MMMX ILS 05R Z does not match the approach chart.
B747-400 pilot reported landing safely after the loss of hydraulic system 4 in cruise flight that was related to tire failure on takeoff.
Air carrier crew reported a return to the departure airport; for an overweight landing; after a failed flap retraction during initial climb.
GA pilot reported an NMAC with powered gliders during approach to L52 non-towered airport. Reporter stated the gliders were not following pattern procedures.
Pilot reported a crew member observed an NMAC between a landing aircraft and another taking off at IKK non-towered airport.

CRJ-900 Captain reported at 800 feet after takeoff; receiving HYDRAULIC 1LO PRESS CAUTION MESSAGE; followed OB SPOILERONS; OB GND SPLRS; OB FLT SPOILERS; L REV INOP Caution Messages. The flight crew ran the QRH and checklists; and coordinated an air turn back at departure airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported conducting a UAS flight in controlled airspace without LAANC authorization.
B737 Captain reported a 'dirty socks' odor during takeoff and climb-out; which dissipated after 5 minutes. Flight continued to destination with no further issues.
An air carrier Captain reported the aircraft rolled uncommanded during pushback when the ground crew disconnected the tug before flight crew set brakes.
Ramp Agent reported a precautionary landing due a water bottle hindering the nose landing gear from retracting.

Air carrier flight crew and Center Controller reported malfunctioning radio altimeters during climbout from PDX resulting in an altitude deviation. The reporters made reference to possible 5G interference.
Air Carrier flight crew reported GPWS warning on approach and flight crew complied with advisory.

Air Carrier First Officer reported during cruise that the Flight Release was not amended to reflect Hazmat loaded on the aircraft. An ACARS message was sent to Dispatch and amended Release electronically.
Air carrier flight crew reported a UAS 300 ft. below on final approach.
Pilot reported an unfamiliarity with the airport; misinterpreting ATC instructions; and confusing taxiway signage contributed to a taxiway incursion.
B737 Captain reported after takeoff the landing gear would not retract and a safe return to departure airport was accomplished.
Air carrier Captain reported a critical ground conflict with a vehicle which resulted in the flight crew taking evasive action.
TRACON Controller conducting OJT reported they did not detect their Trainee issued an altitude clearance to an aircraft which placed it below the MVA.
GA pilot reported an NMAC in aerobatic practice area with an aircraft from a flight school. Pilot stated they would now use the school's frequency to maintain situational awareness.
Beechcraft King Air 350 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while on initial approach to DAL from a preceding B737 that was 7 miles ahead at the same altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported an EFB is missing multiple approach procedures for the CYYC airport; yet those approaches are listed in the aircraft database and available for use.
C-150 pilot reported engine failure in flight and diverted to a safe landing.

B767-300 FO reported the aircraft rolled about 6 feet after brakes were released at the gate; even though they were signaled by the ground crew that chocks were installed and they were clear to release brakes.
Center Controller reported an aircraft descended below the assigned altitude and flew below the minimum published altitude for terrain clearance.

Instructor Pilot with Trainee reported while performing maneuvers the engine began to run rough with a loss of power. With assistance from ATC the Instructor diverted to a nearby airport. Fuel contamination is suspected by Maintenance.

C680A Flight Crew reported continuous banging when attempting to retract landing gear after take off. The Nose Landing Gear would not retract. The flight crew  performed an air turn back and a precautionary landing with the nose gear strut not extended.

Pilot reported experiencing a NMAC in the traffic pattern at FNL and suggested that a radar feed be installed so Controllers can better track aircraft operating at and nearby this remote Tower airport.
G450 Captain reported ATC issued them a routing below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude resulting in a Ground Proximity warning.

C162 Flight Instructor with student reported that while practicing touch and goes at a non-towered airport; the student inadvertently started the takeoff without getting the carburator heat in and the flaps up resulting in runway excursion.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported overriding GPWS alert on approach.
A321 flight crew reported performing an air turnback after experiencing an engine failure on takeoff.
A321 Flight Attendant in Charge reported a fume event during boarding.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering GPS interference that led to the momentary loss of the left and right GPS receivers and the flight crew notified ATC and completed supplementary procedures.
Light aircraft pilot reported an NMAC on approach to TKI airport when a faster twin turboprop aircraft; who was reportedly not in contact with the Tower; overflew him.
Captain reported cargo door departed the aircraft in flight and continued to destination airport.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict occurred when an aircraft failed to yield to them during taxi. Flight crew used aggressive braking to avoid a collision.
Technician reported that an out of calibration borrowed tool with altered calibration date was used to conduct maintenance on an aircraft.
A Light Sport aircraft pilot attempting to exit the runway reported they attempted to apply the brakes but placed their feet in the wrong position resulting in a taxiway/runway excursion.
GA flight instructor reported an NMAC while approaching the airport and took evasive action to avoid the other aircraft.
BE-55 Instructor Pilot reported while the student was attempting to exit the runway they were too fast; resulting in a runway excursion.

B737 air carrier crew reported loss of control during push back when the tow bar disconnected from the aircraft. The ground crew notified the flight deck; Captain smoothly applied the brakes and no injury or damage occurred.
Air carrier maintenance technicians reported possibly not securing oil caps after servicing a B757 which resulted in loss of engine oil and engine shutdown inflight.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without authorization.
Recreational / Hobbyist UAS pilot reported flying in controlled airspace without LAANC authorization.

Air carrier Captain reported a temporary loss of directional control during the takeoff roll by First Officer. The aircraft control was transferred to the Captain who executed a safe rejected takeoff.
A321 First Officer reported an engine failure.
General aviation pilot reported entering the runway for takeoff at a non-towered airport causing an aircraft on final approach to go-around.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC while entering the downwind at a non-towered airport.
B737 First Officer reported ground personnel said right engine gushing fluid was confusing to flight deck as to which engine; resulted in both engines being shut down.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing GPS jamming and equipment going inoperative after takeoff from LLBG Airport. The equipment eventually recovered after some time.
A321 pilot reported an engine temperature issue during climbout resulting in a return to the departure airport.

Air carrier crew reported an altitude deviation on final approach resulting in the aircraft being below the glide path and below the maneuvering speed. The crew recovered airspeed and glide path then landed.
B737 flight crew reported pressurization issues during climbout resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Part 107 UAS pilot reported they were flying during times of intermittent VFR weather conditions. The UAS crew chose to land and wait for better weather.

A Tower Local Controller reported a departure deviated from its clearance and turned back to the airport to land and into confliction with an arrival on final approach.The Tower received an alert from an automated system (ASDE X ATAP) warning that the aircraft was aligned with a taxiway and not the runway although the aircraft had corrected back to course. The reporter states that false alerts from the ASDE X ATAP (Airport Surface Detection Equipment Taxiway Arrival Prediction Software) is a recurring issue.

Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a fire warning bell during takeoff roll. The flight crew continued takeoff and returned to the departure airport where the crew evacuated as a precautionary measure after a heat signature on the aircraft was found by airport responders.
Pilot reported during cruise the engine began running rough and the oil pressure rapidly dropped. Pilot diverted and performed an off-airport landing.

Air carrier pilot flying reported experiencing GPS jamming in the area that caused the GPS to malfunction and led to the flight crew needing to hand-fly the approach. The reporter stated there were no NOTAMs.

Air carrier flight crew reported that the engines remained at idle when the thrust levers were advanced in order to taxi off the runway after landing and the takeoff warning horn sounded. After thrust reversers were re-stowed; engines responded normally.

ERJ-170 Flight Attendant reported to the Captain and Gate Agent that two passenger bags with laptops that had lithium-ion batteries were mistakenly gate checked in cargo during boarding. The Captain then deemed it safe to not remove as the devices were off and approved gate departure.
EMB-145 Captain reported a hydraulic system 2 low fluid ECAM. The system fluid level continued to decrease to zero. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.
B737 Captain reported #1 engine rolled back to zero thrust during final approach. Captain continued to landing.
Fractional captain reported receiving an aircraft-generated terrain alert while on approach into ASE. Flight crew had terrain in sight and continued approach.
EMB-175 flight crew reported nose wheel steering failure during taxi.
ZID FLM and Controller reported CMH airport going ATC zero and described all the issues associated with the closure.
Flight Instructor reported during landing the engine failed resulting in the aircraft being able to exit the runway without power.  Flight Instructor was able to restart the engine and taxi.
B787 Captain reported they had to brake during gate arrival to avoid hitting a truck that sped in front of the aircraft.
Helicopter pilot reported taking evasive action during an NMAC event.
Fractional Captain reported a total loss of GPS and failure of associated systems immediately after takeoff; and stated it may have been caused by a GPS jamming device or some other external cause.
C-170 pilot reported they observed another aircraft off their right side taking evasive action to avoid a collision with them.
C182 Pilot reported encountering a crosswind on the takeoff roll which resulted in a runway excursion and contact with a taxiway sign.
Air carrier Captain reported hearing non-aviation information on a ZMA 126.52 frequency interfering with regular communications.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC at a non towered airport when an aircraft departed a runway underneath them.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict critical during taxi. The Captain took evasive action to avoid a collision.
G200 Captain reported they were flying an aircraft that had an engine with turbine blades that were beyond the life limits due to incorrect records input. The aircraft was grounded for maintenance.

Air Carrier Captain reported a Dispatch directed ground gate return to correct a Hazmat cargo load configuration error. Ramp personnel corrected configuration error and flight continued to destination with no further issues.
EMB 145 Pilot reported loss of engine bleed air caused loss of cabin pressure and anti ice protection; resulting in an emergency descent and a diversion.
B737 flight crew reported they had to maneuver off the center line to avoid contacting the tail of an aircraft parked at the adjacent gate.
Air carrier flight crew reported being notified of a DG load placement error during departure taxi. The flight returned to a parking stand where the weight and balance issues were corrected.
Air carrier flight crew reported a critical ground conflict while on short-final approach due to an aircraft crossing runway. Flight executed a safe go around.
B737 flight crew reported a loss of Hydraulic System A pressure and fluid; resulting in a diversion to the nearest suitable airport.
PA-28 flight instructor on training flight with student pilot reported brake failure upon landing.
Air Carrier Pilots reported a communication dead spot at EWR airport.

First Officer reported that during a lavatory break; both pilots were out of the cockpit at the same time and the flight attendant was swapping seats in the cockpit; leaving the aircraft uncontrolled.
PA-30 pilot reported while starting the aircraft experiencing a left engine fire resulting in engine shut down and evacuating the aircraft.
DA20-C1 Pilot reported aircraft shaking during flap retraction resulted in a safe return for landing.  Pilot also reported lack of attention to pattern traffic resulted in an airborne conflict.
B777 flight crew reported a passenger's electronic device experienced a thermal runaway during boarding. The device was placed in a Thermal Containment Bag and removed by Maintenance.
Pilot reported ground loop occurred after landing in windy conditions; resulting in a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft.

B737 flight crew reported a failure of the First Officer's airspeed indicator on final approach. The crew verified that the Captain had two good Airspeed Indications and continued the approach and landed without issues at the destination airport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported on arrival into DEN the aircraft's navigation system lost the ADS-B and GPS signal.

Cessna 172 Flight Instructor trainee reported a loss of controlled flight and aircraft flight control upset when receiving stall and spin training from another flight instructor. The trainee indicated a loss of situational awareness by the instructor before recovering the aircraft and returning for a landing.
B757 Captain reported the aircraft slid on taxiway during a turn due to surface conditions. The aircraft was towed to a different taxi route to continue taxi and the flight eventually departed.

Corporate jet flight crew reported the aircraft descended below the cleared altitude while on autopilot which led to having to hand-fly the approach visually. The reporter noted that there were numerous aircraft that were reporting issues with the approach.
B757 Captain reported Anti Skid Brake system failure n flight. The flight crew requested priority handling and landed at destination airport with Anti Skid or Auto Brake Systems available.
Air carrier pilot reported an unstable approach and hard landing.
Air carrier pilot flying reported encountering GPS jamming that led to a loss of ADS-B and all GPS satellites but both components were later restored.
Flight instructor reported students unstable approach led to a hard landing that resulted in the nose gear to collapse.

A Corporate pilot reported parking brake failed while starting engines on the ramp causing the plane to roll forward. The ground power unit was still attached and was knocked over on its side; causing a fuel spill.; "Pilot reported a dual FMS failure during flight. The fight crew performed an air turn back to departure airport."

During departure climb air carrier flight crew reported the left fuel tank showing a high rate of fuel consumption compared to the right tank. Flight crew elected to return to departure airport and landed uneventfully.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported a hazardous area of bags and parcels; including Hazmat labeled parcels; obstructing the aisleway adjacent to the shipping office.

S-76 EMS pilot reported after asking ATC to cross through their airspace on a medical mission; the controller asked the pilot to remain a mile north of the runway. Traversing the area; the EMS pilot needed to use evasive action to avoid an aircraft departing the runway.

CRJ-200 Captain reported being notified that he neglected to sign required Hazmat documents while operating as PIC during the previous week. Reporter stated they did not receive any information or documents that DG cargo was loaded onboard.
Ground Marshal reported a technician drove a lift platform truck into the safety zone and under the aircraft wing during pushback. Ground Marshal gave a stop signal with wands but pushback continued.

Flight instructor and student reported during touchdown the nose wheel had a strong shimmy and collapsed after the flight crew made several attempts to dampen the shimmy. The aircraft slid to a stop off the runway.

MU-2G/K pilot reported descending below minimum vectoring altitude on approach and not realizing the autopilot was descending on glide path while the aircraft was one mile off course. Pilot was vectored back around for another approach which was conducted safely to a landing.

B737 MAX 8 Captain reported a distracting high pitched tone on descent that led to being high on a fix. With the help of Maintenance; the Reporter discovered the problem may be caused by the ground crew's unsafe plug removal procedure.
Air carrier ground personnel reported discovering that the HAZMAT was unsecured and the aircraft had already begun pushback prior to the flight being finalized.
PA-28 student and instructor reported a propeller strike as they attempted a last second go-around after realizing the landing gear was not extended during a power off 180 landing attempt.
Air carrier flight crew reported laser events while on approach to SJC which distracted the pilot flying resulting in an unstable approach and a go-around.
Air carrier Captain reported a fume event on the flight deck during gate pusback. Flight crew executed a gate return where passengers and flight crew deplaned promptly.
MD-11 First Officer reported during engine start at the gate the aircraft began to roll so he advised the Captain who activated the brakes.
Cessna 150 flight instructor reported experiencing engine failure after takeoff.
Flight crew flying 737 MAX aircraft reported missing the before takeoff checklist and Flight Attendant notification prior to takeoff due to engine warm up time constraints.
Pilot holding short of the runway at a non-towered airport reported observing a critical ground conflict involving three aircraft in different phases of takeoff and landing.
A319 Captain reported a fuel disagree message during arrival. Flight crew continued to destination and found a fuel leak during post flight walk around.

Air carrier Maintenance Lead Technician reported accepting a late arriving important parcel. The late parcel caused a departure delay in order to load in the cargo compartment and to issue a revised final load plan.
CRJ-900 Captain reported temporary loss of control and Flight Attendant injury due to unexpected severe turbulence.
Air carrier Captain reported a maintenance diversion due to fuel system malfunctions during departure climb. Flight crew diverted to a suitable airport where an overweight landing was made.

Air Carrier Dispatcher reported being notified after gate departure that Hazmat cargo was not secured in accordance with Hazmat regulations. Aircraft was summoned back to departure gate to correct configuration error.
Technician reported referencing the aircraft Log; the aircraft experienced cabin depressurization during flight. Technicians found the right-side pack duct had blown off from the bypass valve.
Observer reported a weather balloon being launched without a NOTAM. They also observed a UAS flying over crowds.
C172 Flight Instructor and A36 Pilot reported A36 pilot failed to see the C172 short final and overflew the Cessna resulting in a NMAC event.

Air carrier Captain reported poor taxiway lighting and signage at ZSPD Airport made it difficult to identify the taxiways and the flight crew deviated from ATC's instructions as a result. The night time lighting conditions along with the rain were also contributing factors that added to the difficulty.

Part 135 Air Taxi Pilots reported entering a small taxiway that was not large enough for their aircraft and there was a faded X on the pavement. After parking the pilots stated they were informed their wing tip had made contact with another parked aircraft.
Air carrier Ground Employee reported software issues while attempting to correct a code error. Ultimately; the error was corrected and flight was closed out.
ZMA Controller reported issues related to weather deviations and Traffic Management related issues.
Flight crew reported ATC issued a low altitude alert while they were on base leg for a visual approach.
Flight Instructor reported taking evasive action to avoid a near midair collision with another aircraft.
BE-23 pilot reported that an alternator failure caused the loss of most of the aircraft's communication and navigation systems in-flight.
Citation pilot reported while cleared for ILS approach they descended below the crossing altitude. Pilot rejoined the glideslope and landed at the airport.
B767 Captain reported refusing an aircraft due to inoperative lavatories despite pressure from company maintenance to accept the aircraft and defer the lavatories for an 8 hour flight.
Air Carrier flight crew reported catering crew loaded dry Ice in the galley oven without notifying flight crew resulting in a potentially dangerous situation.

C172 pilot and a Learjet 45 Captain reported a NMAC. The C172 pilot was landing and the Learjet pilot was departing from a non-towered airport. Both pilots took evasive action to avoid a conflict. The Learjet 45 Captain describes the situation as 'the root cause of this near miss to be the challenges of separating arriving VFR traffic from departing IFR traffic.'
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a rotten egg odor to the Captain during boarding. The Captain determined it to be a fume event and instructed everyone to deplane.

Gulfstream IV Captain reported receiving a HYD QUANTITY LOW EICAS message after landing during taxi. Captain stated the aircraft had been previously serviced for a spoiler related service bulletin. Post flight inspection revealed a leak under the right wing.
Air carrier ramp employee reported a near-collision with a ground vehicle while he was wing walking an aircraft into the gate.
Q400 Captain reported receiving a master warning during a reposition flight after maintenance. The flight crew then correctly configured the cabin pressure control panel and continued the flight.
CRJ-200 Captain reported while flying a visual approach they experienced AC power loss resulting in landing with emergency power only.
A320 flight crew reported a loss of engine oil during climb out that resulted in engine shut down and divert.
A220 pilot reported a flight control malfunction resulting in return to departure airport and possible deviation from ATC instruction.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported setting incorrect altitude for approach. Flight crew realized mistake; corrected glidepath and landed uneventfully.
Flight Instructor reported encountering wind shear on final approach which pushed them below glide path resulting in a Low Altitude Alert from ATC and a missed approach.
SR20 student pilot reported they diverted due to an unreliable fuel quantity indication and a high oil temperature warning.
Air carrier flight crew reported aircraft refusal with failed TCAS unsafe to fly into congested airspace.
ERJ 170-100 First Officer reported that an escape slide inadvertently deployed after aircraft was delivered to maintenance hangar due to doors being left in the armed position.
Air carrier fight crew reported an 'overwhelming dirty socks' odor during pre-flight. Captain requested Maintenance and submitted a logbook entry.
B767 air carrier crew reported being issued an ATC directed go-around while on final approach due to another aircraft remaining on the runway. The aircraft returned to the airport and landed safely.
A Flight Instructor reported taking the runway with landing traffic and continuing takeoff resulted in a critical ground conflict and NMAC on climbout.
Ramp worker reported their lead released the brakes on the aircraft in preparation to push the aircraft and the aircraft rolled into the safety zone.

B777 flight crew and the flight Dispatcher reported airframe vibrations during climbout which worsened as the speed increased.  The crew contacted the company and decided to return to their departure airport.  Reportedly; the cause of the issue was a spoiler float was not locked down.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a passenger's laptop experienced a thermal runaway during cruise. The laptop was placed in a containment bag where the device was monitored until arrival and turned over to ground personnel.

Ground employee reported observing a near ground collision between a cargo tractor and an aircraft under tow-in procedure.  Reportedly; communication issues between a wing-walker and the tractor driver contributed to the event.
C172 flight crew reported the engine began running rough and oil pressure dropped to zero during cruise. Flight crew diverted safely to an alternate airport.
Ground employee reported an aircraft departed the with erroneous Hazmat cargo location; as well as incorrect weight and balance data.

EMB-145 Captain reported two EGPWS terrain alerts on a night takeoff from airport without operating control tower. The crew complied with the escape maneuver then contacted ATC and departed on course.
B737-800 First Officer reported Dispatch Release missing required Dangerous goods information.
Corporate pilot reported that while on approach to a Class C airport; they observed a UAS pass over the right wing of the aircraft. The aircraft landed safely.
Flight Instructor reported a critical ground conflict during takeoff with a non-reporting aircraft on runway at CPT non-towered airport.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to DTW in trail of an A350.

GA instructor pilot reported that while a student was on a solo training flight; the student encountered wind shear during the takeoff roll; resulting in the student losing control of the aircraft and a runway excursion. The student was unable to control the aircraft during the takeoff roll and damaged the aircraft during the runway excursion.

Air carrier flight crew reported the flight deck Electronic flight bags and Battery charging bricks pose a safety hazard in the cockpit. Reporter requested cockpit containment bag be added to flight deck equipment.
B777 First Officer reported the nose gear failed to retract after take off. The flight crew returned to the departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported failure to annotate Dispatch's copy of Final Flight Release and inform Dispatch prior to gate departure.

MD11 Relief Pilot and Dispatcher reported an engine compressor stall during takeoff.  The flight crew contacted Dispatch and Maintenance and decided to return to the departure airport after dumping fuel.
Dispatcher reported providing a clearance through an ozone forecasted area resulting in injuries to the flight attendants.

General aviation instructor reported a near miss with another aircraft after receiving an IFR clearance near a non-towered airport in visual conditions. The instructor maneuvered to avoid the other aircraft.
EMB-145 Captain reported a fumes event during cruise. Flight crew diverted to a suitable alternate for a safe landing.
GA aircraft flight crew reported during engine run-up prior to takeoff; the aircraft tail was lifted by nearby jetblast; resulting in propeller and wingtip strike on surface.
An Airbus 321 pilot reported experiencing severe engine/airframe vibrations during climb out; resulting in an inflight diversion.
PA28 pilot reported the engine failed during initial climb. Pilot made an immediate turn back to airport and due to landing long was unable to stop aircraft on runway; resulting in runway excursion.
C172 pilot reported electrical failure resulted in divert.
EMB-145 Captain reported after takeoff the pitch trim was stuck in the takeoff configuration. Flight crew used the backup pitch trim controls and returned to departure airport safely.

Cessna 172 Instructor reported excessive control inputs while landing during a training flight; resulting in a hard landing. The Instructor took control of the aircraft and performed a go around then returned for a landing.

MD-11 First Officer reported failure of both tail fuel pumps. Subsequently; the #2 engine showed zero fuel flow and began to roll back. The flight crew requested priority handling and performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
EMB-505 flight crew noticed vibration in cruise flight and reduced speed to minimize the rumbling. After landing it was noted that the left engine was missing half its cowling.
Luscombe 8A pilot reported losing directional control on landing that resulted in a runway excursion.

General aviation instructor reported electrical smoke in the cockpit after landing at an intermediate destination on a VFR cross country training flight. The instructor decided to takeoff and continue to the destination without electrical power and landed safely.
Air carrier flight crew flying 737-800 aircraft reported altitude deviation on approach.

B737 flight crew reported aircraft swaying and noise on landing as the aircraft rolled to a stop on a taxiway. The aircraft right main tires had failed and the aircraft remained on a taxiway where the passenger were deplaned by bus to the terminal with no injuries.
A Flight Instructor departing a non-towered airport reported they rotated early to avoid another aircraft taxiing across the runway.
B737 flight crew reported the aircraft's main gear rolled into the grass and got stuck after the Captain started the pushback.
Pilot reported landing on an icy; snowy crosswind runway at PABE resulted in momentary loss of control.  Pilot also reported airport would not open the runway most aligned for conditions.
First Officer reported a Go Around and precautionary  landing after landing gear lock problems.
Pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC even though they were in compliance with the published segment of the approach.
A319 First Officer reported they did not disarm a door after a ferry flight; resulting an inadvertent escape slide deployment when maintenance personnel opened the door.

CRJ-900 Captain reported receiving a GND Spoiler Caution message alongside a HYD 3 LO PRESS message in cruise. The decision to continue to the destination airport was made and the flight landed without incident.
A Center Controller reported the airspace chart at their sector had not been updated with a revised higher Minimum Enroute Altitude resulting in the reporter working an aircraft flying below the MEA.
Air carrier Captain and a Flight Attendant reported a Flight Attendant was still standing during takeoff roll due having to deal with a passenger not complying with face mask policy.
A Flight Instructor and Student reported encountering a wind gust or shear on short final over the runway causing them to land off the runway striking a runway light.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft losing its autopilot equipment and descending below the MVA.
Air carrier flight crew reported a bird strike/engine ingestion during takeoff roll. The flight aborted safely and returned to the gate.
Air carrier Captain reported descending below the final approach fix crossing altitude and receiving an advisory from ATC. The flight crew then returned to the correct altitude.

A320 Captain reported a fume event during initial climb which dissipated shortly after departure. The odor returned more intensely during descent. Flight crew completed the Flight Crew Operations Manual procedure which produced negative results.

Air taxi crew reported a runway incursion during daylight VMC when turning onto an incorrect parallel runway for taxi; when landing aircraft where still on the runway. The controller immediately directed the flight off the runway and the crew turned around and exited the runway; then received a new taxi clearance.
A Tower Controller reported an aircraft deviated from their assigned SID and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
EMB-175 flight crew reported near miss with a small General Aviation aircraft during descent.

CE-680 Captain reported the stabilizer became jammed after takeoff rotation. CAS messages indicated inoperative autopilot stabilizer trim and primary stabilizer trim failure. Flight crew attempted to use secondary trim but that also did not work; requiring both crew members to exert forward pressure on the control column and slow the aircraft to maintain control and divert to a suitable airport.
Flight Instructor reported while attempting to depart an non-towered airport they experienced a NMAC with another aircraft that was not on the CTAF frequency.
B737 Captain reported an oil pressure problem resulting in a return to departure airport.
Piper Aztec Pilot reported the nose landing gear collapsed during landing rollout.

Air Carrier Captain reported while in a 737 MAX Simulator; with an airspeed unreliable situation; the unreliable airspeed indicator with associated stick shaker warning is too loud and makes communication difficult.
Cirrus SR22 pilot reported they were unable to taxi off the runway when the right side main gear tire lost pressure on the landing roll.
A321 flight crew reported engine failure during takeoff roll. Flight crew rejected takeoff and returned to gate.

EMB-145 Captain reported a strong fuel odor during cruise which was assigned to Maintenance upon arrival. Maintenance discovered and corrected an APU fuel leak that was misting and entering the air conditioning system.

Air carrier flight crew reported descending on a visual approach to PSP Airport before being cleared to do so. Flight crew stated that due to the hazy conditions and angle of the sun they had difficulty visually finding the field. Flight crew added that issues with unclear ATC communications contributed to the event.
Air carrier Captain reported an oil leak or spill in the cargo bin during preflight. Ground personnel along with the Fire Department cleared the spill and the flight departed.

General aviation Instructor reported a near miss on the ground with another aircraft during takeoff on a training flight at a non-towered airport. The Instructor identified another aircraft taking off opposite direction without communication; took control and maneuvered off the runway to avoid a collision.
S56 TRACON Controller reported air carrier descended below MVA assigned altitude resulted in a CFTT event.

Air carrier flight crew reported a CFTT event during a visual approach to ROA airport.  Reportedly; ATC advised the crew of the terrain right before they received a GPWS warning.  The crew exercised an escape maneuver.

General aviation pilot reported receiving a response on Unicom frequency while inbound that indicated the tower at non-towered SGH airport was operational.  Reporter stated charts do not depict the tower or frequencies and it appears to be operational only during military drone activity.
B757 Captain reported loss of hydraulic pressure while on short final which caused a loss of nosewheel steering. Aircraft landed safely.
A320 Captain reported having to return to the gate as the dangerous goods were loaded and documented in an unaccepted format.
Corporate pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC when the aircraft descended too quickly on final approach. Flight crew returned to correct altitude and continued.
BE36TC flight instructor reported traffic on ADS-B was unknowingly conducting aerobatics resulted in taking evasive action and a NMAC.

B757 Captain reported loss of both packs caused the loss of cabin pressure. The flight crew requested priority handling and descended to 10;000 feet. The flight crew elected to divert and make a precautionary landing.

Air carrier Captain and ground personnel reported there was a back-and-forth conversation between Operations and Load Planning regarding the policy on securing DG. The flight crew was told to return to the gate to resolve the issue but ultimately was then told returning to the gate was not needed and the flight took off.
BE60 Pilot reported receiving an unsafe gear warning indication while on approach followed by a manual gear extension procedure and a landing in which the nose gear collapsed on roll out.
