Air Carrier CRJ-900 crew reported an 'EFIS COMP MON' warning during descent due to a degraded AHRS system.  Crew landed safely after their second hand flown approach to minimums.
Air carrier flight crew reported not setting parking brake after taxi into gate and the aircraft rolled back several feet.
Pilot and Local Controller reported a pilot misidentified aircraft to follow on final resulted in a NMAC.
CRJ-200 Flight Attendant the emergency exit rows were not working to pre-flight check due to the batteries being dead.
CRJ900 First Officer reported a low altitude alert from ATC while on final approach.

Air carrier flight crew reported pushing back from the departure gate without knowing that HAZMAT was onboard as notification was not received through ACARS. Acknowledgment of the dangerous goods onboard did not occur until the flight crew taxied out to the runway.
D10 TRACON Controller reported problems with aircraft being too low on the approach to DFW 17C/35C with possible glide slope issues.

Air carrier flight crew reported after push back they started the #1 engine. The aircraft started to drift to the right and the captain applied the brakes after they felt a jolt. The aircraft had struck a tug at the #1 engine.
Air Taxi flight crew reported a descent deviation while executing the WAYVE1 arrival into LAX; which resulted in a terrain alert being issued by Air Traffic Control.
Center Controller reported a common issue when aircraft departing SAF either misunderstood to fly present heading or not knowing how to read the departure chart.

Air carrier Captain reported a course deviation resulted from an ATC re-route and a crew misunderstanding of similar sounding navigation fixes in international airspace. The crew realized they were navigating to the incorrect fix; confirmed the fix with ATC; then proceeded to the proper fix.
Air Carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation and explained the PF had been on reserve and flown little in the last two months which may have contributed to the event.

Navion pilot reported landing on the runway gear up and was assisted by airport personnel in tugging the aircraft to the hangar. It was a hot day and the pilot reported not following the checklist procedures properly; and did not confirm the landing gear was down and locked.
BE30 flight crew reported landing safely after experiencing a frozen pitch trim issue.
B787 flight crew reported the Captain suddenly stopped the aircraft to avoid a baggage cart crossing their path.
Tower Controller reported an MSAW alert and loss of separation resulted from misunderstanding of the traffic situation.
PA34-200T pilot reported an NMAC event shortly after takeoff with an opposite direction landing Caravan aircraft. The PA-200 Pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Corporate Captain reported an altitude deviation that caused an airborne conflict (TA) and cites Controller/Pilot communication and the First Officer's (PF) lack of flying as contributing factors.

Air carrier flight crew reported while in cruise at FL400; the aircraft began to descend approximately 500 feet due to a downdraft/wind shift. They took control of the aircraft and climbed back to their assigned altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported the removal of a Hazmat container due to an odor emanating from the container prior to pushback. Captain further reported that he failed to submit a required logbook entry prior to departure.

Air carrier Captain reported a fume event during preflight described as wet socks. Logbook discrepancy and Odor form submitted to maintenance who MEL'ed the APU and re-dispatched flight which departed safely.
B767 First Officer reported high vibration on the right engine. The flight crew performed an air turn back and precautionary landing at departure airport.
Pilot flying PA28 aircraft reported NMAC with V35B aircraft in cruise.
CRJ200 First Officer reported receiving EGPWS warnings during approach. Flight crew continued to landing.

Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported not following SOP's and failed to turn the air conditioning packs on prior to takeoff. During climb the cabin was unpressurized; the high cabin altitude warning activated; the crew descended and reset the system; and continued to the destination.

B757-200 Captain reported an uncommanded steep turn of 45 degrees during departure climb. The Captain took control of the jet and returned to normal flight and followed by a crew debriefing the incident.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an aircraft terrain warning on final approach due to excessive descent rate. Flight crew corrected and continued approach to landing.
Flight Instructor reported on downwind entry a small aircraft knowingly overflew them resulted in a NMAC event.
A Tower Local Controller reported a departing VFR aircraft reported a NMAC with a preceding departure.
C-172 Instructor and Student pilot reported a runway excursion.
Regional Jet First Officer reported distractions from wake turbulence on arrival in trail of a B757 led to an unstabilized approach.
Air carrier Captain reported LED lighting at the Coyote Point Recreation Area underneath a 4 to 5 mile final to SFO Runway 28L '...destroys night vision...'
Citation pilot reported they were instructed to hold short of an intersecting runway but the pilot taxiing entered the runway as an aircraft taking off passed immediately in front of them.
Air carrier technician reported they replaced the number two engine IDG oil seals on a B737. They were informed the aircraft had to divert due to low oil pressure on the number two engine.
Air Carrier Captain reported lead-in lines need repainting. Captain followed ground marshals signals.
B767-400 flight crew reported hydraulic system failure during cruise. Flight crew used alternate procedure and landed uneventfully at destination.
Air Carrier pilot reported they had to unexpectedly stop taxiing on the ramp to avoid a truck driving in front of them.
Cessna 150 pilot reported a NMAC when climbing out on departure an overtaking aircraft which had reported them in sight passed 300 ft. over them.
ZZZ TRACON Controller reported observing Aircraft X off RNAV track and altitude while Pilot recovering dropped Electronic Flight Bag; resulted in an MVA violation.
C172 Flight instructor and student reported burning wire fumes in the cockpit in the traffic pattern. Flight Instructor landed immediately and aircraft was towed to hangar.
Helicopter Pilot reported a NMAC while transitioning through class delta airspace on a local transition route. The congested airspace and ATC workload were sited as contributing factors.
Air carrier Captain reported the Integrated Standby Flight Display (ISFD) had failed and the flight returned to the departure airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict during taxi with another aircraft taxiing at a high rate of speed crossing from their right. The Captain abruptly stopped to avoid a collision; causing one flight attendant to fall.

Tower Controller reported clearing an air carrier for takeoff but subsequently cancelled the takeoff clearance as the ASDE alarm alert had gone off. An unknown vehicle that was not in communication with the Tower had entered the runway.
A319 flight crew reported nose gear failed to retract which resulted in return to departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew was on a final for an ILS approach when they came within 50 ft. of a large sized UAS. Crew notified ATC and landed safely.
A Tower Controller reported they issued a vector to an aircraft that was below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A320 flight crew reported an 'electrical burning smell' during initial climb resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Pilot reported ATC cleared them for takeoff with arrival traffic on short final so they held short of the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an EICAS alert RALT 2 on approach to EWR and cited possible 5G interference as contributing.
B737 Captain reported the airspeed decayed unnoticed during climb resulting in a buffet alert. Flight crew increased thrust and descended to regain airspeed.

Air Carrier First Officer reported a pallet loaded with DG cargo which was not in compliance with the Air Carrier's restrictions was scheduled to be loaded. After conferring with the Captain the DG cargo was refused.

Flight instructor with student pilot reported having to take evasive action multiple times at MLU airport during touch and go training.  The reporter stated this is a common occurrence at this location.
SR20 Flight Instructor reported engine oil system problems while in flight. Engine problems became severe; forcing the flight crew to make an off-airport landing.
A321 pilot reported a Slats Fault message during arrival.  The crew continued to destination airport for an uneventful landing.
Air Carrier flight crew reported IT issues when loading Dangerous Goods Form Data during preflight. After multiple attempts crew removed Dangerous Goods to avoid further delays.
Air Carrier Pilot Crew reported a critical ground conflict during taxi.
First Officer reported that while performing a post flight walk around; a slat retraction arm was discovered protruding from a slat retraction panel.
Civilian reported there was a lack of aircraft warning lights on a local radio tower and stated it could be a potential safety hazard for aircraft; especially for operations at FUL Airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported they descended below the glidepath crossing restrictions on an RNAV approach.
Flight crew reported NMAC with another aircraft while on approach.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was required to remove a mesh face mask; while the Gate Agent did not agree; and use a company supplied mask before departure.
Military helicopter pilot reported a NMAC with a tour operation helicopter during cruise. The military pilot took evasive action to avoid a collision.
Air carrier ground personnel reported a passenger bag containing lithium batteries ignited during bag loading. The bag was removed and secured.

Tower CIC; the CIC in TRACON and the tower Local Controller reported an air carrier was cleared for takeoff with another aircraft on short final. The Local Controller was unsure how to resolve the conflict so the Tower CIC took over their position.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a low altitude from ATC after descent below glideslope. Flight crew executed a go around and diverted due to weather considerations.
Gulfstream G100 Captain reported once the aircraft leveled off at FL400 the right windshield shattered. The flight crew requested priority handling and diverted to make a precautionary landing.
Light aircraft Instructor Pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC in the vicinity of TRI airport. Temporary loss of communication with ATC was a factor.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC during a visual approach. Flight crew leveled off and continued to landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported when cleared across runway; an aircraft overflew them landing on the wrong runway assigned; resulted in a runway incursion.
ZJX Controller reported being overloaded by traffic and weather.
Air carrier pilot crew reported the failure of both FMS flight computers during cruise. Crew continued to destination airport and landed.
Flight Crew flying Hawker 4000 aircraft reported loss of multiple electronics and avionics failures inflight.
Pilot reported fuel starvation caused engine power loss during climb; resulting in a return to the departure airport and a precautionary landing.
Pilot reported elevator control issues and an altitude deviation.
Pilot reported that during a flight to another airport for maintenance; the aircraft had an engine lose power and was shut down; and communications were lost with ATC.
Gulfstream Captain departing a non towered airport reported they entered the runway to depart and observed another aircraft taking off on the opposite direction runway which rotated before them.
Multi-person UAS flight crew reported operating two aircraft at a towered airport. The UAS crew reported misunderstanding ATC instructions and took off without being cleared.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a landing gear malfunction after takeoff resulting in a return to the departure airport.
Light jet Captain reported the Garmin 5000 failed to execute the charted turn at the required rate during initial climb on the Mecca1 SID departing TRM.

Flight crew reported a TAT probe malfunction led to the loss of numerous flight instruments and both EECs on both engines. The flight crew performed an air turn back and landing at the departure airport.
Mitsubishi pilot reported an NMAC with a VFR aircraft while on approach in instrument conditions.
Air carrier First Officer reported they had numbers for a full runway takeoff; but were given and executed an intersection takeoff.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to comply with instructions for her two children to use headphones and caused a disruption during the safety briefing.
HS 125 Series 700 Captain reported an altitude excursion probably caused by a wake vortex encounter.
SLC Controller reported aircraft below MVA due to a local traffic procedure and aircraft conflict.
B737-700 Captain reported a momentary lost of flight path guidance while conducting an ILS approach at the same time a flight was departing from the approach runway.

C172 instructor pilot reported taking evasive action from a medium sized UAS with a transponder which ATC observed. Reporter mentioned appeared to be a military aircraft because no cockpit windscreen was visible as it passed overhead.
Flight Attendant reported concerns relating to health and oxygen levels in the body due to constantly wearing a face mask while working.
Air carrier flight crew reported they began taxiing out without clearance. Captain stated lack of flying was a contributing factor.
RV9A pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from a B737 while transiting a MSP arrival area.
Pilot reported a taxiway incursion and cited being off for a month and a half as a contributing factor.
CRJ200 flight crew reported feeling pressured to accept an aircraft for service that they felt was not in compliance with the applicable MEL.

ARTCC Controller reported accepting a handoff on a military aircraft. The flight plan information stated the aircraft would enter the Controller's airspace; but upon reaching a fix the aircraft turned to a different direction from what the flight plan showed. The aircraft never entered the Controller's airspace. The Controller coordinated with the appropriate sectors.
Piper pilot was told to call Approach Control after being vectored away from a TFR and into the Class Bravo.
RV7A Single Pilot reported a new tower controller failed to provide departure aircraft alert thereby resulting in an NMAC event.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a track deviation after missing a transition waypoint during an ILS arrival.
B737 Captain reported receiving disagreement while requesting removal of tankering fuel due to a flight into a performance-limited airport.
Pilot reported a snow plow cutting off aircraft while taxiing to gate.
Learjet 45 Captain reported experiencing 'violent' wake turbulence on the PUFFR4 RNAV Arrival into APA in trail of a B737.

An air carrier Captain reported being notified that another air carrier also on approach to SFO would be landing on a parallel runway. The crew then received a TCAS RA from a small aircraft that was below them.
HCF TRACON controllers reported that a Special Operation aircraft was allowed to fly at an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

BUR Tower Controller describes an operation where a departure was slow to get airborne causing an aircraft on short final for a crossing runway to go-around. Now; the conflict is worsened by having no missed approach procedures to separate the aircraft.
A pilot and Center Controller reported a communication problem with an altitude clearance and descended below the minimum IFR altitude.
Two Maintenance Technicians reported that replacing a rudder on a 737 became a very difficult task due to inadequate equipment.
Air carrier Captain reported landing without a landing clearance.
BUR Tower Controller reports of inadequate procedures for missed approach/go-around aircraft and the conflictions related to aircraft going missed approach/go-around.
Air carrier flight crew reported mistakes in flight release documentation relating to the number of jumpseaters on board.
A319 flight crew reported the aircraft captured a false localizer on approach to MMMX.
Two Maintenance Technicians reported an inadvertent escape slide deployment while replacing a girt bar floor fitting.

Although they were originally cleared 'as filed' and; thus; via the BRDJE RNAV STAR to DFW the flight crew of an A320 was cleared '...direct to SEEVR; descend via' the SEEVR arrival. Aware that SEEVR was a fix on their filed route AND on the BRDJE STAR they mistakenly assumed the FMGC was properly programmed and planned to cross SEEVR between FL230 and FL200. As they approached SEEVR ATC advised they looked to be too high and; only then; did they realize that--although sub-titled (SEEVR.BRDJE1)--their latest clearance was for the SEEVR RNAV STAR--sub-titled (SEEVR.SEEVR1)--and they were to cross SEEVR between 16000 FT and 13000 FT.
Ground Agent reported an inadvertent pressurization of the cabin on the ground when the door was closed while conducting door training with the APU running.
Air carrier flight attendants reported having to care for a passenger who had several incapacitating medical problems; but was allowed to travel alone.
A320 pilot reported both the Captain and First Officer side sticks had gust locks installed with no notation in the logbook. Reporter stated this was unusual and easily overlooked.
CMI Tower Controller reported an aircraft that could not see due to snow on window landed on a closed runway with out incident.
Flight Attendant reported cleaners came on board before passengers were off and started spraying the aircraft with a reportedly toxic product.
Technician reported that he declined to endorse a logbook entry that he felt it was falsified to get the aircraft to depart on time.
Aircraft Technicians reported that proper documentation was not done following slat repair.
Individual reported ATL TRACON/Tower has been allegedly clearing Heavy aircraft to fly too low over Douglasville; GA which is located 25 miles from the airport.
Captain reported conflicting company policy and FAR duty-day limitations.

A TRACON Arrival Controller reported a Supervisor disregarded the Sector Controller's wishes and approved a route for an aircraft that caused the controllers to have to move other aircraft out of the way.
ZJX Center Controller reported they worked combined sectors of traffic deviating for weather consistently exceeding the designated limits for the sectors with not enough staff to assist them.
GA pilot reported owner of airport did not re-chart airport as private.
C-150 pilot reported loss of aircraft control on landing resulting in a hard landing and causing the nose gear to collapse.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported being pressured by his Supervisor not to write-up discrepancies found during aircraft inspection.
A Pilot on a night visual to SDF was issued a low altitude alert by Tower after descending rapidly from a high turning; descending base but intercepted the final at 900 feet AGL.

ABQ TRACON Supervisor reported observing a Controller vector an aircraft away from conflicting traffic. The vector was insufficient to maintain separation and placed the aircraft in an area where it was below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Burbank Tower Controller reported that an aircraft departed Burbank and became an airborne conflict with a Van Nuys departure on the same routing.
B767 Pilot reported the the FMC Database was missing the approaches to their international destination.
ZNY Controller reports of an NMAC due to poor coordination of altitudes between two aircraft and possibly a slow climbing aircraft.
A Controller reported being told by their supervisor and Traffic Management Unit that they could approve an altitude below the Minimum IFR Altitude for a military aircraft conducting training.

ZAU Controller reports of another sector taking an aircraft off a specific route and questions why the Controller would do that. The aircraft has to be sequenced in a flow to handoff to another center with certain miles in trail.
Pilot inquired how ATC expects them navigate to the airport when vectored to a north downwind for a visual approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation and cited fatigue and lack of flying as contributing factors.
Air carrier pilot reports of two instances where they were instructed to maintain a speed higher than 250 knots while below 10;000 feet.
TRACON Controller reported assuming a position to act as CIC because in reporter's mind the Supervisor CIC was not properly monitoring.
EMB-145 pilot reported a loss of spacing on final approach that resulted in a go-around.
ZAU Center Controller reported the Radar Controller did not follow proper procedures to ensure required non radar separation criteria were met for the receiving facility.
SCT Controller describes a loss of separation between a HHR arrival and a LGB departure.
A320 flight crew reported a cell phone fire in the cabin led to a diversion and landing.
CRJ-200 Captain reported being notified by Ramp Agent that a gate-checked bag was transported with a Lithium Ion battery attached.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported returning to their departure airport after receiving an EICAS message 'SMOKE AFT LAV' and smelling fumes in the cabin.
High Performance Aircraft Single pilot reported receiving confusing instructions from the Controller and believes COVID-19 ATC staffing issues were a factor.
A300 Technician reported he was tasked with deferring the high pressure valve for the Number 1 engine. The wrong valve was locked out.
B737 Captain reported a near midair collision while on final approach; and going around on the second approach; both due to lack of traffic separation from ATC.
B757 flight crew reported they were not supplied with the SKBO engine failure missed approach chart.
Technician reported he was asked to back date an EA after it was determined that the aircraft had flown for five weeks with an open EA.

Pilot questioned the procedure of automatically cancelling system annunciator lights from known causes (such as the 'ELEC' and 'HYD' lights resulting from single engine taxi) without confirmation. Pilot suggests that such a procedure can foster a bad habit pattern.
EMB-550 flight crew reported severe turbulence during initial climb resulting in a momentary loss of aircraft control while attempting to level off.

A TUL Tracon Controller reported of a helicopter that was on an approach being followed by a jet. The helicopter elected to break off the approach because of the close proximity. The Controller wanted the facility to brief all controllers on the appropriate procedures for having IFR aircraft vectored for the visual approach behind VFR aircraft.
CRP Local Controller reported observing an aircraft going to an adjacent airport fly into his airspace on a conflicting heading with an aircraft conducting an approach.

B737-700 Captain reported both his and the FO's iPad were in need of a charge; but the charging cords supplied by the company were inoperative. Reporter stated the iPads are the primary source of charts and that the charging cords are not tracked as maintenance equipment.
Washington Center [ZDC] Controller reported of a problem associated with notification of airspace hot times and altitudes.
B777 Captain reported a continuous issue with the crew rest area.
Pipistrel Sinus Pilot reported failure to release brake level at takeoff resulted in runway excursion with loss of control.
Technician reported installing an incorrect tire and wheel assembly on an in service aircraft.
MD-11 flight crew reported Dangerous Goods container leaking fluid during preflight. Dangerous Goods removed from flight.
Captain reported numerous Hazmat and FOM procedure violations specifically pallet loading and providing flight crew required documents.
B757 Captain reported clearance heading deviation after changes were made on the CDU on approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported conflicting traffic during their takeoff roll that was cleared to land by tower on cross runway. Flight crew executed a rejected takeoff.
A Pilot reported their Foreflight displayed a TFR when there was no TFR in effect and that this is a known problem related to FAA ADSB signals.
Air carrier pilot reported concerns with the periodic jamming of GPS signals by the military in the Southwest United States and its effect on flight safety.
Lear 45 Captain reported receiving instructions from ATC to go around at the same time as another aircraft; resulting in a NMAC.
After landing on DWH Runway 17R then clearing at Taxiway E; a Pilot held short of Runway 17L but was informed by Tower that he may have incurred the Runway at the hotspot. The pilot disagreed.

A fire fighting aircraft departing a Southern California airport is given a heading to fly on climbout that conflicts with terrain. ATC scolds them after they deviate; while trying to advise ATC of the terrain conflict.
B737 flight crew reported an uneventful resolution to an airborne conflict due to a communications breakdown between flight crew and ATC during approach.
Air carrier Captain reported disagreement with current policy of the timing of door closure.
A319 Captain reported a ground gate return due to improperly secure Hazmat cargo in cargo compartment.
Turboprop pilot reported an unexpected terrain warning on approach to BHM just prior to the MDA.
A321 pilot reported while operating with two fuel system MEL items they developed a third problem enroute. Flight crew elected to divert.

A ZMA Controller reported of an airborne conflict between an aircraft under ATC Control and an unknown aircraft. The unknown aircraft was found to be a high speed aircraft that was being worked under a special memorandum. The Controller wanted high speed aircraft to work under a LOA that might stop this problem.

CE680 First Officer reported departing DAL on the Kingdom departure from Runway 13L and heading 190 as instructed in the initial climb box. ATC advises to fly runway heading; that the 190 heading is for props only and that note 3 at the top of the departure chart instructs all jet aircraft to contact Clearance Delivery for departure instructions.
A319 flight crew reported airport broadcast incorrect Instrument Landing System identifier.
Aero Commander pilot reported requesting priority landing due to low fuel condition; and was unable to go-around when a Cessna was slow to turn final approach.
Air carrier First Officer could not locate the Obstacle Takeoff Notes for 3 departure procedures from SEA.
C172 pilot reported entering Class B airspace without a clearance.

A Cessna jet pilot reported flying through the newly established parachute drop zone at WVI following a windshear encounter. Reporter recommended the drop zone should be moved to a different location in the airfield.
A Flight Instructor reported an alternator failure during an IFR training flight. The flight diverted and completed an uneventful landing.
Ramp Supervisor reported the requirement of 15 bags surrounding HAZMAT shipment creates a hazardous situation during loading/unloading of cargo.

A B737 on the SJC SILCN1 RNAV appeared high on profile to NCT but the crew explained late ATC clearance to 'join the 30L Localizer' prevents the FMS from calculating distances correctly. The FMC calculates the altitude to KLIDE; back to GILRO and the return to KLIDE and causes a high approach. Crew managed the error manually.
A321 Captain reported accepting a reroute just before takeoff that resulted in a marginal fuel situation at destination.
Corporate pilot reported ATC cancelled the approach clearance without any further instructions so the pilot cancelled IFR; but was then advised should not have descended.
B777-200 flight crew reported an altitude deviation during cruise due to fatigue.
Pilot reported that a multi engine aircraft closed on and descended towards him; requiring evasive action to avoid a midair collision.
C90 TRACON Controller forgot to turn an aircraft onto the final approach course. The aircraft proceeded into confliction with parallel runway traffic.
Pilot and Controller reported an airborne conflict on departure.
A321 pilot reported that the transponder was deferred due to a missing digit.
ZNY Center Controllers reported about a problem with a restricted area due to traffic in and out of it that caused airborne conflicts with multiple aircraft.
King Air C-90 Pilot reported an NMAC event during a NOTAM'ed sky diving operations area.
SCT TRACON Controller and a CE-560XL flight crew reported a communication breakdown resulting in the aircraft descending below the MVA.
JCF TRACON Controller reported an aircraft was directed to make a 360 degree turn by the adjacent facility; but the turn was not sufficient to maintain separation from the adjacent traffic.

An air carrier Captain described a go-around while flying the EWR Runway 29 Stadium Visual in gusty conditions. He misunderstood the EWR Tower's go-around heading while attempting to climb and maintain aircraft control.
Pilots report of rejecting a takeoff due to ATC cancelling takeoff clearance.
Air taxi pilot reported that a VFR aircraft which had them in sight flew too close to them.

Kansas City Center [ZKC] Controller reported of a loss of separation due to weather; workload; traffic deviating; and having to come up with deviation routes on the fly.  Reporter wanted to accept the aircraft instead of taking a point out but did not and a loss of altitude separation ensued.
PAO Tower Controller reported problems associated with the close proximity of Moffett Tower airspace.
Medium transport flight crew reported that a special company EAT departure procedure conflicts with ATC expectations.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.
Pilot was concerned why a site was temporarily closed for cleaning and what caused the closure.
A ZDV Controller reports of issuing a clearance to an aircraft in another controller's airspace. The Controller did not know position of aircraft before issuing the clearance.

During a busy arrival period a Tower Controller approved an aircraft for a low approach which resulted in a traffic conflict with a second aircraft transiting through the airspace.  During the same time period the controller issued instructions to one aircraft that were meant for another as well as cleared an air carrier for takeoff into the crossing route of a VFR helicopter.
NCT TRACON Departure Controller had to amend flight plan routes in the computer for aircraft after a runway change but thought that should be a Tower Controller responsibility.
An air carrier pilot reported the EMZOH3 STAR to OAK published speed is above the speed limit for being underneath the SFO Class B airspace.

GEG Tower Controller working all Tower positions combined instructed an aircraft to hold its position. The Controller meant to issue the instruction to a taxiing aircraft; but mistakenly issued it to an aircraft beginning its takeoff roll.
A321 Captain reported maintenance discrepancies are signed off without proper maintenance action.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported that a GPWS warning activated during an RNAV approach.
Dispatcher reported improper turnover of flights during shift.
B777 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence at 100' AGL departing LAX in trail of another wide body aircraft.
B737-800 Captain reported the runway condition report at this location was too old to use. Asked Clearance for update and received a reply that an update needs to come from the city.

An ISP Controller reports of the ISP radar being released to Maintenance by the New York District office against the Controller's request. The Controller describes the airspace as chaotic and dangerous without the use of the ISP radar system.
First Officer reported failure to complete FMS setup following three approach clearance changes.
GA pilot reported uncontrollable climb from turbulent updraft; resulted in airspace deviation.
Company Safety Manager reported Dangerous Goods had not been loaded in accordance with Dangerous Goods Regulations.
Upon descending to exit moderate to severe turbulence; the flight crew of a B747 was met by a hydraulic system one failure. They continued to descend to an efficient altitude and landed uneventfully.
Tower Controller reported problems with TRACON turning aircraft into each other causing conflicts.
An Air Carrier pilot reported ATC failed to advise them of traffic 400 to 500 feet below them and they responded to an RA for the traffic.
ZKC Center Controller reported their sector had a loss of separation between two aircraft during a period their sector was overloaded due to weather deviations.
An Air Carrier Captain noted an ORF Runway 5 glideslope deflection near 700 ft at about the point which the ILS 5 approach chart advised 'autopilot coupled approaches are not auth. below 744 ft.'
Flight Instructor reported student attempted to exit the runway too fast resulting in a runway excursion.

BUR Tower Controller reports of a go-around and aircraft following it that need to be sequenced due to the go-around. BUR does not have a DVA so aircraft are turned over to Approach Control for sequencing to the airport. This causes airspace violations and losses of separation.

A Boeing 737 crew reported deteriorating weather conditions while on final approach. Unable to acquire the runway at approach minimums the crew elected to perform a missed approach and successfully diverted to a suitable alternate airport.
General aviation pilot reported a newly installed solar panel left of SQL Runway 30 at mid-field that caused a blinding reflection during landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported flying a missed approach while attempting the SAN RNAV Y Runway 27 due to weather.
ZNY Controller reported recurring unsafe events within an oceanic sector.
Luscombe pilot reported reactive maneuvering while landing with skis resulted in a damaged landing gear leg and ground strike.

Technician completing a routine check on stored aircraft reported finding torn speed tape in the Pack 1 and 2 inlets and exhaust. Reporter could not visually verify if the tape had entered the system due to the duct bends.
EMB-175 Captain reported excessive number of ATC route modifications and instructions that resulted in a high workload and task saturation.

Air Traffic Control Manger reported Flight Service Station is disseminating the wrong information regarding squawk codes used to enter / exit a practice area. Wrong codes could create a possible pilot deviation.
Phoenix TRACON Controller reported a departure that was coordinated; pointed out; and then had a conflict due to the other facility not knowing what to do.
Ramp employee reported receiving conflicting information as to when to go back to work after being exposed to COVID-19.
Air carrier First Officer pointed out the ambiguity of approach procedures when combining RNAV approach with ILS at RNO.
Air carrier cargo handler questioned the safety aspects of a new company procedure that requires that the cargo belt-loader not be placed inside the cargo pit.
Ramp Agent reported an accident between a catering van and a tug pulling a cart.
An air carrier on the SFO 28R localizer took evasive action and executed a go around after another air carrier on the 28L localizer drifted 400 feet over them with no ATC intervention.

ZNY Controller reported of an unsafe situation when a fellow Controller climbed an aircraft to avoid a loss of separation. The other Controller climbed the aircraft in what the reporter thought was too short of a time period.
A321 Captain reported Dispatch notification of Hazmat cargo on board during cruise. Flight crew not provided with required documents or NOTOC at departure airport.
PCT TRACON Controller reported assigning a heading to an aircraft that caused it to enter prohibited airspace.
EMB-175 Captain reported the taxi diagram in ORD can be misleading and suggested a HOTSPOT should be added to the airport chart.

An Air Carrier pilot reported after successfully landing a CATIII approach the crew discovered the runway centerline lights were NOTAM'd out of service at the time they began their approach but began working by short final.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported rejecting DG cargo assigned to passenger flight with labeled for 'cargo only aircraft'.
Air carrier ground personnel reported HAZMAT shipment missing required documentation for transport after aircraft departure.
C750 Captain reported severe weather resulted in several missed approaches and a diversion.
SCT Controller reports of a loss of separation he watches happen; tries to make others aware but the aircraft are in a frequency change.
Air carrier First Officer reported taxiing out to the runway and finding items were missed during their before taxi flow checklist.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an incorrect passenger count at the gate.
EMB-175 Captain reported severe turbulence encounter resulting in momentary loss of control.

Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer did not want to wear a mask in the cockpit. The Captain decided due to concerns about exposure to COVID-19; to take the only option given of dropping the trip.

During an IMC climb on a test flight following autopilot maintenance; a PA-46 pilot discovered he had no airspeed; vertical speed and an incorrect altimeter. He returned to the departure airport where the static plumbing was found disconnected inside the cabin.
Dispatcher reported the flight was re-routed because ATC was closed due to COVID-19.
Minneapolis Center Controller reported that Denver Center handed off two aircraft on a collision course.

JAN FLM reported of a problem with the facilities Airport Surveillance Radar (ASR) generator. When turned on during bad weather it has a 50% probability of working. Tech Ops is aware of the problem; but is unable to fix it. FLM is concerned something bad might happen related to the ASR being out of service.
B737-700 Captain reported Flight Attendant notification of hot burning plastic in passenger cabin during passenger boarding.
The A330 crew departed without their final weight and balance.
TRACON Controller working combined sectors with weather deviations observed an aircraft entering their airspace without a handoff conflicting their arrivals.
Bonanza 36 pilot reported confusion with ATC while conducting and approach to a Towered airport with the intention of breaking off when in VMC to proceed to a nearby private airstrip.
The pilot of a Cessna 172 reported a near collision while flying under foggles.

A Tower Controller reported that an aircraft was cleared to depart towards an arrival conducting an opposite direction circling approach to the same runway. The TRACON Controller was not sure if facility opposite direction orders apply to circling approaches.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy and became belligerent with the Flight Attendant in Charge.

SCT Controller reported that there was confusion whether an aircraft that went on a missed approach was on an IFR code. Controller did not know if the crew were IFR or VFR and asked. Pilot stated VFR on the missed. More confusion ensued when the pilot then said they wanted to cancel IFR and go VFR to another airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion due to an ambiguous taxi clearance and poor airport signage and charting at DTW.
ZJX Controller reported a shift working combined sectors due to lack of staffing during periods of weather deviations that created an unsafe workload.
A-300 First Officer reported  departure cargo load ground agent failed to properly secure Dangerous Goods container prior to departure.
A Trainee Center Controller reported allowing two aircraft to enter an adjacent sector without a handoff. The Trainee recommended new procedures for the arrival flow through the sector.

ZAN Controller reports of issuing an altitude he thought would be safe for an aircraft. Moments later the MSAW alarm went off and the Front Line Manager (FLM) came over to investigate. The Controller then checked the approach plate and discovered that he was wrong and attempted to contact the pilot to have him climb. Contact was not made. The pilot later called and asked if he was supposed to be higher and the Controller then climbed the aircraft and cleared it for the approach.

A MD-11 crew suffered smoke inhalation during preflight and required medical treatment after exposure to Type 1 deice fluid vapor which was sprayed into the number two engine while the APU was running.

Pilot reports of flying VFR to WHP under flight following with SCT. SCT Controller advises pilot that Controller will help him all the way to WHP; then vectors the aircraft around VNY due to traffic. Pilot continues descent and then Controller advises pilot that aircraft entered the VNY class D airspace. Pilot was under the assumption that he had approval to descend into Class D as long as he told ATC about it and AATC would take care of the point out to VNY Tower.
ZLC Center Controller could not vector an aircraft away from conflicting VFR traffic.
A VFR aircraft maneuvering around clouds observed an airliner descending towards them also deviating for weather. The VFR pilot thought he violated VFR rules maneuvering to avoid the airliner.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported Hazmat container found 'fallen over' due to inadequately securing at departure point.
A B777 Maintenance Technician reported that procedures were not followed when the rudder was lubricated.
A319 flight crew reported that they had an unaccounted passenger on board.
Ramp personnel reported concern regarding unnecessary delays regarding Hazmat (Biologicals/Blood) cargo from Coronavirus Testing. This cargo has a limited risk of spillage.
B737 NG First Officer reported weather issues at destination resulted in them notifying ATC of a low fuel emergency and landing with minimum fuel.
B737NG flight crew reported that while flying the RNAV RNP Z approach to 35R at DEN the separation to the traffic ahead decreased to 2.5 miles; but ATC seemed unconcerned when advised.

B737 First Officer reported receiving a PDC clearance to climb via the SID EXCEPT maintain 10;000 feet. When Departure was contacted after takeoff the instructions were 'climb via SID.' The reporter asked for clarification as he was not sure if the climb limit was the 10000 feet in the PDC or the FL230 on the RNAV chart.
B737 flight crew reported receiving a new release with a weight increase after takeoff.
A IAH Ramp Agent reported that his company is slow to clear ramp personnel to safety with lightning in the vicinity even when it is close; over head and within the airport area.
Captain reported a passenger was non-compliant about wearing a mask.
Airline Ramp Agent reported checked bag label contained lithium ion batteries. Batteries removed prior to departure.
B737 First Officer reported hazardous liquid spill in cargo pit from passenger checked bag.
Dispatcher reported a communications breakdown between Dispatcher and Dispatch Supervisor regarding the legality & interpretation of a takeoff alternate distance regulation.
B737 Captain reported that a sole Controller was working all positions; from taxi to departure at MSY.
SCT Controller reported difficulty guiding descents into SNA that meet the revised MVA limits.
B737-800 flight crew reported an encounter with severe mountain wave that resulted in large altitude and airspeed excursions.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B777 on approach to ATL that resulted in rapid left and right rolls.
A321 Captain reported disagreement in procedures between MEL and PRC when attempting a manual air start on engine.
C172 flight instructor reported the pilot flying the aircraft landed long and fast resulting in a excursion from the runway end and damage to the aircraft.
An Embraer 145 Flight Crew reported that they received a 'Terrain-Terrain Pull up; Pull up' warning during climb while following ATC instructions.

A Large Transport flight crew held twice inbound to their destination. When finally cleared for the approach they went missed at 160 feet because of the crosswind and the aircraft feeling unstable. They proceed to their alternate; declared minimum fuel; and landed without incident.
Air carrier flight crew on approach to ORD had difficulty picking up the runway lights after breaking out of the clouds due to the sporting arena sign west of the airport.
Vehicle Driver reported possible collision with aircraft.
Maintenance personnel reported that the incorrect circuit breakers were pulled when deferring a Vacuum Waste Blower.
B777 Captain reported the cabin crew was fatigued due to a mechanical delay plus an early schedule call; however; scheduling did not understand the fatigue situation.
Ground employee reported Dry Ice was erroneously loaded in forward cargo pit.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence 3 miles in trail of a B757 shortly after takeoff from DEN.
Air carrier Captain reported a speed deviation and flap overspeed on final.
Air carrier flight crew reported a conflict with another aircraft while taxiing at DFW.

An ERJ-190 Captain describes the event surrounding his charter flight being illegally dispatched to an airport with NIL braking and freezing drizzle; then diverting to a third airport because the alternate was not suitable.
B737NG Captain reported being dispatched with less fuel than calculated.

The Captain of a cargo aircraft and the Dispatcher responsible for the flight; reported a deviation of company policy regarding communicating an additional fuel upload to dispatch in a timely fashion.
GV flight crew reported that the aircraft's wing hit a hanger while taxiing.
An AS-350 pilot described a helicopter dolly rotating on an icy ramp during engine start while preparing to fly off the dolly.
Dispatcher reported a continual shortage of dispatching staff and possible violation of FAR 14 CFR 121.395.
Ground personnel reported ammunition marked as mail arrived on an aircraft.
A Center Controller reported working an aircraft deviating for weather allowed the aircraft to enter an adjacent sector airspace without a point out or handoff.
ORD Local Controller reported issuing an aircraft a go-around was then issued nonstandard go-around instructions by the Departure Controller.
C172 pilot reported communication issues with remote JYO Tower Controller and traffic conflicts while flying in the VFR pattern.
A CE-188 conducting AG spray operations near a Midwest airport had a near miss after a helicopter pilot approaching the airport did not make CTAF position reports.
Flight Attendant reported safety issue related to duties versus crew seat assignments for arming/disarming doors.
Air carrier Captain reported lighting strike during climb; resulting in internal and external damage.
HIO Controller reports of an airborne conflict between two helicopters and the lack of coordination required by Tower SOP to enter or use another position's airspace.

DAB TRACON Controller reported doing an automated point out to another sector. The other sector took radar on the aircraft and the DAB Controller did not recognize the handoff and assumed the point out was good.
AA1A pilot reported experiencing an uncommanded 45 degree bank on short final from prop wash associated with a taxiing turboprop aircraft.
B737 First Officer reported fuel leak #2 engine; requiring maintenance to inspect; subsequently aircraft departed with no log book entry being made and tools left attached to engine.
SLC Tower Controller reported an aircraft went around twice due to windshear and also reported that the way the Tower cab positions are split up are not flexible and not safe.

A small aircraft pilot towing a banner had a near miss with a parasail. His evasive actions prevented a collision. He suggests that parasail operators need more training and that parasail operations should be regulated.

Air taxi flight crew reported being informed by their company after the event that they violated noise standards during arrival to OAK. The crew was not aware of the procedures and was following published arrival procedures and ATC vectors until cleared for the visual approach.
ROA TRACON Controller reported airborne conflict between two aircraft; after being distracted by a third aircraft that was VFR in IMC.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to ATL 3 miles in trail of an A321.
Commercial Airline Mechanic reported shipping aircraft parts package without completing Hazmat shipping procedures for Company Materials.
Air Carrier First Officer reported missing an altitude constraint during arrival and cited workload; fatigue and lack of flight currency as contributing factors.
A320 flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert on arrival into SAN after descending to ATC cleared altitude.
Cessna 172 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from an opposite direction B787 in SEA airspace.
B757 Captain reported weather related ramp conditions delayed passenger deplaning for an hour.  Captain stated company operations never answered calls for support.
A pilot who had flight plan approval to operate in a special use airspace reported having their flight plan cancelled by ATC after stopping on the ground for a short period of time.

CE560 Captain departing Runway 30 at SUA experiences lost comm with PBI Departure on 132.8; with the crew able to hear Departure but Departure could not hear the crew. No problems were experienced on Tower frequency prior to or ZMA after the event. The exact same scenario repeated three days later.
Two Ramp Personnel described a bag room waste water leak from overhead pipes; that they did not believe was adequately handled by the airport authorities.
A Beach Bonanza pilot reported that he lost situational awareness when he entered a cloud.
A gate agent reported that due to wet conditions the agent's hands slipped and the loading bridge started to fall towards the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported GPS equipment failure alerts. While evaluating the alerts with the flight crew and operations personnel; a NOTAM detailing GPS outages for the area was discovered.

Corporate First Officer reported descending prior to a crossing restriction during arrival. First Officer referenced being distracted talking about COVID-19 related logistical issues that may have contributed to the event.
B737 Flight Crew reported that after landing a passenger commented that the flight departed with snow on the wings.
UAV pilot reported a TFR incursion.
Dispatcher reported being overwhelmed with aircraft due to staffing shortages and computer system difficulties.
Dispatcher reported Operations delayed an aircraft without contacting Dispatch.
Pilot reported attempting an RNAV Approach using their Electronic Flight Book data for navigation but were off course so ATC vectored them to a Contact Approach.
P80 Controller reported an aircraft departing HIO; on initial contact; was in conflict with non-participating VFR traffic. The Controller reported this as a frequent issue.
Honolulu Center Controller reported that HCF's (Honolulu Control Facility) Traffic Management Unit does not always alert or inform the controllers about flow changes at HCF.
Caravan 208B pilot reported flaps inoperative after landing; caused by flap switch guards in the wrong position after maintenance to replace the O2 control cable.
Air Carrier pilot reported the airline schedulers routinely schedule pilots from night flights to morning flights which causes pilot fatigue.
Pilot and co-pilot report of confusion on departure of flying the RNAV departure or flying to a fix.  ATC corrects them and advises that they aren't the first ones to do that.
B757 First Officer reported the Captain used a crash axe to open a cabinet.

CE680 pilot reported they parked in an area designated for commercial aircraft because it was not properly marked and had to be towed to the correct area. The tug driver told the pilot to release brakes and the aircraft rolled backwards prior to being attached to the tug.
SCT Approach control cleared flight crew for approach to LAX Runway 25L. Then advised that clearance was actually to Runway 24L and reissued the approach clearance.
A320 Captain reported an unstabilized approach resulted from a higher than normal approach in an effort to avoid wake turbulence.
PA-28 Cherokee Pilot reported an NMAC during landing with an aircraft taking off from a perpendicular runway.
Flight crew reported requesting relief from Center; after receiving a late descent clearance. Approach controller later queried about the previously issued clearance.
B737 First Officer reported exiting the runway a bit further down than the Tower Controller was planning on.
Flight Instructor encountered possible skydiver activity.

BUF TRACON Controller reports of a communication problem that he is associating with the radios at the facility. Transmissions are made and not heard at times; making the controller repeat them. Controller describes a situation where there was an airborne conflict and he had to repeat transmissions and is not sure if there was a loss of separation due to the radios.
Captain reported fatigue and confusion resulted in a delay due to misapplication of an MEL.

Dispatcher reported using an illegal airport as an alternate. The Dispatcher reported another operations center was closed for COVID-19 sanitizing. This had increased the workload for the Dispatcher and contributed to the event.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude overshoot due to a light aircraft.
Controller reported that someone in the facility had tested positive for COVID-19. Controller was concerned about the decontamination process.
C172 flight instructor reported ATC issued taxi instruction that created a ground conflict.
Air carrier Captain reported a ground conflict with non-yielding vehicles during taxi-out at DEN airport.
Technicians reported aircraft engine found damaged during shipping.
An air carrier pilot reported ATC advised them they were 5 miles south of course. The pilot verified they had the correct route programmed and their instruments showed them on course.

Air carrier Captain reported that an ill passenger boarded and was not wearing a face mask. Captain decided to have passenger removed from flight due to length of the flight and passenger's condition.
Air taxi flight crew reported landing at an airport and being advised that the runway was closed.

EMB-190 Captain reports an injury to a Flight Attendant when moderate turbulence is encounter at 10;000 feet. The Flight Attendants had been warned by the Captain but the B Flight Attendant had been unable to secure his cart before the turbulence was encountered and it caused his injuries.
Air carrier First Officer reported the company flight planning system failed to provide sufficient routing instructions from point of non-redispatch to an intermediate dispatch airport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported that the use of multiple departure frequencies at  MDW airport resulted in an increased workload during a critical phase of flight.
Pilot reported a critical ground conflict while taking runway for departure with aircraft on final approach.
A Maintenance Technician reported that when he removed a pitot cover on a B767-300; a rubber piece was left in the end of pitot tube.
Air Carrier flight crew reported flying uncomfortably close to terrain on a night visual approach to Runway 15 at BUR; and suggested that the procedure be restricted to daytime operations.
PA-28-201 pilot reported after the aircraft was painted there were problems with the gear hydraulic pump. This landing ended with the left gear retracting during roll out.
CE560 Captain reported confusion and high workload resulted from an ATC clearance to a point that was not part of the ILS in their FMS.
CRJ-900 Captain noted MFD Low Fuel Reserve indication in cruise and discovered that the Alternate Fuel had not been included in the fuel load. Flight diverted for refueling.
B787 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to EGLL 3 miles in trail of a B747.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non-compliant with face mask policy.
Air carrier First Officer reported altitude deviation on a departure and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
PA-11 pilot reported aircraft ground strike on takeoff which damaged the aircraft.
A319 flight crew reported a wake turbulence encounter at FL370 from an opposite direction B747 that was 1000 feet above them.
Pilot reported having to divert due to weather at intended destination; then having to stay on the ground for over 2 hours at diversion airport due to weather at the intended destination airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported missing a speed restriction clearance possibly due to the Controller wearing a face mask.
Captain reported a missing or expired NOTAM concerning an Arrival Procedure into San Juan airport.
Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach resulting in a go-around.
ATC Center Supervisor reported arrivals routinely experience laser events but no action has been taken to resolve the problem.
P50 TRACON Controller reported receiving a jet departure on a conflicting route overtaking a turboprop departure from the Tower which was permitted according to current facility directives.

Fractional turbojet aircraft flight crew reported LIEO Tower issued a non-standard clearance of line up and wait after landing traffic. Flight crew misunderstood and lined up on runway causing landing traffic to go-around.
A319 Captain reported First Officer flying into turbulence resulting in an injured Flight Attendant. First Officer was unaware of option to deviate clear of cloud
PA32 pilot reported an NMAC with a Cessna 172 during simultaneous practice approaches to FDK which does not have access to radar service.
Technician reported not following procedures to replace a cable due to fatigue and confusion.
A321 Captain reported a cargo door warning light illuminated on climb out; causing an air turn back and precautionary landing.

Atlanta Center Controller and pilot not flying the aircraft reported of a turn that ATC did not expect. ATC questioned the pilot's course and then pilot explained. Separation may have been lost with another aircraft that was on a parallel course.
A320 Captain reported a Traffic Alert on TCAS while climbing out of SLC.
Air Carrier flight crew reported confusion and miscommunication with ATC due to a similar callsign situation.
Business jet flight crew reported difficulty catching altitude restriction departing HOU on the PDC print out.
Air carrier First Officer reported tolerance errors in procedures for fueling and checking correct fuel loads on Company aircraft.
EC135 Technician reported making a mistake during the write-up and sign-off of records and releasing an unairworthy aircraft due to fatigue.
Airbus Captain reported encountering a mountain wave during a climb from FL290 which oversped the aircraft during an uncontrollable climb to FL330.

B747 flight crew and Load Planner reported the Dispatch software system crashed which prevented the final weight and balance numbers to be delivered prior to takeoff. During cruise; the flight crew was notified their max taxi weight was exceeded and max takeoff weight of 875;000 LBS was exceeded at 876;686 LBS.
Turboprop flight crew reported an issue with conflicting traffic during arrival to MYF.
Citabria pilot reported a wind gust resulting in a ground loop during landing rollout.
A Ramp Agent reported some newly purchased ground tugs have faulty driver seat bushings which have failed. A driver may fall backward off a failed seat and into a towed cart's path.
CRJ-900 Captain reported that due questionable radio reliability the aircraft was not accepted and refused.
Technicians reported misreading an ECO; resulting in non compliance with established procedures.

A flight crew cleared on the SFO SERFR ONE RNAV with multiple set down altitudes managed in Flight Level Change. When cleared to 3;100 feet; the MCP was set but the flight descended below the MENLO 4;000 foot constraint resulting in an airborne conflict with lower crossing traffic.
GSP TRACON Controller reported an aircraft deviated from its assigned course.
Approach Controller reported they vectored a departing aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported they unintentionally missed the revised route segment given in their PDC.
B737 Captain reported receiving a TA at FL340 while on an ATC vector in the vicinity of EWC.
B737 First Officer reported overshooting altitude and climb speed on departure due to FMC programming mistakes.
A320 Captain reported a communications breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch regarding the substance of Hazmat on board. Ultimately Hazmat was removed prior to departure.
Airline Ramp Loader reported correcting erroneous Hazmat documents and providing corrected Hazmat data to flight crew.
Falcon 2000 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into MIA 17 miles in trail of an A330.
Air Carrier flight crew descended below their cleared altitude. The crew cited distraction and the FO's lack of flying as contributing factors.
Air carrier flight crew reported equipment problems and having to change destinations due to COVID-19 restrictions.
EMB-175 Captain reported a momentary loss of aircraft control during an encounter with severe turbulence on descent into SDF.

EMB-175 flight crew reported that the MCDU (Multipurpose Control Display Unit) provided inaccurate fuel calculation which was far more than FOM (Figure of Merit); causing landing with less than minimum fuel.
Flight crew reported flying Standard Instrument Departure (SID) from ABQ were issued vectors due to proximity of terrain.

While giving instruction to a Developmental; ZJX Controller did not list in on a phone call and relied on the Developmental to tell him what the conversation was about. The activation of restricted airspace was what the conversation was about. The airspace was considered hot by the military but the Developmental thought they would call back to activate the airspace. Aircraft were in the now hot area and the controllers had not coordinated with other sectors in the Center.
Flight Attendant reported the misconduct of a group of passengers during the flight regarding compliance with face mask policy.
Pilot reported a traffic conflict on an instrument approach that resulted in a missed approach.

An ERJ175 flight crew reported Maintenance MEL'ed both FMS's found inoperative during preflight. Following the day's flight the crew questioned the MEL and believed 1 FMS was required for flight even though the database was out of data.
Air taxi pilot reported being issued a low altitude alert while on a Visual Approach with a slightly above glideslope indication.
Pilot reported a truck drove directly in front of his aircraft and required him to brake suddenly.
CL30 Captain reported confusion over the wording of a SID.
NCT TRACON Controller reported a go-around was vectored below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Center Controller reported descending an aircraft they handed off to Approach to an altitude contrary to the procedure in the Letter of Agreement and discovered that the Approach Control's altitude minimum is higher than the Center's minimum altitude in that airspace.
A pilot on short final to ORD Runway 27R reported distraction caused by a bright electronic sign in the sporting arena parking.
Air carrier flight Crew reported a track deviation due to confusion with the departure clearance delivery format.

Air carrier Captain cited distractions that occurred on vectors to a visual approach to Runway 31 at LGA as contributing to a failure to run the before landing checklist and to arm the thrust reversers. Landing rollout was uneventful.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported their flight tracking tool displaying an airborne aircraft's position was off by over a thousand miles from their correct location.
B737-700 Captain reported flight departure without amended Dispatch release stating Hazmat cargo on board.

Center Developmental Controller reported a Tower went ATC Limited and the confusion that ensued with determining how to handle the situation. Controller cited lack of planning; explanation and preparation as contributing factors.

Technician reported a proximity switch target that was improperly installed during the heavy check process; caused a #1 System Prox Fault.

E10 Front Line Manager; Controller; and pilot report of not seeing the correct airport and lining up for another airport incorrectly. Tower Controller gets the aircraft heading towards the correct airport.
C172 instructor pilot reported an NMAC while enroute to PWT.
A Flight Crew reported receiving a GPWS warning during a Visual Approach but continued since they had the terrain in sight and were stable.
Flight attendants on an international flight reported their company forced them to remain working beyond their legal duty time due to an extended ground delay.
Air carrier Captain reported a taxi chart error at SUX airport.

TYS Controller reported of a loss of separation with a small aircraft behind a heavy. Controller was charged with a 'Wake Remnant' separation error. Controller would like a wake remnant indicator to be able to be used by controllers.

C172 pilot reported NMAC in congested airport traffic pattern during practice crosswind landings.

Center Controller reported disagreement with Operations Manager when Controller put in aircraft flow coming into the sector reporter was working.

Oakland Oceanic Controller reported an airborne conflict when an aircraft descended to a non turbulent altitude through the protected airspace of another aircraft.

Aircraft landing at SMF delayed runway exit at taxiway. Following arrival noticed the aircraft still partially on the runway and initiated a go-around. The Tower Controller had failed to turn on the taxiway lights after sunset causing the first aircrafts confusion and delay exiting the runway.

SCT Controller describes a release off of an airport in his airspace that needed to be coordinated with another Tower. Controller coordinates and gets the aircraft airborne. Other Tower Supervisor has an issue with the way the aircraft was released and why their other traffic waited on the ground for this aircraft.
MD-10 flight crew reported misinterpreting fuel quantity on release.
A B737NG Captain noted [an] often reported L-GPS FAIL alert by crews on approach to MMMX between the MAVEK and PLAZA waypoints.
B737 NG flight crew was cleared for a LAS 'RNAV Visual 19R Approach'. The approach was selected in the FMC but not found in the iPad database.
CRJ-900 pilot reported of a final altitude of 8;000 feet for the flight from the New York area to Georgia.
First Officer reported continuing to a landing after receiving a wind shear warning.
PDX Tower Controllers reported an arriving airliner responded to a RA and executed a go around due to VFR traffic in the pattern at nearby VUO.

A pilot began taxiing at SEA on the new BLUE line closest to the terminal. An ORANGE line aircraft's ground crew; slightly behind and outboard of the BLUE line; headed to the terminal and ran in front of the lead taxiing aircraft.
After receiving a direct clearance to PEVEE while flying the LAX HOLTZ9 SID; a slight track deviation resulted as the waypoint was inadvertently deleted while intending to execute direct PEVEE.
EMB-175 Captain reported that a text conversation with Dispatch resulted in a high workload during a diversion.
Ramp employee reported passenger carry-on baggage that is gate checked to cargo due to lack of space in over-head bins; is frequently not checked for hazmat items.

Air carrier Captain reported phone calls every 10 minutes and people knocking on the doors in the hotel resulted in a fatigue call for the crew.
Air Carrier Captain reported a taxiway incursion and stated Taxiway T2 which was included in their clearance is not charted.
