Air carrier Captain reported not recognizing until cruise that the transponder was set incorrectly. Captain recommended that crews visually confirm checklist items since many pilots are flying infrequently due to COVID-19 reductions in travel demands.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to comply with the face mask policy during flight.

B737 Captain reported that one of the Company Tailored Jeppesen charts for Runway 31L at PSP erroneously indicates that there are VASI lights to the left of the runway which may lead to Taxiway C being mistaken as a runway.
B737 flight crew reported they could not comply with MEL procedure to verify leading edge flap position in flight because they could not see the device from the cockpit.
A319 pilot reported that they did not hold short of Taxiway F while taxiing to Runway 25R at LAX.

An air carrier flight crew reported an EGPWS aural OBSTACLE alert on final to DAL Runway 31L during visual approach with the ILS glideslope out of service. The alert ceased immediately after initiating the terrain clearance maneuver.
Air Carrier Captain reported a GPWS Terrain alert during a visual approach to KDRO.
PA-28 pilot reported NMAC during cruise with aircraft who was possibly on incorrect VFR altitude.
SCT Departure Controller reported a departing aircraft was in immediate conflict and received a TCAS RA with VFR aircraft transitioning the airspace not in communication with ATC.
A pilot reported that the navigation database confused the VOR identifier MGM with a heliport known by the same name.
Eurocopter AS350 pilot reported the aircraft entered Inadvertent Instrument Meteorological Conditions (IIMC).

Embraer 145 Captain reported confusion from an altitude clearance that did not conform to the published approach and clearance; which then resulted in a course deviation. The Captain disconnected the autopilot and continued the approach manually.
B757 First Officer reported refusing flight due to lack of clarity with MEL and QRH regarding fuel leak checklists.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported experiencing faulty GPS signal to navigation displays. ATC was informed and provided heading clearances.
RNO Tower Controller in Charge; CIC; reported due to sun glare blocking controller's view of final; traffic landed on wrong runway resulted in traffic executing a go around.
Flight Crew reported erroneous Airspeed Indicator readings due to icing.
Flight crew reported taxiing their A321 into the gate with no Marshal or Agent to operate the jet bridge. Ground personnel began fueling the aircraft while the doors were still closed.
Air Carrier Captain reported that extreme winds at LAS created unsafe landing conditions. The flight diverted to a nearby airport with more safe conditions and landed normally.

Air carrier Captain is dispatched on a transpacific flight with the APU deferred; which it had been for four days. Upon arrival at the gate; ground power is connected and the engines are shut down. Ground power quickly fails and cannot be reestablished leaving the aircraft on battery power alone. After five minutes the battery is turned off leaving the aircraft in darkness.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported loss of aircraft control during taxi due to NIL Braking action.

The TRACON Controller observed an unidentified VFR aircraft depart an airport on a converging course with an air carrier on a visual approach to an adjacent airport already in communication with the Tower. The TRACON Controller pointed the traffic out to the Tower Local Controller. The air carrier responded to a TCAS RA.
PC12 pilot reported near landing touchdown an opposite direction aircraft starting its take off roll.
Pilot reported the flight was not accepted by TPA Approach Control due to staffing/workload issues. The Captain circumnavigated the area and proceeded VFR.

Captain reports that deferral of the Engine AC Generator Bearing Annunciator light with a 20-flight hour continued operation should not be allowed in the MEL for Cessna CE-560XLs. Primary Bearing failure of the Right Engine AC Generator after only three hours operation since deferral caused an electrical fire that burnt and scorched the nacelle; AC Generator; and wires during taxi-out. Pilot suspects the engine fire bottles would not have been sufficient to extinguish that type of fire in flight.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a flap over-speed due to a rapid climb rate; resulting from a light passenger load.
Aircraft Technician working on EMB-145LR was not provided correct safety equipment for fuel tank entry.

A distracted and overworked MD-80 Mechanic forgot to close the nose gear door following a maintenance procedure the previous evening. After takeoff the next morning a severe hydraulic system shudder during gear retraction caused the flight crew to lower the gear and return to the departure airport.
A B767 FMC SEEVR TWO RNAV lists the NECKK altitude constraint below FL280 while the Jeppesen chart NECKK constraint is between FL280 - FL240.
An air carrier aircraft encountered un-forecasted severe turbulence and mountain wave activity over the Andes Mountains in South Central Argentina.
Air carrier Captain reported a Flight Attendant advised that a passenger was non-compliant with face mask policy.
Tower Controller and small aircraft pilot reported a runway incursion resulting in an aircraft having to go-around.

An Epic pilot reported refiling prior to departure to avoid severe weather over Mississippi. XM radar is used to navigate around the southern end of a line of thunderstorms; through weather that appeared as green and light green on the XM image. ZHU agreed that the intended route was good showing no severe weather. The weather turned out to be anything but benign with two separate periods of severe turbulence with lightning. The autopilot remained engaged throughout the incident and the reporter continued to his destination.

Controller had a data tag of an aircraft being worked by the adjacent Controller displayed on his radar in his sector airspace. No verbal coordination had been accomplished. Normally verbal coordination in addition to the data tag display is required in this situation. The controllers agreed for this time period that verbal coordination would not be necessary. The reporter did not notice the displayed data tag in his sector in a timely manner and had to turn one of his aircraft away to achieve separation.
Mechanic reported being unknowingly exposed to a COVID-19 positive employee and not being notified of the exposure by company management.
Ground employee raised concerns with the health effects of people wearing a COVID-19 type mask for long durations of time.
An Air Carrier pilot reported encountering unforecasted moderate to severe turbulence over the Rocky Mountains resulting in minor injuries to Flight Attendants.
An Instructor pilot reported a NMAC with a powered parachute.
The First Officer of a B737-800 reported that both EEC's and both PFD's went into ALT mode and airspeed deviation in yellow.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported ground gate return of aircraft due to required Hazmat documentation errors. Captain refused to transport the Hazmat item.
Air carrier flight crew reported landing without a clearance.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported some shortcomings with their software tool.

Air carrier Load Planner and Ramp Supervisor reported Hazmat cargo not configured correctly for air transport. Hazmat documents were updated to indicate corrective action completed but the cargo configuration was not corrected.
Air carrier flight crew reported taking off without a clearance and stated that being distracted by the COVID-19 pandemic and the state of the airline contributed to the event.
B767 Captain reported possibly completing MEL process paperwork incorrectly across different platforms.
Dispatcher reported that an aircraft was fueled with fuel transferred from another aircraft; resulting in a policy violation.
A319 flight crew reported that ATC commanded the flight to go missed.
Air Carrier Agent reported DG articles found in passenger baggage by TSA screening.
A321 Flight Attendant reported she wanted to reseat a passenger who was using a cane from the emergency exit row; but was overruled by customer service.
A320 flight crew encountered a bird strike after takeoff.
P-210 Pilot reported an engine out landing due to improper operation of a fuel boost pump.

A twin engine heavy jet flight crew departed RNO 16L after discussions about the max power takeoff and the aircraft's performance. After takeoff the hill off the end of 16L unexpectedly activated the radio altimeter with an EGPWS alert at 500 feet climbing.
Tower Controller reported an IFR clearance was issued by Departure Control to an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A300 Captain reported the aircraft moved after engine shutdown with nose chock already inserted. No damage or injuries were reported.
B767 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence at FL320 in trail of a B777 departing EGLL that resulted in injuries to a Flight Attendant.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy.
First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to BET in trail of a B737 that resulted in violent uncontrolled rolls.
ORD Tower Controller reported an aircraft conducting a go-around flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude because they did not hear their go-around instructions the first time they were issued.
DEN Tower Controller attempted to use a new procedure that allowed a helicopter and arriving aircraft to get too close causing the helicopter to take evasive action.
EMB-145LR Captain reported an uneventful landing after receiving and complying with a 'Terrain' EGPWS alert during approach for landing.
EMB-175 First Officer reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence seven miles in trail of a B747 on a visual approach to LAX.
B737 Captain reported ATC was uncooperative when he requested more spacing to avoid wake turbulence on approach to LAX.
A Regional Carrier Pilot reported they did not know their destination had an operating Control Tower until checking in on the UNICOM frequency.
Air Carrier First Officer pilot reported that after resting and getting ready for an early morning flight; scheduling called and pushed back the show time past the maximum duty day.
Local Controller reported during receiving communications transfer an airborne conflict resulted in a NMAC.
B737-700 Flight Attendant reported a security issue with inaccurate passenger count procedure.
Technician reported not having enough air stairs and having to wait to accomplish a task.
A heavy McDonnell Douglas First Officer reports a windshear warning at 80 knots during takeoff and the Captain rejects the takeoff at 95 knots. After brake cooling the next attempt is successful.
Air carrier Captain reported exceeding 250 knots below 10;000 feet during descent. Captain cited fatigue and lack of flying as contributing factors.
Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict between an IFR arrival and a VFR aircraft while working combined Local positions.
Tower Front Line Manager reported a runway incursion and concerns with the Local Controller not issuing a go-around instruction to an aircraft landing on the same runway.
BE58 pilot reported an NMAC with NORDO pattern traffic.
ZHU Controller reported I90 TRACON transferred communications in I90 airspace; on aircraft that were in conflict with I90 traffic; requiring ZHU to take action to avoid serious conflict.
Air carrier First Officer reported negative training due to maintenance issues and malfunctions with the fixed-base training device used for transition training to a new aircraft.
GA pilot reported being unable to contact ATC due to possible Controller under staffing resulting in a Class C airspace violation.
Center Controller reported an airspace violation and cited having to take approach control's airspace as a contributing factor.

A Tower Controller reported that his trainee did not use correct phraseology to apply visual separation with successive departures. The second departure lost sight of the first departure and the pilot declared a NMAC.
B757 Captain reported Hazmat DG Manifest was incorrectly separated from Hazmat shipment.
Ramp worker reported that a tailstand was not available to support the plane while passengers were deplaned from the front of the aircraft.

C-182 Flight Instructor reported a sudden NMAC event during a training flight in which the offending aircraft approached from behind maneuvered to avoid a collision. Instructor provided useful ADS-B information.
A flight crew on a visual approach to SAN Runway 27 received an ATC LOW ALTITUDE alert while descending through a thin cloud layer prior to clearly seeing the VASI.
A Tower Local controller reported they cleared three aircraft to taxi across a runway which they had cleared an aircraft to depart from.

B787 First Officer reported a faulty enroute oceanic wind update affected the FMC which resulted in an insufficient fuel alert; a cruise flap disagree and an LNAV/VNAV disconnect. A flight plan reinstall with manual winds corrected the fault.
A Center Controller reported they cleared a corporate jet aircraft for an approach to an airport that only authorized Air Taxi operations.
B737 First Officer reported observing the incorrect arming of evacuation slides and that this common mistake has not been addressed in training.
Air Carrier Load Planner reported informing Ramp Lead of Hazmat configuration requirement for securing Hazmat shipment.
PAO Controller observes people not authorized near a runway on a taxiway. Controller stops operation until a airport vehicle removes the people.

PDX Tower Controller and an air carrier pilot reported a go-around event at PDX due to a TCAS/RA triggered by a light aircraft in the pattern at an adjacent airport underneath the instrument final approach course.
Local Controller reported that bad phraseology that is becoming too common at the facility. Controllers are not telling the aircraft to go-around; but are telling them to start your go.
Pilot reported a runway incursion at JAC airport and cited snow obscuring the hold short line and the position of the Holding Position sign contributed to the event.
Air Carrier flight crew reported that a Cessna aircraft landing on Runway 17 overflew their aircraft at a dangerously low altitude as they were holding short of Runway 13 at HOU.
A Maintenance Technician reported that the flight crew inaccurately wrote-up the crew oxygen required servicing after it was signed-off as being within limits.

SCT TRACON Controller reported losing the data tag; secondary beacon and primary target on an aircraft on downwind. Pilot reported transponder failure when questioned; Controller wondered why all contact was lost and an assumption that it had to do with STARS Radar was made.

An EMB-145's filed destination closed because of weather and a microburst alert so the crew planned a diversion but were advised of a ground stop. As the crew prepared for a diversion; their destination airport opened and the crew continued on.
Aeronca 7AC pilot reported a gust of wind caused a loss of directional control on the ground; resulting in some damage to the aircraft.
Captain reported a TA and RA caused him to disconnect the autopilot and take evasive action to avoid a NMAC.
Crossing altitude restriction charted at later intersection was assigned to earlier intersection; which flight crew mistook and descended late.

A CRJ-700 Captain rejected that takeoff for the TAKEOFF CONFIG alert because of a miss set trim. The First Officer complained the new Takeoff Checklist forces heads down; task saturation operation before takeoff.
B737-800 Captain reported being pressured by company personnel to lower minimum dispatch fuel request.
Air carrier pilot questions the classification of glufosinate ammonium that was loaded as cargo on the flight; as non-hazmat.
Flight Attendant reported that a communication breakdown resulted in dry ice being brought onboard the aircraft without proper documentation.

A300 flight crew reported returning to land after the FWD CARGO DOOR NOT CLOSED ECAM alerted. The alert was a repeat discrepancy. While on the ground; maintenance informed the crew the previous YELLOW SYS JAM DETECTION servo deferral was signed off in error.

ZMP Controller reported of ZDV giving him a situation where three aircraft will be a conflict within thirty miles of entering his airspace. Controller tried to get Denver to fix the problem; but instead was given control of the three aircraft to fix it himself.

SCT TRACON Controller reported that LAX Tower gave him a loss of separation on a departure due to running the second departure too close to the first departure. Controller had to turn first departure southwest bound and second departure was too close.
Lear 35 First Officer reported a wing tip damage during a post flight inspection after diverting due to high wind gusts.
Citabria pilot reported a gust of wind during landing caused the aircraft ground loop.
Flight Attendant reported the majority of passengers were not complying with face mask policy.
Helicopter pilot reported that he flew over another helicopter that was air taxiing after a parallel approach; but was on another frequency.
NCT Controller disagreed with their Supervisor about which facility should retain communications with aircraft conducting a special operation.
B737-700 flight crew reported delaying rotation on takeoff at LGA to increase separation from another aircraft executing a go-around on a crossing runway.
ZDC Controller reported during a period of weather deviations lack of Traffic Management flow initiatives caused an unsafe workload.

A Controller receives a call from an aircraft that isn't in his airspace that needs an IFR clearance due to weather. Aircraft is found and sent to correct frequency which was in another Center's airspace.

A Control Tower departed a jet behind a slower moving VFR prop aircraft on the same route without taking action to ensure separation. The Tower issued traffic to the jet and switched it to Departure Control frequency.
Air carrier pilot reported last minute cargo upload which included Hazmat items with no flight crew notification.
S46 TRACON Controller reported multiple incidents where VFR aircraft have caused airborne conflicts in the BFI area.
Pilot reported flying an aircraft with an MEL deferral that had expired its allowed 500 hours.
Air carrier Captain reported having an aircraft that was fueled incorrectly for a ferry flight.
Air carrier First Officer and D10 TRACON Controller reported that a clearance to descend was given; but due to a congested radio frequency the instruction was misunderstood.
Flight Attendant reported being called out by a passenger when the mask covering the Flight Attendant slipped and revealed part of the nose.
A ZMA Controller descended one aircraft and climbed another aircraft head on. The Controller issued turns to the aircraft but had less than required lateral separation.
Customer Service Representative reported COVID-19 issues relating to agents in the terminal at check in.

Air carrier flight crew reported observing the Runway Status Light system turn red near the end of the takeoff roll from LAS Runway 25R; possibly due to an aircraft being cleared to line up and wait behind them.
Miami Center Controller reported of problems associated with Restricted Areas being hot or cold.
Small aircraft instructor reported ATC informed of Tower LOA traffic pattern altitude which is not published or available on pilot resources.
C172 reported his passenger alerted him to an aircraft landing on a runway he was preparing to taxi across.
ZLA Center Controller reported an airborne conflict between enroute aircraft and aircraft in restricted airspace.
Air carrier First Officer reported being high on arrival due to ATC being overwhelmed working several frequencies.
Maintenance Technician reported unknowingly failing to accomplish a required engine windmill inspection.
ALO Controller reported being unable to hand off an aircraft due to an erroneous beacon code between separate HOST computers.

SFO Local Controller reported that certain aircraft which taxi low to the ground have difficulty receiving transmissions in a Runway 10L dead zone along portions of Taxiways S and S2. Antenna location seems to be the problem.
Embraer jet Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to JFK with possible sources identified as a B767 or an A380 landing . Captain regained control and executed a go-around.
B747 Captain reported that an aircraft was operated in RVSM airspace without a required component.
ATC Center Controller reported improper coordination by an adjacent Center Controller; due to an apparent misunderstanding of standard procedures.
Flight Attendant reported a family; part of a traveling group; were not compliant with face mask policy.

A C-208 jump plane pilot reported advising ATC two minutes prior to jumpers exiting and was told of one aircraft headed away from the jump zone. After jumpers departed; the pilot detected an aircraft over the jump zone which ATC had not advised him about.
B767 Captain reported refusing Hazmat cargo due to a communications breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch; Load Planning regarding erroneousness Dangerous Goods Hazmat documents.
EMB-145 flight crew reported that confusion caused by an outdated taxiway map resulted in a taxiway incursion.
First Officer reported that some crew oxygen shut off valves are not restored properly after servicing and how to diagnose such a situation.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported aircraft ground return to correct Hazmat cargo incorrectly configured for air transport.

The Local Controller did not notice an aircraft entering downwind was in close proximity to another aircraft already on downwind. Due to workload the Controller had requested an assist position to help them but the request was denied.
Corporate pilot reported having an altitude deviation while hand-flying the aircraft and attributed it to lack of flying due to the COVID-19 slow down.
Maintenance Technician reported during maintenance preflight a tow crew member went to the cockpit and turned off the battery power without authorization.
Maintenance Technician reported failing to secure the floor boards and bulkhead panel after completing a functional check.
Air carrier flight crew reported deviating off of cleared route due to ATC change through CPDLC that was not recognized by the crew.

Certified Professional Controller; CPC; at Denver TRACON (Pueblo Sector); reported a recurring automation failure between D01 (PUB Sector); and Springs departure; COS.  Automation handoffs received; do not transfer necessary aircraft data to provide required service. Further interfacility automated handoffs to Denver ARTCC; ZDV; do not display and transfer correct data.
Airbus Captain reported experiencing GPS interference during cruise.

A dispatcher was concerned that the increased workload resulting from the surprise assignment of additional flights to/from stations with which she had not previously worked; was excessive and compromised her ability to provide proper flight planning and support.
Ground personnel reported removal of package which indicated corrosive markings and returned to sender.

Flight attendant reported passenger sneezed directly into Flight Attendant's face without attempting to cover the sneeze. Flight Attendant suggested passengers should be given hygiene tips during pre-boarding announcements.
B757 Captain reported the current use of the Electronic Logbook (ELB) makes it difficult to look back at the aircraft's history; therefore the current process compromises safety.
A300 First Officer reported a thump; followed by aircraft pitching up on short final; but corrected and landed without issues.
A B737 Captain noted some new first officers are making a verbal call 'Speed Checks' before moving the flap hand during approach. The procedure is non standard and distracting.
Air carrier Captain reported that during recurring ground training individuals did not always wear a mask to protect from COVID-19 exposure.
B737 Captain reported a potential terrain issue with a recent ATC clearance at MROC airport.
A320 Captain reported while entering a high workload environment an ACARS message came requesting additional flying.
Aviation Maintenance Technician reported concerns about the company imposing a corporate culture on maintenance personnel that puts an emphasis on business goals and may be contrary to safety.

GA pilot reported experiencing an airborne conflict during departure having to take evasive action.  Reporter cited an increase in traffic due to the lifting of stay at home restrictions as a contributing factor.
Air carrier Captain reported that charts do not show the correct location of gates.
B737 Captain reported that the autopilot had difficulty maintaining a stable approach due to turbulence.
B787 flight crew reported an uneventful landing after experiencing an airborne conflict with a UAV during final approach.
Pilot reported flying without proper registration.
Flight Attendant reported witnessing the aircraft's cleaning crew did not follow Covid-19 procedures and only sanitized the galley counter tops.
ZMP Center Controller reported an aircraft had not been handed off to the adjacent facility due to new symbology in Center radar data tags.
Air carrier First Officer reported HAZMAT container improperly loaded. Error corrected on third request.
Light sport aircraft pilot reported a loss of engine power led to an off-field landing; likely caused by carburetor icing.
Flight Attendant reported that upon arrival at destination; a COVID-19 positive passenger on board as well as the crew; had to be medically assessed before being allowed to leave the aircraft.
APA Airport Controller reported watching an aircraft depart; then observed the aircraft bank to the left; then correct to level wings. Aircrafts right wing apparently hit the ground when correcting.
Flight Instructor reported swerving off the runway onto grass while taking off to avoid a skydiver.
GA pilot reported an altitude excursion and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.

B757 Captain accepted the duty time extension available under FAR 117. After the flight; the pilot felt in hindsight that by accepting the extension; crew scheduling had filled their coverage issue; and they were no longer concerned about possible fatigue issues.
B777 pilot reported deviating from SID heading due to traffic; moments after ATC gave conflicting traffic evasive clearance.
PA-12 Instructor Pilot reported his student lost directional control on the landing roll resulting in a runway excursion.

Air carrier Captain reported attempting to cancel a trip due to safety concerns from a forecast of hazardous air quality related to wildfire smoke and being penalized for not wanting to conduct the flight.
Maintenance Control Technician reported numerous errors when completing the required paperwork for an ETOPS ferry flight with numerous flight legs; resulting in time limited items expiring in flight.
Flight Attendant reported a non-revenue passenger was not wearing a face mask and had to be told to comply with the policy.
B777 First Officer reported communication difficulties with Amazonica Center led to a traffic conflict at FL380.
ZFW Center Controller working combined sectors reported an airborne conflict and cited workload and staffing issues as contributing factors.

Flight Attendant reported refusing to be in the cockpit to provide break relief because the First Officer would not wear a face mask. The Flight Attendant reported that the Captain asked for other flight attendants who would be willing to provide break relief under those conditions.
B737 flight crew experienced a GPS outage during the VOR DME RWY 05L/R approach at MMMX. An overshoot at PLAZA resulted but the crew was able to land visually.

A U.S. air carrier departed MMGL Runway 28 and while climbing through 7;000 feet responded to a TCAS RA from a domestic Mexican airline aircraft speaking Spanish with ATC after initiating a go-around. The event resulted in a NMAC with no prior warning from ATC.
GA flight crew reported a loss of aircraft control resulting in a runway excursion.
GA pilot reported that most commercial operators flying to MYEF use the wrong frequency for traffic advisories on UNICOM versus the correct AFIS frequency.
CRJ900 Captain reports landing Runway 18R at CLT in moderate rain and an 8 knot tailwind and questions the wisdom of not turning the airport around to land to the south.
Student Glider Pilot encountered a Near Mid-Air Collision with tow aircraft.
B777 Captain reported that the CDU indicated different altitude restriction than the arrival chart; resulting in a crossing restriction error.
E170 flight crew reported that a possible wake turbulence encounter resulted in an unstabilized approach and a go around.
A Flight Instructor and their student reported they flew too close to lighted tower when the student mistook a lit street for the airport.
B737 Captain reported landing after an unstable approach due to thunderstorms and wind shear.
Two air carrier Flight Attendants reported an encounter with severe turbulence marked by a lack of communication from the flight deck.
ORL Tower Controller reported an NMAC between a helicopter departing a hospital and traffic in the pattern.
An air carrier Dispatcher reported that important NOTAM information regarding a runway closer was not published in the company's flight planning software.
King Air Captain reported encountering severe turbulence prior to landing and then departing again prior to completion of a severe turbulence inspection.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported that on an ILS approach they nearly lost their course.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger that had a valve on their mask was asked to replace the mask or cover the valve.
FWA TRACON Controller reported losing radar on an aircraft when it went over the radar site.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported an unexpected communication issue with company personnel due to equipment problems.
ZLA Center Controller working sectors with new arrival/departure procedures discovered an error in one of the routes.
A319 First Officer reported that recent EFB updates have negatively impacted usability.

An air carrier gate agent reported the leveling system of the jetway he was utilizing to deplane a B737 malfunctioned; resulting in it sinking below the passenger entry/exit threshold and potentially impacting the fully open cabin door. After calling for maintenance the reporter implored the cabin attendants to expedite the passenger deplaning.
ZLC ARTCC Controller reported they were handed off an aircraft below their radar coverage on a random direct route instead of an approved non radar route.

ZDC Controller reported of an aircraft that transited in RVSM airspace; but was not qualified to be in that airspace. Controller reported that there was a loss of separation due to the aircraft not being RVSM equipped. Controller thought someone upstream should have verified if the aircraft was RVSM qualified.
C172 flight instructor reported severe mountain wave resulting in uncontrolled descent below the MVA.

An Air Carrier Ramp Lead expresses his safety concerns and also describes his work environment where company ramp personnel are being assigned to work a Regional Operator's CRJ-700 aircraft that they are not qualified or trained on. Company Management have been unresponsive to his concerns for safe operations.
A Maintenance Technician working on a B777 was asked to install an incorrect part number seal by a customer representative.
Air Carrier Loading personnel reported Hazmat cargo of ninety-nine Lithium Ion batteries scheduled for transport removed from flight due to incomplete DG documentation provided by shipper.
Technician reported difficulty in locating correct reference for replacing the Rudder Trim Knob.
Airbus Captain descending into KOA reported a near miss with a small transport aircraft after ATC cleared that aircraft on course to its destination which crossed the same waypoint.
ZDC Center Controller reported the Traffic Management Unit did not effectively manage traffic flow during weather deviations allowing the sector to become overloaded.
CLT Tower Controller reported problems associated with Ground Control taxiing an aircraft into the ILS Critical Area when it should have been avoided.
Air Carrier flight crew reported being fueled improperly leading to a fuel balancing issue in flight.
ZAU Controller and pilot reported the aircraft was at the wrong altitude due to an incorrect altimeter setting.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B757 departing DTW; and questioned the spacing.
Tower Controller reported being delayed by the TRACON due to the TRACON not releasing an aircraft resulting in an airspace incursion.
A320 pilot reported the MEL for a malfunctioning fuel cross feed system was reset several times with repairs being continuously pushed to later dates.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was removed for non-compliance with face mask policy.
Flight Attendant reported having to tell one passenger to wear a face mask while another passenger spoke up to the Flight Attendant.
Pilot reported cross-channeling transmissions between Comm 1 and 2 radios.
ZME Center Controller reported exposure to Methyl Ethyl Ketone fumes in the facility for two days.
Air carrier Captain reported being issued the wrong frequency to activate pilot controlled lights at an airport where the Tower was closed.

Air carrier Captain; while transiting Indian airspace; reported being issued holding while the flight's Transit Permit was verified even after the crew communicated the permit number. Only one holding turn was needed but the flight had no holding fuel.
A Flight Attendant reported not being given information or notice when deadheading as the only passenger on a ferry flight which had to return to the departure airport.
B747 Captain reported a rejected takeoff when the STAB CONFIG warning message appeared.
Customer Service Agent reported concerns with the daily under staffing in the airport lobby and the difficulties it is creating during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Distracted by a landing gear that failed to retract; the flight crew of a BAE-125-850XP noted a gaggle of geese too close ahead to avoid; and felt one or more strike the aircraft. The impact was followed by erratic indications and a report of smoke from the left engine heard over the radio. They performed an inflight shut down; and returned to their departure airport for an uneventful landing.
Cessna 150 pilot reported encountering significant jet blast while taxiing to parking.
A321 flight crew reported inadvertently flying below the floor of the DEN Class B airspace sector due to unclear charting.
EMB First Officer reported EWR ATC issuing complicated taxi instructions and hesitance to provide expected departure runway led to confusion and distraction in the flight deck.
Dispatcher reported that Airport Operations employee stated that they did not have required training to perform assigned duties.
Pilot reported having to descend to avoid a collision; then realized aircraft may have been too low over a group of people.

PHL Tower Controller reported an aircraft conducting a Visual Approach to a runway which the PAPI's have been inoperative for more than four years was lined up with the parallel taxiway; but the ASDE-X never alerted.
Pilot reported the IAF for the ILS approach to Runway 32 at DIK is not in the FMS database.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that a new procedure resulted in increased workload causing temporary loss of operational control.
Small Aircraft Pilot reported a NMAC on an instrument approach.
SLC Local Controller reported a strong wind shift caused go-arounds of two aircraft into conflicting courses.
Dispatcher reported noticing that the PGV airport AWOS was transmitting incorrect information. Dispatcher stated the PGV AWOS is frequently unreliable.

ZME Controller reports of a STAR that goes to three runways. ATIS stated a specific runway in use; pilot sets up aircraft to land on that runway. Unknown to ATC pilot is going to a different runway than cleared and there is a 4;000 foot difference of altitudes between runway Controller wanted and the runway the pilot was descending for.
Technician reported that a maintenance lift struck the elevator of an aircraft and caused damage.
CRP TRACON Controller reported an opposite direction landing and takeoff occurred at CRP airport with less than the required miles of separation according to the local directives.
Aircraft Mechanic reported that management stated that a Functional Flight Check was not required after working on flight control components.
Air carrier flight crew reported a waypoint crossing altitude dropping out of the FMS and not knowing why resulting in an altitude deviation.

CRP Controller reports of the lack of a data link between his facility and NGP. Both facilities use STARS but the system does not communicate and the controller has to make manual hand offs on all inbound traffic. Controller reports this is a safety risk.
NCT TRACON Controller reported a VFR NORDO aircraft crossed a runway's final approach course with traffic descending on the approach.
Technician reported that a procedural error caused an aircraft to enter service with passenger oxygen test stop buttons deployed.

Pilot questions why their dispatcher(s) are not getting a copy of their Maintenance Check Flight form prior to planning a Functional Check Flight (FCF) flight plan. He also questions the practice by the Director of Operations of determining; once airborne; where the airplane will go after a pass or fail Functional Check Flight becomes known. The perceived secrecy does not allow the check flight crew to effectively plan the flight and leads to degraded ATC coordination.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing an unstabilized approach and electing to go-a round.
Helicopter crew reported encountering degraded weather conditions and a lack of current weather reporting.
G450 flight crew reported they offset to the left on an oceanic route due to an A380 1000 feet above them to avoid potential wake turbulence.

Maintenance Technician reported many part numbers are incorrect due to the 'harmonizing' of part numbers for a B737-900 Illustrated Parts Catalogue. An installed battery charger that was supposed to be compatible failed the test due to this problem.

C421 Captain questions an ATC clearance departing Runway 12 at JVW to 'turn left heading 010 for obstructions upon entering controlled airspace'; but accepts the clearance. C177 non-instrument rated pilot reported flying an approach to destination airport in IMC conditions because of fuel concerns.
Captain reported navigating on via the wrong transition by executing the RNAV instead of the cleared ILS approach; following an onboard passenger problem.
Airbus Captain reported observing a B737 taking off where crosswind component was exceeded.
A TRACON Controller reported they allowed an aircraft to fly below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A320 Captain reported UAV less than 1 mile abeam the ILS.
An EMB-175 First Officer reported that the APU bleed was unnecessarily deferred when other pneumatic system faults were displayed on EICAS.
A Corporate flight crew on the TEB 19 ILS at 2;000 feet received a TCAS TA from an inbound EWR jet at 3;000 feet and a TCAS RA CLIMB from VFR traffic 400 feet below.
ZTL Center Controller reported weather moved through the area and the Traffic Management Unit refused to accommodate.
Center Controller reported being overloaded with traffic; MAP [Monitor Alert Parameter] was in the red; having a loss of separation; and an airspace violation due to COVID-19 staffing.

BTR Controllers report of an aircraft being too high for the approach. The pilot requested a 360 turn to get lower. This was approved by Developmental; then corrected by him to fly runway heading. The aircraft making missed approach now becomes a conflict with another aircraft. Controllers' report of having an ARTS failure during this operation.
G-V flight crew reported responding to a TCAS RA on departure from TEB.
Air carrier flight crew experienced moderate to severe turbulence while deviating around and through weather.

MDW Tower Controller reported an unsafe situation when an aircraft lined up with a taxiway instead of the runway.  Controller reported this approach is likely to be problematic because the angle it brings in aircraft on; lines up with Taxiway Y; until the last minute.
General aviation flight instructor reported noticing a crane rising to approximately 1;000 FT AGL in the vicinity of CPT airport that is not mentioned in the NOTAMs.

Air Carrier Captain reported the flight deck door chime systems are inoperative on several aircraft. During crew briefings the Captain found many crew members are unaware of the chime system and its operating characteristics.
B767-300 Captain reported that company Contract Maintenance are not afforded direct access to the company reporting system.
ZJX Controller reports vaguely of a loss of separation; but his major complaint is about the lack of work and planning that he thinks the TMU (Traffic Management Unit) does.
MD-11 flight crew reported that instructions were unclear for an MEL item for the pneumatic system.

Air carrier flight crew experienced difficulty communicating with the ground crew during deice/anti-icing of their aircraft. The first problem was ensuring that the crew deiced the entire aircraft. Secondly; the crew was not sure if both types were applied as required and requested. After the flight the crew could find traces of Type I deicing fluid; but not Type IV anti-icing fluid.
SFO Tower Controller reported an unsafe operation with a foreign departure and an arrival to the same runway.
Airline Dispatcher reported disparities between current volcanic ash activity in the airport vicinity and active SIGMET utilized for airline operations.
Palm Springs Tower Controller reported an unsafe approach due to weather and possible piloting issues.
MD11 Technician reported the maintenance platform was left partially open causing the elevator tip to make contact with the patio door.
Dispatcher reported that random windshear alerts occur at RIC airport when no significant weather exists.
When a ferry permit was requested for an Airbus 319; the Maintenance Controller refused because he was uncomfortable with the items that were deferred.
Pilot reported flying twice with an out of date registration.
Technician reported company deviated from approved maintenance procedures when repairing a fuel leak.
Carbon Cub Pilot reported wheel hit sign on aborted landing with loss of control resulted in off airport landing with damage.
Pilots reported routing and fuel problems associated with a ARTCC airspace closure.
Captain reported that the First Officer's tablet had locked up. A Maintenance Technician corrected the problem; but did not make a logbook entry.
Air carrier Captain descending on a Visual Approach to ABE reported they received a Terrain Alert.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft was unable to comply with the climb rate required on the SID and flew below two Minimum Vectoring Altitudes.
Flight Attendant reported that a passenger was reminded several times to wear a face mask; but would have the mask down and put it back on only when a flight attendant walked by.
B747 flight crew reported that the Captain became unresponsive; causing the crew to divert.
Air carrier flight crew reported losing communication with ARTCC. The reporter stated they were on the appropriate frequency the entire time; but may have been in an area of limited radio coverage.
A319 Captain reported receiving multiple runway and arrival changes on approach to IAH; resulting in increased workload and distractions.
B767 First Officer reported executing a go-around after receiving a GPWS terrain warning on an RNAV GPS approach to SFO Runway 10L.
B777 First Officer reported that an ETOPS flight was assigned an unsuitable alternate.
An air carrier Captain reported the First Officer descended below glidepath below the ATC Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with COVID-19 guidelines on blocking or not blocking seats and making reference to Flight Attendant/passenger safety.
A-320 Captain reported the de-icing crew improperly de-iced the aircraft resulting in a dangerous situation and a significant delay.
B777 First Officer reported the flight crew was distracted at a critical time in the takeoff roll when Tower radioed with a non-essential question.
The procedures in the MEL for deferring the potable water system were not accomplished completely which led up to the canceling of the flight.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported that during descent they misunderstood instructions from ATC.
B737-800 flight crew reported a runway excursion into the overrun area occurred when they lost braking and directional control near the end of the runway in a heavy downpour.

BE36 pilot reported loss of directional control after landing due to strong winds and ice on the runway. Reporter stated the controlling Center and his onboard XM and ADSB weather gave him inaccurate field condition and weather information.
The Captain of a B737-700 reported he told the first officer to sit the flight attendants down; they encountered turbulence and the flight attendants were all up.

ZDV Center Controller reported trying to coordinate traffic flows to the D01 TRACON during a period of weather deviations. The Center Controller was receiving conflicting instructions from different people at the TRACON. The Center Controller reports this is an ongoing problem when trying to coordinate with the TRACON.

A B737NG crew reported descending below the SFO Class B floor flying the SERFR RNAV STAR. NORCAL alerted about the deviation but the crew had been unaware of the Class B boundary which was not depicted in either the FMS or on the SERFR chart.
A Flight Attendant reported that a passenger requested to get off the airplane without claiming their luggage.
Center Controller reported a hand off issue with adjacent airspace resulting in an airspace violation.

A US carrier in Cuban airspace deviated for weather and was placed a route not in the original flight plan. An apparently task saturated ZMA Controller did not respond to calls for a clearance after entering US airspace.

ZAN Supervisor reported of a situation where 20 ATD (Along Track Distance) separation was improperly applied to two aircraft at the same altitude. Controller requested clarification on the proper way to set up ATD separation.
B757 Captain reported an increasing performance windshear event on departure from SFO which resulted in a near overspeed of the flaps during the initial climb out.
B737 flight crew reported encountering severe weather on approach that resulted in a flap over speed and altitude deviation.

A Passenger Service Representative attempted to board a disabled passenger in an emergency exit row. The Flight Attendant cited the FAR prohibiting certain passengers; but the agent stated the passenger could perform the duties even if unable to exit.
EMB-145 Captain reported rejecting takeoff due to jammed rudder pedals. Taxied back to gate for maintenance corrective action.

B737-800 Flight Attendant reported a teen unaccompanied minor had a doctor's note saying he had small seizures but weren't anything to worry about. Flight Attendant reported that during the flight the teen had 3 seizures and was foaming at the mouth.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger stated that another passenger had not been wearing a mask.
B787 flight crew reported fuel concern enroute to YSSY was exacerbated when ATC required a declaration of minimum fuel in order to avoid a delay.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with re-qualification training courses after not flying due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Air carrier pilot reported there is no safe aircraft emergency egress while parked at IAH with the terminal and ramp doors in the jetway both locked.

Both pilots of a B737 were a little surprised to see a Flight Manager Computer (FMC) unit without an Automatic Communications Addressing and Reporting System (ACARS) access function. No abnormal log data or Configuration Deviation List (CDL) was found so it was assumed the older aircraft simply wasn't so equipped. They subsequently learned the lack was unintentional and should have been entered in the Aircraft Maintenance Log (AML) and investigated.

ZID Controller reports that everything is normal reference a specific tracked aircraft on his display. The aircraft is shipped to Approach Control and then the data block disappears. The advised the Front Line Manager (FLM) to investigate and called the Approach Control to ask them what happened. Approach said they still had a data tag; they did not do anything strange but they did get a remove strip message.
BE-58 pilot reported that during rotation a single engine Cessna was on approach 100 feet above him coming in the opposite direction.

The flight crew of this 737 flight; and the responsible dispatcher; reported an enroute encounter with severe turbulence. No passengers were injured. However; two flight attendants were injured but elected to continue to the destination where they received medical attention.
B737 pilot reported exceeding the aircraft's maximum tire speed during takeoff.
During push-back; B737 crew experienced a hung/hot start while attempting to start engine number two. Crew was reassigned a different aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported forgetting to turn on three of the four window heat switches during pre-flight.

Air carrier Captain reported a series of miscommunications regarding a boarding passenger who was possibly contagious with COVID-19. After the Captain spent time resolving the issue; it was determined that the passenger could be allowed to board.
ZAU Controller reported after accepting a point out; another sector changed the data block information without coordination.
B767-200 Captain reported ground crew pulled chocks before hydraulics were pressurized and parking brakes were set; causing aircraft to roll.
MD-82 flight crew reported a loud banging noise due to an open communication panel shortly after gear retraction.

HSV controllers reported of a runway incursion involving a vehicle. The controllers just thought that vehicles had to be clear of the runway and did not realize that vehicles had to be on the safe side of the hold short bars.
Air carrier flight crew reported they were given taxi instructions that did not agree with the airport chart.

An IAH Controller departs an aircraft and then less than 2 minutes later departs another aircraft. The Controller didn't have the required separation between the two aircraft due to the lack of knowledge of the re-classification of wake turbulence rules.
C560XL air taxi flight crew reported a rejected takeoff when traffic was observed departing from the crossing runway.
Small Aircraft Private Pilot reported loss of control during landing.
CRJ-200 reported tight clearances operating into Gate at CYYZ because there was a B737 parked at an adjacent gate.

Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft had a bounced landing resulting in a go-around. The crew cited the handling and performance characteristics of the lightly loaded aircraft as a contributing factor.

Local Controller issued taxi instructions to an aircraft instead of switching the aircraft to Ground Control frequency to receive the taxi instructions. Reporter states this is a common practice at this facility in certain circumstances. Ground Control had traffic taxiing in confliction with the aircraft Local Control issued taxi instructions to. The pilot noticed the traffic and stopped on the taxiway and asked the Tower if they knew about the conflicting traffic.
EMB-505 / Phenom 300 flight crew reported taking off without a clearance.
Captain reported that the Dispatcher failed to notify the crew of a modified Release.
Portland Tower Controller reported running an arrival and a departure too close causing the arrival to go-around.
SCT TRACON Controllers reported a Controller forgot to issue a coordinated heading to an aircraft so it turned on its filed route into confliction with other traffic.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported excessive workload related to insufficient staffing.

A pilot reports about cracks that develop in a bracket attached to the Nose Landing Gear (NLG) strut that are not deferrable on Hawker HS-125-800 XP aircraft; causing delays and cancellations. Bracket supports a squat switch that controls the wing landing lights and taxi lights.
Multiple CRJ-200 flight crews reported that minor damage on the right wing tip from a crosswind landing went unnoticed for several flights.

Corporate jet SIC reported that during a circle to land maneuver at night; the PIC lined up with the wrong runway; allowed airspeed to decay; and did not execute an ATC-commanded go-around. The PIC did not respond to the SIC's guidance and CRM was compromised.
Ramp Agent reported incorrectly operating the cargo loader and made reference to being off of work due to a leave of absence for COVID-19.
Air taxi Dispatcher reported company Part 135 dispatchers are allowed to work very long hours and that fatigue has compromised safety.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a NMAC during final.
Flight Attendant reported several passengers were removed from the flight because they would not comply with face mask policy and were interfering with cabin crew duties.
ERJ-145 flight crew reported landing below minimums and conducting a hard landing.
Center Controller reported a staffing problem and weather related issues that made the sectors overloaded with traffic.
Air Carrier flight crew reported an unstable approach in gusty wind conditions. First Officer (PF) cited having minimal flight time this year.
An air carrier Maintenance Technician reported a health and safety concern over a delay in addressing mosquitos in the cabin after a flight from Buenos Aires.
Air Carrier Captain reported he has observed pressure from his company on flight attendants to work even if they are sick.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from ORD Tower while descending on a visual approach with LOC backup to Runway 27L. PAPI lights were obscured by the sun.
Pilot reported being pressured by a flight school owner to fly an unairworthy aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported they inadvertently took off with the wrong data loaded in the FMC.
B737-800 flight crew reported in flight notification of Hazmat cargo onboard without proper flight crew documentation. Required Hazmat documents completed upon arrival at destination.
B767 Maintenance reported that the hoist equipment failed when raising a main landing gear actuator.
B737NG flight crew reported the BNA CHSNE 2 RNAV Arrival design keeps aircraft high on the arrival making the crossing restriction at BGSTR difficult.
Passenger reported the flight attendants erected a curtain in the coach cabin as a rest area during flight. Passenger expressed safety and security concerns with this incident.
Boeing 777 Captain reported a fuel critical situation during descent due to unforeseen deviations.
S46 TRACON Controller reported having to vector an aircraft off their approach to avoid VFR traffic not in communication with ATC.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a stick shaker event after executing a go-around due to windshear.
B737 NG Captain reported the lack of NOTAMs regarding the shortened length of Runway 27 in SAN.
Air carrier pilot reported being advised by flight attendants during taxi out that passengers were not complying with face mask policy.
Air carrier pilot reported their FMS could not navigate the LAS RNAV-V 19R Approach properly due to the fixes on the SITEE Arrival not blending into the approach.
Extremely gusty winds prompted a crew to elect to do a go-around just prior to touchdown. The second approach was uneventful.

SCT Controller thought an aircraft that was on an IFR flight plan was on a VFR flight plan. The controller used 500 feet vertical separation in a holding pattern instead of 1000 feet. The targets on the STARS radar display were jumping back and forth from their actual position.

During the takeoff roll a flight crew receives an advisory message yet continues the takeoff. Once at altitude it is discovered that the message should have warranted a rejected takeoff procedure and the crew should have remained on the ground.
B737-800 Captain experiences confusion during the ANTHM RNAV to BWI due to the late runway transition assignment by PCT Approach; and their reluctance to issue the transition on the initial check in.
EMB-145 Captain reported a runway incursion at CAK airport.
A321 crew reported that on the LUCKI1 STAR into SAN it is very difficult to make the restrictions without overspeeding the flaps.

A very heavy A380-800 departed a domestic airport with a 285 kts minimum clean maneuvering speed and was criticized by the TRACON for exceeding 250 kts below 10;000 feet. The Controller disregarded the minimum safe maneuvering speed permitted by FAR 91.117(d).
Pilot reports of not being advised of traffic that ATC knew about and that the pilot received an RA on. Controller kept aircraft too high and eventually the pilot had to make a go around.
EMB-505 First Officer reported a track deviation on arrival into SFO when he became task saturated following multiple runway changes.
EMB-175 First Officer reported that Maintenance and the flight crew disagreed on the tire damage limits.
B737-800 Captain reported Flight Attendant notification of passenger illness due to Staph infection. Flight crew requested Biohazard assistance on arrival.
SA227 pilot and dispatcher reported the flight returned to departure airport after noticing smoke in the cockpit that was later traced to boxes placed near bleed heat vents.
A PHL Tower Controller reports of having a runway incursion because he forgot about traffic crossing and cleared an aircraft for takeoff.
B757 flight crew reported a TCAS RA and low glide slope warning while on a visual approach to SFO Runway 19L with parallel traffic for 19R.
Salt Lake Center Controller reported a problem with Big Sky Approach not accepting a hand-off in a timely manner.
Air carrier flight crew reported poor communications with ground crew while being towed into the gate.
Air carrier flight crew reported a laser strike during approach to MGGT airport.
Air carrier Captain reported uncorrectable erroneousness NOTOC resulting in the removal of Hazmat cargo.

An aircraft landed long. The reporting Local Controller stated the aircraft stopped on a taxiway edge and had to taxi off the surface onto grass to return to the taxiway (the taxiway extends from the end of the runway). The reporting pilot stated that other than landing long due to wet runway nothing unusual happened. The pilot stated the aircraft never left the runway/taxiway surfaces.

A Dispatcher described his difficulty manually revising an A320's inflight terrain clearance release over the Rocky Mountains. An engine anti-ice valve had been MEL'ed open and special procedures were required because of the available alternate airports.
A Corporate pilot conducting a Visual Approach reported receiving a Terrain Alert but continued the approach.
Controllers had a disagreement over the requirement to hand off a VFR aircraft from a TRACON to an ARTCC.
MEM Local Controller reported that a procedure for simultaneous departures off of parallel runways does not ensure the required degrees divergence for legal separation.
Captain reported not being stabilized for approach prior to 1000ft. because they had difficulty requesting lower and slower from ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported that a customer gate-checked a bag with lithium batteries; resulting in a return to the gate.

B757 flight crew discovers apparent damage to the right engine cowl; which appears to be an old repair that has failed. The damage is written up but Maintenance indicates that it has been previously documented and signs it off.
ZOB Controller reported a recurring lack and method of coordination for scheduling MOAs.
K90 TRACON Controller reported an unidentified VFR aircraft was a NMAC with a VFR aircraft the Controller was working.
B737-700 First Officer (FO) reported encountering icing conditions at FL390 that could have been a factor in the later failure of both bleed valves that resulted in loss of cabin pressure.
PA28 pilot reported a NMAC with a UAV.

Pilot reports of requesting a deviation for weather. Controller did not want to give the deviation but did. Controller let aircraft deviate for a while then told pilot they had to return to the previously assigned heading. Pilot stated this would put aircraft back into the storm and did not want to. Controller demanded the aircraft return to the heading. Pilot was in VFR conditions and cancelled IFR.
An ARR Tower Controller reports of staffing issues at the Tower. Reporter sites a specific day where he thought they did a good job with the short staffing; but management thinks they did not.
B737 Captain reported the autopilot disengaged and flight director guidance was lost after the aircraft encountered moderate turbulence during an ILS approach in IMC.
ZLA Center Controller reported losing separation due to traffic volume; watching too large of an area and the COVID-19 configuration.
ABI TRACON Controller describes a situation where an aircraft under their control does not auto handoff and violates another Controller's sector; resulting in an airspace deviation.
Air carrier First Officer reported that after setting up his EFB tablet to display the QRH; all the tabs had closed at pushback.
B777 Captain reported that the reserve First Officer had not flown in 8 months and was not proficient in the aircraft.
Technician reported receiving two portable fire extinguishers from the stores without parts tags filled out correctly.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported ground service personnel attempted to open the galley door when it was still armed shortly after arrival; and explained they could not see the flag through the window because of sun glare.
A single engine pilot reported approach control vectored a military aircraft over his aircraft which the pilot felt was unsafe.
B737 Captain found the new cold weather section of his operations manual to be very confusing; especially the section on freezing precipitation.
Medium Transport aircraft Captain reported making contact with a Ground Power Unit while being marshaled in to their parking spot.
ZLA Center Controller reported an aircraft was cleared via an OAPM STAR; but the aircraft had an extra fix in their database compared to the chart.
C177 pilot reported a loss of control during landing after encountering a significant crosswind.
GEG Radar Approach controllers reported their sector vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A Tower Controller reports an arriving aircraft initiated a go-around due to traffic crossing the runway. The Controller forgot to issue complete go-around instructions to the aircraft prior to switching them to the Departure Controller.

Four reports from an SCT Controller providing training; his trainee Controller and the crew of an airline aircraft documenting a near mid-air caused by an aircraft coming out of the Banning Pass and conflicting with the airliner turning final for PSP.
A Flight Instructor conducting training in IMC reported a near miss with an aircraft not identified or in contact with ATC.
Ground Personnel reported that a lack of training on new jetbridge equipment created a ramp safety hazard.
Captain reported GPS jamming at this location.
UAV pilot reported pilot error in not obtaining authorization for the flight.
Technician reported that task cards for servicing the APU of aircraft in storage do not have the correct reference number.

While several Maintenance personnel were performing an engine wash on a B777; there was a lack of communication between the personnel hand washing the engine fan blades with a blade lock strap in place and the personnel in the cockpit accomplishing an alternate method by motoring the engine.
GA Pilot reported that two skydivers were encountered approaching the vicinity of TCY.
A319 flight crew reported the aircraft slide forward with the parking brake set at the gate due to ramp conditions.
B737 Captain reported the flight plan they were on did not require an alternate due to good weather; when they arrived the weather was at minimums with no alternate.

A low time instrument rated pilot departed a CTAF airport for night landings in his PA-28 and it became IMC. He contacted the TRACON; was honest about his situation and was vectored without charts for an ILS to a nearby airport.

P50 TMC describes a situation where an aircraft is supposed to make three runs over a target; then when airborne changes its plan and drops skydivers unknown to ATC. Departing traffic has to deviate around falling skydivers.
ZBW Controller reported an unsafe situation involving a VFR aircraft and numerous gliders that were NOTAMed to be where they were. Most gliders did not have Mode C capability.
Pilot reported unsafe flight condition due to racetrack lights pointing directly into the sky disorienting the pilot.
Air carrier Captain reported returning to the gate to remove four passengers who were disruptive and refusing to comply with face mask policy.

GA pilot on approach to 7FL6 Runway 23 reported landing on the east-west taxiway while confused by haze; smoke; and a windscreen obscured with mist. The deactivated runway extremely faded X was detected while taxiing to parking.
ZAU Controller reported of not being briefed or even sure that a specific airspace was hot while working the position.
A320 First Officer reported executing a go-around at ZZZZ airport after receiving a GPWS terrain warning.
AT-602 pilot reported failure to lock the tailwheel led to a loss of directional control on landing roll and a runway excursion.

A Center Controller reported a neigboring Center Sector allowed an aircraft to fly through a corner of their airspace without coordination and then observed the same Sector handoff two aircraft on converging headings at the same altitude.

A widebody transport crew inbound to LGB responded to a TCAS/RA advisory to 'maintain vertical descent.' The crew saw a Bonanza crossing left to right. According to the report no traffic information was issued to the crew.

EMB-175 First Officer reported ATC issued THEM a descent clearance from FL350 to FL340. At FL345 a TCAS RA alerted so the Captain leveled off then climbed back to FL350 as the TCAS target passed 400 feet beneath.
First officer reported incorrect wording on Jeppsen chart departure.
First Officer reported that the PAPI (Precision Approach Path Indicator) is located on the left side of the runway whereas it's listed on the approach plate as being on the right side.
B737 First Officer reported three TA alerts while on approach to Runway 10L at PDX due to pattern work at VUO.
S56 Controller reported receiving a handoff that was in conflict due to change in departure procedures.
Dispatcher reported that flight crew reported a previous dispatcher had sent an entire NOTAM package to aircraft printer which tied it up entire flight.
DFW Tower Controller mistakenly departed aircraft using RNAV departures rather than vectors. The information display system wasn't showing the types of departures in use.

A revision to a Letter of Agreement (LOA) was published. Previous preapproved coordination rules were accidentally omitted from the new LOA. The rule allowed TRACON controllers to descend aircraft while still in ARTCC's airspace. Controllers were not briefed on the omission and continued to operate under the previous rule which no longer existed in the new publication.

CRJ-200 First Officer describes a windshear encounter during approach to MKE with the Captain flying that results in stick shaker activation as flaps 45 are selected. The autopilot disengages and max power is called for. As the alert ceases the Tower is advised and the go around is continued and vectors received for another approach.
ZAB Center Controller reported an airspace violation and loss of separation when an off course aircraft approached an active parachute jump area.
UAV pilot reported possible violation of maximum altitude regarding FAR Part 107.
B757 Captain was issued a speed restriction and runway change just prior to KONZL on the SEAVU2 arrival to LAX and is unable to comply; crossing KONZL 300 feet high.
B737-800 pilot reported diverting with minimum fuel after PHX began experiencing dust storms and microbursts.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported concern about a proposed new procedure that would generate suggested route changes directly to flight crews without Dispatcher review in the interest of cost savings.

ZNY Controller reported an MD80 in 10-12 mile trail of an A380 experienced wake turbulence at FL330 that resulted in a 30 degree bank. Controller recommended increasing minimum separation to 15-20 miles.
B737NG Captain reported high workload resulted in some missed SOPs on arrival into DEN following multiple ATC clearance changes.

B737-700 flight crew commenced slowing the aircraft for final configuration after being cleared by ATC for a Visual Approach. ATC advised that the previously assigned speed was to have been maintained until the Final Approach Fix.
Tower Controllers reported sending an aircraft around due to Airport Surface Detection Equipment alert for an aircraft exiting the runway.
Air Carrier Lead Technician reported Hazmat shipped incorrectly without appropriate labels and required documents.
Air carrier flight crew reported a miscommunication during initial climb in a lightly-loaded aircraft resulted in retracting the flaps prematurely.

During the process of assembling a Pratt & Whitney PW100 gearbox the incorrect tooling was used and the incorrect pressure was applied causing the hydraulic ram to depart and crashed onto the bullgear and propeller shaft.
Captain reported that task saturation during a go around resulted in an altitude deviation.

Two Center Traffic Management Specialists and a Controller report the Command Center ignored requests for weather reroutes; causing numerous Center sectors to be dangerously overloaded with traffic deviating for weather.
B777 Captain reported that tow-in crews are not using standard communication from ground to pilot.
Regional jet flight crew reported turbulence north of ADM VOR which caused them to gain 1200 feet of altitude and exceed redline airspeed.
Flight Attendant reported a family was not wearing face masks and had to be reminded often during flight.
A maintenance technician reported that after removing the tail stand it was observed the Teflon tip remained attached to the aircraft.
A319 Captain reported a contradiction from standard operating procedures when asked to release the brakes prior to push clearance to facilitate use of a new style tug.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported a hazardous condition at a maintenance facility which could result in injury or death.
Ground personnel reported that panel screws are being found loose or missing on pre and post-flight inspections of B777 aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported they experienced a Resolution Advisory on approach to a parallel runway at DEN and expressed concern about the length of the RA and the amount of the altitude loss required to resolve the RA. Captain recommends reevaluating the practice of non-staggered approaches to parallel runways at DEN.
Air carrier flight crew taxiing wide-body aircraft mis-interpret missing hold short sign.
Student pilot report loss of aircraft control during landing possibly due to wind gust.

ZNY ARTCC Controller reported a TRACON Controller entered a remove strip message on a flight plan. The Controller had to override the flight plan control to enter the message. An aircraft with the same call sign was active in ARTCC airspace. The data tag on the ARTCC Controllers radar disappeared from the display.
A B777 Flight Attendant was injured after very short notice about its impending severe turbulence event.
Air carrier Captain reported call to Dispatch to clarify missing Dangerous Goods Manifest statement regarding cargo air condition ON requirements.
A Tower Local Controller reported an aircraft initiating a go-around was in confliction with a helicopter transitioning the airspace.
Air Carrier Captain reported experiencing a momentary stall warning after encountering wake turbulence departing RJAA in trail of a heavy B787.
Commercial fixed wing pilot reported a taxiway incursion due to poor signage and pavement markings at LAX.
Air carrier First Officer reported a NMAC after takeoff at BWI.
Flight Attendant reported that the aircraft did not have adequate cleaning/sanitizing supplies on board and the aircraft did not appear to have been properly cleaned.
Eugene air traffic control Tower operator reported Tower lighting panel out of service; hindering IFR operations.
B737 Captain reported refusing Hazmat cargo due to lack of information provided to flight crew regarding the nature of the cargo.

A Boeing 737 pilot reported being hit in the eyes by a laser while on final approach at a foreign airport. The preceding aircraft also reported a laser directed at them. The pilot was not injured; and the incident was reported to airport authorities.
Air Carrier Dispatcher mentioned workload exceeds available resources to safely dispatch and monitor flights.
Captain of a Part 121 twin turboprop reported getting too slow when transitioning from clean to the first flap setting.

An air carrier flight crew reported a GPS anomaly during arrival and departure at Manila Airport (RPLL). The anomaly was transient and not thought to be aircraft related. Manila station personnel indicated that this was a known issue and was being investigated.
Air carrier flight crew reported unanticipated icy ramp conditions while parking their aircraft at the gate. They were unable to stop the aircraft before hitting a tug vehicle.
B737 First Officer reported the BUR RNAV Runway 15 Approach possibly violates WHP airspace.

A B737 crew flying the BWI Runway 10 ILS descended to 1;500' inside COLUM on the localizer as per the ILS approach chart but were to told by PCT the minimum MDA altitude 2;000'. The crew could not find the basis for that statement.

Roanoke Tower Instructor reported of a loss of separation reported by a pilot a week after the incident. The Controller advised the aircraft was through the crossing intersection before the landing aircraft was at the runway threshold.
B737-700 Captain reported concern with the policy of tankering fuel to save money; stating the increased weight reduces the safety margin on landing.

GFK Approach Controller reported of an operation at the airport that is not working correctly. Pilot attempted to enter the airspace; but does not respond in a timely manner and is a conflict with opposite direction traffic.
Pilot reported a taxiway incursion; citing distraction with aircraft condition and lack of recent flying as contributing factors.
ZLC Center Supervisor reported a parachute jump aircraft released their jumpers over an airport even though they knew there was an aircraft departing the airport.

Air carrier First Officer reported that while attempting to lose altitude on an unstable approach the Captain exceeded a 45-degree bank angle and a flap overspeed occurred. A second overspeed occurred during the subsequent go-around.
B777-200 flight crew reported an unstabilized approach on ILS approach due to the influence of a strong tailwind.
Technicians reported a flight that lost an excessive amount of oil in-flight due to a missing oil cap.
Air Taxi flight crew reported an early descent led to ice accumulation and possible tail stall.
Maintenance Technician reported inconsistent company oil and consumable reference numbers; causing confusion when performing service.
Air Carrier Captain reported a ill passenger was removed from the flight before departure.
Air carrier Captain enroute to SGF reported MLC ATIS interfered with SGF ATIS when approaching from the southwest. The two airports are 183 NM apart.
B737 flight crew reported rejecting takeoff due to conflicting wind information from the tower takeoff clearance.
Flight Attendant reported that a flight attendant had come in contact with neighbors who were positive for COVID-19 and wanted guidance on whether she should continue with the trip or not.
GA pilot reported entering a sporting TFR and wasn't sure if these type of TFRs are still in place during the COVID-19 pandemic.
B737 flight crew reported that an unknown aircraft created a traffic conflict on final; and resulted in an RA and a go-around.
Pilot reported an airspace violation he attributed to ATC not switching him soon enough to get clearance to enter Class Charlie airspace.

Captain reported being told specific taxi instructions to a runway by Ramp Control. The crew taxied as instructed then contacted Ground Control. Ground informed the crew that they did not have clearance to taxi as they did. Flight crew then received different taxi instructions from Ground and were given a phone number to call.  Pilot called the number and was told they would look into it.
Tower Front Line Manager reported problems with the ASOS failure and notification from the Contract Weather Observer.
C172 flight instructor reported an issue in the Obstacle Departure Procedure for LUA airport.
B737 Captain reported passenger no easy access information to determine procedures for Lithium powered devices.
An air carrier pilot reported a TCAS RA CLIMB during a near miss with an unidentified aircraft at about 5;500 feet near SLI .
Air Carrier personnel reported a Customer Service Representative was forced to operate a jet bridge without proper qualification.
A321 Flight Crew reported a loss of oil quantity followed by loss of oil pressure causing the number 1 engine to be shut down and a diversion.
Air carrier pilot reported DFW Ground Controllers routinely issue improper taxi instructions by using a spot number in lieu of a call sign.
An air carrier crew reported confusion caused while flying the ATL WINNG 1 STAR after ATC cleared them for visual approach; uncleared them with a vector then recleared them for the visual.
ERJ-175 First Officer reported they operated a flight with UN3373 aboard; a restricted item; without it being listed on their hazardous goods manifest.

EMB145 Captain reported a difficult flight due to turbulence and thunderstorms. Two missed approaches occurred at the destination airport due to windshear warnings on short final. The crew then diverted to an alternate; landing with 1200 pounds of fuel; and elected not to continue their duty day.
Bell 407 pilot reported an airspace violation due to busy frequency.

HCF Controller reported a problem with traffic entering the sector that the Traffic Manager had put into place. Reporter stated this is a reoccurring theme at the facility and wants the problem to be looked into.
B737 Captain and Dispatcher reported serious weather that resulted in a diversion to an offline airport.
SAT TRACON Controller reported a pilot reported an NMAC with a glider while on Approach.
