B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence descending through FL320 12 miles in trail of a B777 on the DYAMD arrival into SFO.
Air carrier ramp employee reported that the company Hazardous Material Screener identified undeclared hazardous materials during preflight inspection.
Maintenance Technician reported installing incorrect parts that another Technician had ordered and failed to verify correct part numbers.
B757 Captain reported maintenance pulled the wrong circuit breakers per MEL deferral procedure.
PA28 Instructor Pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern at SNA airport.
Aircraft landed and turned off runway onto a closed taxiway with a barricade. The Taxiway closure was broadcast on the airport ATIS and reported in a NOTAM.

The Controller was working two aircraft on converging courses at 35;000 feet. The Controller attempted to descend one of the aircraft but could not because it was on the wrong frequency. The Controller climbed the other aircraft; but not before a loss of required separation occurred.

A New York Center (ZNY) Controller reports of an aircraft that drops sky divers while conflicting traffic for it is inbound to an airport in close proximity. The Controller thinks this operation is not safe. The pilot was not on frequency all of the time and was off coordinating on UNICOM.
B737-700 flight crew reported severe turbulence accompanied by GPWS and windshear warning on approach.
EMB-145 Captain reported that flight was cancelled due to bad weather at arrival airport; although they felt pressured go continue flight by Dispatch and Chief Pilot.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reports using an infrared thermometer to measure food temperatures brought on board by catering. All of the food is in the high seventies or low eighties and the aircraft is not equipped with chillers.
ZME Center controllers reported smelling strong chemical odors in the control room. The reporters felt ill from the fumes.
Embraer 175 Captain reported night visual approaches unsafe with current aircraft equipment; company training and procedures.
Air carrier Captain reported a disparity between the Jeppesen 10-9; 10-9B; and the actual signage in the area of k; K-3; M; and L Taxiways from Runway 27R to Spot 9 at PHL.
Air carrier flight crew reported a GPWS warning during a visual approach into DAL at night.
CRJ Captain reported that a laser shone repeatedly into the cockpit resulted in distraction and persisting vision spots.
First Officer reported encountering a drone while on final approach to Newport News airport.
Carbon Cub pilot reported a ground strike occurred following a wake turbulence encounter with a military transport just before landing at GJT.
BOS Local Controller sent an aircraft around that was lined up to land on the wrong runway.
Technician reported flight crews are concerned that when they receive an aircraft that has sat for a few days some switches are in the wrong position.
Balloon pilot reported encountering power lines during landing when the wind unexpectedly picked up.
A Flight Crew reported after reprogramming the FMS to a different runway than planned they descended below a crossing restriction on the RNAV Arrival.

When communication between the flight crew and pushback was lost for unexplained reasons the flight crew set the aircraft brakes but the tug kept moving and struck the nose of the aircraft. Passengers were evacuated on the ramp and walked to the terminal rather than attempt to tow aircraft back to the gate risking further damage.
HPN controllers reported an unsafe operation when a landing aircraft was asked to hold short of an intersecting runway and could not resulting in a ground conflict.
A B737 on a transcontinental flight diverted for fuel due to unplanned fuel usage and extended their duty day two hours to comply with FAR 117 limitations.
CE-680 Captain reported experiencing multiple autopilot/yaw damper anomalies that were later traced to the #2 AHRS unit not being properly secured in its rack by the previous maintenance personnel.
ZID Controller reported an airborne conflict with a mode-C intruder.
EMB-175 Captain reported they initiated a go-around below 1;000 feet due to severe weather and a windshear warning.

B767-400 flight crew experienced a bird strike at 300 feet after takeoff causing fan vibration. The engine was idled but kept running and the center tank fuel was dumped; before returning to land 5;000 pounds overweight.
EMB-145 First Officer reported an uneventful Missed Approach and landing due to an unstable approach during previous approach.
DCA Tower controllers reported that they requested 4 miles in trail from Potomac TRACON for aircraft landing DCA. It was agreed by both facility Traffic Managers; but PCT gave DCA 2-3 miles.
Dispatcher reported ACARS was working intermittently.
Maintenance Technician reported a Crew Chief may have falsified documents by signing off items that were not accomplished due to time pressure.
Pilot reported  missing a taxiing seaplane that landed in a prohibited area by 100 feet.
First Officer reported climbing to the charted SID altitude instead of the initial altitude restriction assigned in their verbal clearance.
GA pilot reported having communication issues with Center.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non-compliant with face mask policy.
Flight Crew reported jet bridge malfunction caused damage to aircraft that was not reported or logged; resulting in a return to departure airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported that four aircraft pushed back simultaneously; causing some of them to be blocked in the alley at BNA airport.
A pilot of a light aircraft; performing pipeline patrol duty; reported inadvertently penetrating a wildfire TFR due to the rapidly changing restricted airspace environment.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing DTW in trail of an A321.

Air carrier flight crew had briefed an ILS approach prior to their descent. During their descent they were assigned a different runway and approach. Unfortunately; they briefed the wrong approach. They discovered their error and briefed and flew the correct approach. Reporter suggests a change be made to the 10-7 chart so others don't mistake the different ILS's to the same Runway.
B737 flight crew reported that the MEL for the cabin lights was misapplied.

After departing; the crew experienced multiple bird strikes which resulted in high vibration and compressor stalls in the number two engine. They accomplished the checklist and were able to operate the engine at idle while returning to land at departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported they left an engine running after parking at the gate. They cited as contributing factors the failure to complete a checklist due to distraction.
EMB-145 flight crew reported the aircraft unable to taxi to the gate under its own power due to taxiway conditions.

An LAX Local Controller; while watching an aircraft on the ASDE-X; sees the target and identification tag disappear. The digital display of a target was still showing. He had seen this before on another small aircraft but didn't do anything about it; so this time he reported it.
Air carrier flight crew reported windshear on approach causing them to divert.

Air carrier First Officer reported that the Flight Attendant safety briefing did not include procedural guidance on how passengers should remove face masks and don oxygen masks in the event of an emergency.

A Flight Attendant and a co-worker reported being sent to Dakar; Senegal late at night with no transportation arranged by the company. After dealing with a shady character they are able to get cab to their hotel.

The Captain reported that while still at the gate; a fast moving storm system with associated high winds caused unsecured ground equipment to be blown into the aircraft; causing damage to the fuselage.
Pilot of an MQ-9 Aircraft (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) reported that due to weather conditions and icing they needed to change altitude in order to maintain flight.

OGG Controller reports of an overtake on final that if not resolved may have resulted in a NMAC.  Controller described that this type of situation is a recurring event where HCF TRACON does not provide adequate spacing on final due to speed issues.
GA pilot reported a Class B airspace violation.
ARTCC Controller reported of a rocket launch they thought had been cancelled. Moments later pilots reported seeing a rocket airborne. Controller reported this to the Front Line Manager.
B737NG flight crew reported severe turbulence encounter while on climbout from Denver.

EWR Tower Instructor and Developmental report of a squeeze play attributed to NY TRACON not providing enough spacing for the configuration they were in. Aircraft departs and slows due to a headwind and the ASDE-X display goes into alert with an aircraft on final to another runway. Controllers complain about the Bridge Visual and its poor procedure.
Maintenance Technician reported being let go after using scissor lift which contacted CRJ-900 engine tail pipe causing damage.
Technician reported that an open maintenance item was overlooked on audit and the aircraft was delivered to the terminal for revenue service.
Air Carrier Maintenance Crew Chief reported being told to direct his technicians to perform an aircraft inspection procedure incorrectly.
GA pilot and Air Traffic Controller reported confusion in regard to instrument operations near BORTY waypoint.
A helicopter pilot reported a near miss with a large military transport. The military plane was flying low; fast; and not communicating on any of the customary frequencies used in the area.
C172 pilot reported NMAC and cited reoccurring issues with ATC Tower.
EMB-145 First Officer reported executing a go-around after experiencing a wake turbulence encounter on approach into ORD.
MD11 flight crew encountered extreme turbulence in cruise.

Flight Attendant reported there are only three jumpseats available for five flight attendants to protect themselves near the aft galley during turbulence on company B787 aircraft. Of the five flight attendants; two were left standing holding onto handholds that are dangerous due to their proximity to the countertop space. Flight Attendant suggested that repositioning certain cabin furnishings would allow for additional jumpseats.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant has written this report plus several others over service carts being placed on aircraft without operating brakes.
GA pilot reported experiencing an airborne conflict while on approach to VLD airport.  Reporter stated ATC instructions contributed to the event.
Captain reported landing without clearance; citing fatigue as contributing.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation during departure.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft security check may have been improperly conducted and cited confusion between the wording in two manuals as a contributing factor.
Technician reported missing a required CAT III upgrade after maintenance actions and the aircraft departed on a scheduled flight.
Air carrier Captain reported a discrepancy in their company manual with regards to parking at gates that do not have a straight lead in line.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing radar/GPS jamming in the vicinity of ABQ VOR.
ZHU Controller reports of a loss of separation due to lack of speed control. Other sector did not advise the working sector that speeds were not given/or removed to continue separation standards.
B737 First Officer reported several unusual maneuvers the FMC commanded to comply with restrictions for the SNA DSNEE1 Arrival.
Air Carrier First Officer reported that during cruise the Satellite-based Augmentation System (SBAS) went out and GPS position was lost for short while; then returned.
Air carrier pilot reported a ramp incursion and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
B737NG Captain reported executing a go-around at BWI when the rain became very heavy near touchdown.
Air carrier flight crew reported landing without a clearance from Tower and stated that fatigue and lack of recent flying contributed to the event.

During the RNAV Visual Runway 31L Approach to PSP; crew experienced an EGPWS 'too low terrain pull-up' warning and followed flight manual procedures for day/VMC EGPWS warning. Once the EGPWS warning ceased; crew continued approach to a normal landing. Crew recommends that the altitudes on this approach procedure be reviewed.
A320 Captain reported a passenger's e-cigarette in their carry-on caused concern which resulted in deplaning and a smoke and fume investigation.
A Maintenance Technician reported that emergency slides are not rendered safe prior to removal on Boeing 737 aircraft.
ARTCC Operations Manager reported of a shift where staffing and sector configuration was questioned.  Controllers became busy with late traffic and weather after all others left on a mid-shift.
PA-28 solo pilot reported loss of directional control on landing resulting in a runway excursion.
B737-700 Maintenance Technician reported that after replacing the tire they realized a washer was not installed.
Air carrier copilot reported he flew through the localizer during a late runway change to an ILS to Runway 30R at MSP and was re-sequenced for the approach.
Flight crew reported failure to execute a go around after receiving a ground terrain warning.
Ground employees reported supertug parking aircraft issues and combining separate parking crews; which undermines current COVID-19 policies.
Las Vegas Tower Controller reported not applying Arrival Departure Window procedures correctly leading to a loss of separation.
Pilot reported a possible airspace violation and states the reason was due to the lack of flying time in the last 6 months due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to MEM in trail of a heavy widebody aircraft.
Unqualified maintenance technicians were assigned to work on 787-10 (Extended-range Twin-engine Operations) aircraft.
A320 flight crew reported an airborne conflict on departure with parallel departure which warranted an evasive maneuver.
Denver TRACON Controller reported of an unsafe situation with an arrival and a departure aircraft. Reporter wants the STAR to be removed from use.
A ramp employee driving a belt loader reported the brakes failed resulting in light contact with the aircraft and minor injuries to himself.
STL Local Controller reports of an aircraft taking off without a clearance. Aircraft was told to line up and wait; but Controller didn't tell him it was for spacing on previous departure.
Pilot reported of being too close to preceding departure. Aircraft received a TA/RA on climb out.  Reporter wondered if the ATC facility was running VFR separation during IFR conditions.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing a speed deviation during departure and stated lack of flying contributed to the event.
Flight instructor reported an NMAC in the landing pattern with an aircraft that was performing aerobatics.
Ground personnel reported aircraft ground return to correct Hazmat cargo configuration loading discrepancy.
A Maintenance Technician reported that work accomplished on a turbine disk was not signed-off and it was unknown what procedures were performed.
Air Carrier Lead Technicians reported being notified that they may have inadvertently contributed to a fume event by leaving FOD on an aircraft they repaired.
CRJ-900 flight crew and the Dispatcher reported the flight diverted for weather to an airport not listed as an alternate.
Air taxi Captain reported that company aircraft have not been properly maintained.
A pilot reported the SJD VOR DME-2 for both Runways 16 and 34 appear to indicate MARUX intersection is located on SJD 003R at 25DME but in fact its location is 47DME.
Ground personnel at arrival airport reported illegal HAZMAT cargo transport due to procedural errors at origin airport.
Pilot reported after landing a crosswind took control of the aircraft causing a runway excursion.

B737-800 Flight Attendant reported 'smoke-fume' event from smoking battery in passenger cabin during passenger boarding. Flight attendants reported experiencing health symptoms due to strong fumes/smoke.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported diverting to an alternate airport due to nose landing gear retraction issues following takeoff in snowy and slushy conditions.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving an RA for nearby traffic while ATC was handling an emergency at their destination.
C525 pilot reported an airborne conflict during their initial climb that resulted in a TCAS TA.
A VFR aircraft climbed into ORD Class B without a clearance. An arriving aircraft reported a TCAS alert with the traffic.
E-175 Captain reported company notification of undocumented air transport of Hazmat cargo on a previously completed flight.
Flight Crew reported terrain alert on approach.
Air Carrier First Officer reported an altitude deviation during departure and cited poor CRM and lack of flying as contributing factors.
Air Carrier flight crew reported upon breaking out of the clouds there was traffic in front of them for the same runway which they had to maneuver to avoid.
A Flight Attendant reported a Company Manager ordered that an oversized carry on be allowed in the cabin even after company policy and FAR were directly identified.
An air carrier Captain reported difficulty avoiding Cotopaxi volcanic ash departing on the SEQM/UIO CONDOCOCHA NORTH FLOW DEPARTURE Runway 18. Avoiding ash on a night departure may not be possible.
Air carrier Ground Operations employee reported that unqualified personnel were assigned to air-start an aircraft resulting in extreme risk to personnel and equipment.
Washington Center Controller reported problems with aircraft on the CHLSY arrival again descending below a required window.

Air carrier flight crew reported rejecting a takeoff in response to multiple warning lights. The aircraft had been in storage and anti-ice circuit breakers had been collared and not removed by Maintenance prior to flight.

While in cruise a turbojet flight crew encountered a lightning strike. The airplane landed without incident with the exception of some possible degradation of nose wheel steering. Minor scorch marks were noted in the nose gear door area.
Flight Attendant reported passenger inadvertently checked baggage containing laptop with batteries installed.

AMA controller and pilot of one of the aircraft involved report of a near miss due to the aircraft on downwind coming in close proximity of the trainer jet on a straight in final.  Pilot was told to make 360's on downwind; but flew close to the final.
Seaplane aborted takeoff attempt due to rough water conditions. When attempting to turn during subsequent taxi aircraft experienced a 'water loop' resulting in aircraft damage.
B737 Captain reported the company manual provided inadequate guidance to determine if a runway surface was to be considered contaminated.
The Pilot of a Blackhawk H-60 Helicopter had to take evasive action during final approach to avoid another H-60 Helicopter that was departing.
Savannah TRACON Controller reported the Tower departed an aircraft with the wrong flight plan and beacon code due to similar call signs.
B737 Captain reported non-standard call-outs used by First Officer caused a distraction during takeoff roll.
A321 pilot reported a loss of 400 feet of altitude due to autopilot failure; weather; and turbulence.
GSP TRACON Controller reported an occurrence which routinely happens of VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC departing one airport into the final approach course traffic of another airport.
CLT TRACON Controller reported receiving a handoff on arrival that was not able to comply with altitude and speed restrictions on the published STAR.
Air Carrier Captain reported fatigue issues while flying as a reserve pilot for a 4 day pairing during the COVID-19 pandemic.
D10 TRACON Controller reported losing minimum wake separation between an A380 and a trailing aircraft on arrival into DFW.

Flight Attendant reported they are not being provided with adequate cleaning supplies and sanitizers during the COVID-19 pandemic. Flight Attendant also expressed concern with the flight being overstaffed for no reason.
B747 flight crew reported that the seatbelt/harness for the second observer seat did not release properly; and that Maintenance initially failed to accurately document the repair.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported a low fuel situation after executing multiple go-arounds due to windshear conditions.
GEG Approach Controller reported an A320 descended below the MVA due to a blocked altitude readback.
Pilot reported recently installed taller power-line poles located near WI14 airport and drop zone pose a safety threat to aircraft and skydivers.
Flight crew reported finding evidence of a bird strike on aircraft during morning walk around inspection.
ERJ-175 Captain reported severe turbulence event.

Air carrier flight crew reported being cleared for a night visual to OAK Runway 30. They were descending towards 2;800 feet after accepting direct to MITOE while still near PARBB. The EGPWS Terrain Warning alerted mandating a go-around which was followed by a vector back to the airport.

After diverting for weather to an off line Canadian airport the Captain reported difficulty with getting company approval to deplane the passengers and accommodate them in hotels for what was developing into a protracted weather delay.
A B767 Captain reported being informed after the flight that his automated computed weight was approximately 8;000 pounds below the as loaded weight.
B737-900 First Officer reported that a misunderstood taxi clearance resulted in a critical ground conflict.
B737-700 Captain reported being unable to load the RNAV approach in the FMC because of a mandatory crossing restriction on the arrival.

Kansas City Center Controller reported of short staffing and aircraft deviating for weather. Reporter stated all other areas in this center staffed accordingly due to weather except the area he was assigned.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to ATL about 10 miles in trail of a B747-8. Flight crew stated they were pressured by ATC to fly a path they were not comfortable with.
UAV operator reported loss of control link and subsequent loss of UAV control. UAV was not recovered and assumed crashed in a forested area.
Helicopter pilot reported sighting a 50-foot crane to right of centerline of the approach to runway 20 at Tangier Island airport.
E175 flight crew reported windshear and severe turbulence after takeoff and exceeded the max flap extend speed.

During a busy air traffic period a Military Restricted Area was in use. There were numerous weather deviations and Air traffic Management did not attempt to get approval from the military authorities to let air traffic transit the Restricted Areas due to weather deviations. One of the reporters believes several aircraft entered the Restricted Areas deviating for weather.
Boeing 787 Captain reported concern about being unable to schedule a trip until several weeks after initial training.

B777 Captain reported responding to a LWR AFT CARGO fire warning by discharging extinguishing agent into the cargo bay while parked at the gate. Crew was advised the warning was triggered by Ground Personnel placing an insecticide spray bottle in the compartment.
Corporate pilot reported that the SuperAWOS at 5T6 airport consistently has outdated information. Pilots using the AWOS information are misled as to which direction to land.
Hot air balloon pilot reports landing balloon in neighborhood yard.
Air Carrier pilot reported at PWM there is a taxiway that is not indicated on their Jeppesen Chart. The taxiway is also not on the FAA Chart Airport diagram.
Flight Crew flying 747-400 encountered stick shaker during climb.
The Captain of a DHC-8-200 on approach took evasive action when he received a 'Climb' traffic advisory from TCAS.
Air carrier Captain reported the marshaller not adhering to SOP; wearing ear pods; tried to marshal into a gate riddled with obstructions.
A Widebody Transport First Officer reported that during a back-taxi on the takeoff runway it was not clear where to make the 180 turn.
B737-700 Captain reported experiencing physiological reactions to fumes associated with engine wash procedures.
Air Carrier Captain reported experiencing an unsafe push-back from the gate.
Pilot reported being on the incorrect frequency and thinking it was odd that no other aircraft were heard while landing.

A CRJ-200 crew rejected the takeoff after the left engine compressor section was damaged during takeoff when a taxi light lens was ingested. Maintenance had just replaced the light and installed the lens prior to the flight.
BJC Local Controller and an instructor pilot reported a near miss while on final. The offending aircraft lined up for the wrong runway which put it into conflict with the instructor's aircraft.
Instructor Pilot reported a NMAC on departure with another aircraft departing a different runway.
B737-NG Captain reported a NOTAM at MPTO affecting their takeoff runway calculations was confusing and easily misinterpreted.
Corporate flight crew reported misjudging clearance with a parked aircraft while taxiing into parking; resulting in contacting and damaging the wingtips of both aircraft.
Flight attendants reported 3 guests were unhappy with seating arrangements.

SAN Tower Controller describes a situation where the TRACON Controller forces a data block onto their position symbol and switches the aircraft to Tower's frequency without any coordination. SCT East Sector Controller violated the LOA between the two facilities.
An aircraft departing an airport was issued incorrect VFR departure instructions. The aircraft went into the Class B airspace of the adjacent airport.
A G550 Captain reports being cleared to maintain 210 knots to the FAF for the ILS to 19 at TEB. He asks to slow prior to the 1;500 foot FAF to allow for a stabilized approach and is ignored.
Captain reported his aircraft slid off the taxiway in icy conditions.

Center Controller describes a loss of separation with two aircraft he was working. Numerous aircraft in the vicinity with data block congestion. Controller had separation; but elected to climb traffic through a diverging aircraft; having less than 5 miles. Another Controller reports that the working Controller is dangerous and puts aircraft in unsafe situations and watches.
B777 First Officer reported that a policy change was made encouraging the pilots to complete dangerous autoland operations.
Dispatcher reported dispatch program crashed several times during shift which did not allow him to safely do his job.
F11 TRACON Controller reported being busy; asking for help; and being laughed at three times by Front Line Managers. The Controller needed the sector to be split.

Pilot reports of a VIP passenger who refuses to sit in a seat and remains lying in the bed. Captain and assistant discuss matter. Pilot reluctantly decides to let passenger remain laying down to avoid a company problem.
B757 Mechanic reported that an aircraft tow crew was unware that external power was not to be activated.
Air carrier First Officer reported requesting a warm up simulation prior to a re-qualification simulation and was denied.
Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a near mid air collision.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with wearing a face mask during flight and exhibited other non-compliant behavior.
Air carrier ramp agent expressed concern about wearing headsets that might be contaminated with coronavirus.
ZOB ARTCC Controller reported problems associated with weather and lack of Traffic Management plans.
A300 Captain reported they failed to document a GPS jamming event in the Aircraft Maintenance Log.
A319 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence at FL340 that led to speed deviations and an altitude excursion of more than 2000 ft.
Lead Flight Attendant reported the red tag was not put in place after disarming the door.
ZOB Center Controller reported that an aircraft was unable to hold due to low fuel and that the aircraft did not have an alternate.
EMB-145 First Officer reported fuel truck hit the aircraft's right wing.
HCF Controller reports of departure procedures off of Lihue Airport that need to be corrected. Aircraft are departing and flying into low terrain areas.
Flight Instructor flying a PA-16 aircraft reports ground loop on landing rollout.
Air carrier pilot reported experiencing a ground conflict and cited reduced level of awareness due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

Flight Attendant reported that new cabin services procedures requires all flight attendants in the forward of the main cabin. Therefore; the aft cabin is both inaccessible and out of their control; which poses a security/ safety issue.
SR22 pilot reported being hit by lightning strike during descent resulting in a diversion.
ERJ-175 Captain reported the aircraft was rocked on the takeoff roll by jet blast from a taxiing B787.
Two A11 TRACON controllers reported a communication breakdown with the nearby MRI Tower Controller during the coordination of a military airport arrival.
Pilot reported due to light weight and high climb rate the aircraft received a Resolution Advisory.

Southern California TRACON (SCT) and MYF Controllers report of a loss of separation between two aircraft; in which the SCT Controller should have issued traffic. An assumption that another SCT controller was watching the reporting Controller's traffic was wrong. The Tower Controller had other priorities and didn't notice the loss until it had occurred.
B757 Technician reported that the MEL stated to 'Rotate a valve 90 degrees CLOCKWISE' to open and the  Illustration indicates that the vane should be turned 90 degrees COUNTERCLOCKWISE.
Flight Instructor reported missing the log book entry for an inoperative oil temperature gauge that was required equipment for night VFR training flight and conducting the flight.

After being cleared to 11000 feet; an EMB145 First Officer is instructed to level at 7;000 feet for traffic passing 6500 feet. As the aircraft leveled at 7;000 feet on autopilot the TCAS called for a descent. The autopilot was disengaged and a descent initiated. The intruder was sighted and passed 500 feet overhead as the reporter's aircraft descended through 6;500 feet.
B737 flight crew reported encountering wind shear; resulting in a diversion.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track deviation on the mid-approach segment due to a high workload environment coupled with the First Officer's extended period of time off.
Flight Attendant reported initially forgetting to move the door flag to the disarmed position due distraction from putting a mask on to avoid COVID-19 exposure.
B737 Captain reported receiving ATC clearance via CPDLC.

Large Transport Twin Turbojet flight crew on the SERFR1 RNAV Arrival to SFO; was given the 'East Transition' by ATC and mistakenly flew the 'West Transition' because it is on the east side of the airport.
B767-300 flight crew reported a discrepancy between a data-link communication altitude and the altitude ATC required.
Cessna 402 pilot reported the upper clamshell door half opened after takeoff.
Lansing TRACON Controller In Charge (CIC) reported an airspace violation due to an airspace relating to new Metroplex airspace.
B737-800 Captain reported receiving inaccurate weather reports from MROC airport on several different occasions.

Fairbanks Controller reported having a flight of aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude and waiting until it went into an area of higher terrain. Reporter also stated the flight additionally descended below another MVA and reported doing so.
A Center Controller reported they issued an aircraft a direct routing and did not realize the route was below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
ILM TRACON Controller reported descending an aircraft too early and having it enter a lower Minimum Vectoring Altitude area.
Maintenance technician reported high power engine operations in poor weather conditions led to a loss of control and contact with another aircraft.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a near collision while taxiing at DFW.
EMB 505 Captain reported that during initial approach; they received a GPWS VOICE ACTIVATION of Obstacle; they initiated a climb and informed ATC.

Air Carrier flight crew reported that ATC cleared them for a visual approach from a base-leg vector to intercept the localizer at DFW; while maintenance was being performed on that ILS. No notification of the maintenance status was received via NOTAM; ATIS or radio transmission from ATC.
Ground Agent reported notification of aircraft ground gate return to remove unauthorized oxygen canister.
B737-800 pilot reported that they had to stop the taxi to avoid a collision with another aircraft being pushed back from an adjacent gate.
A320 Captain reported descending out of assigned altitude during final approach; requiring evasive action.
Flight attendants reported issues with crew members not complying with face mask policy.
HCF TRACON Controller reported that an aircraft flew through the final approach course and into a higher MVA.
EMB-145 flight crew reported receiving a terrain warning from the aircraft while in VFR conditions.
GA pilot reported being cleared for takeoff with another plane on short final.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported conflicting NOTAMs referencing a runway with restrictions and ATC issuing an alert after the crew was in position ready for takeoff.
CE680 Captain reported a NMAC while on downwind for the active runway.
A UAS pilot operated their drone on a different day than what was approved by the FAA.
Jumpseater seated in rear reported aircraft flew through a wildfire smoke column for several minutes.
MD-11 Captain reported encountering jet blast from an aircraft taking off on the runway they were landing on and they felt the spacing was too tight for safety.
MD-80 Captain reported both air conditioning packs failed inflight at FL230; perhaps due to icing of the inlet doors. Packs resumed normal operation after exiting icing conditions.
Pilot reported misidentifying landing airport due to MVFR weather and sun glare.
A BTV TRACON Controller reported observing an aircraft descend below its assigned altitude and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A319 Flight Crew reported excursion from assigned altitude after departure.
B767-300 Captain reported experiencing airframe buffet probably related to ice accumulation on approach to IND.
B737 Captain reported a communications breakdown between flight crew and several Operations departments following a fuel spill.
B767-300 Captain reported being assigned to accomplish a Maintenance Ferry Flight for the purpose of retirement with open items in the logbook.

A flight crew executed a wind shear warning go around at ISP and because of strong winds diverted to nearby airport; but declared minimum fuel because alternate fuel planning in the northeast corridor is not realistic considering traffic and ATC workloads.
EMB 170 flight crew reported encountering unreported severe turbulence.
Air carrier First Officer reported inconsistent use of masks per guidelines by crewmembers.
B757 Captain expressed concern with ATC procedures during approach to SFO that limited the options for runway separation during landing or a safe go-around should one become necessary.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported assisting a flight in avoiding an area of volcanic ash. Flight diverted to an alternate airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported declining an all-nighter flight due to fatigue from an emergency on the previous leg which involved a lengthy procedure and delay.
An aircrew observed 15 or more laser illumination events immediately after takeoff from SCEL (Santiago; Chile).  They reported their observations to the Tower who responded 'Roger'.
Corporate pilot reported deicing truck did not comply with the agreed upon deicing procedure and began before the crew was ready while the APU was running.
A320 Captain reported a break away from the tow bar during push back; requiring the aircraft to be stopped abruptly when the tug driver called for the brakes to be set; with the aircraft in motion.
LR60 Captain reported a severe updraft caused the aircraft to climb 1;600 ft before crew could regain control.

When the B737-800 engine and APU bleeds were opened after de-icing; the cockpit filled with a thick white mist so the aircraft was towed back to the gate and deplaned. The pilots became ill and were treated according to the MSDS guidelines then departed after recovering from de-ice fluid ingestion.
An Inspector working on an Airbus reported that the MLG door bushings had migrated out of the lug and engineering was not contacted as required per the job card.
ZFW Center Controller reported about an aircraft altitude at transfer that was below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B767 Captain reported misgivings when contract maintenance needed extraordinary assistance from maintenance control to complete tasks required to prepare the aircraft for departure.
Air carrier flight crew reported setting up for the incorrect runway during approach.

JCF FLM and Controller reported of a fast climbing aircraft that was out of 10;000 feet within 1-2 miles of the end of the runway. The departure was traffic for an enroute aircraft and traffic was not issued. The Departure Controller never received a rolling call. A loss of separation did occur.
A320 First Officer reported multiple bird (bat) strikes on final approach that resulted in damage to the aircraft.
Air Carrier Captain reported false reading from the ILS on final approach to LGA airport.

Pilot reports being instructed by Ground Control to taxi to a specific runway and in doing so was given crossing instructions for two runways. Pilot was told to hold short of the last runway while non-flying pilot checked with Tower due to seeing traffic on the runway. Pilot then gets on locals frequency and is told they weren't given crossing instructions. Aircraft was over the hold short line by 3 feet while departing 1R traffic passed and became airborne.
Aircraft Technician reported hydraulic tubing not secured with safety wire on a B717 aircraft.

Flight instructor reported that while on the downwind leg of the traffic pattern; another aircraft flew directly below the instructor's aircraft; performed a 360-degree turn and joined the pattern. That aircraft then proceeded to land on the runway while the instructor was on the landing roll.
G650 Captain reported difficulty stopping within runway due to poor braking action.
A VFR General Aviation pilot returning from a sight seeing flight reported ATC did not provide the proper level of service.
B737 Captain reported being vectored inside of the FAF and at too high an altitude for a Visual Approach.

NCT controller reported issuing climb out instructions to an aircraft in accordance with JO7110.65; 5-6-3; instead of facility application of DVA. Controller reported confusion between the two applications.
HIO Tower Controller and pilot reported of a Class Delta airspace violation and a NMAC.
An Air Traffic Controller and Trainee reported two aircraft converging at the same altitude that required late action to avoid a loss of required separation.
C-150 Private Pilot reported runway excursion on landing resulted in runway light and aircraft damage.
Reporter described AD overflights due to errors and incorrect data entry into a commercial maintenance tracking program.
Gate Agent reported computers at this company's terminal need a deep cleaning for employee safety. The only cleaning items currently available are wipes.
Flight Attendant reported being injured during turbulence.
Air carrier Captain reported questioning the wisdom of using BFF as an alternate with no tower; no gates; one frequency for CTAF and UNICOM; and no ACARS reception.
Air Taxi Captain reported flying an RNAV Approach which was not authorized at night.
B787 Flight Attendant reported that the aircraft ventilation system is being neglected causing dirty cabin air.
Pilot reported not rechecking main rotor components during preflight for a maintenance check flight after completion of maintenance.
First Officer reported impending conflict due to ATC clearance issued in error; which was attributed to similar call signs and congested airspace.
Technician reported lack of communication during a thrust reverser operational check resulted in damage to the engine thrust reverser.
Large Transport aircraft Captain reported that while conducting a Runway 30 visual approach to MSO per company procedures; the approach path brought the aircraft uncomfortably close to terrain.
B737 First Officer reported there was only one Controller working all positions at night during heavy traffic at SFO.
Air carrier pilot stated he felt his company's refusal to board crew meals on international turn flights poses a safety risk.
Air carrier Flight Crew reported they received a GPWS alert even though they were descending to an appropriate assigned altitude.
B737 Captain reported that flight attendants do not have knowledge of the SOP to gain access to the flight deck using the keypad.
Dispatcher reported operations delayed flight without proper notification.
Captain reported aircraft turned towards wrong runway due to multiple localizers operational simultaniously.

A319 Captain reported when the autothrust was turned OFF on final; number one engine rolled back to idle. One ENGINE ELECTRONIC CONTROL Computer had been MELed; but he was concerned not properly disconnected. The aircraft was removed from service.

Two Asheville Tower Controllers reported of problems with ground vehicles entering the ILS Critical Area when traffic is on final. Controllers contacted proper authorities; but vehicles continued to enter the area.

CL604 Captain experiences severe turbulence over ALS at FL390 resulting in a 1;000 FPM climb at idle thrust and partial spoilers. Flight eventually descends to FL350 with ATC's approval; but the Captain cannot read the altimeter during the initial descent due to the turbulence. The crew elects to divert to inspect the aircraft for damage before continuing to destination.
B737 Captain reported performance correction for MEL no longer exist in Performance Handbook.
An Air Carrier First Officer leveled off following GPWS 'PULL UP' and 'TERRAIN' warnings while turning final on a visual approach to AVL Runway 34.
Air carrier First Officer reported there is a discrepancy on the missed approach instructions per the Jeppesen chart versus the Company 10-7 chart.
Pilots reported a hung start due to a tailwind on the ground.

JFK Controller reported being asked by a pilot which runway another aircraft was going to land. The pilot stated the runway as assigned but had cut through the other aircraft's path and looked as though it was going to land on the same runway. Controller insured visual separation was being applied and had preceding aircraft go to the correct runway. Both aircraft landed their assigned runways.
B777 Captain reported that; along with other crew members; iPad was missing QRH section.
After multiple ATC ordered go-arounds; no suitable alternate options; and a low fuel state; flight crew was cleared to land on a technically closed runway.

A321 Captain experienced an airborne conflict during the FMS Bridge Visual to SFO with a C206 which NCT had cleared onto the approach path on a photo mission. The reporter took evasive action and the C206 was instructed to turn south by NCT.
Air carrier flight crew reported a passenger who had tested positive for COVID-19 was on the flight.
MD-11 First Officer and Maintenance Technician reported the FMC was loaded with the wrong type of aircraft data. This mistake was not noticed until the aircraft was already in flight.

A pilot reported about runway changes and confusion flying into IAH. Various runway changes lead to confusion and an altitude deviation. The pilot was told to maintain 6;000 feet on the approach but descended to 5;000 feet for a crossing restriction. The rest of the approach and landing were uneventful.

RV-7 pilot reported maneuvering over a congested area at 1;200 ft and later considered he may have violated FAR 91.303 with 'intentional maneuver involving an abrupt change in an aircraft's attitude' during the flight.
An air carrier crew landed on ABQ Runway 8.  They mistakenly exited the runway at closed Taxiway E5 due to poor signage indicating closure and lack of signage indicating the location of E6.

General aviation pilot reported being directed to fly counter to an approach with a published restriction to circling west of the airport. Coordination between TRACON and Tower was an issue. Controller working the aircraft also reported and stated he advised aircraft to circle 'north' of Runway.
PHL TRACON Controller reported mistakenly handling an IFR aircraft as if it were VFR; leading to an MVA violation.
Air carrier Captain reported they encountered birds just after takeoff. One bird is believed to have struck the engine resulting in some blade damage.
PA-28A Single Pilot reported a runway excursion on landing rollout due to a nosewheel first landing.
Small transport Captain reported ATC issued an unrestricted VFR climb which penetrated Class B airspace.
HCF TRACON Controller reported of two different times that numerous aircraft crossed a fix at different altitudes then the SOP states.
Technician reported allowing aircraft to fly with parts missing on cargo door.

A CRP Controller; while instructing; has an aircraft that is supposed to fly heading 350 and join V13. The developmental sees the aircraft not tracking correctly and instructs the aircraft to fly heading 350. The aircraft later enters a MOA because it didn't join V13. The Controller didn't realize at the time that the developmental should have reiterated the heading and also to join the airway.
B767 Captain reported a lack of consensus in an MEL change.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported they had to return to the airport because the left main gear pin was not removed after Maintenance was performed the previous night.
Air carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation during arrival and cited communication issues with ATC as a contributing factor.

After complying with multiple ATC speed and heading clearance modifications during two visual approaches to EWR; air carrier crew performed two Tower-directed go-around procedures. The third visual approach; to a different runway; was successful.
Citation Excel Captain reported he has found several errors in the quick reference emergency/abnormal checklists and the company has been slow to correct the situation.
DVT Tower Controller reported increased workload and complexity due to staffing level.

CHS Tower Controller reported of a problem with traffic into the airport. The pattern traffic was working until the reporter was given a point out on a Lifeguard aircraft which caused the pattern to be disrupted and caused a wake turbulence problem.
D01 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below the MVA after not reading back the assigned altitude.
Cessna pilot reported a deer strike that resulted in an aborted takeoff and damage to the aircraft.
Corporate jet Captain reported a close encounter with a quadcopter UAS on approach to MKC.
Airbus Captain reported a near miss with a UAV while on final approach to SAN.
CRJ-700 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence on a flight from an airport in Mexico to ORD.
B737NG Captain reported that the aircraft has an operational ceiling of 41;000 ft; but the crew O2 mask is only certified to 40;000 ft.
Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft was very light weight. The pilots were unprepared for the quick low altitude level off and deviated from the clearance.

During final approach to SFO in weather; the glideslope became intermittent. Flight crew executed a go-around and landed on the second attempt. Other crews commented about the quality of the ILS signal.
BTV TRACON Controller reported issuing a climb to an aircraft; the aircraft didn't climb as well as the Controller thought it would and ended up in a lower MVA.

An aircraft was issued a clearance to cross a fix at 14000 feet to avoid parachute jumping activity along its route at 13000 feet. The pilot readback 12000 feet. The Controller did not detect the erroneous readback.
Pilot reported taking evasive action on takeoff to avoid a midair collision with an aircraft not following controller's instructions.
C172 flight instructor reported endorsing two students for solo flights without proper documentation due to confusion over FAR Part 61.85.
A330 Captain reported refusing a flight due to safety issues related to an MEL and ETOPS noncompliance.
A319 flight crew reported the aft cargo door opened and they returned to departure airport.
Captain reported refusing aircraft after multiple attempts to troubleshoot and repair a recurring engine fuel leak were unsuccessful and procedures were not adhered to.
A319 Maintenance Technician reported that an incorrect part was installed causing a bleed air monitoring computer fault.
Flight Attendant reported that wake turbulence during approach caused injury to herself and two passengers.
Pilot reported being unsure if he had a release to get airborne on a flight.  Fatigue from COVID-19 restrictions was cited as a contributing factor.
DFW Local Controller reported clearing an aircraft for takeoff with an arrival landing on the converging non intersecting runway.
C172 pilot reported having difficulty maintaining control of his aircraft on short final when he encountered wake turbulence from a military helicopter operating near the end of the runway.
First Officer reported late runway change during approach to LAX. Crew was able to reprogram FMC and complete the approach to the newly assigned runway.
B737-700 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence at FL400.
GSO Controller reports of a loss of separation with an arrival and a departure aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported a problem with the alignment of the PAPI lights at LEX airport.
B737 flight crew described the extra workload and opportunities for errors when they received multiple departure runway assignment changes on taxi out at DEN.
B737 Captain reported ambiguous ramp and taxiway markings and the use of different frequencies in a hot spot area resulted in a critical ground conflict.

King Air 90 pilot reported deviating without clearance for a thunderstorm that suddenly appeared on the nose during transition from IMC to VMC. XM weather and the aircraft radar did not show the buildup.

ZOB Controller instructing describes a situation where the developmental is working a combined sector. The developmental is certified on one of the combined sectors where all the problems occur. The developmental has aircraft head-on at the same altitude; then refuses to use positive control. The instructor fixes the problems so there were no losses of separation.
Air carrier First Officer reported erroneous clearance provided to flight crew by Dispatch and ATC was corrected by Oceanic ATC sector.
C150 Pilot reported a critical ground conflict as Stearman overflew the C150 on takeoff roll.

An air carrier Captain flying the DCA LDA Z Runway 19 on IOE; apparently descended below the WEVPU fix altitude and momentarily responded to the EGPWS 'OBSTACLE' alert before becoming VMC and landing.
A321 Flight Attendant reported inadvertent slide deployment during deplaning.
A Light Transport pilot reported ATC used non-standard phraseology which led to the crew executing a right 360 degree turn instead of turning to a heading of 360.
Airline office employee reported scanners were moved into their small room creating social distancing concerns as multiple people walk in their area to pick up the scanners.
A320 flight crew reported they received a GPWS terrain warning on a night visual approach to SFO Runway 19L.
B737-700 flight crew reported they lost the flight directors and autopilot following a static discharge.
Technician reported a third party maintenance contractor failed to make correct repairs on an A319; resulting in a recurring airframe vibration in flight.
B757 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B757 on arrival into LAX.

Air taxi pilot reported experiencing whiteout conditions while VFR on approach to TRK. A passenger in the right seat panicked; distracting the pilot which lead to a stall and recovery during the IMC missed approach.
B767 flight crew reported that during a Tower directed go-around; they received multiple EICAS messages; and shortly after that; they got a GPWS alert.

Pilot reports of flying below the Class Bravo while in a descent into SFO on the SERFR1 STAR. Pilot advises there may be something wrong with the arrival that takes the aircraft out of the Class Bravo which is built to protect the aircraft.
Small aircraft pilot reported hitting a deer on takeoff at N79.
B737-800 flight crew reported experiencing a tail strike on takeoff in gusty winds at TPA.
ZBW Center Controller reported an aircraft that needed to deviate for weather and immediate descent for turbulence.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation due to the aircraft going into vertical speed mode for the descent after deviating for weather.
Flight Attendant reported a commuting Captain was non compliant with face mask policy.

Ground employee reported being told to return to work after completing a 14-day quarantine. Reporter stated that they had not been previously told to self-quarantine nor had they been notified they had been exposed to a co-worker who tested positive for COVID-19.
Embraer First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence while on descent into ORD.

An aircraft heard a garbled transmission saying 'cleared for takeoff.' The pilot assumed it was for their flight. The takeoff clearance was for a different aircraft. The adjacent airport Control Tower also controls this airport and was transmitting and receiving on two frequencies.
A321 Captain reported difficulty contacting Company via ACARS or phone while on the ground.

A flight crew with an injured passenger on board requested EMT assistance upon arrival. However; the aircraft was parked at a non-functional jetway with no EMTs and was towed to another gate. Inattentive ground operations puts people at risk.

An air carrier Captain on JFK Runway 31R final at about 1;000 feet reported two UAS aircraft hovering about 200-300 feet away from them and left of the final approach course. Evasive action was not required.
CRJ-900 Captain reported Ground Proximity Warning System alert on descent.
SR22 pilot aircraft reported that during climb he saw a high winged Cessna closing up into his flight path from below. His TCAS or ATC did not give him warning.
B737 Captain reported the ship set bag restricts the cockpit seat to less than full aft travel; possibly inhibiting rapid egress.
A320 Captain reported that he refused to accept the aircraft due to multiple AC Generator and MEL problems.
SBN TRACON Controller reported malfunctioning shout line; as well as problems with old equipment and poor logistics.
Air carrier Captain reported they did not completely exit the runway at ELP as instructed causing the Tower to have to send a subsequent arrival around.
A Dispatcher reported flight releases were delayed due to being scheduled as soon as shift started and without a proper briefing.
Air Carrier flight instructor reported the 'no touch exemption' as written for O2 masks; headsets; etc.; does not cover all types of training and checking periods.
Pilot reported a go around after tower cleared an aircraft for takeoff with reporting pilot on short final for a touch and go.

A ramp agent reported that while driving a second jet bridge to an aircraft's door 2; another ramp agent commanded a stop because the jet bridge was too close to the running engine 1; which may have ingested an air conditioning hose. Only a camera fed monitor is viewable by the jet bridge operator to check travel.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into LAX in trail of a B777. He recommended that separation between light and heavy aircraft inbound to LAX be increased.

An instructor and student flying VFR in a non-IFR equipped aircraft asked ATC for vectors to the RNAV GPS approach for Runway 17R at COS and reported entering unexpected IMC in turbulence which made aircraft control difficult. ATC would not issue a clearance out of IMC so the instructor descended to VMC at 500 feet AGL.

Air carrier Captain reported that after the flight the Flight Attendant stated that a passenger had been non-compliant with mask policy during the flight and that other passengers had been complaining.
B767 flight crew reported they received a low altitude alert from ATL Tower while on the Runway 8L PAPI glidepath.
Aircraft engine and APU halon fire bottles received from the vendor have been found with squibs not properly protected for shipping and handling.
First Officer reported flight missed an altitude level off because they were a little rusty.
Flight Attendant reported being ordered to continue working the flight after being vomited on by a passenger during boarding.

Instructor pilot reported departing PUB for the training area west of the airport when ATC reported an aircraft orbiting north of their path. As they climbed the other aircraft entered above the airport crosswind leg causing a very close encounter. ATC apparently was not aware.
D10 TRACON Controller reported working two busy combined sectors and allowed in trail spacing between two to decrease to less than required spacing.

Air carrier flight crew reported that while departing LGA via the LA GUARDIA FIVE SID; the flight continued on a heading and did not intercept the charted VOR radial. The crew cited fatigue; lack of familiarity with the departure airport and confusing SID procedures that must be manually input into the FMS; as contributing factors.
G-V Captain reported Pitot Static System anomalies due to high altitude icing.
A drone operator taking photos reported they allowed their drone to fly over a moving vehicle.
A General Aviation pilot landing at a non towered airport reported a Flight Check aircraft which the pilot was aware of had to take evasive action to avoid them on short final.
E175 First Officer reported after engine shutdown; a loud banging noise was heard coming from the cargo pit area.
B737NG Captain reported a track deviation occurred on the JAGGR3 arrival into DEN. Reporter stated a late clearance change in a time-limited situation led to an FMC programming error.
B737 First Officer reported receiving conflicting clearances from ATC; which resulted in confliction with other departing traffic.
EMB505 Captain reported receiving excessive delays after refusing ATC clearance to maintain 250 knots below Class B Airspace.

Ground worker reported not being informed by management that a co-worker tested positive for COVID-19. The ground worker had worked with the co-worker and then visited family; resulting in possible exposure.
A321 flight crew reported severe wind shear during approach resulted in a diversion.
GA pilot reported flying the incorrect pattern after the Tower closed.
B737 Domestic First Officer reported an improper reassignment to an international flight between previously assigned domestic trips.

Flight crew experienced fumes in the cockpit during climbout followed by an equipment cooling alert. Crew elected to return to the departure airport. Landing was uneventful followed by taxi to the gate.
B737-900 flight crew reported executing a go-around after receiving a GPWS warning 'Too low; flaps' on approach.
B737 pilot reported their aircraft was unable to meet altitude and speed restrictions published on the CLT BANKR2 STAR.

PCT Controller conducting training did not realize their Trainee did not have the radar display setup properly to observe a possible conflicting route from an adjacent sector. The Trainee climbed a departure into the path of another departure on the conflicting route.
Captain reported receiving instructions from ATC to climb via a SID while on the UDGUV1A departure from MMUN; for which the chart did not have published altitudes.
Two PBI TRACON Controllers reported receiving aircraft handoffs from another sector that were already in conflict.
Air carrier flight crew reported departing with an incorrect pack configuration.
Corporate pilot reported not being current for a night flight.

Small aircraft pilot reported multiple sets of power lines near BDR are set with wire markers placed on one of the wires. That wire and its markers are actually at a lower elevation than the other unmarked wires nearby.
CE-680 flight crew landing at TEB reported the Tower canceled a takeoff clearance for an aircraft on an intersecting runway when the crew failed to make an earlier turnoff.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported that their aircraft started rolling forward at the gate when ramp personnel removed the chocks without informing the flight crew.
A-24A Pilot Flying reported an NMAC event with a skydiver.
ZMP Center Controller reported an adjacent Center controller handed off two aircraft that were converging inside their airspace.
An air carrier Captain reported pets being transported by the carrier are routinely mistreated by ground personnel.
Lead Technician reported ground damage that was misidentified by Ramp and CSM personnel was severe enough to remove the aircraft from service for repairs.
Flight crew of large turbojet aircraft reported an issue with an ATC clearance to meet an altitude restriction.
A Center Controller reported working two sectors combined without a Radar Assist did not notice an aircraft deviating for weather and enter another Controller's sector without coordination.
B757 Captain reported concern regarding company policy regarding cell phones which are lost/left on aircraft and not recovered before the aircraft is dispatched for subsequent flights.
A320 Captain reported that inoperative APU that was being deferred over a period of 4 days caused elderly passengers to suffer in 86 degree heat for 30 minutes; causing them extreme discomfort.

A CRJ-900 air crew; after starting one engine and shutting down the APU observed an APU SOV OPEN status message. The message remained on after starting the second engine but they elected to continue and flew one flight. At their destination the Captain troubleshot the aircraft and was convinced that the APU PWR/Fuel switch was inoperative. Maintenance disagreed. The crew swapped into another aircraft for the flight.
Captain reported that on multiple occasions; the aircraft was not properly de-iced prior to departure.

A320 Captain reported that the ground crew used a baggage cart to push out the aircraft instead of a proper tug. He expressed concern about using this equipment and the lack of training for ground crews throughout the system.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported Load Planner pressured Ramp Agent to falsify Hazmat departure documents to reflect initial Hazmat loading configuration error had been rectified.
DAB TRACON Controller reported new radar identification rules delayed the ability to identify an aircraft for service as the aircraft flew more than 3 miles offshore.

B767 Captain reported due to company policy; the Flight Attendant staffing has decreased resulting in the forward portion of the cabin understaffed during cruise flight. The Captain states this is a security issue for the flight deck and forward passenger cabin.
BFI Tower controller reported a Local Control Trainee sent an aircraft around and issued instructions below the MVA.
Beech 24 pilot reported issues with ATC instructions for a VFR entry into SFB.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing a track heading deviation while on the ROLLN2 arrival to CLT. Captain stated similar sounding fix names BETYL and DEVYL contributed to the event.
B737-800 Captain expressed concern that ATC often delayed runway and arrival assignment information to flight crews; increasing workload in the terminal area.
ZMA Controllers report of landline problems with Nassau Approach that are intermittent and unreliable. Reports includes information of an airspace violation attributed to the outage.
A Controller issued a frequency change to an adjacent sector for an aircraft. The Controller then realized they had not handed off the data block and target of the aircraft.

CPR Local Controller reported the Approach Controller was being handed off an aircraft from the Center indicating 100 feet below the MVA. He ordered the Approach Controller to not take the handoff until the aircraft was in a lower MVA.
Captain reported problems with JeppPro software on the electronic flight bag.
ZAU Center Controller reported a loss of separation when an aircraft was unable to comply with their issued crossing restriction.
A Controller and flight crew reported the pilot flew the SID incorrectly causing conflictions with other aircraft.
ZTL Center Controller reported the new arrival procedures into ATL are causing confusion for pilots and controllers.

First Officer reported that they flew the departure via radar vectors to a fix. ATC questioned them why they were not flying the SID. Controller then gave them a turn to join the SID. First Officer took the word of the pilot in command.
Air carrier Captain reported a flap misconfiguration leading to an unstablized approach and go-around.

Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non compliant with face mask policy during flight.  Reporter stated the passenger said he had a medical condition; but it was not noted in the crew's records.

M98 controller reports of receiving a briefing that the relieving Controller said there was only one aircraft on the frequency. Controller observed another aircraft in the airspace; and Controller assumed it had been handed off and communications transferred. The aircraft was on a different tag so this also led the Controller to think it had been handed off. The aircraft was found to be on the reporters frequency and ended up violating the Center's airspace without a handoff. Possible related issue was someone possibly had changed the data tag; and this showed the Controller that it was someone else's aircraft.
Ramp agent reported the cabin door was closed while fueling was in progress.

Technician reported requesting to know the company policy that governs the safe removal and disposal of HEPA filters and used PPE.  Technician is also unsure of what qualifications are needed to perform those tasks.

JFK Ground Controller reported a taxiway incursion and another taxiing aircraft's wing went over the aircraft that was supposed to be holding short. Pilot attempted to advise Ground Control that they were not where they were supposed to be.
MD-11 flight crew reported encountered severe turbulence causing the airspeed to fluctuate below the desired airspeed.
A320 Captain reported that aircraft are being dispatched with less than an adequate amount of crew oxygen.
DA-7X First Officer reported GPS/IRS failure on approach and ATC reporting happening numerous times.
Technician reported that the company requested a rewrite of his safety report.
B767 IOE Captain reported being summoned to additional screening during crew briefing.
Air carrier pilot questioned why DFW operated all day with a 5-8 knot tailwind against the stated interests of safety and economics.
Helicopter Pilot reported that multiple times aircraft were flown in known icing conditions even though the aircraft were not equipped for icing.
EMB-145 Captain reported a rejected takeoff for a cargo door warning light.
Air carrier Captain reported being unable to see out cockpit window at night during taxi into gate due to ground equipment headlights shining into cockpit.
Maintenance reported aircraft engines were over serviced with oil.
Flight Attendant reported the Captain did not wear a face mask throughout their flight pairing.
A B737 Captain reported a ramp employee walked too close to their running engine during taxi-in. The Captain feels a new company policy of delaying APU start until just prior to gate was a factor.
Pilot reported that the Runway 16 PAPI lights at MYL were not on and there was no NOTAM regarding the outage.

Helicopter pilot reported a communication issue with ATC resulted in a landing without a clearance.  Pilot stated the Tower Controller was working multiple frequencies; which may have contributed to the event.

A flight crew flying a river visual approach in light rain received a 'Caution Obstacle' warning from their EGPWS. They corrected by turning away from the buildings they had in sight. Then ATC issued a low altitude alert. The First Officer corrected and the alerts stopped.
Embraer Legacy pilot reported a traffic conflict on approach to SDL which resulted in an RA. They took evasive action and notified ATC.
A319 Captain reported they diverted because of weather at destination and realized later it was not an authorized airport for his fleet.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving a revised CPDLC than entered it manually rather than by selecting the load prompt.
Flight crew reported ATC advised them of a possible low altitude alert even though they complied with the assigned crossing restriction in their Visual Approach clearance.
S46 Controller reported of a loss of separation due to a distraction and the pilot of the aircraft not flying the correct heading.
B757 Captain reported refusing a flight assignment because of fatigue.
Single pilot reported loss of directional control on landing resulting in a runway excursion and collision with perimeter fence.
B737 flight crew reported uncommanded aircraft movement occurred following pushback without brakes set.
Captain reported that a third-party maintenance vendor had not been properly testing completed work.

BNA Controller observed the Tower Supervisor order the Local Controller to instruct an aircraft to line up and wait even though the legal requirements for this procedure were not in place. The Supervisor was issuing orders to all the Controller positions which he felt was unnecessary and increased the complexity of their operation.

A pilot of a regional jet reported executed a missed approach due to the inability to obtain a landing clearance in a timely fashion. A subsequent approach was successfully completed; however a flap limit speed was briefly exceeded.

Ground Agent reported current level of lithium ion battery powered smart bags continue to be accepted as checked bags with batteries installed in violation of Hazmat company procedures and DOT regulations.
B787 Captain reported discrepancy between FAA InFO 17021 and B787 Fire Containment instructions available to pilots.
UAV operator reported inadvertently flying in Class D airspace after misinterpreting charts provided in a commercial 'App.'
General aviation pilot was flying into a soon to be new airport. The pilot saw a legally operated UAS and noted it may become a safety concern after the airport opens.
ERJ First Officer reported that a bird strike on takeoff resulted in a return to the departure airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported taking a clearance intended for a different aircraft with a similar sounding callsign.

ZOA Controller reported an air carrier on arrival responded to an RA of a VFR target not in communication with ATC. The Center Controller did not notice that the VFR target was a factor for the arrival.

Pilot reports not being able to contact ZME on frequency; had another pilot relay his cancellation of IFR and landed at intended airport. Pilot departed airport and along with another aircraft could not contact ZME on assigned frequency until 13 miles west of departure airport.

An aircraft at FL470 avoiding weather with no traffic was told to descend to FL450 for proper direction of flight. The pilots argued to remain at FL470 in smooth air; but the request was denied. In turbulence; at FL450; the crew was allowed to return to FL470.
C182 pilot reported entering a small cell not depicted on the NexRad image displayed on the ADS-B equipment. An uncontrolled ascent of 500 feet occurred and ATC was advised.

Boeing 737 flight crew reported that several company managers came on board the aircraft and directed operations while flight attendants and crew were trying to accomplish required preflight safety checks.
Pilot reported a NMAC while in a traffic pattern with another aircraft that was wanting to land.
GA pilot experienced a runway excursion while performing a rejected takeoff. Pilot reported confusion between the pilot and Tower Controller about whether the take-off clearance had been canceled.
P50 Controller reports of ZAB over delivering aircraft and questions if TBFM [Time Based Flow Management] tool is set correctly.
A321 Captain reported that while on visual approach to landing; TA followed by RA caused NMAC; verified only by information on PFD.
Air carrier flight crew reported a hard landing after the aircraft did not flare during a CAT 3 approach.

EUG TRACON Controller reported that after being unable to communicate with an aircraft; was advised that the transmitter/receiver site routinely fails and it is a known problem; but nothing has been done to fix it.
Air Carrier Captain reported confusion and conflictions from Ground Controller's clearance to gate.
Pilot reports of being under tow and crossing a runway. Aircraft on final is not sent around; but overflies the aircraft under tow and lands down further on the runway.
Air carrier Captain and Dispatcher reported that the company elected to not notify the crew of a seismic event in the area of the destination airport.
Flight Attendant reported failing to conduct a required briefing after many distractions during deplaning/boarding including cleaners entering while passengers were deplaning.

Minneapolis Tower Controller reported that an aircraft was cleared for takeoff when an aircraft was outside the Arrival Departure Window (ADW). Departing aircraft delayed and arrival entered the ADW and was not sent around.
ERJ145 Captain reported the parked aircraft began rolling on the ramp and the First Officer put the brakes on and the parking brake causing an abrupt stop injuring a Flight Attendant.

B767-300 flight crew reports being vectored into a thunderstorm during approach after requesting that the storm be avoided. Previous aircraft had reported severe turbulence and this crew reported stick shaker and flap overspeed along with bank angles to 40 degrees before exiting.
SAT Tower Controller reported that a Supervisor cleared an aircraft for takeoff while another aircraft was crossing the runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation during arrival. Captain stated rustiness due to lack of flying was a contributing factor.
C340 Captain reported an airborne conflict during departure climb-out.
PA28 pilots reported that during landing rollout; a deer walked in front of the aircraft and was struck by the propeller.
Helicopter pilot reported an inaccurate METAR prevented continuing to the airport VFR when the weather was IFR and getting worse.

Air carrier Captain reported they were on the DSERT2 Arrival to SDL then cleared for a visual approach. Reporter stated ATC then asked them to fly '...east of Pinnacle Peak...' which put them over an area of high terrain.
Load Planner reported aircraft departed with blocked rows being occupied by passengers; contrary to the weight and balance report.
Cessna pilot reported flying in marginal VFR weather at night; and returning to departure airport to pick up IFR clearance.

After landing the pilot's boss calls approach control and finds out they are still using expired charts.

Air Carrier Captain reported the crew made the decision to suspend in-flight service for COVID-19 social distancing reasons contrary to company policy.

Air carrier flight attendant reported that items were missing from the aircraft's medical kit. In addition; it was noted that some commonly used medical testing devices were not included in the air carrier's medical kit inventory.
Air carrier Captain reported inadvertently flying with an expired FMC database. The Captain noted that distractions due to numerous added cautions for cockpit cleanliness may have contributed.
An air taxi aircraft struck a bird on the takeoff roll from PHMU and continued to their destination where Maintenance found bird strike evidence on one of the propeller blades.
An observer reported that Ham Radio Antennas are blocking a water tower obstruction light.
Corporate Captain reported a speed deviation and cited lack of flying during the COVID-19 pandemic as a contributing factor.
Center Controller reported they issued an approach clearance with incorrect altitude restrictions that placed an aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitude and activated the Center MSAW.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported that miscommunication with ATC resulted in evasive action to avoid another aircraft.
TRACON Controller and air carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict situation between the Captain's commercial aircraft and an unknown VFR aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported a turn inside the Final Approach Fix; an altitude excursion and having to make two attempts to land.
A90 Approach Controller reported that a sector could not accept a handoff; causing the aircraft to fly below the MVA.
Pilot reported the Notam is for another airport located to the northeast.
Technician reported finding DANGER; Do Not Operate; tags still installed on hydraulic components on an in service aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported lack of disinfectant wipes and cleaning agents on the round trip flight.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported a parcel of cargo not loaded on board resulting in incorrect aircraft weight.
A B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence 12 miles in trail of a B747 on approach to MMMX.
B777 flight crew on a trans-Pacific flight reported large excursions in altitude and speed during an encounter with severe turbulence in the vicinity of Kodiak; AK.

ORD Local instructor reports the Developmental crosses traffic downfield; then clears an aircraft for takeoff. Departing pilot questions clearance and Local Control cancels takeoff until crossing is complete. Reporter does not recall using the memory jogger for crossing aircraft.
Dispatcher reported a severe turbulence encounter that resulted in an aircraft removed from service.
C172 student pilot reported that they elected to land on a closed runway due to unforecast winds that exceeded personal minimums.
B737-800 First Officer reported confusion regarding CPDLC clearance messages.

ARTCC Controller reported relieving a position; but did not initially catch that an aircraft had not been coordinated with its next ARTCC. The error was caught only a minute before the aircraft crossed the boundary and the receiving ARTCC Controller could not be contacted before the aircraft crossed the boundary resulting in an airspace deviation.
ZTL ARTCC Controllers reported a loss of communications on primary and backup frequencies; resulting in an NMAC.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non compliant with face mask policy and also changing seats while in turbulence and on final approach.
B737 Captain reported the RNAV STAR they were issued placed them in a position for an unstable approach.
