ZID Center Controller reported an airborne conflict while working all sectors in Controller's area during COVID-19 scheduling.
CRJ First Officer reported an unstable approach due to heavy workload and miscommunication with ATC that led to a taxiway incursion.
Air carrier ramp employee reported a co-worker was struck by lightning because; in his opinion; the company failed to close the ramp during a weather event in a timely fashion.
PA-24 pilot reported a lack of support from ATC led to a possible Class B airspace violation.
B737 Captain on short final at SFO reported executing a go-around after encountering jet blast from a departing A380 that rocked the aircraft.
B737 Captain questioned the wisdom of a new APU procedure within the ETOPs area in flight.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an ambiguous clearance from Tower during the landing rollout at SAEZ.
Air Carrier Captain reported there is not a NOTAM or company flight plan note regarding runway construction.
B767 First Officer reported a heading deviation after takeoff due to three runway changes during taxi and the failure to input the latest change into the FMC.
A321 flight crew reported encountering windshear on approach with no predictive windshear indication and then severe turbulence on the go-around with thunderstorms in the vicinity.
Air Carrier Captain reported a laser incident while climbing out of SBGR.
C402 flight crew reported encountering severe icing on climbout.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported extreme workload due to weather causing major delays.
Air Carrier Captain reported encountering severe turbulence which caused them to lose 500 feet of altitude and descend into a Military Operation Area.
Citation CE560XL crew reported Tower issuing an untimely hold short on landing roll out that the pilot could not comply with.
A Controller reported that the Traffic Management Unit routed aircraft through areas of known severe turbulence.
Maintenance Technicians reported that while working on a B737-800 elevator tab they neglected to remove rags placed in the tab to prevent any hardware from falling into the tab.

GSO Controller reports of a busy session working a combined position due to a training class. Controller had a loss of separation with two aircraft and attributes it to not being a strong controller and being unable to work busy sessions with non-combined positions. Controller indicates it was the lack of staffing that led to combined positions; being overloaded; and having a loss of separation.
Cessna-172 pilot reported an NMAC event in VFR landing pattern with trailing aircraft .
Captain reported confusion regarding a main tire and wheel assembly replacement and the logbook entries that resulted in a flight delay and maintenance action.
An air carrier pilot reported they missed a crossing restriction and did not reduce to 200 knots below Class B airspace.

Flight Attendant reported there were no masks on the plane for flight attendant use and was concerned that the gloves and alcohol-free hand sanitizer were not adequate as personal protective equipment.
Pilot reported misgivings from degraded navigation systems place on MEL and obtained an aircraft tail swap to complete the flight

C90 Controller reported an ORD arriving aircraft on a STAR issued 'descend via' clearance descended through a crossing restriction on the arrival. The reporter spoke to the pilot who did not understand how to comply with the restrictions on the approach once issued 'descend via' ATC phraseology.
A flight crew reported they had to shut down an engine but continued to their destination instead of landing at a closer airport as stated in the QRH.

Air Carrier flight crew reported they rejected a takeoff at LAX when the Tower Controller cancelled takeoff clearance and called abort. The crew reported the ATC ground surveillance radar had sounded a false alarm.
Arrival and departures at SJC operate under the SFO Class B and crews need to observe the 200 knot airspeed restriction.
A B737 flight crew reported that on an oceanic flight to HNL they deviated from assigned altitude because of moderate turbulence when they had difficulty getting a response from ATC.
Dispatcher reported that too many flights are being assigned to each desk; leading to heavy workload and safety concerns.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion regarding Type IV fluid holdover times when mixed types of precipitation are present.
A Tower Controller had to stop the descent of an aircraft on a Visual Approach for VFR departure from a satellite airport on a converging course.
B737 First Officer reported taxi clearance confusion to a departure runway; allowing the crossing of runway along the taxi route.

ZJX Controller reports of a scenario where he used emergency authority for an aircraft that had to enter a Warning Area due to weather. The Controller tried other options but none were safe except for the aircraft to fly into the Warning Area. Controller was later told by management that the pilot should have been the one to use emergency authority.
Wide body transport flight crew reported speed and altitude deviations resulting from an encounter with moderate to severe turbulence at FL370.
S56 Tracon Controller reported vectoring an aircraft encountering icing and unable to maintain altitude and airspeed into lower terrain and out of the conditions.
A Ramp Agent discussed the physical hazard's ramp workers encounter when hot liquids are poured into aircraft drains while the aircraft are parked at the gate.

TRACON Controller working parachute jump aircraft did not coordinate the Jumpers leaving the aircraft with the Tower. Reportedly; the jumper's area is in confliction with the VOR Approach. The Tower Controller had to break the aircraft off their approach to resolve a conflict.
TRACON Controller reported a BE35 departure; at less than standard climb rate; falling below MVA.

King Air 200 pilot experienced loss of GPS signal after departing ELP and passing through FL250. The failure was due to NOTAMed GPS jamming in the area and ATC assigned a heading to destination. With the loss of NAV capability the autopilot also lost altitude capture; resulting in an 800 foot overshoot of FL270.
Aircraft technician reported nitrogen bottles improperly painted as oxygen bottles.
PA-28 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence from an air carrier aircraft in the vicinity of TKI airport.
B737 Captain reported a lack of consensus regarding the requirement to apply an additional MEL before departing.
Ground personnel reported finding an undeclared lithium ion battery HAZMAT designated for transport.
Dispatcher reported working an overloaded desk and overall staffing issues.
KZAK Controller reported that a course deviation by one aircraft resulted in an airborne conflict.
A pilot reported skydiving operations continue when jumpers must pass through clouds during descent.

Maintenance Technician reported that a cleaning employee began spraying disinfectant inside the aircraft while the Technician was still inside performing work. The Technician experienced adverse physiological reaction as a result.
GA pilot reported taking off in a C172 into known icing.  Ice built rapidly and forced a diversion to the nearest airport.
Boeing 737 Captain reported erratic ILS course and glideslope indications while proceeding inbound in visual conditions.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported Dangerous Goods air transport configuration procedures.
Pilot reported a runway incursion and cited lack of flying and unfamiliarity with the airport as contributing factors.
PA30 pilot reported departing an airport after he believed a NOTAM for runway closure was no longer in effect.

A corporate pilot reported receiving a TCAS climb RA at 1;000 feet on the SNA Runway 20 visual approach. After landing he learned an avionics shop in the vicinity of his alert causes occasional false TCAS alerts.

BE-55 pilot reported that their autopilot disconnected inadvertently in turbulence which resulted in an altitude and course deviation. Contributing factors included Center radar down; ATC confusion on destination flight plan; a frequency change; and moderate to occasionally severe turbulence which created distressed passengers.

Air Carrier First Officer reported not obtaining an ATC departure clearance prior to pushback; and cited a nonstandard pre-flight briefing given by the Captain and lack of flying as contributing factors.

The flight crew of a business jet reported becoming disoriented while taxiing to the gate in blowing snow and low visibility conditions which resulted in the aircraft departing the paved taxiway surface.
B737 flight crew reported receiving an RA on approach.
B757 First Officer reported a near miss with a Citation 525 which had just cancelled IFR. The 757 leveled in response to a TCAS RA command.
A319 Captain reported returning to departure airport after birds were ingested into Number 1 engine shortly after takeoff; resulting in catastrophic engine failure.
A321 Captain reported that improper use of the APU bleed after engine shutdown caused fumes to be ingested into the cabin.
GA pilot reported being distracted by an unlatched cabin door and climbed into Class B airspace. This situation was exacerbated by an incorrect altimeter setting.

VNY local controller working three locals combined describes a situation where he isn't sure what to do and asks for help but doesn't receive a timely answer. Aircraft in the pattern flies underneath a VIP aircraft which leads the TRACON call to complain about the operation.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported they had no information reporting that a taxiway at their destination was closed. After landing and turning onto this taxiway; the aircraft became stuck in deep snow.
Cessna pilot reported an NMAC event in the landing pattern.
A Controller lost radar contact with an aircraft. The Controller was not sure if they maintained required separation standards between this aircraft and another aircraft which was in radar coverage.
BE36 Pilot reported a near collision with another aircraft during landing rollout.
B767-300 Captain reported that contract ramp personnel are not properly trained regarding aircraft handling procedures.

EMB-170 Captain reported ramp personnel discovered bags had not been unloaded from the aft cargo pit at the previous airport. This caused the aircraft to operate with 390 lbs of undocumented bags on the flight to ORD.
B737 Captain stated that the Metering frequency at STL has out lived is usefulness and caused confusion when used to issue taxi instructions; with a ground control frequency also in use.
Pilot reported  being advised of a possible overflight of a required maintenance inspection.
B777 Captain reported receiving closeout weights that were 27;000 lbs under the actual weight.
Technician reported a Captain was confrontational when he entered the flight deck.  Reportedly; the Captain had dealt with passengers not wanting to wear their COVID-19 masks.
Fractional pilot reported a track deviation occurred when the flight crew failed to properly load the route in the FMC.
C172 Pilot reported a near mid air collision after takeoff.
ZMA Center Controller reported several near losses of separation and possible airspace violations due to the workload.
Flight Attendant reported that a passenger refused to wear a face mask. When customer service came on board the passenger agreed to wear the face mask.
Ramp personnel reported Cargo Aircraft Only HAZMAT shipment mistakenly shipped on passenger flight.
ATC reported an aircraft under the controller's area encountered a NMAC.
PA-28 Pilot reported a NMAC during takeoff with an aircraft transitioning the airport traffic area.
Technician reported not being able to make a correct log entry due to confusion regarding proper aircraft parking/storage procedures.
A300 Captain reported a loss of aircraft control at FL330 during an encounter with severe turbulence and windshear.
An A320 dispatched with a deferred AC Pack Flow Sensor was unable to heat the aircraft interior and diverted to an enroute airport for maintenance.
Air carrier Captain reported an anomaly in the NOTAMs depicting an area of taxiway construction.
Following a flight with many children on board; the flight attendants realized that they did not have the required number of spare life vests on board.
Center Controller reported that the adjacent Center handed them off two aircraft on converging headings at the same altitude; causing an airborne conflict.
EMB-140 Flight crew reported a Terrain warning during visual approach.
Air Carrier Captain reported LGB 10-9 Page does not depict the Runway 26L Approach hold short line.
GA student pilot reported frequency communications issues while flying to an airport whose Tower services were not in use.
CRJ First Officer reported receiving a GPWS Terrain warning on a night visual approach to AVL.
CRJ-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on takeoff from SFO from an Airbus that took off on a parallel upwind runway. Reporter stated full aileron and rudder was required for recovery.
ATC Tower Controller reported TRACON confusion on Opposite Direction Operations.
Helicopter pilot believes that he may have passed through a law enforcement TFR; during a patient transport; that did not show up on any web sites or the Foreflight app on the iPad.
B777-200 Captain reported pushing back prior to receiving Dangerous Goods final report.
ZBW Center controllers reported that the Controller working radar assigned a climb to the wrong aircraft which lead to a loss of separation.
Sailplane pilot reported he was contacted by Center and advised some of the sailplanes flying with him violated Class A airspace.

An aircraft ACARS printer was inoperative so Dispatch emailed the performance data to the crew. During takeoff the nose heavy aircraft extended the takeoff roll. In flight the crew determined the V speeds were too low for the installed engine type.
Air carrier Captain reported an EGPWS TERRAIN alert during a stabilized; daylight visual approach to ABQ Runway 26.
Dispatcher reported ramp load personnel neglected to notify Dispatcher of Hazmat [dry ice] loaded in cargo compartment.
GA pilot reported the approach for Runway 30 at FCH is unsafe because the PAPI lights are non-operational.

Air carrier flight attendant reported a passenger was upset with being told to comply with regulations regarding their child seat. Flight Attendant suggested many passengers seem more stressed than usual because of COVID-19 pandemic procedures.
Embraer 175 flight crew reported being dispatched without an alternate and then being unable to land at the destination airport due to weather and low fuel reserve.
B737 Captain reported they refused aircraft after Maintenance misapplied an MEL to the left ram air door.
BE35 pilot reported sighting a UAV (drone) near UGN in very close proximity to his aircraft requiring evasive action.
Air carrier pilot reported they misunderstood Departure Controller's altitude assignment due to new phraseology associated with new OAPM RNAV departures.

Air carrier First Officer reported being assigned to fly with a Captain whose previous First Officer co-pilot called in sick with COVID-19 symptoms.  Reporter described concerns with management's handling of the situation stating it goes against all the guidelines delineated by the company.
Technician reported that after testing an FMU and finding it inoperative; a Supervisor ordered it to be retested by an unqualified Technician.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with having to wear a face mask and the use of hand sanitizers.
Phantom Pro 4 UAV Operator reported a collision with a tree during climb.
Captain reported an NMAC event with a Cessna 182 during landing.
B737 Captain reported that a GPWS Terrain warning on a visual approach was apparently as a result of flap displacement due to wind gusts.
SCT Controller reported descending an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Local/Ground Controller and both pilots reported a runway excursion due to NIL braking action by the landing aircraft.
When clearing an MEL item on a B737-700 the Mechanic found an improper seal installed (zip tie) on a life vest pouch. The zip tie was installed by someone other than Maintenance.
PC-12 flight crew reported a rag was left in such a location so as to prevent full retraction of the flaps after takeoff. The distraction led to failure to retract the landing gear.
B737 Captain reported an uneventful landing after two GPWS alerts during approach due primary weather conditions.
SR22 pilot reported inadvertently entering clouds after VFR takeoff.
C-172 instructor pilot reported a failure in CTAF communication by another aircraft on short final when they reported taking active runway and crossing the hold line.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL ten miles in trail of an A321.
The Captain of a Falcon 2000 reported that many write-ups were sign-off but never repaired by Maintenance.
Great Lakes 2T pilot reported a ground loop occurred late in the landing roll when he encountered suspected wind shear.
Center Controller reported working combined sectors and not noticing an aircraft on a route through Special Use Airspace (SUA).

The pilot reported that nonstandard phraseology for a critical flight management switch was being used by instructors in the training center environment. The phraseology used was a slang term; and does not comport with the airline's Flight Manual.

DAB CIC reports of an NMAC between two aircraft and didn't know what happened until he saw a replay later. Aircraft was supposed to turn south; but local 2 turned aircraft north; coming close to other traffic local 1 was working.
Air carrier pilot on approach to BUF Runway 23 was confused by the presence of a PAPI because the Jeppesen 10-9 Chart does not indicate a PAPI installed.
B767 First Officer reported an airspeed deviation during arrival on a STAR that contained vague charting.
Flight Attendant reported two passengers would not initially comply with face mask policy. Both passengers complied after another passenger began complaining about them.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing three minutes of GPS jamming while flying at FL340 in Iraqi airspace.
Air carrier Captain reported a Flight Attendant did not agree with the Captain not wearing a mask in the cockpit.
Captain of a fractional operation commented on fatigue issues with scheduling flight crews in 135 and 91 operations.
A319 Captain reported a new TRACON procedure that resulted in increased fuel burn and minimum fuel status on multiple flights.
A320 flight crew reported they encountered ice on the ramp and lost braking capability.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to wear a mask.
Air carrier Captain reported several go-arounds due to windshear warnings at DTW; then finally landed during a windshear caution alert on the third approach.
ZTL Controller reported the Traffic Management Coordinators were not complying with requests during a busy period with weather deviations causing them to miss several point outs to adjacent sectors.
ZZZ Tower Controller reported a military UAV exited the Delta airspace against current agreements between the FAA and military.
Air carrier flight crew reported a close encounter with a 'fixed wing drone' at 4000 ft while on left downwind to Runway 22 at LGA.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a minor track deviation occurred when they encountered wake turbulence on approach into PHL in trail of an A320.
Air carrier Captain reported a communications breakdown between flight crew and ATC regarding routing direct to similar sounding waypoints.

A [small transport] pilot comments on HCF TRACON keeping smaller traffic high until last minute and expecting the pilots to make a dive and drive approach within approximately 3 miles from HNL. Report mentions on this day they had to ask for a runway farther away and still had trouble making it due to the required descent angle.
C172 Pilot Flying reported FBO filling CTAF with excessive information and blocking critical pilot communications.
An Air Carrier pilot reported ATC refused their request for a weather deviation.
MD-11 Captain reported executing a go-around at JFK after encountering gusty winds and wake turbulence at touchdown.
AS350 helicopter pilot reported concerns that a photo mission in the vicinity of a power plant might be misinterpreted as a security issue.

An air carrier crew on vectors for GRK ILS Runway 15 was given a descent to 3;000 feet but read back and descended to 2;000 feet. The crew felt low and when asking about the cleared altitude received an EGPWS 'TOO LOW GEAR' aural warning.
B777 Captain reported electric wheelchair transported to destination without required documents or NOTOC at departure airport.
After the fact; a B757 First Officer was made aware that he and Captain taxied with a ground crewmen head set still attached to the aircraft. Neither pilot saw the person.
C172 pilot reported landing on a highway after the weather degraded rapidly.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reports that component was installed on aircraft without proper signoff.
Flight Attendant reported several passengers were non-compliant with face mask policy.

During a scheduled ASDE-X outage this Tower and the TRACON also experienced Primary Radar software outages on some of their radar displays requiring the back-up system to be necessary.The reporter states that the appropriate maintenance office was not notified of the Radar display outages. The reporter wanted to know if this was a coincidence or a known issue that the controllers should have been briefed on.

A B737 flight crew reported receiving the HAZMAT NOTOC report which they examined and discovered lithium batteries and flammable liquid in the same cargo compartment. Upon questioning Operations they were told the flammable liquid was removed. Upon arrival; the crew discovered the flammable liquid and lithium batteries were still on board.
EMB-175 Captain reported system anomalies were annunciated after encountering wake turbulence 5 nm in trail of an A321 on approach to LAX.
EMB-145 Captain reported a terrain issue associated with the RNAV Runway 33 Approach into Bangor; Maine.
An Instructor pilot reported they detected conflicting traffic on their ADSB and had to take evasive action to avoid a collision. They received no traffic information from ATC.

GGG Tower Controller reports of having a procedural error due to an opposite direction arrival being less than 10 miles from the airport; [9.5] while another aircraft was approaching the other runway. Aircraft were never closer than 12 miles from each other according to the reporter.
Captain reported DIK airport is under construction; the runway lights are inoperative and there are no NOTAMs regarding these conditions.
A-321 First Officer reported a wake turbulence encounter while climbing out from SFO.
Business jet Captain reported an encounter with an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV); at approximately 2;600 feet; during initial approach to LAX Runway 24L. No evasive action was required.
Philadelphia TRACON Controller reported of an operation that the Front Line Manager and the reporter were not sure about. The reporter wanted a briefing on this operation and how it was legal.
Flight Attendant reported problems associated with wearing the required COVID-19 type mask while performing duties.

Pilot reports of marginal weather inbound to LAX and being asked if they could maintain visual with another aircraft. Pilot responded that they could; but would lose the aircraft when it went into the cloud deck. Controller told them to maintain an altitude; then cleared them for the ILS. Pilot wondered why controller would issue the visual when they were aware of the clouds and why did the Controller not issue a clearance cancellation.
NCT Controller reported that a trainee's failure to follow instructions caused an aircraft to descend below MVA.

VFR aircraft requested an altitude to drop parachute jumpers. Controller restricted the aircraft 1;000 feet below its requested altitude and advises there will be a delay for approval for jumpers due to traffic. VFR aircraft releases parachute jumpers anyway and advises Controller to move traffic away from the jump area.
A319 flight crew reported an altitude overshoot on descent due to confusion with the charted transition level.
An Air Carrier Captain reported during a flight crew switch and cabin distractions they did not comply with a crossing restriction.

After returning to gate for compass headings that were not in agreement and restarting their IRUs; a contract pilot describes an inflight event that followed where a 40-degree difference in their Hawker-Siddeley HS-125-800 track headings were noted by an ATC Controller; even though the aircraft's HSI and Multi-Function Display (MFD) compass headings were in agreement. Flight diverted. Aircraft was powered down overnight and complete system reset appeared to resolve the issue.
Cessna 182 Single Pilot reported a critical ground conflict on landing with opposite direction landing aircraft.
DAB TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict was exacerbated by faulty voice communications equipment.
EMB-145 Captain reported the BUF Runway 5 ILS signal is weak and unreliable.
Air carrier tug driver reported a near collision with another air carrier aircraft during pushback procedure.

ZNY Center Controller was instructed to reduce the speed of an in-trail aircraft by an adjacent Center to comply with non-radar oceanic separation standards. The Center Controller was using appropriate separation and states the adjacent Center's instruction was unnecessary.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving an RA alert during initial approach to BUR airport.
Pilot reported issues with the service provided by SLC Tower and cited Controller workload as a contributing factor.
An air carrier descending into MFR activated the EGPWS while descending over the mountains on a visual for Runway 32 but arrested the rate of descent to stop the alert.
Air carrier Captain reported loss of GPS signal apparently due to jamming from White Sands.
AUS Approach Controller reported an airborne conflict due to a VFR aircraft in the path of an IFR arrival on a descend via clearance.
LJ31 flight crew reported encountering clear air turbulence and loss of aircraft control at FL450 with an altitude loss of 800 feet.
Technician reported that engine bore scope plug was not installed correctly; causing damage to engine heat shields.

Crew of a Challenger 300 was cleared for ILS approach to 31C at MDW and to maintain 210 knots until RUNTS; a fix 6.7 miles from the localizer. The crew believed that flying the glideslope from their current position would require them to fly below the Class B airspace; which has a floor of 3600 feet in this area. Crew asked ATC about the clearance to violate the FAR restriction of airspeed below 200 knots below Class B airspace. ATC confirmed the 210-knot clearance; the crew elected to maintain 200 knots as required by FAR 91.117(c).

A First Officer questions whether a logbook corrective action entry by Maintenance was appropriate or just ridiculous. He found the emergency brake and landing gear pneumatic vent lines dripping hydraulic fluid during a post flight walk around of their Cessna Citation X aircraft. Maintenance had disconnected; vacuumed; cleaned with alcohol; and re-secured the lines.
Air carrier Captain reported a compromised fuel state on an international flight and subsequent difficulty obtaining expeditious handling from air traffic control.
CRJ Captain reported experiencing a 'severe' wake turbulence event on arrival into ATL in trail of a B747.
Air carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation during arrival to DEN airport after confusing similar sounding fixes HIPPE and HIPEE.
Air carrier First Officer reported calling in fatigued for an assignment that followed a six-day trip which included interrupted off-hour sleep and multiple time zone crossings.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported ramp employees were subject to jet blast when the flight crew taxied without ramp clearance.
Center Controller reported working 3 sectors combined during the COVID-19 pandemic and did not point out an aircraft resulting in an airspace violation.

Air Carrier Captain landing at a Foreign Airport; reported being required to make a written statement of apology when they did not contact the Tower 30 minutes prior to landing to get approval to land. This requirement is in addition to normal communications including actual landing clearance.
Air Carrier First Officer reported 3 passengers would not comply with the face mask policy during flight and became belligerent toward the flight attendants.
B787 Maintenance Technician reported the nose gear pin can mistakenly be installed into the NLG drag brace hollow bolt rendering the gear unsafe for ground testing.
A319 Captain reported diverting to a nearby airport after shutting down #1 engine because of high oil temperature reportedly due to oil over-service.
B737 First Officer reported that they deviated from clearance due to flight management system not accepting new arrival.
A320 Captain reported being high crossing FAF then receiving 'terrain; terrain' warning from aircraft.
Captain; jumpseating on a B737; reported a close encounter with a small UAV on final approach which was not seen by the flying flight crew.

A Private Pilot bounced a C-206 multiple times on landing. Winds were reported as gusty. The front landing gear fractured and the propeller struck the ground.  The aircraft had to be towed off the runway.
B737-800 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after ingesting birds into the right engine.

Air Carrier pilot reports of incorrect data on the LIDO chart that they were using to fly to the destination airport. The frequency of the ILS was in error on one chart; but correct on another chart. The change had taken place two years ago.
Aircraft deviating for weather was issued a right turn. The pilot had to refuse the clearance due to weather. The pilot observed traffic on their TCAS descending below them.
C525 pilot reported ATC initially denied their requested routing to avoid weather at their destination.
Pilot reported a non-towered airport's publications and ASOS broadcast have not been updated to reflect a recent name change; with the inconsistency in naming causing NMAC events.
Captain reported that another aircraft landed on the opposite direction runway after they were cleared for takeoff.

Small aircraft pilot reports frequency congestion when trying to obtain flight following with SCT while departing enroute to Burbank. Pilot offers concern as to why one Controller is responsible for two different frequencies.
Air carrier flight crew reported exceeding the speed restriction that limits aircraft to 250 knots until 13;000 feet on the ORD2 SID.
A321 flight attendant reported giving CPR to an ill passenger until paramedics arrived.

After deadheading in; running late for departure; heavy snow; finding a cold unpowered airplane already being boarded by passengers and dealing with a harried; uncooperative gate agent all combined for a rushed and incomplete preflight environment during which the flight crew failed to note there was no weather packet contained in the flight papers until after takeoff.

A crew was on the ORD TRTLL 2 RNAV leveled at 13;000 FT and was queried about their altitude then notified the digital ATIS was wrong and their aircraft was 700 FT low. Digital ATIS nationwide was apparently in error.
CRJ First Officer reported workload; weather; and distraction resulted in an altitude excursion during a go around.
ZFW Controller reported of flow control problems into the sector and bad radios in this same area.
San Juan Center Controller reported radio problems and similar sounding aircraft call signs that led to an aircraft take another's descent clearance; which then became an airspace violation.
Technician reported confusing dates relating to work that needed to be performed on an aircraft that had been in storage.

Air carrier Captain reported while on a vector to ZSPD ILS 17R; the crew did not get a response from approach so they switched to the Tower frequency on the approach plate (124.35) and did not get a response either. Captain reported approach gave the crew a different Tower frequency than what was listed on the approach plate.

STP Tower Controller reported an aircraft landed at the wrong airport and a second aircraft went missed approach. Both aircraft were lined up for the wrong airport. Pilot that landed on the wrong airport also reported the event.

BFL Approach Controller reported of not being able to see an aircraft's transponder. The Controller gave the pilot the option of being routed non-radar or cancelling IFR. Pilot cancelled IFR and Controller tried to provide flight following. Controller reported that ZLA and FAT Approach could see the aircrafts transponder. Controller requested the radar be updated during NextGen technology.

SCT Controller reported of a target making a hard turn and descending into a lover MVA. Controller couldn't tell if the aircraft was in fact acting erratic or an issue with STARS. Controller stated he and others at SCT would like to see a return to CARTS.
A11 Controller reported descending an aircraft below the MVA to resolve an airborne conflict.

Controller reports of a medical issue which is coordinated to be direct the airport. Management while observing; lets it be known to the Controller that direct a fix direct the airport would be better for the TRACON. The Controller disagrees and states the aircraft is distress. Discussion ensues about managements part in the movement of distressful traffic.
Cessna 425 pilot reported he encountered severe clear air turbulence on descent to APA on the LARKS8 Arrival.
Air Carrier Captain reported a higher than expected fuel burn due to an APU malfunction and a reroute after pushback.
Flight Attendant at jump seat position was struck with shards of dry ice from bag exploding.
Check pilot reported a NMAC during takeoff from a non-towered airport.
A pilot taxiing to the gate reported LGA Ground taxi clearance included giving way to a MD-88 at 'N' which he thought happened but the controller meant a different MD-88 at Taxiway 'Z.'
B787 Captain reported a lack of guidance in the flight manual for a blocked or frozen static system.
ZOA ARTCC Controller reported that while conducting training during a high volume; and complex period; a VFR pop-up was issued an IFR clearance below the MVA.
ZAU Center Controller reported that an aircraft had to go-around after being issued an RNAV Approach due to equipment and personnel on the runway.
Air Carrier Captain reported power lever over travel event while executing windshear maneuver and resulted in an overspeed condition.

B777 flight crew is informed by ATC of a volcanic ash plume ahead that will require deviation to miss and the crew accepts a revised route. This particular volcanic ash plume had not been part of the Dispatch Release and the crew only became aware when informed by Magadan Control.

Several ATC Controllers reported that an aircraft was allowed to get less than the required distance from an active ATC Assigned Airspace. A Controller had used incorrect procedures to allow the aircraft to proceed toward the airspace.
Dallas Love Controller reported not being briefed for a VIP movement and what resulted afterward.
Piper 32 pilot reported an NMAC with a B737 aircraft.
Technician reported uncertainty in processes in determining whether or not to replace a crew O2 bottle that was five PSI over the minimum limit due to a restraining order in place.
Single pilot reported ATC assigns multiple step-down altitudes in a short time and distance on the PITBL STAR.
A Pilot reported that the MEL requires the fuel quantity to be read by sticking the tanks; but the MEL does not include a fuel stick chart.
S46 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on final approach for BFI conflicted with VFR aircraft transiting the approach course not in communication with ATC.
RSW TRACON Controller reported an aircraft that needed vectors away from extreme precipitation and did not point out the aircraft until after the fact.
Tower Controller reported a ground conflict and cited reduced staffing due to COVID-19 and training at combined positions as contributing factors.
ORD Tower Controller reported a recurring problem with momentary loss of runway lights.
Corporate flight crew reported an altitude deviation during departure and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Air carrier storekeeper reported that the new Parts Inventory Management System has raised safety concerns among parts delivery personnel.
Flight Attendant reported multiple concerns with wearing a face mask.
C90 TRACON Controller reported there was no Traffic Management Coordinator on their shift during a period of weather causing delays and deviations.
B-737-900 Flight Attendant reported passenger with Lithium Ion battery powered smart bag unable to remove dead battery prior to flight.
Pilot reported they were advised by ATC they forgot to cancel VFR flight following.

A B737-700 crew reported severe turbulence at FL390 which resulted in a stall recovery descent with both stick shaker and overspeed warnings. No injuries were reported but the aircraft was removed from service.

Technician reported that after checking several aircraft; he found them to have malfunctioning RH ram air door actuators requiring replacement and made logbook entries; after which Company Management berated him for doing so.
ORD Tower Controller and Developmental reported of a problem with gates that are on the end of terminals that have to be pushed onto a taxiway.
B787 flight crew reported a severe wave encounter at FL380 which resulted in a 35 knot overspeed and uncommanded 1000 foot climb.
B737 flight crew reported that they had a near miss on the deice pad with a deice truck.
PA-28R Cherokee Pilot reported an NMAC event during takeoff roll with a nordo aircraft that initiated an opposite direction takeoff.
B767 pilot reported being unable to comply with an ATC request; resulting in an altitude deviation.
Air carrier First Officer reported ORD procedure dictates the second COM is used for as a monitor frequency and for normal Tower operations which causes distraction.
Maintenance Auditor reported critical airplane component failures are not being reported as required by the Code of Federal Regulations (CFR)'s.
Flight Instructor reported that after lining up on the runway; another aircraft was departing from the opposite end and had made no radio calls of their intentions.
ZMA Center Controller reported an aircraft in holding did not hold as instructed because of weather; but did not advise ATC.
A320 Captain reported an EGPWS alert during approach to ONT airport.
ATC Center Supervisor reported aircraft on the wrong routings and in conflict with other aircraft.
B737-700 Captain reported two flight attendants were injured when the flight encountered unexpected moderate turbulence.
B737 flight crew reported landing instead of initiating a go-around despite receiving a brief wind shear warning on final.

ZDV ARTCC Controller became overloaded with traffic due to the workload created by weather deviations from other sectors routed into his airspace. The Controller feels the Traffic Management Unit did not properly control the workload of this sector.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at 10;000 feet after departing LAX in trail of an A350.
A320 Captain reported the procedure to perform a flight control test per the MEL was incomplete.
SAT Approach Controller reported a B737 on final approach received an RA for unidentified VFR traffic departing an adjacent satellite airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported aircraft encountered Maximum Speed exceedance in cruise.

The pilot reported a near mid-air collision at CMA while cleared for the break and following another aircraft on the same break. He had told the Tower several times that he did not have the aircraft ahead in sight.

A Pilot reports of an airspace issue relayed to him by approach control. The aircraft departed OSU airport and climbed to 3;400 feet; made a right turn to a 240 degree heading. Approach stated that the aircraft entered into the Class C airspace of CMH and they had to restrict a carrier above this aircraft.
One TRACON Controller did not realize an aircraft would be below the MEA; while the center controller accepted the handoff with the aircraft below the MEA.
HCF TRACON reported conflicts with military aircraft not following procedures and not using airspace as designed creating airborne conflicts with commercial airliners.
B737 Captain reported a Controller assigned them a speed to ALISN which is on a 4.6 mile final at LAX.
Air carrier Captain reported discovering passengers had been boarded by Station Personnel with no flight crew on board; and the emergency exit light switch was found in the off position.
ZLA ARTCC Controller reported a loss of separation between a flight of military aircraft and an arriving descending air carrier aircraft.
ZJX Center Controller reported an adjacent military ATC facility handed an aircraft off to the wrong sector while it flew through his airspace.
ZAB Enroute Controller reported seeing a number of wake turbulence events recently; and questioned current in-trail techniques.
A320 Captain laments frequent ATC changes to RNAV arrival crossing restrictions requiring the use of speed brakes and defeating the purpose of the OPD.
MD-11 Captain reported wake turbulence encounter departing MEM four miles in trail of a B777.
Maintenance Technician reports that 737-800 tire pressures are reading low after landing.
Air carrier Captain reported multiple ground delays and lack of proper sleep resulted in physical degradation/fatigue and decided to remove himself from the flight.
An aircrew experienced a CSD (Constant Speed Drive) failure and mistakenly used the wrong checklist to remedy the situation.
The pilot of a twin engine Piper reported accepting an ATC descent clearance in IMC conditions with significant turbulence resulting in a momentary loss of control.
B737 First Officer reported ATC excessive use of vectors for sequencing; rather than holding; resulted in great and unnecessary increase in fuel burn and workload.
B767 Captain reported inadvertently shutting down the APU after pushing the battery switch because it indicated off when it actually was on.
Dispatch Supervisor sent staff home and dispatchers were over worked and understaffed.
Private pilot flying C-172 reported entering instrument meteorological conditions due to smoke from nearby wildfires.
B737 Captain reported an erroneous GPWS warning; resulting in a missed approach.
Air carrier pilot stated hotel was not keeping crews separate from each other on crew transportation from the hotel.

ZJX Controller reported another facility handed off an aircraft to him. The Controller then sees a target in the warning area climbing towards the aircraft just handed off. Controller issued traffic and called the other facility.  Other facility stated aircraft is restricted to a specific altitude. Aircraft busted altitude and buzzed the aircraft just handed off. Controller reported the violation to the other facility.
A321 First Officer reported being issued numerous delay vectors based on ATC Time Based Flow Management requirements. The pilot was concerned about not being able to plan for enough fuel.
Tow vehicle driver reported making contact with a pushback tractor while towing an aircraft to a gate.
Dispatcher reported being out of the loop when an aircraft was delayed and no one advised the Dispatcher.
Tower Controllers reported they had less than required separation criteria for an Opposite Direction Operation.
Ramp employee reported a string of baggage carts rolled onto the runway when the brakes failed on the lead cart.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unstable approach when circling to land.
Corporate Captain reported LUF Approach Control issued routing through a busy training area.
Air carrier Captain reported a CFTT event during approach to SJC airport. The crew immediately climbed to avoid the terrain and ATC re-vectored them for landing.

Air carrier Captain departed SBGR from Runway 27R on the CGO 1A Departure does not believe his aircraft is capable of flying the ninety degree turns required at heavy weights to the satisfaction of ATC.
B737-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL310 from an opposite direction B747 at FL320. Reporter stated he would like to see the separation standards increased.
Air carrier Relief Pilot reported the Captain and flying First Officer were not climbing as instructed by ATC due to inadvertently being in altitude hold during departure.
B767 Captain reported that distraction resulted in an altitude and airspeed deviation.
Gulfstream flight crew reported hydroplaning during their landing rollout which led to a runway overrun.
Maintenance Technician reported confusing documentation in regards to Supplemental Oxygen Units.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion.  Captain cited lack of recent flying.
Ground Controller reported inadvertently taxiing an aircraft into a restricted area.
Traffic Management Coordinator reported an Operations Manager assign a non certified Supervisor to work a sector during an aircraft emergency event.

A Flight Attendant (FA) noticed an ill passenger during the boarding process. An investigation revealed that the passenger had recently travelled to Africa. The Passenger was observed to be sweating and vomiting. The FA notified the Lead FA and Captain of the situation. A Complaint Resolution Official (CRO) was called and the passenger was removed from the flight. The reporter was concerned that no precautions were taken for the crew and that the aircraft was not sufficiently cleaned and disinfected upon arrival at the destination.
B747 flight crew reported that poor weather caused a diversion; and hazardous cargo without proper paperwork created an issue when parking.
A Tower Controller reported Tower Supervisors did not comply with runway use directives during a period of snow and freezing rain.
PA-44 pilot reported encroaching on a jump site that conflicted with a practice missed approach.
B737 flight crew reported poor communication on final approach leading to an unstable approach and the wrong flap position for landing.

An EMB-145 Captain placed a passenger's cat and crate in the cockpit before takeoff; ignoring the First Officer's objections to blocking the exit. This violated company policy and ignored the First Officer's objections because of his cat allergy.
Air carrier First Officer reported an APU drain mast/hydraulic reservoir vent was discovered missing by ground crew after landing.
Pilot reported forgetting to arm the thrust reversers upon landing and cited distraction and lack of flying as contributing factors.

Air Carrier First Officer reported that on a coupled approach to the ILS Z RWY 8 BUR above the NA coupled altitude the aircraft was at half scale deflection right trending further right. First Officer reported disconnecting autopilot and correcting for an uneventful landing.
Air carrier flight crew and a Controller reported an unsafe situation where an air carrier and a sky diving aircraft became to close; resulting in a Resolution Alert for the carrier.
ASE Tower Local Controller trainee reported they vectored aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude after an aircraft departing opposite direction to an arrival turned in the wrong direction.

An airfield maintenance crew was authorized by Ground to proceed onto a runway but hold short of the intersecting runway. Several minutes later an aircraft performed a touch and go on the runway they were on then passed close overhead.
An Air Carrier First Officer descending on a Visual Approach reported receiving a GPWS alert even though in their present position they were at an appropriate altitude to avoid terrain.

Pilots reported of being taken off an arrival; vectored; and given an altitude to maintain. ATC then gave the crew a direct to and maintain an altitude. The crew was issued a frequency change and the new controller wanted to know why they were not descending on the arrival. The crew replied they were given direct to a point and to maintain an altitude. ATC was confused.
B-787 flight crew reported pushback was initiated without required Hazmat documentation.
EMB-190 Captain reported on descent to LGA he took evasive action from a VFR aircraft that was supposed to maintain visual separation.

S46 Controller describes a training session where he and the Developmental do everything correct; in their mind. Two weeks later the instructor (reporter) is asked by a Front Line Manager if he thought the positions should have been spilt that day. The Controller then finds out he is being charged with a loss of separation that he thinks management is wrong in placing the blame. Controller cites from 7100.65 and LOA between facility and tower; reporting who has the responsibility for separation.
Ramp personnel reported a B757 being parked at a gate that was not designed for that aircraft type.
General aviation pilot in the pattern at 4B9 reported a loss of control after a wake turbulence encounter with a B737 inbound to BDL.
B777 flight crew reported incompatible Hazmat cargo items incorrectly loaded in same compartment in violation of company procedures.
ZOB Controller lost their main radio transmitters. The Controller was working two aircraft head on at the same altitude. Both aircraft responded to TCAS/RA alerts.
Air Carrier Check Airman reported non-standard marshalling procedures and a lack of attention by the ground crew at the gate.
ZDV Center Controller reported a pilot needed an immediate descent due to severe turbulence.
Technician reported that APU generator wires were mismarked; resulting in incorrect installation.
Air carrier crew reported the FMS would not allow the aircraft to descend to a hard altitude on the KRATR 2 arrival to PDX because it was below the IAF altitude for the ILS 28L.
A Stationair pilot reported a near mid air collision while being overtaken on a well traveled route where pilots normally self report location for separation.
SCT Controller reported of targets jumping in a certain area on his radar scope. Facility is aware of the problem. Controller wants the problem fixed.
Ramp Agent reported issues with temperature check procedures at work and the company's lack of communication regarding employees that may have contracted COVID-19.

Air carrier Captain unfamiliar with MDPP noted that because of high terrain west of the airport; a pilot could fly the Runway 8 RNAV/GPS Arrival then fly from EMIVO direct to Runway 8 and pass 0.72 miles from the highest point.
Air carrier First Officer reported Chart+ software crashing and inaccurate information on the charts.
ZDC Controller reported having issued altitude below Minimum IFR Altitude to light transport.
C-172 Flight Instructor reported an uneventful NMAC event with an unreported drone during training flight.
B777 First Officer reported encountering 'moderate plus' wake turbulence departing EGLL behind another B777.
Center Controllers reported an aircraft landed on a closed runway due to snow removal equipment and not being able to identify the correct NOTAM for the runway closure..
B767 Captain reported that he has found crews carrying electrical cords with dual outlets; non-Apple chargers and using them in the aircraft cockpit.
Ramp controller reported preventing a nose to nose situation at a major airport during a particularly heavy workload time.
B737 Captain reported high workload resulted when P50 Controller issued multiple clearance changes on approach to PHX.
GA pilot reported a bright spotlight was shining on his aircraft during a night visual approach to Runway 32 at Eagles Nest Airport (31E); a privately-owned airport.
Tower Controller in Charge reported coordination issue with a TRACON Controller during a runway configuration change.

A government aircraft's oceanic flight plan was not forwarded to Enroute Oceanic Air Traffic Control facilities which required a delay within a protected military warning area prior to entry into oceanic airspace.
GA pilot reported lighting at DVT airport is too bright at night and could cause loss of night vision.
An EC130 helicopter pilot reported he unexpectedly entered IMC after doing a due diligence weather preflight which indicated VMC to his base airport. An IFR clearance was obtained enroute.
Air carrier Captain reports that a lengthy ground delay and adverse winds at the destination airport required a diversion to the alternate. This resulted in landing with a low fuel state.

ZLA Controller reported of an automation problem between ZLA and Joshua Approach. Controller pointed out multiple aircraft using the flash-through procedure. One aircraft did not flash through to the correct sector and the Controller ended up having multiple airspace deviations.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft lined up for the wrong runway during approach.
Captain reported that the Flight Attendant told them that passengers in a specific row were not wearing face masks and were instructed to do so.
Due to adverse winds on approach; flight executed a go around. Due to low fuel amounts then diverted to another airport.
CRJ-900 Flight Crew reported taking a climb clearance meant for another aircraft due to an apparent mistake by ATC.
Air Carrier flight crew reported they had configured the bleed air system incorrectly before takeoff.
Instructor pilot reported confusion with a similar fix name that caused the flight to be off course.
Flight Instructor reported violating Class B airspace after evading an NMAC.
C208 pilot reported landing on a closed runway as the safest option given the weather conditions at the time.
SR22 pilot reported difficulties during heavy weather and turbulence; requiring ATC help and a diversion.

B737-800 flight crew reported a passenger's cell phone overheated emitting smoke was transported in containment bag. Flight crew further reported no procedures available regarding handling of containment bags.
A station supervisor reported aircraft engine acceleration during taxi blew several containers causing damage to them and nearly hitting an agent.

BFI controllers described a runway incursion involving a departing cleared for takeoff small aircraft and a vehicle that was already approved to be on the runway. The Controller did not use the RID (Runway Incursion Device) when he allowed the vehicle onto the runway. Pilot advised Tower of traffic on runway; Controller removed the vehicle off the runway and the aircraft departed.
Maintenance personnel reported that they received emergency medical kits that were incomplete.
S46 TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict between an air carrier IFR aircraft on the RNAV 34L Approach to PAE airport and a VFR helicopter.
Citation pilot reported an altitude deviation due to a loud window leak noise interfering with radio communication and altitude warnings.
PA28 pilot reported a wake turbulence encounter with a military transport aircraft in the vicinity of MCN airport.
EMB-ERJ flight crew reported a NMAC with a turboprop resulting in executing a RA.
B737 flight crew reported ORD Tower canceled the takeoff clearance on Runway 22L because of conflicting landing traffic on Runway 28C.
Pilot reported that a trim malfunction resulted in a diversion.
Challenger 300 flight crew reported filing and flying the Cottonwood Departure out of EGE which is not allowed by the company's policy.

A Dispatcher reported the Automated Delay alert once activated cannot be easily overridden and after an aircraft out time is established the flight's clock is running even at the gate which can affect FAR 117 crew legality as well as other company and FAA considerations.

General Aviation pilot who operates in the Anchorage Alaska terminal area reports complying with local Special Federal Air Regulations places aircraft in a position that they are unable to safely reach an airport to land in the case of an engine out landing.
A320 flight crew reported failing to engage the correct descent mode upon receiving arrival clearance.
Approach Controller reported that a traffic conflict and RA caused an aircraft to fly below the MVA.
EMB-505 flight crew reported landing and not holding short of a crossing runway as instructed by ATC.
Instructor pilot reported a NMAC while conducting touch and goes with a student at an uncontrolled airport.
B737 flight crew reported overshooting an altitude restriction climbing out of LAS. A wake vortex encounter reportedly caused some distraction.
IAD Tower Controller reported the procedure of allowing air ambulance helicopters to depart nearby hospitals non radar with inability to communicate in Class B creates a collision hazard.
Ramp personnel reported finding undocumented Hazmat in cargo compartment which had been mistakenly transported.
B737 Flight Crew reported the aircraft overshot the altitude restriction because the autopilot disconnected.

After passenger deplaning begins at the gate; a B737 flight crew experiences a strong wind gust that moves the aircraft two to three feet from the jetway; damaging the door. The ramp had a thin layer of ice and snow.
Flight crew reported pushing back from gate without receiving required final Hazmat report.

Upon breaking out at the completion of an instrument approach; crew of PC-12 noticed that the runway was closed with a large yellow X on the runway threshold. The entire airport had been closed for construction; but no NOTAM was issued. Crew diverted to an alternate airport.

A319 Dispatcher was informed of a possible bird strike during takeoff by one of his flights with no apparent ill effects. Airport personnel discovered aluminum debris at the end of the departure runway. The crew was informed and elected to continue. Post flight revealed missing pieces of acoustic liner on the number two engine.
First Officer reported being vectored to final approach course intercept inside the final approach fix and neglecting to complete all checklists as a result.
Air carrier Captain reported significant safety compromises with wearing face masks during the COVID-19 pandemic and associated problems with oxygen masks.
ZLA Center Controller received a handoff on an RNAV departure climbing to the wrong altitude.
A small aircraft pilot intercepting V21 southwest bound from HND was temporarily blinded by the Ivanpah Solar Field reflective glare.

A C150 Instructor conducting a checkout with a licensed pilot failed to select carburetor heat during stall training which resulted in the engine quitting. A successful off airport landing followed unsuccessful inflight start attempts.
EMB-175 flight crew reported that maintenance personnel noted lightning strike damage after a flight in which no lightning had been encountered.

ZLC Controller was working military aircraft recovering from their airspace to their destination. No coordination was accomplished ahead of time specifying routes or altitudes for the aircraft. One of the flights was NORDO and entered TRACON airspace without a handoff.

General aviation pilot reported the yellow runway turnoff lines at some points along DVT Runway 25L/07R were improperly painted with the reflective beads being added post-painting. This results in slippery conditions and ineffective braking.
Air carrier First Officer reported executing a go-around at SAN after encountering wake turbulence just before touchdown. Reporter stated the ATC spacing was 'too tight'.

C206 pilot reported encountering lower than forecasted conditions while on a VFR flight; in non IFR equipped aircraft. The pilot diverted; and conducted the flight into IMC; as this was the best option; until reaching VMC.
CL350 Captain reported parachute jumpers landing outside published landing zone creating a safety hazard for arrivals and departures.
Boeing flight crew reported flying to CID without a current weather report due to the ASOS being out of service.
Air carrier Captain reported that the PAPIs on left and right side of Runway 28 at SBGL gave two different indications.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with company's actions relating to COVID-19 possible exposure.
A320 Captain reported an NMAC event under ATC control during arrival.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned altitude and below the Minimim IFR Altitude.

An aircraft attempted two approaches to an airport but could not activate the airport/runway lights via Unicom frequency. The controller found out through the local Flight Service Station and Airport operator that the runway was closed due to snow on the runway and other equipment outages. The pilot stated none of these airport status issues were available to him from the Notams he checked prior to flying. The FSS also did not have this information in their Notams. The airport is in Canadian airspace.
A Ramp Dispatcher reported they reported fire coming out of an aircraft's engine to their ramp controller who did not take action to notify the flight in a timely manner.

SUX Tower Controller reported an aircraft taxied onto a closed taxiway due to the airport diagram chart not being updated and ATC being instructed not to include the taxiway change on the ATIS broadcast.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported undocumented Hazmat cargo was mistakenly transported.
B-737 Captain reported a communications breakdown with Dispatch and Ramp Supervisor regarding onboard Hazmat resulting in Hazmat cargo removal prior to departure.

Air carrier Captain reported a complete loss of GPS signal in the vicinity of Manila (RPLL). This anomaly was observed both during the arrival and subsequent departure to the airport and was not mentioned in neither the NOTAM nor ATIS information.

During takeoff above 100 knots a B737-700 encountered a windshear with 30-40kt airspeed fluctuation. Maximum thrust was maintained and aircraft turned away from mountains and out of maintain wave area.
B737 Captain reported concern that supplemental B737 Max Angle of Attack Disagree guidelines are contained within a Bulletin rather than the aircraft Quick Reference Handbook.
A B777-200 Technician reported being unable to find the proper procedure to clear a fault that appeared on the primary engine display.

ZJX Controller described a situation where weather was causing deviations. Close by airspace was a warning area that the Controller was trying to keep aircraft out of. Another adjacent Controller gave the reporter an aircraft on a heading to avoid weather but not avoid the warning area. Reporter advised front line manager of violation and aircraft not being handed off correctly.

Small aircraft pilot reported not being handed off to MYF Tower by SCT TRACON. Pilot was VFR and did not avoid delta airspace. Pilot reported he had been under IFR training lately and was expecting a handoff from the TRACON to the Tower.
First Officer reported that a flight attendant was injured after abrupt braking to avoid a ground collision with a fuel truck while taxiing.
B737 Captain reported that they encountered severe weather on approach. Due to heavy rains; the glideslope went full deflection high with an 'airspeed low' aural warning and momentary stick shaker.
D10 Controller reported IFR traffic departed without a release; resulting in a traffic conflict.

ZID controller reports of releasing and aircraft with a void time; then moving to the D-side. Controller never observed the aircraft tag up; and after void time called Tower to see what was up.  Tower advised they should be talking to aircraft. Aircraft was still on Tower's frequency; 30 miles West of airport at assigned altitude.
The pilot of a PA46 landed on a closed runway at a non-towered airport. Although the runway had an 'X' over the runway number; it was reported to have been very difficult to see.
Air carrier Captain climbing through FL240 reported a near miss with a UAS approximately 2-3 feet in diameter.
A Boeing 777 Maintenance Technician reported that it was unsafe to install a broadband antenna without an overhead hoist.
Single pilot reported an airspace deviation occurred while attempting to get an IFR clearance. Pilot was unable to get an IFR clearance due to ATC workload and told to try again in 30 minutes.
PA-28 Pilot on an IFR flight plan reported being issued opposite direction traffic in IMC; but the Controller took no action to separate the traffic.
Departing Cirrus pilot reported a NMAC; while on the upwind climb; with an incoming helicopter.
Air Carrier Captain reported ground return to gate to correct required Hazmat document errors.
Air carrier flight crew reported turbulence and a flap overspeed. One crew member reported the flying pilot had not flown for an extended period of time.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a bird strike near rotation and the subsequent return to the departure airport minutes later.
Business jet Captain reported operating at TRK with inaccurate and limited airport condition reports during airport surface icing conditions.
Pilot reported inadvertently flying into Class Bravo airspace and cited lack of flying during the COVID-19 pandemic as a contributing factor.
Local Controller reported an aircraft ground loop led to an airport closure.
Dash 8 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to SEA in trail of a B737. Reporter suggested a review of in-trail guidelines.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported that a washer was not installed after a main tire and wheel assembly was replaced; causing aircraft to be operated while un-airworthy.

ZLA Instructor and Developmental Controllers report a loss of separation due to an aircraft that had an autopilot failure. The aircraft advised ATC and the Developmental descended the aircraft out of RVSM airspace; but ended up less than the required 5 miles away from another aircraft.
B737 Captain reported that an improper maintenance procedure resulted in both thrust reversers failing to deploy on landing.
Certificate Holder reported helicopter was dispatched without approval.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported concern that minimum FAR staffing was inadequate for proper cabin operations.

B737-800 Captain described a situation he encountered under which a light passenger load can cause a nose heavy; out of CG situation unless the gate agent takes special precautions to disperse the passengers throughout the cabin.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unstabilized approach; citing FO's lack of flying and a late change by Approach Control as contributing factors.
GA pilot reported they landed on the opposite runway than what was in use at a non towered airport.
PHL Tower Controller reported an aircraft was squawking 7500 when it actually had a radio problem. Reporter was concerned that the aircraft was allowed to land at a major airport.
A319 flight crew reported a Flight Attendant sustained minor injuries in a wake turbulence encounter in cruise flight at FL350.
A Ramp Agent discovered 784 pounds of mail on the ramp which was listed on the weight and balance of a departed flight.
Phoenix TRACON Controller reported an after the fact loss of separation between two aircraft and confusion over the regulation.

EMB145 First Officer refused to continue flying an aircraft with both ACARS and the FMC on MEL after being told it would be taken out of service after return to base on the basis of safety of flight issues.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC while in the traffic pattern.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation during departure.

A11 Controller and A321 Captain reported a CFTT event occurred while the flight crew was flying the Anchorage 8 SID procedure. Controller stated aircraft on the Anchorage 8 SID often fly it incorrectly; resulting in low altitude alerts.  Controller suggests a SID redesign.
Cessna 150 pilot reported an NMAC with a B737 being vectored for approach to RIC.
Air Carrier Captain reported being vectored toward terrain on a visual approach into ABQ.

Air Carrier First Officer reported that when flying the ILS RWY 1R circle to land runway 30 at IAD following the glideslope sets one up for an unstabilized approach and questions why there is not an RNAV or FMS visual to runway 30.
A300 Flight Crew reported they had a flap problem but continued to their destination then later discovered they were not in compliance with the MEL.
Flight Crew operated 737-700 aircraft in violation of Minimum Equipment List restrictions.
LAX Tower Controller reported of a loss of separation when a SMO departure was too close to an LAX departure.
On an Airbus A320; an A-check job card called out to clean the Pitot Probe using Nitrogen in an enclosed environment.
CRJ-700 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence and deviating from assigned altitude; along with a Traffic Advisory on another aircraft.
Captain reported fatigued resulted in a go around due to aircraft not being properly configured.

DAB Instructing Controller reports of a NMAC that was reported to the facility a couple of days after it happened. Controller was training and was busy with traffic. Developmental did not call traffic until the two aircraft had passed.
B737 First Officer reported the FMC did not comply with the published procedure when selecting the IF for the approach.
A PA32R pilot reported a NMAC with a skydiving aircraft while on an IFR flight plan.
EMB-145 First Officer reported executing a go-around and diverting to an alternate following an unstable approach and an EGPWS windshear warning.
HOU Tower Controller states that that he has been controlling for two weeks with wind indication inoperative due to parts availability.
Air carrier Captain reported a near collison during taxi in LGA.
Airbus A319 flight crew reported that due to extreme turbulence they were unable to maintain altitude or speed.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported a disembarking passenger stumbled and fell through a space between the jet bridge and parked aircraft onto the pavement below.

A report from a Front Line Manager; a Controller and a flight crew about a Local Controller that used non-standard phraseology 'stop your roll' which was not understood by foreign flight crew.  The foreign aircraft was originally cleared for takeoff while another aircraft was clearing the same runway after landing.
A Helicopter pilot landing on private property reported their downwash blew a chair over which hit a bystander.
B787 Captain reported continuing to a long landing following speed deviations on short final following gusty winds and possible wake encounter.
ZAN Center Controller observed an IFR aircraft break off the approach and circle to an adjacent airport. The aircraft had not reported cancelling its IFR clearance.
B737 Captain reported the location of the Lima Pad at STL is incorrectly depicted on the 10-9 Airport Diagram.
P80 Approach Controller reported an unidentified VFR target not displaying a transponder created a conflict with several air carriers on final approach.
MD-11 flight crew reported that after loading UFOB into the FMS; a Vr mismatch message was received due to the UFOB information not being shared with the other FMS.
Captain of B737 reported the lack of visual cues needed to safely park the aircraft in the presence of close proximity ground equipment and material.

A Captain reported the gate agent retracted the jetway before taking the closeout paperwork and closing the main cabin door. No jetway presence exposed the flight attendant to a potentially hurtful fall while closing the heavy door.
EMB-175 Captain reported no NOTOC of the inflight ACARs notification of undocumented Hazmat cargo [battery powered wheelchair] in cargo compartment.
B757 Captain reported refusing a last-minute clearance for the RNAV (RNP) Z RWY 35R at DEN; stating ATC allowed insufficient time to prepare for the procedure.
TUS Tower controller reported not noticing a rejected takeoff; due to heavy workload from combined sectors and lack of staffing; and landing traffic causing runway conflict with the RTO aircraft.
B757 Captain reported that an engine reverser has been deferred for an extended period. The Captain also stated the deferred issue reoccurs quite often despite maintenance action.
C170 pilot reported a loss of control and runway excursion while landing with a crosswind.
ZBW Controller reported having to work combined sectors with no assist position during busy complex traffic periods due to weather deviations and lack of controller staffing.
KC-135 Pilot reported while attempting to refuel another aircraft; turbulence made their aircraft go into a full nose-up pitch after an emergency breakaway; resulting in an altitude block deviation.
FWA TRACON Controller reported potential loss of separation due to a late handoff.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a loss of control and aircraft upset following a wake turbulence encounter on approach to BWI in trail of a B737 which caused a 90 degree bank.
A pilot towing a glider at a non towered airport reported a NMAC with traffic entering the pattern.
First Officer reported that a taxi driver driving recklessly and possibly under the influence caused the crew to be unfit for flight.
Two separate pilots reported communication issues with ATC while attempting to cancel IFR flight plans during approach to C09.
Air carrier First Officer reported not recognizing a charted speed restriction on departure out of DAY.
A Gate Agent reported being asked to operate the jet bridge to the aircraft during severe lightning.
NCT Controller reports of aircraft not flying the correct arrival to a specific runway.
Technician reported discovering the #2 engine high stage valve disconnected when installing the Pressure Regulating Shut Off Valve.
SLC Airport Diagram Taxiway G is closed north of Taxiway V. Taxiway H also shifted west; north of Taxiway H11. Current NOTAMs do not reflect Taxiway G and Taxiway H changes.
A pilot reported they had to respond to an RA for known traffic that was restricted above their assigned altitude.
Pilot reports deviation from Air Traffic Control vectors.
Flight Attendants reported COVID-19 seating related issues involving flight attendant pre-assigned seats.
Tower Controller reported departing aircraft were cleared passed ILS critical areas despite heavy fog and inbound IFR traffic.
B737 flight crew reported an abrupt stop during pushback due to tow pin failure resulted in a flight attendant injury.
C210 pilot reported a runway excursion likely caused by a crosswind condition.
EMB-145 First Officer reported responding to a TCAS RA on approach to PHL to avoid VFR traffic in the approach corridor.
Air carrier First Officer reported that while on the MSO RNAV Runway 30 approach; it was discovered the waypoint ROKNY is misspelled as RONKY on the Jeppesen chart.
Maintenance reported they removed vinyl wrap to accomplish fuselage skin repair.
An Air Carrier pilot conducting a Visual Approach at night with the airport and terrain received a GPWS alert and continued their approach.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported Hazmat listed on departure cargo manifest was not on aircraft at destination.
Corporate jet flight crew reported a heading deviation while on the JAWBN SIX ARRIVAL to BFI.

A flight crew was being vectored for an approach that was not in use. ATC realized their mistake when the crew reported no localizer signal. While being vectored for another approach; they received a terrain warning and climbed appropriately. They were then vectored around for a normal landing.
BE33 pilot reported correct maintenance procedures were not followed resulting in an engine seizure and off airport landing.
Flight Attendant reported feeling pressured to sit in a cabin jumpseat near passengers assigned to company blocked seats. Flight Attendant was later informed of exposure to COVID-19.
DFW Tower Controller reported of an aircraft landing on a runway without a landing clearance.

A Chicago Center (ZAU) Front line Manager (FLM) reports of confusion concerning a reference to a Special Use Airspace (SUA) and whether it is hot or not and tries to figure it out. Aircraft are in the area and then they are notified that it is hot and has been for about 15 minutes.
Air carrier Captain questioned the legality of a new electronic tablet case that is magnetic and could affect the magnetic compass.
Captain reported accepting an overwater clearance that exceeded the 162NM limit due to inability to verify the route on charts or the FMS.
Air Carrier First Officer flying CRJ-900 aircraft reported flying toward terrain on departure.

A B737 crew reported a ground taxiway incursion while taxiing for takeoff because the First Officer was heads down with checklist duties; out of the situational awareness loop and the Captain is not aware of checklist status.

PA-28 pilot successfully used all weather reports available to select best airport for landing to wait out passing thunderstorms. The pilot chose the airport because the ground station reported the airport as VFR clear. Hours later; the pilot discovered that the ground station had a NOTAM for being unreliable on cloud clearances.
ZSE Controller reported an aircraft flying below the Minimum IFR Altitude after relieving another controller.
ZID Controller somehow entered into the computer that an aircraft was landing at an intersection instead of the intended airport by mistake. The receiving Controller was confused.
CRJ-200 Captain reported some confusion when he received a PDC in ATL that seemed to indicate a clearance to 10;000 ft; but was really a clearance to 8;000 ft.
PCT TRACON and DCA Tower Controller reported an unsafe situation involving VIP movements.
A Boeing Captain reported the need for additional training on new checklist procedures.
PA28R pilot reported a NMAC with a medical helicopter while setting up for a flight maneuver.

An air carrier crew anticipated an RNAV approach to MAF but was assigned the VOR RWY 16R. Unfamiliar with either the airport or approach; the crew got behind the aircraft and executed a go around during the unstabilized approach. The aircraft oversped during the go around.

Air carrier Captain reported receiving erratic signals from the CKB Runway 21 ILS while on approach in IMC. The crew disconnected the autopilot and the approach was completed uneventfully. Ground personnel mentioned to the Captain that several pilots referenced the irregular quality of the ILS signal at CKB.
Pilot reported flying out of radio contact with the Center due to complacency from the reduction of air traffic caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.
B737 Captain reported his concerns with his company's new policy of entering weight and balance information into the FMC during taxi instead of prior to leaving the gate.
Maintenance Technician reported questions and concerns relating to cleaning techniques for aircraft during the COVID-19 pandemic.
ERJ-190 Captain reported a Flight Attendant was injured during a turbulence encounter.
B737 flight crew reported that several ground vehicles failed to yield; resulting in a critical ground conflict.
Airbus Captain reported cabin crew not sufficiently trained for Airbus safety procedures.
Airline pilot reported receiving a Terrain Warning after preparing to land initially at the wrong airport.
