Pilot reported requesting approval to fly through the Class Delta; which he is granted. Pilot was told to report clear of Delta. 6.5 miles west of the tower the pilot reports clear of the Delta. Controller gave pilot a frequency change. Pilot then realized they are within the confines of a restricted area. Controller is later told by another facility that he needed to call the center for a possible airspace deviation. Pilot wonders why controller let him do what he did without warning him.
Pilot reported ATC instructing them to hold short of a runway due to a small aircraft landing on another runway.  Pilot advised ATC unable to LAHSO but did so.
C182 pilot reported a prop strike after allowing a pilot that was not familiar with the aircraft to attempt landing.
MD-83 Captain reported he felt his operational authority was being infringed upon while dealing with a mechanical issue at the gate prior to departure.

Flight crew reported that while on a night arrival to LFPG they were issued a new arrival by ATC. They had difficulty finding the new arrival because; recently Jeppesen Charting expanded the category APPROACH to include RNAV ARRIVALS and RNAV NIGHT ARRIVALS which are not approaches. Reportedly the RNAV; NIGHT NAV distinction appears arbitrary and confusing.
CNO Controller reports of an airspace violation and an airborne conflict caused by the overlying TRACON not giving them a point out or remaining clear of the Tower's airspace.
A maintenance Technician reported that after accomplishing an oil service on a Saab SF340B; the aircraft departed then returned to the airport due to the oil service door coming open in flight.

A B737 Captain reported the recent preflight checklist response changes contained some system setting and selection responses which were not completed or were inaccurate. This situation lead him to feel the policy was promoting a normalization of deviance.
SCT TRACON Controller reported that an aircraft went through the localizer and ended up in a higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude then was issued.
B737 First Officer reported departing after a 14 hour maintenance delay due to multiple MEL issues.

While encountering turbulence and deviating for large build ups; the crew experienced a flap overspeed; then was caught high and fast so executed a go-around. There was some confusion as the Captain assumed aircraft control during the go-around.
RV6 single pilot reported a Restricted Airspace Violation.

B737NG Captain reported flying a visual approach with LOC Back Course back up to SNA Runway 2L; executed a missed approach at an EGPWS TERRAIN Warning in weather far worse than reported on ATIS. The second RNAV 2L approach was flown to minimums for a successful landing.

A CLT TRACON Controller reported that he had issued an approach to an aircraft that reported the ILS out of service. The Controller thought the ILS was operational even though it was not because of information he observed on the National Information Display System (NIDS). The Controller reported the NIDS does not effectively show the status of each runway.
B737 flight crew reported that a wake turbulence encounter resulted in altitude; track; and speed deviations.
Stearman pilot reported a conflict at touchdown with another opposite direction light aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported cross-contamination concerns with the increase in food and beverage services provided to passengers during flights.
Air carrier Maintenance Technician reported Functional Flight Check not accomplished per Company Maintenance Manual requirement.
Load Planner reported ramp personnel incorrectly loaded HAZMAT.
SLC Local Controller reported sudden wind shifts to a crosswind caused simultaneous go-arounds and one aircraft below the MVA.
ELP TRACON Controller reported a NMAC between a VFR and an IFR aircraft. Controller lost situational awareness while being distracted with a third aircraft.
Cessna reported flat-spotting the main gear tires after applying brakes with too much force following a wake turbulence encounter from a B767 landing on a parallel runway.

A DFW Front Line Manager (FLM) reports of a procedure that was not followed which led to a wake turbulence event and a procedural error involving a heavy aircraft. The FLM questions why this procedure is in place.

A320 flight crew and the Controller involved described a landing on Runway 11 at EWR during which the crew was instructed to hold short of Runway 22L for landing traffic. The instruction came during the rollout and the crew believed it left them few options but to brake hard and ultimately stopped before Runway 22R.

A Captain flying the NAT C oceanic route westbound discovered the iPad JEPP PRO incorrectly depicted GAPLI as the Oceanic Gateway waypoint when DINIM at the EISN to EGGX airspace transition is the correct Gateway waypoint.

ZOA Controller describes a read back hear back issue resulting in an aircraft descending lower than the Controller wanted. Controller complains about the noise level in the control room as a distraction. Descending aircraft was traffic for another aircraft 1000 feet below what controllers thought was the altitude assigned; resulting in a loss of separation.

ZDV Controller reports of opening up a position and not having the correct information displayed on ERAM (Enroute Automation Modernization). No one entered information into the computer that a certain airspace was going to be hot. An Aircraft was in the hot airspace. Controller thought it was the Front Line Managers duty to enter this information.

A TRACON Controller reported they were distracted with radio and communications problems and did not notice an aircraft conducting a missed approach had conflicting traffic that the missed approach aircraft received a TCAS/RA for.
Air carrier First Officer reported a full bus of people on the employee bus which put everyone at risk of COVID-19 and increased risk for injury on the bus ride.
S46 TRACON Controller reported separation was lost; before pilot applied visual separation was obtained.

B777 encountered severe turbulence at cruise prior to descent which resulted in injuries to flight attendants and passengers. An announcement had been made about 15 minutes prior to warn of the turbulence.
J3 Cub pilot reported loss of directional control and a ground loop on the runway during a crosswind landing.
A321 Captain reported that the ferry flight checklist was inadequate; causing an unsecured galley cart to impact the forward bulkhead during landing.
Flight Instructor reported that the close proximity of several airports using the same CTAF causes frequency jamming.
EMB-175 flight crew reported a passenger's portable battery overheated so the Flight Attendant placed the battery in a water container as per company procedure.
Banner tow pilot reported operating below the FAR mandated floor of 1000 feet AGL while circling an outdoor event due to turbulence and downdrafts.
MIA Departure Controller and flight crew reported a NMAC with a VFR aircraft operating in the departure corridor.
CRJ-200 Captain reported the First Officer observed a UAV on short final to JFK.
Atlanta TRACON Controller reported problems associated with the Optimized Profile Descents into Atlanta airport. Aircraft are reportedly slowed which keeps them from descending on the profile.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger misconduct incident.
Dispatcher reported having to work too many flights during shift.
Ground employee expressed concern that the aircraft pits are not being sanitized; potentially exposing workers to COVID-19.
B737 Captain reported experiencing a lightning strike after which there was an aircraft vibration of an unknown source.
Pilot reported misidentifying conditions requiring additional log book entries and MEL actions and operating the aircraft.

B787 First Officer reported that late notification in flight regarding a Flight Attendant's illness put the Flight Attendant's health in jeopardy and resulted in possible action against the Company by Customs and Immigration at the destination.

Reporter states that there is inconsistencies in the application of MEL deferrals as well as conflicts between the information in the MEL and the Flight Operations Manual in regards to the pilot EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) and the MEL associated with the EFB mount on the aircraft when it is missing/inoperative.
Air carrier flight crew reported forgetting to turn off APU and later exceeding the APU ceiling.
Pilot reported being on a visual approach into Atlanta; slowing for separation; and then after landing being told by TRACON that they should not have slowed.

ZBW Controller reported receiving a non-automated handoff from BDL below the Minimum IFR Altitude (MIA) indicating climbing to an assigned altitude of 4;000. The aircraft was actually assigned an altitude below the ZBW Controller's MIA.
Wing Walker reported he would be exposed to jet blast if he took up his normal position during pushback from a particular gate.
TRACON Instructor and Developmental reported a military aircraft that was issued holding instructions went past the turning point and entered an area of a higher MVA.
Air carrier flight crew reported being high on the approach due to task saturation; communications errors and focusing outside for traffic.
Small aircraft pilot inadvertently entered a sporting event TFR; near BFI;  even though he was working with ATC and had previously checked the NOTAMs.
EA50 pilot reported a momentary loss of control due to what was believed to be clear air turbulence.
Ground employee reported concerns with being notified that someone he had contact with tested positive for COVID-19.
Chicago Center Controllers reported a problem associated with incorrect routing and being told of a fix they do not like to use for it.

Air Carrier Captain reported that two go-arounds had to be initiated at DEN while being vectored for visual approaches to Runways 16L and 16R due to side-by-side placement of aircraft resulting in TCAS RAs.
CRJ200 Captain reported communications breakdown with Dispatch resulting weight and balance errors.
J-3 Piper Super Cub pilot reported an NMAC event during landing pattern entry with departure traffic in crosswind turn.
B737 flight crew reported similar sounding fixes on the SERFR STAR caused confusion and navigation to the incorrect fix.
A TRACON Controller reported a vector was issued to an aircraft which directed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

B737-700 flight crew reported overshooting an altitude restriction on the RNAV RNP Z arrival to 16R in DEN. Captain reporter stated multiple ATC changes to the arrival clearance contributed to the event; and the F/O expressed desire for airline procedural changes.

F11 Radar Controller reported of a conflict between two aircraft. The reporter was vectoring one aircraft to final; while the second aircraft was also being vectored. A loss of separation ensued. Reporter attributed loss of separation due to workload and staffing.

A crew flying the ORD WATSN 2 RNAV VOLGR transition to ILS Runway 27L failed to selected the 281° heading at WATSN to intercept the localizer because the RNAV procedure did not indicate the transition. The transition was only on the ILS 27L chart.
Air Carrier First Officer attributed a traffic conflict on approach to PHL to apparent controller inexperience.
Fractional Captain reported departing non-towered airport without IFR clearance.
SR22 pilot reported he returned to the departure airport after encountering icing conditions in the initial climb.
Trainee Controller reported they observed an aircraft cleared for an approach fly off course and below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
DA40 pilot reported a near miss with a VFR aircraft not on CTAF frequency while conducting an RNAV approach to HAF airport.
Pilot reported a NMAC while attempting to land at uncontrolled airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported an entry error while loading the STAR into the FMC/FMS.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger sprayed Lysol on and around his seat.
Flight crew reported ATC commented on a missed crossing altitude that may have been a due to clearance confusion.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a Tower called them repeatedly on a direct line instead of a line through the loudspeaker while it was busy causing them to miss aircraft transmissions.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on a visual approach to destination airport.

ZID Controller reported observing a loss of separation between two aircraft that he was not working and in another Controller's airspace. Reporter stated the flash through procedure may have contributed to the event.
Air carrier Captain reported making a wrong turn on an arrival; but corrected soon after Approach Control pointed out the error.

ZID Controller and Front Line Manager (FLM) reported of an aircraft that was encountering icing conditions and had to descend. The aircraft advised ATC and started a descent below the MVA. Controller asked if the aircraft had the terrain insight and was VFR. FLM confused the Controller and the Controller then cancelled the aircraft's IFR clearance. The Controller was relieved before knowing if the aircraft landed at its destination safely.
PWM TRACON Controller reported he observed a NMAC reported by an aircraft with an unidentified VFR target during a partial radar outage.

Captain reports about a B777 aircraft with a chronic history of Left Engine Bleed Trips after takeoff. He questions why the Left Engine Pressure Sensor that is causing all the in-flight Bleed trips remains deferred and not fixed? Also; why was the Left Sensor deferral NOT on the Pilot's Dispatch flight plan; instead of being hidden deep in the paperwork?
Center Controller reported they did not recognize the route an aircraft filed placed it below the Minimum IFR Altitudes.
B737-800 Captain reported the process of taking delivery of a new aircraft from Boeing is flawed in the areas of weight and balance as well as fuel density calculations.
B737 flight crew reported unexpected turbulence and windshear causing a 3;000 feet altitude loss.
Glider pilot reported landing in a field when weather conditions made a return to the field impossible.
BE35 pilot reported encountering thunderstorms enroute that were not displayed in a timely manner on his XM weather datalink.
First Officer reported becoming task saturated due to ATC clearance modification resulting in altitude excursion and momentary EGPWS Alert. Fight completed an uneventful landing.
Maintenance Technician reported the flight department communicated to pilots that maintenance is not to be involved in engine fuel leak check involving an engine idle run.
C172 Flight Instructor reported an airborne conflict with a UAV in the airport traffic pattern.
Widebody transport aircraft First Officer disagreed with the applicability of an MEL maintenance used to defer the battery voltage selector switch.

HCF TRACON Supervisor reported a go around and a departure from parallel runways were cleared to conflicting headings with one of the aircraft assigned an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger vomited multiple times during push back. Captain elected to return to gate and have passenger removed.
ZDC controllers reported being flooded with traffic and weather then having a disagreement with another Controller which led to unplanned holding and an airspace violation.
Technician reported finding 5 chaffed wires in the First Officer's control column and made an unauthorized splice repair to one wire.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported difficulty with their available tools increase their workload.
Seattle Center Controllers reported an aircraft descended below the cleared altitude as well as the Minimum IFR Altitude.
An A319 Flight Attendant observed an excessive gap between the aircraft which the Gate failed to close. A young boy slipped on the icy rubber boot and was caught by his father before he fell through.
NCT Departure Controller observed an aircraft fly the lost communication procedure for an RNAV departure which put it into confliction with other aircraft.
Center Controller reported confusion when the Front Line Manager walked by and said some airspace was going hot; but did not know which airspace.
Center Controller reported an aircraft left its assigned altitude and descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
AC11 pilot reported encountering weather enroute that forced him to exit and reenter US ADIZ airspace without a clearance.
EMB-175 First Officer reported that an altitude excursion; aircraft overspeed; and moderate turbulence resulted from a mountain wave encounter at FL350.
Air carrier flight crew reported a flap configuration problem on departure resulting in a speed deviation. First Officer made reference to lack of flying as a contributing factor.
B787 First Officer reported that PDC clearances are confusing and should be reformatted.

GA pilot reported misinterpreting an altitude alert and descended from assigned altitude while on approach. Lack of familiarity with the instrument panel and lack of recent flying due to the COVID-19 pandemic were cited as contributing factors.
SAN Tower Controllers reported receiving multiple aircraft on final from the TRACON resulting in insufficient separation.
Captain reported descending below the charted TBARR crossing altitude; following a misinterpretation of ATC's; 'descend via; except maintain' instructions.

A ZJX Center Controller reported the Traffic Management Unit did not manage the flow to another sector causing Warning Area airspace violations and a traffic confliction during a period of weather deviations.

A Controller had two aircraft cleared to land on parallel runways. One aircraft was lined up with the wrong runway. Later; the same Controller was working Ground Control and received approval for an aircraft to cross the runways.
ZAB Controller reported about an aircraft that had permission to spill out of a restricted area; and the associated problems.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering windshear conditions while on approach; executed the escape maneuver; and diverted.
Pilot reported a track heading deviation when executing a 360 turn resulting from confusing a fix name which sounds like the number 360.
A helicopter pilot reported a disagreement between them an ATC about the departure instructions.
PA28 instructor reported three traffic conflicts requiring evasive action.
Flight Attendants reported a passenger with infant sitting near emergency exit.

A flight crew reported encountering windshear conditions on final approach and performed a go-around. The missed approach maneuver was successfully accomplished; however dynamic flight conditions resulted in a deviation from their assigned altitude.
MEM Controllers and Air Carrier Captain reported a go around due to aircraft on a nearby taxiway.
A PNE Controller reports of three different issues he had while working all the Tower combined.

B737 Captain reported two pushback events at the same airport during which different tug drivers placed the aircraft's nose gear in a jack knifed position severe enough that a more experienced ramp agent was required to reposition the aircraft properly. Poor training was cited.
Pilot reported that a crosswind on landing resulted in a runway excursion.
SFO Ground Controller reported a pilot was confused by the departure clearance received via CPDLC.

Air carrier Captain flying the OAK BANND ONE RNAV Arrival to Runway 12 noted the procedure design requires crossing the WHYEE and HIRMO waypoints below SFO Class B at or above 5;000 and 4;000 feet respectively at 230 kts which is above the 200 kts speed constraint.
C172 pilot reported another aircraft taxied across the runway during touch and go.
TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict and cited traffic volume being very high for one Controller.

ELP TRACON FLM reported that he approved a radar outage request from maintenance with a 30 minute recall. As time came close to the end of the requested time the FLM checked with maintenance and the operator requested more time. FLM denied request and said he needed radar back in 30 minutes. He then contacted Maintenance and they told him tough luck it is down.
Ramp Crew reported two electric drycell wheelchairs incorrectly tagged; loaded; and shipped.
Ground employee reported incorrectly loading Hazmat. Additionally reported being summoned to correct the Hazmat load.

An SNA Controller reports of working a combined position with a helicopter that requested landing at a specific FBO. The position gets split and the Controller keeps the aircraft that is now in another controller's area. The Controller clears aircraft to go where they requested and has what he describes as a NMAC with a departure that the other Local was working.

Cessna Skymaster pilot reported inadvertently climbing VFR into Class A airspace due to convective activity. He negotiated with the Center Controller and a Military Controller for a new IFR clearance away from the convective activity; restricted airspace; and out of the MOA to his destination.
B737-900 flight crew reported overshooting final on a visual approach into LAX; citing a wake encounter and a confusing clearance as contributing.
Two Center Controllers reported an aircraft responded to an RA for traffic as well as encountered moderate turbulence causing injuries to some of the flight crew.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of GPS signal in cruise operations reportedly due to GPS jamming exercises.
Helicopter pilot reported after maintenance work was performed; the mechanics failed to complete all required testing to return aircraft to an airworthy condition.

ZBW controller reported of a similar sounding fix that the pilot had entered and was flying towards. Aircraft was questioned and the confusion of the fix name was determined to be part of the problem. ATC had one clearance the crew had a different one.
B747 flight crew reported that ATC instructed them to maintain greater than 250 kts. airspeed below 10;000 ft.
A pilot on approach to SJC Runway 30L near KLIDE at 6;000 ft was told by his passenger their aircraft was passed by a UAS traveling north.
Balloon pilot reported loss of control while landing in gusty wind conditions that led to minor passenger injuries.

Large Transport Aircraft Captain described a sports team charter that he believes highlights the company's lax attitude toward these operations in general and weight and balance calculations in particular.

ZAN Controller described a situation where the trainee he was instructing did not ensure the correct facility was coordinated with. This caused an error of altitude to be coordinated with another Center.
Cessna 310 pilot reported TRACON advised him he had entered BUR Class C airspace.
Pilot reported a possible microburst or other weather event resulted in a hard landing that caused a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft.

Air carrier flight crew reported overshooting a charted altitude during a go-around. The Captain stated the very low aircraft weight associated with the COVID-19 pandemic resulted in an abnormally high climb rate.
C182 Instructor reported an airborne conflict with opposite direction traffic due to failure of understanding radio transmission at a non-towered airport.

Fractional jet First Officer reported that while approaching ELD they were kept high during the arrival due to thunderstorms. While descending through 1;300 feet at a high descent rate the EGPWS alerted 'CAUTION TERRAIN' followed by 'PULL UP.' As the descent rate slowed; the alerts ceased.
Maintenance Technician reports Air Carrier tries to prevent maintenance personnel from completing safety reports through harassment and intimidation.
Commercial Airline ground personnel reported observing multiple cleanup protocol violations dealing with large fuel spill.
C-152 Pilot reported a NMAC event during landing pattern training at non-towered airport with an non-transmitting aircraft.
CDW Tower Controllers reported traffic off of nearby N07 airport entered Class D without communications; which conflicted with ATC controlled pattern traffic.
Air carrier First Officer reported a brief terrain warning while descending into a mountainous area airport in VMC conditions.
B737 Maintenance Technician reported that the flight crew ignored the safety requirement while maintenance was working on the aircraft.
B747-400 First Officer and relief pilot reported executing a go-around after an altitude deviation on approach to WRI when the ILS signal malfunctioned.
C182 pilot reported that gusty wind conditions on final resulted in a loss of aircraft control.
B737-700 Captain reported having several FMC malfunctions and questioned whether the GPS MEL used in this event fully covered all the FMC issues encountered during this flight.

Two instances of military aircraft exiting the confines of the MOA without clearance. In one case an aircraft came into conflict with an air carrier. In a separate instance an aircraft exited below the base of the MOA and returned to its base without clearance or contacting the ATC Facility.

While in cruise flight; DHC8-400 flight crew received word from Dispatch that they had departed with an incorrect load report and that the correct load would have put the aircraft out of CG limits for takeoff. However; the revised load report did not reflect an occupied jumpseat which had put the aircraft within CG.
Air carrier First Officer reported FAR 117 violations due to a change in Company policies.
OAK Tower Controller reported an aircraft turned off the runway onto a closed taxiway. Reporter stated this has happened before and the exit needs to be repainted.

SCT Controller describes a situation where an aircraft is issued the published missed approach at BUR and starts the turn early causing a conflict with another aircraft along with an airspace violation.
The pilot of a vintage military aircraft reported that while in cruise an airliner crossed overhead without warning.
A ZHU ARTCC area Controller In Charge allowed an aircraft to operate in MOA/Air Traffic Control Assigned Airspace even though it had not scheduled the use.

A ZDV Controller and pilot report confusion after the aircraft departs IFR at one airport while the Controller was waiting for the aircraft to depart off of a different airport. The identifiers for the airports are close and caused the confusion.

ZOA Controller reports of an aircraft that is slow in turning and ends up in another facilities airspace leading to an airspace violation. Controller states this aircraft turned like an Airbus even though it was a different company.
Small transport pilot reported a runway excursion due to snow on the runway.
PA-18 pilot reported that during landing roll-out; the tires contacted some weeds which caused the airplane to flip over.

ORF Local Controller requested release on a departure aircraft from the Departure Controller. Aircraft was released on an unusual heading; Local Controller thought this heading was to avoid traffic inbound.  Loss of separation occurred when inbound traffic turned inbound.
ZOB Controller observed a false aircraft target at 29;000 feet in the path of another enroute aircraft.
A Center trainee controller reported they waited too long to climb an aircraft to a higher altitude to stay above the Minimum IFR Altitude for its route.

ZMA Front Line Manager (FLM) reports of flow control through a sector that TMU had in place; but the surrounding Towers were not complying with the Call For Release (CFR) that was in place. By not complying; one sector had to hold traffic to attain 10 minutes in trail separation. While working on this problem the FLM noticed a possible loss of separation.
B777 First Officer reported that the flight had difficulty with Dispatch in locating their position with the company system.

A Center Controller reported they handed off an arriving aircraft to an Approach Control and coordinated a plan to resolve a confliction with a departure from another Approach Control but observed the arriving aircraft descend through the altitude of the departing aircraft.
A B737 Captain reported inaccurate runway condition from ATC; air-field condition reports (FICON); and dispatch; which led to unsafe operations.
Captain reported missing a required crossing altitude on LINKK ONE STAR and ILS runway 27 into IAH.
ZLC Center Controller reported the need to delay issuing IFR departure clearances due to the sector being overloaded with traffic with no Assist position open due to lack of staffing.

A CL604 Captain reported an airborne conflict after receiving clearance for an ILS approach. The reporting party stated that the airspace is poorly designed to protect the ILS approach and should be reviewed.
A Flight Attendant reported on some confusion about whether a Portable Oxygen Concentrator was FAA approved.
Air carrier First Officer reported database update issues while transiting through Brazilian airspace. Crew were cleared to waypoints not in the updated database.
Air Carrier First Officer reported their flight had been rerouted but the reroute had never been passed to the crew; causing confusion when they checked in with Potomac Departure.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with wearing a face mask.
A321 Captain reported a conflict with a drone near the outer marker of the approach to MCO airport.
Air Taxi Captain reported excessive vibration on descent which caused the aircraft to be removed from service at the destination airport.
B737 Captain reported encountering jet wash on short final at SFO shortly after a B777 departure.

B737 flight crew experienced nil braking after landing on a 7;500 foot runway which had reported good braking. The aircraft is unable to stop and rolls onto the overrun; requiring the aid of a tug to be extricated. Tight spacing on approach led the reporters to use flaps 30 and auto brakes 3 for the landing. Snowfall was increasing during the minutes leading up to the landing.
Pilot reported that ATC issued a terrain clearance warning while aircraft was turning in the vicinity of mountains.
ZLC Center Controller reported that an altitude reservation was started earlier than planned which caused an aircraft to have to alter its route.

Ground Controller reported they issued runway crossing instructions to an aircraft and as the aircraft crossed the hold short lines the controller noticed an aircraft on short final which was issued go around instructions.
A Tower Supervisor reports that a Local Controller placed two aircraft head to head to each other at the same altitude and failed to take appropriate action to separate successive departures.
ZME Controller reported two aircraft had similar call signs. One aircraft may have taken the other's clearance.
A321 Captain reported airborne Dispatch notification of passenger cargo bag containing unconnected Lithium Battery onboard belonging to a passenger who did not board flight.
Corporate Captain reported that the published NOTAM was ambiguous using two airports rather than one.
Air Carrier Captain reports Departure procedure does not specify correct departure runway.
Technician reported incorrectly installing leading edge slat actuator torque tube; causing flaps and slats to be  unable to retract.
Air carrier First Officer reported having a problem communicating with Tower Controller at RDU because of a NOTAM'd outage of the frequency.

Air carrier Captain reported a disabled aircraft on intended landing runway resulted in their flight being vectored away from their approach path.  The additional vectoring caused them to be concerned with their fuel state and made a request to be cleared as soon as possible.  The crew was cleared for another approach and landing and reported being unstable/high before safely landing the aircraft.
GA pilot reported experiencing a NMAC that resulted from ATC sequencing 3 aircraft for landing by using 270- and 360-turns.
Air carrier flight crew reported mis-communication issues with ramp crew during pushback.
GA pilot reported making an off-airport landing that resulted from fuel exhaustion.
B-757 Captain reported Hazmat company procedural error of gate departure without required Hazmat document.
Dispatcher reported having to work too many aircraft due to staffing related issues after several dispatchers called in sick.
B747 Captain reported issues with ground personnel while parked on the ramp resulting in spontaneous movement of the aircraft.
Ground employee reported wanting to know why the airline is using aircraft that do not have a functioning Auxiliary Power Unit.
Flight Attendant reported that another Flight Attendant refused to occupy the appropriate jumpseat due to social distancing concerns.

A DA-40 instructor pilot reported executing a go-around on short final after a military helicopter on an adjacent runway converged on his aircraft. ATC questioned the need for a go-around; but the reporter felt ATC should not question a pilot's go-around decision based on safety concerns.
ZTL Center Developmental Controller reported issuing an altitude to an aircraft that was below the MIA.

LAX Controllers reported of a loss of separation that they did not know about until well after the fact. Someone from the service center advised the Front Line Manager (FLM) about the loss of separation; and in doing so were not sure about the rules and needed more clarification. The controllers want the procedure fixed so this does not happen again. Controllers thought they were using the 15 degree course separation rule.
A Controller In Charge also working control positions in the Tower reported not noticing the Local Controller clear an aircraft for takeoff with a vehicle on the active runway.

An aircraft was cleared for a Visual Approach. The pilot made the approach to the wrong airport to a closed runway. Pilot narrative states the airport was closed however the airport was not closed only the runway they made the approach to.
Air carrier Ramp Agent reported receiving mail from the USPS with 'Dangerous Goods Lithium Battery' labels covered up with stickers by the USPS.

Flight crew reported they departed without an ETOPS Pre-Departure Check and an open AML write-up for the same issue. Maintenance personnel reported the aircraft was not assigned to anyone; therefore the work was not accomplished.

Flight crew received a late runway and arrival change to LAX. When they programed the new data into their flight computers they experienced difficulty determining the final published altitude. They attributed this difficulty to a design flaw in the way altitude restrictions were depicted on the chart.

Local Controller at LAS reported of aircraft landing on Runway 19R at the same time as another aircraft landed on Runway 25R. These runways intersect at the departure end. The Local Controller working Runways 25L and 25R failed to advise the Local Controller working Runways 19L and 19R of their traffic which is a required advisement.

Pilot reported going to the de-ice pad; but not knowing the de-ice pad they were supposed to go to was further down the taxiway. Reporter claims the further east de-ice pad is not displayed on the DTW 10-9 page.
Air Carrier Captain reported poor braking on landing in LEX on a snow covered runway that had been reported as 'wet'.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on final approach into ATL.
Airline Ramp Personnel reported aircraft gate return to correct Hazmat transport violation of dry ice loaded in same cargo compartment as live animals.
Pilot reported a hard landing and then a couple days later was informed the aircraft had a dent on it.
A B737-800 Maintenance Technician reported an MEL is confusing when relating to 'MINIUM MANEUVER SPEED' Indication.
GIV Captain reported altitude deviation on climb out.
B747 Captain reported extended maintenance delay and extreme heat prior to departure leading to exhaustion and a fatigue call.
B747 First Officer reported Hazmat cargo improperly loaded. Hazmat shipment not loaded in accordance to Hazmat loading procedures.
Technician reported concerns with company procedures related to notification of possible exposure to other employees who have tested positive for COVID-19.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported severe turbulence during descent resulting in injury to one of the flight attendants.
CRJ-900 Captain reported returning to the gate after rejecting the takeoff because of nil braking conditions on the takeoff runway.
Tower Controller reported the facility was not staffed sufficiently resulting in the Controller having to work a sector longer than the mandated time on position.

Flight Attendant reported that due to aircraft weight and balance considerations; passengers were required to sit close together in specific rows. The crew member expressed concern that this was not conducive to health and social distancing during the COVID-19 pandemic.
B757-200 Captain reported refusing aircraft due to improper MEL application.
Flight Crew reported last minute runway change on a requested VOR approach and low on FAF due to lack of familiarity with airport.

An IFR single engine aircraft pilot reported a near miss with another IFR aircraft at 8;000 feet after both aircraft were advised of the other but were unable to detect the other until 150 feet apart. ZNY provided no separation guidance to either IFR pilot.
CNO Tower Controller reported of an unsafe operation when an aircraft on the downwind turned to follow the wrong aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported an unstabilized approach resulting in a go-around.
B737 flight crew reported exercising Captain's authority during landing after tower instructed a go-around.

Captain reported contradictory and confusing information contained in the PSP approach charts and the airport diagram regarding the use of Taxiway C as temporary Runway 13R. This resulted in an unstable approach while correcting to the temporary runway.

A TRACON Departure Controller reported two aircraft flew dangerously close conflicting courses due to one aircraft taking the instruction of another aircraft with a nearly identical call sign departing at the same time.

Flight Attendant observed two passengers wearing face masks who were seated by exits and asked them to remove the masks during takeoff and landing in the event they needed be heard while giving commands during an emergency.

A CRJ-700 First Officer noted a specific aircraft's FMS loaded both north and south JAWBN STAR waypoints with discontinuities even though only the north transition was selected. This aircraft's FMS software is apparently unique to this aircraft and creates an additional distracting workload.
Technician reported that confusion over a bleed valve part number caused an incorrect part to be installed.

EUG TRACON Controller reported being distracted by a data block on the wrong target and failed to realize traffic he thought was a departure was conducting a Missed Approach. Controller allowed it to fly below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
BE36 pilot reported increased workload resulted when he was cleared on a different route than filed to CLT perhaps due to an LOA between GSO and CLT TRACONS.

ZNY Controller reported of an unsafe practice where another Controller changed the data block altitude of an aircraft they were to receive before the transferring Controller issued the altitude and communications to the aircraft.

An aircraft was in a Controller's sector with only a 'limited' data block displayed. The Controller did not notice the target or realize the aircraft was on their frequency and flying through their airspace.
GCN Tower Controller reported a NMAC between a helicopter flying through their airspace without coordination and into conflict with another helicopter.
Center Controller reported that an aircraft that did not display a radar data block flew through a corner of the airspace without coordination.
Hydraulic Quantity Indicator was deferred incorrectly because the Crew Alerting System indicated in CYAN faults for flaps; gear; and spoilers.
Air carrier Captain reported missing a crossing restriction during departure and referenced lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger complained because other passengers were not wearing a mask while they were eating a snack.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns regarding the products used to disinfect the cabin.
ZJX Center Controllers reported sector workload was excessive due to lack of effective traffic flow management during a period of weather deviations.
Flight Attendant reported that Embraer ERJ Captain left the cockpit momentarily while on the taxiway; leaving the cockpit door unsecured.
Legacy 600 Captain reported they had to go-around because the aircraft landing ahead of them was still on the runway.
D01 Approach Controller reported the procedures instituted for parallel approaches to Runways 16L and 16R do not work well.
Air carrier Captain reported a close encounter with a UAV on climbout from JFK Runway 31L after being informed by Departure Control that the preceding aircraft had reported sighting a drone.
SBA TRACON Controller reported ATC trainee vectored an aircraft off the approach into an area with a higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
C172 student pilot and instructor reported an NMAC at a non-towered airport.
Maintenance Technician reported that aircraft repair was accomplished in violation of proper procedure to avoid affecting aircraft schedule.

Flight attendants are now required by the Company to carry iPads to perform preflight and safety duties. The Flight Attendant reported that there is no place to store them near the jump seats or on their person.
Technician reported leaving their personal flash light in the up lock roller of the left Main Landing Gear; causing an air turn back due to the gear not retracting.
B737 Captain reported starting an engine at the gate without all ground personnel being aware.
Vans RV-3 Pilot reported encountering a dust devil on landing resulting in runway excursion.
Cessna pilot reported deviating from ATC instructions due to expectation bias.
Airbus Captain reported experiencing an uncommanded heading change and navigation display disruption due to GPS signal interference.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported a publication date discrepancy on an approach chart.
Flight Attendant reported that the Braille Safety Guides installed on the aircraft lack Covers; Table of Contents; and are printed in an obsolete format and require upgrade.
SR22T pilot reported encountering wake turbulence at 100-150 AGL after takeoff from SNA in trail of an A319 that resulted in a near 90 degree bank before control was regained.
Airline employee reported Hazmat not properly secured for transport.
B737 Captain reported landing without a clearance.
An Airbus Captain on final to PHX Runway 26 reported another aircraft apparently overshot the Runway 25L centerline; which resulted in an extremely close airborne encounter.
Technician reported an improper windshield change lead to aircraft's inability to pressurize.

A Mooney transitioned from an instrument approach to a visual about three miles from a CTAF airport but received no CTAF responses. An opposite direction VFR departure passed 200 feet over his aircraft. After landing; he learned the active was Runway 11; he landed on Runway 29 opposite direction.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported having difficulty in sending a new release to a flight before departure. The flight had to alter their route due to weather; but had sufficient fuel.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unstablized approach and cited fatigue; time pressure and reduced flying as contributing factors.
Maintenance Technician reported discovering wrong part listed in Illustrated Parts Catalog; error was fixed and correct part was installed instead.
C172 pilot reported an aborted takeoff because the tow bar was still attached.
B767-300 flight crew reported a bird strike on takeoff roll; resulting in aircraft damage.

Pilot reported executing the wrong missed approach procedure after not being issued missed approach instructions. ATC cleared the pilot for the approach and issued instructions to contact airport advisory frequency; however there was no such frequency published for the airport and the Tower was closed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Pilot reported an altitude deviation due to excessive climb rate.
ERJ-170 Flight Attendant reported she and the other Flight Attendant were injured while they were working in the galley during a sudden turbulence encounter.
A maintenance technician reported a dangerous procedure being used to service oxygen bottles on aircraft.
B757 Captain reported a track deviation on a holding pattern that was not depicted on the fix and not visible during the close-in EFB view.
GA pilot had difficulty finding a flight instructor during the COVID-19 pandemic to conduct a flight review.
Luscombe Pilot reported loss of directional control during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
Air carrier Captain reported a charting issue with Taxiway T2 at BNA.
AS350 pilots reported landing in an open field when a VFR flight encountered IMC. After the snow shower had passed; the flight continued to their destination.
Helicopter pilot reported SGF airport was reporting incorrect weather information due to possible equipment failure.

ASE Tower Controller reports of a flight of two aircraft that encroached on the Delta airspace not being controlled and cause an airborne conflict with another aircraft under their control.  Controllers in the Tower could see the aircraft on radar but not out the window until they were no longer a factor. Controller stated the aircraft were doing 500 knots.
B737 Captain reported that an aircraft flew several times with an incorrectly applied MEL.
Air carrier flight crew reported landing without a clearance.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a GPWS 'Pull Up' warning on a visual approach to XNA.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger did not comply with wearing a face mask during the flight.
Air Carrier First Officer reported the transport of Hazmat cargo with incomplete required Hazmat labeling.
Flight Instructor reported that his student flying a C172 solo encountered freezing rain weather shortly after takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported runway and taxiways signs at MMUN Airport were not illuminated at night.
Air carrier Captain reported the KAENA2 RNAV Arrival to ILS Runway 8L at HNL shows MAKOA to be crossed between 3;400 and 3;700 feet while the ILS 8L shows above 3;300 feet at MAKOA.

ZFW Controller reported of not issuing an aircraft 'resume the arrival' clearance. The aircraft had been left on a heading and the Controller didn't realize it until later. Controller tried to point out aircraft and advise sector; but someone else appeared to be talking to the other Controller about the aircraft in question. Controller did not issue correct instructions to aircraft leading to a LOA and airspace violation.
B737 Captain reported they were unable to stop in a timely manner at a taxiway intersection and then disregarded ATC's subsequent taxi instructions to avoid another aircraft.
Pilot reported the AWOS at OAR airport was not reporting accurate weather.
ERJ-190 Captain reported there is no guidance in the Operations Manual on how to deal with ice in the engine cowl aft of the inlets.
Air carrier First Officer noted his company's iPad charts do not include suitable emergency diversion airports in the area between DEN and SLC.
Air carrier Captain reported unsafe parking procedures occurred when pulling into the gate.
A319 flight crew reported being low altitude alert on approach due to heavy workload on final.
Ramp agent reported HAZMAT cargo not properly loaded and secured at departure airport.
Flight Instructor on training flight reports lawnmower incursion on active runway.

B737-700 flight crew reported the crew oxygen pressure dropped significantly during the preflight procedure. The valve was found to not be fully open; however the signoff did not indicate so; and the delay was charged to the crew.

RSW Instructing Controller reported a loss of separation due to the following aircraft being faster and the lack of visual separation. Wake turbulence was involved and though they could provide visual separation; it is not allowed in this instance. Instructor could not read data tag due to overlapping of tags and did not move the tags to determine if visual separation was being used correctly.

A Dispatcher reported the Lido weather forecast process apparently does not update to current conditions if an airport is already below Lido minimums during the dispatch process. A destination change to below minimums was discovered on a National Weather Service forecast.

An EMB-145 First Officer reported while flying the IAH DRLLR4 Arrival and after multiple runway changes; the aircraft did not make the turn to final so the crew overrode it to prevent an overshoot. The error may be in the FMS design after multiple changes.

The Captain of a B737-800 reported that in an attempt to keep the passengers comfortable and not have to deplane them; he decided to open a door. The Captain inadvertently deployed a slide and had to deplane the passengers.
SR22 pilot reported an issue with the avionics system design that induced an excursion from the cleared altitude on final approach to an altitude below the MVA.
Flight Attendant reported being dropped off by the crew bus at a remote gate during a thunderstorm and subsequent passenger boarding when the ramp was closed due to lightning.
Technician reported confusion over a check interval required on a component not being present in a check package.
Y90 Controller reported that Developmental forgot aircraft was IFR and changed the display information to indicate VFR. Aircraft was not climbed as it entered a higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

ZLC Controller reports of switching an aircraft over to Tower; then at 300 feet the aircraft reports to the Center Controller that he just broke out and can see the runway.  Pilot did not switch to Tower frequency and landed on a runway with a snowplow on it.

B737NG First Officer reported encountering windshear on two separate approaches into DEN; executing a go-around after the first encounter; but landing after the second; even though they received a terrain alert.
Air carrier First Officer reported another aircraft was cleared to takeoff at SFO on a crossing runway which they had been told to hold short of; but they had not yet come to a stop.
B737 Captain reported the flight crew did not receive an important message via ACARS from the company until airborne.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reports that company violates procedures regarding security seals and stickers.
CRJ-700 Captain reported striking the runway with a wingtip on landing flare in shifting wind conditions.
An MD-80 had normal seats installed in two of the emergency exit seat rows. The seatback tray tables could block the emergency exits.
A300 flight crew reported a MEL discrepancy regarding an inoperative interphone system; resulting in maintenance action.

Air carrier Captain reported that FAR has a new taxiway that has been open for several months; but is still not shown on the airport diagrams (NACO and Jeppesen). The new taxiway connects the terminal ramp to Runway 9 at Taxiway C5.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a GPWS alert while in IMC conditions on radar vectors.

Controller was being handed off two aircraft on converging headings at the same altitude. In the controller's opinion it was an imminent situation. The controller called the adjacent sector and instructed them to issue a heading to one of the aircraft to ensure lateral separation in the receiving controller's airspace. The reporter states this is a common situation between these two sectors.
EMB-145 flight crew reported company notification of undocumented Hazmat cargo transported on previously completed flight.
Flight Attendant reported being told by Gate Agents and Managers to allow catering to bring carts down the aisle after passengers are boarded in violation of company safety policy.
GA pilot reported a NMAC with a powered parachute at 52F airport. Reportedly; this is a common occurrence around this airport.
A Ramp Agent reported an Ground Power Cable hoist broke and fell next to a ramp employee narrowly missing him.
B777 Captain reported hazardous cargo omitted in the flight crew documents.
ZSE Center Controller reported that an aircraft deviated from its assigned vector into a higher MIA area.
Pilot reports of flight following being terminated due to SFRA and then he is advised later that he violated Class C on the other side of the SFRA.

F11 Approach Controller reports of the Tower waiting to change the runway of an arrival that was catching the preceding arrival. The second aircraft was changed to a parallel runway and landed safely.

A light jet crew on approach to HEF received an ATC low altitude alert call at 1;600 ft on the Runway 16L ILS before DORGE. The autopilot altitude set had somehow been changed unnoticed from 2;600 ft to 1;600 ft.
A First Officer reported the flight crew deviated from the assigned SID.
General aviation pilot reported damage from air carrier jet blast while in position on runway at SNA.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at cruise altitude from a crossing A320.
Dispatcher reported message from Flight Crew returning to departure airport due to cracked windshield.
A Center Trainee Controller did not apply approved vertical separation procedures which resulted in a loss of separation between two aircraft.
Air Carrier Captain reported ATC assigned holding fix in excess of 50nm offshore due to weather limitations. Reported added Navigation system malfunctioning.

Tulsa Radar Controller reported of an unsafe situation when he turned an aircraft too soon and did not avoid a parachute operation. Pilot reported after landing that it was; 'the closest he has come to parachute jumping.'
Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach resulting in a go-around. The Captain cited crew fatigue and lack of flying contributed to the event.

An air carrier crew failed to select the TCAS TA Mode on approach to DEN Runway 16L. After a RA; the First Officer remembered the Company TA Mode policy. He reminded the Captain who selected TA and continued the approach.
B737 Captain reported aircraft damage inflicted by ice to the flap tracks which was discovered on post-flight inspection by maintenance personnel.
ZKC Center Controller reported that an aircraft with no transponder caused a NMAC.
Captain reported ramp loaders placing cargo and bags above the height for proper fire suppression in the cargo hold.
Pilot reported missing the OAKLAND FOUR SID restriction due to published procedure design.
ZOB Center Controller reported having to go into no notice holding and the problems that followed.

B737 flight crew reported executing a missed approach due to confusion over which runway was assigned to his aircraft. The confusion was attributed to ATC workload and communication issues exacerbated by radio congestion.
EMB-145LR Captain reported momentary loss of control after encountering 'extremely strong' turbulence.

After intercepting the glideslope and localizer for Runway 28R at ORD; a Captain experienced a dramatic pitch over as the autopilot attempted to follow a rapidly descending glideslope indicator. The reporter disconnected the autopilot and commenced a climb back to the appropriate altitude at the same time the Tower issued a low altitude alert. Tower indicated that a heavy jet entering Runway 28R was the likely cause of the signal disruption.

ZHU Center Controller and AUS TRACON Controller reported two aircraft conflicted with each other due to a different interpretation of their responsibilities according to the LOA between the facilities.
SFO Tower Controller reported a procedure error with holding a large aircraft between the runways.
DFW Tower Controller reported a runway incursion with two arrivals on the same runway.
Air carrier Captain reported a Runway 22 localizer anomaly at LIT airport.
Air carrier Captain reported the Tower directed a go around due to conflicting landing traffic on a crossing runway at EWR.

ZMP Controller reports of a procedure where the high altitude controller clears the aircraft to descend into another controller's airspace.  The aircraft is handed off to the underlying sector but transfer of communications is not completed until the underlying sector asks for the aircraft.
HondaJet flight crew reported overrunning the runway due to contaminated runway conditions leading to poor braking on landing.
DH8-400 Captain reported difficulty understanding the MEL procedure and the need for instruction.
After a DCA takeoff a flight crew took evasive action following a TCAS RA nearing 5;000 feet from a small aircraft at 5;500 feet. ATC had advised the crew about the target in their climb.
ZMA Controllers reported that an aircraft took a heading as a climb instruction and became a conflict for another aircraft. Controllers reported the sector was combined and busy.
Flight Attendant reported another Flight Attendant was ill during the flight and not able to wear the face mask at all times; especially when coughing.
787 First Officer reported encountering a wind shear at FL 400 resulting in airspeed exceedance; deviation from assigned altitude and loss of auto throttles.
B737 Maintenance Technician reported they did not have the proper diagnostic tool to test the air conditioning system as required.
Air carrier ramp agent reported that aircraft parking procedures do not have adequate safety constraints.
B737 Captain reported a cabin fire extinguisher leaked out all its pressure.

A LAX Local Controller reported an airborne conflict between a helicopter at 1;500 feet transiting north bound on the Sepulveda Route and an air carrier aircraft at 1;400 feet landing Runway 25L. The air carrier Captain also described his crew's actions during the go-around.
A320 First Officer reported that the aircraft was dispatched with numerous MELs which caused extra workload for the flight crew.
Dispatch is not being informed of changes in fuel loads.
B737NG First Officer reported new checklist procedures increased their workload while conducting a Bleeds Off takeoff from a busy airport.
PAO Tower Controller in Charge reported the adjacent Tower facility would not delegate needed airspace to them so aircraft experienced departure delays.
TYS TRACON Controller reported their trainee vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
BFL Tower Controller reported  ground conflict when an aircraft lined up for a closed runway. Ground crew questioned Tower and the Controller sent aircraft around.
Air carrier flight crew arrived at their destination to find a slow moving thunderstorm over the field. They consulted with Dispatch; diverted and landed with emergency fuel level on board.

A corporate jet flight crew reported that after being cleared to cross an active runway they noticed another aircraft commencing takeoff on the same runway. The flight crew stopped prior to crossing the runway; avoiding a potential critical conflict.
Pilot reported attempting to land at an airport with a closed Control Tower and other aircraft landing on an unlit runway at night.
Pilot reported a NMAC on final to home airport with someone in an ultralight/sport aircraft that has been a nuisance around the area.
An SFO Controller and CIC report of a go-around caused by an aircraft not exiting the runway completely in the CIC's eyes; but was clear enough in the Locals eyes to let the operation continue.
Air carrier Captain reported GPWS alert during approach to land to MTJ airport.

TRACON Controller reported being overwhelmed by distractions from satellite airport operations and forgetting to climb an air carrier departure; resulting in the aircraft flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Dispatcher reported aircraft departed without required HAZMAT form Notification to Captain.
Ramp Supervisor at destination arrival inspection reported Dangerous Goods container not strapped down prior to loading at departure point.
Pilot reported attempting to receive flight following from Center; and being involved in a NMAC.

The pilot of a Caravan with a TKS system reported concern over the lack of sufficient fluid supply to provide options during icing conditions. In addition; the pilot commented on TKS odor and effects.
ZKC Controller reported of an aircraft that had difficulty reaching its destination due to weather and turbulence. Aircraft turned away from destination and had to return to departure airport.
Air Carrier pilot reported their initial request for extra fuel was denied by Dispatch which was not in accordance with Company Policy.
BE35 pilot reported accidentally retracting the landing gear on the runway during a touch and go that resulted in a prop strike and partial landing gear collapse.
ZBW Center Controller reported not having enough controllers on duty to open all the sectors and their workload reached unwanted levels.

B737-800 flight crew reported being informed by the lead Flight Attendant of an overheated battery charger. Fifteen minutes later he was informed that some of the flight attendants were experiencing headaches and nausea and the Captain elected to divert to a suitable airport.
EMB-175 Pilot reported of confusion flying into LAX with speeds and restrictions from ZLA and SCT.
Air Taxi Pilot reports aircraft airworthiness certificate missing.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a GPWS terrain waring while on a visual approach to GEG Runway 21. Terrain awareness had been briefed.
An A320 pilot reported receiving a GPWS alert while on ATC vectors and descending to an ATC assigned altitude.
A B737-800 Captain reported going around at SNA after the tower cleared an aircraft for takeoff in front of them; with what the reporter felt was insufficient spacing.
Airline Dispatcher reported a ground gate return request of flight to correct Hazmat documents.

A B757-200 First Officer reported they did not receive a complete NOTAM packet for their planned flights and were advised that Dispatchers sometimes withhold NOTAMs they feel are not necessary to include.
ERJ-145 Captain reported that a bird strike on takeoff resulted in a precautionary landing.
Captain reported a NMAC while climbing on their departure.
Flight Attendant reported a family with a car seat sat in an unapproved location.
A pilot reported he was on a base leg vector for a visual approach and was issued a frequency change to the Tower without receiving an approach clearance.
Air Carrier flight crew reported exceeding 250 knots below 10;000 feet while departing CLT to avoid inbound traffic.
Dispatcher reported a flight was dispatched over foreign airspace without the proper approval and had to divert.
Flight Crew reported ground personnel advised them to release brakes when they were not connected to a tug and the aircraft rolled backwards.

B737-800 First Officer reported a possible bird strike at 2;000 feet in the left engine during climb out causing a popping noise and some vibration. An odor is also detected and the crew elected to return to the departure airport.
A80 Controller reported an example of why they feel their facility's new arrival routes and procedures are unsafe.

Mooney 201 pilot reported electing; per mission needs; to disable by circuit breaker the landing gear system. Later; upon approach and landing; the breaker was not reset and the aircraft landed gear up.

A Corporate crew checked M91 destination weather from two sources before departure and found none. While preparing for the GPS Runway 22; ZME notified the pilots the runway was closed. Aviation Digital Data Service (ADDS) was the only site with the NOTAM which a contractor erroneously issued while the LED PAPI and runway lights were installed.
Flight attendant reported several passengers not properly wearing face masks.
A Center Controller reported attempting to climb an aircraft entering higher terrain; but lost radio contact with the aircraft.

After a commuter flight crew landed at UNV and were taxiing clear of the runway; they were queried by operations personnel if Center 'had advised us of the 15 minute PPR.' They told him that they had not been advised. They were unable to find the NOTAM and the Center controller was also unaware of the NOTAM.
Pilot reported landing without clearance and cited the COVID-19 environment as a contributing factor.
B737MAX Captain reported confusion regarding switch function and display annunciations related to 'poor training and even poorer documentation'.
SCT Controller reported a known recurrent anomaly; which renders the Downey sector frequency unusable. Maintenance has failed to fix this.
Commercial Airline Load Planning Personnel reported rejecting passenger box for cargo compartment with 'Dangerous Goods' labels.

Air carrier First Officer reported a bright billboard with flashing lights on the final approach to Runway 18R at MEM. The billboard is very distracting and about one mile from the end of the runway along I-240 facing north.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing a track heading deviation after missing an intersection on the routing received from the PDC.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with face mask policy during flight.
Air carrier Maintenance employee reported adverse health symptoms after exposure to an aircraft just cleaned using electrostatic spraying.
C172 flight instructor and student pilot reported the student had a runway excursion during a solo training flight.

A Controller working alone on a mid shift reported leaving their radar position to enter a PIREP in to a computer and did not notice an aircraft descending below its assigned altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight Crew reports confusion with intersection naming same as navaid.
LGB Tower Controller reported two losses of separation by the same Local Controller; who in the reporters mind knew the correct procedure; but did not apply it.
Pilot reported the company provided face masks do not fit the reporter's face; causing fogging of glasses.

ERJ 145 air carrier Flight Crew reported that during climb; the cabin pressurization was not climbing correctly. It was determined that the manual controller knob was set closer to the dump position; instead of in the auto position.
Air carrier flight crew reported Runway Status Lights illuminated while crossing runway after receiving Controller's verbal crossing clearance at DFW airport.

Flight Attendant reported that her company's A321s have only two aft cabin jumpseats; but during flight three flight attendants work in the aft. When turbulence was encountered a Flight Attendant was without a jumpseat and harness.
B777 Captain reported difficulty plotting company-provided areas of turbulence when the aircraft WiFi is unavailable.
EMB-145 Captain reported they did not receive localizer signal until 3 miles from the FAF. Reporter stated this was a common occurrence on this approach at EWR.
The flight crew of a Boeing 777 reported that an engine regularly exceeds the maximum vibration limits.
ZHU Controller reported being told by Supervisor that he may have not issued appropriate weather information to some aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported they ran into a snowdrift that had blown onto the taxiway which auto shutdown the right engine.
GSP Tower Controller reported an unsafe operation that was between an IFR arrival and a VFR departure from a different airport.
A300 Captain reported the aircraft pitched over abruptly following a false glide slope; resulting in a low altitude alert from the Tower.

B757 flight crew reported being dispatched to an airport before the Tower opened with weather near CAT I minima and automated approaches NOTAMed not applicable. After Tower opened; weather improved and the flight landed following the fourth approach. With poor weather; the flight should have been dispatched to arrive after the Tower opened.
Pilot reported miscommunication with ATC; causing ground conflict and a possible airborne conflict when preparing for takeoff.
Cargo Operations Dispatcher reported becoming aware of Cargo Fire Suppression System being deactivated with Hazmat on board. Flight crew executed a precautionary diversion.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported the overhead bins being heavy to close due to weight of numerous heavy bags in the bin.
A300 flight crew reported refusal to transport HAZMAT cargo due to faulty HAZMAT container.
PC-12 pilot reported encountering severe turbulence during descent even though all attempts were made to avoid the thunderstorm.
B777 flight crew reported that the Flying Pilot overshot final and was fast when cleared for a visual approach to a different runway than had been briefed.
B777 First Officer reported a communication breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch regarding Hazmat documentation.

An Atlanta TRACON (A80) Controller reports of having to use two different radar systems at one position to monitor traffic safely. The reporter states that it is stressful on all of the controllers involved.
B737NG Captain reported altitude and airspeed deviations resulted from a mountain wave encounter near DEN.
Helicopter pilot reported a large bird struck the aircraft shattering the canopy.

Air carrier flight crew reported transitioning from the MSY RYTHM Arrival to the Runway 20 RNAV at 2;000 feet and were notified reentering Class B . The crew was not aware the aircraft was below Class B at 210 knots in IMC.

A Yankee TRACON (Y90) controller describes an airborne conflict due to VFR aircraft being in the vicinity of the airport and the final approach course. Controller recommends changing the airspace from a Class Charlie to a Class Bravo to offer better protection from the VFR traffic.
ZBW Controller reported high volume of traffic and not enough people to work the sectors correctly.
MRI Tower Controller reported the Approach Control was not complying with an automated point out procedure causing unnecessary delay and flight time over water for aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported experiencing symptoms similar to a cold or allergies during flight and did not think it could be COVID-19 symptoms until at home.

Air carrier Captain reported encountering wake turbulence descending into BOS in trail of a heavy B767; and expressed concern about an apparent recent change to ATC procedures allowing reduced separation to heavy B767 aircraft.
An Air Carrier flight crew lost communication with ATC in the final phase of flight and during approach. Communication was regained prior to landing.
Dispatcher reported that Maintenance Operations authorized a non check Captain to do a dual engine change In flight Functional Check; who was unsure of requirements for this type of flight.
Pilot reported missing a crossing altitude restriction due to high airspeed assigned by ATC.
Air Carrier Captain reported an issue with the fuel quantity gauge with one fuel tank which was deferred by Maintenance. Crew was unsure of the actual quantity in the tank.
Dispatcher reported confusion with ATC when a flight needed to divert; but ATC would not allow the aircraft to divert to the airport the Dispatcher had chosen.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL in trail of an A320. Reporter suggested different procedures might help when departing in tailwind conditions.
ZJX Center Controller reported the facility controlling Restricted/Warning Areas approved point outs for weather deviations than called back to tell them to exit the areas for traffic.

BE400 Captain reported that the aircraft departed with less fuel than was needed for the trip and got a low fuel light during cruise. The flight landed with less than the required amount of reserve fuel.
Air carrier Captain reported difficulty taxiing with just one engine in high wind conditions.
ZOB Controller reported problems associated with reading and understanding NOTAMs that may or may not be pertinent to the operation.
A319 Captain reported being vectored twice too close to following traffic and landing on the second attempt.
Air carrier Captain reported taking off with the pneumatics panel misconfigured and the left pack in Auto.
A pilot makes commentary as to the challenges with RNAV descents for aircrews.
An ERJ145 First Officer reported they declined to take an aircraft with a fuel leak in spite of pressure from management to fly it.

C208 flight crew reported loss of directional control during taxi.
A Maintenance Technician reported that a repair and follow-up procedures were not documented as per company procedure.

Captain and First Officer both reported a flight where they had to go around due to a heavy aircraft taking too long to depart. The aircraft then had to make a go-around; and come back to land. Pilot tried to advise ATC of aircraft fuel issues but could not speak to Controller. Pilot turned off downwind to land aircraft. Traffic had to be vectored out of the way. Aircraft landed uneventfully.
A General Aviation aircraft following taxi instructions from ATC had to suddenly brake to avoid another aircraft taxiing into the same intersection.
Pilot and Controller reported a runway incursion due to the pilot misunderstanding of ATC's clearance.
Flight crew reported executing an evasive maneuver in compliance of GPWS Terrain Alert during approach due to altitude excursion.
Air taxi Captain reported ATC issuance of incorrect routing and an altitude deviation.
Air carrier crew reported confusion about DAL Taxiway Q versus P after pushback from the terminal north side gates.
A C172 student pilot reported an unstabilized approach that resulted in a runway excursion.
Air carrier Non Flying First Officer reported a near taxiway incursion due to Jeppsen 10-9B chart not depicting the island at spot as is depicted on 10-9.
C172 Instructor Pilot reported the student lost directional control on landing roll after overreacting to a light crosswind.
GA pilot reported having an airspace incursion while on an instrument currency flight and cited lack of recent flying due to COVID-19 and unfamiliarity with the airspace as contributing factors.
As an A320 approached a known 7;840 foot obstacle on the MMGL ILS 10 approach; the EGPWS alerted CAUTION; TERRAIN nearing the 8;400 foot descent constraint.
A B737 encountered severe turbulence with loss of control and cabin injuries at FL300.
PA-28 pilot reported a near mid-air collision in the vicinity of OSH; asserting that current ATC procedures for AirVenture traffic are not safe.

Houston TRACON (I90) reports of having to vector and climb aircraft off of final due to weather. The reporter stated that they received no help from Traffic Management Unit (TMU) as this developed into a loss of separation.
