B737 Captain reported Hazmat FAA ramp check resulted in flight delay with no anomalies detected.

An EMB-145 air crew were given instructions to taxi to the gate with no restrictions. While taxiing; another aircraft being taxied by maintenance personnel cut them off at a high rate of speed. Ground control would not answer calls. The Captain called the Tower on the telephone and was informed that they were training a controller that evening.
Maintenance Technician reported that the company did not follow the manufacturer's guidelines concerning loading passengers while maintenance was performed on the aircraft.
A319 Captain reported receiving a GPWS terrain warning when vectored off the charted course approaching TUS.

A 737 flight crew reported experiencing a fuel tank boost pump pressure issue after pushback and engine start. Maintenance personnel resolved the issue at the aircraft; however it was determined later that an improper procedure was used to troubleshoot the problem.
Flight Attendant reported a row that is typically blocked for Flight Attendant use was released for passenger seating.
A B737-700 Captain taxied to very end of SJC Runway 30L on Taxiway A1 at which point both pilots noted how close the wingtip passed by the blast fence. Runway 30R was closed for construction.
Flight Attendant reported jump-seat harness to be installed incorrectly.
Air carrier Captain reported having non-compliant passenger.
B737 Captain reported encountering a windshear and microburst on short final into DEN.
Flight instructor reported difficulty talking to passenger while wearing a face mask and had an airspace violation while attempting to fix the communication issue.
C402 Captain reported his aircraft was rolled into a 90-degree bank from wake turbulence at 300 feet AGL on approach into BOS 4 miles in trail of an A340.
CE-750 flight crew reported an NMAC event during cruise.
Two Center Controllers reported working a busy sector with weather deviations and did not notice an aircraft overtaking another aircraft descending to the same altitude.
A Tower Controller reported that the Local Controller did not tell the truth about a close call relating to an aircraft exiting a runway with an arrival landing the same runway.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger who had previously violated the face mask policy was allowed to board this flight.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering fatigue due to flying schedule.

Cessna 182 Pilot reported being alerted by Air Traffic Control that LOC Approach was not working and therefore being cleared for an RNAV approach completely unprepared which caused a descent below minimum altitude.
Air Carrier personnel reported Dangerous Goods were incorrectly loaded due to the Load Planner's lack of knowledge.
During a high workload environment while landing at DEN; with changing weather and winds; the crew landed with a tailwind in excess of limitations which resulted in hot brakes.

Due to a radar outage; K90 TRACON was receiving their radar information from ZBW ARTCC. The ARTCC has to make a data entry of the current altimeter for the TRACON airspace yet the ARTCC did not realize this requirement. Therefore the Mode C readouts of aircraft in the TRACON airspace were 400 feet off from the actual altitude of the aircraft.
Air Cargo Captain reported that after landing on a runway contaminated with sand the aircraft filled with dust that had been ingested by the packs following use of reverse thrust.

A320 flight crew reported encountering a bird strike at high speed during the takeoff roll. The takeoff was continued; however a significant vibration in one engine was noted. They reduced power on the affected engine and safely returned to the departure airport; where major damage to the engine was discovered.

Captain reported consistently receiving a clearance route different from the one normally filed by company Dispatch. Captain recommended that the normal route filed by Dispatch be changed to that assigned by ATC.
AS350 Technician reported that the tail rotor was not properly inspected after it encountered debris from landing in a grassy field.
The procedure(s) as to where to position a ground air start unit on an Embraer 170 are not defined clearly. The procedures which engine to start first is also not defined.
B777 flight crew reported encountering severe mountain wave turbulence.
N90 Controller vectored an aircraft for spacing on the final approach course into the path of an aircraft in the adjacent sector.
Pilot reported that windshear on landing resulted in a runway excursion.
GA-7 Instructor Pilot reported he and his student had a track deviation on an IFR flight when they went off course to avoid icing conditions.
Center Controller reported that an aircraft declaring Minimum Fuel departed his assigned altitude without notification resulting in a conflict with another aircraft.
EMB-145 flight crew reported diverting to a second alternate after two go-arounds at the original destination and the primary alternate being closed.
B767 First Officer laments the lack of information provided by his company on the location of volcanic ash departing SCEL and the company's response to his earlier report on the subject.
Air Carrier Captain reported the Terrain Critical Depressurization Procedure conflicts with the ETOPS Critical Point on a specific transatlantic route.

ARTCC Controller reported of a staffing issue on a particular day. No overtime was called in and the sectors were combined at times in ways the reporter had never seen before. Reporter thought it was due to short staffing and the micromanagement of the Operations Manager that lead to all of the problems.

Pilots report of being issued a climb via clearance but do not climb via. Controllers issues the clearance but the pilots delete a crossing restriction and climb above it. The Controller has pilots descend to the crossing restriction altitude.
B767-300 Captain reported that due to weather conditions the aircraft had a hard.
Pilot reported NMAC with aircraft nonreporting aircraft.
B767 Maintenance Technician reported that an incorrect O-ring was installed on the engine's oil Magnetic Chip Detector.
B737-700 Captain reported landing with 3;300 lbs of fuel after two go-arounds at ABQ triggered by windshear warnings.
Captain reported experiencing intermittent GPS/ADS-B signal interruption during ILS approach.
GA pilot reported parachuting activity in the vicinity of E60 airport near a private airport that is not located on aviation charts.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to comply with COVID-19 mask requirements in flight.
Pilot reported the rotating beacon on top of the new JLN Control Tower is hard to see at night.
BE-58 pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
CE-560XL flight crew reported failing to make a crossing restriction on descent into BOS; citing a late clearance and a wake turbulence encounter.
B737-700 First Officer reported deviating from assigned airspeed on approach.
BJC Controller reports of an Opposite Direction Operation that Local Controller is unaware of its close proximity and violates the facilities ODO Policy.
B737-800 Captain reported a communication breakdown between Captain and Tug Driver regarding brakes release for pushback from departure gate and resulted in improper pushback movement.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger complained about another passenger coughing and sneezing on the flight and not wearing a face mask.
Air carrier Captain reported about the limits of single FMC/ACARS operations.

Air Carrier Captain reports a potential issue where SFB Ground issues a hold short clearance for Runway 09C in the original taxi clearance followed by a clearance to cross Runway 09C shortly after. Reporter believes this and having the Controller working Runway 09C on a different frequency can lead to an error where an aircraft inadvertently crosses Runway 09C without a clearance.
Maintenance technician reported that during a landing gear tire change; the aircraft was hoisted using incorrect jacks; resulting in a safety risk.
Flight Crew reported Ground Proximity Terrain Warning on approach.
B737 Captain reported severe turbulence during climb to smooth altitude.
Embraer flight crew reported executing a go-around after experiencing windshear on initial approach during a snow squall at EWR.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with being in close contact with a Flight Attendant that had called in sick the day before for COVID-19 like symptoms.
M20 pilot reported an upset from a building storm that resulted in a 50 degree bank and 1;500 FPM rate of descent. Aircraft control was recovered after a loss of 1;000 feet.
A pilot began the takeoff roll on SGS Runway 16 after making the suggested CTAF communications; then aborted and exited the runway when another aircraft was seen taking off opposite direction.
Air carrier flight crew reported a GPWS activation on short final to JAN at night in marginal VFR conditions.

PCT Controller reported of a problem that happened 17 days prior. The question was; what type of separation was being used. Tower controller reported visual separation after being asked. Reporter thought this was a cover up for the lack of a timely investigation.
Air carrier pilot reported asymmetrical thrust on start of takeoff roll resulting in a rejected takeoff.
A320 Captain reported that with the maintenance history concerning a system that was deferred he did not accept the aircraft.
B767 Captain stated he feels his company's policy of flying some East Coast to Europe North Atlantic flights with just two pilots is unsafe.
EMB-175 Captain reported the First Officer sighted a UAV 200 feet below the aircraft on final approach to Runway 25L at LAX.
SFO Tower Controller reported a radio 'dead spot' identified at the approach end of Runway 1R that continually affects lower profile jets when in position on the runway.

A flight crew descended below the DCA RNAV RNP Runway 19 crossing altitudes and at about 700 ft the EGPWS alerted 'CAUTION TERRAIN' 'OBSTACLE; OBSTACLE' 'PULL UP.' The pilot flying responded and continued to land. The crew did not see the autopilot transition from VNAV PATH to VNAV SPD.
A Center Controller recieved a handoff from the TRACON of an aircraft below their Minimum IFR Altitude.
B737 Flight Attendant reported that a crewmember brought onboard a portable battery powered vacuum cleaner that would not fit into a fire containment bag.

C-182T Pilot Flying reported a possible encounter with hypoxia.
Pilot reported receiving a low altitude report from ATC while on published SID.

CE-750 Pilot report possible clear air turbulence resulted in temporary loss of control.
EMB-175 First Officer reported observing a UAV near their course while on short final.
B757 Maintenance Controller reported that the maintenance procedures were ambiguous whether to require a test or confirmation flight.
A TRACON Controller reported a photo mission aircraft flying an unsafe route was allowed to proceed through the airspace.
New York Center Controllers reported that aircraft were deviating due to weather and the sector was not controlled with aircraft turning into each other.

Captain inbound to MMMX reported that he was sent around because of conflict with traffic at 6 o'clock and 2 miles after being instructed to The Captain further stated that MMMX controllers routinely require aircraft to fly high and fast into MMMX and complained about 'substandard arrival/approach/ATC procedures being used in MMMX.'
Maintenance Technician reported the C560XL Maintenance Manual and Illustrated Parts Catalog are not clear on what parts are required or procedures when replacing elevator trim cables.
B737 First Officer reports his air carrier's Before Takeoff Checklist is too long and beginning the checklist late into the taxi flow means something is bound to be missed.
While replacing the #1 and #2 main landing gear wheel assemblies on a commercial aircraft; the technicians did not find any washer under the axle nut for either wheel assembly.

Medium Transport aircraft Captain reported flying several legs with a known worn tire until it possibly failed during the last takeoff. The flight continued but the tire shredded to pieces during the landing roll causing the aircraft to shake.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a Low Altitude Alert from ATC due to towers close to the flight path of the aircraft.
ZMP Center Controller reported an airspace deviation while working combined sectors. COVID-19 staffing levels contributed to the event.

MD-80 Captain reported a main cabin door problem was not documented by the off going crew and entered the discrepancy in the aircraft maintenance log. A mechanic verified the problem; but the Supervisor overrode the decision resulting in the crew refusing the aircraft after enduring pilot pushing and threats from maintenance.
Cargo personnel reported refusing cargo due to undeclared lithium ion batteries in freight shipment.
Technician reported not verifying part numbers and serial numbers prior to shipping main rotor blades out for repair.
Air carrier Captain reported a difference of opinion between himself and the Controller involved as to the necessity to deviate around weather that the Controller could not see.

Captain lamented the training he received for new procedures instituted by his company in order to mirror the procedures of a main line carrier. He also does not believe that the CRJ200 to CRJ900 differences training is adequate.

Air Carrier flight crew noted problems with loss of situational awareness while dealing with traffic on parallel visual approaches to Runways 28L and 28R at SFO particularly when transitioning from the BDEGA Arrival.
A Ramp Agent offloading an inbound flight discovered 12 U.S. mail sacks containing packages clearly labeled with lithium batteries.
B737 NG Captain reported recurring confusion over how a SID clearance was issued; resulting in deviation from the expected course.
Air taxi First Officer reported a track heading deviation during departure.
PA18 pilot reported being pushed off the taxiway into the grass by wind gusts and the nose of the aircraft contacted the ground.
ATC and B767 pilots reported a security threat from a passenger that prompted an air return.

Air carrier flight crew departed LIH while the Tower was closed at night and maintained runway heading without other instructions. ATC instructed them they should have known to intercept V15 even though they were never cleared via that route.

A supervisor did not meet their Area currency requirements and did not realize it. The Operations Manager did not advise the supervisor of their currency default. The supervisor worked for several months without meeting currency requirements.

MDW Tower Controller reported an unsafe situation where the overlying TRACON terminated IFR services with an aircraft and transferred communications to the Tower without any coordination. Reporter thought this was not a good practice because of the position of the aircraft related to final.

A320 Captain reported concern about an MEL on the fleet that allows aircraft to be dispatched with the cockpit access keypad inoperative; since that would preclude any emergency access to the cockpit.
B757 Captain reported the need to remind pilots it is in violation of the FAR's to text and fly. The Captain suggests the company shut off the free Wi-Fi inflight.
Albuquerque Center Controller reported a loss of separation in Tucson Approach airspace.
Captain reported receiving an aircraft with incomplete maintenance.
Widebody Transport flight crew reported failing to recognize a change to their PDC clearance.
Pilot reported being under pressure and flew a ferry flight without proper documentation.

A Honolulu Control Facility (HCF) Controller reports of losing a data tag with an aircraft on a 9 mile final. The Controller coordinates with tower; but doesn't know why the data tag went missing. The Controller assumes someone dropped the data tag because of a different flight with the same call sign.

ZDC Controller reported of an airspace deviation where no one knew who was talking to an aircraft in his airspace. The target autopopped on the radar scope and the Controller called the Controller who the aircraft was tagged to. The aircraft was not properly handed off.
The pilot reported on his encounter with a street lamp after landing his hot air balloon.
Captain reported refusing to fly an EMB-175 aircraft that had been written up 11 times for excessive engine vibration.
Pilot reported confusion between the difference in Out of Service and Unserviceable in NOTAMs.

Two Center Controllers reported a C172 cleared for an approach to a non towered airport did not follow the procedure and flew below the Minimum Enroute Altitude. One of the Controllers reported a low altitude alert was not issued in a timely manner due to the other controller's lack of proficiency with this situation.
Maintenance reported that 'unserviceable' tags were removed from several tow bars and; the tow bars were put back into service without being repaired.
Ground operations employee reported that Boeing 737 flight crews are told not to write up engine leaks.
Air Carrier Captain reported a ramp incursion due to inadequate ramp chart.
CE680 Flight Crew laments the numerous changes issued by PCT after assigning a descend via clearance.

B747 Relief Pilot described a bird strike on takeoff that damaged the number four engine. After consulting with the company the crew elects to return to the departure airport after dumping fuel down to maximum landing weight.
B777 flight crew of a large passenger turbojet reported an issue after takeoff of unsecured cargo pallets. Flight diverted to fix the issue then continued on to its original destination.
Pilot reported experiencing a taxiway incursion at BED airport and cited physical visual obstructions contributed to the event.
MRI Tower Controller reported LOA problems with the overlying A11 TRACON.
Flight Attendant reported the gate agents tried to board the passengers right after the aircraft was sanitized; disregarding the mandatory wait time.
ZOB Center Controller reported that an aircraft had requested deviations and higher altitude to avoid severe weather which were not approved by the previous Center Controller.
Flight Test crew experienced engine fire warning after takeoff due to borescope plugs not being installed before flight.
A319 Captain reported high workload and confusion resulted from last minute changes to approach and runway assignments during arrival into MMMX.
PHL Tower Controller states his facility Letter of Agreement with Ramp Control is not being complied with and is unsafe.
Dispatcher reported confusion over a Dispatcher desk being closed and who was responsible for certain flights.

Air carrier First Officer reported chronic anomalies with domestic clearances (KUSA) obtained via the Harris CPDLC-DCL (Direct Clearance) system which resulted in incomplete or erroneous route clearances on more than one occasion.
Pilots reported being advised that the aircraft was flown with open write ups on three legs.
Minneapolis Center Controller reported that Denver Center would have given him a loss of separation if he had not called them and fixed it prior to aircraft entering his sectors airspace.
An Enroute controller reported using three miles in trail separation in an approved environment however the leading aircraft was a Heavy jet requiring 5 miles separation.

TPA TRACON Controller reported of losing a data block along with the primary target. The aircraft was in conflict with the downwind traffic. Reporter advised the FLM; who told him this had happened several times before he took the position. Controller would like this looked into to avoid it from happening again.
General aviation pilot reported severe interference from a commercial radio station in the vicinity of PGV airport affecting communication with Washington Center and PGV Unicom.
Customer Service Agent reported passenger inadvertently exploded lithium battery at ticket counter during check-in.

A general aviation pilot reported that after filing all known paperwork to return to the United States from Canada; the United States Customs Border Patrol advised that proper permission had not been requested and granted to enter US airspace.

A pilot reported that the Tower Controller advised them they penetrated an adjacent Class C airspace. The pilot states according to his GPS and reviewing his route on an aviation software program they did not enter the Class C airspace.
B737-700 First Officer reported receiving an inflight notification from Dispatch regarding an aircraft battery on board. Flight crew was not notified of the Hazmat while on the ground.
A ZMP Controller reports of a loss of separation because a UAV was in a MOA and another aircraft was in the same MOA. The aircraft he was in control of was inside of the MOA and should not have been.
Air Carrier Captain reported diverting due to thunderstorms at the destination airport and fuel concerns.
Pilot reported a NMAC while on base leg.
Crew reported crossing the runway hold-short line after turning on the wrong taxiway during taxi for takeoff at Reno.
A CJ700 Captain reported temporarily losing control of the aircraft while conducting a missed approach.
Instructor Pilot reported a NMAC with a UAV while in the traffic pattern.

ZLC Center Controller reported a CRJ-900 encountered wake turbulence at FL350 when it passed beneath an A380 that was at FL370. Controller recommended that new procedures be instituted for the A380 aircraft.
B737 Captain reported refusing an aircraft that had come out of maintenance with flight control anomalies.

An air taxi Captain reported receiving confusing taxi instructions to a different runway than originally issued. Once airborne the aircraft was issued headings to an airway not contained in their flight plan.
TRACON Controller reported having to break up a flight of 6 fighters to get them to land.

Air carrier Captain reported that the crew was transported to the aircraft in the same bus as the passengers to a remote hard stand; and that a separate bus would lessen the risk of exposure to COVID-19.
Flight Attendant reported passenger refused to comply with the face mask policy.
B737-800 flight crew reported striking a large flock of birds during takeoff; resulting in damage to the Number 2 engine; and an immediate return to the departure airport.
Pilot reported a loss of directional control that resulted in a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft.
Maintenance Technician reported air carrier released aircraft after replacement of critical flight control items without a pre-revenue check flight.

A Balloon pilot preparing for departure reported unexpected gusts of wind blew the balloon across the ground uncontrolled. The pilot became airborne for a short period before coming to rest against a chain link fence with no injuries or damage to the balloon.
CRJ-900 Flight Attendant reported difficulty safely exiting the aircraft because of the steep jet bridge extension angle.
Air carrier Captain reported that one Controller was working combined frequencies during low visibility conditions at the airport. Reporter stated safety concerns with the situation.

A Load Planner delayed final weights after discovering a possible error in handling dangerous goods; live animals and bag locations. Information passed from ramp personnel regarding loading appeared inadequate.
AS350 Instructor reported that during autorotation training; the rotor was oversped; requiring a major inspection for components.
B-737 Captain reported refusing Dangerous Goods due to incomplete Hazmat documentation.
While accomplishing preventive maintenance on an aircraft recovery kit Maintenance found that the airbags were out of time limits.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported navigation charts had incorrect and out-of-date information.
Center Controller reported they forgot to climb an aircraft in a timely manner and it flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Heavy aircraft Captain reported that aircraft chocks were not removed prior to aircraft taxi.
Air carrier Captain reported issues flying into a busy GA airport.
Heavy Transport aircraft Captain reported being assigned the SMUKE2 STAR to IND without a runway transition; resulting in confusion.
An air carrier being vectored onto the final approach to DAY took evasive action to avoid a VFR aircraft not displaying a transponder or in communication with TRACON.
Q400 Captain reported unauthorized APU operations by ground personnel violated company policies and safety procedures.
Boeing 777 flight Crew reported that in an effort to comply with the SID the flaps were repositioned which created a flap overspeed.
Air carrier flight crew received Obstacle Alert for towers during approach and climbed to avoid them.
ARTCC Controller reported individuals in certain areas of the sector do not follow procedures correctly.  This behavior has reportedly led to confusion and airspace violations.
Customer Service Representative reported a crowded and unsafe area in the lobby; which did not allow for proper social distancing.
CE-750 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on the Quiet Bridge Visual Approach to SFO when an air carrier aircraft was sequenced ahead of them.
Pilot reported a communication issue resulted in an altitude deviation.
Maintenance Technician reported that a Task Card is missing a second Signature Block.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported difficulty intercepting the ORD Runway 27R localizer due to a misprogrammed FMS. At the same moment; an issue with the airport's ILS system prevented them from reintercepting the localizer.
Air carrier pilot reported that climb gradients to DRSET should be published for Runways 13L and 13R on four SIDs at DAL.
Flight Attendant reported allowing a child to fly without wearing a face mask. The child had refused to wear it.
B737-800 flight crew reported during departure they noticed a smoky odor after a bird strike. The flight returned to departure airport. After landing; damage to #1 engine fan blade was found.
Pilot reported being out of communication with ATC in Class A due to inadvertently changing frequencies and not noticing it until some time later.
Gulfstream Captain reported moderate to extreme turbulence while descending with a momentary loss of control.
A Bombardier Global Express HYD 1 LO QTY CAS alerted so the crew completed the HYD 1 LO PRESS Checklist. They then secured the #1 engine as a precaution and diverted to the nearby airport.
A B777 Captain reported that the new procedure as to when to use the landing checklist has increased the workload during approach.
Air carrier Captain reported aborting a takeoff roll when the tiller was accidentally bumped; causing the steering to disengage.
Corporate pilot landing at a non-towered airport reported they forgot to communicate their arrival on CTAF and noticed a snowplow on the runway after landing.
Flight Crew arriving at airplane parked at gate; was not informed that maintenance was being accomplished.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Runway Status lights indicated traffic on the runway and after asking Tower about it Tower cancelled their takeoff clearance.

Houston Tower Controller and air carrier pilot reported a possible pilot deviation that led to a loss of separation with previous departure; however the pilot was unaware of any problem.
MSP Local Controller reported of a windshear at the airport. Reporter stated they should stop arrivals to a specific runway 35. Arrivals continued for an other 22 minutes before stopping.

While coordinating departures; a C90 TRACON Controller told a Chicago Tower Instructor and trainee to send an aircraft around due to slowing. The Tower Instructor disagreed with the TRACON Controller and instead did what he felt prudent citing the fact that the TRACON should not be dictating what happens in the towers airspace.
A330 Captain reported they lost communication with ATC and did not realize it until they observed unidentifiable traffic near their aircraft.
Flight attendants reported that a deadheading pilot did not follow face mask policy during the flight.
Part 121 Captain reported COMAT material had been added without HAZMAT documentation or crew notification.
A320 Captain reported encountering tubulence so severe he compared it to his previous experience in planned hurricane penetration flights.
Dispatcher reported difference of ETA between Dispatch and Operations.
A320 Captain reported questioning the engine out missed approach procedures specified by his company which requires the SFO VOR which was NOTAMed out of service.
B-767 First Officer reported flight pushback without final Dangerous Goods report.
BOI Departure Controller observed a ZLC Center Controller turn an aircraft they had given them into arrival traffic in the adjacent sector.
B737 flight crew reported momentary stick shaker while being diverted to COS after weather caused a missed approach into DEN.
Pilot reported after announcing on Unicom intentions to enter runway and starting to back taxi; an opposite landing aircraft performed a go-around.
Air Carrier Dispatcher [at main base] reported Hub Dispatch neglected to provide Dry Ice cargo information to main Dispatch resulting in no  'over burn fuel' on Flight Release..
Pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at FFZ airport.
Ramp personnel reported the need of Hazmat procedural update to address FAR loading requirements.
B737 First Officer reported a rejected takeoff after the red runway lights illuminated when the power was advanced.
A300 First Officer reported momentary loss of control during a moderate turbulence encounter at FL230.
G650 Captain reported an NMAC during takeoff with an aircraft drifting towards them from a parallel runway.
ATC Center controller reported vectoring several aircraft for sequencing to an airport unnecessarily due to faulty instructions from the Traffic Management Unit.
Pilots flying into BOS were told to fly the Runway 27 transition and they misunderstood and thought they also had descent clearance on the approach.
CRJ-200 Captain reported a loss of hydraulic fluid that resulted in a diversion.
Flight crew reported Air Traffic Control instructions may mislead unsuspecting flight crews to unsafe descent on approach.
Maintenance reported that Management wanted to load passengers and cargo on a B737 while at the same time the tires were being replaced.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was removed due to non-compliance with face mask policy.
MAF Controller reported VFR aircraft were allowed to conduct operations in an IFR Class C airspace.
An MD-80; prepared to push back; was evacuated at the gate after the flight attendants reported smoke in the cabin. Maintenance believed the APU was the smoke's source.
B737-800 flight crew reported their aircraft's right winglet struck an EMB-175 at the adjacent gate during pushback.
A TRACON Controller reported the Tower allowed a VFR aircraft to climb into confliction with traffic TRACON was vectoring on downwind.

The flight crew of a MD11 reported that they departed with an aircraft with an MEL item that prohibited them from entering icing conditions. It turned out; the weather was not as forecast and they had to deviate several times to avoid icing.
Air carrier First Officer reported approach clearance deviation resulting in ATC assigned vectors to restart the approach for an uneventful landing.
Air carrier Captain reported Dangerous Goods cargo was unloaded due to packages being crushed.
A320 flight crew reported receiving Dispatch notification during cruise of Hazmat [Dry Ice] loaded on aircraft without providing NOTOC [Notice to Captain] prior to departure.

A Local Controller at TEB reports a loss of separation given to him at Local; he then tries to use visual separation but cannot. Finally sees both aircraft and lets the second aircraft land instead of sending it around.
Technician reported while following guidance in applying an MEL for a yaw damper fault; errors were made in the maintenance log.

B777 Flight Officer mentions improper flight planning policy with regards to terrain clearance/avoidance for Depressurization scenario due to high terrain along their route in South America. Crew contacts Dispatch while enroute asking for a new route to properly avoid terrain in case of a depressurization issue and is left on their own to file a new route after Dispatcher and on duty Chief Pilot question the flight crew's reasoning. New route is filed by crew and accepted by Colombian ATC some 3 hours later.
S46 TRACON Controller reported their sector reached an unsafe workload level during a runway change operation because they were working combined sectors.
Air carrier flight crew reported three aircraft on the same arrival with similar call signs caused confusion for pilots and controller.

Maintenance Technician reported an aircraft arrived at the facility as a result of a 'Corona issue' and needed deep cleaning. The Maintenance Technician reported that there are no policies in place for this issue.

Air carrier Captain describes a severe turbulence event where the aircraft gained 700 feet and lost 40 knots of airspeed. No injuries initially reported; however one of the flight attendants; who bumped her head in the turbulence; did seek medical attention after landing at the destination.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a passenger disembarked the aircraft during boarding without permission and violated security protocol.

An Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) pilot reported his aircraft and a law enforcement helicopter had a near miss at 550 feet even though his UAS computerized flight plan's altitude restriction was 400 feet. The QNH was 30.40 at the time.

B747 flight crew reported loss of Number 1 hydraulic system in cruise after being dispatched with Number 1 engine driven hydraulic pump on MEL. Flight crew returned to departure airport with limited flaps.
Air Carrier Captain reported multiple flight deck electronic component failure to ATC. ATC stated very likely caused by GPS jamming.
B777-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding wide body aircraft in cruise flight over the North Atlantic.

Air carrier flight crew described a Runway 19L departure from SFO in bad weather. During the required low altitude turn for terrain; moderate turbulence was encountered along with several bank angle warnings and a windshear warning.
GA pilot reported having to go-around after being high on the final approach to FHR airport. Reporter stated a missed approach should have been executed rather than a go-around.
A Tower Local Controller accepted a point out of a Flight Check aircraft which did not maneuver as expected causing conflicts with traffic on the final approach course.

Flight crew of a Large Transport aircraft reported descending approximately 700 feet due to using the local altimeter setting instead of 29.92. ATC asked about their altitude change and then assigned FL200 to fix the discrepancy.

Aspen Tower Controller reported of a runway incursion where an arrival was told to go around due to arrival traffic on the runway. The second aircraft landed and did not go-around as instructed. Pilot reported an equipment problem after landing.
Ground employee reported concerns with touching bags on a daily basis and would like the bags to be sprayed to prevent infection.
SR22 pilot landing #6 in a flight of 7 reported encountering 'very significant' wake turbulence during the landing flare.
Air carrier First Officer reported ATC failure to provide separation of traffic overshooting parallel runway resulted in an NMAC.
GA pilot reported a NMAC at SAV airport.
A TRACON Supervisor reported a light aircraft in a satellite airport airspace reported a wake vortex encounter with a Super aircraft on approach to another airport.
Pitts S-2 Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft.
A Center Controller reported they had difficulty communicating with an aircraft that lost an engine and was diverting due to poor radio coverage at low altitudes in their sector.
B737 Captain reported that a procedures bulletin that had not yet been activated resulted in an inadvertent policy violation.
Flight Attendant reported problems with procedures and company safety culture that could result in a safety issue.

An air carrier was vectored off the RKSI GONAV2M arrival and told to expect the ILS 33R then given direct KOTRA cleared for the approach. The Pilot Monitoring selected KOTRA JISUN bypassing TOMAL and the descent began early. A better ATC clearance would have been ILS 33R; KOTRA transition.
Technicians reported confusion over training related to prioritization of work requirements.
A320 flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from AUS Tower on approach to Runway 35R.

A procedure allowing pilot provided visual separation between successive departures caused a conflict between an A321 aircraft climbing at a much slower airspeed than a B737-800 departing behind the A321.
First Officer reported the flight exceeded the 10;000 foot FAR speed restriction. Task saturation and time off during COVID-19 were cited as contributing factors.
ZLA Center Controller reported a radar site was producing radar track jumps and dual radar targets on their radar displays.

ZOB Controller reported of clearing an aircraft for an approach to a closed runway and switching the aircraft to the advisory frequency. A controller sitting near the reporter asked him if Runway 18 was still closed.  The reporting Controller realized what he had done and attempted to reach the landing aircraft. Eventually the landing aircraft called the reporter and cancelled IFR and went to land on a different runway.
B737 First Officer reported his concerns with what he sees as an inadequate recurrent training program at his airline.

A B737 Captain reported that when he refused to fly an aircraft to SNA because of heavy light aircraft traffic and an inoperative TCAS his company 'shopped' the aircraft to other flight crews hoping someone would take it.
PA-18 Amphibian Pilot reported failure to raise landing gear during water landing resulted in aircraft capsizing.
Gate agent reported a Lithium Collapsible wheelchair was checked in at the ticket counter; without the Lithium battery being removed.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported difficulty communicating with a flight that was experiencing an engine problem and was returning to departure airport.

Flight Attendant reported having to explain to a passenger that there were no exemptions to having to wear a face mask.  Passenger and two others traveling with him were not compliant during the flight.
EMB-135LR First officer reported an NMAC event during arrival.
A320 Captain reported experiencing a low fuel state due to questionable vectors and a Tower directed go-around at EWR.

A321 flight crew reported being advised by ATC that the SERFR2 arrival would take them below Class B airspace. The design of this arrival caused them to momentarily exceed the speed requirement below Class B airspace.

SF340B Captain reported being advised just before departure that the aircraft was being grounded to a maintenance issue related to a cockpit window. Reporter stated the aircraft logbook did not reflect the discrepancy and the aircraft had already operated two flights earlier in the day.
B737 First Officer expressed concern that ATC was not sure what altitude they were climbing to.
Boeing 787 Maintenance Personnel reported writing up a hydraulic leak and the item was signed off by a Maintenance Manager without any actual repairs accomplished.
SR22 pilot reported deviation for weather and severe turbulence without ATC approval.
ORD Tower Controller reported concern with aircraft departing on the Runway 28s and possible go-arounds on the Runway 27s.
Corporate pilot reported leveling off at the wrong altitude while being distracted with the cowl flaps.
PRC Tower Controller reported a NMAC when an aircraft turned too close to the traffic it was to follow.
B787 Captain reported flight crew Hazmat procedure deviation of 'Final DG summary' review and approval prior to takeoff.
Vehicle Driver reported striking and injuring a pedestrian.
Air carrier Captain reported questioning Company Management's policy of not reporting certain aircraft maintenance issues.
ZID Controller acting as an Instructor/Monitor while a Supervisor gave a position certification to a trainee reported an airspace violation and violations of Letters of Agreement.
Air carrier flight crew reported being out of contact for ten minutes in Fukuoka airspace due to a late hand off from Manila ATC and an error in ATC frequency information in the Jepp-Pro application.

A Dispatcher reported his company's delay activation authority to many users/stations in addition to Dispatch is a possible FAR violation regarding control of a flight's progress by other than Dispatch. The procedure has safety implications.
Medium transport Captain reported losing contact with ATC for approximately 4 minutes shortly after takeoff from EWR.

A330 First Officer reported receiving a very tight vector for an approach; resulting in the aircraft becoming low on the glideslope and generating a GPWS warning. A timely correction was made to the glideslope and a successful landing was accomplished.
Air carrier First Officer reported overshooting the assigned altitude due to high speeds.
B737 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence at FL350 while deviating around thunderstorms.
Airbus Captain reported receiving a SMOKE AFT CARGO ECAM during deplaning. It was later determined the alarm was caused by insecticide sprayed into the compartment by ground personnel.
Captain reported the First Officer mistakenly deleted the route in legs 1.
ZAB Controller reported an E175 11 miles behind a B737-700 encountered wake turbulence causing a 35 degree un-commanded roll.

A Dispatcher reported a flight crew checked their communications to Dispatch via the Iridium SATLINK system while on a polar route over ABERI intersection. The crew's message took 50 minutes to arrive at Dispatch whereupon the Dispatcher immediately issued a reply to the aircraft. Twenty minutes after the Dispatcher sent the reply; the crew sent back confirmation that they had received the Dispatch reply.
B787 flight crew reported that the information for two deferrals for the same system were very confusing.
GA pilot reported an airborne conflict as a result of ATC not providing adequate separation between aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported being issued a crossing restriction that was not in the FMS. Post flight the crew found out the fix was in the FMS and may have been accidentally deleted it during the flight.

B737 crew reported being cleared for takeoff while taking the runway at MSP.  They switch frequencies to which a Clearance Delivery Controller answers and sends them back to previous Tower frequency.  First Officer verifies they are still cleared for takeoff.
Air carrier ramp employee reported becoming ill after cleaning up a hydraulic spill without using a respirator or mask.

B777 flight crew reported being unable to receive a rereleased fuel required at their planned redispatch point due to the fact that the flight planning software was currently down. The crew was told to continue toward their destination and the required fuel was finally sent 16 minutes past the redispatch point.
A pilot back taxiing to depart a non towered airport reported an aircraft landed opposite direction.

A TRACON Controller reported they had to cancel an aircraft's approach clearance and subsequently the aircraft responded to an RA for an unidentified VFR aircraft flying through the final approach course.
Ramp personnel reported aircraft not clear of frost at the completion of aircraft deicing.
B747 First Officer reported a course deviation resulted when the ANC 07R localizer signal displayed anomalies. Reporter stated he has seen this issue in the past.
ZOA Center Controller reported clearing an aircraft for a company specific approach procedure but the aircraft did not comply with the procedure as depicted.
An air carrier First Officer on an ILS final approach reported responding to a RA and taking evasive action to avoid an unidentified converging VFR aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported that re-qualification training was inadequate.
A Center Controller reported the Area was understaffed and Traffic Management flow was not in place during a period of known weather deviations.
EMB-145 flight crew reported landing after an unstable approach.
Ramp personnel reported Dangerous Goods cargo not properly secured in aircraft.
A Center Controller reported they observed an aircraft fail to intercept an airway and flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

S46 Approach Controller reported a conflict between an air carrier on an ILS approach to BFI and unidentified VFR traffic crossing the final approach course. Controller immediately turned the air carrier to the right in order to resolve the conflict. Reporter stated this is a common occurrence with VFR aircraft transiting a very busy and narrow corridor without communication with ATC.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with face mask policy.

A Lead Technician reported that when called out to check the Main Landing Gear wheel on an A320; due to high brake temperature; found it unserviceable after the ground crew released the aircraft calling the wheel okay.

GA pilot reported during last snow and ice event in the Midwest many smaller airports were not listed as closed on the NOTAM list even though flying over many he confirmed they were indeed closed. The pilot reported selecting a larger airport that was reporting conditions.
B737 Flight Crew reported they forgot to retract landing gear after takeoff.

An Air Carrier crew flying the SLC NORDK3 RNAV arrival had a track deviation because of confusion about the two D Ball Notes with separate instructions to either Runway 16L or Ruways 16R; 17. The SKEES3 Arrival caused the same confusion.
Maule MX-7 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control occurred during landing.
EMB-135 flight crew reported that due to a communication error between pilots and ATC there was confusion when instructions stated to join the approach not clearance for the approach.
Air carrier Captain reported a flap setting issue during climbout and stated that a callout sounding similar to the flap callout may have caused confusion.
Approach Controller reported vectoring three aircraft to a closed runway and not realizing it until the first aircraft went around.
Pilot reported a NMAC during approach.
ZMA Center Controller reported managing aircraft conflicts caused by difficulty coordinating with adjacent airspace.
NCT TRACON Controller used insufficient spacing for an Opposite Direction Operation at SCK.
C210 pilot reported an NMAC with a glider.
While accomplishing an 'A' check on a B747; Maintenance found that an aft mount inboard tangential link was loose with radial and axial play.
A320 flight crew reported sighting a UAV in the vicinity of TEB at 3;300 feet.

The KZAK/ZOA Oceanic Controllers are supposed to transfer flight plan information of aircraft entering Manila's airspace 30 minutes prior to the Flight Information Region (FIR). A KZAK Oceanic controller thought he coordinated with the Tokyo controllers that the Tokyo Controller would forward the information to Manila on four flights. The Tokyo Controller thought they were only forwarding information on two flights. A subsequent KZAK Oceanic Controller and Tokyo Controller coordinated information and realized that Tokyo had only passed information on two flights. The KZAK Controller coordinated the other two flights five and fifteen minutes from the Manila FIR. The Letter of Agreement states the information needs to be passed to Manila 30 minutes from the FIR.
Mechanic reported that after pitot inspections are accomplished prior to departure; aircraft frequently sit on the ground for hours with no pitot tube covers to protect against contaminants.
B767-300 Captain reported incorrect bulbs were installed in cockpit switches; causing lights to illuminate incorrectly and requiring a diversion.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reports that a Maintenance Manual procedure is unsafe.
Air Carrier pilot reported the company failed to update aircraft navigation database.
A319 flight crew reported that during preflight; high winds blew an airstair into the Number 1 engine cowling.

SCT TRACON Controller reported an unsafe operation with a departure off of Long Beach Airport and a IFR aircraft close to the departure end of the runway. Controller recommended that LGB become a Class Charlie airspace.
Boeing 737 Captain reported that the aircraft hydraulic system has been routinely overserviced.
B737 NG Captain reported diverting to an alternate when the crew oxygen pressure dropped below 500 PSI while enroute.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that a flight he was working landed in CMH with less than the required fuel reserve due to unforecast weather.
Air carrier Captain reported being cleared to cross a runway with an aircraft on takeoff roll at MSY airport.
A TRACON Controller reported an aircraft they had just shipped to Tower went around in response to a TCAS/RA for an unidentified VFR target in the vicinity of the Final Approach course.
ERJ-170 flight crew reported an incursion into P56 during a go-around after encountering wind shear on approach to DCA.
Air carrier First Officer reported not meeting a crossing restriction and cited several distractions which contributed; including problems with call sign and concerns with COVID-19.
ERJ-145 Flight Crew reported taking evasive action after receiving a GPWS terrain warning on vectors to an approach to ROA.
A319 Captain reported on taxi out; ECAM message showed both thrust reversers inoperative.
ZZZ Center Controller reported that an adjacent area went ATC Zero; resulting in a complex and difficult traffic situation.
B737 Captain reported that the navigation database in the FMS was in conflict with the approach plates for La Aurora Airport (MGGT).
JAX TRACON Controller had a loss of separation between two arrivals while utilizing new arrival routes and procedures after confusing similar sounding call signs.

Due to a distance-shortening vector and late change of STAR clearance; crew was above required airspeed when crossing a fix. Crew had been unable to contact ATC for speed relief due to saturated frequency. ATC provided vectors to achieve required spacing.

An IFR light twin pilot in VMC was advised by ZMP Center about a single engine VFR aircraft at the same altitude. ATC assumed the light twin had TCAS; but it did not and the aircrafts had a near miss.

ZKC Controller reported of an aircraft departing; but not tagging up. Aircraft was never identified and minutes later the pilot asked if he should be on another frequency. Aircraft was 15 miles into someone else's airspace. Controller called adjacent airspace Controller and made handoff and told receiving Controller the plane was never identified.
ZJX Center Controller reported two ALTRVs (Altitude Reservations) were in the same area and altitude.
First Officer reported discovering a box with arrows pointing down when they should have been up. The box was labeled 'FLAMMABLE LIQUID' and 'CARGO AIRCRAFT ONLY.'
Air Traffic Controller reported that he advised an MD-88 crew to descend to clear opposing traffic. MD-88 crew complied despite receiving a TCAS RA to climb.
Air Carrier flight crew reported uneventfully landing from an unstable approach; whereas per SOP; a go-around should have been executed.

Phenom 300 Captain reported a serious degradation of his ability to navigate due to suspected GPS jamming while descending into SLC. Captain received radar vectors from ATC to the airport; however; they experienced several RAs from an undetermined source during the approach.
B717 Maintenance Technician reported conflicting procedures resulted in autoland test not being performed prior to aircraft departure.
Helicopter Captain reported overflying a service interval that was missed by maintenance tracking.

DVT Tower Controller reports of a language problem with pilots. An aircraft was cleared for a full stop; but did a touch and go. Controller reported that pilots do not understand or respond; they just do whatever they want.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to CLT in trail of an A321.
Pilot reported being rusty after lack of flying due to the COVID-19 pandemic resulting in communication issues with ATC.
New York TRACON Controller reported that an aircraft descended below a crossing restriction.
C172 pilot reported a loss of control after landing due to crosswinds that resulted in contacting runway lights and a runway excursion.
Captain reported that the deicing location had several safety hazards; including no wing-walkers; poor lighting and unplowed snow on the surface.
Air carrier Captain reported not entering required verbiage onto a form and said that lack of recent flying contributed to the oversight.
Small Transport pilot reported a passenger medical situation inflight.

Corporate jet with passengers attempted a landing at an uncontrolled airport with heavy traffic in the pattern. Additionally; windshear conditions existed prompting a go-around. Because of the heavy workload there was a lapse in the coordination with ATC.

A CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence shortly after takeoff from DTW in trail of an A330 that resulted in up to 50 degrees of uncommanded roll. Reporter stated he felt ATC spacing could have been more generous.

After towing an aircraft to the terminal; with the tow tractor disconnected; the Mechanic riding brakes left the cockpit seat before ground power was available to pressurize the hydraulic system so he could set the brakes.
C182 Student Pilot and ATC Controller reported the former taking the clearance of another aircraft and descended below the MVA as a result.
B747-400 flight crew reported being notified by the outbound crew that the emergency door slide was bolted closed rendering it unusable.

MEM Tower controllers reported a Tower tour distracted the Ground Controller allowing an aircraft to taxi to the wrong runway and Local departed traffic off that runway. Departure was in conflict with arrival traffic; so the Local Controller vectored the departure and sent the arrival around.
Passengers started boarding before the flight attendants were ready for them.
Tower Controller reported inadvertently giving an incorrect heading to a departing aircraft resulting in a conflict with an aircraft on final.

An aircraft on an RNAV arrival with a transition to the ILS approach was vectored off the arrival to join the localizer then cleared for a visual approach. There was confusion between the pilots and ATC if the crew was navigating in accordance with their clearance.
GA pilot reported experiencing a ground conflict while hold short of runway ready for takeoff.
Air Carrier Captain reported a passenger was not complying with face mask policy during flight while sleeping.
Captain reported stopping abruptly to avoid a baggage tug that refused to yield to his aircraft taxiing to the gate.
PA46 single pilot reported an uneventful diversion due to an in-flight encounter during cruise with an unknown object resulting windshield damage.
B737-800 Captain reported that several ground crew members were not complying with the new company policy.
A300 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence at FL240 during climb with no 'G' limits exceeded.
Flight Attendant reported a group of passengers would not comply with face mask policy.

An air carrier Captain reported executing a missed approach on a SFO Runway 28L visual after the second TCAS RA climb command from another aircraft which apparently erroneously lined up on 28L instead of the assigned 28R.
MSP Controller reported of windshear at the airport and management not changing the runway configuration.

Pilot reports of Controller descending them to an altitude; then telling pilot to maintain an altitude they are at and going through. Pilot advises ATC and climbs up to specified altitude. Traffic is below their aircraft 1;000 feet. Controller forgot about the other aircraft orbiting.

A Dispatcher reported ground personnel notified him 30 minutes after an aircraft departed that 11 unreported bags were in fact in the aft pit. No performance or center of gravity issues were discovered.

A PA-32 pilot reported distress when; on final approach cleared and configured to land on Runway 33 at ISM; he was directed to turn left to 270 degrees apparently to accommodate a faster Gulfstream closing behind him that had performed a 360 to slow and lose altitude. While on the heading he was alerted of towers ahead by his ground proximity warning system.
ZAB Center Controller reported vectoring an aircraft without knowing the aircraft's altitude; which may have been below the Minimum IFR altitude.
Customs personnel reported hazardous shipment arrived incorrectly labeled and transported.
A319 Captain reported an incorrect MEL action by an unqualified Maintenance Personnel; resulting in a loss of cabin cooling during the flight.
Flight Attendant reported communication and procedural issues experienced with another Flight Attendant.

An EC-135 pilot reported when using Night Vision Goggles (NVG) and flying an aircraft which requires the instrument panel night vision external light shielding; the chin bubble mat blocks all downward vision thus obstructing obstacles during steep landing descents. The glareshield curtain permits downward vision but also filters the light from the instrument panels.
Q400 First Officer reported landing gear failed to retract when selected up; caused by the landing gear alternate release panel cover which was not properly closed.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that Dispatchers were not advised by their company of missile launches which could have affected the safety of flights operating near the launch area.

A Controller reported that while de-combining previously combined sectors he observed an aircraft assigned a downwind heading turn base leg on its own into confliction with traffic straight in to the runway.
Air Carrier First Officer reported experiencing an airborne conflict after takeoff from RDM airport.

Two flight instructors reported that an aircraft might be unairworthy due to a required annual inspection not done. When the instructors asked maintenance to show the aircraft logbooks; the request was refused and the Chief Flight Instructor reprimanded the instructors.
Air carrier Captain reported having a passenger who was coughing throughout the flight.
EMB-135 First Officer reported overlooking a circuit breaker that was pulled and collared by Maintenance and caused an Air Turnback when the landing gear would not retract.
Pilot flying a small jet aircraft reported a near mid air collision with another small jet aircraft.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported concerns with crew rest facility restrictions during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Air Carrier First Officer reported encountering closed taxiways at CHO that were not included in NOTAMs for their arrival time.
SCT Controller did not specify the direction of turn to a fix and the descending aircraft turned the opposite direction resulting in a conflict with another aircraft.
Two dispatchers reported requesting an additional Dispatch desk be opened for late night Caribbean departures and arrivals due to forecasted weather; but the requests were ignored by management.
On a Dassault Falcon 7X the basic inspection procedures were not being followed per the aircraft maintenance manual.
Air carrier flight crew reported being distracted prior to departure and neglected to receive an amended release for an MEL item.

While flying the initial segment of the IZZZO 3 RNAV DEP out of PHX; B737 crew was given a 'delete speed restrictions' clearance by Departure Control. Due to the short spacing between the first three fixes on the SID; the crew induced a 'Route Discontinuity' in the current segment of the FMC route immediately after removing the speed restriction on the third fix. Since they were nearly on top the first fix; the crew proceeded direct to the second fix. Crew was unsure if they had followed the correct SID track exactly during the time it took to correctly reprogram the FMC. Crew recommends that ATC not issue this 'delete speed restrictions' clearance until flights are clear of these closely-spaced fixes.

B737 Captain reported a communications breakdown between flight crew and ramp personnel regarding Lithium Ion battery powered scooter loaded in cargo compartment. Reporter added the Hazmat distraction item affected crew focus on departure routing adherence.
B737 First Officer reported the #1 engine and some leading edge devices were damaged from a bird strike on approach.
A Maintenance Technician reported that the Auxiliary Power Unit was serviced with Turbo Oil 2197.
A PA-27 engine lost power inflight. An emergency was declared; and the pilot landed at a nearby airport where the fuel pump diaphragm was found to have been damaged by an overtightened screw.
A PA28 instructor pilot reported that during climb they had a near miss with another PA28 that was on descent.
C172 flight instructor reported NMAC in pattern due to inadequate ATC service.
IND TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict resulting from an air carrier flight descending through their assigned altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported being given multiple schedule changes in a short period of time; which caused fatigue and distraction.

A B737NG crew at FL380 entered thunderstorm blow off clouds at a -47ºC OAT and a short time later the FMC issued a BUFFET Alert with a -27ºC OAT; N1 at max CLB and a max altitude of FL350. The flight descended with light icing.
B747 Captain reported that inability to communicate with the tug crew caused the aircraft to momentarily depart the taxiway centerline.

A CVG Controller reports of losing a total loss of electrical power. The Controller attempts to use the backup radio but it does not work. The Controller plugs the backup radio into a different outlet and it then works. The Controller reports that he/she is appalled at the lack of maintaining the equipment at the airport.
B737 flight crew reported a track heading deviation during approach to MCO airport.  Similar sounding fix names CRUZE and HUWES were cited as a contributing factor.
Flight Attendant reported that passengers were boarded onto an aircraft that was too hot.
EMB-175 Captain reported being struck by lightning on approach to SLC.

B-747 crew; after checking enroute charts and querying ATC; discovered that the holding pattern in their FMC was incorrect. Crew used manual heading to enter the holding pattern and iPad information to reprogram the FMC. Crew mentioned a lack of training in locating holding data in the iPad.

B737 flight crew reported that while on the CREDE3 RNAV Arrival into DEN; ATC changed the clearance from the chart-depicted crossing at POWDR between FL220 and 17000 to at 17000 ft. The crew was unable to make that crossing restriction and the STAR's speed restrictions and were vectored off arrival as a result.

PA31 pilot and local FBO at a non-towered airport heard no transmissions on CTAF by a Gulfstream V aircraft holding short of the runway nor from a landing commuter jet. Reporter expressed concern with the practice of corporate and commuter aircraft communicating only with the local ATC Center and not utilizing CTAF.
B737 Captain reported on arrival into BOS he received 3 reroutes in 5 minutes. Due to unfamiliarity with the routing he requested help with the fixes and was told to look it up himself.
Captain reported that Company DGS [Docking Guidance System] system is not recording flight time correctly for FAR compliance.
Maintenance employee reported that employee buses are full and social distancing on them is not being properly addressed.

Air carrier flight crew reported that while flying the MHT ROZZE RNAV STAR into deteriorating icing and runway conditions; encountered a very high workload as ATC changed arrival transitions and monitoring runway and icing conditions.
Flight Attendant reported a failure to inform other flight attendants that the First Officer was an FFDO.
A student at a flight school reported that the school's aircraft are not maintained in a safe condition.
PA-34 Instructor Pilot reported his student retracted the landing gear before takeoff on a touch and go; resulting in a prop strike and gear up landing.

A320 Captain reported communications breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch regarding Hazmat cargo issues resulting in ground return to gate. Incident highlighted the need for additional Hazmat training of ground personnel.
B737 First Officer reported experiencing a TCAS Resolution Advisory which resulted in a Go-Around from his approach to Runway 27 at San Diego.
Salt Lake Center Controller reported that an aircraft came close to descending below a Minimum IFR Altitude but did not.

SF340 First Officer reports departing for TPA with a clear forecast and no alternate required. Enroute developing thunderstorms are reported and an alternate is requested from Dispatch. During the approach; a 20+ knot speed increase is noted and heavy rain is encountered. Aircraft ahead and behind the SF340 break off their approaches and this crew soon follows and diverts to their alternate.

PIT TRACON Controller reported of releasing an aircraft off of an airport. The airport was not the one the aircraft was departing. Once airborne the Controller realized his error; realized a loss of separation was occurring; and worked to separate traffic.
Air Carrier Captain reported concern with overweight and oversized baggage being allowed on the aircraft.

EMB175 Captain reported severe turbulence associated with a thunderstorm on departure. During a 30 degree banked turn with the autopilot engaged the aircraft momentarily rolled to 45 degrees before recovering. Flight continued to destination.
Air carrier Captain reported a medical issue that caused a large distraction for the pilots.  The reporter recommended that Medlink calls be handled by the cabin crew.
Cessna T210 pilot reported an airspace violation departing WHP; citing terrain concerns as contributing.
Dispatcher reported that a flight was delayed without notification of the Dispatcher.
SAN Tower Controller reported confusion with guidance on issuing crossing clearance to aircraft too early.
Air carrier First Officer reported the flight encountered wind shear and severe turbulence associated with a line of storms resulting in altitude/airspeed excursions; a conflict and a divert.
Load Control Agent directed taxiing aircraft to return to gate due to incorrect Hazmat load configuration.
CRJ-200 Maintenance Technician reported that allotted time is insufficient to allow safe and proper inspections on the aircraft fleet.

Air carrier flight crew reported encountering multiple delays as passengers and crew members deplaned and went through a foreign country's required COVID-19 protocols. Crew also reported concerns with the layover accommodations.
An instructor pilot took the aircraft from his student at 1;100 feet to take evasive action during a near miss with a student pilot who strayed from the HEF closed pattern.
Air Carrier First Officer reported multiple TAs and RAs during approach to DFW.
CRQ Local Controller reported a NMAC when an aircraft flew beneath another aircraft after not complying with ATC instructions.
The flight crew of a B737-800; after encountering severe weather at FL380; diverted to land at the nearest airport.
B787 First Officer reported a severe wave encounter at FL380 which resulted in an overspeed and uncommanded 500 foot climb.
Maintenance controller reported that an aircraft flew without the required maintenance documentation.
GA pilot reported becoming NORDO and flying through an MOA.
A11 TRACON Controller reported a low altitude notification for an aircraft flying the KNIK2 SID out of ANC airport.
A Piper Arrow made a gear-up landing on a highway due to an engine failure. The engine failed due to loss of oil because of an incorrect 'quick drain' oil plug installed.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported poor communication between the flight attendants; flight crew; and ramp personnel after returning to the gate due to maintenance.
Pilots report of a aircraft arriving and one departing which came close to colliding.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence 7 miles in trail of another B737 on descent into MCO that rolled the aircraft into a 40 degree bank.
MD11 First Officer reported accepting the aircraft and flew with 13 listed MELs.
Cessna 150 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control during landing resulting in a runway excursion.
B787 Captain reported that an MEL weight penalty resulted in a maintenance action and flight cancellation.

The Local Controller cleared an aircraft for takeoff. The crew of the aircraft told the Controller they would wait because it appeared a taxiing aircraft had not cleared the runway. The Controller's ASDE-X (Ground Radar) indicated the taxiing aircraft was clear of the runway.

A pilot reported that because MCX and OXI are in close proximity and share a common CTAF frequency; sometimes an aircraft's location is confused. Reporter stated some pilots do not clearly speak the respective airport names adding to the confusion.

B737-800 Captain reported that; according to ATC; during descent the aircraft was 700 feet high over a crossing restriction of 8000 feet. The Captain stated the aircraft was in VNAV PATH the whole descent with no scratchpad advisories.
CRJ-700 First Officer reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence after takeoff from CLT in trail of an A319.
B767 flight crew and Dispatcher reported during takeoff the trim setting felt incorrect and later discovered undocumented cargo was on board.
G550 flight crew were knowingly given an improper count of adult and infant passengers by the aircraft owner in order to accommodate more adult passengers than the crew would have accepted.
The pilot of a C402 reported severe turbulence that resulted in a momentary loss of control and injury to one passenger.
A321 pilot reported being too high on approach by ATC resulting in unstabilized and missed approach.
Maintenance Lead Inspector reported a step was being skipped during lavatory faucet sanitation procedures.
Large Turbojet flight crew reported a taxiway excursion at LFPG resulting in damage to two main wheels.
A pilot reported a Ground Controller scolded him on frequency for not complying with their taxi instructions in an expeditious manner.
ELP Tracon Controller reported a developmental controller took handoff on aircraft at an altitude below the MVA.
Small aircraft pilot reported mislabeled SAV airport runways 1 and 19 on the Garmin G3X database.
A CRJ-700 First Officer reported the flight crew was distracted by a company communication while awaiting an ATC clearance and holding short of the runway.
A Lead Technician reported that a B737 did not have the proper inspection performed after a lightning strike.
EMB-135 Captain reported a traffic conflict with a helicopter near the final approach corridor.
ZSU Controller reported a loss of separation and an NMAC even though they complied with the rules in the FAAH 7110.65.
Tower Controller working combined positions reported an aircraft did not call Ramp Tower as expected.
Air Carrier pilot reported confusion during taxi due to inaccurate airport diagram.
A flight instructor distracted by a student struggling with the autopilot reported he allowed the student to descend below the assigned altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported momentarily exceeding the aircraft's maximum operating limit speed on descent. The Captain cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
B737-800 flight crew reported communication issues between the flight deck crew and flight attendant crew regarding odor in the cabin.
A heavy freight aircraft crew rejected their takeoff at about 100 knots complying with a windshear alert. The QRH was completed and a successful takeoff followed.
Atlanta TRACON Controller reported an arrival into Atlanta did not cross a fix at the Letter of Agreement Altitude.

CVG Local Controller reports of a pilot missing his turn-out off the runway. Controller instructs pilot to taxi on parallel runway where sweepers were working. Before Controller could advise the vehicles; a Sweeper calls the Tower to alert him of an aircraft on the runway.
ZLA Center Controller reported an aircraft using a new OAPM departure procedure checked on their frequency climbing to the wrong altitude.
MEM Tower Local Controller reported they had to issue last minute instructions to separate an arriving aircraft which initiated a go-around just as a departure was airborne.
ZMP Center Controller reported an unsafe conflict situation that was being handed off by another Center.
ZDV Center Controllers reported the trainee failed to take appropriate action to resolve a traffic conflict.
Bonanza 36 Single pilot reported an NMAC event due to wrong altitude.
EMB-145 flight crew reported severe turbulence on departure resulting in altitude deviation.

Maintenance Technician reports that passenger seat row was installed in the wrong position.

Maintenance reported they installed an incorrect bearing on an MD-11 nose gear wheel assembly.

MWH Tower Controller reports of another Controller who the reporter thinks is complacent in their duties. Reporting Controller reports of a Call for Release(CFR) for traffic that is in the pattern and eventually going back to the airport that has the CFR. Aircraft leaves the traffic pattern and goes to departure who then issues a beacon code and clearance. Controller did not call for release on the aircraft. Reporter states the other controller is not held accountable for his actions.
ZAU Controller reported difficulties with an airport closing and IFR traffic into the airport after the nightly closure.
First Officer reported refueling panel access door was not closed by fueler; resulting in an air turnback and precautionary landing.
B757 Airline Mechanic reported a procedural violation regarding a non qualified technician performing maintenance on aircraft.
Boeing 737 Captain reported that an MEL item appeared inconsistent with the fault light that illuminated.
A pilot reported the need to take evasive maneuvers to avoid an amateur rocket launch.
A321 Captain reported difficulty interpreting and troubleshooting Fuel System messages due to an MEL item related to the fuel system.
Citation pilot reported that a descent on their arrival was a factor for other traffic departing a close by airport.
B777 flight crew reported three Flight Attendants were slightly injured in a wake turbulence encounter at FL320 near TNT VOR in England.
