Air carrier flight crew reported encountering an EGPWS activation during a visual approach.
B777 Flight Attendant reported area of vomit that was inefficiently cleaned created a health hazard to passengers and flight attendants.

Pilot reports of flying into SDM and not being familiar with the jump zones that are close to the airport. Controller vectors aircraft for a visual approach; leaves the aircraft high; and then ships the aircraft to the Tower. Aircraft turns away from airport to continue its descent and Tower Controller advises pilot he is going into a jump zone. Pilot finally lands at SDM. Pilot reports that this is an unsafe operation and offers suggestions to make flying into SDM better.
B737-700 Captain reported missing a taxi clearance that was issued during a critical phase of flight during reverse thrust application on landing roll.
Pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft while receiving instruction for a checkout.
Ramp Personnel reported bag unloaded from baggage cargo with batteries attached.
Air Carrier Maintenance Technician reports improper use of Engineering Authorization to defer maintenance action.
C172 pilot reported an NMAC with an IFR aircraft while on a VFR practice approach.
EMB-145 First Officer reported the flight crew lost directional control on landing roll due to workload and weather; resulting in a runway excursion.
Ground Personnel reported that the jetbridge overhangs the safety zone at several gates; resulting in a collision hazard.
B787-800 flight crew reported possible lightning strike.

One sector pointed out an aircraft to another sector and handed it off to the subsequent sector. The sector the aircraft was being handed off to had conflicting traffic and attempted to coordinate control instructions to the Controller working the aircraft. Due to weather complexities; sectors that were combined were being split to alleviate the workload. In the ensuing confusion of closed sectors being opened and busy traffic; the aircraft was not pointed out to one of the newly opened sectors.
ERJ-175 Captain reported that passengers deplaned via air stairs in front of a running engine.
A widebody air carrier Captain reported executing a go-around during an unstable approach because of the high speed assigned by ATC close in to the airfield.
Jacksonville Center Controller reported an airspace violation due to a busy TRACON and lack of non-radar procedures.
Ground employee reported removing passenger luggage from cargo due to Hazmat restriction. Reporter also noted FAA security breach by leaving baggage in unattended on the jetbridge.
Flight Crew reported being uncertain of the taxi instructions from ground control; causing them to cross a hold short line of an active runway.

An air carrier Captain noted while flying the SFO SERFR ONE Arrival ATC issues a 'Descend VIA except maintain 10;000 feet' so while flying the SFO BDEGA ONE after a runway change from 28L to 28R he automatically selected 10;000 feet as the CORKK crossing restriction. Consistent final crossing altitudes would help.
B737-700 Captain reported a laptop in the cabin that was sparking and smoldering was placed in a containment bag by a Flight Attendant.
Air carrier Captain reported weather and associated planning issues at many airports and in particular one that was an alternate for the flight.
B757 Captain reported that Maintenance did not comply with the MEL procedure prior to releasing the aircraft.

Atlanta Center Controller reported problems associated with aircraft that had minimal time in the Sector's airspace. The airspace design will not allow ample time to descend aircraft landing HSV; update the datablock; and initiate the handoff in proper time.
Medium Transport aircraft taxiing out on Taxiway C at KLAX stopped abruptly to avoid a possible collision with a Heavy Transport being pushed back onto the taxiway.
Crew reported that local operations delayed a flight without contacting Dispatch.
Flight Instructor reported the first time solo student pilot lost control of the aircraft during landing.
Air carrier Ground Personnel reported Hazmat cargo in 'Nesting System.'
An EMB175 Flight Attendant describes an unexpected encounter with severe turbulence that causes them an injury.
S46 TRACON Controller reported a non-mode C target tracked toward the localizer course while another aircraft was inbound.

Three B737 Flight Attendants reported their air conditioning was unable to sufficiently cool on the ground or in flight because of extreme heat. The Lead Flight Attendant received emergency medical attention because of the prolonged triple digit heat exposure.
ERJ-190 Captain reported the nose swung 20 to 30 degrees left on the takeoff roll in response to a wind gust or wake from the previous takeoff.
The ERJ Captain reports that the EFB is installed in a poor location that is subject to water damage among other things.
D10 Controller reports of another position being inundated by traffic and taking late handoffs. Controller requested a handoff for the position but none was provided.
Flight Attendant reported confusion about airline policy relating to the face mask policy.
Flight instructor reported there was no NOTAM for a runway closure at JAN airport.
B737-800 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of an MD80 on approach to ATL.
Center Controller did not point out an aircraft and violated airspace within another center's airspace.

A Flight Attendant reports an aggressive and loud encounter with an unidentified uniformed airport person on the ramp after opening the aircraft rear service door upon arrival at the gate. This person demanded to see the crew's IDs and claimed that opening the service door prior to the departure of all passengers was a security violation. The door was opened to reduce the smell from day-old rotting meals in one of the service carts.
ZSE Controller describes an aircraft departure procedure that was not followed; and blames the tower for not issuing the correct routing.
B777 First Officer reported there were no company instructions or procedures for two unfamiliar device chargers in the cockpit.
Customer Service Representative reported that the airport is pumping artificial fragrances that are irritating and can cause headaches.
Flight instructor reported executing a missed approach from an instrument approach after descending below minimums.
Mechanic reported seatback tray tables were installed; and approved; in emergency exit rows.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported a sudden wind gust disengaged the autopilot and caused a momentary stick shaker on approach to ORD.

A320 First Officer reported not noticing the 6000 ft minimum vectoring altitude between MARUX and the IAF on the MMSD VOR DME-2 Runway 16 Approach. The reporter stated the altitude is not coded into their navigation database.
Flight Attendant reported experiencing fatigue from extended work hours and additional duties due to change in flight schedules in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Air carrier First Officer reported taking off without a required alternate. A delay due to spraying for COVID-19 was cited as a contributing factor.
J3 Cub pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at the non-towered airport.
Piper Cherokee pilot reported ATC vectored the aircraft toward high terrain in the vicinity of VNY.

PA-28 flight crew reported loss of directional control during instructional flight resulting in runway excursion.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC of final resulted in Evasive action.
Technician reported that operational and leak checks were not accomplished after component replacement because of failure to track required checks in the MEL procedures.

Air carrier Load Planner reported requesting Ramp Lead to move additional bags in order meet the minimum bags to configure Hazmat for air transport. Destination offload audit disclosed Hazmat did not have minimum bags required to secure it.
Learjet 60 First Officer reported an encounter with severe turbulence at FL400 resulted in an uncommanded 45 degree bank and loss of 3300 ft.
A300 flight crew reported that shortly after touchdown the aircraft lifted off again; possibly from a wake encounter or a wind gust.

Flight Instructor reported at take off seeing opposite direction traffic resulted in evasive action.
Air Carrier Maintenance Technician reported a personnel lift safety rail failure caused damage to aircraft.
A320 Captain reported extreme fatigue resulted from his three day assignment that included a 'red eye' leg on day two.

After querying ATC multiple times they were given a right turn. Later conversation with ATC revealed the Controller was using nonstandard phraseology by saying 'turn west' which the crew misinterpreted as 'turn left.'
Flight Crew reported a go around due to tail winds exceeding limits.

ONT Tower controllers reported of a miscommunication relating to a clearance. Aircraft departing was restricted to 4;000 feet; the Controller mistakenly heard then issued 5;000. Distractions were reportedly involved.

B737-800 Captain reported he recommended the crosswind landing limitation for scimitar winglet equipped aircraft be amended to allow for the use of the normal crosswind landing technique without potentially damaging the aircraft.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with a non-communicating aircraft approaching on a crossing runway.

Pilot reported encountering multiple delays from ATC while on an IFR flight and was given an approach clearance into the wrong airport.

I90 Controller reported failure of facility to brief Controllers on tactical activities resulted in a UAV altitude deviation and conflict.

Flight Attendant reported concerns about safety and security issues relating to new COVID-19 jumpseat arrangements and social distancing guidelines.
Air carrier flight crew reported loss of directional control while exiting the runway due to 'incredibly slick' conditions.
N90 Controller was vectoring an aircraft at 5000 feet. The aircraft descended in response to an RA for VFR traffic into an aircraft the Controller was vectoring at 4000 feet.

B757 Captain reports having to penetrate heavy rain and turbulence while conducting a localizer only approach. Due to gusty wind conditions the final flap selection was delayed until the indicated airspeed permitted; resulting in the final configuration not being achieved prior to 1000 feet AGL. The captain evaluated landing versus going around in increasingly hazardous weather conditions and elected to land.
Corporate jet Captain reported experiencing significant engine thrust turbulence from a B747 that had exited their takeoff runway and was holding short of a parallel runway.
B737 Captain reported the JeppFD-Pro app on his iPad malfunctioned while on approach. The problem was fixed on the ground once the iPad was connected to Wi-Fi.
SF340B flight attendant reported Captain allowing what may have appeared to be an overload to weight and balance.
Center Controller reports of an aircraft returning to departure airport; which could not be handed off to Approach automatically; and had to be handed off manually.
UAV operator became aware of FAR Part 107 violation after flying the UAV in MHT Class C airspace.
Air Carrier Captain reported of possible confusion when interpreting JEPP RNAV SID charts for LAS due to the placement of inset information.

B777 Flight Crew reported that an unexpected autoland due to a change in weather; resulted in a shorter approach and insufficient time for Flight Attendants to secure carts; one of which struck a passenger.

ZME Controller reports of a Mode-C intruder target moving NE towards their airspace at a fast rate. Controllers checked with pilots in the vicinity to see if they saw it on TCAS and they did not. Controller referenced it to something like ring-around but said it was not because the targets did not correlate to ring-around. Controller reported the incident to Front Line Manager (FLM).

Cirrus SR20 pilot reported receiving a traffic advisory from ATC; locating traffic and descending approximately 400 feet to the avoid conflicting traffic.
CRJ-700 Captain reported activating GPWS Obstacle alert during during high descent rate during approach.
BJC Tower Controller reported an aircraft making evasive actions due to a VFR aircraft trying to shoot the gap between the Class D and Class B airspace.

A Large Transport flight crew reported being assigned a runway different from what they expected while taxiing out for takeoff. They neglected to brief the revised taxi route and missed the airport sign 'all aircraft hold here' short of Taxiway F. They did not hold short and ATC called to admonish them.
B787-8 First Officer reported an unstable approach due to late descent clearance from ATC and tailwinds on final approach.
PA-32 pilot reported losing altitude in turbulence and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC.
Technician reported the aircraft was parked and the parking orders never updated requiring a further maintenance delay.
It was reported that Boston Tower used a frequency that is not published in any publication.
B737 Flight Attendant reported catering was loading items into locations with weight limitations that were being exceeded.
Single pilot reported a NMAC with opposite direction landing traffic.
B747-400 flight crew reported a strong updraft in oceanic airspace that caused a gain of 300 feet in altitude.
Center Controller reported frequency congestion and a high workload caused them to not detect an erroneous altitude read back and an aircraft flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

N90 Controller reports of losing RADAR and missing an aircraft when it is finally changed to another RADAR feed. The Controller describes how he found the aircraft and what he did to identify it again.
A Center Controller reported a NMAC; after opening a Radar Assist position at combined sectors the two aircraft began flashing conflict alert.
Lead Aircraft Technician reported an aircraft in storage had both engines run with no oil in either the main oil tank or accessory gearbox.
BE1900 Captain reported being unsure if they should complete a procedure turn for an approach.
Air Carrier flight crew reported over shooting an altitude during departure.
B757 flight crew reported ATC provided inadequate traffic spacing on final approach.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger could not breathe; so oxygen was administered until passenger felt well.
B737 Captain reported a communication breakdown with Flight Attendant regarding setting up for a bathroom break procedure during flight.

Pilot reported that a plane landed over them while they were instructed to position and hold on the runway.
A321 flight crew reported a communication breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch regarding transport of Hazmat material on ferry flight.

Air carrier flight crew reported that an airport was using fireworks to deter birds; causing a distraction on final approach.
Airport Ground Agent reported the employee bus is very crowded and is concerned social distancing is not being enforced.
Captain reported that it is unclear if company policy allows brake release to be done prior to receiving clearance to push.

CRJ900 Captain experiences severe turbulence during approach to ORF at 3000 feet; followed quickly by a bird strike. The Captain allows another RJ to go ahead then attempts a second approach which is successful.
A CHA Tower Controller reported they had to vector an aircraft out of the pattern and it flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
An SLC Controller reports of an operation that resulted in a go-around due to traffic on the runway and an arrival being higher than normal.
Captain reported that the conditioned air hose attached to the jetway air conditioning unit was ingested by the number one engine on taxi into the gate.
Technician reported a B767 air turnback was caused by an additional NLG safety pin left installed.
B777 First Officer reported that a company-issued app used by pilots on their iPads is confusing to use.
Captain of A319 reported that he was ordered to continue flying with a sick FO and was becoming ill and fatigued in the process.
Air carrier First Officer reported a P56 airspace violation after the Control Tower instructed the flight crew to square turn to final while on the RIVER VISUAL RWY 19 to DCA airport.

The flight crew of a small transport aircraft reported a runway excursion during landing after an un-stabilized approach with windshear conditions. When the crew exited the aircraft; they noted that the runway was covered in glazed ice.
Flight attendants reported a passenger did not comply with face mask policy during flight.
Airbus Captain reported executing a go-around at MIA after encountering jet blast from a departing Heavy aircraft.
B777 Flight Attendant reported aircraft containing incorrect Flight Attendant interphone placards.
A321 Captain reported a Flight Attendant was injured after encountering severe clear air turbulence.
Air carrier flight crew reported they lost the localizer signal while conducting a Visual Approach to MCI airport.

MIA Approach Controller reported of a VFR aircraft skirting below the MIA Class Bravo. While legal; this causes separation conflicts with traffic inbound to MIA. Reporter suggested changes to the Class Bravo.
Dispatcher reported excessive workload and lack of relief is causing safety of flight issues and work rule violations due to COVID related staffing cuts.
Dispatcher reported not properly planning for a Center to be ATC Zero for COVID cleaning.
SR22 pilot reported avoiding a deer on landing resulting in a runway excursion; nose gear collapse and prop strike.
The pilot of a Cessna 172 was unable to maintain altitude due to icing conditions.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported being dispatched with the oxygen panel inoperative at an occupied seat row. After arrival; Maintenance said this was an error.

Anchorage Center Developmental reported an unsafe situation where they thought the aircraft was ok in relationship to its heading and altitude. Supervisor advised both Controllers there was a problem and Controllers reacted by climbing the aircraft and getting it back on course.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to CLT in trail of an A321 that rolled the aircraft 40 degrees.
C210 pilot reported that while under flight following he was not handed off by CVG TRACON to Columbus TRACON in a timely manner to avoid DAY charlie airspace.
An ERJ-175 First Officer was concerned after the fact that he and Captain; both as low time pilots; had been paired together.
A Captain reported that the 'Pilot in Command' and 'Second in Command' persons are not recording their time per the FARs.
A Pilot giving instruction at a non towered airport reported a NMAC with an aircraft in the pattern not responding on the Unicom frequency.
An ERJ-170 Captain reported that the First Officer noticed that the ground crew had loaded baggage over the forward baggage bin fire line on the previous flight.
B737-800 Captain reported refusing ATC's instruction maintain visual separation from parallel runway traffic.
A Northern California TRACON (NCT) Controller reports of being told that he had a wake turbulence loss of separation according to the Traffic Analysis and Review Program (TARP).
A Center Controller reported insufficient action was taken by the Traffic Management Units to prevent sectors from being overloaded when forecast weather manifested.

Front Line Manager (FLM) reports of having to supervise other areas that he has little or no knowledge of. Facility is mandating this to the FLM and the FLM reports that he feels this is an unsafe practice.
GEG TRACON Controller reported  having to turn an aircraft on to final and realizing later that the aircraft was below the MVA.
Pilot reported that an engine cowl popped open during takeoff; resulting in a return to the airport.

Pilots reported of flying an approach; but did not see a mandatory crossing altitude on the approach. The pilots briefed the approach but did not see the mandatory altitude. ATC corrected the problem.
ZOA Controller reported a foreign registered G280 should be a special interest flight therefore not given route changes.
During arrival procedure; B-737 crew overshot published minimum altitude. Crew had received numerous altitude and airspeed changes from ATC and neglected to engage VNAV.
Third party observer reported a BE-33 pilot experienced a Critical Ground Event during landing rollout with a 'NORDO' PA-18 landing with the BE-33 still on runway.

An Captain reported that he has the takeoff minimums for Runway 28 at NGU have been changed to N/A on the Jeppesen 31-1 plate and believes that this is incorrect. ZID Controller reported of a weather balloon that was not equipped to show where its location was. Controller reported aircraft saw the balloon and reported it to him. Controller thought this was an unsafe practice.

While doing so the flight entered airspace given via LOA (Letter Of Agreement) to Providence Approach when BOS is landing on 4R. The reporter cited the 'cumbersome and unwieldy' nature of 'descend via' clearances when addressing returns to the STAR after interruptions.
Flight Instructor reported their student flew in front of another aircraft causing a go around.
A Tower Controller conducted an Opposite Direction Operation. The departing aircraft did not turn to the heading designated in the SID even after being prompted to by the Controller.
C170 flight instructor reported the inability to recover from the student's loss of control on landing; resulting in the aircraft inappropriately exiting the runway.

BED Tower Front Line Manager reported of a Local Controller who; while distracted by a Developmental; led to a runway separation event. FLM reported Controller's speed of actions was not up to the tempo of traffic. FLM reported Controller should have done something different to avoid the loss of separation.
Center Controller reported an airspace violation due aircraft hand-off issues with a TRACON facility.
Citabria pilot reported using a restricted airport without prior permission.
EMB-145 Captain reported being notified by the Flight Attendant that the aircraft was short 1 child life vest prior to departure.

Tower Controller reported loss of radios and equipment requirements that were not being followed.

Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non-compliant about wearing a face mask.
Technician reported that Company training does not meet FAR criteria for qualifications required.
Midnight shift Dispatcher reported the workload was close to violating FAR 121.395; and that even with furlough recalls and new hires; dispatchers are overloaded.
BE35 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence on approach in trail of a Ford Trimotor that resulted in a hard landing and damage to the landing gear.
B767 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in cruise flight at FL340 on a trans-Atlantic route; afterwards; ATC restricted their offset to 2 miles right deviation.
Maintenance Controller requested that deferral applicability for duct louvers be clarified.
Air carrier First Officer reported concerns with the LGA ILS 4 approach identified as 'Autopilot Coupled approach not authorized.'
A Maintenance Technician reported the ramp and hangar area lightning warning system does not work in a timely manner.
A319 Captain reported SCT TRACON controllers often make multiple runway assignment changes during the approach; greatly increasing pilot workload.
SoCal TRACON Feeder and Final Controllers reported a handoff that was not made correctly which resulted in an aircraft having to be resequenced due to not seeing traffic.
A Tower Controller reported a departure was launched on a heading toward arrival traffic due to miscommunication between the Tower and TRACON.
Corporate flight crew reported responding to two RA's for traffic they had not been advised about when switching from Approach to Tower frequency.
Flight Attendant reported inadequate time for their preflight responsibilities.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported sliding off the pavement while taxiing on an icy surface.

Captain of a Part 121 turbojet reported difficulties interfacing via CPDLC with ATC.

A Boeing 737 Maintenance Technician reported that an Engineering Authority document was written and it was in direct contradiction of FAR's.
E-175 Captain reported undocumented Hazmat cargo was transported without flight crew notification.
GA pilot reported the PAPI was not working properly at PUB airport.
B767 First Officer reported noticing a deflection in the standby compass after placing the Electronic Flight Bag on the glareshield.
A Maintenance Supervisor reported that an oxygen servicing booster blew a brass cap due to extreme heat.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended below the charted assigned altitude of an RNAV arrival. The controller stated this is a recurring issue.

An EMB175 Captain reported while departing on Runway 10L at PHL of an aircraft that crossed the runway; but had not completely cleared and with power on.  Its exhaust blast yawed his aircraft violently immediately prior to liftoff.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported an autopilot anomaly forced a go-around and the weather deteriorated during the second approach; resulting in a minimum fuel diversion to the nearest alternate.

A80 TRACON Controller reported the ZTL ARTCC Controller did not issue a 'descend via' clearance to comply with new Optimum Profile Descent Arrivals.
Pilot flying jet trainer reports canopy departed after takeoff.

ZLA Controller reported forgetting that a local restricted area was about to become active and descended an aircraft into the airspace.
Cessna 172 Single Pilot reported an NMAC event during cruise over shoreline.

B737-700 First Officer reports that after hot brakes were observed on the walk around; Maintenance Control interpreted the Pilot's Aircraft Operating Manual temperature limitation differently than the pilot crew.

A319 Flight Crew reported that during pre-flight found over wing emergency slide panel had two missing fasteners as well as some fasteners that were glued in place.
An EMB-145 Captain reported losing reliable airspeed indications while operating in IMC.
Embraer Jet flight crew reported a NMAC with two hang gliders.
Center Controller reported an ATC Zero event at another Center facility resulted in communication issues and an airspace violation.

A ZME Front Line Manager (FLM) reported of a loss of separation due to an aircraft getting a mountain wave and descending. The other aircraft first reported to ATC that they were descending for traffic.

A BNA Controller; while riding jumpseat hears and confirms with pilots an altitude assignment. Minutes later an aircraft passes with less than standard separation. A working Controller asks the pilot to say altitude and pilot reports altitude. The Controller asks who gave you that altitude and pilot reports that the last Controller did. The flight plan of aircraft was only up to FL260.
Pilot reported insufficient navigational signal coverage at the changeover point on V23; between EUG and BTG VORTACs; while at the MEA of 5;000 feet.
Technician reported that unqualified technicians are being assigned to do ETOPS work in violation of GMM.
PRC Ground Controller and pilots reported a runway incursion. Aircraft was initially told to hold short; but they read back cross which was not corrected by ATC.
ZLA Center Controller reported a departing aircraft was issued the wrong SID according to the new OAPM arrival/departure procedures implemented for their airspace.
A320 Captain reported complying with aircraft indication instead of ATC instruction to avoid traffic.
Bell 407 pilot reported he had a TFR incursion and stated the TFR was not listed on the FAA briefing website. Both TFRs were for sporting events.
A320 flight crew experiences GPS jamming in ZLC airspace resulting in a momentary track deviation. The jamming was NOTAMed but the aircraft's reaction to the jamming was unexpected.
SCT TRACON Controller reported it is difficult for aircraft to meet the PHIYA crossing restriction from RHODE on the DSNEE 1 Arrival which requires a 4000 foot descent in 11 miles.
Ramp personnel reported the discovery of a Dry Ice Pack that had been erroneously loaded on an aircraft after the flight had departed.
B737 Captain reported Hazmat cargo was not offloaded at intermediate destination and erroneously transported to subsequent destination.
Embraer 505 Captain reported an in-flight failure of the integrated flight instrument system.
Air Carrier Captain reported diverting to the alternate airport due to a UAV blocking traffic at the primary airport and holding fuel becoming an issue.
Air carrier Captain reported the ramp lights on the north end of the SEA North Satellite are blinding while taxiing on Taxiways B and W between Spots 66; over Spot 55 and beyond.
Air Carrier flight attendants reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy and was removed from the flight.

Flight Attendant did not want to sit in the appropriate jumpseat for takeoff/landing for social distancing concerns. A Supervisor was contacted and the Flight Attendant was given the option to move to a different jumpseat or to be pulled from the flight. The Flight Attendant moved to another jumpseat.
Pilot reported landing without clearance due to a sticky radio switch and lack of incoming radio transmission; and thinking the Tower was closed due to COVID-19.
ATC Center Controller reported traffic management initiatives during a known weather event were late and insufficient to help manage the traffic flows to the airport.
ZOA Center Controllers reported a loss of separation between a UAV and a Small Transport.
C206 pilot reported entering IMC while on a VFR flight plan.
ZOA Supervisor reported a trainee issued an IFR clearance to an aircraft that was below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
A321 flight crew reported the company adjusted schedules to avoid exceeding the hour limitation in the FARs.

When replacing an MO1 Module on an Arriel 2B1 Helicopter Engine; three bolts are removed from the MO5 Module casing to accommodate the tooling. These bolts were left off when the engine was installed.
C172 pilot reported momentary loss of control on the landing roll related to prop wash from an aircraft taking off on a nearby intersecting runway.
Flight attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.
BE-200 Captain reported extreme turbulence in the descent near BREWS.
C210 pilot reported ceasing photo operations in conjunction with a UAV when the Airport Manager complained.
Two company aircraft with similar call signs were taxiing out and departing at the same time. As one aircraft was lifting off it was cleared for takeoff by the Tower Controller.
Pilot reported taking off in the wrong runway direction.
Mooney M20 pilot reported being advised by BOS ATC to contact them before operating in close proximity to the overlying Class C airspace.

A Phoenix TRACON (P50) Controller reports the information that they have for reference aircraft flying in and out of the airspace for a car race is useless because it tells them nothing. No altitudes; no route of flight; times; etc.; were given.

Air carrier First Officer reported an altitude deviation after descending to avoid turbulence. The crew thought they were still cleared to a block altitude; but realized they were not after ATC asked them to confirm their altitude.
Ground employee reported social distancing concerns during peak times at the airport.
Flight Instructor reported critical ground conflict during taxi.
B737 Captain reported loss of aircraft control due to NIL braking action from icy conditions in the gate area.
Mechanic reported company's poor guidance; time pressure; and lack of communication resulted in a stand hitting the aircraft.
B737 Captain reported two circuit breakers were damaged after maintenance work in the cockpit.

Air carrier First Officer reported being distracted by a reported UAV in the area of Runway 31L at DAL; missed setting flaps 30 and received a 'Too Low Flaps' alert. Crew set flaps and landed uneventfully.

A flight instructor reported being informed that a city noise abatement altitude had not been met. The pilot was not informed of the noise abatement restrictions nor were any procedures found following the notification.
Air carrier First Officer suggested a moratorium on the use of oxygen masks in the flight training devices in order to minimize the possible transmission of COVID-19.
ZAB Controller reported of an aircraft under his control that was lower than the MIA. Controller climbed aircraft; but had already entered the higher airspace; resulting in an airspace deviation.

An air carrier Captain reported receiving a terrain warning received during a visual approach at night. The warning was triggered by the uniqueness of surrounding terrain; man-made structures; and the descent rate during approach. The crew executed mild evasive procedures and landed uneventfully.
E175 flight crew member reported being cleared to a point that was not on the flight planned route. Reporter mentioned new fixes and routings in Florida possibly contributed to the issue.
CRJ-200 Captain reported disagreement with maintenance over a flap control discrepancy.
Air Carrier Cargo Captain reported he is concerned about the possible health effects of insecticide spray being used before departure to Australia.
SCT TRACON Controller and a GA pilot reported evasive action by GA pilot caused an RA for opposite direction traffic.

P31 Controller reports of taking a handoff on an aircraft then the data tag mysteriously disappears a minute after taking the handoff. Aircraft flies through airspace for 30 miles without a data tag. Controller wonders who or what dropped the data tag.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger; whom had acted erratically and refused to wear a face mask on a previous flight; was allowed to board the flight.
EUG TRACON Supervisor reported they observed an aircraft IFR below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A pilot reported questioning if SFO has a waiver (due to displaced landing threshold) to conduct parallel dependent ILS approaches.

A SFO Front Line Manager (FLM) reports of an airborne conflict he observes that local is having a problem with. Coordination with Northern California TRACON (NCT) is attempted but the resolution is denied by NCT. In order to save 1;000 feet between the aircraft the FLM instructs local to have the aircraft maintain a specific altitude below the higher aircraft. The altitude to maintain was below the MVA which had recently changed by 100 feet higher; causing an airspace deviation.

An Albuquerque Center (ZAB) Controller reports of working an aircraft IFR that makes a request he is unable to approve due to a restricted area being hot with aircraft operating in that area. He issues traffic to both the aircraft and the second aircraft group; and one of the aircraft attempts to buzz the single IFR aircraft. The Controller reports this to Front Line Manager (FLM) and Operations Manager (OM).
An Air Carrier Captain and a Departure Controller reported on a traffic conflict between two aircraft departing LAX in IMC.
Controller reported that the Local Controller did not follow the SOP and cleared aircraft for takeoff from a part of the runway that Local did not own.
B737 First Officer reported being reprimanded by ATC related to a speed assignment during a critical phase on approach to ATL.

A B737-800 Captain reported while on RAJEE SID; ATC issued numerous amendments to published procedure using non-standard; or nonspecific phraseology. This; along with new Visual Jeppesen SID altitude depictions contributed to altitude deviation.
A320 First Officer reported his iPad didn't charge when plugged in on the ground. This resulted in a flight being conducted with only 1 operational iPad.

Pilot reported that after exiting the runway their wing tip remained over the runway while another aircraft landed off centerline due to the runway incursion. Pilot reported not hearing any communication from the landing aircraft.
A320 Captain reported concerns over a Minimum Equipment List procedure.

A B737NG crew crossed the SFO DYAMD 2 RNAV ARHCI waypoint at 7;000 ft because they had not seen the NOTAM constraining the crossing altitude above 8;000 ft to prevent descent below SFO Class B airspace.

A CRJ200 First Officer experiences a TCAS RA at 5;000 feet near PVD; initially showing red above and stating 'Monitor Vertical Speed'. Then the RA switched to 'descend descend' and as the First Officer began the descent; ATC issued a climb to 5;500 feet. The Captain takes control and climbs to 5;500 feet.
Helicopter pilot reported sighting a drone over the Griffith Park Observatory while operating at 600 AGL. The pilot altered his climb to avoid the drone.
Captain reported an NMAC with a UAV on approach to ICT.
During receiving inspection; the Inspecting technician discovered that an escape slide was stored incorrectly and had mold growing on it.
B737 Captain reported taking evasive action to avoid another aircraft departing in their direction.

An air carrier aircraft on final approach to ORD 10/28 was cleared to land but ATC cleared an aircraft for takeoff. A blocked transmission; which may have been a go-around call; was missed so ATC ordered the aircraft rolling to abort.
Pilot reported a gear up landing.
Pilot reported being on a visual approach; but having to go through clouds.

Air carrier Mechanic reported that wearing a mask and safety glasses causes the glasses to fog up; resulting in safety concerns when working on ladders; lifts; and in tight places. Mechanic also expressed concern that while working with chemicals; they could inadvertently be transferred to the mask.

Air carrier flight crew reported ATC requested they descend back to their cleared altitude of 14;000 during departure.  The flight crew stated they never heard ATC cleared them to 14;000 feet and cited frequency congestion as a contributing factor.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft while under ATC flight following.
An Air Carrier pilot reported departing with less than minimum takeoff fuel due to ATC delays while taxiing.

A320 flight crew experiences turbulence and strong gusty winds during approach to Runway 16R; resulting in a decision to go-around. During the go-around; flap overspeed occurs; and again while configuring for the subsequent approach.
Pilot reported conflicting traffic pattern information provided at FA83; resulting in inflight conflicts.
B737 Flight Crew reported ACARS failure and accepted bad clearance by Dispatch which was resolved by ATC during climb.

Air Carrier First Officer reported the pilot crew failed to set the correct altimeter setting causing an altitude deviation. Pre-flight distractions; including the current pandemic situation; were cited for the error.
A B737 crew on the ATL WARRR1 RNAV STAR were descending slower than expected due to weather which caused the TRACON Controller grief as it was conflicting with their outbound aircraft.

A B737 was dispatched to LAS and arrived to find gusty winds and dust. The crew executed a go-around on the first approach but with fuel considerations landed on the second approach by flying beneath the gusts near the surface.

An ARTCC facility cleared an aircraft for an IFR approach to the airport which authorizes the published Missed Approach. The published missed approach for this airport enters military Restricted Areas. The Airport Tower; when open; is responsible for coordination use of the Restricted Airspace for an aircraft on approach. The ARTCC Controllers called the Tower to coordinate the inbound approach. Someone took the coordination calls. Both ARTCC Controllers assumed the Tower was open since they received and acknowledged the coordination. On Missed Approach the aircraft called the ARTCC Controller which alerted the ARTCC Controller that the Tower was not really open. The ARTCC Controller was able to vector the aircraft away from the Restricted Airspace prior to it entering.
B737 Captain reported initiating a go-around after observing an unexpected Caravan lined up ahead for the same runway.
Mechanic reported that aircraft may not have been evaluated as required due to confusing maintenance orders.
B-777 Captain reports an 'echoing' problem on an ATC frequency. No corrective action is reported.
B737 First Officer describes a difficult arrival to DEN due to weather; holding; and fuel considerations.
Commercial Airline Ramp Agent reported Hazmat procedural deviation requiring aircraft ground return to correct error.
Falcon 20 First Officer reported a dual engine rollback while encountering icing conditions; which required a descent and diversion to the nearest suitable airport.
There are two procedures for reduced spacing of aircraft on parallel final approaches at SFO. The reporter stated the TRACON changed from one procedure to the other without the proper ATIS broadcast.
Flight crew reported not flying the assigned heading on departure and being corrected by ATC. The crew referenced lack of flying as a contributing factor.
B737 Captain reported a misunderstanding between the flight crew and ATC regarding a passenger illness resulted in an aircraft security inquiry.
PWK Local Controller reported of sending an aircraft around on final when a student pilot entered the runway when they were supposed to hold short.

The flight crew of a De Havilland Canada DHC-8-200 reported that maintenance authorized the deferral of the autopilot when both of the flight guidance computers were inoperative; which would make this an illegal deferral.
B737 flight crew and ATC clearance confusion associated with the West transition versus the WESLA fix on the East transition of the SFO SERFR1 STAR.
The pilot of a PA28 reported a loss of control and runway excursion due to jet blast from an aircraft during a maintenance engine run on a taxiway.
B757 First Officer reported ATC irregularities on approach to SKBO; including clearance below MVA and an airborne conflict.
Captain reported observing non compliance with daily inspection requirements.
R90 TRACON Controller reported a recurring ADS-B problem that indicates erroneous groundspeed readouts.
GFK Tower Controllers reported an aircraft was cleared for takeoff while the preceding arrival was still on the runway.

ZID Controller reports of an aircraft popping up in his airspace. He tracks down who is suppose to be working the aircraft and then later finds another flight plan on the same aircraft. Controller reports of lack of concern from ZDC controllers who were working the aircraft.
E170 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing EWR in trail of a H/B777.
Flight Attendant reported only serving sealed drinks and no coffee during flight.  Flight Attendant did not feel safe serving coffee.
B737 First Officer reported a VHF/NAV 1 anomaly resulting in a missed approach.
C72 Pilot reported loss of control on landing resulting in prop strike.
Two air carrier maintenance technicians reported that some required inspections on a B777 are documented as completed; but have not actually been addressed.
Air carrier flight crew reported landing without a clearance after dialing in the incorrect frequency.
Air cargo crew executed two go-arounds at IAH due to windshear system activation with an approaching thunderstorm and then diverted.
B737-700 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B747 at FL320 that resulted in a possible flight attendant injury.
TRACON Developmental reported a loss of separation and cited lack of training in high traffic volume as a contributing factor.

CRJ-200 Captain reported while on short final to Runway 27R at PHL; ATC cleared a turboprop aircraft on an intersection departure from Runway 35 which caused a momentary shift during the landing flare caused by the prop wash.

A Dispatcher is informed by one of his flights that they have been assigned FL380/M85 on North Atlantic (NAT) A instead of FL350. The computer software will not accept this altitude and a flight plan with accurate times and fuel burns cannot be generated.

ZJX Controller describes a loss of separation between two aircraft. Controller attempts to use speed control; but first aircraft slows while second aircraft has its speed as instructed. Loss of separation occurs and Controller blames fatigue but doesn't expand on that.

DAB Controller reports of an automation problem with the wrong scratch pad information showing up in aircraft data block.  This lead to confusion for the Controller; pilot and resulted in an airspace deviation.
ZNY Controller reported of an airspace violation on an aircraft that was in another Center's airspace. Aircraft routing was changed prior and not updated in the computer.

Following an SMF night departure on the FTHIL2 RNAV; the air carrier flight crew had a 3;000 feet climb clearance limit. ATC apparently forgot the flight which had a near miss and approaching terrain before a climb was issued. During the climb; a TCAS RA 'DESCEND' was received at FL286.
A C182 pilot cancelled IFR with ATC prior to landing; but he did not close out his flight plan after landing. ATC requested that local police contact the pilot to verify his well-being.
PHL Tower Controller and a First Officer involved reported the First Officer took another aircraft's takeoff clearance.
Technician reported difficulties while delivering aircraft training to technicians via distance learning.
Flight attendants reported a partial slide deployment while opening the cabin door due to the door inadvertently not being disarmed.
Air carrier flight crew reported a Flight Attendant was not seated for departure due to having to deal with passengers not properly wearing face masks.
Flight Attendant reported multiple passengers did not comply with face masks policy.
737 flight crew reported entering restricted airspace due to severe weather deviation.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported they caught a gust of wind in the landing flare. The left wing dipped and struck the runway.
GA pilot reported error in VFR Charts.
Light twin instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the vicinity of LEE airport.
Air carrier Captain forgot to do a SELCAL check and cited as contributing factors multiple tasks in a short period and having not flown in a long time.
Technician reported conflicting information in the IPC with regards to effectiveness of Nose Wheel assemblies authorized for installation on aircraft.
Approach Controller and turboprop pilot reported a near miss with a helicopter during visual approach.
Dispatcher and flight crew reported a night landing without runway lighting.
Air carrier Captain reported Ground Personnel at FLL airport have microphones turned on at all times during pushback causing communication issues.
Pilot informed to Go-around by Air Traffic Control due to a glitch in a new system.
EUG Controllers reported unknowingly accepting a handoff on an aircraft which was 1;000 feet below the Minimum Enroute Altitude until it was pointed out by the Supervisor.
ERJ flight crew reported that Maintenance failed to repair and log one of three reported aircraft issues; insisting that the crew was legal to fly.
Oakland Center Controller reported unsafe airspace problems with a Navy Facility when military flights enter Oakland Airspace without approval.

A B737 crew reported while descending through 10;000 feet to ANC Runway 7R; ATC changed their Runway to 33. Confused; the crew began setting up for Runway 33 when ATC cleared them for a Runway 7R visual approach; [and to] track the localizer which they discovered out of service. By then seriously distracted; the crew descended below their assigned altitude.
ZHU Controller reported taking over a sector and realized an aircraft in the sector was below the minimum safe altitude.
A319 flight crew reported Terrain Warning on approach.
Reporter reported conflicting guidance on the BUF ILS 05 instrument approach chart referencing autopilot use.
Air Carrier Captain reported 'cleared for visual approach' then descending too low and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC.
CE525 flight crew report Minimum Fuel to Air Traffic Control.

While taxiing for takeoff; Tower notified a B737 crew about a tornado 10 miles away headed toward the airport. The aircraft was returned to the gate but because of terminal evacuations was delayed parking and evacuating occupants to safety.
B737 First Officer reported company HAZMAT procedures were missing significant tolerance information.

B-737 Captain reported being notified at destination gate by Ramp Agent of Hazmat cargo shipped with label 'cargo aircraft only...'. Captain further reported not notified of any Hazmat information planned on flight.
B737-800 First Officer reported overshooting a charted altitude on arrival into MMMX. Reporter also stated they received a GPS-L invalid message.
Air carrier First Officer conducting a Visual Approach into ONT reported receiving a Terrain Alert.

Two ZAB Controllers report of an aircraft inbound to the airport that could not see it. During this time another aircraft at the airport was departed. Both aircraft were known to the Tower and Center. Tower departed the aircraft and a loss of separation ensued.
P80 TRACON controllers reported a near mid-air collision due to lack of communication and a plan between the controllers.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a runway incursion at ORF due to a lack of runway markings and signage.
B737 Captain reported several aircraft with similar call signs were taxiing for departure at the same time causing confusion on the frequency.
HIO Ground Controller reported of an aircraft that taxied onto an active runway causing a runway incursion. No other aircraft were nearby.
Helicopter ambulance Captain reported an empty baby incubator caught fire while in flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an altitude deviation during approach. Lack of flying and proficiency were contributing factors.
Air carrier Load Planner reported advising Ramp Lead that aircraft must return to gate to correctly comply with required Hazmat loading configuration for air transport.
Embraer jet flight crew reported they were uncomfortable with ATC spacing on approach to EWR.
SCT Controller reported a low altitude on an aircraft into LGB runway 26R and wondered if it was an actual low altitude alert or one that might have been in error.
Technician reported large bore valve stems installed on nose wheel assemblies and not documented correctly.
Air carrier flight crew reported having an aircraft pressurization problem thinking it was an equipment problem but the Packs were not turned on.
There was an inconsistency in the various manuals related to carriage of poisonous Dangerous Goods loaded close to foodstuffs.
A319 flight crew reported that a large bird strike just prior to V1 resulted in a rejected takeoff and a minor wheel fire.
Student pilot at a non towered airport reported an aircraft departed opposite direction to them from a taxiway adjacent to their landing runway.

PDX Tower Controller reported instructing a VFR pilot to maintain visual separation from an arriving aircraft on approach. The pilot acknowledged the instruction; but the resulting conflict was logged as an NMAC.
Flight Crew after diversion due to weather encountered extended ground delay at diversion airport.
Heavy aircraft First Officer reported that ATC computer assigns shorter runways for bigger aircraft.
After several reroutes and holding; the crew declared minimum fuel but were able to land at their original destination of ORD instead of diverting.
A Flight Attendant reported that she became extremely ill after getting up from her crew break. The reporter noticed this because she was coughing and congested after her break in the crew bunks.
GA pilot reported reduced Tower services at a Class D airport due to COVID-19 has resulted in an unsafe environment and described several conflicts occurring while the Tower was closed.
Air carrier Captain reported he did not realize they were supposed to wait for frequency change instructions. They switched from Tower to Departure Control on their own.
Air carrier Captain reported alcohol from cleaning products may be entering the boom mike causing the equipment to malfunction.
Light Sport Aircraft pilot reported a helicopter took off over him with about a 50 foot clearance while he was taxiing at FFZ airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported loss of communication with ATC prior to landing.
B777 Captain reported a non-standard fire protection panel installed in crew rest area; resulting in a delay and 5 seats to be reserved for crew rest.
Air carrier Captain reported not descending via the STAR due to selecting LVL Change Pitch instead of VNAV on the MCP.

A PA-28 instructor reported his student's control and landing difficulties ended with a high flare and an early power reduction. Subsequently; the aircraft landed hard which resulted in a tail strike.
B737 Captain reported multiple procedure violations by ground crew that resulted in a safety risk.
A MKE TRACON Controller reported they vectored two approaches to parallel runways with less than standard separation.
Air carrier pilot and Dispatcher reported a required alternate airport was not filed prior to departure.
Air taxi flight crew reported a bird strike while taking off from MAZ. They indicated that the bird hazard at this airport is significant and needs to be mitigated.

B737 Captain discovered that the nosewheel steering was inoperative after pushback and called Maintenance. Maintenance found that the nosewheel steering lockout pin had sheared off at the head. The push back crew removed only the head and the streamer; but not the shaft.

An ATC Supervisor was working a sector around Restricted Airspace. The Controller/Supervisor got busy and did not notice a VFR aircraft they were working enter the Restricted Airspace. An arriving air carrier was vectored for its descent to land and got within three miles of the Restricted Airspace boundary. Required separation from Restricted Airspace boundary is 3 miles or greater.
Dispatcher reported the problems with their computerized flight planning system.
Air carrier pilot reported an airspace error with regards to the Runway 9 ILS approach to MIA airport.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with a low wing aircraft above them.
A US carrier aircraft operating internationally with ADS-B installed did not have the ADS-B Operations Specification on file indicating its aircraft were authorized flight above FL290.
Flight Attendant reported there should be a change to the in-flight beverage/snack service to limit exposure time to Flight Attendants while passengers have their masks off.
CRJ-700 First Officer expressed concern that luggage with a built-in Lithium-Ion battery should not be loaded with checked baggage.

Boeing 777 Captain reported a discrepancy in the FMC fuel remaining estimate. Initially it showed they would be landing with extra fuel; then 5 hours into the flight it showed they would be landing with less fuel than planned. It may have been due to an un-forecasted wind shift.
S46 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft departed an airport IFR and was immediately in conflict with two VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC. The departing IFR aircraft responded to an RA.
Embraer 175 flight crew reported a missed approach from an unstabilized position due to weather conditions.
Air carrier First Officer stated that NOTAM was poorly worded and required several hours of plotting to comply with; causing NOTAM to be violated.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported that a TCAS RA commanded a descent shortly after departure that caused a GPWS 'Don't sink' alert.

While conducting an approach the flight crew encountered unknown VFR traffic in close proximity which was not displaying on TCAS; possibly due to no or inoperative transponder. ATC was queried and requested help in identifying the aircraft type. No RA was generated by this event.
Air Traffic Controller and Air Taxi Captain reported another aircraft had to respond to an RA and told the controller the climbing aircraft was too close.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with another Flight Attendant showing up for work after a family member had tested positive for COVID-19 the day before.
A B737 First Officer reported the distraction caused by a newly issued iPad window tray mount protruding low into his elbow area while hand flying.
A319 Captain reported his aircraft was deiced early in the morning prior to his arrival without following his airline's procedures for doing so.

A general aviation pilot reported his Garmin 530W database provided by Jeppesen did not include several Canadian waypoints which were part of his IFR flight plan between two domestic U.S. airports. Reporter stated some Canadian waypoints are available; but others are not.
B737-700 F/O reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL350 10 miles in trail of a B777 that resulted in a pitch down and left and right rolls.

A Large Transport Aircraft was high on the SILCN 1 arrival profile because the ILS 30 transition point is GILRO which occurs after the common SILCN 1 to ILS 30 transition point KLIDE. Therefore the FMC will fly from KLIDE to GILRO to KLIDE which requires the additional altitude.

A Dispatcher reported a flight's Captain was removed at his FAR 117 duty time limit. Two hours later during the takeoff roll; the First Officer was notified via ACARS that he had exceeded his duty time limit. Reporter stated they should have received notification earlier and ACARS messages during takeoff should be prevented.
Saab 340 pilot reported the Tower cleared an aircraft for takeoff resulting in a conflict with them as they were conducting an approach.
S46 and ZSE Controllers reported confusion with similar call signs that led to a loss of separation.
An aircraft descended on a visual approach and received a GPWS alert.
B737 flight crew reported ground conflict during taxi with another taxiing aircraft.

Flight Attendant reported arriving early to an airport; parking at a remote spot; and then finding out cockpit crew left the aircraft with everyone else onboard. Attendants had no way to communicate with ground personnel.
Captain reported that an MEL was applied to an aircraft in error; resulting in the loss of cockpit oxygen mask interphone capability.

Air taxi Captain reported difficulty locating the BIL CRAZI1 Arrival in the Volume 1 of FAA US Terminal Procedures Publication. It was not where he expected it to be and not in alphabetical order. The reporter descended approximately 1500 feet below assigned altitude while searching.
Pilot reported a airborne conflict with another aircraft.
A fuel indicator on a B737 was deferred using an incorrect MEL reference.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported some NOTAMs for inoperative airport systems preclude use as an alternate airport while others do not.

A Northern California TRACON (NCT) Controller reports of a problem associated with the BDEGA1 arrival when an aircraft flies the wrong heading and enters another controller's airspace. The Controller describes this is an ongoing issue and states that management has not provided guidance or proper training on how the routes should be issued.
Flight Attendant reported aircraft was struck by another aircraft under tow.

A ZDC Controller was assigned to work two sectors combined. The Controller felt there was too much traffic and too many complex situations for combining sectors. The Controller allowed another Controller to descend an aircraft in his airspace with opposite direction conflicting traffic.

Tracon Controllers and associated pilot reported the flight descended below their assigned altitude on an RNAV STAR. Controllers indicated that numerous aircraft do not comply with the published arrival procedure.
B737 Flight Attendant reported that an employee; who was riding on the plane's fourth jump seat; appeared to be overweight for the seat and had difficulty getting the seat belt fastened.
Ramp personnel reported electric wheelchairs loaded sideways creating a potentially hazardous situation with wheelchair battery. The situation highlights the need for more comprehensive training.
Air carrier Captain reported that while diverting to an alternate airport; the flight crew encountered difficulty obtaining information and assistance from the dispatcher handling the flight.
Pilot reported a critical ground conflict that resulted in a prop strike.
Boise TRACON Controller reported vectoring an aircraft into a higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude [MVA] area.
B757 Captain reported experiencing an uncommanded 45 degree bank angle at FL370 that the reporter felt was related to jet stream activity.
Air carrier Captain reported being distracted by a lighted sign on a street adjacent to DEN airport.
Instructing Controller reported experiencing a ground conflict due to Developmental using the wrong call sign between aircrafts.
B737-800 flight crew reported an unstable approach as a result of gusty winds at the end of a divert from another airport also experiencing gusty winds.
Technician reported that Company is using Non ETOPS Qualified Technicians to perform ETOPS predeparture checks in violation of the GMM.
B737 Captain reported that a fuel truck was parked in the safety zone of gate; preventing aircraft from taxiing clear of the runway after landing.
Air Carrier flight crew reported misunderstanding a revised Controller Pilot Data Link Clearance to LAS.
C172 instructor was pilot flying and reported not following published procedures; causing a track deviation.
B737-700 First Officer reported that they encountered moderate turbulence causing momentary loss of control.
Small aircraft pilot reported inadvertently entering a firefighting TFR that was not displayed on Foreflight.

A PHL Tower Controller reported of a pilot making comments about the taxi instructions. The pilot complained about the way he was to taxi to the gate. The reporter advised the LOA was a safety hazard and created traps.

A MD-80 First Officer discovered towels wrapped around cables in the wheel well which maintenance was able to remove with some difficulty. It was discovered the towels were paper; but the aircraft remained grounded for an inspection.
Minor to moderate injuries were reported by flight attendants and a passenger when their B737 encountered 'severe' turbulence during descent for landing.

Ground employee reported that the addition of a temperature check station nearby an equipment room creates congestion; making it difficult to social distance during busy times when many people are in the hallway.
Instructor and Re-certification Controller reported a MVA [Minimum Vectoring Altitude] violation when an aircraft under their control turned the wrong anticipated direction leading to the violation.
A student and instructor parked inside the CRW Runway 5 non movement area during the aircraft runup. ATC requested they taxi onto Taxiway A.
Maintenance Technician reported that Safety is no longer the primary goal.
Captain reported a rejected takeoff after number 2 engine acceleration lagged significantly.
A B737 Captain discovered excessive carbon monoxide and exhaust fumes inside the aircraft at PHL prior to passenger boarding.
B737-800 Captain reported Dispatch ACARS notification regarding Lithium Ion battery in a passenger checked bag.
Air carrier First Officer reported the crew did not notice taxiway designator revisions in the Jeppesen charts causing confusion and a taxiway incursion.
ERJ-170 flight crew reported that a severe vibration encountered on landing was caused by missing bolts in the left main gear shimmy dampener.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger refused to comply with face mask policy.

Captain reported that after arrival; a pair of vise grip pliers was discovered clamped to the outside of #2 engine; left there by Maintenance at departure airport with no logbook entry made for a #2 engine fuel leak.
Air carrier flight crew reported track heading and altitude deviations during departure and cited confusing ATC instructions as a contributing factor.
Student pilot flying R22 Helicopter reports an off airport landing due to low fuel.

Aircraft Y; a VFR parachute aircraft; was descending out of 11;500 feet. Aircraft X; an air carrier; was below Aircraft Y while climbing on a converging heading.
