Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion in snow/icing condition and cited poor braking as a contributing factor.
ERJ-190 F/O reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to DCA five miles in trail of an E175.

Local Controller reported a false ASDE-X warning of conflicting traffic on the runway while an aircraft was on short final. The controllers in the Tower scanned the area; but were unable to find a reason for the erroneous warning and the aircraft landed uneventfully.
A small aircraft pilot reported departing LGB Runway 25L and was asked by Tower to turn southwest at 200 feet. In the turn; TCAS alerted traffic nearly directly ahead.
TRACON Controller reported problems with overlying Center not complying with LOA and the contingency plan.
B737NG flight crew reported a rejected takeoff following a bird strike in the Number 1 engine. Maintenance found major damage to the engine.

A Center Controller reported they intentionally vectored a VFR aircraft encountering IFR weather below the Minimum IFR Altitude because the aircraft was very low on fuel and needed to land as soon as possible.
B767 Captain reported both his and his First Officer's iPads suffered from low battery that he felt was due to new company procedures.
C172 Instructor reported deviating to miss a possible drone resulting in TFR encroachment.
BED Tower controllers reported an aircraft was supposed to make a 360 on downwind; but did not and caused a conflict with a helicopter.

A Controller observed a Beacon Target squawking 1200 with a Mode C indicating 29;300 feet. Numerous aircraft were vectored away from this target. No aircraft reported seeing any traffic at the position of the beacon target.

Deadheading pilot reported that passengers in the back of the aircraft were told to move to seats at the front of the aircraft to accommodate a zone count; which required abandoning social distancing and sanitizing new seats.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported being concerned about passenger use of electronic devices during safety checks and would like to see the policy changed to not allow their use at those times.
B737-900 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into LAX in trail of a heavy jet.
Air Carrier flight attendants reported a uniformed passenger company employee was non-compliant with face mask policy.

P50 TRACON Controller reported being very busy with aircraft conducting instrument approaches to the main airport as well as the satellite airports. Reporter suggested an additional sector in their unit be opened instead of having to perform both functions by himself. The Supervisor was not able to open the combined sector due to lack of staffing at the time.
B737 First Officer reported the taxi lead-in line does not align with Jet Bridge.

Pilot reports of a problem with a helicopter that would be in conflict with them as it crossed midfield and they were on short final. The crossing aircraft looked closer to the pilot then midfield and he was concerned how close they would be if they had to make a missed approach.
A B737 Captain reported that a new pre-departure/taxi procedure resulting in the First Officer being distracted and 'heads down' during taxi.
Flight Crew reported overflying a fix which led to a loss of separation from terrain reported by ATC.
B737 Captain reported an anomaly with the cockpit oxygen masks that did not seal properly.
Beech 1900C pilot reported a near collision on the ramp at TIST when a truck failed to yield.
B737-700 flight crew reported returning to the departure airport after a bird strike in the Number 1 engine that caused significant vibration.

Flight crew reported about confusion when departing the airport. Their PDC had two SIDs and the crew was flying one and controllers were issuing headings. The pilots are concerned that the PDC format is unclear.
EMB-170 Captain reported uncommanded aircraft movement after misinterpreting Marshaller's instructions.
Flight Attendant reported seating issues and concerns with the close proximity of passengers when boarding the flight.
Maintenance employee reported that they are ordered by management to operate tugs in an unsafe manner.
Cessna 182 pilot reported wind shear during landing rollout which caused them to become airborne.  Pilot regained control but landed off of the runway surface with damage sustained to the propeller.
Seaplane pilot reported Long Beach Tower advising pilot that their operation was too close to swimmers in the area.
B737-700 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on descent in the IAH area.
P80 Controller reported vectoring off course and delaying descent clearance for two aircraft on approach due to an unidentified VFR aircraft conflicting in the vicinity of the Initial Approach Fix.
EMB flight crew reported two wake turbulence encounters on successive approaches to IAD.
Small aircraft pilot reported a loss of control of aircraft caused by wind after landing.
Pilot reported hitting a bird while on downwind.
Approaching the aircraft for preflight; crew noticed extensive aircraft damage from an apparent lightning strike of the ramp adjacent to the aircraft.
Small Transport Captain report a near miss by another aircraft during landing rollout.
UAV pilot reported an airspace violation.
B737 First Officer reported Maintenance installing incorrect System Annunciator Panel; causing aircraft to fly several flights in an unairworthy condition.

ZMP Controller while instructing has an aircraft climbing faster than previous aircraft; Developmental gives higher for faster climbing aircraft. Developmental transfers communication on faster climbing aircraft before separation is ensured.
CE-750 Captain reported that post flight; it was discovered that not all instrument panel bulbs were illuminating as required due to a missing part.
A mechanic lost control of an aircraft while pushing aircraft out of the hangar onto an icy ramp area. The tug slid under and engine cowl and broke off drain mast.
ZOB Controller reported of a airspace violation due to an unidentified computer problem that resulted in an aircraft not being handed off.

Pilot is filed and flying in a formation flight with another IFR aircraft. ATC Front Line Manager (FLM) asks pilot why they are filed as a flight of two and states that is reserved for military flights. Pilot doesn't argue with ATC FLM and flight breaks up. Pilot wonders why they had to break up as a flight and ATC told them they would call later and explain. ATC never called pilot to explain.
CRJ-900 Captain reported concerns regarding FAR 117 limitations.
Air carrier Captain reported new ramp tractor lights are so bright that pilots are blinded to everything on the ramp.

ZLC Controller reported of an interpretation change of the FAA Order 7110.65 at the ARTCC. The reporter stated 'routes of aircraft not covered by radar could not be amended.'  Reporter wanted a safe solution that allows the assistance of aircraft that may need to deviate for weather or approaches.
A11 TRACON Controller reported vectoring an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

PCT Controller and an air carrier Captain reported some confusion with clearances that resulted in the aircraft missing the crossing restriction. As a result conflicting traffic was vectored into the Washington DC Flight Restricted Zone.
Flight crew reported that while taxiing across a runway they had a near collision with another aircraft that was taking off from that runway.
A TRACON Controller was not notified of a runway closure. The Controller states that this is a routine occurrence.
Flight Crew flying 767-300 aircraft report using illegal departure procedure.
DCA Controller reported they failed to issue traffic information to multiple VFR flights on approach.
Ground personnel reported a discrepancy in HAZMAT cargo in the company load planning software.
Pilot suspects that his rotor wash may have contributed to another aircraft ground looping.
Super tug driver reported being physically injured by the stiff suspension and bouncy ride that it produces.
A Maintenance Technician reported that tooling used for repair on an Airbus 319 exhaust nozzle was not as depicted in the AMM and was improperly marked.
B737 First Officer reported a bird strike on takeoff that resulted in a maintenance action at the destination airport.
Air carrier Captain reported ATL TRACON vectors commercial aircraft outside of Class B airspace on approach.
E175 Flight Crew reported the air hose from the air starter unit unexpectedly disconnected from aircraft; causing damage to the aircraft.
C182 pilot reported a CFTT after takeoff involving towers located in the departure path.
Flight crew reported a taxi deviation occurred when Ground referenced a taxiway not charted on their 10-9 page.

ORD Tower Controller reported of a loss of separation that was caused by the Developmental Controller releasing the aircraft with less than standard separation. Reporter questioned RECAT wake turbulence.
B777 Captain reported incorrectly completing MEL procedures for an inoperative APU.
Flight attendant reported a passenger was non-complaint with face mask policy during the flight.
GA pilot reported experiencing a bounced landing; followed by a hard landing which damaged the propeller and caused the aircraft to be stopped on the runway.
ZAB ARTCC Controller reported an unsafe operation involving skydivers and a VFR aircraft below the jump plane.
CRJ flight crew reported descending below the ATC Minimum Vectoring Altitude from their assigned altitude for an RA of traffic that ATC did not report to them.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft missed a turn and came within less than the required separation from a tall obstruction.
C208 pilot reported a loss of the HSI in IMC conditions resulting in a diversion.
Air carrier Captain reported an uneventful airborne conflict with a Drone (UAV) during approach.
Air air carrier pilot reported they overlooked a runway closure in the NOTAMs.
S46 Controller reported vectoring an aircraft off of an ILS in order to avoid conflicting unidentified VFR traffic.
A Challenger 350 pilot reported Approach handed him off to Center with an incorrect descent clearance.
Pilot reported experiencing a track heading deviation and cited rustiness and lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Air Carrier Captain reported maintenance and ground personnel with no experience on specific aircraft types are being utilized due to staffing cutbacks.
Flight crew reported entering an expected fix in the FMS before it was issued by ATC but forgot to hit execute; leaving the aircraft high and not making the crossing restriction.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported a Flight Attendant was injured after they encountered wake turbulence at 14;000 ft possibly from a B777 50 miles away.

A B747 flying a CPDLC down loaded enroute to LAX clearance modification and where to by ATC it did not receive the waypoints correctly. Additionally; on short final to LAX a go-around was issued because ATC Tower automation detected a conflict with landing traffic even though that aircraft was clear of the runway.
EMB-175 Mechanic reported attempting to fix a Deferred Maintenance Item; but lacked the necessary equipment. Supervisor said to put the aircraft back together and defer it again.
Air Traffic Controller reported a VFR aircraft did not comply with instructions resulting in a NMAC.
Air Carrier pilot reported encountering unexpected severe turbulence which caused an uncontrolled altitude deviation.
MD-11 First Officer reported a go around receiving an Anti-Skid R Fail Level 2 Alert.
Corporate jet First Officer reported a NMAC 15 NM southwest of TTN with a general aviation aircraft.
EMB-145LR First Officer reported receiving a stick shaker after inadvertently entering a thunderstorm at FL360.
B737-700 flight crew reported a Flight Attendant was injured when they encountered turbulence.

S46 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft being vectored for an ILS Approach had conflicting VFR traffic climbing through the localizer course. The Controller had to vector the ILS aircraft off the approach to maintain separation.
Technician reported lack of clarity regarding aircraft records and longer than normal service intervals.
Commercial UAS pilot realized after several flights they had been flying with an expired Part 107 license.
B737-700 flight crew could not find checklist for Leading Edge Devices Not Extended.
Flight Attendant was told by an unaccompanied minor that the child's mother had COVID-19 and the child was on the way to stay with family.
B737 Captain reported a NMAC with a light twin during climb.
B737 pilot reported communication problems associated with the 8;000 foot change at intersection ARCHI.

CRJ200 dispatchers described a computer outage that resulted in the loss of operational control of the airborne aircraft they were flight following. Management did not want to delay any flights that were ready to pushback at the hub; even though the dispatchers could not communicate with or monitor the flights they were responsible for.
B737 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence and hail during arrival into IAD.
GEG TRACON Instructor reported a Developmental Controller turned an aircraft too late and entered an area with a higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer would not wear a face mask in the cockpit when asked to.

B737 flight crew experiences loss of GPS signal at FL350 in ZLC airspace due to NOTAMed GPS jamming by the military. Several other crews report GPS interruptions to ATC and ATC detects some track deviations.
Air carrier flight crew and a Flight Attendant reported diverting to a nearby alternate after several injuries resulted from a turbulence encounter.
Air carrier Captain reported that a passenger refused to comply with face mask policy and was removed from the flight before departure.
Light turbojet aircraft pilot reported they were instructed to maintain an altitude they had already passed while descending at 4500 FPM.

Air carrier First Officer reported flying the breakout procedure in response to an RA triggered by an aircraft on approach on the parallel runway that had overshot the localizer and was directly overhead.
Airline Ground Personnel reported Hazmat cargo listed for air transport without proper cargo documentation.
After returning to departure airport with unreliable airspeed indication; the Captain suggested moving necessary charts to the relevant portion of the QRH for ease in location and use.
Flight crew experienced an apparent ground spoiler problem on landing roll out and decide to watch the spoilers closer on landing next leg.

LBB Developmental/Front Line Manager (FLM) reports of a loss of separation due to compression on final. The Developmental/FLM asked his instructor if they were capable of running 2.5 miles on final and the instructor replied he didn't think so. After the two aircraft landed the Developmental found out that they needed three miles instead of 2.5.
NorCal TRACON Controller reported an unsafe situation with a departure off of Livermore airport.
Chicago Center Controller reported that Indianapolis Center controllers are not using the letters PH (Present Heading) correctly when entering the information in the fourth data block.
Flight Attendant reported two passengers who were not complying with face mask policy did not want to remain seated while the cockpit door was open.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported a GPWS alert after descending below the MSA on a night visual approach.

B747-400 First Officer reported a communications breakdown between Captain (PF) and First Officer (PM) during unstabilized approach. First Officer further stated Captain refused to consider Pilot Monitoring recommendations.
B737 Maintenance technician reported required tools were not available to complete repair.
C560 flight crew reported that a dropped iPad broke a circuit breaker collar and tripped the breaker; causing a loss of brake pressure and a taxiway excursion.
Airline ground personnel reported a discrepancy between electronic load report and Ramp Service Manual regarding dry ice cargo pit loading locations.
Technician reported that Work Cards associated with the use of Biobor JL in aircraft fuel tanks during storage are missing critical information and should be revised.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy and also moved to a better seat without asking FAs.
CRJ-200 Captain reported loss of aircraft control due to sliding on ice during taxing onto runway.
The Captain of a B757-200 performed a wind shear escape maneuver when they got a red wind shear alert with aural and annunciated warnings displayed.
Air carrier pilot reported encountering a challenging approach while dealing with fatigue.
BE35 pilot reported that during the landing flare his aircraft was affected by the rotor vortices of a nearby helicopter.
Customer Service Representative reported crowding and social distancing issues near company provided kiosks in the terminal area.
A11 TRACON controller reported air carrier on ANCHO8 SID entered higher MVA resulting in low altitude alert.
Pilot reports of poor service with Travis TRACON on multiple times and especially on the trip reported.
SR22 pilot on an IFR flight plan reported taking evasive action to avoid another aircraft that was 500 feet below him.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft departed after the release time and became a conflict with an arrival aircraft into another airport.
A pilot departing 2J8 on CTAF reported glider tow aircraft and landing gliders created multiple unsafe situations.
B737 Flight Attendant reported dangerous situation during deplaning at trip termination due to ground power interruption.
CRJ-900 Captain reported receiving a low altitude alert from DCA Tower on the LDA Z Rwy 19 approach.

CE750 Captain reported becoming distracted after capturing a false localizer signal during the ILS Runway 06 at TEB. The mandatory crossing altitude of 1500 ft at DANDY was forgotten and the fix was crossed at 1700 ft.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving altitude alert from ATC on approach to DEN airport.
EMB-135 First Officer reported executing a go-around after receiving a GPWS 'Sink Rate' alert on a visual approach associated with a wind shift.
Q400 Captain reported landing gear failed to retract when selected up; caused by the alternate gear release panel cover being open.
Airbus Captain reported missing published crossing restriction on the Jeppesen plate; due to the lack of contrast.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL370 from an A380 that was 20 miles away and 1;000 feet above them.
B737 Captain reported not receiving NOTOC [Notice to Captain] of Hazmat on board on final close out release.
B737 flight crew reported descending to a lower altitude after experiencing a power loss at FL410 while penetrating weather.
Technicians reported aircraft settled on Nose Landing Gear Door during a Strut Height Check.
Air Carrier pilot conducting a Visual Approach reported receiving a terrain alert from the aircraft and ATC.
EMB-175 Captain reported responding to a TCAS RA for traffic below while descending from FL350 to FL340.

Air Carrier Captain reported the TRACON Final Controller placing the crew too close to a preceding heavy jet arrival's wake turbulence and issuing a Brasher warning for a speed violation despite complying with ATC instructions.
Air carrier First Officer reported concerns with SUN Tower opposite direction procedures resulting in traffic conflicts.
Chicago Center Controller reported an unsafe situation relating to weather; traffic volume; and airspace.
Corporate jet flight crew reported a runway incursion due to Ground Controller's ambiguity and lack of situational awareness.

An aircraft on a visual approach circling to land ASE Runway 33 in gusty conditions and high on approach developed an excessive rate of descent which triggered the EGPWS TERRAIN PULL UP alert. A go-around was executed followed by a second successful visual circle to land Runway 33.
B757 Captain reported problems with his iPad apps while trying to obtain company information.

Air Carrier Captain reported on more than one occasion the landing instructions to the aircraft from the Tower are changed to accommodate the needs of this airport. The reporter gives examples/reasons for this report.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that Federal Air Marshals were allowed to board prior to the crew boarding.
Small Transport Captain reported ATC refusal for weather diversion resulting in severe altitude gain; airspeed loss; and loss of aircraft control.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported smoke outside the aircraft after the cabin doors had been closed. This caused some worry in the cockpit and cabin until it was discovered that the smoke was coming from a belt loader.
Maintenance Technician and a Flight Crew reported the log book was not signed off by maintenance and did not comply with an Airworthiness Directive.
Technician reported an aircraft operation in Part 91 flight without all documents signed and the log book update correctly.
B787 flight attendant reported reduced staffing in the cabin creating an unsafe situation.
B737 Mechanic reported the aircraft he worked on diverted due to engine oil loss caused by a loose engine oil line fitting.
Air carrier Captain reported a Flight Attendant became ill during flight.
A Pilot expressed concern about an RNAV Arrival which requires the aircraft to descend below Class B airspace and reduce speed to 200 knots unexpectedly.

Maintenance personnel completed and cleared a B737 bird strike maintenance log entry. On the next takeoff; the First Officer's airspeed lagged in relation to the Captain's and the ENG light accompanied the autothrottle disconnect. During the turn after takeoff the First Officer's stall warning alerted.
Center Controller reported an unsafe situation with Tracon not taking hand-offs.
A flight crew discovered three tires weighing 300 pound had been offloaded upon arrival; but had not been accounted for in the weight and balance prior to departure from CLT.
Cessna pilot reported aircraft was struck by an object during initial climb causing a dent in aircraft.
A319 Captain reported departure was delayed when an iPad began a download during taxi out that inhibited access to the departure plate.

A PIT TRACON Controller reports of a VFR aircraft that had to climb to be able to stay VFR. Controller issued new altitude and told aircraft to report if unable to maintain VFR. IFR aircraft in vicinity climbed due to RA with VFR aircraft.
B737-700 First Officer reported that a bolt was installed in a gear pin slot for the main landing gear.
Air carrier Captain reported that prior to gate departure a lavatory smoke warning occurred. The passenger allegedly smoking in the lav refused to leave and was ordered off by the Captain.

ZID Center Controller reported he handed off an aircraft to the next sector; but kept the aircraft on his frequency. During this time the next Controller was editing the data blocks causing confusion to who was actually working the aircraft. Controller also stated many are creating 'flash through' procedures that are not in the SOP/LOA.
Air Carrier Captain reported being over fueled for a flight.
LR60 Captain reported overshooting cleared altitude in climb to FL340 after encountering wake turbulence from a wide body transport aircraft.

Center controllers report of a civilian aircraft receiving a TCAS alert from military aircraft operating within a mistakenly deactivated MOA. Confusion resulted from the misunderstanding that the military aircraft was not a single aircraft but rather a flight of two that had ceased joint operations and were subsequently operating independently.
Air carrier Captain reported that approach charts at certain military airports do not have a note requiring cold temperature altitude corrections.
A pilot reported that while ferrying a single engine amphibian to SMO from the deserts; unexpectedly entered IMC near EMT at 1;000 feet; but continued until encountering VMC below Class B.

Cessna 208 Captain reported that the windshield became electrically charged and attracted small black particles from a deteriorating instrument visor. The Captain also reported that the First Officer was electrically shocked when he touched the inside of the windshield.
BK-117 pilot reported that after the main rotor contacted tree branches during a landing attempt; the helicopter was repositioned to land in a nearby field.
Center Controller reported airspace violations due to another sector approving point outs in which they should not have approved.
A319 Captain reported encountering turbulence at FL360; probably either wake or clear air turbulence related.
B737 flight crew reported pushing back without required Hazmat documentation.

S46 Controller reported departures were stopped at an interim altitude for a photo mission and they were unable to climb one aircraft; due to workload; which flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
ERJ-190 flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert while on a visual approach and descending below the glideslope.
A Maintenance Technician reported that ramp personnel are moving aircraft with unauthorized personnel and not following procedures.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC perceived an altitude excursion; possibly due to ground equipment problem.
Student Pilot reported entering Phoenix Class Bravo Airspace due to winds.
Pilot reported airfield lighting at PDK is poor and erratic. Surrounding ground lights not associated with the airport compound the issue further.
Air carrier F/O reported slowing below ATC assigned speed when directed to do so by the Captain; who saw a potential conflict occurring with the aircraft preceding them.

A corporate jet pilot reported the VNY Runway 16R ILS/LOC frequency 111.3 erroneously reported an 86.3 DME on a 6.4 NM final at 3;450 feet. The I-VNY ILS/LOC does not transmit a DME signal and so the false DME can be confusing for some flight crews.
Seattle Center Controller reported a situation where an aircraft was below the MIA and above the MEA; resulting in the MSAW to alarm.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger using the restroom numerous times; coughing and having to constantly be reminded to wear a mask.
Air Carrier Captain reported an RA while on final approach to SEA.
A320 flight crew reported runway incursion.
CRJ-200 Captain reported that the First Officer failed to follow FARs and company policy by looking at his cell phone while on an active taxiway and during sterile cockpit.

B737-800 Captain reported refusing an aircraft with an MEL for center tank fuel quantity due to the erroneous indications produced affecting the FMC and PFD airspeed limits. An inoperative center tank fuel quantity would have been acceptable.
A B737-700 ready for pushback was struck by another B737 taxiing into the adjacent gate and sustained wingtip damage. The aircraft returned to the gate.
BE33 pilot reported multiple bird strikes during landing rollout.

A320 Captain reported donning on oxygen mask after experiencing mild fumes during descent. The Captain further expressed concerns about hesitation of Flight Attendants to call-in unfit for duty due to fears of reprimand from management.

A jet crew on a left downwind visual approach to APC Runway 18R was given an extended downwind and because of raising terrain received several ATC low altitude alerts; as well as an EGPWS CAUTION TERRAIN.
An E170 First Officer reported that there was an inset on the approach plate which included another IAF (initial approach fix) that was missed.
B777 Flight Attendant in-charge reported an inflight turbulence encounter that injured flight attendants and passengers.

ATC cleared on aircraft onto DAL Runway 31R. That aircraft interfered with the ILS glideslope and caused an aircraft on final to pitch down and receive an EGPWS alert. The crew executed a missed approach.

First Officer reports his EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) failed and requested the applicable trip charts to be emailed. His Dispatcher reported he later determined either an EFB should have been borrowed or the pilot replaced. An EFB is required.
Pilot reported an issue with an overlying TFR at 47N.
A general aviation pilot reported the challenge of approaching and landing at a busy airport particularly with unfavorable wind conditions.

A Flight Attendant suffered anaphylactic shock following a full day's flights on an aircraft returned to service that morning following two days of maintenance. The aircraft had areas of wet paint; but the etiology of the anaphylaxis is unknown.
Flight Attendant reported observing multiple events of non-compliance with face mask wearing among passengers and crew members; and cited a lack of policy enforcement as a contributing factor.
EMB-140 flight crew reported responding to a TCAS Resolution Advisory during climb triggered by a military fighter.
Air carrier flight crew reported while maneuvering into parking spot; aircraft made contact with another airplane.
B787 flight crew reported they were enroute to their destination when Dispatch asked them to divert to a different city due to routing needs.
ZDV ARTCC Controller reported being unable to communicate with aircraft due to faulty transmitters during mid-shift while working combined sectors.

Air Carrier First Officer described a windshear event on final to ABQ Runway 21 which resulted in a go-around and ultimately a Runway 8 approach and uneventful landing. A flap overspeed occurred during the go-around.
Mechanic reported that management disregarded Maintenance Manual procedures and assigned a maintenance task to be accomplished in conflict with published procedures.
B757-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to MEM in trail of a B767.
Pilot reports of a busy day in the VFR traffic pattern at VNY airport. Aircraft confliction with traffic and the controller not knowing who was where; added to the pilot's traffic problem.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that while searching for a passenger's phone the passenger was left on the plane without the minimum crew required on board.
ZLA airspace representative reported DOD GPS testing that caused an aircraft to deviate off course.  Representative reports that GPS testing does not include facility in concurrence process.
Air carrier Captain reported reaching an emergency fuel state after diverting and at the alternate airport being directed to go-around.

A ramp agent reported having to unload a Boing 737 without enough help. Also he didn't have a tail stand available so he took extra time and hooked up the tug to keep the nose on the ground. All of this led to him exceeding the expected unloading time of 25 minutes and he was approached by management about why this had happened.

A CRJ-200 Captain was advised by a passenger on his aircraft that significant ice had built up on his right wing.  He learned of this after the flight and wondered whether the manufacturer would consider this to be an expected situation.
A Maintenance Technician reported that used consumable parts are tested and re-certified using bogus procedures.

D01 controller describes an Opposite Direction Operation [ODO]; which he wasn't sure if it was legal to do or not. Later that day he does another ODO like operation; then after review is told to cease ODO's.  Controller asks for clarification on what he can and can't do reference ODO's.
Experimental aircraft pilot reported a loss of engine power due to a non-standard fuel system and lack of system knowledge.

While in a descent; cleared to FL330; the flight crew of a CL30 responded as directed to a TCAS TA followed shortly by an RA. Upon completion of the avoidance maneuver they continued their descent while monitoring the traffic visually. While doing so they inadvertently descended approximately 500 feet below their cleared altitude.
Dispatchers reported problems with taking an aircraft out of storage and an associated MEL that was not applied before takeoff.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL210 12 miles in trail of an A330.
ZME Center Controller reported the RNAV Runway 22 Approach at SRB has an IAF altitude 100 feet lower than the minimum IFR altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a low altitude warning from tower despite cockpit instruments showing that they were above the RNAV glidepath.
Air carrier First Officer reported aircraft sliding on an ice and snow covered taxiway.
ZDC Controller reported of problems with flows into the airspace. High workload and distractions led to a conflict alert.

A B737-900 crew parked and with no ACARS AFT CG or TIPPING alert; deplaning began. The Captain felt aircraft movement in time to move people forward and cease forward cargo baggage removal which prevented tipping damage.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track and altitude deviation during departure.  Flight crew cited task saturation and lack of flying were contributing factors.
ZDC Center Controller reported a loss of separation between two aircraft while working 6 sectors.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving a TCAS RA during departure.  The TCAS RA was reportedly due to the aircraft's high rate of climb as a result of having a light gross takeoff weight.
C182RG pilot reported a runway excursion following a crosswind landing.

ATC Controller reports of issuing a release to a tower for a departure and issues traffic they are working in close proximity. Tower departs the aircraft and gets visual on the pointed out aircraft. Tower then calls TRACON Controller and says that was close. TRACON Controller does not like that the Tower departed the aircraft so close to the point out.

An air carrier on the PHX EAGUL6 RNAV was vectored off the arrival and given altitude changes so that when cleared back onto the track; the aircraft failed to capture a 10;000 foot altitude constraint. Similar malfunctions occur at other airports where ATC makes extensive RNAV changes causing the FMS to not respect crossing altitudes.
Citation pilot reported the Tower changed an instrument approach to a Visual Approach against the pilot's wishes.
B737 Captain reported that the pushback driver did not use standard verbiage during pushback.
A Center Controller reported being required to issue lengthy reroutes to aircraft around Mazatlan Center due to equipment outage.
Center Controller reported communication issue with a TRACON that was ATC Zero involving an aircraft inbound to an airport with a non-NOTAMed closed runway.
Center Controller reported that under-staffing due to COVID-19 has resulted in numerous aircraft reroutes.
Small aircraft Pilot reported runway excursion due to inability to maintain directional control on landing.

An air taxi crew at 6;000 ft was issued a traffic advisory at 5;500 ft by ROA TRACON on vectors for ROA. The crew did not see the aircraft but responded to a TCAS RA CLIMB with the traffic 500 ft beneath.
Center Controller reported they missed an incomplete readback of a clearance and an aircraft flew below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Mechanic reported that lack of proper equipment may lead to a major fuel spill when obtaining the fuel sample for the pre-flight fuel freeze test.
Instructor and trainee pilots reported experiencing an airspace incursion into Class C airspace.
B737 NG Captain reported NorCal Approach uses ROGGE as a fix in their clearance for SFO Runway 19L approaches but ROGGE is not in the FMC database.
First Officer reported he was concerned with an ATC procedure at HNL that he felt could lead to an unsafe conflict if a go-around was required.

Albuquerque Center Controller reported an unsafe parachute operation that permitted jumpers to exit the aircraft above enroute IFR traffic. Reporter recommended a marker be put on the map to identify the area as a skydiving zone.

A flight crew tried to help a Flight Attendant solve a dilemma posed by a passenger wishing to bring aboard a number of lithium batteries. The flight attendant Manual had a chart which provided guidance but unfortunately; they misinterpreted the chart.
Flight Attendant reported the disinfecting spray is making individuals nauseous.
TRACON Controller reported they did not realize an aircraft read back the wrong instructions and then observed it fly into confliction with gliders.

Piper Cherokee pilot reported difficulty with aircraft pitch control immediately after takeoff due to an improper rigging of the pitch trim system. Pilot reduced power and and landed safely on the remaining runway surface.
ASE Controller describes an opposite-direction arrival and the loss of separation that ensued with a departure.
B737 flight crew reported an FMS anomaly in its database resulting in non-standard power reductions after takeoff.
B737-700 Captain reported being high on a crossing restriction on the RNAV Z Runway 12R approach into SJC after receiving a late ATC clearance.

A air carrier crew flying a CANPA profile descent on the to JFK set the RNAV GPS 4L MDA. The crew; distracted by a PITCH TRIM caution; returned to the profile after ATC called a low altitude alert at less than 1;000 feet before KRSTL.
B737 Captain reported deviating around an area of reported severe turbulence on a trans-Pacific flight without ATC approval.
P80 TRACON Controller reported an IFR arrival had to respond to a TCAS alert with a VFR parachute jump aircraft even though the Controller had attempted to vector the aircraft away.

TRACON Controller had an aircraft on approach to one airport and a second aircraft released off an adjacent airport flying to the same airport as the one on approach. The departure aircraft did not contact the TRACON Controller but immediately contacted the arrival Tower Controller. The TRACON Controller vectored the aircraft on approach away and attempted to get contact with the departure. The TRACON Controller discovered the departure was on Tower frequency and relayed control instructions to further separate the traffic. In the meantime the Tower departed another aircraft which was climbing in to confliction with the arriving aircraft being vectored for traffic. The Controller stopped this departure's climb; but not in enough time to prevent a loss of separation.
An EMB-145 crew was notified after arrival that a 26 pound Lithium-Ion battery shipment had been transported even though clearly labeled 'CARGO AIRCRAFT ONLY FORBIDDEN IN PASSENGER AIRCRAFT'.
B737-900 Captain reported lining up with the wrong runway after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding aircraft on a visual approach to LAX.
Air carrier Captain reported departing with an inoperative safety item that should have been inspected by maintenance prior to departure.
B737-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while 8 miles in trail of a heavy in the vicinity of SMO on arrival to LAX.
CE750 Captain reported a lightning strike; severe turbulence; and wind shear which resulted in a 70 degree bank while on approach to AUS.
Air Carrier office employee reported the office area for work would benefit from the installation of a higher-grade air filter to combat COVID-19.
Technicians reported log book entries were not made before returning the aircraft to service after scheduled maintenance.
A Tower System Support Specialist observed the Local Controller allow a simultaneous helicopter arrival and aircraft departure in violation of facility directives.
Air carrier Captain reported various issues relating to the COVID-19 pandemic including FAs not properly using masks; passenger complaints regarding seating issues and lack of flying time.

Air carrier flight crew reported severe turbulence approaching OGG with speed fluctuating +- 40 knots and went around twice before diverting.
An instructor flying a DA40 reported a NMAC on initial climb from a Cessna 182 in the pattern.

The Captain of a B737 reported that on decent he confirmed speeds with the First Officer and set his bugs. The First Officer was not aware that on the older model B737's the procedure to set both the VREF and VTGT bugs are still required.
A CRJ-200 First Officer reported ATC unnecessarily complicated their approach 3 miles from the Final Approach fix.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported turbulence so severe that the shoulder harness had to be removed to avoid injury.

Controller reported that a pilot reported encountering a drone while on a visual approach to New Orleans Lakefront airport.
Approach Controller reported that radar targets were showing rapid erroneous speed readouts and were jumping around the radar displaying false heading changes.
The flight crew of an A321 reported that on final approach they encountered severe turbulence; so they executed a go-around. They had continuous turbulence associated with wind-shear.

B737-800 flight crew rejected takeoff at approximately 105 knots as directed by ATC due to aircraft performing a go-around on adjacent runway. Crew noted some initial confusion as to whether the 'Cancel takeoff clearance' instruction was for them.
Pilot reported ground looping the aircraft after landing with no injuries.
An ATC Tower Controller reported an aircraft maintenance facility located on airport tests transponders without coordinating with ATC which can interfere with normal flight operations.
Air carrier First Officer landing at LSE received a field conditions report indicating good braking action. The braking action was not good and there was unreported snow on the runway.

A ramp crew member reports his concerns about a safety hazard caused by the 100 percent Oversteer Warning System siren sounding off inside the cab of their supertugs during aircraft towing operations. The loud; shrieking siren sound overwhelms the Ground Controller; Ramp Controller and Tug Operator's efforts to communicate with each other; especially when crossing active runways and taxiways. The siren cannot be turned off unless the aircraft is disconnected from the Supertug.
Pilot reported seeing a small drone on the left side of the aircraft at 1;000 ft MSL and 3 NM from the runway while the flight was on final approach.

Small aircraft pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at EUL airport.
DHC-8 flight crew reported turbulence during descent that injured the Flight Attendant.

PCT Supervisor reported failing to not make the correct system macro entries after de-combining a sector. The Restricted Area status displayed not in use on the Controller's radar display when it was actually in use. The Controller allowed an aircraft to enter the Restricted Area.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft got off the ground that was in a ground stop due to its destination airport being closed for COVID-19 cleaning.
Air carrier First Officer reported landing without a clearance.
Air carrier pilot reported a potential fuel saving technique to not manually delete the reduced thrust setting during the climb phase of flight.

Pilot reports of confusion caused by similar sounding waypoint names while on the FOOXX4 Arrival into TPA. The pilot hears; reads back; and proceeds to the wrong waypoint after being cleared by Approach Control.
I90 Controllers reported that a PA28 aircraft was on a vector below the Minimum vectoring Altitude and less than three miles from the obstruction depicted on the radar display.
A320 series pilot reported that they did not receive a response to their ACARS message or the operations radio frequency.

BE36 pilot reported ATC will not approve IFR flights to EYW via V3 due to 'traffic conflicts'; requiring flight along the west coast of Florida over swamp and open water. The reporter believes this is not safe.

Pilot reported being denied a request to reroute around a wildfire TFR and convective activity. Pilot diverted to avoid the adverse conditions.
B737 Maintenance Technician reported observing incorrect installation of life rafts on the aircraft.

Captain reported that Lithium Metal batteries can be carried onboard the aircraft under IATA shipper exemption UN 3090 Section II but not declared on the Dangerous Goods paperwork. The reporter recommended a change in policy.
Air Carrier Captain reported a TOO LOW FLAPS warning and an Unstabilized Approach.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing DFW from an A321 above them.
Beechcraft 99 pilot reported flying through severe turbulence.

Maintenance Inspector reported concerns with company signing off of annual inspections of aircraft with several known maintenance issues and discrepancies.
Grumman American AA-5 pilot reported flying in VFR conditions when the weather closed in and they found themselves in IMC.
A Controller reported a loss of separation following a military formation flight break up.
B787 mechanics reported unknown substance residue in cabin and galley area.

Air carrier First Officer reported the taxiway between Taxiway D and the ramp; between Spot 9 and 10 is labeled as D5 on airport signage and on the 10-9 airport diagram but labeled as D2 on the 10-9B and 10-9D charts.
B737NG First Officer reported airspeed and vertical speed issues related to a flaps 25 takeoff.
Flight Attendant reported severe turbulence and injury during climb out.

Air carrier flight crew reported they flew a different route than expected and Center stated the mix-up was an ATC error.
A319 Captain reported observing snow accumulation on the flaps after de-icing. Returned to gate for second de-icing.
Pilot flying PA28 aircraft veered off flight path on approach and landed off runway.
B787 First Officer reported gate pushback initiated without final DG report.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a TCAS RA related to an abnormally high climb rate associated with very lightly loaded aircraft because of the COVID-19 pandemic.
PA-28 pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from ATC on approach.

ZOA Controller reported of a balloon descending in his airspace and having to issue traffic on the balloon to other aircraft. Controller was not briefed on the balloon's mission prior to working the position.

Controller implements plan for future handoffs and requests Miles In-Trail [MIT]; which are denied by management.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported arrival Hazmat cargo incorrectly configured for air transport.
C182 Pilot reported a near mid air collision while on approach; which caused the other aircraft to take evasive action.
LAF Tower Controller reported an aircraft landing on the wrong runway and have a ground conflict with a departure aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy.

A Captain reported receiving a traffic alert (TA) during climbout. This was followed by an RA which commanded a deviation from the cleared climb profile. After the RA was resolved; a normal climb was resumed.

A Flight Attendant identified during boarding that young children were incorrectly issued exit row seats. After requesting that they were reseated; Flight Attendant was informed that Customer Service was too busy to confirm seat positions during the boarding process.
CE-560XLS Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into SAN 5 miles in trail of an A319 that resulted in a 'violent' roll.
Heavy transport aircraft First Officer reported GPS failures on the approach at this foreign international airport.

Ground held crossing aircraft; and asked Local as to why he didn't tell him it was a touch and go.
ZBW Controller reported Traffic Management Unit refused to assist their sector while they were in high volume and complex situations.
Ground Personnel reported a hazardous condition from leaking fuel carts and fuel cart spillage.

Small Aircraft pilot reported a NMAC with another Small Aircraft landing at the same non towered airport and not announcing their intentions on CTAF.
Air carrier Captain reported executing a 360 degree turn on final at TNCA at about 1100 MSL when ATC was late advising them of a cruise ship crossing the approach path.
B777 flight crew reported an airborne conflict on their flight through China airspace and expressed some discomfort with China ATC procedures.
ZMA controllers and flight crew reported ATC display indicating erroneous mode C of a CL300. ATC issued control instructions for separation based on their display information.
Pilots report of an equipment stuck microphone that was used between the cockpit and the cabin. The problem could not be rectified so they had to divert and landed in an emergency condition.
Tower Controller reported Quality Assurance staff suggested a pilot initiated go-around due to preceding traffic was an ATC Operational Error.
787 Captain reported encountering a wind shear at FL400 resulting in airspeed exceedance; deviation from assigned altitude and loss of auto throttles.

A320 Captain reported that MEL items applied to the aircraft were confusing and did not apply to the situation as written.

Captain reported that use of a company-required app on iPad depletes the battery rapidly.

CE560 Captain laments his company's policy of rapid schedule changes that in this case; may have led to an airborne conflict when the crew is told to return to their departure airport twenty minutes after takeoff.

A300 flight crew reported diverting due to unforecast weather combined with inadequate fuel on board.

Captain reported difficulty with dispatch getting the correct aircraft suffix filed on the flight plan.
First officer and Captain reported of a mistake in typing an intersection name that caused ATC to question where they were going. Similar fixes separated by 27 miles and only different by one letter.
Air carrier flight crew reported an SOP error by not informing Load Planning and Dispatch of the actual FOB.

Air Carrier Captain reported a passenger became verbally abusive and would not comply with face mask policy.

A Center Controller reported the sector became overloaded with traffic; but no action was taken to reduce the traffic flow into the airspace.

B737 flight crew reported that the FMC had a different name for a point than the EFB chart in JEPP FD-PRO.
An air carrier descending into ABQ was given a descent clearance to 5;000 ft; but when the Captain asked ATC if 5;000 ft was correct they were assigned 8;500 ft. ABQ field elevation is 5;315 ft.

A Local Controller reported not noticing a student pilot line up to land on the wrong parallel runway. The aircraft cut in front of a corporate jet aircraft on short final who initiated a go around on their own.

A HS-128-800 Captain reported a hydraulic leak written of as over serviced but the leaking reported again on the aircraft. Due to crews move among aircraft they cannot track the reoccurring maintenance discrepancies.

An MD82 Captain describes the circumstances surrounding a weather diversion to MMMX and the problems this causes with the Mexican aviation authorities.
A corporate jet flight crew reported the FRG Jeppesen and NOAA charts depict the FRG Runway 19 approach end overrun differently. NOAA correctly depicts the overrun on either side of Taxiway M.
Air carrier First Officer reported contacting a vehicle during taxi to the de-icing pad.
B777 flight crew reported Dispatch failure to file a re-release #2 with ATC resulting in a course deviation.
Air Carrier flight crew reported an RA while on approach. The Captain reported the Tower at this location was closed earlier than expected and this contributed to the event.

An A320 pilot reported confusion with an MEL regarding if an Air Conditioning Pack was operational when a Class II Maintenance Message 'Pack 1 or 2' was displayed on the Centralized Fault Display System.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an unstabilized approach; disconnected the autopilot and stabilized at 500 feet and landed uneventfully.

Flight Attendant reported deciding to serve only sealed snack and beverage items to customers due to COVID-19 safety concerns.
Air carrier flight crew reported conflicting radio and CPDLC messages from ATC that could have resulted in a traffic conflict.
Air carrier First Officer reported crews are not wearing COVID-19 mask in briefing rooms.

Richmond Tower Controller reported seeing an aircraft on final to a closed runway. Reporter called TRACON and advised. Reporter then heard local change an aircraft on 3 mile final to a different runway.
PA-28 flight instructor reported taking evasive action when the weather conditions and aircraft performance did not appear to guarantee safe terrain clearance.
Pilot reported encountering two tethered paragliders at 3;500 feet above a Lake Berryessa requiring evasive action.

Air carrier Captain commented about engine out procedures at Bogota (SKBO); a high altitude airport in mountainous terrain.

B737 First Officer reported the SFO DYAMD2 STAR may have some errors related to Class B floor.

VNY Ground Controller reports of an observation where an aircraft receives an RA on final and is told to descend below the MVA to fix the situation.

B757 flight crew reported rejecting the takeoff after experiencing a bird strike at approximately 100 kts.

Dispatcher reported differences in the way NOTAMs are written and possible confusion.

An ADS-B equipped aircraft in oceanic airspace was deviating for weather approximately 100 miles off course. The Advanced Technologies and Oceanic Procedures (ATOP) system is a radar Data processing system for ATC to use in Oceanic airspace where there is no radar coverage. It relies on Automatic Dependent Surveillance (ADS) equipped aircraft transmitting position reports and forecasts routes and possible conflictions. Due to the extensive deviation of this flight for weather the ATOP system was unable to accurately track the position and projected route of this aircraft. The controllers realized there may be an error in reporting and descended a different aircraft along the route. Once they verified an accurate position of the deviating aircraft they realized it would have been in confliction with the other aircraft which they had descended.
