Helicopter pilot reported violating Class D airspace while conducting aerial survey and  inspection work.

While flying the RNAV GPS 18 Approach to F70 a medium sized turbo jet crew reported an EGPWS 'TERRAIN; PULL-UP' on profile at about 2;400 feet; past DISEY; with an 800 feet overcast. Terrain reference was obtained as the terrain avoidance maneuver was initiated so the flight continued to land.
B737 Captain reported noticing during preflight that the Evacuation Checklist on First Officer's yoke didn't match the QRH Evacuation Checklist.
C206 pilot questioned why PBI TRACON terminated radar services as they were still in BCT Delta airspace.
GA pilot reported a taxiway incursion and cited construction related distraction and lack of flying as contributing factors.
TBM-850 Captain reported moderate vibration from severe icing event.
Embraer EMB-175 First Officer reported a discrepancy on the main landing gear that maintenance felt was airworthy. Eventually the issue was addressed and the flight departed.
R-44 pilot reported a NMAC with a fixed wing aircraft.
Helicopter pilot reported not having the correct documents to transport a COVID-19 patient.
Flight Instructor reported a near midair collision in the airport traffic pattern.
Flight Attendant reported that another Flight Attendant who reportedly had not flown for over 5 months; sat in the wrong seat for takeoff and did not follow procedures for opening a door.
During cruise climb after takeoff the aircraft encountered severe clear air turbulence. The event incurred cabin crew injuries and the flight returned to originating airport.
A pilot reported he confused the fixes on an RNAV STAR and descended below a published restriction of the RNAV STAR.

A319 First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence and mountain wave at FL360 that resulted in loss of control; including loss of 500 feet in altitude and rolls of 30 degrees in both directions.
Tower Controller working 3 Local positions reported an airborne conflict between two departures.
A US air carrier on approach to TNCA Runway 11 localizer at 7DME had to take evasive action from traffic which TNCA Tower repeatedly stated did not exist but which the crew saw.
PAO Tower Controller reported a loss of separation between an IFR GA aircraft inbound to PAO and a departure off Moffett Field (NUQ).
Pilot reported issues relating to a circling approach to Runway 32 at RDU and ATC communication issues for the circling approach.
B737 Captain questioned the safety of a new company procedure that allows passengers to board before flight attendants can perform safety checks or determine if the aircraft is suitable for boarding.
Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) operator reported that he was unsure whether he was in violation of FAR 107 while operating his drone due to ambiguity on the airspace charts.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering 'extreme' wake turbulence at approximately 1;200 feet AGL departing DFW in trail of an A321.
ZLA controllers reported an airborne conflict when a departure aircraft entered their airspace without proper handoff from SCT TRACON.

Air carrier Captain reported that the flight attendants were not comfortable coming into the flight deck while the pilot was on a restroom break; until the remaining pilot was wearing a face mask. Reporter described safety concerns with having to wear a face mask in the cockpit.
Air carrier Captain reported that while waiting to depart; they were locked for 2 hours in a small bus with not enough seats for everyone and no bathroom.
Denver Center Military Coordinator and Traffic Management Controller reported of problems related to military airspace usage and coordination between ATC facilities.
ORD Tower Controller reported a CRJ-200 encountered wake turbulence 2.7 miles in trail of a B757 on approach to Runway 10C.
HCF TRACON Developmental reported an aircraft got below Minimum IFR Altitude on approach to ITO airport.  Radio coverage in the HCF areas were cited as contributed factor.
Airline Dispatcher reported the company event form for reporting a NMAC is missing necessary information.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a runway incursion.

A flight crew reported a 'TANK #2 FUEL QTY LOW'; followed by a 'TANK #3 FUEL QTY LOW' and suspected a fuel leak. They later discovered a dispatch weight and balance error listed the payload at six tons instead of the actual 92;900 tons.
B787 flight crew reported being issued a go-around at low altitude.
A LR-60 Captain reported ATC vectored them to the final approach course and within 300 ft. of an unidentified aircraft.

ZSU Controller reported a busier day than normal. Reporter said they were too busy to do coordination and the correct staffing was lacking. Controller reported that they were receiving aircraft with no coordination from non-radar airspace.
Air carrier flight crew reported severe turbulence on descent.
C208 First Officer reported descending below the glideslope and receiving a low altitude alert from ATC.
A Lake Buccaneer Instructor pilot reported striking a foreign object while demonstrating a water takeoff; causing damage to the aircraft.
Burbank Local Controller reported C172 departed WHP not talking to ATC and had a NMAC with a CRJ9 arrival.
Flight Attendant reported the Captain advised the crew that he would not be wearing a mask in the cockpit.
A319 Captain reported they had a late pushback due to discrepancies with the Maintenance Release Document.
Flight crew reported a concern when the Controller asked them why they slowed down. The reporters noted the regulation that ATC speed assignments must be restated with an approach clearance.
EMB-175 Captain and SAT Approach Controller reported traffic conflict with an unidentified VFR aircraft over 5C1.
Flight Attendant reported an Unaccompanied Minor failed to understand and refused to comply with safety instructions; resulting in disruption of service.
Air Traffic Controller and BE200 Captain reported confusing instructions which created an airborne conflict resulted in pilot reporting a NMAC.
B737-800 flight crew reported there was confusion about their descent clearance due to a similar sounding call sign on the same frequency.

Air carrier Captain reported taking an evasive maneuver after identifying a cluster of balloons; thought to have been a UAV at first; traveling in the opposite direction towards them while on approach to LAX airport.
Pilot on final approach reports a near miss with another aircraft.
EMB-175 flight crew reported executing a go-around on a visual approach to LAX after encountering wake turbulence from the preceding EMB-175 that was only two miles ahead.
Technicians reported an aircraft in work did not have required documents and logbook entries for a pass down task completed.
RV-10 pilot reported they were distracted by an electrical issue and forgot to switch fuel tanks causing the engine to stop in flight. The pilot landed the aircraft successfully.
Technician reported an aircraft battery had to be replaced due to incorrect storage procedure.
A general aviation pilot reported many pilots are confused by the charted frequency depiction for Anderson Regional Airport.
Corporate Captain reported the original First Officer (FO) was replaced because of COVID-19 and the replacement FO was still in FAR rest period when the flight took off.
Pilot reported flying in possible violation of FAR 61.57 attributed to COVID-19 related disruptions.
Airline Ground Personnel reported Hazmat shipment incorrectly transported due to no Hazmat required labeling.

Air carrier flight crew on arrival to LAS reported being vectored around at lower altitude. Eventually they were cleared for a visual to Runway 1L; had an aircraft vectored in front of them for Runway 1R; and were told to go around. They advised ATC of their low fuel state and were then cleared to land Runway 1L.
McDonnell Douglas First Officer reported a jammed flap lever; which was eventually freed.

A Tower Controller reported of an NMAC. Local Controller turned one aircraft towards another aircraft making the second aircraft take evasive actions to avoid turning aircraft. Reporter wanted this to be looked at and stated it was due to the Local Controller having issues relating to home and fatigue.
Pilot reported clearance was different than what they had filed and would like to see HPN ATC Facilities re-open.

Small Transport aircraft Captain reported there are no NOTAMs at GAI airport with regards to the current unavailable taxiways and work in progress. Discrepancies between signage and taxiway charts were also cited.
A Tower Radar Controller reported an IFR aircraft departing a non towered satellite airport rejected its takeoff due to an opposite direction arriving aircraft.
Flight Crew reported a propeller strike on landing due to oscillations following a ballooned landing.
Flight instructor reported returning to their departure airport during an approaching thunderstorm.
SR22 pilot reported experiencing inflight loss of control while trying to correct to the proper track during a go-around.
Flight Crew reported departing airport late due to lack of RVR values and below minimum weather.
PA46 pilot reported after a UAV encounter the PFD in the aircraft produced a large navigational error. The reporter suspects GPS jamming.
Instructor pilot reported that while flying with a student they struck the ground with the tail of the aircraft during landing.
DHC-8 flight crew reported a TCAS RA for unreported traffic while on approach.
An airport's one runway was closed via NOTAM. A controller noticed the NOTAM and realized no one knew the runway had been closed due to snow.

The pilot of an MQ9 Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) reported that; while conducting a tactical mission; he had to abandon his control station during a thunderstorm; leaving the drone without a pilot at the controls for approximately one hour.

B-777 Technician reported illness as a result of work assignment of vacuum waste tank liquid separator/filter replacement. Reported strongly recommended the need for improved protective clothing and respirator equipment.
Aircraft Technician suggests a link between aircraft cleaning and windshield damage.
PHL TRACON Controller reported working the midnight shift with all sectors combined; and became to busy to allow departures to depart and had several go-arounds for final spacing.

Regional Jet Captain reported that after being cleared to descend via the SERFR2 arrival to SFO at the SWELS intersection they were cleared the TIPP TOE VISUAL 28L approach which has a 1000 foot higher crossing at MENLO than the SERFR2 and they had started their descent below the crossing altitude.
High performance aircraft bound for the satellite PHX airports are often routed through an alert area (A-231) northwest of PHX.

MD-83 Flight Attendant reported boarding passengers up the aft airstair in hazardous conditions because de-ice fluid from the previous flight leaked down on the stairs making them slippery. A woman carrying a child slipped on the top step entering the cabin.
ZDV Controller reported receiving an aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
An A319 flight crew reported a Flight Attendant was injured when they encountered severe turbulence at FL350.
Air carrier flight crew deviated from an ATC request in order to avoid severe weather.
Air carrier pilot reported being advised about a possible non-compliant passenger regarding mask wear.
B737-800 flight crew reported moderate turbulence due to weather deviations being unavailable.
Indianapolis Center Controller reported of a problem with an aircraft transponder. Aircraft did not show up on reporters' scope but showed up on another long range radar site.
B737 Captain reported that the planned fuel load for a flight did not provide an adequate buffer for unforeseen situations that may arise.
A ZDV ARTCC Controller cleared an aircraft for approach to a runway that was closed for snow plowing. A NOTAM had not been issued. Airport personnel did not advise the ATC facility of the closure.
G-200 flight crew reported inadvertent transport of undeclared Hazmat material.
TRACON Controller reported missing an incorrect readback.

A local Pilot; familiar with the area and TFR NOTAMs; was confused by the format and depiction of the TFR area; and unintentionally entered the area. He added that a clearer presentation of the affected area would likely have prevented this encounter.
Helicopter pilot reported that Maintenance personnel advised him to immediately take the aircraft out of service due to time sensitive parts that may have been overflown.

An aircraft departed JNU Runway 26 after helicopter traffic but at a 3;500-4;000 FPM climb rate was only able to take modified evasive action from the TCAS RA 'DESCEND NOW' command. The First Officer reports the traffic 3;000 ft abeam.
RDU Tower Controller reported that the windows in the cab were dirty and needed cleaning. Controller reported that it appeared the visibility was one mile when the ATIS reported it at 7 miles.

NCT Controller describes an aircraft departing receives a RA immediately on initial contact. The controller then tells the aircraft to climb; the pilot refuses. Miles later the aircraft is not climbing and due to the MVA the controller issues the altitude again to climb. The pilot realizes there is an equipment problem  with the TCAS and returns to the airport.
Airline arrival ground crew reported Dangerous Goods containers not securely loaded at departure point.
Air carrier ground personnel reported that HAZMAT cargo was mistakenly loaded on aircraft.

A Captain flying the ATL VYPPR 1 RNAV Runway 10 with a 250 kt constraint; reported being told after landing to call for a possible deviation after all apparent arrival constraints were met. Later; he learned 40-50 aircraft that day received the same warning for an undetermined reason.
SFO Tower controllers reported a loss of separation. One of the reporters told the Local to turn one of the aircraft to get them separated.
Flight crew reported that following multiple speed adjustments per ATC's request; they misprogrammed the aircraft; which resulted in not satisfying the mandatory crossing altitudes.
CE-560 pilot reported returning to the departure airport after shutting down the left engine because of low oil pressure following a bird strike shortly after takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported Airbus aircraft are difficult to see on the ground due to strobe lights only turning on after becoming airborne.
Flight Attendant reported passenger was non-compliant with face mask policy.
B737 Captain reported confusion about their speed assignment due to the Controller using incorrect phraseology.
CE-560 flight crew reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft.
Technician reported that the #1 engine HCU was locked out by the previous shift and was not documented; subsequently the aircraft flew with the lockout pin still in place.
Passenger turbojet flight reported of an unexpected holding for the destination resulting in landing with minimum fuel.

Chicago TRACON Instructor reported that an aircraft was turned off final; into a lower Minimum Vectoring Altitude area. Aircraft was then instructed to a different heading in order to exit the lower MVA area.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain's authority was overridden by operations.
A B737 first officer reported that while taxiing in; a fast moving tug crossed without looking. The tug caused them to brake firmly and immediately avoid impact.
Gate Agent reported concerns with social distancing and lack of manpower in their work environment.
Lead Technician reported that an ETOPS check was missed due to lack of proper supervision and workload with previous aircraft.
CRJ-200 Captain reported a track deviation occurred departing ATL due in part to distraction related to wake turbulence from preceding MD-88.
MIA TRACON Controller reported vectoring several aircraft conducting instrument approaches off course to avoid a VFR aircraft they were not in communication with.
Flight attendant reported that they inadvertently let passenger bring onboard a smart bag that contained a 'non-removable' lithium battery; which is violation of company policy.
Air carrier First Officer reported that despite multiple PAs; passengers would not comply with face mask policy.
B747 Captain reported notification by company audit department of FAR Flight Time Limitation violation due to company monitoring system error.
Captain encountered conflict with departing aircraft.
A320 First Officer momentarily lost control exiting the runway on a wet surface when the nose wheel skidded in the turn.
Air Carrier Maintenance personnel reported an incident suggesting that the MEL be revised to clarify instructions to flight crew.
Air carrier Captain reported the city is dumping sand in the gate Safety Zone area at ISP.

IAH Local Controller (LC) describes a situation where the other LC and other final monitor had a loss of separation; due to the final monitor not slowing the aircraft and/or the other local not sending the aircraft around. The other local asked the Front Line Manager what to do; but got no response.

ZSE Controller reports two aircraft joining final with the second aircraft being a little faster; but on a visual approach. The Controller has the second aircraft do a 360 to gain more spacing but does not assign an altitude to maintain. Controller realizes this and asks pilot if he has ground contact and can maintain clearance. Pilot says yes and the aircraft continues into the airport
A320 First Officer reported MEL may or may not have been applied correctly as associated with the fuel system.
Flight crew reported an in-flight battery smoke and fumes in the main cabin from a passenger iPad's; they contained the device; and continued to the destination.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a momentary flap speed limit exceedance following a possible wake turbulence encounter.
A321 First Officer reported performing two air turn backs that were caused by a malfunctioning Cabin Pressure Control System.
An SJU Controller failed to issue a full route clearance after missing the note in the remarks of the flight plan. Aircraft became airborne on a closed airway.
ZHU Center Controller reported issuing a clearance to a helicopter that was below the Minimum IFR Altitude and not procedurally separated from an arriving helicopter.
Airbus A300 pilot reported departing with greater than 400 lbs in the center tank while still in payload mode.
B787 flight crew reported high speed RTO.
A Center Controller reported an IFR departure did not use the published departure procedure and was below the Minimum Enroute Altitude.
Center Controller reported an altitude excursion and referenced wearing a mask may have muffled the transmission.
Piper Archer pilot reported an airborne conflict with a B737-800 that had apparently descended below the MSP Class B airspace on arrival to MSP.
Air Carrier First Officer reported course deviation because he did not notice the nonflying pilot not execute an assigned route from ATC.
A TRACON Departure Controller reported a departing aircraft made an unusually shallow turn on departure and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger would not comply with the face mask policy even though the passenger had a special exemption to take it off for periods at a time.
Air Carrier Captain flying CRJ-200 aircraft reports unsafe tailwind condition during approach to landing.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported short staffing and high workload was a factor in the failure to advise two flights about freezing rain at their destination.
An A300 Captain reported numerous equipment malfunctions in flight. After landing; water was observed in the electronics compartment.
CE-680 First Officer on short final to BOS Runway 04L reported encountering jet blast from an Airbus departing Runway 09.
A possible CFTT event departing RDM was avoided by the crew through good situational awareness and an expeditious climb.
Air carrier Ramp Lead reported aircraft ground return to correct Hazmat cargo for air shipment.

A flight of two aircraft at 2;000 feet were told after landing at their destination they had violated SMO airspace by 1 mile. Both aircraft had Garmin GPS equipment and saw themselves remaining outside SMO airspace.
Flight Attendant reported inadvertently not briefing exit row passengers due to other passengers making special seating requests.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported having a reaction to fumes coming from a passenger carry-on bag; and questioned the safety of relocating the bag to the checked baggage compartment of the aircraft.
E175 Captain reported experiencing severe turbulence during initial departure from ABQ.
Reporter was concerned that there weren't safety masks or respirators available for use.
BUR Tower Controller reports of lack of procedures for go-around aircraft which often leads to airspace violations and possible losses of separation.
Air Carrier Captain reported similar sounding fix names AAILE/FLAIL caused confusion and a route deviation during arrival to DEN airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported a slight taxiway excursion causing the aircraft to run over a taxiway light at LFPG Airport.
Pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft.
Air Carrier FA reported being pressured to depart with an aft jump seat problem that had safety implications that the company was unwilling to address.
B737 Captain reported that during preflight Maintenance was contacted to discuss a maintenance discrepancy; after pressure on pilots to continue with flight aircraft was refused.
B737-700 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing DCA in trail of an A321.
EMB-175 First Officer reported issues with company load planning accuracy.
UAV operator reported a TFR miscommunication that resulted in an airborne conflict with an aircraft.
GA pilot reported compliance with unsafe ODP with turbulence and wind required a turn greater than 45 degrees resulted in spatial disorientation.
Air Carrier flight crew turned onto an incorrect gate lead-in line when having difficulty seeing the line during a night time arrival.
Air taxi Captain reported the Avidyne IFD 540 automatically switched frequencies without the flight crew's input or knowledge when he opened up the Avidyne IFD 100 app on his iPad.
Single pilot reported deviated from IFR clearance by flying direct course to destination instead of following the ODP on departure without ATC authorization.
Air carrier flight crew reported an overweight landing.
B767 Captain reported encountering unforecasted and undetected moderate turbulence at FL240 that resulted in injuries to three flight attendants.
A321 Captain reported executing a go-around because the preceding arrival did not exit the runway in time.
Air carrier Captain reported they are not being provided with proper disinfectant wipes and PPE.

ZOB ARTCC Traffic Management Supervisor reported that significant weather traffic flow strategy implemented by the FAA Command Center was insufficient to control the amount of traffic allowed into their airspace.

Aircraft was deviating for weather from facility A airspace into another Facility B airspace. Facility B controllers delegated some of their airspace to Facility A to accommodate weather deviations. This caused Facility A to work airplanes 40 to 60 miles in Facility B airspace. Facility B Supervisor overrode their controllers and required Facility A to make individual Point Outs. Facility B declines to take handoffs and work these aircraft. This increases facility A's workload greatly. These facilities are in different countries further complicating a possible solution.
Light Transport aircraft Captain reported a track/heading deviation due to similar sounding fixes CONLE and COLIN located on the same airway.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported an unstable approach during icing conditions due to weather and multiple crew errors during the approach; subsequent go-around; and second approach.
Balloon pilot reported that a wind gust on landing caused contact with power lines resulting in minor damage to the envelope fabric but no injuries.

Flight Attendant reported having a headache; elevated temperature; nausea and feeling fatigued during the flight. The crew was concerned it could be COVID-19 symptoms; so the Flight Attendant called in sick for the next flight.
Ground Personnel reported shear pins breaking on tow bar; resulting in the aircraft striking a parked vehicle.
Instructor pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft on final at an airport with the Tower closed at the time of the event.
Salt Lake City TRACON Controller reported a military jet had descended below the MVA.
MDW Tower and C90 TRACON Controller reported that an aircraft departed MDW and was instructed to turn and contact Departure.  Aircraft did not turn and was not on either frequency.
Embraer E50P pilot reported that the aircraft tracked to the right upon landing.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence; while in a 5-mile trail of an A300 on approach to SDF.
B757 flight crew reported encountering severe weather after takeoff.
CE-750 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while in cruise at FL240 12 miles in trail of an A320.
M20 pilot reported experiencing an NMAC in the pattern at FIT airport.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a ramp incursion at SEA airport.  First Officer stated more detail is needed in the airport charts.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft departed an airport requesting an IFR clearance but the pilot was not aware they were adjacent to restricted areas and heading into them.

A C172 student pilot reported unusual indications in the right fuel gauge and discovered after landing that the right fuel cap was missing. The student pilot concluded that it had not been secured adequately prior to flight.
C172 pilot reported an NMAC with two other aircraft in a traffic pattern.
Air carrier Captain reported having to ferry a flight for storage due the COVID-19 pandemic without correct maintenance release documentation.
Flight crew reported a chart showed PAPI installed at an MMU airport runway. When crew questioned ATC; ATC advised no PAPI has ever been installed at this runway.
Pilot Instructor reported a NMAC with a UAV.
A321 Captain reported a near mid-air collision on approach to PHX airport.
SCT Controller reported of being inundated by Gorman VOR departures. Controller reported asking others to stop giving him these departures; but it was not done.
C182 pilot deviated from cleared altitude while performing flight tests of ADS-B systems new to the aircraft.
Pilot reported misinterpreting MEL and applied the incorrect MEL after failures of the Attitude Indicator on the Primary Flight Display.
Flight attendant reported passengers did not comply with the face mask policy during flight.
Flight Crew taxiing aircraft encounter excessive Foreign Object Debris (FOD) in ramp area.

A C208 flight crew inbound to PHNL requested a Freeway Visual approach but soon learned that the altitudes required for doing so overwater were incompatible with their requirement to remain within gliding distance of land when operating over water.

A crew had difficulty contacting ATC for a clearance while on the ground at COE and finally received clearance through Lockheed Martin Flight Service. After departure they were unable to contact GEG Departure on the 133.5; the assigned frequency. They finally used GEG charted frequency; 133.35.
ERJ-175 Flight Attendant reported calling in 'not fit to fly' because of fatigue; but was forced to work under threat of punitive action by the company.
CL605 pilot reported initial training was incomplete and inadequate.
CRP Controller reports of having to hand off every inbound and approach going into an adjacent facility manually. Both facilities have STARS but they do not communicate.

ZLA controllers reported believing they had handed off an aircraft to the adjacent ARTCC; but the handoff had not been completed. The adjacent Center Controller had to restrict the aircraft's climb for opposite direction traffic.
B747 First Officer reported Chief Pilot authorized two deadheading crewmembers on flight that allows only required crew due to Class 1 explosives on board.
C172 pilot reported a porpoised landing that resulted in a nose landing gear collapse and propeller strike.

Pilot reports of being under flight following then told to contact the Tower. Tower controllers advise pilot of Class D Violation. Pilot thought since he was under flight following the Tower would have known his position.
Air Carrier Captain reported receiving a terrain warning while on a visual approach into Las Vegas.
P80 TRACON Controller reported an arriving aircraft responded to a TCAS RA for a VFR aircraft that had the arriving aircraft in sight and was maintaining visual separation.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving conflicting information for a departure procedure from a NOTAM.
An air carrier crew reported taking evasive action from an aircraft which mistakenly entered ORD Runway 28R; during takeoff.

Air carrier Dispatcher noted that the computer generated altitude for the alternate airport route was below the MEA. Further inspection revealed that a step climb was planned above the MEA but this information was not transmitted to the crew.
B737 Captain reported departing the high speed taxiway after clearing the runway due to ice and snow on the surface.
Controller reported previous IFR released to Tower made unexpected climb towards holding traffic resulted in an airborne conflict.
Air Carrier First Officer reported unexpected severe turbulence encounter during our climbout of BWI airport.
EMB flight crew reported overshooting a cleared altitude after encountering wake turbulence on arrival into DFW.
A Pilot reported they departed with an IFR Clearance and when they checked on with Departure Control they were told to maintain VFR and their IFR clearance was not honored by ATC.
ROC Tower Controller and a CRJ-700 flight crew reported problems with the XEROX6 departure which caused an airborne conflict.
NCT Controller reported an air carrier responded to a TCAS RA for a VFR aircraft that had been instructed to maintain visual separation.
Air Carrier Captain reported concern over a note on Jeppesen charts that states aircraft will be exposed to direct radiation.
Air carrier flight crew reported they flew off course when cleared direct DRAKE which is the name and same pronounciation of a VOR in Arizona and an airspace fix in Colorado.
B767-300 Captain reported returning to the gate after the crew's CO2 detector alarmed because of a large quantity of dry ice that had been loaded under waiver.
Air carrier Captain reported noticing a discrepancy on a GPS point that has different altitudes on MSLP airport STAR and approach plates.

A B777 Relief Pilot at the controls and Captain resting in the bunk reported a brief; severe; turbulence jolt which injured several flight attendants. The pilots were surprised by the lack of warning and mid north latitude at which the event occurred.
P80 TRACON Final Controller forgot to clear an aircraft for an approach and had a loss of separation with parallel runway traffic based on based on a new interpretation of rules.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that two flights had been routed around weather were re-routed by ATC into the weather areas.
B737 flight crew reported aircraft wiper blades on many next generation aircraft are shorter than the metal frame.
Pilot while under flight following is not sure if he penetrated a Class D airspace.  Pilot described a possible departure procedure to help with this problem.
A321 pilot reported the LAS Tower frequency is incorrect on the approach chart.
One facility handed two aircraft on converging courses at the same altitude off to another facility without initiating any action to ensure separation.
Technician reported that aircraft overflew required reinspections due to improper computer entries.
C180 pilot reported a loss of control and runway excursion while landing in gusty conditions.
B737 Captain reports using his Captain's emergency authority to avoid turbulence associated with a thunderstorm; beyond what the Controller would authorize due to Radar coverage.
Air Carrier Ramp Personnel reported aircraft ground gate return to correct Dangerous Goods cargo loading errors.
Agricultural aircraft pilot reported that the aircraft hit power lines during spraying operations.

An Approach Controller had a jet following a slower single engine prop airplane on final approach to the same runway. The controller felt there was sufficient space between arrivals to account for the jets' faster speed. After shipping the first slower aircraft to the Tower he observed the speed of that aircraft reduce drastically; more than normal for that type of aircraft. The controller issued the preceding traffic to the jet in an attempt to achieve Visual Separation. The jet acknowledged the traffic call without stating they saw it and switched frequency to the Tower on their own. The Approach Controller saw the spacing reduce to less than minimum required while both aircraft were on the tower frequency. Both aircraft landed.
SCT ATC Front Line Manager reported losing all data tags on the Stadium Radar scope. The Front Line Manager reported the confidence level in the equipment is low due to a decrease in reliability.

CL300 First Officer departing Runway 16R at VNY experiences an airborne conflict; with general aviation aircraft that had just performed a touch and go on Runway 16L; at 1;700 feet. The Tower apparently did not advise the crew of the potential conflict and a TCAS RA is generated as the crew levels at 1;700 feet and turns on the NUAL8 Departure.

A pilot and co-pilot report of being cleared for takeoff. The co-pilot reports rotate and at the same time the control tower tells this crew to cancel take off clearance. While stopping; the aircraft sustains a flat spot on the left main tire and a rupture to the right main tire. The pilot announces they are disabled on the runway; emergency equipment meets the aircraft and the aircraft eventually gets towed back to the FBO.
B737 flight crew reported diverting after encountering a severe mountain wave over the Southern Rockies.
The Captain was uncomfortable being assigned to fly as an ERJ 170 First Officer with minimal experience and training in that position.
Pilot reported a missing logbook entry was discovered after takeoff.
ZSU Controller describes an area in the vicinity of TNCM airport with a lot of VFR traffic and area in the departure and inbound gate for IFR arrivals.
B737 flight crew reported a momentary loss of control climbing through FL360 after encountering severe wake turbulence from an MD-11 at FL390.
SLC Local Controller reported a fixed wing aircraft landed on a dirt road in the Bravo airspace surface area. The Controller had no prior information that the aircraft would be landing.
Pilot reports of DCA controllers 'climbing into my cockpit' and how they are telling the pilots various things that he feels they shouldn't be doing.
BE-99 pilot reported a track heading deviation after an evasive maneuver to avoid a flock of birds near BTG VOR.
TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict and cited distraction caused by having to wear a face mask.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another small aircraft that ATC issued traffic for and was displayed on the reporter's ADS-B.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an unstabilized approach resulting in a go-around. Unfamiliarity with the airport and rustiness from lack of flying were cited as contributing factors.
B737 flight crew reported receiving a late crossing restriction from ATC that ultimately resulted in a TCAS RA.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported physiological symptoms resulting in a ground return to the gate. Fumes were caused by non-hazardous cargo spill.

An aircraft on a VFR Flight Plan cancelled flight following and advised Center they were turning around for weather to land. The ARTCC controllers amended the flight plan to route the aircraft flight plan back to its departure airport. The destination Tower did not notice the revision or the software did not process the revision to the destination Tower. They correctly assumed the aircraft was overdue. An ALNOT was issued and search procedures for the aircraft were initiated. ARTCC Supervisors and controllers who worked the aircraft at the ARTCC researched sector recordings and radar display replays. They discovered the aircraft had cancelled on the ARTCC frequency and returned to an airport other than its original departure airport.
EMB-175 Captain reported miscommunication with ground crew led to the aircraft rolling backwards before control was regained.

A pilot reported that BDR has recently completed extension of Taxiway A and the addition of a runway safety area for the approach end of Runway 24. Reporter stated the Airport Diagram needs to be updated to reflect the modification.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B777 on approach to ATL that resulted in an uncommanded 45 degree bank.
Center Controller reported an adjacent Center going to ATC Zero due to COVID-19 cleaning and having aircraft that were not allowed on filed routes.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported improper belly loading on MD-11 that could allow a load shift if not caught before departure.
EMB-175 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into LAX that resulted in a minor injury to a Flight Attendant.

DAL Tower controller reports of an airspace violation by a D10 controller. The D10 controller then has his aircraft become a conflict for the DAL controller's aircraft. The D10 controller wanted his aircraft to be able to do a downtown tour; but due to the runway configuration at DAL it was not approved.
EMB-175 Captain reported refusing a trip due to severe weather conditions.
Technician reported concerns over Manager ignoring company policies and pressuring an unsafe; expedited Job Card completion.

B737 flight crew reported severe turbulence during cruise.

Washington Center Controllers reported of an unsafe condition when an aircraft was turned the wrong direction after coordination which led to a loss of separation.
The flight crew of an airbus A319 reported that during approach they got a GPWS terrain call while they were one dot fly up on the glideslope.
Mechanic reported finding two ELT's out of date during a routine inspection.
CRJ900 flight crew reported taking evasive action on a visual approach to avoid traffic deviating off course on the parallel runway.
MSP Local Controller reported an aircraft hesitated on takeoff roll causing a loss of separation during Converging Runway Operations.
TWF Approach controllers approved an aircraft entering their airspace at an altitude below their Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier flight attendants reported that a passenger was vaping in the lavatory and was not wearing a personal protective mask during the flight.
A Simulator Technician reported that an Air Handler Unit's 480 Volt input feed line had a wire that was not secured to its connector.
Air Carrier Captain reported of similar sounding intersections that caused confusion for their flight to AUS.
PA28 Pilot reported a string of self-described 'poor aeronautical decisions' that resulted in VFR flight into IMC.
Ramp employee reported company is spraying the break-rooms and other areas while personnel are still in the area.
Air carrier Captain reported removing the face mask to drink coffee after completing pre-flight.

Arrival procedures within the DC area and into BWI are restricted to limited types of operating regulations. ATC cleared flight for the RAVNN6 STAR BKW transition into BWI normally prohibited to part 135 operators.

An Airbus Flight Attendant complained about passenger illness; migraine headaches; cabin stuffiness as well as feeling sweaty; clammy; and lethargic following a company mandated air conditioning system inflight airflow management policy change.
FAI Controller reported strong wind aloft resulting in a military transport aircraft on vector to enter a higher MVA.

A B737 flight crew used their company's procedure to calculate takeoff data for a high altitude airport. They subsequently chose a better flap setting for the runway and still used all the runway to become airborne; almost hitting the runway end identifier lights. They reported that the situation was not uncommon to use all the runway to become airborne when using the company generated takeoff data.
Air Carrier flight crew reported navigating to the wrong fix due to similar sounding fixes (GOATZ and COAZT) on the same RNAV STAR.
Large Transport flight crew; given a descent from 8000 feet to 6000 feet by Approach Control; were told at 6800 feet that they were supposed to be at 8000 feet.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a windshear warning on takeoff and failing to perform the Wind Shear Maneuver.
Lead Crew Chief questioned the validity of changing and down-grading priorities after work has begun.
ZLC Controller reported an aircraft flew off course due to GPS jamming and went below the minimum terrain clearance altitudes.
A CRJ-900 First Officer reported a fuel burn of approximately 2;000 pounds over planned because climb temperatures were between ISA+15 and ISA+25 coupled with ATC delays.
A CRJ-200 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence shortly after takeoff at ATL in trail of a B737 that triggered a momentary stall warning.
Pilot reports of not being sure if he was cleared into PHX Class Bravo airspace by controller.
Lubbock Tower Controller reported an unsafe opposite direction operation.
First Officer reported being instructed to secure the number two engine for a single engine taxi to the gate; but accidentally shut off both engines.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy during flight.

An ERJ-170 First Officer reported a deferred maintenance item exceeded the days allowed between inspections. At an outstation; the Company had Contract Maintenance further deferred the item without an inspection. The crew protested; but felt forced to fly.

DHC-8-200 Captain deplaned passengers from the aircraft and took a boarding delay when a checked bag was on the ramp and passenger gate checked bags were being placed alongside it on the same cart. A Ground Security Coordinator was sent out and after a full security search the aircraft was reboarded.
Helicopter pilot reported that a misunderstanding between Tower and a fixed wing aircraft resulted in a NMAC.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a late approach clearance from ATC forcing them to conduct a missed approach.
Flight Crew reported that during taxi for takeoff; ATC cleared aircraft for takeoff with a B747 on short final; resulting in a go around for the B747.
A Maintenance Inspector reported that a Vendor did not accomplish the repairs required due to lack of information.
CRJ-200 Captain reported an encounter with severe turbulence resulted in momentary loss of control on arrival into IAD.
Ground Controller reported a procedural error that resulted in delaying a departing aircraft.

D10 TRACON Controller reported a Wake Turbulence Remnant issue that Front Line Manager brought to his attention after the fact. Controller reported to work; Front Line Manager had him review a replay and was told that there was a wake remnant issue. Controller wanted to know what a wake remnant issue was since nothing was in the FAA Order 7110.65.
B737 flight Crew reported that the SJC TECKY THREE RNAV has a speed requirement that contradicts speed regulations below Class B airspace.
Flight Attendant reported severe injury attributed to pilot not giving weather/turbulence briefing.
JNU Tower Controller in Charge reported Local Control was not issuing sufficient traffic information about helicopters on a VFR route to an air carrier on final approach.
C-172 Single Pilot reported a critical ground conflict while taxing across the runway with a aircraft executing a departure over the runway.
EC145 Medical crew member reported PIC failed to follow company policy; entered known IMC.
C-172 Pilot Not Flying reported encountering an NMAC event during cruise while on flight following.
Flight Attendant opted to practice social distancing by sitting in a First Class passenger seat instead of the assigned jumpseat next to another Flight Attendant who was coughing.
Air Carrier Captain reported ATC turning them through a very small area which had moderate turbulence and mountain waves due to surrounding weather.
EMB flight crew reported an excursion from assigned altitude and airspeed due to losing control of aircraft in severe turbulence.
Technician reported outbound Captain was pressuring him to allegedly falsify a logbook entry to avoid off loading passengers due to a malfunctioning passenger door.

MIA TRACON Controller reported FLL Tower has a constantly practiced procedure of allowing aircraft to line up and wait while traffic is on a short enough final to require a go-around. They then have issued a takeoff clearance which creates a conflict on climbout.
B767 Captain reported that a faulty headset resulted in a communication breakdown with the ground crew during pushback.
Air carrier Captain reported PAPI availability was not published on the RIC ILS to Runway 34 approach chart.
Air carrier First Officer reported no Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) or sanitizing equipment were provided for a round trip flight.
EMB175 Captain reported multiple service failures at SAN including passengers waiting to board aircraft and standing near the fuel service truck.
ZMA Controller reported confusion relating whether two aircraft could self-separate from each other.
ZOA Center controllers reported confusion and misunderstanding regarding the lowest assignable altitude.
A Center Controller reported a pilot reported severe turbulence that caused an injury.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported a very high workload and management pressure interfered with his duties.
B737 Captain reported that a go-around and diversion resulted in landing with low fuel.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was removed from the flight for non-compliance with face mask policy.

Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation due to distraction. The pilot crew was listening to a Flight Attendant describe details of a non-compliant passenger regarding the face mask policy.

C208 Captain reports encountering unforecast freezing rain approaching his destination; with similar conditions throughout a widespread area. The reporter elects to continue to destination with the help of ATC to achieve a rapid descent and approach.
A Passenger reported they observed an opposite direction aircraft in conflict with their flight.
Barnstormer replica pilot reported losing control of the aircraft on takeoff roll.

Airline Dispatcher reported flight crew refused to transport Dangerous Goods that did not require flight crew notification. FOM directs flight crew to be provided Hazmat documentation when transporting Dangerous Goods.
A Flight Attendant reported passengers were boarded an hour and a half before the pilots were on board and were using a lavatory that was supposed to be out of service.
Air carrier flight crew reported many delays encountered to get a flight going that eventually departed the next day.
B737 Captain reported that a fuel filter problem resulted in an aircraft refusal.
Flight Attendant reported an issue with the service carts whereby the braking mechanism does not work.

A first officer reported unsafe practices for flying night-time approaches into MKK airport using the published MKK VOR Approach. The practice of using radar altimeter for terrain clearance at night reduced adequate altitude clearance above terrain of varying heights.
Aerostar 600A pilot reported encountering severe icing conditions after takeoff from a Midwestern airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an RA inbound to Newark and cited Controller workload as a contributing factor.
ZAU Center Controller reported their session was out of control due to traffic and no flow control from the Traffic Management Unit.
Center Traffic Management Specialist reported two different agencies had scheduled the same Warning Area at the same time.
Corporate pilot reported they lost communication with ATC and could not cancel IFR and then had to go around for a wind shear at an uncontrolled airport.

Air Carrier flight crew on the SERFR1 Arrival into SFO; descended below 5;000 feet enroute to the MENLO fix prior to being cleared for the Quiet Bridge visual approach. Crew noted that are different crossing restrictions for the MENLO fix on the SERFR1 Arrival and the Quiet Bridge Visual Approach.

Cessna Citation Excel flight crew reported evidence of a brake system leak during the preflight inspection even though the issue had been previously documented and reported as corrected by maintenance personnel.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported finding a large centipede in the aft galley on a flight from MROC.
Airbus Captain reported an NMAC after departure from SJU. He received a RA to climb which could not be complied with due to aircraft performance limitations.
Pilot reported a runway incursion due to communication issue between Ground Control and Pilot in addition to intersecting departure ends of two runways.
CRJ200 Captain reported a close encounter with a UAV at 4000 feet on the downwind leg for Runway 27R at ORD.
CRJ flight crew reported they forgot to remove one of the pitot tube covers before takeoff but were able to continue to their destination.

A B757 pilot described the design error and conflict between the SFO DYAMD RNAV Arrival 7;000 foot ARCHI constraint below Class B and the FMS BRIDGE Visual and the QUIET BRIDGE Visual 8;000 foot ARCHI constraint.
Air carrier Captain reported two flights with similar callsigns departing the same airport around the same time created confusion in communication between controllers and flight crew.
B737 Captain is confused by the MAYSA3 RNAV SID departing PHX; in that there is a top altitude of 8;000 feet; but in the initial climb instructions it says to cross GOALY at or below 9;000 feet.
SLC ARTCC Controller reported that an error occurred because he did not enter the interim altitude in the data block of a departure that then entered a lower MOCA/MVA/MIA/MSA/MEA.
SJC Tower Local Controller reported an arrival 2 miles from the airport was not established on final and TRACON relayed a Low Altitude Alert to them to issue to the pilot.

ORL Tower Controller reported of an unfamiliar operation that led to an operational error. TRACON did not want to change runways but due to winds the Tower Controller took the aircraft and one ended up circling to the runway and then causing a loss of separation.

A Flight Attendant reports that young children cannot be properly secured in the new ACRO passenger seats installed on their Airbus aircraft. The same seatbelts from the removed seats were reinstalled with the new seats; but will not tighten up enough to accommodate the increase curvature of the new seatbacks.
B787-900 Captain reported directing Hazmat cargo of Lithium Ion batteries removed from aircraft prior to departure.
Air carrier Captain reported failure to call for the Before Push checklist; then also failing to call for the Before Taxi checklist; citing lack of flying due to COVID-19 cutbacks as contributing.
Flight Instructor reported another light aircraft overflew him by approximately 30 feet during his landing roll at N40 airport.
A TRACON Controller reported providing instructions for a military formation flight break up in accordance with FAA H 7110.65 but did not relay instructions through the flight lead.

Air carrier pilot reported of numerous go-arounds that he has had in the past few months. Pilot reported that he would like ATC to give him some time so the aircraft can get configured before giving instructions.
A GA pilot reported his feet became stuck in the rudder pedals causing a runway excursion during takeoff.
Flight Attendants reported a passenger complained that another passenger did not wear a face mask during the flight.
A320 flight crew reported departing on a transcontinental flight with insufficient fuel due to flight plan not including baggage or passengers.
A319 flight crew reported a track deviation occurred during recovery from a wake turbulence encounter.

A ramp ground support tractor driver describes his efforts to safely push a company outbound B757-200 off of their west facing terminal gates past another air carrier's south facing gates to access Taxiway Bravo. Although cleared for continued pushback; an inbound A321 aircraft suddenly appeared; taxiing into the ramp area narrowly clearing; missing the back of their B757 aircraft.
Pilot reported ESN Tower advertises a GPS approach when the airport has RNAV (GPS) approaches.  Pilot reported this makes it difficult to look up the approach.
A B737-800 pilot reported they refused an aircraft for maintenance issues even though the Chief Pilot and Maintenance tried to persuade them to take the aircraft.
GA instructor reported improper BVI METAR weather dissemination via AISR. Instructor reported AWOS 3P is not auto processing and measures to correct this have not resolved the issue.
ABQ Tower Controller reported their radar went out of service for unexpectedly.

Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a heading deviation while on the RNAV Z 28R approach to FLL due to procedure confusion and communication issues with ATC.  The crew stated high traffic volume hindered the ability to clarify the situation with ATC.
ZBW ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft not flying the Obstacle Departure Procedure and entering an area of lower terrain.
Aventura AVII pilot reported forgetting to use the landing checklist to prevent the gear up landing.
A319 pilot reported making a mistake with the aircraft's packs because of an unclear checklist.
Air carrier Gate Agent reported concern about the safety of gate-checking luggage that has internal lithium ion batteries.
GSO TRACON Controller reported improperly turning an aircraft onto final with another aircraft on approach to the parallel runway.
BTV Tower Controller reported clearing an aircraft onto a closed taxiway.

ZMP Controller report of having to cancel an approach clearance into an uncontrolled airport because of an IFR departure that had missed its void time. The Controllers did not have a loss of separation and recommended that they issue a hold for release in the future.
Baton Rouge TRACON controllers reported an unsafe operation relating to opposite direction operations.
Ground Personnel reported assisting Ramp Lead in correcting Departure Staging Guide (DSTG) Hazmat cargo loading errors in order to legally dispatch flight.
Center Controller reported an aircraft attempting to land on the wrong runway resulting in another aircraft executing a go-around.

A maintenance person replaced the Auto Pilot roll actuator on an Airbus A300 which required a preliminary rig check for the ailerons. After replacement of the actuator the ailerons appeared out of rig.
BE-90 Captain reported he deliberately departed the taxiway to avoid a collision with a Citation that was lined up for landing on that taxiway.

A law enforcement UAV pilot reported operating a drone after civil twilight in violation of FAR 107.29. He and a fellow UAV operator were both night qualified and operating in support of an active law enforcement action.
Air carrier flight crew reported being vectored off route toward Tehran ADIZ; placing the flight at risk for penetrating the restricted airspace.
Air carrier Captain reported the difficulties experienced with Dispatch and Clearance Delivery as they requested an alternate route to avoid thunderstorms.
Captain reported concerns about safety of flight issues due to ATC controllers being under staffed and overworked while having to work multiple frequencies.
Technician reported insufficient training as the factor in not following procedures during a horizontal stabilizer jackscrew lubrication.
Air Carrier flight crew reported that a low altitude warning activated during the approach into FSD. Crew took evasive action and landed without incident.
BWI Tower Controller reported allowing an aircraft to land while vehicles were on the runway.

TMB Local Controller puts an aircraft into position on an active runway in between an aircraft rolling out and one on final. The Controller then notices another unknown aircraft approaching opposite direction; he tells the aircraft that is in position to exit the runway and then the arriving aircraft lands on a parallel runway. Controller did not know anything about the opposite direction arrival.
Helicopter pilot reported an encounter with extreme atmospheric heat conditions.
A319 flight crew reported that an erroneous altimeter setting resulted in a GPWS warning and a go around.

P80 Controller reports of an airborne conflict that was saved by the instructor. The reporter also states that the positions were combined and caused an unnecessary situation when the final position should have been open.
Air Carrier Captain reported an issue with the charting of a taxiway at LGA.
A90 controller reports of ZBW not following the LOA reference speeds on aircraft descending into A90 airspace.
A ZJX Center Controller reported requesting D-Sides to assist but there is routinely insufficient staffing to provide assistance.
TRACON Controller reported a separation issue relate to wake turbulence separation and reclassification of separation.
Air Carrier Captain reported multiple go-arounds due to wind shear alerts from ATC at DEN; he finally decided to land on runway 26 with a crosswind very close to limits.
Technician reported being distracted and frustrated caused him to forget to set the parking brake on an aircraft tug; causing the tug to roll into the aircraft; causing ground damage.
Helicopter pilot reported a NMAC during departure with another helicopter.
Pilot reported concerns with some flight schools not requiring face mask to be worn during flights.
C-172 Flight Instructor reported executing an uneventful go around due to a critical ground conflict with golf cart during final approach.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at 14;000 ft on descent into LAX that resulted in left and right rolls and a 7 degree pitch down.

AUS TRACON trainee reported a VFR aircraft was attempting to contact them for service; but they were too busy to acknowledge the aircraft. The aircraft climbed into an aircraft on approach. The Final Controller had to vector and climb the aircraft on approach off their course to avoid the VFR traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported volcanic activity while airborne.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a speed deviation during departure.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence departing PHX in trail of an A321.
ZSE Controller in Charge reported observing a sector with an aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported skidding 50 feet on ice on Taxiway F past Runway 28 at ROC. The aircraft was subsequently towed to the runway for a normal departure.

A SAV Tracon Controller accepts a handoff on an aircraft that has an ILS in the scratch pad area. The ILS refers to the ILS for runway 28; but 10 is in use. The Controller assumes coordination has been done; hands off aircraft; and flashes the aircraft to Tower. The Controller then realizes; with aircraft on a 10 mile final; that coordination hasn't been done and then coordinates with the Tower. Tower advises there is no problem; they see the scratch pad. The Tower does not have automatic releases.
Flight Attendant in charge reported an inadvertent slide deployment.

PA34 pilot reported that supercooled liquid droplet icing conditions resulted in a loss of all flight instruments except for the standby attitude indicator and magnetic compass. Pilot exited icing conditions with ATC assistance and successfully diverted.
Denver TRACON Controllers and air carrier First Officer reported multiple losses of separation and airspace violations.

A pilot and flight instructor were surprised when ATC cleared an air carrier to land on the crossing runway during their final approach. The flight instructor reportedly took evasive action on the ground to mitigate a perceived ground conflict.

A regional jet flight crew reported experiencing a minimum fuel condition due to ongoing delays at the destination airport; which were attributed to multiple runway changes. They also cited their company's fuel policy as a contributing factor to their fuel situation.
Phenom 300 flight crew reported a NMAC on departure while following ATC instructions.
A pilot was cleared to cross the runway and hesitantly began crossing but detected an approaching aircraft and stopped just over the hold short line. The approaching aircraft executed a go-around.
GA pilot reported a recurring (ADS-B) Automatic Dependent Surveillance - Broadcast reception failure below 3500 feet seemingly due to antennas blocking the ground stations' signal.
Maintenance Lead on CRJ-200 observed improper aircraft jacking procedures during maintenance visit.
A distracted BE35 owner/student and his instructor incurred a runway while taxiing for takeoff as the instructor copied and read back a full route clearance.
Center Controller reported a loss of separation event due to being distracted while cleaning equipment.
Single Pilot reported a possible deviation from the clearance and problems communicating with the controller while airborne.
B737 flight crew reported continuing to land with wind shear warning to avoid weather and airborne conflict on go around.
ZME Center controller reported confusion in regard to the authorized method to separate a military refueling flight.
Center Controller reported the Area was not staffed according to directives and had insufficient oversight by Traffic Management.
Flight Attendant reported several passengers did not complying with face mask policy during flight.
M20 pilot reported a NMAC with a glider; followed by a G1000 malfunction.

A B737 encountered 19 knot gusts on short final which caused a flap blow up from 30 to 25. The EGPWS TOO LOW alert sounded but the First Officer thought flap asymmetry. After landing the flaps were cycled with normal results.
ZSE Controller reported of an aircraft that went to an incorrect fix causing conflict with arrivals. The fix names were almost identical and off by one letter.
Air carrier flight crew reported an altitude deviation during arrival resulting in an airborne conflict.
TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation.

A B737-800 First Officer on a walk around found the Contract baggage handlers had loaded bags above the red bag restricted line which blocked the Smoke Detectors / Fire Suppression system. Bags were off loaded; but on a return trip; the same over height baggage load developed. Better education to mitigate this dangerous situation.
Flight Attendant reported a Gate Agent filled seats that were not supposed to be filled.
B737-800 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of an A321 on arrival into PHX.
Ramp person reported a close encounter with an aircraft inbound to the same gate from which he was pushing an aircraft back for departure.

Chicago Center (ZAU) Controllers reported a situation where a military airspace is supposed to be going hot in a few minutes. Another sector had an aircraft going into the airspace and the Controllers questioned the other Controller. The other Controller advised them that they have extended the cold time for 10 minutes to allow this aircraft to go through it. A few minutes later another aircraft was handed off to them which will go through the airspace. Controller dialogue ensued. The other sector thought it was ok to delay the airspace for traffic; but the reporting Controller had military aircraft that needed to use the airspace.

Airbus First Officer reported LAS temperature at 46C with winds 230 at 12 kts. The crew could only accept Runway 07L. Dispatch had METAR winds variable at 3kts; so was forceful about a legal 07L departure but the crew refused a tailwind departure.
Air carrier First Officer reported a laser strike during approach to SAN airport.
A Tower Local Controller observed the other Local Controller allow an aircraft to land with less than the required minimum separation from the preceding aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported wanting to know the results of a First Officer's COVID-19 test before upcoming trips due to concern about exposure.

Upon post flight inspection a gash was noticed on the underside of the aircraft near the single point refueling station. The reporter attributed the damage to FOD encountered during the takeoff or landing phase.
Air carrier Captain reported hitting a flock of birds during takeoff resulting in a return to land.

MD11 flight crew reported going around at low altitude from an approach to Runway 4R at EWR due to windshear. They then discovered that they were in conflict with a departure from Runway 4L and level off at 1;500 feet just as TCAS announces the conflict. Post flight reveals that the EWR2 SID and the missed approach procedure for Runway 4L are initially identical.
B777 Captain reported multiple mechanical issues which caused a delay and a crew swap.
PCT Tracon Controller reported a loss of separation between an IFR and VFR aircraft. He recommended modification to the Bravo airspace.
B737-700 First Officer reported the aircraft out time in ACARS changed resulting in a possible FAR 117 violation.
B777 Captain reported noticing anomalies in the allowable operating weights generated by his performance computer.
CRJ-200 Captain reported stick shaker on climb out due to a wind gust.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL350 from an A380 10 miles away and 1;000 ft above them.
Ground employee reported concerns about having to wear commonly shared wireless headsets during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Seattle TRACON Controller reported that while instructing; the sector became overloaded and aircraft where in Minimum Vectoring Areas below the required altitudes.
Dispatcher reported late notice for a Center to be closed for COVID cleaning; which resulted in a divert.
CID Controller assigned as Controller in Charge combined with Radar responsibilities reported an aircraft was cleared into ALO airspace without proper handoff coordination.
A320 Captain reported a passenger who wants to bring an electric scooter in the cabin rather than the cargo hold.
Air carrier First Officer reported that the Captain initiated a low speed rejected takeoff when the Surface Movement Guidance System (SMGS) lights illuminated on the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported the PAPI depiction on the Jeppesen 12-5 Chart for the PHL RNAV (GPS) Runway 27L approach; dated 22 Jun 2017; is shown on the incorrect side of the runway.
Center Controller reported that during a controller de-brief/switch an airborne conflict occurred due to the distraction.
B757 Captain reported the lights atop the Goethals Bridge can be mistaken for Runway 4 REILs at EWR.
B757 First Officer reported ATC issued multiple changes in the clearance during arrival into LAX; causing a safety concern because of the increased workload.
A330 First Officer reported moderate turbulence resulted in a speed reduction before making the request with ATC.
An air carrier Captain flying the SFO SERFR 1 STAR reported NCT alerted them about the possibility of a laser highlight which did in fact occur about four miles north of EPICK.
An air carrier Captain called in fatigued on the his sixth day of duty after scheduling added an additional round trip. As a result; the pilot was concerned about disciplinary action.
A ZMP Center Controller reported receiving hand offs on two aircraft that were on converging headings at the same altitude.
Ground employee reported a Dangerous Goods cargo loading violation.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger brought on board a smelly spray container made with an alcohol base mixture. Passenger reportedly started spraying walls; windows; seats and armrests.
Pilot reported listening to the ATIS which stated the Tower was closed; and started a taxi towards the runway when Ground called the pilot and advised Tower was still open.

Corporate First Officer reported that company SOP was that minor issues are not generally written up; but passed along to maintenance and management personnel verbally by phone. The First Officer believes a better procedure is needed to deal with minor maintenance issues.
Technician reported that distraction and pressure from a coworker resulted in a procedural error and installation of an incorrect oxygen bottle.
Air carrier flight crew experienced unstable glideslope on short final.
Technician reported fatigue and workload resulted in an erroneous logbook entry.

ATC Supervisor reported a Controller complied with the rules and cleared an aircraft for a visual approach which subsequently conflicted with parallel runway traffic by flying through the final approach course.
A320 Captain reported that the push-back crew did not follow the SOP and disconnected the tow bar while the brakes were released.
SFO Tower Controller observed the Departure Controller turn an aircraft into another departing aircraft from an intersecting runway.
ZHU Center Controller was not made aware of an aircraft estimating their airspace from an adjacent non radar facility that was descending into a conflict with their traffic.
Air carrier Flight Crew reported two fixes with similar names close together.
Pilot reported that turbulence and updrafts caused loss of aircraft control and altitude deviation.
Reporter describes ambiguous guidance in company manuals with regard to avoiding known windshear.
An aircraft was issued go-around instructions for two approaches in a row. The pilot felt they were too busy to read back the ATC instructions.
Captain reported two safety ideas to assist with reducing exposure to COVID-19 in the flight deck.
Local Controller reported an aircraft departed wanting to fly just under the clouds; but encountered IFR and the pilot was not certified for IFR flight.
BAE-125-850XP Captain reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence twelve miles in trail of a B777 on RIIVR Two Arrival into LAX.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported his workload was so high he allowed a flight under his supervision to enter a tornado watch area without notification.
Pilot reported receiving a climb RA at 500 feet on final approach to EWR.
Ramp agent reported that procedures concerning dangerous goods are not being properly followed.
Air carrier Captain reported a 'quite violent' wake turbulence encounter on takeoff from ATL in trail of two B757 aircraft.

Flight Attendant reported airline's A321s are inadequately configured with jumpseats; seatbelts; and shoulder harnesses to provide safety for three flight attendants working in the aft of the aircraft during turbulence.
Maintenance Technician reported that an RDS (Remote Data Station) was possibly infected by a virus.

Single engine small aircraft pilot reports a collision with an object; possibly a small UAV; at 4;800 feet over the Del Valle reservoir after departing LVK. Scratches on the cowling and two of the three propeller blades were noted on post flight.

Air carrier Captain reported ZHU Center issued them an 'illegal' RNAV STAR clearance because they did not state which runway transition to fly into IAH. However; the notes on the GESNR1 chart say to fly the Runway 8L transition.
PNE Controller and transport pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter while on visual approach.
A Pilot flying the PAO VOR/DME 31 got low on the approach and remained VMC; clear of obstacles even with an ATC low altitude alert and climb request.
B737 flight crew reported a bird strike on climbout that resulted in momentary power reduction.  After an overweight landing; the crew encountered locked brakes.
A pilot reported not receiving the latest ATIS information for an airport and did not have the fix they were cleared to programmed in their FMS.
B737 NG Captain reported confusion communicating with ATC due to similar sounding call signs.
Air carrier Captain reported executing a CAT II confidence check approach which was not authorized.

ZDC Center Instructor and Developmental reported a military fighter auto-popped on the scope and continued to climb unrestricted. Separation was lost due to the military traffic climbing aggressively and without coordination.

A pilot departing ACK reports the Tower Controller told him to turn left to 010 heading. In doing so; the co-pilot then gets control of the aircraft and attempts to turn the aircraft to the runway heading because of the Obstacle Departure Procedure (ODP) 800 foot restriction. The pilot regains control of aircraft and turns to a heading of 010 and at that time the aircraft is at 800 feet. The pilot is not sure if the 010 heading should have been followed until he was at 800 feet; like the co-pilot was trying to do.
ZAB ARTCC Controller reported being inundated with traffic and staffing issues which cause reporter to be constantly fatigued.
The pilot of a PA25 glider tow aircraft noticed damage to the elevator. It was later determined that the incorrect part was installed on the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported using firewall thrust to cope with an unexpected tailwind shear event during takeoff from BOS.
A corporate helicopter pilot reported a NMAC while flying in SOCAL airspace.

SCT Controller and Front Line Manager reported of an aircraft that went around due to traffic; then was being sequenced to same runway as another arrival. Pilot attempted to question and advise the controller but; due to congestion on frequency; was not able to get a word in. Pilot squawked emergency and turned in to final. Traffic in front and alongside aircraft had to be turned out.

Air carrier Captain reported his company's confusing NOTAM format which presents large amounts of route and airport data in a random fashion making reading and comprehension difficult. NOTAM usability is questionable yet probably FAA acceptable.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a routing change after takeoff which differed from Pre-departure clearance routing; resulting in fuel reserve burn.
Aircraft Inspector reported while accomplishing a routine inspection he missed an extra washer that was installed on an elevator tab control rod.
ZNY Center Controller reported of a skydiving aircraft that ignored ATC instructions; argued with controllers on frequency; and gave misleading information to have his requests approved.
B757 Captain reported entering into a holding pattern on the RIV VOR instead of the RAL VOR as cleared.
Air taxi Maintenance Controller reported Company communication directed employees to ignore company policy/procedures and follow e-mail instructions instead.
Lead Technician reported refusing to sign an Engineering Order due to compliance issues.
Ground employee reported concerns with COVID-19 sick leave related policies.

NCT TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft in HWD airspace flew under the OAK Runway 30 final approach course causing two conflicts with OAK arrivals. The final approach course goes through HWD Delta airspace.

Maintenance Technician reported an AS355 helicopter airspeed indicator was replaced with an incorrect unit. The original faulty indicator was reinstalled and the aircraft returned to service without documentation.
A helicopter pilot had just departed LAS at night; VMC when another helicopter pilot reported a near miss with his aircraft. The other aircraft was unseen until after the fact.
ZAB ARTCC FLM reported observing an unidentified VFR aircraft fly through the path of skydivers at 7500 feet.
Center Controller reported confusion with taking over a TRACON's airspace and difficulty with coordination with Tower on how to handle some flights.
Ramp personnel reported Hazmat cargo not listed on DSTG [Departure Staging Guide] was incorrectly loaded in different cargo pit.
Two GA pilots reported interference on WDR airport CTAF frequency 123.00 that appeared to be commercial radio activity.
A Corporate aircraft departed FOK IFR and had near miss below 1;500 feet with a glider and tow aircraft off the runway end with Class D airspace in operation.
ATL Tower Controller reported an NMAC between departing traffic and an aircraft conducting a photo mission.
Air carrier flight crew reported BIF airport Taxiway H is not stressed for heavy aircraft; which is not addressed anywhere in the charts.
EMB-145 FO reported the flight crew decided to proceed to an alternate VFR when PHX TRACON shut down because of a fire.
A C172 instructor pilot reported that during his student's first solo flight; visibility decreased enough after that ATC assistance was sought to locate and navigate to a nearby Class D airport.
Air carrier Relief Pilot and Captain reported forgetting to do the pre-flight walkaround and cited communication; distraction and lack of recent flying as contributing factors.

An air carrier pilot questioned the SJC BMRNG2 RNAV Departure wording clarity about 'CLIMB TO 3;000 feet'. Should the climb continue while expecting vectors or should the statement be 'CLIMB TO AND MAINTAIN 3;000 feet EXPECT VECTORS'?

GA pilot reported diverting to GUP due to stronger than forecasted headwinds; but the airport was closed for construction. The runway was clear and the reporter elected to land due to his fuel situation.

A300 Flight Crew is dispatched with MEL 29-24-01 for an inoperative Yellow electric pump. The MEL does not allow the Captain to check the Yellow system/accumulator pressure prior to engine start nor does the MEL require maintenance to perform the check. The reporters believe that both the MEL and the push back procedures need to be modified to accommodate this MEL.
A C-525 pilot landed on a closed runway portion because he did not see the NOTAM referencing the closed runway section. Upon arrival there were no runway barricades or closed runway indications.
PA-30 pilot reported not being able to intercept the localizer and thought he had GPS equipment problems.  Pilot eventually realized he had not flipped the localizer frequency to correct position.
CDW Controller reported issuing a traffic alert to a departing aircraft for traffic departing an adjacent uncontrolled airport where there is no radar coverage below 1;500 feet.
A320 flight crew reported that ATC instructions combined with a loss of situational awareness caused them to hit a taxiway light.
Pilot reported being task saturated during a training flight with bad weather; resulting in an airspace violation.
Falcon FA-10 pilot reported getting unclear communications from ATC. The flight was VFR and ATC kept directing them towards clouds. The pilot was unclear of the location of conflicting traffic.
A-321 Captain reported refusing an aircraft because a Navigation Display had been deferred inoperative for four days; even though it had passed through maintenance facility stations multiple times.
Air carrier flight crew reported taking evasive action after receiving an EGPWS terrain warning on a visual approach to Runway 32 at MFR. The ATC vector was possibly a factor in the incident.
