Pilot reported a go-around due to tailwind limitations and after landing at OAK airport; called Tower to question where the wind indicators were on the airport.  Tower personnel did not know the answer.
Airbus A320 flight crew reported that after landing; during taxi; they encountered ice on the taxiway.
Business jet Captain explained the confusion and workload following a late HOU runway change which necessitated an arrival change from the PUCKS 2 to BAYYY 2 RNAV Arrival.
Technician reported multiple failures of personnel to follow maintenance procedures during a flight control cable rigging; resulting in control cables requiring replacement on all three systems.
Fractional jet Captain reported that they had a near miss during climb.
Air carrier Captain reported PDC uplink to aircraft FMC was incorrect based on route required by ATC. Problem was reported as ongoing at this location.
Commercial airline Lead Technician reported documentation discrepancies during shipping and receiving of aircraft parts due to confusing company handling procedures.

Helicopter Captain reported encountering IMC conditions during a night VFR ambulance flight. Pilot reported a turn to return to VMC and mentioned that wearing Night Vision Goggles can make it difficult to detect reduced visibility conditions.
Pilot reported that the Flight Service Station (FSS) frequency associated with CIU Airport is not correct in FAA and Jeppesen charts and needs to be updated.
Air carrier Captain reported that Jeppesen RNAV Arrival charts for MMUN do not include the additional waypoints after the transition points.
GA pilot reported a rejected take-off resulted in a runway excursion and contact with a perimeter fence.
An AMT's thumb was amputated when an actuator on a passenger seat he was working on inadvertently moved.

Air Carrier flight crew reported they were cleared for the RNAV (GPS) Z Runway 17 approach to MTJ in visual conditions. They deviated from their IFR clearance to avoid an aircraft on final without obtaining clearance for a visual approach from Denver Center.
A ZID Controller reported a heading deviation incident that resulted from an improper handoff.
Tailwheel pilot reported a prop strike occurred during taxi when he encountered wake turbulence or jet blast from several B737 aircraft operating in the vicinity.

An air carrier crew on approach to IAH received an EGPWS WINDSHEAR alert with a 15-20kt speed increase and a flap overspeed. The approach was continued; even though unstabilized; because severe weather was visible at the other end of runway.
ZLA ARTCC Controller reported a military aircraft pilot reported a near miss with a weather balloon.
Captain of an Air Carrier jet which had just departed RPLL at night; reported a near miss with unreported traffic which did not activate a TCAS alert.
A319 Captain reported that the Pilot Reconciliation Report can be accessed electronically.
PIT TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict with an IFR aircraft and a VFR aircraft that may have been VFR in IFR conditions.
Ramp Supervisor reported Hazmat cargo was incorrectly configured for air transport.

General aviation instructor pilot reported a near miss with another aircraft in the traffic pattern. The instructor pilot took the controls and maneuvered away from the other aircraft when they did not follow the traffic sequence instructions from ATC.
Controller reports of observing a poor performance by the Local Controller resulting in a NMAC.
Air carrier Captain reported two passengers were abusive to flight attendants after they were reminded of mask requirements.
Small aircraft pilot reported a conflict with a helicopter while taxiing in the ramp area at FFZ airport.
PIC reported encountering a drone approaching SLI VOR.
Maintenance Technician reported potential for CRJ-700 aircraft spoiler damage during flap rigging.
A Controller observed a Supervisor walk away from the Controller-in-Charge while the Controller-in-Charge was giving the Supervisor a position relief briefing.
Pilot reported ATC being too busy to communicate with them while attempting to request a clearance to descend.

IAD Tower Controller reported that a flight crew missed the taxiway turnoff; turned around to exit; causing aircraft on final to be sent around. Instruction was too late and aircraft landed on occupied runway.
B737-700 flight crew reported executing an escape maneuver due to a CFIT event resulting from a compressed approach clearance. Crew completed subsequent approach in an uneventful landing.
ZOB Center Controller reported sectors in their area were unsafe during weather deviations due to insufficient flow control and lack of Supervisor's proficiency.
Tower Controller reported not being sure if a landing clearance was given to an arrival aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported a GPWS event during approach to ROA while in visual conditions.
Captain reported a lack of consensus regarding his decision to deny boarding to an intoxicated passenger.
Albuquerque Center Controller reported of parachute operations that were being conducted within the arrival routes and the confusion surrounding the operations.
Air carrier Pilot Flying reported refusing aircraft due to streaks of oily substance being present on the aircraft's exterior despite Technician stating otherwise.
Corporate Captain reported poor judgment resulted in flying VFR into IMC. Captain reported outside pressures to depart contributed to the event.
ATC reported flight flew through final approach course and descended below their assigned Minimum Vectoring Altitude; ATC Low Altitude Alert did not activate in a timely manner.
A Dispatcher reported the flight tracking software Fusion incorrectly displayed his aircraft's position 200 NM off the filed route.
Captain reported taking evasive action while on the climb out from EWR due to an RA caused by traffic bound to level off 1000 ft. below their aircraft.
B777 flight crew reported altitude deviation due to weather.
EMB-505 Captain reported severe turbulence during approach resulting in momentary loss of aircraft control.
ZGSZ Ground Control cleared a B777 to taxi K2; A; Hold Short of A5 then via Route 1; D; C1; Holding Point 15. The crew found no Route 1 or published taxi routes on any available charts.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a terrain warning during approach to ROA.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a ground collision with light pole during tug pusbhack from gate.
CRJ-900 maintenance technicians reported they did not have the proper equipment to safely replace an APU battery.

A pilot in a flight of two C-337's at 13;000 feet reported detecting the other pilot's conversational hypoxia symptoms so they descended and diverted. The Mountain High O2D2 regulator was a decade old and not serviced regularly.
PA31 pilot reported having difficulty maintaining altitude and airspeed in icing conditions.
A321 First Officer reported being distracted by duplicate Tower radio transmissions on the Monitor frequency while conducting a PRM approach.

A B737 inbound on the ILS 08 to BUR responded twice on separate approaches to TCAS RAs with conflicting VNY VFR traffic operating below an intervening under/overcast. One reporter suggested modified restrictions on VNY traffic during such conditions to lessen the exposure to conflicts.

SDF TRACON Controller reported giving an IFR clearance to an aircraft he thought was on the ground.  Aircraft was actually airborne and in close proximity to another aircraft leading to a loss of separation.
B737 flight crew reported the flight deck door came open upon rotation due to a switch being in the wrong position.
A flight crew reported that their aircraft was struck by an eagle during the takeoff roll. The takeoff was successfully rejected and the aircraft returned to the hangar for damage assessment.

B737 flight crew at 100 knots on the takeoff roll; heard ATC clear another aircraft to cross the same runway. Crew elected to continue the takeoff and Tower subsequently advised that the crossing traffic was behind the takeoff aircraft.
B777 Captain reported that when the autopilot autoland was tested on several aircraft; the aircraft touched down to the right of centerline on EWR Runway 4R.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported a loud noise and a STALL FAIL caution message on takeoff; resulting in a return to departure airport caused by the crew O2 service door being unsecured.
An air carrier crew reported taxi instruction confusion resulting from ambiguous; conflicting taxi instructions after landing on SDF Runway 17L with clearance across the airport on Runway 29.
E170 Captain reported aircraft flew six flights with an open write up.

ZFW controller reports of an aircraft that doesn't climb because of a false target being displayed on their TCAS. Controller had climbed aircraft to requested altitude and then became traffic for another aircraft because it didn't climb to requested altitude. Pilot told controller this problem happened with the aircraft before. Event lead to a loss of separation.
COS Tower CIC reported an airborne conflict when an arriving aircraft on final approach disappeared from the controller's radar display due to a scheduled radar outage; limiting radar coverage.
A B737-700 Captain reported the left wing fuel tank transferred fuel into the Center tank creating an imbalance. A checklist procedure did not exist for the anomaly.
New EMB-170 Captain reports being assigned to fly the majority of her flights after training as a First Officer and believes this is an unsafe practice due to inability to consolidate the learning.
ZLC controller reported a C402 lost an engine; could not hold altitude; and descended below the MIA before a visual approach clearance was issued.
B757 Captain reported being dispatched with a lost cell phone in the cabin; and expressed concern they did not have enough information to make a decision on whether to accept the aircraft.
SR20 pilot reported while climbing to avoid traffic they inadvertently flew into SFO Class B airspace.
Helicopter Pilot reported being VFR in IMC and making a precautionary landing in a field.
A B737 flight crew reported they encountered unforecasted turbulence and several flight attendants were injured.

Center Controller reported an aircraft at a non towered airport that had been issued a hold for release called the Operations Manager on the phone was released without approval from the Controller at the Sector.
Pilot reported during a diversion; he was forced to make a priority landing in a neighborhood on a paved street and struck an electrical box with the wing.
ZWY Controller describes a situation where two aircraft that he was responsible for were on a course to collide. Controller separated traffic.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering heavy rain; hail; sever turbulence; and a lightning strike. Aircraft landed uneventfully; but did sustain hail damage to nose cone.
Flight crew reported receiving three STAR changes and multiple vectors from ATC; consequently increasing the workload to the point that errors were made.
Single Pilot of a general aviation light aircraft reported a problem with the autopilot during an approach resulting in a low altitude alert from ATC.
A319 Captain reported rejecting the takeoff on ORD Runway 22L at approximately 85 kts after encountering probable wake turbulence from aircraft landing on 28C.
Corporate First Officer reported a severe turbulence encounter during approach to BNA airport resulting in an altitude excursion and momentary loss of aircraft control.
JFK Controller reported two aircraft with nearly identical call signs caused the wrong flight to be coordinated.
ZME Center Controller reported a radio problem that is ongoing and has not been corrected.
ZMP Center Controller reported an airborne conflict. Communication with ZDV ARTCC was cited as a contributor to the event.
A PA28 Student Pilot reported that after damaging the aircraft during a go-around; the repair made by a local mechanic did not have the proper documentation.

B737-800 Flight Attendant reported passenger carry-on bag containing 80 lbs of Lithium Ion batteries caused concern due to weight and insufficient containment bags available to extinguish a Lithium Ion battery fire in the event of an in flight fire. Reporter further stated there is no guidance in Flight Attendant Manuel regarding weight/quantity restrictions.
B737-800 flight crew reported encountering moderate turbulence at FL290 that caused injury to two flight attendants. Flight continued to destination.
PA28 pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in the vicinity of ZZZ airport.
Flight Engineer reported a progressive loss of cabin pressure as the result of a water separator unit clamp coming undone.
Cessna 172 Flight Instructor reported the student ran off the runway while landing.
Ramp worker reported their Supervisor made them temporarily open a closed ramp to park an airplane during a period of lightning strikes.
P80 TRACON Controller reported losing separation between two in trail aircraft when the first aircraft was vectored off the approach to avoid unidentified VFR conflicting traffic.
A flight instructor and student pilot experience unexpected IFR conditions on climbout when the reported weather and visual conditions indicated that VFR operations would exist.

A military aircraft routinely conducts a mission while blocking airspace in a radar and non-radar covered airspace. The controllers in this area feel the mission is not coordinated according to rules and the airspace block is not properly protected.
R90 Controller In Charge reported an aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
BOI TRACON Controller reported commercial fixed wing failed to turn adequately to avoid higher MVA. ATC sector does not display weather to assist aircraft safely avoid weather.
An FAA employee reported that National Weather Service volcanic ash reports were not being properly disseminated to provide pilots with accurate Alaska information.
TRACON Controllers reported an unusual sector configuration and combination caused the controllers to miss point outs which resulted in a confliction between a departure and an arrival.
B737 flight crew reported windshear conditions on short final caused an overspeed of the flaps.
ZLC Center Controller reported an aircraft turned early leading to a climb below the minimum IFR altitude.
An Airbus flight crew reported losing traction while positioning the aircraft on an ice-contaminated ramp; and consequently making contact with ground equipment.
Air Carrier Captain makes recommendations regarding changes to flight plans due to weather.
Technician reported that employees are not being issued PPE; especially masks; which are needed to avoid contracting the COVID-19 virus.
Air carrier Captain reported an ATC reroute was received via ACARS; however it went unnoticed; resulting in a course deviation. Captain cited not having flown recently as a contributing factor.
B737-700 Captain reported that during ground hold; the spoilers were extended and were never reset prior to take off roll; resulting in a rejected takeoff.
A Controller working three sectors combined had less than required separation between two aircraft during a period of weather deviations and higher than normal workload.
An A320 pilot had been unable to update his IPad at several different locations. An FAA inspector; during the course of a line check; showed him how to use the airport Wi-Fi to update his IPad.
Aerial pilot reported an NMAC event during takeoff with another aircraft in landing pattern due to a communications breakdown.
S46 TRACON Controller reported canceling a Visual Approach clearance to avoid VFR traffic that was not in communication with ATC.

EUG Tower Controller reported that he was questioned by Airport Operations if he had spoken to a particular aircraft. The Controller did not recall talking to the aircraft. Aircraft was found to be at the north ramp. Pilot stated he had ATC clearance to land. It was found out later that the pilot never talked to ATC.
B737-700 Captain reported the Decision Altitude for the RNP Z runway 30L increased to two different values after two different NOTAMS were applied.
A-300F flight crew reported a hard landing/tailstrike by First Officer during first flight of OE. Captain executed a go-around to an uneventful landing.
Air carrier Load Planner reported a communications breakdown with Ramp Lead regarding Hazmat shipment incorrectly configured for air shipment. Hazmat shipment was corrected.

Air carrier flight crew reported a clearance misunderstanding resulted in an altitude deviation and an airborne conflict during departure.  First Officer cited ATC staffing levels due to COVID-19 as a possible contributing factor in the miscommunication.
Air carrier Captain reported that a passenger who was approved to fly displayed disruptive behavior before and during the flight.

An A319 flight crew after landing discovered a pair of vice grip/pliers wedged in the landing gear during the aircraft walk around. Local Maintenance stated that they had not yet touched the aircraft. The Captain concluded that the pliers had been left by the departure station Maintenance.
Air Carrier First Officer reported experiencing a CFTT incident while on a visual approach to ELP airport.
ZLC Center Controller reported an unsafe situation when the sector was understaffed by two people and the radios went out.
Pilot departed airport without communication with Tower. Pilot mistook LOCAL time for ZULU time; and departed the airport when Tower was open.
CLT TRACON Controller reported receiving handoffs from Center at very high altitudes that will not allow the aircraft to comply with restrictions on Optimized Profile Descents.
While on approach into Las Vegas; flight crew experienced ambiguity in required altitudes between STAR and RNAV Visual approach charts.
Flight attendant reported a passenger was wearing a vented face mask which is not in compliance with policy.
A320 Captain reported MMMX 05R Approach does not load properly in the FMCGS.
Air carrier Captain reported temporary loss of ADS-B and GPS likely due to jamming. Captain recommended disseminating information to all flight crews.
Widebody transport Captain reported that they were instructed to go-around due to base under attack.
Air carrier Captain reported being blinded by the tug's headlights after pushback; negatively affecting ramp safety.

B737 Captain reported departing DEN Runway 34L during variable windshear conditions and recorded 192 kts at Vr; 209 kts at lift off with indicated airspeeds of 158 kts and 163 kts respectively. The maximum tire speed was exceeded.
Air carrier Captain reported the fix AEROS is missing from their Jeppesen database.
A320 Captain reported many interference calls on ACARS; which blocked ATC calls on communication 1.
Air Carrier First Officer reported missing a clearance during taxi-out due to possible radio interference at FLL airport.
A319 flight crew reported a fuel distribution irregularity due to improper fueling procedures. After determining that center of gravity limits were satisfied; the aircraft was dispatched.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict resulting from similar sounding callsign confusion.
EMB-505 Captain reported denying Lithium Battery powered skateboards to be boarded due to insufficient FOM HAZMAT guidance.
Ramp Supervisor reported an aircraft being towed with a towbarless tug came out of its cradle and went out of control. The aircraft came to a stop before making contact with any objects.
Flight Attendant reported being distracted and not arming two assigned doors.

Air Carrier Captain reported crossing into LTAA airspace at VESAR; then detected a note on the VESAR way point requiring a call ten minutes prior to entering Turkish airspace. The reporter suggested that important notes be highlighted in some way on the chart; so as not to be missed.
Helicopter pilot reported being distracted during preflight due to concern about avoiding COVID-19 and left the chocks at the departure heliport.

ZME Controller reports of an overdue aircraft that he becomes aware of. Controller checks information and finds later that this aircraft is at a different airport than he expected. Controller noted that two fixes may have helped the automation problem; RUNDE and RUNOE.
Center Controller reported there was no official FAA guidance regarding how to handle traffic in the airspace when the facility went ATC zero for COVID-19 cleaning.
ZHU Center controllers reported a computer error related to aircraft routings.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS warning while on approach over terrain at night.
Falcon 2000 Captain reported that a failure to remove a pitot cover on preflight resulted in a return to the departure airport.
ZLC ARTCC mandated that controllers cannot approve weather deviations for aircraft not in radar coverage unless the aircraft is given special attention.
Air Carrier Captain reported setting and taking off with an incorrect flap position.
Flight Attendant reported the flight crew did not wear face masks on the flight deck while the Flight Attendant was assisting with lavatory breaks.
ERJ-175 flight crew reported executing an escape maneuver after encountering windshear on short final.
Two flight attendants reported having to work a flight a few days later after being on a flight with a passenger who had tested positive for COVID-19.

Technician reported that; during troubleshooting for an unrelated EICAS Message; he found numerous circuit breakers pulled and collared and he reset them without documenting the corrective actions tasks in the aircraft logbook.
Pilot reported a suggested modification to crew takeoff procedure when using TOGA to reduce the chance of the autothrottles engaging in the wrong mode.
ZLA Center Controller received multiple aircraft from an adjacent Center on the wrong STAR.
Pilot reported a NMAC on final approach.
Ground crew reported that during pushback of an aircraft the tow bar malfunctioned and a breakaway occurred.

C90 TRACON requested a crew fly 250 knots below ORD Class B which the Captain questioned as an FAR 91.117(c) violation. ATC cited that FAA Order 7110.65V; section 5-7-2 allowed an operational exception but the pilot disagreed.

ZLA Controller reports of high altitude balloons in which the sectors didn't have any information on. Traffic Management Unit said they knew about them the night before and should have paperwork in the area.
Flight attendant reported exit door placards are out of compliance and that over sized baggage was stored improperly in violation of FAA regulations.
Air Carrier Captain reported landing without a clearance and attributes the event to lack of flying and complacency while flying with another Captain as the First Officer in this flight.

SAV TRACON Controller reported accepting an automated handoff of an aircraft with 'AMBQ' in the data tag. The actual position of the aircraft was ninety miles northwest of the position of the data tag the controller accepted for handoff.
A321 flight crew reported that first flight of the day the Number 2 engine was slow to spool.
Pilot reported limited communication with ATC while on the ground leading to unsafe situations.

While performing a retest; mechanics pulled three different circuit breakers which affected the nose wheel steering and the brakes which caused the loss of control and caused the aircraft to run off the taxiway into the mud.
Houston TRACON Controller reported a partially incorrect readback which lead to an aircraft descending below the MSA in the area.
Corporate pilot reported being distracted by the bright lights from a new golf center near Spirit of St Louis Airport.
Flight Attendant reported that the kit on the aircraft did not have required sanitation supplies or masks. In addition; these supplies are not being replenished in between flights.
Center Controller reported the adjacent sector intentionally delayed in accepting a handoff causing the Controller to have an airspace deviation.
Air carrier flight crew approaching ZBAA; the crew reported that they had to use emergency authority in order to deviate around a large thunderstorm.
B767-300 flight crew was forced to divert to an airport not filed as an alternate due to unforeseen thunderstorm buildups.

Pilot receives flight following from Approach Control. Pilot is told to squawk VFR and contact the Tower at 3.1 miles from the airport. Pilot calls Tower and Controller advises pilot that he should have called them earlier. Pilot responded that they were under flight following and Approach told them to call when they did.
First Officer reported issues relating to NOTAMS and towers closing for COVID-19 cleaning.
Air carrier flight crew reported overshooting an assigned altitude during departure and cited the rate of climb due to the aircraft being light in weight as a contributing factor.

Captain reported that actual slant range visibility conditions are often worse than reported on ATIS for their early morning approach and landing to SBGR; but the Tower is resistant to their requests for the full ILS.
CLT TRACON Controller reported that his Supervisor assigned some of his airspace to ZTL Center without his knowledge.
Air carrier Captain reported ZDC controllers tried to clear them with a failed FMS to a VORTAC (PXT) that was out of service.
A319 Mechanic reported that an idle engine check was insufficient to check for oil leaks.
Airline Dispatcher reported flight crew notification of Hazmat cargo on board aircraft but not listed on load manifest.
The pilot of an RV6 airplane reported an engine failure on take-off. The pilot switched fuel tanks and power was restored; followed by a safe landing.
PA-28 Instructor Pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at X04.
Dispatcher reported they changed an aircraft's call sign due to a similar sounding call sign departing at the same time.
ZTL Center Controller working combined sectors with weather deviations reported their area's airspace became overloaded with traffic and he observed two aircraft head on at the same altitude.
Air Carrier First Officer reported responding to several RAs due to a potential TCAS system anomaly during an approach.
737-800 captain reported a UAV at 2500 ft. (MSL).
B777-200 Captain reported the charted minimums on the temporary RNAV (GNSS) U Runway 14 chart; for SBGL; are too high to enable a safe landing from a VDP.
Denver Center (ZDV) Controllers report of a loss of separation due to NON RVSM aircraft going through the airspace refueling with a tanker.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non-compliant with face mask policy.

A flight crew accepted a visual approach to an airport where they were aware of the high terrain surrounding the airport. On base; they received a terrain warning commanding them to climb. They complied but were still in an adequate position to continue the approach. They stabilized the aircraft on final and landed safely.
A Learjet 70 flight crew described the entry and recovery from an extreme clear air turbulence encounter which damaged the aircraft while losing 7;000 FT and exceeding Mach .83.
GA pilot reported runway excursion to avoid departing traffic. No NOTAM was available for airport event.
C172 pilot reported a strong gust of turbulence from one side forced his plane to veer off the runway.
A Maintenance Technician reported that maintenance took a delay because of the required inspection for a bird strike was not accomplished per work cards.

The flight crew of a large transport reported that the recent implementation of a new before takeoff checklist resulted in greatly reduced situational awareness by the pilot performing the checklist; and increased the potential for taxi conflicts or mishaps.
Air carrier First Officer reported being exposed to COVID-19 and cited concerns with the company not notifying other employees who may have also been exposed.
A pilot of a Cessna 421 reported of encountering turbulence in IMC and icing conditions in downdrafts of over 1200fpm.

Controller reported issuing traffic to an aircraft on a missed approach. Both aircraft were below the MIA and the Controller could not turn the aircraft to avoid traffic. Controller later questioned if he should have done something different.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger complained about a deadheading pilot who was coughing and requested deadheading pilot put on a mask. Passenger ended up moving to a different seat.
A300 Maintenance Technician reported that after completing a brake deferral the flight crew experienced MLG brake dragging during taxi out.
737 flight attendant reported the aircraft encounter turbulence; with two lightning strikes and passengers were emotionally upset; but there was no communication from the flight deck crew.
A light transport flight crew reported entering RVSM airspace with incomplete RVSM paperwork. They realized their mistake; notified ATC; and were then climbed above RVSM airspace.
Helicopter instructor pilot reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern due to an aircraft departing in the opposite direction.
Small Transport pilot reported a track deviation due to confusion over two waypoints with similar sounding names; NAVJO and NAVHO.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing DTW in trail of a B757.
Dispatcher reported poor communication between the various parties involved caused the aircraft to be fueled incorrectly.
First Officer of a large passenger aircraft reported getting low on the approach profile while conducting a visual approach at night.

MIA TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft skirting just outside of the Class B airspace and below MIA arrivals was causing issues with the arrivals. The Controller recommended reshaping the Class B.
CRJ-200 Non Flying Pilot reported a system wide GPS and ADS-B failure.
Technician reported not having correct qualifications to perform and sign off the log book for a Captain's Pitot Probe replacement.
Beech V35 pilot reported noticing unmarked and uncharted power lines have been installed near the final approach course for Runway 35 at CWC Airport.
B787 flight crew reported multiple bird strikes on departure.

A GFK Controller reports of a near midair caused by traffic using the wrong call sign and one that is similar to an aircraft in the pattern. This causes the Controller working local to become more confused. Additionally; the Controller was working combined locals.
C172 Instructor Pilot reported a NMAC with a twin engine jet shortly after takeoff. The jet had executed a missed approach moments before.
Balloon pilot reported downing two light poles due to interference with the hanging crown line.
B-737 Captain reported refusing USPS cargo of undeclared propane tanks that rampers discovered during cargo loading.

A LEX Controller reported of descending an aircraft outside of the lateral boundary of his airspace. A new LOA does not give LEX control of the aircraft. The Controller wanted to find out if the 'Control' was left out of the new LOA.
Flight crew reported that with severe weather; ramp crew could  not use headsets during pushback; resulting in confusion and a collision with another aircraft.
C525B Captain reported he was confused flying into HOU when ATC cleared him to another initial point on the same arrival.
An A320 Captain is informed that Taxiway U2 has been designated as U1 at BWI but the runway available from the takeoff data message does not reflect the new designation.
A319 flight crew reported executing a go-around due to an unstable approach resulting from inadequate spacing and 30-knot tailwind.
Air carrier flight crew reported returning to departure airport due to a bird strike shortly after takeoff that resulted in failure of the left engine.
B737 First Officer reported climbing to FL380 from FL360 to find a smoother ride; but instead found severe turbulence passing FL370. The crew then requested FL340 and descended.
Air carrier Captain reported flying while fatigued due to multiple schedule changes during 8 day international rotation.
Technician reported that a records check discovered an unqualified technician had performed maintenance tasks on an aircraft and did not make the required log book entries.
An Air Carrier pilot reported receiving a GPWS warning on short final then realized it was due to incorrect flap configuration.
Maintenance Inspector reported that a Radio Frequency leakage occurred during maintenance ADS-B ground test.
ATC TRACON Controller reported using a procedure no longer authorized which resulted in vectoring an arriving aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

E175 flight crew reported ATC approved weather avoidance clearance where crew received a terrain alert that the Captain doubted resulted in failure to call and allow First Officer proper escape and recover maneuver.
Pilot reported making a track deviation; contrary to ATC clearance; due to rapidly developing weather on the arrival into EWR.
Air carrier First Officer reported missing the IFR release time due to general aviation traffic in the pattern and the Tower being closed.

ZME Controller reported observing an untagged aircraft climbing. A few minutes later an unknown aircraft called the controller asking about routings through weather. Controller had unknown aircraft ident and verified it was from unknown calling pilot.

SFO Controller reported a company restriction to using the TRUKN2 SID that the Controller was not made aware of. Controller reported Supervisor instructed company aircraft to disregard company policy; and reprimanded Controller while on control position for complying with pilot request.
B767 flight crew reported a track heading deviation due to similar sounding fix names BEERI and BERYS.

C680 flight crew reported an uneventful air return for bird strike during takeoff.
B737-800 flight crew reported that an MEL was not applied correctly causing a fuel imbalance.

Technician reported policies and procedures regarding talley checks and Bill of Work forms are confusing and contradictory in reference to who is responsible to complete and what are the qualifications required.
Technician reported non compliance with Company manuals and procedures caused an aircraft to fly in revenue service twice with falsified log book entries.

ZHU Center Traffic Management Unit Controller issued routes and restriction for traffic entering ZHU during a period of weather deviations. The surrounding facilities did not comply with the Controller's Traffic Management plans.
Flight crew reported that as they were landing Tower told them to go around for an aircraft crossing the runway but the crew could not comply as they were already configured on their landing roll.
Technician reported that incorrect parts were installed on both thrust reversers.
Guam Center Controller reported staffing issues at their facility.

An A319 First Officer disconnected all automation including autothrust to practice a manual visual approach; but got high on profile. S-turns along with a high sink rate were required to attain a normal profile before landing on speed in the landing zone. Fatigue was an issue.
Departing pilot reported a NMAC with an arriving helicopter.
Pilot reported a critical ground conflict with a non reporting departure aircraft.
B737-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing SNA in trail of an A321.
A321 First Officer reported becoming uncomfortable when they were holding in position on the runway for an extended period of time and finally asked to clear the runway.

NCT TRACON Controller reported when two aircraft activated Conflict Alert on their radar display they were unable to separate the overlapped data blocks to issue appropriate control instructions and a Traffic Alert.
A B777 Captain reported programming the FMC incorrectly during preflight and lined up for the parallel runway.

ZKC Center Controller reported conducting a flight break up after aerial refueling according to previous procedures; but was advised of a procedural error due to a new interpretation of the FAA guidance.
A320 flight crew reported that during cruise the fuel temperature dropped down to -39 degrees Celsius.
Air carrier Captain reported a greater than planned fuel burn on an international flight; likely due to erroneous weight and balance calculations; along with deviations for weather.
Ground personnel reported a violation of lithium ion battery shipment policy.

A pilot reports of not being able to continue with flight following due to controller workload. The pilot is then advised later that he possibly entered the JFK Class Bravo. The FAA looked at the tapes and determined there was a minor incursion of the Class Bravo; but the aircraft did not come close to another aircraft in the Class Bravo.
B757 First Officer reported misidentifying an EICAS message; which lead to the aircraft flying in a non-airworthy status.
An Air Carrier First Officer reported incomplete taxi instructions which caused a taxiway incursion.

ZOA Controller reported seeing a confusing data block. Controller checked with pilot and then called transferring Controller to verify what had been done. Reporter then discussed the actions with the transferring Controller.
VNY Tower Controller reported separation and runway issues relating to a runway closure at Van Nuys airport.
Pilot Flying reported a runway excursion due to a loss of control on landing during a training flight.
Air carrier mechanic reported that often mechanics are assigned tasks alone which can have dire consequences if a seriously inured were to occur.
Flight Attendant reported concerns over a recurring engine oil leak that a Captain refused the aircraft over.

A general aviation pilot who approached and landed at MD50 reported inconsistent pilotage by another pilot prior to landing and a runway incursion by that same pilot as the reporter rolled out after landing.
ZMA ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft they thought was an intruder; but was actually an aircraft whose radar track was dropped by ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported departing without proper takeoff data; citing high workload and career uncertainties related to COVID-19 cutbacks as contributing.
A General Aviation pilot reported a NMAC due to communication issues with similar sounding call signs.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing thunderstorm weather demanding a divert to a nearby field.
Air carrier Captain reported speed and altitude deviations due to moderate turbulence combined with a late runway assignment resulted in unstabilized approach and a go-around at LAS airport.

An aircraft checked on to the Controller's frequency and advised they were Non-RVSM qualified. The aircraft flight plan suffix or radar data tag did not indicate they were Non-RVSM. The controller only used 1;000 feet vertical separation between this aircraft and another one.
B787 Captain reported misgivings over procedures following a generator failure in flight during ETOPS operations.
Flight Attendant reported requesting a passenger be moved to accommodate for COVID-19 social distancing.  Passenger was not moved and in the process Flight Attendant forgot to brief emergency row.

D01 Controller reported a request for Opposite Direction Operation (ODO) from BJC Tower. The Controller was not sure how to handle it; and neither did anyone on duty. Eventually one of the aircraft had landed so it was no longer on ODO; so the reporter released the VFR aircraft.
A320 Captain reported an altitude excursion and a possible lightning strike or static discharge during approach flying through weather/turbulence.
B737-700 Captain reported significant uncommanded roll in both directions on initial climb related to jet blast from an aircraft on the ground performing an engine run for maintenance.
Flight Attendant reported an aircraft cleaner employee would not comply with face mask policy.
A Maintenance Technician reported that a Fan Trim Balance on a Boeing 737 was not accomplished due to incorrect documentation.
S46 TRACON Controller reported that SEA Tower routinely launches successive departures with minimum separation regardless of aircraft performance differences.
First Officer reported not verifying the correct waypoint sequencing on RNAV approach due to a late clearance change from ATC.

M98 controller describes a day that the normal radar was out and they had to use another site that gave 12 second updates instead of the usual 1 second updates. According to the reporter the procedures in place did not work. There were many deviations and they had to separate traffic on various STARs. Controller requested a procedure which he felt would have made the traffic manageable; but was not approved by management and Traffic Management Coordinator.
B777 Captain reported recurring issue of not receiving required Hazmat documentation for Lithium Ion battery shipment.
A320 Captain reported passenger was smoking in lavatory and activated the fire/smoke ECAM warning. Reporter stated dissatisfaction with the response to the incident.
A B757-200 filled with smoke while at the gate with the APU running. The smoke dissipated with the packs off and bleeds closed. Apparently the de-ice crew allowed fluid to enter the APU intake.
C172 pilot reported entering an area of extreme precipitation and severe turbulence while using NEXRAD XM weather to circumnavigate storm areas.

ZMA Center Controller reported an aircraft's radar target on their scope jumping and giving erroneous heading indications led to a erroneous indication of a separation error with opposite direction traffic.
Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict at an uncontrolled field due to a lack of communication by the conflicting aircraft.

A pilot approaching an airport was warned on CTAF frequency by an unknown operator; that a drone was operating nearby. The reporter returned to his departure airport to avoid conflict with the unseen drone.
NCT Controller reported assigning an air carrier an altitude to stay above unidentified VFR traffic. The VFR traffic climbed toward the air carrier which responded to a TCAS RA alert.
Portland TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation due to an aircraft overshooting assigned altitude.

Tower requested approval to release a departure from ARTCC. ARTCC approved the release then Tower advised they had amended the departure route. The amended departure route was in confliction with ARTCC traffic and below minimum terrain requirements. ARTCC cancelled the release. Tower is required to enter amended routes into the flight data processing more than 15 minutes before the proposed departure time or verbally get approval for amended route. The Tower did not comply with those requirements.
ZLA Developmental Controller reported a loss of separation due to the Radar Controller not taking the advice of the Developmental.
CRJ 900 Captain reported tow bar became detached from aircraft on push back resulting in damage to the front wheel.
Air Carrier Captain questioned the safety of implementing Wake Turbulence Recategorization separation standards at JFK and other major airports.
A BE20 pilot reported that flight following advised him of an approaching aircraft so they took evasive action to avoid a mid-air collision.

Dispatcher was unable to maintain High Frequency data link contact with crew during a trans-oceanic flight and recommends that the company discontinue the use of this type of aircraft on these routes until better data link radios are installed.
B777 flight crew reported an aircraft overspeed for 15-20 seconds while in cruise at FL410 due to a severe mountain wave encounter.

SLC Tower Controller reported that he was distracted and almost let an aircraft land on a closed runway. The Controller did not use memory joggers to remind him of the closure. The AMASS alerted along with another Controller advising him. The aircraft was sent around and landed on an open runway.
ZSU Controller reported a miscommunication with a pilot in regard to a lower crossing altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier First Officer reported requesting the definition of point of dispatch; as it affects maintenance and alternate issues.
Pilot reported encountering opposite direction traffic at uncontrolled airport.
ZMP Controller reported failing to acquire a Departure Controller's data tag on their radarscope and forgetting about the aircraft; which entered adjacent airspace without a hand off.
Ground employee reported that it is difficult to get from one area to another because of out of service equipment and the equipment that is usable is not cleaned correctly.
Air carrier flight crew reported an unstabilized approach into an unfamiliar airport.
An air carrier crew climbing on the FLL ARKES 4 RNAV SID took evasive action from an unreported single engine aircraft at 8;500 following a TCAS TA which then quickly became a TCAS RA.
Air carrier pilot reported he was more fatigued under FAR 117 than under previous regulations.
Flight crew member reported the company was sending crews out during pandemic without proper safety equipment.
A pilot reported; twice; recently they were on a departure and had to take evasive action to avoid adjacent airport traffic that ATC did not advise them about.
ERJ Captain reported that ATC confused them with a different aircraft and caused them to taxi around in circles.
Tower Controller reported a NMAC between opposite direction traffic.
A320 Captain reported requiring additional fuel resulting in being aggressively challenged by Operations for his decision.
Captain reported that upon arriving at destination airport; Ramp Personnel advised flight crew that none of the lateral locks were installed on cargo containers in the forward cargo compartment.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported experiencing a low fuel state when their destination changed runway configuration due to shifting winds. The Captain was critical of company fuel policy.

While making a go-around at a small uncontrolled airport with a student a flight instructor pilot was surprised to see a pickup truck parked on the runway and a motorcycle speeding down the runway itself. He was further surprised when; after a subsequent landing; he confronted the individuals involved and ultimately learned that one of them was the Airport Manager.
Air Carrier Captain reported issues with a published NOTAM and with a Dispatcher's response.
SCT TRACON Controller reported not noticing a VFR aircraft conflicting with an IFR RNAV arrival due to focus required on a different portion of the airspace.
B737-700 Captain reported ATC assigned airspeed 250 kts below the ORD Class B airspace on an RNAV arrival into the ORD area.

A Controller had to cancel an aircrafts' approach clearance and issue instructions to avoid a VFR aircraft crossing the final approach course not in communication with ATC. The reporter states this is a routine occurrence in this area.
Air carrier Captain reported a taxiway incursion in a foreign country citing language barrier and unfamiliarity with the airport as contributing factors.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing ORD in trail of an A319.
Flight Crew flying 737-900 aircraft found empty beer can in cockpit during pre-flight.
A B737 Captain reported that the Ground Crew gave all clear and chocks in before they even had engines shut down or gave cut signal.

Ground employee reported the belt loading systems become slippery from de-icing operations creating a safety hazard for ramp personnel.
DA-2000 flight crew reported being struck by lightning in their destination terminal area. They noted some popped circuit breakers; burn marks; and a burnt smell.
FCM Tower Controller reported while aircraft on takeoff roll; aircraft on back taxi utilizing crossing runway; may not have held short of active runway.  Tower cannot see the hold short markings.
AFW instructor reports of a loss of separation due to not sending an aircraft around after Developmental clears a jet for takeoff.

Pilot reported low visibility due to smoke enroute.
Air carrier flight crew reported that they did not get the fuel service form before departing due to significant delays and late receipt of paperwork.

B737 Captain raised concerns about ATC procedures into MCO that forces pilots to fly an unstabilized approach.

C172 flight instructor reported stopping just past the hold short lines when an aircraft was observed on short final.

Air carrier Captain reported concerns with landing procedures to Runway 21 at LIH airport in go-around or missed approach scenarios.  Reporter cited terrain and tower obstacles as possible hazards.

EMB F/O reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding Airbus on approach to CLT.

B737 First Officer reported the RNAV approach at this airport causes an unstable approach and more training or a procedure rewrite is needed.

ERJ-175 crew reported 2 missed approaches due to wind shear followed by a diversion because of low fuel.

A320 Captain reported a firm landing occurred following a wake turbulence encounter on short final at SFO with a B737 landing on the parallel runway.
Flight Attendant reported staff shortage and poor customer service due to the COVID-19 pandemic contributed to some passengers not being able to deplane in a timely manner.
ZOB Center Controller reported forgetting to ask 3 different aircraft if they had weather and NOTAMs for their landing airport.
Cessna V pilot reported that he flew as a single pilot into a foreign country where his exemption letter was not valid.

Air carrier Captain reported that the aircraft departed with passenger distribution data different from the actual distribution due to one passenger changing seats. CPR Tower Controller reported clearing an aircraft for takeoff from a runway that had been closed per a NOTAM.
CMI Tower Controller reported a Near Mid Air Collision between an aircraft under their control and another VFR aircraft.
Pilot reported being allegedly terminated for raising airworthiness issues to company director of operations.
Technicians reported tasks on work cards were unclear and technicians performed the task incorrectly.
A ramp agent was ordered by his supervisor to remove the belt loader from the Aircraft's cargo pit which forced him into unsafe; potentially harmful baggage loading practices.
Controller observed aircraft radar data tag jumping off the aircraft target in different directions and indicating false airspeeds. Reporter states this has occurred other times in the same area.
Air carrier flight crew reported an uneventful landing from an unstable approach instead of executing go-around per standard procedures.
Air carrier First Officer reported a maintenance delay combined with COVID-19 procedures resulted in being unfit to fly due to fatigue.

CRJ-700 reported that they performed a rejected takeoff due to landing traffic on a crossing runway; additionally; during the subsequent taxi off the runway; they had an excursion and struck a runway edge light.

Pilots departing RJAA report requesting a high speed climb below 10;000 feet. The request was approved by Departure Control. The next Controller asks about the aircrafts speed and the crew tells them that they were approved for a no speed restriction climb. The Controller responds that they had not been approved for the high speed climb.

Ground employee reported that while attempting to connect the headset to the aircraft; the flight crew flashed the nose gear light three different times directly into the crew member's eyes; resulting in vision impairment.
Technician reported that Dispatch had assigned an aircraft for an ETOPS flight that was not serviceable for ETOPS.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger kept switching seats and did not comply with face mask policy during flight.

Flight crew was cleared to taxi on SLC Taxiways H; Y to de-ice pad 6Y but did not identify the H to Y Taxiway transition. After missing Taxiway Y; the crew was cleared H3 to Y but they could not identify the Taxiway H3 transition to Y off Runway 34R. The large physical island in the deice pad south of H3 and north of 6Y is not depicted on any airport or taxi diagram.
Ramp employee reported COVID-19 related concerns regarding used gloves and paper towels repeatedly left in aircraft pits.
TRACON Supervisor reported that Tower allowed an aircraft to depart IFR without a flight plan on file.
A Citation Excel Captain stated he feels RNAV approach charts should be simplified to reduce errors; citing the BNA SWFFT2 STAR as an example.
SR22 pilot reported that a runway incursion resulted from the pilot headset being connected to the copilot position.

After departing DCA Runway 1; a CRJ-200 pilot reported encountering a windshear warning and remained wings level while following the flight director commands. The crew was notified P56 was violated but felt safety required caution at their low altitude.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported being dispatched into known icing conditions with an inoperative wing anti-ice system.
C172 instructor pilot reported extreme downdrafts in the vicinity of JLI VOR.
Captain reported that the First Officer did not want to be on the flight deck during pushback and tow operations so Captain performed duties solo.

RST TRACAB Controller and an involved pilot reported of an unsafe situation where the pilot was told to turn base leg at their discretion. Pilot reported following traffic not in site. The pilot landed; but the aircraft it was supposed to follow was cutoff and had to go around.
B737 Captain reported severe turbulence on approach that resulted in a flight attendant injury.
PA31 pilot reported the ALT INOP light and oil leak occurred after flying with six gallons of Jet A fuel mistakenly mixed with the 100LL fuel in the right main tank.
C182 pilot reported his aircraft violently rolled approximately 120 degrees to the right after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B737 in the DEN airport area.
Center Controller reported clearing an aircraft for visual approach and handoff to Tower prior to receiving a landing assured call for previous aircraft.
Air carrier pilot reported the lack of Pilot Controlled Lighting during daylight hours at MMH makes it difficult for crews to discern the runway boundary.
A TRACON Controller reported aircraft on an RNAV STAR are routinely too high and fast to efficiently sequence even while complying with the published crossing restrictions.
B767 Captain reported that company was using MEL to move aircraft instead of repairing it after noticing that the VOR head had been written up 8 times in the last 10 days.

Two Line Aircraft Maintenance Technicians (AMTs) explain why different procedures must be used to access the correct MEL illustration for deferring a High Pressure Shut-Off Valve (HPSOV) on a B777-200 engine when using a desktop computer or maintenance tablet. They had locked out a Pressure Regulating Shut-Off Valve (PRSOV) on the right engine instead of the HPSOV.
B777 Captain reported diverting to an alternate after the Purser reported electrical burning odors related to hot chargers in the cabin.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing ATL in trail of a B737-900.
B737 Captain reported he refused to taxi into a gate area with baggage carts in the safety zone.
B737-800 Flight Crew reported they descended below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude while conducting a night time Visual Approach.

An aircraft was instructed to depart a fix on a heading. The aircraft acknowledged the clearance but read back a different similar sounding fix. The Controller did not catch the read back error. The aircraft did not turn to the heading the Controller wanted due to the communication confusion.
A80 TRACON Controller reported radio issues and a back up frequency that was not available for use.
Flight Attendant reported air carrier scheduling of duty period resulting in extreme fatigue.

An employee reports he was informed by their Lead Mechanic that a Mandatory Service Bulletin inspection of Engine Front Support Pins issued by the engine manufacturer for their BK-117C1 Eurocopters also included a logbook entry requirement after the last flight of the day. The logbook entry requirement had not been included in the pilot maintenance checklist/signoff form from their company.

Flight Attendant was interrupted during the briefing by a passenger who was concerned about the seating arrangement due to COVID-19 reasons. Flight Attendant was not sure if the briefing was completed.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving excessive and contradictory clearances from ATC while descending into CLT on the STOCR 1 Arrival.

ASE TRACON Controller reports of ASE Tower sending three aircraft off the runway into his airspace that needed to be separated more. He tries to separate the aircraft and hand off to ZDV but has difficulties. He violates airspace and eventually hands off all three aircraft.
Corporate pilot reported an altitude deviation and cited lack of recent flying as a contributing factor.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a runway at this location is out of service and there are no NOTAMs.
C172 student pilot reported an NMAC after being instructed by SCT to start a descent.

Air carrier Captain reported they executed three go-arounds before successfully landing on the fourth attempt. Captain cited gusty conditions; lack of recent flying and unfamiliarity with airport as contributing factors.
A Challenger 300 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence in cruise flight at FL400 that resulted in a 35 degree roll and a 500 foot altitude excursion.

A helicopter pilot departed after a weather review indicated clear and greater than 10 miles visibility but entered a cloud layer at 550 feet. He descended to 500 feet and continued toward the destination where the weather was in fact clear.
B737NG Crew reported that while flying IMC on the DFW Runway 17R ILS inside of 15 DME; they descended below the glideslope as they took evasive action from a Runway 17L aircraft TCAS RA.
An Air Traffic Controller reported conflicting information regarding proper procedures for clearing military aircraft into block altitudes and for an Altitude Reservation (ALTRV).
Flight Crew flying 737NG aircraft received Alert from Air Traffic Control on approach.
B-737 flight crew and ATC confusion regarding 'West' and 'WESLA' transition terminology on the SFO SERFR1 STAR results in a heading deviation.

Controller worked a military flight of five C130s under the base of a MOA. The flight requested clearance to enter the MOA. A military facility is the controlling agency for the MOA.  The ARTCC Controller attempted to handoff the aircraft to the military controlling agency; but they would not immediately take the handoff. The aircraft was heading for an area of high terrain where the minimum vectoring altitude was 1;500 feet higher than the aircraft. The Controller could not climb the aircraft since the MOA was above. The MOA control facility called back to take the handoff but not in enough time to prevent the aircraft from entering the higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude area.
HCF TRACON Controller observed an aircraft descend below the published FAF altitude.
Airline Pilots reported an unstable approach. The Captain had not flown for an extended time.
ZLA ARTCC Controller reported a conflict between two aircraft on same path but different speeds.  Controller stated coordination with Departure Control contributed to the event.
B737-800 First Officer reported they pushed back onto a taxiway without clearance from Ground Control.
Ground employee reported concerns regarding the unfiltered air that employees are breathing in breakrooms and offices and recommends filters be installed to mitigate COVID-19 exposure.

A Captain reported a recent SOP change moved the required callout from 1;000 feet above approach minimums to 1;000 feet AGL; which produces unclear procedural requirements on approaches to airports in mountainous terrain.  The Captain cited three examples from recent flights and urges a return to the old procedure of requiring a callout at 1;000 feet above minimums.
Q400 Captain reported encountering wind shear and wake turbulence on approach that resulted in a go-around.
B737 First Officer reported observing passengers come in close proximity to hot tailpipes and brakes during ramp deplaning process.
