A Ramp Agent reported that after an evening arrival and parking; an aircraft beacon was turned off with an engine running while awaiting ground power. Jet blast or engine ingestion were a concern.
ZJX Center Controller reported the data tags for two aircraft with similar callsigns acquired on the wrong target and the flight plans were not accurate.
EUG TRACON Trainee Controller left an aircraft on a vector too long and it flew into airspace below the MVA.
ZDC Controller and A321 flight crew reported not being able to make the time restricted climb and receiving a TCAS Traffic Advisory.

A D01 TRACON Controller reports of ZDV ARTCC transferring communication of aircraft while still on a C tag to Denver Approach control. Similar call signs did not help the situation. Weather was a factor which lead to traffic being a complex scenario. The Controller stated ZDV does this often during this scenario.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with passenger use of masks during the COVID-19 pandemic and the lack of instructions to remove them in order use the oxygen masks in case of a rapid decompression.
B737 flight crew and aircraft move crew reported that the aircraft was damaged due to a B777 jet blast.
DH8-400 flight crew reported receiving a Master Caution fuselage doors light warning in flight.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported battery powered bag was transported as cargo with batteries attached.
A ramp agent's hand was caught beneath a belt loader's moving belt which nearly pulled him out of a B757's rear cargo. Both belt emergency stop activation buttons had been removed.
Flight Crew reported an MEL applied at a previous station was incorrectly applied and the aircraft flew in revenue service.
ZAN Controller reports of lack of supervision to attain adequate coverage to be able to adhere to the LOA between ZAN and Murmansk Center.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported a communications breakdown between Dispatch and Ramp personnel regarding Dispatch notification of DG handling.
Pilot reported misjudging the distance of an approaching aircraft and took the runway for takeoff; causing the other aircraft to initiate a go around.
Center Controller reported clearing an aircraft to an altitude that was not in his sector; citing an unusual configuration due to special COVID-19 social distancing procedures.

Two aircraft at the same altitude were entering the ARTCC (ZHU) airspace on non-radar Oceanic routes. The flight plan information ARTCC had indicated the two routes were separated. One of the aircraft saw the other aircraft and asked the ARTCC about the traffic. ARTCC had the aircraft verify their route and realized one of the aircraft was on a different route than ARTCC had on the flight plan. The controller descended one of the aircraft to ensure separation.
EUG TRACON Controller reported an aircraft that should have been climbing to 11000 feet leveled off at 6000 feet which was below the MVA.
Airline Ramp Personnel reported smart bag with Lithium Ion battery attached was loaded in cargo pit because battery indicated 'dead'.
B737 Flight Attendant reported when selected for secondary screening at BOS; TSA refused allowing crew to avoid lines; FA states lack of consistency throughout TSA.
CRJ-700 Captain reported failure to make a crossing restriction after receiving last minute runway and arrival procedure changes.
Dispatcher reported incorrect fuel burn numbers were given to a flight crew and cited workload; COVID related cleaning interruptions and ATC closures as contributing factors.
EMB175 flight crew reported momentary EGPWS warning on final approach.
SR20 Flight instructor reported a NMAC on climb out.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to wear a face mask during flight.

Air carrier flight crew and an enroute Controller reported an airborne conflict occurred when the flight crew took a descent clearance intended for another aircraft. Controller suggested that increased workload associated with COVID-19 procedures contributed to the event.
Flight crew reported overshooting altitude during climb.
Maintenance Technician reported that a course required for qualification to perform a specific task does not exist.

An Air Carrier on the SFO SERFR ONE RNAV was cleared to 6;000 FT approximately 35NM from SFO which is below the SFO Class B 8;000 FT shelf. The Captain delayed to the descent at 7;700 FT to remain near the Class B before descending to 6;000 FT at the 240KT mandated speed.
GIV flight crew reported an altitude deviation occurred when they became distracted by a cabin pressure issue and did not notice that the autopilot had disconnected.

Fractional Captain reported the ILS frequency for Runway 34 at XNA; as published by Jeppesen chart 21-2 (110.55); was incorrect. ATC provided the correct frequency of 111.55 and the approach was completed uneventfully.
A321 flight crew reported receiving takeoff clearance with a tailwind component.
Air Carrier ramp agent reported contacting an aircraft on the adjacent gate with a fuel truck due to icy conditions.
Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach and cited lack of proficiency after an extended period of time off as a contributing factor.

A TRACON Controller reported receiving handoffs of two aircraft proceeding via a new published arrival procedure that were in conflict. The Controller reports this is a recurring issue with the new procedures.

A pilot reports of being held up high by what he describes as a very poor situational awareness on the controllers part. The controller issued clearance to intercept ILS but did not clear aircraft because of crossing traffic. The controller descended aircraft and asked pilot if the could get to the glideslope ok. The pilot said no and the aircraft was then sequenced for another approach.
B737-800 Captain reported refusing an aircraft that had several flap/slat write ups and requested a test flight before a return to revenue service.
B737-700 Captain reported a lack of consensus in applying an MEL to an inoperative boost pump.

ZOB Center Controller reported ZDC Center did not want to take an aircraft due to weather; so they rerouted it into ZID Center's airspace.  Aircraft deviated for weather and went on original routing through ZDC Center's airspace.
F11 TRACON Controller reported receiving numerous arrivals simultaneously which were deviating for weather. Two aircraft passed with less than required separation.
A flight of foreign military aircraft would not fly their pre-coordinated route due to weather. One of the aircraft in the flight got lost and ATC assisted it in rejoining the flight.
Approach Controller reported vectoring an aircraft off the final approach course for sequencing placing it below the MVA.
ONT Tower and SCT TRACON Controllers reported an air carrier was vectored into higher MVA; and noncompliance with opposite direction operations resulting in an airborne conflict.
Flight Instructor reported that while taxiing behind a Falcon performing an engine run up; the airplane encountered jet blast resulting in a loss of aircraft control.
A TRACON Controller reported that aircraft were supposed to depart in one direction; but ended up departing in the opposite direction.
ZJX Center Controller worked a small aircraft and issued a suggested heading around weather; but the aircraft experienced major downdrafts.
Pilot reported aircraft began moving during a hand prop start and struck object before regaining control.
A Bombardier Challenger 650 Maintenance Technician reported that there is some confusion identifying a required inspection item for the fuel system.
Air Carrier Pilots reported the First Officer became ill inflight and was unable to perform pilot duties.
Pilot reported an incorrect tachometer and loss of power during initial climb; resulting in an air turnback and precautionary landing.
First Officer reported experiencing GPS jamming during departure from Beijing Capital Airport.
A pilot attempted to update his iPad because of an FOM change and discovered that all Company material and manuals had been deleted from the APP folder. Others reported the loss also.
Air carrier Captain reported flying the wrong transition on a STAR due to a possible FMS entry error.
Air carrier First Officer reported no sani-wipes were on the aircraft; nor available from operations.

A GA pilot attempted to land at his home airport in gusty conditions; but after a bounced landing attempted a go-around then struck a runway sign as the aircraft struggled out of ground effect. A flap was damaged on the go-around.
Air carrier Ramp Personnel reported being short staffed and unable to marshal an aircraft into the gate; resulting in a third-party ramp service improperly marshalling the aircraft in.
B-757 flight crew noticed that the preferred approach was not in the FMC database. The crew flew a visual approach coupled with manually inputted waypoints to the final approach fix.

B737 flight crew reported being cleared to line up and wait on Runway 1L at SFO after entering at Taxiway M1. A B777 is cleared to cross at M and the B737 crew believed there was insufficient clearance and has them stop. Tower sends an operations vehicle to monitor the maneuver and insure that a collision does not occur.
C90 TRACON Controllers working a mid-shift without a Supervisor were unsure how to handle ELT reports.

A B757 Captain reported that Ground Control cleared them for takeoff prior to sending them to the Tower. When checking in; the Tower cancelled the takeoff clearance as the aircraft started the takeoff roll.

Air Carrier Captain reported rejecting a takeoff on SEA Runway 16L when Tower was heard clearing another aircraft to cross that runway and the same type of aircraft was seen by the takeoff flight crew approaching the runway ahead. Tower did not advise that the crossing aircraft was behind the aircraft taking off.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track deviation occurred due to misreading a changed departure clearance.
Maintenance technician reported inadvertently servicing hydraulic system with stripper instead of hydraulic fluid; stored in an identical container.
Air carrier First Officer reported an unstable approach while on flight to maintain landing currency.
Center Controller reported that while working through COVID-19 Operations; they became very busy working combined sectors resulting in an airborne conflict.
Flight Crew flying EMB-175 aircraft encountered a Near Mid Air Collision with helicopter during approach.

A90 TRACON Controller reported a B737-900 flight crew executed a missed approach while complying with a published charted visual. The aircraft was reportedly to high on approach.  This type of occurrence is allegedly common to newer airframe aircraft according to the Controller. The descent rate is slower.
Maintenance Technician reported that an aircraft returned to service without accomplishment of proper maintenance procedures.
A320 Captain reported refusing to accept an aircraft until an insufficiently cleaned area of vomit in the cabin had been addressed.
Flight Attendant reported social distancing concerns with seat assignments.

A pilot discovered a HS 800XP's nose gear strut bracket pushed or smashed ¼ inch which may cause the strut to crack in the area of the hole where a zerk head protrudes. Maintenance will not properly address this repeated issue.
B737 Captain reported a near collision on the taxiway with an A330.
ABQ Tower Controller reported of a TCAS RA event during an opposite direction operation. The departure did not turn as instructed leading to a loss of separation.
Flight Instructor reported  student's hard landing resulted in left main landing gear brake damage with limited aircraft control.

After gate arrival; a Ramp Agent notified a B737-800 Captain that all cargo had been loaded in the forward bay while the weight and balance indicated all cargo was in the aft. The previous station ramp crew may not have been familiar with this aircraft type.
The Captain of a Bombardier CL-35 reported that he was asked to accomplish procedures NOT FAA approved in the AFM.
GA flight instructor reported inadvertently entering IMC on a VFR night cross country flight due to smoke.
Maintenance Technician reported concern from Flight Attendants of Cabin Seat installation blocking emergency egress.
Flight Attendant reported confusion about airline policy relating to the face mask policy.
B777 Captain reported he was uncomfortable with ATC handling of his flight in IMC conditions that was conflicting with a light aircraft.
Dispatcher reported flight crew neglected to notify Dispatcher of an engine component failure during flight. Flight crew contacted Maintenance directly.
SFO Controller reported NorCal was utilizing a parallel approach procedure without notifying SFO Tower or advertising the procedure on ATIS as required.
Air carrier flight crew and Dispatcher reported an inflight volcanic ash encounter which resulted in a diversion to the nearest suitable airport.
B737 Captain reported the STAR altitudes and intercept altitude from MORAM STAR to ILS 07; does not allow FMC to successfully and safely execute the modified route.
First Officer reported receiving an RA and overshooting the assigned altitude by 500 feet due to a light load.
Air carrier mechanic reported maintenance supervisor signing off maintenance work without proper qualification.
A ZAU Center Controller reported he repeatedly receives aircraft from an adjacent control area that are not in compliance with facility Standard Operating Procedures.

An aircraft needed to deviate for weather. The Controller would only approve weather deviations to the south. The aircraft could not accept the south deviation due to the increased flying miles that would cause them to not have enough fuel to reach their destination. The Controller who had just taken over at the sector did not realize this issue. The aircraft declared 'Critical Fuel' and deviated to the west on their own and advised ATC.
C172 pilot reported finding the NDB at T85 out of service and advised ATC who still showed it in service.
Motorglider pilot reported they temporarily lost control of their aircraft and climbed into Class C airspace.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy during flight.
A319 flight crew reported a small cargo fire that resulted in a diversion.
Air carrier Captain reported they encountered a brief period of severe turbulence at a reportedly smooth altitude. Three people were injured.
Private pilot encountered airport ground maintenance activity in vicinity of runway during landing.
A flight was delayed from the gate when; during the removal of the manually operated passenger loading stand; a cable was preventing the door from closing.
A B737 crew reported calling in fatigued before a third coast to coast all-night flight on the same trip with inadequate hotel sleep due to interruptions.

Pilots reported a series of poorly lighted and unlighted windmills nearby 15MO; a private airfield; that are not marked on VFR sectional chart. Pilots believe the lack of lighting causes a hazard to arriving traffic; especially at night.
Technician reported that references for removing and installing the ELT have no step for ensuring that the ELT is switched to on when complete.
SR22 Pilot initiated a climb prior to receiving ATC approval in an attempt to avoid icing conditions.
C182 pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft that did not appear on their TCAS.
Single Pilot reported seeing lights on the runway while departing which ended up being two vehicles that the controller did not acknowledge knowing about.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft did not make the AASPN crossing restriction on the DSNEE 1 Arrival. Aircraft routinely cannot comply with many restrictions on this arrival.
A Tower Local Controller observed a vehicle entering a runway with an aircraft on short final. The Tower ASDE-X equipment did not alert the controllers.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion when on a Visual Approach above the FAF altitude ATC asked them to climb to 2;500 feet for the Minimum vectoring Altitude.

Air crew in Phoenix Class B took slight evasive action for an aircraft. The pilot observed three different aircraft that he was alerted to by TCAS. TRACON only issued traffic for one of the aircraft; not the one the pilot had to evade.
A B737 Captain reported confusion after receiving a PIREP from Dispatch that ATC had no knowledge of.
A Pilot reported a NMAC with an aircraft from a flight school whose aircraft are not equipped with ADSB.
Super Tug Driver reported being advised of ground conflict with parked aircraft after tow completion.
A B737 flight crew reported a bird strike during the takeoff roll; damaging one of the engines. The takeoff was safely aborted and the aircraft returned to the gate for maintenance action.
J3 Cub pilot reported that a ground loop and runway excursion resulted after landing with a quartering tailwind.
Center Controller reported consistently working sectors with excessive workloads due to weather deviations and insufficient staffing.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported losing communication and operational control of one of his flights because of a software glitch.
B737 flight crew reported encountering multiple bird strikes after takeoff.
737 flight crew reported experiencing severe turbulence for a brief period of time.
Embraer Legacy 450 Captain reported that he inadvertently made an error entering data onto i-preflight screen which caused him to takeoff with with inaccurate performance information.

ZDV ARTCC Controller reported an airline that on numerous occasions did not fly a published departure procedure off the airport. The Controller is concerned this is a company-specific procedure and that it might become a problem when busy during the ski season.
B737 Flight Attendants reported questioning Company Operations and the Captain's decision to depart with a group of intoxicated passengers.
Air carrier flight attendants reported they feel they were coerced to work beyond the FAA mandated 20 hour duty day maximum.
A Pilot reported they recieved an RA for traffic and sighted the traffic but ATC stated they showed no traffic in that position on their radar.
Pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter at SNH airport.
MD-11 flight crew reported a communication breakdown between with ATC regarding departure clearance.
EMB-145 flight crew reported a GPWS terrain alert on a night visual approach into ROA.
Air carrier Captain reported social distancing concerns during TSA screening.

ZAB Controller reported a shift working sectors alone that were unsafe due to being over-saturated with traffic deviating for weather and the adjacent facility sector allowing airplanes to enter their airspace even though the reporter was not accepting the handoffs and had told the facility they could not accept anymore aircraft.

TEB Tower Controller reported of a NMAC between an aircraft under ATC control and a VFR aircraft. Reporter stated this is in the vicinity of an uncontrolled VFR corridor and is a very busy transition area. Reporter would like someone to control this area to help avoid conflicts.
TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation event and made reference to COVID-19 staffing levels as a contributing factor.
A320 Captain reported a CRJ being taxied by maintenance personnel entered the ramp area and jet blasted the pushback crew.

SBN Controller reports of being told by Maintenance that they would be working on frequencies and to use the main transmitters and receivers; [there are a main and stand-by].  The Controller then reports of receiving a loud noise in his ear.
Light Twin flight crew reported ATC slow to issue climb clearance resulting in two avoidable 'low altitude alerts.'
A Los Angeles; California observer reported someone flying a UAV for profit over a neighborhood. Exact location unknown.
A319 Captain reported refusing aircraft due to non compliance with MEL procedures.
EMB-145 Pilot reported receiving an EGPWS terrain warning and believes it was caused by winds pushing the aircraft towards a tower located just south of the approach course.
Ground employee wrote that maintenance failed to complete a barrier between employees to help prevent the spread of COVID-19.
Technician reported being distracted by possible lay-offs and did not complete a work order correctly.
General aviation pilot reported that a NOTAM for aerobatic activity did not provide adequate information to prepare a non-participating pilot for arrival.
Center Controller reported a problem with staffing that may have led to an airspace violation and increased workload.

A D-NOTAM changed the published missed approach altitude for the RNAV GPS Runway 17 approach at UAO. The TRACON was not aware of the change. The change was not included on the 1800wxbrief.com website.
A corporate jet Captain reported the co pilot descended too low on a Visual Approach despite their instructions and received a Terrain Alert.
B737 flight crew reported encountering a minimum fuel situation due to severe weather and lack of timely information from Dispatch.
Flight Attendant reported forgetting to brief the over the wing passengers until at 10;000 feet.
Flight Attendant reported moving passengers around due to COVID-19; but didn't brief the emergency row until after the main cabin door was closed.
Flight attendant reported a passenger had to be continuously reminded to wear a mask.
A321 Captain reported abruptly stopping on taxiway due to employee bus cutting across in front of the aircraft.
A B737 Captain and Flight Attendant describe an unanticipated encounter with severe turbulence during descent through FL300; causing injuries to passengers and flight attendants.
Technician reported misreading work cards and performing the maintenance task incorrectly.
A Tower Controller reported following all the guidelines for Tower Visual Separation and Degrees Divergence rules that may have been considered as an operational error.
B737 Captain reported ATC was operating all positions from one frequency during heavy traffic demand; with several mistakes to clearances and generally an unsafe situation.
Air Carrier Captain reported a weight and balance problem that resulted in aircraft control issues.
Air carrier First Officer reported difficulty receiving a revised clearance for weather deviation because ATC was saturated with traffic.
AC50 pilot reported entering a stall and nearly a spin due to icing in IMC conditions.
First Officer reported not knowing the airport had an operational Control Tower until Tower Controller called the crew on CTAF and advised them of the correct frequency to be on.
Atlanta TRACON Controller reported an arrival into Atlanta did not cross a fix at the Letter of Agreement Altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach. The First Officer's lack of recent flying was cited as a contributing factor.

DEN airline ramp employee reported leads have to deal with in-transit animals; including a wild wolf that was the subject of this report. Reporter stated they had no training in how to deal with animals.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving GPWS alert during approach to land at ROA airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported two passengers were aggressively arguing while the aircraft was parked at the gate during a maintenance delay.
Air carrier flight crew reported the aircraft did not start the turn as expected because they had not activated heading select.

Flight Crew and Controller reported of the flight being off course and entering Restricted Area P-56. Controller reported of aircraft being below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude and needing to vector aircraft out of P56. Pilot reported of improperly identifying the Potomac River and the aircraft to follow.
ZSE Center Controller reported they observed another Controller issue a clearance which placed an aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
ZKC Center controllers reported an aircraft had icing problems; and what ensued to get the aircraft into VFR conditions.
Air carrier Captain reported discrepancies relating to charting issues.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a failure to call for a go-around when the high minimums Captain flew an unstabilized approach to Runway 19 at DCA.
A319 Captain and Flight Attendant prevented a contract Maintenance Technician from opening armed cabin door and blowing slide into occupied jetway.
Pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter in the traffic pattern.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger reported having health issues during the flight.
MD-11 Maintenance Technician reported 3 incidents of nose wheel bearing failures.

Air carrier flight crew reported accepting the Ramp Supervisor's incorrect information regarding Lithium Ion batteries were approved for cargo compartment provided they are installed in a device.Flight crew added that the FOM provides not flight crew guidance.
B767 First Officer reported Dangerous Goods cargo (Dry Ice & Lithium Batteries) incorrectly loaded in cargo pits.  DG cargo load corrected prior to departure.

Captain reported receiving an incorrect altimeter setting from Approach Control and elected to remain on QNE for the visual approach. The Controller meant to say 981 HPA for QFE; but kept stating 2981 HPA.
Air carrier First Officer reported the flight departed with incorrect release documentation. The First Officer referenced not having flown in the last 6 months due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
EMB-175 Captain reported a misapplication of an MEL which resulted in the aircraft flying in a non airworthy condition.
A MEL'd spoiler was not locked out and floated open during flight.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported that company routes are not in compliance with FAR limitations for no over-water capable aircraft.
HPN Tower Controller and Gulfstream flight crew reported an altitude overshoot and airborne conflict when the flight crew responded to a windshear warning.
The shut-off valve on the Crew O2 Bottle was only open a small amount; therefore the O2 pressure would not recover in a timely manner.
Salt Lake Center Controller reported an unsafe situation where an aircraft was given an altitude to maintain below the Minimum IFR Altitude.
Local Controller reported clearing an aircraft for takeoff with another aircraft on short final in the opposite direction on the same runway.
Air Carrier Flight Crew reported un-stabilized approach.
Lead Crew Chief reported FAR violations due to non compliance of multiple policy and procedures regarding a smoke/fumes event.
B737 flight crew reported that the de-icing crew did not de-ice the winglets; and believed that it was no longer necessary to do so.
Instructor pilot reported UIM VOR is NOTAMed out of service.  However; the VOR approaches for F51 and 3F9 which use UIM VOR as primary navigation are not NOTAMed out of service/unusable.
Air Carrier flight crew reported an airborne conflict on departure from DTW with an aircraft on missed approach.
Center Controller reported an unsafe operation where he did not remember giving a higher altitude to an aircraft that later climbs and became a conflict with opposite direction traffic.
Maintenance Technician reported that after doing an engine run; the exhaust from the Bombardier CRJ-200 damaged other aircraft parked behind it.
Ground Personnel reported discomfort with space between to parked aircraft; one a large transport and the other mid-sized.

A N90 Approach Controller reported two instances of IFR aircraft that were in a conflict with a VFR departure. The Controller recommends changing the Class Bravo or Class Delta to a Charlie to protect these IFR aircraft.

Instructor pilot reported a loss of separation due to confusion on where they wanted to land. Their aircraft came close to aircraft departing ONT. Pilot did not advise ATC prior to descending that they wanted to land at ONT.

Boeing 787 Flight Attendant reported an adult passenger required a diaper change in his seat. The Flight Attendant questioned whether the passenger should have been boarded under this condition; citing sanitary reasons.
Air carrier Captain reported departing at night on a VFR climb without an ODP or SID available.
Air carrier First Officer and Captain reported four missed approaches due to weather and then a safe landing at an alternate airport.

A First Officer reported requesting Dispatch make a new release available because they could not print from Operations. After discovering the TCAS inoperative with an international destination; Dispatch still refused to issue new release up until the flight was delayed without it.
Balloon pilot reported that a sudden gust of wind during landing caused the balloon to impact power lines.

A ZJX Controller reports of an airborne conflict and an aircraft altitude deviation; in which he questions another ZJX Controller about the altitude the aircraft is descending to. The other Controller states he doesn't know. The reporter is also reporting the complacency and laziness of another group of ZJX controllers.

A Reserve Captain discussed his company's pilot scheduling policy which includes minimum rest periods at home between duty periods after transcontinental time changes and alternating on duty times varying from night to day.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported a flight the Dispatcher was working was denied landing clearance while on final approach because of curfew closure.
Flight attendant reported a deadheading Captain and a passenger were non-compliant with face mask policy.
Center Controller reported a traffic conflict between two aircraft when one aircraft had not been given clearance to descend.

Pilot is cleared for takeoff and is approaching V1 when he hears Tower clear another aircraft for takeoff on the same runway. Pilot wonders what would happen if his aircraft had to abort; and also wonders what type of separation is required by ATC to have two aircraft on the same runway.
UAV Operator reported an airborne conflict during routine inspection with a Crop Duster.
Commercial Airline Ramp Agent reported unloading Smart Bag with Lithium Ion battery installed.
International Air Carrier B767 Captain reported he was concerned about the safety of his airline's operations in the far Northern routes to Europe.

PRC Tower Controller reported of constant wind changes and trying to get all aircraft to land safely. Towers SAWS (Stand Alone Weather Sensors) was out of service and the Controller had to cross the room to read the ASOS (Automated Surface Observation System).
B737-800 Captain reported that the First Officer found an elevator hinge cover plate installed backwards; resulting in aircraft damage and a cancelled flight.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported not following COVID-19 seat policy in order to assist a passenger.

An Air Carrier flight crew on approach to Runway 27L at ORD is issued a 'contact Tower at the TAFFS intersection' clearance. The Captain switches to Tower immediately after and is told by ORD Tower to go back to Approach Controller. C90 Controller reminds them of the importance of not switching frequencies too soon.
NCT TRACON Controller reported two aircraft going around from parallel runways resulted in less than the required lateral separation.

BTR FLM reports of a vehicle wanting to go to the other side of the airport from their present position. Instruction is given with a hold short of a specific runway; which is the third runway that the path will cross. Vehicle doesn't hold short at first runway and crosses while an aircraft is departing that runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported an altitude deviation was the result of too much time away from work because of COVID-19 lack of flying.
B737NG Captain reported difficulties with ATC procedures and practices at MROC led to a low altitude event on approach.
B737 Captain reported lack of proper aircraft maintenance action following previous crew report of navigation equipment malfunctioning.
Mooney M20P pilot refused to fly a heading issued by RDG Tower because they were concerned about terrain clearance.
GA pilot reported penetrating a TFR.
Aircraft departed after it was removed from service due to maintenance work.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) reports about an unrecognized critical safety issue that exists for pilots to lose control of B757/B767 aircraft on approach; if the pilot seat shifts unexpectedly due to broken Seat Track Lock Control assemblies. Technician suspects the routine seat inspection task card is not sufficient to maintain adequate serviceability of crew seats.

HWD Tower Controller reported of Class Delta airspace violation.  The Controller working the Approach Control position was called by HWD Tower Controller and asked about the violating aircraft. Converging traffic was being worked by the Tower Controller. Overlying TRACON Controller never asked for a point out or advised that visual separation was being used.
Center Controller In Charge reported an unacceptable workload was placed on the facility which resulted in controllers being unable to practice social distancing at the facility.
A321 flight crew reported exiting and reentering Class B Airspace while complying with an ATC clearance.
MDW Tower Controllers reported miscommunication caused an aircraft to be cleared for takeoff with another aircraft beginning to cross the same runway.
Captain reported descent below altitude restrictions due to strong tail wind and distractions.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported feeling pressure to accomplish a beverage service even though the flight crew knew there was turbulence ahead.
Airline Captain reported difficulty establishing communication with ramp personnel prior to push-back.

An ERJ flight crew reported that; due to cloud conditions; they were unable to maintain visual reference from preceding traffic. After advising ATC of the situation they were told to level off immediately; resulting in a momentary speed excursion to 280 knots below 10;000 feet. A correction was accomplished and the flight continued uneventfully.

Small aircraft instructor reported a NMAC on final due to the second aircraft not having the correct frequency for CTAF. The second pilot was using a sectional chart that displayed the incorrect frequency.

An aircrew while on descent for landing encountered severe turbulence. One flight attendant was injured enough to require hospital care. The reporter stated that earlier warning by ATC would have been helpful.

B737 flight crew reported departing BWI on the CONLE3 to 4;000 feet. The initial heading is 285 but Departure issued a heading of 090 and level at 3;000 resulting in a conflict with a C152 and a TCAS RA. Remaining on the CONLE SID would have avoided the conflict.
A wide body freight aircraft flight crew reported severe clear air turbulence with a 40 KT airspeed fluctuation; a rapid climb and roll. The crew descended from FL380 and into smooth air at FL340.
Flight Attendant reported a group of passengers not wearing face masks during flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion at EWR airport. Inaccurate charting; poor markings; and ATC phraseology were cited as contributing factors.

Pilot reports of flying to an airport which he knows has a Control Tower operating during football games. Pilot calls CTAF and reports position. Tower Controller answers and pilot responds asking if the Tower is open today. Controller says yes its been open for 21 years. Reporter lands at airport with out any other issues. Pilot would like the Towers hours listed on the sectional; approach plates; and on the ATIS.
Ground agent reported two undeclared Lithium Ion Batteries in freight tendered for transport.

B737-800 flight crew reported clearance was tight while passing another aircraft taxiing into the alley gate. The reporter stated that in the future he would wait for the other aircraft to clear the area before proceeding.

A TRACON Controller clears a locally departing IFR aircraft and a transitioning VFR aircraft to the same 3000 foot altitude. The Controller issued instructions to expedite separation yet the IFR departure still receives a TCAS RA.
EMB-175 Flight Attendant reported hitting his head on the overhead bin when the flight encountered moderate to severe turbulence. Reportedly; no warning was given by the flight crew.
Air carrier Captain reported they were unclear on the correct communication procedures for an approach to ORD airport.  Tower was reportedly transmitting on both tower and monitor frequencies.
The Captain; while enroute to ORD; discovered that he had departed without a required alternate airport. Dispatch was unable to provide him with additional required information.
B737-800 Captain reported that while on approach to a foreign airport they were unnecessarily delay vectored and slowed down by ATC to allow other aircraft go ahead of them.

A B737-700 flight crew discovered frost on the leading edges and surfaces of the wing and stabilizer during preflight. After making the required request for de-icing; they were alarmed and irate when local operations--and eventually their Dispatcher--instead suggested they needed to review a recent company bulletin which they believed superseded the Captain's request. The Captain succinctly suggested the airplane was unacceptable to all parties and the de-icing was completed.
Airline Captain reported the crew were unaware of the need to contact ground control before pushback on the cargo ramp.
A321 Captain reported difficulties with Maintenance providing clarification in Aircraft Maintenance Log and wording on Minimum Equipment List.

A319 flight crew reported their aircraft had been sitting at the gate since the morning prior and had 24 hours of accumulated ice/snow. The flight crew noticed after the de-ice procedure that ice remained on the wing from the fuselage outward about 10 feet.

A B737 Captain reported pilot preflight distractions; interruptions; and task loads resulting from the company's current Flight Attendant training policy to include the crew in more of the cabin activities; needs; and wants.

Air carrier Captain reported a group of passengers appeared to be intoxicated during boarding.  One of the passengers was removed from the flight; the others were reportedly not compliant with face mask policy during the flight.
An airline Safety Observer reports that a new company procedure for chocking arrival aircraft could lead to injury of ramp personnel.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a wake vortex encounter at 700 feet AGL departing MSP when he was sequenced closely behind a departing B757.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported performing two go-around maneuvers in gusty wind conditions before a successful landing was made on the third attempt.

When maintenance performed a Reverser Operational Check on a B787-8 Aircraft in the terminal area; the Technician exceeded the reverse thrust idle position causing the engine to exceed the thrust limit for the check.
Captain reported updrafts in weather resulted in overshooting altitude.
Falcon 900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into IAD in trail of an A330 that resulted in a violent 45 degree roll.
Pilot reported communications issues due to Tower Controller working multiple positions on same frequency due to facility staffing issues.

An Air Carrier pilot reported their company had four flights with similar sounding call signs departing within a ten minute time period.  The reporter states this problem occurs at all their hubs.
ZMP Center Controller reported the adjacent Center handed two aircraft off to them on conflicting courses at the same altitude.
A Center Controller reported taking handoffs on two aircraft they did not recognize were converging at the same altitude due to stress from the government shutdown.
Air carrier pilot reported an in-flight passenger misconduct event.
MD-80 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B737-800 on arrival into DFW.
A321 Captain reported encountering a UAV after departing LGA.
Aircraft Technician reported using a vibration analyzer tool with overdue calibration.
B767 flight crew reported that flight attendants were injured due to turbulence during descent.
Turbojet flight crew reported a missed approach due to the winds at ASE. The aircraft entered an area with a higher MSA due to a higher speed and greater turn radius.
A321 flight crew reported that a wind shift during final approach resulted in a long landing and activation of the Runway Overrun Prevention System.
A320 Captain reported an increasing number of reroutes received from ATC enroute to DEN that resulted in fuel concerns.
A TRACON Controller reported they may have observed a false radar target on their display.
Air carrier Captain reported the displayed routing in the Flight Management System was not in agreement with the published SID for MFR.
B737-8 Technician reported not replacing a gasket on an oil line during an oil cooler replacement.
First Officer reported windshield wiper failed out of park position; causing a reduction in performance penalty that the crew was unaware of.
ZMP Controller reported of problems associated with a military flight break up after refueling. Controller reported this was a problem the day before and that it was reported to the FLM on duty.

Pilot reported of being told to cross a runway by Ground Control and to monitor Tower.  Pilot held short of crossing runway while an aircraft departed.  Pilot called Tower and received crossing instructions; pilot told Tower that Ground had earlier given them crossing clearance.  Pilot crossed two runways as instructed and taxied to parking.
Captain reported the weight manifest was over 6;000 LBS under actual weight because load planning relied on the shipper's weight figure.
E-175 flight crew reported being notified that their previously completed flight they unknowingly  transported undocumented Dangerous Goods.
GA pilot reported a NMAC with a police helicopter that aggressively blinded the pilot with a search light.
Airline Captain reported NMAC with a UAV during the turn to final approach.
BJC Ground Controller reported observing an aircraft departing from an active runway at the same time he had an aircraft taxiing across the runway.
NKT TRACON Controller reported an ATCAA (Air Traffic Control Assigned Airspace) activated even though there was an aircraft within the airspace.

Flight crew reported windshear warnings while attempting to land at the COS airport that resulted in two missed approaches. A third attempt was attempted in lieu of a diversion; and a successful landing was accomplished.
A C177 landed in a crosswind and weathervaned toward the runway edge. The pilot then executed a go-around but struck a runway sign causing a small skin puncture below the passenger door.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving a GPS EICAS alert related to possible jamming from White Sands.

HEF Tower Controller reported of a Near Mid Air Collision between two aircraft under his control. Controller issued traffic; but did not use the statement; 'Traffic Alert.' Reporter did not think aircraft would get close.

VFR aircraft that was on a Class B airspace clearance had their radar service terminated upon exiting the Class B. They were given a frequency change to contact the Tower of their destination; but were already in the Towers airspace. TRACON could have accomplished the coordination with the tower issued a late frequency change or TRACON did not coordinate and just shipped the aircraft to the Tower.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) states that aileron flight control cables had frozen on an ERJ-175 aircraft after he had deferred a Forward Galley coffee maker for lack of water. Technician noted the lack of any information in the MEL about deferring coffee makers and the possibility of flight controls freezing needs to be addressed.
A Tower Controller reported an aircraft turned base and lined up with the wrong runway; causing a NMAC with traffic on short final.

Air taxi Captain reported that several terrain warnings were received while on a visual approach to HND airport while flying through mountainous area. Reporter stated that due to the challenging nature of the area; straight-in visual approaches are difficult.
EMB-175 First Officer reported a passenger informed the Flight Attendant that she had gate checked a bag containing a Lithium-Ion battery.

B737 Captain reported being confused with either to follow the Local Control instruction or the Departure Control instruction of being on a vector or climbing via a SID. This is the third time the reporter has had this problem at IAD.
PA-18 pilot reported numerous airborne conflicts with helicopters at MYF.
EMB-175 flight crew reported exceeding 200 kts below LAX Class B airspace while flying the OHSEA2 RNAV arrival into SNA.
EMB-145 Captain reported being notified on arrival of a HAZMAT violation.
B767 flight crew describes the conditions leading up to an encounter with severe turbulence that injures several flight attendants.
A Controller allowed an A380 to taxi on a taxiway at the same time another A380 was departing the runway parallel to the taxiway. This simultaneous operation is not authorized at this airport.
BOI TRACON controllers reported radar vectors were issued to an aircraft into an area below the MVA.
Baron single pilot reported a communication breakdown and ATC error that resulted in a NMAC.
EMB-175 reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to SEA in trail of a widebody transport. Reporter recommended increased separation from this type aircraft.
B777 Captain questioned the safety and suitability of a Dispatch assigned ETOPS alternate airport due language issue; approach procedure and runway slope.
Air carrier Captain reported a speed deviation during departure.
ANC Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict between two aircraft. The reporter blamed a specific airspace as the problem and that the pilot was never issued traffic from a previous Controller.
Dash-8 First Officer reported proceeding to an alternate after weather conditions at destination caused delays.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with lack of flying and the possibility of being paired with a First Officer who has also not flown in months.
A B737 Captain reported bright reflections from three solar fields southwest of their departure airport caused temporary blindness during climbout.
C172 instructor pilot on short final reported an NMAC.
Pilot reported not receiving a traffic alert from ATC regarding another aircraft on a similar departure.
CRJ-900 First Officer reported completing an uneventful landing after activating EGPWS and ATC low altitude alert.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with another aircraft on final.
B767 Captain described the conditions under which flight planning is completed at a foreign airport with no Company Operations in a small room with inadequate communications.

A New York Center (ZNY) Controller reports of a SOP violation caused by another sector not descending an aircraft to the appropriate altitude which; in turn; caused multiple conflictions for the receiving Controller.
An aircraft was climbed through a Controller's sector without a handoff or point out.
CRJ-700 Technician reported installing wrong main landing gear piston and axle assembly on aircraft.
Maintenance Technician reported reduced bus schedules are causing overcrowded buses and increasing the possibility of catching COVID-19.
B737 flight crew reported a wake turbulence encounter at FL360 in 8-mile trail of another B737 that resulted in loss of 500-600 feet of altitude.

ZBW Controller reports of deviations due to weather and precipitation with traffic inbound to BOS. Controller let aircraft descend early and assumed BOS had the airspace; but ended up violating airspace belonging to Providence Approach.
MLI TRACON Controller reported an aircraft executed a turn wider than they expected and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B-767 First Officer reported during cruise that flight crew was not accurately informed of actual DG cargo loaded after a ground gate return.
MLU TRACON Controller used nonstandard phraseology and did not confirm that an aircraft they received was climbing to the correct altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a hot wheel warning during climb. The crew inadvertently exceeded the maximum operating altitude when extending the landing gear for cooling.
RSW Tower Controller in Charge (CIC) reported the TRACON CIC did not follow published procedures concerning changing active runways.
A319 Captain reported being issued a go-around by CLT Tower below 50 feet. When queried as to the reason for the go-around the Tower stated 'ground radar alert.'
An aircrew received non-standard; ambiguous instructions from the Tower Controller.
SoCal TRACON Controller reported an airspace violation attributed to the lack of radar through the Banning Pass.

MDW Controller and four involved pilots reported of a runway incursion. Landing aircraft attempted to follow Tower's direction; but Tower changed instructions causing confusion in the cockpit. Departing aircraft was told to cancel takeoff clearance; but was close to V1 and continued.
B737 flight crew reported a windshear encounter while on approach to ORD. They executed a missed approach and elected to divert due to the weather and their fuel situation.
ATC Developmental and Instructor reported an aircraft that did not depart as anticipated with an arrival on final.

ATC had aircraft on parallel Runway ILS approaches with monitor controllers. The Controller turned the second aircraft on to final late causing it to overshoot across the final approach course. Although greater than minimum vertical separation existed at all times; if both aircraft are cleared for approach the FAA interprets this as a loss of separation.
B737 Captain reported that company policy directs the crew to accept to specific international SIDs even though they cannot meet certain altitude restrictions.
A pilot within two miles of HMT at 2;520 FT had a near miss with a radio controlled model aircraft which passed very nearby while climbing above his altitude.
Ground employee reported the use of shared headsets exposes ramp employees to COVID-19.

SDF TRACON Controller and SDF Tower Controller reported a close airborne conflict between an IFR aircraft conducting an approach and a VFR aircraft in the vicinity of the final approach course which had just contacted the TRACON Controller. Both Controllers insist the airport needs more restrictive airspace to protect aircraft on the Instrument Final Approach course.

ZID Controller reports of observing an airborne conflict due to not being comfortable with the situation he was observing. Controller called the other area and told them to not turn their aircraft to the right as he was turning his to the left. Other sector did not point out an aircraft.
MD-11 Captain reported the company was not taking advantage of maintenace opportunities to clear the many MEL items on the subject aircraft.
EMB-505 flight crew reported that ice on a taxiway surface resulted in a taxiway excursion.
Airline ground personnel reported Hazmat loading error with no method to prevent aircraft from taking off.

LJ31 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence while on approach to ANC Runway 7R. Reporter was in trail of a B777 landing on 7L; which resulted in an uncommanded roll 'well beyond 90 degrees'. Reporter recommended ATC be more aware of the wake possibilities when using this runway configuration.

B737-700 Captain described the aircraft's condition after cold ground air conditioning operated all night condensing humid air. The flight station; passenger seats; and equipment were dripping liquid which was evaporated with warm APU air conditioning.
A flight crew had difficulty having load planning change maximum take off weight to reflect real time weather conditions.
L30 TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation due to not having an aircraft maintain visual separation while using FUSION Radar.
The pilot of a Piper PA18 recognized wind conditions approaching the recommended limit for the aircraft. During landing rollout the pilot lost control; resulting in minor damage.
B737 Captain reported that Tower issued them go around instructions due to insufficient wake turbulence spacing on the final approach course.
A TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft in conflict with an overflight helicopter they were working.
Air taxi First Officer reported inadvertently exiting the runway into a non-movement area rather than a taxiway.
CRJ-200 Captain reported responding to an RA while on final approach to 28C at PIT due to opposite direction VFR traffic also under ATC control.
ZBW Controllers reported that an aircraft was descended below the Minimum IFR Altitude in error to get it below clouds to see the airport.
B737NG flight crew reported a rejected takeoff due to a runway incursion downfield.
B757 Captain reported a track excursion occurred when they encountered wake turbulence on final approach to MEM Runway 18L.
B767 Captain reported safety concern with the current ATC procedures regarding 'Descend Via' clearances without transition; approach or runway information.
A general aviation pilot reported ATC advised of an adjacent airspace violation after being given a clearance to land by the Tower with no restriction cited.

A MD-80 engine had an Exhaust Gas Temperature (EGT) exceedance on takeoff; but no maintenance was performed upon arrival at the maintenance station nor was it MEL'd. During the ground turn at the next out station; Maintenance Control called Contract Maintenance to MEL the engine.

A80 Controller reported an aircraft descended through their airspace without being coordinated. Reporter made reference to the Optimum Profile Descent (OPD) arrival procedures as contributing to the event.
Flight attendant reported an issue with a door seal that resulted in a flight delay and maintenance action.

A new A319 Captain failed to set the autothrottles in the takeoff detent which prompted an ECAM (Electronic Aircraft Centralized Monitoring) caution. A partial reject turned the autothrottles off but the takeoff continued. At cruise the aircraft over sped because thrust was not reduced automatically.
Pilot reported flying over a sporting event during a game and wondered if they had violated a TFR.
A TRACON Controller reported radar went out of service unexpectedly and that it is a recurring outage.
Air carrier First Officer reported the crew experienced an altitude deviation while the Captain was hand-flying the approach.
ZLC Controller reports of a failure from BOI Approach to follow the LOA reference handoff altitudes into ZLC airspace.
Controllers reported a situation where aircraft could not land at their destination due to the unavailability of parking space. Contributing to the problem was poor communication signal.
Controller reported that workload and a system malfunction caused a delay in issuing a low altitude alert to an aircraft that had descended below the MVA.
Flight Attendant reported checking a personal bag with a can of Lysol inside; which was considered HAZMAT.
Tower Controller in Charge reported the Local controller did not notice a departing aircraft had aborted their takeoff and allowed an arriving aircraft to land.
PA28 flight instructor reported a taxiway excursion that resulted in minor flap damage.
ZME Controller reported an aircraft was approved to deviate left for weather. Minutes later he noticed the aircraft was turning right into oncoming traffic without permission.
Flight Instructor reported a near midair collision while executing a missed approach and go-around that was alerted by reported by ADS-B.
ZSU Controller reports of saving a loss of separation by using altitude. Tower Controller did not provide enough spacing to allow faster climbing departure following slower climbing departure.
Dash 8 flight crew reported starting an engine for cabin heat on the ground; then leaving the engine unmonitored when they left the cockpit.
C172 instructor reported a loss of control during taxi with high winds resulting in flipping over into a snow bank.

An aircraft departs TRACON Airspace; enters ARTCC Airspace; departs ARTCC Airspace back into TRACON Airspace. Automation software does not process the flight data properly; handing off the data tag to the wrong sector. This also causes confusion for controllers as to what if any verbal coordination is required for aircraft on these type of routes. This is a common problem in ATC facilities everywhere. As of this report software developers have not been able to or have not been tasked with figuring out a remedy to processing these type of flight plans.
NCT TRACON Controller reported working four sectors and failed to notice an aircraft descending to an altitude into conflict with another aircraft; resulting in a loss of separation.

HCF Controller describes a situation where an aircraft departs an airport under their control but does not follow the standard departure. Aircraft turns incorrectly and is in an area with a low MVA. Controller issues a vector towards a lower MVA and flight resumes safely.
Flight attendants do not believe that their current ground school training philosophy is in compliance with the flight attendant manual procedures.

B737-800 Captain reported being pushed back onto HNL Taxiway Z noticed a widebody aircraft off their right also on Taxiway Z. The Captain commanded the push crew to stop and noticed both wing walkers were riding the tug and not paying attention to traffic.
Maintenance Technician reported confusion and conflicting information regarding an aircraft at their station that was possibly contaminated by Coronavirus.
A pilot reported they made a turn off their final approach course for converging traffic on a base leg.
A light twin pilot departing the 88NV (Burning Man) dirt strip in a dust storm realized at rotation that he was on a taxiway and turned left to avoid overflying an aircraft on the taxiway.
Flight crew reported being notified two days after flight that they transported undeclared Hazmat cargo; lithium Ion; which experienced a thermal runaway at a downline station.
C182 pilot reported inadvertently descending below assigned altitude while monitoring traffic passing overhead.
RV-8 pilot reported a loss of directional control and runway excursion due to a gust of wind.

SCT Controller reports that hundreds of incidents of radar targets jumping away from the aircraft's actual position on the radar display have occurred since the installation of new STARS ATC processing software. Other facilities using this software have also reported the same problem.
B737-700 Captain reported receiving a windshear warning and a loss in airspeed at 800 feet AGL after takeoff from AUS.
CMH TRACON Controller reported descending an aircraft that had a fuel issue below the Minimum vectoring Altitude so they could visually acquire the nearest airport and land.

LGA Tower Controller reported of a go-around due to traffic not being airborne. Controller separated traffic and was told to transfer communications to the Departure Controller. Initial altitude of 2;000 was given. Controller thought the aircraft should have been given a higher altitude due to MVA of 2700 feet.
A320 flight crew reported cockpit windshield heat was MELed and Captain's side window froze allowing no visibility from left hand side windows.
A Maintenance Technician reported that a log book entry may have been signed as accomplished when the work was not done.

Pilot is issued clearance and advised to hold for release. Crew gets aircraft airborne and realize they didn't call for release. Center advises they listened to tapes and the controller didn't catch that they didn't say hold for release.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to ATL five miles in trail of a B757 that resulted in a 'sharp' left roll.

Departures from Runways 28L and 28R at SFO have the potential for conflict and the resulting RA when a strong crosswind is blowing. One departure is an RNAV track and the other is a runway heading. The aircraft departing in heading mode does not correct for drift and may be blown close to the track of an aircraft on an RNAV departure which is corrected for wind drift.
ATC Controllers reported after 'ATC Zero' was enacted in their building there were no procedures to assist controllers to safely close sectors.

B777 Captain reported a discrepancy between FOM and Ramp Operations Manual regarding loading dry ice and live animals in cargo compartments. The specific incident was rectified but the conflict between the manuals requires a revision.
A Center Controller reported being instructed to clear an aircraft through an Altitude Reservation area; in conflict with the guidance in 7110.65.

IND Controller describes a loss of separation due to an aircraft turning later than expected and a midnight shift procedure that runs aircraft close together. Discussion about the direction of leader lines and possible offsets were recommended by the Controller.

Flight Attendant reported that another Flight Attendant refused to wear a mask during boarding and throughout the flight. Reporter expressed concern that the health of passengers and other cabin crew were put at risk.

A Traffic management Coordinator at ZHU inadvertently approved the release of Warning Areas to the military controlling agency for a seven day period but meant to release it for the present day only. The Controller realized his error a few days later when reviewing the appropriate forms for the release. It is possible that the military agency could have been performing operations in the Special Use Airspace at the same time as the ARTCC was using the airspace for their routine traffic.
Mechanic reported experiencing questionable maintenance practices.
GA flight instructor reported a wildlife taxi hazard on a taxiway.
A VFR aircraft was not aware of a temporary MOA along its route of flight; until advised by the ZID ARTCC Controller.
Flight Attendant reported communication issues related to wearing a face mask.
Pilot reported an NMAC with an aircraft in an SFRA due to being at the wrong transitioning altitude.
DCA FSS Specialist reported a system outage affecting flight safety and described their working environment as stressful due to staffing issues.
Ramp Agent reported that while unloading the aft pit of an aircraft they encountered a chemical smell. The fire department was called and found that the smell originated from a mail bag.
Maintenance person reported heavy smoke was noted in the left hand main landing gear area accompanied by smoke that filled the bay after connecting ground power.
Flight crew reported a go around due to unstable approach and late handoff from approach controller.
First Officer reported loss of cabin pressure caused by engine bleed air switches not selected to on after a bleed off takeoff.
Helicopter pilot reported an unsafe situation involving reduced proximity to pedestrians and automobile traffic while operating into a public parking lot for a charity event.
An air carrier pilot reported the runway incursion red lights activated but they felt it was safer to continue the takeoff instead of aborting.
Air carrier Captain reported a confusing published speed restriction on KUSTR RNAV Arrival to BIL contributed to a track heading deviation.

Air carrier pilot conducting preflight inspection noticed hydraulic fluid leak around left main gear. Maintenance was called to inspect and provided a contradictory conclusion that the leaking fluid was de-ice fluid.
737 Captain reported the flight crew was encountering multiple navigation equipment failures at the same time ATC directed a late runway change.
The Tower supervisor reports controllers are not setting the airport lighting in the proper position when they close the Tower at night.
Maintenance Technician reported confusing MEL requirements for crew rest seats.
B777 First Officer reported the autopilot veered off course with a hard bank to the right while on final approach to VABB.
ZAN ARTCC Controller reported their Front Line Manager refused to disseminate pertinent SIGMETs to the appropriate sectors.
CRJ900 flight crew reported descending below minimum altitude for the area due to a misunderstanding of assigned altitude.
C172 Instructor reported multiple errors precipitated by not thoroughly considering the weather prior to flight; then encountering icing conditions during flight.
B737 Captain reported that a last minute runway change from ATC resulted in an unstabilized approach.
B737-700 Captain reported inconsistencies in the procedures regarding Lithium Ion battery powered assisted devices provided to passengers and flight crew's FOM [Flight Operations Manuals.]

An aircraft was dispatched to a foreign destination with the TCAS inoperative but the crew did not realize it until later that the Ops SPEC and MEL prevent dispatch to a foreign destination with the TCAS inoperative.
Center Controller reported clearing an aircraft for a NA (Not Authorized) Approach; and observing the aircraft descend below the MEA.
The Captain reported that Dispatch filed the SKORR SID out of JFK that was rejected by the ATC computer resulting in confusion for the flight crew when receiving their clearance.

Air carrier Captain reported taking off with a deferred item resulting in the autothrottle arm switch to be in the 'OFF' position. The deferral requires the crew to look up the EPR climb settings before takeoff; which they failed to do.
C-172 pilot reported a ground strike caused by gusting winds on touch down.
EMB-175 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival to LAX in trail of a B747.
G-V Captain reported a wake turbulence encounter at FL320 that resulted in an uncommanded 40 degree bank.
M20 pilot reported a NMAC with a C172 while on approach to RYY.
Air Carrier Captain reported being pulled back to the gate to receive 3 passengers who were not on the flight yet due to being transported in a different bus for COVID-19 distancing.
PA28 Pilot; cleared on a designated airport transition route; reported a near collision with an Air Carrier flight that had been cleared to takeoff towards the PA28's position.

Air Carrier flight crew reported being confused by a clearance to fly the FILPZ1 RNAV arrival to CLT with the north transition. The ATIS showed landing north so the crew assumed this meant to fly the transition leading to the north facing runways. The Controller meant for the crew to fly north to land south.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was suffering from the chills and a runny nose during flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC canceled take off clearance in error due to similar call signs.

ZDC controller reports of something he hadn't ever seen before. PCT has the ability to change altitudes of data tags on his center scope. Controller reports that a sector at PCT did this and it caused confusion and an airspace deviation by them doing so.
CL60 Captain reported a service door open light illuminated in flight; later determined to be a lavatory service door; which had departed in flight.
EMB500 Captain described the minor air conditioning problem; the incorrect loading of the FMS and subsequent track deviation.
C172 Instructor Pilot reported an NMAC shortly after takeoff from TTN airport with an aircraft on a go-around.
B737 Captain reported that after landing; they were still taxiing on the runway when another aircraft was given clearance for takeoff.
EMB550 flight crew reported moderate turbulence at FL450 that kicked off the autopilot and climbed the aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to wear a proper face mask citing a medical condition as the reason for not wearing it.
First Officer reported a late runway change posed time management challenges on arrival into CLT.
ZME Controller reported of an aircraft that encountered severe turbulence on its route. Controller then took steps to advise others of the turbulence.
Center Controller reported an airborne conflict while working combined sectors and cited handoff related issues contributed to the event.
B737 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to LAS that resulted in a minor flight attendant injury.

FAI FLM (Frontline Manager) reported of collision alerts that are caused by the aircrafts ADS-B. FLM states there are multiple nuisance alarms due to aircraft equipped with ADS-B. FLM would like some type of a software upgrade or an equipment upgrade to stop the spurious alerts.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing a course deviation and stated being away from flying was a contributing factor.

A flight crew was cleared for an approach different from the one they had briefed. They were able to complete the new briefing but were high and fast on final and executed a go-around. On the go-around both pilots thought the gear had been raised but it was not. Task saturation was a factor in their confusion.
Air carrier Captain reported not being fully configured for landing before 1;000 feet and cited difficulty slowing down in high density altitude conditions.
Air carrier Captain reported a runway excursion upon landing rollout due to un-reported nil braking action due to ice.
Flight Crew flying A319 aircraft landed with lower than planned fuel.
The crew received PDC's with a 'route change' note but the routing appeared to be identical to the filed route. This happened at both SAN and SLC.
EMB-145LR Captain reported flight crew departure document contained errors regarding Dangerous Goods; when there were none on board.

Flight crew did not detect that their PDC was different than what was filed by their company. The Tower Controller did not believe the TRACON's departure procedures were efficient and caused noise pollution over certain communities.
Air carrier First Officer reported that push back crew prematurely positioned aircraft behind departing B757 and encountered jet blast.
Air carrier Captain reported being confronted with operational issues with a B777 model with which they were unfamiliar. Pilot handbooks provided scant information on the aircraft differences.
J3 Cub pilot reported a near miss approaching the airport.
Pilot reported landing after broadcasting on the wrong CTAF frequency for MPR airport resulting in a conflict with another aircraft taking off on a crossing runway.

A321 Captain reported that a Flight Attendant advised of a passenger loading limitation affecting weight and balance which the Captain was not aware of and which was not noted on the takeoff performance data.
ZJX Center Controller reported needing a D-Side Controller but none were available due to a lack of staffing creating an unsafe workload on the sector.
B757 Captain reported that the Flight Attendants were required to work a 30.5 hour duty day.
Embraer 120 Captain reported that a Convair's wingtip made contact with the rudder of the Embraer as the Convair was taxiing behind the aircraft.
CRJ-200 Captain reported encountering moderate to severe wake turbulence in trail of a widebody transport on arrival into MEM.
CHA Controller reported being distracted and forgot to turn an aircraft onto the final approach course; causing it to descend below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737 MAX 8 Captain reported this aircraft could tip on its tail at the gate since a tail stand is not currently required to be used on the aircraft.

Air carrier Captain and Relief Pilot reported receiving notice from ATC; while on FLOSI 3 RNAV arrival to EWR; that it was the third time they were issuing a clearance. Reporters referenced the frequency was very busy.
Flight Instructor reported a Class C airspace incursion.
GA pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at VDF airport.
A321 Captain reported that his company's fuel dispatch protocol does not provide an adequate buffer for inflight exigencies.

Helicopter pilot; doing a clearing turn on the ramp in the vicinity of a U.S. Marshal aircraft; was approached by two men pointing assault rifles at the helicopter. After a call for assistance to local police; the situation was resolved.
An air carrier Dispatcher reported multiple problems with his software program that prevented him from properly handling a flight that encountered weather and diverted to another destination.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft had an NMAC with an unidentified VFR aircraft in the vicinity of LGB.

Immediately after takeoff; CRJ-200 crew experienced smoke in the cockpit which they determined to be Type 4 anti-ice fluid being burned by the APU caused by runoff from the fuselage. The smoke quickly dissipated and none entered the cabin. Crew recommends that until the recently imposed requirement by Canada Transport; that the fuselage of any CRJ-200 be anti-iced if the wings and tail require anti-icing; is rescinded that company policy be modified to permit unpressurized takeoffs following deicing/anti-icing.
Tractor driver reported losing control of the tug while towing across an icy ramp.
Technician reported that an MEL/NEF item on an aircraft required a reinspection and log book entry before every departure and was not accomplished as required.
B757 Captain reported having difficulty staying awake and alert on an unaugmented transatlantic flight and stated this type of operation is unsafe.
JCF Controller reported assisting radar controller during an emergency situation who was unable to handle the traffic complexity.
B777 Captain reported loss of cabin pressure on climb out caused an expedited descent and an air turnback to a precautionary landing.
BE36 pilot reported wake vortex encounter on approach to FRG from a B757 inbound to JFK that resulted in a hard hit and a 45 degree bank.

Flight Attendant reported they found a 3/4 used cigarette on the aircraft while doing initial inspections and wondered how the cleaning crew missed it.  Also; there were no alcohol based cleaning/sanitation supplies on the aircraft.
Due to weather conditions and wrong parts sent; Maintenance was unable to make necessary repairs to the damaged wire harness.
CRJ-200 Captain reported an airborne object; either a balloon or UAS; near 10;000 feet while departing MSP.
Air carrier flight crew experienced IMC conditions at a VFR only airport. ATC coordination was problematical.

The Captain of a Bombardier Challenger aircraft reported being tightly sequenced behind a heavy Boeing 747 while in the traffic pattern at LAX. He felt this placed the corporate jet in jeopardy of encountering serious wake turbulence. The situation was further exacerbated by the Tower clearing a Super Airbus 380 for takeoff while the Challenger was still on final approach.
Ground employee reported concerns about peers not wearing face masks.
An air taxi pilot reported questions related to company scheduling procedures and FAR rest requirements.
Maintenance Technician reported flight crew generated a write up when they found the circuit breakers for the pitot system with CB collars installed. The aircraft had been sitting for three days.

Tri-Cities Regional Radar Controller reported not complying with a directive that no longer allows the departure Controller to vector aircraft off an airport until they reach the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Pilot reported that the Hammond (HMU) VOR has been out of service for an extended time; nonetheless; the NAVAID is still depicted as usable on the current sectional charts.FAA NOTAM states HMU VOR OTS 1801052049-18007052111EST.
A319 flight crew reported an unstabilized approach to CYYZ due to shifting winds.
MD-11 Co-pilot reported receiving a WINDSHEAR alert; performed a go-around and returned for a visual approach with autopilot and auto throttles off.

CE525 Pilot is advised during the GPS A approach to SMO; that if the field is not in sight at CULVE (1;120 feet) a missed approach is required due to the Tower being closed. With a 700 foot ceiling the field is not seen and the flight diverts. The reporter believes that this restriction should be published as part of the approach and not announced at the last minute; especially to a single pilot aircraft.
ATC reported issues with prearranged coordination on Gulf Arrivals.
Air carrier airport operations crew is confused on who to contact for a landing gear contaminated with snow/ice.
P80 Controller reported numerous unidentified VFR targets routinely fly in close proximity to the final approach course; and suggested that the airspace be evaluated for a redesign.
Tower Controller In Charge reported that a flight of four aircraft came into the airport and while they were arriving the Local Controller departed an aircraft which resulted in an airborne conflict.
Person affiliated with a company which operates UAVs reported that an inexperienced operator lost control of a UAV which apparently crashed in an unknown location.
TB-20 single pilot reported an NMAC event during landing with a non-reporting aircraft that abruptly initiated a takeoff.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing difficulty communicating on JAX ARTCC frequency 134.85 since it was blocked by Dulles ATIS; which is the same frequency.
GA pilot reported instrument departure procedures ATC imposed upon an airport with a nearby TFR in effect are unsafe.

Regional Jet Captain was pressured by management to fly with an APU problem that required a slower speed at altitude very close to the margins. Captain instead required Maintenance to make an adjustment and logbook entry that allowed the use of the APU & normal speeds.
ZID ARTCC Controller reported that an aircraft was in icing conditions and the process that was taken to get the aircraft safely on the ground.
B767-300 Captain reported severe turbulence; causing a loss of aircraft control.
A320 Captain reported that after an oil smell in the cabin report; there was no effort to clean the pneumatic ducts.
A320 Captain reported that while being marshalled into the gate; the aircraft nose gear hit the chocks.
Transport Captain reported an area with lack of communication coverage with ATC.
B737-700 First Officer reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B757 on arrival into DTW.

Maintenance Technician expressed concern over company policy regarding duties of wing walkers during aircraft tow.
SR22 pilot reported NMAC with skydivers.
