Air carrier Captain reported on pushback from CLT that ground crew was utilizing wireless headsets and the Captain was communicating with a push crew from another gate.
Airline pushback Tug Driver reported an unsafe company policy procedure regarding tow bar handling.
Air carrier Captain reported shortcomings in the iPad device that is intended to replace paper charts.
Pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft while attempting to land at an airport where the tower was closed due to COVID-19 staffing.
Air carrier pilot reported being distracted by the Tower Controller working Ground and Local Control.
B777 Check Pilot reported the Navigation Display and FMS had different altitudes displayed when compared to the Jeppesen chart for the DIVLA 2A STAR.
TRACON Controller reported issuing a late vector resulting in air carrier entering higher MIA.

An A320 was hit by lightning prior to landing at the previous station but the Captain failed to have an inspection. On arrival at the next airport; the Lead Flight Attendant informed the oncoming Captain about the strike and no inspection. The previous Captain returned and had words.
Air carrier Captain recommended the FAA require a temporary face covering policy inside all interior buildings which serve 121 and 135 crew and passengers.
B737 Captain reported a discrepancy between the CDU Data on the aircraft and the Jeppesen plate for the SEATTLE SIX departure.
First Officer of a part 121 passenger aircraft reported exceeding FAR flight time limits.
Air carrier flight crew reported during climbout they received a route change that was not on the filed route.

Air carrier Captain reported a possibly intoxicated passenger was removed from the flight for non-compliance with face mask policy.
Ground employee reported pushing an aircraft into a parked aircraft while maneuvering through a congested parking area.

Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a CFIT event during approach to MMGL airport.
Airline Dispatcher reported receiving corrected Hazmat cargo weights after aircraft departure. Updated Hazmat data was sent to aircraft.
ZDC Controller reported a near loss of separation when a climbing aircraft unexpectedly reduced climb rate.
Falcon 900 flight crew reported that a late handoff from TRACON to Tower may have resulted in a pilot deviation.
Center Controller reported increased workload and complexity related to COVID-19 airspace closings.
Ramp Load Agent reported Hazmat cargo improperly configured for air transport.
SCT TRACON Controller reported VNY Tower broke an aircraft off the approach and turned it on downwind early below the MVA and into confliction with other traffic.
SJU Tower Controller received an aircraft from the TRACON at the same altitude and on a conflicting course with orbiting traffic known to both Controllers.
Maintenance Controller reported that a Hawker 125-800 tire pressures had not been checked for several weeks.
Air carrier Captain reported not being able to talk to ATC due to Controller working multiple frequencies and being task overloaded.
PA-12 pilot reported an NMAC with a powered parachute while he was on livestock patrol.
Balloon pilot reported making contact with a power line on descent for landing. The power line was not seen by the pilot; but was observed by a passenger too late to avoid contact.
Maintenance Technician reported the external equipment hoist failed and damaged the aircraft.
A B-737 flight crew was reported by company as having had a hard landing. The Captain under instruction reported being distracted by the HUD display.
Denver Center Controller reported an issue with similar sounding call signs and a consistent frequency problem in the area.
GA pilot reported ATC clearance issues involving similar call signs and cited Controller workload as a contributing factor.
B777 Flight Attendant reported being injured when the aircraft encountered unexpected turbulence and there were not enough jump seats in the aft galley for Flight Attendants to occupy.
GA pilot reported flying with a passenger; but not being current for takeoffs and landings to do so.
E175 Captain reported the aircraft was damaged when the jetway was pulled away with the external air conditioning hose still attached.
GTF Local Control and a pilot of one aircraft reported an NMAC. Tower Controller instructed one of the aircraft to go-around in order to separate the traffic.
Orlando TRACON Controller reported an airspace incursion and a loss of separation due to another Controller being overwhelmed with traffic and weather.

A Tower Controller was assigned to work combined Ground Control positions during a busy period. A taxiing aircraft reported to the Controller they had to 'slam on the brakes' to avoid conflicting traffic on the taxiway.
CRJ Captain reported being concerned about flying aircraft with too many MELs open.
Air carrier Captain reported having a passenger removed from a flight due to non-compliance with face mask policy and passenger misconduct.

Air Carrier pilot reports scheduling changing the crews' report time in the computer to not reflect a limo into the first flight; in order to allow the crew to fly the last flight of the day within duty limits. The crew detects the 'error' and the last flight is refused.
Air carrier flight crew reported inadvertently departing with one less Flight Attendant than required for the flight.
BOI TRACON Controller working Bozeman Handoff reported that an aircraft clipped a higher MVA during a very busy traffic session.

Air carrier First Officer expressed concern about the erosion of skills and knowledge while awaiting OE and consolidation flights. A restrictive flight schedule due to the COVID-19 pandemic was cited as contributing.
B777 flight crew reported being dispatched with a MEL restricting airspeed to 270 knots or Mach .82. They felt that the computer flight plan did not reflect these limitations in its calculations.
Flight Attendant reported three passengers would not comply with face mask policy.
Flight Attendant reported that communication with a passenger regarding exit row seating may have been misheard due to speaking through a face mask.
A321 flight crew reported difficulty taxiing at an unfamiliar airfield due to a scheduled runway closure and lack of taxiway information.
Ground employee reported a fellow co-worker is not complying with face mask policy nor properly social distancing when around other personnel.
B747 flight crew reported a hard landing; resulting from insufficient flare; caused the inertia escape reels to dislodge from their housings.
Air carrier pilot stated that the training provided by the company for the new Lido app for the iPad; was inadequate and that critical information often requires too many keystrokes to bring up.

A Miami Center (ZMA) Controller reports of a loss of separation with two aircraft after he handed them off and transferred communications to another sector. Someone had removed the assigned speeds from the data block indicating normal speed had been given; resulting in a loss and an aircraft was climbed through the others altitude.
An instructor pilot in the VFR pattern reported incorrect ATC instructions and other aircraft not responding put them resulted in a NMAC.
Air carrier flight crew and TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict for skydive operation aircraft which maneuvered too close to a commercial fixed winged aircraft.
Ramp service was able to stop in order to avoid a collision with an aircraft which taxied around a corner during push back from ORD C Concourse.
Tower Controller reported an operational error while working five positions at once.
A80 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft was 700 feet high at a crossing restriction of a new RNAV Arrival route.

ZLA Controller was notified that an aircraft they cleared for approach to an uncontrolled airport attempted to land on a closed runway. ATC did not ensure the aircraft had the current ATIS information or NOTAMs.

GA pilot reported inadvertently leaving the transponder code set from the previous flight. The pilot was accustomed to getting a code from the Tower; however the Tower had closed early due to COVID-19 hours and the pilot did not check the transponder.
A B737 flight crew diverted to an airport not on their flight plan when their destination airport closed and weather at their filed alternate precluded landing.
B737 Captain reported ATC requested them to maintain visual separation from a preceding departure while they were still taxiing to the runway.
B777 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence; windshear; and lightning during approach to ORD and diverted.

A corporate flight crew arrived back into the United States with a company employee on board. The Customs Officer threatened to fine the Captain because the employee on his aircraft did not have a passport and was undocumented.
EMB-175 First Officer reported a Flight Attendant felt pressured to continue flight despite an ear blockage.
Corporate jet pilot reported receiving several revised clearances in a short amount of time from a very busy ZLC Center Controller due to GPS jamming occurring in the area.
Flight Instructor reported fuel starvation during descent resulting in a forced landing.
Flight crew of two small aircraft in formation reported flying below minimum safe altitude.
A320 Captain reported that he was not notified of special needs passengers aboard his flight; causing a potential safety risk in the event of an evacuation.
HS-125 First Officer reported Oceanic ARTCC was not happy with their decision to operate without an HF radio on a flight from an international airport to the U.S.
HIO Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict when an arrival did not comply with instructions and came into conflict with a departure.

An Airbus flight crew reported that; while performing a parallel visual approach; ATC assigned several speed control clearances which resulted in a wingtip to wingtip situation on final approach. An RA was generated by the proximity of the two aircraft; but visual contact was always maintained; and the flight crew continued the approach and landing without incident.
Center Controller reported weather deviations caused their sector to become overloaded; and Traffic Management software was not working correctly.
Captain reported slipping on icy ramp which had previously been reported as a hazard.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported interacting with a sick passenger in flight who was possibly exhibiting COVID-19 symptoms.
CE-560 flight crew reported overshooting assigned altitude on descent due to inadvertent autopilot disconnect and distraction with iPads.

Due to weather deviations; sectors at ZKC ARTCC became saturated with traffic. Controllers stated the supervisors were not doing enough to relieve the congestion. A Controller issued an instruction to an aircraft which the aircraft did not respond to causing conflict with another aircraft at the same altitude.
B737NG Captain reported window visibility issues when flying in heavy rain.

An First Officer reported that upon meeting a FAA Maintenance Inspector for the first time at their departure gate he said 'I hope I don't have to bust you guys today.' The Inspector's normal work was at a desk and his comment did not help build a trusting relationship.
A Tower Controller reported allowing an aircraft to land even though the Airport Surface Detection Equipment (ASDE-X) alarmed for preceding go-around traffic.
CE-750 Captain reported they were unable to make assigned altitude at their coast-out point when the temperature rose above forecast.
B737 Captain reported iPad Jeppesen FD Pro taxi chart defaults to small scale; difficult to read; moving map on landing resulting in a dangerous situation.
ZDV Center Controller reported an aircraft departing VFR; needing IFR; and entering a lower Minimum IFR Altitude area.

Technician reported after conducting a seat belt inspection; the aircraft was removed from service due to parts not available; the aircraft was released for service by management with incorrect seatbelts installed.

EMB-140 Captain reported that after being informed about approaching the duty limit in FAR 117; declined to accept an extension and was threatened with disciplinary action even though the First Officer was not.
A B737-900 Flight Attendant placed a passenger in a flight attendant jumpseat during turbulence when no passenger seats were accessible because of a beverage cart in the aisle.
Maintenance Technician reported that aircraft was operated without legally required flight data recorder.
The pilot initially refused a clearance from Chicago Center for weather; but he was convinced to accept it and later was able to deviate with Rochester Approach.
Flight Attendant commented that a particular aircraft cabin crew jump seat is positioned so as to restrict appropriate access to oxygen bottle.
A Maintenance Inspector reported that some Ram Air Turbines (RAT) are not properly stowed; when this occurs the RAT is unable to deploy.
Aircraft tug driver reported multiple loss of communication incidents since transitioning to wireless headsets for pilot/tug communications.
Captain reported not being able to find guidance information regarding a passenger's portable Heart Pump device batteries.
Instructor pilot reported an NMAC on approach to AUO airport.
B-737 Captain reported failure of the FMS to capture final approach course following a vector to final.
Jet Bridge Operator at IAD reported being ordered to place the jet bridge on an aircraft that was not chocked in accordance with Standard Operating Procedure.

A ZDV Controller reported of a lack of coordination from the overlying sector. Aircraft descended with a hand off into his airspace. Other than data block information the Controller reported the other sector should have coordinated something verbally. The reporter stated there was no clear direction in any documents as to what should happen.
A Part 91 flight had difficulty with clear radio communications with ATC on an IFR flight plan.

Air carrier First Officer reported there was no food available at the hotel and food was not boarded for crew to consume during the flight. COVID-19 related time constraints were cited as contributing factors.
Maintenance Technician reported that a Service Difficulty Report (SDR) was submitted for a crack found on an EC135 helicopter nearly a month after discovery due to the assessment of the condition.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing a flap overspeed during initial climb and cited distraction while talking to ATC and recency of experience as contributing factors.
ZSU ARTCC Controller reported an unsafe situation relating to frequency blind spot.
On a CRJ-700; an incompatible Ground Proximity Warning System (EGPWS) was installed because the parts manual was not correct as to the compatibility of the EGPWS for this aircraft.

EMB175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of an A320 on visual approach to SFO. Reporter was critical of ATC procedure that put him in trail of an aircraft that was upwind of them.
An Air Carrier Captain conducting a night time visual approach reported receiving a Terrain Warning due to not following Company guidelines but continued the approach.
Air carrier Captain reported a 30 minute delay in response from Dispatch regarding a deviation for weather.  Dispatcher cited reductions in staffing due to COVID-19 as contributing.
Two ASE Controllers and an air carrier flight crew report of an Opposite Direction Operation [ODO] where an aircraft is late to depart and does not take new heading to avoid inbound traffic.
B737 Captain reported a ground conflict with a tug and baggage carts as they moved through the ramp area.
A Denver Tower Controller reports of an Opposite Direction Operation (ODO) that led to a loss of separation.
Commercial Airline Ramp Personnel reported on going issue of recording Hazmat items and passenger baggage.
EMB-175 Captain reported that because of weather conditions and low fuel on board the crew landed below restricted captain minima.
B777 First Officer reported a passenger's auxiliary lithium polymer battery began smoking in flight and was hot to the touch.
B737 flight crew reported being unable to depart prior to fatigue setting in due to an equipment problem at the destination airport.
B767-300 Captain reported being on short final at 300 ft AGL and having to execute a go-around after ATC cleared an aircraft for takeoff; but did not depart in time.
Columbia 400 pilot reported he was picking up ice and unable to maintain altitude. Pilot descended below MVA to get clear of clouds.

An Air Carrier pilot on an RNAV STAR at night in moderate turbulence and heavy rain reported taking evasive maneuvers to comply with a TCAS/RA advisory for conflicting traffic. They received no traffic advisory from ATC.
Air carrier aircraft First Officer reported that while taxiing into position for takeoff; their wing was lifted by jet blast from a departing B777.
Flight Crew flying CRJ-200 aircraft encountered a Near Mid Air collision with a Cessna aircraft.

A Flight Crew and Dispatcher reported the flight departed with incorrect weight and balance information due to being distracted after issues refueling on the ramp with an engine running. The flight crew was not familiar with refueling with an engine running and could not find any guidance or published procedures that allowed this.

A large transport flight crew reported missing a speed restriction note on an arrival page in their iPads. They exceeded the published 280 knots published speed restriction. ATC asked another flight on the frequency if they saw the note and they replied that they did not see the note either. ATC stated that there was another flight that day that did not see the note.

Two flight attendants reported being notified that another Flight Attendant on the same flight had possible exposure to COVID-19 and as a result occupied a different jumpseat in order to maintain social distancing from cabin crew.
UAV operator reported unknowingly entering class D airspace.
GA Pilot reported his flight plan was not closed soon enough before search and rescue was initiated.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing an altitude overshoot during climbout while hand flying and attributed it to lack of flying.
MD-10 Captain reported Hazmat procedure violation of not signing DG document prior to departure.

The reporter observed that there is no written standard for the FAA commonly used phrase; 'Report a 3 mile left/right base;' and that the phrase is sometimes misunderstood by pilots; resulting in a pattern that alters aircraft spacing; separation; and sequencing.
ZDC Center Controller reported working an overloaded sector with weather deviations due to lack of traffic flow restrictions was unsafe.
C172 pilot reported a hard landing resulted after he encountered rotor wash from a helicopter operating on the ground near the approach end of the runway.
C175 Flight Instructor reported a runway excursion occurred when he failed to properly communicate to his student his intention to take control of the aircraft.
ORD Tower Local Controller complying with noise abatement procedures reported a loss of separation between successive departures.
Flight crew executed two LDA approaches into DCA resulting in missed approaches. Flight diverted to nearby alternate.

A large transport's EGPWS alerted PULL UP on the BZN ILS DME 12 at 11;000 feet. The First Officer took the appropriated action and the alert ceased at 14;000 feet. The terrain beneath the aircraft rose to about 9;400 feet which may have activated the terrain closure feature.

An Embraer regional jet Captain reported his aircraft's Pack 2 chronically malfunctioned. The MEL permitted pack off flight with a functioning recirculation fan; but after he discovered his aircraft's recirculation fan was inoperative; Maintenance was reluctant to address the situation.
CRJ 900 First Officer reported that a post flight inspection revealed a blown main gear tire and associated damage to the flaps and wing.

The Captain of a Bombardier CRJ-900 reported evidence of hydraulic fluid dripping from the left main landing gear door. Maintenance called it 'dirty water'; but it was verified that was indeed hydraulic fluid.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported aircraft en route with Hazmat cargo was incorrectly loaded at departure. Flight continued to destination since no immediate safety issue existed.

MDW Local Controller reported that an aircraft taxiing for departure crossed the hold short line. The hold short line and other taxiway/runway markers were obscured with snow. ASDE-X sounded an alert. An arriving aircraft was issued a go-around.
EMB-175 Captain reported that chart holders on the DV windows have been installed such that they impede the window from being opened.
A Pilot reported several instances of VFR Flight plans being dropped from the system when filed electronically in Alaska
Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict. The Captain stated fatigue and months without flying contributed to the event.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation and cited First Officer's lack of flying as a contributing factor.
S46 TRACON Controller reported having to vector an aircraft off of their final approach course to avoid an unidentified VFR target.
Air carrier Captain reported the Tower issued them a 'Standard Taxi Route' which was not available in their Jepp Charts or EFB.
Aircraft was assigned 270 knots at 13;000 feet and did not reduce to 250 knots below 10;000 feet.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy.

Helicopter pilot reported that after landing; another aircraft was cleared for takeoff from the opposite end of the same runway; resulting in the helicopter taking evasive action. Pilot reported that several other aircraft and confusing ATC radio communications were part of the overall situation.
ATC Controller reported fatigue and safety concerns due to 6 day a week shifts and short staffing related to the COVID-19 pandemic.

Citation Captain (who had been the SIC of the inbound flight) reported that during the preflight inspection he discovered two electrical box covers laying on top of the flight control cables and aft pulley on the bottom of the aft accessory compartment. The aircraft had been flown for nine flights by this crew after picking up the aircraft two days earlier. One of the discrepancies cleared had been a repeat write-up for the right Environmental Control System (ECS) fault tripped in that equipment bay.
An Airline Captain reported that the aircraft was not configured and had to abort takeoff.
MD-11 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence that resulted in a 'rapid roll' from opposite direction B747 1;000 ft above their altitude of FL380.

Center Air Traffic Controller reported two aircraft 'descending via' on crossing STARs were going to have less than required separation due to one aircraft not complying with its crossing restriction.
ZJX Center Controller reported a complex traffic workload due to weather deviations and insufficient traffic flow management through their airspace.

Air Carrier Captain reported that his company airport page states that Taxiway D5 north of Taxiway C at CMH is restricted and it should read that the portion of Taxiway D5 north of Taxiway D is restricted.
B737 Captain reported concern over what he saw as inadequate and ineffective maintenance on their Number 2 engine strut.
A320 Pilot reported being told; post flight; that a passenger had boarded the flight with an oxygen tank.
Pilot reported flying aircraft without proper maintenance inspection documentation.
Airline ground personnel reported undocumented Hazmat transported to subsequent downline station due to not being off loaded at first station.
Air carrier Captain reported that continual; significant changes in crew schedules over the past months are causing cumulative fatigue.
Air carrier First Officer reported the Captain of the flight created a 'toxic' cockpit environment.
A pilot reported their engine quit so they landed safely on a road where they discovered the fuel selector valve was in the off position. They departed the road and returned to their airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported failure of deicing crew to properly deice aircraft; resulted in multiple efforts to get deiced
ZAU Controller reported receiving a point out that was not coordinated according to procedures. Controller reported the automated point out procedure currently practiced is unsafe.

B737-700 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into LAX in trail of a B777 that resulted in multiple 30 to 45 degree rolls in both directions. A Flight Attendant sustained minor injuries in the encounter.
B757-200 Captain reported they over-sped the flaps during climb and a subsequent stick shaker event; and that afterwards; they neglected to document these events into the logbook.
EMB-175 flight crew reported two wake turbulence encounters inbound to IAD in trail of a B777 that resulted in 'abrupt' rolls and loss of altitude.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a ramp incursion and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.

A300 Dispatcher reported a weight and balance discrepancy was discovered by a Ramp Agent after the flight had already departed. The Dispatcher sent the correct weight and balance to the flight crew enroute.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering a severe mountain wave.
Captain reported that a ferry flight  was missing the Ferry Permit.
B737-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing JFK in trail of a B757.
MSY TRACON Controller working combined sectors during heavy traffic with weather deviations allowed an aircraft to fly less than the mandatory 3 miles from an obstruction displayed on their radar.

An MCO Controller reports of a loss of separation between two aircraft on final. The Controller tried to break out the second aircraft but it was not on frequency until the loss had happened. The second aircraft landed.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not complying with the face mask policy.
First Officer reported missing a crossing altitude on the DRLLR FIVE Arrival into IAH.
A private individual reported being advised by the CLL Airport Manager that he should cease flying his drone below 400 FT within 2.6 miles of the airport.
Tower Controller reported similar sounding call signs from a local flight school routinely cause confusion and increase workload.
FAR TRACON facility scheduled a radar outage. The Controllers were not advised or briefed about procedures to implement during the outage.
C180 pilot reported loss of directional control on landing rollout when he encountered wake turbulence and rotor blast from a helicopter landing nearby.
Flight Attendant reported that cleaners were barging onboard and 'swimming' upstream while passengers were trying to deplane.
A Glider pilot reported a NMAC with a fixed wing aircraft while in a published glider flying area that is close to a busy airport on one side and a mountain range on the other.

Air carrier Captain reported being issued taxi instructions in at SEA airport to hold at a geographic position marking that is not depicted on the normal SEA airport taxi diagram. Reporter suggested that the marking which appears only on the low visibility chart should not be used in taxi clearances when airport is not in low visibility conditions.
GA pilot reported a communications breakdown with ATC regarding clearance resulted in airspace violations.
Aircraft parking chocks were removed without the parking brake being set causing the aircraft to move a short distance.
Air Carrier flight crew reported deviating from clearance on go-around.

Air carrier First Officer queried his Captain; the flying pilot; if he saw the towers southeast of the BHM Runway 6 MCDEM outer marker while turning and descending on a night visual approach. The positive response was followed by an GPWS obstacle caution; a turn towards the towers; and then evasive turns away from the towers.
A Controller was informed of a pilot who received an erroneous altitude assignment through PDC.
Pilot reported difficulties in resolving airspace conflicts with a TFR which overlies airport 47N.

Air Carrier Captain reported company procedures were not followed during a mechanical irregularity. Captain stated outside issues; including pressure to maintain schedule; disrupted the normal flow of crew duties.
Flight Attendant reported the disinfecting spray used for sanitizing aircraft is irritating to eyes and nasal passages.
B777 Captain expressed concern that recent approach procedure SOP changes at his company are confusing and not as safe and efficient as past practices.
Balloon pilot reported flying in unforecasted IFR conditions for about 15 minutes before finding a place to land.
B737 flight crew reported that due to turbulence VNAV PATH would not engage and causing the autopilot to disengage.
MD11 Captain reported encountering severe clear air turbulence at FL380 near SLN VOR which resulted in a momentary overspeed and stick shaker.
A 737 First Officer reported being unsure of their assigned altitude climbing out of BWI on the TERPZ6 SID due to the phraseology used by ATC.
B767-300 Captain reported hazmat was incorrectly loaded; ramp personnel corrected the error.
B737 Captain reported the SNA DSNEE 1 RNAV Optimized Profile Descent (OPD) altitude constraints between ROADE at 17;000-FL190 and PHIYA at or below 13;000 ft are beyond B737 descent capabilities.
B737 Captain reported that after deicing in freezing drizzle conditions ice was noted on the aircraft nose. A wing check revealed the need for additional deicing.
Corporate jet Captain reported a communication issue between ATC facilities resulted in an airborne conflict.

B737 Captain reported difficulty and stress in working with Guatemalan controllers. He was given a revised; unacceptable takeoff clearance by MGGT Tower and did not receive the winds for takeoff. Captain also stated the RNAV (RNP) Runway 20 Approach is allegedly flawed in its design. It consistently positions the aircraft low when on the glidepath at the decision altitude.
The crew encountered a fireworks display on approach to KLAN 28L.
Flight crew and Maintenance reported departing without the correct ferry permit because of the addition of an MEL prior to departure.
Maintenance Technician reported confidentiality breaches by Company Management.
Captain reported the transport of undocumented Hazmat package.
A Center Controller reported their sectors are being overloaded with complex traffic and Traffic Management Units are not doing anything to affect the flow.
Technician reported that a B737 aircraft flew with missing Krueger flap deflectors and fairings.

Air carrier Captain reported becoming ill after working on a trip with a sick Flight Attendant who did not inform all crew members that she was feeling sick and then later tested positive for COVID-19.
Corporate turbojet Captain reported issues with Approach Control while landing in marginal VFR conditions.

IAD Tower Controller and flight crews reported a ground conflict at IAD airport.  Controller reportedly forgot about clearing an aircraft for takeoff and then clearing crossing traffic downfield on the departure runway.
Air Carrier flight crew reported declaring minimum fuel due to weather hold and windshear go-around.
Clearance Delivery issued a clearance to an aircraft which was read back incorrectly. Clearance Delivery did not catch the mistake.

Four Controllers reported on a loss of separation with a VFR and IFR aircraft. The IFR aircraft did not have its transponder on and SLC Tower Controller did not advise the pilot. Aircraft departed and climbed through VFR aircraft's protected airspace and was not noticed until passing the VFR aircraft.

Air carrier Captain reported the First Officer made a sudden steep turn on departure while following command bars. The Captain took control of the aircraft and noted that the FO was very tired and had not done a takeoff or landing in over a year.
Air carrier flight crew reported exceeding 250 knots in order to remain separated from an aircraft conducting an approach to a parallel runway.
Helicopter pilot reported concerns with 1;000 foot towers that have had inoperative lighting for extended periods of time.  The tower and guy wires are reportedly difficult to see at night.
Aircraft tug driver reported difficulty with the new wireless headsets used for communicating with flight crew during pushback.
Air Carrier Captain reported taking evasive action responding to an RA for an unidentified VFR aircraft on their approach course to PAE airport.
B737 First Officer reported that MDW airport taxiways are not correctly charted during construction.
EMB-175 Captain reported Hazmat transported without required crew verification.
Flight Attendant reported having difficulty maintaining social distancing while at the jumpseat.
A Flight Crew reported they had to make a sudden stop to avoid conflicting taxiing aircraft.
An aircraft flew into the BJC Class D airspace without an ATC clearance. The aircraft was confused by the VFR flyway planning chart and thought they were outside of the Class D.
Air Carrier flight crew reported obtaining an IFR clearance before Tower closed; however; they departed after Tower closed and did not obtain an IFR release for departure.
B757 flight crew reported accidental depressurization in cruise when troubleshooting a pack malfunction.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported that they were late configuring the aircraft for landing.

GA pilot reported that; due to deteriorating weather; an overnight at a location other than the destination would have been a better idea than continuing the flight. But the COVID pandemic made the intermediate stop unappealing.
B737-800 flight crew reported a Flight Attendant was injured during a wake turbulence encounter on approach to PHX in trail of an A321.
Center Controller reported an airborne conflict.
Captain reported being notified of a 'significant accumulation of ice' remained on left wing after deicing at departure airport.

NCT Controller reported of having an aircraft inbound to a destination and descending it to an incorrect altitude. Center called and reminded the Controller of the MVA. Reporter tried calling aircraft three times to give it a correct altitude. Aircraft went back to centers frequency because reporter's frequency was coming in broken.
A flight crew reported responding to an RA for an aircraft descending head on to the same altitude they were descending to.

A C172 pilot reported encountering rain; sleet; and icing enroute. He made some quick decisions that included diverting to exit the weather and a modified instrument approach to minimize his time in the icing.
A B737 Captain reported his Dispatcher would not return a requested call about winds inflight because their carrier's dispatchers are overwhelmed and understaffed.
B-737 pilot reported neglecting to change the descent altitude after receiving a change in assigned runway from ATC late in the Arrival procedure.
A Mechanic reported a Cessna 172 was flown in an unairworthy condition due to the annual inspection being 4 days overdue. The ferry permit was inaccurate therefore illegal.
Flight crew reported difficulties with the FMS while conducting approaches into MRY below published minimums.
B747 Flight Crew reported the Flight Management System directed an incorrect departure procedure.
A Boston TRACON (A90) Controller reports of having difficulty with an aircraft transponder. The Controller stated the data tag would go into a coast mode and he had to tag up the aircraft often.
Captain reported confusion with ATC over procedures followed during a precautionary landing due to a slow tire pressure leak reported inflight.
D21 TRACON and DTW Tower Controllers reported a loss of separation due to different climb rates on two departures.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving an RA after ATC cleared them to climb to another aircrafts altitude.
Two air carrier First Officers reported during climb from SBGR airport taking evasive action to avoid a balloon.

After landing on and clearing EWR Runway 29; a flight crew was cleared across the open ramp to Taxiway J. The Captain was confused because the commercial EFB airport depiction did not look like the airport's massive open ramps and indistinguishable taxiways.

B737 First Officer on the ROBUC1 RNAV arrival to BOS is cleared to 6;000 feet and to intercept the Runway 4R localizer when able but comply with the altitude restrictions. This does not make sense to the reporter and is not clarified prior to landing.
BE33 pilot reported battery failure in iPad and landed at the wrong airport.
A300 Captain reported he was advised by ATC of weather ahead that the crew was unable to see on radar which resulted in an altitude excursion above and below assigned altitude due to turbulence.
Ramp procedures were not followed properly at EWR; and ramp personnel wandered close to a running engine intake while parking which forced the crew to shutdown the engine prematurely.
B747 Relief Pilot reported encountering windshear on landing. Upon parking; Maintenance discovered damage to the outboard engine.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a deviation for weather in oceanic airspace; after deviating a message was received from ATC that denied the deviation.
First Officer reported a crossing restriction overshoot during departure.

A300 Captain reported arriving to aircraft with main cabin door open while it was raining. Inspection was completed and signed off by Maintenance; but Avionics Tech found E&E compartment filled with water.
Airline Safety Personnel reported the removal of Hazmat shipment due to insufficient required Dangerous Goods documentation upon loading.
A319 flight crew reported that when flying the noise abatement departure from LGA Runway 13 the track appears to diverge from the TNNS6 departure.
A TRACON Controller reported an unidentified VFR aircraft climbed to an altitude below Class C airspace; but was still in confliction with another aircraft on an approach to the Class C airport.
CE-525 pilot reported impact with bird on takeoff roll and rejecting takeoff.
First Officer reported maneuvering for a TCAS RA; while being vectored for an ILS to EWR; that resulted in a second RA with another aircraft.
GA Pilot reported a NMAC after another aircraft entered the runway and took off as the reporter was landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported he had inadvertently pushed the thrust levers into detent setting on takeoff; which was not recognized until reaching cruise flight.

Tower Controller reported the Full Route Clearance (FRC) indicator printed in the remarks section was located where it may not be visible to the Controller resulting in an aircraft not receiving the correct departure clearance.

After flight crew received a clearance to expedite crossing PHL Runway 27R; they were subsequently told to hold short. Unable to comply due to having already passed the hold short line; they were re-cleared to cross runway. This resulted a go-around for an aircraft on final approach to the same runway.

An A-319 crew inadvertently struck an air conditioning cart with their number one engine while taxiing in to their gate. Both piloted report that there was no left side wing walker present. The Captain stated that the lead marshaller continued to direct him forward; but that he stopped when he noticed the concerned expression and gestures of another company employee who was observing the parking operation.
SCT TRACON Controller reported observing a false radar target on a conflicting heading with traffic they were vectoring for an approach.
B777 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence during cruise flight.
Gulfstream IV flight crew reported a wasp was discovered in the cockpit after takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported having to use bedding and pillows during crew rest that were previously used.
B777-200 flight crew reported they queried Dispatch about a high fuel burn and were told the operating empty weight used to calculate fuel burn was more than 92;000 pounds light.
CRJ200 flight crew reported an airspace violation shortly after takeoff from DCA due to execution of a windshear escape maneuver.

A single piloted aircraft on takeoff saw another aircraft further down the runway. The pilot rotated early; nearly missing the other aircraft. The reporter mentioned that there were 'many foreign students in training...' and that that fact contributed to his not detecting the traffic conflict before takeoff roll because the other aircraft did not respond to his departure announcement.
Pilot reported an unstable approach and requesting a 360 turn on final from Approach. Reporter cited lack of flying and distractions during the approach as contributing factors.
Flight Attendant reported moving to an unassigned jumpseat for social distancing purposes. The move conflicted with FAR regulations.

A MD-11 Dispatcher reported the company's computer system does not allow full MEL's to transfer from one computer to another sometimes cutting text off in mid sentence or creating new lines mid sentence so weights could be hundreds or thousands of pounds. Flight Plans and Releases are affected also.
ZDC Center Controller reported training related to intercept procedures.
Air taxi flight crew reported a wingtip strike during a go-around following a wind shear warning.

Air carrier flight crew reported the FO may have pressed the autothrottle disengage button rather than the TO/GA button during takeoff roll. The flight crew rejected the takeoff and cited the FO's lack of flying as a contributing factor.

A321 flight crew received a TPS for departure from Runway 19L at SFO indicated flaps 1 was required. The normal and engine out procedures for this runway both required a 30 degree banked turn immediately after takeoff which required a flap setting of 2/3.
N90 TRACON Controller and ZBW ARTCC Controller reported disagreeing on the interpretation of the facility directives regarding handoffs from the Center to Approach Control.
Flight Attendant reported being asked to continue flying after being notified of being in contact with a crew member who had tested positive for COVID-19.
Airline Cargo Agent reported being advised by TSA of paint spray can in checked luggage.
Flight Instructor reported an NMAC while on short final.
A passenger reported being initially startled by a Flight Attendant PA banter on landing until he realized the theatrics were not aircraft related noise.
Airline Captain reported an unknown individual asked the crew to release the aircraft brakes for pushback; resulting in a ramp safety issue.
ZMA Center Controller reported attempting to stop an aircraft's descent at the last minute for VFR traffic; but receiving no response.

A Controller radar identified a departure and noticed they had converging VFR traffic. The IFR departure was below the Minimum IFR Altitude so the Controller could not vector the aircraft to avoid the traffic.
Pilot reported the Tower closed due to COVID-19 staffing; and was not sure as to what they should be squawking.

An air carrier flight crew reported encountering significant wind shear while conducting an approach. They executed a missed approach and; due to a critical fuel state; requested a traffic priority and diverted to an alternate airport.

An Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) reports that the current fueling and defueling procedures in their maintenance hangars result in employees being exposed to high amounts of fuel vapors; environmental non-compliance; and an unsafe workplace. A previous system of fuel tank venting did not allow vent ducts on the hangar floors.
Air Carrier maintenance person reported Company advised maintenance procedure does not require misplaced tool to be located prior to aircraft departure.
PAO Tower Controller reported a NMAC between an aircraft in their pattern and an aircraft deviating from the adjacent airports final approach course.
Air carrier First Officer reported a miscommunication problem with TPA TRACON resulting in an altitude deviation and attributed it partly due to not flying because of COVID-19.

MD-83 high minimums Captain diverted due to weather at destination and alternate going below legal minimums. Problems with airport equipment and obtaining a gate turned into a lengthy delay. Flight arrived at destination airport 9 hours after departure.

ZMA Controller reports of airborne conflict between commercial traffic and unknown traffic at the time.  Pilot of unknown aircraft reports that he was in the confines of a MOA when he saw a commercial aircraft off his right side in a 10 degree bank.
MD11 Captain reported that prior to departure from a foreign country; the government aviation inspectors were doing an unscheduled physical check of the aircraft and the paperwork.

B737-700 First Officer reported missing a crossing restriction on the BRUSR1 RNAV to PHX. ATC had assigned one altitude change and two speed adjustments during the arrival and the crew experienced a TCAS event just before the altitude overshoot.
Air carrier flight crew reported an over speed during departure and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Falcon 2000 First Officer reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence approaching LAX at FL260 14 miles in trail of a B777.

CRJ900 Flight Attendant described an unexpected encounter with severe turbulence during beverage service. The service cart hit the ceiling and landed back in the aisle without injuring anyone. No warning was received from the flight crew.
B737-700 flight crew reported landing from an unstabilized approach to DEN.
B767 First Officer reported that they received information of a possible Flight Information Region closure while in cruise.
Flight Attendant reported a cleaning crew had to be reminded to properly sanitize the aircraft prior to passenger boarding.
C172 pilot reported his aircraft became airborne when it entered an area of jet blast; rose about five feet in the air; then nosed over and incurred a prop strike.

The Flight Data Processor (FDP) was out of service at this facility. The back-up processor had already been out of service for about two weeks. FDP is how the facility receives; updates and processes all the flight plans automatically. When this is out of service all data must be accomplished manually.
ZMP ARTCC reported ZDV ARTCC handed off two aircraft at the same altitude on converging courses without taking action to ensure separation.
Airline Ramp Personnel reported Hazmat cargo not secured in accordance with Hazmat transport requirements.
Pilot reported a NMAC with a powered parachute while on final approach to Y23.

D10 Controller reports of an aircraft that is having problems with the altitude portion of the Mode-C that is being displayed on his scope. The Controller thinks he issues the aircraft 4;000 which it is supposed to be at but aircraft is level at 6;000. Another aircraft is level at 6;000; the Controller thinks they are separated but they are at the same altitude.

D10 TRACON Controller reported a departing aircraft could not contact the correct D10 TRACON sector. The aircraft was assigned a departure altitude which would take it below the minimum vectoring altitude.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported Operations wouldn't stop boarding for medical emergency.
B737-800 pilot flying reported over speeding during go-around due to weather.
Technician reported aircraft engines were operated without oil during preservation for aircraft storage.
A320 Captain reported receiving Dispatch message stating Hazmat cargo weight was erroneously under reported by 250 lbs.
C172 Instructor reported an NMAC with another light aircraft on approach to GPM.
Maintenance Technician found smoke detectors in cargo bays covered with oily substance.
LJ35 flight crew reported an unstabilized approach and missed approach; along with severe turbulence; culminated with a hard landing in microburst; windshear conditions.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC descended them below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
EC135 pilot reported that FOD became caught in rotor blades during landing.
B767 Captain reported that the O2 masks were not compatible with the smoke goggles and did not provide a good seal.
B737 flight crew reported pushback from gate prior to receiving Final Dangerous Goods report.
A Controller cleared an air carrier crew to 4;000 feet on SAN LYNDI THREE RNAV arrival and then issued a terrain alert with a clearance back to 5;000 feet. The crew suspected a SCT Controller error.
B777 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence behind a Heavy aircraft during departure from IAH airport.
A320 flight crew reported a TCAS RA event with a VFR aircraft not in communication with ATC.
A Flight Attendant reported that a fellow cabin crewmember did not comply with company procedures or the FARs.
Dead heading Flight Attendant reported issues with a passenger who was not wearing a COVID-19 mask at all times on the flight.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation.  Controller reported FUSION Radar contributed to the inability to catch the error sooner.
Air carrier pilot reported an unstabilized approach resulting in a go-around.
Flight crew reported ORD Tower canceling their takeoff clearance around 80 KTS after expediting their taxi through the 22L pad. The brake cooling procedures were followed.
ZSE Controller had to vector an aircraft off course because the aircraft was experiencing downdrafts and descended below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

Air Carrier Dispatcher reported a communication breakdown with MC coordinators regarding handling aircraft mechanical diversion. Reporter recommended the need for an SOP delineating responsibilities of Dispatch and MC coordinators.
B767 First Officer reported Maintenance dangerously approaching the aircraft before the engines were shutdown.
Miami TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict with a general aviation aircraft and an air carrier inbound to Fort Lauderdale.
ATC Center Controller reported supervisory personnel ordered the controllers to split a sector in the middle of a busy traffic period.
CRJ-200 First Officer reports encountering wake turbulence just after takeoff from ATL in trail of a B737.
EMB 175 Captain reported an updraft causing the aircraft to climb.
Pilot reported dispatch scheduled crew in violation of FAR duty time regulations. Pilot used sick leave rather than violate FAR.
Observer reported filing report regarding paperwork requirements for another Mechanic that received a threat from the Supervisor.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) reports observing a Regional jet aircraft that was towed on an active taxiway at an Airport without Navigation or Anti-collision lights on and no personnel in the cockpit.

Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that customer service representatives are staging wheel chair passengers in the jetway at some stations in order to speed up boarding once the flight attendants are ready. He believes this is unsafe and a violation of company policy.
Airline Dispatcher reported being notified of departed flight had unreported Dangerous Goods on board.
Maintenance Technician reports that the EROS Flight Crew Oxygen Mask has a defective supply line.
Atlanta Center Controller reported aircraft being cleared into known weather.
CE-680 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to MDW in trail of a B737.
VNY Controller reports of observing an aircraft roll right and left; then regain control. Controller states it was possibly due to rotor wash from a helicopter.
Technician reported overlooking the axle sleeve being removed with the old tire and wheel assembly and not reinstalled; causing repetitive damage to the aircraft at other stations.
Embraer Regional Jet Flight Crew reported that the aircraft did not turn toward the correct fix on departure.
Flight Attendant reported Gate Agent tagging passenger cabin rollerboards for cargo without inquiring status of Lithium Ion batteries.
Piper Warrior pilot reported loss of control of aircraft during touchdown due to a wind gust.
Flight Attendant reported the Captain did not have a face mask on while exiting the cockpit numerous times.
P80 TRACON Controller cancelled an arrival's STAR and vectored the aircraft to avoid an unidentified VFR aircraft flying through the final approach course.

ZKC Developmental Controller releases an aircraft from a Tower controlled airport and instructs the Tower to use visual separation. Aircraft comes onto ZKC Controller's radio and Controller is confused on how the Tower provided the use of visual separation.
Pilot reported entering restricted airspace and referenced lack of flying as a contributing factor.

B737NG flight crew member reported he had difficulty with poor visibility through the windshield in heavy rain during an approach. He reported he was concerned with his company's decision to not reapply rain repellent coating to the windshields.
Air carrier First Officer reported a near collision with a van during taxi at ANC.
B757 First Officer reported that an anti-skid MEL issue was not able to be included in a takeoff performance data calculation which caused the crew to takeoff with incorrect data.
BHM TRACON controllers reported an aircraft entered adjacent airspace without coordination after it's tag was dropped when splitting off combined positions.
A321 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into PHX 10 miles in trail of a B747 that descended through his altitude.
B737 Captain reported ramp personnel opened the cargo door with the left engine still running.
ZAU Controller stated that having two people scheduled for the midnight shift is insufficient for staffing.

Pilot reported of being cleared for a visual approach. Pilot set up instruments to follow the glideslope; when finished he looked up and was in the clouds. Pilot flew an instrument approach despite being cleared for the visual.
A pilot reported they failed to contact ATC for taxi instructions. The pilot reported that while avoiding congestion on the ramp they had a taxiway incursion.
CRJ200 First Officer reported landing below minimum fuel requirements.
Air carrier Captain reported several passengers were not complying with the face mask policy.
HNL Controller reports of an operational error due to two aircraft landing at the same time on crossing runways. No go-around instructions were issued to any aircraft.
Pilot reported a gear up landing.
B737-800 Flight Crew reported receiving a BANK ANGLE followed by a DON'T SINK warning at 1;000 feet on climbout.
HUF Local Controller reported of staffing issues that were blamed for the operational error of an aircraft turning base before it was supposed to.
A VFR aircraft departing VNY was issued instructions which confused the pilot. ATC restricted their climb and in order to avoid terrain they entered BUR airport's Class C airspace without approval.
Pilot reported receiving clearance too late to comply with the speed and altitude restrictions given and unable to request relief because of radio congestion.
Maintenance Personnel reported that incoming Boeing 737 slides have a different style lanyard and battery pouch than on previous slides. Maintenance was not notified of part number change.
ZLC Controller reported observing an aircraft at 10;000 feet in a MVA area designated 11;000 feet.
First Officer reported an airborne conflict with what they perceived to be a weather balloon half a mile off right wing.
PA-28 pilot reported an airspace violation due to the inability to communicate simultaneously with destination CTAF and adjoining Class D airspace during initial approach.
B737-800 Captain reported making an emergency stop as directed by ground personnel while arriving at the gate; narrowly avoiding a collision with a catering truck.
A Maintenance Technician reported that during track and balance on a EC-135 helicopter; a main rotor blade was needed. They upgraded the existing blade per a Service Bulletin.

A pilot reported a new 'no taxi' area just beyond the new IWA Taxiway E and where it turns onto Taxiway B. IWA NOTAM 07/040 dated 1507282315 permanently changed the ramp Taxiway to E; but airport diagrams do not reflect changes.

A B737 Captain flying the SFO SERFR2 was cleared descend via except maintain 6;000 feet and then NORCAL alerted they descended out of SFO Class B. Numerous altitude clearance changes on the arrival created a high workload environment.
A Controller in training allowed a parachute jumping aircraft to release jumpers with conflicting IFR traffic in the vicinity.
Flight Attendant reported that when passengers were ordering their drinks; most of them took their face masks off; even though an announcement was made to leave masks on.
Small Transport pilot reported high workload resulted in a track deviation on approach into MDW following multiple late changes to his clearance.
Air carrier First Officer reported they called for a go-around due to an unstable approach; but the Captain landed the aircraft anyway.
Technician reported that during the clearance of an MEL; he did not reinstall the light bulbs for the LE SLATS IN TRANSIT light on the overhead panel.
B767 Captain reported main landing gear extended indicator not functioning correctly.
C510 pilot reported encountering a wind gust resulting in a runway excursion.

On visual approach to a foreign airport with the First Officer flying; the aircraft was vectored in high and close such that the stabilized approach criteria was not met at 500 FT AGL. The Captain took the aircraft and elected to land.
SAT TRACON Controller reported of an unsafe issue when an aircraft on a visual approach was following traffic and became extended over another airport; and traffic around that airport.
Y90 Controller reports of an known automation problem within Y90 and N90. While training the Controller noticed an airspace deviation related to this automation problem.
B737 reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B787 on visual approach to LAX.

Air carrier flight crew reported a NMAC with VFR traffic while on the ILS 13R approach to BFI.  The flight crew received an RA/TA; but did not take evasive action because they had the VFR traffic in sight.
Maui Tower controllers reported an airborne conflict resulting from inadequate sequencing.
B757 flight attendants reported pushback; taxi and takeoff occurred with minimal contact and time provided by the flight crew; and contrary to SOP.

Flight Attendant reported that during taxi out a passenger refused several times to wear a COVID-19 protective mask and also mocked the flight attendant when he was reminded to wear the mask. The flight taxied back to the gate and the passenger was removed from the flight.
DHC-8-100 flight crew discovered incompatibility with ATOG procedures off gravel surfaced runways.
Airbus A300 had a fuel center tank pump MEL deferral; but ramp personnel and Dispatch had difficulty getting the weight and balance computer to accept the MEL fuel correctly.
Small aircraft student pilot reported an NMAC in the vicinity of IWA airport.

Pilot reported of being told of an aircraft in close proximity to them before they departed. While becoming airborne they get a TCAS alert showing the aircraft 100 feet below. Copilot remarks; 'that was close.'
Air Carrier First Officer reported an altitude deviation during descent and cited lack of recent flight experience as a contributing factor.
On a visual approach to IWA 30L; a crew responded to an RA with both a commanded climb and descent which was caused by a nearby aerobatic aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported not having proper charts for navigation.
EMB-175 Captain reported missing several checklist items because of confusion caused by the interface of de-ice and normal checklists.
A Tracon Controller observed an aircraft descend below its assigned altitude and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
An MRI Tower Controller observed the TRACON Controller terminate VFR services to an aircraft instead of handing off the aircraft to the Center.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported compliance with a wind shear alert departing PHL.
B737 flight crew reported deviating to avoid a thunderstorm without clearance when ATC could not allow a turn or climb.
Flight Attendant reported passengers were not compliant with wearing a face mask.
CRJ-900 Captain reported a low fuel situation due to weather diversions.
Fuel Truck Operator questioned the safety implications of relaxing the Company requirement for strict adherence to the use of dust covers on fuel nozzles.
Air carrier First Officer reported being high and fast on an approach and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
A B737 flight crew and a Cessna 172 pilot reported a near collision and late instructions from ATC.
B737 Maintenance Technicians reported the flaps were lowered onto a ladder that was positioned under the trailing edge of the flaps.

An Air Carrier pilot reported the tower controller was overloaded with traffic while working Local and Ground Control combined when they were cleared for takeoff even though they were number two in the departure lineup. The pilot stated this airport needs to be staffed with more controllers.;
Airline ground personnel reported a 'new bag needs to be added' to aircraft equipment for Lithium Ion batteries handling.
Air carrier Captain reported their international flight had to divert for weather causing a fuel situation and the crew to violate FAR's regarding duty time.
An air carrier First Officer reported a UAV near miss at 7;000 FT while flying the PHX BRUSR1 Arrival near the KUCOO intersection.
Dispatch reported flight departed with missing HAZMAT documents which indicated procedural inconsistencies exist among company department manuals.
A319 Captain reported that during climb; on an IFR flight plan near a parachute jump zone; they got a TCAS advisory of an aircraft nearby.

ANC Tower Controller reported that while a two minute controller overlap was in play; he cleared an aircraft for takeoff.  The relieved Controller asked another position where a specific aircraft was. Local cancelled the takeoff clearance until the other aircraft was found.
CE680 Captain reported that after Maintenance replaced a primary flight control surface; the aircraft did not require a test flight.
B737 Captain reported aircraft encountered a thermal downdraft causing a tail strike while landing.
A320 First Officer reported that the OAKES2 STAR will take you below class B airspace if flown at the lower altitude limits.
Air carrier Captain reported flight attendants did not have correct sizes of Personal Protection Equipment to work safely.
Pilot reports of a confusing transmission and instruction to climb to a different altitude then assigned and then descend back to the previously assigned altitude.
B737 Captain reported the company lacked a proactive maintenance procedure to update the aircraft NAV database and relied upon the MEL to operate in a restricted condition.
B737 Captain reported an NMAC with a flight of 3 helicopters on approach.

P80 Controller reported a wake turbulence remnant issue. Controller reviewed a replay that appeared to be a fusion radar stitch. Controller wondered how he could be charged with an operational error when he had no tool available to verify wake remnant.
Air carrier Captain reported noticing a potential for a navigation error involving two fixes with similar spelling - PRYMD and PYRMD - while preparing for departure from MEM.
B737 Captain reported concerns over complying with DOT Tarmac Delay regulations and Company Manuals.
CRJ Captain reported receiving clearance for takeoff while previous arrival was not clear of the runway safety area.

Aircraft operating approximately 5 miles West of VNY at altitudes around 5000 feet; while clear of VNY class D and BUR class C was advised by VNY Tower they had a potentially caused a  conflict with aircraft flying the ILS/LOC Z Runway 8 BUR.
GA flight instructor reported a near miss with a subsequent light twin aircraft departing APA. ATC's right turn instructions were ignored because of the overtaking aircraft.
First Officer reported loss of cabin pressure after maintenance had replaced a cargo door sill; resulting in an expedited descent and an air turnback.

Flight crew reported that ATC instructions vectored aircraft into extreme weather instead of avoiding it; resulting in lightning and hail damage; requiring aircraft to be removed from service after landing.
ZTL Controller reported they vectored an aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

ZBW Controller discovers an untagged aircraft in his airspace. Controller tries to identify aircraft and checks on frequency for the aircraft. Controller calls possible previous sector and Controller transfers communication to the investigating Controller. Controller asks pilot what happened and pilot advised a Controller possibly forgot about them.
B737 crew experienced a weather radar failure enroute. Due to predicted thunderstorms at the destination; crew elected to divert and have the problem corrected.
Sector was notified of a radar outage affecting separation standards 11 minutes late. The Controller worked four aircraft during this period none of which had separation which was less than required.
Piper 28 Single Pilot reported a loss of directional control during landing resulting in a runway excursion.

Pilot landing TRL experiences excessive use of the CTAF 122.8 resulting in stepped on and missed communications. Reporter suggests that the frequency be evaluated and perhaps the number of airports in the area using 122.8 could be reduced.
L30 TRACON Controller reported they issued VFR clearance instructions to an aircraft that was on an IFR flight plan which resulted in the aircraft flying below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737 Captain reported the approach to ABQ airport needs review because; as currently designed; it increases the chances for an unstable approach.
CRJ-200 Captain reported that on several occasions A80 controllers have issued descend via clearances and then a heading off the arrival without an altitude assignment.
Corporate Captain reported a speed deviation during climbout. Captain referenced distraction while trying to communicate with a busy ATC and lack of flying contributed to the event.
Lead Technician reported not following published maintenance procedures after being out of practice for an extended period of time.
A321 Captain reported being targeted by a laser while performing an approach to LAX Airport. This event resulted in a momentary loss of situational awareness and injury to his left eye.
Ground Agent reported that a passenger had tested positive for COVID-19; however; agents in the terminal were not notified of this until 24 hours after the flight had departed.
Traffic watch pilot reported entering a TFR on correct code and was later questioned by ATC as to not having a clearance into the TFR.
Air carrier Check Airman reported not following the published checklist due to fatigue and not having flown in the last 6 months.
Maintenance reported that work was accomplished on a passenger seat on a B787 by a non-certified engineer.
Air carrier flight crew reported not filling out Fuel Door verification paperwork because they thought it wasn't necessary.

A reporter states that a VFR aircraft; after being terminated for flight following; enters a restricted area. A call in the blind is answered by the VFR pilot and is told that they entered the restricted area. The pilot disagrees and says the altitude that is not is higher than they are. The Controller disagrees and advises the pilot. The pilot finds out that the depiction on his GPS system is incorrect.

An air carrier Captain reported the First Officer mistakenly pushed the TOGA button on short final while attempting to disconnect the autopilot. A go around was required because of the resultant unstablized approach.
Corporate flight crew reported they departed with a Pitot Tube cover still attached.
Flight attendant reported a CRM issue developed with the flight deck crew after reporting there was a minor maintenance issue with an aircraft door.

BE36 pilot reported after he cancelled IFR and was receiving VFR flight following he deviated from a Controller's instructions.

Las Vegas TRACON Controller reported an uncomfortable situation between a VFR and IFR aircraft. Controller reported that staffing was a problem along with the combined sectors.
Air carrier Captain reported that during boarding they noted a child passenger had COVID-19 like symptoms; but at the time did not know what the correct protocol was to address the situation.

Captain reported a disagreement with Maintenance Control over a hydraulic system MEL and refused the aircraft.

C208 pilot reported a runway excursion after hydroplaning during landing rollout on a wet runway.
SCT TRACON Controller observed an arriving air carrier on an ILS approach respond to an RA due to a conflict with an unidentified VFR aircraft.
The flight crew of a G-IV failed to note the initial level off at 5;000 feet on their assigned SID from SJC. Fatigue was cited as a contributing factor.

Technician reported discovering a component that was missing and not installed while troubleshooting an anti-skid discrepancy;
