Airline Ramp Personnel reported aircraft gate return to correct three erroneous Hazmat transport configurations.
C-185 pilot reported that when returning from an out of country flight he learned that an exit report; on top of a reentry report; was required.
PA44 commercial student with an instructor reported attempting to navigate through thunderstorms using their iPad's displaying Nexrad images.

ZMA Controller reported of a situation where an aircraft was climbing to assigned altitude and went through some clouds. As the aircraft exited the pilot reported that a skydiving aircraft was illegally jumping due to close proximity to the clouds.
Pilot reported experiencing a Class B airspace violation.
C172 instructor pilot reported two separate near-midair collision situations during successive visual approaches for landing to CMA airport.
S76 pilot reported ATC had improperly withheld issuing IFR clearances.
B767 flight crew reported HazMat loading and documentation procedural errors requiring ground return to  gate.
PIE Tower Controller reported confusion relating to the term 'hold for release' and hearing only 'released;' allowing an aircraft to get airborne which should not have.
Maintenance employee reported that social distancing on the bus is not possible due to lack of spacing.

Local Controller working Ground combined reported that hour breaks are common at this facility and safety is being jeopardized. Controller was too busy at times and because of this aircraft were delayed.  Controller recommended keeping ground control open during a specific time.
Flight Attendant reported that an old version of the safety demo was presented due to being rushed.
Pilot reported a NMAC after takeoff.

Air Carrier flight crew reported that when returning to the gate at SAN to remove a pitot cover; they encountered difficulty finding Delta Taxiway in the fog due to lack of signage and a lead-in line when coming off Runway 27.
B757 First Officer reported encountering windshear shortly after takeoff that resulted in an 'almost instantaneous' 40 knot drop in airspeed.
EMB-175 Captain reported failing to complete the checklists prior to beginning takeoff with one engine still not started.
A Seattle Center (ZSE) Controller reports of becoming busy and while vectoring an aircraft to final; the aircraft drifted close to a lower Minimum IFR Altitude (MIA).
B737 Captain reported that during engine start ground personnel advised that fuel was leaking from the APU fuel drain.
Pilot reported failure to detach tail tiedown and taxied and departed with the tiedown rope and pad eye still attached to the aircraft; requiring a diversion and precautionary emergency landing.
Captain reported communication breakdown with Ground Control and changes to taxiways that were not on available charts or NOTAMs.
An A321 crew was forced by the company's inaction to complete a flight in violation of the FAR 117 thirteen hour flight duty period limitation.
A320 Maintenance Technician reported that the aircraft was improperly dispatched because the MEL was improperly applied.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence 4 miles in trail of a B757 on approach to ATL.
Air carrier Captain reported he was unable to update his iPad prior to departure from TIST.
Flight Attendant crew reported parents with small children were having problems with the children complying with the face mask policy.
Center Controller reported Traffic Management Unit issued weather re-routes that took aircraft into known weather instead of around it.
B737 Captain reported ramp agent approached aircraft at remote parking spot during lightning storm to open door and triggered ACARS.
EMB-175 flight crew reported an excursion from assigned altitude after receiving an RA on approach into LAX.

General aviation pilot reported the 47N Terminal Procedures Publication still lists a GCO frequency decommissioned years ago; and suggests operation would be useful because of frequent TFRs. Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an EGPWS alert while on initial approach into an airport surrounded by high terrain.
Ramp Agent reported damaging the aircraft during cargo loading because the agent's eye glasses were fogging up due to the face mask.
D01 TRACON reported an aircraft not following directions and entering a lower Minimum Vectoring Altitude (MVA) for a short while.
B737-800 flight crew reported they encountered a severe mountain wave event causing injuries to three flight attendants.
Air carrier Captain reported a UAV flying near the final approach path to PHX Runway 8.
F11 Controller reports of an automation problem with aircraft tagging up on the wrong scopes; even after clearance delivery modified both aircraft to the correct tab list.
Cessna 175 pilot reported that the aircraft was removed from service due to maintenance issues that were not in compliance with FARs.
A B767 Captain reported he had two newly qualified First Officers on his overwater international flight and feels this is not a safe procedure.
B737-800 Captain reported noticing during walk around that the aircraft was very lightly weighted on the nose wheel when parked at a gate that had a rearward slope.
A Local Controller reported an inbound aircraft still on Approach Control frequency descended through the altitude of a VFR departure.
A Trainee Controller working Local Control had to send an aircraft around due to conflicting landing traffic on an intersecting runway.
Technician reported confusion over the correct completion of Do Not Operate tags installed on the aircraft during an engine change.
Airline Ground Personnel reported Dangerous Goods quantities loaded in cargo compartment exceeded compartment Dangerous Goods limit.
ZOA Center Controller reported the adjacent TRACON sectors took point outs on aircraft that were head on into confliction instead of taking communication and control of the two aircraft.
ERJ-145 flight crew reported fuel quantity indication malfunctions during a diversion; that forced them to return to the original destination airport.
Airline Maintenance Personnel reported aircraft part erroneously shipped in unlabeled container.

B737 flight crew realized during the MROC ILS DME Runway 07 missed approach a tight turn was required to comply with the constraint to remain north of TIO 113 radial and avoid the terrain exceeding 7;000 ft so LNAV was disconnected and the go-around flown manually.

A Controller refused to take handoffs from adjacent sectors because the aircraft were supposed to be routed away from their sector due to weather deviations. Three aircraft entered the controller's airspace without the controller accepting the handoffs.
Captain reported flying over 250 knots while descending below 10;000 feet due to confusion around a runway clearance change.
Corporate jet Captain reported using his Captain's authority to avoid thunderstorms during climb.
B737 Captain reported auto pilot not engaging as required and non QRH procedures were used to get autopilot to function correctly.

ZTL Controller received an aircraft whose routing in the ATC computer showed IVANE STAR. However; ZDC had cleared the aircraft via the MAJIC STAR and the pilot advised he would not accept a descend via clearance for the IVANE STAR. The previous facility made a computer entry to suppress the IVANE route from the flight plan; but this update did not get forwarded to the ZTL Controller.

Technician reported that during the removal of seats from Aircraft X; it was noted the life vests had the wrong safety seals installed. Reportedly; the same issue has been found on three other aircraft during heavy maintenance work.

Parallel runways were in use at the reporters airport. ATC cleared an aircraft for an opposite direction approach to the left parallel runway. The Local controller thought traffic inbound on approach was for the opposite parallel runway. The aircraft checked on Tower frequency inbound to the same opposite direction runway as the other inbound. The local controller switched the aircraft to parallel runway in a timely manner.
CL-605 Captain reported a 'severe' wake turbulence encounter at FL310 in trail of a B777 on arrival into LAX.
Technicians reported incorrect bolts used on device to lift engine compressor disc resulted in damage to blades.
A319 Captain reported momentary loss of situational awareness resulting in excursion from assigned altitude when assigned base turn to final until established on final.
ATC Controller reported allowing an aircraft to remain on the previous (combined) frequency; then lost communications as the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
CL30 Captain reported a brief altitude excursion resulted from a wake vortex encounter departing an international airport.

A MWH TRACON Controller reports of an airborne conflict attributed to the Local Controller not preparing the aircraft for what was intended. The Local Controller has been known to run a similar operation where nothing was done by management to correct the issue.

A B757 air crew performed a rejected takeoff after multiple bird strikes at around 100 knots. They returned to the gate. After maintenance inspected the aircraft they once again departed for their destination uneventfully.
Air Carrier First Officer reported that the Jeppesen Electronic Flight Bag and the FMC database have different update times.

After landing; B737 crew cleared the runway via a high-speed taxiway and immediately began to follow the gate marshaller's directions to their gate. ATC radioed them to stop and after a few minutes cleared the aircraft to continue to the gate.
TBM-900 pilot reported he flew into RVSM Airspace without the required Letter of Authorization from the FAA.
CRJ-700 Captain reported navigational issues relating to the FMS and GPS problems.
Pilot reported violating taxi clearance and crossing an active runway.
An A320 Captain noted that when his flight release is revised; his air carrier's revised SABRE flight plan briefing packages do not always include the alternate's weather and NOTAM information.

Salt Lake TRACON Controller reported observing a departure on a heading towards conflicting terrain and airspace. Reporter stated the local tower retains aircraft on their frequency for extended periods without proper monitoring.
Tower Controller reported that a loss of spacing on final resulted in a go around.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported executing a go-around from their approach to Runway 35 at PHL after encountering wake turbulence from an A330 landing on Runway 27R.
Air Carrier pilot reported their takeoff clearance was cancelled after they questioned the timing of the Controller's clearance.
Air carrier First Officer reported he selected TOGA before the engines had reached 40%.

Controller report aircraft turning wrong direction to enter the pattern. Controller lets aircraft continue and changes the aircraft runway. Controller gets busy and then notices aircraft landing on wrong runway.

Aircraft was descending on the SILCN2 STAR to SJC. NCT TRACON had departing traffic climbing. TRACON attempted to verbally coordinate with ZOA ARTCC to stop the descent of the inbound at 20000 feet. An adjacent ARTCC Sector took the call from the TRACON to help the ARTCC sector working the arrival but the coordination did not get forwarded to the Controller working the arrival.
Ramp employee reported finding USPS mail cargo labeled 'Surface Transport only' during aircraft cargo unloading.
B737 Captain reported software issues with company issued EFB could potentially result in a safety risk.

Air carrier First Officer reported the company training center and simulators could benefit from more sanitation and cleaning supplies for staff and trainees in order to support a safe training environment. The crew member suggested that the company pursue FAA approval to extend training events deadlines in order to have time to deep clean the training facility.
Air carrier Captain reported the signage in LAR Airport does not match the Jeppesen plate.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported that an aircraft diverted due to the crosswind limitations listed in the Flight Crew Manual not being in agreement with the limitations available to dispatchers.

Pilot reported a Low Altitude alert on approach to NEW airport.

A HCF Approach controller reported confusion with the Tower over a procedure to separate arriving IFR aircraft from VFR departures.
B777 Captain reported the guard had been removed from the Emergency Light Switch and a passenger accidentally activated the emergency cabin lights with no means to deactivate them.
Turboprop pilot reported receiving abbreviated and unclear taxi instructions after landing at IAD.
A B737 crew questioned MU value methodology and validity after departing MKE Runway 1L with a 'Wet Good' report; when poor controllability during takeoff indicated 'Fair to Poor' conditions.
B737 Captain reported a TRACON Controller was aggravated and acted unprofessional when requested to provide the clearance prior to tower opening.
A320 First Officer reported a return to the departure airport after sustaining damage to the #1 engine and #2 pack inlet door.
Seaplane pilot reported that during touchdown; the plane did a water loop; possibly as a result of the nose getting too low.
Captain reported due to ATC workload and failed communications; Captain entered TFR.
A pilot reported he was concerned with an SFO arrival crossing restriction appearing in NOTAM form but not yet charted.

ZTL Controller reports of a handoff issues with ZDC.  They couldn't handoff a specific aircraft because there was another aircraft with the same call sign that ZDC was attempting to handoff to ZTL.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to LAX in trail of a B757. Reporter suggested increasing the in-trail separation for a higher level of safety.

Reporter went on to say the Local Controller was newly certified and that the facility is pushing people through training and certifying them at minimum time.

Pilot reported a flight where they encountered severe turbulence and a lightening strike that was not predicted and loss of control of aircraft.
S46 TRACON Controller reported a VFR aircraft caused an airborne conflict north of the BFI area. This is a recurring issue.

Air carrier Captain reported that several intoxicated passengers were being disruptive and not complying with face mask policy. The Captain stated that while one passenger was being removed from the aircraft; he became angry at the Captain and used profanity.
While flying in the vicinity of thunderstorms; CRJ-700 crew encountered severe turbulence and windshear resulting in altitude deviations and stall warning activation.

Pilots were confused and did not do what the last instruction from ATC was.
Dispatcher reported that a flight departed without the minimum required fuel due to a miscommunication about an MEL.

PCT TRACON Controller reported a NMAC from an aircraft they were no longer talking to.

Captain reported their Disabled Checklist for loss of Center (C) Hydraulic System quantity and pressure directed the pilot to select an Air Driven (Demand) Pump (ADP) on B777 aircraft. The Electronic Checklist logic stopped the continuation of the Disabled checklist due to a momentary spike in the 'C' System pressure. As a result; the Center System hyd pumps were left on even with the Center Hyd quantity and pressure at zero.
ZAB Controller reported a military aircraft entering a higher MVA due to known area of communications limitations.
PDX Local Controller reported an air carrier on short final reporting close proximity to aircraft at nearby VUO.
Technician reported that fatigue contributed to not following MEL procedures and he locked out the wrong thrust reverser in error.
Air carrier First Officer reported inadvertently moving the pack switches to off instead of high flow causing a cabin depressurization during climbout.

An ERJ-175 Captain questioned operations before takeoff about the weight and balance accuracy and was given a revision. Inflight Dispatch advised the crew of an error and later diverted them because the load was off at least 1;800 lbs. The cargo compartment weight limitations were exceeded.
The crew misread the charted DME on the LGA5 departure procedure and started their turn two miles late.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft declared minimum fuel to be able to get into an airport that was underneath an overcast layer.
Air carrier Captain reported a hard landing in gusty wind conditions and subsequently went around for another landing.

Air Carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation.
Maintenance personnel could not complete an 'A' check on an MD80 in the time allotted. Staffing issued were cited as contributing factors.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a heading deviation and cited lack of recent flight experience and task saturation as contributing factors.

DCA Tower Controller realized a plan was not going to work and sent an arrival around which came close to a departure.
A Center Controller reported an unknown aircraft descended into the sector with less than the required 5 miles lateral separation.
Air carrier ground personnel reported Hazmat transport violations on arrival shipment.

During flight in a B737-700 the crew climbed to FL260 when they were restricted to FL250 due to a single pack operation.
An ARTCC Controller worked a period of traffic of extreme complexity due to numerous weather deviations and too many aircraft routed through their airspace.
EMB-175 Captain reported that during the arrival a duplicate flight number was being used for a company departure.
Flight Crew reported refusing an aircraft with an inoperative Auxiliary Power Unit due to anticipated severe weather.

B737-700 Captain reported that while departing SNA on the CHANNEL 2 Departure he received an RA that commanded a descent at 2700 ft MSL.
Air carrier Safety Analyst reported Hazmat material was found to be improperly packaged for transport.

A80 controllers report of using fusion radar on the final sectors.  Controller turns an aircraft into a hole; the aircraft did not turn as the controller thought it would; then jumped showing it on a different then assigned heading.;  The controller had visual separation so all was good separation wise; but didn't like the way the aircraft jumped.  Controller exclaimed and another controller also saw what happened.  Controller thought the jumping of radar targets on final is unacceptable.

PIT Tower Controller reported of a situation where an aircraft taxied onto a closed taxiway. Reporter wanted some type of a review of the operation because this issue with Ramp Control not knowing has been ongoing.

EMB-145XR flight crew reported a low fuel situation resulted from a wind-shear induced go-around and diversion to an alternate.

A B737 Captain reported preparing to depart from a state where marijuana use was legal; had a passenger's TSA identified marijuana removed because the destination was Texas where marijuana was illegal.
Pilot reports of a disagreement between pilot and ATC when issued an approach clearance.

A320 Captain reported problems with the Accupark system at the gate.

Denver Center Controller and Corporate Jet aircraft flight crew reported a track deviation incident while on the Cottonwood 2 engine out procedure. Controller stated the Cottonwood 2 SID is not the engine out procedure and routinely gets flown incorrectly due to confusion.

B757 flight crew reports a loud impact on the left radome; from a large bird at 7;000 feet. G280 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on descent into FTW 20 miles in trail of a B767 that resulted in an uncommanded 45 degree roll to the left.

B737 Captain reported a Controller distracted them with instructions while the aircraft was still in a critical phase of landing.
Air carrier Captain reported calling in fatigued and being told months later they are expected to fly fatigued.

Air carrier First Officer reported concerns regarding Chart plus software.
Flight crew reported an altitude excursion on arrival into IWA when they encountered turbulence and windshear.

EMB175 Captain reported diverting to the alternate airport after two missed approaches at the planned destination.
B737 reported an engine bleed valve problem that resulted in the use of an MEL with restrictions to flight operations.

Airbus A320 diverted after takeoff due to an Autobrake Fault.
B737 First Officer reported the SJC ALMDN3 RNAV SID listed two top altitudes with the first at 15;000 feet and the second at FL190. ATC was uncertain about which was correct.

A large transport aircraft on descent into a Southern California airport encountered severe turbulence. The crew made a PIREP and a maintenance logbook entry; but failed to notify dispatch as per company policy.

MKG Tower Controller/Instructor reported of an instance where an aircraft landed on a NOTAM Closed runway. Reporter did not show the runway closed and was briefed that the runway/taxiway closures were not pertinent.
SR22 pilot reported passenger grabbed control stick while adjusting seat.

BE300 Captain states that his company has not been operating in compliance with FAR 135.157 due to only 9 passenger oxygen masks being available while carrying 9 passengers plus one lap child. Air carrier pilot reported receiving a ground proximity warning twice while being vectored for an ILS Approach.
BE33 pilot reported flying VFR into IMC. The pilot exited IMC and continued VFR to destination.
B737 Captain reported the BUR Runway 8 ILS LOC signal was unreliable and stated he has seen this anomaly several times.
B737-800 flight crew reported advising ATC a need for a turn for weather; ATC did not allow. Captain claimed Captain's Authority and turned the aircraft to avoid weather.

An aircraft departed WHP and mistakenly thought it had clearance to fly through VNY airspace. Aircraft penetrated VNY airspace without proper clearance.
A320 Captain reported ground return to gate due to loading errors of Hazmat cargo.
B737 Captain reported encountering severe mountain wave in the vicinity of PUB that resulted in speed and altitude excursions.
Tower Instructor and Developmental reported overlying Center not complying with the two facilities' LOA.
Small aircraft pilot reported frequent erroneous weather AWOS reports at CEC airport.
B777 First Officer reported switching aircraft due to maintenance issues and having to cancel the flight altogether due to maintenance delays.

Air carrier Captain reported a deviation while performing a 'descend via' type of arrival. He debriefed the issue with the controlling air traffic facility and was told that there have been several pilot deviations associated with this particular arrival; possibly due to a design flaw in the procedure.
Lead Crew Chief reported HAZMAT transported without required HAZMAT documents.
Air carrier First Officer reported that a mistake during the FMS programming caused the aircraft to descend when the autopilot was engaged after takeoff.
Flight crew reported confusion regarding their taxi clearance that resulted in a taxiway incursion.
A Tower Controller reported assigning a go around 2;000 feet in a 2;300 foot Minimum vectoring Altitude area.

Pilot reported of following traffic into an airport and slowing for spacing on short final. Pilot observed the preceding traffic exit the runway and at the same time ATC told the aircraft to go around. Pilot went around but felt it was not necessary.
Ground employee reported a person came to work after a family member had allegedly tested positive for COVID-19.
B737 reported descending below a crossing restriction during arrival.

EMB-170 flight crew reported they were instructed to line up and wait on Runway 22R at EWR from Taxiway Z. During the turn onto Runway 22R they were instructed to hold short of Runway 29 after the nose of the aircraft has already crossed the hold short line.

As they climbed through icing conditions in a heavy CRJ shortly after takeoff; the Departure Controller several times repeated a request that they accelerate more quickly to 250 knots due to faster traffic behind them. In part because of the resulting disruption of normal SOP they discovered some time later that the power had never been reduced following takeoff and had been operating at or near takeoff thrust since the start of the takeoff roll.  Rules at the air carrier reportedly allow pilots to cross qualify on CRJ models with and without autothrottle/VNAV capability; and was cited as a contributing factor as the SOPs for accomplishing routine tasks vary between the various types.
SCT TRACON Controller reported that Los Angeles Center Controller did not follow the Letter of Agreement (LOA) with two aircraft inbound to the reporters sector.
BJC Tower Controller reported that an aircraft on visual approach was concerned that an aircraft in a VFR corridor was too close.
B757 Captain reported two wake vortex encounters in trail of a B777 departing LAX.

EMB-175 First Officer reported a wake turbulence encounter climbing out of 500 feet AGL departing LAS in trail of an A321 that resulted in a stick shaker and an uncommanded descent that triggered an EGPWS warning.
Student Pilot reported a loss of control during landing.

After experiencing a departure delay; an international flight crew attempted to determine from crew schedulers what their duty-limit time would be per FAR 117. The crew attempted to resolve the two answers received from schedulers by contacting the Chief Pilot who gave them a third time. Crew determined that they were legal for the extended duty day; but recommended that the company refine its training of schedulers to enhance FAR 117 requirements during irregular operations.
Sport/Recreation Pilots reported their transponder was not in the ALT position during the flight and this probably contributed to the NMAC.
Pilot flying C-501 aircraft reported deviation from landing clearance.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported being unable to meet a crossing restriction during approach to CLT due to a late descent clearance by TRACON.

Tower Controller reported a loss separation that could have been avoided if the Local Controller would have listened to others in the cab. Converging runway operation was done incorrectly causing an unsafe situation. Reporter questioned the Local Controller's training.
Flight Attendant reported concern regarding the station's lack of aircraft sanitizing procedures.

An air carrier First Officer reported confusion when flying the MSO RNAV-D approach after ATC cleared the flight UBIJO direct BOTBY. Because the STACC waypoint was deleted; the flight descended to 10;300 ft which is below the STACC 11;300 ft MEA.
GA pilot reported entering BUR Class C airspace following departure from WHP.
Air carrier Captain reported that differences in aircraft were poorly documented in the company flight manuals.

An EMB170 Flight Crew experiences a wind shear event during the River Visual approach to Runway 19 at DCA. At 300 feet the GPWS annunciates 'Sink Rate . . . PULL UP PULL UP' and a go around is initiated by the Captain. Within seconds a 'CAUTION WINDSHEAR!' annunciates and an increasing performance winds hear is encountered resulting in a flap over speed. The airport is turned around and the next approach is to Runway 33.
Learjet 60 Captain reported an unstable approach to landing due to weather.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with the face mask policy.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was confrontational and refused to wear a face mask.
ZSE controllers reported a B737 not meeting a crossing restriction which resulted in an airspace violation of an active MOA.
LEX Tower Controller reported landline outages that occurred on two different dates. Reporter wanted to know why there is not a redundant system concerning the phone lines.
Flight Captain reported Near Mid Air Collision with twin engine aircraft during Climb.
Captain reported departing with less than minimum takeoff fuel.
ZSE Controller working combined sectors reported a loss of separation and an airspace violation due to lack of staffing.
Tower Controller in Charge reported an arrival aircraft reported a drone close to them on final.
Air carrier Captain reported that airline is not following the SAFO2009 guidance during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Air carrier Captain reported issues with the passenger count on board and social distance seating guidelines which delayed the flight.
Technician reported misidentifying the correct part number due to bugs in a computer program; and installing the wrong part.
Flight Attendant reported that a passenger was wearing only a face shield and was asked to wear a face mask to be in compliance.
An Instructor pilot reported they had to take over the controls when a student did not compensate for a crosswind properly resulting in a momentary loss of aircraft control and a rejected takeoff.
Hawker 850XP Captain reported that a wake turbulence encounter on departure caused them to miss a crossing restriction.
B767 Technician reported discovering fuel lines on engine were loose and caused an inflight shutdown and return to departure airport.

A B737 Captain attempted to stop passenger boarding to protect a Mechanic working near hazardous equipment but a company zone person refused his request; overriding the Captain's authority as described in the FOM.
B737 First Officer reported problems with the recently updated moving map display readability.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a taxiway incursion at LGA airport.  The crew cited experience and confusing ATC instructions as contributing factors.
Flight crew reported flight pushed off gate without required Hazmat documents. After a minimal delay documents were received; flight complete an uneventful flight.
A TRACON Departure Controller reports SFO Tower routinely allows departures to depart with the wrong route indicated on the flight plan and not coordinating the correct departure runway.
B737-800 crew reported encountering severe turbulence at FL300 in the vicinity of OMA.
ZDC Center Controller reported that New York Center violated the airspace of ZDC and did not coordinate with ZDC Controllers.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported receiving notice of weight and balance change about 45 minutes after departure.
GSP TRACON Flight Line Manager (FLM) reports of a loss of separation by a training team on West radar. The controllers did not separate two aircraft one VFR climbing and one IFR descending.
CE-560EP Captain reported the right engine starter failed to disengage and the procedure to resolve the issue was not in the QRC.
EMB-175 pilot reported that aircraft was not downgraded correctly after ground damage to the RVSM area.
C180 pilot reported losing directional control after landing resulting in a ground loop.
EMB-145 flight crew reported rolling off the end of the runway after struggling with directional control while rolling out on the snow-covered runway.
The pilot of a tailwheeled aircraft was unable to control the tailwheel steering during landing rollout; resulting in a runway excursion.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported damage to the left wing from an encounter with migratory geese at 7;000 ft.

RST Tower Controller reported of problems with a runway configuration and aircraft not being able to hear the transmissions from Tower correctly. A taxiing aircraft crossed an active runway instead of turning onto a taxiway.
Corporate flight crew reported flying with an open maintenance write-up. The Captain stated COVID-19 concerns were a distraction.

Turboprop flight crew reported departing EYE VFR due to not being able to contact ATC for an IFR clearance. During departure the aircraft encountered opposing traffic requiring an evasive maneuver into Class C airspace.
Center Controller reported the adjacent Center sector attempted to hand an aircraft off to them in an altitude block that was partially below the minimum terrain requirement.
International Tower issued duplicate takeoff clearances to two aircraft on the same runway with one at the beginning of the runway and the other on an intersection departure.
Pilot reported overshooting final and possibly having a NMAC with another aircraft also on final.
Ground employee reported that some personnel are allowed to bypass the temperature check.
PA-28 pilot reported losing control during landing and ran off runway into dirt.
B737 First Officer reported noticing that his company's new smoke goggles do not fit well enough to seal out smoke and contaminants.
ZFW Center Controller reported that an aircraft that was part of an aerial refueling mission departed the formation without a clearance.
Air carrier Captain on approach to ELP received a windshear warning; executed the escape maneuver; and proceeded to their alternate.
A maintenance person reported that a move crew towed an aircraft into a gate without marshalers and nearly clipped the wingtip of an adjacent aircraft.
ATC controller directed a B-737 crew inbound to EWR to descend to 7000 ft during the arrival and to keep the aircraft speed at 305 knots below 10;000 ft altitude.
ERJ 175 Flight Crew reported a near ground collision with a van.
B757 flight crew and company dispatcher reported thunderstorms along the route resulted in turbulence with large airspeed/altitude variations and a lightning strike.
GA pilot reported inadvertent VFR flight into IMC that resulted in an airspace incursion.

DWH Tower controller becomes confused when he doesn't follow facility procedure of telling landing aircraft to turn left and stop. Front Line Manager (FLM) yells at controller and distracts him from remembering if he told the aircraft to stop. Aircraft passes a hold short line becoming a conflict with an arrival to another runway.
ZBW Controller reported a Learjet 35 pilot required vectors due to FMC malfunction; possibly as a result of military operations.
GA pilot reported landing on a closed runway; that was NOTAMed closed after pilot had already checked NOTAMs and had departed.

General Aviation Pilot reported that on visual approach to GMU RWY01 he lined up on closed runway at GYH; 6 miles south; before realizing his mistake. The Supervisor indicated that this situation was the third time it happened in the last two weeks.
Air carrier Captain reported turning right instead of left on departure. ATC and First Officer immediately pointed out the error and heading was corrected.
Pilot reported exceeding 250 knots below 10;000 feet.
B737 First Officer reported fatigue event due to conflicting work-duty rest periods.
S46 TRACON Controller reported of a coordinated procedure that was not followed which led to a loss of separation with a departure and an arrival.
B737 flight crew reported executing a missed approach at EWR after ATC issued a low altitude warning and advised the ILS had failed.
Pilot reported flying an aircraft with an expired medical certificate.
Corporate jet Captain reported traffic above and below the aircraft flight path in the LAX area which triggered an alert on the TCAS.
ZNY ARTCC Controller aircraft on an oceanic track routing was issued a new route. The new route was not entered into the system and a conflict with another aircraft resulted.

Technician reported that during an ETOPS Pre-Departure check; it was discovered that aircraft had been struck by lightning and repairs were not accomplished correctly or recorded as required in the damage history.

DVT Tower controllers observed a partially certified Controller lose situational awareness and issue instructions to an aircraft which placed it into confliction with several other aircraft in the airport traffic pattern.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a terrain alert form ATC during approach to DSM airport.

Two ZDC Controllers reported bad weather where aircraft had to deviate and weather was restricting movement. Controllers attempted to get use of Special Use Airspace but had to coordinate on a plane by plane basis which added to the workload. Controllers wanted the Front Line Manager and Traffic Management Unit (TMU) to ensure restrictions were being held and that aircraft should have been held on the ground instead of becoming airborne adding to their traffic load. Controller wanted to review this session with TMU; and a safety council. Traffic was inundating the controller workforce and due to weather they were limited in what they could do.
CRJ900 Captain reported not complying with crossing restrictions published on a SID.
Flight Attendant reported a disruptive passenger demanded wine and his aggressive behavior caused some passengers to be reseated. Law enforcement met the aircraft.

RSW TRACON Controller reported he lost radio contact with a VFR aircraft and observed the aircraft's target merge with another aircraft target 100 feet apart. The Controller reported facility radios do not provide radio coverage for all of their airspace.
ZDC Center controllers reported a pilot lost control of their aircraft in severe turbulence other aircraft were still allowed to fly through the area.

FFZ Tower Controller had to delay an aircraft to accommodate an opposite direction operation. The Controller does not feel opposite direction operations should ever be approved if there is traffic in the pattern.
B787 flight crew reported a low quantity indication in the liquid cooling system caused a delayed flight; only to reappear the next flight when a leak was discovered.
A Captain of a corporate aircraft reported an airborne conflict; and subsequent RA; while conducting a visual approach.
A Departure Controller reported an aircraft departed on the wrong heading and was below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B757-200 Captain reported receiving a TE FLAP DISAGREE message on approach. On the ground; evidence of a bird strike on the flaps was noted.
PA-31 pilot reported not reconnecting the nose landing gear scissors pin prior to flight; resulting in a runway excursion on landing.
Dispatcher reported a failure to place a MEL item on the flight release and neither the crew or dispatch corrected the error.

CRJ Captain describes a diversion caused by a windshear on the first approach and a microburst alert from ATC on the second. The diversion airport has equally unstable air; and the Captain elects to fly through a windshear warning at 1;500 feet to land due to a low fuel situation.
Center Controller reported a conflict with Management regarding a VFR aircraft that was assigned an altitude below the sector MVA.
Chicago TRACON Controller reported adverse weather and a malfunctioning glideslope caused aircraft to go around.
B747 Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after it became clear the fuel system was compromised by improper maintenance at a foreign station.
UAV flight crew reported that their misunderstanding of a clearance resulted in an altitude deviation.
Flight attendants reported a passenger not wearing a mask due to a reported medical condition.
Ground wing-walker reported ramp safety concerns because of the unusual aircraft parking areas needed during the COVID-19 reductions.
PC-12 pilot reported on initial approach in IMC conditions and encountered severe turbulence and a downdraft the disconnected the autopilot. The pilot recovered and made an uneventful landing.

An ADW Tower Controller reported something they had not seen before. The airport vehicle plus one wanted permission to enter the runway. Permission was granted. The plus one was a motor coach on a tour of the airport.
Center Controller reported while in holding; an air carrier did not descend to assigned altitude resulting in an airborne conflict.

An air carrier aircraft diverted to SDF because the filed destination was closed. During the extensive ground delay the Captain attempted to access the terminal and was told only one person could open the jetway. The Captain questioned the safety implications of no passenger access in the event of an aircraft emergency.

An LAX Local Controller reports of a Super aircraft landing and then traffic following reports the ILS signal as being interrupted. The Super was exiting the runway ahead when the disturbance was reported.
A319 First Officer reported that the aircraft was refused due to the misapplication of a CDL Procedure.

DFW Controller loads the runway while an aircraft is on final to same runway. Controller gets distracted by discussion with Local Assist. Pilot of landing aircraft asks Local if this is going to work and is sent around. Controller blames the assist for the distraction and complains that Local Assist should not have been open.
Air Carrier Captain reported GPS position unavailable and ADS-B OUT L. Possible GPS jamming.
Air Carrier pilot reported miscommunication between flight crew while initiating a go-around due to windshear and turbulence.
B757 Captain reported an go around and priority landing after flaps failed to fully deploy.
B737 First Officer reported that ATC gave the same taxi clearance to two aircraft; resulting in a critical ground conflict.
B737 Captain reported unsafe gate parking process due to non-standard marshaling crew; ramp conditions and vehicles illegally parked.

A flight crew reported multiple distractions due to weather and an icing equipment malfunction. These distractions resulted in several speed excursions before the flight was finally stabilized and a successful landing was accomplished.
Air carrier Captain reported not signing the flight release prior to departure.
A balloon pilot reported having to land on a residential street due to inability to safely continue to an open area.
Captain reported overshooting final approach course on a visual approach into Denver.
ZID Center Controller reported observing that the next sector changed the assigned altitude in the aircraft data block to a different altitude than the aircraft was cleared to climb.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a taxiway incursion and stated that Ground Control did not issue direction of turn instructions as they typically do and appeared to be overworked.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported Ramp Lead incorrectly completed Hazmat document resulting in shipment not properly configured for air transport.
Dispatcher reported confusion in city pairing used for a Part 91 Ferry Permit; which resulted in the aircraft flying to a different destination airport.
A TRACON Instructor Controller and their Trainee reported a VFR aircraft reported evasive action to avoid a parachutist.

ZOB Controller reported of staffing issues on a shift. Controller did not like the fact that Management did not call in overtime for the shift and reported that Management went looking for someone to keep over; but that person had already left. Reporter felt that hold over and overtime was an unsafe call that created at least 3 unsafe situations/sectors.

Pilot reported incorrectly reading back their landing sequence number and turning in front of another aircraft on final approach. The pilot of the other aircraft reported taking immediate evasive action to avoid a collision. Both pilots reported the traffic pattern was busy and the Controller was handling both Ground and Tower operations.
C172 Pilot reported a critical ground conflict while in position on active runway.
EC-130 Pilot reported undocumented maintenance was performed on the aircraft he flew.
Tug Driver reported their drive-by-wire tug lost all steering due to a malfunctioning computer.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC gave them a clearance meant for another company flight.
A-321 Captain reported he allowed the First Officer to land long from an unstable approach.
A Center Developmental reported they issued a control instruction to an aircraft through another controller that placed it below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude and Minimum IFR Altitude.
A Maintenance Supervisor reported that during a tire change on a Boeing 757; a Technician lacerated his finger when attempting to change a fitting on a nitrogen cart.
GA pilot reported an NMAC after takeoff from LGU airport.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to IAH 4 miles in trail of a B737-900.
TRACON Controllers reported social distancing; during the training period; may have contributed to an incorrect altitude assigned to the aircraft.

Maintenance Technician had to spend an inordinate amount of time researching whether a particular part is correct for a particular aircraft tail number. Streamlining this process will decrease the chance of an incorrect part being installed.
Technician reported that Company is using unqualified technicians to do ETOPS checks in violation of the GMM.
Pilot Flying reported smoke obscuring flight whilst in the pattern; leading to a landing on the wrong runway.
GA pilot reported a NMAC in traffic pattern occurred when another aircraft turned in front of them requiring an evasive maneuver and go around.
Pilot reported winds driving him off the runway heading; causing him to land on an embankment instead of the runway.
Ground employee reported two team members were less than 6 feet apart and not in accordance with social distancing government guidelines.
Air carrier flight crew on approach to HRL was cleared to 2000 ft from 4000 ft while IMC on the LOC BC Runway 35L. When level; the crew was told to return to 3000 ft because of towers in the area.
Dispatcher reported problems with the SATCOM lines going unanswered due to COVID-19 staffing levels.
Air carrier flight crew taking off from Runway 27 at BOS took evasive action responding to 2 TCAS RAs to avoid helicopters on departure.
A Mechanic reported that when installing the #1 engine hydraulic pump he neglected to install the carbon seal causing engine loss of oil and an inflight shutdown.
HPN Tower Controller reported that TRACON handed off an IFR aircraft on approach without coordination; resulting in a traffic conflict.
An air carrier aircraft departed IAH Runway 15R and took evasive action to a TCAS RA 'DESCEND DESCEND' warning during a near miss with a small high wing aircraft in its departure path.
CL-300 First Officer reported an uneventful diversion after attempting two weather challenging mountainous airport approaches.
Airline flight crew reported their forward visibility was obscured on takeoff roll and initial climb when Type IV fluid flowed over the windscreen as their speed increased.
UAV pilot requested FAA authorization before a commercial video operation. After an extended delay; the FSS issued a NOTAM about the operation at the pilot's request which was then completed safely.
ZLC Center Controller reported they observed an aircraft descend below the assigned altitude and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
MD-11 flight crew reported an Airborne Conflict with a drone during final approach for landing.
A B737 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence that resulted in a 1;400 foot excursion from their cleared altitude.
Flight Attendant reported the Captain did not wear a COVID-19 type mask during the flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported a runway confliction resulted in an ATC directed high speed RTO. Captain also report a late ACARS message conflict.
Flight Attendant reported not being sure if a commuting Flight Attendant is allowed to sit in a specific seat when another Flight Attendant is sitting in adjacent seat.
PCT TRACON Controller reported assigning an operational error conducting a flight break up procedure even though they were in compliance with the 7110.65.
GA pilot reported an NMAC departing A39 airport.
VNY Tower Controller working as Controller in Charge in addition to Local Control forgot to restrict a helicopter's altitude resulting in a NMAC.
A B777 Captain reported requesting a reroute that resulted in excessive fuel on-board and a weight-limited aircraft; and consequently some passengers were unable to board.

HCF Controller describes a situation where ZOA Oceanic wouldn't accept an aircraft at an altitude because the ZOA controller thought they needed 15 minutes of separation. They only need 10 minutes of separation according to the HCF Controller.
SCT TRACON Instructor reported the Developmental did not transfer communication to the Tower until the aircraft reached an area beneath the MVA.
Air Carrier First Officer reported they called in fatigued due to not being able to get sufficient rest from the previous four days flying schedule.
C182 pilot reported a prop strike resulted when windshear was encountered at touchdown.
NCT Controller reports of a problem with aircraft that do not fly the correct approach to the correct runway.
During approach to BZN; aircrew experienced a GPWS terrain warning and executed the escape maneuver.
B737-700 Flight Attendant reported a fume event caused by chemicals to repair a FA jumpseat. The FA reported experiencing physiological symptoms.
Light Transport aircraft pilot reported a track heading deviation during departure from BHM airport after confusing similar sounding fixes GUMMP and GUMPE.
Corporate flight crew reported flying the incorrect pattern to a landing.
PA20 instructor reported loss of control on landing resulting in ground loop and aircraft damage.
Pilot reported a near mid air collision with another aircraft while on final approach.
Single engine aircraft pilot reported the aircraft incurred minor damage during a wake turbulence encounter with an MD11.
Ground employee reported concerns with others not wearing face masks; nor taking COVID-19 seriously.
B737 Captain reported minor injuries to multiple passengers and flight attendants after encountering unexpected moderate turbulence.
B-737 Captain reported departure without amended Release for late COMAT Hazmat addition.

HCF Controller reported of an emergency inbound and the tower crash phone being out of service. Tower Supervisor called the Ramp Tower as per the SOP and made appropriate notifications. People at the Ramp Tower did not know what they were supposed to do. While aircraft was over the numbers the emergency equipment arrived.
ATC Controllers and small aircraft pilot reported a miscommunication between Local and Ground Control that resulted in a critical ground conflict.

Air carrier flight crew reported that while descending on a night visual approach to ABQ Runway 26 they received a GPWS caution message. The escape maneuver was completed followed by an uneventful landing.
Air carrier ramp agent reported being uncomfortable with a company procedure requiring wearing shared headsets amidst the COVID-19 pandemic.
Flight Attendant reported allowing passengers to use a lavatory that had been placed on MEL.
B747 flight crew reported ATC questioned their track departing RJAA when they deviated slightly while in continuous wake turbulence.

E-175 Flight Attendants reported the Battery Containment Bag (BCB) procedures are in need of additional information regarding the handling and disposition of BCB after landing. This BCB procedures deficiency became apparent after dealing with an electronic device's hot battery.
Air carrier Captain reported filing the wrong destination and not realizing it until the FO advised they were going to the wrong airport.
The First Officer of an Airbus A320 reported they were issued a proper clearance; but asked for clarification to ensure the proper altitude.
A Jet crew on the TEB Runway 19 ILS at 2;000 ft took evasive action following a TCAS RA from VFR traffic in IMC without ATC contact. A go around was executed because the aircraft was too high.
C680 Pilot reported having issues with the FMS while flying a STAR requiring a large turn and altitude restriction.
B737-700 flight crew reported being unable to maintain altitude during an encounter with a severe mountain wave over the Rockies.
A Tower Controller reported a Controller had to jump around on the catwalk to get the attention of a fire truck for an inbound emergency aircraft.
Embraer flight crew reported the Tower informed them they received and altitude alert.

Air Ambulance pilot reported being chastised by a supervisor for canceling a patient flight enroute due to weather. He felt this pressure was directly in contradiction with the company policy of making conservative calls.
EMB145 flight crew reported rejected takeoff when First Officer's seat slid back.
A320 flight crew reported executing the escape maneuver after receiving a terrain alert at minimum vectoring altitude on arrival into SJC.
Small aircraft instructor reported on takeoff traffic took off opposite direction resulted in taking evasive action. Instructor reported runway is higher in the middle which may block transmissions.
EMB-145 Captain reported the acquired landing speeds from the approved takeoff and landing data booklet fell into the white band of the low speed awareness tape.
Gulfstream V First Officer reported requesting a vector from ATC to avoid a wake turbulence encounter in his climb out.
Cabin Crew flying 737-700 encountered turbulent flight preventing inflight service.
A319 flight crew reported while on short final to 18C at CLT; ATC cleared an aircraft to cross their runway. Flight crew observed the runway incursion and executed a go-around.
Gulfstream IV Captain reported an upset with a 45 degree bank from wake turbulence in trail of an MD-11 at FL340.
Instructor pilot reported taking evasive action after a near-mid-air-collision while in a holding pattern over the CKN NDB.
ZHU Controller reported a flight that was not flying the correct route.
B737 Captain reported having difficulties with completing the preflight check when the nose wheel-lifting tug is connected.
DH8-400 flight crew reported landing gear retraction issue after takeoff.

FCM Tower Controller reported one aircraft lined up for the wrong runway twice causing one airborne conflict. Controller also stated the controllers cannot determine which runway aircraft are lined up for due to Tower placement.
TRACON Controller working combined positions reported an airborne conflict that could have been worse; but was saved by another Controller.
Captain reported losing localizer signal while on final ILS approach to LGA.

A CRJ-200 pilot reports that an overabundance of deicing fluid applied to his model aircraft results in the fluid being ingested by the APU and passed into the aircraft interior as 'smoke' and fumes like vapor on takeoff. He suggests alternate methods of deicing as well as alternate methods of configuring the aircraft for takeoff to alleviate the problem.
SA-227 Captain reported he was unable to start the engines because of the extreme cold ambient conditions on the ramp.
Piper J3 Cub pilot reported an NMAC at an uncontrolled airport requiring evasive action.
Flight Attendant reported that airplane passengers were boarded onto a hot airplane with no air conditioning.
D01 Controller reported of a mandate that has the controllers slowing every aircraft to 170 knots on final. Controller reported they did not like this and wanted the mandate changed.

TRACON Controller vectored an aircraft to an ILS Approach. The aircraft advised the Controller they were not receiving the localizer and flew through the final approach course. The TRACON Controller's monitor equipment indicated the ILS was selected. The Tower Controller informed the TRACON Controller that the localizer had gone out; but someone had inhibited the aural alarm from sounding by wrapping rubber bands around a toggle switch. The Tower Controller reset the localizer and the aircraft completed its next approach.
Maintenance Technician reported new hose clamps have been installed on air start units that are potentially a safety risk.
B737 First Officer reported two weather related go-arounds and a flap overspeed on approach to DEN.

ZNY Controller reports of an airspace that is going to become hot. He coordinates with the airspace authority to have one last aircraft go through this airspace; which is approved. Minutes later the Controller sees targets entering the area going towards his pointed out aircraft. Coordination ensues; and his aircraft is still approved into the airspace. His aircraft is stopped above the other targets and the pilot reports a RA due to the fast moving targets.

A B777 First Officer made a firm landing which was entered in the Maintenance Log as a hard landing. At the gate a Radio Altimeter C Status alert was discovered and the descent altitude calls were about 10 feet late.
Technician reported aircraft released for service with wrong part installed.
Technician reported disagreement over procedures for releasing an aircraft for service after an extended maintenance event with an open logbook item.

Flight Instructor reported a near miss event at a controlled airport between VFR traffic in the landing pattern and an aircraft on final for an intersecting runway from a practice instrument approach.
Embraer 145 First Officer reported that they received a GPWS Caution Terrain aural warning during takeoff on runway 6 in BHM.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was non-compliant with wearing a face mask during the flight.

Dispatcher reported a B777 crew on an international flight was rerouted by foreign ATC which cost time and fuel. Dispatch discovered later that a 45 minute airspace restriction along their filed route necessitated the reroute.
B737 Captain reported concerns about the weather radar not operating.
B737 First Officer expressed concern about using Flap 1 takeoff procedures; because with the longer takeoff roll a wake turbulence encounter is more likely.

An air carrier reserve Captain reports receiving a missed call from scheduling after going into a FAR 117.21 mandated ten hour rest period. The company disagrees with the pilot union on how this FAR should be interpreted.
SYR Tower Controller reported a runway incursion due to the MQ-9 UAV taxiing too slow and an arrival landing on the runway while the drone was still exiting.
B737 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on descent to LAX in trail of an A380. They stated 'perhaps more separation is necessary' behind super jumbo aircraft.
EMB-145 Captain reported it was likely they departed with some frost on the horizontal stabilizer because of improper deice procedures.
Ground employee reported Hazmat cargo improperly configured as well as incorrectly classified for air transport.
E145 Captain reported a go around due to conflicting traffic landing on an intersecting runway.

Commercial Airline Ground Agent reported Hazmat shipment in AKE (Cargo container) without proper Dangerous Goods documentation. Agent further stated the shipment was initially refused on earlier flight and subsequently reloaded on next flight without documentation corrections.
Dispatcher reported being out of the loop because a flight was delayed by operations without contacting Dispatch.
GA pilot with a safety pilot reported an altitude deviation during climbout.  The pilot cited distraction from goggles fogging up and turbulence was a contributing factor.
Instructor pilot reported a NMAC with a parachute drop plane that made a nonstandard entry into the traffic pattern.
Cessna 400 pilot reported entering Class A airspace without an IFR clearance then IFR conditions when issued a descent.
B737 flight crew reported the SJC2 SID route provides a turn that could lead into converging traffic.
A Captain of a Bombardier CRJ-700 reported that the entire overhead panel was completely dark.
A319 Captain reported encountered severe turbulence enroute at cruise altitude.
B757 Captain reported confusing language found in a temporary taxi alert.
Reporter stated issues with training during COVID-19 pandemic relating to use of correct mask; gloves; and PPE to protect each other in class from exposure to the virus.
A Customer Service Gate Agent reported a known fault with a jetbridge requires using non standard procedures to operate correctly.
Cessna 560XLS Captain reported that the FAA's ATC system does not seem to recognize the subsequent altitude changes included in the ICAO flight plan.
B737-800 Flight Crew reported missing Hazmat required documents at departure airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported taking corrective action after the Enhanced Ground Proximity Warning System was triggered due to a non-standard departure.
Ramp personnel reported aircraft departed with dry ice packs erroneously loaded in forward compartment.
Ramp personnel reported Dangerous Goods Declaration did not match actual cargo shipment received.
Air carrier flight attendants reported an ill passenger remained in the lavatory during landing.

A321 flight crew encountered large hail at 14;000 feet during descent into LAS. The crew was passing through stratus clouds with no radar returns and no large thunderstorms were in the area. After landing the horizontal stabilizer was found to be badly damaged.
CRP Controller reports of two facilities that have the same equipment but do not communicate with each other. Controllers have to make manual handoffs.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported an operational error occurred because of workload distractions.
Citation 550 First Officer reported a loss of oil pressure caused an air turn back and emergency landing.
BJC Tower Controller reported an unsafe but legal operation involving VFR and IFR aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported ATC requested 250 KTS approaching KILDE; but after overshooting final; failed to slow and descend sufficiently so executed a go-around.
A320 flight crew reported deviating from the published procedure due to not having their IPAD/EFB set to display the complete procedure.

An A320 flight crew experienced problems with deicing.  The pad is not well lit and marshallers were not in place to provide guidance. Additionally; the de-ice crews had to be called a couple of times for the start times of the applications and to verify that both Type 1 and 4 fluids were used. Also; there was confusion as to whether or not the equipment was actually clear.

B737 Max flight crew reported that an Auto Shutdown of the Number Two engine on engine start was probably due to the First Officer activating the Isolation switch and the Pack switch during the start.
Air Carrier First Officer reported not following SOP's during a missed approach and stated that an extended period of time off was a contributing factor.
B757 Captain reported that flight delays and fatigue caused him and the First Officer to fall asleep during flight.

ZAB FLM reported that IFR aircraft that transition to TUS Approach airspace; on return flight auto-pop back on the Centers scopes and should be on VFR code not the previous IFR code which causes confusion to the Center Controller.
Air taxi Captain reported while on approach and receiving a heading and altitude instruction from ATC; shortly thereafter receiving a terrain alert from ATC.
Ground personnel reported carelessness with door codes; allowing unauthorized personnel access to secure areas.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was wearing face shield; but no face mask.

Embraer Lineage 1000 flight crew reported the Captain was having difficulty with pitch control after takeoff. Control was reestablished using the FO's pitch trim switch. After returning to the departure airport it was discovered the Captain's pitch trim switch was installed upside down.
Flight Attendant reported sitting in the incorrect jump seat.
Air carrier flight crew reported being on final to a Class Bravo airport and having to go-around due to a small aircraft that was not talking to Tower; which caused the airborne conflict.
B747-800 First Officer reported TCAS warnings during climb.
CE-750 flight crew reported they experienced a track deviation departing SAN when they followed heading guidance from a NOTAM that was not in effect at the time.
Ramp Supervisor reported during arrival audit Dangerous Goods shipment improperly secured in cargo pit and heavy items were stacked on top of 'Do not Stack' label.

A crew expecting the DCA Runway 19 River Visual did not hear the clearance issued so they did not program the FMS transition. When the aircraft did not turn to EYESS; PCT noted the track deviation but then chastised the crew for not asking about the clearance after the EXPECT had been issued.
A319 Captain reported concern about the non-standard procedures and ATC inconsistencies during arrivals to MMMX.
SR22 pilot reported a NMAC while on approach with Tower.
Technician reported an aircraft in storage had an inspection come due without being placed back in service.
Air carrier Captain reported flying an arrival to CLT that company had directed not to accept.
Air carrier Flight Attendant expressed concern that current procedures related to hover boards may not be sufficient to prevent a serious in-flight fire.
An A319 Captain reported taking evasive action to an EGPWS alert level at 5;000 feet preparing for an approach to MDPC/PUJ Runway 9. The NAVIGATIONAL TERRAIN Display did not activate.
Air carrier ground personnel reported moving Dangerous Goods to a cargo pit that did not contain live animal.

ZDC Controller reports about confusion with an aircraft in holding and a skydiving aircraft that wanted to drop above the holding aircraft. The pilots of the two aircraft communicated with each other and the skydiving aircraft stayed below the holding aircraft.

A DO328-300 Captain and Ramp Agent miscommunicated about pushback procedures which resulted in the aircraft being pushed onto an active taxiway then blocking other taxiing air carrier aircraft. The major problem was lack of an active communications method.

ZSE Controller reports of an aircraft that doesn't climb at a faster rate and enters a restricted area after change of communications. Second Controller reports of the same operation; but states it was the Controllers fault for not shipping the aircraft and waiting to the last minute to climb the aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported cleaners did not sanitize the aircraft per company standards/policy.
B737 Captain reported continuously receiving inaccurate weather forecasts for the Seattle area.
B737-800 Captain reported an oxygen mask on his aircraft was placarded certified to 40;000 ft while the aircraft is certified to 41;000 ft.
Flight attendant reported a child who refused to wear a face mask was allowed to board the aircraft causing a delay.
BE-400 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of an Airbus on the CHSLY3 Arrival into CLT.

The Captain thought that the weather was improving and that it would be at or above minimums at the ETA and therefore it was legal to depart; while the Dispatcher did not. The Dispatcher held the flight until the weather improved to minimums.
Load Control Agent reported Ramp Lead corrected misidentified HAZMAT shipment.
Tower Controllers reported an airborne conflict between arriving and departing aircraft that had confirmed visual contact.
Air carrier First Officer reported overshooting an assigned altitude during departure.
CE-560XLS flight crew reported experiencing a ground conflict with another aircraft at TEB.
Pilot reports of LED that are so bright that it was difficult to see the actual runway.
Air carrier pilot reported a galley cart fell on fell on Flight Attendant causing an injury.
First Officer reported refusal of Dangerous Goods shipment due to undefined cargo load weight on board; additionally; FOM does not provide clearly stated weight limitations.
Air carrier Captain reported having inadequate cleaning supplies to sanitize their cockpit equipment.
Captain reported that a ceiling panel came loose during takeoff when the tape securing it failed and it was discovered to be not recorded in the NEF deferral list.
NCT TRACON Controller reported a conflict with a VFR aircraft and an IFR arrival to SJC in the vicinity of KLIDE.

Descending into DFW on the SEEVR THREE RNAV arrival; the flight crew was instructed to cross the SEEVR waypoint between 13;000 ft to 16;000 ft. The flight was re-cleared on the BRDJE THREE RNAV arrival with a new crossing restraint yet somehow the MCP transitioned to SELECT and the flight crew was unable to level for the constraint in time.
BUR Local Controller reported of a loss of separation due to lack of understanding of wake turbulence rules.
Maintenance Technician working on an Airbus A319 reported that they were unable to accomplish a task for engine vibration due to a lack of information.

A Dispatcher reported the difficulty he experienced when required to maintain operational control of 20 flights while concurrent training on an unfamiliar system and completing computer based training. An understaffed Dispatch during multi-airline integration was a contributing factor.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger violated COVID-19 social distancing rules when approaching the galley mid-flight to ask for a beverage.

ZJX controller reports of weather deviations and advises Front Line Manager that the sector was going to become unmanageable if something wasn't done to spread out the traffic. Reporter told of aircraft deviations and possible losses of separation due to deviations. Controller requested fifty miles in trail.
A80 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on the WINNG 1 RNAV Arrival checked on their frequency descending to a lower altitude than prescribed.
Air Carrier First Officer reported an unsafe clearance given by ATC during a night landing in IMC.
An A320 Mechanic reported being unable to clear an MEL item; and then improperly re-deferring the valve due to time pressure and inexperience.
Air Carrier Maintenance Stock Clerk reported being notified that he shipped a Hazardous material part. Stock Clerk is aware of SOP of shipping Hazmat but does not recall the event.

Air carrier Captain reported the DTW 10-8 airport chart is in a west up orientation and is very confusing. The data is dense and relating the 10-8 notes to the 10-9 is difficult. Finding the equivalent 10-9 location in a north up orientation versus the 10-8 west up alignment is mentally difficult.
A Tower controller reported clearing a B757 for takeoff behind another aircraft with less than the required wake turbulence separation.
Sky dive pilot reported possibly canceling the jump due to clouds in the area; but decided against it.
Captain reported a GA aircraft departed in opposite direction while they were on the runway ready for takeoff. The Tower was closed at the time of the event.
Air Carrier pilot reported a discrepancy on altitude between the published charts and the aircraft's navigation database.
A B767 Captain reported dangerous goods loaded in the bulk cargo hold which was not tied down as per company procedures. This issue is on going for his international flights.
C172 Pilot reported VFR in IMC and non-compliance with ATC instructions due to safety of flight and PIC authority.
EMB-175 flight crew reported overshooting a crossing restriction departing LAX when they were distracted by a wake turbulence encounter.
A Fractional flight crew flying the VNY Runway 16R ILS reported taking evasive action from VFR traffic as they began their descent at 5;000 feet. The crew cited fatigue as a factor.
Air carrier flight crew reported moderate to severe turbulence over Utah. A Flight Attendant might have pulled a muscle.
B737 Captain reported concern regarding some training techniques that are presented to the new hire pilots.
SCT TRACON Controller reported confusion from an inbound LGB pilot on a the new Metroplex RNAV Approach clearance.
Aircraft parts stock clerk reported conflicting company procedures regarding the shipping of critical aircraft parts.
Technician reported discovering the tang washer on the old nose tire and wheel assembly instead of reinstalled on the aircraft after a tire change.

Captain refused aircraft due to repeat CSD (Constant Speed Drive) oil leak on left engine. Company reassigned aircraft to another crew which accepted and flew the trip. That crew experienced the same CSD leak.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with face mask policy.
The Captain of an Embraer 175 reported going around after receiving a GPWS warning to pull-up during initial approach.

An Air Traffic Controller reported that a VFR aircraft did not display on the radar display; and an air carrier departure received an RA on the VFR target as the Controller was issuing a vector to avoid.
B737 flight crew reported after descending to the altitude assigned by ATC; they were told to climb back to their previous altitude because they were below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Pilot reported a sporting TFR airspace incursion due to not having it selected on the app being used.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy.
Technician reported that maintenance entries with technician's employee number and log book entry were incorrectly used to clear an unrelated MEL item.
The flight crew of a Boeing 737 reported that ATC cleared them 'Direct To' a way point without further instructions or clearances after they arrived at the way point.
Dispatcher reported receiving a flight crew message indicating a temporary loss of aircraft control during severe turbulence encounter.
Air carrier Captain reported severe icing in cruise in the vicinity of IAH.
CRJ700 Captain reported a NMAC with a UAV 20 NM south of LGA at 3500 ft.
Air Carrier flight crew reported confusion with ATC and receiving multiple reroutes on short flight resulting in increased workload.

ZID Front Line Manager (FLM) reported of a loss of separation when a controller climbed an aircraft through another aircrafts altitude then turns and descends the same aircraft. Controller realized it and the voice inflection is what brought it to the FLM's attention. FLM debated on what could have been done after the fact of the loss of separation.

A crew on approach to ORD Runway 28R executed a go-around when they realized at the outer marker that ATIS reported the runway closed. The FMS had been programmed during preflight hours earlier; but the fatigued crew forgot to change the arrival data.
Flight Attendant reported that passenger headphone chords often block the aircraft aisle.
A320 First Officer reported diverting due to deteriorating weather and a fuel critical situation.
Flight attendants reported a passenger needed to be continuously reminded about having to wear a face mask.
A330 flight crew reported inability to communicate with ground crew during pushback; resulting in a pushback without a clearance.

B767 Captain reported a CAT3 status message was incorrectly deferred then Maintenance changed the deferral to the left Air Data Intertial Reference Unit (ADIRU) which caused the SATCOM to be unusable.
B757 First Officer reported he was concerned with ATC procedures on arrival into ATL that increased workload.

B737-800 Captain reported severe turbulence and injuries to flight attendants; even though they were strapped in; during descent on the KEPEC3 Arrival to LAS. He suggested that the arrival be revised because it is always turbulent in that area when the winds are from the southwest.

Traffic Management Coordinator reports of issues related to flow control and the fact that when asked for an extension the Air Traffic Control Command Center (ATCSCC) denies the request. The reporter then goes on to report that the ATCSCC cannot see the traffic and should not deny the controllers what they need for flow.
Flight crew reported crossing a runway; taxiing to parking; while not sure if the clearance instructions were for them due to Tower muffling the callsign.

EUG TRACON Controller reported being distracted by an aircraft not in his airspace that was giving off a low altitude alert. While observing this aircraft; one under his control entered ZSE airspace without a correct handoff.  Reporter cited STARS as being part of the problem.
A TRACON Controller reported being forced to work an overloaded finals sector to two runways for extended periods of time due to the TRACON not having the staffing to de-combine sectors.
A319 flight crew reported that the rescheduled departure was pushed 2 hours later than the crew desk wanted to reduce earthquake induced stress and fatigue.
EMB-145LR Captain reported a communications breakdown between flight crew and Dispatch and Load Planning personnel regarding Dry Ice shipment.

Captain reported ATC issued a climb 2;000 feet above the filed altitude; crew took the altitude and did not question ATC.  Another Center Sector Controller eventually asked crew why they were going to newly assigned altitude instead of filed altitude.
An air carrier Flight Attendant reported that a Gate Agent knowingly issued a jump seat to a non-crew; standby passenger who was accompanied by a jump seating Flight Attendant.
Customer Service employees reported social distancing concerns at the airport lobby due to it being overcrowded.
SUX Tower Controller reported that an airport taxi diagram is out of date; causing confusion among aircraft taxiing.
P80 TRACON Controller reported that airspace structure resulted in an airborne conflict.
Air Carrier Captain reported a track deviation and cited task saturation; hunger and lack of flying as contributing factors.

EMB175 flight crew performing walk around inspection discovered cargo in the aft hold. The cargo was supposed to be unloaded at a prior destination but it was not and it was not included on the weight and balance.
A TRACON Controller reported they mistakenly assigned an aircraft an altitude below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was not compliant with mask policy and being disruptive. Captain reported multiple interruptions during the flight in order to communicate with company about the situation.
Approach Controller reported a disagreement with another controller over rules for vectoring aircraft below the MVA.
Flight Attendant reported a possibly intoxicated person was removed from flight.

Corporate Captain reported that New York (N90) TRACON instructed the crew to maintain 250 knots below the shelf of New York Class B airspace. The reporter stated N90 controllers consistently ask aircraft to maintain greater than 200 knots below the shelf.
An air carrier crew flying the RSW TYNEE ONE RNAV were cleared direct ODERY but heard RODDE; 5 NM from ODERY because they were phonetically similar on the radio.
Helicopter pilot reported encountering icing conditions and landed at the nearest airport.
SCT TRACON Controller reported STARS or Fusion radar erroneously displayed a target which caused a fake conflict with a SNA arrival.

On preflight exterior inspection the Captain of a B737 discovered low #1 engine Integrated Drive Generator (IDG) oil level. He proceeded to document the discrepancy and call for maintenance servicing. Upon arrival at the aircraft; the Maintenance Technician questioned the Captain's authority to be inspecting inside the engine cowling to check the IDG oil level on preflight. The Technician serviced the IDG oil and documented the action in the aircraft logbook while kneeling behind the flight-deck center console. Prior to taxi the Captain noticed that the PA microphone; which is stowed on the rear side of the center console; had oil smeared on it.
Air carrier First Officer reported needing to deviate around an area of forecast severe turbulence and was unable to get complete clearance to do so.
ZMP Controller reported a ZKC controller climbing an aircraft in his airspace without approval.

Air carrier Captain reported deviating from charted course on departure in part because the aircraft was climbing quicker than normal due to a very low passenger count as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.
