Flight Crew reported concerns over a fuel imbalance with an inoperative Fuel Quantity Indicator .
UH-60 Pilot reported encountered icing; received accumulation warning; but failed to understand and activate the rotor ice protection system. Pilot reference UH-60 Operator's Manual suggestion.
B737 flight crew reported they encountered moderate turbulence on approach causing injuries to crew.

LAX Local Assist reports of two Super aircraft east of Taxiway Whiskey which is not allowed if an aircraft is on the ILS to Runway 24L. Controller did not advise local which was a developmental to ask the pilot if they could accept a visual approach instead of the ILS. This would have solved the problem.

SDL Local Controller reports of a attempted handoff late from P50. SDL Controller advises he's unable handoff due to other traffic. Minutes later the aircraft is on his frequency cleared for a visual approach. SDL Controller had to send an aircraft around due to the overtake from the un-handed off aircraft. A new LOA went into place 3 days prior.
Air carrier First Officer reported programming the FMC incorrectly for the SID; which resulted in a track deviation. Reporter noted several delays that contributed to rushing through cockpit set up.

ATL Tower Controller reported of crossing two aircraft and then doing other duties. Controller then cleared an aircraft for takeoff; second crossing traffic advised Controller they have not crossed yet. Controller had crossing aircraft hold short of runway allowing departing aircraft to get airborne. Controller thought the second crossing aircraft was slow in its taxi and that is why they forgot about it.
TRACON Controller reported the incorrect aircraft read back the approach clearance which caused a confliction with traffic landing on a parallel runway.
EMB-175 First Officer reported operating a delayed flight with passengers even though the Dispatch Release indicated the flight was to be operated as a ferry flight.
Pilot reported heavy smoke from wildfires and becoming sick while flying because of the smoke.
Pilot reported descent below safe altitude while on base and final.
B737 Captain reported confusion during fueling with inoperative fuel quantity gauges; resulting in a fuel imbalance and delay for correction.
B737 flight crew reported in flight notification from Dispatch regarding undocumented Hazmat onboard.
B747-800 Captain reported severe turbulence and provided recommendations.
B737 Captain reported multiple incidences of close proximity events with other aircraft while on parallel approaches at DEN.
GPT TRACON Controller reported descending aircraft below the MVA.
B737-700 Captain reported passenger boarded with Lithium Ion battery incorrectly packaged for transport. Ground personnel were not aware of the HAZMAT battery safety procedure.
Helicopter air taxi pilot reported entering instrument conditions and an unusual attitude while operating with night vision goggles.
C560XL flight crew reported an upset when they encountered 'severe' wake turbulence from a preceding A380 on arrival into the LAX basin.
PA28 pilot reported losing nearly 1000 ft in a mountain wave encounter in the vicinity of MOL VOR.
A Controller vectored an aircraft to an ILS Approach below the MVA (Minimum Vectoring Altitude) and ILS interception inside the Final Approach Fix.
Flight Instructor reported VFR aircraft violating cloud clearance requirements.
B737-800 flight crew reported the aircraft was unable to meet the published crossing restrictions.
Front Line Manager reported that there were high winds causing drift of aircraft on departure. This lead to an unsafe situation when the faster aircraft departed behind the slower aircraft.
An air carrier maintenance Technician reported on the events that led to the incorrect installation of escape slides on a B737-700.
A-319 crew had difficulty coordinating service from ground service contractor.

Air carrier flight crew requested new STAR prior to TOD; but multiple Centers failed to issue new STAR. When issued; there was insufficient time to brief and load; leading to an overflown crossing altitude.
A ZDC Controller received approval for an aircraft to fly through a Warning Area. While in the Warning Area the aircraft was involved in a NMAC and responded to an RA.

A Jacksonville Center (ZJX) radar associate Controller reports of confusion around restricted areas being hot or not and how high the altitudes were if they are hot or not. The Controller tried many times to get the correct answer from the Front Line Manager (FLM) and Traffic Management Coordinators (TMC). The Controller reports of; 'The entire scenario was one of mass confusion; unnecessary movement of aircraft and potential loss of separation.'

An air carrier on frequency with a company similar call sign was called incorrectly by ATC which confused everyone. Finally; with a frequency change; the crew descending to 14;000 was told their clearance was to 17;000; but that crew never heard 17;000.
EMB-175 Captain reported a SLAT FAIL EICAS message appeared each time flaps were selected on approach.

Boise TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict during heavy traffic period; which he quickly separated.  Reporter stated airspace complexity and traffic volume related distractions contributed to the event.
CE525 pilot operating as an insurance professional reported the PIC drifted towards the parallel runway while departing. It was discovered the aircraft was in heading mode rather than VNAV.
Air carrier flight crew reported landing without contacting Tower.
Technician reported difficulties in the installation of an O-Ring on a lavatory sink stopper without the required special tool being available.

Air carrier Captain reported that the First Officer developed signs of hypoxia and noted that during the layover the area had experienced heavy smoke which had been drawn into the First Officer's hotel room through the air conditioning system.
Pilot reported a NMAC while in the landing phase of flight.
CE560E pilot reported getting a low altitude alert from ATC.
B737 Captain reported that he refused an aircraft due to safety concerns over an MEL that was not being addressed for repair; but continued in service.
Pilot reported diverting due to weather; then received email notifications from FAA that were confusing.
C172 pilot reported an NMAC with a Beechcraft Bonanza.
Private Pilot reported an uneventful landing after a power line strike during final approach to landing.
B737 Captain reported a baggage cart cut in front of the aircraft during taxi into the gate.
B737 Captain reported that logbook issues and a lack of consensus caused a lengthy flight delay.
SCT Departure Controller describes coordination problems with LAX Tower and the tower not following SOP.
A PIT Ramp Agent reported the new digital Ramp Control radio transmissions were garbled; muffled and unintelligible which caused confusion and is a potential safety issue for personnel and aircraft.
Pilot reported not obtaining a Ferry Permit after loss of electrical power caused a runway excursion.
Flight Attendant reported losing focus and using a cell phone app while parked and waiting for a gate.
Pilot reported an NMAC event during approach for landing by a non-reporting aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger appeared intoxicated during boarding; but was allowed to fly.  During the flight the passenger was non-compliant with face mask policy.
Air Carrier Maintenance Technician reports that Ground Service Equipment is not being properly maintained.
San Jose Tower Controller reported departing an aircraft after being told to hold by the Departure Controller. Reporter did not use the hold placard made just for this purpose.
B737 Captain reported no formal training on new procedures before introduction; insufficient explanation of differences between triggers; flows; procedures; and policy compliance.
Two Lake Front Tower Controllers reported that an aircraft cut off the sequenced first arrival; causing the first aircraft to go around.
Center Controller reported an aircraft descended below what the Controller thought was the assigned altitude.
Observer at LMO airport reported skydivers were crossing the runway in their descent to the ground; posing a safety hazard.
B777 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence in departure climb.
Air carrier Captain reported a weight and balance change was not cross-checked due to COVID-19 company guidelines as cited by the Load Master.
A Pilot landing at a non towered airport reported after landing encountering another aircraft on the opposite direction runway.

A pilot reported of incorrectly entering a waypoint that sounded the same; but was spelled differently. The FMS took the incorrect waypoint because it is in the database. The pilot saw a discrepancy and asked for a vector. Pilot stated that it was unfortunate that there are two fixes so closely spelled and that he and his student both missed it.

Air carrier flight crew reported getting confusing intersections and routings they were not familiar with and were also concerned about being routed more than 50 miles off shore by ZJX Center; which reportedly does this often.
ZTL Controller reported an airspace deviation occurred due to excessive workload related to insufficient facility staffing.
B757 First Officer reported having maintenance items that were not properly written up in the aircraft maintenance logbook.
Flight Attendant reported that a child was not wearing a face mask and was coughing.  Parents attempted to put mask on the child; but were not successful.
Small aircraft pilot reported that after flying over the aforementioned area; a NOTAM and / or chart notation regarding the bird migration should be issued.
After pushback ground crew failed to complete proper steps to reengage nose wheel steering. Air carrier flight crew noticed immediately upon taxi out they had no control over aircraft steering.
ZAU Center Controller reported a foreign air carrier refused to comply with a speed assignment.
A Boeing 787 Captain reported that on pushback the Marshaller gave salute and release signal prior to acknowledgment from cockpit.

After a straight out departure in IMC from an airport surrounded by high terrain; the flight crew engaged the FMS and the aircraft made a right turn which was not immediately recognized as an error. The pilot monitoring and departure control advised of the incorrect heading. The flight crew was unable to identify why the FMS turned the aircraft to the right.
A B737-NG crew reported not executing the Escape Maneuver after an EGPWS Caution Terrain alert while flying a night VMC visual approach to BUR Runway 33.
Air Carrier Captain reported an unstable approach due to poor CRM and lack of flying.
A300 flight crew encountered anomaly with avionics equipment during approach.

Air carrier flight crew reported the iPad's navigational software did an update automatically causing it to go to a black screen then to the home screen. This causes the iPad as a navigation aid to be undependable.
Air carrier Captain reported a UAV at 8;000 feet near Jamesburg; NJ while on vectors to LGA Runway 4.
Pilot reported that there is an unlighted communications tower by an interstate freeway that poses a hazard.

A Southern California TRACON Controller reports of a loss of separation with an aircraft he was vectoring to an airport while another faster aircraft departed a different airport. The departing aircraft speed was too fast and the aircraft was wide in turning leading to the loss of separation.

UNV Tower released a departing aircraft into a conflicting route with an aircraft conducting a missed approach. The Tower Controller put the aircraft on the ZNY Controller's frequency without taking any action to separate the aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation during climbout.
Commercial flight crew reported inability to communicate with ramp crew.
A Traffic Management Coordinator reported an aircraft departed on an RNAV SID that could not comply with the crossing restrictions.
Maintenance personnel reported that oil service bowsers were not properly calibrated.
Ground crew reported brakes were released prior to notification.
The 737 First Officer reported receiving the take-off data and it contained errors for the airport.
The First Officer of an Embraer EMB175 reported that they aborted takeoff because the throttles were inadvertently pushed past auto-throttle position for takeoff.
Corporate turbojet Captain reported not noticing the altitude restrictions on the electronic chart display.
DH8-400 Captain reported refusing the aircraft after maintenance improperly complied with MEL procedures.
ERJ-175 flight crew reported taking evasive action on departure due to a TCAS RA.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing windshear on final approach; and a subsequent go-around.
Embraer Regional Jet First Officer reported confusion with similar spelled fixes HEVEN and HAVEN resulted in a heading deviation.
C182 pilot reported an NMAC with a UAV at 8000' in the vicinity of CXP airport.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an altitude deviation while crossing an arrival fix.
Technicians reported non compliance with Company and Manufacturer's manuals when attempting to repair a door problem.
ZOA Center Controller reported forgetting to handoff an aircraft to ZLA Airspace.
Air carrier Captain reported an incident with ground crew during gate departure operations.
Air carrier flight crew reported the REL [Runway Entry Lights] activated as they started to taxi across Runway 35 center following ATC's instructions.
B777 Captain reported a discrepancy relating to the flap settings and speeds during pre-flight.
P50 Controller describes a new procedure for the arrival sectors. One aircraft overshot their turn to final causing a conflict.
Piper 34 pilot reported loss of GPS signal when approaching RFD led to a Class C airspace penetration.
Air carrier pilot reported that a building nearby EWR airport has high intensity; flashing LED type lights that sometimes appear to be REIL or RAIL lights and could be confusing to pilots.
ZJX Center Controller reported not having enough time to point an aircraft out to another sector due to workload and weather deviations in their sector.

ORD Tower Controller reports of having a loss of separation due to an extended time period passing before the aircraft was airborne. Controller approved a departure that would enter his airspace; but the aircraft was not ready. Time period passed and Controller let one of his departures go around the same time the other approved departure went airborne. Separation was lost due to both aircraft being on the same heading and close proximity.
D01 Controller reported the facility was unable to provide clarification on issuing an Obstacle Departure Procedure clearance per the facility and national directives.
PA-28 instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the vicinity  of CMA airport.
Jetbridge driver reported the B737 cabin door was popped open during the docking maneuver.
Air taxi Captain reported ATC question an early turn on departure from SJC.

ZKC Developmental reported of releasing an aircraft off of a controlled airport and not getting radar contact on it until 24 minutes after it departed. This aircraft also violated another Approach Control's airspace. Procedure to call reporting the aircraft as rolling or departing; is reportedly not being followed.
First Officer who was deadheading reported a disagreement with a Flight Attendant regarding interpretation of mask policy.
Airline pilot reported scheduling problems.

An air carrier crew departed a CTAF airport VFR while another air carrier jet was on approach but in hindsight felt they should have delayed to prevent the perception of conflict given CTAF communications limitations.
Flight Attendant reported that the cockpit security procedure for the Boeing and Airbus aircraft does not match.

A Ground Controller issued taxi instructions for an arriving aircraft to proceed down an alley to gate. Unknown to the Controller; that gate was already occupied by an aircraft not visible from the newly constructed Control Tower.
Cessna 402C Captain reported returning to the departure airport after experiencing significant ice accumulation on approach to their destination.
Dash 8 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to EWR in trail of a B757.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported dangerous goods cargo incorrectly configured for air transport.
Air Carrier flight crew reported arrival crossing restriction not met due to pocket of moderate turbulence encountered during descent.
CRJ Captain reported receiving a wind shear warning and traffic conflict on final approach; resulting in a missed approach.
Technician reported distraction and fatigue interfered with proper procedures and caused an aircraft to ingest an engine plug.
ZAU Instructor and developmental reported descending an aircraft 100 feet below the MIA.
E145 Pilot reported getting a shock when attempting to check an aircraft window while in the rain.

A B777 crew enroute to Western Asia over an easterly routing; realized the high terrain beneath their route offered no emergency high terrain escape procedures. The dispatch software apparently contained no check for flight contingencies over high altitude terrain.
C182 Flight Instructor reported ATC issuing a vector into traffic resulted in pilot taking extreme collision avoidance maneuver.
B737 Captain reported a Flight Attendant was slightly injured during a wake turbulence encounter on arrival into LAX following an A380.

ZLC Controller reported of an overdue aircraft notice. Aircraft was found to have returned to the departure airport soon after takeoff.  Reporter blamed ERAM and supervisors for not knowing where the aircraft was.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.
Maintenance Technician installed incorrect tire on 737-900 aircraft.
Regional jet flight crew reported exiting the runway on a taxiway that was closed per NOTAM. There were no cones or other airport markings to indicate that the taxiway was closed.
B777 Captain reported wake turbulence encounter 20 miles in trail of a B747 at FL340; which resulted in a minor injury to a flight attendant.

A ramp controller reported observing a conflict where one aircraft was given instructions to taxi; while another aircraft was not. The aircraft would have been a major conflict if one of the pilots did not wait for the other aircraft to move without instructions.
An air carrier Captain reported receiving a low fuel indication during final approach and suggested that the company's fuel management program did not allow enough fuel for adequate safety margins.

B737 Captain described the confusion created when a deice crew failed to utilize the standardized communications before; during; and after deicing his aircraft. The process nearly resulted in a ground collision with a deice vehicle.
B737 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence associated with a weather system; resulting in several non-crew injuries in the passenger cabin.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported missing an alternate airport requirement; attributing the error to being ill and not wanting to leave due to a company 'negative attendance' policy.
Air Carrier Captain reported going around after ATC advised off course; even though there was no off course indication.

A Dispatcher reported that their flight planning program can have routes filed blocked as warning areas become active. There is no indication to dispatchers of warning areas going active in regards to conflicts with flights that have already been filed through that airspace before the activation.
EMB-170 Captain reported landing with less than required reserve fuel due to routing change and lower enroute altitude because of turbulence.
HCF Controller reported problems with an air carrier departing VFR; flying outbound on the localizer with traffic inbound to the airport resulting in a NMAC.

HTS TRACON Controller reported of an airspace violation with a VFR aircraft and a MOA. The aircraft came too close to the airspace. The Controller did not turn on the MOA display on the scope which is something he normally did. Controller believes this led to him forgetting the airspace was hot and the possible airspace violation.
GA pilot reported that airport 1TE4 (Zuehl) is no longer on the San Antonio Sectional chart and near its place is TT45; a private airport.
Air taxi Captain reported taxiing the nose gear of the aircraft into a drainage culvert.
A321 Captain reported better training required for cabin crew regarding handling and reporting of fume events.
Air carrier flight crew reported multiple changes to the arrival and runway resulted in a track heading deviation.  Flight crew stated lack of flying was a contributing factor.
ZOB controller reported sector workload being overwhelmingly unsafe; due to weather and aircraft.  TMU and management failed to help.

Fairbanks TRACON Controller reported of anticipating a pilot's descent to be above the Minimum Vectoring Altitude when he issued the descent. The pilot descended quicker than the Controller thought and ended up having a Minimum Vectoring Altitude violation.
Light sport aircraft pilot reported concern that uncoordinated helicopter traffic could affect light weight aircraft operations.
Air carrier Captain reported an unreliable airspeed indication resulted in an over speed condition.

Air carrier First Officer reported flying into an airport with the Tower closed due to COVID-19 and described the associated issues flying in the same environment with inexperienced general aviation pilots.
A321 Captain reported a repair made to his aircraft by a non-mechanic. This repair was made by a ramp manager then an actual Mechanic came out shortly after to re-do the repair correctly.
After ATC issued a turn toward the airport; a C750 flight crew descended from assigned altitude thinking they were cleared for a visual approach.
Airline Maintenance Technician reported refusing to ship Hazmat aircraft parts due to procedural violations.
B737 Captain reported a taxiway incursion occurred when he missed a taxi clearance issued during the landing roll out.
A330 flight crew reported that a fuel system MEL was removed and replaced with a different fuel system MEL; causing a greater work load for the flight crew.
Piper pilot reported that float gear would not extend; causing a diversion and precautionary emergency landing.
Ground personnel reported Captain started engine without obtaining clearance; almost ingesting a ground agent.
Embraer jet flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence departing DFW in trail of an A321. Crew suggested increasing separation on departure.
ZDC ARTCC Controller reported being unable to provide separation to aircraft in conflict and subsequent approach clearance; due to limited radar and radio coverage in the area.
D10 TRACON Controller reported numerous aircraft coming from ZFW late and not in agreement with the D10/ZFW Letter of Agreement.

ZLA Controller explains that an aircraft reported an aircraft 500 feet below them. The area and altitude of the aircraft was in SCT airspace and also an area of known parachuting. The aircraft was on a discreet code indicating its involvement. The ZLA Controller advised that SCT should have issued traffic to the aircraft or kept the aircraft until the conflict was no longer a factor.
CRJ-900 Captain reported that a gust from a nearby thunderstorm caused a right roll during the landing flare; which resulted in a wingtip contacting the runway.
EMB-145 First Officer reported a lightning strike during arrival to GSP and faulted Dispatch for not warning them about the weather.
Pilot reported confusion over whether or not a TFR was active; and reported ATC was unhelpful.
A BE-300 flight Captain reported experiencing a flap failure during approach and landing. Flight crew had difficulty controlling aircraft in the final phase of landing.
CRJ-900 Captain reported an NMAC event while under control of Approach Control during approach with a departing helicopter.
Air Carrier Captain reported a low fuel situation that resulted from poor fuel planning and a delay for taxi at the departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported that while in the flare to land a helicopter was cleared to cross their runway.
An airline Dispatcher reported the computer system overrode their plan without their concurrence or knowledge.
GIV Captain reported encountering wake turbulence descending out of FL400 from preceding A320; resulting in 45 degree bank in both directions.
B737 Captain reported encountering severe mountain wave activity that resulted in altitude excursion.
Air carrier flight crew reported airspace; heading; and altitude deviations during approach.  The Captain stated a late runway change and Controller workload were contributing factors.
B737 Captain reported a miscommunication when asking for advice for documenting a scratch in the logbook at an outstation.
Air carrier flight crew reported not calling Ramp before taxiing.

An air taxi turboprop Captain reported being advised by air traffic control of possible birds in the vicinity of his aircraft. It appeared the target was actually a drone operating nearby; however it did not appear to be a threat; and no evasive action was required.
EMB145 First Officer reported mis-communications with ATC and CRM failure resulting in altitude deviation.
The flight attendant questioned the wisdom of refueling with the cabin doors armed while still connected to the jetway; reportedly to keep the crew from going illegal.
CL600 flight crew reported their right engine shut down after they experienced a lightning strike on approach.

The solo pilot was surprised to be advised in-flight that an sporting event TFR was in place around the ballpark when his weather briefer had been specifically stated that no TFR was planned during the flight briefing two hours prior.
Air carrier Load Planner reported Hazmat document errors resulting in Hazmat cargo incorrectly configured for air transport.
Upon aircraft power up at the start of preflight; crew experienced TAWS fail warnings. Maintenance Control and Dispatch provided the crew with a manufacturer bulletin that cleared the problem.
Air Carrier Captain reported written dialog and depiction on chart do not agree.
Flight Attendant reported a confrontational passenger who did not want to comply with face mask policy.
Local Controller reported that a small aircraft was hit with jet blast while on the runway at BFI airport. Controller stated that this happens frequently and suggested adding a message on the ATIS.
ZTL ARTCC Controller reported issuing the wrong arrival procedure to an aircraft.
Load Planner reported compliance inconsistency between company policy and HAZMAT requirements.
ZDV Controller reported of problems with skydiving aircraft that allow jumpers out when traffic is below them.
C172 Flight Instructor reported a runway excursion following a crosswind landing and an attempted go around.
CRJ-200 Captain reported failure to comply with an AD; in part because of poor communication on the issue from the company.
C208 pilot reported executing an improper engine shutdown procedure; causing damage to the fuel control system.
MSP Tower Controller reported a conflict when clearing an aircraft while an arrival was within the Arrival Departure Window.
PA-28 Student Pilot reported a NMAC with a Beechcraft in the vicinity of a non-towered airport.
A320 Captain reported his aircraft was dispatched with SEC2 (a flight control computer) fault deferred; and expressed concern about the safety of this procedure.
Air carrier First Officer reports chronic fatigue problem flying the schedules his airline builds; with no help from FAR 117.
An Air Carrier pilot reported responding to a TCAS RA for a flight of two military aircraft.

An Air Traffic Controller reported a rocket launch protected airspace within a TRACON boundary. The TRACON was working two aircraft heading toward the airspace and had not been advised the airspace was in use.
B787 Captain reported receiving an erroneous CPDLC message from ATC regarding assigned flight level.
NORCAL TRACON Controller reported an unsafe situation attributed to IFR/VFR traffic and the lack of Class B airspace overlying the large airport in the airspace.
Remote pilot reported a failure to research airspace and operated UAV in restricted airspace without authorization.
Technician reported that a bird strike inspection was not accomplished as required by ETOPS prior to aircraft departure and caused a flight delay.
BE200 pilot reported an error in sequencing waypoints when loading arrivals into the G1000.

Flight Attendant reported a family with child were removed from the flight because of non-compliance regarding face mask policy. The family was incorrectly told by station agents; a mask was not required for the 2 year old.
Captain reported conflict in new aircraft performance manuals.
Flight crew deviated off course while on VOR approach to maintain VMC conditions to the airport.

A controller working airspace above 24;000 feet reported taking the handoff of a departure about 11;000 feet. The pilot advised the controller of TCAS traffic in front of them at 15;500 feet and that they were responding by reducing their climb rate. At the same time; the Conflict Alert appeared on the controller's radar display.
Technicians reported leaving a flashlight in the nose wheel well after maintenance; causing the nose landing gear to not retract; causing an Air Turn Back and precautionary landing.

Beech A36 pilot reported being on approach to land after a vintage bomber and before an approaching King Air; and elected to land after being told to go-around by Tower due to the proximity of the King Air. A36 pilot cited his aircraft's speed and altitude and the probability of encountering vintage bomber's wake turbulence in disregarding the Tower's instructions.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with the face mask policy; nor cell phone regulations.
B767 First Officer reported calling in fatigued after cancellation the previous day; being unable to rest due to no air conditioning at the hotel and changed circadian rhythm.

B737 Captain reported that; during deplaning of passengers; a Ramp Agent advised that the aircraft's nose gear was rising and the tail was falling. Passenger deplaning was stopped until aircraft stabilized.
ZFW controller reported ZME failed to comply with traffic management initiative and rerouted aircraft without updating routings.
Boeing 737 flight crew reported TCAS announced a climb directive when traffic was above.
Flight Attendant reported the requirement to wear a mask during flights is resulting in having low blood oxygen levels and associated headaches.
Pilot reported an NMAC with a powered parachute resulted in taking evasive action.
A pilot ferrying an aircraft reported a rejected takeoff due to abnormal engine indications from not removing the cowling inlet plugs.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing severe turbulence which caused momentary excursions from their assigned altitude.

LAS Tower Controller reports on the use of visual separation during a training session; with the second aircraft performing better than the trainee expected. TRACON Controller turned aircraft further to avoid more separation issues.
Center Controller reported multiple airspace violations resulting from hand-off issues with adjacent sector controllers.

B767 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence over the North Atlantic injuring several passengers and flight attendants. The crew elected to divert to an alternate airport rather than continue to their destination.
Embraer aircraft flight crew reported confusion on the SJC2 SID; resulting in a deviation from clearance.

A319 Captain reported a TCAS RA on final into CLT. Pilot responded to the RA by climbing 100 feet. The resolution cleared and the aircraft landed on the assigned runway. Traffic was for a parallel runway.
B737-800 Captain reported the CAVLR2 arrival into IAD and the CAPSS1 arrival into DCA are not flown properly by his aircraft's autoflight system.
Aircrew agreed to let a deadheading company qualified in-type pilot join the crew as a working crewmember for the trip.
A ZSU Center Controller reported they were forced to combine sectors against their wishes. The Controller assigned a climb rate to an aircraft but the aircraft did not comply with the instruction.
Denver TRACON Final controllers reported a loss of separation when the leading aircraft went through the other aircraft's final.
Dallas TRACON Controller reported not being able to run an RNAV approach while running simultaneous ILS approaches.
Captain reported experiencing a speed deviation below 10;000 feet and stated First Officer's lack of flying contributed to the event.
Ground employee the B787 has an illuminated door lock light on the passenger entry door which can be misinterpreted.
Captain reported cleaning crew came onto plane after passengers left; but most did not wear face masks as per company instructions.
Instructor Pilot reported a NMAC.
A Tower Controller reported they issued a departure clearance to the wrong aircraft due to similar sounding call signs from the same company.
ADW Tower Local Controller reported two helicopters departed without a clearance conflicting with departing traffic.  The helicopters also entered Class B airspace without clearance.
Air carrier Captain reported communication issues with Departure Control and made reference to Controller possibly being overworked or stressed out due to COVID-19 staffing levels.
SCT TRACON Controller reported false targets on the STARS display that were put there by Maintenance. He recommended an independent operational evaluation on the STARS system at SCT.

A B757 stopped short of the gate during taxi in because the autoparking and Marshaller gave conflicting directions. While waiting for a tow in; a another aircraft's ground crew pushed their aircraft to where it's winglet struck the B757's horizontal stabilizer.
TRACON Controller reported receiving a maintenance test tone through the headset.
A CRJ-700 Captain reported challenges when flying an approach into airports at night as their company does not allow visual approaches at night with no operating Control Tower.
Air carrier Captain reported overseas GPS jamming.

Memphis Center Controllers reported an IFR aircraft that flew from one sector to the next without coordination. Neither Controller had information on the traffic and it was found that someone had removed the flight plan on the aircraft.
PWK Tower Controller working multiple combined positions reported allowing an aircraft to taxi across an active runway with another aircraft on takeoff roll.
Flight Attendant reported difficulty and confusion with guidelines when trying to enforce the mandatory mask requirement.
First Officer reported asking to be removed from the flight after disagreement with Captain over correct actions to be taken after excessive vibrations on an engine were not followed.

Pilot entered Montgomery airport's airspace without clearance due to a congested Tower frequency and nearby Class B airspace as well as conflicting traffic. Pilot climbed above pattern altitude and eventually received clearance from Tower.
Salt Lake Center Controllers reported problems associated with a refueling operation and air traffic close proximity to a Restricted Airspace.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported receiving an 'AFT CARGO OVERHEAT' caution message and had difficulty finding the procedure in the QRH.
Captain refused aircraft due to multiple maintenance issues.
Maintenance technician reported an inspector refused to sign-off the final document associated with the work per the procedures manual.
Air carrier ramp personnel reported that smart bag was mistakenly transported in cargo with battery attached.

A general aviation flight instructor and student departed in marginal weather in an attempt to fly around or under the weather. When this did not work out; the instructor obtained an IFR clearance and entered instrument conditions; but then began to pick up ice. After turning around and shedding the ice; ATC assisted with continued flight with intermittent icing conditions until locating VFR conditions.
PA-28 Flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during training flight.
Air carrier First Officer reported ingesting aerosol fumes in the flight deck from cleaners that were on board sanitizing the aircraft without making sure it was empty.
A Fractional jet pilot reported a rejected takeoff due to striking a dog on the runway.
Flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence and a downdraft causing excursion from assigned altitude.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) reports observing three Regional jet aircraft that were towed with high speed Tugs on active taxiways at airport without Navigation or Anti-collision lights on and no Mechanic in the cockpit to ride brakes.
A maintenance person reported that the aircraft was pressurized when the door was opened; causing the door to explode open sending the mechanic back about 20 feet.
Lead pilot of a warbird formation flight in Washington DC reported he deviated from assigned altitude because of concerns for wake turbulence and bird encounters.
A Tower Controller reported on the difficulties with single engine piston aircraft complying with a new departure procedure.

ZLA controllers reported a UAV that could not accept holding instructions and was not allowed in restricted airspace. One Controller was confused and had busy airspace. The MOS did not have any special instructions to help and the UAV made circles until a resolution was found.
ZAN Controller with a trainee issued a descent to an aircraft which was below the Minimum IFR Altitude for its route.
Pilot reported experiencing an altitude deviation during departure. Reporter cited routing clearance issues and lack of flying due to the COVID-19 pandemic as contributing factors.

HCF TRACON Controllers reported a problem with three military aircraft that were not following instructions and wanting to return to their ship which was right in the middle of the departure arrival corridor.
Flight crew reported that an MEL for Fuel Quantity Indication was misinterpreted by Maintenance and the aircraft was dispatched in a non airworthy condition.
An aircraft entered ARTCC airspace on a different route than what the ARTCC flight plan showed.
HCF TRACON Controller reported a concern with procedures and rules implemented by the facility management regarding arrival departure separation standards.
B747-800 flight crew reported being issued a hold without EFC time due to weather.
G650 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence that resulted in significant pitch and roll deviations on arrival into SLC.
B737 flight crew reported continuing the approach to LAS Runway 1R despite a continuous RA due to traffic behind for landing on Runway 1L being too close.

A corporate flight crew was asked to keep their speed at 300 KTS for traffic at FL240. During descent they asked the TRACON about speed and were told 'Keep it up;' then reduce. After landing they were notified a pilot speed deviation had been filed for over 250 KTS below 10;000 feet.
Air carrier flight crew reported having difficulty complying with the IVANE5 RNAV STAR into CLT; believing that it is unrealistically designed to execute in actual operations.
Pilot reported an increase in the number of pilots who are non current due to the current schedule reduction and reserve staffing levels.
Ground Controller reported a runway incursion due to communication issues with Local Control. Reporter stated staffing issues contributed to the event.
Light Transport Captain reported that proper performance data was not provided for a gear-down ferry flight.
Air Carrier Captain reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy and was removed from the flight.
ORD Tower Controller reported receiving a report from a departing B757 flight crew that they encountered wake turbulence after takeoff five miles in trail of an A330.
Air carrier Captain reported difficulty with electronic displays of ground taxiways along with ambiguous taxi instructions led to a taxiway incursion.
HS125 Captain reported being alerted to an NMAC by a TCAS RA after shortly after departing the airport.
A330 Captain reported that the aircraft was dispatched with MELs that compromised the safety of the passengers and crew.
A Gate Agent reported they maneuvered the jet bridge into contact with the engine of the aircraft.
Air taxi Captain reported writing up a maintenance discrepancy for a severely worn tire and grounding the aircraft while disregarding pressure by a Company Maintenance Controller to not.
AFW Controller reported an airborne conflict and stated it is a common occurrence in the area.  Controller would like the airspace changed to protect aircraft inbound to AFW.
R-22 Instructor reported NMAC during final approach with an aircraft doing a go-around.
Air Carrier Captain reported overspeed during descent.
An instructor pilot reported a NMAC with another aircraft on the downwind leg.
B767 Captain reported unsolicited ATC instructions and suggested CPDLC encryption.

Air carrier pilot reported that a flight scheduled for 6.5 hours was planned for 8.5 hours due to a company routing change that has become standard practice in order to avoid a no-fly zone. The flight was dispatched with 2 pilots; however an additional relief pilot was needed.

B737-800 Captain experienced conflicting loading procedures and calculations of weight and balance and takeoff performance data. Captain reported that the load planning assumptions in the weight and balance computer and the station lading procedures results in nose-heavy flight characteristics on takeoff and recommends a review of those assumptions and procedures.
B737 First Officer reported a 'strong jolt' of wake turbulence at FL340 when they passed another aircraft going in the opposite direction at FL350.
Air carrier Captain reported having to divert due to ATC COVID-19 issues at landing Center facility.

An air carrier Captain discovered while climbing to cruise from ABQ that the passenger manifest count was off and the aircraft weight was off by 5;800 pounds making the actual takeoff weight heavier than what was shown on the loading schedule.
ZMP Center Controller reported the adjacent sector handed them off two aircraft at the same altitude on converging courses.
A321 Captain reported having brake aggressively to avoid a collision on a taxiway at LAX.
Air carrier Captain reported two passengers became verbally abusive to the flight attendants when they were asked to follow face mask policy.
ZFW Controller reports of a NMAC between an IFR and VFR aircraft.
Air Carrier Captain reported chart instructions do not agree with ATC ground instructions for push-back.
B737-700 flight crew reported diverting to an alternate after a lightning strike resulted in multiple FMS anomalies.
ZOA Controller reported allowing a pilot to proceed direct to a fix while at an altitude that was lower then the MIA.

Captain laments the current ATC philosophy of using RNAV arrivals then making changes to the airspeed and altitude restrictions; then vectoring off and back on the arrival without a clear altitude assignment.
A319 flight crew reported executing a go-around after receiving a low altitude alert from ATC as well as a GPWS terrain alert on approach to RNO RWY 16L.

EMB-500 pilot reporting departing VFR with the intention of picking up previously filed IFR flight plan inflight. After inadvertently flying through scattered clouds in approaching darkness; pilot was advised by ATC that the flight plan was never received. New flight plan was filed and clearance issued by ATC.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not adhere to the cell phone or face mask policy. FA stated the passenger was rude; loud; combative and used profanity.
A AFW Tower Controller reported an arriving aircraft on an instrument approach had a NMAC with an unidentified VFR aircraft departing a satellite non towered airport.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to comply with instructions to properly stow her pet and became loud and abusive.
CE-560XL First Officer reported several instances of pressure from his company to ignore maintenance items during preflight; including corrosion issues.
PC-12 Captain reported an airborne conflict with a UAV on initial approach to BED.
E145 Captain reported a GPWS warning on approach with the terrain in sight.
A B737-800 flight crew described a low altitude encounter with severe windshear including rapid acceleration; deceleration and loss of altitude.

Air carrier Captain reported losing both GPS signals; which he attributed to military activity in the area. He successfully switched to an alternative means of navigation until the GPS signal was restored.

B747 Captain learned that his ferry flight into a high priority charter would be delayed by a ground stop due to weather. Dispatch indicated they could work around the advertised ground stop and the flight got right out. Enroute ATC queried the crew about their medical emergency which the crew knew nothing about.
PAE Tower controllers reported Approach Control gave them an aircraft on a base leg in conflict with straight in traffic which resulted in an NMAC.
A Maintenance Technician reported that an aircraft departed with an entry door handle out of alignment.
A Center Oceanic Controller reported proper procedures and equipment requirements were not implemented during a planned radar and radio outage period.

A Center controller reported being unexpectedly notified by the adjacent Center sector that they would no longer accept any traffic. The Controller had to hold all traffic and felt this caused an unsafe environment.
Tower controllers reported aircraft on approach from TRACON violated procedures and Local airspace.
RNO Tower Controller reported receiving successive arrivals to parallel runways from NCT TRACON with less than required wake turbulence separation.

While a controller change was happening; the Local Controller reported crossing two aircraft and did not observe a departing aircraft on the runway. The ERJ170 Captain reported they did not move because they saw the departing aircraft.
Bombardier Challenger Captain reported encountering severe turbulence that resulted in speed deviations and momentary altitude excursions.
A321 Captain reported a flap overspeed resulted from a wake vortex encounter in climbout from PHL behind an MD-11.
A320 Captain reported deviating to an alternate after encountering heavy rain and severe turbulence on approach to IAD.
Maintenance Technician reported inadvertently not installing a backup battery while complying with a return to service task card.
EMB-135 Captain reported returning to departure airport following fuel quantity display anomalies due to fuel contamination.
B737 flight crew reported that due to runway clutter; ice accumulated in the flap and wheel well area preventing the flaps from completely retracting.
Dispatcher reported being notified by Load Planning that flight crew was not provided required Hazmat transport documents prior to departure.
A320 Captain reported they temporarily lost control of their aircraft while taxiing.

An A319 with conflicting MEL's was issued a Maintenance Release and taxied for takeoff. The crew realized a #1 Pack Flow Control Valve MEL'ed inoperative and a failed #2 Engine High Pressure Bleed Valve could not simultaneously be inoperative. The flight returned to the gate.

B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of an A380 on approach to DFW. Reporter recommended in-trail separation of smaller transport aircraft behind Super Jumbo aircraft be raised to 20 miles.
C-402 Pilot reported being overflown by a C-182; while trying to give way to a C-172.
Air carrier Flight Crew mistook a runway at AVL Airport under construction as an active runway.

An A320 First Officer reported that multiple traffic targets in the vicinity of Whiteman (WHP) caused TCAS alerts and necessitated maneuvering that prevented the A320 from achieving a stabilized approach by 1;000 feet AGL on the Visual Approach to Runway 15 at BUR. A go-around was performed and the aircraft subsequently landed uneventfully at BUR on runway 08.
B737 Captain reported discrepancies on the application of an MEL procedure.
A319 Captain reported deviating from assigned track as a result of communication difficulties related to AUS ATCT degraded capabilities.

JCF Controller (Edwards AFB Approach) reports that the facility experienced ATC Zero due to equipment issue. No losses of separation were noted and the airspace was given away. Reporter stated the facility needs to come up with a contingency plan for this problem.
Ramp worker reported that damaged freight had leaked in aircraft pit; causing aircraft to be taken out of service.
ZLA Center Controller reported that the RNAV HAYEZ5 departure procedure placed aircraft in conflict  with arrivals to multiple airports in the LA Area.

B737 Captain reports being unable to comply with ATC instructions to avoid restricted airspace while deviating around thunderstorms and tells ATC to inform the restricted airspace controlling authority of his presence.
CE680 Captain reported requested an inoperative TCAS system be repaired prior to returning to revenue service.
Heavy Air Carrier First Officer reported communication failure between foreign maintenance and company maintenance team resulting in failure to repair aircraft.
B767 Captain reported returning to departure airport after noticing a cracking noise from the Captain's windshield.
ZHU Center Controller reported a procedural problem with AUS Approach Control that continually takes point outs on aircraft that fly long distances in the Approach Control airspace.

ATC requested an air carrier crew make a short approach from the EWR ILS Runway 22L circle to land 29. The approach became unstabilized so a go around was initiated which result in oversped flaps; late landing gear retraction and an altitude overshoot.
Air carrier flight crew reported that following two go-around maneuvers due to windshear; diverting to alternate in a low fuel condition.

An MD-80 pilot reported that following a logbook write up for a fuel temperature control problem; Maintenance personnel deferred the indication. Communication between the pilots prevented the flight from departing.
B737-800 flight crew reported departing PHL runway 9 and getting close to traffic on go around from runway 35.
A Corporate crew in ZTL airspace was cleared waypoint to CARAN but heard KAREN which was 283 NM separate and entered in the FMC. ATC issued an urgent descent to separate traffic.
Air carrier First Officer reported that while taxiing at DEN they realized a ground collision was imminent and stopped short of the crossing taxiing traffic.
Boeing 737 flight crew reported being unable to locate the takeoff warning test switch.
Ramp Agent reported battery powered wheelchair in cargo pit with batteries improperly removed and transported.
F11 Controllers report of a shift where the Arrival Controller was overloaded with traffic and nothing was done to help the Controller.
Wide body air carrier Captain reported diverting to an alternate airport after ATC vectors and deteriorating weather resulted in burning more fuel than planned.
B737 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence at FL310 7 miles in trail of a B777 on arrival into EWR.

Dispatcher encountered difficulty getting the weight and balance program to accept a new fuel load in calculating aircraft weight and takeoff data sent to the crew during preflight. Coordination between the crew and the Dispatcher ensured that Load Planning manually entered the correct fuel load.

An MD-82 flight crew; exiting the runway on a highspeed taxiway encountered a regional jet established on the parallel taxiway. ATC did not inform them and the bright lights of the construction area and terminal area obscured the RJ until the MD-82's landing lights illuminated it. The Captain applied heavy breaking and avoided colliding with the RJ.
Tower Controller reported an NMAC that was attributed to procedures that were not clear to the reporter relating to a TFR and go-around procedures.
Air Carrier Dispatcher reported noticing two company flights were in the air simultaneously with the same flight number.
B737 Captain reported that Flight Attendant Audit Inspection disrupted and delayed crew briefing; preflight responsibilities; and boarding.

While working a trim freezing problem on a Cessna CE-560E; Maintenance could not determine if a Service Bulletin was accomplished on this aircraft to determine which type of grease to use on the trim actuator.
A320 First Officer reported being cleared below class B airspace without notice from ATC.
C172 student pilot reported returning to departure airport after encountering unforecast icing conditions.
First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence when ATC vectored them over a powerplant that was emitting vapor plumes.
Part 91 aircraft encountered moderate to severe turbulence while deviating around weather.

Due to an inoperative performance computer; crew relied on ACARS takeoff performance data. Crew believed that the ACARS performance data generated incorrect V-speeds for takeoff. The crew was using max power because of LLWS at the airport. As a result; the takeoff was uneventful.
GA pilot reported striking a runway edge light during a crosswind landing.

A Controller was working an aircraft flying under the Open Skies procedure. In this case the route was below the minimum IFR altitude for a short period. The Controller advised the aircraft twice and issued a vector off course. The aircraft refused and stayed on its route advising the Controller they were in VMC. The reporter feels the controllers are not trained and briefed sufficiently to work aircraft on this procedure.
Two air carrier flight attendants reported injuries from a hard landing due to wind shear.
TBM-850 pilot reported an uncontrollable loss of altitude due to a severe turbulence encounter.
Air Carrier First Officer reported receiving an EGPWS caution for uncharted towers in the vicinity of SBN airport.
CRJ-200 Captain reported experiencing vibration and buffeting probably related to ice accumulation on approach in icing conditions.
B777 Captain reported that while following a parking guidance system they still overran the stop line by 3 feet.

A controller reported discovering an unprotected flight due to the entry of an incorrect fix estimate time for a flight plan through non-radar airspace causing the flight plan to be removed from the system.
A320 flight crew reported receiving an 'ENG 2 AIR SYS FAULT' on ECAM during descent. The published procedure did not provide sufficient information; so the crew landed with the engine at idle thrust.
Ramp Agent reported Dangerous Goods incorrectly loaded/stacked resulting in container damage en route.
B737 Captain reported the ramp personnel told him to release the brakes even though the wheels were not chocked and tow bar was unhooked.
Flight Crew reported departing with RVR under minimums.
B737 Captain reported there were three company aircraft on the same frequency with similar call signs; which caused problems for both the flights and ATC.
Private Pilot reported NMAC due to not having radios or transponder.
DH8-400 Captain reported confusion regarding logbook maintenance entries and MEL procedures caused aircraft to be flown with an open item in the logbook.
ZME ARTCC Controller reported a VFR aircraft encountering IMC weather was assisted by ATC to a safe landing. The brasher warning was issued to the pilot as assistance was being provided.

ZAB Controller reported of SAF ATCT personnel who removed the SID for an aircraft. Reporter stated this is a violation of FAA Order 7110.65 paragraph 2-2-11.c. By removing the SID; the minimum initial release altitude goes up 4;000 feet higher due to terrain.
B737-700 flight crew reported several aircraft system malfunctions due to multiple bird strike on approach.
General aviation pilot in Class B airspace reported a close encounter with another aircraft.
A flight Crew of a B737-700 reported that on initial approach they were cleared to follow traffic for a visual approach to the runway. Due to their speed they were unable to maintain spacing.
B767-300 Captain reported company notification of Hazmat documentation error ten days after flight operated.

A320 Captain experiences severe turbulence during descent; upon entering scattered white puffy clouds with tops at 13;500 feet; at a speed of 340 to 280 knots. Two passengers and a Flight Attendant are tossed into the overhead; with the Flight Attendant requiring medical attention.
A330 Captain reported that the Company provided insufficient training to pilots for international flights.
PC-12 pilot reported encountering wake turbulence departing DAL in trail of B737 that produced an 'overpowering' roll.
Mooney 20 pilots reported a minor inflight collision while formation flying.
Technician reported disagreement over policy and procedures not complied with during a road trip to remove and replace a main entry door slide.

A B757-200 Captain made the decision to land Flaps 20 with reported winds of 190/40G60. Later; he realized the Aircraft Operating Manual permitted Flaps 20 landings only in specific conditions and winds were apparently not included.
ZNY Oceanic Controller reported detecting a potential conflict with two aircraft that was not predicted by ATOP.
Ground employee reported problems associated with sharing headsets.

During a repositioning flight; the flight crew was asked to an Operational Check Flight (OCF) and deploy the Air Driven Generator (ADG). There was some confusion weather the flight was dispatched properly.

ORD Tower Controller reported clearing an aircraft for takeoff. Pilot reported they had 'red lights on the runway'. Controller advised pilot to hold position and observed an automobile on the runway. After vehicle was cleared aircraft was given takeoff clearance again. Controller liked the fact that the Runway Safety Lights Worked.
Flight Crew flying A319 aircraft discover a Manual discrepancy pertaining to the number of required survival kits on board.

A DHC2 floatplane pilot reports landing on a river and entering a microburst just before touch down. A tailwind results in a high touch down speed and insufficient time to stop before reaching the shore. Damage is minimal but contact with the shore activates the ELT.
Cessna 182 pilot reported the rudder pedal separated from the rudder control post during taxi.

A B767 flight crew detected a damaged LI-ION shipment and had it removed as per the FOM. The next day shipping clerks attempted to ship the same damaged container but were prevented because the flight crew was forewarned.
MD-11 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B757 on approach that resulted in speed fluctuations and a go-around.
B737-800 Captain report an NMAC event during approach with a drone within 50 feet from aircraft.
B737 Captain reported high speed rejected takeoff.
PA-28-181 pilot reported losing control of the aircraft during the takeoff roll due to a patch of ice.

A Flight Design CLTS pilot detected a surging motor; shut it down then landed safely on a road. Fuel exhaustion was the cause but more than enough should have been on board. After securing fuel he flew to his destination where he found a fuel cap seal missing which allowed inflight fuel syphoning.
Technician reported installing the incorrect part on aircraft due to misreading the manual.
Ramp agent reported that an extremely loud warning signal in the tug being used for a push made it diffucult to hear Ground Control's instructions.
EMB-175 Captain reported that due to unfamiliarity with aircraft; Captain did not select correct setting for Auto Throttles; causing unanticipated warnings and confusion.
A Maintenance Technician reported that new circuit breaker collars do not allow the high demand circuit breakers on panel in the Airbus fleet to be deactivated.
A320 Captain reported his schedules often do not offer an opportunity to eat prior to departure.
Flight crew reported EWR ATC instructed them to maneuver below Class B and exceed 200 kts.
ZNY Center Controller reported they were not advised their sector was decombined and the ATOP Sector was being worked by a different Controller.
Tractor driver reported the tug spun out while towing aircraft in windy and rainy conditions.
Employee at air carrier reports management intimidation; falsification of reports and unethical maintenance practices.
Air Carrier Captain reported ATC error when issued a climb clearance; into opposite direction traffic 900 feet above; and stayed at present altitude to avoid conflict.
Cessna 750 Captain reported an NMAC during approach with unreported powered parachute operating in close proximity of airport.
Instructor pilot reported confusion regarding a new note the FAA has attached to the RNAV approach at POF.
HUF Local Controller reported an unsafe situation due to the D-Brite being expanded to 60 miles for use by the Radar Controller.
Aircraft was pushed back prior to completion of ETOPS and other checks.
ZTL Controller reported of a clearance error because an airport and an airway are in the same area with the same identifier.

A Montgomery (MGM) Approach Controller describes advising an aircraft that the runway direction is changing; and an arresting cable needs to be lowered. The pilot elects to land over the cable after being given a few options by the Controller. The aircraft is switched to Tower and lands impacting the cable and damaging the aircraft.
S56 TRACON Front Line Manager reports of a VFR aircraft that doesn't move for IFR traffic and causes a NMAC to be reported by a pilot on a STAR into SLC.
EMB-175 First Officer reported they received a lavatory smoke warning while on approach. They were vectored around weather with a late runway change.
Airbus pilot reported on challenges regarding the company's new checklists.
Technician reported refusing to work on aircraft because the verbal instructions he was ordered to comply with deviated from the references and were non compliant.
EMB-175 First Officer reported a takeoff with incorrect performance numbers attributed in part to a new ACARS software revision.
The Captain of a Boeing 737 reported that during climb they encountered several lightning strikes and diverted.
Air carrier Captain reported no published procedure for pushback from gate.
ATC controller reported an aircraft flying the wrong departure route; and the pilot reported that they had not noticed the revised routing and failed to enter it into their FMC.
During inspection on a B757 engine due to a bird strike; company supervisor threatened the inspectors with insubordination if they inspected beyond what was required per the maintenance card.
A Cessna 172 pilot reported that gust of wind caused him lose control during landing.
D01 TRACON has mandated controllers to assign a specific speed to every aircraft at the final approach fix into DEN which led to concerns on specific scenarios.
737-800 Captain reported a Terrain Warning and executing the Escape Maneuver after maneuvering for weather without assistance from ATC.
A321 First Officer reported rejecting the takeoff at LAX due to crossing traffic downfield.

A pilot reports of not being issued traffic by ATC and observing traffic on the Navigation Display. The pilot questioned controller. The controller turned and descended aircraft. The pilot reported aircraft was 200-300 feet away when he spotted it and the aircraft passed 500 feet over the top.
Air Carrier Captain reported concerns regarding company COVID-19 related plans pertaining to sick leave; vaccinations; training schedules; etc.
Falcon 50 Captain reported a near mid air collision with a VFR aircraft while departing FTW Runway 34.
SR20 pilot reported an airborne conflict with another aircraft requiring evasive action. The traffic was detected by an onboard TCAS system and also reported by ATC.
Flight attendants reported a customer service agent advised them a passenger was not complying with face mask policy.
A300 Captain reported observing a Center of Gravity warning after takeoff.
ZAN Controller described problems associated with non-radar and radar aircraft; and separation requirements. Controller was unsure of what standard applied to the aircraft involved.
A Cessna 182 pilot reported that while landing at a non-towered airport another aircraft was taking off on a crossing runway.
CMH TRACON Controller reported their NIDS (NAS Information Display System) routinely fails to depict current adverse weather conditions.
Pilot reported while conducting a Charted Visual Approach at night they received a low altitude alert from ATC.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was not compliant with face mask policy.
Embraer 175 Captain reported returning to the gate after receiving 'ENG REF ECS DISAG' caution message when preparing for takeoff.
Corporate jet Captain reported a NMAC in the D10 TRACON area while on the JFRYE 3 arrival.
AVP TRACON Controller reported an IFR aircraft with avionics trouble descending below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737 Captain reported enroute clearance deviation due to FMC loading procedural error.
B737 flight crew reported an engine failure on takeoff due to a bird strike. After the crew accomplished checklist items; a successful single engine approach and landing was made.
P50 TRACON Controller reported not noticing a departure climbing through the altitude of an arrival until the pilot reported responding to a TCAS RA.

ZAN Controller reports of an almost operational error that happened just by luck. Controller was working both aircraft at the time but due to many Aircraft Position Symbols he climbed an aircraft that he should not have. Controller suggested that ATOP be able to predict conflicts in the terminal area and not just oceanic.
B737 flight crew reported deviating while on an ILS approach due to an RA from an aircraft departing a nearby airport.
A ramp service employee reported that while mechanics were running the engines on an aircraft; two other mechanics crawled under the aircraft and pulled the chocks.
A319 Captain reported an unusual vibration was noted during climb; but it disappeared after speed was reduced.
CRJ-900 Captain reported ATC issued a clearance that would have taken them beyond the 50 nm from land limit; at least according to their Dispatcher.
CRJ200 Flight Crew reported encountering thunderstorms during descent and approach; resulting in a missed approach and divert to an alternate airport.

Pilot reported receiving a traffic alert shortly after takeoff requiring immediate evasive action. Pilot noted that the PHL TRACON Controller seemed incredibly busy; thereby potentially missing the conflict.
Cessna pilot reported being unable to maintain altitude and airspeed due to a downdraft on approach to COS.
Airline Ground Personnel reported gate return aircraft to comply with Hazmat load configuration requirements.
A Pilot reported they became distracted recovering an unstable approach and did not complete the landing checklist resulting in a gear up landing attempt and prop strike.

Air carrier flight crew reported being high and fast as they were 'leading the pack' during a visual approach.  The crew cited lack of flying and pressure to maintain their speed to not hold up other aircraft behind as contributing factors.
Crew reported that Dispatch was not notified of an aircraft delay.
Air Carrier flight crew reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on a visual approach to ITO.
B737 flight crew reported that a strong tailwind on final approach resulted in a go around.
A319 First Officer reported executing a go-around due to a conflict with an aircraft of the runway.
Inspector reported they failed to verify the thrust rating after an engine change resulting in the aircraft returning to departure airport.
Air carrier Captain reported that the new area being used for de-icing presented several safety hazards; including a slippery surface and a small area for maneuvering the aircraft.

C90 instructor observes a speed related issue with two aircraft; the second on faster than the first. He advises the Developmental; but waits too long for the Developmental to do anything and has a loss of separation.
CRJ200 First Officer reported wind shear on approach that resulted in a go around.
Sport pilot reports near miss with air carrier aircraft.
B737-700 flight crew reported rejecting takeoff due to bird strike.

Three ZDC Controllers described a loss of separation event that occurred during chaotic deviations around a line of thunderstorms. Lack of planning and control from the TMU were cited as factor in the event.
ZMP Center Controller reported ZDV Center attempted to hand off three aircraft that were in potential conflict.
ERJ-145 flight crew notified of improperly transporting a Smart bag in cargo compartment.
The Captain of a Bombardier Dash 8 reported that when the aircraft was being dei-ced; he did not have any communication with the de-ice truck.
Corporate pilot reported turning the wrong direction to a heading issued by ATC. The pilot stated they were new to the airplane and had not flown much recently due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
EMB-145 First Officer reported that requesting additional wait time prior to departure behind a B757 resulted in a canceled takeoff clearance.
Air Traffic Controller and trainee reported a loss of separation between aircraft deviating for weather.

An aircraft departing TRACON airspace climbed through the overlying ARTCC sector without coordination. The automated handoff was in effect with the next ARTCC sector along the aircraft's route of flight.

An A320 encountered severe turbulence at FL280 flying through the Midwest and lost 400 ft; however no one suffered injuries. As a result of the turbulence the right front windshield outer pane cracked. The flight continued to its destination.

A Corporate Jet flight crew filed to JFK was cleared direct the ROBER waypoint; but heard RUBER resulting in a track deviation. ATC informed them about their error and reissued the correct waypoint. RUBER and ROBER are about 50 nm apart in the New York area.
SR22T pilot reported an altitude excursion resulted when he encountered severe turbulence from nearby convective activity.

C172 instructor pilot with his student; performing a simulated forced landing south of BUY; reporte detecting three towers at N35 56 49.5 W79 26 09.8 near the location of twin towers noted on the sectional at 1845 MSL. The third tower appeared taller than the other two and was unlit.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger boarded the aircraft with a mask that had holes.
A pilot flying VFR encountered IFR conditions due to unexpected smoke from wildfires.
Maintenance technician reported that an engine fire warning loop was improperly installed; causing a safety hazard.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving ATC instructions for taxi during the critical landing phase of their flight.
Captain reported an uneventful ground gate return event to correct Hazmat documentation error.
Flight Attendant reported that by providing passengers several food services allowed passengers to eat constantly and not have to comply with the face mask policy.
CRJ200 flight crew reported being informed by ATC that they were off course. They informed the crew that there was reported GPS signal jamming in the area.
CRJ-900 Captain reported diverting to an alternate after receiving two windshear warnings on approach to ILM.
B737 Captain reported that while established on the final approach course at DEN; he received an RA for traffic established on the final approach course of a parallel runway and had to go around.
B767 flight crew reported confusion due to reports that Kingston FIR was NOTAMed closed.
Air carrier Captain reported a Control Tower that was ATC Zero; leading to a disruption in communications.

D10 Controller reports of a loss of separation between two aircraft. Controller did not turn aircraft soon enough to avoid other traffic and reported he was unsure of what airspace he had due to an airport configuration that he was not too sure of.
B737 Captain reported difficulty locating hazmat procedures in FOM and confusion over labeling and documentation procedures.
A Captain; preparing for an Advanced Qualification Program (AQP) oral; reports on the many shortcomings of the IPad as a replacement for traditional aircraft flight manuals.

GA pilot reported being stung by an unseen; unidentified insect while fueling his aircraft at POC. Light-headedness and loss of unconsciousness occurred shortly thereafter and required hospitalization. He recommended at least a 30 minute delay for pilots who may be stung by an insect prior to any departure.
Pilot of a SUAS (Small Unmanned Aircraft System) reported that while conducting a mission in controlled airspace; local law enforcement and ATC were unable to locate the filed flight plan.
Air carrier Captain reported a near ground collision due to communication issues with ATC. Captain stated that Tower staffing levels may have contributed to the event.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported an aircraft was over burning fuel and required priority handling on arrival.

Flight attendant reported that there are two types of CPR devices available on board; however flight attendants are qualified to use only one; which requires removal of the personal protective mask in order to use. Reporter requests training on the other device to keep everyone safe in a COVID-19 environment.
Air carrier First Officer reported three windshear go-arounds during approaches to DEN Runway 16L. After declaring a low fuel alert; a successful landing was accomplished on Runway 26.
Technician reported installing wrong effectivity part during MEL procedures for inoperative Number 1 VHF Comm.
Gulfstream Captain reported a NMAC with a Cessna aircraft while on descent in the New York City area.

Controller reports facility staffing issues and working combined positions contributed to a probable loss of separation/runway incursion situation that was prevented by timely questioning of clearance by a locally familiar pilot.
GA pilot experienced an airspace incursion while returning from a practice flight. Pilot reported not being fully prepared for the flight and had not flown lately due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
ATC Center Controller reported vectoring an IFR aircraft for sequencing behind a priority aircraft through a parachute jump zone and below the altitude of the jumpers.
Air carrier First Officer reported a UAV passed off the left side of the aircraft while the flight was beginning an instrument approach.
B757 Captain reported being taught to ignore a Master Caution Alert while on a missed approach.
B737 Captain reported his aircraft rolled right and descended after encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B777 at FL340.
Pilot reported departing a runway and then observing an aircraft departing from a crossing runway causing an airborne conflict.
First Officer reported that a Flight Attendant discovered the security seals were blocking the O2 panels from opening in the Lavatories.
Skydiving pilot and TRACON Controller reported an aircraft was permitted to fly through the skydiving operation active jump zone.
Flight Attendant reported ill passengers on the flight and was concerned regarding lack of COVID-19 testing for the crew.
B777 Captain reported that the BGC2A RNAV departure from SGBR may not provide sufficient terrain clearance if the aircraft is heavy and the turn is initiated at 4000 feet.
IAD Tower Local Controller reported they had to send an aircraft on final approach around because their Supervisor approved Maintenance Personnel to work close to the runway without a radio.
EMB-140 Captain reported ground crew over turned the nose wheel while using a tug.
Air carrier flight crew reported being pushed back with only 1 wing walker which nearly led to a collision with another aircraft.
B767 Captain reported operating an aircraft that was equipped with features he was not trained to use.
Maintenance instructor reported that new supervisors are unclear on company policy regarding what they are allowed to stamp; resulting in a potential safety issue.
Center Controller reported a possible loss of separation and cited COVID-19 related staffing issues as a contributing factor.
Grob 103 glider pilot reported landing short of the runway when the winds changed during his approach.

An air carrier departed a Class B airport flying an RNAV SID but because of communication congestion missed a heading clearance issued concurrently with a frequency change. An airborne conflict resulted; but the RNAV SID contained no heading guidance beyond an initial heading.
A330-300 First Officer reported airspeed fluctuation on approach probably due to wake vortex encounter caused by a heavy aircraft ahead.
Air carrier Captain reported being scheduled a flight at full passenger capacity and was concerned about social distancing and exposure to COVID-19.

A B777 flight crew encounters turbulence at FL390 approaching a thunderstorm over South America. A sudden temperature increase and wind shift make FL390 untenable and a descent is requested. At FL370 the crew is able to maintain airspeed and altitude.
Air Carrier First Officer reported an unstabilized approach.
A Controller reported they vectored an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A Center Controller reported an aircraft reported a Wake Vortex encounter and diverted due to injuries.
B737 flight crew reported confusion over their assigned altitude on a go-around from an unstabilized approach to DEN.
B777 Captain reported noticing a flight planning anomaly related to ETOPS fuel calculations.
A319 Captain reported receiving a rerouted flight clearance by ATC due to weather.
Air Carrier flight crew reported flying an unstabilized approach. First Officer was the Pilot Flying and had not flown in 30 days.
Medium Transport Captain reported receiving a low altitude ATC alert on a visual approach to TTN airport.

PCT Controller reports of an aircraft that entered his sector with a late point out from ZDC; which later crosses onto another sector. Reporter realizes he should have pointed this aircraft out to the other sector in PCT's airspace.
ZOB Controller reported an aircraft had a loss of climb performance to FL410 resulting in the aircraft descending into conflict with an aircraft at FL400.
B747-400 Captain reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on approach to RKSI after an inappropriate clearance from ATC.
A Center Supervisor reported the adjacent Center intentionally violated the Letter of Agreement regarding flow control.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported being unable to properly perform his duties due to high workload and no assistance from management.
ZAU ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft with pressurization issues was vectored and descended into a TRACON's airspace without proper coordination.

FSS controller approved SVFR operations for an inbound and departure simultaneously. The Controller thought he heard one of the aircraft agree to 'Maintain Visual Separation'.
