During a review of a tape recording of this period it was determined that the aircraft did not volunteer to maintain visual separation. Simultaneous SVFR operations as this require one of the aircraft to maintain visual separation. The Controller misunderstood one of the aircrafts' transmissions.
A319 was reported that it was released for flight with known wire damage in violation of the GMM.
Denver TRACON Controllers reported an NMAC with an IFR and VFR aircraft due to Center's lack of separation.
B737 flight crew reported a moderate turbulence encounter while on descent that injured a flight attendant and caused some passengers to get sick.

IFR aircraft inbound to an airport cancelled IFR with Approach Control. The pilot attempted to check on to the Tower frequency for arrival instructions several times; but was not able due to frequency congestion. During this time the pilot sighted a departure from the airport on a converging course and climbing. The pilot took evasive action to avoid the departing traffic.
A pilot reported during takeoff roll they observed an aircraft on TCAS that was going around from the parallel runway was close to them.
E175 First Officer reported pressure from maintenance to not write up an engine problem.

A B737 flight crew departed LAS Runway 1R behind a regional jet on the same runway and were told to maintain visual separation but they were unable because the B737 performance over took the other aircraft. ATC issued a vector for separation.
B737 flight crew reported receiving a CPDLC that contained a fix for a nearby ILS when the fix needed was an enroute waypoint.  The spelling was the same for each location.

Joint Control Facility (Edwards) TRACON Controller reported of a TFR that was not updated and the reporter did not know the exact position. Reporter eventually had to look up coordinates and then advise others.
TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported it took a long time to get the needed planned altitude from ATC to avoid an area of turbulence.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a complaint with company procedures that permit pilot crews to shut down all power after parking at the gate with passengers still deplaning.
B737 flight crew reported that ATC cleared the aircraft to cross the runway while another aircraft was cleared to takeoff.

ZAN Controller reports of an aircraft returning to ANC; which entered ANC airspace without a correct handoff resulting in an airspace violation. ZAN Controller did not enter fix pairs; so the computer would not hand off correctly.
Pilot reported moderate turbulence that caused injuries to flight attendants.
Heavy Transport aircraft Captain reported ATC clearance error resulted in a runway conflict.
Dispatcher stated that there were insufficient dispatchers to handle the workload.
A Tower Controller cleared an aircraft for takeoff. The Controller did not notice an aircraft was in the path of the departure due to their view being blocked by Tower Display equipment.
BFI Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict that may have been prevented if Class D was larger or TRACON had been more proactive.
TRACON Controller reported they were late vectoring an aircraft from downwind to base leg and the aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

A fatigued B747-400 crew was advised their departure time was being moved up and in the process several weight and balance reductions were received. The First Officer incorrectly entered the cargo weight in the FMS Aero Data zero fuel weight field causing V Speed errors near 30 KTS low. After landing a tail strike was discovered.
Five Boston Center Controllers reported a loss of separation when a flight of aircraft were descended through an aircraft's altitude.
First Officer reported NOTAMS at destination are ineffective in providing a useful taxi tool to navigate a huge airport construction area.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported that they kept the power up for de-icing causing the aircraft to be fast and unstable on approach.
ZTL ARTCC Controller reported their radar display quit working.
Air carrier Captain reported that many of the first officers he flies with are not current; especially in the landing environment.
Lead Technician reported that during a part swap; the proper procedure was not followed resulting in the incorrect installation of a pressure switch.
B737 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence departing DFW.
Beech B95 pilot reported a safe landing followed an inflight engine failure related to fuel mismanagement.
Flight Attendant reported being notified by company 10 days after another Flight Attendant tested positive for COVID-19.
Air carrier First Officer reported an aircraft went around due to winds; while they were departing a parallel runway causing an airborne conflict.
Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach leading to a go-around; which then resulted in an altitude deviation. The Captain cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Light Transport Captain reported rejected takeoff by ATC due to Air Carrier failing to clear runway safety area.
Maintenance Technician and helper reported being instructed to reinstall a part because replacement parts were not in stock.
ARR Tower Controller conducting on the job training reported observing a trainee clear an aircraft for takeoff while a landing aircraft was still on the runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported aircraft deviation off runway centerline during takeoff roll.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was removed from flight due to not complying with face mask policy.
B737 First Officer reported that the crew oxygen mask was not the right type.
Air carrier flight crew reported a runway incursion after landing.

ZDV Center Controller reported he was advised by an arrival to an uncontrolled airport that another aircraft had been given a departure clearance by another controller to departed opposite direction and too close to them.
BE36 pilot reported not receiving the correct approach clearance from the TRACON.

Air carrier flight crew reported an aircraft swap caused a departure delay for an international flight. Some flight attendants go illegal and were taken off the flight as reserves were summoned. The flight departed with only 13 of the 15 flight attendants on the weight and balance and without the flight crew being aware.

A Tower Controller receiving training described an incident at PANC during snow that required a B747 to go-around twice due to loss of spacing. On the third approach the crew is told to go-around again after they have touched down due to a preceding B747 being unable to completely clear the runway. The Captain elected not to go-around again and the crew was able to stop well before the stuck B747.
B757 Captain reported that he was concerned about the proper procedures for an MEL item that required other systems to operate normally.

B737 flight crew reported a bird strike below 1;000 feet on departure which caused a momentary compressor stall before resuming normal operation. Flight returns to the departure airport for an overweight landing.
Air carrier Captain reported communication issues with Center and attributed it to COVID-19 ATC staffing of facilities.

ZDC Center Controller reports of a close call because an aircraft was on a different route than what ATC had. This has been a problem in the past but was reported to be fixed. Problem of incorrect routing is surfacing again.
Piper PA32 pilot lost control of the aircraft after landing and encountered black-ice on the runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation.
B737-700 flight crew reported that they got an 'OBSTACLE; PULL UP' alert when they turned base early.
Air carrier Captain reported aircraft had inoperative cabin cooling and was re-assigned to another flight crew.
A321 Captain reported a mountain wave encounter that caused an altitude deviation and an overspeed condition.
Maintenance technician reported that an aircraft was not serviced with the proper procedure; and was signed off and returned to service without being repaired.
Denver Tower Controller reported the facility has not been in compliance with the new nationally imposed midnight shift procedures.
Air carrier flight crew; cleared to cross an active runway at BOS; stopped before entering the runway when they saw an aircraft taking off on that runway.
Air Carrier flight crew reported unreliable Visual Glide Slope Indicator and going around due to a GPWS warning due to an incorrect flap setting.
CRJ-200 Captain reported ATC communication difficulties on arrival to MMHO.
Air Carrier Captain reported fatigue after a challenging duty day involving weather and an air turnback after destination airport reported severe icing.
Embraer-145 Captain reported that the aircraft had an evaluation flight with weather down to minimums and the flight crew was not familiar with the aircraft.
B777 flight crew reported refusing to fly an aircraft across the Pacific until a lost cell phone was found.
MDW Controller and an air carrier flight crew reported an unsafe situation due to similar sounding call signs.
Pilot reported doing a practice approach into an airport with approach control and then was advised by ATC that he did not contact the tower in a timely manner and violated the Class Delta.
Air carrier Captain reported reported incorrectly flying the RNAV (RNP) Z RWY 03 arrival to ABQ airport.  Captain stated they missed a fix; which is reportedly a common occurrence on this arrival.
B737 flight crew reported taking off with a mid-field RVR of 400 feet.
A319 Captain reported being advised by the Tower after departure a light aircraft in position reported being was 'blown over'; apparently from jet blast.
B787 flight crew reported pushing back from the gate without required Hazmat documents.

Descending on the BDEGA ONE STAR into KSFO; corporate jet flight crew received clearance to Runway 28L which had not been programmed in the FMS. After aircraft turned toward routing for Runway 28R; ATC issued vectors and crew reprogrammed the FMS. Ongoing problems associated with changes to expected routing on KSFO STARS were noted by the reporter.

Air carrier flight crew reported retracting the flaps during climb before the appropriate airspeed and stated that fatigue; hunger; and excessive heat in the aircraft contributed to the event; and the COVID-19 mask was a distraction.
EMB-175 First Officer reported a miscommunication with Dispatch resulted in departure out of compliance with the MEL.
Ground employee reported the break-room is not properly being cleaned.
Flight Attendant reported a blocked jumpseat was subsequently used possibly violating FAR rules.
A Flight Attendant reported confusion during tail tip procedures when passengers are not allowed to get up from their seats until the seat belt sign is turned off and the aircraft is stabilized.
ZAN Controller reported aircraft was below the MVA during missed approach hold. Controller reported incorrectly aircraft cleared VOR approach executing MA was not authorized holding.
B787 Flight Attendant reported an issue with fasten seat belt sign usage with a particular crew.
B747 First Officer reported that an unstabilized approach resulted in a go around.

Scientist/Observer reported that during a smoke in the cabin event; only one oxygen bottle with two masks was onboard with three crewmen; resulting in a crewmember becoming incapacitated; requiring medical treatment.
General Aviation pilot departing from a Non Towered airport reported a NMAC with an aircraft conducting a low approach on an intersecting runway.
ZAN Supervisor observed a Controller using incorrect oceanic reduced separation procedures to climb an aircraft to the same altitude as an aircraft it was following.
Air carrier Captain reported receiving an RA on arrival into RDM from light plane traffic in the vicinity of the DSD VOR.
Super Cub pilot reported experiencing windshear during landing.
ZLA Center Controller reported aircraft climbing into their sector via new OAPM departure procedures were climbing to the wrong altitude.
ZDC Center Controller reported being inundated with traffic at the end of a mid-shift due to weather and no help from anyone.
B737 Captain reported oil leaking from engine exhaust.
Air carrier flight crew reported track and altitude deviations occurred on approach to BNA following a late change in approach procedure. Lack of recent flying experience was cited as contributing.
Air carrier flight crew reported difficulty in executing an RNAV approach using aircraft systems automation which resulted in going below glideslope mid-approach at SDF.
Instructor and student pilot reported a planned engine out exercise resulted in a runway excursion.
F/A 18 pilot experiences a NMAC with a large bird or small aircraft over the Owens Valley in California; during a low level training exercise.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported not being aware of the Precision Runway Monitor in use because it was not advertised on ATIS.
Citing inadequate training; an air carrier ramp worker reported that in addition to the proper nose gear pin; he pulled another pin that should not have been removed.
BHM TRACON Controller reported he cleared an aircraft for an approach to Robins Field (20A).The Controller did not check the NOTAMs for the airport and the runway was closed.
M7 seaplane instructor reported the engine quit on landing while doing touch and goes. Fault was due to carburetor fuel contamination.
FBO Flight Supervisor for flight school reported Airport Operations Official notified the Duty Officer of a Critical Ground encounter of an FBO SR-20 with air carrier during taxi.
Air carrier First Officer reported airborne notification of on-board Hazmat shipment incorrectly listed on aircraft manifest.
TRACON Controller reported a departure did not tag up on the radar display and was not detected until flying 40 to 50 miles.
Air carrier Captain reported the amount of similar sounding callsigns is a safety concern and a distraction.
B737 Flight Crew reported overshooting an altitude restriction in descent due to a programming error.
Hawker 800XP Captain reported that an autopilot malfunction during approach resulted in an altitude deviation.
Air carrier Flight Attendant in Charge reported a passenger became belligerent about the face mask policy and was removed from the flight.
Learjet 40 Captain reported that the aircraft rolled during engine start after the SIC failed to set the parking brake.
A320 flight crew reported an NMAC on departure with a VFR aircraft.
TRACON Controller reported routing issues relating to a specific airline company and their procedures.
Maintenance Technician reported finding an emergency light on a B757 that did not illuminate because the wires had been disconnected.
ZDV Controllers reports that an aircraft under his control reported a balloon at FL350. The controller did not know anything about the balloon but the FLM was aware of the balloon.

After receiving takeoff clearance; A320 crew advanced thrust levers and commenced takeoff roll. Tower then cancelled their takeoff clearance. Crew stopped aircraft and 1-2 minutes later received a second takeoff clearance.
During flight a B737 encountered severe clear air turbulence which caused an injury to a Flight Attendant. The flight crew advised ATC and landed at the destination airport.
S46 TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation due to confusing coordination and complex airspace between adjacent airports.
HCF Controller reported a problem that might have been caused by FUSION Radar; or the Controller not using the correct procedure.
SFO Local Controller reported a pilot took too long to read back their RNAV departure instructions with their takeoff clearance. The Controller had to cancel the clearance.

B757 Captain lamented the lack of any indication on arrival charts of where an aircraft landing at a secondary airport will pass below or arrive below the Class B veil; requiring a 200 knot speed restriction.
Air Carrier flight crew reported being unable to contact Dispatch due to EFB malfunction.

ZME Controller reported a CRJ-900 flight crew was issued an arrival and a transition; but descended below the assigned altitude.  Controller reported their is confusion from the ATIS informing the runway in use and possibly pilots setting up for it instead of just the transition.
Air carrier Captain reported the Lido Airport diagram did not accurately depict the geography of the taxi route at DTW.

An air carrier flight crew slowed to meet the charted RNAV arrival speed constraint after being cleared to 'descend via' the arrival. ATC advised that they were expected to maintain the previously assigned speed.
Pilot attempted to locate and land at an uncontrolled field at night. Was unable to locate the runway and thus diverted to a nearby airport.

Air carrier flight crew received an EGPWS 'Terrain; Terrain' and 'Too Low Terrain' warning while flying a visual approach to DAL Runway 31L at night. The warning is silenced and the crew continued for a safe landing.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing jamming of GPS signal at FL340 while flying in Iraqi airspace.
Air carrier First Officer reported ambiguity issues with the CPDLC domestic system.
ZMP Controller reported descending an aircraft. A different aircraft with a similar call sign of the same company descended.
Air Carrier First Officer reported severe turbulence at FL320 after level off west of DCA.
Flight Attendant complains of a headache and is informed by another Flight Attendant that the pilots had been instructed by the company to provide less oxygen to the cabin.
Captain reported in flight notification of Release document missing required Hazmat information.
An Air Carrier First Officer reported ramp agents opened the main cabin door without coordinating with the crew and ordered the First Officer and B Flight Attendant off the aircraft.
A321 Captain reported noncompliance with Maintenance Logbook and MEL procedures for an ELAC 1 FAULT caused a gate return and flight delay.
Flight Attendant reported another flight attendant did not sit in the assigned jumpseat.

IND Controller and pilots of violating aircraft report of confusion while flying into IND. Controller states pilots were late in checking in which lead to a separation loss also caused by the previous controller not coordinating correctly. Pilots do not know they were late in communicating and thought the problem was with another aircraft they had an RA on.
Air Carrier Captain reported a track deviation during approach and cited fatigue and low flight time during the pandemic as contributing factors.
Flight Attendant reported that a catering cart was used to transport trash and dirty trays and was not cleaned before being loaded with clean dishes and food.
Maintenance Technician reported that fumes in the cabin has become a difficult issue to resolve.
Single engine aircraft pilot landed in marginal VFR conditions at OTH.

Air Carrier Flight Attendant reported a possible FAR violation due to an insufficient number of FAs on board while passengers were aboard. The Flight Attendant cited long lasting symptoms from COVID-19 that may have contributed to the event.

While approaching the runway; getting ready for take-off after stating my intentions on UNICOM; we received an acknowledgment from another aircraft and that an ultralight was in the area. We delayed our departure and avoided a conflict with the ultralight.
GA pilot reported an NMAC during climb out.
DLH TRACON controllers reported not being aware of a TFR and vectoring an aircraft through it. A contributing factor included how NOTAMs are distributed.

B737 Flight Attendant reported passengers were evacuated to the terminal via aircraft entry doors after a start cart caught fire on the ramp next to them.

Air Carrier Dispatcher described a confusing situation that developed when his dispatching computer automatically assigned a 'D' suffix to the flight number of a stubbed flight taking the place of a late inbound flight with the same flight number. ATC rejected the 'D' suffix to avoid confusion with another carrier.

Pilot reports of being issued a heading and altitude by Tower Controller to avoid weather. Controller gave pilot a frequency to change to for 'Traffic Advisories.' Pilot did not call the new frequency because he thought he did not need to since the tower had issued a heading and altitude for weather avoidance.
B737 Captain reported a go-around was executed due to a blurred vision through the windshield caused by heavy rain.
ZMA Controller reported TMU and facility management forced controllers to work aircraft through weather.
Air Carrier Captain reports runway braking action is nil prior to takeoff.
GA pilot reported communication issues with ATC while attempting to receive a Class Bravo clearance.
Tower Controllers reported allowing an opposite direction operation to occur with less than required separation.  Training in the Tower was in progress at the time.
P28A Flight Instructor reported a wingtip scrape due to a ground strike during landing pattern practice.
SAT Approach Controller reported having to vector an aircraft off an approach to avoid an unidentified VFR target departing from an adjacent airport underneath the final approach course.
Air carrier First Officer reported descending below the SPIDR2 Arrival published altitude depiction on the chart.
Air carrier flight crew reported a flap overspeed issue on initial approach.
Medium Large Transport Captain reported automation mismanagement resulting in undershooting altitude restrictions on approach.
C172 Pilot reported weather diversion due to inflight icing conditions with aircraft not equipped with anti-ice protection.
Air Carrier Captain reported the aircraft arrived from the maintenance hangar with switches and fueling in nonstandard conditions.
Air carrier First Officer reported flying without a current Airmen Certificate.
A Drone pilot reported colliding with a vehicle.
B737 flight crew reported they were informed of possible damage near the top of the vertical stabilizer. After maintenance examination; the aircraft was taken out of service.
A Controller allowed an aircraft to enter SFRA without an operating transponder. The Controller failed to brief the relieving Controller about the traffic in the SFRA.
An EMS Helicopter pilot reported many hospital heliports are not in the FAA Airport database provided to drone operators to use to avoid the airspace.
B767 Captain reported they diverted because the filed destination airport had been NOTAMed closed during the scheduled arrival period; but neither the Captain nor Dispatch caught the closure notice.
B737-700 Captain reported Washington Center appears to be consistently changing the crossing altitude at RIDGY on the KORRY 4 arrival into LGA and would like to see the chart changed to reflect that.
Cessna pilot reported landing in a field following an engine failure related to fuel mismanagement.
Lear 45 flight crew reported an uncontrolled climb to FL412 from FL400 as a result of moderate to severe turbulence.
B767 pilot reported a company practice of issuing similar sounding callsigns that resulted in ATC confusion.
Technician reported the FAA found irregularities after the 100 hour inspection on a Cessna 172 was completed.
A Fractional jet pilot reported cancelling their trip due to fatigue due to the scheduling practices of their company.
PRC Tower Controller reported an unsafe operation when an aircraft had to maneuver out of the way of another aircraft.
ZOB Controller reported receiving a handoff on an aircraft which the previous Controller had not pointed out to an adjacent sector.

A general aviation instructor pilot reported noticing that the CJR RNAV 22 approach could; if flown as depicted; possibly intrude on the IAD Class B airspace. Reporter recommended an adjustment to the procedure turn to preclude a possible airspace violation.

Air carrier Captain reported a problem obtaining critical flight information from his Microsoft Surface EFB in a timely fashion. He stated that the problem is chronic and is directly related to the brand of electronic device chosen by his company.
Beech 99 Captain reported that selecting the incorrect CTAF frequency resulted in a critical ground conflict.
SCT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft checked in on the frequency that had not been handed off by Center due to computer issues.
An air carrier crew cleared to SEEVR intersection was confused about their DFW STAR because their arrival changed from the BRDJE 1 RNAV to the SEEVR 1 while enroute and SEEVR is a shared waypoint.
Denver Center Controller reported uncoordinated airspace violations due to windshear and high workload into DEN Airport.

An air carrier Captain flying as a passenger on a B737 witnessed an incomplete Type IV de-icing application prior to gate departure; a failure he had previously experienced himself.  As there was no current precipitation he opted not to attempt to contact the flight crew until the end of the flight; but they had deplaned prior to his being able to do so.
F11 TRACON Controller reported working a busy combined sector that failed to notice an overtake between two aircraft resulting in a loss of separation.
Air carrier pilot reported the Airport Diagram page does depict current ramp and terminal construction zones.
Captain reported multiple safety and security issues during an ongoing maintenance delay with passengers on the ramp area waiting for the aircraft.
NCT TRACON Controller reported being too busy to comply with newly implemented facility coordination procedures. The Controller accomplished the coordination using the previous procedure.
Helicopter Pilot reported misgivings due to suspected unapproved procedures used to return an aircraft to service.
AA5B pilot reported receiving a low altitude alert from Center while experiencing symptoms of hypoxia.
Air carrier Captain reported a situation with general First Officer proficiency among the international relief pilots.

CHA Tower Controller reported of an unsafe situation related to traffic in the pattern and ground traffic.  Reporter sited the memory aides as being visually distracting and causing controllers to be distracted from looking out the window.
ZJX Center Controller reported an aircraft exited his airspace without a handoff to the next controller.
Technician reported that a lack of qualified personnel is causing delays and excessive workload.
Air Carrier First Officer reported pilots are not adhering to face mask policy in the crew lounge area.
Captain reported navigational course error due to published Navblue charts depicting an erroneous course westbound from ELX on V-100 airway.
Cessna 182 pilot reported operating VFR in IMC after being stuck above clouds with ATC assistance during the descent.
Flight Attendant reported having a compliance issue with a family that had to be continuously reminded to wear their face masks.

A Helicopter pilot departed SAT transiting east over RND Class E but during the return flight RND had become Class D without notification and not in accordance with the AFD stating Class E on weekends.
Air carrier flight crew reported being targeted by a green laser.
B737 Captain reported; at gate block-in; a Lithium Ion battery powered wheelchair had been transported in cargo with no required Hazmat documentation provided prior to departure.
B767 flight crew reported encountering strong wind shear and moderate turbulence during climbout which triggered an aural warning.

Air carrier flight crew reported a concern that fuel reserves required to fly to SKBO are less than required to fly to a domestic destination in the system; which makes no sense considering terrain; limited alternates and language difficulties.
CE-750 Maintenance Technician reported a pressurization problem was not repaired correctly and an incorrect part was supplied for a thrust reverser lock-out panel.
C172 Instructor reported a NMAC with an opposite landing aircraft.

A Center Controller reported of a radar display indicating an aircraft had the correct routing but the information on their EDST (Enroute Decision Support Tool) did not default to the proper routing display for the type aircraft. The controller was not made aware the aircraft was not on the correct routing.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing an unstabilized approach and setting up to land on a closed runway with anticipation of changing to landing runway later.

Air carrier Captain reported experiencing an airspace excursion after being vectored off the TYSSN5 arrival to LAS. Captain stated workload increased while trying to find the correct charts needed to stay within the LAS Class B airspace contributed to the event. Captain recommends the LAS Class B airspace be included on the arrival charts.
ZDV Center Controller reported an unsafe situation due to weather and the Traffic Management Unit not implementing flow for deviating aircraft.
B737-300 First Officer expressed concerns with TRACON's excessive clearance changes during arrival to SFO airport.
An air carrier on arrival to MSP was given an expedited clearance to 6;000 feet followed by clearance to maintain 4;000 feet. The flight descended out of MSP Class B at 250 KTS.
MD-11 First Officer reported nil braking action during landing rollout on a wet runway.
B777 Captain reported several issues during pushback including being notified HAZMAT was erroneously loaded on aircraft.
MSP Tower Controller reported an unsafe situation with a departure rolling slowly and an arrival on a converging runway.
B777 Flight Attendant reported of sudden severe turbulence that caused injuries to other flight attendants and a passenger.
Air carrier pilot reported that there was not a mechanic's signature or employee number on the balancing logbook entry regarding an APU door status message.
Tower Controller working combined positions described safety of flight concerns due to added workload issues.
A Private Pilot from another country was unaware of the private pilot license requirements when flying in the United States.
B737 pilot reported the JeppFD-Pro display blocks from view the altitudes required by some SIDs when the display is rotated.

An MD80 Captain reported multiple flight crews requested that the ATL Runway 9L runway lights be dimmed for evening takeoffs in low ceilings and fog. The Local Controller advised they were unable to do so per the FAA. On their subsequent takeoff the reporter found the intense reflected light made it difficult to read the flight instruments.
Maintenance Technician reported wording in maintenance instructions needs to be changed to accommodate for COVID-19 policy.
PA-28 owner; riding as a passenger; reported pilot flying experienced a loss of aircraft directional control during landing resulting in runway excursion.
Falcon 900 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence that rolled the aircraft to a 90 degree bank while descending on the FISEL7 Arrival into FLL in trail of an A350.
Air carrier Captain reported the NOTAM format needs many improvements for safety reasons.

Controller Training in progress. Trainee gave radar vectors for sequencing and separation to the final approach course. Aircraft turned at a much slower rate than Controllers anticipated. Instructor took over sector and issued vectors to both aircraft but it was too late to maintain required separation standards. Pilots also reported and did not realize that the loss of separation had occurred.
Pilot reported a misapplied MEL was used and the aircraft flew in revenue service.
Air carrier Captain reported they routinely encounter aircraft with similar sounding call signs.

An ARTCC Controller allowed a TRACON Controller to climb an aircraft into their airspace reference traffic the Center controller was working. The TRACON Controller transferred communications of their aircraft to the ARTCC Controller. The two aircraft were separated according to TRACON rules; but not Enroute (ARTCC) rules.
B747-400 flight crew report ATC intervention to prevent gross navigation error due to missing the second portion of revised CPDLC while enroute.

A solo pilot made his CTAF communications before departing SGU then after takeoff noticed a large aircraft taxiing off the far end of the runway. He had not heard a response from that aircraft which apparently did not hear him either.
B747 First Officer reported finding improperly loaded hazmat during their pre-flight check.
B757-200 flight crew reported confusion departing MIA because of what they called a 'confusing and non-standard' PDC format.

On taxi out; A321 crew received 'RIGHT WING OVERFLOW' ECAM message. After completing the QRH procedure and visually confirming the venting fuel; crew shut down both engines to stop the fuel venting and the aircraft was towed to the gate. Cause of fuel venting was not reported. MEL for fuel pump or valve had been cleared before the flight.
Flight Attendant reported that a passenger had to be reminded four times to wear a face mask. Flight Attendant stated that the passenger and his wife were coughing without covering their cough.
Corporate Captain reported experiencing an undershoot of a crossing restriction and cited lack of recent flying experience due to the COVID-19 pandemic as a contributing factor.
Air Carrier flight crew reported attempting to reconfigure their aircraft after receiving and ATC directed go-around; initially climbed into a 'monitor vertical speed' TCAS Resolution Advisory.

Pilot reported parachute operations in the vicinity of LMO put skydivers and aircraft in danger because of the zone's proximity to V220 and V85. Reporter also stated; regular CTAF communications may interfere with the 'jumpers away' call.

The Center Controller in Charge (CIC) noticed radar contact was lost with an aircraft that should have been within radar coverage. The CIC attempted to call the Radar out of service but was told not to.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a runway incursion during taxi and cited several factors including a runway change and communication issues.
B737 Captain reported tugs that are outfitted with LED headlights blind the flight crew resulting in difficulty seeing the safety zone and/or the Marshaller.
B737-800 First Officer reported that departing out of BWI; the aircraft is unable to make the 17000ft. restriction at FOXHL on TERPZ 6 departure.

A Dallas Fort Worth (D10) Controller reports of an aircraft being cleared for an approach that they later find out was out of service. The facility was not aware that the approach was not working correctly.
B737-800 flight crew reported that during approach a laser light beam entered the cockpit.
Hayward Tower Controller reported losing radios and having to use one back up radio between all the positions in the Tower.
Air Carrier Captain reported the requirement of wearing a face mask with a Flight Attendant on the Flight Deck is a safety concern; especially if there is an event such as a rapid decompression.
Air Carrier First Officer reported pre-departure ATC communication difficulties almost led to a track deviation departing TNCM.
Air Carrier pilot reported flying a Visual Approach instead of the RNAV they were cleared for at a non-towered airport.

A BJC Local Controller reports on asking an aircraft direction of flight. The pilot reports direction and also says something unintelligible to the Controller. Local observes aircraft in position with another aircraft on final. The Controller sends final traffic around and realizes he did not listen correctly to the read back or receive clarification.

An air carrier crew flying a GPS equipped aircraft flew the LAS RNAV RWY 19R after a late runway change and were told they should have flown the LAS RNAV VISUAL RWY 19R. The LAS RNAV RWY 19R is meant for non-GPS equipped aircraft.
B767 Captain reported two block turn backs caused by deferred maintenance; resulting in a flight delay.
EMB-175 First Officer reported they continued the flight to their destination regardless of the thunderstorms in the area.

ZDC Center Controller and flight crew reported the aircraft turned early on an RNAV SID.
Pilot reported an airspace violation while experiencing radio difficulties. Pilot questioned if he should have continued inbound or if ATC was ignoring him do to traffic load.
A320 First Officer reported a windshear alert just prior to touchdown. They elected to land rather than execute the windshear recovery as required by SOP.
Widebody Airbus Captain reported he was unhappy with the new company procedure for contaminated runway computations.
Air Carrier Captain reported a course deviation due mistaking similar sounding fix names SURNAM and SULOM.
EMB-175 Captain reported diverting after encountering a microburst while on approach to SLC.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported a sick passenger boarded a flight; but was removed from the flight prior to departure.

A B777 crew; pilots and flight attendants; describe the confusion after discovering the flight attendant crew is understaffed in flight. The events surrounding the procedural and human errors are discussed.

B777 flight crew reported a greater than planned fuel burn on an international flight resulting in a diversion. Further investigation revealed the drag factor in the flight planning software was incorrect.
Corporate jet flight crew reported ATC issued land and hold short off clearance; at touchdown and rollout while at high speed. Crew reported they were not given an option to readback or comply.
B737 First Officer reported moderate turbulence with minor injuries to passengers and flight attendants.
Technician reported undeclared Dangerous Goods were transported on passenger flight.
Air carrier Relief Pilot reported removing himself from a flight due to a disagreement with the Captain about wearing masks in the flight deck.
A Captain reported confusion with the SJC BMRNG1 procedure chart.
B757 Captain reported on three separate occurrences; the EFB app deleted its content while the aircraft was in flight; depriving the flight crew of all charts.
EMB-175 flight crew airspace reported taking evasive action and receiving an RA for an unidentified VFR target operating just below the base of the Class B airspace.
CRJ-200 Captain reported concern over the level of training at his company.
Air carrier Captain reported that many departure clearances do not take into account the forecast weather and runway usage when being issued.

A321 Captain reported flap problems on approach that required checklists that were; in the Captain's words; 'way too long and are inefficient and largely unusable.' They were unable to complete them because of their fuel situation.
Air Carrier Captain reported an altitude overshoot resulting in an RA.  The Captain stated the aircraft was very light in weight; which contributed to the event.
TRACON Controller reported that their frequency stopped working for several minutes; resulting in a NMAC.
A Fractional Jet Crew described a moderate to severe turbulence encounter at FL410 which resulted in an uncontrolled 600 ft altitude increase upwind and 20 miles from the nearest cell.
Bell 206b Helicopter Pilot reported a precautionary landing due to EICAS Baggage Door  annunciation. After discarding a smoking lithium flashlight flight completed an uneventful destination landing.
A TRACON Controller reported that the Center was handing off aircraft recovering from a military exercise with less than the prescribed spacing specified in the Letter of Agreement.
The pilot received a TA and leveled off with military aircraft in sight after departing DTS with a clearance from Elgin.

S46 TRACON Controller reported of a conflict between and IFR arrival and a VFR aircraft. Controller reported this is an ongoing problem with VFR aircraft using a corridor that conflicts with IFR aircraft arrivals at the same altitudes.
ERJ-175 Captain reported a Flight Attendant was slightly injured during a wake turbulence encounter on approach to LAX.
Air carrier flight crew reported poor CRM when they entered an area of severe turbulence.
PA-28 Pilot reported a NMAC event in landing traffic pattern with an unidentified twin aircraft in class D airspace.
Air carrier First Officer reported that they chose to utilize visual cues to crosscheck the LNAV/VNAV information; as a result they received an obstacle alert and a low altitude alert.
Three skydive pilots flying separate C182s reported two of them had a conflict with a B737 while descending.
Air carrier Captain reported a GPWS alarm while flying into the Burbank airport.
SAT Approach Controller and an EMB-175 flight crew reported a traffic conflict with a glider that had infringed on the EMB-175's approach course.
Captain of a large turbojet reported issues with ground personnel procedures that resulted in a rejected takeoff and aircraft damage.

Air carrier Captain reported that the company's prescribed hand signals for marshaling aircraft into parking is inadequate for purposes of identifying the ramp lead-in line at night with a wet ramp. Captain suggested returning to a marshaling signal that had previously been used.

Air taxi pilot reported that while deviating around thunderstorms; the track resulted in flying farther offshore than allowed by company Ops Specs. The pilot noted that due to COVID-19 the co-pilot's overwater training had been cancelled.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing a RA at 5000 feet near EWR while being vectored by ATC.
B737-700 Captain reported that an Operations Agent boarded the aircraft and a commuting Mechanic started the APU without any pilots aboard.

D01 controller describes a situation where an aircraft is displayed on his scope by another controller. Displaying controller asks other controller if he can miss his displayed aircraft. Separation is ensured by the controller turning his traffic 90 degrees away from the displayed traffic.

A Flight Attendant reported that ramp personnel were not accepting oversized bags during boarding. When confronted with policy and FAR violations; the lack of ramp management oversight and lack of ramp employee concern for their actions' consequences was cause for concern.
A319 flight crew reported a windshield crack that resulted in a return to the departure airport.

Air carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation during departure from SBA airport.  The crew reported they had entered DRAKE Intersection in their FMS rather than 'Drake' DRK VOR as cleared.
Part 135 Captain reported mistakenly accepted HAZMAT at stopover.
SLC Tower Controller reported runway and taxiway closures increased their workload causing them to have to issue an arriving aircraft go-around instructions.
Dispatcher reported a flight departed early into a Flow Constrained Area (FCA) and questioned company procedures that allowed this to occur.
Air carrier Captain reported making a procedural error during preflight and cited a distraction and lack of recent flying as contributing factors.

Flight crew received a new clearance enroute to avoid weather. During vectors to final approach they realized that the vectors were for the wrong airport. Destination airport was apparently changed by ATC when the new routing was issued.
B737 flight crew reported severe turbulence with injuries 80 NM east of MMUN at FL360.
SCT TRACON Controller reported a Helicopter descended below the MVA while issuing a wildfire report.
ARTCC Controller reported descended aircraft below the MVA.
B787 Captain reported a track deviation in oceanic airspace while avoiding a thunderstorm without informing ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported on descent into FSD the ZMP frequency 128.0 had a loud side-tone that was almost deafening and made communication with the Center difficult.
B757-767 First Officer reported that initial and recurrent training does not cover operation of the air-conditioning packs with pre-cooled air being supplied to the aircraft.
A319 First Officer reported instructing Pilot not Flying on the procedure to activate runway lights.
Flight Instructor reported they turned the wrong direction on their climb out which resulted in a NMAC.
The flight crew of a corporate aircraft reported insufficient deceleration during landing on 6;500 foot runway in poor weather that led to a runway excursion.
B737 First Officer reported executing a go-around at MCO following a 'violent' 30-40 degree roll on short final. It was not clear if the roll was a result of weather or a wake vortex encounter.

An Anchorage Center (ZAN) Controller reports of receiving information on an IFR aircraft that; because of its altitude; will be below the Minimum IFR Altitudes (MIA). The Controller coordinates with another facility to get the aircraft a higher altitude. When the pilot checks on the Controller issues a low altitude alert and tells the pilot to climb. The pilot says he wants VFR on top and the Controller lets the aircraft remain VFR on top. Controller is concerned about correct procedures for this type of operation.
Three B777 Flight Attendants B777 reported their aircraft encountered some Clear Air Turbulence injuring several flight attendants.
A pilot on an ILS Approach into SJU reported they had to break themselves out when the Tower sequenced a VFR aircraft too close in front of them.

B737 First Officer discussed the complexity some aeronautical charts present when pilots are required to mentally calculate the required climb gradient in feet per mile on SIDs. For example; the STL NATCA2 RNAV did not have the calculation charted but did have a required departure waypoint minimum crossing altitude.
A319 Captain reported being unable to accomplish a proper preflight inspection with the towbar-less tractor in place at the gate.
LJ45 flight crew reported preflight procedural mistakes resulted in a rejected takeoff and engine damage.
R90 Approach Controller reported frequency outages; at this location; during a peak period.

ZLA Controller reported an aircraft they assumed would continue on a southbound heading and turn after leaving the US. The aircraft was not pointed out to the adjacent sector causing an airspace deviation.

Flight crew reported going around from 50 feet due to a departing aircraft not having rotated yet. The go around was self initiated and resulted in these two aircraft climbing in close proximity to each other without any direction from the tower.
Instructing Pilot reported a NMAC with another small aircraft during traffic pattern work with student.
B737 cabin crew reported that the entry door slide was not disarmed prior to opening of the door; causing injury to a flight attendant.
B747 flight crew reported that ATC advised them of military activity ahead. The flight crew got a TCAS RA after a fighter jet intercepted them.
B737 Captain reported not making some of the crossing restrictions while flying the CHSLY3 STAR to CLT.
Air Carrier Flight Attendant reports that the Survival Kit Lanyard is placed in an inaccessible location on the aircraft.
Flight Attendant reported family boarded with a child not wearing a face mask. The family refused to comply with the mask policy; but was allowed to remain on the flight.
Flight crew reported having to move passengers from two rows for weight and balance causing a delay; and then once airborne crew was told they took off with incorrect final weights.
Flight crew reported a low visibility taxi chart discrepancy.

HCF Controller reported of the lack of help from the Traffic Management Unit. Three Traffic Management Coordinators were on the floor helping; but according to the reporter did nothing to help. None of the TMC's were certified to work any of the areas where the flow control problems were.
B737 Flight Attendant reported the crew and passengers were unknowingly exposed to pesticide spray in the form of a heavy fog when they boarded the aircraft.
Pilot reported terrain warning on approach.

A LEX Tower Controller reports of an airborne conflict that traffic has been issued to. One of the two aircraft sees the other. The other aircraft gets an RA and descends. Legal separation was continued but the Controller is reporting that this type of conflict happens all the time in the Class C environment. The Controller is concerned about the 'boy yelling wolf' scenario that pilots might lose faith in the RA System.
Technician reported unserviceable test equipment caused a misdiagnosis of a pitot/static problem and an aborted takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported lack of taxiway signage and an outdated airport diagram resulted in confusion during taxi at PIT airport.
Two B737 flight crews reported that a localizer overshoot and cloudy conditions resulted in the inability to maintain visual separation.
B767 Flight Attendant reported both being unable to hear announcements and the new seating configurations make evacuation difficult.

ZKC ARTCC sector requested an aircraft be routed around its airspace due to weather and traffic saturation deviating for the weather. The request was denied. An aircraft was issued a vector to avoid another aircraft but the vector was not enough to maintain required lateral separation.
MD-11 Captain reported a severe turbulence event in oceanic airspace. Captain stated they experienced a 1;500 foot loss of altitude during the event.
Aircraft Technician reported wrong fuel filter was installed.
Flight Attendant reported a deadheading pilot pushed his way past her while doing the safety demonstration to use the lavatory and coughed profusely.
Air carrier First Officer reported experiencing an unstabilized approach to CZUL airport.
First Officer reported a speed and altitude deviation during departure and cited fatigue from a lengthy ATC COVID-19 related delay as contributing factor.

An airline Dispatcher reports inadequate company policy for delineating required procedures for changing destination field conditions; especially required aircraft landing performance calculations during extremely cold conditions.
Aircraft Maintenance Technician reported that when B737-800 CFM engines exceed EGT limits. The AMM is not clear when the high pressure turbine EGT inspection is required to be accomplished.
B737 flight crew reported rejecting the takeoff after experiencing a bird strike just below V1.
Flight Attendant reported the aircraft cleaners are proceeding onto the aircraft before all passengers are deplaned which does not conform with social distancing.
Pilot reported an FAA ramp check looking for third party flight sharing platform activity.
CRJ900 Maintenance Technician reported incorrectly performing maintenance procedure.

Flight Attendant reported sitting in a passenger seat and checking the seat belt; Attendant noticed the safety pins were missing.  She and another Flight Attendant checked other seats to see if they also had safety pin which they did not.
Falcon 10 flight crew shut down left engine due to low oil pressure. They discovered the O-Ring on the oil filler cap had a gap that allowed oil to escape.
Instructor pilot reported an NMAC event immediately after takeoff.
Air Carrier flight attendants reported that a passenger's Emotional Support Animal defecated on a passenger and in another row during flight.
PA-28 flight Instructor reported an NMAC event during landing practice.
C172 Instructor reported a runway excursion during a cross-wind landing and regained control after aircraft slowed down.
Air carrier Captain had an airspeed deviation and cited being rusty due lack of flying as contributing.
Maintenance Supervisor and a Captain reported an aircraft was left unattended with the Auxiliary Power Unit running.
Flight Attendant reported opening UPK (Universal Precaution Kit) to clean up and dispose of human waste in the biohazard bag.
Air carrier First Officer reported a 'Slow Airspeed Warning' situation during a weather related go-around event and cited lack of flying time as a contributing factor.
Air Carrier Captain reported that Company SOPs of using Radar Altimeters for altitude callouts can provide inaccurate information when operating in Central American and South American airports.
Student Pilot flying C-172 reported loss of aircraft control during touch and go with crosswind that resulted in a runway excursion.
Dispatcher reported being out of the loop when departure station operations delayed an aircraft.
M20 pilot reported encountering IMC conditions while flying VFR. He elected to divert to an enroute field and wait for the weather to pass before continuing to his destination.
A321 Captain reported a ground conflict with a fuel truck while taxiing into the gate.
Air carrier Captain reported that two passengers who were possibly inebriated were acting belligerently and not complying with face mask policy.
Private pilot reported acting as Pilot In Command beyond required flight review date.
LFT Tower controllers reported a ground conflict when an aircraft began crossing Runway 4L while an aircraft was taking off.
PCT Controller reported a miscommunication issue with DCA Tower contributed to an aircraft entering Prohibited Airspace P56B.
Seattle TRACON Controller reported issues relating to the VFR traffic in and around Boeing Field and the Renton airport.
Tower Controller trainee and trainer reported not noticing an aircraft taxiing across a runway while another aircraft was landing due to distraction from traffic in the pattern.
B747 flight crew reported beginning to divert due to weather and minimum fuel but ended up going to the original destination.

A Line Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) describes why he believes competing interests on the ramp make it difficult; if not impossible; to comply with his air carrier's policies and procedures. He uses an incident involving an aft cargo compartment damage on an MD-80 series aircraft to illustrate the dysfunction of the situation.
Dispatcher reported a communication breakdown with Operations Agent regarding the removal of noncompliance Hazmat.
Center Controller reported an airspace violation and traffic conflict while opening a sector from one Controller working three sectors which were unmanageable due to weather deviations and volume.
CE560 Captain reported a near miss with a UAV.
Captain reported pilots are being exposed to COVID-19 because the company requires the use of specific headsets on the flight deck.
Air carrier Captain reported interference from a local church broadcast interfered with his communication with the tug driver at BOI airport.
Newly trained A321 Captain reported he was uncomfortable with the small amount of flying he has been doing since training.

Pilot reported being told by ATC that they have left the class Bravo airspace but will enter again in around a mile. Pilot acknowledged the call and felt they could not have been out of the Class Bravo for more than a few seconds; but were at 230 knots while under it.
ZAB Controller reported an aircraft on a direct routing that placed it below the Minimum IFR Altitude
Instructor Pilot reported another aircraft entered the same taxiway resulting in emergency braking.

An A320 Captain and Flight attendant discovered biohazards in the aft of the aircraft cabin. The cleaners who came on board only took out trash and were unable to properly clean the aircraft. On the next leg; passengers in the same area became ill.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was non-compliant with wearing a face mask during flight.

ZAU Controller reports of an aircraft coming in high from a previous sector during handoff which resulted in a missed crossing restriction and an increase in controller workload. The enroute Controller makes recommendations on how to remedy the situation.
A Flight Service Station Specialist reported receiving a report from an aircraft that a construction vehicle entered the runway while they were landing.
A TRACON Controller reported being issued an unsatisfactory performance report by their supervisor; but feels they did nothing wrong.
A319 flight crew reported they were advised of an injury when a ramp employee walked behind a running engine while the aircraft was parked at the gate.
A pilot departed DAL Runway 13R and took evasive action from an aircraft which had executed a go-around from Runway 13L and turned to cross the 13R departure path.
Aircraft Technician reports that an incorrect aircraft part was deferred per Minimum Equipment List.
The company is harvesting parts from parked aircraft without following the guidelines in the GMM (General Maintenance Manual).
Flight crew reports conflicts with helicopter arrivals and departures during aircraft taxi in airport ramp area.
CRJ900 First Officer reported that they rejected takeoff due to a cowl anti-ice warning.
B737 First Officer reported multiple TCAS alerts on departure from LAS.

Air carrier First Officer reported noticing their aircraft was not properly de-iced; but the Captain disagreed and decided to take off.  Reporter stated that during climbout possible 'chunks of ice' hit the aircraft resulting in maintenance inspection.
Flight Attendant reported that a crewmember had possibly been exposed to COVID-19. For social distancing purposes; Flight Attendant sat in a cabin seat for takeoff and landing.
Air carrier First Officer reported that flight dispatchers are filing flight plans that use the wrong arrival into an airport; causing problems for flight crews during arrival.
Air Carrier flight crew reported thinking they were cleared for takeoff; but were only cleared to line up and wait.
Technician reported disagreement between departments concerning Fan Blade mapping and reporting for tracking purposes.
Center Controller reported a facility Supervisor continually does not follow state and NATCA guidelines regarding face mask policy.

ERJ-170 First Officer continued approach and landing after complying with a momentary 'climb' TCAS RA and subsequently found that company SOP requires a go-around if the aircraft was configured for landing.
B737 flight crew experiences a false TCAS RA to climb apparently generated by two B-1's above the B737. After initially climbing the RA switches to a descent.
Medium Transport flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence resulting in altitude loss of approximately 1400 feet.
C172 pilots reported they did not recall an instruction to contact the Tower until already having entered the Tower's airspace.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported ATC was not notified about their aircraft which was operationally restricted to 200 knots.
Technician reported inadequate Task Card and Aircraft Maintenance Manual references; exacerbated by wrong tooling caused difficulties in purging as contaminated aircraft hydraulic system.
B737 Captain reported executing a go-around at MCO after encountering a 'microburst type event' on short final.
B737 First Officer reported a glideslope anomaly on the ILS to Runway 30 at MIA apparently caused by B747 aircraft holding on the taxiway in the vicinity of the end of the runway.

A B777 Captain attempted to comply with his company's policy to determine which aircraft contained AIMS (Airplane Information Management System) BP-16 upgrade; but was unaware of the process to access the data display.

A MD-80 fuel quantity gauge MEL 28-4B required a complex alternative fueling procedure which the crew; Dispatch; and Maintenance became confused about. The aircraft pushed and taxied from the gate over max zero fuel weight by a small amount.
TRACON Controller reported aircraft incorrectly starting descent on ILS which creates a conflict and wake turbulence error.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to wear a mask during the flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported a ground conflict with a small aircraft during taxiout at BNA airport.  Reportedly; ATC was not communicating with the small aircraft.
ZAU Controller reported issuing a VFR on top clearance and confusion ensued as to what the pilot and Controller responsibilities were.
A321 Captain reported a possible error in the company's Alternate Brake Check procedure which is to be accomplished before the first flight of the day.
EMB-145 flight crew reported incorrect aircraft Dispatch code due to 'Critical' Human Tissue. Flight was re-dispatched as Medevac flight.

ZLC Controller has an aircraft that is encountering icing conditions and requests lower altitude. The Controller issues the lowest altitude he can due to the MIA and the pilot is still getting icing on the aircraft. The Controller aids the aircraft and issues an even lower altitude and vectors it towards lower terrain. The aircraft eventually gets into VMC conditions and no longer is getting iced up. The Controller then gets a briefing that he shouldn't have done what he did and asks for clarification on how to help the pilot when in this type of condition.
GA pilot reported executing a go-around on their own to avoid a possible wake turbulence encounter with a Gulfstream on approach to GRI.
The use of portable electronic devices during the safety briefing and during takeoff and landing is a potential safety problem.
An Air Carrier pilot reported a leaking coffee pot caused a burning smell.
Airbus Captain reported ATC issued complex taxi instructions during their landing rollout.
Captain reported encountering issues with a ferry permit.
Maintenance Technician performed incorrect Minimum Equipment List deferral procedure.
A319 First Officer reported being dispatched without proper fuel load nor an alternate; resulting in a diversion.
ZAB Controller observes a beacon coded target. Another aircraft in his sector had the same beacon code. The original flight was tracked as a Mode-C intruder.
B737 Captain reported confusion regarding a 'climb via' clearance departing BWI.
NCT TRACON Controller reported an operational error and trouble understanding an Order relating to the proper use of Closely Spaced Parallel Runways.
Airline Captain reported no required Hazmat documents were provided to flight crew at departure resulting in transport of undocumented Hazmat cargo.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion regarding the note to add a fix to the ZEEKK 2 STAR when the company policy does not allow it.
Flight Crew reported turbulence caused a loss of aircraft control; requiring evasive action to regain aircraft control.
ZSU Controller reported an aircraft initiated their go-around instructions from the approach end of the runway at TISX instead of the departure end which placed the aircraft below the MVA.
PA-21 Student Single Pilot reported a go around; including a ground strike; to a final landing with a runway excursion.
B747-400 Captain reported a track deviation occurred departing ANC.
A320 flight crew reported they were charged with a 'crew cancellation' because they refused to assist maintenance with an engine run test for smoke in the cabin.
Air carrier Captain reported having to remove a passenger showing symptoms of illness.
Air carrier Captain reported a late runway change on arrival to IAH airport resulted in undershooting the crossing restriction during STAR transition.
B737 Captain reported that a communication breakdown between ATC and another aircraft resulted in a runway incursion and a go-around.
DAB Controller reported new required procedure creates an increased workload that jeopardizes safety.

A passenger on an EMB170/175 reported noting that the safety card for a water landing shows one variant opening only the front doors while the other shows opening doors at both ends of the aircraft. He did not know which variant he was flying on.
Flight Crew reported a momentary terrain alert while on a visual approach with the terrain and airport in sight and continued their approach.
A CE-550 departed on the BOS Runway 22L BLZZR3 RNAV SID but did not make the turn to TJAYY because the FMS did not correctly process the departure.
A jet aircraft crew on approach to BED described the VFR traffic conflicts in the crowded airspace below BOS Class B.
Air carrier First Officer reported chart deficiencies related to Class B floor awareness on RNAV arrivals into SFO and OAK.
A320 Captain reported not receiving correct Fuel On Board (FOB) numbers in flight plan.
B737 flight crew reported being cleared to taxi past the ILS critical area hold bars as an air carrier was performing a CATIII approach.
B737 NG Captain reported the new CPDLC clearance procedure has caused confusion and is difficult to interpret.
Rescue Helicopter pilot reported a near miss at night with what the crew thought was an accompanying aircraft. In reality was an unidentified aircraft.
Memphis Tower Controller reported a loss of required separation after takeoff when the leading aircraft did not comply with the speed designated on the Standard Instrument Departure; (SID).
Dispatcher reported a similar callsign issue and cancellations attributed in part to COVID-19 pandemic related work environment.
Air Carrier Check Airman reported pilots lack of understanding concerning the technical data and logic behind climb gradients.

Air Carrier First Officer reported executing a go-around while flying a right base; night visual to ABQ Runway 26 in strong winds and turbulence. A landing was successful following a left base visual; but the reporter suggested Runway 26 night visual approach should not be allowed.
B737-800 First Officer reported returning to departure airport after experiencing increased vibration associated with ingesting a large bird into #1 engine.
Air carrier Captain reported the J-line used for alignment guidance entering the ramp area is hard to see in heavy rain conditions; and the marshaling procedure should be changed to account for that.
TRACON Front Line Manager reported a loss of separation and cited traffic complexity and staffing as contributing factors.

ZDC Controller reports of problems with the IVANE 5 Arrival into CLT and a specific type of aircraft having problems continually making crossing restrictions. This report also removed the IVANE 5 but the aircraft in question was given a crossing restriction which they didn't meet as well.
SoCal TRACON Controller reported a problem with the STARS Collision Alert System.
ORD Tower Controller observed that a different runway Tower Controller was not correctly following rules for intersecting runway arrival/departure separation.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with face mask policy.

A B737-700 pilot engaged the APP Mode on the SFO 28R ILS when tracking the Localizer in LNAV about 14NM out at which time the aircraft made a hard right 30 degree turn for no apparent reason. The APP Mode was reselected outside AXMUL without incident. An airport equipment anomaly is suspected.
SR22 pilot reported an NMAC on final to RNT airport with a warbird.
Air Carrier Ramp personnel reported DG cargo AKE container not securely strapped in cargo box.
ZLA ARTCC Controller reported a loss of separation due to an aircraft not descending as fast as the Controller assumed it would.

A pilot approached the San Francisco Bay Area from the South intending to land at PAO. They were vectored to the airport by NCT; but once nearing the PAO pattern he had difficulty seeing the airport; entering the pattern; maintaining altitude. Turning final for Runway 31 they had a NMAC.
SAN Tower Controller reported sending an aircraft around; which then caused the controller to stop a departure below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude to maintain separation between the two aircraft.
B737 Captain reported four STAR changes by ATC arriving DEN before being reassigned a previous arrival; costing 32 minutes.
Air carrier Captain reported taxi drop spots are not on airport charts.
Air carrier flight crew reported multiple traffic alerts departing JNU and expressed concern at ATC procedures that do not seem to effectively mitigate these conflicts.
PC-12 Captain described a near-collision on the ramp when a Citation was not properly secured at shutdown and began to roll toward them.
ZAN Center Controller reported two aircraft that were VFR needed help due to weather and icing; both aircraft were helped and landed safely.
Turbojet Captain reported an anomaly in the SID procedures in the Houston area which conflicted with expectations with ATC.
A Cleveland Center (ZOB) Controller reports of a loss of separation attributed to Severe Weather Avoidance Plan SWAP0 and aircraft not being pointed out correctly.
B737-800 Maintenance Technician reported that while replacing the rudder; a restraining sling broke loose; subsequently swinging the rudder into their working platform.
Air carrier flight crew reported being distracted and not arming the autothrottle prior to departure resulting in a rejected takeoff.
HCF TRACON Trainee Controller reported a military helicopter did not comply with its missed approach instructions which placed it into conflict with another departure.
B737-800 First Officer reported concern with the new company checklist and policy changes related to crosswind training.
Air carrier flight crew reported they may have landed passed the touchdown zone. First Officer stated lack of flying contributed to the event.
A corporate pilot reported the Ground Controller was overworked and lashed out at the pilot for multiple radio calls.
Balloon pilot reported a Cargo aircraft descended through the area of their balloon flight.
A321 Captain reported being given a modification to the RYDDR1 arrival to LAX that was beyond the aircraft performance capability.

An Air Traffic Control Procedures Specialist was working in the Tower for currency. The reporter cleared a VFR aircraft along a published VFR procedure route. The reporter believed the aircraft would proceed along certain highways and directions for that procedure. Other persons in the Tower including Supervisors told the reporter they were wrong regarding the direction the procedure directs the aircraft to fly. Upon review the reporter was correct and the other control personnel were incorrect. The reporter states this is a recurring issue in this Tower and recommends supplemental/refresher training for Tower Controllers.
CRJ-900 Captain reported taking evasive action during taxi-in to the ramp to avoid another aircraft under tow on the taxiway.

B737 Captain reported he was put into an unstabilized approach situation when ATC held him high on the SILCN1 arrival into SJC. Reporter commented that deviations from 'cleared via' clearances often cause problems.
Flight Attendant reported that flight attendants are blocking off an excessive number of seating rows not allowing passengers to sit there due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
A319 Captain reports hitting two geese on rotation. The only damage appears to be to the gear doors and the crew returns for an uneventful landing.

B737 First Officer reported during preflight an aircraft just returned from the hangar; ground personnel notified him about pitot covers not removed from the RUDDER FEEL DIFFERENTIAL PITOT tubes. The pilot stated he may never have seen them from his vantage point.

Several flight crews refused to conduct a gear down; overwater ferry as doing so would violate provisions for conduct of such flight. The operation was ultimately shopped to a crew who agreed to do so despite the refusal of others.
Technician reported an aircraft he was assigned to work was previously not evaluated properly for ground damage and subsequently flew in revenue service in a non airworthy condition.
B737 NG flight crew reported a suspicious device hanging from the spare bulbs panel resulting in a flight delay.
E170 Captain reported that a towbar broke during pushback; resulting in a return to the gate.
ZID Center Controllers reported a Handoff Assist controller removed an active flight plan from the computer causing the radar data tag to drop off their radar display.
EMB-145 Flight Attendant reported noticing a missing placard above the PSU panel that instructed how to close the MCD.
B737-700 Captain reported exceeding 200 kts below Class B airspace during arrival into the DAL area.
Cessna pilot reported that ATC did not help him with Class Bravo clearance when he requested flight following on the ground.
A pilot in the busy; hazy RNT VFR traffic pattern became distracted while concentrating on following traffic and descended to about 200 feet AGL turning base.
Pilot reported an airspace violation due to a fast climbing; lighter than usual aircraft.
Pilot reported experiencing a runway incursion after landing.
A Seattle Center (ZSE) Controller reports of confusion about the procedure for a missed approach off of RDM airport with an aircraft arriving on the opposite direction arrival.
B757 flight crew reported exceeding 200 knots below Class B airspace.
ZAU Center Controller reported that a C90 TRACON Controller climbed an aircraft into the Center's airspace without coordination.
737-800 flight crew reported a high speed rejected takeoff due to an aircraft on the parallel runway executing a missed approach.
B737 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence on climbout.

Light Transport pilot reported that while inbound the A80 TRACON Controller acknowledged the plan to depart VFR and pick up an IFR clearance in the air; but later the subsequent controller denied the request.
A Boston Center (ZBW) Controller reports of an airborne conflict between an IFR and a VFR aircraft. The Controller did not issue traffic as they didn't observe it until the pilot called about it.
Maintenance Controller reported miscommunication over an MEL that allowed an aircraft to fly in a non-airworthy status.

A Southern California TRACON (SCT) Controller reports of read back that was a caused an operational error. The pilot did not read back key words and because of this the Controller is charged with a loss of separation.
CE-560 flight crew reported that declining a ferry flight due to fatigue.
Ground Controller reported an airliner taxied in the wrong direction toward the ramp.
B-787 Captain reported receiving unrevised Dangerous Goods form prior to taxi.
Flight Attendant reported a mother and young daughter would not comply with face mask policy.

ZAB Controller saw something in the fourth line of the data block that he did not know the meaning of. Reporter called other Controller and was told. Controller then received aircraft on frequency and asked where it was going. Reporter then had pilot spell the name. Reporter then knew where the aircraft was going. Due to misspelled intersection; Controller had no idea where the aircraft was going. Controller wants people to stop putting information in the fourth block unless it is approved.
SFO Tower Ground Controller reported observing an aircraft taxi in front of another aircraft; when intended to taxi behind it.
Regional jet pilot reported wake turbulence concerns related to departing behind and following aircraft into and out of airports. Pilot stated ATC appears to be in a rush.
A319 Captain reported the ground crew was not aware the APU was inop and did not understand hand signals for connecting ground power to the aircraft.
BE99 flight crew reported a gear up landing while training; as a result of inattentive supervision and an incomplete abnormal procedures checklist.
Flight Instructor reported a NMAC with a helicopter.
Air carrier ramp employee reported jet fuel still leaking onto the ramp from fuel carts even after a safety report.

During climb out from DTW; Cleveland Center cleared the aircraft to resume normal speed; but the crew did not know about the 280 kt restriction above 10;000 ft until they changed their iPad screen size. Similar issues are seen on iPad ORD and LAX SID speed constraint placements.
Air carrier Captain reported the right wing engine pods were scraped during a gusty crosswind landing.

A300 flight crew experiences a bird strike on takeoff that seems to be in the nose gear area. Holding is initiated to contact Maintenance and ATC informs that another crew saw fire from the left engine at rotation. The crew elects to return to the departure airport where three bent fan blades are discovered by Maintenance.
I90 TRACON Controller's trainee did not use proper procedures to ensure wake turbulence separation between successive arrivals.
Ramp Agent reported passenger bags were being used to surround hazardous material to keep it secure in the cargo compartment.
C172 pilot noticed flashed outside and initially thought it was a laser. On closer inspection they realized it was flashes from holiday decorations.
Air carrier Captain reported having no pillows on flights due to COVID-19 and not being able to adequately rest.
A Center Controller reported an air carrier descended without complying with a crossing restriction and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
SAN Tower Supervisor was notified by the Center that the clearance issued to a departure via PDC was different than the clearance shown by the Center.
CRJ-200 Captain reported an inflight near miss with a group of towers during a visual approach.

A VFR aircraft being transferred between TRACON's was told radar service terminated but to remain on their present squawk and monitor the next facility's frequency. The receiving facility never contacted the aircraft. After landing at its destination the pilot was asked to call the facility to ensure he had landed. The pilot is confused as to if they did anything wrong.

Flight Attendant reported that two passengers were non-compliant with face mask policy; despite multiple requests from other flight attendants. Flight Attendant reported one of those passengers became argumentative and used profanity.
B737NG flight crew reported a trim problem during climbout resulting in a return to field.

IAD support specialist reports of a flight inbound and outbound to/from IAD that is using the same call sign at the same time. He reports this has been happening for over 7 months and nothing has been done about it.
GA pilot reported NMAC with unreported traffic on transition through Class C airspace.
An A320 pilot refused an aircraft with an inoperative APU because of terrain and weather considerations.
CRJ flight crew reported a Nose Gear warning message on takeoff resulted in a return to the departure airport.
C182 pilot reported an NMAC with a CJ2.
Ramp Agent reported moving HAZMAT from aft compartment in order to accommodate must ride military kennel.
SR22 pilot reported diverting to a nearby airport after noting a CO LEVEL HIGH aural alarm.
B777 Captain reported the company procedures at international stations needs to improve to prevent fatigue and safety issues.
Air Carrier flight crew reported performing two missed approaches at FAY due to weather and an un-stabilized approach before diverting.
Air Carrier pilot reported operations was forcing the flight crew to try to extend their Flight Duty Period when known delays were in effect due to weather and a ramp closure.
Air carrier flight reported due to ATC staffing issues the flight would have to be delay vectored. They opted to land at an alternate airport and receive more fuel; then continued on to destination.
Ground personnel reported Hazmat cargo load configuration errors after gate pushback.
PA32 pilot reported not being able to restart engine after an unsuccessful air-start attempt.
Air carrier Captain reported performing an aborted takeoff after getting takeoff clearance cancelled during the initial roll.

Denver Center Traffic Management Coordinator reported of a false target that looked like a Near Mid Air Collision. Controller stated it was the scariest thing he had seen and wanted someone to work on the RADAR/FUSION problem.
