ILS coupling during approach to 22L EWR resulted in oscillating pitch changes above 2000 feet.
PAO Tower Controller reported an airspace deviation with an aircraft due to adjacent facility not allowing penetration for sequence.

EMB-175 Captain reported reprogramming the FMS several times after being issued ATC instructions to expect a landing runway different from what ATIS reported; then being issued opposite runway instructions by the next Controller.
PA28 pilot and safety pilot reported an airborne conflict and wake turbulence encounter when ATC cleared a Falcon jet for approach to the same runway they were flying a practice ILS to.
Pilot reported being cleared for takeoff while on a taxiway; turned onto runway and while turning; observed an aircraft on final.  Pilot advised Local Controller; who sent aircraft on final around.
Air carrier Captain reported during an inflight medical emergency with an unconscious passenger; neither two physicians nor the flight attendants could operate a new model blood pressure cuff.

Light Transport flight crew reported experiencing communication issues with Miami Center and Nassau Approach. Flight crew elected to divert due to departing traffic from the original destination and the inability of the ZMA Controller to give them a missed approach clearance.
Maintenance Technician reported the maintenance documentation did not address engine spinner protective coating deterioration or separation.
Small Transport Pilot reported an NMAC event during landing followed by incorrect traffic pattern entry.
A80 Controller reported an example of why they feel new Optimization of Airspace and Procedures Metroplex (OAPM) Optimum Profile Descent (OPD) procedures are inefficient and unsafe.
HCF TRACON Controller was told by their Supervisor that they had a loss of separation. The reporter stated they did not have a loss of separation.
EMB175 Captain reported failure to follow procedure for an unstabilized approach.
B737 flight crew reported being chastised by an FAA inspector post-flight for not making a standard announcement when the seat belt sign was turned off in cruise.
Flight Attendant reported being unable to log into the electronic manual during a medical emergency.
An Air Carrier pilot reported while on short final to their filed diversion airport Dispatch requested they divert to a different airport.
B737-700 Captain reported the island at position 8A on the Jeppsen 10-9 chart is not depicted on the 10-9B chart; causing confusion and possible taxiway incursion.
B737 Captain reported that ground crew were not fully trained on the special handling procedures unique to the aircraft model.
LJ35 Captain reported an air turnback after an IFSD during a maintenance check to test an engine fire loop was unsuccessful.
An air carrier crew was issued multiple arrival STARs and runway assignments during descent into DEN resulting in a very high work load and a momentary low speed alert with the speedbrake extended.
A Tower Controller reported they coordinated a go around for an aircraft with Departure Control but the aircraft did not climb to the assigned altitude resulting in a loss of prescribed separation.
Flight crew reported both right hand main tire and wheel assemblies fell off the aircraft at take off.
Flight Instructor reported the student lost directional control of the aircraft during landing roll resulting in a ground loop and tire failure.
The B737 Flight Attendant reported that final cabin checks are not accomplished if the flight attendants are advised by the Captain to be seated early.
Maintenance Technician reported a 787-900 engine oil fill cap was not closed and locked before departure.

ZLA Center Controller receiving a handoff told an adjacent sector to stop the aircraft's climb for converging traffic. The Controller making the handoff was distracted by other tasks and forgot to comply with the instruction causing less than required separation with the converging traffic.
An ERJ-175 Captain noted his company's pre-deice checklist turns all exterior lights in contact with deice fluid OFF but nowhere after deicing does the checklist turn the lights back ON.
B737 flight crew reported NMAC during climb out.
The pilot of a C180 reported a loss of control during landing rollout in gusty conditions that resulted in a runway excursion and damage to the aircraft.
B737 First Officer reported Dispatch notification during cruise of Hazmat material on board.
Tower Controller reported an aircraft experienced engine issues resulting in a runway excursion during landing.
A321 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence in the vicinity of TUL VOR at FL350.

Controller reports of a VFR pilot who didn't initially turn as instructed away from another aircraft that was IFR. Pilot took turn and then questioned Controller as to why they had to turn. Controller advised it was for traffic. Pilot questioned if something had changed to the LOA. Controller advised no; they were turned for traffic. Company owner called TRACON and wanted the Controller 'tuned up.'
B787 Captain reported they taxied on restricted taxiway after landing roll.
Falcon 900 Captain reported some confusion of airspeed requirements on the KRITR1 RNAV SID and the statement 'Upon reaching 10;000 ft.' and 'Prior to reaching 10;000 ft.'
Pilot reported an NMAC at a closed towered airport.
A TRACON Controller instructing a trainee observed the trainee allow an aircraft to fly below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier Captain was concerned about the removal of special qualifications for operations into SEQM.
ZAB ARTCC Controller reported a NORDO aircraft flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B767 emergency medical kit was found discarded in the jet-bridge.
Air carrier flight crew reported conflicting; confusing; and combative taxi instructions from the ORD Ground Controller.
Air carrier Captain reported being unsure if they were supposed to descend on the approach clearance received from ATC resulting in an altitude deviation.
Several mechanics reported that a BHT-407 Helicopter aft tail rotor gear box fairing had an undocumented hole added to access the gear box oil level site gage.
B737NG First Officer reported rejecting the takeoff at 110 kts after experiencing multiple bird strikes on the takeoff roll.
Technician reported work environment concerns that aren't being taken care of by management.

B737-700 Captain taking off on Runway 01; heard Tower Controller clear another aircraft for an intersection takeoff on intersecting Runway 10. Captain had to ascertain from the 10-9 page that the given intersection was past Runway 01 and would not be a conflict.
Air carrier First Officer reported not realizing that Dispatch did not add an MEL to the dispatch release until they were airborne.
TRACON Controller reported NMAC with two aircraft within their sector.
A-300 flight crew reported discovering during cruise receiving erroneous Dangerous Goods documents at departure.
Technician reported that both electrical connectors were installed on a single squib during fire bottle installation.
Pilot taxied without clearance because he thought Tower was still closed; but it was actually 15 minutes after the Tower had opened.
Flight Instructor flying DA-40 aircraft reported engine running rough during taxi out due to mixture setting.

ZSE Controller issued VFR traffic to an aircraft and restricted the aircraft above the traffic. The Controller issued a pilot's discretion descent to the aircraft. The aircraft received an RA for the VFR traffic.

A Lead Technician with multiple years of experience as an Aircraft Maintenance; Engine Start; Run-up; and Taxi Instructor; reports about the recent policy changes to the training and re-qualification requirements in their Procedures Manual. They believe the changes are flawed and will increase 'at risk' behaviors due to the lack of not accomplishing fleet specific proficiency Check rides on each type of fleet aircraft as previously required.
Air carrier flight crew and Dispatcher reported ATC routed the flight offshore beyond the non-EOW (Extended Over Water) limit.
A helicopter pilot reported tower wire markers are placed on lower wires and higher wires do not have markers.

Customer Service employee reported there is a lack of proper staffing in the lobby; which makes it difficult to follow CDC social distancing recommendations as passengers bunch up in groups while waiting in line.

SCT Controller and Frontline Manager reported of a problematic operation that has been going on at LAX. Aircraft departing inside each other when the aircraft on the right needs to turn left; but cannot due to the other aircraft that was departing from the south complex.

Atlanta Center (ZTL) Controller reported a Near Mid Air Collision he attributed to lack of sufficient radio coverage. Controller had to use a backup emergency radio and transmit into the blind. Aircraft heard transmission and turned away from traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported that the Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) freezes while using Jeppesen FliteDeck-Pro application; and functionality is only restored after a restart of the EFB.
Light transport pilot reported ATC was confused as to their direction of flight after departure; due to a communication breakdown while picking up their IFR clearance via telephone.
LJ60 Captain reports sighting a UAV at 5;000 feet while on downwind for Runway 18L at CVG.
B737 Captain reported possibly encountering a drone that flew by the right wing while descending through 9000 ft on approach to ATL near POORS.

An A319 ECAM displayed 'L/G Shock Absorber Fault; AUTO FLT A/THR Fault; CAB PR LDG ELEV Fault; ANTI ICE STBY Fault; AUTO FLT AP Fault; and VENT BLOWER Fault' after the landing gear was lowered for landing. A go-around was executed followed by a normal landing.
SR22 pilot reported experiencing GPS signal interruption on successive approaches to Santa Monica airport.
Turboprop First Officer reported momentary stick shaker after encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B737 on approach to SEA.
Air carrier First Officer reported losing control of aircraft due to icing conditions during taxi at CYYZ requiring reverse thrust to stop the aircraft.
Ground Crew reported a near strike with a B787 during pushback due to a communication breakdown with ATC.
Captain of a sky diving aircraft reported losing consciousness on a flight at FL250; even though he was on oxygen.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported a sidestep maneuver to another runway and with gusty conditions the approach became unstable. The flight crew executed a go-around and circle back for a normal landing.

Denver TRACON Controller reported of a SID that is going away with metroplex; but recommends it goes away sooner. He has seen eight to ten losses of separation with the SID and arrivals into the Denver area.
Oceanic Center Controller reported they did not receive updates for a flight entering their airspace non-radar.
Small transport Captain reported the First Officer taxied the aircraft off the taxiway onto a non-movement area.
A320 Captain reported a log book entry was incorrectly signed off causing a delay assigned to the flight crew instead of maintenance.
A Center controller reported an aircraft was overtaking a leading aircraft even though the ground speed readouts indicated the trailing aircraft was slower.
Flight Attendant reported changing seats during takeoff and landing in order to stay a safe distance from a passenger who appeared ill.
Small aircraft pilot reported striking uncharted wires while performing low altitude training.
Air carrier flight crew reported an encounter with severe turbulence.
B737-700 Captain reported windshear on approach requiring a diversion and resulting in a low fuel situation.
Air carrier pilot reported losing the green hydraulic system while on arrival.

A Captain reported a disagreement with his company about the condition of the aircraft's airworthiness certificate and the length of time the aircraft was considered flyable following a report about the certificate's illegible condition.
B737 Captain reported being instructed to taxi to the gate via a non-standard procedure that required taxiing off the taxiway.

ZAB Controller reports of two aircraft that are being handed off from another ZFW. Controller takes the first aircraft and then uses a tool to verify the route of the second aircraft. The second aircraft is in conflict with the first. The reporter calls ZFW and asks what they are going to do to separate the aircraft. The other Center Controller laughs and then transfers communication without the handoff being accepted. The reporter then separates the two aircraft.
Glider Pilot and Observer reported a Critical Ground Conflict after landing while still in the runway safety area with departing traffic.
B737 Captain reported severe weather on approach that resulted in a diversion.
B767 Captain reported discovering a crew oxygen issue during preflight that had been missed by multiple parties earlier.

During the takeoff roll the First Officer's airspeed lagged behind the Captain's so the flight returned to the departure airport. The aircraft had been washed; the pitot system inspected by the IRO; but yellow tape was found inside the First Officer's pitot tube upon inspection after landing.
A319 Flight Crew experienced turbulence in cirrus clouds at FL360 which caused abrupt altitude gain and resulted in passenger injury.
Tower Controller reported a 'plethora of deals' due to working with small arrival holes and not using the designated departure runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported the parking brake was still set during pushback resulting in a broken tow bar shear pin. Both pilots referenced lack of flying as a contributing factor.
A321 First Officer reported deviating for weather without ATC clearance.
Captain reported that a new Takeoff Checklist policy could result in departure delays and interfere with ATC departure sequencing at SFO.
B747 pilot reported the scheduling practices used at her international airline were extremely fatiguing and unsafe.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC on approach with aircraft that penetrated BNA airport airspace.
A Tower Local Controller reported the ASDE-X alerted on traffic that was already airborne and past a vehicle holding short of the runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported an unreliable localizer signal for SBY Runway 32.
Tracon Controllers reported a Frontline Manager not taking control of a situation; due to staffing and the Frontline Managers lack of training.
B737NG Captain was told to contact the Tower Supervisor after requesting to exit the runway after a lengthy line up and wait delay.

Large transport flight crew were cleared for an RNAV Visual Approach. They were fully configured; but at about 500 ft AGL they received a TCAS Traffic Alert. They observed two helicopters flying close to each other and about 200 feet lower than them. They executed a missed approach and returned for an uneventful landing.

A JNU Tower Controller reported the airport authority closed a portion of a runway unnecessarily. They parked their vehicle close enough to the closure point that a landing aircraft took evasive maneuvers to avoid.
PDX Tower Controller and an A320 Captain reported a traffic conflict on the Localizer due to VFR traffic at VOU.
Air carrier First Officer reported flying in known icing conditions; resulting in airframe ice accretion.

A crew departed the BOS North Cargo Ramp with clearance to Taxiway B via Taxiway L but turned left after leaving the Cargo Ramp and taxied via the General Aviation Ramp to Taxiway B. The event occurred during night conditions with snow. More accurate North Cargo airport charting was suggested.
Pilot reports that he was asked if he had the aircraft on three mile final in sight. Pilot reported yes; and then was told when he passes line up and wait. This happened at a foreign location.
MOB TRACON Controller reported lost communications with two VFR aircraft which were attempting to avoid adverse weather in an area where radio communications are routinely lost.
B737 Captain reported that Maintenance failed to properly sign the logbook and remove a placard; and attempted to defer responsibility to the flight crew instead.
B737-800 flight crew reported diverting to a nearby alternate when the cockpit filled with smoke shortly after takeoff following ground de-ice procedures.
B767-300ER First Officer reported they used Captain's authority to deviate around thunderstorms.
Local Controller reported an airborne conflict between two air carriers.
B737 Captain reported getting dissuaded by Maintenance from initiating a logbook entry after discovering the shut off valve was discovered closed on the crew O2 bottle.

Tower Controller reported poor quality connection on the crash phone; and no one taking responsibility to repairing it. Controller also cited a need for training on coordination procedures to the downtown Sioux City response; or eliminated from tower line.
B757 Captain reported fatigue on international trip.
Air carrier First Officer reported forgetting to turn on the probe heat and stated lack of flying was a contributing factor.

SAN Tower Controller reported of a loss of separation attributed to Approach Control not spacing the two aircraft correctly. The Controller reported this was a serious problem and they were getting massive compression on final.

A320 First Officer reported pre-departure high workload coupled with a runway change that resulted in taking off with an improper flap setting. Once flaps were retracted; the flight proceeded uneventfully.
Pilot reports the AWOS system at VKX includes lengthy foreign language reports which block out UNICOM frequency communications.

Flight crew missed the mandatory crossing altitude NOTAM change from 7;000 to 8;000 feet at the final fix (ARCHI) on the DYAMD TWO RNAV Arrival to SFO. Due to the amount of time that the NOTAM has been in effect; crew recommended that the charted altitude be changed at the next revision.

EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in 3-mile trail of a B757 just before touchdown that required aggressive aileron input. Reporter stated he felt more separation behind B757 aircraft would be a good idea.
A tailwheel Flight Instructor reported losing control on landing; running off the runway and subsequently going around with a damaged wing.

The reporter describes an instance of an aircraft conflict alert activating when there is no traffic. Reporter stated this is a common problem at facilities that have upgraded to FUSION radar display software.
S56 TRACON Controller reported an A320 on a vector to intercept a Localizer flew through the Localizer course placing the aircraft below the MVA.

MD80 Captain reported being dispatched with an inoperative APU and a Note that required the aircraft to be routed through maintenance bases due to engine driven generator problems a week prior. This note is not compatible with the APU MEL and the Captain asks that it be removed since a week has passed since the generator was repaired. This required a great many phone calls and a long delay to be accomplished.

ZMA Controller reports of conflicting information from TMU and Management about vectoring aircraft close to or through weather. Controller describes a scenario where two aircraft are close to weather and the impact it may have on the flights.
A large twin engine transport aircraft flight crew reported blocking in with 5100 LBS of fuel even though the FMS projected 7100 LBS.
B737-700 flight crew reported 2 MELs that when combined did not provide sufficient bleed air to properly pressurize the aircraft during descent.
A pilot with a Class B airspace clearance received conflicting and confusing control instructions.
B737NG flight crew reported overshooting their cleared altitude departing EWR after encountering wake turbulence.
B737 Captain reported a NMAC with a UAV on approach to LAX.
Two maintenance technicians reported that after installing an engine in a Eurocopter it was discovered they failed to install the bushings in the aft mount.
A319 Captain reported loss of situational awareness; resulting in a GPWS warning and requiring evasive action to continue approach.
Tower Controller reported that the ASDE-X triggered a go around due to the helicopter not being recognized by the software.
Air Carrier flight crew reported they forgot to receive clearance to land. First Officer reported this was the first flight after an extended period of time off.
Kansas City Center Controller reported missing a handoff due to weather; workload; traffic deviating; and having to come up with deviation routes on the fly.
Air carrier First Officer reported seeing pilots in the lounge not wearing face masks.
B737 flight crew reported two flight attendants were slightly injured in a moderate turbulence encounter during cruise.
Pilot flying experimental aircraft encountered city vehicle on runway during takeoff.

Air carrier First Officer reported difficulty communicating with the tug driver during pushback because the tug driver was wearing a mask in compliance with COVID-19 procedures. Reporter suggested the mask was not necessary when the driver was alone in the tug.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering 'severe' wake turbulence in trail of an A321 descending through FL210 for a CLT arrival.
B737 First Officer reported exceeding an altitude deviation during a go-around in turbulence and strong winds.
Gate agent reported a customer with a smart bag with lithium battery attached. Battery was removed and bag was loaded.
MWH Tower Cab Radar Controller In Charge reported a loss of separation after inadvertently descending an aircraft into another aircraft.
Air carrier Captain reported food that is provided for passengers and crew is unfit for human consumption on specific flights.
Small Transport pilot reported a near-collision with a fuel truck while taxiing at PDX.

B777 Flight Attendant reported the flight encountered unreported severe turbulence and several flight attendants were hurt. Flight Attendant noted there were not enough jumpseats in the back for all flight attendants.
B737-800 pilot reported inadequate training on use of new checklist procedures.
Captain reported refusing an aircraft due to a inappropriate MEL; as well as miscommunication with supervisor.
B767 international Captain reported he felt less rested and therefore less safe following the FAA mandated international rest break schedule.
Student pilot on solo cross country reported a VFR into IMC encounter.
A319 Captain reported he felt safety was degraded when I90 TRACON issued multiple changes to the SKNRD1 RNAV arrival.
PC-12 flight crew reported hitting a turkey on departure; then returning to airport as a precaution.
GA pilot reported that on two different occasions; a loud noise on frequency in the ZLA sector resulted in a loss communications with ATC.
EMB-175 flight crew reported the ramp crew pushed the aircraft without clearance; towards conflicting taxiing aircraft.
Aircraft Maintenance Technicians reported that proper safety procedures were not followed by the contract vendor while working on aircraft.

A commercial UAS Operator was granted permission from local ATC to fly 3.5 miles from the Class C airport; but later questioned whether a Section 333 Letter of Agreement and Certificate of Authorization were legally required for his intended operation.
B737-800 Flight Attendant reported Raft #2 in overhead bin missing mooring line and activation pin exposed posing a hazard of accidental inflation in the passenger cabin.
Pilot reported an NMAC event during landing with a non reporting aircraft in traffic pattern.
ZDC ARTCC Controller reported an airspace violation; a low altitude alert; and communication problem with a local Tower.
A320 flight crew experienced a GPWS terrain warning descending to 2000 feet 5 NM south of TOLOC during a night visual approach to PDX.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with communication and possible health issues resulting from wearing a face mask for so long.
Maintenance Technician reported that aircraft are coming out of storage with circuit breakers and other systems in the incorrect setting/position.
Technician reported an aircraft was supposed to be deep cleaned after possible COVID-19 contamination; but when cleaners where questioned they had not been advised to deep clean the aircraft.
B737 flight crew reported severe turbulence in the initial stages of the approach for landing.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported they made four go-arounds due to windshear.
A B737NG crew on approach to ATL reported communication issues and confusion with ATC inquires.

ZMA Controller describes two fights that depart and are going towards Havana's airspace. Havana is ATC-Zero and cannot accept the aircraft. Both aircraft violate Havana's airspace and Havana tries to accept the aircraft via non-radar.
B767 First Officer in the cockpit jump seat reported an NMAC during climb.

JCF TRACON (Edwards Approach Control) Instructor and Developmental reported of issues with aircraft that are not squawking VFR (1200) when instructed to. Later in the shift when trying to identify other aircraft there is confusion because the aircraft tag up incorrectly.
The crew responded to an RA after departing OKC while trying to check in with Kansas City Center.
Agriculture aircraft pilot reported a near miss with a UAV approximately 10 miles southeast of LRJ.
CRJ-700 Captain reported an improper maintenance procedure caused a flight delay.
Ramp worker reported that a co-worker tested positive for COVID-19. The ramp worker was not notified by management and continued to work.
Widebody Captain reported encountering un-forecast windshear and microburst activity upon arrival at PHX. After 20 minutes of vectoring the crew elected to divert; landing with 10;800 LBS of fuel.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger's displeasure with other passengers not wearing masks.

Denver ARTCC Controller reported an unsafe situation with Denver TRACON having an aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitude and attempting to hand it off to him. Center Controller could not accept the aircraft until it was 3;000 feet higher; which ended up being in another Denver Center Controller's airspace.
Air carrier flight crew reported a deviation from the ramp taxi procedure and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Captain reported commencing approach too high due to TFR; resulted in unstabilized; misconfiguration to a go around followed by visual maneuver at night in high terrain.
A Tundra pilot failed to remove the engine cooling air intake plugs which caused an engine overheat and seizure. A safe off airport landing followed.
Technician reported not completing the correct paperwork for a ferry fight.
LAX Local Assist Controller reported of a traffic conflict with a go-around and HHR Airport traffic. During this event; Local Control forgot to issue a heading to a departure.
CRJ flight crew reported stowing the spoilers late on short final as well as communication issues between both pilots.
B767 Captain reported that prior to flight there was a lack of coordination between flight crews and maintenance in regards to APU deferrals.

LAX Tower Controller reported of a runway incursion involving a foreign carrier. Controller had cleared an aircraft for departure while landing foreign carrier was rolling out on parallel runway. Aircraft did not stop on taxiway between the runways. Controller advised aircraft to stop. Controller told departing aircraft to abort and cancel takeoff clearance.
HUF Tower Controller working arrival radar reported efforts to provide separation utilizing a D-Brite tower scope were limited and ineffective.
Embraer Captain reported encountering a drone at 7;500 feet in the vicinity of area.
Air carrier Captain reported a TCAS RA departing COS caused by a GA aircraft that had departed just before. COS Tower was controlling both aircraft initially.
PA-18 Cub pilot reported experiencing a ground loop during landing in gusty wind conditions.

Air carrier pilot reported being cleared to lineup and wait; then cleared for takeoff with an aircraft on final. ATC then ordered to crew to hold short but they stated the Hold Short line had been crossed by 10 feet. The traffic landed anyway.

B787 flight crew encountered a thunderstorm at FL210 during departure. Hail caused the L2 window to crack and apparently caused the Radar to fail. The crew elected to return to the departure airport for an overweight landing.
CRJ-900 Captain reported refusing DG cargo because weight exceeded maximum weight allowed per FOM.
DAB Controller describes a possible situation due to the Tower TDW [radar display] maps not showing the correct position of intersections on the map.
Center Controller reported that an aircraft descended below the minimum IFR altitude in a non-radar area; resulting in CFTT.
B737-8 Captain reported Approach Control procedure regarding tall vessels affecting landing aircraft needs to be updated to eliminate unnecessary multiple go-arounds and diversions.
Air carrier First Officer reported problems associated with required training and social distancing.
A319 Captain reported receiving an RA in climb from an A318 that had departed before them from a parallel runway at LAS.
B757 Flight Attendant reported difficulty in fighting an in-flight electrical fire with an expired fire extinguisher.
MD-80 Flight Attendant reported noticing moisture issues resulting from a company change to using regular ice instead of dry ice to cool beverages.
SBA Tower controllers reported miscommunication with pilot resulted in entering a higher MVA.
C-172 Single Pilot reported a go-around on landing due momentary loss of directional control . The right main landing gear struck a runway edge light in the process of the go-around.
P80 TRACON Controller reported they had to vector an aircraft off of their approach to avoid a merging aircraft target with no transponder.
Pilot reported passing under a parachutist on his landing roll at ASD airport and suggests relocating the drop zone to a safer area.
A GSP TRACON Controller reports of a deviation to the Opposite Direction Operation because the second aircraft was not within the standards for the operation to occur.
Stores shipping personnel mistakenly shipped a tool kit with HAZMAT contents as non-HAZMAT cargo.
ZTL Controller reported a weather re-route issue that put aircraft into a Level 6 thunderstorm with no assistance from Traffic Management.

A B737 pilot reported landing on LAS Runway 25L with taxi clearance taxiway B6 to B but after crossing 25R he entered taxiway A6 because his tablet's Jeppesen airport diagram chart incorrectly depicted A6 but no adjacent; yet separate B6 exit off the Runway 25R.
Two Hot Air Balloon fabric envelopes made contact during their approach to land because of significant terrain changes including the mix of road surfaces; open field; water; and trees.
Air carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation after accidentally pressing the heading select switch.
B737 flight crew reported diverting to a nearby alternate when the weather at destination started to cause missed approaches. The forecast at departure did not require an alternate.
Air carrier reporter stated there are QRH errors in the performance table accompanying the B737NG Unreliable Airspeed Non-Normal procedure.
Flight Attendant reported social distancing concerns during boarding and deplaning the aircraft.
A-320 captain points out potential fatigue issues with certain flight pairings.
Technician reported that new seats are installed in the exit row on 737NG with Seat Back Tray Tables which could block egress path in violation of FARs.

A Quad City Challenger Rotax Engine wooden propeller departed the aircraft inflight. The aircraft landed safely on a nearby road. The wooden propeller has not been re-torqued after being flown several months earlier from a high humidity area to a dry climate.

Air carrier flight crew reported a runway incursion which resulted from a call sign misunderstanding. A contributing factor was that the Tower Controller was managing multiple frequencies and runways.
TRACON instructor reported an aircraft departing and not squawking; flying close to 100 miles through various different airspace at 12;000 feet.
SCT controller reported the new MVA requirements east of SNA do not allow controllers to get aircraft low enough for a stable approach.
Gate Agent reported jetbridge collision with an illegally parked van as the jetbridge approached the aircraft door.
E145 Captain reported encountering severe turbulence which caused the auto pilot to fail.
Pilot reported the Ipad Electronic Flight Bag became unusable shortly after takeoff.
Air carrier Captain reported that after landing in SKBO he noted differences between taxiway designations and their charting.
ERJ-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to CLT in trail of a B757 that resulted in an uncontrollable roll with subsequent system anomalies related to the unusual attitude.
EMB-145 Captain took issue with the VFR corridor below the Runway 4R approach path at JFK. The VFR traffic at 500 feet causes a TCAS RA with the EMB145 passing over at 900 feet.
HPN Tower Controller reported an opposite direction operation was conducted which was not in compliance with directives.
Heavy transport flight crew reported encountering unexpected convective weather resulting in a course deviation. Aircraft briefly entered the weather and sustained hail damage.

D01 TRACON controllers reported that an aircraft on final that was using visual separation on an adjoining aircraft went around due to a Resolution Alert. This made the scenario worse because the aircraft was heavy and flew over another aircraft.
C-560XL flight crew reported being incorrectly re-routed by ATC.
B737 Flight Attendant reported difficulties and challenges operating a flight with the PA and cabin-cockpit intercom inoperative.
An instructor pilot reported the student lost control of the aircraft during landing resulting in a prop strike and the aircraft running off the runway.
DH-8-400 Captain reported having the APU on while being deiced when it should have been off. The checklist layout is cited as contributing to the mistake.
Ground employee reported the employee bus carries more than 10 passengers and 6 feet social distancing separation is impossible.
IFR pilot reported switching frequencies early while on a visual approach from Approach Control to the Tower.
Maintenance Technician was given an assignment to incorporate two different test methods into one Computer Base System for an ILS panel.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.

B737 First Officer reported due maintenance issues and weather causing them to divert the flight was delayed from its planned destination 11 hours at which point passengers became disruptive and demanded to be let off the aircraft at the diversion airport.

A CRJ-900 First Officer had extended the spoilers around 3;000 feet to help his descent profile. The spoiler handle appeared fully down; but at 250 feet the SPOILER DEPLOYED caution alerted. The Captain found the spoiler handle not fully in the retracted detent.
BE36 pilot reported a NMAC with paraglider; ATC stated it is a common occurrence for the ORL area.

Pilot reports of an approach into LAX where SCT Approach wanted them to do a visual approach. The weather wasn't good enough for them to do so due to a cloud layer and inability to see traffic to follow. Crew was sent around and then sequenced again for a visual approach which they told the Controller that they couldn't accept a visual because of the weather.

ZNY Controller stated sectors should have been split due to high workload due to weather deviations. Controller stated Front Line Manager ignored requests to split sectors and inferred the Front Line Manager should have assisted the Controller but did not due to lack of proficiency.
Air carrier Captain stated two very bright billboards prior to the Approach Light System inbound to LAX are a distraction and a hazard to landing aircraft.
A321 pilot reported encountering wake vortices as he was taking off full length on JFK Runway 31L from an A380 that had just landed on that runway.

BE40 crew reported moderate turbulence encounter that caused dutch roll action and loss of control of aircraft. Flight crew was able to descend to a lower altitude out of turbulence/windshear and regain control of aircraft.
B737-800 Captain reported a ground conflict with an Airbus which had been instructed to give way.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving a GPWS terrain alert on arrival into BOI; followed by recognition of an unstabilized approach that resulted in a go-around.
Flight crew reported GPWS warning on approach to landing and elected to continue.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported omitting a step during takeoff; requiring an air turn back.
B787 flight crew reported being told by Tower to expedite their exit off the runway well into their landing roll. Aircraft on final behind them was issued a go-around.
Boeing 777 Captain reported dangerous goods on board were not properly documented for the flight.
Inspector reported not following established procedures while borescoping an engine and failing to enter maintenance items in the electronic logbook.
A Maintenance Inspector working on a Cessna Citation reported that the Owner/Operator failed to properly document completion of tasks previously accomplished.
ZMP Center Controller reported that another Controller does not follow procedures; is lazy; and incompetent.
DC-3 flight crew reported a runway excursion resulted from loss of directional control on the takeoff roll.

B737 flight crew reported confusion about the location of DAL Taxiway P on the ramp because the airfield chart does not sufficiently describe a large aircraft parking area north of Taxiway P which can be taxied into.
Air carrier First Officer reported ZMA ARTCC assigned routing in violation of FARs even after repeatedly asking ATC if the route was legal.
Air carrier Captain reported a Jeppesen 10-9 Chart for ELP was incorrect.
A Flight Instructor observing from the ground and their student reported the student turned base leg and followed the wrong aircraft causing a subsequent aircraft to take evasive action.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported the first aid equipment was defective when needed for an ill passenger.

ZNY ARTCC Controller reported an ATC Computer assigned a beacon code to an aircraft 200 miles from the Controller's airspace. The inconsistency was only realized after the Controller issued control instructions to the aircraft and none were complied with. Eventually the error was caught shortly before the aircraft transitioned out of the sector.
B737-800 First Officer reported while at V1 speed encountered a bird strike so they aborted the takeoff.
ERJ-170 pilot identifies discrepancies between Jeppesen and LIDO charts at MDW.
B737 First Officer reported confusion about clearance to either climb via or climb unrestricted resulted in a clearance deviation.

Air carrier flight crew reported flying an incorrect departure transition that had a similar sounding name as the clearance transition. The crew stated that lack of recent flying was a contributing factor.

On short final; an MD-11 was impacted by a bird; which sheared a hydraulic line in the nose gear assembly. This resulted in a loss of the Number 3 Hydraulic System and ability to taxi. The crew requested and received a tow to parking.

A Finals sector at Atlanta Approach became extremely busy. Due to weather deviations this sector was being fed more aircraft than planned for by the Traffic Management Program and controllers. The sector was receiving aircraft at high speeds and insufficiently sequenced. The controller advised the supervisor of the situation. The controller was to be relieved from the sector but felt they were too busy to take the time to give a new controller a briefing. The controller felt the supervisor was not responding to his requests for spacing from the sectors feeding him. The controller advised the supervisor he would not accept any more aircraft handoffs to his sector. Another controller was sent to relieve him but he felt too busy to give a complete briefing. The relieving controller assumed the position after observing for a while.

An Air Carrier Captain expressed specific concerns about the environment surrounding the new Runway 10R/28L at FLL which is significantly elevated in order to allow it to run over a freeway system at the east end. Of concern were insufficient lighting designating steep drop offs; mis-located turnoff signs; the failure to use red lighting to highlight threat areas; and the use of reflectors rather than red lighting as the reflectors are not illuminated by landing lights in a timely manner.

A B737 Captain reported his experience after a runway change during thunderstorm conditions while on the IAH NNCEE1 STAR. I90 clearance revisions and the resultant FMC entry work created an extreme workload environment near the airport in weather below 10;000 feet.

An aircraft was receiving VFR Services from Seattle Center (ARTCC). The ARTCC terminated service with the aircraft and advised them to contact Portland TRACON overlying the aircraft's route. The TRACON advised the aircraft that they had transitioned through UAO's Class D airspace. Even though this designated airspace is not yet published the pilot was aware of it. The pilot believed since he was receiving ATC service from the ARTCC he was allowed to transit this airport's Class D airspace.
Cessna 182T pilot reported a propstrike on the runway after landing during a windshift.
Cleveland Center Controller reported an airspace violation due to working a non-RVSM compliant aircraft and neglecting to properly hand-off to the next sector.
Helicopter pilot reported loss of GPS signal near CRQ.
Ground employee reported concern over his employer's Coronavirus related procedures.

An air carrier flight crew reported having to brake abruptly while taxiing; due to a ground vehicle which was not giving way to their aircraft. There was no contact with the vehicle; however a flight attendant was slightly injured due to the sudden braking.
Purser reported multiple issues and difficulties during the boarding process and cited not having enough flight attendants to properly staff the flight as a contributing factor.
EMB-145 First Officer reported Hazmat cargo labeled not suitable for air transport in cargo compartment. Package removed prior to departure.
C172 pilot reported that during a go-around the aircraft's poor climb performance resulted in close proximity to trees and houses.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported being assigned the MUSCL3 Arrival Runway 30R transition at MSP. Later he was made aware that a loss of separation occurred while turning final. The Controller explained he was expected to maintain heading after the last point on the arrival since he had not been cleared for the ILS.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering unexpected severe turbulence in cruise causing an overspeed warning alert.
LGA Tower Controller reported of an unsafe situation due to approach not separating two arrivals better.

The Captain of a Bombardier Global 5000 reported that the oil in the engines were in need of service and the tires appeared to have low pressure. This aircraft just had an overnight check accomplished.

Flight instructor reported a confusing note on the RNAV (GPS) RWY 16 approach at DMW that informed the decision to make a procedure turn; however ATC subsequently advised the procedure turn was not authorized.
First Officer reported two EICAS messages on final; causing a go around to allow time to run the QRH; landing safely on next approach.
Flight Attendant reported a mother leaving a baby in the car seat and sitting in the row behind the infant.  Mother refused to wear a face mask and return to row where infant was.
G500 First Officer reported an air return after odors were detected after advancing throttles for takeoff.
Air carrier flight crew reported executing a go-around due to a ground proximity warning.
A Cessna Citation Captain reported a loss of GPS signal and satellite coverage along V139 over W-107B at 17;000 feet. GPS function returned a short time later.
Air carrier Captain reported taxi confusion due to conflicting information between the airport chart; ATC's instructions and safety information received from the company Ramp Control.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported experiencing physical allergic symptoms when wearing the new company uniforms.
An Approach Controller reported an aircraft did not comply with their vector in a timely manner and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
MD80 flight crew reported airspeed indication anomaly in IMC that resulted in an altitude excursion.

Pilot reports of confusion after receiving his clearance from VNY ATCT. He is given a SID and also told to comply with the Obstacle Departure Procedure. Pilot departs and controller directs his flight to another direction away from the Burbank final.
ZAN Center Controller reported an aircraft entered a non-radar sector on a route that did not ensure separation from other traffic.

F2TH Captain reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of B737 while flying the LUCKI ONE STAR to SAN; and that it is plausible that STAR procedures could play a role in increasing these incidents.
SFO Tower Controller and a Supervisor reported the Local Controller had to issue a go-around to an aircraft on short final due to departing traffic.
Due to incorrectly applying the MEL for the 'AutoSpoilers' the crew incurred an 'AutoBrakes' aural warning during takeoff roll leading to the decision to reject the takeoff.
Flight crew reported receiving an obstacle alert on a night visual approach to OKC.
C185 pilot reported inadvertently entering a TFR that he thought had been terminated.
A300 Captain reported Hazmat was loaded prior to departure without crew notification.
B737 Captain reported taking evasive action after receiving an RA from a light aircraft on descent into EWR.
Air Traffic Controller reported a departure on a SID that did not make contact in a timely manner and flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
GSO TRACON Controller reported the radar scope went blank for a few seconds.
Pilot reported receiving amended clearance inflight repeatedly.
Pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter while attempting to land.
Flight Attendant reported having a disagreement with the Captain and First Officer regarding their compliance with wearing face masks.
B787 Mechanic reported unknown dried substance residue throughout the cabin. Entered in aircraft logbook and reported to Supervisor.

ZTL Controller reported a situation while using TBFM [Time Based Flow Management] into Atlanta when he observed a shift in times that made his job more difficult. Controller wants TBFM stopped until everyone at ZTL understands how it supposedly works.

A military flight of aircraft requested clearance into a MOA. The ZKC Controller had none of the required approval information to allow the aircraft to enter. The Controller's Operations Manager told the Controller it was approved to clear the aircraft in to the MOA. The Operations Manager told the Controller they should take time to look at the sectional chart displayed above his radar display and read the NOTAM times the MOA is active even though this action would take the controllers attention away from their radar display. Upon reviewing the incident it was discovered the Military office responsible for coordinating the MOA use had forgot to schedule the airspace for the aircraft.
Air carrier First Officer reported that the first four rows of the aircraft were to be left unoccupied for weight and balance reasons; but found out when airborne some of the seats were occupied.
A321 Captain reported finding an oxygen pressure drop while checking the crew O2 masks during preflight that was caused by a partially open shutoff valve on the crew oxygen cylinder.

A80 Controller reported the design of the WINNG 1 Optimized Profile Descent (OPD) Arrival does not allow arriving aircraft to comply with crossing restrictions without violating adjacent airspace. Reporter recommended eliminating the OPD arrivals.
A Ramp Controller reported an aircraft pushed back without contacting them at the same time there was an aircraft in the ramp waiting for parking.

ZLA Controller hands off an aircraft to another sector. The handoff; because of a Temporary Altitude in the scratch pad area; goes to another sector. The handoff should have went to the sector that the aircraft was 'temped' at; not one that is possibly a future scope.
A Flight Attendant reported being thrown around the galley while encountering severe turbulence.

An ERJ-175 Captain reported dry ice loaded in the aft cargo bin; a hazmat violation in this aircraft type. Additionally; ground personnel began the pushback without crew clearance after brake release. Ground personnel training appears to be a problem.
A319 Captain reported concern regarding low amount of fuel on board per flight plan.
Pilot reported a NMAC with drone at 9500 ft.

A B777 Captain described one part of his company's FAR 117 international ETOP operations compliance shortens preflight time to less than 60 minutes which is unsafe and inadequate for security; crew briefing; flight planning; and aircraft preflight.

MHT Local control reported of attempting to clear the runway of vehicles for a departure. Controller though he cleared the runway and departed the aircraft. Minutes later vehicles were observed on a runway. Controller found out the vehicle he thought was in charge; was not. There was another vehicle that was in charge they found out.
Beechcraft Travelair pilot reported the use of a voltage converter damaged handheld supplemental electronic equipment.
A320 Captain reported receiving an Resolution Advisory (RA) shortly after takeoff from Runway 1L in SFO; presumably from another aircraft that took off from Runway 1R.
PAO Tower Controllers and a pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern. The GPS Runway 31 approach was cited as a contributing factor.
B777 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence several times until descending to smoother conditions.
Flight crew reported ATC sequencing error on approach.
Pilot reported flying aircraft with an overdue inspection.

TRACON could not perform an automated handoff of a departing aircraft to the ARTCC. The reason was a flight plan with the same call sign as the departure was an active flight already in the ARTCC system. Unbeknownst to the ARTCC Controller the ARTCC active flight plan was an arrival to the same airport as the departure which would not handoff. The TRACON Controller mistakenly told the ARTCC Controller the ARTCC active flight plan had already landed. The ARTCC Controller then removed the flight plan from the ARTCC system. This caused the ARTCC sector working the active arrival to lose data on this aircraft.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported flying the aircraft with the gear pinned down and arriving with less fuel than planned; noting that the burn rate was higher than planned for.
First Officer reported concerns over crew oxygen pressure dropping below limits and then MEL compliance.
B737 Flight Attendant reported the Captain dismissed a request to remove a verbally abusive passenger.

Air carrier Captain reported being given direct routings in mountainous terrain and repeatedly having to explain to ATC controllers that he wanted to remain on his filed route due to drift down considerations.
Dispatcher reported aircraft was delayed without contact to dispatch.
Technician reported errors and omissions in Work Cards relating to portable fire extinguishers on company aircraft being returned to service after storage.

Air carrier flight crew reported that while taxiing to DAL Runway 13R at night and following another aircraft; they failed to hold short at the Runway 13R displaced threshold line. FO stated they did not expect the Hold Short line to be that far back from the Runway entry.
Air carrier flight crew had a challenging approach into difficult airport with malfunctioning flight director and thunderstorm conditions.

An air taxi jet crew leveled at FL240 on the BACHR1 RNAV to DAL and when cleared VIA before ANGST; the lowest STAR MCP altitude was selected. The aircraft began a descent before the crew realized the error but they recovered to FL240.
Tower Controller reported an airborne conflict and cited fatigue from being understaffed as contributing factor.
B737-900 Captain reported a wake turbulence encounter on arrival into LAX that resulted in 'limited aircraft control'.

A Helicopter Pilot reports of being issued a clearance for departure and not being given a frequency change or told to contact Tower. When the pilot departed and was airborne they were chastised for not talking to Local Control before departing.

The Tower Controller in Charge observed a truck entering a taxiway. The Ground Controller did not see the truck. The CIC reports this portion of the airport is blocked from the view of the Local and Ground controllers.

Embraer Legacy 550 Captain reported that his company operations attempted to alter his crew's off-duty time in order to meet flight schedule requirements. The Flight Operations Manual justification that the company was using was found to be in error.
EMB-145 First Officer reported company pressure to operate with lack of proficiency and comfort from the right seat.
A Flight Attendant reported that during some light turbulence the aircraft suddenly dropped injuring a Flight Attendant.
Observer reported helicopter flying low on multiple occasions.

A Dispatcher reported an aircraft departed NTD with incorrect ZFW numbers from CLP.  The aircraft was 19;700 lbs. heavier than what CLP was told by 'ride along.'  The air crew were given incorrect numbers prior to takeoff.
MD-11 Captain reported Dispatch informed crew that Fire Suppression System was not reinstalled correctly; resulting in a diversion for correct re-installation to be accomplished.
Captain reported receiving a dispatch message during cruise of unreported dangerous goods onboard.
ERJ-175 Captain reported correcting erroneous preflight cargo weights uplinked to flight crew during preflight in order to depart with accurate takeoff data.
CE560 First Officer reported while taxiing onto the runway; another aircraft flew over them and landed on the same runway.
Pilot reported a loss of control and downward spiral in weather conditions until safely recovering the aircraft.
A crew experienced severe windshear on final approach.
C172 pilot reported having transponder issues; then additional equipment problems having to land to determine what was wrong with the aircraft.
C-172 pilot reported that while attempting to get into VFR conditions he climbed through clouds without an IFR rating or an IFR flight plan.
An aircraft conducted a Go Around due to wind shear. The aircraft climbed above the missed approach altitude and turned to a different heading than was assigned by ATC.
Air carrier Captain reported losing control of the aircraft while taxing on an icy ramp.
An air taxi flight crew reported an issue with the ILS Runway 6L signal at CLE that caused an unexpected pitch up and down with the autopilot engaged.
Technician reported that the residue film left from fogging the aircraft for COVID-19 may affect temperature sensors.
A320 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after experiencing multiple bird strikes in both engines.

An air carrier First Officer reported that an Air Traffic Controller had changed the line-up-and-wait clearance after they crossed the hold short line but not yet on the runway. The pilot observed several aircraft landing on the runway while they were in the assigned position.
B787 Captain reported two Final Dangerous Goods summaries with different information formats provided to Captain.
A330 First Officer reported they started to taxi prior to the ground personnel giving a salute.
A Dispatcher for an Embraer EMB-175 reported that while pre-planning a flight the Dispatcher misread the weather report and dispatch the aircraft with too little fuel onboard.
Flight Attendants are finding heavy duty seals on the Portable Breathing Equipment (PBE) that has to be cut off in order to use the equipment.
Air carrier Captain reported being distracted by a Flight Attendant call and the autopilot disconnected during flap extension.
ZLC ARTCC Controller reported their facility has implemented rules in addition to the Federal Aviation Administration Handbook (FAAH) 7110.65 regarding issuing IFR clearances to departing aircraft.
B767-300 flight crew reported executing a go-around after receiving a low altitude alert from the Tower during the Stadium Visual to EWR Runway 29.
Air carrier First Officer reported receiving multiple approach clearances to different runways.

At this airport aircraft on approach to parallel runways must be separated by 1000 feet vertically until established at a certain point on the approach. The controller believed the aircraft were established on the approach and discontinued vertical separation clearing the aircraft for approach. Upon a later review using more precise displays it was determined one of the aircraft were not established within the confines of the approach. The controllers working the aircraft do not have the detailed precise radar display that is used afterwards to analyze their performance.

Air carrier Captain reported that due to weight and balance needs; the Operations Agent directed passengers on a nearly empty flight to sit close together; violating social distancing requirements. The Captain suggested that weight and balance be reworked to allow appropriate spacing between passengers.
Air carrier maintenance technicians reported that the rudder was inadvertently damaged while preparing to perform rudder lube on an aircraft that was being returned to service from storage.
A ZLC Center Controller reported they received a non-radar aircraft from another sector that was below the Minimum En-route Altitude.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence 9 miles in trail of a heavy jet on arrival into SEA.

2 ARTCC Controllers from different Centers' reported a situation where an aircraft checked on frequency on an unapproved altitude for their direction of flight.  In addition the ZDC Controller reported the handoff was not being taken by ZJX due to an improper route which was later amended by the TMUs of both ZDC and ZJX.
CRJ Captain reported issues getting cabin cooled before boarding. A Flight Attendant suggested they boarded too early.
A319 Captain reported the new photoluminescent escape path lighting system had discharged overnight because the cabin lights were dimmed during the down time.
LIT TRACON Controller had a loss of separation after LRF Tower allowed an aircraft on approach to break out and reverse course into the path of a subsequent arrival being worked by the TRACON.
Air carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation citing distraction and not working for several months as contributing factors.
The pilot of a M20J reported a loss of control due to severe turbulence that resulted in a descent prior to regaining control.
CRJ-900 Captain reported ordering evacuation of the aircraft due to a ground equipment fire.
ZDV Center Controller reported receiving a NORDO aircraft that wasn't in radar coverage below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Local Tower Controller and Radar Controller reported a conflict between a go-around aircraft and a slower moving departure.
The Tower Controller reported that rescue/fire vehicles did not follow proper communication procedures during an emergency.
Pilot reported cleared for a visual approach; while Approach Control advised pilot they were not cleared for the approach.

Boeing 737 Captain reported being unhappy with the alternate airport on the flight plan; so he attempted to contact the Dispatcher to change it. He couldn't get ahold of him on the SatPhone because he said it's sub-par equipment on their aircraft.
A320 Captain reported incorrect HAZMAT battery procedures applied during preflight.
First Officer reported LNAV was not engaged during departure resulting in a track heading deviation.
SEA Local Controller reported an aircraft enter their Class B airspace on a conflicting course with an air carrier arrival.

An AZO Tower Controller tells an aircraft to maintain VFR on the go-around due to a gear problem. The Controller is confused after the aircraft lands in that he told the aircraft to maintain VFR and cancelled his IFR clearance.
Ground personnel reported that due to hand held radios being unuseable; normal work assignments were not being accomplished.
Flight Attendant reported that when flight attendants are in the flight deck; pilots do not wear face masks.
An Airbus aircraft climbing out at much slower speeds than other similar aircraft could cause a loss of required separation.

A flight plan for an active enroute flight was removed from the ATC system. The aircraft's 'full' data block dropped off the Controller's scope and only a partial data block remained on the radar display. The controllers did not notice for approximately 50 miles and did not realize an aircraft was flying through their airspace.
Air carrier First officer reported an unstablized approach and an altitude deviation during a go-around.  First Officer cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Flight Attendant reported another Flight Attendant locked the bathroom door and put an out of order sign on it; so he would not have to get close to people.

Northern California TRACON (NCT) Controllers report of a loss of separation due to him not having an aircraft maintain an altitude until crossing/lower traffic had passed. The controller thought he told aircraft to maintain specific altitude but did not. The other controller failed to use the new procedure and vector the aircraft past a fix to avoid the inbound aircraft.

Pilot reports of Center telling them to stay with him; because of traffic in close proximity. Center switches aircraft over to Tower and Tower complains that they had violated the Class Delta airspace and should have called them sooner.
B737-800 flight crew reported encountering light to moderate turbulence in their climb to FL380 that resulted in an uncommanded descent.
SCT Controller reported a Parachute jump zone has been established which is in conflict with RNAV Arrival routes and busy VFR transition traffic.
Captain discovered that FMC's are designed to sometimes auto load a runway with a particular RNAV departure.
ZLA Center Controller working with new OAPM arrival procedures received an aircraft that did not have the new procedures available in their navigation database.

An Air Carrier Captain describes a series of flights over time in which similar sounding company call signs created the potential for ATC and pilot communication errors. No apparent conflict mitigation was evident.
An EMB-145 ferry flight with ballast fuel diverted because of poor destination weather after holding and learned ballast fuel cannot be burned as holding fuel.

ZAU Controller describes handing off an aircraft but the receiving facility doesn't take it. He then calls the facility and the other facility tells the Controller the aircraft is in another sector's airspace without a handoff.
Ground personnel reported installation of permanent posts inside the Operational Safety Zone at aircraft gate.
Air Carrier flight crew reported wing tip contact with parked aircraft due to improper ground crew marshalling.
A B737 Flight Attendant reported that slide safety covers were not installed properly. The covers are being packed inside the bustle.
B737 pilot reported the aircraft was unable to meet published crossing restrictions.
Flight Attendant reported feeling hot; sweaty and nearly fainting while having to wear a COVID-19 mask during flight.
A pilot reported there is no way to receive the current weather for 3B1 non-towered airport while airborne.

Air carrier Captain reported checking in and hearing the Controller tell everybody to stand by.  They continued on assigned heading and altitude; finally being told they were on the wrong frequency. Controller reportedly was working multiple frequencies.
Atlanta Center Controller reported an unsafe situation relating to traffic; weather; and Minimum IFR Altitude.
SCT TRACON Controller reported receiving handoffs from an adjacent TRACON of two aircraft on conflicting routes.
Embraer jet First Officer reported executing precautionary evasive maneuver from an airborne conflict.

Air carrier Captain reported experiencing jamming activity which resulted in loss of GPS position and multiple other system failures. Shortly after the Captain reported the issue to ATC; the jamming stopped and systems returned to normal.
Air carrier Captain reported bleed-through from a nearby radio station into the headset of ground crew blocked some communications during pushback at MIA airport.
CRJ-700 Flight Attendant reported the passenger entry door handrail came forward more than it should not allowing the door to close using the normal procedures due to a missing retaining clip.
EA-500 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence from a preceding Airbus on arrival into CLT.
CRP Controller reports of the discontinuity of the STARS at his facility and the STARS at NGP. The interface between the two systems does not allow handoffs; which must be made manually.

CNO Local Controller reported an aircraft departing on downwind turned into traffic on the ILS final approach course to avoid ONT Class C. Based on local agreements the airspace belongs to CNO Tower during its hours of operation; but this is not indicated on the VFR charts.

A pilot of a light aircraft reported icing conditions where the application of carburetor heat was ineffectual; resulting in degraded engine performance. He requested a lower altitude and the icing threat dissipated and engine operation returned to normal.

Air carrier Captain reported Denver Approach issued two changes to their cleared arrival procedure and expected landing runway. Initial clearance was for the BOSSS TWO RNAV Arrival; landing 35R. That was changed to the QUAIL SEVEN Arrival; and then changed back to the BOSSS TWO RNAV Arrival; landing runway 35L. While reprogramming their navigation computer for that latest clearance; the crew did not program the entire approach transition and as a result failed to fly the arrival/approach transition as cleared. ATC noticed the aircraft track error and issued a vector to intercept the final approach course.

ZDV Center Controller reported an aircraft that had deviated from their assigned altitude and descended towards another aircraft that was in a priority handling condition resulting in a loss of separation.
ZJX Controller describes a situation where aircraft were in or in close proximity to a warning area and the lack of coordination leading up to the airspace becoming active.
B737 Captain reported that a brake assembly was improperly installed.
When accessing the Fan Air Valve; Maintenance was unable to secure the Fan Reverser in the open position per the Maintenance Manual because the proper tooling was unavailable.

Air carrier Captain reported an altitude deviation during approach to CLT while on the JONZE 2 RNAV Arrival.  Captain stated the CDA arrivals to CLT are allegedly problematic and should be eliminated or a 'smoother process' should be implemented.
Air carrier flight crew reported finding evidence of bird strike on walk around and difficulties finding procedure limitations for their destination airport.

CRJ-200 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence from preceding A320 on takeoff from DTW that resulted in a 'sharp' roll at about 500 feet to 700 feet AGL. Reporter was critical of ATC protocols in managing departures.
Single pilot reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with another aircraft.
ZAU Center controllers reported an A330 encountered a jolt of moderate to severe turbulence that injured a Flight Attendant.

RV-4 pilot reported that while climbing out of a Common Traffic Advisory Frequency (CTAF) airport at about 3;900 feet; another aircraft on decent for the same airport came within 50 feet of his aircraft.
A pilot over San Francisco reported a commercial radio broadcast loud enough to block NCT and SFO Tower necessitating the flight's exit from Class B airspace.

ZLC Controller reports of working a one man mid shift and not being able to answer an aircraft that called on a frequency he didn't have at the position he was working. The frequency was at another position; but since he was working two positions combined already; he couldn't answer the aircraft. An adjacent approach called via the landline and he gave them the correct frequency.
First Officer reported that during pushback ground personnel used nonstandard commands and phraseology. The reporter felt this issue was due primarily to the use of outsourced ground staff.
Air carrier flight crew reported executing a go-around after seeing there was a vehicle on the runway. The Tower was closed at time of the event and the crew was on the wrong frequency.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger moved into the exit row after the briefing.  Passenger was asked to move back to original seat.
PA28 pilot reported that while on final a Cessna 172 turned base to final below him.
TRACON Departure Controller reported becoming overloaded when a departure deviated from course and allowed another aircraft to fly below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air Carrier First Officer reports multiple TCAS RAs during descent to airport.
Air carrier ramp employee expressed concern about the safety of reconfigured belt loaders.
Cargo personnel reported Dangerous Goods shipment missing shipper's declaration on master airway bill.
Pilot reported a few days after a flight he realized that they did not have the correct passenger close out.

Commercial Airline Lead Ramp Agent reported closing out cargo departure report for colleague due to pending aircraft departure. Lead Ramp Agent further reported frequent occurrence of Supervisor directing ramp agents close out Load Reports due to pending aircraft departure.
Air carrier ground personnel reported HAZMAT item was erroneously shipped even though item was not meant to be transported.
B737NG Captain reported encountering moderate wake turbulence departing LAX in trail of an A320.
A Cessna 172 pilot reported that due to severe weather conditions he inadvertently flew into a TFR.
TRACON Controller reported the Supervisor suspected them of turning an aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
Air carrier flight crew reported misinterpreting a hand signal from the ground crew resulting in the aircraft moving unexpectedly before re-applying the brakes.
Light aircraft pilot reported executing a go-around at non-towered BDN airport after another light aircraft taxied onto the runway and took off when the reporter was on short final.

After lift-off; float plane pilot reported encountering converging traffic that was on an instrument approach to a nearby airport. Pilot met with his home airport manager in order to develop procedures for ensuring future separation of traffic in similar situations.
Flight Attendant reported survival kit lanyard was incorrectly installed; causing confusion over its location.
B737 First Officer reported a potential FOD problem due to debris often found in the gate area at EWR airport.
Air carrier First Officer reported momentarily overspeeding the flaps while configuring for approach.

Dispatcher experiences a loss of ACARS communication with one of his flights while inbound to MMMX during a weather event. Attempts at voice communication through Commercial Radio are also unsuccessful. The communication outage lasts for over one hour; until the aircraft lands safely in MMMX.
A pilot reported their takeoff clearance was blocked by frequency congestion causing a delay and canceled takeoff clearance.

CRJ-200 Captain reported that the First Officer left the spoilers extended during approach and both crew members became distracted after the glideslope failed to capture. While troubleshooting the glideslope issue; the stall warning activated when flaps 45 were selected.

An Observer near Texas Tech University Medical Center (TA79) reported a new crane 120 ft high is lighted but its boom; over 100 ft long horizontally; is not lighted and presents a hazard for helicopters taking off and landing at the adjacent helipad.
C560XL flight crew reported encountering severe wake turbulence on the arrival into LAX in trail of a B757 that resulted in a 60 degree bank.

Cleveland Center Controllers reported a loss of separation when the Radar Controller climbed an aircraft through the altitude of another aircraft. Radar Controller gave instructions to climbing pilot to be level in two minutes. Pilot did not comply and vectoring both aircraft did not help the separation loss.
MIA TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict and a request to change the Miami Class Bravo.
Air carrier Captain reported noticing a helicopter hovering about half way down the runway they were landing on. That could have posed a safety hazard had a go-around been necessary.

An ETOPS B737 required an APU inflight verification which the crew thought included running the APU; shutting it down to colds soak; then starting it for landing. After landing; Maintenance said no inflight shutdowns were allowed.
A general aviation pilot reported symptoms of hypoxia due to smoke from widespread fires in Northern California led to a diversion.
Maintenance Controller reported two deferrals for the MD-11 fuel system were not compatible and the aircraft was removed from service to correct the problem.
A300 First Officer reported encountering severe to extreme turbulence at FL340.
Small Transport pilot reported a TCAS warning on initial approach.

Cessna 172 pilot is under flight following and never gets handed off to the next facility due to traffic and frequency congestion. Pilot fly's through intended airspace and attempts contact with ATC. Pilot lands at destination and describes how he should have been handled.

A Miami Center (ZMA) Controller reports of having to spin an aircraft because the Jacksonville Center (ZJX) Controller would not take the handoff. When the aircraft was in its turn; the ZJX Controller took the handoff. The ZMA Controller reports this type of thing happens often.
ZMP Controller reported of a Sector in Denver Center that for 25 years has been a problem. The sector delivers tied; conflicting traffic at another Center Sector's boundary.
GA pilot reported while downwind; they almost had a NMAC while trying to avoid an unknown aircraft.

A regional airline Captain writes to suggest that Company policy be changed to require that one engine be started thirty minutes prior to aircraft boarding at stations which do not provide air carts for aircraft cooling during the hot summer months.
Lancair pilot reported discontinuing an approach after becoming disoriented.

An I90 TRACON Front Line Manager observed a Controller using visual separation with a large category aircraft behind an A380. The Manager advised the Controller visual separation rules are not permitted following an A380. The required seven miles separation was lost and the Controller broke the second aircraft out for re-sequencing.

MSY Ground Controller reported of having a separation issue with a taxing aircraft and a departing aircraft. Taxing aircraft was on a runway owned by Ground Control; but the aircraft crossed the extended departure path of a crossing runway. Controller stated the safety event occurred due to a position overload and to complexity and traffic saturation.
S56 TRACON Departure Control trainee reported a loss of separation between two aircraft departing SLC airport.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported diverting for IDG and APU problems. They discovered that a month earlier another crew had a similar experience with the exact same outcome.
B767 First Officer reported HAZMAT container content labels were obscured due to improper loading. DG Agent reloaded container to permit proper preflight inspection.
Phenom 300 Captain reported denying an instruction from ATC to speed up beyond the 200 kts restriction while below Bravo airspace.
Air carrier Captain reported entering 'KLINE' into the FMS instead of the correct spelling 'KLYNE.' The two fixes are in close proximity and the error was not initially apparent.

D01 TRACON had to evacuate their facility and declared 'ATC Zero' due to an emergency situation. There are procedures in place in which the ARTCC takes over the airspace of the TRACON. The ARTCC personnel did not know how to implement the procedure for this situation.
ZOA Center controller reported that a sectors' workload has become unmanageable resulting in delays and aircraft conflicts due to the variety of traffic situations and equipment limitations.
B737 flight crew reported a TCAS RA with a small aircraft while on approach to EWR.
EMB-505 flight crew reported confusion with ATC instructions.
C172 instructor pilot reported an NMAC event during final approach with a non-reporting departing helicopter traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported a UAV flew 300 feet above their aircraft as it was beginning an instrument approach in VFR conditions.
Technician reported concerns over non compliance during repairs and rework to the rudder during a scheduled C Check; including RII steps not accomplished.
UAS operator reported a conflict with a taxiing aircraft just prior to launch from an airport taxiway. The operation was published informing all users of the airport of the planned UAS operation.
LGB Controller describes an operational error because a Local Controller did not send the slower aircraft around until it was already past the threshold.
F11 TRACON Controller reported a conflict after an aircraft came over at the incorrect altitude.
General aviation pilot reported two small dogs on the runway on landing rollout. Pilot took evasive action with excess braking to avoid striking one of them.
A pilot reported his B737-700 had 11 complex MELs with a 27 page print of MEL procedures needing to be checked or procedures needing to be completed prior to flight.

B777 First Officer Relief Pilot reported the aircraft was operated for 'quite some time' with an FMS Navigation database installed that was for another aircraft type. Maintenance Technician responsible for the data load suggested improvements to prevent a future occurrence.
Maintenance personnel reported Hazmat violation regarding oil storage drum.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported a momentary stick shaker due to turbulence descending from FL360 to FL340.
Captain reported a passenger was not complying with face mask policy and was eventually removed from the flight.
B737 Captain reported new company procedure calling for no APU start on taxi in are causing some safety concerns.
B737 Captain reported that maintenance did not comply with MEL procedures resulting in fuel tank quantity gauge reading errors.
G-IV Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL340 from another aircraft about 10 miles ahead of them.
Air Carrier Captain reported encountering wind shear on final approach and concern regarding fuel remaining.
