Air carrier First Officer reported that aircrews are not being advised of multiple taxiway closures at IAH and the NOTAMs provided are incomplete.
NCT TRACON Controller reported about confusion; an airspace violation and constant problems associated with an airport where one sector works the arrival and another sector works the departure.
Air carrier Captain reported departing without correct paperwork and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Lead Maintenance Technician reported that a main rotor mast and transmission were improperly indexed during installation and required corrective maintenance action.
LR31 flight crew reported that an altitude overshoot on descent was due to the First Officer using improper trim procedure.

A Controller reported an aircraft that lined up for the wrong runway due to lighting of the runway. The Controller reported the lights were on step one and he would have had them on step three and also stated the Front Line Managers (FLMs) are having issues with lighting at the airport.
CRJ-900 Mechanic reported avionics equipment was not properly reinstalled following previous maintenance action.

An Aircraft Maintenance Technician (AMT) describes his work environment where the lack of adequate Ground Power Units (GPUs) to supply electrical power to all their ERJ-170 aircraft requires the running of APU's all night in their hangar. As a result; due to the excessive noise levels; mechanics have chosen not to wear ear protection in order to communicate even though they face long term hearing loss.
A B737 Captain reported that procedures for flap torque brake activation should be reviewed.
B737 pilot reported similar sounding call sign on the same route could have caused confusion.

NCT TRACON Controller reported of an unsafe operation with a VFR photo mission aircraft and a departure off SJC. The VFR aircraft had the IFR departure in sight and the reporter thought the VFR would do a better job of staying away from the IFR departure. Controller took action to resolve the situation and turned the VFR aircraft.
A pilot reported the fueler began fueling the aircraft before the passengers deplaned or the jetbridge had approached the aircraft.

An air carrier operations agent reported a customer service agent was uncertain about proper steps and documentation for handling a lithium battery removed from a wheel chair but requested load planning document it regardless.
A medium transport jet crew on approach to MFR needed S turns and activated TAWS during the LOC BC DME-B Runway 32. They were surprised by the 5.98 glide path angle discovered after landing.
Technician reported that company policy had allowed ETOPS Pre Departure Checks to be signed off by non-ETOPS qualified personnel.
Maintenance Technician reported that a proper daily check would not be possible due to special equipment that would be needed but not available.

A Fractional pilot reported while on initial approach they received a destination change from Dispatch which did not include enough flight plan information so they landed at their original destination.
B737-800 flight crew reported ground personnel notified Captain of a passenger's bag that ignited; because of undeclared Hazmat in a suitcase.
Air Carrier Captain reported miscommunication with ATC which resulted in a missed approach.
Flight attendants reported dealing with a sick passenger during flight.
A321 flight crew reported returning to departure airport after encountering what appeared to be party balloons climbing through 8;000 ft. Remains of multiple balloons were found in the right engine.
B787 Mechanic reported flight crew identified aircraft repairs incorrectly documented in an aircraft logbook.

C182 pilot reported being aware of a potential baseball game TFR before departure; but could not get any specific information from the FSS. Enroute information was requested from PCT and BAL Tower about the TFR without receiving any conclusive answer.
GA pilot had a Class B airspace incursion after assuming they were cleared through the airspace by ATC.
A319 Captain reported inoperative APU and excessive temperatures; coupled with insufficient cooling systems caused unsafe operations.
CRJ-700 flight crew held east of a fix rather than west as directed by ATC due to the erroneous belief that they should hold on the inbound radial to the fix.
Ramp personnel reported incomplete HAZMAT documentation during unloading of HAZMAT cargo.
Air carrier flight crew reported ATC gave them a low altitude alert and his aircraft's EGPWS alerted after they descended to 3;000 ft prior to WEXIN on visual approach to Runway 22 at AVP.
P50 TRACON Controller reported that during a high volume of traffic they did not notice an airborne conflict after an aircraft descend out of Class B airspace and into Class D airspace.
B737 Captain reported that unfamiliarity with a new aircraft caused them to inadvertently disconnect the autothrottle during a go-around.
Maintenance technician reported that careless placement of wheel chocks on the airport ramp created a safety hazard.
TRACON Controller reported a small aircraft at 8;000 feet entered a higher Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
CRJ-900 flight crew reported encountering convective activity with an altitude gain of 300 feet at level off and communication issues between the crew members.
B737NG Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into PHX in trail of a B737.
Air carrier Captain self reported not following the latest update regarding face mask policy.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing CLT in trail of an A319 that resulted in an uncommanded 50 degree bank.
A Captain reported having difficulty activating his new iPad as an EFB for a trip. Company procedures and policies complicated the effort.
Ground employee expressed concern over being the only employee who wears a face mask and works in close proximity with other team employees.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft flew below MVA while correcting back to localizer course.
ZSE Center Controller reported issues relating to NOTAMs and how they are written.
B737 crew reported a course deviation on the STAR to LAX when runway assigned was not the runway briefed and programmed into the FMC.
Air carrier Captain reported an unstabilized approach and stated they should have executed a go-around.
Air carrier flight crew reported a late runway change during approach due to heavy volume of traffic.

PHL Local Controller reported a loss of separation when a helicopter passed under an aircraft transporting skydivers. The Controller had not been advised that the jumpers had exited the aircraft. Reporter noted a lack of communication between TRACON and Tower supervisors.
E175 Captain reported executing a go-around from short final on approach to CYVR after encountering probable jet blast from an aircraft departing on the same runway.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger refused to adhere to face mask policy.
Pilot reported another pilot had a loss of electrical power on a C182 during flight after engine replacement due to alternator connection not installed correctly.
ZNY Center Oceanic Controller reported they had less than required separation between two aircraft on crossing courses even though the ATOP tool did not identify a separation problem.

SPI Tower Controller departed a slow aircraft and minutes later departed a faster aircraft. The controller forgot to advise the departure controller not to turn the slower aircraft until they were turning the faster aircraft. The controller then went to work on putting in a new flight plan on the faster departure because somehow it was removed. The controller does not know if there was a loss of separation.
Ramp employee reported a smart bag with Lithium Ion battery installed was reassigned from cargo to passenger cabin per HAZMAT regulations.
Flight Instructor reported that one of his students flew an approved solo flight before obtaining a Student Pilot Certificate via IACRA.
CRJ Captain and Flight Attendant reported they noticed a faint burning odor which was traced to an overheating cell phone charger in the cabin.
Two maintenance inspectors reported that incorrect fuel nozzles were installed on a Beechcraft BE-300 engines.
B767 Captain reported required documentation for hazardous material was challenged and incorrectly filled out by gate agent.
B737 Captain reported a climb gradient requirement appears to be missing from the SFO ILS Rwy 28L SA CAT II approach chart.

An air carrier Check Airman noted that lost communications procedures written 40 years ago and not updated since are difficult to correctly apply to the complex STARS designed for 'Descend Via' clearances.
B777 Captain reported un-commanded aircraft movement while at the gate prior to departure.
ZLA Center Controller reported that two Center sectors are unsafe when there is weather.
A DFW Tower Controller issued a non-standard go-around instruction to an aircraft. The Tower Controller did not point out the aircraft to the appropriate TRACON sector.

An air carrier flight crew flew the MFR RNAV (GPS) D and was surprised by a EGPWS TERRAIN Alert while on profile at 8;500 feet. The EGPWS escape maneuver was completed in IMC; but the approach was completed normally.
C310 pilot reported a NMAC in the traffic pattern.

NCT Controller described how procedures into SFO are allegedly not correct. Aircraft are going to the wrong runway; leaving the Bravo airspace; and are violating speed restrictions below the Bravo. Pilot of aircraft described how they set up for one runway and then were assigned another; which led to confusion in the cockpit.

Air carrier flight crew reported turning the wrong way on taxiway due to not clearly understanding Controller instructions. FO stated controllers wearing face masks could have contributed to them not clearly understanding the clearance.
PC-12 pilot reported coming close to another aircraft while descending into an airport.

A Captain reports about deficiencies in the flight planning function of the computer system and its effect on dispatch functionality. The clumsy automation increases workload and decreases effectiveness for dispatch.
A Center Controller reported a handoff of an aircraft whose radar data indicated it was climbing to 35;000 feet. The aircraft was climbing to 39;000 feet; and conflicting with traffic at 36;000 feet.
A80 TRACON Controller reported ZTL Center did not issue an aircraft a descend via clearance in accordance with a new LOA.
Air carrier flight crew reported questioning the legality of being dispatched with ground based equipment limitations.
Dispatcher reported release issues relating to flights that are not assigned to Dispatcher in a timely manner.  Reporter stated this has been a problem since the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Air carrier First Officer and Tracon Controller reported a missed crossing restriction on the DRMMM1 arrival into ATL.
Observer pilot reported a runway excursion due to a strong gust of wind.
C182 pilot flying chase during a UAV test; reported an airborne conflict with a VFR PA44. ATC issued advisories to the PA44 pilot.
Technician reported installing a new PBE on an aircraft without informing; nor training the flight crew on how to use it.

Air carrier Captain reported that during an ORD ILS 10C PRM Approach; ORD PRM frequency was used operationally by TRACON; Tower; and Ground Control which potentially interferes with breakout commands. This caused confusion and increased pilot workload.
ERJ-175 Captain reported a failed thrust reverser was not properly deferred at a contract maintenance station.
B737 flight crew reported receiving an amended release from Dispatch during the takeoff roll.

A Ramp Agent reported Maintenance personnel started an aircraft engine at the gate with no ramp observer and ground traffic nearby. Also Maintenance personnel near the engine wore hats and loose clothing.
B737 First Officer reported a ground conflict while entering the ramp due to confusing company and ATC policies.

Air carrier First Officer reported encountering turbulent; windy conditions as well as incorrect LOC frequency; causing fault in the autopilot; difficulty maintaining altitude and airspeed and a course deviation.
A pilot of a Cessna 206B reported while on approach into an uncontrolled airport using CTAF came within 150 feet of colliding with another aircraft below him.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a PDC from SJC Control Tower that was not open and departing; without calling the overlying TRACON.
ZAN Controller reported of three operational errors caused by communication inadequacies; and automation issues.
B737-900 Maintenance Technician reported that the information in the IPC was incorrect and misleading.
Air carrier flight crew reported diverting due to an impassable line of thunderstorms. Crew stated that Dispatch was pressuring them to continue on planned routing to destination.
Pilot reported a near miss; during takeoff roll; with an aircraft flying overhead on short final.
C182 instructor reported severe icing resulting in an immediate descent to exit the conditions.
A Piper Cherokee pilot reported a nose gear failure when a gust of wind picked up the aircraft and dropped it in a nose down attitude.
C-172 Flight Instructor reports Near Mid-Air Collision during takeoff.
ZAN ARTCC Controllers reported the current IFR Low Altitude Enroute Chart for their airspace had an MEA error.
B767 Captain reported flying through the MKJK FIR after it had been closed by the controlling agency without issuing a NOTAM officially closing the FIR.

Air carrier flight crew reported exceeding an airspeed restriction during departure. The crew cited several factors as contributing; including lack of recent flying; and multiple delays and distractions.
Boeing 737 Captain reported a loss of ability to taxi due to wheel slippage caused by glycol on the tarmac surface.
ZHU Controller reported deviations due to weather that may have been reduced if the airspace had been better controlled.

General Aviation pilot flying VFR reported experiencing an airborne conflict with an airliner. Pilot stated that lighter traffic during the COVID-19 pandemic was a factor in the decision to not contact ATC; which may have contributed to the event.
Jacksonville Center Controller reported staffing problems associated with no one available to assist another Controller whose sector was in the red.

A B747-400 First Officer inspected a Lithium Ion battery pallet and after detecting pallet damage uncovered the contents. Damaged batteries were found under the 'Received Damaged' sticker which was further damaged by another pallet stacked on it.
Flight Attendant reported one lavatory sink inoperative and no hand soap available on multiple flights.
Maintenance installed an incorrect tire on a CRJ-900 aircraft.

NCT TRACON Departure Controller reported an aircraft departed on a different route than was coordinated via automated strip scanning from the Tower. The Tower Controller states they accomplished the automated strip scan correctly.
Technician reported performing a routine; scheduled inspection on the number two engine VG actuating system and finding a screwdriver resting on the W1 wire loom of an EMB-175 aircraft.

An Air Traffic Controller Trainee reported failing to take sufficient action to keep a VFR aircraft away from an IFR aircraft executing an approach to an airport. The Trainee Controller mistakenly believed he could not vector the VFR aircraft below the MVA.
A pilot orbiting in a MOA reported a NMAC with a fighter jet.
B737NG First Officer reported ATC expected them to honor a speed restriction on an arrival associated with a fix they did not fly over.

TRACON Controller reported of two aircraft that traffic was called to on one another. One aircraft climbed into the others proximity and the Controller questioned the pilot as to why they climbed. Pilot responded they thought the other aircraft was descending. Controller reported that one aircraft's air carrier is not required to have water safety gear on board and is confused as to why they do not have it; but others are required to. This puts more strain on the Controller by having to keep the aircraft within gliding distance to land.
Air carrier First Officer reported a speed deviation and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.

SCT Controller reports of a loss of separation he attributes to the pilot descending early next to another aircraft and the radios associated to his position. The Controller has noticed a degradation of radio clearness and the experience of upgraded pilots in that area using the radios.
ZNY ARTCC Controller reported allowing less than required separation between successive departures and failing to enter the correct altitude in the data tag.
B737 flight crew reported a bird strike with about 20 pigeons while on short final to BUR.
CRJ200 Captain reported gear horn warning due to passing higher terrain on visual approach.

ZBW Controller has two aircraft in conflict and then is distracted by another aircraft asking a question. The Controller responds to the questioning aircraft and another Controller yells at that they have two at 17;000 feet. The Controller then resolves the situation by turning and descending the conflicting aircraft and does not have a loss of separation.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with having to wear a face mask for so long throughout trips.
EMB-175 Captain reported the First Officer's mismanagement of the FMC and altitude alerter resulted in descending through an altitude restriction.
Tow driver reported towing an aircraft into a gate and having the towing aircraft's wing hit a parked aircraft at the adjacent gate.
E-175 Captain reported communication breakdown between flight crew and Ramp Supervisor regarding load report errors. Captain further stated FOM Hazmat loading directives needs to be expanded.
B737NG First Officer reported experiencing windshear shortly after gear retraction. An EGPWS wind shear warning and an airspeed loss both occur as the Captain recovered using maximum thrust.
The pilot was cleared for an RNAV Arrival. The pilot was assigned altitudes by ATC instead of being allowed to descend on their own via the RNAV profile.

During climb in an A320 in contact with ATC controllers for vectors for traffic and clearance for flight levels; the flight crew was distracted by a company message via ACARS about a return flight causing a slowing of the climb rate.
Air carrier Captain reported a miscommunication with Air Traffic Control during the final segment on the approach.
CMH TRACON Controller reported staffing issues at the facility.
The aircraft passed between two paragliders during climb out. There were no reports and no NOTAMs about their activity nearby spanning multiple days.
Air carrier pilot reported problems flying into a specific country relating to COVID-19.

A320 Dispatcher was coerced into releasing an aircraft with two section 27 CDL's even though the CDL limitation stated 'No more than one part of one system may be missing except if otherwise specified. Parts of different systems may be simultaneously missing; unless otherwise specified in this list.'
Gulfstream Pilot reported that strong winds that made the aircraft uncontrollable on takeoff roll resulted in an aborted takeoff.

EMB-190 Flight Crew at 8;000 feet experiences a TCAS RA generated by a VFR aircraft at 7500 feet but shown as -400 on the TCAS display and visually 200 feet laterally. The RA is to monitor vertical speed and no deviation is required.
EA50 pilot reported that ATC did a poor job of vectoring them for the approach.
B737-800 pilot reported encountering severe turbulence on descent that resulted in injuries to a flight attendant and a passenger.
JCF Controller reports of an Opposite Direction Operation that worked out fine; but he critiqued his operation and determined it could have been done better.
Piper Warrior instructor pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft on final approach at LSE.
B737 Flight Crew reported a hard landing which resulted in a temporary loss of control on roll out and the aircraft's left main gear left the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported typing the incorrect letters of a fix name DAWNN vs DAWWN.
GA pilot reported that he mistook a neighboring airport for his destination resulting in an airspace violation.
Regional Captain reported clearance information was not processed from STL tower to the NAS (National Airspace System) resulting in crew filing while airborne.
ZAB Controller describes a loss of separation he attributes to one of the aircraft taking five transmissions to receive information.
757 Captain reported the crossing restriction at ARCHI on the SFO DYAMD4 STAR; as published; is incorrect and requires ATC to amend the altitude.
ZHU Controller reported working 4 positions combined with heavy traffic and no help.
Air carrier Captain reported refusing aircraft because of a recurring hydraulic leak and was challenged by Maintenance personnel.
Technician reported that Company is using non-qualified technicians to perform ETOPS checks.
Captain reported a cabin crew member became incapacitated due to a food allergic reaction.
Flight crew reported an altitude deviation on a STAR into CYYZ and were informed in Canada a 'descend via' clearance only means speeds not the altitudes.
B767 Captain reported that a non-MEL item had not been repaired at the first available station and other items had not been placed on MELs.
B737 Captain reported he was concerned that their Data Link departure change message does not have the same format as the predeparture clearance message.

During a turn around flight; ground personnel discovered a cargo door handle broken but failed to notify the crew who departed and were informed by Dispatch after landing about the malfunction. The previous station personnel knew of the anomaly but failed to notify the crew.
A319 Captain reported confusion on approach to SFO regarding an altitude assignment after NORCAL TRACON issued an ambiguous clearance.

B767 Captain questioned the de-ice crew procedure to not deice the fuselage top; their piecemeal deice procedure; and reluctance to deice any surface other than flight control surfaces. The Captain directed all ice and icicles be removed.
B737-900 fight crew reported a bird strike during takeoff which caused damage to hydraulic lines; resulting in a subsequent loss of fluid in Hydraulic System A.
Air carrier Captain reported a conflict with traffic while on a visual approach into TLH.

Jacksonville Center Controller reported not noticing an unidentified VFR aircraft on a converging heading with an IFR descending aircraft that responded to a TCAS RA due to high workload from lack of proper staffing at facility.
A helicopter pilot; after making appropriate calls at an uncontrolled airport; reports conflict with another aircraft who had not responded but appeared to be departing or overflying the runway.
Air carrier First Officer reported landing without a clearance; citing fatigue and scarcity of other aircraft operating due to COVID-19 reductions as contributing.
E175 flight crew reported 'low altitude alert' from ATC during a mountainous terrain departure.

Learjet 45 crew reported entering IMC at 400 feet and executed a go around at minimums. After two failed attempts to enter the holding pattern because of First Officer FMC errors; a visual approach was flown when the weather suddenly improved.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence in trail of a B747 on approach to ATL. Encounter resulted in a low altitude upset; with a bank angle in excess of 130 degrees.

A Controller reported shipping an aircraft to the Tower assuming it would turn from downwind onto base leg in time to be in front of straight in traffic. The downwind aircraft turned base late and into conflict with the straight in traffic.
B767 flight crew reported that inaccurate Hazmat cargo loading information resulted in a return to the gate.
EMB-140 flight crew reported they encountered severe turbulence on approach.
Air Carrier pilot reported the wing walker told them they missed contacting a parked truck at an adjacent gate by one foot while taxiing in.
B767 Captain reported encountering windshear departing ONT.
GA Pilot reported nonstandard TFR dissemination resulting in TFR violation.

An air carrier Captain on an overwater international flight reported difficulty dealing effectively with a sick passenger in large part because of what he feels are less effective manuals that stem from bad decisions following a merger.

A Pilot taxiing for departure at a non towered airport reported they taxied on the runway to avoid a taxiway closure and lost control of their aircraft when taxiing off the runway exiting the taxiway; striking a taxiway light; and encountering a prop strike.
BE35 pilot reported he was told he did not comply with UAO Tower departure instructions. The pilot believes he did comply with the instructions and ATC was mistaken.
A CRJ pilot reported departing SEA with a clearance to climb to 7000 feet; then told to maintain 2000 feet too late to comply with the restriction.
B737NG First Officer reported overshooting an altitude restriction while hand flying climbing out of DEN after being distracted by a wake turbulence encounter.
Air Carrier Captain reported several operational errors occurring after a long ground delay long due to an 'ATC Zero' event.
Maintenance Technician reported being forced to work overtime; describes a high stress environment due to low staffing resulting in fatigue and an unsafe situation.
Center Controller reported a mistake involving handing off an aircraft to the wrong sector and cited staffing levels as a contributing factor.
First Officer reported a near miss during approach with GA traffic.
B-737 crew member reported emergency equipment improperly located to effectively manage a lithium battery thermal runaway.
Air carrier Captain reported concerns with security checks in the terminal area that do not allow for proper social distancing.
HCF Controller reports of a new procedure where they tag up aircraft; not related to a primary radar return. Data tags swap at times causing confusion about the aircraft's position.
Air Traffic Controller reported working with a new arrival procedure that was issued an incorrect crossing restriction to an aircraft.
A321 Captain reported a bird strike occurred on the Captain's side pitot tube; causing erratic airspeed fluctuations.
TBM pilot reported deviating below MEA to avoid weather.
A Center Controller received a handoff on an aircraft at an altitude not in compliance with the facility SOP manual.
TOL TRACON Controller reported a loss of separation related to an opposite direction operation. The arrival and departure were not within the tolerance limits.
ZLA Center Controller reported an aircraft did not have the updated OAPM Arrival procedures for their destination.

FAAH 7110.65 allows ATC to climb/descend an aircraft to an altitude as soon as they observe aircraft previously at that altitude's Mode C indication that the aircraft has left the altitude. It also notes aircraft performance must be taken into consideration. The controller climbed a B757 to the altitude of a CRJ2 when the CRJ Mode C indicated it was 200 feet above the altitude assigned to the B757. Climb rates were not monitored and the B757 climb rate was much higher than the CRJ2. The Controller issued radar vectors and new altitude assignments but the two aircraft had less than required separation. The sector was very busy at the time due to a medical situation. Also on the job instruction was being conducted on the radar position and the radar-assist position.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track deviation while on the SEA HAROB6 departure due to a charting discrepancy.
A Controller reported a VFR aircraft depart an airport that was going to be in confliction with their aircraft on approach so they issued a vector away to the the VFR aircraft.
Technician reported an Agusta-A119 helicopter shifted because the tail rotor was not connected correctly while doing ground runs after maintenance was performed.
B777 First Officer reported the EFB timed out on rollout; making him unable to use the 10-9 airport diagram for taxi.
B767 Flight Crew reported that an inoperative APU and lack of electrical power for hydraulic pumps along with improper ground handling procedures resulted in the aircraft rolling down a taxiway.

Destination Load Planner reported a communications breakdown between Load Planner and origination Load Crew Chief regarding restricted articles limitations. Dangerous Goods removed from flight prior to departure.

Following an unscheduled stop; a B777 replacement crew noted a large weight and balance error on the aircraft when nothing was taken off or added because of Custom's restrictions. Three weight and balance revisions finally produced an acceptable and safe report.
B737 First Officer reported a problem with the iPad mounting tray interfering with their ability to manipulate the flight controls.

A B737 flight crew reports receiving delay vectors before being finally cleared for a visual approach to Runway 26R at ONT. A descend RA is received inside the FAF and is complied with. A heavy jet for Runway 26L receives a climb RA and goes around.
Ramp employee reported a smart bag was transported with battery installed in violation of Hazmat regulation.

Four Tower Controllers reported the Center was coordinating IFR arrivals too late and not in compliance with the Letter of Agreement; causing airborne conflicts and arrivals being issued go around instructions.
EMB-175 Captain reported misunderstanding a deferred item and did not realize the APU was operational for electrical power.
Air carrier Captain reported he refused to take an unescorted deportee on a flight even though it was company policy.
Air carrier flight crew reported an improperly worded CPDLC reroute from ATC on an oceanic flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported clear air turbulence that caused minor injuries to the flight attendants.
CL-350 flight crew reported landing at the wrong airport.
First Officer reported a deviation in heading caused by the FMS not operating correctly.

A VFR Helicopter pilot at 2;200 feet with TOL ATC traffic advisories reported a near miss after a single engine aircraft passed 200 feet beneath him without an ATC advisory. ATC reported a faint 'ghost' target.
Flight crew reported altitude deviation on confusing clearance.
Air carrier Captain reported ORD can be reluctant to 'turn the airport around' to east arrivals; causing potential issues with tailwind limits.
Air carrier pilot reported that an Electronic Flight Bag mount interfered with the movement of the flight controls and could be a potential safety risk.
Private Pilot reported green laser emittance inflight which resulted in affecting the vision of a passenger.
Maintenance Technician reported that after replacing a reverser actuator using an alternate part number; he was informed that the part may have had an incorrect parts tag.
Airbus 320 Maintenance Technician reported that external power cord malfunctioned.
Super King Air pilot reported hydroplaning on landing and hitting a runway light.
CL-300 flight crew reported issues with SAN departure procedure.
Air Carrier flight crew reported a bird strike during departure.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported failing to retract landing gear during a go around due to ATC vectored traffic.

B787 First Officer reported the controllers at ZSPD did not convey the actual delays to be expected during arrival; and did not expedite a clearance to the alternate after being advised of the low fuel state.

ZLC ARTCC Controller reported receiving a wake vortex encounter report from an A320 that passed 17 miles behind a B747-400. Controller stated he is concerned about the number of reports he gets from aircraft encountering wake from 'heavy' and 'super' aircraft. He recommended more separation for these aircraft.

ZDC Controllers reported during a busy complex period; issuing a descent clearance resulting activating both conflict alert; and the aircraft RA's. Controller reported declining proficiency level due to other duties; and poor ability to recover from ATC conflict situations.
Flight Attendant reported having to remind two passengers about the face mask policy. FA backed off for fear the situation would escalate.
Air carrier flight crew reported they were cleared to taxi on a taxiway that was not authorized for their aircraft. They noted the current Jeppesen chart was not correct.
E175 Captain reported distractions related to turbulence resulted in a track deviation and a missed approach.

ZOB FLM while working traffic observed two aircraft on different routes and is not aware of a conflict. Moments later FLM observes two other aircraft in not in conflict and asks the developmental handoff what he thinks he should do. Developmental then suggests a 30 degree turn; which now makes it a conflict. Moments after the first conflict the two aircraft he did not think were in conflict now alarm and are in conflict resulting in a loss of separation. FLM thought he was helping the training of the developmental but realized he should have just worked the traffic and left the instruction to a qualified instructor.
C525 is informed by ATC prior to descent into SLC that GPS jamming is occurring and a non RNAV arrival is assigned. After landing the NOTAMs are checked and none can be found for the jamming event.
L-39 flight crew reported a NMAC during unusual attitude training.
UAV Operator reported thinking he was cleared via a waiver to operate within ONT airspace; but after the flight determined the waiver had never been obtained.
PA28 student pilot reported a loss of control during takeoff that resulted in a runway excursion.

ZOA controller reports of issues with the BUTTES Sector at NCT not accepting hand offs. NCT controllers admit [according to reporter] not setting up their scopes to see far enough into ZOA's R-44 airspace.

A de-ice crew reported a CRJ-700 clean of ice and snow but when the First Officer completed a walk around after returning to the gate to reduce takeoff weight; ice and snow were discovered on the wings and flaps.
Private Pilot reports on non-standard Air Traffic Control procedure at MYF.
B737 NG Captain reported the FMS showed an extra point on the departure that was not on the SID.

C208B pilot was dispatched with minimum fuel to a remote airport forecasted to remain VFR. Upon arrival; the airport was IMC and he missed the first approach; but landed on the second approach on the runway opposite direction. TAF forecasts to remote airports should be questioned.
B-777 flight crew reported passing over a drone by 1000 feet while at 4000 feet on the HYPER 7 ARRIVIAL into IAD.
NCT TRACON Controller reported in trail departures from SJC on different procedures passed with less than the required lateral separation. This is a recurring issue at this sector.
SNA Tower Controller reported issuing a turn to a departing aircraft beneath the MVA to avoid confliction with another aircraft going around.
B737 flight crew reported rejecting their takeoff when ATC cancelled their takeoff clearance because of a potential airborne conflict.
PWM Tower Controller and Air Carrier Captain reported an aircraft in the VFR pattern not complying with ATC instructions and conflicting with traffic; resulting in a TCAS RA.
B737 First Officer reported ATC assigned them FL180 when the altimeter setting was below 29.92.
Flight Crew reported that the aircraft was pushed back without a clearance and onto an active taxiway.
Cessna 195 pilot reported that due to wind gusts the aircraft ground-looped and nosed over.
Air carrier Captain reported a passenger was removed from the flight due to non-compliance with face mask policy.
Technician reported experiencing confusion and deferred the wrong bleed valve and applied the wrong MEL.

An aircraft on the Cottonwood 2 Departure from EGE turned left towards JABAN after RALPE rather than to the right to JESIE. The left turn is meant for the engine out procedure. This resulted in a conflict with ASE traffic.
Air carrier baggage handler reported experiencing nausea and other physical issues presumably related to fumes from one of the bags that came off an international flight.

An air carrier Captain reported a CPDLC enroute oceanic weather diversion clearance revision included an E180 waypoint; but the FMC always recognizes 180° as W180. Sabre and ATC recognizes 180° as E180; a uniformity discrepancy which should be corrected.
B767 flight crew reported receiving a CFIT alert from the Approach Controller. Pilot crew also described difficulty communicating with the very busy controller.

Flight crew of a CRJ-200 repositioning flight for maintenance were planned for FL320; gear down flight at 250 KIAS. Enroute aircraft is only able to climb to FL240 max altitude thus dipping into their fuel reserves and forcing a diversion. Flight refuels and continues on to intended destination.
PA28 Instructor reported student go around resulting in a NMAC with crossing traffic.

Reporter describes incident of a go-around at Burbank Airport. When aircraft are conducting a Visual Approach and have to go-around Burbank Tower is required to keep the aircraft in their VFR pattern as opposed to a heading and altitude and handoff to TRACON for re-sequencing. The reporter states that it is impossible to keep certain jet aircraft executing a go-around in their VFR pattern within the confines of their airspace. These aircraft routinely enter adjacent Tower's airspace.
Arriving aircraft was issued a go-around due to insufficient spacing with the preceding aircraft.
B737 flight crew reported being cleared direct to KOTAA; but mistakenly entered KOTTA which is 221 NM southwest.
P50 Developmental Controller reported observing an aircraft descend below the MVA.

HCF Controller and turboprop Captain reported possibly losing separation when a turbojet on approach overtook the turboprop and entered IMC. The turboprop aircraft was no longer able to maintain visual separation and requested a 360 turn for spacing.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with face mask policy.
B737 Captain reported Maintenance was unaware of the aircraft's periodic windshield rain repellent treatment.
CL-350 pilot reports confusion regarding the initial climb segment on the RNAV SID chart and climb restriction was not in aircraft database.
A pilot landing at a non towered airport reported initiating a go around due to opposite direction landing traffic not communicating on CTAF.
Air Ambulance pilot reported experiencing a near mid-air collision with an opposite direction aircraft while setting up for landing at a hospital. Both aircraft took evasive action by banking right.
B737 Captain reported that the brand-new Tipp Toe visual approach into SFO runway 28L; has a waypoint that is not in the current FMC database.
DHC-8-100 Captain reported entering stratus clouds but experiencing severe turbulence. Ice also quickly accumulated while in the cloud.
AA5B Tiger Single Pilot reported momentary loss of control during landing resulting in a prop and wing strike.
NCT TRACON Controller reported an aircraft on the OAK CNDEL3 Departure turned into the SFO departure path.
Captain failed to verify that the release was properly electronically signed prior to departure.
Air carrier flight crew reported experiencing severe turbulence resulting in injuries to the flight attendants and minor damage to cabin furnishings. The flight diverted and successfully landed.
Air Carrier flight crew report a four pod drone in the vicinity of their aircraft while on final for Runway 26 at PHX.

A SCT TRACON Controller reported of a FUSION Radar tracking problem.  The Controller reported of a track that turned to parallel another aircraft that he was separating the turning aircraft from.  The Controller reported the STARS system showing Not ARTS Tracked (NAT) on the aircraft then it jumped back to where it was originally supposed to be flying.  Controller wants the STARS turned off and Common Automated Radar Terminal System (ARTS) used again.

EMB-175 Captain reported receiving several EICAS messages on system anomalies that were determined to be a result of a wake turbulence encounter during approach. All items were successfully cleared after landing.
Flight Attendant reported not briefing an exit row passenger due to the late arrival of the passenger.
Salt Lake Center Controller reported an aircraft was allowed to operate below the Minimum En-route Altitude.
NCT Controller reports of aircraft assigned one arrival; but turning towards another runway that was not assigned. Controller complains about the procedure they are using.
CRJ-900 Captain reported he was unhappy with the Dispatcher's lack of knowledge with the company method for determining an appropriate fuel load.
Embraer regional jet First Officer reported executing a missed approach after the BUF Runway 15 localizer signal appeared to be unreliable.
Glasair pilot reported encountering a severe downdraft enroute. This pushed him down 600 feet creating an airborne conflict which he deviated his track 30 degrees to avoid.
B737NG flight crew reported receiving a low altitude alert from Tower on approach to SFO.

C182 pilot on an IFR flight plan executing a circling approach noticed an aerobatic aircraft flying acrobatic maneuvers directly over the airport above and below their flight altitude. Reporter stated there was a waiver in place for the acrobatic box over the airport; but feels it was unsafe and should have been modified or withdrawn.
Air carrier flight crew reported being too low while flying an RNAV approach because of a rapidly decreasing atmospheric pressure in the area.
Air carrier Captain reported departing with a passenger count discrepancy resulting in a weight and balance issue.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with storing a bottle of sanitizing agent aboard the aircraft.
The flight crew of a 737 reported the need to comply with an RA to avoid an airborne conflict that ATC was seemingly unaware of.
ERJ-175 Captain reported track and altitude deviations resulted following late clearance changes on arrival into DEN.
Gate Agent reported the electronic boarding pass readers at the gate need to be sanitized after each flight.
A300 flight crew reported losing 500 feet in altitude and experienced large airspeed fluctuations during an encounter with severe turbulence at FL370.
CRJ-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into JFK more than 20 miles in trail of a B777.
PA-28 Instructor reported an NMAC event while complying with ATC departure instruction.
Bonanza pilot reported difficulties with fuel crossfeed while using tip tank fuel resulting in a temporary engine failure due to fuel starvation.
B737 Maintenance crew reported that they installed an over-wing slide and it deployed when they connected the electrical connector.
Ground employee reported being in contact with a coworker who tested positive for COVID-19 and asked to report to work the next day.
Center Controller reported descending an aircraft to an altitude below the Minimum IFR Altitude because the numbers on the radar display were unclear.
A320 Captain reported a weak Wi-Fi signal hindered the effectiveness of the WSI (Weather Services International) program.
P50 TRACON Controller reported a false radar target movement caused him to issue a vector to an aircraft which placed it into conflict with another aircraft.

The Controller took a handoff on a departure thinking it was climbing to 8000 feet. The departure was climbing to 9000 feet. There is conflicting information between the Controller's procedures manuals.

A Fractional Captain reporting being cleared direct to LEXAD intersection. The pilot read back LEXIM another fix on their route. ATC did not notice the read back and the pilot proceeded to the wrong fix.
UAS pilot reported that while operating a drone to photograph an airport building; a helicopter company at the airport had concerns about safety of operations.
Center Controller reported Traffic Management Unit did not take appropriate measures to help during weather deviations and Supevisors did not have the Sectors staffed accordingly.
CRJ-700 flight crew requesting ATC revised clearance due to missing crossing altitude restriction.
Maintenance technicians reported that while performing a test of the pitot/static system on an aircraft; the job card failed to ensure the Mode S transponders were disabled.
Air carrier Captain reported an RA for an unidentified VFR aircraft while on an RNAV Approach to PAE airport.

GA Pilot reported an airborne conflict after confusion identifying the correct runway for a night visual landing at BED airport.  Reporter cited lack of flying due to the COVID-19 pandemic was a contributing factor.
Air carrier flight crew reported encountering moderate turbulence while flying through a cumulus cloud; which resulted in injuries to cabin crew.

Flight Attendant reported a passenger was asked several times to comply with the face mask policy during the flight. The passenger did not comply and was met on arrival by a Customer Service Supervisor.
Pilot and Controllers reported an altitude overshoot that caused an airborne conflict.
Phoenix TRACON Controller reported problems with the Phoenix Class Bravo and VFR aircraft close to it which causes arrival problems.
PBI TRACON Controller and a GA pilot reported miscommunication; which resulted in the aircraft descending below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
B737-800 Captain reported refusing to accept an aircraft with one operating generator in operations outside the contiguous 48 states.
Regional turbojet flight crew reported experiencing a brief stick shaker after entering heavy preceipitation while situated in the holding pattern.
Air Carrier flight crew reported of confusion over a waypoint that was in their flight plan. ATC had another waypoint that was spelled differently. This caused confusion and being off course.
Air Carrier Captain reported a NMAC with a UAV on approach to SHV.
FSD Tower Controller reported a runway incursion with an aircraft departing and an approved truck on the closed runway. Controller was using memory joggers; but departed traffic anyway.
CL604 flight crew reported altitude and course deviations resulted from an encounter with severe turbulence associated with thunderstorm activity.
Boeing 767 Captain reported that IDG oil out temperatures have been abnormally high in cruise flight due to servicing issues.
B737-800 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL360; apparently from an unreported heavy aircraft.

While on final approach; the flight crew was advised of an active microburst near their approach path. The flight crew reported that the Tower did not respond in a timely fashion to their request for a vector away from the fast moving cell.
C-402C Captain reported an NMAC event on takeoff caused by a nonreporting aircraft departing cross runway.
BE99 Captain reported inadvertently deploying flaps in cruise.
Citation First Officer reported the REDDN4 Arrival STAR chart does not show the mandatory heading after BACHR.
A Citation crew encountered severe turbulence on departure and diverted for required inspection and maintenance.

An air carrier pilot requested that ATC modify the approach clearance to enable a stabilized approach. Instead; ATC turned the aircraft off the approach for an extended vector. The reporting pilot stated suggestions for improved ATC service.
B777 Captain reported that the minimum oil quantity prior to engine start is 22 quarts; but the right engine indicated 21 quarts when serviced to full.

A G550 MEL 52-6-0 permitted dispatch with the Main Entry Door Acoustic Curtain/Door System immoveable in the lock/secured position preventing access to LEFT ELECTRONIC EQUIPMENT RACK and POWER DISTRIBUTION BOX circuit breaker panels. The flight crew questioned the aircraft's airworthiness because the QRH requires access to that area for certain abnormal procedures.
An air carrier maintenance worker reported that while a Mechanic was preparing Protective Breathing Equipment for disposal; it exploded injuring the Mechanic.
B777 flight crew reported encountering wake turbulence during initial climbout from SFO in trail of another B777 with minimum separation.

CRP Tower Controller reported a conflict between a parachute operation and a flight of training aircraft that flew underneath the skydivers. Reporter responded to a pilot inquiry and resolved the situation.
B767-300 Maintenance Inspector reported that a slat failed due to an asymmetry issue; a week after he inspected the installation of the same slat.
LAX Local controllers and a 737 Captain reported an aircraft was issued a go-around because the aircraft in trail of them was too close.
Flight Crew reported they read back to ATC descend to 2000 feet when ATC meant 3000 feet and then flew through the localizer and received a Ground Proximity Warning.
A319 Captain reported the FMC started his aircraft's descent early causing them to descend 300 feet below the BURRZ crossing restriction on the CHSLY STAR.
SR-22 Instructor reported executing a successful evasive maneuver during initial climb due to an NMAC event with another aircraft landing on the opposite runway.

A320 Captain reported that the first aircraft his crew were assigned to was refused by tech ops and the second aircraft was refused by the dispatch. The final report stated 'Captain's refusal' for both aircraft.
A321 Captain reported encountering a drone while on approach to Orlando International Airport.
An Instructor Pilot landing at an uncontrolled airport reported an aircraft on a missed approach to an intersecting runway flew 200 feet over them.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving a flap setting warning horn during initial power push for takeoff. The Captain cited lack of sleep as a contributing factor.
CRJ-200 flight crew reported an overweight landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported the ATC Tower changed hours of operation and was closed due to COVID-19; resulting in a takeoff before curfew ended.

Air carrier Captain reported the aircraft blocked in; shut down; and was chocked at a backup gate is DSM. There was snow and ice on the ramp and after brake release the plane slid backwards taking the chocks with it.

Pilot called tower to transition their airspace enroute to destination airport. Pilot turned ESE and was told by the controller to turn north. Pilot complied and then stated he was about to go into another airspace if he continued on the assigned heading. Tower told pilot they would call the turn and to ignore the Class C airspace he was about to enter.
PA-24 pilot reported course deviation towards terrain on departure.
ZMA Center Controller reported a track heading deviation involving an aircraft holding incorrectly and had to be pointed out to an adjacent sector.
B787 flight crew reported a near collision with a B777 on a taxiway.

ZID Controller reported having to plug in with an unfamiliar Developmental during a checkride on a position the Supervisor was not checked out on. An airspace violation ensued when a handoff was not accepted until a phone call was made with the aircraft in the other sector's airspace.

ZMA Controller reported working a combined sector during weather deviations and active restricted areas with very heavy traffic volume. TMU and facility management would not make an effort to help until the complexity reached an unworkable level.
Flight Attendant reported asking a passenger multiple times to wear a COVID-19 protective mask and to not sit in the emergency exit row before the passenger complied.
CRJ-200 Pilot reported being pressured to accept flight in unsafe weather conditions.
B737 Captain reported confusion during MEL procedures for determining fuel onboard.
EMB-175 Captain reported Maintenance Technician argued that a cockpit window latch was safe; when the indicator showed unsafe.

Radar Approach Controller working in the Tower reported difficulties during his shift due to high workloads; the TRACON being closed; distractions in the Tower; and an inadequate screen size for his radar. The Controller believes that scheduling more controllers to open the TRACON would remedy the situation.

Wide body flight crew reported a near miss event while on an approach to RJAA. ATC had alerted the flight of nearby traffic which resulted in a resolution advisory; followed by a successful missed approach.

Air Carrier flight crew reported deviating from clearance on departure.

The pilot reported that it is often difficult to achieve a stabilized approach profile at DCA as approach control is 'slam dunking' arrivals; clearing them for the approach while high and fast and close to the FAF.

Upon advancing throttles for takeoff; a 777 pilot reported receiving an EICAS takeoff configuration warning due to Main Landing Gear not locked for takeoff. A low speed rejected takeoff was performed.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger being confrontational about wearing a face mask.
ZMP Center controller reported they had to unexpectedly take over TRACON Airspace due to staffing into a Center sector that was already combined.

Center controller reported the Traffic Management Unit Command Center refused to implement a flow control program for weather in the airspace causing the sector to become over-saturated and compromising safety.

A TRACON controller reported difficulty with handoffs from the Center. One of the data tags disappeared from the radar display and displayed a *splat* symbol. The other handoff data tag was in a different location than the TRACON controller's radar display indicated.
Instructor Pilot of an air taxi operation reported attempting to land in windy conditions.
Technician reported miscommunication during a functional check of the engine fuel shut off valves; resulting in the inadvertent discharge of both engines' fire bottles.
A Center Controller reported they observed an aircraft that descended below the assigned altitude and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude did not climb back to the assigned altitude when instructed.
J-3 pilot reported returning to departure airport after taking off in what appeared to be VFR conditions but encountering clouds at 100 feet AGL.
Dispatcher reported an overwhelming amount of flights the Dispatcher was advised they would be working; due to staffing issues.
Air carrier Captain reported that red and green lights (possibly Christmas laser lights) illuminated the aircraft while on approach under New York TRACON control.
A pilot landed on RNO Runway 34R/16L and reported the new LED centerline lights as too bright. The centerline LEDs obscured the runway ledge lights and made landing height estimates difficult.
Air carrier Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL340 when a Widebody Transport aircraft crossed their path 20 miles ahead of them that resulted in an uncommanded 45 degree bank.
ZLA Center Controller reported working an aircraft that did not have new OAPM arrival procedures available on the aircraft.
On climbout from BFL; an air carrier took evasive action from a near miss at 2;200 feet which ATC did not report until after the event.
B757-200 Captain reported during single-engine taxi to the gate; jet blast knocked over a can behind the flight line into a Caravan. Crew shut down and obtained a tow to the gate.
A non IFR rated helicopter pilot describes his experience after inadvertently entering IMC; then requesting ATC assistance for an IMC diversion to a nearby airport.

A pilot and his examiner took off with a clearance out of Class C and began Commercial licensing maneuvers. Unknown to the pilots; ATC had filed an IFR flight plan and threatened a track deviation violation.

B737 Captain reported difficulties with his EFB contents manager application. He was unable to access required and necessary information during flight and spent excessive time booting; installing; uninstalling; and rebooting the EFB contents manager application.
GA pilot reported that they had to communicate with Departure Control with flying the traffic pattern at PDK airport. Pilot also stated that airport runway lighting is inadequate.
A VFR Pilot reported they performed evasive maneuvers to avoid an opposite direction VFR aircraft flying at a wrong altitude for their direction of flight. The aircraft did not appear on their ADS-B.
B737 Captain reported a concern with a company special engine-out procedure off Runway 1L at SFO.
Flight Attendant reported the main door Flight Attendant is distracted during passenger boarding from their safety duties because of having to hand out sanitizing wipes to passengers.
A BE36 pilot reported that due to a lightning strike the wing tip tank exploded.
Air carrier First Officer reported their Dispatcher flight planned through known turbulence areas for an oceanic flight.
Ramp employee reported receiving medical attention after sustaining an electrical shock while plugging a tug into charging unit.
B737 First Officer reported the pushback tug had very bright lights that affected night vision.
First Officer reported that a new Electronic Flight Bag hard mount is installed onto the side window causing interference with the control column.
Maintenance Technician reports that air carrier is harassing him for writing up discrepancies.
Rans S20 pilot reported that he did not check if the runway was clear prior to takeoff causing a NMAC right after liftoff.
EC130 pilot reported encountering IMC during a VFR tour flight and received ATC assistance to return to VMC and an airport landing.
A Boeing 777-200 Captain reported receiving a RAAS (Runway Awareness and Advisory System) alert for short runway takeoff.
Helicopter pilot experiences loss of GPS reception on two seperate GPS units near PMD at 7500 feet. Both units return to normal operation after about four minutes.
B757 flight crew reported smoke in the cockpit during takeoff. Oxygen masks were donned and the flight returned to the departure airport.

ASE TRACON and ZDV ARTCC Controllers reported aircraft FMS databases are programmed incorrectly for the EGE Cottonwood 2 Departure causing aircraft to fly the incorrect departure procedure into confliction with ASE's ILS approach.
HS-125 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence climbing through FL340; probably from a B757 15 NM to their northwest.
ZMP Center Controller reports two aircraft were handed off to them on conflicting courses. Reporter stated this is a recurring issue at this particular sector.
Cessna 210 pilot reported a near-mid-air-collision with a glider that was not observed on ADSB.

A JCF controller reports of bad radios along with an orbiting UAV that was in the way of descending aircraft. The Controller reports of a loss of separation between IFR aircraft a VFR aircraft leaving a MOA. The Controller reported of other issues that may have led to his lack of awareness and not feeling competent about his work that day.
Technician reported a procedural deficiency in the Aircraft Maintenance Manual that required an Engineering Authorization.
CRJ-700 flight crew reported conducting ILS approach inside the FAF when ATC state the glideslope is not re-certified. Flight crew was not sure how to interpret ATC's statement.
Pilots reported entering the incorrect routing in the FMS resulting in a track heading deviation.

A Flight Crew were cleared for a visual approach by Center. After landing they were asked by the Ground Controller if; 'they are allowed to do visuals since he had never seen a crew accept that clearance'. They looked over their 'notes' and found 'Visual Procedures N/A'.
DA40 Instructor reported wind shift on final that caused his student to touch down on the blast pad before the runway threshold.
EMB-XRJ 145 Captain reported executing a go-around after a wind gust lifted the aircraft 20 feet above and beyond the touchdown zone.
B737 Captain reported concern for a potential safety hazard of flying through volcanic ash.
A Boeing 737 flight crew reported that a tail stand; used to protect against a tail-tip situation; was not installed by the ground crew in a timely fashion.
B737 flight crew reported missing a crossing restriction after encountering wake turbulence during descent into ORD.

Embraer Phenom 100 First Officer reported losing a reliable GPS signal; which he attributed to military activity in the area. This resulted in a deviation from the published departure which was remedied with ATC assistance.
HCF Controller reported of an aircraft in an ALTRV (Altitude Reservation) that had a block altitude below the MIA. The area was over the ocean.
Flight Attendant crew reported a family did not comply with face mask policy.
After accomplishing checks on two different airplanes; Maintenance neglected to sign off the airworthiness release for an MD11 aircraft.
GA pilot reported a NMAC on final to SMO airport. Tower closure was cited as a contributing factor.

ZJX Center Controller reported losing the situational picture and an airspace violation occurred while working an overloaded sector due to weather deviations and receiving aircraft on incorrect routes.
Air carrier Captain reported of possible shortcomings in company policy to train line pilots in new procedures.
B737-700 FO reported the cockpit filled with de-ice fluid fog shortly after takeoff. Fog cleared and flight continued to destination.
ZHN Center Controller reported a conflict between opposite direction aircraft.
Center Controller also working Approach Control airspace due to the COVID-19 pandemic; reported communication issues with another Approach facility resulting in an airborne conflict.
PCT Controller reported climbing an aircraft into military special use airspace.
Air carrier flight crew reported receiving adjusted 'final weights' in cruise flight.
Flight crew of a turbojet reported a brief severe turbulence encounter which resulted in two injured Flight Attendants.
Technician reported difficulty in reinstalling engine pneumatic ducting and overlooked re-torqueing duct clamps.

A flight crew was cleared from BOS via HYLND4 HYLND MERTO YSO but entered HYLND4 HYLND METRO YSO which caused a track deviation after HYLND. The error was very difficult to detect on the FMC even though Company SOP verification was completed during preflight.
Maintenance inspection on light transport aircraft was incorrectly recorded as being completed.
MIA TRACON Controller working combined sectors may have had less than standard separation between two aircraft.
EMB-140 fight crew reported flying an unstable approach which resulted an EGPWS and ATC low altitude alert.
Piper Cherokee pilot reported a NMAC with a helicopter on final approach.
Pilot reported deviating from assigned altitude due to experiencing aircraft structural icing while in a 'dead zone' of Anchorage Center's communications equipment radio coverage.
Pilot reported a runway incursion partially due to incorrect DXR airport taxiway diagram.

ZNY Controller reported of a conflict with two aircraft at the same altitude. Confusion ensued because aircraft progression reports showed one of the aircraft at a different altitude. Reporter climbed one aircraft to avoid conflict.
A330 Maintenance Technician reported deficiencies in the Main Landing Gear Tire replacement procedure.
CE-560 flight crew reported landing after a heavy rain. After touchdown the aircraft hydroplaned and exited the runway. The aircraft came to rest off the runway facing the opposite direction.
CRJ-700 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at 16;000 ft on Arrival into CLT that resulted in a 'violent' roll right and left.
Air carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a small aircraft on approach to LBB airport.

CRJ-200 crew depart MKE on the UECKR 1 departure. Initial altitude was to climb to 8;000. Controller told pilots to 'Climb via the SID except maintain 13;000.' Pilots questioned each other if they were to cross PRFEC at 8;000 and continue via the SID. Aircraft was not at PRFEC yet.
C501 Captain reported GPS system anomalies in cruise flight near FLO VOR.
ATC reported a loss of communication with a UAV and its chase aircraft.

Captain reported finding during preflight that the flight plan waypoints on UJ9 between PVR and COL were different from those displayed on their FMC and EFB. Dispatch refiled to resolve the discrepancy.
Air carrier flight crew reported a taxiway incursion after receiving a non-standard taxi instruction.
TRACON controller reported vectoring an aircraft below MVA to avoid conflict with a VFR aircraft not on frequency.
Denver TRACON Controller reported an emergency aircraft inbound could not turn early due to a SOP that had been implemented; which then had the aircraft fly a 15 mile final.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported that with deliveries of used aircraft; these aircraft are not listed by number in the appropriate manuals; resulting in wrong safety briefings being conducted.
Pilot reported a near mid air collision while conducting flight training in the VFR traffic pattern.

Maintenance Technician reported finding a slide pressure hose with frayed braiding; collapsed and contaminated; which was subsequently signed-off by a supervisor and engineer as serviceable; contrary to the maintenance manual.
EMB-175 flight crew reported that they descended below the FAF crossing altitude while approach; and subsequently received a low Altitude alert from ATC.

A B737-700 Captain reports clearance for four different arrivals to DEN in rapid succession by four different controllers; along with runway changes. The First Officer attempts to reprogram the FMC but becomes confused. ATC eventually issues a clearance direct to FQF.
Dispatcher reported losing equipment due to an unscheduled IT repair.
DPA Local Controller stated a C560XL reported encountering wake turbulence on approach in trail of a Falcon 7X.
A Center Controller reported numerous airspace violations between sectors working aircraft abeam active Restricted/Warning Area airspace during a period of weather deviations.
B737-900 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence at FL350 7 miles in trail of a B757. Reporter stated they were not advised of possible wake.
Regional jet Captain reported the Navtech MERIL7 SID chart is poorly designed and does not depict well on the iPad mini.
Air carrier First Officer reported not contacting the ramp before entering and cited as contributing factors a lack of recent flying and ATC not instructing them to contact ramp.
Air Carrier Captain reported the field conditions and PIREP at PVD did not accurately portray the braking action he encountered.

Pilot reports of discussion with his First Officer about getting more spacing between them and the previous departure before they take the runway. When they take the runaway they ask for an additional 30 seconds for separation. The controller advises the distance they already have and they depart.

LAF Controller describes a situation where the facility is short staffed and the Controller departs two aircraft in sequence; thinking the second is VFR. The second aircraft is IFR and the Controller has a loss of separation.

An instructor pilot approaching the CHD Runway 22L landing pattern in a crosswind to downwind entry had a near miss with an aircraft in the landing pattern after ATC failed to notify either aircraft of the others presence or issue controlling guidance.
Flight Attendant reported issues relating to non-revs and where to seat them.
ZDV Controller reported both aircraft responded to RA when standard separation was applied; resulting in an airborne conflict.
Air taxi Captain reported rapid onset of moderate continuous icing while in cruise and on initial approach.
EMB-140 Captain reported an air turnback due to backup pitch trim failure.
EMB-145 Captain reported aircraft flap speed exceedance during Missed Approach maneuver while experiencing wind shear.
While unloading bags from an A320; ramp crew noticed that the nose of the aircraft started to rise. They stopped unloading from the forward cargo bay until the aft bay was unloaded.
B737-900 Flight Attendant described injuries received during a severe turbulence encounter.
Flight Attendant reported confusion over removal of the Halon fire extinguisher from the aft cabin for use in the cockpit.
NCT Approach Controller reported their frequency was working intermittently even though the Tech Ops personnel reported the frequency had been fixed.
Reporter stated that new procedures at his station are causing excessive COMAT shipments on company aircraft and are not being tracked correctly.
B737-800 flight crew reported breaking off the approach to MCO when a wake turbulence encounter contributed to a track deviation during localizer intercept.
Small aircraft Pilot reported marginal radio communications deteriorated from IAF [Initial Approach Fix] altitude to an uneventful landing.
TRACON Controller reported failing to coordinate an Opposite Direction Operation with Tower.

CRJ-900 Captain reported the First Officer was very uncomfortable flying the aircraft with both FMS's on MEL. The Captain would like to see a better description in the MEL regarding how to operate in this situation.

When the reporter; a simulator Maintenance Technician; noted the presence of yellow contamination in the media of the three compressed air purification systems distributing breathing air to the simulator oxygen masks he disabled them in compliance with an adjacent 'Danger' sticker advising such a yellow contamination indicates an unsafe condition. The next day he noted all three purifiers back in operation; the yellow contamination still present in two of them but the 'Danger' stickers removed. He was advised by his managers that the purification system contractor was the expert and deemed the status of all three systems acceptable and was qualified to remove the 'Danger' stickers.
Global Express (BD70) flight crew reported difficulty complying with ATC clearance during a go-around at BFI because of a traffic conflict and wake turbulence considerations.

Two student pilots reported taking evasive action during a go-around to avoid each other in the pattern. The instructor took control of the aircraft to more aggressively increase the separation between the two planes.

JFK Tower Controller reported a loss of separation that he attributed to the runway configuration the airport was on. Controller had a loss of separation due to speed of second aircraft being 80 knots faster than the preceding traffic. Controller did not send aircraft around but tried to get visual separation. Aircraft went around on its own and turned into parallel traffic which Controller had to separate it from.
Air Carrier flight crew reported being notified on arrival by ground crew that the Hazmat can door was not properly secured.
Flight Attendant reported sustaining an injury when a large cart rolled in turbulence because it did not have any brake pedals on one side.
NCT Controller and flight crew reported the pilots flew the SID incorrectly into confliction with a previous departure.
Maintenance Technician reported that a CRJ700 made contact with a pole while being tugged into the hangar.
CRJ-200 Captain reported executing a low altitude go-around at CLT when the flight ballooned as a result of either a gust of wind or a wake turbulence encounter.
Air carrier Flight Crew reported Flight Management Computer Database was out of date.
A Maintenance Inspector reported that someone from his operation had removed parts from an accident aircraft without proper documentation.
Air carrier First Officer reported multiple issues while operating in winter conditions in an attempt to land at DCA. First approach required a go-around but the second approach was successful.
CRJ-200 First Officer reported 'surprisingly violent' wake turbulence encounter in trail of an A380 on approach to DFW at 11;000 feet
B777 flight crew reported confusion with ATC following a CPDLC request to deviate around unanticipated weather.
GA pilot reported taking evasive action in response to a TCAS warning near DEN Class B airspace.
Line Technician reported a B737 returned to the gate due to a pitot static cover not being removed prior to departure.

Air carrier Captain reported being issued instructions to hold short of a taxiway that no longer exists at SEA airport and that they could not visually locate. ATC subsequently advised that the aircraft had passed the taxiway.
Small aircraft pilot reported a NMAC with a skydiver.
ZNY ARTCC Front Line Manager reports of a skydiving operation that is not signatory to a LOA with the FAA. The operation has skydivers dropping through an LGA arrival route.

MCO Tower Controller reports of a loss of separation due to a departing aircraft not being airborne when an arrival was over the threshold. Controller thinks the action was legal; but complained that someone who sits behind a desk made the determination that it was not legal.
A Controller allowed final approach spacing to decrease to 2.9 miles before breaking the second aircraft out for re-sequencing. Three miles spacing was required.
Air Taxi Pilot reported he encountered IMC conditions while on a ferry flight on a VFR flight plan.
Air carrier flight crew flying DHC8 aircraft report Aircraft Limit Speed exceedance on approach.
ZFW controllers reported an aircraft was deviating around weather when they encountered severe turbulence and lost close to 4;000 feet of altitude.
Airbus Captain identified confusing fix names KI45O and KI450 and recommends avoiding the use of the letter 'O' along with numbers in naming waypoints.
Air Carrier Captain reported difficulties with weather and ATC communications resulting in a diversion.

JAX TRACON Controller reported of issuing a turn to an aircraft to the right to go behind traffic. During the relief briefing; pilot reported the traffic came within 20 feet of the aircraft. Reporter said aircraft turned left instead of right.
G280 flight crew encountered a ground conflict with another aircraft on the runway.
Dispatcher reported being advised of an aircraft that took off with incorrect weights and the need to ensure a certain row in the aircraft was empty to be within limits.
Flight Attendant reported a communications breakdown with Gate Agent during preflight regarding local ramp access procedural policy.
Ground employee reported concerns regarding the lack of social distancing in the airport lobby area.
A320 Captain reported an over application of an MEL and contacted Maintenance to address the deferred item.
ZDV ARTCC Sector controllers were handed off two aircraft on converging routes at the same altitude. The reporters state this is a common occurrence of aircraft received from a particular sector.
Instructor Pilot reported an NMAC with a gyrocopter in the pattern at DWH airport.
PSP Tower controllers reported allowing an aircraft to enter TRACON airspace without coordination and with incorrect departure instructions.

Following a continuous duty overnight; a DHC-8 First Officer failed to see a left engine cowl plug in place during his walkaround in very cold weather. The fatigued crew operated the flight after the plug was ingested noting only lower torque and higher fuel flow.
Air carrier Captain reported two passengers were not complaint with face mask policy during flight.
Air carrier flight crew reported rejecting the takeoff after hearing a load shift at power application.
A321 First Officer reported multiple Resolution Advisories on approach to land which eventually resulted in a go-around.
Captain of a large passenger turbojet reported experiencing a failure of one IRS system which inhibited their ability to enter ETOPS airspace.

An air carrier on approach to MDW encountered severe turbulence with momentary losses of control. Unable to completely avoid the weather and with windshear warnings at MDW; the flight diverted to its alternate.
ORD Tower Controller reported an unsafe situation where the Local Controller sent aircraft around to do a runway braking action check every 25 minutes.
B737 Captain reported taking evasive action to avoid a collision with a vehicle.
EMB-175 F/O reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence from a preceding B737 that resulted in multiple system anomalies.
Private pilot rated passenger reported he took the controls after the flying pilot lost directional control on the landing roll and departed the runway surface with no damage or injuries.

A B737-800 aircraft deviating for weather was issued a reroute. The crew did not have the charts depicting the route issued to them. The flight did not comply with their assigned route and flew out of radio and radar coverage for a period of time deviating on their own navigation for weather.
Air carrier Captain reported his concern with his airline's decision to remove one of the hard copy QRHs from the cockpit; leaving one hard copy and the iPad as source material for QRH procedures.
Air carrier Captain reported a track deviation occurred on the arrival into SEA following a confusing clearance from ATC.
A B737 flight crew reported they received a TCAS RA after SAT TRACON vectored them too closely to pattern altitude.
Air carrier pilot reported that a UAV was spotted within 100' of right wing-tip on approach to SLC.
The flight crew of an A319 were in non-compliance of an MEL when they flew the aircraft with a nose gear door removed as a passenger flight when the MEL required it to be a ferry flight.
GA pilot reported forgetting to turn on Navigation Lights so the ADS-B would activate.
B737-900 Captain reported tail strike during landing.
B747-400 First Officer reported nuisance altitude alerts in cruise flight due to incomplete Maintenance procedures.
B777 flight crew reported a temporary loss of thrust in one engine after a bird strike.
A TRACON Controller reported they forgot to issue a climb clearance to an aircraft and it flew below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

The flight crew of a regional jet misinterpreted a published climb profile and overshot a crossing restriction. There were no traffic conflicts and ATC subsequently issued the flight crew a new climb clearance.

LGB Tower Controller reports of a session where the other Local Controller did not follow the SOP and/or the 7110.65 for the safe separation of traffic. The other local had a loss of separation with an aircraft on the runway and one over the landing threshold; and advised the landing aircraft to hold short of a runway. This was against the LAHSO rules and the SOP.
B757 flight crew reported diverting due to a Cargo Fire EICAS Master Warning.

A Ramp Service Employee reported new procedures require chock prepositioning on the ramp in front of the engines which is an ingestion safety hazard for both personnel and chocks when the engines may be left running for minutes after gate arrival.

Flight Attendant reported that announcements regarding the safety demonstration changes were not made and that passengers were not advised to remove face coverings and masks in the event of decompression.
Near miss caused by lack of communication between aircraft in an uncontrolled environment.
Air Carrier Captain reported an NMAC in IMC inbound to FAI.
Boeing aircraft flight crew reported severe turbulence on descent into SDF.
