S46 TRACON Controller stated Airbus type departing aircraft routinely climb at much slower airspeeds than similar size type aircraft.

An NCT Controller reported releasing a departure on a departure procedure which reverses course towards inbound traffic. The pilot of the departing aircraft did not contact the Controller in time for the Controller to vector the departure out of the way of two arrivals.
Cleveland Center controllers reported radio problems along with similar sounding call signs led to and unsafe situation.

A pilot reported confusion with ATC on a waypoint. When the crew entered the new waypoint it wiped out the existing route and took the flight slightly off course. The Controller issued one heading but the crew heard another then corrected it.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not go through proper channels to clear medical in regards to not having to wear a face mask.

A Tower Controller issued a frequency change and approved a missed approach for an emergency aircraft prior to coordinating with the TRACON. The TRACON had conflicting traffic for the missed approach procedure that the Tower Controller was not aware of.
B737 Captain reported being advised during flight by Dispatch of HAZMAT on board. No HAZMAT forms provided with flight release at departure.
Flight Attendant reported lack of staffing caused crew to work without mandated rest.
Air carrier flight crew reported they were informed by ATC of a possible altitude deviation.
EMB-175 flight crew reported that distraction following a wake turbulence encounter resulted in a flap overspeed.
Pilot reported APU was operated incorrectly; causing aircraft to be without external power; activating the emergency exit lights on three occasions.

ZAU Center Controller reported that another Controller was unsure of how to separate a flight of military aircraft. Reporter wanted to confirm a few things with the Controller who was handing off the aircraft; but the other Controller did not know the answers.

During takeoff; a B767 Takeoff Configuration Warning alerted but as the Captain called 'Reject;' the First Officer reset the Stabilizer Trim into the Green Band silencing the Warning. The takeoff was continued.
EMB-145 Captain reported experiencing windshear on final approach and executed a go-around. They declared minimum fuel and were given clearance to land without delay.
EMB-175 reported encountering severe turbulence in the vicinity of FCA VOR at FL320.
A300 Captain reported several similar sounding call signs on the same ATC frequencies repeatedly.
ZLC Center Controller reported handing aircraft off to the wrong sector during high volume traffic.  Reporter stated staffing issues and fatigue contributed to the incident.
EMB-145 Captain reported a potential dangerous situation in company policy with the boarding process of armed passengers.
Flight Crew flying EMB545 aircraft report ground conflict on takeoff roll due to landing traffic.
Technician reported a work order was missed and not completed.
Air Carrier Captain reported an NMAC with a light aircraft on approach to MDW.
A B757 Captain questioned his Company's new Before Takeoff Checklist procedure which takes the First Officer's full attention during taxi; leaving the Captain solo while taxiing for takeoff.
Air carrier ground personnel reported revised dangerous goods transport procedures as related to configuration and live animal limitations.
Light Transport Captain reported being confused by PKB ATC procedures and a single hold line in the vicinity of Runways 10/28 and 3/21 at Taxiway G.

An Air Carrier First Officer reported having to initiate a go around on their second approach attempt due to the glideslope fluctuating. The pilot reports this is a recurring issue on this approach and other crews also reported the glideslope fluctuating.
B767 Check Airman reported an incorrect part installed on an emergency enter/exit window.
A321 flight crew reported an airspeed exceedance resulted when they experienced wake turbulence after takeoff in trail of a B777.

A Houston Controller reports of having to listen to the ASOS on the telephone because the wind indicators are out of service. The Controller reports this is a distraction. There was severe weather in the area.
Air carrier flight crew executed a rejected takeoff after seeing a vehicle on the departure runway.
Air carrier flight crew reported the GPWS sounded an alarm as it was descending for an approach.
EWR Tower Controller reported an aircraft failed to follow directions when exiting the runway causing a go-around and loss of separation.
A321 Captain reported unfamiliarity with RNAV entry procedures into MWCR caused an airborne conflict.

EMB-145 First Officer reported nonstandard Approach Control handling resulting in flight crew being in a less than optimal position when cleared for approach. Flight crew 'played catch up' in a short time frame to complete a stable and safe landing.
A Maintenance Technician reported that the MD-11 Cargo 'Load Shuttle Beam' needed to be deferred because the required part was not in stock.

A MD-80 Captain discovered his aircraft's compass correction card was out of date and made a maintenance log entry. Maintenance cleared the discrepancy citing the MEL which said to install new compass card. Maintenance replaced the card without swinging the compass.
Maintenance Technician reported problems with other technicians not following the correct company policy for aircraft checks.
B737 flight crew reported undocumented Hazmat cargo loaded on aircraft with no flight crew notification.
Pilot of an aircraft that is towing another aircraft reports of a near miss with a third aircraft. Pilot speculates that the third aircraft never saw the two aircraft formation.
B757-200 Captain reported inflight ACARS message regarding Hazmat documentation errors; including exceeding max allowed limitation as well as load location error.
CRJ-900 Captain declared minimum fuel after unexpected holding on descent to their destination. The Captain attributed fuel issues to unrealistic company fuel reserve policies.

ZDV Controller reported of traffic overload and weather deviations at the end of the shift. Mid person came in and Front Line Manager left. Controller asked mid person for help and it was given. Reporter stated this happens too often and something needs to be done to help with traffic at the end of this shift.
Captain reported not turning engine bleed air switched to on after an engine bleed off takeoff; causing a loss of cabin pressure control.
Air carrier flight crew reported a track heading deviation during departure from TUL due to confusion between similar sounding fix names BARNS and YARNS.
Captain of a corporate jet reported a TFR violation occurred in part because their XM subscription had expired.
Air carrier flight crew reported difficulty executing a taxi clearance at AUS airport resulting in a taxiway incursion.  The crew cited charting deficiencies as contributing.
Student pilot reported an NMAC with an aerobatic aircraft in the pattern at LVK airport.
Flight Attendant reported refusing the flight due to unresolved multiple write-ups.
Captain reported various issues with having to wear a mask during the COVID-19 pandemic.
Technician reported work card discrepancies for two different types of aircraft and being pressured to continue working after finding these errors.
A Foreign Air Carrier pilot reported a conflict with traffic on the parallel final approach course.

CRJ-700 flight crew reported being issued a taxi clearance from a gate which required a tight turn and full tiller. In the turn; jet blast was directed at a Twin Commander; which spun that aircraft around.
ZBW Center Controller reported communication issues with ZNY leading to an aircraft entering his sector without coordination.
Approach Controller reported of a possible loss of separation when an aircraft went around and was in conflict with an inbound opposite direction aircraft.
LR60 Captain reported momentary loss of control after encountering a developing thunderstorm on descent.
A11 TRACON Controller reported an aircraft had an NMAC with another aircraft the Controller had just terminated radar with.
Air Carrier pilot reported the taxi chart at EWR is not accurate.
A90 TRACON Controller reported that IFR departures were being issued vectors below the minimum vectoring altitude.

ZAB Controller reported of short staffing and management's policy related to working traffic when the sector is in the red (Monitor Alert Parameter).  Controller would like to see shift staffing up to what the requested numbers are.
A widebody McDonnell Douglas crew rejected two takeoffs between 90-100 KTS after GPWS windshear warnings. The third takeoff was successful.
Aircraft Maintenance Lead is pressured by Management to do work in an unsafe environment.
B747 flight crew reported a disagreement between the Captain and First Officer on the landing flap configuration resulting in two go-arounds.
D21 TRACON Controller reported an operational error due to not knowing airspace had changed with Metroplex program implementation.
C172 flight instructor reported an engine malfunction after takeoff resulting in a return to field.
MD-11 Captain reported discovering the incorrect Hazmat documents during cruise.
Maintenance Technician reported not being given necessary information to work at unfamiliar airport not staffed with regular company maintenance.
ZSU Controller observed an aircraft intercepting the final approach course at 1800 feet. The assigned altitude to intercept was 2800 feet and the MVA is 2700 feet.
ZDV Controller reported they descended an aircraft to an altitude below the Minimum IFR Altitude.

B777 flight crew reported anticipating turbulence ahead due to thunderstorms and informed the flight attendants and passengers to take their seats and fasten their seatbelts. Two flight attendants did not and were slightly injured by severe turbulence.
Airline Ground Personnel reported an unsecured and undeclared Hazmat violation due to a communication breakdown.
B737 Captain reported that an aircraft failed to vacate the runway; resulting in a go around.
Flight Attendant reported that during takeoff the galley trash door opened but the trash bin did not move.
A320 flight crew reported unexpected severe turbulence; resulting in passenger injuries.
B737-700 Captain reported the quick-donning oxygen mask harness did not fully inflate during cruise.
B737-800 Captain reported that during push-back the tug had a brake failure.

A flight crew cleared for the BOI Runway 28R visual was advised about downwind traffic and deviated north to gain space. The EGPWS 'CAUTION TERRAIN' alerted and the crew initiated an escape maneuver followed by an uneventful approach.
Air carrier Captain asked for more efficient and helpful input from the company when a divert decision is made.
ZLA Center Controller reported that SoCal TRACON East Feeder sector told ZLA to re-sequence traffic leading to a loss of separation.
Air Carrier pilot reported receiving a GPWS alert during a visual approach.
B737 Captain reported severe turbulence as well as issues with the medical kit used to administer first aid to passengers injuries.
ZMA ARTCC Controller reported that two aircraft lost separation due to weather and the lack of a traffic management initiative to help relive the volume.

A B737 flight crew; departing LAS on the TRALR RNAV SID; was surprised when ATC asked why they had stopped their climb at 13;000 FT rather than continuing to FL190 as directed on their departure chart. Reporters cited the primary contributing factor as the inability to read the tiny print on the large and complex SID chart when viewed on their iPad EFB; necessitating zooming in to be able to read it. The chart directive to MAINTAIN FL190 being located at the very bottom right in the ROUTING box and therefore; due to the need to scroll to find it when zoomed in; in the most inconspicuous location on the page.
Air carrier Captain reported the lack of flying was a factor in experiencing an unstabilized approach.

An ARTCC Controller in Charge reported no action was taken by the FAA Command Center to effectively manage traffic flows during known weather events causing Center sectors to be overloaded with traffic.
Captain reported concern regarding visual RNAV approaches.

ZDC Controller reported issuing a point out due to weather; getting it approved; and then learning some special airspace was in fact hot. Reporter did not know the airspace was hot until the point out and other Controller advising.

The First Officer reports that the charted crossing altitude at LIMMA on the LAX ILS 25L approach is above the glideslope which creates problems with capturing the glideslope and getting stabilized on the approach.
A Pilot approaching to land at a non towered airport reported an NMAC.
Technician reported inconsistencies in paperwork and procedures required to place halon fire extinguishers in stock and ready for installation.

A Captain questioned the EGSS FL055 ATIS transition level while descending to 6;000 feet and was told it should have been FL070. A transition at FL055 would have resulted in a 600 foot altitude error.
Pilot reported the HNL ATIS as very lengthy; repetitive; outdated; and of poor quality.

Three Tower Controllers report that a Front Line Manager (FLM) had an aircraft land on a closed runway. The FLM also reported that he did land the aircraft on what the Controllers thought was a closed runway; but it was closed due to noise abatement and this aircraft did not have a noise abatement issue; so landing on this runway according to the FLM was not an issue. Controllers thought otherwise and tried to stop the FLM from landing on the runway he chose.
Flight attendant reported two passengers had to be reminded several times about face mask policy.
An ERJ-175 pilot reported windshear on final which resulted in a 20 kt increase. Speed brakes and landing gear were extended to regain a stable profile for landing.
B737-800 Captain reported slowing below an ATC-assigned speed on arrival into DEN.
EMB-175 flight crew reported overspeeding the flaps on go-around.
B777 Captain reported possible GPS jamming.
Flight Attendant reported discrepancy between Qualification Training Hazmat material and Company Hazmat Policy.
Air Carrier Captain reported an airborne conflict following ATC clearance.
Air carrier flight crew experienced a GPWS warning in visual conditions while executing a steep visual approach to the airport. Nearby high terrain was a factor.

B767 Captain questioned the loading of Class 8 dangerous goods on top of Class 9 and the dangerous goods department was queried. The load was apparently legal but there was no specific mention of this situation in the Flight Operations Manual.
CE-750 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence 12 miles in trail of a B747 that resulted in 'violent' banks in both directions.
A319 Flight Crew reported working around conflicting deferred items may have violated the MEL.
Air Carrier flight crew reported passing under a UAV while on the ILS Runway 13 at LGA.
A330 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence on a North Atlantic track at FL370.
B747-400 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence departing VHHH in trail of a B777.
EMB-145 Captain reported overshooting the assigned altitude on a missed visual approach to IAD.
LAX Tower Local Assist reported releasing an aircraft off SMO. There was a loss of separation between it and a LAX departure.
Passenger reported a near mid air collision with an opposite direction commercial airliner during cruise; requiring evasive action.
CRJ-200 Captain reported an NMAC event during final approach with an nonreporting; no transponder/ADSB glider operating along localizer approach course.
A Center Controller reported decisions by their management to cancel flow restrictions during a shift with busy weather deviations created an unsafe workload for the controllers.
Flight crew reported a similar callsign issue resulting in a takeoff without clearance.  The crew attributed this to a lack of alertness and complacency due to the irregular times.

Flight Attendant reported having mild symptoms of being ill; but decided to take another two day trip.  Flight Attendant continued to feel ill after the two day trip warranting a COVID test; which was positive.
A flight crew flying a less automated aircraft than they were accustomed to and somewhat overloaded; took another aircraft's clearance which resulted in a TCAS alert along with a call to ATC.
Captain reported that EWR policy has changed to tolerate departures with tailwind conditions even when braking action reports are present.
A319 flight crew reported receiving a GPWS terrain warning on approach to MKJS.
A&P mechanic reported failing to sign the back of his previous certificate; resulting in an email from the FAA.
Air carrier Captain reported questioning an internal company policy in regards to load planning changing fuel loadings without consulting the flight's Dispatcher and Captain.
A321 Captain reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence during their landing flare in trail of a B757 to DFW airport.
Flight Attendant reported gate agent issued seats that according to company policy were not supposed to be used.
Cargo agent reported unauthorized HAZMAT shipment without required documentation.
Pilot reported confusion on whether a sporting TFR is in effect during the COVID-19 Pandemic when spectators are not allowed at the game.
Air Carrier Captain reported confusion regarding medical certification expiration date in reference to the COVID-19 extension.
LR-40 Captain reported a critical Ground conflict during takeoff roll with a runway incursion aircraft at far end of departure runway.

CNO Tower Local Instructor reported seeing a conflict and hoping the developmental would fix the problem. Instructor prompted developmental to no avail. Arrival and departure had a loss of separation due to timing and lack of communication from overlying TRACON.
CRJ-200 Captain reported a navigation app on the Electronic Flight Bag stopped working while being vectored for an approach.
ZKC Controller reported an aircraft in marginal weather went NORDO due to insufficient ATC radio coverage around AVK airport.
MD-11 Captain reported a TCAS TA during climbout.
PC-12 flight crew reported they incurred some damage from an inflight lightning strike.
ZID Center controller reported HTS Approach routinely does not accept handoffs or answer the landline.

B767 flight crew reported after an in-flight emergency; air return; continuing ground delays and reaching an agreement with management regarding the crew timing out/fatigue; the crew was later placed on Fatigue status and compensated from their sick leave bank.
Pilot reported taxiing in a congested area resulted in a collision with a parked aircraft where both aircraft were damaged.

A Maintenance Technician working on a Global 5000 reported that after accomplishing some operational checkouts and resetting some circuit breakers the air data probes heated and two probe test adapters and the two aircraft air data probes were damaged.
ZDV Controller report a PIREP filed via the Enroute Information Display System did not show up for 37 minutes.
Air carrier flight crew reported not setting the flaps resulting in a takeoff configuration warning on takeoff roll.
A319 Captain reported concerns with approach spacing procedures into DEN.

B737 Captain reported the First Officer's window came open while taxiing. The Captain reports this is a recurring issue in the aircraft type with First Officer's not be trained correctly to ensure their window is secured.
B737-700 Captain reported issues with planning for compliance with landing weight limits.
ZLA Center Controller reported vectoring an aircraft into an area with a higher MIA.

A Chicago Center (ZAU) Controller describes a situation where Special Use Airspace (SUA) is scheduled to go hot. Another controller is observed missing phone calls and becoming very busy. The reporter asks for a D-Side to help this person; but no one is able at the time. The reporter found out later that the other controller is vectoring traffic away from the SUA because it is going to be hot. Help arrives later for the overworked controller and it is later discovered that the information posted about the SUA is for the next day and was not going hot.
Pilots; Dispatcher; and Tower Controller reported of a ground conflict that resulted in injuries to flight attendants.

Due to severe flooding in Austin Texas the TRACON was closed and the Tower was intermittently open and closed. This situation caused confusion for the pilot/reporter who began taxiing without a clearance from the Tower. The pilot contacted FSS which told them all ATC facilities had been evacuated. The flight crew had dialed the wrong ground control frequency leading them to believe the tower was not staffed. They eventually realized their mistake and contacted Ground Control.
All cabin and flight Crew left airplane with cleaning crew still on board.
B737 Captain reported wheelchair Lithium Ion battery was transported in a unauthorized Hazmat container.
Air carrier First Officer reported becoming distracted and not getting a valid load close out.
A B737-700 Captain reported he was not sure he was legal to continue on his approach to DEN when severe rime ice might have been in the area.
Dispatcher reported a possible aircraft volcanic ash encounter during night cruise.
EMB-175 Captain reported rejecting the takeoff when Tower cancelled their clearance because of a conflict on a crossing runway.
General Aviation pilot reported an NMAC with another light aircraft in congested airspace.

A PVD TRACON Controller reported of a loss of separation when an IFR aircraft reacting to an RA descends into the altitude structure of a VFR aircraft. The Controller would like a review of airline TCAS policies and/or a software update to the TCAS System.
An A11 TRACON Controller reported that ZAN Center had just handed off an arriving aircraft whose altitude indicated it was in confliction for the arrival. The TRACON could not detect a Mode C.
C310 pilot reported a loss of aircraft control due to a possible wake turbulence encounter.
Air carrier Captain reported a family ate and drank during the entire flight to avoid having to wear face masks.
A Ground Controller observed the Local Controller clear aircraft for intersection takeoffs with less than required Wake Turbulence separation.

After diverting due to windshear during approach at their original destination; a B747 flight crew was unable to complete all required coordination and procedures in a timely manner prior to departing the divert airport back to their original destination. The crew was given an incorrect Critical Crew Off (CCO) time prior to departing the divert airport and subsequently exceeded their FAR 117 duty day limit.

Air carrier Captain reported wanting to remove a passenger for not complying with face mask policy; but was over ruled by supervisors. The passenger intimidated the Purser during flight and was still non-compliant with face mask policy.

B767 Captain refused aft cargo compartment loose package lithium ion batteries because they were loaded near airbag pyrotechnic initiators. Before departure the forward cargo 'lithium batteries' were thought to mislabeled and subsequently removed. Total 'battery' cargo weight removed was 10;000 pounds. Dangerous goods cargo should be suspect because of faulty labeling and documentation.

ZFW Controller reported an aircraft under his control reporting a NMAC. The Controller had traffic 9;000 feet above this aircraft and did not see any other traffic. Controller zoomed radar scope in and saw nothing. Seconds later the Controller observed a primary target 3 miles south of the reporting aircraft.
Miami TRACON Controller reported an airborne conflict with an arrival into Fort Lauderdale and a VFR just below the Class Bravo.

CRJ-200 Flight Attendant reported that the seat backs in rows 12 and 13 were not secured in the upright position. There are no procedures; information; or education regarding these seat backs and how they are supposedly meant to allow access to emergency equipment.

CRJ-200 First Officer reported a go-around due to windy conditions. The crew decided to try another approach during which they descended below their assigned altitude which caused a TCAS RA. The second approach resulted in a landing.
Air carrier flight crew reported a runway incursion.
The Emergency Evacuation Safety Briefing card does not describe how to operate the overwing exit door on a 757-300 airplane.
GA pilot reported a critical ground conflict after landing with a helicopter landing on the taxiway.
An Embraer ERJ flight crew reported that while on the intercept for the final approach course the aircraft descended below the vectoring altitude.
PCT controller reported DCA Tower issued a go-around to an air carrier arrival due to poor communication and lack of procedure for a VIP movement.
ZOA Center Controller reported an unsafe situation when an aircraft was switched over from NCT TRACON and the pilot took it upon himself to climb to avoid traffic at the same altitude.
Citation Captain reported reentering Class B airspace after encountering wake turbulence on approach to BFI.
BE-400 First Officer reported executing a go-around after encountering wake turbulence on approach to MIA. Reporter stated the Tower did not warn them of possible wake encounter.

An A319 Captain reports the lack of a loadmaster at a down line station. That station wanted him to sign the weight and balance worksheet. He refused to do so since he was not a 'Loadmaster'. He called Central Load Planning and had them generate a computer generated weight and balance. He signed one copy of that weight and balance and left that copy with the station.
B737 flight crew reported sighting a drone in close proximity to their aircraft while on approach to SJC near KLIDE.
ZAN Controller reported a disagreement with the Front Line Manager about entering SIGMET data into the Information Display System.
B787 flight crew reported the aircraft was struck by lightning that caused a partial systems loss; resulting in a return to the departure airport.
EMB-175 Captain reported a NMAC with a UAV on approach to LAX at 9000 ft in the vicinity of FUELR.

An A320 Flight Attendant detected an oil odor during an early morning preflight. The flight departed but then diverted with the pilots on oxygen after the odor intensified once airborne. The reporter experienced painful headaches and dizziness days after the exposure.
P51 pilot reported a NMAC enroute to the airport.
GA pilot reported aggressive aborted takeoff when alerted by ATC; as another aircraft taxied onto the runway.
C172 pilot reported losing directional control while attempting to turn off onto a taxiway after landing.
Dispatcher reported that company policy concerning fuel tankering to international destinations needed to be clarified.
Air carrier flight crew reported they had multiple terrain warnings on approach; the ATIS aforementioned possible GPS interference.

Pilot reports of being left high and advising ATC that they could not make the assigned restrictions. ATC never advised the crew on what to do. Aircraft crossed every restriction high. Pilot was concerned about the center and their disregard for the flights clearance issues.
Pilot instructor and student reported distraction in the cockpit resulted in executing a touch and go without ATC clearance and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
EMB-145 First Officer reported encountering wake turbulence on arrival into CLT.
C172 Instructor reported that they encountered a near miss because they were unfamiliar with the airport and misunderstood the Tower when to report.

ZLC ARTCC Controller the facility issued a memo defining extra rules not contained in the Federal Aviation Administration Handbook (FAAH) 7110.65 handbook. The Controller did not comply with the local rules.
Pilot reported a possible signal reception issue that resulted in them being unable to communicate with ATC while lined up on the runway.
Air carrier Captain reported confusion when Air Traffic Control issued instructions to follow 'standard taxi routes' that were not published.

Air carrier First Officer reported a smoking external battery when it was connected to the EFB prior to departure. Attempts to turn it off were unsuccessful. The battery was disconnected and left with a company agent.
Pilot reported forgetting to set the correct code in the transponder due to not talking to Clearance Delivery; but rather the overlying TRACON since the Tower was closed due to COVID-19.
Ground controller reports of a runway incursion due to emergency equipment entering the runway for the wrong aircraft.
E-175 flight crew reported an uneventful diversion due to Hazmat loading issue due to missing NOTOC at departure.
A Gulfstream IV pilot reported encountering a downdraft on short final and hitting a light pole. The aircraft landed successfully.
Air carrier Captain reported being cleared to descend below JFK Class Bravo airspace at a speed above 200 KTS.

A Jacksonville Center (ZJX) controller describes a situation where on common occurrence aircraft in the area start down earlier then he would expect in order to alleviate traffic problems in a Washington Center (ZDC) sector. Miscommunication and bad headings cause an aircraft to come close to a restricted airspace area.
Air Carrier Dispatch reported a communications breakdown between Dispatch and Ramp personnel regarding DG cargo documentation error provided to flight crew.

ZAB Controller reports of a balloon sighting reported by a pilot. Controller issues unknown traffic and tries to avoid the area with the balloon for other traffic. Controller reports information to the Front Line Manager.
PC-12 pilot reported loss of altitude and airspeed control after encountering severe turbulence descending through 9;000 feet in the vicinity of CAE.
Pilot reported taking the COVID vaccine and was unaware that it was unapproved for general use and a possible FAR violation.
GA pilot reported a track deviation during departure and cited clearance confusion as a contributing factor.

BJC Controller in Charge had to prompt the Local Controller to take appropriate action when a low altitude alert sounded in the Tower. The CIC reported the Controller did not issue the advisory to the aircraft in a timely or appropriate manner.

Center Controller reported a frequency transmitter site failure caused a lack of ability to communicate with aircraft directly in a portion of their airspace increasing their workload during a busy period due to weather deviations.
Air carrier Captain reported A80 controllers refuse to grant weather deviations around hazardous weather.
Pilot Not Flying reported encountering a NMAC with a skydiver.
Air carrier First Officer reported the SBGR ILS Rwy 09R approach chart is inaccurate and confusing.
Technician reported incorrect external markings on numerous aircraft in violation of FAR's.
Pilot reported damage to aircraft at the pressure fueling connection that was not reported to the flight crew.
First Officer reported they were receiving erroneous and unreliable VOR information during a GPS outage.
Pilot reported Near Mid Air Collision in traffic pattern.
PA-46 pilot reported an inability to get the aircraft manufacturer to provide technical guidance for required inspection procedures following an in flight structural failure.
B737 First Officer reported an airborne conflict with another aircraft shortly after takeoff from PGUM.
CRJ200 Captain reported ASOS was broadcasting inaccurate weather.
Flight Attendant reported falling down an open maintenance hatch while performing duties.
Tractor driver reported the tug began to lurch and bounce when the brakes were applied.
Air Carrier First Officer reported a disagreement with the FAR Part 117 requirement for long haul flights as it pertains to required rest breaks.
Ramp Controller reported requiring guidance on the B737-900 since the aircraft requires a tail stand.
Air carrier Captain reported that a NOTAM for OGG was confusing as to applicable conditions.
Eclipse 500 pilot reported deviating from assigned heading on arrival into BWI when he experienced wake turbulence in trail of a B737.

A pilot reported that he penetrated Class B airspace when he encountered moderate to severe turbulence and updrafts at approximately 200 feet below the bottom of class B. He had contacted ATC attempting to get Class B clearance; but had been told to standby.

B737 Captain reported pressure to accept an aircraft with inoperative auto throttles with the destination weather close to minimums. Captain reported the end result was that maintenance fixed the aircraft and they blocked out about 25 minutes late.
ZHU Controller reported a NMAC between an air carrier and a jump plane due to the proximity of the jump area to the SID routing.
B737 Captain reported concern with the tight spacing in use between departures and arrivals at SNA.
Heavy jet flight crew was unable to comply with unrealistic ATC instructions.
B-777 flight crew reported refusing cargo due to Lithium Ion Hazmat and the documentation referenced a Hazmat code that not listed in the Flight Operations Manual.
Air carrier Dispatcher reported he was unable to establish contact with his flight.
Challenger 350 Captain reported severe turbulence during climb.
Air carrier Captain reported undesirable pairing assignment along with operational fatigue.
The flight crew of a Bombardier CRJ-900 reported that they neglected to reduce thrust and passed through the assigned altitude resulted in requiring a turn to avoid traffic.
The pilot reported that the marshaller had to panic stop the pushback because of a baggage cart tug driving directly behind the aircraft.

A ZJX Controller attempted to handoff an aircraft to a neighboring facility while also maneuvering the aircraft away from a MOA. The receiving Controller was unable to immediately identify the aircraft due to conflicting beacon codes already used for ADIZ helicopter operations within the area. The problem may have arisen due to errors between ERAM and STARS not interacting correctly resulting in separate beacon codes not being tagged to incoming aircraft.
Air carrier flight crew reported a RA on approach.
Air carrier flight crew reported entering restricted military airspace and conflicting with tactical aircraft in the airspace.
C182 pilot reported VOR was out of service before the applicable NOTAM time.
ZSE Controller reported BE20 entering a higher MIA due to MIA map being difficult to read on mid-shift scale.
SLC Tower Controller reported controllers were not familiar with non-radar rules and procedures to follow during busy traffic made more complex due to a scheduled radar outage.

A B767 returned to the departure airport after the business class flight attendants became light headed. Coach flight attendants were unaffected. Forty four pounds of dry ice were in the forward cargo hold.

ZFW Center Controller reported receiving a handoff of an aircraft believed to be descending. The pilots had been issued discretion descent which delayed the descent; causing a confliction with opposite direction traffic.
Dispatcher reported working additional time under fatigued conditions due to staffing shortage.
Air carrier flight crew reported they deviated from the assigned route due to last minute runway changes and incomplete communications from ATC during arrival to DEN airport.
An aircraft on a VFR flight landing at an alternate airport due to approaching inclement weather ran off the end of the runway.
An air carrier flight crew reported the Tower failed to advise them of a tall ship off the departure end of the runway which prevented them from being able to safely depart.
Instructor pilot reported not checking the fuel status before departure resulting in a temporary loss of power due to fuel starvation in one of the tanks.
M20 pilot reported encountering an updraft which caused him to climb into TPA Class B airspace.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger would not comply with face mask policy throughout flight.
Air carrier Captain reported turning off the runway at the incorrect exit point after landing during low visibility conditions.
Flight instructor at BDR reported the Runway 11 PAPI on the right side of the runway; but the airport diagram shows it on the left side.
HCF Controller reported that they were not briefed properly on a new LOA with OGG Tower.
Air carrier Maintenance Technician reported Hazmat aircraft parts were incorrectly packaged and shipped following an aircraft field service repair.
A Cessna 172 reported that being #3 on approach he made a left 360 for spacing prior to calling the tower for clearance.
Cessna-172 Flight Instructor reported student's loss of directional control on landing resulting in a runway excursion.
A320 First Officer reported encountering severe weather/turbulence while being vectored for landing.
B737 Captain reported difficulty getting cooperation with ATC concerning which STAR transition to fly.
Air carrier flight crew reported descending below charted altitudes while on approach and cited lack of recent flying as a contributing factor.

An aircraft being vectored for an instrument approach reported the airport in sight and was cleared for a Visual Approach. TRACON relayed a message to the aircraft that they had a mandatory crossing restriction even though it no longer applied due to them conducting a Visual Approach.
EMB-175 Captain reported contacting Ramp Control for clearance into the ramp without receiving communication transfer from Tower Ground Control.

The Tower Controller asked the TRACON to break an aircraft out because there was insufficient spacing with the preceding aircraft. The TRACON Controller refused and told the Tower Controller to break the first aircraft out. After the second aircraft contacted the Tower; the Tower issued a go-around instruction and put the aircraft back on the TRACON frequency.
B737 Captain reported an ORD Ground Controller issued ambiguous taxi clearance.
Captain reported station ground personnel pressured him to accept nonstandard procedures for deicing; resulting in a possible procedural deviation.
Air Carrier Captain reported the First Officer was affected by a laser light on final to Runway 27 at KIAH.
Flight Crew evaluating new Navigation Charts recommends staying with current charts.
ZAB Controller who had just been relieved reported observing the new Controller use incorrect procedures to clear an aircraft for an approach.
Air Carrier Captain flying over the Middle East reported the GPS signal was jammed.
CRJ-200 Captain reported a NMAC with a PC-12 in the pattern at a non-towered airport.
A Ramp Service worker reported an aircraft was pushed back too far extending over a vehicle service road and into conflict with a snowplow.
Air Carrier Captain reported that new checklist response procedures and policy are interfering with cockpit and radio communications.
EMB-175 Captain reported encountering wake turbulence on visual approach to LAX Runway 24R in trail of a B737 that resulted in several system failure messages.
First Officer reported  a passengers' cell phone became lost in cabin on inbound flight creating a potential fire hazard. Maintenance ultimately retrieved prior to departure.
S46 controller reported a B737 departed on wrong code; data block; nordo; and nearing higher MVA. SEA Tower did not verify correct code with proper aircraft.
Helicopter Captain reported an NMAC during descent to destination. Received TA followed by RA.
NCT TRACON Controller told an aircraft to fly heading 180. The pilot misunderstood the instruction and initiated a 360 degree turn putting it into conflict with traffic behind it on downwind.
Air Carrier Captain reported during passenger boarding one of the flight attendants received a call from a physician reporting a positive COVID-19 test result.
Air carrier Captain reported pushing back before brakes were release causing the tow bar shear pin to break.
Flight Attendant reported that another Flight Attendant assigned a non-rev to a seat that was against current policy for social distancing.
HCF TRACON Controller reported a new procedure has been implemented at their facility which does not ensure required wake turbulence separation.
Ground personnel reported that persons exposed to COVID-19 were allowed to continue to work.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger was argumentative about wearing a face mask during the flight and also would not adhere to social distancing.
Air carrier flight crew reported taking unauthorized deviation to avoid smoke and turbulence.
ZLA Controller reports of becoming overloaded with traffic during a weather session.  No coordination had been made reference headings or altitudes.  Aircraft were just handed off to the Controller.
Air carrier Captain reported failing to secure galley carts on takeoff for a ferry flight.
A pilot reported a tug driver and ramp crew did not follow proper procedures during pushback.
CRJ-700 Captain reported executing a go-around after receiving a windshear aural alert on approach to LGA.
Air Carrier Ground Personnel reported that several Dangerous Goods shipments were not documented correctly on the manifest.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with cleaners sanitizing the aircraft too quickly.
Air Carrier First Officer reported calling in fatigued due to chronic and acute fatigue.
Pilot stated SQL Tower does not issue correct ATIS information; nor comply with ATIS information.
Air carrier Captain reported a flap overspeed on departure climbout.
Air Carrier pilot reported that the 'climb via' verbiage used for climbing instructions is flawed.
B767 First Officer reported the Captain used improper taxi procedures causing the taxiway excursion.
A flight attendant reported that the company issued uniform made her ill.

The Captain of a regional jet reported that 2 extra static wicks were installed on a recently replaced winglet. After consulting with maintenance personnel it was determined that the aircraft was airworthy and was dispatched under a Part 91 ferry permit.
A319 Captain reported that damaged skin on an engine pylon was illegally deferred.
Customer Service employee reported lack of sanitizing spray in the airport lobby.

Flight attendant reported that the Flight Attendants' Manual fails to address cabin lighting matching outside environment; thus posing a risk to both the cabin crew and passengers in the event of an emergency evacuation.
A pilot reported encountering wake turbulence on approach to BOS in trail of a B737. Reporter stated he would have been happier with 'descend at pilot's discretion' clearance.
SoCal TRACON Controller reported false targets appearing on the scopes on a daily basis and thinks it might be related to a STARs problem.
Helicopter pilot reported potential bed bug contamination on helicopter and crew.
B737-700 flight crew reported crossing the hold short line on Taxiway E at Runway 28C in PIT; in part because the hold short lines are not displayed correctly on the Jeppesen chart.
Pilot reports of being under flight following with SDL Tower and then being dropped with very limited airspace to maneuver and avoid DVT Class Delta airspace.
Aircrew used non-standard methods to clean wing surfaces of snow.
Air carrier Captain reported distraction while being issued a revised clearance while taxiing. Ground Control provided clearance to cross a runway; but the aircraft was past the hold short lines.
A320 Captain reported BNA Ground was not using charted low visibility taxi routes while visibility was less than 1200 RVR.
Air Carrier Captain reported a track heading deviation during arrival to CLT airport due to similar sounding fix names CEDOX and DEFOX.
Helicopter crew experienced a near miss with a drone which required evasive action.
MKC Tower Controller reported the Approach Controller advised them of a Low Altitude Alert for an aircraft on a Visual Approach.
B777 Captain reported ground crew did not follow SOP during pushback.

A Corporate flight crew became distracted when SFO issued a new SID and departure runway while taxiing at night. Thinking that Taxiway L was Runway 1R; the takeoff was begun. The Captain detected the error and rejected the takeoff.
Boston Center Controller reported that during a high volume traffic session an aircraft entered another sectors airspace without a handoff.

An IFR Helicopter flying the SBN ILS 9R IMC was vectored too close to the final approach fix which caused erratic autopilot behavior. The pilot executed a missed approach and was vectored back for a successful approach. While enroute SBN; communications with Center was erratic because of low altitude flight.

Air carrier recently changed show time for wide body aircraft and reduced the amount of time for flight attendants to conduct preflight safety checks. The flight had three infants and the appropriate amount of infant life vest were not aboard the aircraft until brought by Maintenance.
TRACON Controller reported an aircraft descended faster than the Controller anticipatedresulting in a MVA violation.
B737 Captain reported he was put under pressure by operations personnel to operate a flight into a below-minimums airport.

An air carrier crew at FL380 transiting the HAAA and HSSS FIR's attempted radio contact for a weather avoidance clearance but were unable. The Captain then deviated and climbed to avoid a nearby aircraft before re-establishing ATC communications. An incorrect enroute chart frequency listing contributed to errors.
Air carrier Captain reported many passengers refuse to wear face masks during flights to a specific international destination.

SAN Tower Front Line Manager reported of duplicate aircraft identifications in the SAN and LAX Area.  Someone was attempting to use a call sign; saw that it was being used; but dropped the data tag anyways.
Flight crew reported climbing 500 feet above assigned block altitudes and cited COVID-19 conversation distractions and fatigue as contributing factors.

ZMA controller reported C501 was issued a descent clearance; yet Controller's display showed a climb which pilot report determined inaccurate. Controller stated several instances of this since ERAM and ADS-B.
An ERJ pilot reported that the misuse of ACARS by dispatchers can be a safety issue.
BD700 Captain reported they were uncomfortable with the departure/arrival procedures used that create opposite direction conflicting traffic scenarios at ASE airport.

As he prepared to taxi; a FBO Lineman notified the BE-400XP Captain the GPU plug into the aircraft overheated and damaged the aircraft's ground electrical connector. The connector was deferred by Maintenance.

A80 Controller reports of an aircraft slowing on final prior to the FAF; reporting it to the Front Line Manager (FLM); and the FLM not doing anything about it. Reporter then goes on to tell this is happening all the time with this one company and nothing is being done about it. Weather is a factor on final.
A Corporate pilot reported they were questioned by FAA Investigators about their company operation.
ZBW Center controllers reported a Controller requested their Assist Position be opened; but was initially denied due to lack of staffing.
Flight Attendant reported concerns with another Flight Attendant bringing a passenger on the shuttle bus and flight during the COVID-19 pandemic.

ZOA Controller reported of an aircraft that was in a lower Minimum IFR Altitude (MIA) then it should have been. Controller called transferring facility; but they advised the aircraft was NORDO. Reporter tried to contact aircraft; eventually did and climbed aircraft to a safe altitude.

The B757 Captain reported on a Part 91 ferry flight that he said pushed FAR legalities and compromised safety. He was having a difficult time staying awake and noticed other crewmembers who had fallen asleep.
C172 pilot reported that he misunderstood the instrument departure clearance.
Light aircraft pilot reported a tree poses a possible hazard on approach to X26 Runway 10.
Technician reported ordering the wrong part for installation on an A321 aircraft.
ZMA controllers reported passing incorrect altitude information to oceanic sector due to workload and misinterpreting flight progress strip marking.
Captain reports that ground crews at ORD are not properly trained in aircraft deicing procedures.

A Pilot began a descent to cross CARTR at 14;000 feet on the SLC Brigham City Three Arrival but passing 15;000 feet was told return to 16;000 feet. The confused pilot commented ATC clears aircraft to cross CARTR at various altitudes but 14;000 feet for turboprops works best.
B737 NG flight crew reported departing illegally due to weather.
Corporate flight crew reported an increased workload on departure when they received a different SID clearance from Tower than what was received from Clearance Delivery.

An A319 crew departed a foreign airport after a two hour delay and a flight plan change which resulted in the FMC dropping the departure airport and flight plan. Because the performance pages were not rebuilt the V-speed and flex power did not set with the TOGA selection; but the crew continued the takeoff.
EUG TRACON Controller reported an unsafe situation when a heavy aircraft approached MFR without contact with Approach and they had to sequence a commercial jet in behind the heavy.
B-757 flight crew reported encountering severe turbulence during departure from Newark; which caused an overshoot of assigned altitude.
Air carrier Captain reported track heading deviation during approach to OBBS airport due to the Jeppesen database not reflecting specific airport procedures.
Dispatcher reported the company is not following the agreed upon 'dispatch workload model' and the workload is too high.
B737-700 First Officer reported discrepancy between CPDLC clearance; and ATC clearance.
Corporate First Officer reported an unstable approach occurred and cited lack of flying as a contributing factor.
Two ZMA controllers discuss interfacility coordination procedures for aircraft out of MIA during high traffic departure period when foul weather limits available departure paths.
Air carrier First Officer reported departing without the correct flight load closeout paperwork.

CVG Developmental Controller training on Local reported a runway incursion when the landing aircraft turned at the wrong intersection to exit then back onto the runway causing the departure to be canceled and another arrival sent around.
Air carrier flight crew experienced non-standard procedures from ground personnel while parking aircraft at SFO gate.
Air carrier Captain reported that incorrect 10-7 chart information on ACARS availability led to rushed manual weight and balance computation resulting in a minor error.
Air Carrier Captain Flying A321 aircraft reported NMAC after takeoff.
Air Taxi First Officer reported flight crew inadvertently departed without complete required Hazmat documents.
C182 pilot reported a Near Mid Air Collision on downwind with an opposite direction SR22.
ZSE ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft encountered downdrafts which pushed the aircraft below the Minimum IFR Altitudes on the airway.

Air carrier First Officer reported a fuel imbalance in flight required the fuel crossfeed valve to be cycled twice before balancing took place. FO noted that aircraft had been in storage for several months.
Small transport aircraft First Officer reported concerns about circling approach criteria at RBG airport.

B737NG flight crew reported experiencing Yaw Damper and Flap extension failures upon initial flap selection on approach. Crew reported confusion in attempting to decide which QRH procedure to use and offered recommended changes to the QRH.
B737-800 flight crew reported they did not comply with an ATC assigned speed.
The flight crew of a regional jet reported a traffic conflict resulting in an RA. The TCAS evasive maneuver was followed and the aircraft returned to the original clearance.
ZFW Controller reports of an operational error caused by him thinking an aircraft was coming off of a different runway than it did.
B777 flight crew reported descending to altitude assigned by ATC; which was an error and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude. ATC Caught the error and issued crew a higher altitude.
ZSE Center Controller reported an aircraft was forced to descend below MVA to escape icing conditions.
A TRACON Controller reported a pilot on his frequency lost control of their aircraft due to vertigo but with the Controller's assistance was able to regain control and land safely.
Air carrier pilot reported that he was close to declaring minimum fuel because of inadequate reserves and unrealistic flight planning combined with arrival vectoring.
A320 Captain expressed confusion over brake release procedures when using a new type of tug.

An aircraft was vectored and sequenced to a RNAV Approach. The first vector was to intercept an intermediate approach segment and above glideslope. This aircraft was again vectored to intercept the RNAV approach. The vectors were issued late and too close to the final approach fix. The intercept angle exceeded the prescribed angle for intercept of a RNAV Approach. The aircraft was told to descend to cross a fix at an altitude it was 700 feet below. The aircraft eventually proceeded via their own navigation to intercept and fly a stable approach. The reporter states that ATC service at this facility are routinely unsafe.

B737 flight crew reported ingesting a large bird at 75 knots on the takeoff roll and rejecting. Upon examination by Maintenance; the left engine was found to have at least three damaged fan blades and the aircraft was taken out of service.
Technician reported that a Boeing 777 made an unscheduled landing at a non-maintenance station and required a repetitive maintenance inspection. Proper documentation procedures were not followed.

An air carrier crew on vectors to DFW Runway 17C and descending to 5;000 ft followed a TCAS RA from an aircraft at 5;500 ft. The aircraft was in Class B; known to ATC but not reported to the pilots prior to the TCAS alert.

IAH Tower Controller reported of an aircraft going around due to wake from the preceding departure. Controller turned go-around traffic to avoid more of the wake. Controller realized that there should have been a larger hole to depart the heavy.
Ramp employee reported a break-away during pushback was caused by a tow bar head being loose in order to fit two different type of aircraft.
Global Express flight crew reported encountering severe mountain wave in the vicinity of the Front Range of the Rockies.
B737-800 Captain reported returning to the departure airport after noticing vibration in climb. On the ground; Maintenance reported 'major core damage' to the #1 engine from a bird strike.
A TRACON Controller reported receiving successive departures form the Tower with less than required separation.
SAT TRACON Controller reported unsafe operations between aircraft landing Runway 13R and the nearby non-towered field 5C1.

ZID Center Controller reported a lack of available map display to assist while working an aircraft that had an electrical failure. The Controller also reported difficulty contacting CMH approach for coordination.
Air carrier Captain reported Dispatch had changed the flight block time without communicating the change with the Captain.

Pilot reports after landing and complying with instructions to taxi from Local. Pilot switches to Ground Control and is asked why they did not contact him first and to stop. Pilot was told to make a 180 and taxi into another ramp area; wait for passing traffic; and then taxi as instructed. Pilot was confused as to what they should be doing.

A pilot conducting practice approaches to FDY Runway 36 in IMC at 2;300 FT; reported being issued a low altitude alert even though the GPS and glideslope appeared normal. The pilot executed a precautionary go-around; but later could find no cause for the alert.
EMB-175 First Officer reported that they had turbulence during approach then they got a windshear warning and had to go-around.
Ramp worker reported SOP and safety were disregarded in the rush to get a flight out on time.
BE36 Pilot reported that on a night landing currency flight at a towered airport; he landed on the wrong runway.

B777 First Officer reported tow crew pulling aircraft into a gate not approved for B777 aircraft.
Atlanta Center Controller reported an unsafe situation that resulted in a loss of separation.
GEG TRACON Controller reported being unaware that an organization was conducting a powered parachute event in their airspace.
Air carrier Captain commented on the critical phase of the landing rollout as not the best time to issue detailed taxi instructions.
B767-300 flight crew endured one pack operation in hot weather resulting in degraded avionics. System was restored to normal configuration.
Air carrier Captain reported the removal of two dangerous goods containers from aircraft due to strong fumes.
Boston Center Controller reported communication problems with Canada ATC during a Non-Automated hand off.
A319 flight crew reported a ground conflict with a 777 during taxi to gate.
PA28 Instructor Pilot reported an NMAC with another aircraft in the vicinity of 18AZ airport.

ORD Local Controller and responsible pilots reported an unsafe situation due to a readback-hearback error. Line up and wait traffic attempted to depart while wake vortices from the previous departure may have still been present.

After servicing the engine the aircraft experienced an engine failure in flight. This happened because the oil filter was not inspected for metal. Further investigation reviled that the oil filter was full of metal.
ERJ-175 Flight Attendant reported broken ankle related to turbulence encounter.
Captain reported that on third leg of the day with an inoperative APU; it became too hot for safe operations and crew refused extension.
Air carrier Captain reported being advised of a near miss near SXQ; possibly during an ADF approach. TCAS had not detected the event.
An air carrier Load Planner reported that there was no automatic notification of an illegal overweight condition prior to flight.
DPA Tower Controller inadvertently deleted the Radar Data Tag of an aircraft in the TRACON's airspace.

During pushback; the ground crew cleared pilots for engine start then requested to open the forward cargo door for late bags. The request was denied; but the ground crew opened the door anyway. The #2 engine start had fortunately been delayed.
An aircraft on final to SFO made two go arounds because of conflicting traffic. One was a crossing runway departure traffic and the second was an aircraft not clear of the runway.
ZOB ARTCC Controller reported that management allowed under staffing against facility operations requirements; consequently compromising safety.
Air carrier Flight Attendant reported concern with new Flight Attendant staffing not providing adequate cabin security coverage.
Pilot reported Ground Controllers are using aircraft's position instead of their call sign while issuing taxi instructions.

A Controller's sector and workload became oversaturated with traffic due to weather deviations and aircraft needing routing corrections. The Controller stated the Traffic Management Unit did not effectively manage the traffic flows through their airspace.
TRACON Controller reported the adjacent TRACON handed them off an aircraft on the wrong route and below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.
A helicopter pilot reported losing GPS reception shortly after departing a helipad near CRQ. Reception returned after about 2-3 minutes.

A B737-900 Flight Attendant reported that the flight attendants experienced headaches and became cognitively foggy and fuzzy during flight. Shortly after the flight landed; reportedly; they were all administered oxygen and all exhibited elevated carbon monoxide levels in their blood.
BJC Controller in Charge (CIC) was also working Local Control did not notice that an arriving aircraft was aligned to land on the wrong runway.
ATL Tower Controller reported of target jumps due to the Fusion RADAR which contributed to a loss of separation.

A ZHU Center Controller reported of having an aircraft at the wrong altitude. The aircraft climbed to an altitude it was not supposed to and a conflict arose with another aircraft. The reporter called the other center for information but they did not have any. The controller had to relay through a third aircraft to get things straightened out.
DA-2000 flight crew reported an NMAC on arrival into TEB; citing airspace structure; frequency congestion; and controller workload as contributing.
Pilot reported finding bird droppings in the cockpit and throughout the aircraft after aircraft had come out of long-term storage.
A pilot reported that RSW METAR at times was inaccurate with outdated conditions or conditions which have not occurred.

B737 Captain reported sighting a large drone while being vectored for an instrument approach near ONT.

Air carrier pilot expressed concern about the safety aspect of the FAA's extension of the landing currency requirement from 90 to 180 days.
Air carrier Captain reported the TPA airport inner part of Taxiway G on the ramp is mislabeled as Taxiway A on the 10-9 page.
A Local Controller Trainee cleared an aircraft for takeoff and issued a right turn towards a helicopter in the pattern upwind.
Air carrier Captain reported miscommunication with ATC resulted in a taxiway incursion during taxi-out.
A Hatz CB-1 pilot ground looped his aircraft in a crosswind causing slight damage when the right wing impacted a fence.
B737 Captain reported being confused by a 'climb via' clearance from ATC that did not agree with his PDC.

Crew faulted the urgency from Tower to move the aircraft and recommended that local procedures be changed so that flight clearances are not broadcast to crews over the busy tower frequency.

I90 Controller reported a loss of Class B separation due to the application of a confusing facility procedure.

A B737 Weight and Balance blocked Row 39 but during flight the aircraft was pitch sensitive and the Pitch Trim system activated trim during approach at low power. Balance may have been near the limit the entire flight.

A CE-560 Captain taxied the left and nose gear off a ramp onto the adjacent grass at night with moderate snow in limited visibility while the First Officer copied a new taxi clearance. The ramp and snow covered grass blended together.

Flight crew reported missing a NOTAM concerning a closed portion of the takeoff runway.
Air carrier Captain reported experiencing an airspeed deviation and cited fatigue and lack of recent flying experience as contributing factors.
A C182 pilot reported they became disoriented in the clouds and temporarily lost control of the aircraft.
B737-800 Captain reported refusing an aircraft due to weather conditions that appeared to make departing unsafe.
Pilot reported receiving a low altitude warning from ATC while on the ILS 27 into IAH; which they attributed to the change from VNAV to approach mode.
ZTL ARTCC Controller reported an aircraft descended below its assigned and acknowledged altitude assignment head on in to opposite direction traffic.
Air carrier Captain reported being distracted and not contacting the ramp before taxiing to the gate.
Air carrier Captain reported they could not establish a visual with the runway at LGA after receiving vectors to presumably comply with noise abatement.

A pilot of a vintage single engine aircraft reported striking a deer during landing rollout. No injuries were reported to either the pilot or his passenger; however the aircraft did sustain visible damage to exterior components.

ZMP Controller reported an operation involving another center and the possible rules they broke attempting to transfer the aircraft to the new center. Reporter also stated the Front Line Manager observed this operation and did nothing about it.
Air Carrier Captain encountered severe turbulence during climbout.
Gulfstream V flight crew reported they refused a descent clearance because it would have placed them into convective weather.
Tower Controller reported that the local control used only one source for wind speed when clearing an aircraft for takeoff. Aircraft had control problems due to high wind and aborted takeoff.

A320 Captain reported that when the parking brake was set with only one engine operating; the brake pressure dropped out of the green arc and generated an ECAM for Low Parking Brake Pressure or Low Accumulator Pressure.
A320 Flight Crew reported severe turbulence after clipping a benign looking cloud.
DSM TRACON Controller reported using an emergency obstruction vector map to assist a disoriented pilot in IMC.

Corporate pilot reported flying the T30 Airport GPS-A 10 DME arc between OXUKE and FEELS at 2500 feet as charted. ATC instructed that his aircraft should have remained at 3000 feet.
Instructor pilot reported an NMAC in the pattern at EYE airport.
Pilot reported an aircraft over flew their aircraft while on departure roll.
Captain activated TOGA intending to activate frequency select; which resulted in an overspeed and altitude deviation.

CLT controllers reported of an aircraft departing on a RNAV departure that turned towards another aircraft. Aircraft was supposed to turn right but turned left into other departing traffic. Controller called the aircraft but they had switched to departure control.

A TRACON routinely issues routes to aircraft that transit a known Jump Zone. There is a procedure in place to issue routes to aircraft away from the Jump Zone. According to the reporter the TRACON routinely does not comply with these procedures.
Airline Baggage Room personnel reported Detachable Lithium Ion powered wheelchair arrived in bag room with batteries attached and no required documentation.
Experimental aircraft pilot reported that that  a hydraulic system malfunction resulted in a gear-up landing.
Hawaii Enroute Oceanic Controller reported their facility policy is misinterpreting the rules regarding aircraft handling when transitioning from a radar to a non radar environment.
B777 Captain reported a discrepancy between checklists and the flight manual while attempting to correct a fuel imbalance.
Maintenance Technician reported an aircraft sanitizing vendor started spraying the aircraft while maintenance personal were still on board. Technician reports one mechanic became ill.
A pilot departing a non-towered controlled airport climbing under Class C airspace reported being vectored away from air carrier traffic immediately upon contact with the TRACON.
SNA Tower Controller reported being unable to provide required Class C airspace services due to a radar outage.
B737 Captain reported having difficulties coordinating pushback procedure with the ramp personnel; citing this has been an ongoing trend in Latin American airports.
Helicopter pilot reported an NMAC in the traffic pattern when a jet trainer made an unannounced high-speed low pass over the runway next to the helicopter.

BOI Approach Controller reported of an unsafe situation when one aircraft did not have the same routing as the Controller did. The pilot turned unexpectedly on a different route than the Controller was expecting.
B757 flight crew reported a communication breakdown between flight crew and aircraft fueler regarding fuel load imbalance.
ERJ-175 First Officer reported executing a maneuver to evade a windshear during final approach; which was immediately followed by severe turbulence.

JFK Local Controller reported an aircraft turned to exit the runway at a closed and blocked intersection. The next arrival had to be sent around. In the go around the aircraft now became a conflict for two other aircraft. Eventually the go around traffic comes back and lands. Controller reported that he thought the Port Authority should have done a better job clearing the intersections from snow.
Flight Crew flying CRJ-200 aircraft encounter challenging night time approach.
Maintenance Technician reported being pressured to reinstall a known inoperative part when the replacement part was unavailable.

A80 Departure Control in Charge (CIC) requests a single flow off of ATL airport due to weather. He coordinates with the Tower and reports that after the coordination the departures are still coming off the airport as normal operations dictate.
B767 First Officer reported low visibility and communications breakdown between flight crew and ATC resulted in ground conflict.
Flight Attendant reported a passenger did not comply with face mask policy throughout the flight.
B737 First Officer reported encountering severe turbulence at FL390 that resulted in 20-40 knot speed deviations and a loss of 800 ft of altitude.

Technicians reported that appropriate AMM references were overridden by Supervisor and the engine mounts and access panels were damaged.
A321 First Officer reported flap overspeed during a coupled approach with a tailwind.
B737 First Officer reported that the the QRH reference was incorrect for an engine over-temp.

Reporter stated heavy training traffic over DSD VOR often results in TCAS RAs for air carrier aircraft. This is reportedly a known issue; but there is no solution yet.
Fractional First Officer reported ATC would not approve a deviation for weather resulted in an airborne conflict and severe turbulence encounter.

While descending on the ORD FYTTE2; ATC issued a 300 knot speed restriction. When the B777 Captain modified the waypoint speed constraints it caused LNAV and VNAV to disconnect. The aircraft descended below profile and deviated from the desired heading until manually corrected.
B737 flight crew reported that they received an EGPWS sink rate warning while on final approach.

ZMP Controller reported an airspace violation because the controller did not ensure the handoff was routed to the correct sector. The reporter thought the En Route Automation Modernization computer would send it to the correct sector based on the interim altitude.
A Helicopter TV crew reported a near miss at 2;000 feet as they departed a balloon festival after providing aerial coverage.
ZDC Controller reported management and TMU failure to support controllers during turbulence and severe weather; resulting in priority handling situations.

Captain of a wide body international flight reported he blocked in at LAX at XA:45 and the Gate Agent would not open the cabin door until XB:00; reportedly because of U.S. Customs restrictions. Captain expressed concerns about the safety implications if an evacuation became necessary.
A flight crew flying the MDW RNAV (RNP) RWY 13C were previously cleared to 3;000 feet on the FISSK 3 RNAV Arrival which put them below the mandatory 4;000 feet at OOPLA on the approach.
Airline First Officer reported lost com; NORDO; with ATC due to being unfamiliar with the local operation.
TRACON Controller vectoring an aircraft in a box pattern pattern vectored the aircraft below the Minimum Vectoring Altitude.

Maintenance Personnel reported that when they were testing the Pitot/Static system on a Bombardier CRJ; they did not deactivate the anti-ice heater properly.
A320 First Officer reported that the ACARS bleeds through on active radio frequency and causing a communications difficulties between flight crew and ATC; and it is an occupational hazard.

EMB-175 Flight Attendant reported suffering an injury during unexpected turbulence.
B737 Captain reported that an aircraft was scheduled to fly despite the fact that the APU was on MEL for four prior legs and was not ETOPS capable.

B737 Captain reported flying to an airport with high winds and heavy snow. The ramp winds were too high to allow the cargo door to be opened. Captain flying in the cabin reported that flight crew did not have two operation flashlights installed; requiring an MEL and delaying the flight.
A Maintenance Technician reported that during an examination of the rudder rod ends on an A320; a discrepancy was found that the rod ends that were previously installed were not safety wired.

A Pilot and Ramp Agent reported that their company is outsourcing station staffing which resulted in this flight departing with a 1;350 pound weight and balance error. The replacement personnel do not know how to operate the weight and balance data entry system.

CCR Tower Controller reports of two fast moving military aircraft that are not on Tower frequency; but have been pointed out. Air carrier flight crew reported they experienced a windshear alert on final to RJAA; executed a go-around; and diverted.
EMB-145 Flight crew reported receiving a bad braking action report; and upon touchdown had trouble controlling the aircraft and ended up sliding off the left side of the runway.

Dispatcher who was conducting training had one flight experience a maintenance issue; another aircraft diverted and he missed a call (received by the trainee) concerning a pilot reporting unable to take a flight due to a qualification issue.
